Comment: This is just an over hyped , money minting business tourism in Vietnam. Nothing special about this place and fully crowded with different cruise ships.

First of all: the boat, the service, the food, communication with Lucas, the transport was a 11/10 everything was outstanding!The reason why we weren't able to enjoy the trip is that the overtourism and the pollution is so so bad, that we felt so bad being part of the problem. When the ship is moving you'll already see the plastic floating around now and then, but as soon as you anchor for the night all we were able to breathe was the scent of petroleum and we had to close our door as the sound of the motor of the ships was too noisy. The pollution in the water is in such stark contrast to the beauty of the landscape, it is really a crime that such a unique natural place is not better protected.We left feeling disappointed — not because of the staff or the service, which were amazing — but because the environmental situation overshadowed everything else. Ha Long Bay deserves stronger protection and more responsible tourism.
Halong is beautiful, but there are between 75-80 boats on the water looking more or less the same places. The water is dirty with garbage and the shores are floating with plastic

An overtouristed and spoilt gem, completely ruined by greed and exploitation.!We are currently  On a cruise boat moored in a bay being deafened by the extremely loud disco from another boat and unable to sleep. It’s unbearable and nothing to do with nature. Today we stopped at a ghastly overpopulated polluted beach which was load and a nightmare. Vietnam should be ashamed of themselves for making this beautiful place a hell hole.

Beyond that everything has to be paid for (if you want to drink water during a meal you have to buy and if you bring from home you have to pay a fine for it at the price of the water itself, as well as for any possible drinking), the staff is not nice, unfriendly, on the kayak tour the guide told my friend that she was fat and heavy and that is why she can't get up. Beyond the body description he gave her far from light years of reality he said it in an offensive and bad way. The guide was nervous and after we were three minutes late, he threatened not to let us go on the kayaks. This is my experience so far from the first three hours of cruising (4 stars, 24 hours, one night)The view is beautiful and amazing in its beauty but the experience under any criticism

Every part of Halong bay we visited inc paddling around islands for hours was full of garbage. On the surface of the water, beaches, rocky out crops and cliffs.Landed up getting skin viruses from paddling in the water.Would never return.Looks better on the computer, Reality garbage, pollution and contaminated water.. Would recommend going somewhere cleaner

Let this paradise take a break please. It is crowded with huge amounts of tourists and boats. Water is so dirty that makes your stay so sad to see that incredible place so bad. It would be amazing to control and limit the amount of travelers in that place, and make everyone respect the nature.

After visiting Halong Bay, I can honestly say it was one of the biggest travel disappointments of my Vietnam trip. While the limestone scenery is beautiful in photos, the actual experience feels extremely commercialized, overcrowded, and frankly, not worth the money or time.From the moment you arrive, it’s clear that Halong Bay has become a mass tourism machine. Hundreds of boats line up in the same areas, fighting for space. The “quiet” parts they promise on the tours? Filled with noise from other boats, tourists yelling, and diesel fumes. Any sense of peace or natural wonder gets drowned out quickly.The water, which looks stunning in pictures, is actually littered with plastic in many areas – a heartbreaking sight. Activities like kayaking and swimming, which should feel adventurous, end up feeling rushed, cramped, and at times, unsafe due to the sheer number of boats.To make it worse, many of the tours (even the “luxury” ones) are built around upselling you: overpriced drinks, packaged excursions to tacky caves or crowded beaches, and photo ops rather than real experiences. It all feels incredibly staged and soulless.Vietnam has so many other places where you can experience true beauty, authenticity, and peace – without the crowds, scams, and disappointment. Places like Ninh Binh (the “Halong Bay on land”), Phong Nha, or even the less-visited parts of the central coast offer stunning nature without feeling like a factory.Bottom line: Halong Bay might be a UNESCO World Heritage Site, but today it’s mostly a tourist trap. Save yourself the hassle – your time and money are much better spent elsewhere in Vietnam.

Ha Long Bay I'm sure was once a paradise, but now full of raw sewage from the 500+ cruise ships that reside there for tourists.  You can clearly see the sewage, you have to kayak through it and swim in it.  Might explain why we heard people vomiting from our room overnight.  Rooms tired, food average.  We expected rubbish but not the sewage issue, was disgusting, the Unesco site ruined by disrespectful tourism.  We left feeling sad.

And each time, die a little...Poor halong berry, suffocated by mass tourism and so guilty of helping to stifle it even further.The site is undeniably remarkable but the view is spoiled by hundreds of boats.And as if that were not enough, the bay was hit by Typhoon Yagi in 2024, leaving behind large amounts of garbage that still floats today all over the bay, what a desolation ...The emerald water that made it famous has turned to a brownish hue, a sad observation that alters the splendor of the place.It would be urgent to limit access to it to preserve its magic, What is UNESCO waiting for to do??As for the city of Halong, it resembles a ghost city, a succession of empty buildings stretching as far as the eye can see. You'd think you were on the set of The Walking Dead.I couldn’t wait to see this heavenly bay... and I leave with a heavy heart, saddened to have discovered it in this state.
beautiful scenery that now presents itself as a dump! I was horrified at how the bay has changed in the last 20 years - since my last visit! Disgusting!

Ha Long Bay is the exact power of human destruction! The greatest expectation and the greatest disappointment of my trip. I counted over 100 cruise boats, with hundreds of tourists. According to the guide it is only possible 2 routes through the islands. My roadmap leads to a beach where you have the option of a climb of more than 350 steps for a view of the landscape or stay below.The beach is a horror, dirty, hundreds of people, smells urine. At night the ships anchor in the bay and you can hear the sound of the other boats. The next day they take a walk in caves quite gracelessly . I threw money out and you're disappointed.

Perfect organization. We stayed 2 days on cruise. Ship equipped with all comforts with a very kind and professional crew.The beauty of the site is totally undone by the disastrous conditions of the sea, victim of constant, daily pollution of organic waste spilled, without any restraint, from cruise ships, changing its color to brown.Obviously the air is affected by this state, becoming unbreathable. Add to that the plastic everywhere. And what does UNESCO do?

The desire to see Halong Bay was always huge, but the disappointment was even greater. The bay is extremely dirty and polluted. You can see the oil in the water, thestrong smell and the streams of dirt coming through the water. It's very sad, total failure. The shipping companies do nothing for nature, which is destroyed. From afar it looks pretty, when you get close it's regrettable. I do not recommend visiting.

One thing first: Avoid! Hundreds of boats, noise, nothing to do on the boats, cheap buffet food, dirty water with loads of plastic bottles. You're literally 30 years too late. Unfortunately all Vietnam attractions (not even talking about the stupid. European villages in the hills) are by now subject to mass tourism. The offers of "small group" is mis-leading because there are hundreds of "small groups".  Vietnam is totally overrated, full with "budget" tourist who only want their Facebook moment and pay nothing for it. It probably needs another 20 years to clean up its act (and its air, lakes, rivers, beaches etc).

During September 2023 my wife and myself spent three very pleasant weeks travelling through Vietnam visiting numerous places. However, the overnight cruse through Halong Bay was very disturbing in various ecological ways.The coastal waters of the bay are filled with rubbish and the scum and breakdown into microplastics is clear to see. The shear number of diesel chugging tour boats was far more than you see on any trip advisor photo. Due to the shear numbers of visitors to Halong Bay you get moved around like sheep and was the most worst experience of our 3 weeks.Vietnam is a beautiful, beautiful place with wonderful people, but please reduce the visitors and pollution in Halong Bay or it will be lost in a eco disaster.
I think it's over rated and dirty. It's very touristic and most places try to take advantage of the tourists. Worst experience we had so far in Vietnam.Also, most expensive so far!!

Hauling Bay was the only disappointment of our trip to Vietnam. This area is so overrun with tourists it cannot be enjoyed. You imagine this beautiful, serene landscape as you prepare your visit. But instead, you are met with throngs of people and boats. It is literally madness, a complete circus!There were boats everywhere. The kayaking was a parade of kayaks. The speedboat was extra and the driver turned it into an amusement ride with some crazy driving. It’s not about the thrill, it’s about the amazing scenery!Boats would fight for position at these massive docks for kayaking, the hike, the beach, etc. because there were too many boats and not enough dock space. I felt headed the entire day. The beach? You are literally standing side by side with all the others visiting the beach. There is no chance of swimming but you would t want to, the water is so sadly polluted. I’m sorry to write such a harsh review, but this day seemed about 72 hours long for my family and me. We were exhausted by the time we returned and all shared strong feelings of disappointment. My advice is to enjoy Halong Bay via the photos - that’s as good as it’s ever going to be!

June 2024 trip to Halong Bay. Spent one night in a so-called cruise junk. What a disillusionment, we are shocked by the mass tourism and the pollution in the bay. It is the horror. Hundreds of tourist boats, tons of floating waste (plastic bottles, plastic bags, polystyrene, etc... Traces of diesel...) it's filthy. However, the site is magnificent and classified as a UNESCO world heritage site. We will not return to this again and do not want to be complicit in this mass destruction. We will talk about it to those around us. We didn't like it at all.

Halong Bay is certainly something special and is also on the UNESCO cultural list. You should visit it if you are already traveling in Vietnam. So we booked a 2 day/1 night cruise. The tour operator and the travel agency were very competent and helpful. The cruise itself was also very well organized with transfers, a good travel guide, good food and very nice cabins. It was a 5* atmosphere. No question about it. But I didn't like what we experienced in Halong Bay at all. It's about a tourism industry with masses of tourists, hundreds of ships traveling on Halong Bay, dirty waters full of garbage and plastic and shore excursions that I wasn't interested in at all. Undoubtedly mass processing! And you don't get to see much of the beautiful scenery either because you're busy on the boat most of the time or the weather isn't playing along. OK, it was a bad decision. But we were a very good group with a few people and we made the best of it. Conclusion: If you don't visit Halong Bay, you haven't missed anything.

Our Vietnam trip included a flowery visit to Halong Bay with a boat trip and overnight stay on the boat. We found these two days to be the worst travel experience in many years. In an extremely overcrowded tourist situation, we were taken with countless other people on countless other horrible and ancient boats on a completely run-down hotel ship through the bay, which was _everywhere_ completely dirty and littered. Visiting this bay is a perversion of tourism, more than questionable in terms of safety and really not recommended. Our indescribably run-down boat even had relatively nice rooms, and the crew and the food were also decent. But that cannot disguise the fact that all other aspects of this event, from the environment to the experience value, lead to only one conclusion: don't go there. Simply skip this part of Vietnam.
So overcrowded with tourists that the waters are now visibly dirty and full of debris. Give this beautiful place a break.

DON'T go to Halong bay. It is completely contaminated and filled with cruises. Tourism has destroyed what probably once was a paradise. It was a waste of our time. The cruises treat you like cattle and have one group of clients after another. They clean the rooms in maybe an hour and then expect the next group of tourists. We saw how they hung our used bathrobes back in the closet. That's how little time they have to prepare. We traveled with Regina Cruises, but it's prob the same for all others. NONE of the cruises look like in the photos either. I would advise to avoid Halong bay all together, not worth the huge amount of money spent

We were in Vietnam over Easter. And according to the motto, you can't visit Paris without the Eiffel Tower and the Louvre, we drove to Ha Long Bay. The picture that presented itself to us was frightening and has nothing to do with the beautiful pictures you find in the travel guides. Thousands upon thousands of tourists are funneled through the bay. The water is heavily polluted with garbage and plastic. Sometimes you get the impression that the boats simply open their latrines. At the end you have the opportunity to go swimming, but only a few people dare to go into the water here. Vietnam is a wonderful country, but if you care about our nature, avoid this bay. And let's be honest, Paris is worth a visit even without the Louvre.

Bad weather (but this is no one's fault), very dirty sea full of plastic and other things, thousands and thousands of people crowding small places, so you can't see anything. They should adopt limited entry.
Rubbish. Dirty water. Fog. Not worth the expense of the cruise. Cave seen in a group of three hundred people running without explanations. I do not recommend

Why would one go there? Because of the nice pics. But the reality is different: Loads of boats anchoring close to each other with noise all over the place and the worst is the pollution with Plastic and oil. It was a phantistic site, now totally destroyed by mass tourism. It feels like the Vietnam government sets top just a few sites for tourism, overcrowds them to keep them from discovering Vietnam. It feels like crowd control.

We were travelling with Papayas Tours. In the travel description, this part of the trip was described as if it were out of a fairy tale, which is exactly what it turned out to be. The description mentioned, among other things, the option of swimming in the bay, which is certainly true for those who are completely carefree. For us, however, the health risk due to the endless garbage floating in the bay was too high. The endless horde of boats and their unfiltered clouds of soot floating into the sky made this trip a special experience. Travellers with respiratory diseases should avoid this part of Vietnam. Travellers whose innocence is limitless can be advised to take a deep breath and use every opportunity to do so, and in any case, as garbage is sometimes dumped into the water by the ships, not to have any concerns. Swim, dive and yes, should some of this "dirty broth" find its way into your digestive tract...Many thanks at this point to Papaya Tours for the benevolent travel description.
A boat ride is really worth €400, with the mediocre service offered on board. Isn't a 5-star hotel in the same bay more profitable?THINK ABOUT IT.
Very polluted bay. The water is boat oil mixed with floating plastic waste and waste from the boats themselves. Mass tourism has destroyed this place (even Bai Tu Bay has the same problems). Avoid.

Avoid it! A great tourist trap. Not true whatever they say that “you have to do it “… hundreds of companies offering tourist group tours basically doing the same thing… unsustainable, unauthentic and in this wonderful country you can do much better than support these corporations actively destroying their own nature.

Once entered, you can see rubbish floating around already. The water is visibly very polluted from mass tourism and also the industrial plants nearby. Locals dumped rubbish into the bay, the whole bay is very crowded with all the ‘luxury cruise’ releasing oil/petro/smoke into the area. There is no such thing as exclusivity when you have hundreds of ships around you, in fact, those cruises are just floating hotels. They barely cruise anywhere. I think they should limit the number of cruises and visitors per day and teach locals about waste management.

Tourists, please boycott. If you care about natural beauty, I think you’d be disappointed. So much litter especially styrofoam cubes everywhere. Saw the tour groups for the caves use styrofoam to keep their platforms elevated so unfortunately, little bits and pieces fall off from the bigger styrofoam and the bay is littered with pieces of styrofoam that’s impossible to pick up with nets.For all the money that HLB get from the tour groups, I hope they switch from styrofoam to something sustainable.Also oil slick as well so would advise not to swim in the bay even though we saw others doing it.Smog everywhere. For all the tours that go on here, it doesn’t seem like there’s care to take care of the bay.We tried to overlook the litter and enjoy it somehow but it was impossible. I visited HLB in 2016 and remember it being beautiful and I was in awe when I visited. I don’t remember it being this bad. Made me very sad to see all the litter and poor upkeep of the bay.

Unfortunately, despite its UNESCO heritage listing, this area is putrid. Despite the fact we visited in “low season”, it was literally teeming with humanity. Every beach and walk we visited was shoulder to shoulder. The water was littered with every form of rubbish. I could count at least 50 boats at any one time, often substantially more. Definitely not recommended.

What a disappointment this place was, the first night at Ha Long Bay we were scammed as we booked ‘ The Ha Long Trendy Hotel’ which was closed down. We were left stranded on the street at night. Luckily we found a beautiful home stay hotel near by that came to our rescue. As for the bay, it looks polluted and the water as dark as the river Thames in London - England where I am from. There was lots of rubbish dumped in the bay too. I would not pay a penny to do any cruise out of Ha Long Bay. As a foreign visitor from the Uk I am highly disappointed. I hope other parts of Vietnam are much better.Avoid!

I have wanted to come back here for years after visiting in 1998.It was a very sad trip as it like sailing in a rubbish tip.The rubbish was everywhere large pieces of Polystyrene floating on the surface and so much just under the surface and around the little islands.Enormous cargo ships using the islands as a short cut. Diesel film over the water surface.The government is aware as it was reported in the national press while I was there. There were boats picked up the large pieces but it’s not enough.This is a  UNESCO World Heritage Site so it’s supposed to be protected. Visit some where else.

We were so disappointed with our trip to Halong Bay, the town is half derelict when you arrive and the boats look really shabby.  But it gets a whole lot worse when you go out into the bay, it’s filthy!  Plastic and rubbish floating everywhere and sewage and oil for the many many boats.  The activities were awful, crowded and badly run.  I’ve never been anywhere so depressing, dont believe the beautiful pictures you see, it must have been gorgeous once but is in a disgusting state now.  UNESCO and the Vietnam government need to clean it up soon or it will be ruined forever.  A real shame.

Wir waren drei Tage in der Ha Long Bay unterwegs und waren leider mehr als schockiert. Das komplette Gebiet ist dermaßen zugemüllt, dass man weder die eigentlich schöne Umgebung genießen kann, noch ins Wasser gehen möchte. Neben dem Müll wird von den Schiffen alles Mögliche ins Wasser geleitet, sodass man teilweise eine braune Suppe vorfindet und zwischen dem ganzen Müll auch noch Ölteppiche. Wirklich sehr sehr schlimm für die Umwelt.. dass es dort viele Boote gibt wussten wir, und hat uns während dem Trip auch nicht wirklich gestört. An einigen Stellen schwimmt zwar kein Müll aber die Wasserqualität und die Luft dort sind durch die ganzen Booten sehr schlecht.Insgesamt hätten wir im Nachhinein keinen Cruise mehr gebucht und würden es auch niemanden empfehlen. Es ist eine Schande dass die Regierung hier nichts unternimmt.

A la lecture des commentaires du mois d’avril 2023, nous avons décidé de ne pas aller à la Baie Halong. En effet il serait inutile de lire les revues et de continuer à participer à cette pollution. Merci aux personnes nous ayant alertés.

Avoid this place at all cost it has been totaly ruined by the amounts  of garbage  and oil film that is constantly drifting around the bay .It is so sad that a beautiful place like this has become a dirty noisey floating garbage dump The vietnamese govenment urgently need to do somthing fast to get this world UNESCO heritage site back to its its former glory.

I can only reiterate what previous reviewers have said.  Halong Bay is absolutely filthy and we were unable to swim in the water, and even avoided kayaking.  We stayed on a wonderful cruise boat, Heritage Ylang, which took us around Cat Ba Island which was supposedly a quieter area.   It was not quiet at all and I hate to think what the busier part of the Bay was like.  Would not recommend to anyone at present.

Far from being the clear blue waters of the brochures, the South China Sea here is a muddy grey/green colour and filthy. There are far too many boats doing the overnight excursion. I thought at one time there were more boats heading to this beach we were going to than took part in the Normandy Landings. The smell of diezel fumes was very unpleasant.The food on our boat, the Victory, was a set meal with no choice other than a vegetarian option. I was ill for two days afterwards.The bed in our cabin was rock hard which ment we didn't get a good night's sleep and we could also smell diezel fumes in our cabin.Not recommended.

I had a lot of doubts about our 3 days 2 nights trip to Halong Bay because I saw no ao appealing pictures of hundreds of boats following the same routes. I should have listened to my gut feeling. The landscape is really unique and you won’t find anything like this anywhere else BUT this mass tourism totally DESTROYS  the beauty of this place. There is visual, acoustic and sensory contamination throughout the whole day. The air is contaminated with motor oil smell (that even enters the cabins of the cruise boats ) and the noises of the cruise engines is unbearable . I was not able to relax even one minute, not even at 6am in the morning. the waters are full of garbage and oil. It was disgusting! So of course we did not go swimming! Even the kayaking through the layers of foam and oil on the surface was horrible. We were told that apparently the government since 2016  has rolled out a plan to dismantle the floating villages and the garbage are remainders of those but in my opinion this is only an excuse. Please don’t support this irresponsible mass tourism until the government and cruise companies clean the waters and protect this beauty! As long as so many people flock in and pay lots of money the companies just see the $ signs and won’t change anything.

Sometimes ago Halong bay was a piece of heaven on hearth now is one of the largest sewage worldwide!Somebody need to seriously do something to clean this place!! A couple of pictures taken from our boat during the night ( during the day could be e en worse!!)

The water is filthy and the surface is covered with hundreds (thousands) of polystyrene boxes and other rubbish. A paradise has been ruined. Boycott this place until it is cleaned up . Search for photos in Trip Advisor. I will add a photo a friend took as I felt too sick to take one.

Really disappointing, didn’t gain anything from going. Really sad to see how much rubbish there is in the water - you’re not even allowed to bring your own bottle of water as people can’t be trusted. So many boats, crowds of people taking selfies, there was nothing beautiful about it to me. I have heard that Ninh Binh is much nicer and more untouched so hoping this will be better :)

There are 200 boats in the bay at the same time with thousands of people. It's all industrial boats, many of them aged, with loud motors. Tripadvisor should update the picture - there are no traditional boats with sails any more. In the harbour, motor oil on the surface of the water. It's sad to see that nobody feels like they can do anything about it.

Wenn man Massentourismus und Müllberge im Wasser mag, dann ist der Ort eine Reise wert… wir hatten auch noch schlechtes Wetter. Dafür kann niemand was, aber so konnte man erst recht nicht über die Menschen und den Müll hinweg sehen. Ich war entsetzt!!
Der Ort ist echt schön, allerdings findet man überall Müll im Wasser. Es wäre schön wenn hier mehr auf Umweltschutz geachtet wird.

Lugar muy bonito, similar a phi phi de tailandia, actualmente estoy en un crucero por dos noches, y lo malo es la basura y la contaminación, el mismo crucero en el que estoy lejourney bota la basura al mar, lo vi con mia propios ojos, las bahias todas con basura, que pena ver una bahia tan bonita contaminada por su propia gente..

Don’t book trips on trip advisor website it’s a scam. Booked a 6 star cruise on halong bay . Upon  arrival in Hanoi I started to receive cryptic  massage about a better deal, better boat ect. I answered that all I wanted was to confirm the time of pick up from my hotel nothing else. Fast forward to today. Got picked up on time from my hotel taken to the holding area for transfer to the boat… while on the boat the cruise guide started to describe the boat hum nothing sounded like the the 6 star I booked on trip advisor. So long story short I floating on a rust bucked in halong bay. Good news guys the tour operator from Hanoi told my how lucky I am as the room I am staying in costs over $1000 not the measly $940 or $950 I payed. Do yourself I favor they got travel stores buy the dozen here book your trip on arrival less likely you will get scammed. Now let’s see if trip advisor will refund my money.. one more thing the boat in the pictures they have on the website very nice just seen it go by.
Too much human pollution. Into the bay. Nature can do only so much to clean the bay. It is time for polluting humans to do the clean up themselves.
The bay is full of floating trash. Very polluted river. We are glad we had masks on, otherwise the smell would have nauseated us.

The landscape is definitely unique and it could be so beautiful, however the extreme overcrowding and the tremendous amounts of rubbish all throughout the water make it in my opinion not worth the small fortune you have to pay to take a cruise. There is literally no direction you can look without seeing rubbish in the water and it makes swimming very unjoyable. Such a shame, I don't think I would recommend it based on these factors. Also currently all tours are cancelled until at least the beginning of April 2020 because of coronavirus.

What a beautiful BEAUTIFUL part of the world to get stuffed over in! My girlfriend and I turned up to Halong Bay hoping to find many available day, hour, 2, 3 hour cruises as we only had a quick overnight stay before moving south and just assumed there’s be lots of options.We made the first mistake of booking a night in Tuan Chau - a building site - and then made a very quick second mistake of trusting the first person to throw a ‘private cruise’ at us. Before you read this and assume we are moronic gullible fools (dispite feeling that way currently) we were very hard to persuade in the first instance and were told that at 1/2pm all the day cruises from the toll had sailed that so we could pay a slight premium and get a boat to ourselves and enjoy everything that the other tours offer, with a couple of beers thrown in.We made it to the caves and were absolutely blown away. Once we’d boarded the boat to continue we were offered photographs by the young guy on board with the other staff and when my phone was handed to the older, more weathered looking gentleman, with a Vietnamese grumble, he disappeared to the back of the boat. 2 photographs later and I realised my bag was on the lower deck with my wallet in so I ran down, though weathered captain number one tried to stop me, to find the younger seimen running off and my bag pocket open. Nothing stolen luckily but this commenced an incredibly awkward and horrible 2 hour chug around the islands where we felt hopeless and honestly a little scared.DO NOT FALL FOR ANY OF THIS. A HORRID EXPERIENCE MADE ALL THE WORSE BY FEELING FOOLISH BY NOT TRUSTING OUR BETTER JUDGEMENT.I’ve got a picture of the boat in question but I’m sure this isn’t the only vessel trying to pillage while you’re blissfully unaware. We still don’t know who was in on this - the greasy guy that talked us into paying 3 million dong between us, or whether it was simply the chancing scallywags aboard but either way this will be a tale we will surely be telling for a long long time, for all the wrong reasons.
Very difficult to find a place to eat and there's even less to do. Cat Ba island is where you want to go, but don't bother with Halong city.

Bed bugs! When confronted about it, the guide (not the company or the STA travel agent?) sent a rude defensive email saying they spray everything regularly. If there are no bed bugs - why would they spray poisonous DET? Moreover, spraying doesn’t kill the eggs hence the infestation continues. Don’t ruin your holiday

We ask for a middle range "two day and one night tour", we ask for a especially one in pier 2, they charged us the price and placed us in Golden Cruises. We pay £190 for instead of £80 tour for two. As soon as you step on the boat you know you have been lie, the boat is not the one you asked for, the photos they showed are not remotely like the real thing.What can you do? You are already in the boat, trying to make the most of it following a rush, unpleasant schedule from the crew. And as tourists we can’t afford time wasting.As a woman you do not feel safe at any time, the rooms are so so depressing like from a cheap two dollars prostitute's establishment, wires? Electric powers system? clean? When we did the activities where we saw other passengers from "fancier" boats all we had the same expression. They took advantage from us in all levels.Vietnam government do something please! Have inspectors take the tours, fine who ever is making a fraud, make places authorized by Vietnam tourist organization the only ones to book a trip with all cruses. Protect your tourists, we are here because we are amazed by culture and beauty but our money we don't enjoy wrong using.Are you willing to Risk your safety? A day trip I will be sufficient maybe value €30 day trip and the real value of the two days like € 60 tops being generous.We were so looking forward to some much needed down-time during our trip to Vietnam therefore we pay the medium premium to Halong Bay. From the pics I expected to get there and gasp in awe at the beauty of the place but it was depressing.The water is polluted. We opted for one of the newer and more expensive boats and got the cheapest and ugliest. The hospitality was none, the comfort level of the cabins awful, the cleanliness questionable. Do not waste your time and money visiting this tourist scam area.
下龍灣美景真不是蓋的，我們參加alisa Premier 二天一夜行程，美味的餐點，晚上仰望星空，星星好多，有登入驚訝洞，造訪英雄島，還有看到鬥雞島，是值得放空的行程與值得再次造訪

Lux tur ücreti ödedim. Turdaki 10 kişide ayni şekilde. Reina isimli tekne icin. Ancak tekne eski, döküntü bir tekne idi. Yemekler de kötüydü. Tum katılımcıların itirazı sonrasında sadece dönüş yolculuğunu Reina isimli tekneyi getirerek yaptırdılar.

The beauty and majestic was outstripped by rubbish of plastic bags, bottles, chip packets, polystyrene that is littered through the bay.  We could not bring ourselves to swim in the water it was so disgusting.  If you do go - avoid the weekend it is even more crowded with Chinese day trippers roaring around in jet boats and swimming in the very overcrowded Ti Top island which every boat seems to go to.  Not enjoyable at all! Vietnam needs to sort out its environment and pollution - unesco disappointment - what are you doing if this is one of your recognised sites you value then please help the Vietnamese look after our world oceans!

While the views can be pretty, the whole place is full of people trying to scam tourists... from our grab driver who tried to get us to pay double, to the woman who had us pay 100k each to “climb poem mountain” but we couldn’t climb because she wouldn’t unlock the gate for us and there was no other way up. She suggested we clamber up some rocks and try and squeeze past the gate. The people ahead of us were trying to do that but couldn’t, so we asked for our money back and she took a cut. We effectively paid her 50k to walk up the stairs of her house. Avoid it.

Mer sale, polluée . Les services sont chers et la prestation est médiocre. Apres un bain a la mer si vous voulez prendre une douche il faut payer !!!L objectif est de prendre le max aux touristes, comme partout au Vietnam. Constructions dans tout les sens , non finies et vides, le décor emblématique de la baie d halong n est plus que sur la carte postale.

We were in Hanoi, recommended by a colleague of my partner , said Ha Long is a nice place to visit, we trusted her, and took a 5 night stay in Ha Long, trust me , it's the worse place to be. I don't understand what is so great about the cruise to overcrowded place. The sea was dirty, and green, the only good thing is the view, the place is overcrowded with Chinese Tourist, construction everywhere, they should just put Ha long for Chinese Tourist only. We went to the Cable car site, the place is overcrowded, there was a Garden with Fake flower, how ironic is that? being 70% of Fake item of the world comes from a specific country, and that specific country's tourist come all the way to see fake flowers.. totally disgusting. To all whom reading this post, please do yourself a favour, Skip Ha Long, Skip Ha Noi, go elsewhere in the world. Don't go here. Seriously, everything here is fake. All the picture you see on the net about Ha Long Bay is utterly blastic lies. Don't trust them. Go to Thailand, its nicer, go to Philippines, better, go to Malaysia (east) , superb, DO NOT GO VIETNAM HALONG bay. I cannot digest the good reviews here, HOW it is even possible? perhaps fake people likes fake things, that's why they enjoy writing good review. My Review is REAL TRUTH. I'm sure there is other place in Vietnam that is better, but definitely NOT HA LONG bay. and they said this is a UNESCO heritage site, I wonder how it is even possible. Only gods know who classified this place a UNESCO Heritage site. Overrated. Money is not an issue for us, we just wanted a good place to rest, unfortunately, this is a wasted mini vacation ever. wasted my time, my money, and there is nothing to enjoy.
Worst, most touristy place ever!! Just do not go and do something else:) No one on the tour had any fun.

I would not recommend this trip as it was far too busy too enjoy!!Too many people and boats you felt like herded cattle.The day started good we were picked up from our hotel the coach was good, as soon as we got near the boat pick up the coaches were everywhere, chaos!!The boat was nice and lunch was good !!As soon as we got to Halong Bay the boats were like bumper cars trying to get in as so many.The queue for the caves was ridiculous we were shoulder to shoulder!!It’s a shame this trip is not enjoyable just far too many people.I am giving the star for the boat as this part was good.

As an adventurer I’m always taking suggestions for places to visit especially national parks, treks, diving and experiencing all of Mother Nature’s wonders. Of course having travelled to almost fifty countries over the last fifteen years I have experienced first hand the increasingly alarming air, water and soil pollution. This is extremely disturbing and alarming in the case of Halong bay. Plastic floating everywhere, a layer of thick chemical and waste matter on the waters surface and shore line, murky waters, styrofoam from destroyed fish farms and the locals and government not doing anything at all to manage this ever growing problem. In the Phillipines for example they closed Boracay island for six months to clean it and they don’t allow plastic bags or plastic bottles. Although locals wrap everything in plastic at least tourists must be conscious. The Vietnamese really should worry more about saving this natural wonder than making short term financial progress as in the end ALL will suffer the consequences. Even the locals eat and serve fish and seafood from Halong bay which are surely contaminated with high levels of heavy metals and chemicals. Be the change.

We did one of the cruises here that are so popular and they all seemed to do the exact same basic thing. There was this beach with 1000 tourists and it was so loud and there was no space or peace and at one point some of the employees started playing sand soccer and nearly decapitated a couple people. The caves were also quite bland. If you’re going to visit Halong bay, I would recommend not partaking on the cruise.

We were so looking forward to some much needed down-time during our trip to Vietnam therefore we did not flinch to pay the premium to board a boat for an overnight trip to Halong Bay. Gauging from the pics I expected to get there and gasp in awe at the beauty of the place but to my disappointment it was no different to sitting in the comfort of my home and flipping through pics. The water is polluted therefore we skipped the swimming experience. We opted for one of the newer and more expensive boats. The hospitality was amateur, the comfort level of the cabins awful, the cleanliness questionable. Do not waste your time and money visiting this tourist scam area!

hmmm..To be my utmost honesty and truth saying, its not worth the trip from hanoi to halong bay and the day tour. I did the day tour, a total of 12 hours is spend on this trip and the actual tour is only 4 hours including kayaking, short cave seeing, meal on boat and back and forth boat ride. To me, the boat ride sitting on top of the deck and viewing the limestones was a great thing but the guide kept asking us to go down..lol..and the cave exploring it was stunning and as said by a previous comment by someone that ''all you need is just to see the halong bay and the cave online or google it and just imagine it.. Yup, i trully agree with that..hahaha..What i notice that people just head there to take picture and thats it..My group that went there all seem to be unpleasant and really bored out. As a solo traveller and a adventures person, SAPA AND HANOI are so far the best place to go to at north if your itinerary is short. Sapa, i did trekking and hiking up fansipan. Hanoi..you got to head there man...

UNESCO Heritage site.  One of the wonders of the world.  Could be but isn't.  If UNESCO put their name to it they should STOP the boy racer driven, thrill seeking idiot- tourist speedboats destroying the natural wonder of the Bay.  It is a wonderous, beautiful natural place and truly a wonder of the world - NOT a bloody race track.  These people, the boy racer half wits and their idiot passengers are an insult to the place.  I think great efforts are ongoing (at UNESCO's insistence ) to clean up the rubbish from the Bay, but that's a losing battle throughout the world.  But for Christ's sake stop these idiots ruining it further.

I can not agree more to my previous travel expert. It is a bloody noisy and dusty place. I did plan, pay and book everything ahaed, but not the sailing boat and the harbour cruise. Weather was moderate to poor in 2 1/2 days, so I was "only" stuck but deeply disappointed as there is really nothing else one can do. The main road will look soon like Las Vegas. About 80  new developments coming up in yellow, white, pink and green. Artdeco style but so does not  fit in the landscape. And yes, there is plenty of loud and inexperienced Chinese travellers in the area. Patience and quietness is not their biggest strength.

This was probably the lowest point of our Vietnam trip. We went on a 1day trip from Hanoi. The trip started of bad since the bus was really small and uncomfortable and the toilet stop was waaaaay too long (same for the way back). We thought we were ready for the massive crowds of tourists, but this was on a next level. Hundreds, if not thousands of people go to the same three spots in the same order, like there is no common sense. The boats crash with each other to get the tourist out and on the boat which can be quite dangerous. I could bear all of that, but when i saw the polluted water, this is what hurt me the most. It’s terrible what they have done with this place. I believe it used to be nice but there is basically nothing to enjoy there. When we went on the kayak and we were close to the water we could actually smell the terrible smell that came from the water. I would not swim in there if my life depended on it. So do yourself a favor and skip it since it’s only gonna get worse unless the government will shut it down and let it recover.

So incredibly sad to see all the pieces of plastic and other fabrics floating around in the sea. How is someone not cleaning this up? The tourists are contributing with billions of dong each year in order to see this "wonderful place", but somehow no one is taking responsibility for all the garbage in the sea. It is so sad, because it is such a wonderful place otherwise. Clean this place up!

What we thought would be a highlight of our Vietnam  trip, Halong Bay, was just the opposite.  People who visited in past years would not recognize it.  Fishing villages have been cleared out and construction companies are building thousands of concrete units lining all sides of the bay, plus casinos and amusement parks.The islands are still there, but the bay itself is full of boats, most in disrepair, following one right after another. The pictures you see have been tailored to show one boat not the hundreds that cruise in every direction.

We were at two minds about a an overnight cruise, as we had been given very mixed reviews by those we knew who had also taken a cruise.Firstly the journey from Hanoi was 3.5 hours each way and a boring way to waste the best part of the day on the road through pretty dull scenery.So the bay itself is a perfect example of how mass tourism has destroyed the very environment that people go to enjoy.  There must have been literally hundreds of tourist boats, both overnight and day trips.The water was terribly polluted with plastic of all varieties as well as other garbage and debris, including human waste!The volume of boats mostly diesel driven produced another layer of pollution both in terms of exhaust fumes and noise from the engines and loud music played from some boats on passage and at anchor during the evening.The many boats form a queue to visit the very few shore excursions - the oyster/pearl farm and a small Karst island which you can ascend by steps with scores of others.Although our cruise ship was quite modern and comfortable we were very aware that we were part of a tourist sausage machine.All in all in all honesty I really couldn't recommend this.  As a UNESCO site it is something of a disgrace that this is not policed properly in terms of numbers of boats and visitors.We had three wonderful weeks in Vietnam and this was definitely the only low point as well as being poor value for money.I wish we had spent the two days visiting one of the many beautiful and unspoilt parts of Vietnam.

Just like similar places in the Philippines and Thailand this bay needs to be closed to all tourist for at least a year so that what beaches there are, can be cleaned up. Regulations should limit the amount of boats in the harbour, and provide real facilities. This area would shame any tourist board it basically is a slum with no quality facilities at all, and hundreds of unfinished properties that will probably never be finished as there appears to be no planning laws. Dolphin and seals kept in horrendous circus conditions.A potentially beautiful area is now a disgrace, AVOID.

Je suis rentrée très très décue de mon voyage au Vietnam et notamment de la visite de la Baie d'Along.Les photos et cartes postales de la Baie ne montre pas la saleté et la crasse qui s'y trouve. Les routes permettant d'y arriver sont jonchées de détritus et de constructions complétement anarchiques et jamais terminées avec des hôtels qui poussent comme des champignons.  Les bateaux permettant de naviguer déchargent leurs poubelles et polluent toutes la baie qui est d'une couleur grisâtre et non pas bleue comme le montre les photos pour les touristes. Les grottes que l'on vous fait visiter ne sont qu'un semblant de trou dans la roche.

Halong Bay - one of the many attractions tourists have on their checklist when visiting Hanoi. We were deeply saddened to see the dirt in the water and having to sail through thrash, heaps of plastic and piles of garbage disposed by the cruise boats. We were thankful we did not have touch the water that seemed entirely contaminated. At one point we felt we were sailing through vomit due to the stench from the water. It was worth visiting the caves on land in the area. We did try to fish and best part was we managed to fish out some plastic! If you don’t get a chance to visit Ha long bay - you definitely contributed your bit to an environment that is in peril!

Ich bin mit viel Optimismus auf den Day Trip in die Halong Bucht gegangen, aber mit jedem weiteren Meter Wasser unter dem Kiel, schwand Stück für Stück mein Lächeln. Diese Bootstouren erinnern an Heizdecken-Kaffeefahrten, in denen gutgläubige Rentner abgezockt werden. Alles, aber auch wirklich Alles ist auf den Kommerz ausgelegt. Minutiös ausgetimed, reihen sich schwimmende Touristenbunker aneinander, um von einem vermeintlichen Hotspot zum nächsten zu fahren. Zwischendurch versuchen einem Händler an Bord unnützen Kitsch zu verkaufen. Würde man dieses Weltkulturerbe ernst nehmen, dann würde man wohl die kommerzielle Ausschlachtung abschaffen. Ich werde jedem Freund empfehlen nicht die Halong Bucht zu besuchen, sondern das restliche schöne Vietnam zu bereisen. Das hat nämlich hier gar nichts mit dem echten Vietnam zu tun. Dagegen scheint Disneyland ein Ort der Muße.

Halong Bay es un hermoso lugar arruinado por la manera en que los vietnamitas lo manejan. No les importa el turista. Los botes economicos estan en muy mal estado pues se chocan entre ellos siempre y se ve que es madera vieja. Les recomiendo que no se tiren al agua. Los guias usualmente animan a la gente a tirarse para despues reirse de la gente picada por aguamalas o aun peor, encontrandose algun animal peor. El trato es terrible. En el hotel nos gritaron , nos ponian caras...fue feo. Lastima que no haya manera de recorrerlo sin depender de estos barcos con estos pseudo guias de turismo. Las cuevas son hermosas pero si es cierto que las luces de discoteca que hay adentro le restan un poco de misticismo. En fin. Iria pero tendria cuidado

For a UNESCO World Heritage site that brings in serious tourism revenue, how is it that the Vietnamese authorities cannot manage the environmental issues?Lots of trash in the sea is not acceptable.The lack of bird and sealife, similarly, is a symptom of larger problems.The saddest site was seeing a troop of monkeys going down to cliffs joining the sea, and ripping open discarded seasoning packets that had collected and eating them. Vietnam - sort this out.Tourists expect you to be responsible guardians of your environment
I would never return. The restaurants and service are not to international standards.It is just a tourist trap

While Halong Bay is beautiful - and at one point i’m sure very serene, it is now a complete tourist trap. There are dozens of boats, herding tourists to and from all the same caves and kayaking and floating village excursion, that there is really nothing peaceful left. The junk boat tours are mostly all the same, keeping you busy with an itinerary full of ‘cooking class’ and tai chi - even the same rest stop/souvenir store.  In my opinion this is a real waste of time. We took a day trip to ninh binh, and while still a bit touristy, beautiful and way more peaceful. Perhaps 10 years it was beautiful but at this point I would skip Halong /Bai Tu Bay.

We booked a private 2 days/1 night cruise with Mr Lap (hoanglaphp@gmail.com and Facebook Lap Hoang Cat Ba) on the recommendation of friends. We were promised a boat with 3 members of staff to cruise around Halong Bay, La Han Bay and some quieter areas in the eastern side of the bay.  When we arrived the boat was different than the one on the photo sent by Mr Lap. We  followed the boats doing a 1-day tour and at 4pm we were asked to change boat. Here the captain was not understanding a word of English and did’t know what to do so we had to call Mr Lap who spoke with him. The boat was unfit to sail, tatty with no running water (or shower), no electricity and actually unsafe (broken bench on edge of the boat, broken glass windows and doors). At the time to go to bed (no bedroom but on side bed in the cabin), we saw a rat on the other bed! At 2am we got awaken by the rat on our bed and decided to sleep outside on the upper deck. The next day we were told that we wouldn’t cruise and would only go to Monkey island on the way back to Cat Ba (which we had done the day before)! Mr Lap didn’t want to hear anything when we got back and behaved like a baby, running away on his scooter. We tried to get some money back but he never replied to any of our messages. We want to warn other travelers to NOT BOOK WITH MR LAP .

So me and my Fiance wanted to come here for over two years we saw A photo of the views and we really wanted to see them for ourselves we decided to stay here for three days and it was 2 1/2 days too long the place is very much designed for Chinese tourists the food was very poor in many restaurants and overpriced for what it was bike rental prices were the highest I have experienced all over Asia, and the bikes are of very poor quality and not exactly roadworthy, please do your research on this place before you come, and try and book somewhere that has a mattress and not a rock.

A day trip from Hanoi by bus takes four hours to reach Halong Bay (and four hours back).  Unfortunately, the area is so polluted by fumes, that the entire area all the way to the horizon, it's heavily hazy, not naturally, but caused by the thousands of motorbikes exhausts hovering over the entire country.  You will be disappointed by the throngs of people descending in a multitude of buses and later embarking on the wooden boats that cruise toward the magnificent rock formation, all at the same time. True! The place is a natural wonder, but unfortunately, its being trample and damage by basic economics... tourism...

I do not recommend going to Ha Long Bay. Water is terribly contaminated. However if you do, be very careful! Most “cruises” are scams. Boats are nothing like the show in the pictures and the people working in them are aggressive and mistreat tourists. We were told the boat had already sailed, so the took us to it in a speedboat, that way you are already there and can’t leave when you see the terrible conditions of the boat. Then there weren’t enough rooms for all of us. Horrible experience. Do not book your trip with Halong Happy Travel!

Having booked a ‘delux large cabin with balcony’, and confirmed with a picture - we were informed a few weeks prior to the trip that ‘boat too old and cancelled, we find new boat same quality’ no mention of boat name or further details - these would be given on arrival.The boat we were allocated had a tiny cabin with a tiny window overlooking a public gangway!Also the new company (Dragon) is based further away and had we stayed on board for the full 2 nights, we would have missed our connecting seaplane back to Hanoi.**This we learnt is common, using other ploys such as ‘ broken air conditioning, faulty engines’ the list goes on.Beware!
How can it be that nobody clean it ? I post some pictures i have taken last week while on the cruise with Bellezza. I Guess the pictures talk by themselves. no comments !

This place is beautiful. The rocks, the formation, the bay. It’s stunning. But.. it’s ruined. Halong Bay is situated in the North of Tonkin, right near the Chinese border. That means the “morning haze” that our guide was talking about at 3pm was actually thick air pollution drifting down from China. You could hardly see the rock formations in the distance. I’d imagine on a clear day, this would look stunning, however, there is then the plastic and other contaminants in the water. Whether from China or Vietnam, who knows but there is a lot of it. The bay is over crowded with boats. The smell of diesel exhaust fumes fills the air. The one and only man made beach is over crowded, filthy and full of jelly fish. Take me back 100 years before humans destroyed this magic place. I was really unimpressed. Don’t bother.
Give this place a miss, overdeveloped , looks like a mini Las Vegas , wait to you get on water w 500! Boats   Much prettier in pictures and cheaper to view on line

We paid $70 for the trip and it was sold to us as a party boat. The entire thing was rushed, the guide Eric spoke bad English and was demanding throughout. He came banging on our door at 6:15am waking us up. The food was average and drinks were overpriced. Beware when you’re told you get 2 hours of free drinks. We were told we had 2 hours but the other groups on the boat supposedly were only allowed 1 hour. (They actually get a timer out) There was only 10 people on the boat, dead atmosphere and disappointing. Staff were unhelpful and boring.

I took a cruise with IbizaTo see if halong bay was coolWhen I finally got to shore Felt 10 years olderBut frig it was somethin to doYou don’t want to be scammed like meNever really know what’s in the beer like meYou don’t want to go to bed with no AC, all alone You don’t wanna see the tourists mad like thisNever knowing where the rats are like this You don’t want to be stuck up on that boat, stuck up on that boatPS halong bay is beautiful but can not recommend Ibiza Cruise 😟

Never have I ever felt so cheated on!! We had booked a “3 days 2 nights tour”, and was expecting a really nice boat and lots of luxury! But... no.. not even close! To sum up what we were promised: WiFi, young people, karaoke, a bbq, a guided kayaking tour, many different caves and open bar - we had really paid a lot for it! But no.. none of those things were there! It wasn’t even the boat we had seen in the pictures - it was a very old and not that nice boat! The food was okay - all though we had the same for all 3 days! It was like the only had a program for 2 days 1 night, and when we stayed longer, they just took us out to do the same things again..  It was really disappointing and the crew was really arrogant and didn’t care that there was nothing of all of those things they promised! We even got a program when we arrived, to see what we were suppose to do - but it didn’t match any of the things we did!! The other guests was nice and we had fun - even though they were 50 years older then me and my friend (20 years)! And the bartender was helpful and kind!

Switch and bait, and worse… We booked a private 3D/2N boat tour of Halong Bay/ Lan Ha Bay with Mr. Lap Hoang (Facebook: Lap Hoang Cat Ba) on the recommendation of a fellow traveler. Unfortunately here is what happened. Mr. Lap sent us photos of a boat. When we arrived, it wasn’t the same boat. The captain and cook spoke very limited English or French. Therefore, instructions on where to kayak was minimal and unclear. Mr. Lap described an itinerary so we would stay in Halong Bay the second night; however, by the afternoon of the second day, the captain was already returning back to return our kayaks (against our agreement that we could kayak whenever we wanted for all 3 days) and we were only 30 minutes from Cat Ba Town. Supposedly he intended for us to stay the night close by and we were to spend the 3rd day (until 3PM) back in day tripper territory, only visiting Monkey Island apparently. When we found out after lunch on the second day that this would happen, we called Mr. Lap, who seemed to say that he had arranged so many different groups that he couldn’t keep track of who wanted what itinerary. We went back to Cat Ba Town with the captain (so it became a 2D/1N tour). Mr. Lap refunded us part of the money (not enough), and also kept the bus money we gave him instead of booking the return bus ride. We want to warn other travelers against booking with Mr. Lap. Perhaps, in the past, this kind of ‘shortened’ tour happened because travelers didn’t know where they were, but with GPS on our phone, we had evidence what happened and how we were swindled.

Please please please get the government to clean up this beautiful area! The whole place is gorgeous but is ruined by a veil of rubbish in the waters and on the shores. I feel sad for the locals and for our planet at the lack of respect shown for this beautiful area.

I knew what to a expect by such an organize tour in Asia, but this is a must do.We decided for a 1 day tour preferring to stay more days in Sapa.on the way to the bay the stopped in the usual AsiaTourTouristTrap, not a surprise.since there was nothing interesting to eat or drink I tried to go in the room where all local guides and drivers were eating and have a energizing vietnamese breakfast, when my guide saw I was rejected as a beggar!once arrived at the port my worst nightmares came true, dozens maybe hundreds of coaches unloading thousands of noisy asian tourists.You are not a traveler, neither a tourist but just part of a environmental killer assembly chain.you travel the bay with dozens of other shipwrecks, often hitting each other to find a space to park, from a cave illuminated and crowded as mall in the weekend to queuing on the stairs to have your little room for a panoramic view on the bay, the boats and the multitude.the best part is the way back, you can breath and enjoy the sunset with no rush and yelling people.this is, together with the massive resorts under construction, how you kill a beautiful dreaming wonder, such a devastation is hurting.

Camarote muy sucio y algo deteriorado, productos de ducha vacíos, la lámpara de la mesita o estropeada o con la bombilla fundida,para ser de 4 estrellas deja muchísimo que desear, nuestro camarote esta pegado al motor, podéis imaginar el ruido toda la noche más el olor a gasóleo, fatal, se oye todos los ruidos de cocina y los comentarios de la tripulación, ya que al parecer tienen estancias pegadas a la mía, no lo recomiendo para nada, muy caro para las condiciones que tiene y la comida no es muy buena bajo mi opinión.

Me decepciono el lugar puede ser hermoso de no ser por la suciedad de las aguas y el mal estado de los botes en los que te llevan.Comienza desde que te ofrecen el paseo aprox 70 dólares x persona y te muestran el barco y los camarotes donde vas a dormir cuando llegas al barco se te cae la mandíbula de ver la chatarra que es, luego vas a pasear mar adentro y en algunos lugares es muy bonito las montañas y el agua pero luego te llevas la sorpresa de que es un asco!

so know this is a big tourist destination in vietnam.  Avoid it.  The beautiful Karst Formations at spoiled by all the debris in the water.  We went kayaking from our boat and there were lumps of pooh floating about near the kayak station.  A mixture of tourists and the boats dumping waste I imagine.

Vietnam has so many places to visit and enjoy and Halong Bay is just NOT one you should choose. Overcrowded, polluted and definitely the worts food we had during our then day trip in the country. Ok, scenery seems picturesque but just this. It did not worth the 4hr drive from Hanoi, we are lucky we did not book to spend a night on board. The ship we boarded for our day cruise was dirty, the stuff impolite, the demanded a tip for their poor service. Just avoid it.

While in Vietnam we did the obligatory visit to Halong Bay.  Yuk!We flew to Halong Bay by SeaPlane which was the best part of the trip, simply because you get to view the enormity of the bay and pinnacles. But the air and water pollution are so bad that it ends up being a trip to view how a Country can contaminate and ruin a natural wonder. We were dropped at a beach to go for a swim..... but I’m not keen on swimming with babies nappies, sanitary pads and condoms not to mention the festering scum that floats on top of the water. The air is so thick with pollution that if you suffer with a respiratory issue ie. asthma..... then be careful.  The guides try to make out it’s a sea haze, just like they do in China. But the funny part is that, just like in China the sky starts to clear in patches by Sunday afternoon. One guide even tried telling us that it was China’s pollution not theirs. I’ve travelled extensively and can tell you that you can get better scenery than this in Thailand, off Phuket there are an enclave of pinnacle islands. The water is pristine, the air is clear, no pollution, the beaches are golden - no rubbish, you can see the sun, stars and the moon. We stayed at the island of Ko Yao Noi, Thailand and there was no comparison. If your travel money is tight, do yourself a favour and compare. Or just look at my photos and know that this is what Halong Bay really looks like.... Grey, Dull and Polluted.

A pathetic boat with overflowing toilets plumbing leaking out on the floor drinks so grossly overpriced that no one had more than a few 6$ us for a cocktail cave and beach tour packed shoulder to shoulder two thumbs down

Wir fuhren mittels einer in Hanoi gebuchten Tour zur Halong Bucht. Schöne Kaffeefahrt. Zuerst eine Edelsteinmanufaktur, dann alle rauf aufs Boot. Mit Millionen von Menschen in die Bucht. Die reinste Massenabfertigung. Schöne Cave besichtigt. Weiter nach Titop Island. Diesmal zusammen mit dem Millionen Menschen Erklimmen eines Aussichtspunkts. Katastrophe.
Classic over promise and under deliver. We spent most of our time transferring between boats. We did less than half of the activities on the itinerary. Would not recommend.

People are offered either a day trip or a 2 day/ 1 night trip on the boat to see Halong Bay. We did the second option and we went to a beach and did a small hike on the first day and went to see a cave in the second day. Both places were awfully touristy, too crowded, toilets super dirty and discusting and everywhere plastic swimming in the ocean. Facit:My advice - don't bother to visit Halong Bay, it's dirty and overrun by tourist boats that spoil the natural environment! It's very sad hopefully the government will take some action here soon.

No doubt Halong Bay is beautiful. And no wonder UNESCO gave it the nod. However, shame on the greed of tour operators and all others responsible for protecting this asset that is their life blood. Word will travel fast among travellers about the DISGUSTING amount of floating trash all over the Bay. It shifted my view from awe to awful. And everyone I talked to felt the same revulsion and anger. I was also appalled watching the cavalier way that the cruise ships were refuelled at sea. Gas was spewing everywhere as the hose was moved from boat to boat. One stray cigarette flicked over the side would be a disaster at sea. Not to mention the stain of gas on the water. The ships are not well maintained in ways that would protect the environment. Everyone making their living from Helong Bay needs to pull together to protect the asset that we know as world travellers is so easy to lose.Sharpen up, Viet Nam!

Without a shadow of a doubt one of the most breathtaking natural sites I have ever seen. However, the amount of trash and general disregard for the area astounds me. Plastic crap floating everywhere which is such a shame get it together UNESCO and Vietnam, manage this marvel and be proud of your beautiful surroundings!

Se pensate di fare il bagno prendere il sole sulla spiaggia, finta, o ammirare la bellezza della baia scordatevi di tutto questo!Dal vivo è il contrario delle immagini cartolina; sporca, inquinata e piena fin troppo di carrette del mare camuffate da hotel galleggianti. Numerosissimi i cinesi che sgraziatissimi si consentono canti gutturali che peggiorano la situazione; un bel posto distrutto anche dalle bruttissime costruzioni sui luoghi di visita un vero e proprio pugno nell’occhio. Noi abbiamo fatto una mini crociera a bordo della Pelikan forse un po’ sopra la media ma niente di esaltante. Peccato una vera delusione speriamo che le autorità facciano qualcosa prima che sia troppo tardi.
The bay is ok but nothing special. The attractions are mostly for old people and the air is super poluted you can barely breath

Vi blev hentet af en bus om morgenen og lige inden ankomst til Halong Bay (3,5 time i bus) blev vi oplyst, at ingen kunne sejle på grund af en kommende storm. Vi havde bestilt en 2 dages tur. Vi var skuffede, men var naturligvis glade for, at vores sikkerhed kom i første række. Derefter gik alt galt. I stedet for at køre os tilbage til Hanoi, så insisterede chauffør og guide på at køre os til Halong bay, hvor de derefter forsøgte at sælge os en åndssvag 4-timers tur rundt i havnen til 50 dollars. Ingen af os i bussen var interesserede i dette og ville bare gerne direkte tilbage til Hanoi. Desværre for os, så blev vi smidt ud af bussen på et sted, hvor der kun var drikkevarer, selvom klokken var 13.00 og vi var meget sultne. Vores bus blev givet til nogle andre og så skulle vi ellers stå og  vente i 40 minutter på en ny bus. Vores guide blev ved med at sige ‘5 minutter, 5 minutter’. Da vi endelig blev hentet af en ny bus, da kørte vi i 5 minutter, hvorefter bussen holder stille i 50 minutter, så folk kan få noget at spise. Vores guides havde sagt, at vi kunne ville komme til at holde stille i 5 minutter. Da vi endelig kørte videre, da vælger guides og chauffør at vi skal holde yderligere 30 minutters pause på en vanvittigt dyr resteplads (i vietnamesiske øjne), hvor vi gav 100.000 Dong for en kop kaffe og en kop te. Vi var først fremme i Hanoi kl. 19.00. Vi var super trætte og ærgerlige over, at vi havde spildt en hel dag i en bus. Hele hjemturen kunne havde foregået meget hurtigere og uden så meget ‘spildtid’. Vi synes, at det var meget kritisabelt, at man ikke allerede ved afrejse oplyste os om risikoen for aflysning og derudover, at man ikke havde en plan for hvad der skulle ske i tilfælde af aflysning. Det blev tydeligt for os, at de ville have os til Halong Bay, fordi de ville give vores bus til andre gæster, i stedet for bare at køre os tilbage. TIL ALLE, LÆS DETTE: Forhør jeres guides om risikoen for aflysning på grund af dårligt vejr! Det var overskyet, da vi tog fra Hanoi, men det var ikke slemt, så vi kunne ikke have forudset aflysning. Aftal på forhånd med jeres rejsebureau, at i skal have jeres penge refunderet i tilfælde af aflysning og hvis de nægter, så stå fast.

Nous avons pris des tickets pour une croisière de 3 heures avec visite de 2 grottes.Le personnel au guichet n’était pas très aimable et on l’impression de les déranger en leur posant des questions.A bord de la croisière aucune explication sur la baie, par contre on nous propose sans cesse des bijoux et foulards malgré les nombreux refus.Clou du spectacle au bout de 2h15, on nous annonce le retour dans la baie où ils nous proposent de faire du sur place dans la marina. Nous protestons donc et la bizarrement plus personne ne parle Anglais. Nous n’en restons pas la et retournons au guichet pour nous plaindre et demander une compensation financière. Le personnel du bateau visiblement un peu inquiet de notre plainte nous a finalement remboursé une petite partie du prix.Il paraît que ce genre de pratique est courant, en tous cas ne vous laissez pas faire ! Même si ça ne remplace bien évidemment pas la vue des paysages.Par ailleurs la baie est plutôt sale, bouteilles et mégots dans l’eau.Les paysages n’en restent pas moins beaux.

Nous avons pris le tour 1 (visite de 3 heures), car pour la visite de 6 heures il faut y être à 8h00. La promenade en bateau a duré 2h15 au lieu des 3 heures prévues ! Nous avons réclamé la durée et on nous a proposé de rentrer en 45 minutes à petite allure. Nous avons refusé de faire du sur-place. Arrivé à quai nous sommes allés réclamer un remboursement et là, étrangement, plus personne ne parle anglais !Après quelques palabres, nous avons obtenu un remboursement de 100.000 Dong sur les 400.000 payés. Il ne faut pas se laisser faire.Notre bateau, c’était le « Phuong Hàng 18 ».
Spent one night here. Totally waste of time and money. Nothing to see or do here and cheated by private car driver.

Whilst the bay is undoubtedly a beautiful place unfortunately it has been completely ruined by mass tourism. The boat trips are more like flotilla excursions and you are always surrounded by people. The places we stopped at were inundated with tourists.This is not an easy place to visit and because of the way it is being developed it is not worth the effort. A shame.

A low point in our trip. Very, very crowded and if you like being treated like cattle going to slaughter, then perhaps this is for you. Find a quieter bay to tour instead of Halong, even if it is an extra hour away.  While the scenery is nice, it’s ruined by what appears to be 1,000,000 other boats and the corresponding diesel fumes.  Hopefully the operators will figure out how to not act like schools of fish and take you away from the chaos of what should be a peaceful and calming environment.

We really wanted to go to Halong Bay for the breathtaking nature. We arranged a cruise on a boat via one of our hostels and were really excited to go. Getting to the boat everything was still great; enough space and good beds to sleep in. The boat is a little old, but in a charming way.As you arrive the first thing you see is that it seems that all tourists in Vietnam are gathered in one place. Then you take off and all the same boats drive the waters with a million canon camera’s, creating an extremely touristic view inside of Halong Bay. It seems all cruises have the same schedule; lunch, which is mainly Western, because it seems they think this is all we eat. Then you can relax on the boat and take in a bit of Halong Bay, which is cool. But then the queuing begins at the “Surprising Cave”. All the tourists are gathered on the same stairs and it’s a waiting game for when to move on. The cave itself is beautiful, but the magic is taken out of it by the many people that are being squeezed through the same pathway at once. Then you go to the little beach and you have the great view over halong bay at the peak. We didn’t even bother to go up, because we were so fed up with the queuing and all the people that are on the same pathway. Then it’s back to the boat for a spring roll snack, which was really nice and fun to roll! This probably varies per cruise. Then dinner came with almost only fried stuff; chicken nuggets, fries, fried fish and some rice. The evening however  was great, because we had a fun group, so we ended up drinking and laughing together. Next morning kayaking at 6.30 for some exercise before breakfast, which was fun to do alongside the bay. Then we had breakfast, again Western, to then go to the pearl farm; another touristic attraction with an expensive pearl shop. All and all this was really not our thing and quite frankly a waste of our money; the cruise was around 100 USD per person for which you get a return bus ride (3,5 hrs) from Hanoi to Halong city, 1x breakfast, 2x lunch, 1x dinner, drinks are not included and are almost twice the price as on mainland. We don’t think the cruise is too expensive for what you get, but we had higher expectations of the whole experience. The nature is amazing, but the magic is completely lost by the many tourists and the commerciality around the area.

Yes, Halong Bay is amazingly beautiful but so dirty and over populated with tourists you wouldn't ever want to come back. We had an ok time but felt guilty to be contributing for the destruction of such anIncredibly beautiful place. It is difficult to enjoy it. There are more than 500 boats littering everywhere. You see plastic bottles swimming together with biological trash. We did not dare going for a swim.

We were really excited about this part of our journey but we're unfortunately very disappointed. We stayed in Ha Long for 4 nights which was our first mistake. The area Ha Long: is not tourist friendly, beautiful or relaxing. There are almost no good restaurants to be found. The locals are not very good at English. The taxis will take you on detours in order to charge more. There are bed bugs and other insects in most low price accommodation. Everything felt like an uphill battle for us in this town. Seeing Ha Long Bay: When looking to go on a one day tour of Ha Long Bay the whole market is monopolized by 1 company. It is very expensive for no options. Your only choice is a big group boat where you see some over touristed caves, can pay extra to see some fishing stuff and then back to the harbour. All we wanted to do was to sail around the Beautiful islands but this was not at all a possibility. Apparently the laws and this company make it that way. Maybe with enough money it is possible to pay your way around this but we did not find any other options. The tour was rubbish. I would suggest people to take a 1 day tour or cruise option from Hanoi. Do not stay in the area.

À croire que toutes les merveilles Asiatiques ne sont plus protégées à proprement parler. Eau sale, pleine de méduses, des détritus partout, une très grosse déception. Pas waouw du tout si c'est ce que vous cherchiez. Excusions à faire quand il fait beau, et La ville n'a aucun intérêt et ses restaurant des bas de gamme.

I had an overnight cruise on Halong Bay in late July 2017. The weather was perfect, the scenery was beautiful. BUT there were boats everywhere, hundreds of boats. The air was full of engine noise and black smoky exhaust fumes. I woke early the second day to see boats all around. And where were the emerald waters? Hidden by miles of floating rubbish, junk of every kind. We were offered a chance to swim and turned it down with a shudder. Shame on you UNESCO and shame on you Vietnam for allowing the polluted mess that is Halong Bay.

Vor Buchung und Reiseantritt informierte auch ich mich bei tripadvisor über diverse Anbieter und Meinungen bezüglich eines Ausflugs in die Drachenbucht.Zu einem Preis von 125 USD pro Person buchten mein Kumpane und ich einen 2Day/1Night-Cruise durch die Halong Bay bei einem Reiseveranstalter namens Halong Dragon Cruise. Nach einer ca 4-stündigen Busfahrt mit Abfahrt in Hanoi gegen 8.30 Uhr und halbstündiger Pause bei einem komplett überteuerten Rasthof erreichten wir die Bucht. Erwartungsgemäß sah das Boot nicht ansatzweise so aus wie es das uns präsentierte Hochglanzmagazin versprach.Glücklicherweise waren die Zimmer in einem besseren Zustand als der Rest des Bootes und Insekten gab es auch keine. Abgesehen vom Frühstück waren sämtliche Mahlzeit tatsächlich wirklich schmackhaft. Die Getränkepreise sind deutlich höher als auf dem Festland. Ein Bier kostet etwa knappe 2 Euro. Eine Empfehlung ist daher, ausreichend Trinkwasser vor dem Trip zu kaufen.Den ersten Halt machte das Boot etwa 2 Stunden nach Abfahrt bei der "Suprising Cave". Die Höhle war durchaus ansehnlich, wobei keinerlei Aufenthaltsqualität aufkam ob der vielen Führungen, die durchgeschleust wurden. Danach besuchte man einen schätzungsweise 150 m langen Strandabschnitt auf einer nahegelegenen Insel, der gnadenlos überfüllt war. Dieser Stop war absolut verzichtbar. An Deck verbrachte man maximal 1 Sonnenstunde an diesem Tag. Mit der Ankunft auf dem Boot wurde sodann eine "Sunset-Party" angekündigt. Was fehlte: Musik, Licht und ein Sonnenuntergang. Im Vorfeld wurde eine Cooking Class vor dem Dinner angekündigt. Diese war dergestalt, dass sich jeder Passagier seine eigene Frühlingsrolle rollen durfte. Eine ähnlich einzigartiger Spaßfaktor stellte das "Fishing" dar. Die drei verfügbaren Holzangeln waren eine Herausforderung. In der Nacht und Tags darauf hatten wir unglücklicherweise denkbar schlechte Wetterbedingungen. Donnerhallen und monsunartige Regenfälle hielten unsere fortwährend nervige Reiseleiterin allerdings keineswegs davon ab, an dem geplanten Programm festzuhalten und die Gäste für beinahe eine Stunde in Kayaks zu setzen. Nach wie vor regnete es literweise. Die Kayaks wie auch die Paddel waren in einem verheerenden Zustand. Damit war der letzte Programmpunkt abgeschlossen und wir traten den Rückweg an den Hafen an, um wieder in den Bus zurück nach Hanoi zu steigen.Im großen und ganzen bereuen wir, dass wir uns gegen Phu Quoc und stattdessen für diesen Trip entschieden haben, der leider alle aus vorherigen Bewertungen resultierenden negativen Erwartungen erfüllte. Wer sich selbst ein Bild von der tollen landschaftlichen Kulisse und der wirklich ansehnlichen Landschaft machen möchte, kann guten Gewissens die Kalksteinfelsformationen nahe Ninh Binh als Alternative heranziehen. Das ist um einiges billiger und von Hanoi deutlich schneller erreichbar. P.S. Bei uns handelt es sich keineswegs um Leute mit speziellen Ansprüchen. Trotz besseren Wissens machten wir diesen Trip in der Hoffnung, dass es in Wirklichkeit doch nicht so schlimm sei wie in vorherigen Beiträgen beschrieben.

We got a public bus from Hanoi to halong and originally booked for 2 nights in a apart-hotel. It was terrible so changed tona hotel at the marina bay. It was decent (golden gem hotel) would advise staying there but would advise against halong in general as the city is the worst place I've ever been to. Opinion all the same from the 3 other people I went there with! We cancelled our 2nd night there - we couldn't find a decent restaurant to eat in and it was pretty much like a ghost town trying their best to become big which wasn't working out quite well for them. It's a pity as the people there are making an effort! We got the boat trip the next day which was meant to be 4 hour long and ended up being 2 hour, much to our delight as it wasn't great anyway.
