The views weren't anything special that I haven't already seen before. Wouldn't waste time and energy going there! A lot of boats apparently sink there too which made us feel uneasy and the water is disgusting! Please please don't go there!!

Après une première visite faite il y a 2 ans, j'ai décidé de revisiter la magnifique baie d'Halong. Cependant, à l'arrivé au débarcadère, grand choc suite à la construction d'une massive marina hôtelière de très mauvaise qualité, ainsi qu'un aérodrome maritime. Cela détruit grandement l'image de la région.De plus, l'organisation du personnel, malgré les dizaines de comptoir, est catastrophique (et cela avec une réservation de bateau privé)!L'aménagement des bateaux sont basiques mais correct. Soyez prêt à supporter la chaleur! Mais les menus repas sont copieux et très bon! Le paysage est toujours autant somptueux. Cependant, il est triste de voir que la culture local et des touristes n'aient aucun respect pour la nature (impossible de trouver 1m carré sans déchet dans l'eau ou le sable).De plus, il est navrant de voir l'accroissement du nombre de navire de tourisme, où vous pouvez facilement trouver une quarantaine de bateau sur un seul site de visite. Le gouvernement vietnamien devrait au contraire utiliser la carte de l'UNESCO et la rareté du lieu afin de limiter les navires, augmenter les taxes, et instaurer des règles quant à la protection du lieu.Les grottes sont vraiment belles et impressionnantes. Et cela apporte aussi un peu de fraîcheur à la journée.De plus, je recommande fortement les visites des lieux en kayak! Cela donne un peu de liberté au touriste afin de s'isoler.Évitez absolument les différentes plages, car elles sont extrêmement petites, mal entretenues, et surtout envahies de touristes et méduses!Et pour finir le tour en beauté, magnifique accident dans le port entre deux bateaux de croisière touristique (destruction du pont arrière d'un des bateaux). Il semble que les capitaines des navires aient reçu leur permis de conduire dans une boite surprise. Ne vous attendez pas à des parcages en douceur, la collision est toujours assurée!

Avoid purchasing at the HaLong Pearl farm-Ngoc Tran Ha Long! After spending on probable over priced earrings and a bracelet,they did not honour their 6 month guarantee on the earrings after the setting broke after 3 months!-was told it was cheaper to repair it in Australia rather than pay the postage to them and return.I had a ring made in Hoi An of which a diamond fell out and the jeweller went out of his way to repair it as well as being apologetic for the issue.-perhaps Hslong Bay pearl shop should learn some business skills from my jeweller in Hoi An!

We did a 2 day, 1 night tour of Halong Bay. We wasn't sure on going here as we thought it would be polluted and busy with tourist boats but we thought we should just because it is a UNESCO world heritage site and it would be a shame to come all the way to Vietnam and skip it. It is an incredible place, There is no denying, however our thoughts on why we should have skipped it were true. It is completely ruined with waste, plastics, packing foam and so on and after visiting the Philippines we couldnt help compare the two places. The Philippines is absolutely beautiful and Halong Bay just wasn't. There are too many boats on the waters pumping out black smoke into the air and Its disgusting.We stopped at a lagoon to kayak and ended up litter picking because there was so much plastic floating around. When we took the kayak back the guys running the kayaking just pushed the boat back out with the others and left the pile of rubbish on the boat. Real nice.The Vietnamese have a total disregard for their environment and you will see this when/If you come here. Such a shame.

Halong Bay's beautiful scenery is completely compromised by the disgusting level of rubbish in the water. It is a disgrace and should be delisted from UNESCO World Heritage sites until the waters are cleaned up. I highly recommend NOT going.

I booked a two day tour with the above company to Halong Bay. Tom did the booking. Me and two other girls (who I did not know) went on a bus with a group booked with another tour as our bus was full. Our tour guide on the bus spoke English however when I arrived on my boat I found that ALL the passengers AND the guide only spoke French! Halong Luxury Travel now say my guide spoke English and they do not know who to believe - me or the guide!  Would NEVER use this company again. They should have told me only French speakers on board!  Tour over priced too - wait until you get to Hanoi and book at one of the many operators for a fraction of the cost.

I was really looking forward to getting out of the busy city and into nature, but was very disappointed when I arrived in Halong Bay. It was completely polluted. We spent a total of three days and two nights in the bay area, one night on a luxury cruise ship and another at a bungalow resort. In both locations we were saddened by the shocking amount of trash in the water. It became abundantly clear to me that the amount of tourism is negatively affecting Halong Bay. The tour companies don't have (or maybe are not using) the proper resources to dispose of the large daily flow of trash. We saw more plastic bottles, plastic bags, and styrofoam than I could count. When our cruise ship anchored on the last day I saw all sorts of food waste, cigarette packs, and plastic bags mysteriously floating out from under the ship. It made me really sad, especially since it was one of hundreds of ships. I have no idea what is done with the sewage, but I don't have a great feeling about that as well. Halong Bay is a stunning chain of islands that could be one of the natural wonders of the world, but the human impact has ruined it. I feel guilty for putting my money towards a companies that are contributing to the destruction of Halong Bay. My hope is that the Vietnamese governement puts some stronger environmental regulations into place, but they won't do that if the tourism continues to increase and companies keep making money. After doing some research I learned that there are a few Eco Friendly tour companies. If you do decide to visit Halong Bay, I would encourage you to book with them and support their efforts to clean up the bay.

comme tous j'en avais rêvé...ça a été un cauchemar.... les paysages sont beaux bien entendu, mais la foule, le bruit des moteurs descentaines de bateaux, les odeurs de gas oil  ça casse le mythe ...sans parler des hordes de groupes asiatiques, dont le guide est équipé d'un haut parleur pour se faire entendre.la visite des grottes où l'on piétine à la queue leu leu, la plage ou l'on vous parque avec des centaines de personnes en vous disant de vous baigner, alors que les flaques de gas oil ( et autre )recouvrent l'eau. il est possible d'échapper aux croisières toutes faites en privatisant une barque ou autre et surtout en quittant la zone dédiée aux visites de masse.  il suffit de le savoir avant et de se l'organiser avec une agence de voyage en france ou en local.le lendemain, nous sommes allés  à la baie terrestre (TAM COC) en organisant sa ballade en barque tôt le matin ça réconcilie avec Halong...

Leider kommt hier keinerlei Romantik auf. Man kann vor lauter Schiffen keine ruhigen Blicke auf die Felsformationen genießen. Das andauernde Brummen der Schiffsdiesel lässt keine Ruhe zu. Dazu kommt noch der Gestank der Abgase. Einfach zu viele Schiffe. Die komplette Bucht ist voller Plastikmüll. Kein genießen der Natur möglich.

Halong Bay necesita vacaciones de nosotros los turistas, de los cruceros, de los botes pesqueros, de la basura en sus aguas, del smog que no se va nunca. Acabo de venir de ahí, 2 días soleados, súper soleados, pero el smog que había era impresionante, parecieron días nublados.De nosotros turistas que pueden esperar, pero los locales... en serio quieren tan poco a sus bellezas naturales? No se lo puedo recomendar a nadie, no vayan, por ustedes, porque lo ves y da tristeza, pero no vayan sobretodo por Halong Bay. Tanto dinero invertido en construcción de hoteles en la costa, pero nadie va a siquiera pasar una red para sacar los kilos de basura que flotan en sus aguas? Cuando dicen que es maravilloso... creo que es por lo que "era", no por como se ve hoy.

A éviter pour les agoraphobes, les croisièristes sont tellement nombreux que des centaines de bateaux s'agglutinent devant les plus belles concrétions de karst les rendant quasi invibles: vive le tourisme de masse !

We visited Halong  Bay in March, the weather was not on our side, it was cloudy and we didn't enjoy the scenery and sun as much as we hoped we would.  Despite the spectacular view, the place is extremely dirty: rubbish and trash in the water everywhere, plastic bags, vodka & wine bottles, oil spills, dead fish floating on the surface of the water. Poor ecological practices, No one is taking care of the bay to clean up the mess of the nearly 500 touristic boats. We had a very strict program and were faced with unreasonable answers from the crew members and our guide. We were taken to a spot to kayak (Kayaking is one of the activities that I heard it won't be allowed anymore as of April 1, 2017). Kayaking is also one the 3 or 4 activities that you can do in Halong Bay. The other activities being relaxing, sightseeing, visiting the beach or the cave with the rest of the 100s tourists or swimming in the dirty water along the dead fish and jellyfish.  Anyways, we were taken to a spot full of trash to kayak; of course from the boat we didn't realize that until we were in the water, rowing in the midst of plastic bags, litter, vodka bottles and dead fish at the surface. Our guide and the cruise crew did not think of taking us to a cleaner sport until later, our experience was totally ruined by what we had seen earlier. It appeared later that there is one company controlling the bay and every activity is scheduled by the minute. As a tourist you won't have a flexibility of visiting the one and only beach that is visited 100s of tourists who can only enjoy sunbathing, swimming or playing volley ball for one hour only. Reason being you need to get tickets to access the beach, and these tickets need to be bought from the mainland. So in case your wanted to visit the beach a second time during your stay at Halong bay, you may as well forget asking your guide or the ticketing office. We saw some tourists enjoy a little excitement with the speedboat, we asked our guide if we can hire one and he replied "NO Speedboat" . Why? he replied: Speedboat only for Chinese. WTF?!!!!We were given orders to shower, drink our tea and relax on the deck.....enjoy some more sightseeing.Honestly I wouldn't recommend Halong bay. Especially that the trip form Hanoi take 4 hours by bus, you may as well enjoy the pictures you can find on the internet and find another attraction that is less crowded and less controlled if you need some freedom while you stay.

Spent 2 nights & 3 days on a cruise, cruise was great but the location was well overrated. There are so many boats its hard to get a photo of the bay. At night they all ''park'' near each other and in the day they all head out for the 4 or so places to visit. The sea has a film of oil and the smell of diesel is hard to escape. I would not swim or kayak in this water.

I booked a 3-day (2-night) tour to Bai Tu Long Bay with AST tours.  The weather was windy, so Bai Tu Long bay was closed.  That meant we did the standard Halong Bay tour.  The other 6 people on my tour had all booked a 2-day (1-night) tour, so while we were frustrated that we couldn't go to Bai Tu Long, they were still getting the length of tour they signed up for.  Me, on the other hand, was not.  AST did not give me any option to stay an extra day, even though I paid for it, and they did not help me in any way get to where I needed to go once the tour was over.  I had to call my travel agency (my hostel in Hanoi) and THEY had to handle it!  It was like AST washed their hands of me.  What would have happened if the weather was perfect and the tour progressed as planned? I have no idea.  AST clearly did not have any intention of fulfilling my original booking.The boat was nice (Lemon Cruise), and the food was good.  No complaints there.  The cooking demo was a little cheesy (all we did was roll up some prepared spring roll ingredients).  And the bay was very, very crowded - Halong Bay is far too tourised for my liking.  I felt rushed by our tour guide to walk thru the caves quickly, and the kayaking was one big circle and that was basically it.Maybe Bai Tu Long would have been worth it, but my experience in Halong Bay was not at all what I had hoped.

Uncontrolled development and no environmental regulations have made this the most polluted place we have ever been in our lives. A permanent haze from nearby coal mines and coal burning power plants obscure the view from shore. Most of the tour boats are scandalously unsafe and dirty. You will drive 4 hours from Hanoi through a really depressing stretch of road where the most interesting thing is wondering which dogs are food and which are pets.

I would even skip the day cruise entirely. Did a 3d2n, 1n on ship and 1n on monkey island. The scenery is good but you get a dozens ships in your view too.Kayaking was ok... but it would be freaking awesome if you manage to get a private or luxury trip where you can do it before sunrise and away from the crowd - seriously, get away from the crowd.Was taken to an island where you can climb 5mins to the top, to find a horde up there. Take a look at the picture.Then we went to a cave and they warned us of the crowd and to stick together before entering We were the first to arrive but within 5mins we had another 10 boats of people alighting. I ended up whizzing past everyone and was the first one to come out, so have no idea how bad is it back there. Cave where so so only, just enhanced by colorful lighting.Monkey island was fun but that's for another review.

Durch unzählige Reiseberichte wussten wir zwar, dass es sehr touristisch wird, aber das dort ist wirklich abnormal und auch reine Abzocke. Das Programm, was bei unserer zweitägigen Bootsfahrt mit Übernachtung dabei war, könnte zwar generell ganz nett sein, aber leider sind alle 10Minuten streng durchgetaktet. Essen gab es reichlich, jedoch nicht gut. Wir waren froh, dass wir nur eine Nacht gebucht haben. Dank des guten Wetters war die Halongbucht einen Besuch wert, jedoch würden wir nie wieder eine Schifffahrt mit Übernachtung buchen wollen! Die Preise für unser Boot haben übrigens extrem geschwankt: von 84$ bis 160$ pro Person war alles dabei.

Myself and two friends booked the cruise as it was meant to be mid-range for Ha Long Bay and I'd found some decent reviews. We were quoted $140pp for 2 nights but got them down to $105 for a triple superior room as no economy rooms were available when we went to book so we paid more. The 3.5 hour bus journey from Hanoi took 6 hours, which is not a problem but just FYI. The guide on the bus was great, very informative and helpful, I think his name was Phro. Unfortunately when we got to Ha Long Bay we were passed over to another guide, Mong, whose English wasn't great and who seemed as though he really didn't enjoy his job and was just going through the motions to get the job done with minimal effort. On arrival we were advised that we couldn't have a triple room and there was no such thing as a superior room, instead I was told I'd be sharing with another man who was travelling alone. As a 27 year old girl I found this to be completely unacceptable. I was lucky that he was a nice guy but being made to share a double cabin with a strange man should not be allowed. Our first stop was the caves which were really impressive but the other guides were informing their groups about the caves, how they had been created and a bit of history. Mong spent the whole time pointing out animal shapes in the rock with a lazer pen so we ended up following another group. The first day was everything being crammed into a couple of hours. After returning from kayaking (soaked to the skin) I was told I couldn't shower because I had to go straight to the deck to 'enjoy small party'. This included standing on the deck drinking the 3 sips of red wine which we were provided with, which was advertised as 'compliamentary wine before dinner. Day 1 in Ha Long Bay was extremely disappointing. The water is just filled with an obscene amount of boats and cruises and the water is filthy. We have just come from the Philippines which has everything this area of Ha Long Bay has and more... with clear water and no tourists! The food was at times mediocre, at times awful (only stale toast and jam for breakfast). I don't eat meat and had to remind them of this at every meal as they kept forgetting and cooking no veggie option. On the first night I was informed by my new roommate that he had tried to go to bed and had been greeted by a bed bug on his duvet and another dead one under his pillow. We asked our guide for help to which he initially said we must have imagined it then when my roommate brought him the bug on a tissue he said there was nothing that we could do, we'd just have to sleep there. He informed us that we must have brought them with us because there was a nice couple staying there the day before so it couldn't have been them! He told me that I'd have to deal with it as it was my fault. His attitude was absolutely appalling the only reason we managed to have any success in finding another bed was because I told him if he didn't do something I would make sure he was kept awake all night and that all the other passengers were woken to check their beds. After that he found us a bed immediately (more than an hour after finding the bugs). After moving to a new (cockroach infested) room we double checked the beds with Mong in the room and when I commented on the amount of dark hairs and dirt on the sheets he told me it had come off me. I have red hair.  Luckily day two of the cruise, after we left Mong was great. Cat Ba Island and the hotel there were great and the areas which we sailed through were much less crowded but I'd strong advise avoiding Golden Bay Cruises and if I was to go back ando it again I probably wouldn't do a cruise at all, I think a trip to Cat Ba and some smaller islands would be much nicer.

We Organised a daily trip in Halong Bay. We expected a beautiful and unusual place to see... Wasn't it.The cruise was good but if you want to see somethings special or a truly Colours of that country don't go in Halong Bay

We stayed 4 nights on Cat Ba Island which  is part of a chain of 366 islands that lie within the larger area of Ha Long bay. Famous for stunning natural beauty it was officially recognized as a World Biosphere Reserve It attracts millions of visitors from around the world.However,   it is cluttered with litter everywhere - Yes everywhere and I have never swam / canoed in open seas (and snorkel) where there are no fishes. None. The whole area is over fished and much worse - grossly polluted. Unbelievable disgrace, a disaster of Global proportions and the Vietnamese peoples should be very ashamed!Action needs to be taken urgently to recover and then preserve this natural beauty.
Extremely poluteted, they even made the restaurant in the cave. Cheated and trick people everywhere. Terible vacation

Ha Long bay wordt gezien als een van de mooiste natuurverschijnselen, en ja het is er ook echt mooi.Wat wel jammer is, is dat de boekingskantoren en organisatoren echt schurken zijn.We gingen niet erg op onderzoek uit daarom hebben we voor $90 pp een Tour van 3 dagen geboekt via White Pearl Cruises. DOE DIT NOOIT. De organisatie is eigenlijk Joy Tours, maar je wordt goed opgelicht. Lange wachttijden, krakkemikkige busjes, asociale en onvriendelijke gids. Erg slecht en vies hotel (Sunflower Hotel Two).Op de boot is door de bemanning mijn nieuwe zonnebril gestolen, en zelfs na veel amok gemaakt te hebben kreeg ik hem niet terug. Ook de gids liet je vallen en het boekingskantoor liet even weten dat ik 'hem zelf kwijt heb gemaakt'. Cynische lachjes van de bemanning maakten de reis af. Door deze ervaring dit natuurschoon met maar 1 bal beoordeeld.Ho Long bay is overtoeristisch, zoek een goede Tour uit met goede ratings voor een betere ervaring. Tours met hoge ratings zullen meer doen om het je naar wens te maken. En waarschijnlijk van je spullen afblijven.DO NOT BOOK JOY TOURS, HO LONG BAY BUDGET CRUISES OR WHITE PEARL CRUISES. Do not go on the boat called 'Hoi An' (reg: QN-4005)

El lugar es uno de los más bonitos del mundo y merece la pena ir, el gravísimo problema que hay es el malísimo trato que hay hacia el turista si se hace el recorrido desde el continente. Robos, engaños, estafas, insultos, suciedad, hostilidad...Llevo tres años haciendolo y las tres empresas con las que lo he hecho se han comportado igual. La mejor opción sin duda es ir en ferry a Cat Ba, pasar una o dos noches y hacer todo desde allí. Nada que ver

Too hot, too long way from Hanoi. It's terrible way to get there due to poor local road condition. Though you get there at last, it's common scenery, just some bunches of high-rise stones on the sea. Nothing spectacular result for your effort on the endless bumpy road and time investment.

We visited Halong Bay. First things first, Halong is beautiful but the wather is worse. We took a cruise with Aloha/ White Preal, do not take that tour. Met de tour van aloha worden je dingen beloofd die niet worden nagekomen. Het wordt aangeschreven als een party cruise. Er is een happy our met gratis bier. De ene groep wordt een uur beloofd de ander 3 uur. Het komt neer op een vaatje pils. Bezoek aan de grotten is zonder uitleg in 10 minuten over, en niet geweldig personeel. Het ergste is dat je de tweede dag om 7 uur op moet, dan gaat kanoën voor een uur, en met volle vaart terug gaat naar de kant. Daar aangekomen. Moet je 4 uur wachten voordat je met de bus wordt opgehaald, ondertussen wordt de boot opnieuw volgeladen. Je vertrekt als laatste uit de haven en komt als eerste weer aan.... Verder is het bier 2 dollar, meer dan de 1 die ze vooraf aangeven en de boot is vrij oud. Neem deze tocht dus niet!

7月22日にハロン湾クルーズ日帰りツアーに参加しました。現地価格で32ドル45人乗りの大型バスに２０人乗り、ガイド付きと言う話なので予約をしましたが、ホテルピックアップも1時間遅れてバスはボロボロの小さなバンで補助席に座ったまま（全ての補助席を使用）片道4時間かかりました。一番ひどかったのはドライバーの運転で、バイクに乗った二人連れの方達と接触事故を起こしその方達は畑に飛ばされましたが、そのまま車を停止することなく走り続けました。ガイドも飲み物を勧める際通訳するだけで、何もハロン湾についての説明はしなかったので強制的にチップを要求されましたが、当然だれも払う同乗者はいませんでした。帰りはホテルまで送るはずが、別の場所で降ろされ途方に暮れました。同情した外国人ツーリストも全員このひどい体験を「有り得ない」と激怒。このツアーは絶対にお勧めできません。

It is a pity to see one of the natural wonders of the world so polluted and unprotected by the government. There are lots of plastic bags, plastic bottles and other kind of rubbish floating in the water. Imagine kayaking or swimming in such a water! When you go for a visit to a Cave or an island, it is full of garbage too: plastic bottles everywhere, different kind of food waste. It seems that the government doesn't care in taking any action to protect or at least clean the area! It is just a source of money. I think places like that should be avoided by tourists as sign of protest for a disrespect to the environment. Never again!

The Managers on the cruise tell us that we are not allowed to stay on the sundeck or any outside of the boats during sailing due to strictly implement by the Halong Management. I don't think that is very good idea. We decided to travel in Vietnam is to see and enjoy the Seven Wonders of Nature and now they give us a limit for this kind of reasons. We tell to our friends not visit Halong Bay and find a better place to go. To be honest we are very disappointed.

Do yourself a favour and spend your money elsewhere. I had always wanted to go to Halong Bay and I was very disappointed when we went for our wedding anniversary. The water is so polluted and so much rubbish is floating through the water. There is no protection of this area. The shallow water shows you all the algae and damage to the area. So gross and I couldn't believe I wasted so much money to come on a cruise here.

For May 20, 2016, my wife and I were booked on one the Paradise Luxury Cruises through our travel agent in Vietnam. We drove 4 hours from Hanoi to Ha Long and found the terminal for Paradise Luxury Cruise. When we tried to check in, the personnel had no record of our reservation! After we showed them our voucher and our guide talked to them, they told us to go to another counter which did not belong to Paradise Cruises. We told them we will not go on another cruise, but only the one we paid for, an "Operations Manager" by the name of Mr. Jade Kamal, came by. He did have our name on this alternate cruise roster because according to him the Luxury Boat we had paid for had developed "mechanical problems". They had sent out an e-mail to our travel agent just as we were arriving to board the boat. This manager had no other way to accommodate us and did not try to explain what was the level of the alternate boat and how they would compensate us for the lower level of the cruise. At that point we decided to abort our trip and we drove three hours back to Hanoi. Our travel agent provided us hotel room in Hanoi and offered us dinner that night. We do understand that Paradise Cruises has given full refund back to our travel agent, but we do not know if they have done anything else for them to compensate for the trouble they caused us.By the way, another overnight boat caught fire on May 6, 2016 and the people on board had to be evacuated. It appears that Ha Long Bay has become a huge touristoperation and the natural beauty that is the object of this type of trip has been replaced by the party cruises and spa treatments on board. There are just too many boats and people on this site now.

I am so disappointed i came to halong bay to stay and see the bay. But here is nothing to see. They are building a huge resort in front of the main bay so yo can see nothing and the small beach available are in dao thuan chau, but you have to pay for staying because are private beaches. Besides the people just want to sell you the tour but nobody give you information about the ferry. That is while i am posting a picture of the ferry schedules which takes one hour to get into dao cat bat.

What a waste of time to go and visit this bay with hundreds of boats lining all together like it was a tourism industry.The crew of the boat were very nice, and the boat facilities were ok.... but the halong bay is just so dirty and polluted by this massive tourism attraction. The sea is dirty, the caves have no interest...I consider this visit as a waste of time, and prefer to see the pictures of the bay in google...!

I booked this cruise for my family of 5 during P.H. We got on the bus from Hanoi thinking that we'd be getting on Royal Wings cruise later on. After an hour on the bus, the guide told me that they wanted to change us to another cruise which we checked on the Internet was not as nice. They told us that the entire 1st floor was defected. They'd give us $15 for each room. The number shot up to $70, but we didn't compromise. The lady on the phone (who claimed to be a manager) was very rude that we decided to cancel and asked Agoda to book another cruise for us.The worst part was when we got to the pier, the line for this Royal Wings Cruise was.... full. Business was normal. Our experience with the one Agoda changed for us was so good, but I do want to give people some head ups about this cruise.

We were really looking forward to this over night cruise, unfortunately misty weather so visibility poor but could see how beautiful the views would be in better weather and this is no reason for our rating. We were encouraged to swim from back of boat which only two of us did. It appeared clear water at the time. PLEASE DO NOT DO THIS. later on during early evening we were squid fishing and could not believe the amount of sewerage and rubbish pouring into the water from all the boats. Absolutely disgusting I am surprised we did not come down with dysentery. You have this wonderful site but as soon as you look down at the water it makes you feel sick at the garbage floating past. unacceptable when the boat companies are making so much money from tourists that they don't clean up their act.This completely took away from what should have been a great trip there was good food on board comfortable cabin and the cave visit was amazing.The boat staff should not allow anyone to swim in this water.
4 hours drive each way from Hanoi to see a bay crowded with shabby boats and a shore crammed with ugly hotels...not worth it. Ha Long means descending dragon... it should mean so long, dragon.

We arrived late AM in the rain and  a tender took us out to the ship.   We sailed shortly and received our room assignments.  We had a small balcony and were thrilled to be able to sit out there and even in the rainy mist watch the other boats.   However, no one mentions the smog of diesel fuel in the air from the large number of boats sharing a small space.  the food was beautifully presented and lots of it.  Many of the group went to the cave but again lots of people to the point you couldn't see or take photos.  We were very disappointed and this was one of the high end ships.
Beau mais malheureusement très très sale. Beaucoup de déchets flottant Balade en bateau d'une durée de 8h, visite d'une grande grotte. Déjeuner à bord.C'est très très long ... Perte de temps

Viele Vietnamreisende haben die schönen Werbebilder der mystisch anmutenden Halongbucht im Hinterkopf, wenn sie die besagte Bucht ansteuern. Selten erwartet einen das strahlende Blaugrün der Bucht und der Vegaetation der Berghänge, da oft der graue Nebel über Vietnam hängt. Doch dies wäre eine natürliche Gegebenheit, die man in Kauf nähme, um die Einzigartigkeit der Bucht zu erleben. Was jedoch den bewussten und sensiblen Reisenden abschrecken sollte, sind die Massen an Plastikmüll, die in der Bucht schwimmen und zwischen denen, vor allem für Touristenboote, gefischt wird. Die Hinweise der Kapitäne und der Besatzung der Touristenboote, man solle über den Müll hinwegschauen und den Horizont und die Silhouette der Bucht fokussieren und genießen, erscheint mir wenig umsichtig und einsichtig. Nicht nur würde der Natur geholfen, wenn der Müll wünschenswerter (aber wohl nicht umsetzungsstarker) Weise nicht in der Bucht landete oder wenigstens würden Arbeitsplätze geschaffen, wenn ein paar Fischer die Bucht regelmäßig reinigten. Für den aufmerksamen und wenigstens etwas ökologisch bewussten Reisenden ist die Halongbucht eine traurige Katastrophe, die zusätzlich so vertouristet ist (vor allem asiatische Gäste, die den Müll kaum wahrzunehmen scheinen), dass sie eine gnadenlose, leider menschengemachte Demystifizierung erfährt. Einen Punkt für den unter dem Müll versteckten Zauber der Bucht.

Ibamos con las expectativas muy altas a este lugar por lo que representa y nos decepcionó mucho. El lugar en si es impresionante, con unas vistas irrepetibles en un mar de islas que te atrapará y no querrás salir, hasta que miras al agua del mar y ves la mayor cantidad de basura que has visto en tu vida. Deberían cerrarlo unos años para limpiarlo o por lo menos prohibir que los barcos tiren toda la basura al mar

Over spring break, we went on a 3-day, 2-night cruise of Halong Bay. The images from the online sites and descriptions ("Hạ Long Bay, in northeast Vietnam, is known for its emerald waters") give you the impression that the area is a pristine natural wonderland. Unfortunately there is too SO MUCH GARBAGE in every direction, so this is a false perception. Part of our itinerary included time for snorkeling and swimming, but there was NO WAY I was going in those waters. It was polluted with everything from used sanitary napkins, to plastic containers, to light bulbs, shoes, clothing, and dozens of plastic bags. I was told that the gov. has begun hiring people to clean-up. During our time on the water, we saw one man in a small boat with a small net collecting some rubbish, but it is going to take a much bigger effort. The area has so much potential, but the trash ruins the whole experience. With the money they are making on the tourism from the Halong Bay cruises, surely there is money to be put into a massive clean-up effort.

Where shall I start...Old dilapidated boat falling apart with the most rude and vile crew I have ever encountered.Having to walk down a series of muddy slippery steps with no help from the staff in order to jump to the boat.Being told we couldn't check into our cabins till 4pm. And we were promptly kicked out of our rooms at 7.30am as they needed to be cleaned!Food was worse than student food. Plain instant noodles for breakfast with nothing else added. Portion sizes are small and aren't big enough for the number of people they are trying to feed!! Drinks are available at an additional cost and very overpriced. Don't expect a free tea or coffee with breakfast!! If you bring your own alcohol be aware the crew steal it from you and charge you if you want it back!!!!The staff were drinking and listening to awful music which they claimed everyone on the boat wanted. After being informed by all the customers they didn't want this they reluctantly turned it off but not before a heated argument and them calling me a few choice colourful metaphors.We sailed out of the harbour for 45 minutes only to drop anchor and be told this is where we are stopping. The boat only moved again to return to the harbour. So we hardly say anything of ha long bay!! The weather is poor at the moment so nothing can be Seen through the fog. The water is disgusting, full of people's rubbish!! I don't recommend swimming.....There is no regard for Heath and safety. The crew don't care. Two people could have drowned as there kayak sunk and they had to swim back to the boat in the freezing waters. Again the crew didn't care, and when the guys complained they were again sworn at.The cave is closed for maintenance !! They take you to another small one which involves a 3 hour coach journey. And to get to the coach they expected up to leap frog over 6 boats in the rain and then be hoisted by our arms up a wall.When we were in the bus on our way back to the hotel, the tour guide refused to drop us back instead dropping us almost a kilometre away saying that we were asking to much! Even though drop off was included and he himself had just said it.A miserable horrible experience that has ruined my trip to Vietnam.

When you're travelling around, you get used to misleading photos and a bit of fancy wording to make a trip sound better. But with this trip, we were straight up lied to, at all turns. To begin with, we were taken on a 5.5 hour bus ride, while the whole time our guide claimed it was 3.5 hours. After finally making it, we were told that we couldn't actually stay in the bay overnight, because it was too foggy, and told we'd have to do the one day tour. After nearly all the bus were planning to take a local bus back to Hanoi and demand refunds, it was suddenly open back to stay.So we got on the boat, as we expected there was no chance of ever getting a refund. We were taking out into what they called the bay, just by cat ba island. Where we stayed for the whole trip, and turned around and went back. A trip that promised to cruise Halong Bay, and Bai Tu Long Bay. When I asked the guide about Bai Tu long, he promised me we were going that way on the way back, saying that as he pointed south at exactly where we came from (the opposite direction.).We were taken to a tiny little cave, that was totally different from the one we were meant to go to, while the guide claimed that one to be under maintenance.They filled whatever chance you might get to look at the lovely scenery with annoying activities, badly executed, and expect you to go along with them.They try overcharge you on your bill at the end, by claiming all the drinks were 1 USD more than what they said on the menu. Everything about the companies are sleazy, a trend that has followed us all through Vietnam. I would never go back to Ha Long bay, as I doubt I'll ever come back to Vietnam. Go to Laos, things are expensive there, but at least the people are honest about it.PRICESThey will try and rip you off on the price for whatever they can. Some people paid as low as 70USD, while others were paying more than 100USD. We didn't get too ripped off on this. But shop around as much as you can.

There were a lot of promises made, but almost none of them actually happened. One of them was going to the caves.The first day we were told we couldn't go to the caves, but we could go the next day. So the first day we almost didn't do anything, except kanooying for 30 minutes. Also, we only sailed for an hour the first day, and then stayed by the peer, doing nothing. We had to wait all the time and there was no one to answer our questions.Next morning we had to wake up at 6 am (one hour earlier than scheduled) because then it was sure we could go to the caves. After breakfast at 7 am, we had to wait for 3 hours on the (cold) deck, before we finally left the peer and headed to the caves. When we arrived by the entrance of the caves, we were told we couldn't go and we had to go back to the boat. We were promised a 50 percent refund, because we by far didn't get what we paid for. Of course we didn't get the full refund, but only 10 dollars, while we paid 75 dollars.At night the heating system didn't work and the beds were as hard as wood with only a really thin sheet. It was absolutely freezing! Also, the bathrooms were really dirty.The cooking classes that were promised were really bad and while we were with 15 people, we only had 3 plates to cook with. Halong Bay is beautiful, but make sure you choose the right company to do the trip with! Don't choose Aloha!!

Obviously the bay would be very pretty but there is one big thing about this. When you go you go together with 500 Boats at the same time. No sailing all motorboats. Then all the boats need to take the same route because there is only one route approaved in the bay. Then all the boat who stay overbight are in the same bay, all boats are the same, the food is terrible bad and it is just the opposite of romantic or poetic. If you want to see the bay take a waterplane from Hanoi, fly through the bay and flight back you save one full day of driving toward,checking on the boat and driving back.

Firstly the bus journey was terrifying ! I thought I was actually going to die! The driving was terrible and was overing taking when there was coming traffic!Healing bay its self was beautiful minus all the tour boats. But we didn't get much chance to see it as on the way back we had to do an inside 'cooking lesson' on how to make spring rolls.When we arrived we were promised welcome drinks, which we were later billed for. Be aware that the drinks they offer with the meal are not free. Even though they don't make it clear. The room was the highlight for me was really nice and Cosy. The 'squid fishing' was just a stick with a line on it with no bait that keep getting dragged under the boat with the current. What a joke.The tour guy went on to the main land and left us on the boat with no explanation for an hour The food was terrible! There wasn't enough and half of it was served stone cold! On the way back on the bus we found smashed eggs in a bag on our seat. When we asked for a bin the driver stopped and the tour guy told us to throw the rubbish out the window!!! No wonder the country is so polluted! To which we refused and they stopped off at a bin 15 minutes later whilst they made us hold on to the eggs! If it wasn't for the view on the way there and the people I would of had a miserable time. But I could of got that on any boat!

Si vous y allez en simple touriste payant ses deux jours de croisière luxe sur un paquebot géant... halong doit être pas mal... sinon c'est simplement un cauchemard. Une jungle de beton et d'acier conçu par des promoteurs ne pensant qu'a construire plus d'hôtel, bars, parcs d'attractions sans se soucier de detruire ce qui a du être (a une époque ) le paradis sur terre...En un mot: si vous esperez voir les photos magnifique... regardez les photos et n'y allez pas

Booked a 3D/2N Halong tour at Santabackpacker Hostel in Hanoi. I paid USD90 for 2 nights (which turned out to be cheap compared to other travelers on the boat who paid the same amount for just one night!). With that price range, it was expected that it'd be a budget tour. Still, the quality is shockingly bad. To start, I was picked up by a large tour bus packed with travelers on different itineraries. Didn't think it was an issue until the tour guide repeatedly (to an annoying level) tried to convince people to spend the night on Cat Ba Island instead of the boat, later he admitted that the boat was completely overbooked. By the time we finally got on board, we sailed about an hour and arrived at the caves where we did a walking tour (which is really more just plain walking since our guide didn't say a word). After that, we headed straight to Cat Ba Island where people who planned/agreed last minute to stay in the hotel left. But what we thought would be a quick drop-off turned out to be our last stop, as our boat just stayed there for the rest of the afternoon & overnight. NO activities were planned. There was a short time of kayaking (about 20 minutes) the next morning, but it wasn't even near any of the caves so people were just basically kayaking near where we parked. And when asked about Day 2 itinerary, I was told that it'd be exactly the same as Day 1, which is entirely different from what the travel agent said (that a 2 nights tour will allow access to more areas in Halong). Moreover, the room condition was really bad. Given that it was very basic (as expected for a budget boat), I was even willing to look pass the broken tap and stained sheet. But, it was bad to a point where there were roaches on my bed, and other bugs just crawling in & out of the room all night. Other fellow travelers I talked to had a bad time on the boat as well. We wasted 80% of our time parked at Cat Ba, waiting for people to get on and off the boat, with no activities planned and didn't even get to see much of the bay. It is a shame that the opportunity to explore the beautiful Halong has been ruined by this extremely bad tour. P.S.The guide refused to tell us the name of the boat company but there's a big banner hanging on the boat that says "Halong Bay Party Castaway Tour"

I visited at the start on 2015.  It was a beautiful place, peaceful and  until one looked into the ocean it was spell binding.  It was disgusting that the pollution have been allowed to get so bad.  As one looked from the side of the boat all one could see was plastic bags, gloves cartons etc.  We were asked if we fancied swimming - I think they were joking.  The other rather disappointing part of the visit was the so called floating village, this is now just an empty shell - all the inhabitants have been moved to the mainland to improve their standards of living and education opportunities for the children.  Disappointing - it should have the UNESCO accreditation taken away until the bay is cleared up.

I joined Aloha cruise tour for two days.That tour guy made me feel terrible. Cuz he kept touching me inappropriately huged me and tried to kissed me. I said i dont like it. Stop it but he said he was joking around me. He ruined my trip. And I couldnt stop thinking about it. Unfortunately i dont remeber his name. This is his pic. If it can be problem i will delete it.

Wij hebben een 2 daagse boottocht geboekt met een nacht op een boot via Vietnam timeless Charm. We zouden om 12 uur vertrekken en dat werd al half 2. Eten aan boord niet zo lekker. 2e dag sloeg nergens op. Na ons ontbijt moesten gehaast onze cabin uit waarna we een uur aan een kade hebben gelegen waarna we direct terug naar de haven gingen. Cabins waren niet schoon. Kakkerlakken en vieze handoeken en beddengoed. Absoluut geen aanrader!
Halong boring, when you have seen 1 rock? you;ve seen emm all!!!This is more of a 2 hour trip, as oppose to a 2/3/4 day trip!!!

La baia è indubbiamente molto bella, ma non so come si faccia a rimanere indifferenti di fronte allo scempio compiuto dall uomo in questa meraviglia naturale: centinaia di navi partono dall orribile Halong city per navigare nelle sporchissime ed inquinatissime acque della baia, rilasciando smog e rifiuti. Non vedo cosa ci sia di poetico nel fare il bagno in un acqua oleosa tra barche petroliere e polistirolo. Molto meglio Malesia o Thailandia dove si possono vedere le stesse formazioni rocciose in un mare limpido, con una popolazione che comprende anche l importanza della conservazione del pianeta e non solo del denaro.
I was very disappointed with the pollution (plastic bags, etc. ) in the Bay and around the Islands. The rocks around the islands have visible pollution and the bottom of the Bay is also full of it.

DO NOT give your passport to anyone, you can give them a photocopy, but when you hand over the passport you can be held at ransom, we were unless we paid to get them back. IF we had of had the passports, we would quite easily of jumped off the junk boat, left our belonging behind and bought new once back on land. The only endearing thing about Halong Bay is the view, but that is short lived when having to deal with the lack of service, dirty and violence. I normally love everywhere BUT seriously something is wrong with these people.
感觉很像桂林山水，尖叫洞则像仙女山芙蓉洞。所以是桂林山水+仙女山芙蓉洞。旅行社很坑爹。因为台风，两天行程变一天，却只赔偿10美金，本来一天行程均价是65美金，两天提前预定则是159美金，酒店价。河内果然骗子多。
Don't know what trip was like.  I got sick and they lied about free cancellation got nothing refunded even after numerous emails and them admitting I deserve refund
天候によっては楽しめないかもしれないです晴れると海の上に浮かぶ島々と船が見渡せることができますこれは非常にきれいで特に夕日は素晴らしいです！！ツアーはいろんなものがあるので当たり外れがあると思います。
Not much to say, but I'm really disappointed to my Halong Bay experience, except for the cave which I really enjoyed. Overall it's not that worth of money and time. No offense folks! Cheers

We went on the Golden Bay cruise which cost us $155. It was dissapointment after dissapointment. Got on the boat and it looked nothing like the pictures, went to the bedroom, which looked nothing like the pictures with a hard matress, rats and cockroaches and went into the dining room, which SHOCK, looked nothing like the pictures. There were 17 people on our boat crammed round 3 small square tables eating off the corners. The food was literally disgusting and the same every day! We did nothing on the itinerary, no kayaking, 30 mins of swimming and then it was bedtime. No squid fishing, no karoke, nothing. Halong bay in itself was of course beautiful, but the tour was a massive rip off and i would advise other people to not even book a tour, but togo through the locals ferry which costs about $10. This was the end of our trip and it just ruined vietnam for us, just feel like everyone here is out to rip us off. Not great.

Dirty water, mediocre outcrop view, just like Patong in Thailand, thanks to the tour agency put us on a cruise taking use to jetty terminal area of Cat Ba island instead of the more popular mooring site. See photos. I am sure there are prettier area of Halong bay based on experience from friends and online review so beware when you book your trip.

What a waste of time!We booked a 2 day 1 night tour with Fantasea Tours. We had been highly recommended it by our hotel and told we were100 percent guarenteed to have an amazing time.On the morning of our departure we got told due to a potential storm that the tour was cancelled. We were told that we may be able to go the following day. But 10minutes later the Fantasea bus turned up and the tour guide 'nanny' said the tour was all good to go. So we got on the bus and headed for Halong bay. According to our itinery we would arrive at the port to get on our boat at 12.30. It was 1.30 before we arrived. On our first day we were supposed to have 4 hours of sailing around Halong Bay with kayayking and a cave visit. We got a 30minute paddle in some pretty rubbish kayaks and visited the cave for 30mins before getting on board. We were told the storm was coming and we had to go to Cat Ba island now instead of staying on the boat. So we headed straight there. A total of 1.5hours on the boat. The boat was nice however and the food was great. The only saving grace from this trip. As part of the itiney after dinner the crew would provide entertainment including games etc... But we just got told there is a bar down the street if we wanted then the guides went to bed.Up at 7am by strict orders so we could get on with the day. After breakfast we checked out and by 8am we were eager and ready to go. We sat on the street for an hour waiting for the bus to take us back to the boat. When we asked what was going on we just got told to wait. Then once we got to the boat at about 9.15 we sat there for nearly 2 hours on the boat not moving. It was a different smaller boat than yesterdays and suddenly there were 3 other groups with us. So there wasnt anywhere to sit upstairs to enjoy the views. Only inside the eating area which was warm and clammy because there was lack of air movement as we were still sat at the dock not moving.I asked when we would leave and got told 10 mins everytime i asked. 2 hours later we started moving. I asked what was going on and when are we sailing around the islands. They told me 'you will see them on the way back to the mainland'. Then i started to get really annoyed. I said where am i supposed to sit? when are we doing the cooking class we were promised? When do we do our hiking? and when do we get to swim? In response i was told "we have too many tourists and have to go back to the mainland now" WHAT A JOKE!It took an hour and 15 to get back to the mainland after spending a whole morning doing absolutly nothing but wait around. We then waited 45mins for the bus on the mainland to take us back to Hanoi.It was a total waste of time. We paid $95US each which was more expensive than other companies and by far the most expensive tour weve paid for in south east asia... (And weve done alot)Back at the hotel we complained and asked to speak to the company manager, which our hotel arranged by phone call. All they would do was blame 'the storm' which by the way was half an hour of light rain at 11pm. I can understand taking precaution, so i accept staying on Cat Ba island was a safety issue, and although disappointing, was acceptable. The total lack of organisation and abilty to provide 90percent of the itinery was inexcusable. After a 2 day arguement we managed to get $25US back each. Which was something but fell far short of what i believe was owed to us. We paid for a service they did not provide. We could have caught a bus to Halong and taken a boat for a day then come back to Hanoi for $30 and seen much more.I see they have lots of good reviews on trip advisor. So its clear somedays they can provide what they offer but others they woefully fail.WOULD NOT RECOMMEND!

Very nice place though the water is being polluted day by day and will eventually be dangerous to swim in. You don't have to be a marine biologist to figure out how bad it is. I asked the tour guide where the waste goes and she said the ocean. I went canoeing around the bay and must have counted 50 plastic bags in the bay. Something needs to be done soon

pessima esperienza bagno non pulito area condizionata in camera dalle 19 alle 7. Tornavamo dalle gite sulla nave alle ore 16 perché pioveva, da quel ora fino alle 19 temperatura in camera 37 gradi con 80 Di umidità  sauna non ordinata per 3 ore

Not a fan. It started out with a crammed mini bus, the guides making stops along the way to pick friends up to drop off to halong bay making a full mini bus over full. The tour was OK. We we got on the boat we sat down and we were offered drinks only to find out later that we had to pay for the drinks, Tell people it costs before just handing them out and saying nothing.To top it off on the way back we had to wait 45 minutes (not the stated 20 minute toilet stop) so the driver could rest "after a very stressful day"To top this off we narrowly avoided a head on collision after our 45 minute toilet stop however by avoiding the head on collision we did hit another bus. All the passengers felt very uncomfortable after this. We also wasted a lot of time dropping passengers off to where ever they wanted to go such as Night Markets meaning we didn't get to our hotel until close to 9:30.I would personally chose to avoid this company in the future.

I first visited Ha Long Bay in 2011. I booked a 2night/3day tour and it was an overall good experience. This time (July 4, 2015) I came with family members who live in Vietnam. We decided on a four-hour tour that was meant to be quick and easy. It was AWFUL!!!The Vietnamese tourism board does not know how to treat its own citizens wanting a vacation experience. There's truly a huge discrepancy in how they treat tourists from abroad and from within. Buying tickets was insane! Posted pricing means nothing and you really get nothing for what you pay. We went on a junk boat that didn't have running water for the bathroom. They squished people in to max capacity and offered no snacks, even to buy. We waited an hour before we could leave the dock. The awful part was that they wouldn't let us go upstairs and the smell of diesel fuel was suffocating. But my biggest concern in this whole experience was how the lack of oversight and abuse of hoarding people and boats looks like Ha Long Bay is on its way to being ruined. Yes, destroyed. They talk about sustainable tourism but how does that happen when they have no regulations in the number of boats that pollute the waters or the number of people that trample through the caves?It's so sad to see how Ha Long is destroying itself. There's a roller coaster, monolith hotels and gaudy sightseeing structures being built all around. The pristine waters that I remember from 2011 are now brown and gross. What will become of this place in the next decade if nothing is done to truly preserve it?I'm SO disappointed by how the government has allowed this area to be crushed into nothing more than an amusement park. I hope something is done to turn back the damage although I'm sure I probably won't be coming back. Here's a photo of the cave we squished through. Notice the swarm of people.

Halong Bay really is stunning and certainly worth seeing, but just don't under any circumstances book a tour. The best way to see it is by public ferry which costs a fraction of the price and you won't get stuck on a cheesy party boat. We requested a none party boat and still ended up on one. Within the trip we saw one cave and later we docked the boat and were allowed to swim or kayak. This was the extent of the entertainment on board and the crew were so uncommunicative they may as well have not been there. It was completely unorganised and you really had to try to make the best out of a bad situation,  which is not ideal when you have paid $55 per person. A lot of people paid more than us and were promised a lot more activities than us within their package,  so I'm sure that I won't be the only one to leave a review about this. We paid for a two day one night tour, the tour started at 2pm on the first day and the guide was more than happy to drop us off at 9.30am the next morning. If we hadn't confronted him about it we would have had to pay for a taxi to town even though it should have been included as should all activities until lunchtime on the second day . We never received lunch on the second day as the guide refused us. In total we were on the tour for less than 22 hours, not even one full day. If you should ever book a tour then be sure to get a written receipt listing everything that is included in your tour.I cannot express how disappointing this tour was, it was certainly the worst one we have been on during our 6 months of travelling. I would strongly advise that you go it alone to see this wonderful area otherwise your memories of it will be severely tarnished.

I have now filled in this review in 3 different ways and still it has not be able to have it displayed, so here goes again. You will be on a merry-go-round, all the boats follow each other (about 15 to 20), you will sail for about 2 hours whistle eating your lunch, your then offered trip to a rock to climb steps to the top then return to the boat, sale a little farther before dropping anchor for the night, where you're be told you need dress as king & queen for dinner you will have eaten late so will have indigestion to go to bed on,  the engine will fire up at 6am before take on trip around before heading back to port.

There is no doubt Holang bay is a must see in vietnam, magnificent place heaven on earthi would raccomend anyone to go and visit the bay when ever visiting VietnamAlthough this is not the reason of my post: we have book through an agency called holangluxurytravel which we though at the beginning to be very professional, we booked and paid for a 2 days 3 night on the glory legend cruise, but unfortunately we didnt get what we where promised and paid for, after we got pick up we realised we board on a different cruise, when we questioned why this they told us they have upgraded us to a better cruise and they have kept the news till last moment as a surprise, now we didnt want to spoil our holiday over the name of a cruise so we sailed and had a great time on our boat when we got back, we went online and checked the website to find out about our cruise - we noticed there was a difference in price, the glory legend cruise (our original cruise) is more expensive the the pelican cruise (the one we boarded)... all of this is ok so far, but when we emailed the company to question them about the difference in price, all they did is keep telling us that the pelican is a better boat then the glory legend, I cannot speak for that but clearly on their website there is a difference in price, after asking for a refund for the difference in price they totally refused to pay us back the difference in priceAfter doing some research we have notice that this upgrade as happen quiet often to few peoplei would advise not to book with this agency simply due to the fact they will promise something and give you something else, you will end up paying more and get something that is cheaper, we didnt mind to go to a different boat but I definetly wont accept being ripped off, is a matter of principle that said do go and visit halong bay on a cruise is worth every single penny

I just want to share my experience with Dugong sail operator. I was recommend at my hotel for 3D 2N cruise. First night on the boat and the second night on CAT BA , at a hotel. The first night on the boat, I was woken up by scratching a lot. And when I switch on the light, I was bitten all over from bed bugs. I show my tour guide as well the boat staff the bites all over my body.The drinks are expensive on the boat but the lunch and dinner was amazing and feeling. The presentation on the boat was good.

I would strongly recogmend not to travel threw halong bay with a cruise offered by apt travel. We were immensely disappointed about the level of service, state of the boat, the crew, the food, the guides and other staff on the boat.. just about everything.First to say: the cabines were nice and had an outside window which was nice. Only problem was, that the toilet flush didnt work for some time, but that was still ok after our experiences with other toilets around vietnam and cambodia.However not okay we found, that our 2 nights / 3 day trip which was meant to have a set program with different activities for each day - at least that was written on our programm description sheet - was not actually complied with. The first day was ok, only when the program of the second and the third day was exactly the same, meaning visiting the exact same pearlfarm twice, because the obviously mixed different tours on the same boat. Anyhow we didnt see as much of the bay as was promised to us in the brochures. The "local vine and fruit" tasting consisted of a tiny drop of not so delicious red wine ( maybe not their fault but still just one single wine for a wine tasting) and about 5 oranges sliced into quarters for 10 people as "local fruits". Food was in total ok but the very same every day. If you are still hungry their was no way of getting anything but rice without anything, getting more fruit basecly impossible. Do not expect to get the hot food main dishes like fish or meat together with the rice - it arrives at the table just about 5 minutes after everything else is finished or icecold. Meals were acompanied with hard bass electronic music which was plaid by the crew throughout the day. Whilst some may find this appropriate for the scenery, we found it nervewrakking and anoying. However the crew was not concerned about our opinion on changing or putting down the volume.After complaining about our problems and speaking to our guide he basecally told us that all wasnt his falt and that he was only the guide and visiting the same pearl farm twice was perfectly ok and adequate and that we should talk to our travel agencie. And I guess you can imagine what that means in vietnam. The guide named himselfe "Franc" and got loud and very impolite to us, resulting in him not looking at or speaking with us anymore -  eventhough we tried to settle everything in a very polite vietnamese way without getting angry.We paid about 240 USD each for the trip which was way to much for the service we got. Summing up: bad service - false brochures and program - activities on board cannot be compared to the brochures, its more like pulling it threw in order to be able to write more on the programm - impolite guide and staff - no individualism, just getting it over with the timetable.Halong Bay is a beautiful place which one should be able to enjoy and that should not be spoilt by such a bad experience. travel with a different company and hopefully you will be able to enjoy more!  GO THERE BUT NOT WITH APT!

My family and I had rented a boat to take us to see Ha Long Bay for the day. We also had lunch on the boat. What a big SCAM. The people over charged us with everything... From double charging us on all the food to the cooking expense and water expense. And on top of all that we were charged extra for coming in early when we told them from the start that we needed to be back by 3pm. We pointed out all the extra expense and they just said that it was the price. We ended up paying for it because they keep fighting with you. At the end we realize that we were also over charged in the calculation of the total bill. We asked to look at the bill again so we can add it up. They said it blew away. Would not let us see it. After all that... I came home to realize that they stole $1000 from my purse. Don't get me wrong... Ha Long Bay is amazing! It's just sad that these people scam and steal from all their tourist. I just don't want this to happen to anybody else. BEWARE! Keep your purse close to you at all time! The boat I was on was called PHUONG HANG35QN-3909

So Halong Bay used to be in my top wishlist trip, i literary grab my backpack when i suddenly got a 6 days off..but omg my expectation go down the drain when i get to the bay.Booked a 3D2N with one of their "supposed" deluxe junk..(by the way JUNK is seriously a perfect name for the boat i was in)..but seriously 3 days was really too much to be in the ares..the water that i thought would be as clear as the one i saw in Southern Thailand islands..turns out to be murky dirty stinky and full of trash..i would never want to jump into the water..it was so disappointing i since they actually promote tourism and put up veeeery pretty pics..it makes it feels like getting deceived and ripped off at the same time.. although all the pics i've taken surprisingly turn out to be as good as the one in the adds..Would never recommend this place to anyone else, but hey you're probably getting a difference experience than mine..

the water is filthy, it just shows you how f this county is because of tourism. not worth the 4 hour drive, though neither is the meekong...vietnam BLAH, the need to look at why they only have a return rate of 5%.. rudest people in the world!

We decided not to get on a tour but get a bus to Bai Chay and from the moment we got on the bus it didn't feel right.The guy who was a tour guide on the bus looked off like he was either on drugs, extremely tired or drunk, very strange his words were slurred and there was something just off about it.The bus was slow, sometimes ridiculously slow, at times knowing I could get out and cycle faster. This is done on purpose so that when you arrive they can apologise that you cannot do what you thought you were able to do, twice on the bus a Spanish guy from the group got up and complained to the tour guide about this scam.We had not been off the bus for more than 10 seconds and a lady tout approached us and seem to know we were not on a tour, how she knew we don't know, maybe we were pointed out, we hadn't moved from the large group. She followed us to the counter where you can buy your own tickets, the ladies behind the counters seemed reluctant to sell us anything, presumably because she was with us and there is now we understand a Mafia-like culture here and it starts from the moment you purchase your tour in Hanoi.So we went across the road to eat some dinner and done some research and quickly concluded we were going to be ripped off or at least wondering when it was going to happen. Stories about going to Cat Ba island, hitting the north end and being stranded from public transport save for some men motorbikes drastically overcharging did nothing to quash our fears of this place.Bai Chay has some very odd characters in it, odd behaviour and nothing reassuring, easily the weirdest and worst place we've been to in our so far 8 month tour. My fiancée hit the spot when she said "this feels like a typical border-town", people who only see you as a wallet from which they need to try and empty. We didn't have anything like this feeling in Hanoi and hopefully not in the rest of Vietnam, for sure most people like this country so I think it's just this awful place.I wish we'd have done more research; being a number 1 attraction and a UNESCO site no less we just figured on going there and navigating ourselves. It must be great right? Wrong.I think we just saved ourselves a lot of money and stress. Do your research and figure out whether just because it's a world wonder, no. 1 on TripAdvisor and a UNESCO site that you should go or whether that actually this place has been ruined by greed and scamming.We knew it was going to be hazy but we arrived here and could hardly see anything for sure January is not the time to visit this place.UNESCO should also re-evaluate whether this place should be given such status, an awful place with awful people.We're now waiting for a bus out of here, by now we've no faith that the man we talked to has arranged a bus for us or not, 4pm, 7 minutes to go, keep your fingers crossed we don't have to spend the night here!

In December we visited the much anticipated Halong Bay for a 2 night stay aboard the Violet Cruise ship.  this trip was fortunately prior arranged through our travel agent in Hanoi.  After a 4 hour road trip from Hanoi we arrived at Halong Bay to be advised that the trip may not proceed and that we would not know until 1pmwe boarded with one other couple and had a nice lunch.   at 1 we were informed that the trip could proceed but that we may not be able to stay overnight or undertake the planned itinerary.  we were take to an island where the options were to climb 400 stairs to the top or sit on the sand.  as it was a cold overcast day there was nothing to be gained by climbing to the top.  we reboarded and were told that we could overnight on the ship.   we had a nice meal and slept well.the next morning we were told that we could not follow the itinerary as we had to return to port at 9am.  we could however choose from 2 tours one being a pearl farm and one to a limestone cave where you had to get down on your hand and knees to traverse.   we chose the pearl farm and the other coupe to cave.   on return the other couple wanted also to do the pearl farm but this was denied as we had to return to port due to weather.once we returned to port we were informed that we would be joined by other couples and would not know if the day cruise would proceed until 1pm and then not until 5pm to know if we could overnight again.   (as it turned out the cruise went nowhere)we decided that rather than sit in the harbour until 1pm biding our time we would disembark and get a refund.this is where the trouble really began as we had paid $1600 for a 2 night stay and were informed that we would get a refund of $300 and have to obtain and pay for our own accommodation onshore.  ultimately our travel agent arranged and paid for the nights accommodation onshore and argued for days with the shipping company about the refund.  the Cruise Manager even detailed our short non event itinerary but the shipping company was adamant that it was our decision to leave the ship and our responsibility.this left a nasty bitter taste in both couples mouths and since that time I have encountered many couples that have had similar experiences in Halong Bay.as this seems to be a somewhat regular occurance the Violet Cruises should pre-warn tourists that there trip may not proceed and if this does happen what their options are.we stayed on shore that evening in a Paradise Cruise hotel which was very nice, serviced by good staff and served good food.   the whole hotel was full of tourists whose cruises were cancelled and they were all accommodated, fed and offered cruises the next morning.My advice is not to book with Halong Violet Cruise and be warned that your cruise may be cancelled or changed at any time.

Choosing the length of stayDont be fooled by differnt routes of the boats they will show you on map. All boats take a similar path and it doesn't matter it if will be 1 or 2 day stay. What will be different is that you will have kayaking, swimming, go see different caves and fishing for the 2 day trip. You'll stay in the same location though not going deep into the islands.Be prepared for loads of boats around you most of the time (and the noise). It's very very turisty and polluted place. We've done 2 days with 1 night stay on the 3* boat for 70$ for 1 person. Starting price was 105$ pp. Keep in mind there is a tax charge on top (10%).BusNot too comfortable especially if you're tall. Ryanair has more leg space. It takes round 3,5h and of course there is the usual half way stop at a gift shop.HLB and the boatAfter the bus you'll be shuffled into a small boat that will take you to your main one and you'll 'check in'.Cabin is way smaller than on pictures you were showed in the agency, its much more dated too. Shower works with hot water! Not electricity though (you'll have that 3h in the evening so make sure you'll charge everything then).Make sure you'll get your supplies before boarding on the boat they will charge you for e v e r y t h i n g (even drinking your own bear! - corkage charge). Beer/ coke on the boat costs 40k (in supermarket 7k). They do have a happy hour in the evening though.. They will also 'quietly' , in your face put a tip envelope at the table the last day.Next day you have to check out from your cabin at 9am which is very early and whole day ahead of you. You will be spending half of day on the windy and cold dec (december) hoping it won't rain. Alternatively you can stay in the restaurant part trying to sleep in the chair.This wasn't a pleasant day..FoodLunches and dinners are ok if you're a girl who is not fussy and doesn't eat much in general. Actually fish dishes were very nice!All food is served to share with the whole table, loads of rice and noodles, fruits as pudding in the end.If you're a table of 20tish/30tish Auz or american guys definitely it wont be enough food for you.Breakfast is a joke actually, omelette of maybe 6 eggs to share with the whole table (6 people). Loads of bread though.. All in all, food was nice but simply not enough for the whole table. Summing up: boat was rubbish. Staff unpleasant, not helpful, overcharging for everything. Kicking you out from the cabin early in the morning is not pleasant too. Bad bad example for vietnam leaving sour taste and bad memories. Moreover you might consider weather for hlb. In December its usually coldish and foggy. Clouds are low and simply you won't be able to see much. I saw friends pictures from September and thats the HLB I want to see. This time it was waisted money and time I'm afraid.Crowds are overwhelming as they are literally queues everywhere to get in (cave, mountain,  swimming).All in all, wouldn't recommend the boat and coming in bad weather at all to HLB.Ps. Very simar views you can see in china (guilin), less less cowds there though.
Lovely place but do not go with Galaxy cruises rip of from start to finish, cabins lovely staff rude & uncaring worse trip in Vietnam, they try to charge for everything

I visited Halong Bay while on a cycling tour in October 2014.  The site itself is beautiful, but the bay is absolutely disgusting.  I am stunned that UNESCO keeps it as a World Heritage Site.  It's so polluted it stinks.  I wouldn't put my big toe into this water.  My advice is to not visit this site.

One of the biggest problems with booking tours in Vietnam is the tour agencies. None of them have the same prices so it's hard to know what you are getting, and they always tell you it includes everything you ask for, but it doesn't.If you book Halong Bay do yourself a favour and book the 4 star as I haven't heard anyone complain about it yet. Almost everyone we met had a rubbish experience on the 2 star.If your budget is like ours and you have to take the cheaper option you absolutely must shop around. We paid US$95 for the 3 day trip and another guy paid $85 and some paid $135. The guy who paid $85 had the bungalow on the island included as well, you want the bungalow as the hotel sucks. Probably the most important thing you need to do when booking is get the tour operator to write everything that you want included on the ticket. This can include free alcohol, free water, kayaking, rock climbing, trip to monkey island and yes the night in the bungalow (make sure they write the word bungalow or you will end up in the hotel). I would also suggest you take a photo of the brochure so you can read back over what should be included and also show the guides, and take a photo of your ticket, in case it goes missing. My last bit of advice is avoid the boat Dragon Pearl, it has a lean to one side and smells like poo, the staff are really unfriendly and don't really want you there. Also take water with you, it's expensive on the boats.Halong Bay is the worst tour I have ever been on, but it doesn't have to be if you know what to expect.

Réservé sur la base d un luxueux prospectus, résultat nous n avons dormi qu une nuit et sommes rentrés à Hanoi...Chambres insalubres, nous avons été traités comme des animaux, personnel irrespectueux, nourriture sans goût et très très limitée. Accès à la chambre juste pour la nuit et changement de bateau y fois...Ce n est qu un résumé !!!

We had a day tour in Halong Bay and we were glad to have had a day tour only. The water was murky, and garbage floated about. Don't swim. It was shockingly disgusting and the area smelled so badly. We stopped over at the cave island and it was very underwhelming with all the kitschy, multicoloured neon lights.Bear in mind that there are over 500 boats in the bay, it can get very noisy. We also decided to have a day tour instead of an overnight one after one of the boats sank in the bay which killed foreign tourists in 2011.

I booked a one day Halong Bay cruise in July, the tour operator was South Pacific Travel. On our way to Halon Bay we stopped at "ABC" shopping center/restaurant in Dong Trieu, Quang Ninh. I bought three ladies kimonoes which by the sales person were 100% silk. There were also big signs advertising sales items to be "silk". The kimonoes have also labels 100% silk. While another sales assistant was wrapping them up, I was taken on a tour around the shop. So it was only in the evening when back in the hotel I discovered those gowns were fakes, 100% polyester.I contacted the shop but they insisted those gowns are indeed 100% vietnamese silk. And of course they refused to refund $100 I paid. I suggested I would send those fakes back so that they could replace them with real silk but was told they will not replace used items. Well, I haven't used them and I never will. As for the cruise: even though it was low season the bay and the cave were overcowded. Lunch was served but the quality of vegetarian choise was poor. Boat was not as luxury as in pictures and the ladders which were used to climb on/off the boat were shaky and dangerous.The whole tour was a huge disappointment. In my opinion there is no reason to to Halong Bay, especially if you have been to Krabi, Thailand. The scenery very much alike.

Booked a 60usd 2 day trip at bamboo travel. The girl told me we would have free drinks, a cooking class and a 3 star boat. Nothing was further from the truth.  We had a rude guide, a awefull disgusting boat almost falling apart. The beds were dirty, not cleaned at all. The toilet was dirty poo inside. The dinner was cold. We had to pay for the drinks though we booked free drinks.  No swimming and kayaking time, no cookings course. Nothing all of that. Eveything took long and we had a rude guide not answering questions. We were packed in a small bus for the trip to the bay like sardines. ..My advise: pay 100 usd per person or don't go at all. People who payed more had a good trip. This was just a skam.The scenery though was nice.  If you don't like touristic places, then skip the tour. The cave is just a normal cave like any other france or austrian cave. Nothing special. .

Be warned. Once on board you are a captive audience and as such will be priviledged to be charged premium prices for drinks. In excess of uk prices. Also dont accept a cup of tea after a meal as this is also charged to you. Halong bay is so polluted you will be swimming with flotsam and a fine diesel coating. Caves are packed out. Really dont bother.
go straight to the cruise and back to Hanoi. Halong Bay has nothing to offer. Cruise should be at leat for one night. We did 2 nights and next time it will be longer
