ハロン湾を見に行くのは、結構楽しみでした。良く考えてツアーを厳選・・・したつもりでしたが、ＧＯ ＡＳＩＡ ＴＲＡＶＥＬの１日ツアーで置いてけぼりを食らうというとんでもない出来事で終わりました。まぁ、無事に再合流できて、無事にその日はホテルで寝れたので良かったのですが。ちなみに、ツアーは４２アメリカドルでした。そして、お客を置いてきぼりにするという事態になったにも関わらず、謝罪だけで返金はありませんでした。そしてハロン湾ですが、もし、景色だけを楽しみたいのであれば、カヤックではなく、バンブーボートを選ぶことを強くお勧めします。カヤックだと、どうしても水面に目が行ってしまって、大量のゴミや汚い泡などを見てしまいます。世界遺産＝綺麗と思っていた私たちが無知だったのでしょうか。カヤックだったら泳いだりできるという考えの下、選んでしまったのです。もちろん泳ぐなんてとんでもない。水の中なんてどうなっているのか皆目見当が付かない。水面ギリギリに出て来た小魚はどうにか見えるけど、あとはもう・・・。こんなのじゃ泳ぐなんてのは無謀な考えです。水に触りたくもないし。最初は浮かんでたゴミを拾っていたのですが、そのゴミの多さに諦めました。第一、カヤックに乗り換えた時の船着き場の人たちが吸っていたタバコの吸い殻を簡単に海の中に捨ててしまうんですから。景観（水を除く）を眺めているならば素晴らしいです。流石世界遺産と言えますけど。途中寄った鍾乳洞は大きく、多色でライトアップされていて、ガイドに「何でこんなライトアップされているの？」と聞いたら、「自然な色は泥で汚い色ですからね」と言われました。ちなみに、この洞窟内に生息していたコウモリなどは、全てに追い出したそうです。足元もしっかりとした階段が作られていて、邪魔な鍾乳石は取り除かれていたり、コンクリートで固められていて、鍾乳石に細工した噴水などもあります。ハロン湾にしろ、鍾乳洞にしろ、これらが見応えのある美しい景色、流石、世界遺産であると捉えるのかどうかは個々によって変わると思いますが、私にはちょっと疑問の残る場所でした。また、別の話になりますが、前述の船着き場で体調不良になった人がいましたが、殆ど適切な対処はされていなかったように思えました。やはり暑い中での観光になるので、特に熱中症には気を付けた方が良いと思います。着替えを持っていく、ホテルから凍らせたペットボトルの水を数本持参する、日焼け止めを使う、帽子を被る、そういった自己対策が必要だと思います。

Un posto incantevole, unico al mondo, una delle "7 Meraviglie del mondo" ma anche "una discarica pubblica". Navigando tra isolotti verdissimi che fuoriescono dall'acqua puzzolente e infestata da pattume,oli di scarico e dal liquame scaricato in mare dalle toilettes dei tantissimi battelli che giorno e notte percorrono la baia. Da suicidio fare il bagno in quelle splendide spiaggette !

Lets start with the positive. Halong Bay is stunning and a real treat to the eyes. Now , on to the negatives : you have seen it better online and in the movies before! All boats have to stop in the same bay ( government regulations), all tourists go swim on the same disgustingly polluted and tiny beach, go kayaking in the same place. And oh, by the way, right before he shows you where to go, the guide lightly specifies that it is pointless to try to fish because its been a long time since the squids have disappeared. You will also visit the same underground cave....And will not be allowed to jump from the boat. No matter which boat you choose, you will experience  the same "disaster in the making". As to the boats, the sleeping arrangements  and the quality of the food will vary based on the price you are ready to pay. More expensive oats have fewer cabins so there will be fewer of you taking the same shot at 5am ( and no, dont think of taking a kayak in the morning, it is not part of the programme!).Dont get me wrong, it might be the best thing ever for the Halong Bay that tourists are confined to a  filthy , tiny portion of it. It may help the rest of the Bay to remain untouched - or at least I would hope so - but it surely makes fro a very poor experience. What is left? Either you go and see it for yourself. Or you dont go and will probably regret it forever. Try to go for a day without sleeping there?( I am not sure this is possible?) it would certainly not have been practical for us with 3 young kids and with a lengthy time to get to and from Halong bay being significant from Hanoi. In any case, 2 nights there is one night too many!

I'll start by saying halong bay is beautiful and amazing, although I was taken aback by the amount of rubbish in and all around halong bay which was disappointing. We along with another couple booked a 3 day 2 night tour with joy tours/travel and oh my god.... Disaster food was a disgrace, at every bus or boat change we were all left waiting 45 min minimum for next boat or bus, we were supposed to do two nights on boat but were shipped to Island to a horrible hotel for one night, the staff on boat were incredibly rude and blatantly ignores any questions! Two girls on board from oz who had saved for months actually started crying at how much of a disaster everything was!!! BEWARE!!!

Went on a day cruise in Halong Bay. Let's just say this was probably once an incredible place, but now the greed of the human race has destroyed it. The water is heavily polluted with oil slicks and plastic everywhere. We could barely see the limestone karsts due to air pollution. Boats everywhere you look. The cave is the biggest tourist trap I've ever been too, simply too many people being herded through like cattle. The caves themselves are quite amazing but you're too busy trying to avoid people to appreciate it. Also litter all through due to the numerous vendors in the caves... Yes in the caves. If you want to see beautiful limestone karsts, go to southern thailand. Vietnam, sort it out you are destroying this so-called sacred place.

We were excited to visit Ha Long Bay and hired a private car to take us from Hanoi up there for the day. A 2-3 hr white knuckle ride on the Vietnamese Road got us to Ha Long in a steady drizzle. The coast line has been developed in what can only be described as 1970s communist holiday resort style. Yalta meets The Costa Del Sol. Apparently due to the proximity to the Chinese border there are buses loads of tourists coming over the border to visit. Once there a long line of junks take backpackers and local tourist out on a well worn trail around the bay. Almost in convoy. Secluded, unspoilt location this is not. As we pulled out into the bay, the Junk navigated through oils slicks, discarded beer cans and tampons, all visibly seen from the side of the junk. Compared to the pristine waters of the Palawans, this is was  filthy. Too many tourists, too many junks, too much pollution. Maybe wonderful in the 1990a but don't bother

My wife and I did a two day trip in Halong Bay. We were in Hanoi, went to Halong Bay to an over nigh trip on the boat. It was very much like a conveyor belt of boats and tourists. The 50 to 100 boats all do the same.The sea is dirty due to all the boats emptying all the water and waste in it. The views are good.I am sounding very negative because I was in the Philippines last year, Palawan Island, and it was soo much better than Halong Bay. The water in Palawan was clear blue, clean, hot thus my lower opinion of the Halong Bay experience.In my opinion, give Halong Bay a miss and head south. Hoi An was the place.I loved our 4 weeks traveling from Hanoi, Sapa, Hue, Hoi An, Saigon. Also made it into Siem Reap and Phnom Penh.Vietnam was better than I was hoping for. The people were very friendly, the food was excellent and the weather was the cherry on the cake.

We visited Halong Bay in early April this year.  We had a day tour to Halong Bay organised by our hotel in Hanoi.  We were picked up on time from our hotel and driven by a small shuttle bus for our 1/2 day tour of the bay.  We had a lovely 4 hours visiting 2 caves and a floating village with a wonderful group of people from all over the world.  As we were staying  a further 2 days on the bay we enquired via our tour group as to a full day trip on the bay the following day and were introduced to a Mr Son.  We were told that we could not do a group tour the following day but could do a private 8 hr tour for a discounted price!  As we were keen we agreed and went back to his office where he tried to make us pay for 3 people instead of 2.  My partner immediately noticed this mistake which Mr Son initially denied but did not know how to rectify.  After 30 mins he eventually gave us some cash to repay his "mistake" which started the alarm bells for us.  We were picked up late, were put on a boat after 1100 with no tour guide or anyone who could communicate with us (completely non english speaking), and tried to take us to the same places we had already been the day prior.  Although promised a 8 hr tour got a 4 hr tour.  We managed to listen to other tours who had proper guides as to any information regarding the caves we did visit at our own cost!!  WERE RIPPED OFF by Mr Son so beware of him!!

If you ever visited Greek waters by boat, Halong Bay will probably be a big disappointment. We stayed on a overpriced private boat, in an fixed run schedule: pick-up in Hanoi, tourist trap under way, mooring at noon, lunch at 1, kayaking at 4, dinner at 7 with overpriced wine ... No room for any changes in their schedule possible. Unfortunately the authenticity is long gone in the bay. For me, it felt all like one big organized tourist trap and if i had to re-plan my trio to Vietnam, I would definitely avoid Halong Bay.

I was very disappointed by my trip to this UNESCO World Heritage site. The rock formations are beautiful, but the tourist boats were tacky despite their "luxury cruise" tags. They were far too numerous and seemed limited to such an extent that you felt you were on a municipal boating lake - about to bump into another boat at any minute, rather than cruising a glorious expanse of bay. The water was very murky everywhere we went, with the smell of diesel all around. Plastic bottles, bags and lumps of polystyrene dotted the surface of the water so much that I did not feel I ever saw a clear stretch. The caves, lagoons and vertiginous outcrops are spectacular, but the authorities and the tour operators need to clean up their act before the whole place is totally ruined.

Very hard to find a good tour. Friends had warned us. Pay a good price so you will have a goog tour. WRONG  !!! In our tour (2 days 1 night) a family of 3 paid 100$ each, 2 others paid 60$ each and us 80$ each. SAme treatment, same boat. Rooms were dirty, we saw rats and the meals TErRRIBLE.My suggestions is go to the dock when tourist return from the tour and ask them directly how it was and how much they paid. Get the names of the boat in written. Good luck!Oh yes i forgot to comment the halong bay is beautiful ! But water is dirty and full of floating  rubbish,

A lot of the time in March there is either fog, haze, mist, or pollution, so that you will not be seeing anything at all, and the boats are not that fun, unless you like low rent booze cruises with tour groups. The water is polluted and full of jellyfish so don't think about swimming, that won't happen either.

We had terrible experience with Papaya Cruise! The same day our friends were on Dugong cruise and almost the same story as well. We booked our cruise through Papaya Travel. We were promised a newly renovated  boat with one night two day stay - $85p/pWe were picked from the hotel on time by their minibus with Papaya travel logo. Ride was ok and 4 hours later we were in Halong Bay. As soon as we arrived we realized that two of us where the only ones who will be there 2 days, everyone else on our bus was just for a day tour. Its fine they separated us and took us to some other boat and delivered 15 of us to the Dragon Cruise Ship. The ship was fine, cabin very comfortable with large window and private bathroom. We were served lunch just after 1pm, but the boat crew got confused that there were 7 of us who do not eat meat. They did not expect that. After lunch plans started changing and things going down the hill. At first we were told we will go kayaking and the beach right after lunch so we all can relax till 3:30. We will go to cave tomorrow since it is too late today.  At 3:30 plans changed, no kayaking and no beach. And the cave is closed. But few minutes later we all were told ok we will go to cave but they are canceling the whole trip due to the weather and it is a government order. We were all ok since we could see the choppy waters and strong winds. Only on our second try due to very choppy water we were able to get in our rescue boat and were taken to the cave. Cave was very nice and for us it was the biggest one we have ever seen. After the cave we came back to our boat at 4:30pm and thats when it got even worse. Next to us there was same size boat parked sideways and tied to our boat. Our guide didn't explain anything. He just kept repeating: "don't worry, same company". The water got very choppy all of us sudden and since boats were tied loosely they started crashing against each other. The boats were swaying sideways and constantly crashing. We couldn't even stand and the noise of a crash was terrifying. It crashed so hard that the top deck of the other boat started falling apart and you could see rotten wood underneath. Some passengers started yelling  at the guide asking what is happening and why the boats are not being untied. He told us to calm down: "its the same company". No further explanation. Passengers on the other boat looked terrified as well. Finally on our request we got life jackets to put on. The boats were not untied and we realized that after about 15 terrifying minutes we were moving. Maybe after half an hour we all realized that next boat must have been broken and this was the way to bring it back. At the same time when we were all terrified, our guide told us to bring our room keys. It meant that from 5pm we cant even stay in our rooms, we were all seated in the dinning room quietly and again no explanations. Boat next to us was build the same way and we all could see what is going on in their dinning room. They were playing music, everyone drinking (or were offered a free drink due to scare) and at about 6pm their candlelight dinner with flowers was served on the table. Everyone was happy and having good time while their guide was kindly smiling and talking to his guests. We were freezing, the drinks were put away and bar was closed, and we couldn't even use our rooms. We just set there dressed in what ever we had to keep us worm and waited till 8pm to reach the shore. We were taken to the hotel, were served everything-deep-fried dinner. Later We got rooms to spend a night. In the morning we had awful buffet style breakfast and were free till lunch. Lunch was again lacking flavor meal. Got into the minibus that said Papaya Travel and were brought to Hanoi. Over all, it was awful experience. We didn't get any refund and we spend only 3 hours of quality time on the boat. The rest of it was lacking any kind of humanity or safety. I would never recommend anyone to book with these companies. When I asked the guide what was the name of the company he said: Bamboo travel.  My advice now is if you can afford - take the most expensive one or none at all. See the reviews for the most expensive cruises and you will see what a huge difference. Also google sinking boats in Halong Bay - you will be surprised with the amount of accidents.

Be careful in booking the day-trip around HaLong Bay.Our boat operator did not furnish us with life jackets while sending us for kayaking.The lunch was not very palatable in dishes were clearly insufficient to go around, even by their local standards.One boat operator charges us additional money for drinks (at exorbitant prices too) even though they claimed they lunch was included in the package. They totally stopped the boat engine, before asking for payment from everyone in the boat.In fact, near the beginning of the trip, they ditched the whole group of us (10-20) on an isolated island, if not for a guy who confronted them with a kayak, and a hp!

Disappointment from start to finish. We were ripped off every chance they got. The boat was falling apart and our tour guide Jerry barely spoke and never explained a thing. We paid for the middle range and were given well Below that and had a room on the boat with no toilet paper bed bugs and no water to drink, unless if course we paid for it.

The really beautiful landscape is only the theater for all kind of antic scams and threats by locals and so called "tour guides" and "operators". I booked a "reliable" company, so better, but still disgusting.Still no serving spoons for the so called "meal" after more than 100000000 complaints about that. Be asured that some smelly  tourist, you wont like to share a bus with, will eat directly from the common "dishes" of "food".  You will  have no dougt about the quality and cleanliness once you see the "kitchen", directly beside the toilets with lots of ... on the floor but not even water to wash hands.The dirt is everywhere, the marine live near extinct, it smells of diesel and worse things.Paper. plastik and sanitary debris  floating over the oilspill is not at all romantic, its awfull.Halong bay turned into one of the biggest dumps I ever saw, beside Egypt ("pigtown") 30 years ago. This within 5 years only!The rudenes of the locals involved in this Tourist attraction is something you will experience for sure, complaining, screaming tourists everywhere- and be prepared for the next scam at the next stop. 30000dong for softdrinks to give the "poor locals" an income! For sure within a few  days a higher income than the local  doctors in a month. No Tourist agency stops that!Some misleaded tourist even give presents to the  locals, that are much richer, compared to local standards,than rich people back home!For 30 minutes Bamboo boat tourist pay more than a worker earns in some days!All this will be worse pretty soon because Vietnam is eager to get the so called "good tourists" to the country. Governement thinks about an extra "entrance fee" of 1 Dollar a day beside the Visa fee of 50Euro (sure, after bargaining at the embassy in Bangkok, they want 90 Euro, that equals the announced 25 US Dollar in their opinion. And btw its illegal to be in Thailand without your passport! Good morning Vietnam is history, welcome good tourists will be soon. We will see whats better!

I cannot recommend Halong Bay or any of its supposed attractions at all.  The region is dirty, polluted and over-exploited.We were promised "gin clear" waters - what we got was water that was so dirty you could not even see a few millimetres into it.  And I don't want to even speculate on what some of the stuff we saw floating in the bay was...We were promised "outstanding views" - what we got was air so dirty and polluted that you were lucky to be able to see a few hundred metres.The "world famous" cave is nothing more than some colourful lighting and a never-ending queue of people being pushed though as fast as possible with no thought to how much damage they are doing to the interior of the cave.The boats servicing the bay are dirty, not maintained and in a lot of cases dangerous to even walk around due to rot in the deck timbers.  We saw boats with serious holes in the hulls which made you wonder about how safe they really are.And throughout all this you are subjected to a never-ending barrage of "you buy, you buy" for overpriced junk trinkets.It may be a Unesco world heritage are in theory.  What it is in practice is a dirty, over-priced, over-crowded, over-exploited dump that is not worth the time or money to visit.

I was very sorry for state of the bay. I could not believe the dirt, it looked like a floating dump! garbage islands were formed. When we went kayaking we saw poop, uneaten food, bags. the natural setting is beautiful but the experience is so disappointing if you like nature. we had already read recommendations to go to Bai Tu instead of Halong, DO IT!  By the way, don't choose v'spirit company.

Just terrible - pollution everywhere, smog so bad you can't see the islands and a thin layer of petrol on the water.Be extremely careful booking tours. We booked one from Cat Ba to Ha Long city - via the bay. They lied about the tour - we didn't go to half the places they said we would and then while we were in a cave they stole all our electorics and two million dong. Our bags were locked tight so I have no idea how they managed to steal our things! They even replaced my partner's tablet with a karaoke DVD.

Amazing halong... это только реклама! ничего похожего на картинку вы не встретите (красные и оранжевые паруса ясным солнечным днем, деревянные корабли, ну может один парус на горизонте, а все остальные - неадекватно дорогие старые корыта выкрашенные одинаковой белой краской и очень - очень много мусора в воде, в погоде доминирует туман и шумный выхлоп старых дизелей (мы заказали один из недешевых вариантов - 3 дня Deluxe Double Cabin - корабль Oriental Sails). Однозначно НЕрекомендуется к посещению. Мне нравится Вьетнам по соотношению цена/качество, но не Халонг - это развод. Если вам повезет с ясной погодой, то сможете сделать пару фоток, но все остальное совсем не айс, не тратьте драгоценное время и нервы, там нечего делать. Лучше ограничиться фотками из интернета и поехать на Фукуок.

I was in Halong bay several times before and always boat ride on clasical Vietnames boats was a very nice experience and boats were all nicely decorated in different shapes and colours. We wisited Halong Bay in the end of october 2013 and found out that all boats are painted in white - dissaster all those nice boats turned into old fishing wessels. They say that the mayor ordered all boats to be painted in white so that they can better see each other at night, the other option is that mayor wife has painting company. Whatever the truth is the boats now looks terible and more than 50% of the Halong bay experience is lost. I mean those hills are quite nice but if there is a charming boat floating by the picture is much nicer. Who would like to drive from Hanoi for almost 4 hours one way to see countless nicely shaped rocks standing  from the sea covered with ugly, dirty white boats that looks like fishing sheeps in need of serious reapir.Thats my experience in Ha Long bay which for me used to be one of highlights of Vietnam now it is a complete dissaster so don't go there and hopefully major will decide that boat owners can paint their boats as they wish, as they did before. Maybe Halong bay will get its charms  back.

Beste reiziger, zoals velen hebben ook wij zitten wikken en wegen of we naar Halong Bay zouden gaan. Uiteindelijk schrijven veel reizigers dat het tegenvalt, maar je toch moet gaan. Daar ben ik het niet mee eens. Ik kan iedereen aanbeveel het links te laten liggen! Neem wat meer tijd in ninh binh en maak daar boottochtjes bij de kalksteenformaties. Qua ervaring een heel goed alternatief. Uiteraard niet vanaf zee maar vanaf de rivier gezien. Als je met de fiets door de regio toert, krijg je met regelmaat de vraag of je een tochtje wilt maken. Je vermijd zo de drukte en kunt volop genieten. Wat er mis is aan Halong Bay? We zijn met Alova Gold Cruises gevaren. 1 overnachting. Maatschappij staat goed bekend. Goed was de chauffeur die ons van Hanoi naar de aanlegsteiger reed. Ook het eten was erg goed (ook het vegetarisch). Vreselijk was de drukte. We voeren rond 11 uur in de file naar het gebied. Een uurtje onderweg. Toen ging (achter de eerste rotsformatie) het anker uit. Vanaf dat moment hebben we eigenlijk niet meer gevaren. Een paar honderd meter naar links, een paar honderd meter naar rechts. Bezoek aan uitzichtpunt was mooi. Strandje om te zwemmen een aanfluiting. Daarna in de kano. File varen. Water is ronduit vuil. We voeren door olievlekken en heel veel afval (plastic). Ook vissersdorp was een farce. Mensen zijn ongeïnteresseerd en de bekkens waarin de vissen zouden moeten zitten waren leeg of vuil. Na de kano-tocht op onze overnachtingsplaats het anker uitgegooid. Tussen de rotsen door uitzicht op de stad!! Later op de avond kwam er vast tegen de boot waarop wij zaten, een tweede Alova boot liggen. De mensen die aan die kant sliepen keken uit op de slaapkamer van de andere boot. Wij keken godzijdank uit over het water. Waar we ook keken, overal andere boten. Niet alleen Alova laat het na om door het mooie gebied te varen. Ik had de indruk dat het gemeen goed is niet rond te varen. Wat een gemiste kans!!! na een avond droog zitten (drank aan boord is zo duur dat niemand aan boord een glas dronk) lekker geslapen in de comfortabele kleine kamer. de volgende ochtend 7 uur opstaan. 300 meter varen en collectief door een grot. Mooi maar wij hadden er al meer (en mooiere gezien). Daarna in een rechte lijn (en niet zoals aangeduid op de borden waarop de routes worden afgebeeld) naar de haven. 11.30 uur lunch en daarna in de bus richting Hanoi! Op onze boot zaten ook mensen die 2 overnachtingen hebben geboekt. doe werden, toen wij naar de haven gingen met een kleine boot opgehaald. Toen onze boot de volgende groep had opgehaald, kwamen de mensen die 2 nachten bleven weer aan boord. We hebben later gehoord dat zij weer constant op een plek hebben gelegen. Niets meer gezien van de regio... Met zijn vieren USD 440 lichter en een illusie armer.

I would like to put a warning about these companies. We booked through booking.com (which have now removed them) and agoda also carries this company. We paid for the trip 276 dollars and the actual cost was around 60 dollars. We were lied to repetitively and the rooms advertised had none of the amenities. The boat was awful. We were effectively put on a hostel boat. We were not allowed to return back to Halong until the second day and we felt very distressed and anxious as we were not sure what would happen as no one would tell us the truth. I am requesting that agoda removes this company also. Please beware.

I just finish today my experience with this company and i am very disappointed, i was in the second floor in a premium cabin, are smaller than the pictures, but for me the problem is the company is very dirty and don't take care with the environment. In the morning when the housekeeping service start to clean my cabin i see my empty bottle of water and the toilet amenities farewell two metres of distance across our toilet window directly to the sea... i went to inform to the manager of the cruise and he tell me is a accident, just dropped because housekeeping service put near the window... Finally are a dirty liars because is impossible one bottle dropped and finish farewell two meters out from the window

What a pity. A world heritage area destroyed by rubbish and pollution!!Mismanaged like soapy heritage sites. It's being smashed for a quick $$$ and no one cares. Needs some STRICT control FAST or it will be lost.

We just had an awful experience with AST Travel. The brochure theo have about the Christine Deluxe Cruise is false and misleading. They say the boat started operation in 2012. I think theeblaren is at least 15 years old. A short summary: room 101 (family room) had leakages on several points (they said it was because of the rain), so baskets and towels were everywhere on the grond in our room. Bathroom was broken on several points, bathroom was very dirty. From the 15 deckchairs, at least 12 were broken for a long period. The sails could not rise because they were broken as well. This about Christina.Then the tour. We did 2 nights on the boat and 1 night at Cat Ba Island (bngalow). From the 4 days we hooked we spent at least 2,5 days travelling. Besides we spent lots and lots ofntime waiting to pick up other tourists, mixing groups, dividing groups. So its really a waist of your time!Wen we travelled to Cat Ba Island our boat stopped working. We spent 1 your at open sea waiting for another boat topics us up!! There were cockroages at the bungalow we stayed in on Cat Ba Island. And so on, and so on.So one advice: just don't book with AST Travel, it's really not worth your money!

I just want to re-issue a warning about kayaking in Halong Bay. My experience was in 2009 but I don't see enough about the dangers in the Bay area. I travelled with a friend and we arranged our Halong Bay Boat trip through a local operator on Cat Bah island. The boat was fine as was the intended programme which included the use of a kayak that we took with us. The kayaking turned into a nightmare because we were sucked into a cave that looked just like the many caverns at the bottom of the limestone karats that are generally only a metre deep. We capsized and were dragged into complete darkness in a very strong current, lost the kayak and fought for our lives, trying to drag ourselves along the cave walls against the current towards what we hoped was the exit. Rescue was slow to come; anyone less of a strong swimmer might have drowned.The greatest shock came though when nobody really cared about our trauma or injuries but we were instead maltreated because we had lost the kayak. There was a demand for 500 US dollars on the spot and when we refused to pay (or couldn't because we didn't have so much cash on us) we were held to ransom on the boat. Only by phoning the guy we had arranged the tour through were we eventually let back on land and the drama continued until a day or so later when we got our passports back in Hanoi. I won't say don't go to Halong Bay - it is beautiful but be weary of the kayaking. There was no warning and our boat crew clearly did not know about the danger either.

We just arrived back from a very disappointing trip to Halong Bay due to a scam. We booked through a tour company that claimed to be "Sinh Cafe Travel" but in actual fact was one of the many fake Sinh Cafe's. We booked for an overnight cruise on a delux boat with Dujong Sails and paid $80 each.The tour guide who arrived at the hotel in the morning was from a completely different company "Joy Travel" and was taking us on a completely different boat. When we asked what was going on our trusty tour guide stated "I'm just the tour guide" which seemed to be his catch-phrase for the trip. He told us we needed to get on the bus and he would contact the tour company and sort it out.Over 2 hours into our journey to Halong Bay we stopped, and after nagging he finally contacted the tour company. We then realised that we were going on a budget boat that most people had paid $55 for - this is not where we planned on celebrating our friends birthday. We explained that we did not want to go on that boat as it was not the same standard that we paid for. We were given the choice of finding our own way back to Hanoi or getting back on the bus. Reluctantly we chose the bus.Trying to stay positive we thought it might not be so bad. We were wrong. When we got on the boat it was very obvious that they overbooked. There were 30 people and only 8 rooms. We crammed in the cabin for lunch and then were on our way to the caves. We were told that another boat would arrive after the seeing the caves and half of us would go on that boat. Another lie.After arriving back from the caves all 30 boarded the boat again. Still without rooms we cruised around the bay. We paid $5 for a "free kayak" and sat on the roof of our boat wondering where we we would sleep. Any time anyone asked a question our trusty guide would say "I don't know, I'm just the tour guide". Our boat came to a stop at Catba island where we were tied to the pier for 2 hours. We were not allowed to to get off the boat or swim here so just sat and waited.Again we asked the tour guide what was going on and where the other boat was. It was very clear that he had no idea - he then asked me to inform everyone on board that he had no idea where everyone was sleeping or eating that night. I refused. Finally at 7pm, 6 hours after the check-in time, another boat arrived and we could all check into our rooms. We were then given the option of swimming, since it was very dark at this stage most people declined.At 7.30 the next morning breakfast was served, we had to check out of our rooms by 8 and was then given the opportunity for swimming - for a whole 2 minutes!!! We then went back to Catba pier and waited again. Our tour guide abandoned our boat here and joint another tour group. We were then taken back to the mainland and were off the boat by 10.30 - three hours earlier than our printed schedule.We were picked up by another tour guide on the wharf and were told we were going to restraunt for lunch. After standing on the side of the road for over an hour in the heat a minivan finally arrived to take us to the restraunt where we ate cold rice and fish. A bus came to pick us up after lunch, we all crammed in and then sat in front of the restraunt aimlessly for 20 minutes. We began the drive back to Hanoi only for the bus to turn around after 10 minutes to go back to the restraunt so that more people could cram into our bus. Finally we arrived back in Hanoi and met with the tour company. After an hour long heated conversation, threatening to contact the police and the embassy we were able to get a $40 back from the trip.Halong Bay was beautiful and in the past I have had an amazing time there, but be really careful who you book through. Do not trust the people at "Sinh Cafe" on 13 Cau Go or "Joy Travel" as they are con-artists and theives.

un lieu magique, mais qui a perdu toute once de magie...des bateaux à gogo, une pollution des locaux et des touristes omniprésente, des odeurs de kérosène aussi fortes que celles du périphérique parisien, un brouillard de pollution qui ne permet pas d'apercevoir les magnifiques formations géologiques...un brouhaha permanent, des ridicules bouts de plages bondés, les grottes visitables bondées elles aussi, surchargées.la nuit sur place est inutile car on se croirait à ibiza ou dans un spring break...si vous aimez la nature, vous aurez le choc de votre vie.

A nuestro parecer, solo se puede disfrutar de este panorama si se logra IGNORAR el OLOR a diésel del motor de cientos de barcos, el RUIDO de sus motores y generadores y los EMPUJONES de miles de turistas.En 10 años este paraíso ha dejado de serlo para convertirse en una trampa para turistas. En el puerto nos asaltó un hombre con las supuestas fotos del barco. Contratamos una noche en clase "superior" a 90 $ por persona (180 $ por un cuchitril doble):La cama tenía solo una sábana bajera y una manta de olor sospechoso.La comida estaba incluida en el precio, pero la bebida NO, ni siquiera el agua, cuyo precio era abusivo (5 veces el precio normal)!Durante toda la noche escuchamos el ruido del generador eléctrico bajo nuestras cabezas.Dormimos rodeados de decenas de barcos que no apagaron sus generadores en toda la noche (adiós al mágico encanto del entorno).El agua está sucia, llena de aceite flotante y toda clase de porquerías.Está prohibido bañarse, excepto en una pequeña playa masificada (aunque esta agua contaminada no invita al baño).A esto hay que añadir que a una pareja le robaron 50 € de la cartera al dejarla en el barco  durante el paseo en kayak (se dieron cuenta al desembarcar).

In december een 2daagse trip gemaakt vanaf hanoi naar halong bay. Het was mistig, beetje regenachtig, maar vooral heel druk. Ik zou het niemand aanbevelen om met slecht weer deze trip te maken. Het uitzicht is maar beperkt door mist. De kou en regen zorgen ervoor dat je niet op dek kan zitten en binnen is de ruimte maar beperkt. Het is enorm druk met boten. de motoren draaien dag en nacht, waardoor je constant lawaai hebt en de geur van diesel de schoonheid van de plek bederft. Jammer!

We took a day trip to Halong Bay with Golden Lotus and it was complete crap! The food on the boat was disgusting (plastic chewy squid, French fries, tofu, a few pieces of fish and rice..this was for 7 ppl altogether, not on everyone's plate..and we had to pay for drinks 3times the price you get in a resto!), the normal activities that are normally included in the price (i.e. get on a little boat to see a cave and I am not talking about kayaking) where charged extra. On top, on the way back, the bus left ppl in the middle of a street saying we are close to the hotel when we had to walk for a while with no map or indication how to get to the right street. Just avoid them if you look for a pleasant trip to HB.

Do not, I repeat do not, use these people to take you down to Halong Bay. You are crammed on to a minibus with 20 or so other lost souls, with no room to move. Your first 3/4 of an hour is spent going around Hanoi picking up people. This is a 3-4 hour trip and there is no room to move. Rip off.We got off in Hanoi, preferring to walk back to the hotel, rather than put up with the ambulatory slum that was provided for us. A great holiday sullied..

There is no doubt that Halong bay is an incredibly beautiful spot in this world..but we messed up.  Because we were on an extended trip, (4 months) and money was getting tight, we opted for a bargin over-night trip.  Unfortunately there was a fellow Canadian onboard with a Vietnamese short-term "girlfriend" to whom he was very abusive verbally.  Later, when supper was served, he comlained loud and long and finally threw his meal out the window into the bay.  All the guests were upset by his behavior but that did not end things.  When he retired, the "girlfriend" said to the staff that she was hungry and a waiter brought a rice bowl down to the room but returned very upset and with a swollen cheek.  Was the nightmare ended?  Not quite.  We then retired only to find that the toilet had overflowed, spilling sewage onto the bathroom floor with the anticipated stink.  No other rooms were available so my wife and I took our blankets out onto the deck and got about 45 minutes sleep on the hard floor...then it began to rain.  Well, the crazy man was bad luck, but next month we will return to Vietnam because we love the place.  We will also book a two night visit to Halong Bay but it certainly won't be on a bargin-basement cruise.  Wish us luck.Butch

Wir buchten den 3days/2 nights trip für unsere Hochzeitsreise und gönnten uns dafür den Suite-Room mit Balkon am Heck des Boots. Das Essen war sehr gut und das Schiff eigentlich gemütlich. Am Abend war jedoch der "romantische" Balkon nicht mehr benutzbar, weil dieser, sobald das Boot seine Ankerposition erreicht hat von den Abgasen des Dieselgenerators (welcher die ganze Nacht läuft) eingenebelt wird.Am zweiten Tag begann dann die Tragikkommödie. Während die 2days/1night Gäste den zweiten Tag auf dem Hauptboot bleiben konnten und gegen Mittag nach Bai-Chay zurückkehrten um neue Gäste aufzuladen, wurden wir auf ein anderes Boot für den Tagestrip verfrachtet (davon stand nirgends etwas). Diese Boot war eine Erfahrung an sich, Ohne jeglichen Komfort, ein schmutziges Hozdeck auf welchem Müll und Zigarettenstummel herumlagen und über den Zustand der sanitären Einrichtungen schweige ich mich aus. Einzige Sitzgelegenheiten waren drei, teilweise zerbrochen Plastikstühle (n.b. für 4 Personen). So verbrachten wir also einen ganzen Tag auf diesem Müllboot fernab des Komforts des Hauptschiffes und unserer Suite-Kabine, die wir ja bezahlt hatten. Das beste kommt jedoch noch: als wir am Abend endlich zum Hauptboot zurückkehrten fanden wir unsere Kabine durch neue Gäste besetzt. Unsere privaten Gegenstände waren während unsere Abwesenheit vom Personal in eine gewöhnliche Kabine verfrachtet worden. Der Reiseführer fand, es hätte leider eine Doppelbuchung gegeben. Offensichtlich hatte er auch entschieden, dass wir es sind, die in diesem Fall über die Klippe zu springen hatten. Die nachfolgende Diskussion war wenig erbaulich und der gute Mann wurde, wie dies in solchen Fällen offenbar üblich ist, gehörig unfreundlich. Da uns die Lust auf jede weitere Minute auf dieser Junke unterdessen gehörig vergangen war, verlangten wir, sofort an Land nach Bai-Chay gebracht zu werden, was der Reiseführer zuerst verweigerte (man stelle sich einmal das vor). Nachdem wir auch diese Diskussion hinter uns gebracht hatte und wir ihm mit der Polizei drohen mussten (wer lässt sich als zahlender Gast schon gerne gegen seinen Willen auf einem Schiff festhalten) wurden wir dann endlich an Land gebracht, wo wir dann endlich auf alle weiteren Dienstleistungen dieses Operators verzichten konnten. Natürlich haben wir sofort beim Operator eine Rückzahlung eingefordert (in Anbtracht des Erlebten wäre eine 100% Vergütung wohl das Mindeste gewesen), was natürlich zuerst einmal katerorisch verweigert wurde. Nach etwa je 12 Emails in jede Richtung und schlussendlich der Drohung mit einem Anwalt wurden uns dann, etwa 8 Monate später 110$ zurückerstattet. Es ist jedermann selbst überlassen, ob er sich das antun will, Wir lachen heute über diese Slapstick-Episode und buchen solche Trips nur noch nach einem Augenschein vor Ort oder verlässlichen Empfehlungen.

The landscape is very impressive, but there are too many tourists.Better go to Lan Ha Bay which has very nice cottages on lonely beaches to stay. Lan Ha Bay is much less crowded and not a bit less impressive.

everything. Not what we paid for. Never again. The advertising ís false and the boats are nothing like in the pictures. The food consisted of rice and cabbage. Be ware that their are no superior or luxery cabins available. So dont pay anymore than $45.00. The tour guide ís very angry and scary person. Dont book through the company Nguyen Dang JSC - 123 Tran Hung Dao in Hoi An and Sinh Cafe Office in Hanoi.

We were very disappointed in this 2day 1night cruise. We paid $244. USD each for a balcony room. The ship was lovely and the food was great but the drinks were very expensive, the cheapest bottle of wine was $40, the kayaking was supposed to be included and they tried to charge us $15 for hire. I had bought books and lollies for the kids at school in the floating fishing village and was told we wouldnt be going there. The people who were on our boat for an extra night got told the boat wasnt going properley and they had to re pack and change boats. The staff were rude when I expressed my disappointment at not going to the floating fishing village. Over all, you get get just as good a trip on another boat for half the price.

sorry but i find halong bay very disappointed and no idea why lonely planet rated it as top 5 picked for asia. it dosen't worth a visit...at all. i bought a day tour to halong bay from hanoi which including 2 ways trasportation (mini bus) 1 meal (seafood) 4-5 hours cruise with 2 stops at caves...well it has all it promised but the sight is not even beautiful...the boat is old and doesnt look like anything the tour agent show me...the food is simple and cold when served...i was asked to share it with 5 other people in the group...it's not buffet...imagine sharing food with no serving spoons :-(  almost 4hrs on the way there and another 4hrs on the way back in a packed mini van was torturing! anyways if youo think you have to visit halong bay to see for yourself...i recommended a more expensive tour/boat (us$50++) my was us$30 it was a nightmare!

I would recommend that all tourist boycott Halong Bay until the locals clean their mess. As some of the reviews here state, the water is so polluted it stinks. You do not want to swim there and even kayaking is nasty business as it will take you to a close encounter with all the waste that floats in the water. Plus all the cruises are over priced and do not offer value for money. All the activities are based on water (apart from the mandatory karaoke...) and you definately do not want to go near it. Halong Bay is the biggest tourist rip-off of Vietnam. It provides livelihood and big money to such a large number of people that if the amount of visitors were to drop dramatically due to a protest movement I'm quite sure they would start cleaning the bay in a hurry.

4 heures d'une route épouvantable depuis Hanoi, paysages sinistres et rien à voir durant le trajet.Arrivée sur place pour passer la journée et une nuit sur une jonque "Valentine" j'ai d'abord été saisie par l'odeur de vase et d'égouts au moment de l'embarquement et nous avons ensuite navigué sur des monceaux d'immondices.Le reste de la journée a suivi le rythme, débarquer sur une minuscule plage au milieu des déchets n'a rien d'enchanteur , se réveiller le matin au milieu des détritus pas davantage.Mon conseil: N'Y ALLEZ PAS ;

The water is green and full of rubbish. There is a sheen of oil over the bay, even far out at sea. Look forward to seeing floating nappies and pads at the beach, would never recommend swimming unless you are trying to contract cholera.

Halong Bay is listed as one of the 7 natural wonders of the modern world and we expected it to be the highlight of our 6 week trip to Vietnam and Thailand. With something like 2000 limestone rock formations and islands, we pictured ourselves sailing around a lot of them and enjoying the magnificent scenery. Were we ever disappointed.We booked a two day one night cruise with Paloma Cruises. The ship basically motored about 3 miles into Halong Bay, anchored, and never moved again until our return the next day. The only thing we saw for nearly 24 hours were the few formations where we were and no more despite them having a glass enclosed smaller boat tied up behind that could have taken us around the bay a lot more.Being January,  it was freezing cold and either raining or a strong mist in the air. They took us to a few floating buildings where those that wanted to could go kayaking by themselves and took the rest of us to an island right by the ship. After having to walk across a very narrow plank over the water and rocks, the only thing to do there was to climb to the top to a pagoda. The only trouble was there were 500 steps of various sizes and heights to do so. Most of the passengers were not in any physical condition to make the climb so we stood at the bottom for an hour with nothing to do but shiver. After leaving the pier at noon, it was now 3 pm. and that was it for the day.The next morning, they did take the passengers to a cave, by tender, but advised my wife not to go because of the steep climb and steps. After they returned, we simply motored back to the dock for the two hour bus ride back to Hanoi.To make matters even worse, the ship did not have any heat anywhere so we all wore our sweaters and jackets to the meals, slept in our clothes, and my wife became extremely sick with walking pneumonia. The crew was rude and did not do any of the activities advertised on their website nor did we go to the floating village or do the bamboo boats. At nearly $300 for two people for this trip for one night, it was a complete ripoff. Save your money and go to Phuket, Thailand. There may not be quite as many islands but they have a lot more to see and do and stay away from Paloma Cruises.

There are 2 aspects to this review as I could not figure out any other way to do this.Part 1 of my review is about the bay itself.  part 2 of my review is about the tour company as it plays an very important factor in your overall experience.We booked with Bai Tu Long company for a 2 days and 1 night tour.  The junk we were assigned was the White Dolphin (though we found out later on that there are other boats that were named White Dolphins as well").Our booking agent was VietnamTripadvisor.  Just to be clear, we used this agent simply because that's what my relative in Hanoi recommended (I am not certain but my relative could be working for this company as well).  Perhaps because of my indirect connection , I felt that I got honest service and advice from my agent.  She warned me that Bai Tu Long was not as good as the other operators and that I am visiting at peak time - end of the year.When we finally got out to the bay, our junk was just 1 of many  that packed the bay like sardines !  At first it felt a little novel ... just like being 1 of the numerous sailboats on the San Francisco bay on a clear sunny day .  But that feeling quickly wore off to be replaced of sadness and disappointment.  There seems to be no stewardship of the natural wonder .Every spot we stopped or went to, there would be souvenirs vendors.The crew was not very professional as some were sparring right in front of us.  They throw thrash overboard even when there are guests nearby.The whole trip, we had to breath in the engine exhaust fumes on the boat, even when we were in our room sleeping at night.  On the 2nd day, we saw a cockroach crawling on the wall next to our table.  Other passengers reported cockroaches in their rooms.Their itinerary listed fishing as an activity.  However, when it came time to do so,  the crew told us that you wouldn't be able to catch any fish there, only squid.  In addition, they didn't have fishing hooks, just squid hooks.  Even so, for an hour, we got banana & orange peels, cabbage leaves, etc ...Bai Tu Long gets a "C" for their transfer points coordination.  There is a lot of waiting but the worst one was on the return leg to Hanoi.  After we got off the junk, we waited for over 1 hour to be seated on the bus.  There were not enough chairs or benches for all of us waiting.  We sat on the steps in front of their office which was closed.  When we asked for an estimate of when our transportation would arrive, the coordinator kept saying another 5 minutes.  The next time we asked, it would be another 5 minutes that kept stretching to 1.5 hrs.  We understood that our bus would be coming from Hanoi, carrying  passengers for the next trip out and those buses seemed to have gotten delayed.  However, fearing delivering unfavorable news, the staff did themselves no good by masking not giving an accurate answer.  They were constantly on the phone with the drivers and all of us could not understand why they were unable to give us a decent estimate.  At times, they blamed the New Year's Eve being the culprit.  But my counterpoint is that they should have prepared for this as it is an annual occurrence.I do have to give the chef on our junk very good credit for accommodating vegetarian meal request.  We will give them an A for that effort.

Premesso che la Baia di Halong è molto bella anche se veramente troppo turistica. Questa è stata la peggiore esperienza da quando viaggio; siamo andati all'agenzia Sinh Cafè di Hanoi per prenotare un un tipo di crociera non lussuosa però ci interessava fare un buon tour della baia e in particolare volevamo  visitare Titov Island; per questo ci è stato chiesto di pagare un sovrapprezzo, specificatamente indicato anche nella ricevuta rilasciataci. La giunca PHUONG DONG 06 è veramente una carretta del mare; probabilmente similare a quella affondata lo scorso anno. Le cabine ci sono state consegnate alle 17.00 e al mattino le abbiamo dovute liberare entro le 8.00...non si capisce il perchè. Le lenzuola erano "nere", bagno con wc "mobile", lavandino intasato e odore nauseabondo. Cibo non male ma risicato. Staff  scontroso e indisponente. La cosa che fa arrabbiare è che questa giunca non fa nessun tour ma solo la spola dal porto di Haipong all'isola di Cat Ba; alla nostra richiesta di essere portati a Titov Island la guida, in principio ha fatto finta di non capire; dopo la nostra insistenza ci ha fatto salire su una piccola imbarcazione e ci ha fatto fare un giro di 10 minuti  poco lontano dalla giunca. Insomma siamo stati presi ben bene in giro; quando siamo tornati e abbiamo chiesto spiegazioni si è irrigidito e ha alzato la voce minaccinadoci di riportarci al porto, questo comportamento sinceramente si accetta male.... questo non è turismo ma solo business senza scrupoli!  Quindi il mio consiglio per visitare la Baia di Halong state attenti anche perchè durante il mio viaggio ho sentito tanti altri viaggiatori che hanno avuto esperienze pessime. L'unica nota positiva il tempo bello per il resto un incubo e vi garantisco che siamo viaggiatori con spirito di adattamento!

Not a 5 star as promise at all, I been traveling in many places and dont ever get me to fussy about ưhat I have exprience at bhaya. The small boat was dỉrty, we arrive and have briefing by the cruise mânager ưho is from philippine I couldnt understand exactly with his strong intonation english and find out it was the cruise itenerary, thanks thêre ứa printed one in the room, the lunch is ok but somehow salad was hot and the hot food was cold, The staff never speak english and find tô dificult to understand my diet cola request. The room is not what I expect, it was vẻry small, no hook to hang my clothes, broaken bathroom door, paint is faded walls and floors full of scratches, Limited hot water old bed sheet and towels.The sundeck is dirty and very dảrk at night, I order my drinks on happy our but it comes 15 minutes (beer not chilled offering me ice) Dinner was serve far from my request (I ák for medium rảre steak and it tủrn out to be well done) The staff are vẻry noisy in the rêception with 2 female and 2 boys chatting wearing undershirt. The trip  totally spoiled my ễxpectations when I saw the other boat Pâradise Cruise sit next to bhaya were I saw it beyond comparison. After our trip I checked the rates at pâradise to compare that I payed more than the other cruise offer. It was tottaly díappointing indeed! I will not recommend this cruise to anybody, please check first before you cònfirm you Halong bay trip

I read reviews on trip advisor to book this and was worst tour you could imagine.Boat they pick you up in is worst boat at harbour. Toilets blocked, nothing works and waited 2 hours in darkness for propper boat to arrive.Dirty dishes everywhere from previous tour, cockroaches climbing up walls and cabins were so bad with fumes had to sleep out on deck.Explained this to company and they didn't care. Had money so couldn't be bothered responding.  There is over 500 boats out there and this is the worst they get. What they promote isn't what you get.

We were on the same cruise, during the same days, and the previous review is terribly right and reflects exactly the sad reality.We have done the 3 days and on the "day boat" was even worse!We argued all day long to respect the program, someone from the crew stole 1 million dong to an english couple ( as well as their watch), and the boat crashed in another boat and two windows broke down...They have done a brochure of the tour, with nice pictures and a program, but it's just nice photos found on "Google" and if you are arguing, you won't do the third of the activities written on the program!When you go in such a nice place, it's really not to "fight" all day long with the crew of the boat... Whose I have to say, are terribly rude!

Our "Halong Luxury Sapphire Sail" trip began with the tour guide arriving 45 minutes late to our hotel and angrily telling us that it was our fault because we had given the wrong hotel name.  Which of course was not true.  Upon boarding the minibus we discovered that all of the seats were taken, so extra child size seats were folded out into the aisle for us - two rows apart.  We had to endure 3 hours of incredibly uncomfortable seats, separated by two rows so that we didn't even have the option to chat.When we arrived, we were herded toward the wharf, being told to 'hurry, hurry'.  Halfway down the wharf we stopped and stood for 10 minutes, all holding our luggage.  Onto the boat and the group were again told to hurry on and to leave our luggage unguarded at the wharf end of the boat, only to have a 15 minute wait with no direction apart from that we were not to move around the boat or onto the upper deck.We sat in awkward silence.Eventually we were told to enter the dining hall for lunch.  We had all been pushed to sit down so quickly that partners and friends found themselves separated and a game of musical chairs followed.  We were each served with a third of a small glass of warm juice and then a haphazard mess of dishes were placed on the table with no serving utensils.  Individually the dishes were OK, but there was not enough of any particular dish to go around so some missed out.  Of course there was plenty of rice and soy to flavor the rice with so we didn't go hungry. We were unceremoniously handed our room keys at which point we assumed that meant we were to find our rooms.  The cabins were all identical, which was rather a shame as we had paid an extra $16US for a room upgrade.  The room initially appeared to be neat and clean, but on further inspection I discovered that the bedside table drawer was full of sand and cigarette butts and underneath the bed had obviously not been attended to in quite some time.  A handle hung off our wardrobe and we had been provided with a quarter roll of sandpaper, sorry, toilet paper that had been left by a previous guest. And the bed was a block of wood with a sheet on it that sloped down toward the head so that we had to sleep on it with our heads at the foot.We were served another hodgepodge lunch and then told that we would be visiting "The Caves".  We were herded through the beautiful sandstone caves in record time with our guide walking behind and demanding that we hurry.  It was quite a climb up and down hundreds of stairs and I felt quite sorry for the older guests who should really have been able to take the climb at their own pace.  There was a snack bar at the end of the walk over which our guide stood guard, making sure that we didn't buy anything that wasn't from the boat.We moved along and were told that we had the option of Kayaking or swimming.  As we were on a three day tour we elected to swim and watched the other guests go off Kayaking.  I decided to take the opportunity while 'The Boss' (as I came to call him) had his back turned to make some purchases from one of the boats full of snacks and drinks that had moored next to our junk. I tried to leave the boat and discovered that they had let the Kayaking people off and then put the ladder back up so that we had to jump onto another boat and then climb down a tyre to reach the dock. My girlfriend bruised her leg quite badly attempting to climb back up. 7000 dong for a can of coke compared to 40,000 dong on the boat!  No wonder they didn't want us getting off! On that subject, beer was 40,000 dong (compared to 10,000 anywhere else).  And of course no water was provided and only small bottles were available.  At EIGHT times the normal price!Twenty minutes later, still having not been told when or where we would be swimming, the boat simply left the dock.  Luckily I was in my room or I would have been left behind, as they had not bothered with a head count.  We moved on to the spot where we would spend the night.  With no swimming :(Dinner was another haphazard mixture of not quite enough of everything, a weird mix of western dishes done badly and Vietnamese dishes done badly.  After dinner we were left to our own devices and fairly soon realised the the promised Karaoke was another laugh on us.  Also, the top deck only had nine individual lounges for 30 people, and we were not allowed into the dining room when we were not being served a meal. So many of the guests simply had to sit on the deck.In the morning we set sail and got to enjoy the only good thing about the trip - the natural beauty of Halong Bay.My girlfriend felt quite ill in the morning so we decided to skip breakfast.  'The Boss' was not impressed.  He repeatedly rang our doorbell and banged on our door every 5 or so minutes from 7 to 7:30, refusing to accept that we weren't interested in breakfast.  When I finally gave in and opened the door it was to tell him that we did not want to continue with the trip. Passengers were transferred to a 'Day Boat' while the main boat returned to Halong City to drop off one night passengers and pick up more hapless victims, so we at least had a way out. Before he would let us off the boat he made me sign a hand written note stating that we were leaving the tour without any complaint. He became quite angry when I asked to be returned to Hanoi on the tour bus but eventually gave in.  Other guests were dropped at their hotel doors.  We were dropped two blocks away in the middle of a busy road.Altogether a terrible waste of $108 US per person for an awful two days.  This is NOT a luxury trip.  I have been consistently impressed by the quality of service and the quality and freshness of food in Vietnam.The street vendors can get it right, why can't they?

We booked at 2 day 1 night trip to Halong bay through our hotel,on their recommendation.  It cost $160 for two people, supposedly a luxury tour ( although we later dicovered that some people had paid as little as $50 per person for the same trip )  The tour guide collected us from the hotel and collected everyones passports in order to get the tickets etc.. something that were very reluctant to do. He told us he would return the passport when we handed the room key in the following morning.  When we did this my passport was MISSING!  They hadn't a clue where it was.  We tried to phone the police and the captain of the boat would not speak to them.  It was a real battle because the supposedly 'english speaking tour guide' had very poor english and couldn't understand us.  We finally got a police report when we got onto land.  It took us 3 days for me to get an emergency passport - as it was the weekend.  I then had to wait 3 days for a new Vietnam visa to leave the country.  The company offered us no money back  for the trip and we had to fight to get the money back for the passport and the visa.  We are very disappointed with standard of service ( as many other customers were as well) and the way we were treated - a complete lack of empathy and compassion, we would not recommend this boat to anyone.  Please be careful when booking a tour to Halong bay and maybe try and give a copy of your passport instead.  We have learnt a very harsh lesson and it has been a very unpleasant experience.

Our family of 6 booked a tour at the Khah Sinh Tours at N2 Booking office for 85$US a night (It's just opposite Paparoti in Hoan Kiem District). It was a deluxe junk tour. We were supposedly given the "best" rate because we had quite a large number (or so she said). We later found out that an Australian couple had only paid $75US. However, we looked past that as we have heard it was not unusually for them to use these kind of pricing. What was worse was that the tour guide, his name was Black Jack, was rude, impatient and  not honest. We initially booked 3 double rooms but he gave us 2 triple rooms instead. Which would have been a different price. Only after my father demanded for all our 3 rooms as book did he give it to us. My father later found out that it was because he wanted one room for himself. If that wasnt bad enough, he rushed us from one place to another. Hardly giving us enough time to even explore the beauties of the caves at halong bay. He was also rude to my father, making uncalled for remarks.The only saving grace for this holiday was that the junk - elizabeth junk, was quite decent. The toilets in our room and the bedroom was clean. The food was edible but left much to be desired. When they say they are going to give you a seafood meal, by seafood they mean fish and boiled prawns and maybe squid. If you are expecting crab and big lobsters-think again. Food on a whole edible but bland. Really could have been much better. If that wasnt all, the worse part of this whole trip was when we got home. We received the credit card bill and found out that we were charged 500,000 vnd more for our tour. We are really very very upset and can't believe that the company has resorted to these kind of means to increase their profits.My family and i will never go back to this tour agency and warn all others against them.

We did a Halong Bay cruise on a 3 star junk boat "Dream Voyage" in Superior category. The boat was in extremely bad condition, the decks and the deck chairs were partly broken, walking on the boat was dangerous caused by the holes in broken wood floor panelling, rusty nails and loose fencing. Life boats or other safety measures were completely unsufficient. The food was very simple (superior?) and the service ranged from unfriendly to nonexsisting. This boat and many similar ones are inacceptable! The Halong Bay is beautiful on a sunny day. Unfortunately there are frequently foggy, misty and rainy conditions despite the warm temperatures. The waters are poluted and not inviting for swimming or diving. Do only consider to go on a so called VIP category cruise (prices from USD 85-140 p.P.) for one overnight!

1. Giá đi 440 (290 - vé tham quan + 150 - thuyền) khá đắt nhưng nhận lại là rác, mùi, nóng và chen chúc, công nghiệp. Mặc dù tôi đi không phải ngày lễ mà chỉ là t7, cn thường. 2. Thuyền quá đông - toàn thuyền kém chất lượng, xả khó đen như công nông. => Cần phải kiểm soát lượng thuyền và khách tham quan, cần nâng cấp tu sửa. Đừng chỉ biết thu tiền vậy.
Sau vụ thảm họa, Quảng Ninh phải có biện pháp chấn chỉnh về an toàn du lịch. Muốn khách quay lại cần bảo đảm an toàn cho khách chứ ko phải chỉ kêu gọi, trấn an.
Tôi đi chuyến tàu 5 tiếng đồng hồ. Phải nói là tệ, dịch vụ rất tệ. Chính quyền cần vào cuộc với những giá dịch vụ ở Hạ Long

Bọn lừa đảo ăn rơ với nhau.. các bạn muốn đi vịnh hạ long đầu tiên các bạn vào quầy mua vé đi thuyền tiêng 150k xong các bạn lại quầy bán vé đi vịnh giá 165k.. chứ các bạn lại thấy nó bán cả vé đi thuyền với các điểm giá 440k và 490k đừng mua tụi nó ăn chia với nhau mình mua riêng mình tiết kiệm được gần 150k lận mà ngồi chờ tụi nó lâu lắc à....cảnh ở đây thì đẹp chứ dân ở đây làm du lịch chặt chém sợ quá
Khách phục vụ không tốt nói xấu khách khách không  phục vụ ko chuyên ngiêp tàu  bản số 2668 mọi người chời thì né nó ra ko lại tiền mất tật mạng
