Tàu 2668 phục vụ tàu không tốt nhân viên ăn nói kiểu khinh thường khách hàng
Cảnh là đẹp nhưng anh tôi chưa get wife nên đánh giá 1 sao nhé
Mình thật tự hào khi là người Hải Phòng ! Nhờ có nơi đây , Việt Nam mình có thể trở thành một nước giàu mạnh 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻😍😍😍😍😍🇻🇳🇻🇳 …
Cá trên tàu rất ngon , nhân viên thuyền thúng quá tệ
Không thấy có gì đẹp. Đúng là đi 1 lần cho biết.
Dơ bẩn, chặt chém khàch du lịch
Cái gì cũng quá đắt .ko bao giờ quay lại
Tuyệt vời
Lấn chiếm phá hoại cảnh quan
Giá cả đắt đỏ
Thật tuyệt vời
Bố chưa đi:))

As many others say, I deeply regret going here and being part of the problem. Such a beautiful place that’s full of bulk rubbish, pollution and waste in the water. Kayaking through trash definitely wasn’t a highlight of the trip nor was getting the waste water on us. So disappointing and heartbreaking to see this beautiful bay full of trash. They try and blame the fisherman but a lot of the trash in the water was from the tourist boats, water bottles, gloves, plastic bags, so much trash, waste water. We even went squid fishing and it was actually just disgusting, even worse to think that people eat the food out of this water. Vietnam you need to save this beautiful area.

I terribly regret, like others, having participated in this.  What could be the impact of hundreds of ships that drop anchor every day in the middle of the seabed of Ha Long Bay which was recognized twice by UNESCO as a World Natural Heritage site !  Mass tourism should be more regulated. An exceptional site which is crisscrossed every day by hundreds of hotel-disco boats, carrying thousands of people on organized trips.  Absolutely avoid if you like places that aren't too busy and still authentic.  Such a site deserves to be preserved from mass tourism. In addition, we must consider the fact that few people like to meet in hundreds or thousands in remarkable places.

The 1-day Cruise Experience:  There are loads of tour agencies in the city centre of Hanoi selling similar tour packages, different prices but the tours are actually run only by a few companies. You could probably negotiate for a more wallet-friendly price because the tour agencies are only acting as aggregators and middlemen.  Once you’re at Halong Bay, you will soon realise it’s not as beautiful as the pictures seem. The water is littered with plastic waste and trash, and the views are spoiled by the infinite hoards of cruises and boats. Beaches are crammed full of tourists and locals trying to hardsell overpriced food and drinks.

Wir waren drei Tage in der Ha Long Bay unterwegs und waren leider mehr als schockiert. Das komplette Gebiet ist dermaßen zugemüllt, dass man weder die eigentlich schöne Umgebung genießen kann, noch ins Wasser gehen möchte. Neben dem Müll wird von den Schiffen alles Mögliche ins Wasser geleitet, sodass man teilweise eine braune Suppe vorfindet und zwischen dem ganzen Müll auch noch Ölteppiche. Wirklich sehr sehr schlimm für die Umwelt.. dass es dort viele Boote gibt wussten wir, und hat uns während dem Trip auch nicht wirklich gestört. An einigen Stellen schwimmt zwar kein Müll aber die Wasserqualität und die Luft dort sind durch die ganzen Booten sehr schlecht.  Insgesamt hätten wir im Nachhinein keinen Cruise mehr gebucht und würden es auch niemanden empfehlen. Es ist eine Schande dass hier nichts unternommen wird.
強烈建議不要住過夜船，看起來很美好，但晚上11點附近其他的過夜船有的還在開party音樂開超大聲，然後還有其他船開著引擎然後也沒有在移動，整晚都沒辦法睡，別浪費錢了
See plastic in the wild for yourself!  It’s such a pleasure to canoe through rubbish whilst looking at the islands.  The polystyrene, breaking up in front of your eyes, is perhaps the most beautiful.

This was supposed to be one of the highlights of our tour of Vietnam. Unfortunately i have to rank this at the very bottom. Absolutely disgusting water with dead fish, floating polistiren everywhere. Kayaking between rubbish to land on a dirty beach where you clearly will not want to put one foot in the water. Also full of jellyfish.. but the 'guide' did not tell us, pretty dangerous especially with kids around. People, please skip this place until it gets sorted out.

Ganz schrecklicher Touristenort. Die schmeißen den Müll einfach vom Schiff runter und die Höhlen die man besuchen kann, da werden die Fledermäuse vertrieben, damit die Touristen ungestört durch die Höhlen laufen können. Macht keine Schifftour. Das Wasser ist dreckig und vermüllt. Das Wasser schmierig von den Schiffsbenzinen. UNESCO-WELTNATURERBE sollte es nicht sein, oder die Schiffe verboten werden.

So disappointing and heartbreaking to see this beautiful bay full of rubbish, polluted and crowded with tourist. It’s beautiful place don’t get me wrong but what is happening there with amount of plastic bags in the water is shocking! Also do not swim there. We went for a swim and our body were covered in brown oily dirt (I have no idea what it was exactly but felt horrible) Vietnam you need to act immediately to protect that Bay!

It’s such a shame that the Viet government allows the mistreatment of this amazing place by their local people: garbage, plastics,  old boats polluting with oil the whole coast. It’s horrible, sad, unintelligible. It’s not, at all, what you expect from movies scenes. I took drone pic from our cruise, take a close look. And the garbage on the shores is constant Shame on UNESCO for not taking care of those places

It’s sad to see so much rubbish! It really ruins the whole experience, especially when you pay to come here for swimming, canoeing and nature experience. If local government, rangers and companies don’t organise recycling and waste management soon, I guess tourists will stop coming here. As it is now, it’s not sustainable. If you’re a tourist around here, please consider how you can minimize your footprint, and also help to inspire local people in doing this! There’s no planet B. 🌏 💙

Halong bay cruise or speed/fast boat cruise? 😂 😂 😂 I booked luxury cruise for 2N3D. Most activities do by fast/speed boad. From the registration port / main land, they will drop you to main ship (I called it ship hotel) that already Park at the same spot every day in halong bay (each ship company that called luxury ha long bay like Orchids classic, Genesis, etc, has already their own park spot there). So you will see them on the same spot every morning.  After check in at the ship hotel, they will take you to the first activity with speed boat then back again to the ship hotel for dinner and sleep. Normally they will depart at the night and back again at the same park spot at the morning ( I guest they only run forward for couple of meters then backward to the same spot) and they do the same things/itinerary for next guest each day. For the 2 nights itinerary they will take you to other destinations near park like visiting village, beach, etc and once again with speed boad. These activities will be repeated again every day. So you do not actually doing cruise. Just doing speed boad cruise and sleep at the ship hotel in the middle of sea. 😂 😂 😂 😂 😂
Cruising approx 2km's all day in the rubbish tip then anchoring 50 metres from boats in all direction listening to dreadful karaoke and following the armada home the next morning - priceless!

If you love mass tourism and unfriendly people, you should go to Ha Long Bay. For us as a family it was one of the worst places we have seen so far.  People give you the feeling of just wanting your money. They tried to scam us 2-3 times within the taxi.  The worst experience was a taxi driver touching my wife when she was on a short ride on her own.  People here have no respect for other people and they don't care about destroying the beautiful nature of the bay.  When we came here with our 2 children, it was NOT main season, but even then there were hundreds of boats polluting the water with there engines. Water didn't seem to be clean in the bay, thanks to tourism.  I will never go here again and would definitely recommend going to Cat Ba Island instead, which is an unbelievable beautiful place compared to Ha Long.

Absolutamente masificado y recargado. Todas las rutas son idénticas y todos los barcos ofrecen lo mismo. Precios desorbitados y borreguismo a tope. Algo tan magnífico de ver debería estar más controlado pero desde el momento en que llegas al puerto de embarque te convierten en un borrego. Masificación, empujones, gritos entre unos guías y otros y servicios a precios del caribe. No es en absoluto lo que era hace unos años. Recomiendo mucho más visitar la isla Cat Ba y desde allí contratar a un pescador en barco cubierto y hacer una ruta absolutamente fantástica, más tranquila y configurable que no te encierra en un barco durante varios días y no te obliga a consumir productos básicos a precio de oro

Halong Bay One of the most stunning attractions in Vietnam - there is no way to describe Halong Bay other than absolutely breathtaking. Ha Long Bay is located on the Gulf of Tonkin, within Quang Ninh Province, northeast of Vietnam, 165 kilometers from the capital city of Ha Noi. Covering an area of ​​1553 square kilometers and including more than 1600 islands and islets, most of which are uninhabited,…. As a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a natural wonder of the world, the bay has thousands of limestone islands and cliffs rising from the turquoise sea.

Avoid. Tam coc is way more impressive! (The ha Long bay on land). It's dirty in the bay, mass tourism place. Dirt and oil everywhere and I felt like a sardin in a can on the ship. Felt tricked! Vietnam should protect this place more, it's being destroyed by overtourism.
Sehr schöne Ausblicke und Aussichten. Allerdings ist das Wasser sehr dreckig und sehr viel Müll schwimmt herum. Wäre schön wenn es mal gereinigt wird.
- It’s no more one of 7 world heritages. As a local and an environmentalist - this is heart breaking to me. May the karma pay well - Plz maintain it better - what a waste of money

Eine wunderschöne einmalige Landschaft, die wir mit unserem Massentourismus leider zerstören. Müll wohin man blickt. Öl im Wasser und ein unglaublicher Smog von mehr als 200 zum teil sehr alten Schiffen. Ich bereue es, ein Teil dieser Tourismusmassen zu sein und werde versuchen umzudenken.

I visited 4 years ago on an organised tour. The water is extremely heavily polluted by the discharge from all the tourist boats. Although it is beautiful, and the Vietnamese people are really nice, I would not go again until they develop a system of collecting all the sewage waste from the boats and getting rid of it on land.

Ort ist zugeklatscht mit Hotels und im Dezember eine uncharmante Geisterstadt. Die Inseln und Höhlen sind ganz schön aber auf gar keinen Fall die Reise wert. Halong bay hätte ich lieber ausgelassen, überrannt mir Touristen und das in der Off-Season. Dann lieber Catba aber selbst das ist nicht besonders

Tourism on an industrial scale I have not seen before. Ha Long Bay is a tourist trap with noisy boats, irritating tour guides and dumb music everywhere. If you look for authenticity, skip this place. The port where the boats leave can compete with a small airport. The water is polluted, tourists are entertained with karaoke and the water is so crowded with boats that making a good picture is impossible. The place lost all its charm and beauty with this mass tourism invading the place. Skip it, it is a waste of time. I regret going as I have been part of it now.

I would not recommend if you like nature. The area you board your boat is a depressing sight, overcrowded with many unfinished hotels. Once on your boat you'll notice how polluted the water is. There are at least 10 huge boats surrounding you, pumping out smoke and pollution. At night the party boats dominate the quiet and at no point on our two night trip could we hear birds, any type of wildlife or even the water against the boat. It could be so wonderful but we left feeling deflated by the reality of what this heritage sight has become. Do yourself a favour and just enjoy the online pictures of what it looked like 20 years ago.

Un désastre. J’ai eu le malheur de décider de venir 2 jours à Halong, quelle erreur. Premièrement, la ville de halong, tout simplement horrible: une ville artificielle fantôme bâtit pour le tourisme de masse. J’ai personnellement résidé dans la partie où vivent les locaux, ce qui était un moindre mal. En revanche dans ce quartier les menus des restaurants ne sont pas traduits et le personnel ne parle pas anglais: la communication est parfois difficile. Pour y avoir été début novembre, la ville semble déserte assez peu de touristes, les rues sont vides. Pour ce qui est de la baie d’halong, j’ai fait une journée de croisière réservé auprès de la réception de notre hôtel, environ 30€/personne pour la journée nourriture incluse. Quelle déception. L’eau de la baie est sale, beaucoup de déchets dans l’eau. Le peu d’endroits où la croisière s’arrête est blindé de touristes, aucune authenticité, des dizaines de bateaux de croisières entassés. Bref, je ne recommande pas du tout cet endroit, ayant fait ce genre de croisières aux Philippines ou en Thaïlande: je vous recommande de vous tourner vers ces endroits bien mieux pour y faire des tours d’îles en bateau.
Dirty. UNESCO and Viet government should be ashamed. Flotsam and jetsam floating in lines, sewerage waste, beaches covered in assorted plastics and films of oil here and there. Shame. Shame. Shame.

If overtourism and cruises with karaoke and huge buffets are for you then this is a great place to go.  If you care about natural beauty, maybe you're looking for something similar to a Norwegian fjord or Milford sounds in New Zealand.. my advice is to run as far away from this place as you can. Don't even be tempted by the FOMO. It's truly awful. Styrofoam, trash, and cigarette butts in the water. In two days we spent just two and a half hours off the boat. 45 minutes kayaking with the entire population of the bay (29 cruise boats I counted), an hour on the island where you wait in line to hike up, and an hour in a cave that isn't that great.  Truly the epitome of overtourism - it's a disgrace that this problem isn't emphasised as much as it should be in the articles and travel guides. I'm embarrassed to have been a part of it and shocked that it still recieves mostly five star reviews.

Such a shame that this beautiful place is being damaged by greedy companies. It is full of litter and big ugly cruise ships. I do not understand why I need to book a cruise and be surrounded by people getting drunk and playing loud music. Activities like kayaking and cooking demonstrations are just tourist traps. The overnight cruises are a rip off, the scenery looks the same. Why can I not just go on a speedboat around the Bay, enjoy the scenery, take some pictures, swim a bit and go home? They say go to lan ha bay, it's better. It is not. It is the same.

This should be an incredible experience. The scenery is truly stunning, but not unique to the area as it is similar to parts of the Philippines, Thailand and China. Unfortunately the actual experience of going there was not good. The area is being destroyed by pollution, rubbish and unsustainable tourism. UNESCO really need to get involved here. The area claims to have banned single use plastic, absolute rubbish. Don't be fooled. Our tour company couldn't give us enough water bottles! Despite it being a beautiful location, if I knew before going what I know now, I wouldn't go. I hope it changes before it's too late (possibly already too late).

A baía está muito suja. O tempo todo você vê plásticos e todo tipo de poluição nas águas e nas ilhas. Uma tristeza. A exploração turística desmedida, sem cuidados, está acabando com o local. Uma pena.
The islands are beautiful but there are so many rubbish floating on the sea. And the water smells bad. It was so sad to see the tourism have been destroying nature.

Podría ser un sitio increíble pero es un vertedero. La gente local es muy sucia y no cuidan nada. Solo quieren aprovecharse del turista y sacarle el dinero por todos lados. Una pena la cantidad de plástico y suciedad que hay. Vergüenza.

Recently visited in May ‘23. So much litter especially styrofoam cubes everywhere. The tour groups for the caves use styrofoam to keep their platforms elevated so unfortunately, little bits and pieces fall off from the bigger styrofoam and the bay is littered with pieces of styrofoam. For all the money that they get from the tour groups, I hope they switch from styrofoam to something sustainable. Also oil slick as well so would advise not to swim in the bay even though we saw others doing it. For all the tours that go on here, it doesn’t seem like there’s care to take care of the bay.  I visited HLB in 2016 and remember it being beautiful and I was in awe when I visited. I don’t remember it being this bad. Made me very sad to see all the litter and poor upkeep of the bay.
Being in Vietnam was my worst experience ever. You are harassed all the  time. No stop. Either you stay at your hotel or you have to say no every 2 seconds.
I think it's overestimated... it wasn't attractive enough

Un sitio espectacular al que hay que ir cien por cien si vas a Vietnam.  Pero no puedo evitar ponerle una estrella, el agua está asquerosa, llena de basura flotando... Es una verdadera pena ver una maravilla natural, patrimonio de la humanidad, de esta manera.  No entiendo cómo no se están tomando medidas para preservar el entorno.
上船後會有人瘋狂推銷，然後船上任何東西都要再自費，下船後還會在跟你要小費；原本說好的全包9人座車，到當天竟然有陌生人跟我們合車，老闆竟然推說你們包不了9人座的，這跟當初說的只有我們5人使用很不一致，大家來這裡消費凡事問清楚，才不會花了冤枉錢。

Wow. It's an amazing place but there are way to many boats and tourists that it ruined my experience.. I cant say I recommend this as it's over the top now. Hertiage listed yes but then let's ruin it by added 100000 boats !! I mean come on ... There should be a limit!  I will never go back!!! It's only getting worse.
Pemain kok ngulur waktu bot bgt sih
Merveille envahie par des milliers de cageots plastique flottants. Pollution atmosphérique importante et multitude de bateaux touristiques en prime. A déclasser d'urgence.

Industrialised tourism. The group tours are horrible and remove all magic from the experience. You are bussed out in a fleet of ships all going to the same cove with shared equipment that have strict time slots. We had the kayak slot at 6:45 am, but the tour doesn't admit why you are getting up so early. After being ushered around the cave with a flag with hundreds of other groups, you go to a small beach where the water is badly polluted and occupied with massive jellyfish that have got through the net and, if unlucky like my girlfriend, will sting and leave you in agony for the rest of the night.

The Norths paradise of rubbish.  Sea is filled with plastic, polystyrene and loads of other garbage.  It is a sad reflection on Vietnam and its attitude to plastic.  Unfortunately only a boycott by foreign travellers would force the greed laden people who run the show here into action.  What a shame that humans have tainted a natural miracle of a location.
Extraordinary natural beauty turned into a total tourist trap; BEWARE of SCAM cruise deals, ONLY booked with trusted intermediaries !

A ÉVITER !!! Pollution ( air, déchet à la surface de l’eau …) ,touriste en masse il y a des centaines de gros bateaux à la ronde Il n’y a plus aucun charme malheureusement la ville est horrible elle est en construction une sorte de Dubai complètement raté Le prix de n’importe quoi et 3 fois plus cher que dans tous le Vietnam et de plus si vous faites un trip (hors HALONG à HANOÏ) il est difficile de partir pour une autres destination Et je vous parle pas des sorties bateaux pour visiter la baie …  Très très déçus

We were looking for halong bay cruises in Hanoi, and settled on Indochina Junk due to their good reviews. For the transfer back to Hanoi, they refuse to drop us off at the airport (10 min drive away) and insist on dropping us off in the old quarters. They tell us that if we want to be dropped off at the airport, we'd have to pay for a private transfer which costs 100USD! That's highway robbery, and because of that, we have to rearrange our entire itinerary. For people on a tight schedule, beware of this company! I do not recommend them.

I have just returned from Ha Long Bay , what a disgrace of a world heritage site the water is so polluted with rubbish of every discription , on entry to the cave you can bye all sorts of junk even a transformer action figure, grey water runs freely into the bay , l went kayaking into a cave where once through you are encouraged to feed monkeys peanuts and banana's , the swimming beach was disgusting .how disappointed l was
Very overrated, the water isn't clean and clear as advertised and it is very much a tourist trap.
super trouristy, dirty water, everything is expensive and the only little beach there is is so crowded you can’t even get in (off season), very overrated - not worth going at all
Halong Bay is covered in plastic, styrofoam and other waste. Water very dirty. Some locations are horrifying. Quite polluted
Non visitatela, troppa immondizia e plastica in mare.

Fantastic landscapes. Cruises – a nightmare tourist trap! Never again! Hundreds of noisy boats next to each other, thousands of people pushing and shoving. A tourist's worst nightmare. Halong Bay probably belongs on the top list of all visitors to Vietnam, which is now unfortunately shown by ‘overtourism’. Yeah, and I'm one of too many. The bay, often depicted in the pictures as a tranquil and picturesque seascape, is not only completely overcrowded, but unfortunately also very polluted and severely affected by the emerging economic performance in the region. On arrival, you look out over high-rises of the nearby town, between the picturesque rocks anchor tankers and drilling platforms, and hundreds of excursion boats tour from one bay to the other. At the popular stalactite cave, you stand in line for 2 hours, accompanied by diesel smoke and the scent of wastewater wafting over the beach. I had booked a round trip with several stops, but did not leave the excursion steamer after the second stop, luckily there was enough cool beer on board to drink the whole thing nicely. Once and never again!

The bay is a beautiful busy hectic crowded tourist area.  Every inch of the bay is crowded with people and boats. Hard to really enjoy all the natural beauty from the many amazing cruise boats. Disappointing and wouldn’t recommend this area!
Mass tourism. I didn't expect much, but once in Vietnam, it's hard to leave without seeing Halong Bay. A day trip is enough to discover this unique natural wonder and proudly say: "I was there."

We were on a conducted group tour of Vietnam and Cambodia, organised by the Australian tour company, Holiday Max.  As part of the itinerary, a "Ha Long Bay cruise" was included.  I had heard so much about Ha Long bay, and I was really looking forward to it, but the experience was a huge disappointment.  Let me explain:  We left our hotel in Hanoi, after breakfast, and drove to Ha Long Bay, reaching around early afternoon. Our group of 25 then transferred to the  "cruise ship", by a motor boat.  The "cruise ship" was anchored in the bay.   We made two excursions from the "ship", one the same day, after lunch, to see some rock formations, and one the next day, after breakfast to see a cave.  So, we were on the "cruise ship" for less than 24 hours, and I don't recall the "ship" moving during that period.  As the "Ha Long Bay Cruise" was part of our itinerary, we just accepted it, but if I was planning an independent tour of Vietnam, I would not do it this way.  In other words, if it is part of a conducted tour, as it was in our case....you do not have a choice.  BUT.....if you are going independently to Vietnam, my advice is DO NOT GO ON A HA LONG BAY CRUISE!  There are many wonderful things to see, in and around Ha Long Bay, but you do not need to get on to a "cruise ship" to see these sights.  It is MUCH better for you to stay in a decent hotel in a nearby town, and get the hotel to organise a guide, with a small motorboat, to take you around the bay to see the karst formations, and various other sights like the caves, rock formations, etc.... There is plenty to see in and around Ha Long Bay, but getting on board a "cruise ship" is NOT the best way to see it.   To recap:  it is NOT NECESSARY to actually sleep for one night (or two nights) in a rather cramped cabin on board a "cruise ship" and eat pretty ordinary food, to see the sights in and around Ha Long bay.  Much better to stay in a hotel, and go around Ha Long bay on your own!   In short, the damn "cruise" was a huge disappointment.  I intend going to Vietnam again, and I intend staying in a hotel, and see the sights in and around Ha Long bay, on my own.

Splashed out and went on a luxury 2 night trip, after being told and also read how beautiful Halong bay is. The boat and staff were lovely and I can’t fault that- but Halong bay itself is ruined by the about of pollution in the sea. ( wood, plastics, rope etc is floating around everywhere! The people of Halong / government need to get on top of this issue quick - before it’s too late.

The bay is well known for its many limestone islands and the emerald green water and rainforest on each island, a very nice place BUT... very careful by the locals, a lot of trash left by the many ships and cruise ships that visit the place, plus trash accumulated on the islands, which is observed with the naked eye or with a kayak ride. A wonder soon to be lost to continue like this.
Beautiful landscape, but it is a shame the poor exploitation of tourism what they do.They don't care for the Mood. Lots of trash floating in the water.

I thought this was going to be one of the highlights of my trip and instead was my biggest disappointment.  I envisioned sailing around the beautiful bay looking at the beautiful limestone formations.  Instead what I got was a 30 minute tender to my ship, whereby the ship moved for about 30 minutes and then dropped anchor until the following morning at which point we did have a nice visit on rowboats to the caves (the highlight of the 2 days).  However, once we returned to the ship, we then had another 30 minute tender back to shore.  If I had known then what I know now, at most I would have booked a day trip out.  The overnight portion of the trip was a total waste of money.The bay itself was very crowded with a LOT of other ships doing the exact same thing.  The water was quite polluted.  And there weren’t actually many interesting things to do on the ship itself. Hardly anyone showed up for the fishing activity on the ship where you simply dropped a pole into the water (and I never got the sense that we were going to catch any squid) and no one showed up for karaoke except for us.  The activities on the ship were basically a total bust for me.  In terms of the food, although we had a really nice lunch on the day we arrived, the rest of the meals were really subpar compared to all  the other meals we had in Vietnam.Overall, a huge disappointment.  If you feel that you must “tick it off your list”, I would recommend you do a day trip.

It's full of garbage. It's floating everywhere in the water, it sits in massive clags in the harbour,  it's caught on the cliffs and is washed up on all the beaches. It's pushed down the hillsides underneath the little businesses that are set up on some of the islands. It was really shocking to see this level of disregard for the environment. The landscape is all very nice, but we have been to other places in the world that are its equal. Where Halong bay is exceptional is in its pollution. I suppose if one was able to look around and manage to not notice what's floating in the water, then maybe it would be all very nice. We were told its all due to Typhoon Yagi, but that was 8 months ago. Greasy opaque green/brown water topped with polystyrene of all shapes and sizes. We had the opportunity to go kayaking, but didn't want to get any closer to the water.The thing that, to me, makes Halong Bay especially worth skipping is the inconvenience of access - it's a two hour drive from Hanoi, so it's not just a casual activity for an afternoon.In fact we did a one night trip in Lan Ha Bay, which is around the corner from Halong but considered "less touristy" and "cleaner". The mind boggles.

This is my experience after doing a 3 day/2 night cruise in Lan Ha Bay, marketed as a Ha Long Bay cruise. I should note that the itinerary for my cruise is similar to most others that go to Halong Bay and Lan Ha Bay, so it is not my cruise specifically, it applies to all cruises that I considered.  If I knew then, what I know now, I would not have booked this type of cruise. This is how the cruises in Halong Bay and Lan Ha Bay work...Day 1- 1 and 2 night passengers transfer by tender boat to the cruise ship. It takes an hour and is NOT comfortable.  This is point A.- Lunch on the cruise ship while it sails to point B.  Cruising time about 1 hour.- Activity for an hour or so, such as Bright and Dark cave by local row boat.- The cruise ship does not cruise anywhere - it stays at point B overnight. Day 2- The 2 night passengers that arrived the previous day leave the cruise ship for activities that last 8 hours.  They don't return until around 5pm.- The 1 night passengers that arrived the previous day have some short activity around the cruise ship for about an hour.- The cruise ship leaves point B and sails back to point A.  (Cruising time about 1 hour.) The 1 night passengers then depart by tender boat back to Halong Bay.- The cruise ship stays at point A, waiting for the new arrival of the next group of 1 and 2 night passengers, to repeat the process.- The cruise ship sails to point B while the new arrivals have lunch. Day 3- The 2 night passengers that arrived on Day 1 have some short activity around the cruise ship for about an hour.- The cruise ship leaves point B and sails back to point A.  The 2 night passengers from Day 1 and the previous day's 1 night passengers then depart by tender boat back to Halong Bay.- The cruise ship stays at point A, waiting for the new arrival of the next group of 1 and 2 night passengers, to repeat the process. As you can see, the total amount of time actually cruising around Lan Ha Bay on the cruise ship is a total of around 2 hours for both 1 night and 2 night itineraries.  That was not my expectation when I booked the 2 night cruise and I am very disappointed, bordering on angry.  To put it in perspective, you spend a similar amount of time cruising on the uncomfortable tender boat.   The views in Lan Ha Bay are lovely, but they are not the iconic views of Halong Bay, which was another disappointing aspect of my cruise. For people who are considering a Halong / Lan Ha Bay cruise, I hope this information helps them to make an informed decision.  I wish I knew it before I booked my cruise, so I could make a better choice for me and my expectations.

It's a beautiful place spoilt by industrial scale tourism.500 boats a day according to our guide, and all seemingly settling off at the same time on the same schedule.I would find somewhere quieter to enjoy

I made the one night cruise in Halong Bay because it was part of a circuit in Vietnam (I am not a fan of cruises), but it would be enough a boat ride, of a few hours, without staying overnight.The landscape is beautiful, especially at twilight and dawn.The cruise consists of leaving the harbor and then anchoring in a zone where all the other ships are. And that's where you stay until the start, late in the morning, the next day. There are activities, but I only went to visit a beach, which is the only one in the area, and where hundreds of visitors are "expelled". That is, confusion, queues to climb the mountain, everything but a beautiful and wonderful beach, which would be without mass tourism. From the beach, you have the sight of hundreds of people taking pictures and a corropium of boats from one side to the other transporting tourists.I do not recommend. It was a waste of time.

But what a disappointment!! I don’t even know where to start... the city of halong is a remake of walking dead, a ghost town with many empty, unfinished buildings. No soul. Abusive prices on food. Locals not really friendly with tourists... (which is certainly understandable when you see the damage)Bay is polluted++++ The landscape could be fabulous because the natural beauty of the site is undeniable but then it is totally ruined! The site, of unquestionable beauty and which could be a real protected heritage, is instead ruined by pollution and the uncontrolled influx of tourists and cruise ships The sea has a brown green color, there are scattered rubbish, ships discharge into the water.A real shame, we had high expectations and we regret that the environment is so sadly cared for

What a disappointment that this 10-day trip to North Vietnam. On pictures halong bay is very beautiful but in real life is crowded with boats. And all of the plastic and garbage in the waters is so sad to see.

It wasn’t worth spending the money on an overnight cruise, in hindsight we should have just done a day trip. The ship didn’t seem to go any distance at all from the harbour, certainly not enough to warrant an over night stay. The excursions we did allowed very little time at each place, the last one kayaking, we only had 25 minutes which was a real shame. I think we had about an hour at Ti Top island to either climb to the top for a viewpoint or spend time on an incredibly overcrowded, very small beach. The amount of litter and grime in the sea shows you the sad reality of so many boats jostling for space in this beautiful bay.

Halong Bay's scenery is undeniably stunning, but as a couple, we found it overrated. The crowds were overwhelming, and there was a lot of trash in the water, which took away from the experience. It's worth seeing, but manage your expectations.

It was SO crowded.  We counted 43 boats in the area and looking at the water, it wasn't hard to figure out where the waste went.  Floating rubbish as well as who knows what under the water.  The beach area was really crowded and no-one was game to have a swim in the muck.  The cave was an arduous trek and not for the older folks or those with any ailments.  A little disappointing overall.

Good:- Views- day trip on day 2 as it was much quieter and peaceful- relaxing on the boat in the eveningBad:- crammed in like sardines to a tiny spot on the bay - schedule is so tight that you barely get time to enjoy each activity and are treated like children by boat staff to ensure you stick to their schedule down to the last second- The litter in the bay is absolutely disgraceful and is a reflection of the tourism in the bay - it’s all about the money and how many tours they can cram in in as little time as possible- The announcements that come through a tanoi in your bedroom is obseen, patronising and repetitive as you’ve already been told the schedule about 5 times previously by the same person making the announcement - being man handled every time you step on and off the day boat as if you’re a 93 year old with a walking stick, gets extremely patronising after the second time- the fact that the ‘cruise’ travels approx 11km from the port and never moves from 5pm until the next morning when it returns to the port and you’re surrounded by 30/40 other identical cruise ships- it’s clear that none of the cruise operators care about return business - as long as they get you out there once that’s all they need to keep the cash flowingIn summary , Ha long bay itself is an incredible piece of nature but it is being completely and utterly exploited by the cruise operators to make a quick buck off ill informed tourists who lap it up like kids at a candy store. It should be stripped of its UNESCO title, spend a few months tidying up the floating rubbish and then massively limit the amount of cruise ships in the bay.

The bay was so packed with so many boats and the water is completely muddy for a day cruise that I would never go again or recommend the bay. You need to go out much further in the water than a day trip to see the emerald waters.

It is a beautiful area and very picture taking worth, but it is just so overcrowded. Never in my life have seen so many large ships in such a confined area. Also it seems the bay is used as a dumping ground. Never ever seen so much garbage in the sea! Just incredibly sad and therefore can only advice to stay away from this area so it can return to its prestine beauty! The water is clearly NOT suitable for swimming as you can see the garbage and oil floating on the water. Also the fisherman dump their toilets in the water. The smell was overall not pleasant, mainly fumes from the boats.I just hope that Vietnam restricts the visitors extremely and that they do something about the waste water and garbage.... if you can find a more quiet part of the bay with a personal boat if you really want to go..
Halong Bay's beauty was spoiled by the sheer number of boats. We saw a lot of trash in the water, which was really disappointing.

From the pictures you see online, Ha Long Bay looks like a gorgeous, remote paradise. It is not. First off, it's about a 3-hour drive from Hanoi. The bus will stop at the cheesiest tourist attraction to try and sell you pearls and souvenirs. Then you wait in an overcrowded, hot, cruise terminal for at least an hour until you get on your boat.The sky is almost always overcast, so you see more gray water and grayish greenish mountains. Not the blue sky and amazing colors you see in tourist pictures. You see other boats everywhere. And the bay is completely filled with trash. Ironically, you're not allowed to bring water bottles on board your cruise for "pollution control," but you ARE sold them at higher prices onboard your ship! The ships also serve other items in plastic and generate plenty of other plastic waste. It's a total ripoff. And then they tell you how traditional fisherpeople were moved by the government from their lifestyles because the fisherpeople littered the bay. It's so sad.On our kayak to a beach, all we did was pick up tons of litter. The beach was filled with litter. The whole thing was depressing.This was the one part of our 2-week Vietnam adventure that we wished we'd skipped. Maybe if you do it, do a day trip instead of overnight. But it's a long drive, so...

While the ship was great I could not believe how many other boats were in the water. I counted 13. We kayaked around the water and it was filthy. Floating trash everywhere. I wouldn’t recommend this trip.

Just went on a 2 day cruise in Halong bay and It is the most beautiful place which makes it all the more sad how  filthy and degraded the area is.It is horrifying how much plastic and garbage is floating everywhere, some areas worse than others. It’s washed up on beaches and shorelines all around the islands. The water has a dirty oily slick on it so getting in is not really an option. We went on a bhaya cruise who do make a big effort to be sustainable but it feels like it needs a monumental government level effort to sort it out. We tourists are part of the problem but the Vietnamese are destroying their own livelihood and fast. I actually couldn’t recommend going there to anyone- it’s literally so depressing to see something so gorgeous being so blatantly ruined.

This wonderful UNESCO site has sadly turned into a sausage factory.Don’t get me wrong, it is a beautiful place on the globe but my word, it’s oversubscribed.More than 500+ day cruisers and hotel cruisers at any one time, and pretty much all of them follow the same format.Go on board, cruise a little then be shipped and bused to some caves ( long, narrow and in stages very low and need to crawl) another bus trip, and back on board.The next day you’ll be offloaded to a village and pad round for a while and learn about Cat Ba Island … but  not sure why.All cruise offer Kayaking (if you like the) and a swim, but one of the biggest disappointments was the huge amount of floating debris in the bay.It has turned into a disgusting mass of turgid water, with a great deal of oil (presumably from the many vessels) floating polystyrene, plastic bottles and bags and other general waste.Who in their right mind would want to swim in that soup!Halong Bay has become a victim of its own success. We can only blame ourselves.However, from a distance it’s beautiful and a must see before it goes to total ruin. Clear blue sky’s always help, but often it’s muggy and cloudy and the limestone islands don’t show their true beauty and colour.Anyway, go and get the best from it.

Took a boat tour the waters around the bay are filthy I wouldn't let my dog paddle in it such s shame nobody cares about this is supposed to be a world heritage site but is really disgusting  trip owners accused the locals but I witnessed several trip boat employees throwing wast into the sea

Such a beautiful place and truly amazing to see cliff after cliff in the shadows. Cruise was fantastic, the boat, the food and the activitiesI came away from Ha Long Bay devastated about the level of trash and pollution and oil in the water. Spent my kayak trip picking up rubbish and refused to swim or snorkel! I hope tourists put their money elsewhere until the government clean up the area for good. Allegedly they had a clean up almost every year...but I cleared several boxes, bottles and countless plastic gloves and bags on my kayak and I didn't even make a dent in the area we were in.I've not seen this level of rubbish in any other South East Asian country, not saying it doesn't exist but they clearly do something with the problem

Why or why when you have such a magical natural oasis do you spoil it by the Government mismanagement of the pollution of the water.Out of the millions of dollars earned from tourism could they not hire 2 tugs with a large net to sail up and down the channels to collect the hideous amount of litter in the water.SORT IT OUT

So disappointing and heartbreaking to see this beautiful bay full of rubbish, polluted and crowded with tourist. We went for a swim and our body were covered in brown oily dirt (I have no idea what it was exactly but felt horrible) Vietnam you need to act immediately to protect that Bay!

So I wanted to share my experience both excellent and not so great so you can decide what you choose to do on the cruise. Price for what you get is generally very good, food is excellent (although we did get sick, must have been the drinks as nobody else was sick), experiences are as expected touristy but amazing scenery.The HUGE negative is that the water is extremely polluted and full of trash, both myself and my wife got a rash which started the day after we went swimming and now as I write this review 1 week later it’s only getting worse and today we’ve decided to go to the doctor because of how bad it’s getting. We did not see any oil carpets, as others mentioned, but a lot of styropor, caused by the local pearl industry, to keep their breed swimming.If clean, a clear 4 star, if restricted visitor numbers, coukd get a 5 star rating.Our cruise members were really friendly, that's why I do not name our cruise in the rating, not to be unfair.

Was so looking forward to our day trip but I came away feeling a bit sad. Such a beautiful place, but horrible to see all the floating rubbish, oily film on the water from pollution and far too many boats and tourists. Unbelievable, considering this is a World Heritage Site. I wish it could be more looked after and respected. Similar all over Vietnam unfortunately.

This was a lowlight of our trip. I’ve never experienced such filthy water and pollution. Boats just leave their rubbish in the water. It’s horrendous. Also there were about 50 boats moored overnight so it isn’t peaceful. Wouldn’t recommend and won’t be back

Halon Bay Dora cruise is not a cruise it’s a very nice floating hotel , it hardly moves . You have to take day boats to see most things. All the cruise boats are the same. It’s a bit of a con really. Halon bay is a wonderful spectacle but the sea is dirty and polluted. The people and staff were lovely. Would I recommend it …. Sorry but no . Pollution is absolutely killing the place.

Overrated and full of pollution. There are no more traditional junk boats, just tacky white cruise ships overcrowding the water. The amount of floating garbage in the water was unbelievable. Every photo you’ve seen of the place is photoshopped to make it look like a nature lovers wonderland. It’s more like a floating Magaluf.

I was very disappointed with my trip to Halong Bay. The scenery was ruined by the trash floating on the water and littering the shore. The boat was dirty and smelled bad. The food was bland and overpriced. The staff was rude and unhelpful. I do not recommend this place to anyone who wants to enjoy nature and relax. It was a waste of time and money.

We went on a so-called two day trip with Alisa premier cruise which in fact is only a 22 hour trip, where you only cruise for 1.5 hours each day, the rest of the time you are stationary. You spend the night on the boat with at least 25 other boats nearby (low season). You can visit Titop island (tiny beach) and kayak for some time. But all the boats come at the same time so you’re definitely not alone. Same thing next morning when you visit the cave Sung sot… be prepared to wait on the stairs to get into the cave! The depressing part however is the amount of garbage floating in the water, so sad to see the beauty of a UNESCO world heritage site being destroyed like that… If you still want to go I recommend a day trip on a sunny day on a small boat.

We are a family of four having a three days cruise. It’s a little shocking seeing how much garbage there is floating around. We used the cayaks picking some of it. Actually NOT tempting at all having a bath. Interesting looking at other tourist and also the reviews at Tripadvisor - it seems people are so used to plastic and garbage that they do not notice any more. They are happy - That is scary…!

Improve the overall experience, it looks like a theme park. The city is ugly, lots of construction everywhere, no respect for nature and city looks like a themed park with cable car and panoramic wheel. The seawater is very dirty and polluted, activities in the cruise are not amazing. The cruise activities are not amazing, you can do kayaking in less well-know places. Everything is overpriced, the cruise services and activities are expensive. The weather wasn’t nice so didn’t help to improve whole experience.

I feel so bad that we contributed to the tourism and pollution here. Millions of styrofoam pieces in the water, rubbish floating everywhere - kayaking past condoms. Our cruise ship crew were throwing cigarette butts into the water. The whole region is dead - no birds, no fish, no wildlife on the islands except for some monkeys where you’re taken to kayak - which have been transported and abandoned there because there’s no land mass nearby.. this was the worst part of our Vietnam trip and I wouldn’t recommend it to anyone. Check out Ninh Binh instead if you’re in Hanoi - beautiful and no rubbish.

This should have been amazing! After all, it looks stunning in photographs! But, the bay is full of rubbish, and the boats all go together to the same places! So dull! And busy! A real lost opportunity!

Dirty waters. Over inflated prices for foreign tourists and just very trashy. Halong bay should be the  one stop you do not need in Vietnam. Poor services at every venue. Dated boats. Tye amount of floating trash for a unesco site is intolerable it’s just one large floating garbage bin

I went to visit Halong Bay as part of a one night cruise. The bay itself is nice - though having explored Thailand a bit before coming to Vietnam, the island structures were very much the same. There was a cave we got to explore as part of the tour. In the past when I have explored caves in other countries, I've appreciated the "untouched" feel of them. The large cave in Halong Bay however very much looked like it was designed for tourism, and kind of lost its "natural" appeal. It was also very crowded and somewhat difficult to appreciate the environment because of that.Not a regrettable experience per se, but your money can be better spent elsewhere.

Did the 6-12hr day cruise from Hanoi old quarter. 6 hrs travelling time, 6 hrs on the boat. Only fed once at 12 noon and only snacks are available at the islands / onboard so BRING SOMETHING SUBSTANTIAL. On the ride back we stopped at a gas station but all marts were shut and not enough time to have food at the restaurant.Amazing view but the route they took us on is overdone. Too many people in the ‘surprising’ cave (children especially running and yelling everywhere), made climbing the stairs a nightmare as half the time you’re just waiting for the people in front of you. Titop island has a small beach but is also packed.IMHO: Pay up for a private 2hr cruise around the bay, perhaps kayak if you’ve got hats and don’t mind the extreme heat, then return to the harbour. Not worth being at the mercy of a huge group. Bring a sandwich as you won’t have anything to eat from 12.30pm to 9pm.

Cánh sắc ở Hạ Long so với nước khác thật sự là quá hùng vĩ, xứng dang di sản thiên nhiên, nhưng vấn đề vệ sinh đã làm mất đi vẻ đẹp của vịnh. Nước trong vịnh quá bẩn và quá nhiều rác thải. Thu phí vé vào rất cao nhưng việc bảo vệ môi trường chỉ làm hình thức, cấm khách mang đồ nhựa 1 lần vào Vịnh nhưng trong Vịnh các của hàng buôn bán lại sử dụng rất nhiều nhựa 1 lần. Thất vọng
I didn’t find much to do here. I took a boat trip on my last day, which was all right, but overall it was one of the most boring places I have ever been to.

Generell ist die Halongbucht sehr schön, wenn man aber die Touristenmassen und -boote sowie die neu gebildeten Hotels am Festland sieht, kann ich es fast niemand mehr empfehlen. Eher nach Sapa und dort das Dorfleben erfahren.Die Hotels sind alle von chinesischen Investoren. Der Rohbau steht noch nicht mal komplett & es sind bereits die Firmemschilder der zu erwartenden Geschäfte (Bäckerei, Massage, Cafe, Hotel, ...) angebracht. Ebenfalls sieht man überall Werbung, dass man investieren kann.Auch sprachlich ist das Personal eher auf asiatische Gäste als westliche Gäste ausgelegt. In ein bis zwei Jahren wird es touristisch überflutet sein. Wenn, dann jetzt noch besuchen. Unser Ausflug mit einem der Touranbieter war in Ordnung und gut organisiert. Abholung und Drop-Off klappte. Erster Stop waren die Felsen die auf der 200.000 Dong Note sind. Falls man davor nicht all die anderen Boote gesehen hat, hier sieht man den Touristenkommerz. Nächster Stop die Sung Sot (Surprise) Cave. Generell sehr schön zu sehen, aber alles sehr sehr getimt. Die Wet Cave / Hang Luan Lake ist sehr enttäuschend gewesen. Was auch immer ich mir unter Wet Cave vorgestellt habe war bestimmt kein See, der durch einen kurzen Tunnel befahren wird. Ich würde es eher als Inlet oder eben Bucht bezeichnen. Ti Top Beach: großartige Aussicht, aber überlaufen und das verhalten mancher Menschen zeigt, dass Menschen auch nur Tiere sind (sehr egoistisch und rüde).

Halong Bay is prachtig, en staat zeer hoog aangeschreven als must-see in Vietnam. Helaas is het massatoerisme op deze nochtans prachte eilandengroep de doodsteek voor deze plek. Het waarschijnlijk eens zo mooie verlaten Titop eilandje is nu overspoelt door mensen. Je voelt je er als een zalm in een stroom om nog maar te zwijgen over alle uitlaatgassen die alle boten uitstoten!

I visited 15 years ago and it used to be a beautiful place. I returned recently and I was shocked. Uncontrolled mass tourism and pollution have destroyed the place. There is an urgent need to put proper regulations in place to save this place.

sooooooo polluted. everything is devastated. we couldn't swim because of several hooks and several big dead fishes. it's been awful. too many tourist (despite was August and not December) and too many plastic everywhere. it's a sin.

El crucero que hicimos estuvo muy bien. El barco (Oriental Sails) estaba en buenas condiciones y el personal hizo un gran trabajo, pero quedamos tremendamente sorprendidos y preocupados por lo sucia que estaba la bahía, la cantidad de residuos, la basura en el agua y el poco cuidado que se tiene. Sorprende que siga siendo un lugar patrimonio de la humanidad por la Unesco y no se tomen medidas al respecto. No sé si el problema viene originado por los cruceros y sus turistas o es ya un problema a nivel mundial con la gestión de recursos y residuos que acaban en los mares. En ningún momento me apetecería darme un baño en las aguas de la bahía y desaconsejaria la visita rotundamente.

Friendly people, good food, quiet good room with shower on the boat, but:100 or 1000 Ships like this around. A lot of oil in the water, too much rubbish in the water and on the rocks. Dirty, unclear water (after 10 min. swimming, I got rash) and dead coral reefs.I think 40 years ago it must have been paradise!

Halong Bay is just gorgeous but as most of other attractions around the world, the area is destroyed by mankind as it’s now filthy and smelly! What a shame for a UNESCO site to be left In such a tragic situation! Water is def not suitable for swimming even in the designated areas/islands. Shame

I can imagine that Ha long bay was a nice place to visit before the influx of tourists have ruined it. We went on a one day tour as we heard it was a must see, but to be honest it was not worth the money. Yes the limestone structures are very impressive and nothing like I’ve ever seen before (Except for in Tam Coc) but it really has been ruined. I wish I didn’t waste my money. The water is very dirty, with plastic floating everywhere and a layer of grime. We went kayaking whilst we were here and I didn’t want to touch the water at all. There are hundreds of boats EVERYWHERE. We viewed the caves too which were impressive but again there were so many tourists which ruined its impressive nature. Considering it is a UNESCO world heritage site I was surprised that it was so dirty and over run. Really disappointed by our day trip here especially as it was so expensive.

If you are the type who does not like to go to an overcrowded place then DO NOT GO..the cave and boat ride is likely no longer an enjoyment as it is to crowded with humans..a lousy 35 to 45 minutes per stop is wasting time..the cave floor had been affected where you can see big crack and a sink hole is visible(dangerous). The WORST part is the titop island(i forgot the name)..its crowded and full of people..still under renovation and the water is ridiculously dirty..we do not understand what is so great about this place..area was not well preserved and rubbish floating (local and tourist dont give a damn for throwing rubbish to the sea)the only good thing is the scenery from the ferry in ha long bay..its breath taking..hopefully the gov of vietnam makes plan to take care of the place and being greedy for making money from the overpriced drinks and entry ticket..

Just my opinion, but Halong Bay is being over exploited. The bay and its islands are stunning, no doubt about it. But the impact of humans is everywhere and it is truly sad to see. When we went kayaking we couldn't believe the amount of plastic in the water. It was disgusting. We were told that its cleaned up every night, if that's the case then there really is a problem because there was so much plastic for one days worth. Ti Top island was crammed full of people. Why the boats take you there I have no idea. If you go I advise its better to stay on the boat. Also in places on our cruise there were little dead fish floating in the water. Surprise cave is huge but when they made the toilet block tile off cuts were just chucked in the water. Wtf? So halong bay has extreme natural beauty but when seen up close you will come away disappointed, bewildered and a little angry.

This was to be the highlight of our 18day track around Vietnam & it could've been if not for the sad state of the water. The immense cost of Tourism is everywhere to be seen. The water is so polluted that we skipped the swimming altogether. We saw tourists from other boats toss empty water bottles into the water without thought adding to the already vast quantities of plastic and rubbish floating by. Not to mention the greasy layer of 'scum'  that floats by in waves. Yuk!!If The Vietnamese govt doesn't act soon to stop this it won't be a place worth visiting. If they cant/ won't stop the Junk boat tours at the very least they should reduce the number that travel these waters each day. On our first night we counted 18 others just in our vicinity, they are everywhere everyday of the year.

I have been to Langkawi, Fiji, Palawan and Krabi so I have seen plenty of beautiful limestone cliffs and paradise landscapes. All of them are nicer than Halong Bay. Sorry to say, but Halong Bay just isn't worth it. The water is dirty, rubbish floats around the sea, the jetty feels like an airport such is the volume of tourists piling onto the hundreds of boats that overcrowd the bay.Basically, Vietnam has killed the goose that laid the golden egg by mismanaging what should be a world class attraction. For anyone going to Thailand or Phillippines after Vietnam, I suggest giving Halong Bay a miss. The bay is overrated, expensive to visit, and by not going you will be doing our planet a favor.

the water is a tad soapy when you feel it and doesnt smell pleasant generally.. and theres plastics littered in the water here and there (abt 5% of the time you will see plastics/litter). phuket's phi phi island was much better.

We did a two days one night cruise in Halong bay (3star). I am very disappointed.. Extremely touristic, super crowded, noisy and not relaxing at all. Even at night the boats have their engines ON to support the electricity on the boat such as air conditioning inside the rooms which is completely pointless. I am sure that everyone has seen these nice pictures on this bay from top but it is not so great from the see level. It is definitely a very special place but cruising it is not suggested. Don’t underestimate the high number of average and poor recommendations given!!
Lots of trash floating on water. Fuel/oil stains. If the people responsible for the Bay keep going that way, there won't be Halong Bay in a few years - just a huge, smelly dump.

Halong Bay. We hadden de pech van bewolking en een overvolle boot. Een van de stops is Luan cave waar je in vaart op roeibootjes of kajaks. Wat echt Helemaal niet leuk is dat ze daar 2 zielige apen op een hele kleine rots hebben neergezet die door de roeiers ook nog gepest worden. Dierenmishandeling! De rest is niet spectaculair maar wel aardig.

Vorab; unser Schiff war gut, Team, Essen und Zimmer alles super. Aber ich konnte leider nicht das Drumherum in dieser eigentlich wunderschönen Landschaft ausblendenden und die 2 Tage in der Halong Bucht genießen: Dreck, Gestank, Tourimassen, was tun die Leute dort Ihrer Natur bloß an! Man wird mit unzähligen anderen Bussen auf einen zubetonierten Anlegerterminal  mit unzähligen Hotels gefahren. Dort warten schon die Schiffe auf die nächsten Touren. Sobald man an Bord ist, geht’s los. Dutzende Schiffe fahren gleichzeitig ab. Auch später in der Bucht verläuft es sich nicht, man ist jederzeit von anderen Schiffen umgeben. Und dann der Müll. Ganze Teppiche mit Müll und Dreck schwimmen auf dem Wasser, unglaublich (s. Fotos). Bei der Kanufahrt  war man bemüht nicht nass zu werden, ins Wasser wollte definitiv niemand. Nachts haben wir den Fehler gemacht und mit offenem Fenster geschlafen; wir wurden aufgrund eines infernalen Fäkalgestanks wach. Offensichtlich hat man die Toiletten abgelassen, widerlich. Auch die Ausflüge machten es nicht besser, man wird auf überfüllte Inseln (tw. mit Blockabfertigung am Einlass und Stau auf der Treppe zum Aussichtsturm) gebracht und in überfüllte Höhlen gepresst. Ich kann nur von einem Besuch abraten. Aber i.d.R. ist das ja Bestandteil einer Rundreise, bilden Sie sich also Ihre eigene Meinung. Und nein, ich habe keinen „Atomkraft nein Danke“ Aufkleber auf meinem Auto, esse nicht nur Tofu von freilaufenden Bauern! Es war einfach nur das erschreckendste  Erlebnis auf einer sonst faszinierenden Vietnamrundreise.

Disappointed with the huge crowds and long lines in the islands and hills we visited. I booked a supposedly luxuryweekend  cruise (Stellar) upon arrival at Noi Bai Airport in Hanoi. Should have waited until I got to downtown where the rates are lower.This excursion could be completed in one day (as a day trip) rather than two days because the cruise did not even take us to the Pearl Farms as I first thought it would. Not value for money.  The islands in the Philippines (e.g. BOHOL) offer a more spectacular experience than this over-hyped tourist trap.
Mythique certes, mais le tourisme de masse en gâche le rêve, et la pollue !!! Les gros bateaux devraient être interdit pour garder la beauté du paysage.

Okay we were in Vietnam so everyone said you should do this tour yes we did so did thousands of others all going to the same places all seeing the same rocks jutting out of the sea. A total waste of money being cynical the people that are promoting this are the ones gaining the most.Obviously I am not a nature lover and rocks sticking up out of the sea does nothing for me but if it is your thing then enjoy and make someone rich!

Although it is an amazing place I honestly think it is not worth visiting in it's current state. There seems to be no restriction on the amount of visitors and when we were there on a 2 day, 1 night cruise there were at least 200 boats. They all do the same activities, visiting the caves, ti top mountain, kayaking etc and it is so overcrowded. The water surprisingly was clear of most rubbish but it was incredibly polluted with fuel and waste water from the boats. This was the only part of our 4 months of travel that we regretted doing and are surprised that UNESCO have not put more of a limit on the number of visitors allowed at one time like they have in many other sites around the world.

Es ist schon ein einzigartiges Erlebnis auf dem Schiff durch die Bucht zu schippern. Leider sind die Stationen, an denen man anlandet (wir hatten Tour 2) total überlaufen. Das geht gar nicht! Tip: In Halong übernachten und schon abends eine private Tour buchen, die möglichst bei Sonnenaufgang los geht, bevor die Schaaren an Touristen auf hunderten Booten regelrecht einfallen und das Naturerlebnis trüben.

Very disappointed with our day trip to Halong Bay. Way too many boats and the water was very polluted. It felt like we were like cattle all packed in together fighting for a little piece of water. I wish they were doing more to protect this area and restore it to its natural beauty. In comparison to other sites like Phi phi island in Thailand, Halong Bay was a big disappointment.
ハノイから日帰り観光でしたが、往復６時間以上かけて見る価値はそれほどでもないと思いました。一度行けばいいかな

Per essere un sito UNESCO esistono ancora troppi turisti maleducati e turismo di massa. Spazzatura in giro di cui molta in acqua, troppe barche a motore tanto da sentire odore di carburante perennemente.Il sito andrebbe regolamentato e preservato davvero: ad oggi non è una tappa che consiglierei a chi si organizza i viaggi autonomamente ed è amante di circuiti non turistici. Peccato perché la natura aveva fatto un gran bel lavoro...

Siamo venuti a Halong Bay con altre aspettative e siamo rimasti abbastanza delusi. È vero che il posto è mozzafiato, ma ti fa male al cuore vedere come la gente del posto non tenga cura di questa bellezza naturale. In acqua sta prohibido farsi il bagno: una perché ci sono le meduse e altra perché fa veramente schifo. Paseando con la barca si può vedere in mare diversi tipi di residui: da la lattina di Coca Cola, bottiglie di plastica, innumerevoli buste di plastica, carta, insomma di tutto. Vedere tutto ciò intorno a un paesaggio così bello fa veramente rattristire. La zona, con il fatto del turismo, è pienissima di barche.Vale la pena andarci, ma andate preparati.

The biggest problem: it's dirty. Have a look at a map and you'll see that the water is surrounded by land, so there's not much chance for it to be flushed out. Visibility was poor, all of the time. I know we shouldn't expect everyone to speak our language, but if you're providing a service then English is, for better or worse, a language that is very useful.
Trop de bateaux de tourisme partout dans la baie dont certain aussi gros que des immeubles .  Des détritus qui flottent partout . Quel dommage ce que cet endroit est devenu .

Como todos vosotros tenemos idea de lo que es la bahía de Halong por las fotos maravillosas que hemos visto a lo largo de los años, pero cuando llegas allí lo que ves es un inmenso depósito de plástico; por todos los lados flotan botellas Y todo tipo de objetos entre un montón  barcos.por la noche el tipo de turismo que hay es de fiesta ibicenca con música disco, mucha luz y juergas alcohólicas .....vamos lo que no esperas encontrar  en un patrimonio de la humanidad.por supuesto si queréis verla viva tendréis que ir pronto porque con los años se saturará de plástico y residuos de una cementera que hay cerca. yo ya sabía que no me iba a encontrar el paisaje de las fotos pero la verdad que no estaba encontrar un vertedero de plásticos y un Ibiza dos.
Amazing place but full of garbage. I spent my kayak trip cleaning as I love nature and breaks my heart to see how polluted such a beautiful place can be.

To travel half way around the world to watch piles of plastics clogging Halong Bay was a real disappointment...why even bother to take photos? This once pristinely beautiful place has literally been polluted by the tourists who visit it. Very sad.

Really disappointed, more than a cruise is like a little ride,  coming out from the Halong Harbour, like one and a half hour  to bee generous, some 35 or more cruises at the same time , going all together to the same place, in certain point they all stop ans stay in the same location, so you come to see nature and you will be seing boats all around you. The best of this treap is visit the amazing cave, all the rest  not value for the price.I think the best it would be try to find, local fishing boat, or traditional boat with local  guide , and really intent to get deep and lonly into this beauty of nature, Overcrowd and durty. I prefer lot more Tam Coc, the halong bay inland

Halong Bay is located three hours away from Hanoi. You can arrange the trip with your hotel or by booking with many travel agencies in downtown. However, I did recommend to book a high-quality ship, it's not cheap, but you will enjoy a better experience for sure.Firstly, I strongly recommend to review this place and your expectations. I think Halong Bay is nice but it's extremely overrated. Study the place and the weather you will have before travelling there. We travelled in March and we did have a full-cloudy and soft rains. I have been in Phuket and Krabi many times and that places are quite quite (and QUITE) better than Halong Bay. Take your time to reconsider or simply to analyse what this place really offers and compare that with your expectations. I won't repeat Halong Bay.

Well, your fun is pretty limited, you can't kayak on your own you can't actually do anything in the bay on your own no matter previous experience. We simply wanted to take a couple of kayaks and go out for the day and thought it would be nice an cheap. Wrong. You need to book with a company and on the day cheapest was 400k and once your booked you are on a trashy boat packed to the rafters. If you wanna kayak it's extra but time is limited and you need to give all you belongings. Stay away unless you have thorough planned.
Masificación y agua sucia. Tampoco vimos barcos tradicionales.La comida que dan en los barcos de excursiones contratadas es bazofia.

作為世界自然遺產，這裡的景色是一流的，但對於整個一日遊來說，的確是有些失望了。首先導遊的英文並不是很好，整艘船有一半越南人，一半外國人，但導遊並沒有照顧到外國人的感受，經常講完越南文就忘記了用英文講。由於選擇了一日遊，往返車程加起來將近9小時，而且回來的時候，並沒有按照約定送大家回到酒店，而是停在老城區就叫全部人下車。整個旅遊區個景點人都超級多，尤其是洞穴，完全走不開。還有tip top島，沙灘沒有落腳點。更讓人不開心的是，很多人在碼頭抽煙。而且由於船隻太多，每一個景點船停靠岸都要花很多時間，經常是幾個船撞在一起，擠來擠去才可以靠岸。如果真的享受風景，不建議這樣走馬觀花的一日遊，美好的景色應該配上美好的意境，如果人多，且大部分時間都在坐船和坐車上面，就不值得了！

Beautiful….BUT WHAT THEY DON’T TELL YOUAccording to the local guide this is the most popular tourist destination in Vietnam, and it is truly quite majestic…..but there a huge number of things you find out after you get here!I have reviewed the tours we took and that may have set the scene, however the bay itself……beautiful, but the most obvious thing is you cannot swim anywhere you like.There is one designated beach, to which we arrived there on a Sunday. If your itinerary has you landing in Halong Bay for one night, and it is a Sunday CHANGE IT! I am not a beach person, but a 40degree day, and I along with everyone else visiting that day wanted a swim…our tour took us to the only beach, which in Australia would be considered a sand bar.  It was about 100m long, and had about 5000 punters there – it sounded like a family of banshees fighting, and the access was worse. Quite simply it was awful…..shoehorned in left there for 45mins which was too long……the water lukewarm, and I assumed this was assisted by many using it as a urinal…..litter everywhere, and just simply unenjoyable……We stayed overnight on the water and this was pleasant, had a kayak/paddle around some caves, saw some monkeys, but everyone simply wanted to go for a swim….we went back to the same beach on a Monday, and whilst still unappealing, with less people it was somewhat more palatable.We looked in some caves, and a bit of a trek up a hill behind the beach to a look out, were all good, but not overwhelming.My biggest tip, if you have been to Thailand and specifically some of the tours around Phi Phi and the like, it is so much more enjoyable as you can have a swim. This is one place I have no desire to come back to.

We visited this site and stayed overnight on a boat the Bien Ngoc 18.  The boat was very nice, the cabins were tiny. The food on the boat was very nice but the whole experience was like navigating through rush hour.  The journey to the boarding site was literally row after row of new buildings going up.  I dread to think what it will look like in a couple of years when all the hotels, restaurants etc are built.  When we boarded there were so many boats all waiting to collect passengers.  Then when we sailed we were just in a convoy of similar boats all heading out into the bay.  Where were the beautiful views and  natural wonders?  Well they were there somewhere but hidden behind so many boats.  We stayed overnight on the boat and in the morning it was just the same.  You could not take a photograph without a dozen boats in the viewfinder and when we left to come back to the mainland again it was just one long crocodile of boats.  I fear that this beauty spot has become far too over-commercialised and the views and scenery in the glossy brochures is no longer available.  If you want to visit please go soon before it turns into just another tourist tinsel town.

There must have been tens of thousands of people there in thousands of boats. At every on land site we visited, we were all herded like cattle. There was always one tour group on top of another. There was poor quality water out on the bay with oil slicks throughout our boat trip along with trash islands. Ha Long Bay really needs a good clean up. In order to preserve some of the natural beauty, the region should figure out how to switch to eco tourism before all of the natural beauty is destroyed.In addition, the boat crew needs to lighten up on the high pressure sales tactics, so people can enjoy their meals on the boat. Asking for tips up front is also rude.Hopefully, the people of Ha Long Bay can save their area from its own demise.

We took an overnight cruise, a party of two adults and three children. The night on the boat and the food was good but every time we stopped to do an activity there were a thousand other people being dropped off by another cruise ship to do the same thing. The photo I am including shows the cave tour. It was an amazing cave but you could barely move because of all the other people.  The beach stop was the same and made you feel like you were on a tourist  conveyor belt.  We enjoyed Tang An boat tour near Ninh Bihn so much more and it was a fraction of the price. Halong Bay was probably the least enjoyable part pf out 17 night trip to Vietnam and so I would recommend either finding  tour that stops at none of the touristy part of spending your money on a different experience.
Halong bay is very polluted. Does not look like pictures. There is no wildlife its such a shame. Locals dont seem to care :(

Secondo me questa baia, seppur affascinante, è estremamente sopravvalutata e pubblicizzata! Noi per sentito dire eravamo incuriositi e per non vederla troppo in fretta (visto che da Hanoi bisogna fare molta strada) abbiamo scelto la minicrociera da 2 giorni e 1 notte (prenotata tramite il nostro hotel ma ci sono tante agenzie che vendono questi tour forse a prezzi più bassi)! La baia di per se è bella ma ti costringono a vederla solo tramite questi tour organizzati, quindi ti fanno fare delle soste brevi e superaffollate di altri turisti (fabbrica di perle e grotte) ma la cosa più fastidiosa è che ti danno pochissimo tempo per goderti il posto! Non abbiamo fatto il bagno (cosa che mi aspettavo di fare) nè siamo andati in kayak perché in realtà faceva freddo e l'idea di tuffarsi in acqua ci faceva rabbrividire. Sarà che il tempo non era dei migliori ma siamo stati più tempo sulla crociera che a visitare la baia, avrei voluto vedere almeno il villaggio dei pescatori o salire a piedi i famosi 400 gradini per goderci il panorama dall'alto, ma niente di tutto ciò! Per ovviare cercano di farti passare il tempo con attività idiote come la pesca notturna dei gamberetti (una vera presa in giro) e la lezione di cucina (che non è altro che la preparazione degli spring rolls)! Mi sono sentita ingannata e soprattutto delusa... oltretutto è la spesa maggiore che ho sostenuto in Vietnam... con lo stesso tempo e per meno soldi è molto meglio andare a visitare i campi di riso a Sapa!

Repeat what many others are saying about Halong Bay: Great potential, but over tourism, too many boats, and outrageous pollution destroys the experience. At a certain point that could have been beautiful scenery and pictures taking opportunity - I counted about 50 boats all racing side by side in same direction as ours, more noise and motor sounds than nature. Everyone decided to gladly skip the swimming opportunity.

Halong Bay and the surrounding islands themselves are stunning and beautiful. The reason for the low scoring is the incredible water pollution that we witnessed during our one night stay on a houseboat. For a protected UNESCO site to be treated this way is shameful. The water was full of plastic and garbage. We were encouraged to swim in the water but opted against this as we saw cigarette butts, plastic bottles, plastic containers, garbage bags and styrofoam debris floating past our boat. Taking the kayak was a great experience, but minimized by the floating needle and syringe that meandered past our initial yards and the dead coral that is likely a symptom of the widespread pollution.  The fishing village we were taken to was upsetting as some of the fish are farmed in garbage-littered/oily water. The attached photos do not represent the scale of water pollution we saw (i.e it is worse). In summary: Halong Bay IS beautiful but the experience is destroyed by the water pollution. Please think hard before spending your hard earned money taking an expensive tour here as you will likely be disappointed. This is particularly true if you go in the busy summer season as, according to our guide, the visitors have no regard for the environment and casually dump garbage off the side of their boats.  The government of Vietnam needs to step up to protecting this and other beautiful assets (the urban community there is riddled by air pollution and garbage pollution of roads and waterways) - perhaps the only way to force this action is to take your vacation dollars elsewhere.

Beware Pirates! In Hanoi I booked a Ha Long Bay 2 Day, 1 Night tour with The Sinh Tourist, So 30 Pho Bao Khanh, Hoan Kiem, Hanoi. I paid a premium $90 plus $10 premium for a private room with a sea view, private bathroom and hot water. I'm a guy, senior citizen, traveling on my own and I am used to paying a little more. I've had great success with many tourist outfits in Vietnam and elsewhere.....but not this time. The boat disembarks from the pier at Ha Long Bay and the tourist guide onboard informs me I have to share a room. I inform him I paid the premium for my own room. Sorry, he says, I have no choice. I check out the room I'm assigned and it's a double bed. Am I really supposed to share a bed with a complete stranger? You gotta be kidding. I follow the guide around as he assigns other rooms and he's putting a couple in a room with two single beds. I guess I have to do all the thinking here. I get him to switch rooms so, at least, I don't have to sleep on the deck instead in a bed with strange man. My new "suite" has no sea view and the sink has been removed from the bathroom and , oh yea, I have a roommate. Later we find there is no hot water. The guide gives me back my $10 "Premium" I supposedly paid for a single room.It's obvious what The Sinh Tourist does is a "bait and switch". They had no intention of giving me a private room when I booked the tour. They take your money, overbook the boat and crowd everyone on board. The prices they charge are all over the place. Most on this trip paid $130 for 2 people in one room. I paid $100 ( minus $10 now) for the same accommodation, and really, much less of an accommodation. I'm in the stern of the boat, next to the pounding generator and the diesel fumes it emitted. Thankfully, the generator is turned off at 11 pm which is great if you want some sleep but not so great if you want electricity in the night to use your computer, charge your batteries or see your way around the room. Remember..... no sea view, no sink and no hot water either.The boat itself is in need of an overhaul. There were 15 paying passengers on the boat and only three chaise lounge deck chairs on the sun deck and they were falling apart, missing slats and armrests. Lunch and dinner were adequate but breakfast was pathetic. They served each table a plate of overcooked fried eggs, white bread, butter and jam. I had to laugh. My fellow passengers were not so amused with anything on this "cruise".Ha Long Bay is a beautiful place despite the garbage in the water. It would have been much more enjoyable had I not booked with a garbage, scam tourist company. I've contacted The Sinh Tourist and asked for total refund which they have refused. They've got their money and have run with it. I will be looking at other avenues of redress. In the meantime, heed this warning: The Sinh Tourist is not a good place to do business. Vietnam can do better.

The islands on halong bay are beautiful. But the place itself is covered in smog which was really disappointing. Also, there is major construction work going on here so it’s probably best to wait until that’s finished before you visit.

Definitely not as nice as the photos they show you in the book. Also they say free beer - this is not true.
