They then told us once we got on the boat that they give us 2 hours of free beer from 8-10pm. This is also not true. They gave us a keg at 8pm and took it away at 8:25pm. We argued with the staff to bring it back out but they argued and said it had been time - which is so false. The keg was still 3/4 full. The cooking class is NOT a cooking class. It's a 3-minute demonstration on how to make a spring roll. It's not hands on. They do not market themselves correctly for this trip. I would suggest paying more to reap the actual benefits of paying more. I tried to get my money back and argued with the tour guide for about 1 hour and they said they'd give me back $5 of my $80 that I paid.

Excellent tour and beautiful tour but tons a of pollutions.  There seemed to have been a multitude of small dead fish in the water.  I did not see a single living bird or fish in the sea on my 48 hour tour.  Many junks throw trash directly off the back of the boat.  The water smells weird.  This place is an environmental disaster.

Mon dieu, on m'a vendu du rêve, j'ai vu du rêve en photo.. Mais quelle triste réalité !Ici, j'ai été surprise par ce que j'ai vu. Des habitants tristes, des employés d'hotel tristes, des locaux qui détestent les touristes, la baie polluée, les rues jonchées de déchets..La vue qu'on m'a décrite -qui semblait incroyable- ressemble en réalité a un chantier permanent. Partout ou j'ai posé les yeux, ils construisaient des complexes hoteliers plus gigantesques les uns que les autres.. Ils ont monté des complexes de mini-villes entiers. Ils sont vides ! Trop cher pour les locaux et pas assez intéressant pour les étrangers. Donc les bâtiments sont abandonnés alors qu'ils sont neufs..Assis sur un banc, un soir, avec mon ami, nous discutions de l'effet du Tourisme de Masse sur les villes et les pays. Ici c'est une évidence, le tourisme détruit.. La vue, la nature, le bien-être des locaux.. Ca m'a rendu triste. Nous avons rencontré un habitant, qui s'est arrêté pour discuter avec nous, il nous a fait part de sa réticence et sa tristesse face à ces changements. Il ne se sent plus chez lui, plus en sécurité.. Il nous a parlé des enlèvements d'enfants, du trafic d'humains et de la pauvreté d'ici.. On a commencé à voir l'envers du décor et je vous jure, je me suis sentie coupable..La Baie est jolie, évidemment cela a du charme, mais ca ne vaut pas tout ce que nous sommes en train de détruire pour en profiter..

Booked a night on the Halong Violet vía the agency at Sofitel Legend Metropole in Hanoi.  Violet is meant to be best vessel that sails on Halong Bay.We were unlucky with very foggy weather which I now understand is VERY common this time of year.It is clearly a beautiful place, but has been destroyed by massive over exploitation.  There are SO many vessels, you simply leave and sail in one continual long line.  As you do so, you gaze over what must have been once crystal clear water, but instead see so much plastic garbage float past you.  It’s soul destroying.You go to a very average cave and then get rowed around a ‘floating village’ that looked like a shanty town built on more garbage.  You get 20 minutes of tai chi in the morning and then if you have a cast iron gut, you can dive into the filthy water for a swim.We had booked sea plane out which was cancelled due to fog.  They then call you countless times to try and get you to take it back as do not want to have to refund.On way back you go to country village, visit a house (very interesting) and have a water puppet show.  Both out and back on 3.5 hour journey they stop at typical mass tourism superstore in case you are dumb enough to pay twice as much as in Hanoi.The Violet was tired, not at all what I expected for the money.  Our room was pretty.  Thai cooking lesson is a joke.  They show you how to roll a spring roll... like making a giant spliff.At +£1,200 for one night it was the only time on my amazing trip I felt utterly ripped off.Should have listened to my friends who knew better.Save money... don’t bother.

Vi blev meget skuffet over ha long Bay, der var meget tåget, dårligt vejr og turister over det hele. Man holdte i kø alle vegne for at se små grotter og huler, der ikke var noget særligt. Der var plastic overalt i vandet. Det er hyggeligt st sejle, men det var også det.

I was really looking forward to this section of our Vietnam trip because of the stunning scenery and walking opportunities. It isn’t that straightforward, though, as an independent traveller. Cruises are extensively advertised but other information is hard to come by. So my tips:If you want to go to Cat Ba, get the local ferry from Tuan Chen (domestic harbour). Get the earliest one possible to have enough time as the last one back is at 4. It should cost 80 dong each way.Don’t go walking around the back streets unless you are fully comfortable with stray dogs as there are lots of them.Be wary of organised cruises - check the prices extensively and the safety record of the boat.Street food is good but stick to the main strip if you want it to be more western accessible. Wine choices are limited so be clear that it may be beer/cocktails only. Beer should only cost 25 dong at a maximum.Don’t go to Sun World unless you like extreme tourist activities.To get to/from Ha Long do not try the train. We did and failed and it was a relatively scary experience rescued only by a kind taxi driver.Private transfers cost around $60-$70 dollars either to/from Hanoi. The bus, however, is only $5 and it is really good and only takes around a 1/2 hr to an hour longer. Very safe as well as the driving is quite hairy.Ha Phong is where you will get to if you are coming from Ho Chi Minh. Book transport ahead as options there are limited. The ferry is a difficult and time bound option which I would not recommend.

My view maybe be tainted because I first visited Halong Bay 25 years ago but this is an example of how not to manage a major tourist attraction.We went on a tour that was well run with good food and transport to and from Hanoi.  So I have no issues with the tour and would rate that highly.  However, the problem was there were three hundred other tours run at the same time.  This compounds the fact that they all visit the same things so your day is spent queuing; queuing up the 400 steps to a pagoda, queuing to get on a small boat to see a lagoon which then, in turn, queue to get in and out, and queuing to see a cave to walk in a queue around them and down and on to the boat.The islands and bay itself are beautiful even if the weather isn’t great, but if your idea of a good time is seeing natural beauty away from the crowds don’t come here.
Polluted from all the boat traffic. Considering the short transit, maybe the Vietnamese government should consider sail power.

Too many trashes left in the water by every travel company. The tours offered are all the same, you have to rush to do/see everything. No respect of this amazing place, in a few years, it will be ruined.
you have to take a van for 3 hour to go to harbour and spend 8 hours on the boat which too tiring and not worth it.

I am sad to report that our visit to the Halong Bay was the low point of our 10-days in Vietnam. Everything you will read about the trash is true and actually much, much worse. We did a 1-night cruise on Orchid, and while I have no complaints about the cruise itself, sailing and kayaking past mounds and mounds of garbage was sickening. It’s indescribable. I was thinking about it for days and was so depressed. I have no idea where the UNESCO money is going, but it is not going to dealing with this ecological disaster. The Halong Bay should be shut down, quite honestly. I have been to Palawan in the Philippines several times and it is aesthetically the same as Halong but CLEAN! You couldn’t pay me to swim in Halong. I was ashamed of myself for participating in this catastrophe. Avoid it.

We were on a 3D/2N cruise here. It is very busy. Ha Long Bay is full of boats and prepare to queue your way through the trips to caves or to the top of Ti Top Island. The water did not seem like you could swim here. If you seek a calm, nature environment: this is not the place to go.

Abbiamo visitato la baia di Halong per tre giorni. La crociera è stata emozionante e soddisfazione. Purtroppo lo sporco e l'inquinamento che abbiamo trovato fa rabbrividire. La popolazione che vive nella baia è da sempre abituata a gettare i rifiuti in mare, ora però i rifiuti sono di plastica e si accumulano ad ogni marea. Scenari da favola rovinati da plastica e oli. Passi per i rifiuti "biologici", vi sconsiglio di fare il bagno, ma è un delitto permettere di rovinare un ecosistema unico al mondo gettando plastica ovunque. Spero che le autorità adottino sistemi per la raccolta delle plastiche facciano capire ai "locali" che preservare l'ambiente è preservare la loro fonte di guadagno.

What a huge disappointment. Perfect weather, outstandingly beautiful scenery, but litter almost everywhere in the waters of HB. Old plastic bottles, plastic bags, polystyrene, crisp packets - you name it - spoiling the environment. Luckily, we found a small area to swim, but even the beach was strewn with rubbish.A nearby noisy "disco boat" and some loud over-excited Chinese visitors really didn't help. Will this place ever be cleaned up ? I hope so.

Halong Bay with its beautiful jutting limestone islands that just appear to have grown out of the waters are a natural wonder. However look beyond this and you will see the destruction and devastation that is being caused by the hundreds of boats that visit daily leaving a trail of debris and a layer of fuel across the waters. Boats drop you at Ti Top island for a swim after a climb to a viewing platform, you are there with the world and its mother and the waters certainly aren’t for swimming. Lack of regulation and thought for the environment are quite simply destroying this this beautiful place. Would you recommend you travel a little further afield to the delights of Cat Bar and other areas past the first bay.

En dehors du fait que le temps était brumeux normal pour un mois de novembre Se retrouver au milieu d’une cinquantaine de bateaux sur la baie les uns derrière les autres à respirer les gazs d’échappements A ne pouvoir faire de photos sans avoir les tonnes de bateaux etc Ce devait être le plus bel endroit du Vietnam et franchement c’était le pire Rien ne vaut mieux que la baie d’ha long terrestre et non maritime !!!

Es una pena que un lugar tan bonito esté tan poco cuidado por la gente que trabaja allí. En las fotos se ve todo precioso, e incluso en los barcos y en la isla que te llevan al bañarte también parece todo limpio. Pero todo cambia cuando ya empiezas a ver las cosas más de cerca. haciendo kayak ves mucha suciedad en el agua: plásticos y muchos desechos de los barcos. Tanto que se ve a la gente de los barcos echando los desperdicios de las cocinas por la borda. Pero lo peor fue el último día. Hay una zona en la bahía dónde atracan todos los barcos para pasar la noche. Está a unos 100 metros de dónde está la playa dónde se bañan todos los turistas.  En mi caso conte 40 barcos aproximadamente. Pues resulta que después de ir al baño veo que por un chorro de agua que sale del barco salen todos mis desperdicios al agua. O sea, que te estás bañando en una playa donde se está vertiendo la mierda de más de 500 personas. Nosotros hicimos dos noches. Creo que En un solo día se puede ver más o menos lo mismo. Ya que es muy bonito pero todo parecido. Fue el lugar más masificado de turistas en todo mi viaje a Vietnam.

Halong Bay i oktober är dött!Men det byggs jättemycket så på sikt kan detta säkert bli en super Plats för semesterfirare. Men som backpacker ska du nog sikta in dig på Cat Ba i norr. Men generellt finns här inga stränder. Men vi hittade en superbra resturang som inte fanns på tripadvicer ✌️

Rubbish everywhere, far too many boats, crowds and ridiculous lines for all the attractions (even walking up a hill!), i ingested so much petrol it's gotta be bad for you. Lovely place ruined by poor Management and to much tourism.

Was very disappointed to see the level of pollution here. Plastic bags, bottles and general waste floating everywhere. Went for one night cruise on a junk style boat. They are all the same and very very run down and don't look like brochures. You don't really cruise anywhere you just motor off shore a few kilometres and park there for the night. You can see the mainland and about 20- 30 other boats when you are anchored. There seems to be no rules with the amount of tourism and it's out of control. If regulated, respected and kept clean this place would be stunning.

This was my 3rd visit to Halong Bay and I won’t be returning. Perhaps it was because we just did a short afternoon cruise on the Bay rather than going further afield, but we found it crowded and polluted. Whereas on our last visit 10 years ago we swam, I wouldn’t consider doing it now. Halong town/city is now an overdeveloped semi-theme park with no remaining charm. The whole bay has become a victim of its own beauty.

Halong Bay has a beautiful scenery which is worth seeing but I would recommend doing a boat tour out of Hanoi rather then staying in Halong Bay. The staff at our hotel (City Bay Hotel) was very nice and the staff at the restaurants were also nice...but the looks on the street were very unwelcoming. Unless you were spending money at their business you were met with a disgusted blank stare. There's not much to do and a lot of the restaurants are only open during certain hours. I would recommend doing a day trip rather then an overnight stay because of the beautiful scenery.

In einer organisierten Reise war ein eintägiger Abstecher aus Hanoi zur Bucht inklusive 4stündiger Bootstour und eine Übernachtung inbegriffen. Insgesamt waren es 1,5 verlorene Tage, da die Bootstour im Schneckentempo nur durch relativ verdrecktes Wasser tuckert und überhaupt nicht an Baden zu denken ist. Wer schon einmal in Thailand oder anderen Ländern SOA war, wird auch von den vielen Inseln nicht sonderlich beeindruckt sein. Eine kleine Höhlenbesichtigung, die obligatorischen Verkaufsgespräche auf dem Boot und fertig ist die Reise. Abends kann man sich dann in der Stadt langweilen. Auf keinen Fall zu empfehlen.

とても良い口コミばかりだったので期待して行きました。なんといっても世界遺産ですから。そしてある程度の日本人価格というぼったくりにも少しは理解していたつもりでした。が、実際のツアーの中身は大変貧相なもので二度と行かないし、余ほど暇じゃない限り丸一日かけるほどのものではない気がします。　お土産物屋さんに往復長時間滞在するので計９０分もそこで時間を消費しました。それでも実際のクルーズが良ければ問題なかったけれど、夫婦岩ですよー、ハチ公ですよーと、たった三つほどの景色を眺めたほかはずっと海のど真ん中を通るので、景色なんて3時間半クルーズの内、行きの数分楽しんだ程度でした。何故かほかの船はたくさんの小島の畔を運航しているのにJTBの貸し切り船だけはそんな感じでしたからツアー会社を選べば満足度は違ったのかもしれません。　船での食事も不味いことこの上なく、とても粗末なもので、市街地の屋台のほうがまともな料理を提供してくれるんじゃないかと思うほどでした。魚介類や果物を売りに来た船も、ぼったくり価格もいいところで、大あさりは500円で普通サイズのシャコが千円というお値段で、船で調理してくれるということでしたがただ単に焼いただけのものが出てきただけでこれが調理といえるのか疑問でした。果物も傷んだものを詰めてよこすし、結局食べれずに残してきました。　洞窟ツアーはどの観光客よりも速足で、どんどん他のお客を抜かして出てきました。まあ、洞窟の中はサウナのような暑さと湿気で普通の洞窟の涼しさなんて欠片もありませんで不快度１００％ですからそれは仕方ないことなのかもしれませんが、日本の鍾乳洞のほうがゆったり楽しく過ごせる気がします。　お土産物屋さんでは店に入ったら最後、一昔前の大塚家具のように店員がずーっとついてきて、安いよ安いよと売り込んできますが、一押しのスタールビーでも安いものは本物とは思えない品でしたし、チョコレートは空港のほうが安かった。　こんな何一つ良い事がないツアーでした。日本人は馬鹿にされてるんでしょうね。悔しい気持ちさえわいてきます。ほかのレビューではとても良いことばかり書いてあったので私が外れに当たっただけでしょうか。

I reluctantly write my first negative review. Ha Long Bay is a reminder of the devastating effects humanity has on the environment. After visiting a crowded cave with litter and construction we cruised through plastic filled water. We were let off to kayak through depressing waters with dead coral, plastic and styrofoam with a pearl farm nearby.  I would highly recommend skipping Ha Long Bay and go to Cat Ba instead. It was more remote, cleaner, and more mellow. Don't get me wrong, this are as a whole can be beautiful and special but the specific Ha Long Bay Area, where the cruise ships anchor, leaves you to wonder what good the UNESCO certification does.

Halong Bay, with its idyllic scenery is considered a must when traveling in Vietnam. Indeed, the limestones, the beaches and the cave should have been wonderful before this place was transformed to a giant tourist attraction.The overnight cruises are actually convoys of boats sailing in muddy brown waters full of plastic bags and other trash. The beach we visited didn’t have space to put a towel and smelled of urine, while the cave was more crowded than a metro in the rush hour and people were touching everything and climbing everywhere.The Vietnamese authorities should seriously consider setting some regulations for the preservation of this area soon, because it is gradually and rapidly transformed into a big rubbish bin.

I came here for a nice quiet holiday. You will not find that here. It is full of tourists. Cat ba bay is beautiful to visit. However to me it was way overrated. Most backpackers I met thought the same. Typical tourist trap. If you want an adventure theme park with tons of tourists and bright lights this is the place. It is a easy travel here. However the real Vietnam in my opinion will not be found here. Go to Sapa instead!

Picture postcard images were in mind when we set out for Halong Bay. Our disappointment was not helped that the day was overcast, but the biggest were the conga line of boats (hate to think what high season is like) and the rubbish. Plastic bags, bottles, general rubbish collecting in many little bays. What a shame.

My family signed up for a nice overnight boat trip and did not get what we expected. I believe when you arrive they just fill random boats. No matter what we paid for.  It appeared like the tours just arranged deals with each other. The staff was very dirty themselves and we had a shortage of food. I felt really bad for the twenty year old guys on the boat that were still hungry . And when we got to the caves it was a cattle call. If i was to return i would just go for a day and hire a boat when i arrived for a quarter of the price. Just be cautious . I am a well traveled person and not picky! Be flexible:)

Hallo zusammen!Wir sind ohne Buchung zur Halong Bay gefahren, da wir wussten man kann ohne Probleme ein Schoff finden, welches eine Rundfahrt anbietet...Kaum da, warten auch scho X Anbieter darauf die Touris zu verarschen und auszunehmen! Es war unser schlimmster Tag in einem Monat Reise...Auf dem Prospekt sah alles top aus, hörte sich gut an und und und...ich bin generell in Asien nicht so leitgläubisch und der Mann hat mich echt überzeugt. Ticket gekauft alles in Ordnung bis wir ans Boot kamen...alles andere als das was wir gebucht haben, das Boot sah aus als würde es jeden Moment auseinander fallen, an Board bekamen wor nichts zu Essen und zu Trinken, das war anscheinend nicht im Trip eingerechnet, wir mussten die ganzen Touristenattraktionen mitmachen und noch extra bezahlen!!! Also schaut gut, informiert euch gut und sichert euch ab so gut wies geht!

Lo pasamos genial, todo el personal de abordo súper amable, pero el barco es viejo mal conservado y además esta bastante sucio.Me parece que Ha Long Bay es un imprescindible en un viaje a vietnam pero igual si hubiéramos gastado un poco mas hubiéramos ido mas cómodos, he de decir que la experiencia no nos deja mal sabor por esto.

We were last in Halong Bay about 9 years ago and it was a lot cleaner back then. This time we went kayaking and swimming and it was like swimming in soup - condoms, plastic packaging etc. Just disgusting! I really hope the Vietnamese government does something to clean the Bay up as otherwise they can expect to see a downturn in tourists. For this reason I won't be back.
I will never go back to this place. Dirty, totally unkept. I cant believe tourists go to this place (including me). Not worth my time and money. Philippines is much better (Palawan, Bohol)

Of course the landscape is stunning, but I was disappointed by HLB city. The town has the same vibe as an amusement park or Las Vegas. Lots of neon, not a lot of substance. The only real reason to be there is the landscape, and there are other/better ways to see it. After being bummed out by HLB we caught the ferry to Cat Ba island and loved it. Rented a motorbike for $5/day and explored the gorgeous green island, went rock climbing (or tried considering we're beginners), went kayaking, the works. All in the midst of the same beautiful landscape you'll see at Ha Long Bay, at about 1/3 the price.
Las imagenes publicadas no reflejan lo expectacular del lugar como verlo estando ahi. Increible lugar, sumamente recomendado.

The 4 hour trip from Hanoi was not the best but I'm glad, in retrospect' to have seen Halong Bay. I'm absolutely certain that the overnight tours are not worth it. I'm surprised people still take them, even after reading so may negative reviews. Most of the tours are overpriced and I'm so glad that we did not take a overnight cruise. Also we would have missed staying at the Halong Palace Hotel (see my review)However, the caves were wonderful to see and it was a relaxing 4 hours on the bay. If I was pushed for time in Vietnam, I would skip Halong Bay and spend more time in Hanoi and Hoi An/Da Nang area.

Auf die Halong-Bucht haben wir uns besonders gefreut. Leider ist das Meer so vermüllt, dass höchsten Wehmut aufkommt. Plastik so weit das Auge reicht ... Wir waren mit Bhaja Legend unterwegs, ein grosses Schiff mir nur drei Kabinen. Das Personal war freundlich und bemüht, aber nicht geschult.

Ha long bay it self was beautiful. Great scenery and enjoyed the tour to Surprise cave, and beach, although over crowded. We mistakenly booked through another operator, rather than the one we were recommended (FANTASEA Tours) and chose to do a one night tour. Our accommodation was good, complete with ensuite with hot water, and good pressure.Lunch was good on the first day, whole fish, pickled vege, rice, tofu and cabbage. This unfortunately was my favourite meal. 500ml bottles of water were US$1 a bottle (24VND) which was normally 7VND, we were told nothing that was purchased from the ship could be consumed on the ship. If drinks or alcohol was bought on to the ship a duty would have to be paid. In the afternoon we were taken to the Surprise caves. The ques were long , as there were many other tour companies arriving at the same time, this also occurred at the beach we visited. The bar onboard, commence with happy hour over the duration of the tour,  buy 2 get 1 free. The drinks were still cheapish (US$2/50VND but not in comparison to Hanoi town (10/15VND) wine was priced at US$30 and was TERRIBLE. As part of the cost of the tour, we were told of a 20l keg was supplied for the paying customers. There was a clause, the "Free beer" was only for an hour. I found out from the tour guide, after an hour the beer was given to the crew. With that; I grabbed and empty 1.5l water bottle and filled this up before the hour was up, and shared this around once the keg was taken off us. ( apparently it was almost empty when they took it, and they werent happy)Our tour guide tried to induce us to drink more by stating 'happy hour' was back on, and said you guys dont know how to "party". With that people were saying to him the drinks were expensive, why we had to pay US$1 for water etc. In the end, I think he was got annoyed about it and headed below deck. our breakfast, was a rubbery fried egg, partially toasted bread, fried rice cake, and coffee. We were then told to "get off the boat" for a compulsory tour of a pearl farm. At the completion of the tour, we returned we conducted a "cooking class" , this was more like rolling up summer rolls for lunch, not actually cooking.To sum it all up, the views were great the crowds werent, and this Company; Ha Long  Golden bay cruise was the WORST!!Top Tips;Smuggle your own water and alcohol, keep it in your room. Drink as much 'free beer' as you can, collect it in a large bottle (dont get caught)Read reviews about other tour companies first.I hope this helps.

Expect to see a lot of buses and cars full of tourists. If you go with a boat sorry to say but you need to go private. We did and that's the only way you could enjoy the ride. Otherwise you will be pack eating cheap food, visiting caves in a hurry...We'll not exactly the images of Indochine movie from Regis Wargnier with Deneuve...and the water was green....So in conclusion if you are in the region as we were (3h drive from Hanoï) you can check it. But don't go there only for this and be aware of the tourist traps...they are all over....

Wir (2 Paare) wollten uns gerne die wundervolle Natur der Halong Bay anschauen. Hierfür hatten wir eine Bootstour (159 pp)  bei Peace Charme gebucht. Das Boot, das Essen und die Kabinen sowie das Personal war wirklich klasse. Die wunderschöne Natur der Halong Bucht wird allerdings durch extrem viel Müll und Autobahnfeeling auf dem Meer überschattet. Die Schiffe bahnen sich ihren Weg eng gedrängt hintereinander durch eine Mischung aus Plastik und Ölfilmen. Wir haben Cat Ba aufgrund der Warnung vor extremen Massentourismus ausgelassen, was eine gute Entscheidung war. Schließlich setzten wir dann auf einem Beiboot zu einer kleinen Insel mit einem Aussichtspunkt über, wo uns die Aussicht auf einen tollen Ausblick und schwimmen versprochen wurde. Man konnte sich quasi auf der Insel vor lauter Menschen kaum bewegen und die lange enge Treppe verzögerte dementsprechend den Aufstieg. Ich denke, die Bilder sprechen für sich... Das versprochene "schwimmen" entpuppte sich als 50 qm abgesperrter überfüllter Strandbereich, an dem die gesamten Schiffe vorbeifuhren. Deshalb ließen wir das schwimmen im Öl und Dieselgestank ebenfalls aus. Schließlich würden wir auf einem kleinen Boot von einem Local in Schwimmwesten durch eine kleine Höhle gefahren um auf der anderen Seite drei Affen zu füttern und diese auch zu fotografieren. Traurig... Als wir dann noch sahen, wie ein Crew Mitglied eines anderen Schiffes volle Aschenbecher ins Meer kippte, war der Ausflug für uns endgültig gelaufen. Wenn man die Halong Bucht besuchen möchte,sollte man möglichst vorher über ein kleines lokales Reisebüro zB in Hanoi an jeder Ecke versuchen eine Tour zu buchen, die abseits des Massentourismus unterwegs ist. Nochmal würde ich dort auf keinen Fall hinwollen, trotz der wunderschönen Natur, die es aus meiner Sicht traurigerweise nicht mehr lange geben wird.

I took a one day trip to Halong Bay (do shop around the old quarter region for the best pricing).It took 4 hours for the van to get there. I was unfortunate to have boarded a van that had spoiled aircond, so the 4 hours was hell.We then boarded the boat at noon. The lunch was decent, but drinks are not included. The boat stopped by a spot for about 20 minutes for canoeing/bamboo boat for an extra cost. It doesn't seem very worth it. Next, the boat stopped by a cave (at least this was included). After the cave, the boat headed back to the terminal.After a total of 2+ hours on the boat, we are now on a four hour trip back to Hanoi. At least this one has air conditioning.So for those who are thinking of taking the one day trip, keep in mind that you'll be spending 8 hours traveling, and 2+ hours on the boat. Also, that the trip is really really hot and everyone seems to want to make extra cash out of you.That said, the rocks are really beautiful. But the boat ride doesn't give you more information about them, only the opportunity to look at them.

La bahía de halong es lindisima!  El tour se paga por si solo! La experiencia de viajar en barco igual! Pero si ves que la bahía por más linda que sea esta arruinada de tanta basura que la gente local incluso el team de barco.. tira al mar.. Muy sucio hagan algo! Que hagas Kayak y salgas apestando a gasolina porque hay tantos botes que derraman aceite o gasolina al agua.. Que si te roban 300dolares de tu habitación en el barco porque confías en dejar tus cosas para ir hacer las demás actividades.. No hay empresas profesionales.. Nadie habla inglés.. Por eso le pongo el 50/50 que debería poner menos pero es lindo lugar..

Our boat trip out into Halong Bay itself was really nice except for the huge amount of rubbish in the sea, presumably from the many many tour boats on the water. Ashore the area of the Halong Bay road where we stayed was very grotty. Except for the few 5 star hotels the area is really run down, cheap and tatty. There is a huge amount of building work going on to build 'Asiapark' a sort of theme park as I understand it. I'm sure it will bring in lots of tourists but will I fear be disastrous for the ecology in the area which was once of great beauty, very sad and disappointed

We went on a Ha Long Bay cruise with Paradise Cruises, that we booked with our Uniworld River Cruise.  I would not call this a luxury cruise at all. The ship was old. The rooms were large enough. Storage was adequate, however one of the closet doors wouldn't open because the bedside table blocked it. There was a balcony, however it took 2 people to open it. The shower did not work well. The water was not hot and it went off completely 3 times while I was trying to take a shower.The room was like an oven for a while until we finally got them to fix it.At dinner, beer and wine were included, but they wanted to charge me for a Diet Coke! Only after another person on our tour told them that she didn't have her included soft drink at lunch.  Pretty cheap of them!  Really, is one Diet Coke worth more than 2 beers???It just keeps getting better and better. For dinner, I ordered "fish and chips".  As is seen in the picture, I received some fish and that one little "chip".  After asking for the rest of the chips, I did get a portion of them.Definitely Do Not recommend this company!

Un paysage a couper le souffle, oui certes. Le seul hic, des centaines de bateaux qui circulent dans cette bay tous les jours, un nombre impressionnant de touristes, qui ne s'arrête jamais. Pour un site classé au Patrimoine mondial de l'UNESCO...entre l'odeur que dégage ces centaines bateaux, les plaques d'huile qui flottent a la surface de l'eau, les sacs plastiques, cigarettes et j'en passe...j'avais espéré bien plus de nature et de tranquillité. Je vous laisse imaginer dans quelques années le resultat sur cette magnifique bay d'halong!

C'est très joli, classé, incontournable,... C'est surtout un site pris en main et développé par des industriels du tourisme. Difficile d'échapper aux prestations standardisées des vendeurs de rêves et autres croisiéristes. J'ai nettement préféré la baie d'Halong terrestre, du côté de Tam Coc, qui offre tant de possibilités de découverte par soi-même.

This was probably the most highly talked about destination in Hanoi but for me it didn't live up to the hype.The area is quite beautiful but is a procession of people all doing the same tour.  Hundreds of people queuing through the cave, transferring to a kayak paddle, then to the island to walk up the hill, then off to the Pearl Farm etc etc.We were there three days two nights and it was a day too long

Unfortunately the expected natural beauty of Halong Bay, due to a complete lack of sustainability, is totally overshadowed by pollution and trash.The water is completely littered and you wont see a spot without oil. A Diesel engine smell is everywhere. Kayaking there was not bad, even though you will try your best not to touch the water. Thien Cung cave contains more concrete and untasteful lighting than actual natural formations. Since Vietnam doesnt appear to do anything to keep the bay clean and original, a daytrip here will be disappointing and probably sad seeing the human impact on this place.Perhaps the situation is better on a more-than-one-day-trip, where you have the chance to travel further into the area.

So upset! Had the worst time! Be very very careful when you book this. We paid for 3 days, 2 nights. Told you get a welcome drink, arrive and spend one night at the hotel on Cat Ba Island and one night on the boat. Arrive and got no welcome drink (not a biggy) had to buy own drinks. Went to Cat Ba Island with a few others who booked the same package. No communication about anything. Got taken to nature park in morning. Man pointed to hill to climb then were on our own. Got picked up late and dropped back at hotel - had to find own way to beach (no communication or signs). Back to boat in afternoon - INFESTED with cockroaches. They were in our bags, on our bed. Tiny babies crawling on us so had to sleep at the table and not our room. Told the man and listened then walked away. NO SLEEP at all. Just moored and didnt go anywhere then back to Hanoi in the morning. Didn't see ANYTHING that was advertised in the book (shown all these islands we go to - one by China). Can't get a refund for such poor service but can recommend none of you do this package. WASTE of MONEY and very upsetting start to our Vietnam trip!! 100Nz dollars down the drain and the most expensive part of our whole 2month trip.

Pour ne pas mourrir idiot on a prit un bateau 2 jours une nuit. (300  $  a 2). Un monde fou. Le tourisme de masse prend ici toute sa valeur.Le bateau au bout de 2h nous emmene dans une anse encombrée d'une centaine d'autres ( oir ma photo) et on admire le panorama. pour nous il est loin de valoir ceux de Thailande (maya bay,mer d'Endeman,jb island et j'en passe). ensuite on prend une barque qui nous emmene sur une plage minuscule avec toujours autant de touristes limite bousculades,pas besoin de vous dire qu'il est impossible de se baigner. Puis visite d"une grotte et retour au bateau. Le lendemain tai chi et rentree a Hanoi 10 h de route infernale aller retour.Bon voyage...

La Baie d'Halong est certes un endroit magique mais la contrariété commence dès que l'on pose les pieds à Halong city. Si vous prenez un bus à Hanoi, il y a de fortes chances pour que vous soyez laissé à quelques kilomètres de la ville à un croisement au milieu de rien où vous attendent des taxis. Ce n'est pas la gare de bus, c'est juste un croisement où taxis d'Halong et chauffeurs de bus se sont mis d'accord pour débarquer les touristes voyageant seuls et pour se faire un peu d'argent sur leur dos. C'est vraiment nul car Halong est une attraction touristique majeure et se faire accueillir par une petite escroquerie ne donne pas une bonne image du Vietnam. La ville d'Halong (deux villes en fait) est vraiment laide: le front de mer n'est pas accessible, il y a une sorte de Parc d'attraction qui empêche toute visibilité sur la Baie, c'est vraiment un triste spectacle. L'urbanisme sauvage fait d'Halong City une ville salle, poussiéreuse, des chantiers partout. Non vraiment, Halong city n'est pas une belle ville. Reste l'attraction de la Baie, c'est très millimétré, très bien fichu mais c'est organisé pour du tourisme de masse et franchement, ce n'est pas ma tasse de thé. Sans doute ma plus grande déception au Vietnam... mais heureusement, il y a Cat Ba et ça, ça m'a plu....

We spent an hr picking up passengers and only left Hanoi at 9am. The first passenger got on at 7am. Only arrived at Halong bay at 1.30pm after a very slw drive. The so called seafood lunch was pretty  average and mostly cold. The ride on the raft/kayak was expensive. Tour guide only paid 30000 dong for each person but he  collected 1300dong for each of us who were going. What a ripoff.. Got back to Hanoi at 9.30pm.

Yes its a nice place to visit, but go to any island in the vicinity of Phuket, Thailand and you will encounter just as much beauty over a more vast area, without being cramped together with hundreds of other tourists.  The guide said that every day over 300 tourist boats traverse the bay, and I believe it!!!

Failed all hope and expectations. Privately chartered a boat. Delayed by half hour setting to sea as captain wanted "breakfast" (most people eat before they go to work !!!) Then throughout the trip we were given the full on hard sell of rubbish "craft souvenirs" by the cabin hostess. Whilst the bay is marvelous; and in itself, well worth seeing, the commercialism of the boat owners/operators leaves a bit to be desired. Finally, and without consulting us, the captain took us back half an hour earlier than scheduled (guess he probably wanted lunch !!!!!!!) You may ask "did we remonstrate with him ?" short answer... couldn't be bothered; as we were just glad get off. Shame really.

Area is really beautiful but you can hardly see anything expect loads of boats and tourists.. I planned a three day tour at the end of our Vietnam travels hoping tp save the best for last...this was not the case. End of the day even when you go on a more day trip you will sail about 4-5 hours and will spent the night amongst a dozen other ships packed with tourists. Truly dissapointed and man do I feel robbed by the cruise company...

Nous sommes passés par une agence estampillé TripAdvisor qui était visiblement un faux et un attrape touriste. Plein de de belles photos de paysages mais à part les guides qu'on a eu qui était super ! Le service dans le bateau était lamentable, le bateau était lamentable, une odeur d'excréments dans la salle de bain. En discutant avec chaque personne, on a tous payé un prix différent. Nous devons dormir dans un hôtel 2 étoiles sur cat ba ! Et nous avons été placé dans une auberge de jeunesse... Vérifiez bien les compagnies avant de vous lancer ! Le service a bord du bateau était si rapide qu'on avait le dessert sur la table avant avoir fini le plat. Sans compter qu'ils enlevaient les assiettes avant qu'on ai fini ! Et tout ça sans aucun sourire mis à part quand elle comptait ses billets ! Lors d'une escale à vélo (qui était sympa) l'équipage ou je ne sais qui ont fouillé dans les sacs à dos (nous avions pris des photos de comment était placés les sacs) et certains n'avaient pas été refermés ! LAMENTABLE ! assurez vous payer votre voyage un peu plus cher pour ne pas avoir ce genre de surprise ! L'endroit est beau, et ces gens là ne nous font pas passer un bon moment ! Sans compter un retard de deux heures au départ... On nous a fait faire du canoë à 6h du matin sans petit déjeuner parce qu'il n'avait pas pu le faire le veille ! Bref essayons de ne retenir que le paysage !

The first thing that hits you when arriving at Halong Bay Harbour is the number of boats moored. There are hundreds of small cruise ships, shuttle boats and tenders. And the dozens of tour groups, those waiting for transfer to their cruisers and those recently returned to shore waiting for bus transfer to Hanoi. At any one time there must be several thousand people on and around Halong Bay and the ‘experience’ is suffering due to the pressure.Crowds at the very average, gaudily lit ‘cave’ were so great, the line of visitors stretched from its entrance to exit, resulting in late arrival at our ‘kayaking experience’ where my fellow travellers spent maybe 20 minutes (out of an advertised 45 minutes) in a dilapidated vessel paddling in circles as darkness enveloped the bay. A tacky ‘add-on’ I preferred not to participate in while the ‘cave tour’ was basically a walk through with no commentary.A visit to the ‘Pearl Farm’ was educational however but an advertised ‘Floating Village visit’ was in fact a ‘sail by’ at four hundred meters. The karaoke machine on our vessel was broken (thank god), the advertised free beer after dinner was a 20 litre keg of foam that tasted of old socks, drinks from the bar were 5 star prices and the cabins were nothing like those depicted by the operator who booked our tour and this confirmed for me the ‘Vietnam accommodation scam’ has taken to the water.Like many hotel rooms depicted in on-line booking sites, it would seem the tour boat operators have jumped on the band wagon. While waiting for my transfer back to Hanoi (forty minutes), I tried my best not to sound like an investigative reporter and asked eight people from eight other waiting groups if their cabin resembled that depicted in their booking agent’s spiel. Unsurprisingly not one person responded in the affirmative.While generally ambivalent about their Halong Bay ‘experience’, everyone I spoke to was disappointed with their cabin and the quality of ‘attractions’ offered but happy with their meals and the photo ops of sailing around the bay. From my perspective the time spent driving to Halong Bay from Hanoi and back along with seemingly disorganised shore transfers is frustratingly a wasted half day in a 2 day/1 night excursion (the most frequently taken option). The only alternative to this of course is to stay at a Halong Bay hotel, but even then the departure time of your cruise will probably be dictated by those travelling from Hanoi.The Bay itself is spectacular of course and the water unexpectedly clean although some garbage is evident and one vessel I saw was billowing so much diesel fumes a fire tender was sent from the harbour to investigate, but I’m voting here on my nuts and bolts experience as a visitor, not making a judgement on Halong Bay’s World Heritage credentials. My overall experience was underwhelming and I wouldn’t recommend this site, especially to travellers who might be ‘time poor’.I would say the time required to properly and thoroughly research multiple cruise companies and their multifarious and confusing (misleading) products to attain a high level of satisfaction is not worth the effort.

Halong Bay was something I built a lot of excitement over. However, the postcard perfect pictures should be retaken with the smog, vessel fumes & thin layer of oily pollution on the water.It was so sad to see. The ocean has no sparkle left. There was 'western' rubbish floating in the water & it was packed to the gunnels with junk boats & shuttles all spewing grey diesel fumes.The solitary fishing village is struggling to survive & there is very little bird life.Swimming in the bay, we could taste the pollution on our lips & feel it on our skin.For thousands of years Halong Bay was home to 3500 fishing families who were asked to leave & displaced in order to make way for tourism. Uneducated, many of them could not find jobs in the city. It ruined an ancient way of life... And ruined this natural wonder.The limestone outcrops were still beautiful. But I left feeling sad. It's.  'world heritage listing' is an absolute farce.... as it won't be 'inherited' by anyone. It's ecosystem & majesty will be dead for future generations without dramatic/harsh change that sacrifices much of the tourists $
have been and seen better  bit of a con with the boats and just not that thrilling. no wildlife not even sea birds.

Halong bay was not how we expected. We have been told by friends and family to visit this famous attraction in Vietnam but were unfortunately disappointed. It's very built up and tacky, lots of large concrete hotels and flashing neon signs. Sadly this is what tourism has done to the place but at the same time it is not tourist friendly at all. There are many hotels but hardly any cafes or restaurants. You are totally dependant on tours and taxis etc you can not really just go explore the bay by yourself as it is difficult to get to and you can't rent your own boat ecerything has to be done through tour and costs money. We booked a four boat tour that was supposed to depart at 12 but didn't leave till 1.45, the staff were rude and pushy and whilst the views were nice it wasn't a particularly enjoyable experience. If you've got the time and money, I recommend doing research and booking a 2 night three day tour of the bay where you can get picked up from Hanoi and they cook for you on boat and take you places. It costs more but seems the best way to do it. If like us you have limited money and limited time, I would honestly say pass on Halong bay. Each to their own but we didn't really enjoy it, bay itself was fairly pretty but Halong and surrounding city like Benidorm without the bars. Concrete and tacky

A money making exercise. Other than 40 minutes Kayaking we did not receive anything we had paid for. Buyer beware. Booking Agents show you brochures and give you pamphlets of your cruise but all you receive is a small excursion on a badly maintained boat. Same food dished up midday and evening. Grosse. Dirty Bed Linen, Next to no running water . a dribble of water out of the shower. Booked for 2 nights on boat and got 1.Tourism here is a RIP OFF. Take money. Don't deliver.

Halong bay is picture-postcard beautiful, as depicted in all travel brochures. But, before scheduling a trip you may want to consider the following:- as Vietnam's number one destination, it is crowded and busy! We were told that up to 600 cruise boats can be found on the bay. - except for sailing around the islets there isn't much else to do. - the majority of the tours will take you to a cave and an observation point, requiring to climb stairs and have a certain physical fitness. - there is heavy traffic not only of touristic cruises, but also commercial and heavy-load ships. We did see some pollution (plastic bottles, cans, Styrofoam, paper,...) on the water, but our biggest concern are the ships and the waste they dump in the waters.- there is a sandy beach on Titop island, in which people do swim. The water looks clean, but when we left we had oil/fuel residues on our skin. Other tourists complained about the same.- you can actually see the same karstic formations in Thailand. Of course, not thousands like in Halong bay, but they do exist in the cost of the Andaman Sea.- it takes 6-7 h (return) by bus from Hanoi to Halong bay.Bottom-line: we do not recommend to visit Halong bay. At least, not until the authorities will regulate the amount of cruises and the traffic within the bay. A serious effort should also be put to clean the waters and the islands.

The Ha Long bay starts to have a serious pollution problem. We were cruising, swimming and kayaking through large amounts of floating cans, plastic bags, foam, and other garbage. Get his environment cleaning program working otherwise this area will go towards a sad ending....

The scenery is great but there are way too many huge boats with loads of tourists. It just doesn't feel right anymore. And the way they treat you at the port to make you pay as much as possible just because you are a foreigner is a put-off. If you have time, go ahead, but if not, I would rather recommend you to visit Ninh Binh instead.

Nice looking, but too polluted, too many boats, too many people, a real tourist trap. Shuffle them in and shuffle them out – a production line.I can see how chartering your own boat and go at your own leisure, maybe stay out 2 or 3 night, could be relaxing, but taking a tourist boat out for 1 night of eating and drinking, is a total waste of money and just contributes to the pollution of the bay.

I don't know where some of these folks went in HaLong that they were not absolutely horrified by the environmental impact tourism has had on this natural wonder. There was a continual flow of plastic and styrofoam  passing by our vessel the entire trip. There are nearly 600 cruise ships running trips continually in just a small area . Adventure? Kayaking and caving . Each fleet of 5-6 large tourist boats has 45 minutes at the various attractions. You are shuffled thru like cattle with masses of other tourists. Very sad to see this magnificent place distroyed.

Ha Long bay is unique and beautiful, if a bit Disney-like. Getting to and from Ha Long from Hanoi, on the other hand, is a horrible experience. The only way to get there is by tour bus or private car, and the traffic is awful. It took a very long time to get there, and it took even longer to get back. Along the way in each direction, you will stop at tourist markets selling everything you can imagine at wildly inflated prices. If you decide to go, I recommend that you plan on spending the night in Ha Long rather than endure the five-hour bus trip back to Hanoi. If you decide to suffer through the bus trip, be sure that you take food and drink along because you are going to get back to Hanoi pretty late in the evening. You also can buy food in the tourist markets on the way, but the prices are very high and the lines to get food are incredible. Your tour bus will not be the only bus there. Your hotel probably will try to sell you the tour when you check in, but tours are offered everywhere and you're going to end up on the same or similar bus whether you buy outside your hotel or pay the small premium to buy the tour through your hotel. Upon arrival at Ha Long bay, you will be ushered into a large "terminal" with absolutely disgusting restrooms. The boats are on the other side of the terminal. Lunch was provided on the boat as it sailed toward the islands, and it was pretty good if you like seafood. If you don't like seafood, you're likely to be hungry at the end. Bring money for drinks.All of the tour boats apparently follow the exact same path through the islands. The first stop is at a dock out by the islands where you will be given the option of a boat ride through some of the islands or a kayak you can use to follow the same route as the boats. There are so many boats and kayaks, all following the same basic route, that you will think you are on one of the rides at Disneyland. After that, you will re-board your boat and it will circle around some of the islands and stop at one of the islands that has a cavern. The cavern itself is alright, but it has lights focused on various parts of the cavern and your guide will point out how some of the outcroppings resemble animals and other shapes as you mingle with crowds approximating Times Square on New Year's Eve. Then it's back to the bus for the long, long trip back to Hanoi. It took us five hours to get back. I asked one of the guides if the traffic was unusual, and he assured me that it was that way every day.

The views are amazing don't get me wrong. Look at any photo and you'll see it is a beautiful place with stunning sights.My negatives with this are very long though:- Did not get the boat and package that I paid for- Booked for 2 nights on 1 boat ended up switching to 3 boats over 2 days (not told about this)- Horribly overpriced drinks - Adamant guides on doing all the planned activities, even if you already said you didn't want to, or that you had done them already- Lots of waiting around, doing nothing whilst waiting for boat transfers or other guests- Incredibly busy, lucky to get a photo without another boat in it- Staff and guides on all boats not very helpful with the problems nor did they care.Those were some of the negatives bullet pointed, didn't want to spend too much time talking about it because pretty much every other person we met on this trip had the same stuff happen to them.Customers just treated like cattle.We came, we saw the bay, won't be back again.

We did a 3d2n cruise which was good and have reviewed that elsewhere. Halong Bay itself is breathtaking in its beauty and I simply loved cruising around the bay and took oodles of photos. However, I was simply horrified at the amount of floating rubbish. This is a UNESCO site for heavens sake!! I think the staff of the many many cruising boats could help pick out this rubbish. I collected some when kayaking. It really really upset me. It's so unnecessary and utterly spoils an international heritage site that should be preserved for all. More needs to be done to deal with the issue of rubbish in the bay. The Vietnamese Govt need to address this issue before it's too late.....

Everything you hear about Halong Bay is true except for the fact that the water is heavily polluted.  After visiting the Whitsunday Island in Australia and their crystal clear blue waters I was expecting a similar experience wit the emerald green waters of Halong Bay.  Not so.  The water was murky and the amount of floating rubbish was astonishing.  Looking after the environment hasn't become a priority of the Vietnaese Governent at this stage.

Having spent several days on Cat Ba, we had a marvellous time exploring the serene beauty of Lan Ha Bay by private boat and kayak. Departing to Ninh Binh we decided to travel through Halong Bay to see what all the fuss is about and hitched a ride on a medium sized cruise boat. Having crossed our beloved Lan Ha Bay once more we finally got into Halong Bay proper and couldn’t believe our eyes, it looked like a naval exercise of the Spanish Armada. Even in February there must have been more than a thousand vessels of all sizes and shapes out on the water and it was virtually impossible to take a picture of the amazing rocks without any boat on it. The stupendous amount of garbage floating in the oil stained waters didn’t help to improve the scene either.If you absolutely want to take a cruise, by all means do, but avoid the cheap short trips out of Halong City which will invariably take you to this spoiled touristy part of the bay. It is definitely better to invest in a multiday cruise that includes more natural, beautiful and tranquil areas like Lan Ha or Bai Tu Long Bay.

We were so looking forward to our trip to Halong bay onto Bai Tu Long Bay which are apparently considered by the Vietnamese to be heaven on earth. A peaceful and serene two days cruising around the thousands of extraordinary shaped limestone islands would be the perfect end to our holiday in Vietnam. I can only say that the Vietnamese are very easily pleased.  The bay is crowded with hundreds of tour boats each day that discharge their rubbish into the bay. Add to this the noise and disruption of the numerous speed boats and the whole area has an air of tourist tack.  Most of the tour boats have snorkelling and swimming on their itinerary but you put your health in your hands if you go in the water.  It is such a shame that a once beautiful bay has been reduced to no more than one large floating rubbish dump. Tourists pay a lot of money to the boat tour companies who should in return be held accountable for the damage they are doing to the bay.

The bays nice limestone formations is great, but only on a clear day, avoid visiting in winter, and becareful on the boats, you sometimes need to jump off at its stops, pet alone some have gone up in flames. It is important to know that Quang Ninh province is the coal mining province, the run off pollutes the bay, and I dont advise eating the coal infested seafood no matter what local tells you. Ha long loves ripping off tourist, and hotels in the city surrounded by prostitution. I visit Ha Long often to work, so Ive seen it all. Also Bay Chai is u der construction for a long time for the new vincom group resort, expect to be dissapointed if staying there, especially at Novotel. Other than the boat rides on the bay which are risky, there aint much more to do. Enjoy your 3-4hr drive from hanoi, its a killer.

Maybe it was the 4 hour drive from Hanoi, but I just don't get it.   This is not an easy four hour drive, mind you.  This is a four hour drive (each WAY) over pot holes, in and our of small towns, one lane "highways" and around scooters.   Sure, once we go there it was nice, but not really in an awe inspiring kind of way.  Plus, the hundreds of other boat combined with the level of pollution in the water offsets any positives.  There is literally trash floating all around and the smell of diesel fuel is everywhere.  And, all the busses also make stops at the nauseating tourist death traps lining the road all the way from Hanoi to Hoi An. At one point I became convinced that Hoi An was moved further away from Hanoi just to accommodate more tourist stops.  If you have a week in Hanoi, maybe.  Otherwise, pass.

Я хочу оставить возмущенный отзыв оттого, что я не смогла посетить данное место из-за слишком высокой цены! И главное - я совершенно НИГДЕ не могла найти сколько стоит данная экскурсия. Знала только, что ее можно купить из Ханой, Хошимин и Нячанга. Я отдыхала в Нячанге и намеревалась оттуда ее посетить. Узнав цену в 650$ (и то это только если наберется большая группа) и за 800-850$, если полечу одна на двухдневную экскурсию  с ночевкой на корабле. Потом хотела поехать сама в Хошимин, оттуда полететь в Ханой (так как это самый недорогой вариант перелета был - порядка 100-150$), но и из Ханой однодневная экскурсия стоит 250-300$+ еще нужен отель минимум на 2-3 дня (день прилета, день экскурсии и день вылета). То есть то на то и выходит плюс дорога. Дорога от Нячанга до Хошимин занимает 9-10 часов на автобусе. Дорога из Ханой до бухты Халонг 4-6 часов. То есть это СЛИШКОМ МНОГО ВРЕМЕНИ И ДЕНЕГ уйдет на посещение данной бухты! Специально пишу свой отзыв, чтобы люди ЗНАЛИ и планировали свой отдых, так как я из-за незнания многих вещей (например о длительных переездах и ценах на автобусы и экскурсии - потеряла ОЧЕНЬ много времени в дорогах и потратила ОЧЕНЬ много денег (в дешевой ранее Азии, а сейчас очень даже не дешевой) во время своего путешествия из Тайланда в Камбоджу и Вьетнам. В итоге из того, что хотела посмотреть - увидела только Ангор-Ватт в Камбодже, а в бухту Халонг так и не попала! Жаль:(( Считаю, что это слишком разрекламированная и необоснованно дорогая экскурсия! Не смотря на обилие предложений с кораблями от простых до шикарных 5-звездочных яхт со "всем включено". Всем удачных поездок и путешествий! Приятных, новых впечатлений и побольше информации для планирования этих самых поездок и путешествий)))

We went on 1 night boat tour of Ha Long Bay. It's easy to see why this was named a wonder of the world and it is absolutely stunning (one of the most amazing natural beauties I've seen) IF you can install a filter from all the rubbish and pollution around you. To be honest I was heartbroken to see what state Ha Long bay is in. I will not exaggerate if I say that it was at times impossible to find a patch of clean water and fresh air. The level of pollution around is staggering. Tons of plastic in the water, smell of fuel in the air because you are constantly surrounded by about a hundred other boats. Our captain said that there are 600 boats leaving each day just from that harbour, many staying overnight running generators for the power/air con and therefore constantly adding pollution. I felt bad because I was contributing to this by being on that boat overnight. I couldn't enjoy it due to all above and wouldn't do it again. I will also write a letter to the ministry of tourism of Vietnam and Unesco asking them to take action to protect Ha Long Bay for the future generations.

I went to Halong bay as it is one of the new 7 wonders of the world for nature, but to be honest, after 4 hours on the tour, I had seen enough. The best part of this tour was going into the caves to see the limestone rock formations, they were so interesting and beautiful! But the rocks and islands in the Philippines and Thailand are just as, or even more so stunning. Maybe it is because we went on an overcast day, or maybe our boat didn't get close enough to the rocks, or maybe its because I only did a day tour? But even the water is just so murky, i wouldn't dare put my feet in the water! It just doesn't compare to Thailand or the Philippines water.

Crew and service on boat was excellent, food pretty good, but having paid over $900 (inc. transfers) for less than 24 hours (2 'days'/1 night), I thought that this price was remarkably excessive, and didn't even include complimentary beer and wine. They also mark up the booze price considerably compared to being on shore.Things that I can't really blame HL for, but potential customers should be aware of, are the potential for grim haze/air pollution, the (literally) hundreds of other operators' vessels that will shadow your every move, litter in the bay and visible on the high tide line of many of the islands, the very tired excursions to the 'most beautiful' Titop Island (fake beach, litter, jellyfish and tat for sale), a fishing village and some underwhelming cave or other.

This was supposed to be the highlight of my trip to Vietnam. It IS a gorgeous place... but between the HUGE amounts of trash in the water (seriously sickening amounts) and the massive air pollution (coal factories dot the shore line), it hardly felt like a natural paradise.  I hope the Vietnamese can get a handle on what's going on and clean this place up or it's going to be ruined forever.

A lovely bay that in my eyes will be ruined if it isn't sorted out soon. I found that they have way to many boats out there most pumping out heaps of fumes and the water has a slick of oil and diesel over it. Rubbish floats as the tide moves around. There where a few boat a saw trying to collect rubbish but it's coming from the rivers that lead out from the towns so it's hard to control until they sort out their littering problems around the country. Beautiful place just afraid it will end up ruined by the tourism trade and pollution. Oyster farms had slick of oil over the top of water and a large dead fish floating in it. That's saying something about the water I think. Hope it gets better soon

Disappointing as I expect to see what I see or at least close to the photos found online. Was told that the scenery will be much better in summer. The visit also includes a visit to a lime stone cave. Personally, nothing to shout about.

Никогда не посещайте бухту весной. Весной тут дождливая и туманная погода, хотя прогноз говорил обратное. Сама природа в бухте очень интересная, но грязь страшная. Никогда не покупайте туры 3 звезды - сервис отвратительный, отношение цена качества не соответствует, отношение персонала не удовлетворительное.

es una maravilla moderna. el lugar es impresionante! te deja con la boca abierta! es muy hermoso. pero esta muy mal cuidado.los locales y algunos turistas son los qeu ensucian, no cuidan lo que tienen. basura por todos lados. es patrimonio de la humanidad y la unesco deberia incorporar o hacer que se cumplan las normas de limpieza. sino, este lugar va a ser un basural dentro de algunas decadas. los barcos que recorren la bahia desprenden mucho smog y contminan el agua, hay basura por todos lados (tanto en el agua como en tierra) :bolsas de nylon, botellas de plastico, papeles. por esto le puse dos estrellas. uno como turista es cuidadoso y da bronca q un lugar tan hermoso como este este tan mal cuidado,

If you have travelled lots of South east Asia then halong bay will not be anything special to you. Although it is beautiful there is not much of activities to do while you are there. Our boat took us to see some caves and it was unimpressive. Our tour guide knew very little about the cave system and instead showed us rock formations and what animals they looked like.The drive to get to Halong Bay is 4 1/2 hours. For what you get out of the halong bay experience it really isn't worth it. Wish I hadn't wasted my time with this part of the trip.

We did an overnight cruise in January of 2010 and the bay was beautiful.  We were there again in Feb 2016 for an afternoon cruise and were dismayed by the amount of garbage in the water.  At one point we actually saw pieces of furniture floating by.  This on top of the plastic bags and styrofoam and God knows what else.  The Bay itself is an amazing natural wonder, but I can't say I would recommend this to friends anymore.  It's a shame because this could be a truly magical place to visit.

Bei schönen Wetter eventuell besser zu genießen. Bei Regen war die Stimmung von Besuchern und Crew eher mies. Aktivitäten machen keinen Spaß. Durch die Lautstärke auf dem Schiff (nicht nur durch die militärisch anmutenden Durchsagen) war auch ein Entspannen in der Kabine kaum möglich. Das war definitiv nicht der Höhepunkt meiner Vietnamreise, wie vielfach postuliert wird.

A 4 hour drive along pot holed roads at high speed was not a good start to the day. 1 comfort stop at a tourist trap on the way was nothing special. By the time we waited for other groups to arrive it was 2.30 pm before the boat left the harbour and 3pm before we were served a very sub standard lunch. A short way to the first island cave then in a constant line of people hustled through the cave. This was followed by a 1/2 hour round the island before back to the harbour and the 4 hour reverse experience home. This is a magnificent beautiful world heritage site being prostituted by tourism

Full of boats on arrival makes sea very dirty and you can see oil patches. Caves is nothins special as is lit up in colors that makes it look like christmas tree. On the way out theres a lot of souvenir shops. People pay something like 35usd for a return trip from hanoi. Not worth it imo. If you like caves go see paradise cave.

Ha long bay is a beautiful place, but it is very hard to appreciate when it is ran so shabbily and disorganized. The nature was beautiful, the water is very polluted and the green hue comes from an invasive algae. We did just a 4 hour cruise to cat ba island. You are mixed in with people doing overnight stays on the boat and they are definitely their top priority. You are better off just taking the ferry direct and avoiding this cluster completely.

Infelizmente, é complicado acessar esse paraíso por conta própria. Há máfias de  taxistas e minivans na rodoviária para extorquir os turistas e impedi-los de usar o onibus local para chegar ao centro. Com a mudança do pier, boa parte da orla está coberta por tapumes (enfeitados com imagens photoshopadas da baía). Uma tristeza!
Не понимаю восторга многих, возможно они мало повидали
Definitely missable
Beau mais outrageusement pollué!
Where is the nature
Crowded & Poluted
Just do your research beforehand
Imbarcazione un pò vecchiotta
Halong Bay: Gran decepción y mucha contaminación
NO VOLVERIA  NUNCA - ESTAFA TOTAL
un flot de touristes qui empeche de réellement profiter du lieu
There is the opportunity for Halong Bay to be picturesque.  If it wasn't for the garbage littering the water and the beaches it could be a stunning natural sight.  Very disappointing.

The view of the Bay was terrific, and I do enjoy the scenery when crusing around the bay. However, the people and service were just terrible, immediately they know I come from the Southern part, they charged me double and try to sell me things that I dont need. I mean it's common in Vietnam but the case in Ha Long is quite extreme. I've been here once for the experience, but wouldnt recommend to go back.

Halong was polluted, busy and just horrid! I was not a fan. It's not as peaceful as you imagine thanks to the hundreds of boats all over the bay.... I would give it a miss. I would really suggest you go to Cat Ba Island and find a boat tour there for Lan Ha Bay. We ended up on a fishing boat and the bay was beautiful! It looks like what Halong Bay clearly was 20 years ago. Lan Ha Bay is just on the other side of Halong and a lot of multiple day tours from Halong will end up here to see the natural beauty of this island.It's untouched and the landscape is just beautiful- we even visited some white sandy beaches! And Cat Ba Island is so peaceful and it just takes your breath away. I really fell in love so have visited as much as I could whilst I lived in Vietnam!

I had high expectations after reading the reviews about Huong Hai Junk's. I expected luxury accommodation and exceptional food. The reality is, the boat is old (Huong Hai 16) and looks old. The cabins are small, the bed is against on wall and the person on that side of the bed has to crawl over the bed to get out. The bathroom is compact, I guess you expect that on a boat but if you want a shower whilst sitting on the toilet then this is ideal. The food was generally ordinary, with the exception of the welcoming seafood lunch the rest of the meals were western and not very good western. Why not have good Vietnamise food? We are in Vietnam after all.All drinks are charged for, water, tea, coffee, beer and wine. We were warned before hand about the wine so we brought our own, the cost of the wine on board was around $35USD for the cheapest. As it was they charged us $7 USD corkage for our own wine. Beer was 60,000 dong. About 4 times the cost from a local shop. I guess when you are a captive audience they can charge whatever they like. Following the advice of previous reviewers we booked 2 nights, on reflection one was ample. The scenery and the tour was good, our only regret was we used this company. I feel let down that a once in a lifetime trip to Halong bay was spoilt by a sub-standard boat and ordinary food.

so much for a UNESCO world heritage site. well it is but you just cant see it for the pollution. the local businesses even throw their rubbish straight into the water. it made me angry coming here to see that there is no respect for the environment what so ever. seriously the water tastes like fuel from the thousands of boats that will share the area with yours. it was a joke (a sick joke) going there. we were disgusted. we went there with papaya cruises which was overpriced and the 'tour guide'was very rude and very unacommodating. the so called kayaking and visit to the pearl farm were a total inconvenience to him and he let us all know it. there was no water provided and the meals were sparse with no option even to get a cup of tea (well i did in the end but not without a very heated discussion). everyone on the tour had paid a different price. no one was állowed 'to go swimming off the boat. we were seriously disappointed. the visit to the cave was rushed as was every other áttraction' on the tour. there are even billboards up on some of the smaller islands (i guess so you can get an idea of what the place would look like without the smog). UNESCO needs to seriously consider making these people accountable for what is happening to this (once) beautiful part of our world. i would like people to boycott this place until something is done about it.

We booked a two night cruise to Halong Bay. One night on the white pearl cruise boat and the other at Sunflower Two Hotel. The cruise was terrible and I wish I hadnt done it. All the boats look the same. No brown indochina junk exists. People had all paid different prices for their cruises which was the same. We had to pay extra for the kayaking which was fine and probably the highlight of the cruise. Apart from the cave and the kayaking the tour company did not stick to anything on the iltenary. The rooms on the boats looked nice and clean. The beds had terrible springs so got no sleep. In the mornng found out there had been cockroaches and rats in other peoples rooms. There was also a terrible leak in another room. Food was rediculous and only a small amount to be shared consisting of rice and everything you can possibly do with cucumber. There is no safety when getting on or off the boat and you have to walk along a plank of narrow wood. The sunflower two hotel as part of our package was worser.Halong bay was nice but I would suggest maybe do a day trip or do it yourself. Dont waste your money booking a cruise as it really was not worth it.

The first impression of the Halong Violet is that you have entered the world of Hercule Poirot and the Orient Express.  The Boat only has 6 luxury spacious cabins offering luxury and glamorous accommodation.  The experience is magical and the scenery is gorgeous.The negative: the staff are under the impression the highlight of the trip is the cuisine they offer.. and yes the food was very nice.  However the principle reason for this trip is to admire the spectacular scenery from the outside (but covered deck) not from the indoor restaurant.  So the out bound journey was spent having lunch & return journey was spent indoors having brunch I did ask to have brunch on the spacious deck but this was ignored.  To ad insult to injury when we arrived back in port we stayed about 1hr docked before disembarkation.. could we have not spent more time where the scenery was gorgeous instead of in port?  all in all I would go back.. stay 2 nights not one and insist I am served food where I want & not where the staff want.  I consider that the staff ruined my trip.

Jolie paysage à voir! Mais dommage qu'on essaie de vous arnaquer en permanence ....Les proprietaires des bateaux qu'ils essaient de vous vendre les billets à 150 000 vdn les tickets alors qu'ils sont à 100 000. Vous dises qu'ils n'ont pas de bateau à louer à leur sauf quand vous allez au bureau officiel ... Et vous font la gueule quand vous prenez un bateau à l'heure pour 4H soit 1 600 000 vdn car ils auraient pu mettre 50 personnes à 100 000 vdn par personne ... Et le parcours en faite ne dure que 2H alors que vous avez loué le bateau pour 4h .... Sinon à part, cela le paysage est jolie dommage qu'ils ont pollué la region avec cette surexploitation touristique. Personnellement je prefere les iles panga en thaillande

Halong Bay is an okay tourist destination, but it wasn't impressive at all. The water wasn't clear, you can see trash floating every now and then. The rock formations were okay. The beach we went to was a small strip of not-so-fine sand, with a small cordoned area to swim in (in not-so-clear seawater either). We had a one day cruise, so I'm not sure maybe longer cruises get to better parts/beaches of Halong.We loved the food and service by V Spirit Cruise, though. :)If you want a similar attraction, I suggest you go to El Nido or Coron in Palawan, Philippines. I bet all my travel fund you wouldn't be disappointed. Wrote a pretty neutral post about our Halong Bay experience my blog | http://roanniduranni.blogspot.com/2014/04/indochina-2014-cruising-along-halong.html

My husband and I are on our second trip around the world and we visited HaLong Bay along the way. We did a 2 night private junk boat cruise with one of the popular local co. (Indochina Junk). The Bay Area looks very beautiful, from far that is. This is a very polluted area and it's been almost destroyed by the locals. (What a shame!!)We are 2 very active individuals and we love kayaking, paddle boarding, swimming, etc. but after our first kayaking experience we did not want to get in the water again. That means we stayed the whole time in the boat (other than when we went to a small beach to eat a BBQ prepared by our boat chef). Pros:- Beautiful pictures for your facebook page! The rock formations are very pretty and Boat captains will open up the sails for you to take nice pics (they us motor so sails are for show)- the food our chef made us was wonderful. We got to try lots of sea foods (have you ever had Mantis Shrimp? We have!)- indochina owns a piece of land in one of the rock formations. There is a nice cave in it to visit (above the little beach area where we had BBQ)- No time to waste. All is done very automated so you don't really wait around when you check in or out (they swipe that credit card faster than light speed) Cons:- 4 hour car ride though Vietnamese traffic. - Trash pollution destroyed the bay. Among many things floating in the water we saw condoms, syringes, tons of bottles, plastic bags, styrofoam, etc. so we did not want to get in the water which made our 3 day stay VERY LONG. - They took us to a "Fishing Village". The truth is no one lives there (on the floating houses made by the government and given to the Viet for tourist to see ) permanently and the little school they have next to the "museum" is fake! (They even left a few books opened on the little benches to pretend kids go there!- Cleanliness and boat upkeep could be improved. While our sheets and towels were perfectly white we noticed finger marks all throughout the boat. And the boats are just old so mold and wear is very apparent. (We got one of the most "luxurious" boats too so that's the best to expect)

I have a mixed reaction to Halong Bay.  The bay has a lot of trash, most tour operators seem to work from a pretty over-scheduled itinerary that isn't very flexible and full of gimmicks (the one hour squid fishing was a joke), and it's hard to picture what goes on here other than tourism. If you want the canned tourist experience, then tours of Halong Bay probably work fine.  If you want something more unique, you'll have to spend more than a few nights so you have the time to get out to some of the more remote islands. Be sure to ask how many boats (estimate) are likely to be anchored near yours. There are hundreds and hundreds, and some parts of the bay turn into party karoake scenes at night.  Would I go back? No.  But that's maybe easier to stay now that I've been there.  Overall, I was disappointed, by how dirty the bay was and how canned and over-scheduled all tours seem to be.

First, Halong Bay is beautiful. The rocks, landscape and scenery is simply stunning. The bay itself is quite trashy and dirty. Luckily it was too cold to swim, because I would not have wanted to get in the water give its dirty state.We booked a 3 day/2 night cruise for $130. This was quite high for Vietnam, so we were expecting something a lot better than what we actually got. The boat itself was a bit sketchy but the rooms were nice. the first day went okay with great scenery, and a visit to the caves and kayaking. It ended with a forced "dance party" with a few hours of free beer, where one of the guides made up a drinking game that involved making out with him - which was beyond sketchy. The next morning we were immediately told to pack our things, but not told we had an event at 9am, meaning the boat nearly left part of my party behind because no times were discussed. After coming back from the oyster farm, we basically sat around until another boat came that was supposed to transport us to the "island" we were staying for the night. The boat arrived, we picked up some new people, lost a few of the people on our boat, and headed to a national park. Before we arrived we were told bikes for the park were $5 USD, which we paid prior to leaving the boat, but I did not see our guide - Frank - pay anyone, leading the entire boat to believe he pocketed the money. Before we left the boat Frank told us to take our valuables with us, which most people did. We then spent about two hours in the park, and when we returned all of our bags had been searched through. While we were biking the park, the crew went through all of our bags and STOLE MONEY. The bags looked like they had been ransacked and money had been removed from places people had "stashed" some of their money (so they weren't carrying it all on themselves). Frank then said that it didn't happen and that we must have lost the money.We we finally arrived at the island, after the bag-searching fight, we were then taken to our "bungalows" which happened to be "full" therefore we were told to stay in a 30-person dorm. This was exactly the opposite of what we had been told, since we were expecting a private room. After nearly two hours of fighting with Frank, we finally got some of the money back, and moved to a hotel with single rooms. The hotel was bad, but better than the dorm for sure. After this event, our tour guide Frank, became extremely hostile and yelled at us nearly every time we interacted. It was a quick 180 and an unwelcome change in his personality, especially since we were forced to spend time with him on the boat. I wish he could have been consistently nice and supportive throughout the event rather than getting hostile for no apparent reason (even prior to the money-stealing incident). Overall, this company is no good. They are not the "booking" agents, but rather the group that implements the bookings, meaning they have basically no idea what you were "promised" when you booked. I do recommend seeing Halong since it is very beautiful and the scenery is unparalleled, but I do not recommend this company, or taking part in the two night program. If you can, avoid the cave and ask to head to the national park instead.
