My only recommendation is that you be careful when booking, to make sure you are working with a legitimate company who can act on what they promised when you booked and paid.

When in Hanoi our guide told us it was a tourist trap and he was right.Do not get sucked into a two night Cat Ba island deal where you stay on the island one night and on a boat the second night with all inclusive meals and canoeing etc.Go to the island independently, there are plenty of hotels close to the water front  and plenty of restaurants etc.you will pay a lot less and get a lot more. the boat we stayed on (captured on and could not leave) was dreadful with rats( eating holes in customers luggage) and terrible staff, food wise every thing was cold except the beer.we order two beers and complained they were hot we were told, no your hand is hot.they recapped the beer to sell to the next person.not a bad place to visit but not on a tour.

I expected much more from Halong Bay. I was not aware that the litter was such an issue. Our cruise ship stopped to allow for swimming and kayaking but the water was so disgusting I did not feel it was sanitary to get in. The litter came in waves; we hit a particularly bad litter pile at the time of the swim. One person did get in the water and came out regretting it. We overheard most of the other passengers asking the crew how they were supposed to swim in that. It was cool seeing all of the islands and the views minus the litter were amazing. But how do you minus the litter? Kind of hard to take it out of an experience that you paid thousands of dollars to obtain.

Don't get me wrong the rocks appearing from the water and the story about the dragon crashing it's way through are truly amazing. But Ha Long Bay is too over crowded everyone is doing the exact same route there are far too many boats. But the worst thing is the pollution and litter! It's disgraceful.. The water has a greasey film and is littered with plastic bottles and peoples food packaging. It could be such a beautiful place, feels like it has been over marketed for tourists.

Was disappointed about Halong Bay and how dirty the waters are, we couldn't even swim... It is sad how a world Heritage site like this becomes a the garbage bin for both locals and tourist, government should take action about that....

Well the Halong Bay is a beautiful place in general and an UNESCO world heritage but the place is totally escavated by mass tourism. The bay is getting dirty with all the garbage swimming around. It seems no one is caring and its all about the money...

Bonjour MonsieurJe ne sais pas si j’en ai le droit, mais je me permet un droit de réponse à l’avis précédent.Lors de votre retour, je n’ai pas eu l’intention de polémiquer sur vos remarques et ma réponse n’est également pas marquée du sceau de la polémique. Cependant :1)Vous avez préparé avec minutie votre croisière. Pourtant lors de votre retour vous avez reconnu que vous n’aviez pas vu sur notre site que vous pouviez, sur demande, avoir des repas sans fruits de mer, qui si j’en crois ce que vous m’avez dit ne vous plaisent pas. C’est pourtant bien précisé sur notre site. Il aurait suffit d’en faire la demande lors de votre réservation.Vous aviez également oublié que le transport retour en voiture privée n’était pas encore réglé. Vous sembliez surpris de cela lorsque je vous en ai fait part. Il a fallu que je vous demande de vérifier vos mails.2)Ce jour là, 3 autres personnes, un couple et un voyageur individuel, ont, devant vous et à leur retour, trouvé les repas bons et copieux. C’était exactement la même nourriture. En matière de repas il est extrêmement difficile de contenter tout le monde, mais très très rare sont ceux qui se plaignent des repas.3)Votre épouse a été malade et je le regrette, bien évidemment. C’est probablement à cause d’un fruit de mer. Malheureusement ce sont des choses qui peuvent arriver. A noter que c’est vraiment rarissime sur toutes les jonques. Je remarque aussi que votre fils qui a mangé la même chose n’a pas été malade.4)Votre fils, qui n’est plus un bébé, mais un adolescent, a, à deux reprises, lorsque nous avons parlé de la croisière et des repas, trouvé cela “très bien” pour la croisière et le bateau et bon pour les repasJe me permets de soulever ce point que vous omettez.5)Sur cette jonque, il passe en moyenne, par mois, une petite centaine de personnes, venant de tout pays et de toutes agences.Les derniers commentaires de déception (relatives) sont plus que rares. Le dernier datant de décembre 2014 (avec nous) et le précédent de juin 2014 (autre agence). C’est quand même très très faible par rapport à la fréquentation.6)Alors que je ne me suis nullement permis d’émettre le moindre doute sur les propos que vous m’avez tenu à votre retour, vous émettez un doute sur la véracité des commentaires qui sont fait à notre égard. Ces commentaires sont pourtant exacts et tenus par des personnes qui ont réellement fait une croisière, par notre intermédiaire. C’est la stricte vérité et ces commentaires sont sur différents forum de voyage. J'en reviens aux 3 personnes qui sont revenus le même jour que vous, ont trouvé leur croisière très bien.7)Alors que nous ne faisons jamais de remises, car nos clients sont satisfaits, le montant de votre remise a été de 100 USD, ce qui a placé notre petit bénéfice, en territoire négatif.8)Vous parlez de 100 USD pour vos boissons. Il aura fallu préciser que c'était pour 3 repas et pour 3 personnes. C'est à dire 9 repas, et avec une bouteille de vinJe suis vraiment et sincèrement désolé que cette croisière ne vous ait pas plu. C’est ce que je vous ai dit à votre retour et je m’en suis même excusé. Mais je tenais, moi aussi à émettre mon point de vue. Cela me semble tout à fait logique.Bonne continuation à vousBien cordialement

Nous avions préparé cette croisière dans la baie d'halong (qui devait être le clou de notre voyage au Vietnam) de manière assez minutieuse en prenant beaucoup d'avis histoire que ce moment soit le plus magique possible dans ce lieu splendide.. les commentaires sur les croisières organisées par Marc Tiberghien étaient vraiment unanimes quant à la qualité des prestations offertes , c'est pourquoi naturellement nous avons opté pour cette agence, la baie d'halong était pour nous un rêve de longue date et nous avons donc choisi de réserver une jonque privée 5 étoiles pour deux jours et une nuit. La prise en charge depuis Hanoi c'est très bien passée ainsi que l'accueil très amical de Marc et de son beau fils à l'embarcadère, c'est après que cela se corse un peu... notre jonque 5 étoiles qui selon le site était le fleuron de la flotte était en fait un bateau passablement défraîchi, et vraiment je reste correct, même si ce premier contact nous refroidis un peu nous n'avons pas vraiment le choix et nous embarquons. Alors effectivement passé la première  heure de navigation pour sortir un peu du tumulte lié aux gros bateaux qui stationnent dans la baie la magie commence à opérer, le paysage est magnifique nous sommes seuls au milieu des îlots et finalement peu importe notre bateau au vernis écaillé le site est magnifique! Passons à table, et là deuxième surprise, on ne s'attendait pas non plus à diner dans un restaurant étoilé mais bon... un minimum quand même, alors comment dire!? sans être mauvaise la nourriture était d'une platitude désolante, aucune saveur, des crevettes jetées dans l'eau chaude, des nems baignants dans leur huile, et un poisson indéfini au goût... indéfinissable! un petit café thermos pour terminer... que l'on me facturera à la fin de notre voyage!Là pour être honnête nous commençons vraiment à passer par touts les sentiments déception, colère et surtout un peu de tristesse que ce moment que nous nous imaginions magique tourne en attrape-touristes.Rebelotte le soir... crevettes, poisson, coquillages... que je ne touche pas , contrairement à ma douce et tendre,qui le regrettera amèrement quelques heures plus tard puisque sa nuit dans la chambre de la jonque se résumera à des allers et retours dans les toilettes afin de rendre à la mer ce qu'elle nous avait mis dans l'assiette. Durant cette nuit cauchemardesque dont je vous passe les détails personne n'a pris soin de s'enquérir de la santé de ma compagne, puisque personne ne nous en avait proposé au préalable j'ai moi même du trouver de l'eau minérale pour qu'elle puisse se ré hydrater.C'est l'estomac à l'envers et le teint blafard après une nuit blanche que nous avons regagné le port , Marc et son beau fils étaient là pour nous accueillir, je lui fait donc un rapide résumé de nos péripéties et de notre déception mais il m'assure que cela n'est jamais arrivé que les gens sont dans la grande majorité absolument enchantés et par les infrastructures et par la qualité de la nourriture... soit.En résumé cette croisière relativement onéreuse (615 euros pour 1 après-midi,1nuit, et 1 matinée) à laquelle nous avons ajouté 100 dollars de boissons et... mon café thermos, me reste un peu en travers de la gorge, j'ai la chance d'avoir fait de nombreux voyages et souvent en Asie un continent que j'apprécie énormément, Tripadvisor m'aide très souvent dans mes choix et je sui rarement déçu, cependant je me permet d'émettre un sérieux doute sur la véracité des commentaires concernant cette croisière.Pour finir j'aimerais tout de même souligner le geste de Marc Tiberghien qui nous a fait cadeau du taxi retour pour Hanoi, un moindre mal qui malheureusement aura du mal à atténuer notre déception.

Ha Long bay is great but 2-3 hours in a van on wet winding roads.   Our driver that only spoke Vietnamese and would not resist passing tractor trailers on this two lane road with buses coming from the opposite direction.  Not worth dying for.....

Just returned from the Paloma Halong Bay cruise and must say.. the boat looks a little tired.The crew is awesome and attentive, but I would say it is most likely similiar to most of the boats in the bay. I am not sure.how other tours are. but our tour was like a shuttle service where they line you up with other hundreds of tourists ... wait in line ( while others go ahead from other boats)give you some tired kayaks, and then wait for again.I would love to say good things about this..because the Vietnamese folks are so gracious, but it is a LONG ride to the bay, and we were so looking forward to this, but it was disappointing.My husband got food poisoning, after eating lunch when we arrived, although the rest of us did not.Food poisoning seems to be a common problem in Vietnam, so just make sure you bring your hand sanitizer, immodium and pepto bismol.Yes, you should go to Halong Bay,, just do some research on which boat you choose.One night during this time of year was fine and honestly all we could have handled.Someone needs to start figuring out how to quit polluting the bay.. as eventually folks will not want to be part of this terrible assault to a remarkable place.

We have seen pictures of this place all our lives and hear about the 7 wonders of the world ratings. I don't know if it was the polluted haze or litter in the water,  but I had a hard time appreciating this place.  Perhaps the distances made it difficult but the jagged cliffs are indeed a site.  City is a dump and hard to find anything to eat outside of the local cafes.  Overall a greatly OVERRATED place. Not worth a 4 hour drive from Hanoi each way.

having wanted to visit here for 6 years and finally doing so today, disappointment is an understatement. Our 4hr tour was less than 3hrs, we only saw one cave which was admittedly very cool, but the water in and around the chicken rock island and dong cau island was filthy. Not sure how this is still a world heritage site. Halong city as well is very grubby with little else to do, I guess the correct thing to do would be to do a 1 night 2 day tour direct from Hanoi which may allow more time to explore and see more of the bay

Halong Bay itself is very beautiful.  It could be a magical place.  However Vietnam has to go a lot further to clean it up. The water is very polluted, and you see  all kinds of detritus floating by.  Seems 80% of the many tourist boats still dump their sewage directly into the Bay.  I understand that as a Unesco site, the Government is in the process of cleaning it up, but not fast enough!There are so too many vessels out there, so it does not feel peaceful, despite the natural beauty. They throw food to the monkeys to make sure they show up for the tourists.  That is just wrong.  Worst of all, its a 3 hour trip by van each way from Hanoi, and they force you to stop for ½ hour at horrendous tourist trap each way, under the guise of a bathroom break.  We loved Vietnam but would not recommend this trip.

After exactley 2 hours cruising we went to the spot for overnight. Their were dozens of boats all at the same place. This is not my idea from a nice cruise and futhermore, i have been to exatly the same place in Thailnd, Krabi, Phi-Phi island and Phuket and thats much more beuatifull then Halong, sorry to say but it was a waste of money

The travel agent initially gave us 3 options, $55, $75 & $95 depending on the level of 'luxury' we required. The activities put forward to us were: world heritage cave visit, fishing village visit & kayaking through natural caves, swimming/kayaking again later, then the following day we would take part in a cooking class on board the boat & sale back to harbour. She stressed that the activities remained the same regardless of the package we chose it was simply the quality of the food & accommodation that you paid extra for. Being on a budget we went for the $55 option. The travel agent told us we would get all foods & drinking water included in the price, but we would have to pay an extra $5 for the cave kayaking. We paid this extra $5. The trip from Hanoi took around 4 hours. When we got to the harbour we had to wait 1 & half hours before getting on the boat (seemingly the last boat to disembark) which was quite annoying as we had been told we would be at the cave by 1.30pm. We set off from the harbour at 2pm. We got on the boat to discover that only food was included, not drinking water, and we had to pay a heavily inflated price in order to get a bottle of water. We got over this thinking everything would be fine from here. When we arrived at the cave there were hundreds (I mean hundreds) of people there, we had to queue to get in, queue all the way around the cave we more or less shuffled round,(which I know cannot be blamed on our tour guide) but it took the attention away from what I felt should be a good experience. When we arrived at the fishing village to go kayaking through the cave, we were asked to pay $5 again. We were not the only people on the boat to have already paid this at the travel agents, therefore many arguments commenced as they said we would have to pay again as we shouldn't have paid at the travel agents. Our frustrations were then multiplied as the crew continued to refuse us entry into the cabins to get changed, so they expected everyone to go kayaking in the clothes we had been travelling in all day. Tempers rose as the afternoon went on & many arguments broke out between passengers & crew, leaving a tense atmosphere across the board. We left the fishing village as it was starting to get dark. When we arrived at the second area where we could swim they finally let us into the cabins, but we had to leave our passports for whatever reason I don't know. It was now pitch black so only a couple of us jumped off the boat to go for a swim. In the evening a few of us were drinking & 2 couples had bought wine prior to getting on board. The crew were quite rude / aggressive in demanding $10 compensation as apparently it was a rule you could not bring alcohol from elsewhere on board ( not that anyone told us this rule) when they were persistent in demanding the money further arguments broke out & there was plenty of shouting/ cursing in a number of different languages. The tour was then topped off as the next day we did nothing but have breakfast & sail back to harbour, no cooking class. Everyone on the boat seemed to have paid different prices, some $55 like us, some $65 & some even $75 for the same experience. Overall I think the problem derives at the travel agents as they make false claims / promises about what services are provided for what amount of money, but this was only made worse by the aggressive, rude & confrontational attitude of the crew/captain. I felt it was an experience that should have been amazing, as Ha Long Bay itself is undeniably beautiful, (some litter problems aside). This was however tarnished by our experience. We also had friends who disembarked at the same time but in a different boat, whom had a very similar experience to us. When we asked for our receipt back to look at the name of the company the tour guide had 'lost it', and told us the company was called, ironically, 'Happy Tours' although I'm not entirely sure on the truth of this.

I had a day to spare and was talked into a trip to Halong Bay.The tour was US$24 and I was told included air-conditioned transfers, Swimming, Kayaking, a trip to a floating fishing village, a cave and a luxury buffet lunch. We would be picked up at 8 am and after a 2.5~3 hour bus ride would arrive at Halong Bay where we would spend the day on the bay returned to our lodgings at 8:30 pm.In this review, I cannot give you the name of the tour company we travelled with as it was not written on the receipt that the tour desk gave me and the tour guide took it at pick up and told me he no longer had it when I asked for a copy later.The customers on the tour were half one day and half 2 day/1 night but there was lots of confusion when we arrived at Halong Bay as the guide didn't explain the procedure very well and when he finally got around to telling everyone the schedule, there were a number on unanswered questions. One group of 5 young backpackers wanted to know where their boat was as they were sold a "Party boat" tour and were told it was for 18~25 year olds with lots of drinking, dancing etc. Another couple wanted to know when they were doing fishing, I was told 1 day trippers could go swimming and 2 other girls just wanted to go to their cabins to sleep as they only wanted to get to an island and were told nothing about it taking all day to get there.It was clear that tour desks had just taken the bookings without checking availability and then just booked the customers where ever they could fit them. It wasn't the tour guide's fault but he wasn't interested in fixing the problems and just kept saying "It's nothing to do with me". When the backpackers ask him to call the tour desk for them, he wouldn't, they wanted out, they didn't want to waste their holiday time spending 2 days on a boat with old people and children but the guide wouldn't help them and to make things worse, kept saying that they (the backpackers) didn't like Vietnamese people and if they didn't like Vietnamese people, why did they come to Vietnam. Needless to say it didn't make it a pleasant environment but we still all got on the boat for our trip around the bay.Next came lunch, luxury buffet lunch it was not, it was a bunch of plates placed in the middle of the table for us to share. A group from Malaysia wondered if this was the luxury lunch, what would the basic one be like, it was only $20 for that boat. Then a couple from Japan said that they had booked the $20 basic lunch boat but here we were eating the same food so clearly we were on the wrong boat.After lunch we arrived at an island where we were to visit a limestone cave, I noticed on the ticket that it was listed as World Heritages. Nothing was said to us about this, there were 2 guides and still no mention of "take only photos, leave only footprints, kill only time". To make things worse the guides told us there was no smoking in the cave and threw their cigarette butts into the water as they left the boat, customers then did the same. They most likely think what're a few butts compared to the plastic bags, shoes, tyres and all the rest of the crap in the water. All I know is that this won't last long, there are over 500 boats in that bay taking people out to that island, I don't know how long they have been doing trips there but it won't last if they treat it like that.Floating Fishing Village - this is a place where it is possible to go Kayaking (day trippers only) they asked me to pay an additional $5 but I refused as it was written it was included. This is where the day trippers and the 2 day/1 night customers split up with the day trippers returning to shore on a smaller boat and the 2 day/1 night customers continuing on their way.After returning to shore, we boarded a smaller non-air-conditioned bus for the much slower trip back to Hanoi. I not sure why it took longer but I think we were not using the toll roads, I wanted to ask but the guide had left us after about 1 hours and the driver didn't speak English.If you go to Halong Bay, please make sure:1. You know what the tour operator you book with, if the tour desk puts you on another tour, they must he held accountable for their actions.2. You have a customer service (English speaking) phone number to call if there is a problem.3. Don't litter the water and spread the word. This is the only way that tourism to Halong Bay will be sustainable, the tour operators don't care, they're not working for customer satisfaction, they are just trying to make a buck.4. If you find a good tour operator, write a review of the tour, this way they will be encouraged to do good tours.5. If you find a bad tour operator, write a review of the tour, they must he held accountable for their actions.

I did the Halong Dugong Sail tour for 2 days and 1 night. The bay itself is gorgeous and there are LOTS of tour boats and tour companies, so my advice would be to choose carefully. Dugong was below average at an average price. Our "guide" was aggressive and annoying. He'd do things like see a couple having a quiet moment overlooking the water and go over and say HI WHERE ARE YOU FROM? Or see someone reading a book and just hover over their shoulder to say WHAT BOOK IS THAT? He offered no information about how the islands were formed, the history of the bay, or even how the various elements of the cave were created.  Do you research, because you're stuck on a boat with these people and it might as well be pleasant and relaxing!

Halong Bay is a National World Heritage site, it should be truly awesome. In reality it is very dirty over fished, polluted and time is ticking. If the environmental issues are not sorted soon nobody will want to visit this place and worse still the people living in Halong Bay will not be able to sustain their lifestyle. The waters are full of jellyfish which is typical of dirty waters. When immersed in the water you cannot see as far as to your elbow, the water is a very murky green and it has rubbish floating on the top. I was so distraught I asked our guide why this was the case, she told me that the issue with rubbish is due to the locals, I didn’t quite believe this but on the last night of our trip I saw it for myself….We stayed on the AuCo II for a 2 night 3 day trip - The cabins are ok but considering the age of the ship it looks a bit tired. The fan in our room was broken, as was the clock which had its arms lying in the bottom of the casing, although not a problem for us it shows that attention to detail to these matters needs improvement.The restaurant food was amazing, truly lovely. 5 courses for lunch and dinner, not one of them bad. Breakfast was a great spread. On our 2nd night the staff brought out a cake to celebrate our honeymoon and some free cocktails to celebrate my husband’s birthday, it was a lovely thing to do and made us feel very special. The cake was shared between all of the passengers which I thought was a lovely gesture. It was very nice too. The BBQ on the final night was ok, a bit of a let-down compared to the rest of the food on the trip, things not cooked consistently, the squid chewy, pork tough but chicken was lovely.The excursions were good, well organised wonderful experiences. As I mentioned above, it’s such a shame that the waters of Halong bay are so filthy, they’re badly littered with cans, bottles, packaging floating on the top, it’s full of diesel from all of the shipping containers and cruise boats and packed with huge jelly fish.The drinks were overpriced for Vietnam, matching that of UK drink prices. They know that they have a captive audience and I would expect the prices to be higher than on the mainland but not that much higher! Even double what you pay on land would have been ok but some of the cocktails were actually more than what you would pay in the UK. I feel this is unfair however they don’t hide the prices from you so you choose to drink what you want. They provide 2 bottles of water free per day, they’re only 330 ml so if you want to stay hydrated you must purchase more at $2 per bottle.Overall the service was good, there were some communication issues with some of the staff (one in particular), when ordering drinks at times we didn’t get wheat we requested or didn’t get it at all which was a little frustrating as when ordering we were given the impression that our request was understood but this was the minority, most of the time we felt that they got the service just right, it wasn’t too much or over the top.**Here’s where it really changes for us and is the reason we would not recommend the AuCo nor would we return** Cleanliness was an issue, the sand from a beach trip on our first day was left on the balcony & on the lip of the doors between the balcony and our room, so it was trodden back into the room / in to the bed etc.. The ash tray was not cleared at all during our trip. The toilet was not cleaned during the entire stay. I’m not sure if the towels were changed or just hung up again, they felt damp on a few occasions.WORST OF ALL; on our final night we were the last passengers to retire to our cabins shortly before midnight, my husband and I sat quietly on our balcony whilst the staff gathered directly above us talking and laughing loudly, drinking beer and chucking the cans into the water, some of them bouncing off our balcony rail and splashing us with the dregs of beer left in the can. What a lovely 5 star experience that was!!! We have no problem with the staff having some down time but I do think it should be in the restaurant so as not to disturb the passengers and we were totally disgusted at the fact that they were littering the water that they expect you to swim in, especially as they make out that they’re helping the people of Halong and how much they care about the state of the environment… this was obviously just for show.Also not very befitting of a luxury cruise was the fact that another passenger went to the toilet one evening to find a member of staff asleep on his bed! On his back hands behind his head fast asleep. The ship was nowhere near full so there were plenty of other rooms he could have slept in.I would personally look at Paradise cruises as an alternative to AuCo, purely based on the fact that they have been operating longer and have equally as good reviews so maybe they have the experience AuCo do not when it comes to the finer detail and indeed the not so finer detail when it comes to how their staff behave. This is not a cheap cruise and I expect much MUCH better overall.

When you get a little boat to get into the cruise my wife saw a died rat floating...first.We expected to swim in the bay, however, at the second day, when we went to the beach we saw the most unusual and disappointing stuffs as trash in the water, in the hill, and beach. everyplace is full of plastic, cans, even we saw a condom floating...second...I would like to recommend it only if you got vaccines. You can see very beautiful landscapes but garbage does makes  to loss your money.Also we saw jellyfish but nobody cares or tells you about it, even more you visit with children.No recommendable for responsible tourism.

все так хвалят. правильнее все же иметь впечатления не как из рекламного буклета. я тоже много ждал от этого места, прежде всего под впечатлениями от фильма "индокитай". увы, пришлось столкнуться с грубой реальностью, а если точнее - с ужасающей организацией вьетнамского турбизнеса. начать с  того, что по велению какого-то высокого чиновника лакированные, как шкатулки, изящные джонки, на которых туристы только и передвигаются, были перекрашены зачем-то в больничный белый цвет! ну, и белая краска, естественно облупилась. и вот в бухту набивается с десяток таких грязных пасудин. остаться наедине - вы и природа - шансов просто нет! суда могли бы вставать в разных местах, но так видимо удобно экипажам и местным торговцам на лодках, которые снуют вокруг, как назойливые комары. про реальных комаров, кстати, тоже не стоит забывать. из-за скопления судов здесь плавает куча мусора. но самое главное, что в таком количестве судов просто нет никакой необходимости! судовладельцы конкурируют между собой и готовы плыть с любым количеством пассажиров. например, наша джонка, судя по количеству кают и столов, была рассчитана пассажиров на 12, а нас было всего четверо, и еще пять человек команды. судя по всему такая же ситуация была и на других судах - людей было практически не видно. А больше судов - больше мусора. Жизнерадостные вьетнамцы предлагают здесь же и искупаться, и для примера сами бухаются в воду - нет, спасибо не надо! но главное для туриста - все эти грязно-белые лебеди заслоняются собой все эти чудесные изумрудные острова-холмы. возможно, это было когда-то чудом света, и возможно, оно когда-то им еще станет, но пока это сплошное разочарование.
水上生活をしている人も多く、プラスチックごみが結構浮いています。遠くの山を見ながらのクルーズはいいですが、カヤックなど水面を見るスポーツは、水が残念なのでおすすめしません。

Let me start by saying that Halong Bay is one of the most amazing places I have ever seen. The bay is incredibly beautiful and the cruise itself was fine. However, we found it very hard to enjoy ourselves because of all of the trash floating in the water. The Vietnamese government needs to step in and clean this bay and regulate the disposal of garbage or it will be ruined.

The Halong Bay area has some of the most stunning scenery I've ever seen.However, it also has some of the worst pollution I've ever seen. The water is dying and you can see it in the amount of trash, plastic bags and bottles which float by. The boat we stayed on was comfortable, the food was incredible  and the hut on Nam Cat was fabulous. Our guide was Tommy and he was very entertaining and made sure everyone was happy. Apart from the pollution the only down side of the boat trip was the head on collision we had with another boat the next morning on our way back. That was one of the scariest things ever. Nobody was injured but I think that was only through sheer luck. Is it worth going to? I think so but be prepared to be disappointed by the pollution. And make sure you know where the life jackets are on the boat!

People who say this is the most beautiful place they have been have obviously not seen much. Halong Bay would be beautiful if it wasn't so polluted by the locals. Monkey Island was filthy and would most likely make you sick to swim in. The locals were tormenting the monkeys and throwing rocks at them too. But it is a nice break from the city and and is good value for money on the cruises. It's also a chance to socialise with other travellers. I liked the kayaking the best but would never go there again.

The joke on our boat was we were trapped in an adult summer camp nightmare... rigid schedule and forced shopping!  We did the typical two night trip much like all that are available from any Hanoi hotel.  One night on the boat and one night at a bungalow.  Here's the itinerary.. The 3 hour bus ride there was fine but we made a planned "break" at a shopping mall where you walk in one side and have to get to the other side and they pick you up again with the bus.  And they give you 30 minutes to do this!  And all the stuff for sale was 2 to 3 times more expensive then in Hanoi.  No thanks!Day one you get to kayak for 30 minutes (!!) around the floating trash piles.  Then a cave tour which is nice and cool from the heat but caves are caves anywhere in the world, nothing spectacular about this one except for graffiti on the walls in Chinese and Vietnamese writing. Then you get to swim for an hour.  After swimming we all smelled like sewage!  Nice dinner on the boat and had fun karaoke with the other passengers.Day two they take you to the pearl farm which is sad, dirty and simply for another contained shopping experience. Skip this and relax on the boat.Then to the bungalow to kayak or do the bike ride on Cat Ba Island.  We kayaked in the super sketchy boats around the island where our bungalow was and stopped at a beautiful deserted beach and found it buried in trash once we landed. Super depressing.They announce to be at dinner at 6pm sharp and food to be served at 7pm (??)  Don't be late!!  Then you get up early for breakfast and head out by 8am (DON'T BE LATE!)"DON'T BE LATE!" we heard all the time.  20 to 30 minutes between events to "relax".  The only nice thing about the trip was the food on the boat and the bungalow night.  We asked to stay an extra night at the bungalow and they were all full :(   The tourist junkers and local fisherman and their floating villages use the Bay as a trash dump and sewage pit.  We were really looking forward to this part of the trip and tried hard to get past the trash but it's just bad.. really bad!  The scenery is nice for an hour or two then it gets quite repetitive.If I could do it all over again I would do a quick trip right to a bungalow for at least two nights and skip the junker entirely.  The trip to get to the bungalow is sufficient for touring the islands any spend more time relaxing on the beach, playing volleyball etc and kayaking around.

El paisaje de las islas que surgen del mar es espectacular, pero no miren hacia abajo porque todo el encanto se va a arruinar. En efecto es deprimente observar la corriente de aguas sucias de materia orgànica, mientras el barco està anclado por la noche entre las islas. Y la misma asquerosa corriente la ves en sentido contrario por la manyana.La UNESCO (a la que Voy a escribir una queja) deberìa tomar cartas en el asunto y amenazar con quitarles el tìtulo de Patrimonio de la humanidad si las autoridades vietnamitas no cuidan mejor de ese esplèndido lugar. Còmo pudieron autorizar por ejemplo a que grandes barcos mercantes estacionen dentro del Parque natural antes de llegar a puerto? Y la ciudad de Ha Long, que se ha literalmente llenado de hoteles, tendrà una planta de tratamiento de aguas negras? Se corre el riesgo de que Halong Bay colapse por culpa de su proprio èxito! Y si pensamos que nuestra visita lamentablemente contribuye a empeorar la situaciòn, dan ganas de aconsejar a los turistas de no ir ... Vietnam tiene muchas otras cosas lindas para visitar!

Wir waren im APR 2014 2 1/2 Tage in der Halong bay unterwegs. Obwohl wir mit unserer Gruppe ein ganzes Boot für uns hatten wurde unserem Wunsch nach einer Tour abseits der Touristenmassen nicht entsprochen. Wir endeten in völlig überfüllten Buchten in denen der Müll schwamm und die wegen der Dieselabgase der vielen Ausflugsboote stanken und Kopfschmerzen verursachten.Paddeltouren in "versteckte Buchten" erwiesen sich als Massenbveranstaltungen. Man paddelte wirklich durch Müll. Alte Schuhe, Kartons, Styropor......alles in Allem sehr entäuschend.

La baia di Ha Long sta al Vietnam come il Colosseo a Roma. Non si può andare nelpaese senza aver visto almeno una volta la baia. Così dicono tutti. Ha Long è una delle 7 meraviglie naturali del mondo e un sito patrimonio dell'Unesco. La verità, invece, è che Ha Long è un paradiso naturale rovinato dall'uomo e dal turismo di massa.innanzitutto il tempo: le foto e le cartoline che vedete mostrano un cielo blu e terso, un'acqua verde smeraldo. peccato che quando ci sono stato io c'era nebbia. Mia sfortuna, ho pensato. Poi mi sono informato e ho scoperto che è quasi sempre così.la cosa peggiore però è la sporcizia: la baia è diventata un'immensa discarica galleggiante. HO fatto 24 ore in barca in crociera e scie di rifiuti spuntano ovunque: lattine, cartoni, persino profilattici usati ed escrementi. Chiazze d'olio ovunque, le barche usano motori vecchi che emettono un denso fumo nero e puzzolente.La baia è sovraffollata: decine di barche alla fonda che portano i turisti e sversano i loro liquami. Il kayak è una sòla: si rischia di scontrasi con i vari motoscafi  e scordatevi di esplorare caverne da soli: ce ne sono a decine di canoe insieme a voi. Merita il villaggio galleggiante dei pescatori, ma anch'esso sporco e maltenuto.Mi chiedo come l'Unesco possa concedere il suo patrocinio a un paradiso distrutto dalla cattiva gestione delle autorità locali

Halong Cityis rather dull, but cruising across the bay between nesrly 2000 islands is a wonderful experience. Plenty of opportunities to explore huge limestone caves. And kayaking amongst the boat people is fascinating. Sadly, and not, apparently unusually, there was a thick mist while we were there. Atmospheric, but not ideal. If you can, steer clear of the flotillas of cruise ships.

We booked through our hotel a Private 3/2 day tour, paid extra for the private tour so we could explore Cong Do Island and not the crowded Halong Bay. We went south on the opposite side of the other boats and Vung Vieng Floating Village, and from then on followed the course like every other tour boat in Halong Bay. On the 3rd day we had to rescue another boat to bring back. I had asked the guide and Yes we will take you, but of course they never followed through and finally he said the Captain did the manifest already. The bartender tried to charge us for coffee and tea although it was free in the brochure, which he corrected. He was the most professional on the boat. The Captain dressed like a farmer and wore no uniform. The guide was good. The food was very good. I would not recommend Annum Junk as they never delivered the itinerary that was in the brochure. The boat was very old and musty. We have spent 4 months in Vietnam and know what to expect and this was a disappoint. Halong Bay was beautiful and serene.

Noi siamo andati in un giorno di pioggia. Non so se con il sole sia lo splendore che viene descritto, ma noi abbiamo visto ben poco di entusiasmante. Se piove o se é nuvolo, come spesso in Vietnam, tutto é grigio. L'acqua é a dir poco sporchissima, posto iper turistico che ha perso qualsiasi genuinità, gente scortese ed infastidita dai turisti, trattati solo come polli da spennare. Nulla del fascino vietnamita, del sorriso di questa gente vera. Personalmente in Italia ho visto posti di gran lunga piú spettacolari di questo. In Vietnam ci sono posti sicuramente più belli da visitare. Se siete ad Hanoi e piove, non andateci!

I took a day trip $25 bus return, boat, cave and lunch trip to halong bay today. Wish I didn't. The weather couldn't be helped; foggy and wet. But it was rubbish. There were far too many people around. The cave bit was so full it was hazardous. The price to use the kayak or boat is pretty expensive and not included if you book a day trip through an agent (although they tell you it is). Anyway, I wouldn't recommend doing it in 1 day. Maybe it's better overnight. But I do regret doing it.

Booked a 2 night/3 day tour of Halong Bay from Hanoi with Dugong sails.  Found the excursion to be extremely disappointing. Dugong sails was particularly bad but I understand from talking to other travellers that most companies are similar. So many tourists come for the tours that the companies no longer care about providing a worthwhile service, they just want your money. Food on board was poor. Dishes were served per table with barely enough food to go around. Drinks are extra, and prices (even for water) are outrageous. I suggest bringing your own on board. Our cabin was o.k. though the sink in the bathroom drained onto the floor and we were uncomfortably cold as the heat is only tuned on after 8 pm. The worst part of it all was the companies lack of concern for hygiene and safety. Rats ran freely throughout the ship  (I spotted several scurrying into and out of the kitchen) and the area where we were brought to kayak was congested with other boats and the kayaks/ life vests provided are laughable. One girl on our tour literally almost died when her kayak was hit by a larger boat, breaking in half and sinking. Our experience of visiting Halong Bay was negatively affected by the poor quality of our tour which is a shame as the Bay itself is so beautiful. Visiting Halong Bay is worthwhile but DO NOT take a packaged tour.  I recommend taking the ferry to Cat Ba island and from there renting kayaks and exploring on your own. I promise you will not miss out if you don't stay overnight on a boat.

I took a canoeing tour of Ha Long Bay & canoed around the bay:Pros:1) Nice scenery2) some very unusual rock formationsCons:1) Not as nice as Phuket's cave2) I thought there were underwater caves but was totally disappointed3)the canoeing was toooooooo long (i think it was 4 hours): my arms grew numb after the 2nd hour4)besides the rock formations NOTHING to do - NO night life or entertainment!!!! so boring!!!!!I will NOT return as I treat this as a 1 off tour so boring ZZZZZZZZZ wasted my vacation!

All the pictures from Halong Bay look amazing...agree.  If you go in winter that is not what you are going to see.  You won't see the vistas that you are expecting.  It is very grey and foggy.  You barely see the rock formations that are right in front of you.  Also, the drive from Hanoi is very long and extremely bumpy due to new road construction at this time.  Expect it to take 4 hrs at 40km per hour.

世界遺産と言うことで行ってみましたが、海は茶色、奇岩といっても、目ぼしいのは２－３岩位しかないく、松島の方が海も景観も良い。洞窟はあるが、岩手の龍泉洞の方が、桁違いによい。フローティングビレッジぽい浮家が数件あるが、生活感はなくカヌーを貸したり、土産物を売っていたりで、１００％観光用。カンボジア　トンレサップ湖のフローティングビレッジとは比べものにならない。まー、観光資源に乏しいベトナムでマッタリと時間を潰すにはよいかな。

Really breath taking views etc etc but considering there are 5+ million turists yearly then we, tourists, have really ruined the place. The water all around Halong Bay is really dirty, all the boats, more or less party boats hosting people drain their sewer straight to the water. We were catching calmary in the night, till someone flushed their toilet and literally [--] and toilet paper started swimming around our boat. Local they the problem is with the few thousand locals who live on the water but I guess 5 million tourists yearly do a bit more damage.About the nice side, the views are incredible and it is worth going to the cave tours etc the locals offer, but for the sake of preserving Halong Bay I would not go back.  If I had read a review like this earlier I probably haven´t gone at all.PS! the drive from Hanoi takes around 5h one way as well.

We travelled to Hanoi to join an arranged tour by Emeraude Cruises. I do not recommend this company for a lot of reasons. Firstly we ere left stranded at the Hanoi airport because their driver did not care to wait or check for other flights coming in from Danang. We had survived the Typhoon the day before and of course there were many hold ups at the airport in Danang. Our tour guide from Hoian who was to say goodbye to us at the airport for the next leg of our journey with Emeraude called them at least 3-4 times to explain we were delayed. He went beyond his duty. Anyway long story made short. I have been emailing Emeraude to tell them how completely disappointing the whole experience was including the cruise itself and the hotel we stayed in LaDolce Vita. Emeraude have only been interested in having the last word and constantly emailed me back with excuses. Not good customer service. In light of this I felt it was best to post on trip advisor. Not recommmended at all.

Like most people visiting Hanoi, we were very excited to visit Halong Bay & we chose a 2 day 1 night cruise to see the area.  Halong Bay is a beautiful location, that has sadly been turned into a polluted circus.  All around, there are way too many cruise boats competing for anchoring space anywhere you stop.  Both where our cruise boat stopped for swimming & for kayaking was dirty, with an obvious film of diesel on the water.  Speaking to other travellers, we were told that the longer cruise - 3 day 2 night - took you to an area with cleaner water & far less other boats around.  If, like us, you have limited time & want if you decide that you really want to go to Halong Bay, a lot of this is unavoidable, but it's honestly not an area we'd recommend or ever consider returning to.  You can see the same scenery in Thailand or China, but not be surrounded by dozens & dozens of boats & such spoilt & polluted water.

I realized before going that a day trip to Halong Bay is probably not the best way to experience it. It is a really long day - 13 hours in fact. Got picked up at the hotel at 8am and got back at 9pmThe trip there and back took over 4 hours each way, half of it road works making for a very slow, unpleasant, bumpy ride. 25 minutes comfort/shopping break each way at the Ruby Emperor - what a waste of time. We had lunch on the boat, which was probably the worst food I have had in 1 month in Vietnam. Walk through the huge cave, pretty kitschy followed by cruise to the floating fishing village, and a bamboo boat ride - once around the block - affording some photo opportunities. Trip back to the jetty, and the horrible bus ride back to Hanoi. Overall the trip left me rather underwhelmed. I suppose the scenery was quite nice, but due to hazy weather conditions, not the best view. The cruise company was Papaya Halong Cruises and I would not use them again.

Les arnaques sont très nombreuses dans ce site paradisiaque. Ne JAMAIS acheter votre croisière à un démarcheur, soit disant ami du patron de l'hôtel.Préférer les départs à partir de la nouvelle marina, à ceux proposés dans la ville d'Halong. Les bâteaux qui en partent sont en mauvais état et le prix des billets, au bon vouloir du vendeur. Sur notre bâteau, pour une croisière de 6 heures, les prix ( pour une même prestation ), allaient de 90 000 VND avec repas à bord compris, pour nous; à 450 000 VND, repas non compris, pour d'autres personnes, pourtant vietnamiennes. Ce genre de procédé est inacceptable et n'existe pas à la marina. Les prix sont fixés à l'avance, les navires modernes et sûrs et tout est compris.Notre bâteau s'est arrêté dans un village flottant, dans lequel on nous a fait embarquer sur des barques pour visiter une grotte ( je la connais, elle ne présente aucun intérêt ), on nous a ensuite indiqué qu'il fallait payer 100 000 VND pour visiter ce site, heureusement, tout le monde a refusé, ormis 2 personnes qui nous ont avoué ensuite s'être fait rouler. Mes nombreuses visites en baie d'Halong m'ont enseigné pas mal de choses. Les touristes ne doivent pas être considérés comme des pigeons. Soyez vigileants !

Before we bought the Halong bay trip I  try to find what is it about but I couldn't find anything good. So I want to share with you my trip. I wouldn´t reccomend this trip.It is not worth the money. It is not about this company but the trip by itself. I think all the companies have the same itinerary.Our guide was great, spoke very well english his name is Viet. We booked 2 days one night trip on Galaxy boat for $ 120 USD it was supposed to be more luxury.one. They picked us up at 8 am at the hotel the way there took more than 4 hours because we were still waiting for someone that was quite annoying. After 2 hours they stopped at some shop with souvenirs (sculptures, furniture and some small things) It is pretty expensive there as well as drinks and food. You stay there like half an hour which is quite long just for toilet so they assume you buy something. When we asked them why are they taking us to place like this when the paid quite a lot for boat cruise and not for shopping excursion. Someone told me that the driver needs to have breakfast.  Nonsense! We got on the boat, there was just 10 of us which was great. The boat and rooms are nice but  different than on the pictures. It is quite well used. You eat only sea food all the time (maybe little bit of chicken or pork).  The food is good but nothing special.  Drinks are very expensive $3 USD for can beer and if you want to drink your own beer you have to pay 50 cents per can or 10USD for bottle of spirit. So it still worth more for us to pay the fee for our drinks that we brought with us.We wanted to enjoy relaxing time on the boat with beautiful view but it was quite hard because there are so many other boats and so many people on that attractions  For me the place is loosing its magic because of it. First stop you go to some cave, then go to beach (really dirty...it was disgusting). After you have cooking class (lame) and dinner. In the evening was karaoke and squid fishing (fishing never happened). Next morning kayaking and fishing village (we didn't go, we are not fans of organised trips). Than lunch and back to the port and waiting for driver.

Der Ort ist voll vom Müll und sehr uberlaufen wirklich schade.  Überall treiben Abfälle und leichte ölfilme im Wasser.Es ist definitiv keine Reise wert, und der Ausblick war nicht wirklich sonderlich schön, dass ich es jedem empfehlen würde.Ein normaler Ort, mit Tourismus und Schmutz.Weltkulturerbe, was ein sschlechter Witz

Would have been a good day but while visiting the caves the second guide pulled the boat back from the entrance. When we returned to land I realised that myself and my girlfriend had each lost 100 usd. It became apparent that we had been robbed while in the cave.This tour was booked on cat ba island. I've heard stories about tourists being robbed also by their hotel staff.Be very careful.

An incredibly beautiful spot on planet earth could not have been spoilt any worse. We attended a cruise on Halong Bay and thought it was going to be the highlight of our trip to Vietnam. Yes the nature is amazing but filthy and over crowded. In the middle of the night we were woken up by an increadible foul smell and noise of water pouring from somewhere. I thought our toilet has flooded. No. They were emptying the sewage into the sea and the cistern was located just beneath our room. There were hundreds of boats which were doing the same thing in that beautiful bay every night. And the next day still anchored at the same spot, we were told we had an hours swimming time after breakfast. What more can I say???

Firstly, let me point out the Halong Bay is a spectacular place and is worth visiting. The 3-4 hour trip from Hanoi is worth it. Unless are young and are having lots of fun with your crazy mates, consider staying on the water for at least one night; driving to and fro to Hanoi is going to smash ~ 7hrs of your day.Why a poor rating then?We chose Sinh Cafe travel: They lied to us from the 1st word. Bien Ngoc cruises wanted my 20 y old son to bunk in with one of the other single travellers. They (other travellers) complained about this as it was not what they were promised. We did not understand what was being asked as we were specifically told we had a family room on the boat (1 double + one single) Bien Ngoc cruises tried to cram me, my wife and my adult son into one double bed! When we complained they tried to put us in another room with two single beds! Duhh. At that point one of the staff was quite aggressive with my son, demanding the key back. It was now that I was thinking "here we are stuck in the middle of a bay, in the middle of a poor foreign country, this could get ugly". We felt quite isolated. All the other travellers had got what they wanted so why would they side with us. We are on our own here. My wife said "I am going to complain to the captain". The 3 of us confronted him and he acquiesced. Turns out he had a spare room that he did not want to sully. The rest of the trip was to a degree what I expected from Halong Bay - they take you to the most visited places which are hammered by tourism. The kayaking was fantastic. The cave was impressive. The weather was against us but that's not halong's fault - we went there in late July!I've since heard that these sort of experiences are not uncommon in Vietnam. I met an Australian lady who was working part time at Dong's travel near St Joseph's Cathedral. She said in Vietnam you get what you pay for. We did go up to the mid level but it seems that was not enough. I wished we booked with Dongs.

It takes about 4 hours by bus from Hanoi to Halong Bay. But it is totally worthy such a hard journey.It called "Guilin on sea", so my advice is to go to Guilin, China. If you go there in the rain season and only plan to stay there one day. You may have the risk that you are not allow to take the cruise because of the rain.The Halong city is also boring.

Halong Bay has a stunning natural beauty and that is what draws the tourists. The locals however have ruined it by treating it as one big garbage dump. The amounts of waste floating around in the bay is huge. You literally can't look anywhere without seeing vast amounts of rubbish floating in the water and the shores seemed to be choking with plastic bags, bottles, sanitary napkins and a few dead fishThe level of tourism is already unsustainable and there seems to be no environmental awareness from any of the large number of tour operators, tourist authorities, local authorities or the Vietnamese Government.  With some 500 ships approximately, 200 are outfitted for overnight cruises, the other 300 do day cruises. On any given day there are approximately 5,000 tourists on the water.The amount of trash in the water is disgusting. The fishing villages dump their trash and human waste directly in the water, there is litter and sewage floating everywhere in the bay, tourist boats and locals freely throw their rubbish in the water,  the water also has a foul smell and there is a film of oil covering it. We also observed tourist boats discharging their toilets directly into the bay. On the back side of the island where they drop everyone off for swimming at the ‘beach,’ there was a large pile of full plastic garbage bags falling into water. Just image the damage that is being inflicted on the marine ecosystems.Keep in mind the everyone is swimming  and kayaking in this water.  Most tour operators advertise a “gourmet seafood lunch” as part of these tours with the seafood is without doubt coming from the same polluted waters. The last time I visited Halong Bay, some 10 years ago, and yes there was some rubbish floating on the water then. Since that time the ship and tourist numbers have grown  exponentially and so has the rubbish and pollution. The local people and government are doing nothing to stop this polluting and it is obviously just getting worse.Halong Bay is a published UNESCO world heritage site and that alone on paper is a credible review of Halong Bay and a statement of the wonderful spot that it is. While that is correct, and indeed there is some beauty, delight, and mystery that envelop these 2000 islets, maintenance should have been stricter to preserve the area. Commercial tourism has taken its toll on this area. Halong Bays is a “must do" when visiting Vietnam, do it before the impending ecological meltdown.

After the quietness and peaceful beauty of the Bai Tu Long Bay we were shocked to see how overcrowded and dirty Halong Bay is. Tourist were swimming between mountains of trash and oil-spills. If the goverment and all others involved don't do anything against the massive pollution, this natural world heritage site will soon be ruined. So sad.

The entire water surface is covered in trash and pollution. Really, it’s incredible! During our entire 3-day journey there was trash floating everywhere! And, when we went kayaking and swimming, the water where we were swimming was completely full of garbage – it was revolting and, given some of the trash, maybe even dangerous. The beach was also covered in trash. It’s very obvious that it’s not just a few wrappers people carelessly drop overboard. Whole trash bags were slit and all the trash from within them dumped. Some of the trash was really disgusting, like vaginal inserts for tampons, and the used tampons, too! Although the crew of the boat and our guides spent as much time as possible cleaning up the little beach of the trash, A) it was not enough, and B) instead of bagging most of the non-biodegradable trash and hauling it out, they just tossed it all into the bush farther up the beach.I think more and more tourists should refuse to go to Ha Long Bay – it’s just a trash dump. I have lived in Vietnam many years and been there many times. This will have been my last time, and I'm not recommending it to any more of my visitors. The Vietnamese people and especially the tour operators had better do something about it!

Halong Bay, zo mooi als het is, zo kapot is het gemaakt.De cruises zijn een totale aanfluiting. Tweedaagse gedaan... De boot, de kamer en het eten is goed. MAAR.. Het programma is belachelijk. Zowel eerste als tweede dag niet meer dan anderhalf uur gevaren. Met ongelooflijk veel boten samen op de zelfde plek zwemmen, daarna met allen kajakken rondom 'vissersdorpje' Gênant voor mensen die er leven!Daarna nog 5 min. gevaren en aan anker voor de nacht. Samen met veel andere boten. Niets idyllisch ... maar massatoerisme. Je zag zelfs nog het stadje liggen! Volgende ochtend nog een mooie grot bezocht (5 minuten varen van waar we de nacht hadden doorgebracht). Na de grot direct weer teruggevaren naar Halong Bay. Om 11.00 weer terug en aan land geluncht. Overigens.. Het water is zeer vervuild, zowel met troep als de vervuiling door de vele boten. Positief waren de kamers op de boot en het eten. Het valt zwaar te zeggen... maar ondanks de schoonheid van de natuur zou ik Halong Bay niet aanbevelen.

We did a 2 day 1 night tour of the bay in late April 2013 and were totally disappointed with the changes, since our first visit in 2006. All the boats were herded together in the one area of the bay for the overnight stay, with the humming of generators all night. On our first visit in 2006, we jumped-off the boat for a swim at sunrise, in a secluded bay without other boats. Now I would'nt dare swim in Halong Bay,it is a filthy polluted rubbish dump! We went kayaking around one of the small islands and all the inlets were full of floating rubbish - thongs, plastic bags, polystyrene, oil and diesel spills (a friend who visited in 2012, saw a sanitary pad floating along the beach they were going to swim in.....). Those people who rave on about views and amazing sights....,should stop looking front and centre and start looking down and around....open your eyes! The authorities need to crackdown on polluters; start spending some of the millions of tourist dollars on clean-up.         World Heritage listing should be removed to shame the authorities to do something, other than taking the tourist dollar!

Instead of staying overnight, we decided to rather do the day trip, to afford us more time in Hanoi to do other things, I was so excited , and it all amounted to nothing.3 and half hour trip, in a very crammed  mini-bus, with a 30 minute break ( oh yes you guessed it right , in one of those mini malls selling anything touristic, for which the tour companies get a cut ) The water is filthy brown/ green, polluted by all sorts of visible materials, like plastics , bottles, etc,its the ones that you cannot see that worry meYou not even allowed to swim.There are hundreds of junks in the water, other vessels too, including the big tankers and container ships , offloading and loading in a port just to the left of departure point, with chimneys spewing smoke, like a industrial heartland. I live in a UNESCO biosphere reserve, I should know what the status implies, I would strongly recommend that the UN takes this status away from Vietnam,  The other UNESCO sites we visited during our trip , especially the historical ones, were worthy of the status.Should you want blue turquoise waters , in this part of the world,  then head for Krabi, the Andaman sea with its very similar " little hill crops " just off Phuket or other spots like Malaysia NOT Halong Bay.

I think the Viets are very smart. They surely know how to make a molehill look like a mountain and call it Unesco site. I don't see anythng great about it unlike Everest or Taj Mahal. There is nothing spectacular about Halong Bay. You can just travel around and look at it from the shore or just take a boat ride. No need to stay 1 night. Waste of time. Cruise are all the same whether it is luxury or deluxe or whatsoever. Give it a miss. Visit Old quarters and Sapa instead, sample vietnamese food and coffee instead.

Sadly this 7th wonder of nature or whatever they are referring to it as had already become a victim if its own successDo not come expecting peace, tranquility, pure clean water to swim in or with any idea that your boat will be the only one as you will be very disappointed Be prepared for a hellish four journey on a bumpy road that seriously gives you back troubles and to be crowded out with boats and people in caves, the water and indeed everywhere On arrival at the new marina (Under construction) with huge plans for many houses, you may be told to wait there and dumped by your tour guide for around half an hour with many others not knowing what us happening The boats are not allowed to enter the marina so you will be transferred by some sort of tender. You will be expected to carry your own bags at all times; including lifting them into and out of various boatsOur boat did not move from Halong Bay for two days which dashed our expectations of cruising around and seeing the sights On enquiry I understand that there are very strict controls for the boat about where they can go but if course we were not told this prior At night we 'enjoyed' thumping party music and karaoke by near by boats and there were a number of them around usWe paid USD 500 for two of us and frankly expected more. Be very careful about what you are getting and research like crazy to make sure your boat is what you like and expect AND THEN book it in advance Sadly we booked last minute and traveled early the following morningYes it is beautiful but not as beautiful as the karats in Thailand where you can see the clear waterIf I went seriously upmarket, paid double what we had and could fly in and back and cruise around to get away from other boats then I may consider going back - if you can't afford top dollar to get away from Halong Bay to say Bai Tu Long Bay which is quieter then you may be better giving Halong a very wide berth Sadly all the boats going out to Bai Tu Long were booked....

Ha Long Bay is gorgeous, with large limestone mounds, covered in tropical forest, rising out of the warm blue water.  However, our cruise company wasn't what we were promised.We had heard the advice to be careful what group you traveled with, so we decided to choose a more expensive one recommended by the hotel, even though we are a young couple traveling on a budget.  Typically, we had found that spending a little extra in Vietnam was worth it.Unfortunately, we felt that our money wasn't used to make the trip more enjoyable.  The room was fine, but something we would have normally paid $12 per night for, with aircon only after 5pm.  Food is included and there is definitely an abundance of it, however, quantity doesn't necessarily mean quality.  Drinks are not included, not even water.  They charged 20,000 VND (~$1 US) for a small water.  We were typically buying large waters, 1.5L, for 7,000-9,000 VND throughout the rest of Vietnam.  The alcoholic beverages were also a huge rip off, at least 2-3x what we had seen on land.  We did get to see a very interesting cave for 1 hour and also kayak for 1 hour.  There was 30mins to swim before dinner and 30mins to swim early in the morning.  As you can tell, the days were strictly planned. We can understand why this might be, but we did feel that, seeing as we were on vacation, we wanted to relax, which there just wasn't room for.  The meals were family style and planned for only 3 tables, so our seating was closely monitored.  Due to the family style, if even a few minutes late, we would be reminded numerous times that we had to be at the meal asap.  The staff did their best, but all in all, we felt that the company spent the bare minimum of our money actually on us.  It wasn't the relaxing, luxurious trip that we were promised in the brochure.

UNESCO-listed Ha Long Bay is definitely something to marvel at but the cruise tour (which i simply cannot remember what is the name of the tour agency) we took is definitely not worth what we paid for.We initially paid for a 1 day trip to Ha Long Bay for $22, planning to drop off at Cat Ba island and stay one night there before making our way back to Hanoi on our own.Later we found out that the the ship will drop us off on the northern pier of Cat Ba which will cost another $5 to get to the main town in Cat Ba. After calculating and comparing the cost of staying in Cat Ba and making our own way back tomorrow, we decided to change plans and stay one night on the ship and having free transfer back to Hanoi for an additional $20 per person. We thought we had agree with the guide about the free transfer back to Hanoi but the guide suddenly told us the next day that the $20 is only for the sleep on the ship, plus dinner and breakfast. And he say that he will do us a favour by sending us back to Ha Long city for free (the ship is going back there anyways!) but we have to pay extra for the transport back to Hanoi which is $5, the standard rate for transport back. Other people have lunch along with their 2d1n itineary but we didnt have and its another $2.50 if we want lunch, which we rejected. Tour-wise, it includes a 30min visit to the Ha Long caves which is quite cool but after the caves in Phong Nha national park, other caves didnt really amaze me that much any more. You can to kayak at some point of time in the waters but once again you have to pay extra to enter the river caves, around $4. In the evening, they will stop around the northen pier of Cat Ba island for the night and you can get to swim. After dinner, be prepared to be blasted by karaoke and depending on the crowd along with you, you might get loud partying throughout the night. Other than cave visit and kayaking for 30mins, bascially theres nothing else for you other than sitting on the ship and enjoying the karst rocks surrounding you. Meals on the ship were only ok, and if you want any kind of drinks, you have to pay extra. Sleep-wise, we had a room, decent beds and passable bathrooms but the promised AC didnt work.In summary, Ha Long Bay should be visited but the  cruise tour is definitely not worth the price you paid. We paid around $50 for the entire thing but we found out from some of the other travelers on baord that they paid from a range of $60-75. Doing Ha Long Bay yourself can be cheaper and more freedom.

So upset! Had the worst time! Be very very careful when you book this. We paid for 3 days, 2 nights. Told you get a welcome drink, arrive and spend one night at the hotel on Cat Ba Island and one night on the boat. Arrive and got no welcome drink (not a biggy) had to buy own drinks. Went to Cat Ba Island with a few others who booked the same package. No communication about anything.  Got taken to nature park in morning. Man pointed to hill to climb then were on our own. Got picked up late and dropped back at hotel - had to find own way to beach (no communication or signs). Back to boat in afternoon - INFESTED with cockroaches. They were in our bags, on our bed. Tiny babies crawling on us so had to sleep at the table and not our room. Told the man and listened then walked away. NO SLEEP at all. Just moored and didnt go anywhere then back to Hanoi in the morning. Didn't see ANYTHING that was advertised in the book (shown all these islands we go to - one by China). Can't get a refund for such poor service but can recommend none of you do this package. WASTE of MONEY and very upsetting start to our Vietnam trip!! 100Nz dollars down the drain and the most expensive part of our whole 2month trip.

We booked a two night (supposed to be 3 day) boat trip on Halong Bay at the end of April.  The weather was great.  It was possibly a little hot for some but I thought we might be swimming - NOT!  There is no way I was getting into that water.  I live in Asia so I am used to dirty conditions but what you don't see in the 'Post Card' pictures of Halong Bay is the floating villages and the filth.  We booked on a Eco Friendly Tour but there is no way a boat company can change what is going on out there.  Yes to see the rock islands coming out of the sea is amazing and the little coves are often very pretty, if they are not full of rubbish.  The thing that spoils this place is the fishing villages which have no sanitation (straight into the water), no rubbish removal and no respect for this once beautiful place.  I agree with other reviews - People will stop coming to see Halong Bay if the government don't do more.  Tourism brings money but that is going to change unless the Vietnamese people see what they are doing to their own country!

Halong Bay is nothing as shown on picture postcards. The beautiful traditional junks do not exist – their place having been taken by ugly, huge, 3-storey, dirty-white liners and there's nothing very pleasant about sailing around with 30 other huge boats in a bay that has plastics and rubbish floating in it. I think you need to point this out to travellers who take the long trip from Hanoi along a rotten road only to be disappointed on arrival. There is now a hotel tourist town called Halong City. The whole experience was very disappointing.

My sisters and I stayed on a Junk in Halong Bay which sounded fabulous when we booked. I really wish we did our research. In spite of the gorgeous geological features (2000 odd islands and islets) supposedly surrounded by beautiful water, the garbage got in the way of the whole experience. Wherever we looked from the moment we boarded our junk we saw floating rubbish, most of it plastic. We thought that we would eventually pass the rubbish but it just never happened. At one stage we kayaked through a magnificent arch into a secluded bay to look for monkeys (along with about 30 other kayaks) and we still saw rubbish, including a couple of styrofoam boxes.We talked to our guide about the rubbish and he said that it washes in and out with the tide and a lot of it comes from the junks and the few fishing villages situated within Halong Bay. He did not seem to grasp the depth of our concern.I know that boycotting the Bay would deprive locals of income. So I ask everyone who visits to voice concern about the rubbish and wherever possible (feedback forms etc) to make a note about the rubbish.We suggested that tourists could spend a night on a junk in return for a day spent kayaking and picking up rubbish and our guide thought this was a silly ideaSue Bellingen NSW.

We spent 1 night on Marguerite junk cruising HaLong Bay.  Our cost was $250.00 US for two  including transportation, food, lodging and 2 bottles of water. Our toue director was Thang and he was very good.  When we got to the bay we had to board a tender.  The air was so poluted from the diesel fuel that I had difficulty breathing.  it was very crowded.  We got out to the junk boat and it was quite run down.  The room was dark and dingy, the bathroom was dark and dirty and the steps were very steep and narrow.  As we cruised out to see the bay the black smoke coming from the back engines was choking as we ate at tables with the back windows opened.  There were fans but they would not turn them on.  No a/c until 8pm so we went to the top deck to get some air.  The lounge chairs were broken down and it smelled horrible from the pollution in the bay.  Such a shame.we saw raw sweage being dumped and it was common to see tiolet paper that had not broken down yet, trash and oil floating on the surface.I have enclosed photos because I would have liked to have seen what I was getting myself into and the other boasts out there were looking pretty sorry as well.  If I would have Known I wouold have been prepared.  My best advice is to stay at a hotel then go out for a day tour.  I can stay just about anywhere but I just wishI had known up front

I'm sorry to disagree with so many folks here. As someone who has cruised around the world, I love being on the water and came to Halong Bay expecting it to be the highlight of our trip to Vietnam. We booked a 2 day / 1 night cruise on the Emeraude (did a separate review of that on Trip Advisor.) The cruise was disappointing in many areas but the thing that concerned me most was just how overly-crowded and polluted the waters were where we cruised. We counted the ships anchored around us overnight and there were 27 of them, all a short distance from one another. The Bay itself is quite large but so many of the boats go to this one area and the smell of gasoline is prevalent in the air. If you take a tour, please check to see WHERE the boat takes you as it may only take you to one or two areas that aren't really all that far from shore so you don't really get to see all that much. Also, the waters appear quite shallow and there wasn't much movement in the waves so it felt like the trash never got cleansed/washed away. We kept seeing empty plastic bottles, plastic bags and all sort of other things in the water floating by our boat. Such a shame as the limestone islands are picturesque. Overall, I worry that if the government doesn't step in and better regulate the area, it will be ruined.

Everybody told us to visit Ha Long Bay when we arrived in Hanoi. So we took a 2 day/1 night tour and it wasn't at all what we expected. The water was full of dirt and everywhere were tour boats. Our tour guide was nice but no one of our group could understand his bad English. We visited one cave and climbed up one hill and did kayaking around the 'floating village' which was just a few people trying to sell you coke and cookies and water or beer. The weather was cloudy so we didn't go swimming but I wouldn't have gone anyway since the water is very dirty with garbage and oil from the village and the many boats.I don't recommend this trip because most tours are expensive or rip-offs. The dry Ha Long area around Ninh Binh is way more beautiful and worth to visit. There almost no tourists and nice temples and pagoda around it.

I went on a day trip with my family. I read several reviews online (mostly by people in my country) which ranged from average to poor (i.e. nothing much to see. Not worth the day trip. You could find much more beautiful place domestically) but I just had to see it myself. So there we went. The drive from Hanoi to Halong Bay was almost 4 hour (x2 consider it's a day trip). The hotel warned me the day earlier that the drive was long and I wouldn't see much with that limited time. I didn't have much time and if the reviews were true then I would have had more time to do something more interesting. Well a decision well made. It was just a boat ride to and fro in the bay with nothing much to see nor do. Bus ride - view not interesting. The stop at a souvenir place each way was an absolut bore and waste of time. I understand that this is a protocol but this is a mediocre way to arrange a tour. You can do much better than this. If you have limited time in Hanoi don't go (I know it's the main attraction but you have better things to do).

The northern Vietnamese are all unfriendly. No one smiles, few people speak English and most will try to rip you off. The caves are semi interesting but not breathtaking. The boats are an absolute tourist trap. The beach is more like a swamp.

In a nutshell, Hablong  Bay is beautiful and the tours vary minimally.  You basically get what you pay for in terms of the service and quality of the boat.  Please keep in mind that Hablong Bay has 300 boats cruising the Bay....it is very busy  and all the tours are virtually the same.   Bhaya offered a tour that included the following:  Cleanliness, safe boat with safety instructions at the commencement of the trip, tour of Halong Bay, Bai Tu Long Bay, floating village, kayak options, cooking demonstration, Lan ya Bay, cycling or kayaking option on Cat Ba Island, swimming /snorkling options at a beach, fishing village, tour of various caves including "surprise cave" . Unfortunately, Mother Nature did not cooperate with the weather for us during our stay (rain and very cold).   I can only assume how nice the pictures would have been with the sun. We paid $265 per person for 2 nights and 3 days.  We booked via our trustworthy concierge at our hotel.   All in all, our service was great. Staff were very attentive and service was excellent.What I liked:Stunning and picturesque geographyQuick introduction to staff and safety instructions at the beginning of the cruiseAttentive service and quick response to all inquiriesStaff was young  and always smilingBoat manager was very accommodatingClean roomGreat buffet at lunchDinner - 5 course meal ( just ok)Check-in was fast and efficientLuggage arrived promptly in cabin upon arrivalLife vests, flash light, fire extinguisher, opening windows in roomBeds were comfortable and sheets were fresh and cleanRooms were relatively soundproofWhat i did not like:Tourist trap and 20 minute toilet/ shopping break from Hanoi en route to boat.  Same scenario on return van ride back to Hanoi.  (I can't stand being targeted to shop and buy)  Too many boats on BaySurprise Cave - really big and impressive but I was one of many tourists marching through the cave like ants.Floating village was too contrived.  Another target for tourists to shop and buyPlease note:Small beer is $3 US/CDN.  Bring your own beverages.  Fridge is in the roomBeverages are extra, including water at dinner.Safe was not provided in room, however front desk did offer a safe.Food was average but service was excelllent.Suggestion for Bhaya management improvements:Exterior deck could use some painting  especially on railings.
