Halong Bay itself is stunning, a real delight and a must do.However, I would not recommend Galaxy Cruises.Cabins were not clean, my bathroom and whole cabin smelt strongly of urine,even after a long airing out by me, and my friend's had dirty floors and black hair all over her towels and sheets.We were told that complimentary water is included in the cruise price, this was not the case.Galaxy charged well over hotel or restaurant prices for a bottle of water (US$3) and a simple can of local beer cost US$2, double the cost of most other places.My friend wanted a cup of tea just after breakfast, and she was charged US$1 for a tea bag.We all felt we were constantly putting our hands in our pockets, despite paying US$120 for two days and one night.Food was very average, and if you didn't eat the mushy fish, it was commented on and we were made to feel guilty.Staff asked for a feedback form to be filled in, and a lot of the guests tactfully suggested including the free water we were promised, and an urn of hot water and tea bags.The reviews must have been read straight away, as the tour leader would not smile or talk to anyone.Not very professional.I would strongly recommend future travellers to Halong Bay check out what is included in the price, and it may be better to spend a bit more to get a lot more for your dollar.

Halong Bay has a stunning natural beauty.  The locals however have ruined it by treating it as one big garbage dump.  The amounts of waste floating around in the bay is huge.  You literally can't look anywhere without seeing vast amounts of rubbish floating in the water.  The water also has a faul smell and there is a film of oil covering it.If it were to get cleaned up it would be a great place to visit.  At this time however I don't recomend it.
ハノイからは遠すぎて、かなりしんどい。運転も道路事情も違って、想像以上に疲弊しました。景観は綺麗ですが、こんなもんかって感じ。一回行けばいいところ。

Although there are some negative reviews about Halong Bay, we didn't care at first. We booked a 2 days 1 night trip worth 80usd. The excitement ended just before we get into the boat. The boat was tottally different from what has shown on the catalogue. After some cave visits and kayaking, we suddenly heard a quarrel between a tourist and the travel guide. We heard that the travel guide want to leave the tourist at the boathouse nearby. On the evening, 80% of tourist with us offboard at Cat Ba Island and leave just few of us on boat. The breathtaking scenery turned lame, boredom has overcomed the excitement we expected. At night, the weather become so cold and we decided to sleep early. The next morning, we just hang out until the boat return to the jetty. There was some place we didn't go such as the famously fighting cock limestone although it was on the itinerary. For us, it was really a disastrous trip and hope it never happened again.

I joined a 2D1N boat trip to Halong Bay in Hanoi during weekend.  Everything seemed to be fantastic until I discovered my money was stolen on the boat afterwards.I joined the trip through booking from the hotel.  I got up early and followed the guide to Halong Bay.  The guide was very nice and helpful.  He gave us a lot of information about Hanoi and Halong Bay.  I appreciated his effort and his professionalism.  It took about three hours from Hanoi to Halong Bay.  We arrived there at about noon time but waited till 1.30pm before we could get on the boat.On the boat, we were assigned our room.  I had my own room because I was a single traveller.  We had good lunch and then we went to see the caves and play kayak.  I would say the meals provided were very good, especially I don't eat meat and poultry and requested seafood meal.  They could all arranged that for me.  I really appreciated that.I had never thought that my money was stolen on boat so I left my money in my bag which was put into a drawer in my room.  My room was absolutely locked before I went out.  I didn't think I should bring money to visit the caves and play kayak.  The thief was clever because he didn't take all my money but just some of it.  If he had taken all or most of my money, I would have discovered that easily on the boat.When I finished my trip and returned to hotel, I checked my money and found that two VND500,000 notes and two USD20 notes were missing.  I was sure the money was stolen because I had not used any USD and it was put in my wallet separately from the VND.  I have a habbit to count the money before and after, and every day I record how much and what I have spent.  It was very bad experience but maybe good teaching.  I should not have trusted people there.  It was so bad that my travel was ruined by that.  If that had not happened it would have been quite a perfect trip.Since the boat trip was arranged by the hotel, I talked to the hotel manager about that incident.  Though the manager called to ask the cruise company and crew members on the boat, certainly nobody would admit he was the thief.  I wanted to report that to the police because it might help if I needed to claim insurance.  However, the hotel manager told me that it was useless because the police would charge you first.I write this review because I want to remind all travellers that they must be very careful.  Even you put your valuables and money in a locked room, they may have gone missing.  I am not sure whether I should write the name of the cruise company here because I don't know if I may violate any rules in this website (TripAdvisor), but if you want to know that, you may contact me directly.

We had the tour on June 25th 2012, Monday. A bit rainy & sweaty. We bought it from Sihn Cafe at #02 Nang Quat St. near Non Viet Hotel for $22/person/day tour. Sad to say a couple of days of walking aroung old quarter i saw a $17/person/day tour price. A $5 difference. On the day, we were picked up at the hotel at 8:30am and pick up some tourists along the way. It was a 3 1/2hr continuous-no traffic journey that i was able to sleep along the way. But, bad seating choice at the back. It was a rough road and alot of bumps that i was awaken during sleeps. grrr! A 20min stop-over at Ruby Emperor for washroom breaks. When we arrived at the BaiChai Tourist Wraft. We waited 15mins for us to get the tickets from our tourist guide and a little 30mins again waiting for another instructions. I was so hungry at around 1:30PM. Then they called our attention to proceed at the junk boat (name Thanh Long 05). Included in the package are the lunch food distributed to 6pax per table: started w/ 1 steamed fish w/ tomatoes on top, 12pcs. fried spring rolls cut in half, peanuts, scrambled eggs, RICE (late when arrived, my acid was creeping bad), Chayote, lastly the seafood sour-soup w/ sea shells. I did not enjoy lunch because i was so hungry that moment that it's hard to share it w/ people you don't know. A long HALONG BAY, i just realized a day tour is just for sight seeing. No time to jump over the water for a nice swimming. It was dark green. So we just took a remembrance by taking photos of us and the limestone mountains as background. It was moist that day and sweat all over. -[Dont forget to bring anti-perspirant. For others concern]. We had a short drop off at the floating houses area and the tourist guide will ask you if your interested for a $5/head boat ride to be driven by paddles by a local. To view the 2 open-cave like mountains. It maybe took us 20-30mins. before heading to the cave to see the limestones. We had about 45mins to view the cave. We had to walk stairs to go up the mountain cave and beware for people w/ arthritis. Inside the cave, it was colorful (they put up light to emphasize the limestones and the guidelines). We had no time listnening to the stories of the cave. Just took pictures and go on to the next one. Im not really a fan of sweaty tour. Didn't realized it before going there. Hehe! My advise: bring extra clothes during summer, 1L of water, face towels. Do not seat at the back area of the bus. And your all good! The tour was a 3 1/2hrs going there, 4hrs in Halong Bay, 4hrs back at the old quarter.

This attraction is vastly overrated, here's why:1. 3.5 hour bus ride one way 2. Dirty green water everywhere you go3. Over crowded with tourists and boats4. The Fishing village seems unauthentic5. Beach is filthy - children peeing in the water with no bathing  suits on 6. Pearl Farm = tourist trap7. Over-priced alcohol on every boat8. You'll see better in Thailand (Koh Phi Phi)Everyone wants those picture perfect moments, but the weather there is so up and down, you may get only 4-5 hours of sun. Do a day trip if you must, but temper your expectations or else you'll be disappointed like I was.

Went to Ha Long Bay on a 2 night tour with alovacruise.com good quality boat and service and fabulous cocktails.  The big disappointment was the mass tourism in Ha Long Bay; hundreds of boats all painted white because of some government dictate which spoils the charm of an Asian Junk and makes then stand out like a sore thumb. The boats are only allowed to take 1 of 5 routes and it feels like 90% of them use the same one; we were lucky on the second day to get away from most of the boats and headed for a cove 90 minutes away with only 3 boats.  Water is very dirty, no big surprise with hundreds of boats all moored in only a few coves and the toilets emptying straight into the sea and other rubbish.Number of boats needs to be halved at least and the bays in which they can cruise/moor increased. . . . and get rid of the chipped white paint.

My girlfriend and I went on the 3 day 2 night tour with Galaxy Cruise at the beginning of July and overall we were not very impressed.if you're going to Vietnam you have visit Halong Bay, the scenery and views are unique and stunning. I would however recommend that you only do 2 days and 1 night. Once you've spent one day looking at limestone formations you've probably seen enough, and you don't really get much extra from the additional day as you never travel that far as your boat needs to pick up new passengers from the port everyday. Before I start my whinging, I just want to say that the Galaxy Tours boat itself and it's cabins are genuinely very pleasant. The bed was large and comfy, the bathroom was fairly modernly decorated with a big shower. The boat is one of the newer ones on the bay and the staff are very friendly.... My gripe with the tour however, is that having paid a fairly hefty price ($450 for 2 people) to stay on there, you did not get the opportunity enjoy it or use the boat or cabins at you own leisure. The company are far too interested in making money off you by dumping you in different places with no choice in the matter, in order that you may spend money there, so they can get some commission. On the second day they get you up at 06:30 and dump you on a smaller tattier boat for the day with none of the amenities or comforts you've paid $450 to use, so that they can return to the dock to pick up more paying customers. However, they tell you it is because the smaller boat can get into the smaller secluded and private bays. Trust me, there is nowhere private and secluded in Halong Bay, there are 500 tour boats operating and they all pretty much take the same route. Unfortunately for us we didn't get the best weather and it rained heavily for 2 of the 3 days. I appreciate that the tour company can't control the weather but seeing as it rains a lot during the summer months in Vietnam, they could however have some kind of contingency plan in place to change their itinerary to activities that are slightly more rain friendly. We ended up sat on uncomfortable chairs in an old leaking boat with no bar, music, games etc for 3 & 1/2 hours in the middle of a bay as we were supposed to be kayaking but as there was a monsoon outside not one of the passengers wanted to go out and get soaked with no means of drying or warming themselves afterwards. If we had stayed on the original boat we could've at least watched TV, had a nap in our cabins or a have few drinks at the bar. The food on the boat wasn't that great either and it was very repetitive. If you don't eat fish or seafood you need to explicitly tell them what that means as their can be some confusion. About 6 of the 16 passengers on our trip did not eat fish and this caused chaos at every meal time with fried eggs or potato fritters being given to those passengers.

I had heard that Halong Bay was can't miss in Hanoi so I booked a tour through my hostel on Fantasea Adventures.  The ride to Halong Bay is about 4 hours so I recommend doing the 3Day/2Night tour. The boat appeared and was nice for the most part however our room smelled of gas because it was right next to the engine room. The crew had assured us that they turn the engines off in the evening so we didn't push the issue much further. Later that evening, my roommate and I went to go to sleep and the room still smelled of gas (because they need it to power the air con and lighting). They closed the door to the engine room however it didn't make a difference so we ended up sleeping on a couch in the dining facility. Because I still felt sick in the morning, I addressed the issue with the tour guide (again) and his response was "you're on a boat, what do you expect?". Luckily, we were in a hotel room the following evening so we would not have to deal with this a second night. No form of reimbursement was offered for the inconvenience and putting our health in danger from the exposure to the gas.The hotel was wonderful, very clean and the staff was very helpfulThe caves are very touristy, I learned very little about them except the various animals that if you look hard enough, you can see in the rocks. Kayaking was the highlight and a must do, there is an option to swim later in the evening. The hike on the second day is a nice form of exercise but the view from the top is nothing to write home about. There is an option to go to Monkey Island later on day two which I didn't take and decided to explore Cat Ba instead which is a nice little Island.The food was good and the staff was nice, however the experience with the room on the boat and getting sick from the gas really ruined my experience with Fantasea Adventures (the guide did eventually apologize). I recommend going to Halong Bay but not with Fantasea Adventures but if you must go with Fantasea Adventures, make sure you are not in room 5.

If dirty water is your idea of a natural word heritage spot, then halong bay is the place to go. Otherwise do yourself a favour and head to south western China for some beautiful unspoilt karst scenery.So disappointed by Halong, albeit I had been warned by a fellow traveller of the dirty water so was'nt entirely surprised. Shame on the Goverment for not enforcing some protection policies.

Spent 3 days/2 nights here.  The amount of trash in the water is disgusting.  The fishing villages dump their trash and human waste directly in the water so it is floating all around you as you kayak around.  Forget swimming, the water is too polluted.  If you keep your eyes above the water line and see only the limestone then this is a beautiful place, but the smell of the trash and excrement is overwhelming in some spots.  The local people and government are doing nothing to stop this polluting and it is just getting worse.

Was convinced by travel agent to book on this junk which was described as most luxurious in Halong Bay. Booked a suite, but mix up with booking ended up with a deluxe cabin - big mistake. The cabin was all differing shades of brown with very stained and damaged yellow curtains. The sofa had cigarette burns in despite the boat being non-smoking. The private balcony attached to the cabin also opened on to the wheelhouse, so there were sailors and the captain talking at all hours so no privacy! Also the doors to the balcony did not lock so no security either.The food onboard was the highlight, only problem is that it took ages to arrive once ordered. The tables in the dining room/reception area/bar were also very close together so no chance of a private conversation here either. The staff were nice, but a bit in your face. Very expensive for 1 night over US$650, not worth the money. Would not reccommend this cruise.p.s. If you do cruise, make sure your cabin safe works. One couple onboard had a suite with a safe that didn't, long story short, they came back on shore a couple of hundred dollars lighter. Management refused to take resposibility because they didn't put their money in the safe!!p.p.s. In your cabin you will find two pairs of flip-flops to use while onboard, whatever you do, do not take them home with you. Two ladies onboard did and they were made to open their luggage and return them before they were able to leave the boat. Obviously they re-use their flip flops!! Verruca alert!!

Took the overnight trip on Halong Bay on the junk, "Valentine" during January 2012. This is one of a half-dozen or so junks operated by the "Indochina Sails" group. The boat and crew were terrific. Excellent service, great food, clean and modern facilities. Everything about the boat met or exceeded our expectations. (Not the best time of year to go...it was cloudy/misty all of the time. Maybe summer would give the sunshine needed to be really impressed.)The tour agent, on the other hand, was terrible. We bought our package from http://www.halong-bay-tours.com/ . The sales staff, Marie, was very helpful during the booking process, but the execution/operation of this group is not acceptable. We bought the optional bus trip to get from our Hotel in Hanoi to the boat, and back again. In spite of calling to confirm the night before, as well as a half-hour before the bus was to arrive, it initially did not. All in all, we called 4-5 times after the 8am pickup time, and each time was told "it will be there in 10 minutes". They were lying...too lazy to see what was really happening. Finally, more than 2 hours later, we were told that the bus had broken down, and that they would have to get us alternate transportation. (We later learned that the other guests had made it to the boat already, so I think they "forgot" to arrange to pick us up!) They eventually got us a driver, and arranged to have us delivered to the boat after it arrived in Halong Bay.So...we made it, and that was OK. The other issue that really burned us was when we got back to the harbor area, once again there was no-one to pick us up to take us back to the hotel. When we called them they said that they thought *we* had made the arrangements to get back! They said that they would get us transportation eventually. Being extremely skeptical at this point, and seeing one of the Valentine boat hosts/staff still there, we asked him if he could help. He really stepped up, and got us our transportation, for which we were very greatful!Again, excellent boat and staff, terrible booking agent. My recommendation is to stick with the higher-end boats. They are a few $$ higher than the lower-end ones, but worth every penny. Yes, you can book these tours from Vietnam and save some $$, but you risk having the boat you want already booked. Stay away from www.halong-bay-tours.com, book through someone else.

We spent 2 days and one night on the Paloma cruise and overall were very disappointed in both the cruise and the level of service offered which we considered fell well below the standard we expected.On the plus side the boat is in excellent condition with modern cabins.  The beds are hard with good quality pillows, and have a good shower with plenty of hot water.The negatives, and there are many, fall into 2 broad categories, itinerary and customer service.In relation to the itinerary, we had two issues, firstly this isn't much of a cruise.  The boat travels out from a reasonably busy port to its overnight anchor within about 1.5 hours.  During this time you don't see much of Hao Long bay.  The boat does not move from this spot except when it goes straight back to port the next day.  That is the case whether you are doing the 1 night or 2 night version.  Of the supposed 2,000 pillars in the bay I think we saw 40 at a guess.    My big issue with the itinerary however was the total failure to plan for the weather, or to customise the tour to the season.On the first day there were to be 3 activities, visit a beach or canoeing, sunset viewing and squid fishing. 2 of these were done by no one.  Our guide cheerfully told us that in January they only see the sunset about 4 times a month and that there were no squid in the bay at this time of year.  I don't understand why they hadn't bothered to tailor the itinerary to reflect these common issues and organised something else.We did visit a beach with a hike to view the bay and some people did the canoeing.  It was ok, but I should point out that it was seriously cold.  It felt about 8 degrees, as I kept checking if I could see my breath.  So I wouldn't do that again.  We also noticed that the 3 day people got the privilege of a beach barbecue as part of the extra day.  I'm delighted we didn't put ourselves through that in the cold. To my second issue, the customer service.  Our guide was borderline rude and really only appeared to care about incurring minimal hassle.  There was nowhere to sit and relax on the boat as there were no heaters or rain covers on deck.  The boat has an indoor bar with some couches but the staff always sat there talking and guests didn't appear welcome, so no one sat there.  Instead guests either sat on the bed in their cabins or at the formal dinner tables if they wanted to read or relax.To crown it all the food was also poor, for example on the last day lunch was served in the harbour almost within view of the Paloma shore office.  It consisted of cold chips, boiled broccoli and 3 roasted shrimp.  No sauce, no flavours nothing. It was the most miserable looking dish.  Actually, on the whole the food was nearly always cold.So in summary, I would not do this cruise in January, and even in fine weather I would do it as a day trip and sleep onshore.  That should save enough to contribute towards a real cruise (I.e one where you actually cruise somewhere).

My impression was not much to see and do especially in winter time.The scenery is more less same even you go more than an hour boat ride. Surrounded water is pretty polluted.Many people seem to spend two nights as they have to travel far from Hanoi, but there are not many things to do for two nights and very expensive.If you have lots of  time and money, you can try this place otherwise i would recommend you to visit Sapa( try topas ecology). That is the only place in Vietnam that i would like to visit again.

Die angepriesene 4 stündige Rundfahrt in der Ha Long Bay haben wir bei einem lokalen Reiseanbieter gebucht. Pro Person 25 $ inkl. Aller Gebühren. Schlussendlich waren es dann nur 3 Stunden, aber das war egal, da wir genug gesehen hatten.Das Schiff war wie viele andere noch knapp Seetüchtig. Teilweise musste man schauen, damit man nicht in einen Nagel trat. Aber sonst war es für uns ok.Zusammen mit uns waren noch 4 Chinesen an Bord, die recht lustig waren und somit war auch während der Fahrt für Unterhaltung gesorgt.Da ich schon viele Tropfsteinhöhlen sah, waren die beiden, welche wir besuchten schön, aber nicht spektakulär.Anschliessend ging es zu den beiden obligatorischen und bekannten Felsformationen die ganz nett waren. Überflüssigerweise konnten wir dann noch eine Fischzucht anschauen, d.h. Ein grosses Floss welches mehrere Becken mit div. Fischen hatte.Empfehlen kann ich hier noch, dass man vom Land aus genügend Getränke mitnimmt, da sie sowohl auf dem Schiff, wie auch an den Anlegestellen viel teurer sind.Alles in allem war es ein durchzogener Ausflug. Von einem UNESCO Weltkulturerbe hatte ich irgendwie mehr erwartet, auch wenn es in Vietnam liegt.Für das dunstige Wetter und die relativ schlechte Sicht kann sicher niemand etwas. Aber das ganze drumherum und vor allem gegen die ewig mühsamen Verkäufer und Abzocker in Ha Long sollte die Regierung etwas tun. Nicht jeder Tourist ist vorsichtig genug und fällt schnell mal darauf rein.
Our peeves:-

The beauty of Halong bay is unquestionable, but the almost Disneyland aspect is very disappointing, in general everything is rushed and bums-on-seats mentality. Be very careful of the 2 day and 3 day option, when sleeping overnight, we thought we were going for 3 days (2 nights) for a continuous boat trip etc. It is not like that, one boat one day then onto a cabinless boat for the 2 day and back onto a cabin boat for the night, no chance to relax and put feet up on the day time in a cabin. None of this has been explained in advance, so opted out of 3rd day came back to Hanoi, no apology or reimbursement of course.

Yes the scenery is attractive, but samey. There is no need to spend the outrageous fees for the so-called 2 day/1 night. The timing is a bit of a scam - you get on the boat at 12.30 and are returned at 11 the next day - and you are turfed out of your cabin at 9 am so they can clean it whilst you travel. So you actually get less than 24 hours on the water (and considerably less out of sight of the harbour) and on the '2nd day' are not able to use your cabin. Pretty unpleasant when it is during a typhoon. Take a one day tour and save hundreds. Or if you have been to other 'similar' island bay type places (NZ, China, Thailand) skip it as it didnt rank in the top 10 for us.

Halong bay was stunningly beautiful , we went on the Christina junk , the rooms were lovely , however everyone who jumped off the boat and swallowed the bay water got an upset stomach , my husband was really ill for a week . So def go just don't swallow any water , the Vietnamese government will have to tackle the problem of 500 junks flushing all the effluent into the bay !

We had a first class boat on the Bay, but I want to warn anyone considering going to Halong Bay to be prepared for all the trash floating in the water.  Plastic of all kinds, paper goods, foam rubber. Enough trash to spoil the sail for many passengers.   The boats now have to take efforts to find areas that are sufficiently clean to allow for kayaking.  It is very distressing to see a once beautiful location so abused.  A real shame.

I just returned from a 2 night 3 day cruise on Halong Bay and although I found Halong Bay beautiful in itself (and couldn't fault the boat, staff, food) I was horrified by the degree of water pollution. There wasn't a single moment where there wasn't some object to be seen bobbing in the water (trust me I tried to avoid looking at it but it was depressingly difficult). Everywhere I looked there was plastic bottles, bags, sandals, chunks of polystyrene and all manner of trash floating in the water in differing levels of concentration depending on the currents. And if that is floating on the top I can only imagine what is sitting on the bottom of the bay, and what concentration of chemicals, pollutants and other unmentionable substances are in the water itself. We went kayaking and paddled through patches that were slick with some oily filthy substance. I love swimming and I could not bring myself to swim in the water.I found it really depressing because this is supposed to be a national park/world heritage site? I spoke to some Vietnamese people about it and the staff on our boat blamed the fishing villages, and the people in the fishing villages blamed currents from the mainland. I suspect it's a combination of the villages (which are floating on polystyrene pontoons which are all disintigrating into the water), the mainland and the large volume of tourist boats. The people in the fishing villages have lived there for centuries so it's certainly no easy problem to fix.Something needs to change before this beautiful place is ruined forever.

Spent a night on Halong Bay on the Violet. It was supposed to be launched in November 2009 but it aint holding up too well! Very tired and in need of repair. A fairly expensive experience at around $500US a night. It amazes me that there is no hot water on the boat during summer yet they have a spa in the bathroom! The food was just OK with possibly the worst pastries I've ever had for "pre breakfast". They must microwave them - absolutely horrible....

There is no doubting the place"s natuarl beauty. However the water is often filthy and the place is overun with us - tourists.We did a trip that involved 2 overnights and at the end of the first day we were thinking of heading off in the morning and cutting it short. At one point we counted 40 tourist boats excluding the high powered speed boats screaming round the part of bay we were in.However the 2nd day was far nicer. We had the chance to swim as well as kayak around a fishing village and lovely lagoon. We would recommend staying for the 2 overnights. Thisd means you get to appreciate the place away from the crowds (we were there off season)>if you are trying to sqeeze a day trip in from Hanoi you may be very disapointed. If the tours are not better managed and the waters cleaned up this place is going to tragically ruined. UNESCO need to get a grip of the Vietnamese authorities.
ハロン湾をクルージングしながら食事しました鍾乳洞に連れて行ってもらったのですが、ベトナム人の夏休み初日にぶつかってしまい、ベトナム人だらけで押し合いへし合いで進まず、鍾乳洞に時間を取られ、ハロン湾は奥のほうまで行けなかったと思いますとても残念です

6 hours of total travel from Hanoi to Halong Bay and back. By this alone, clearly NOT an option if you only have a day allotted for this.A day of seeing nothing much - especially after seeing similar (and more!) in Guilin, China or Coron, Palawan, Philippines, which are more impressive. No swimming option for such a short time (the water looked murky too - don't know if there's any nice under the sea land to see). I think it's an excuse to say that you have live up the junk for 2-3 days  to see the beauty of the bay. It's a rip-off for tourists since you can do the same day tour in Guilin and island hopping in Coron w/o living up a junk that costed hefty.Took the tour with Open Sinh Cafe - whose package's lunch  was surprisingly good and of no huge difference from the deluxe seafood meal. Slight irritant is the "wait time" for filling up the van and the nerve of the tour guide to drop us off not exactly on our hotel door.

After a recent rip to Vietnam, we visited halong Bay for a 2 night 3 day trip with ET Pumpkin tours. We where very disappointed with the tour, with the only up side, the other travellers that where on the same trip. Our trip from Han Oi to Halong Bay was un eventful and comfortable. On arrival we where shown to the junk that we would see the bay with. After boarding and leaving port, we where given lunch, which was quite good, during lunch, a few announcements where made.1. To drink your own drinks on the boat incurred a US $10 surcharge per bottle. We where given a price list for all drinks on the boat. All drinks where 4 times dearer than in Hanoi, Coffee and soft drinks where 25,000vnd each. After lunch we started to admire the scenary of the bay, but was also comfronted by a lot of polution. Every thing from plastic bags, polystyrene containers, drink bottles and what looked like oil slicks where in abundance throughout the trip from the port to Cat Ba island. It was very unfortunate that along with the polution, the exceptionally high prices for drinks, and the unfriendly crew of the boat, we will not be returning nor recommending the place as a must see. On our way back to Hanoi, the bus we where traveling in actually hit a moped, it took some time and yelling from passengers for the driver to stop. After stopping, the driver inspected his bus, then got back in and drove off, leaving the moped driver some way back on the ground.Other events that occured on the trip where a bike ride on Cat Ba island, with bikes provided by APT Tours. These bikes must have come from the tip. All along our trip every 1 had problems with their bikes, but that was ok as help was at hand in the form of a moped rider willing to take us the 2 km for a mere 100,000 vnd. If it wasn't for the moped's and poor quality push bikes, this would have been a very pleasant excursion.  On Cat Ba island, we did find that, although there was no power, some of the local bars where open. We found 1 that sold cold beer (big bottles) for 15.000 vnd each, or buy 2 and get 1 free. All in all, Halong Bay could be a beautiful place, it just needs some TLC, clean up the floating mess, and if the tour operators provided slightly better equipment, there would be no need for noisy, harrasing moped riders on such nice pleasant excursions on the islands.

In November 2007 a group of 8 of us booked a 2 night cruise on Santa Maria Cruiser  and 1 night Cat Ba Island accommodation at the Sunrise. This was confirmed at the time and also the following March 2008.We were to depart Hanoi 29 April 2008and to meet and pay Columbus Travel the preceding night. On that night we were advised that the accommodation was not available on Cat Ba Island.Due to the late notice we were forced to travel on the Santa Mariaand unable to make another booking on Cat Ba Island.I can only suggest that noone trust Columbus Travel with any of their Vietnam travel arrangements

My husband and I visited last spring  for a 3 day cruise. After a harrowing drive from Hanoi we finally arrived to our "junk".  The staff was pleasant and we were taken to our a very nice, clean cabin. I had been looking forward to this part of our trip for sun and a lot of swimming but the weather was foggy and chilly most of the time; however,  the scenery is spectacular and the water the color of jade. There were monkeys and birds everywhere.  Unfortunately, and this really tainted my whole experience, was the amount of trash, soap, and oily residue in the water. I saw crewmen on other boats heaving waste directly out of people's cabins into the water.  I took a day long kayak trip and the shores seemed to be choaking with plastic bags, bottles and a few dead jellyfish. I would still recommend taking a kayak trip just to get away to a peaceful enclave, away from the boats and the smell of diesel.This is a marvelous, natural place and a World Heritage site. It is such a tragedy that this is going the way of just one more polluted,  tourist trap.For an unspoiled and beautiful place try Sapa in the north.Thicket123Brooklyn, NY

You read the guide books and look at their pictures and expect Halong Bay to be a paradise, however the reality when we were there is early Nov 2007 was far from it.Positives:* The geological magnificience is undisputableNegatives* There are 500 junks in the bay at any one time and where we anchored overnight we countered 50 junks alone (it is not quiet and secluded at all)* The pollution was awful!  We could only see about 100 metres because of the smog (the bay is near one of Vietnam's most industrial areas) which meant you couldn't appreciate the view.  We asked our guide whether this was normal and she said we were there on a nice day and sometimes you can't see 10 metres.  * The water wasn't very clean and I didn't go swimming more than once.  There was a slick on top from all the junks and quite a bit of rubbish in the water* Although we paid nearly $100 for a two day/one night stay the food wasn't that good and we felt like trapped rats with people all around trying to sell us stuff (on the boat and on the water)* In a two day trip you are only on the water for less than 24 hours.  There is a 3 hour drive each way through very unscenic country with stops at tourist trapsWe would not recommend bothering going out there unless you are really keen.  We went with a great company and had good transport but this can't make up for the disappointment.  Others on our trip recommend Sapa over Halong Bay anyday.The photos you see really are misleading - even my photos make it look beautiful!Unfortunately industrial progress and lack of tourism controls are ruining this natural wonder

We are just back from a brilliant Vietnam holiday. We went to stunning Halong Bay and did a three day two night package with Hai Long Junks - discoverhalong.It was the most disappointing experience. Our two night three day trip was really two one night trips put together.After night one we were taken off our comfortable junk onto an uncomfortable boat  to chug around for the day to the sound of a diesel engine while our comfortable boat and luggage went back to port to pick up the next lot of tourists.We met our boat that afternnoon with new shipmates at an attraction we had done the day before.. essentially repeating our tour.We then requested to go out in Canoes ... 20USD and we were allowed no time to go.Halong Bay is beautiful with the 600+ diesel boats chugging around ...but don't waste your time or money with this company doing the two night trip ... not worth it.
Làm ăn chụp giật, bỏ tiền trải nghiệm dịch vụ mà như đi ăn xin, vịnh thì toàn rác, ra ngoài thì chặt chém, trải nghiệm tồi tệ

Vịnh Hạ Long lần đi thứ 4 hay 5 gì đó. Có phải đi nhiều nên thấy nó không còn mới mẻ. Tuy nhiên việc di dời bến tàu ra cảng Tuần Châu làm thời gian lênh đênh trên vịnh ngắn đi rất nhiều. Khách chưa kịp cảm nhận vẻ đẹp của Vịnh thì đã hết giờ. Khiến chất lượng tourso với ngày trước kém xa. Bên cạnh đó nếu TP QN không quy hoạch lại cho hợp lý chất lượng tour trên vịnh, thì chỉ có lượng khách đến 1 lần thôi. Dù phát triển thêm nhiều khu vui chơi. Nhưng chất lượng bán hàng ( nhất khu phố cổ) kém xa Đà Nẵng, thái độ bắt chẹt khách du lịch quá kinh khủng. Tiếc cho 1 di sản văn hoá đã từ lâu mà khai thác q lý  quá kém.

I gave 2 stars for services here. You guys better check on tiket or the cruiseship ticket office (the one right by Halong bay)will charge more than the real price a lot. Employees on the cruise will ask for tips before we tip them. They're not allow anyone bring water ( force you to buy from them) so ridiculous  Tôi cho 2 sao cho dịch vụ ở Hạ long rất tệ. Cái văn phòng bự bán vé tàu (mà có chỗ cho tàu neo đậu ở ngay Hạ long) nói giá gấp đôi khi tôi check online,  càm ràm trả giá như cái chợ cá, ko rõ ràng. Lên tàu thì nhân viên trên tàu xin tiền bo sỗ sàng (trước sau gì cũng cho mà). Hi vọng sẽ thay đổi thái độ phục, vụ tư cách và rõ ràng.
Củ và sơ, Quảng Ninh phải làm mới nhiều hơn

If you like mass tourism and crowds, this is definitely your gig! You are welcomed on one of the hundreds of tourist boats. Dropped off at one of the artificial islands and popular caves with hundreds of like minded people. You will love it. From your sundeck you can witness a wide variety of cruise ships or if you are lucky you might catch a glimpse of the sunset.

The region really needs to commit to spending more to clean and maintain this beautiful place. Tourism is currently left to run rampant.  Step 1) issue limited number of permits to boating companies, charge for those permits Step 2) charge visitors to travel into the bay Step 3) use money from 1&2 to pay locals without jobs to clean and maintain bay Step 4) enjoy  So much potential to be beautiful. Needs to be saved.

Tourisme de masse, c'est Disneyland. Je ne me faisais pas beaucoup d'illusions mais difficile de venir au Vietnam sans voir la baie d'Halong. Si vous y tenez, inutile de dépenser l'équivalent de 3 mois de salaire d'un vietnamien pour une nuit sur un bateau de "luxe" à moins que le lit King size et le repas entrée  plat  dessert soit votre motivation. La baie de Lan Ha n'est pas épargnée. Un tour à la journée sera suffisant pour decouvrir cette originalité de la nature et dire j'y suis allé.

Honest Review  Here in winter. Peak season. A bleak and dreary place absolutely chock full of noisy tour boats and swarming tourists.  The water is churned up, grey and muddy. It’s not as per the manipulated images you might see.  The limestone pagoda islands look like they are populated by nothing by dying weeds. There are a few caves and the largest is the tourist cave. Quality is fair but you are pushed through in a sausage machine  The day trip kayaking is in dirty and shallow grey water surrounded by concrete pontoons.  Perhaps Im spoiled having enjoyed the clean & clear waters of Australia, New Zealand and the Phillipines to name a few.  Those are beautiful places to visit and swim/ enjoy the ocean in.  Halong Bay most certainly is not.

Great cruise ship with lots of activities but the sea has been ruined! So much rubbish floating around that some wouldn’t swim in it. Such a shame this beautiful place is being destroyed by us humans who prefer to throw rubbish overboard!

Disappointing.. The sea was sadly extremely dirty and there where so many tourists that we couldn't enjoy much. I went on an overnight cruise there and wouldn't recommend it. Ultra overpriced, with extra touristy activities (150$ per person organized by our hotel) and drinks and basic hygiene not included. The two stars are only going for the place itself because it would be nice under other circumstances to visit. The pearl farm was interesting to see as well but nothing wow

As someone from the Philippines, I was pretty underwhelmed with the sights and spots, especially the beach. Our Palawan Islands are definitely more vibrant  Blogs said online that Ha Long Bay was a must, but if you’ve been to Palawan, Philippines, you can skip this part in my own opinion

Die Bucht könnte schön sein, wären da nicht massenhaft Müllberge an jeder Insel.  Achtung beim Tourenbuchen: Es scheint dass man willkürlich einem Boot zugewiesen wird. Auf unserem 5* Schiff (2Tage/1Nacht) wurden auch 3 mit 3/2 eingebucht welche dann sich im Nachhinein mit dem Reisebüro herumärgern mussten bezüglich einer Entschädigung/Rückerstattung. Das Schiff war auch nur von der Kabine her 5* wert, der Rest war Maximum 3*. Die Crew war zwar steht’s bemüht, nützen tut es aber nichts wenn der trockene Reis 10 Minuten nach dem Hühnchen/Fisch/Fleisch serviert wird. Unsere 140$US war definitiv nur maximal 50$US wert.  Auch wenn der Cruise Anbieter und das Prospekt fantastisch sind auf den Bildern, skeptisch bleiben und sich alles schriftlich geben lassen.

It is beautiful, if you ignore the water polluted by plastic and by petroleum, the air polluted by hundreds and hundreds of boats, islands occupied by thousands of tourists at the same time (you basically cannot move and wait in line to see anything in the cave or on the mountain).  Please don't come here. I feel sorry that I contributed to this over tourism chaos, it was so sad to see what we do to the nature. If I knew - I wouldn't come. Government should limit the number of boats allowed into the harbour.
La géologie du site est extraordinaire mais c’est tellement pollué, hyperfréquenté que cela gâche tout, les bateaux s’entre-choquent pour s’amarrer, l’eau est marrant et tachée de gasoil.

We did the day tour and we were quite disappointed with everything actually. Every thing seemed rushed and we really didn't feel like we had time for anything. The food was okay. Not a big fan of sea food. The caves were dismal (as we had just visited the massive Phong Nha caves the day before) , the kayaking was okay and the beach was just terrible. Would not recommend the day tour to anybody as it cost us 1million vnd per head. Probably the 2day 1 night is a little more worth it. Ha long is overrun by tourists and I would suggest you all to head to Sapa instead if you are running low on time and money.

Landschaftlich absolut sehenswert und ein einmaliges Erlebnis. Aber einfach traurig, wie ein Kulturerbe so verdreckt sein darf. Das Wasser ist voller Müll. Flaschen, Kanister und Tüten. Plastik wohin das Auge blickt. Hier muss mehr Wert auf die Umwelt gelegt werden.

Hay basura flotando en gran parte de la bahía. Muchos barcos que arruinan la vista. La cueva sorpresa huele a cigarrillo. El lugar donde se hace kayak tiene agua turbia y sucia. El paisaje es muy bonito pero no para morirte de emoción. Cómo turistas seamos más limpios y exigentes porque pagamos por ver un lugar que está evidentemente descuidado.  Respecto al crucero, la mayoría tienen malos comentarios sobre calidad, antigüedad, comida, servicio, etc. Así que decidimos pagar más para asegurar algo decente, La Casta Cruise estuvo súper bien en todos sus servicios, tómalo como opción.

Fantastisk natur - men den tas ikke vare på! Det er altfor mange båter, søppel i vannet, en overflod av turister.. Det var rett og slett trist å se. Jeg var der utenfor høysesong, jeg kan bare innbille meg hvordan det er i sommermånedene. Forvent lange køer dersom du besøker grottene.

So sad to see all the trr4ash floating around.  This is a beautiful place but over toured.  There should be a visitor fee to every cruiser dedicated to cleaning up the bay...that would provided plenty of cash.  There must be hundreds of boats with 10-40 passengers.  Charge an extra $10 per person.

Globalement déçu par les prestations qui sont trop touristiques.  Nous avons organisé une croisière depuis la France en demandant (à notre hôtel d'accueil) une petite jonque de 10 personnes maximum mais nous nous sommes retrouvé sur un bateau identique à tous les autres avec une quarantaine de personnes.  150 dollars par personne pour 24h.  Cabine repas et 2 excursions compris ainsi que le transfert en minibus de Hanoï. Les repas étaient passables.  Le planning de la journée est chronométré.  Cette expérience convient au voyageur habitué des all-inclusive. La baie est assez polluée.  La visite d'une grotte nichée dans un piton rocheux est assez périlleuse au regard des normes européennes (attention pour les personnes âgées et enfants). Cependant tout le personnel est dynamique et très serviable.  Comptez 3h de trajet depuis la capitale. Pour une expérience plus typique il faut vous rendre sur l'île de Cat Bâ à partir de laquelle de plus petits bateaux proposent une croisière plus intimiste (jonque pour 2).

La Bahía de Ha Long es, sin duda, uno de los paisajes más impresionantes que he visto en mi vida. Navegar entre esas formaciones kársticas es como estar dentro de una postal o en una película. Es un lugar que te deja sin palabras y que todo viajero debería ver al menos una vez.  Sin embargo, la experiencia pierde parte de su magia por la masificación. Hay demasiados barcos navegando al mismo tiempo y se nota bastante suciedad en algunas zonas del agua. Además, durante la noche, algunos barcos se quedan anclados muy cerca del tuyo con la música a todo volumen hasta altas horas, lo cual rompe bastante la tranquilidad que uno espera cuando las actividades empiezan a las 6 de la mañana. A ratos parecía más Ibiza o Magaluf que un enclave Patrimonio de la Humanidad.  Aun así, la belleza de la bahía es incuestionable. Solo recomendaría gestionarlo con más control para preservar el entorno y ofrecer una experiencia más acorde con el lugar tan especial que es.

Bohužel znám místo nejen z fotek, ale i z osobní návštěvy. Na toto místo jsem se několik let velmi těšila.  Bohužel nejen díky horší viditelnosti, ale i všudypřítomným odpadkům v moři jsem měla pocit, že výlet byl ztráta času. Moc mě to mrzí, ale nedoporučuji - duben 2023
ベトナム世界遺産 昇る龍 中国語でシャンロンからハロン湾の名前に繋がったという話もあるハロン湾 ハノイから車で約一時間半！ 正直ハロン湾観光は一度で十分。 何度も行くところではないです 鍾乳洞などライトアップが微妙です。 すべるので注意が必要 スニーカーがベスト♪ 中国の桂林に似てるというが。。。どうなんだろう 食事つきのツアーはあまりけちらない方がオススメです！

The bay is beautiful, that is for sure...HOWEVER, it is very sad to see how dirty the water is and the little care the vietnamese people give to this unique place that bring so much money to them. We went kayaking in Halong Bay and cut it short because we were litterally kayaking in garbage. I wish I was exagerating but I am not unfortunately. Even our tour guide was embarassed about it.

The water along the bay is very, very polluted which really ruins the experience. You can't appreciate the place while the plastic bottles, slippers and noodle soup floats next to you while you do kayaking. We didn't even think of swimming there. I counted around 20 cruiser ships in the bay and they all earn money from this place and should be the first one to keep the place clean.  We won't be back for sure.

Mejor venir en cruceros los mejores posibles ya que las intalaciones pueden ser algo viejas y poco cuidadas. La higiene suele escasear, pero tambien por los propios turistas. Le pongo una puntuacion baja porque pese a que el sitio es precioso esta lleno de basura, vimos al propio capitan del barco tirar toda su basura por la borda. Si no cuidan este sitio no va a durar mucho mas, varias zonas ya estaban cerradas para el baño, y el kayak consistia en ir esquivando islas de basura, terrible...

Le doy dos estrellas porque la bahía está LLENA DE BASURA. Es una vergüenza el poco cuidado que tienen de la zona. No sé quién es el responsable de todo esto pero tampoco veo que se estén tomando medidas, incluso lo tienen normalizado. No me he querido bañar del asco que da el agua. Una lástima tremenda

Tłumy ludzi, dużo statków. Masowe wycieczki. Brudna woda, pływające śmieci. Z plusów to rozsądna cena za piwo, taniej niż na statku, co pozwoliło przetrwać wyprawę. Niestety miejsce jest przereklamowane, każdy się nim podnieca, a jak ktoś był w podobnych miejscach to nie robi żadnego wrażenia. Owszem jest ładnie, ładny zachód słońca. Ale ludzie robią syf, na statkach, na lądzie, wciskają się w kolejki dramat. Ogólnie zorganizowana wycieczka to typowa masówka. Jeśli ktoś pisze ze było super, to naprawdę musiał być pierwszy raz i nie widzieć innych wysp np. Tajlandii czy Filipin
岸上大酒店林立，街邊海鮮店一家比一家沒生意，都是完全一模一樣的商品以及海鮮，花了85usd參加了一天下龍灣行程，海面上的山水一開始驚艷，接下來就是一模一樣的景色重複到了下午，水下是一片黃澄澄，看不到東西，不會建議任何朋友去玩，真的無聊。

Most crowded touristic place I’ve been in my life. Because of that, I wasn’t able to enjoy it much. I preferred places like Ninh Binh 100% more. If you don’t like crowds or making queues… avoid! Putting 2 stars because despite all of what I said, views are nice. But you can get those views in maaany other places in Vietnam.

Cuidado con la agencia con quien contratas el crucero pq hay demasiados intermediarios y al final no tiene nada que ver lo que contrataste con lo que realmente ofrece el crucero. No merece la pena pasar la noche y pagar 150€. Recomendamos tour de 1 día por 35€. SUERTE 🤞 …
想像していたものとは違かった。開発しすぎたホテル群 さびれた熱海みたいな街並み。 海はゴミばかり。ボードから捨てるゴミ。 残念… 洞窟の中は照明入れすぎでディズニーランド。 自然のままがいいと思うけど。 みなさん行ってみてください。

Contratamos Alisa premier Cruise para pasar la noche allí.  El lugar es bonito pero hay tanta masificación de gente que no te permite disfrutar. Hicimos una noche de barco y creo que no hace falta, con solo dar una vuelta por allí en barco, ver la cueva y Ti top ya hay más que suficiente.  Fuimos a contrarreloj en todo momento las actividades que realizamos (cuevas, kayak, Ti top island, clase de cocina) estaban masificadas de gente, cosa que no te permite relajarte ni disfrutar del momento. Aunque esto ocurre con todas la compañías.  El barco estaba muy limpio y bonito. El personal inmejorable, todos muy atentos en todo momento.

The area is being destroyed by pollution, trash, and unstoppable turism. Limeston cliffs are beautiful, the problem is that we are destroying everything around them. It's probably the most turist place in Vietnam and unfortunately the one I liked the least. We should do something to preserve this area before it's too late. If you are in Vietnam I would tell you not to come here since it's not worth it, just go to Ninh Binh which is nicer and a more dynamic experience. During the boat trip in halong bay you will see how dirty and oily the water is, and the only place where it's allowed to swim is a tiny Beach packed with people and brown, smelly water. The whole boat trip has just been so sad...
Mejor visitar Nihn Bihm que es lo mismo y lo ves todo mucho mejor y más bonito, yo no volvería a ir

Only visit Ha Long Bay for a 2D/1N cruise if you want a cookie cutter tourist experience shared with a thousand other people. Despite spending nearly 24 hours on a boat, you actually see very little of Ha Long Bay, as the cruise ships spend most of their time parked right next to each other in a spot from which you can still see the main departure port not too far off. The cruise companies are unfortunately all on the same schedule - it would be a much nicer experience if they coordinated and didn’t all go to the same spots at the same time. Although the scenery is nice and our cruise staff did their best, our experience wasn’t worth the price we paid. We’ve seen similar scenery in Thailand and Malaysia with cleaner water, nicer beaches, and fewer crowds at a fraction of the cost.
Very disappointing. A lot of SMOG, A LOT OF tourists and the water is full of plastic and dirt. Maybe it is the wrong season to visit the Vinh Ha Long but I would not recommend it.
Przereklamowane, woda jest wstrętna, a cale miejsce jest mocno zmienione przez edytory graficzne podczas prezentacji na internecie
NUL ! Je sais pas si c’est à cause du temps mais ça n’en vaut clairement pas la peine Ne dormez surtout pas dans le bateau c une perte de temps et d’argent

Beautiful scenery but the tours themselves are not good at all 🤯 We did a day trip from Hanoi and everything was rushed, lunch at 11.30, extremely crowded (and its off season). An overall experience that doesnt enhance the natural beauty at all unfortunately! We have travelled all over south east asia and it was probably the worst tour we have ever done. The worst thing is that we dis the luxury version and supposedly it was the beat option we found.

Un bonito lugar, lástima que el agua esté muy sucia, desde el barco pudimos ver mucho plástico flotando en el agua, las autoridades deberían imponer un mayor control y grandes sanciones a quien tire basura. Fuimos a hacer piragüismo y vi como el personal que barría el muelle tiraba la basura al agua. Por lo demás la vista de los islotes es una maravilla.

Lots of natural beauty but unfortunately mass tourism has led to a considerable amount of waste and pollution, all visible from boats.  Government needs to intervene to organise and mass clean up, manage tourism capacity and potentially tourism tax.

The place is beautiful, but don`t book 2 days 1 night cruise. We regret doing that, we were very tired by the itinarary, our day had to start at 6 am, as the checkout was at 9.30. 10min tai chi at 6.00, fishing squids with a bamboo stick, trip to the overcrowded cave - all cruises have the same itinerary and it is just not worth the money. I think it would be better to take a 1 day trip or rent a private boat.
Too many boats and tourists, pollution in the air detracted from experience
以一個台灣人來越南玩20天的感覺，台灣的風景遠比越南漂亮，台灣人也比越南人熱情（對於外國人來說），台灣的食物也比越南好吃選擇更多，台灣也不會三不五時有人想坑你的錢錢，我不知道為什麼越南外國遊客那麼多，但也無所謂，我生在台灣決定實在很幸福。

A party of friends spent 2 nights  and 3 days cruising the Bay.  Our Athena cruise was good.  Friendly staff, very attentive, good food and plenty of activities.  However, this area is an ecological disaster.  If you want to see party boats and listen to Abba, across the water, at night, looking at multicoloured lights...this is your place.  If you are a geology  freak and like caves...this is your place. I saw less than 10 native, living things in 3 days.  3 Eagles ( looking for a feed) an Egret, a crab... and a sad fish in a fish farm swimming in the diesel residue. Ecological disaster.
I traveled through Vietnam from the North to the South. This was the only place I did NOT like. There is trash everywhere on the water. Shame on you!

Sarebbe un bellissimo posto ma ha un livello di sporcizia imbarazzante. Canoe che gitano tra immondizia che galleggia. Come il governo vietnamita non intervenga con l esercito in maniera radicale per pulire non è  sopportabile. Un  patrimonio del genere totalmente deturpato

as an Indonesian, i found ha long bay as below par. the water is green and cloudy, unlike the water in most of indonesia's island. besides, I found a lot of trashes while i was kayaking. this fact indicates how polluted the water is.  we went there by cruise and the activites that they provided were kayaking, visiting ti top island, and visiting a cave. the kayaking and visiting the cave were an amazing experience except ti top island. it was crammed with a lot of people to the point where u didn't even get enough space to just strolling around the beach. seriously if budget isn't an issue, just visit Bali, gili trawangan, or sumba instead of ha long bay. they are totally much more worth it than visiting ha long bay  for your information, i spent 125 usd for 5 stars cruise and 15 usd for the transportation from my hotel to ha long cruise port. what a priceless experience that i don't want to undergo it again

The Ha Long bay is magnificent and we enjoyed our visit.  However the site is overly exploited... there are hundreds of large boats leaving at the same time, parking in the same spot... Activities feel rushed (30min kayak, 30min commercial pearl farm, 60min cave walk).  The water is polluted and we avoided going for a swim. The „beach » is tiny and overcrowded. We did see plenty of trash and plastic floating in the bay...  Lavender cruise felt very commercial... the crew seems to be motivated to sell more drinks, or pearls on shore...  Ha long bay is for sure very impressive but the overall experience was mixed because of the pollution, and over exploitation of the site.
汚れた海、人の多さに辟易した。明らかなオーバーツーリズム。風景も東南アジア、中国西南部によくあるカルスト地形にすぎないと感じた。静かな旅を好むなら避けるべき。ただ巨大な鍾乳洞は一見の価値がある。

Very pretty but unfortunately polluted and wrecked…(tourism like me is probably at fault for that) and just poor rubbish disposal. Water is not great to swim in, very murky and u can see oil floating on top pretty much everywhere very disappointed… so far Europe has better beaches than alot of the asia places ive been to, Phi Phi islands in phuket is the only one i loved so far… i wouldnt waste 120 dollars to go here tbh and rather spend it in another place.

Ha Long bay is beautiful, but the experience is sullied by overcrowding, corralling of tourists through a few specific sights at the same time. Expect to join a continuous queue through 'surprising cave'. To be fair, lack of time meant we only did a standard one night cruise package, and maybe with more time you can find quieter options. Our visit also marred by high levels of air pollution at the time (AQI 160+) which affected visibility. There was a continual small of fumes from boat diesel engines.

I saw what was being released from the - 5 star cruise ship- as we were coming up to check out and the garbage boats came along. Some brownish effluent as the boat maneuver was taking place. Yes the bay is polluted with more than just the visible obvious plastic wastes. Can’t believe I swam in that.  I began to question hygiene and quality onboard after they served a dinner over a couple of hours. Always a fan of spring and summer rolls. However to serve up crab sticks as three of the courses under the disguise of squid is insulting. I guess the amazing staff on the boats don’t realize themselves as they were seriously questioning whether I had a problem with the meal and returned dishes untouched but Someone is laughing at all us dumb tourists paying for 5 star accommodation and food and receiving deep fried crab stick in seaweed and shredded crab sticks as a seafood soup!!!! I must admit I laughed too as I realized that was an intentional insult and explains the general attitude towards tourists  Vietnam is only a few years away from becoming an Egypt or a Morocco type experience  I I strongly suspect the 5 star reviews of this place on here and trip advisor etc are gamed by the owners of the boats who are screwing the bay for as long as possible to squeeze out as many dollars before it loses the protected un status  Unesco park should question what is happening here

If you were In Phillippines El Nido palawan, Thailand Phi Phi islands or other places with rock formations and lagoons - you will not like this experience. Full of indians, tourists and the tour is boring itself@

Well...yes. See it, but, avoid the overnight tours. Do a day cruise. Did a 3D/2N in early November, 2022 - with a minimum of 17 other boats as everyone travels together and does off-boat activities at the same time. The whole place was too busy and with things like kayaking all being done concurrently, it feels like rush-hour traffic every morning - and morning is when everything happens. Also, "cruise" is misleading. The ships are only underway after they pick-up new passengers and before they get rid of some. Over my three-day "cruise," I estimate we were actually underway for less than 30 minutes total.

Ok, so the setting is unique and beautiful, from a distance, but when you take a closer look it is difficult to see past all the tourist boats and the trash in the water.. is it a jellyfish?... no it’s yet another plastic bag.. basically if it’s trash and it floats you’ll find it somewhere in Ha long Bay.  I actually saw tourists collecting trash and putting it in their kayaks as they paddled through one of the bays, it’s pretty embarrassing.  In my opinion if you have limited time in Northern Vietnam don’t waste your time with Ha Long bay, visit Ha Giang and do the 4-5 day motorbike loop instead, the scenery is truely spectacular, and it’s loads of fun.

Yes, Halong Bay with it's limestone islands is beautiful but what didn't escape me on this 2 day, 1 night visit to this heritage site is the pollution and the total disrespect for this once beautiful, pristine wonder. Shame on us humans and shame on the officials responsible for looking after this part of the world. All I came away with was anger, no, rage at what I saw!!

This place is littered with lots of floating rubbish. Waters are so polluted that there is no fish left and no marine birds either. Too many boats full of tourists and fishermen villages full of junk and polystyrene boxes. Some islands beaches are covered of rubbish. There is an enormous lack of respect and education for this natural beauty. I m sure that mother dragon would not approve of this human behavior. Very heartbreaking.

One of the most beautiful places on earth! But there was so much trash on the water that it made me sick to see. I'm giving it a two star rating so the government and UNESCO know that this is a big issue

Byłem na Ha Long Bay 3 razy (2010, 2013 i 2019). Niestety z każdym pobytem było gorzej. Pierwszy był wspaniały, drugi - gorszy a trzeci - totalna strata czasu. Pozmieniało się na Ha Long wiele. Nie dopuszcza się turystów do dużych jaskiń, nie pływają już te same łódki tylko stare łajby. Obsługa na nich także pozostawia wiele do życzenia.
Disappointing. Very crowded. The water is full of garbage. If you’ve been in Thailand Phuket or Krabi, it is not worth going to Halong bay

The place as a nature spot is amazing, unique in the world. It’s really sad to see the exploitation of the place, the sea is full of garbage and there’s dirty everywhere as I never seen before. You literally navigate in a sea of dirt floating everywhere. Totally sad and disappointing
The view and landscapes are beautiful but the water is filthy full of rubbish. Not much to do or see in the area.
Beautiful but so polluted with plastic and packed with tourists. Ruined by over tourism and trash everywhere.
A very beautiful paradise indeed but its so commercialized. Overcrowded with tourist, it feels like we were in a crowded supermarket.. Boats everywhere, extremely polluted.
Dwudniowy rejs statkiem przereklamowany - tyle zapłacisz za krótszy rejs - widoki te same.
The best way to see this place is just google an image . The experience is similar to Phuket. The locals are aggressive. Not worth it
The scenery is wow... The sea is disgusting... Plastic floating everywhere... Beaches covered with plastic. It was truly traumatizing....
La bahía en si es preciosa pero como está organizado es horrible..super turístico,.mal cuidado, sucio.
The place beautiful, but some the local people not honest.... specially motor riders...
Meh.  Plastic all boats park in 1 spot.
We stayed on the Dragon Legend 1  in a lovely room, great service & food
하노이가면 가볼만하긴한데 시간이 아깝네요
Leider sehr überlaufen und nicht genießbar
my friend drowned
dissapointment

We just stayed one night, already there was no sun, which takes away some charm, but the most amazing thing is the boats, a real highway, some real nightclubs, a lot of pollution and the smell of diesel oil, after we participated in all this, so I'm in a bad position to criticize

The place has so many boats.  Tourists get in and off like cattle.  The water in photos shows a clear blue water.  It was more brown. I would not swim there.   When we went on excursions, it was like a train of people.  You could only take one small step at a time because of all the people.  The boat staff were very nice. I cannot say it was something I would do again.
Many visitors visit the bay and go island hopping. Beautiful nature.  Nice scenery. Good for relaxing and enjoying the bay.
Mass handling, full despite heavy rain, turbo tourism and (Asian friendly) mass transit)Scenic very interesting.

It was totally too crowded and overwhelming. Every route is the same, every boat is the same. Prices are unusually high and people act like a pack. More control is needed to protect such spectacular scenery. From the moment you arrive at the harbor, you too will be turned into just a sheep.

The location is very suggestive but very different from the official photos. It is very crowded and very dirty, especially because of the many ships that “station” there to allow tourists to spend a day and night, with a relative excursion to visit the attractions of the bay. It is a UNESCO site to be protected!

We chose to take advantage of Klook's relatively cheap day cruising tour, visiting three sites: caves, kayaking, and Tea Top Island. Personally, kayaking was recommended (even a couple without physical strength had no problem enjoying it), but I felt like I didn't need to visit Tea Top Island because it was so crowded and the water quality was so low I couldn't bring myself to enter. I think it's better to visit at least once, but I think a day trip is enough. Also, if you are visiting on a day cruise tour, you may want to carefully look at the course you are visiting (we recommend you choose a course that will allow you to visit a less crowded area, even if the price is slightly higher).

We had a lovely time exploring Halong Bay and staying overnight on one of the boats. The vessel itself was beautiful — comfortable, well-kept, and with just the right amount of luxury. What really made the trip special, though, was the staff. They were incredibly kind, attentive, and genuinely went out of their way to make sure we were well looked after.That said, it was honestly upsetting to see how much pollution there is in the bay. Floating plastic and debris were a common sight, and it felt like such a shame in a place that should be teeming with natural beauty and wildlife. Halong Bay is stunning — it deserves better protection and care.It’s an unforgettable place, but we hope more attention is given to preserving its environment for future generations.

This was a well organized three day cruise. The bay us full of these cruiseboats. It becomes a little too crowdy, and the bay is full of floating litter. It was better further out, but sad to see so much pollution in a place that could have been very beautiful.

When booking transport from Hanoi airport, be cautious. There are many scammers around. We were charged ten times the normal fare—they claimed it was a metered taxi, but it wasn’t.” It’s safer and more reliable to arrange an airport pickup through your hotel.”

We went there in good spirits and booked a deluxe cruise , the ship was fine, but at some point we come to an island where a great beach would be seen. 50 boats docked at once and thousands of people walked onto the island, it was busier as in tokyo, the conclusion is that it is beautiful, but too crowded!

The place is stunning and glad I have had the privilege of seeing it but when you look close the insane amount of boats to looks to be taking its toll on the place would I go back no as I would only be contributing. There is a lot of rubbish in the water when you look close and oil/diesel scum floating in the water not one person from our boat wanted to swim or fall from there kayaks.

We were happy to make a day at Halong Bay so well known. So it's a motorway with a boat, fuel oil on the water, floating pieces of polystyrene and we take everyone to the same places, Tip Top and the cave of surprises and then bamboo boat or kayak. Really such a beautiful place should be protected. On the boat we were given a snack at the end of the trip and we were served small snacks in bags that flew everywhere and in the water, except that at the entrance you are forbidden bottles of water? Really you have to save this magical place but it will not be done it generates too much money. We were really sad to see this landscape so damaged

Ha Long is indisputably beautiful. On occasion during our visit the crowds thinned and we were able to appreciate the rock formations, islands and inlets. But unfortunately the golden goose is in danger. Day trips and overnight tours now flood the region. The quayside is lined with derelict or incomplete buildings, giving it a dilapidated air. The water in the bay is brown and filled with tourism’s grim signature, the plastic bottle. I was told that as many as 1000 small boats visit each day. It’s a waterborne invasion. The authorities need to limit licences, clean up the bay and insist the harbour developments are completed. Different cruise routes need to be agreed as at the moment they all follow the same itinerary, leading to overcrowding.I’d suggest looking for independent suppliers, or look for other options in lovely Vietnam.

Our overnight cruise was quite disappointing. We're not exactly into cruises but this seemed the only way to get around. If you like cruise boats then this might be fine for you.Honestly, if you don't mind floating around in a sea of garbage then you might enjoy it. The bay is really polluted, with plastic and styrofoam floating everywhere. This alone made it difficult to get past the immediate pollution problem in order to enjoy the views. The islands are very pretty in themselves.The experience of transferring from Hanoi to Halong Bay, then back again, for a one night stay on a boat was not worth it for us. It's at least a 2-3 hour journey, and you end up spending less than 24 hours on the boat.

Our family trip to Halong Bay was a blend of awe and exhaustion. The mystical caves were truly enchanting, with their eerie formations and hidden stories. However, the sheer number of tourists made it feel more like a theme park than a serene escape. The various activities and tours offered something for everyone, from kayaking to squid fishing, but they often felt rushed and overcrowded. While the bay’s beauty is undeniable, the hype can feel overrated when you’re jostling for space. A memorable experience, but not without its flaws.

We did a so called 2 day cruise (reality is less than 24 hours on the boat). The boat was fine and we had an upgraded cabin which gave us a nice upper deck cabin and balcony. Decor was a bit tired. The staff were nice and the food perfectly decent (although tea or coffee at breakfast was not included in the price!). There was kayaking and trip to caves in a small boat included. It all felt quite rushed and, not surprisingly, formulaic. What was so disappointing and sad was how much rubbish there was in the water and piled up in inlets and the water was dirty with an unpleasant oily slick on the surface. Perhaps if you go further out on the longer cruises you get away from this. There were a lot of large cruise boats in the area. It is a great shame that the cruise companies making money from tourism don’t work together to keep the area clean. I wouldn’t want to do it again and to be part of the cause of the pollution in the area.

We visited Halong Bay with high expectations, and while the limestone karsts were undeniably stunning, we couldn’t help but feel it’s a bit overrated. The views were breathtaking, especially at sunrise, but the overcrowded boats and touristy vibe took away from the serenity. It’s worth seeing once, but don’t expect a hidden paradise. For couples, it’s romantic—if you can find a quiet spot. Overall, a mixed experience!

Cruising on the bay is quite crowded. I counted 38 other tour boats and those were just the ones that I could see. If you’re looking for serenity and isolation, this is not it. Plus the air quality was so bad from the pollution in Hanoi, that visibility was poor. It’s unfortunate, as this was probably an unspoiled area in the past. The caves are OK to visit but nothing spectacular. We expected more.

Very very crowded. Be prepared to fight for your photos! If you go from Hanoi it’s a long day. The beach for swimming okay and the swimming area is tiny. All the boats leave at the same time so pick wisely.

It's definitely...spectacular, I guess. All those limestone karsts sticking out of the water are pretty impressive, can't deny that. We did the boat tour thing, which was fine, I suppose. The peaceful atmosphere they promised? Well, it was peaceful-ish, if you ignore the other 50 boats doing the exact same thing. We kayaked for a bit, which was nice, but also a bit sweaty. The food on the boat was okay, nothing to write home about. Overall, it was a thing we did. Glad we saw it, but not sure we'd do it again.

No, I would not visit Halong Bay again. Tragically empty and unfinished hotels, on shore, and multiple glorified houseboats in garbage strewn waters. Mostly at anchor, if not for the friendly and hard working people, I would have given our one night cruise two stars. The food was better than average, but drinks expensive for Vietnam.

Although this place has been widely advertised by numerous films, first of all 007, the reality is less beautiful than you might imagine: if you are not lucky to have a beautiful sunny day, the cloudy sky will cover the colors and everything will turn a greenish, from the islands to the sea. the sea is quite dirty with large pieces of polystyrene floating, and you will find yourself sailing between these places among many other boats full of tourists. So if you plan to visit Vietnam just for this bay, forget it, but if you come to Vietnam for all its other beauties then one day in this bay you can do it.

Weather is too hot and water is smelly.Unclear water lately.The cruise was very good and tour guide was very kind and provide many information with good english speaker. We purchased 4 hours cruise and for these season it enough for only 4.

Halong bay is undeniably stunning, with its emerald waters and towering limestone formations. We enjoyed a cruise with kayaking and a cave visit, among various other tours available. While pollution is sadly noticeable, the natural beauty still shines through. A must-see in Vietnam, but be aware of environmental impact.
Hè đến cho các cháu đi chơi mà chặt chém kinh thật. Phòng hơn 1 triệu mà thua nhà nghỉ ở hn, giá Hải sản thì đắt gấp 2-3 lần. Làm ăn thế này thì thua, sau chỉ vào biển miền trung cho lành .

Ha Long Bay itself is beautiful. We took a day trip on one of the junk ships, and the views were breathtaking. Contrary to some other reviews, we didn't notice any litter or pollution while on the boat.It's true that the bay is full of cruises and junk boats carrying tourists. However, when you're on the boat, it's not much of a nuisance as the bay is so vast you hardly notice them. You can even take pictures of the bay without any other boats in sight if you frame them correctly.However, most cruises include a few stops, and this is where it feels crowded. There simply is not enough space for all the ships to dock simultaneously, so they end up fighting for docking space, constantly honking and ramming each other to make way. The ships drop off tourists and then sail to a further location to wait. After each activity, you have to wait for your boat to ram its way back to the dock so you can board. This aspect of overtourism degrades the entire experience.We all agreed that a simple cruise with lunch or dinner served on the boat and no stops would have been sufficient. None of the stops we made (beach, caves, kayaking) were worth the hassle and crowds.

We took a luxury cruise to celebrate our honeymoon. The boat was spectacular however the surrounding area is filled with rubbish and floating litter. Even during excursions you are constantly surrounded by polluted water.
Hạ long đẹp nhưng thực sự quá đắt đỏ và đông khách du lịch. Mình thấy không xứng đáng với số tiền bỏ ra. 1 lần ngủ đêm trên tầu là quá đủ.

We went on a 5 star tour , one night It was a nice experience but in contrast to some of the other things we did it was expensive It was comfortable , lovely ship , good food and attentive staff , but 1 night trip was plenty The bay itself was crowded with ships and very polluted We had a nice trip on a boat run by locals If I was to do this again , I would choose a trip that went to a less popular part of the bay , further north or just do a day boat trip.

Halong Bay offers an undeniably beautiful landscape with its emerging reddish formations. Unfortunately, the site suffers from overcrowding and visible plastic pollution.  Our cruise, although pleasant, was surrounded by other boats, and the presence of waste spoiled the natural beauty. A site to visit with conscience!!!
Was a nice cruise with some extra stops, but boat crews got very pushy at the end for tips and extra charges. As I was hoping for a lot from this part of the trip to Vietnam was disappointed overall.

The stunning limestone karsts rising from emerald waters created an exotic landscape that left us in awe. However, the bay can get quite crowded, taking away from the serenity we sought. Despite the crowds, we managed to find moments of tranquility, especially during early morning and late afternoon. 😒

The natural beauty of Halong Bay is truly remarkable. The islands are iconic and general scenery is well worth the visit. Unfortunately, there are simply too many cruise ships for the area to be managed sustainably. Surface oil is evident in many places, as is plastic rubbish that blights the whole experience. Hearing karaoke blast out from ships moored in the bay overnight just felt like this compounded tourism gone wrong. I’m glad I visited, but wouldn’t ever visit again.
Not good as expected. The area was quite crowded tourists. The boats were packed, making it difficult to fully enjoy the scenery.

Disappointing but probably because it was gloomy and cloudy and we did not pay to go for the longer tour where iconic karsts are. The walk to caves was interesting given the formation of stalactites and stalagmites. Seafood lunch on board was exceptionally good.

Just after twelve we reach the large harbor building from where smaller tender boats take people to their floating hotels. After all, those hotel boats are moored far from the quay, in the bay. The beauty of Halong Bay, which should have been a highlight of this trip, loses its glory because the weather gods are not favorable to us. It won't get any better on our second day here either. Too bad, but no one's fault!Once on the boat we are treated to a welcome glass and can immediately sit down at the table. It will be a variation on the 'set' menu.The extra in this menu comes when the waiter puts on plastic gloves and starts peeling the prawns at the table. They accompany a stuffed crab shell.
