The spring rolls are well done and covered with a delicious sauce. The squid with vegetables is also a hit and the fish fillets are just as good. The enthusiasm for lunch and dinner is in stark contrast to the cabin. This one is substandard. Too small and worn. The wardrobe of 1 x 1.2 m and 25 cm deep is full of clothes hangers, but nowhere to hang them. The tropical wood of the cabin is in serious need of restoration. We have to put our clothes on the floor. The double bed is barely 1.40 m wide and only accessible on one side. I don't want to waste any more words on it. Perhaps I was blinded by the beautiful photos on the internet, but this cabin does not meet even lower expectations. Add to that the constant stench of fuel oil that permeates everything (our cabin is at the back, above the engine) and the noise between adjacent rooms (horizontally and vertically).Based on the travel brochure, we thought we would take a nice luxurious cruise between the 1900 karst rock islands, but then it turns out that the boat barely moves from place to place. This is not a cruise, it is a hotel in a boat almost anchored a few hundred meters from shore. The visits to Luon cave and Titop island are done with small tender boats.

Part of a guided tour with a night on a boat on the Bay.  Not at all as beautiful as I expected as the water was not clear, and rubbish accumulating in nooks and crannies on the islands, with lots of floating oil type slick.  Too many boats out there at the same time, visiting the same highlights.  Glad I've done it, but not worth going again, ever.

So very very sad to see the pollution in what should be one of the most beautiful places!  We chose an overnight cruise to a 'quieter' part of the bay, Lan Ha Bay!  So many boats and nothing visibly being done to preserve this UNESCO World Heritage Site.  We were invited to swim before dinner by the boat staff but the water has a 'film' on the top and lots of rubbish of all sorts floating around so there's no way we would have risked it.  I didn't see one person swimming in the water over the two days we were here.  Canoeing was the closest we came to the water.  I tried to find an excursion which was classed as 'eco friendly' but this doesn't exist as far as I could find. Please please the local government and excursion groups need to sit up and take action before this place is spoiled beyond repair.This is such a beautifully stunning place which is being ruined by lack of respect/action.

Entering Halong bay on a cruise boat gives a great impression of the uniqueness and scale of this place.  Unfortunately, look a bit closer and you see how polluted and litter strewn the bay has become.  Options for a small boat cruise through the islets had been fully booked up and as it happens, we were lucky as the alternative trips were really good.  This beautiful place needs better management.

Halong Bay is one of the most breathtaking places on earth. The beauty of nature is beyond compare. But, the pollution and the plastic just everywhere … no words. It’s terrible to see what mankind is doing to this planet, and it’s difficult to understand why the Vietnamese authorities are not reacting

I felt a bit conflicted about this location when I left. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site but is far too touristy, polluted and lifeless to really enjoy. I felt guilty about just being there and contributing to it all. There appears to be no marine life in the water, there's garbage floating around everywhere and I certainly wouldn't swim in the water. The Vietnamese Government have cleared out most of the fishing villages which have lived in the Bay for generations because they cause some of the pollution apparently. The locals are now entirely dependent on tourists. It is a sad place. I would have loved to have visited 40 years ago.

This area is a Unesco World Heritage site yet is treated like a waste disposal bin. We had an overnight cruise as part of a tour we were on. The boat and the crew were lovely and the islands are stunning apart from the horrendous amount of litter, either floating or piled up on the beaches. It is a terrible shame and something that we see across the world wherever we travel. We stopped at a beach and I refused to swim as there was litter washing up and so many boats in the water that I concluded that the water would be highly polluted. My Husband did swim and a couple of days later his swim shorts absolutely stank, despite being washed through.  I actually did a litter pick and cleaned the shoreline. I know that people are trying to clean it but we saw a local fisherman throwing all of his waste into the water, you have so many commercial ships coming back and forth and of course, litter washed in by the tides and tourist litter that there should be fines implemented for anyone seen doing it. A place I had so been looking forward to visiting and such a shame for the local people that are trying to earn a living and who try so hard to keep it clean.

Ha long bay is breathtakingly beautiful until you look at the water. So much pollution it was heartbreaking. You can no longer swim anywhere - just one enclosed area that is fenced off from all the items floating in the water. Very disturbing and disappointing

A two day one night typical “cruise” will give you 22 hours in the bay. With up to 12 hours of darkness you’ll see the bay for just over 10 hours. The cruise boat you booked will be moving in the bay for 2-3 hours, and there will be another 2 or so hours moving on a small day boat for excursions. There are three groups of boats, one for each of Ha Long, Lan Ha and Bai Tu Long Bay. Only the smaller Halong bay registered boats can dock at Halong Bay harbour. The others are reached by a small day boat. Each cruise boat has a designated point A where they anchor  overnight and a point B they go to during the day. Arriving at lunch time you will move very slowly from A to B, anchor and go on an excursion, then move back from B to A  to anchor overnight. There will be another excursion in the morning, then you’ll go back to shore by the day boat, or on the cruise boat if it’s one for Ha Long bay that’s small enough. This applies to most cruises. Some cruise boats that are smaller move about more because they have more flexibility and don’t use a day boat for excursions. By small I mean under 10 cabins. Relatively few people book a 3 day 2 night cruise. If you do on one of the larger more luxurious boats they don’t change their itinerary but send you of Kayaking and swimming on another small day boat for the extra day. You will move around your chosen bay a little more, but maybe not a lot. Each night will still be at the cruise boats point A. The land/sea scape is stunning in good weather. In grey cloudy weather with low visibility it’s still very atmospheric. But the boats, especially the small ones, are not brilliant at keeping you warm in winter. If you don’t go in an excursion from the bigger boats you will see the tenders arriving with laundry, food and to pump out the waste. The trip from Hanoi is longer than you think. It’s 2.5 hours drive in Google maps, but it’s 4 or more when you figure in multiple hotel pick ups, the obligatory stop at the pearl market, and the wait at the quayside until everyone has arrived.

Halong Bay is undoubtedly a stunning place with its natural beauty and impressive limestone formations. However, I was disappointed by the amount of tourists that disturbed the peace and serenity of the place. It felt crowded and commercialized, which I think took away from the charm of the bay. Additionally, I found the prices of the tours and activities to be overpriced. I was hoping to have an authentic and tranquil experience, but instead I often felt overwhelmed by the crowds and high prices. Nevertheless, Halong Bay is a must-see place, but be prepared for the crowds and high costs.

Water quality is awful, a dumping ground for the area. Signs all around telling you not to swim as it’s that bad. Not much mention of this in travel books! Nothing to do in the town, certainly not worth staying here, day trip or overnight visit at best.

This experience was much more of a party boat, and less of a natural beauty excursion than I expected.  Travel in the bay is somewhat restricted in where boats can visit, which means that at times they were so many boats in one area that it seemed like rush-hour. The bay itself is beautiful, but it is not a wilderness experience.  I’m not saying that it’s not a worthwhile excursion, but you set your expectations to be very touristy much more crowded than anticipated.

Unfortunately, it's very touristy and there's also an incredible amount of rubbish floating around. The bay itself was very beautiful, and despite the rain, we were impressed by the magnificent landscape!

Given this place has been recognised by UNESCO as a world natural heritage site, I was shocked to see raw sewerage being pumped directly from the junk boat I stayed on into the waters of Halong Bay. Given that there are hundreds of boats on these waters one can only imagine how much sewerage that equates to daily. So, dont go swimming and dont eat the seafood from these waters as you risk getting Campylobacter!

I give the Heritage cruise line ship 5 stars. The accommodations were luxurious and modern, the staff was friendly and provided great service, the food was delicious. There were great activities from which to choose each day. However, the water is disgusting! The local fisherman who live on their boats treat the water like a garbage can, and the cruise ships dump their sewage directly into the bay. During a kayak tour, our experience of the natural beauty of this place was overshadowed by the ever-present plastic, styrofoam blocks, food wrappers, and plastic bottles.So sad! I hope the government and environmental agencies in Vietnam can clean it up. You couldn't pay me to go swimming in that water!

Halong Bay is so beautiful, However, the reason for my average rating is because of the amount of rubbish that was in the bay. It was so disappointing to see. We watched a seabird dive to catch a "fish" only for the bird to find it was rubbish when it reached the water. I hope that there can be more done to look after this beautiful place.

I was so excited to visit Halong Bay, one of the natural wonders of the world. But boy, was I disappointed. The place was full of tourists, trash and chaos. The management was terrible, they didn't care about anything but money. I felt like I was in a nightmare, not a paradise. I love Lan Ha Bay more than Halong Bay.

I was very disappointed with my trip to Halong Bay. The place was dirty and chaotic, with trash floating on the water and noisy boats everywhere. The management of the bay was very poor, as they did not enforce any environmental or safety regulations. The scenery was ruined by the pollution and overcrowding.

Halong Bay is a beautiful destination with stunning scenery and rich culture. I enjoyed the cruise and the activities on board, such as kayaking, cooking class and tai chi. The staff were friendly and helpful, and the food was delicious. However, I was disappointed by the cleanliness of the cabin and the bathroom. The bed sheets were stained and the towels smelled musty. The shower was also clogged and the water pressure was low. I think the cruise company should pay more attention to these details to improve the overall experience of the guests.

Wonderful scenery covered in trash all over. Terribly shocked about the pollution everywhere. Nobody feels responsible!Please join this appeal to the cruise agencies to take action! Together we can make an impact!

Went with friends on Ambassador Cruises for a one night trip to HaLong Bay. The rooms were spacious and clean though in terms of international cruises left a lot to be desired considering this was the best they could offer. As it was very reasonably priced the crowd aboard was not exactly the most desirable but we expected it. The on board manager had organised a welcoming event with a little dance and introduction by the various screw members which was quite nice. The meals were served in the Piano Lounge and the food was mediocre. Not to mention the fact that once the dessert was over it was not replenished! We went ashore once to explore the caves on an island which was a fascinating experience. But the entire Halong Bay is filled with such cruise ships and it makes for an unseemly sight in such beautiful and breathtaking natural surroundings.

In reality.... You could just do a day trip around the Islands..... We did a 2 day 1night trip which included food.... But in reality it was 1 day from 12.00 mid day to 11. 00am the next day..You travel about 4 kms you can see the port... So why stay...Food average.... Enjoyed the accommodation but for 250 plus dollars... You can stay in a very nice hotel. Day trip cost is around 50 dollars including transfers from HanaoiIt is a wonder of the world.... But visit a cave.. Walk 500 steps... Sit on a beach.  HummmThe islands are stunning... But it is being exploited so beware....Should you go..????... Hell yes.... Its a UNSECO site and the Avatar film set... but protect you hard  earned.. 👍👍👍As always... Your callHope this helps..

Don't know how to write this review. The bus ride from Hanoi and the boat itself was fine. High quality service and food. Looking at the extremely large number of vacant hotels, accommodation and boats in the harbour place was running at about 30% of it's normal capacity so you wonder what it would be like when things "return to normal levels". During our stay overnight, there were 27 boats in the bay, with several playing very loud party music and lots of bright lights. Is that what people have come to do ?Also why create an artificial beach ? Unfortunately my advice is to avoid this bay. There are other bays with karst hills and peace and quiet, what this place offered is no longer here.

One thing for sure, Halong Bay is beautiful with its breathtaking scenery. However, is it worth the money to take a one night cruise on a luxury cruise?  As a family we spent a while debating. The distance from Hanoi is fine - you leave around 8.30 and get to port around 11.30 (after a half hour stop at some Pearl and coffee tourist pitstop) and then take off in a mini boat to take you to your cruise boat - ours had 24 bedrooms. Don’t be disillusioned - yours is not the only boat in the bay! In fact there are loads of other lovely boats in your patch. In peak times we were told that there were 200 cruises in the whole of the unesco area spread over different sections. The smell of diesel fuel is quite overwhelming sometimes. On board we were directed straight to the buffet which to be fair was quite good (tho not as good for pescatarians). What is hysterical is that it’s a bit bun fight like as the locals literally emptied the serving dish of prawns and clams. So don’t be all British and dilly dally when it comes to choosing what you want. Then you head to your room and chill on your bed and balcony soaking up the majestic scenery. To be honest this was our favourite bit - it’s all beautiful and simple gorgeous watching the limestone world drift past you. After that the cruise lines you up for things to do - trip through the caves, swimming, kayaking, fishing, happy hour, karaoke, breakfast, checkout, brunch and it seems like every hour there is an announcement over the tannoy. In the evening there is a set dinner with about 6/7 courses - way too much food and actually it felt awkward not eating much of it due to fussiness. For me most of the courses were 6 or 7 out of 10 but there were a few courses that really weren’t suited to our pathetic western palates or pescies again. What was weird for us was that our family of 5 were almost the only westerners aboard - we were expecting a different probably more fun vibe on the boat. But obviously this will be different on different days. The staff are well-trained, remembering your first names throughout. The male manager was very welcoming and spoke really good English. And Tam, the senior host was a delight.So is it worth it? Our verdict was I hate to say it ‘not really’ (Obviously this is from our point of view so am sure lots of people love it). It’s expensive for one. We could have stayed in a really luxurious spa hotel for 3 nights for the same price. The cruise boats all go to their patch together, all moor together, all pretty much do the same thing, all queue up for the cave trip. It all feels a bit processed. In the evening we looked out and there was loads of litter floating around which was really sad and hopefully can be sorted. If cruises are your thing - with buffet style food, organising reps and function rooms style hospitality then you will probably love it. The food wasn’t really our bag - too much and all a bit corporate. If you want to go, you might be better to take a day trip or we quite fancied the sea plane for an hour which I was told is a similar price. Or better still Ninh binh.

Wonderful enchanting place spoiled by tonnes & tonnes & tonnes of plastic .What a shame !I can only describe Halong bay as sweet & sour !The beauty of the place deserves a five The pollution of plastic deserves 0!When are companies going to take responsibility for what’s going on ,I’ve witnessed this in Greece ,Italy & Cambodia!The planet will die if we don’t do something, tourism is to blame yes but it’s apparent the vast majority are too thick to be responsible for what they leave in the sea !Additionally these countries have no means of disposing of plastics safely !Please shut Halong bay before it’s too late whilst a solution is found.Astounding!!!☹️

Infelizmente o turismo em massa tirou um pouco do charme do local, são centenas de barcos fazendo o mesmo roteiro que sempre vai estar ligado a roteiro pré estabelecido e horário a ser cumprido. Infelizmente pegamos dias nublados e chuvosos, não conseguimos fazer todos os passeios propostos em nosso pacote, mas pelo que li a respeito não é muito comum conseguir todos os dias com bom tempo. o roteiro é interessante, mas nem tudo aconteceu como o planejado, ou pelo mau tempo ou porque a tripulação não se emprenhou muito em cumprir os programas. Fizemos o cruzeiro com La Regina Cruize, 2 pernoites com 3 dias.

We had booked a 3 day 2 night so called luxury private cruise (4 pax). Not cheap. Whilst the scenery is stunning, even with bad weather, the whole experience in Halong Bay reminded me of a crowded "tourist meat processor". Food on board was good, service ok, room hygiene questionable, drinks overpriced  but what really let us down was the private cruise itself. Little or no information from the "guide" and pretty much the same program as the day cruises.Average rating is because of scenery otherwise would be Poor.Had taken a private cruise on Mekong Delta during previous trip to Vietnam and it was 100% better. Halong Bay has the name, the nature but no quality service.Day trip cruise from Hanoi would have been more than sufficient.

The nature scenery is good.  But there were too many visitors especially those came from Mainland China made the islands so overcrowded that you couldn't really enjoy the place.  For example, when you walked up to those caves, you had to follow closely to the person in front of you without sufficient space and sometimes had to stop and wait for 5 minutes before you could continue walking. Overall speaking, the visit was not pleasurable.

ハロン湾自体は魅力的な景勝地だと思います。とはいえ、島国生まれの日本人にとって、そこまでの驚きはないかも。次々と現れる島々も見慣れてくると、正直飽きてしまって…。数時間ののショートクルーズだったため、この世界遺産の神髄に触れていない可能性はありますが。一方で驚嘆したのが、ハロン湾を中心とした一帯の凄まじい発展（途上）ぶりです。現在でも海上は大小の観光船で犇めきあっていますが、陸上は陸上で建設途中の疑似ヨーロッパ風のショッピングビルやホテルや別荘が山ほど。全部完成すれば、東京ディズニーリゾートなんか目じゃない規模の一大リゾート地が出現しそうですが、本当に完成するんだろうか？という雰囲気も漂わせています。そして、その一大リゾート地が日本人を惹きつけるかというと、それはまた別の話。ちょっとセンスが違う気がするので。（我が国にも安っぽい疑似ヨーロッパ建造物が山ほどあるとはいえ）現地を訪れてわかったのは、この一大リゾート地のターゲットは中国（と韓国）人観光客なんだということ（内陸部の中国人にとって、島々の景色は面白いに違いない）。おそらく莫大な中国資本がつぎ込まれているはず。しかし、風光明媚な景色だけを堪能したい人にとっては、周辺を覆い尽くしつつある過剰な娯楽施設は興ざめ以外の何物でも無いと思われます。
Wish there was a 1or 2 hr trip.  Would have been adequate. Takes 2.5 hrs each way from Hanoi and then 5 hrs cruising.Everything seems similar after an hour.

Tenía las expectativas muy altas con Halong Bay, y ojo hay que visitarlo sin lugar a dudas. No obstante, tuvo que ser un lugar paradisíaco hace muchos años cuando el turismo no inundaba estas aguas. A día de hoy, verás que las aguas están muy sucias y que hay multitud de barcos. Hicimos excursión de 3 días y dos noches, pero si vas muy justo en Vietnam te recomiendo que con un día es más que suficiente (ya que las montañas, que son increibles, son siempre iguales)
Die Halong Bay ist nur bei gutem Wetter wirklich beeindruckend, sonst kann das meiner Meinung nach nicht viel
大小2000個の岩が海につき出ています。海の桂林と言われているらしいです。船の中で食事もしました。ハエが飛んでいて食欲はおちました。海産物が多い献立でした。季節により、風景は異なるかもわかりません。

Natural wonders always hypnotized me. But then I heard noises from tour guides and their clients, the bamboo boat rower keeps yelling at each other, I no longer feel serene. This area is so crowded and it is impossible to just sit there with your kayak and enjoy the beauty.

Hmm... this visit was a part of the G Adventures tour to Vietnam. Ha Long Bay is serenely quiet in places but in others it is a mass of tourist boats all docked together for the overnight stay. Our group stayed from Christmas Eve to Christmas Day. The bar on the boat closed at 10.30pm while other boats were noisy with their karaoke singing. Lots of pollution in the sea and the boat was tired and shabby. Dinner was nice but the breakfast was grim. Could be lovely, but it is all about piling in tourists to make money.
Visited halong Bay whilst on a cruise . Took a taxi into town and visited the market an a shopping mall . SWAN CRUISES so decided on the above
奇岩で有名なハロン湾ですが、世界遺産になっているのはハロン湾の一部だそうです。行きかう船が多くて、ゆったりとした気分には浸れませんでした。

as people say how beautiful the place is -there is no second thought about it. The place is amazing . however there is only one island opened for the public which is the Titop island which is extremely crowded at any time you go . the view how ever from the top of the island is nice. they should keep some more island open to the public instead just one but then i think its not going to be great for the place it self and tourism will spoil it.

Chiariamo immediatamente un aspetto: la Baia è fantastica sotto il profilo naturale, ha un fascino mistico e le rocce che si ergono dalle acque salmastre danno vita ad un dedalo inestricabile di cime verdi in mezzo al mare. L’unico modo corretto di visitarla è farlo con una barca e qui, ahimè, veniamo al punto dolente: il turismo massivo è letteralmente esploso in questa meraviglia e si è ormai declinato (e degradato!) nella sua sfaccettatura più becera è fastidiosa. Ogni panorama è rovinato da centinaia di barche e barconi, da strutture turistiche atte ad arraffare soldi, da agenzie di viaggio totalmente scellerate, da turisti della domenica che non hanno la minima educazione e rispetto per la natura. E questo si riflette sulla qualità della visita: urla e risate fragorose in ogni dove, panorami umiliati dai selfie più beceri, guide turistiche che utilizzano le stalattiti delle grotte come tamburi fino a danneggiarle, avventori trattati come polli in un allevamento intensivo e quindi sempre mossi in gruppi numerosissimi e privi di ogni capacità decisionale anche in merito al tempo da dedicare ad una determinata zona o ad un dato panorama. Nemmeno sto a dire la quantità di plastica e di inquinamento acustico presenti. Esistono peraltro vari modi di visitare la baia, così come varie imbarcazioni.Io ho optato per una barca a 5 stelle con crociera di 2 notti e 3 giorni; francamente dedicare meno tempo a questo luogo è follia perché il tragitto resterebbe limitato ad una zona pullulante di benzina, smog e barche mentre, quantomeno, in 3 giorni si ha modo di arrivare (seppure per poche ore) in una zona meno frequentata e quindi leggermente più genuina. In sostanza si tratta di una bellissima gemma ormai opaca e graffiata dall’incuria e dalla inciviltà umana. Auspico con il cuore che accada ciò che è già successo a Maya Bay in Thailandia: la chiusura!!!

Halong Bay is, without doubt, a very beautiful place. You can see stunning sunsets there etc. However, there are so many companies offering cruises and so many boats of varying standards, the Bay is crowded with them. At all the excursion points, there are hundreds of tourists all trying to see everything- I felt overwhelmed by the crowds - it just wasn’t enjoyable. The climb to the top of Titov provided stunning views of the bay, but again there were so many people it was difficult to get a spot to see properly. With hindsight, I wouldn’t have booked a 2-night cruise - it wasn’t worth it. I would instead have done a Day cruise and had more time in Hanoi or even visited Ninh Binh or one of the other many beautiful places. Having been to Ba Be and Brian Gioc, the Bay pales by comparison. It’s very commercialised and feels “false”.

We went on a 2day one night cruise with lavender cruises and their accomodations and meals were awesome, good food, perfectly scheduled meals and day tours..Halong bay in itself was a disappointment, the water was murky brown, no swimming to be had at all, the view was stunning but the sheer amount of visitors just makes the experience disappointing and I think destroys their reefs and clean ocean.
船酔いはないです。料理も美味しく、写真もたくさん撮ってもらいました。ただ、物売りの勧誘がすごかった。

Il contesto naturalistico è spettacolare ma purtroppo la bellezza del luogo è notevolmente rovinata dall estremo inquinamento del luogo (plastica e spazzatura di ogni tipo) Personalmente non ho trovato molte imbarcazioni quindi ho potuto godermi il panorama ma è inevitabile pensare a quanto male stiamo facendo alla nostra terra. Forse le aspettative erano troppo alte ma sinceramente non ne sono rimasta colpita come immaginavo dalle immagini da cartolina che si vedono ovunque.

我是参观过三湾(兰下湾，下龙湾，拜子龙湾)之后才写这个评论。对我来说，下龙湾最大的特点就是天堂岛！景色：大名鼎鼎的下龙湾，景色不必多说。需要注意两点：游轮没有想象中多，拍照的时候反而是特色而不碍眼；在这里，由于房屋数量多的优势，而不是兰下湾，你才能体会到船行驶过程中忽然四面八方都被连绵不绝的群山包围、与大陆隔绝的感觉。水质：下龙湾的水质明显被污染了。尤其靠近码头的地方味道较大且海水泛黄，远离停靠点颜色好些，但仍不及兰下湾和拜子龙湾那般翠绿。行驶过程中能够发现漂浮的污染物。天堂岛：这是整个下龙湾的高光，几乎所有的下龙湾旅游宣传照都是在山顶拍摄的，一日游如果只有3小时的不去天堂岛亏大了！需要注意的是河内出发的路线2一般12点出港，到达天堂岛只有半个小时的游览时间，放弃海滩吧(虽然也很美丽)，抓紧时间爬上去拍照(但一定要注意安全，老年人尽力而为)。游客拥挤度：下龙湾的游客明显是最多的，而且多为大妈大爷旅行团，在这里可以遇见非常多的中国人。任何景点几乎都要排队，各种旅游线路也行程固化且老套。游客友好度：下龙湾的出发地是下龙市的团洲岛，对游客的友好度三湾最低。不可否认的是它的旅游业高度发达，但管理混乱。团洲码头的工作人员对游客爱理不理任由私人船长拉客，而价格永远云里雾里，你不知道真实的情况到底怎样。下龙汽车站的官方售票员可能会以虚假的微笑卖给外国游客高一倍的价格。汽车的价格认准LP吧，路线2的价格我提供参考：490K不包含导游餐费皮划艇。团洲岛本身像是平壤一样，豪华的高级住宿实际上没人，空洞、诡异，宛如鬼城。其它特色：路线2的溶洞终于放弃中国国内那帮五颜六色的探照灯了，统一采用昏黄色调打光，不得不说是一大进步，出口处有自然光照进来，一瞬间觉得自己到了峰牙的山水洞。对于在中国国内绝对拒绝洞穴旅游的我推荐你来看一下。
２月の後半に、ハノイから日帰りツアーでハロン湾に行きました。期待して行ったのですが、天気もどんより曇り空で、期待外れな感じがしました。でもクルーズしながらの昼食や途中で船をおりて鍾乳洞に行ったのですが、それは良かったです。

The day tour is good enough to see the sights. Won't be easy to DIY going to the harbor, so should join an arranged tour. The caves are amazing!!!However, too touristy!!! I won't swim on Halong Bay waters. The bay & the limestones are an amazing sight. But if u want paradise u can own by yourself for a day go to Palawan, Philippines instead.

For a UNESCO heritage site, Halong Bay needs to be taken care of better. Near the habour, the seas are murky and yellow—it gets better as we go in but it's nowhere near pristine clear. Lots of rubbish lurk in corners of the bay and there is a layer of oil on the surface. Cruising through the thousands of islands is a wonderful experience but it could have been better. If you want to see nicer seas and islands, Thailand is a better choice.

Le côté magique est difficile à trouver . Beaucoup de bateau ,certains avec la musique à fond  sur Le pont !!! Nous avons fait une croisière 1 nuit 2j . A faire mais finalement la baie d’halong terrestre est plus sauvage. La balade en  kayak est à faire pour comprendre que les déchets et indirectement leur traitement est un vrai problème. Ça gâche quand même pas mal le ressenti .

From Hanoi, depending on the traffic situation, it can be a 4 hr by road to the harbour. Upon arrival, the guide went off  to look for the boat captain, and purchase the tickets. Was a 1hr wait in the crowded departure complex. Rather chaotic to unfamiliar visitors! Anyways, the cruise included lunch with local dishes and white rice. Drinks will have to be purchased separately. The boat took us to Bo Hon archipelego, stop at Surprising cave to have a look at the cave chambers. The cruise also included a ride on the bamboo boat to Luon. Trust me, the boat anything but bamboo! The boatman rowed the boat around the bay and rivers. No motorised boats were allowed. The sunset is not a sight to be missed.

Nice to do a local bus tour which takes about an hour. We had a day trip around Halong Bay on the boat visiting Sung Sot Caves, some kayaking and Titov beach for a swim. It’s a great way to see the bay and we meet some lovely people on the way.
El lugar precioso pero la accion del hombre está acabado con el lugar. El agua sucia y con mucho plástico flotando . Una pena

We’re fairly impatient people so we had to dig deep on this trip!  Although the rocks are incredible to look at, there’s a lot of them, so after a while it loses its “wow” factor. As you’d expect, it’s fairly crowded with tourist boats so getting a decent picture can be hard. The boat we were on was very outdated (most of them looked the same) and uncomfortable. We had a private tour so we’re able to cut the length of trip. The chaps on the board were very pleasant and helpful (incentivized by a large tip!). The pearl farm was more of a highlight than the bay to be honest. If we were to do it again, they have the option to take a sea plane, which I think would have been way more fun and time efficient
ハロン湾は遊覧船で回ります。まずは船上ランチ。そのあと色々な奇岩をみたり、シーカヤック、鍾乳洞見学をしました。海が汚く、そこまでの景色もなく。リピートはないかな。

Che dire, l'arcipelago è stupendo. Le quasi 2000 isole offrono uno spettacolo incredibile. Però ci sono troppe cose che proprio non vanno- lo sporco. Tutto, anche l'acqua, è davvero sporco. Per strada tanti rifiuti, in mare plastica e mercantili che passano a 30m dalla costa, con un perenne odore di nafta- troppo turismo. Sembra di essere s Sharm nel periodo iniziale di crescita, cantieri ovunque, molti complessi ultimati ma completamente vuoti. Fogne a cielo aperto. E tutti i turisti, forzati ad andare tutti negli stessi posti- difficile da godere. In pratica, se non fai una crociera, non c'è nessun punto da cui si possa godere bene la vista delle isole. Se poi non fai la crociera di 2gg, il famoso tramonto (che mostrano in tutte le foto) è ovviamente invisibile, il sole tramonta verso l'entroterraInsomma, è ina destinazione ok per 2 o 3 gg, a patto di organizzare tutto bene prima e limitare le aspettative
ツアーでチャーターということで行ったのですが。食事は揚げ物や焼き物などが出て40分ほどで終わったと思いますがポーチ類やアクセサリーの売り込みが次から次へと来てハロン湾の景色を楽しむ感じではないですこれが往復続くのですかたまったものではありません、購入する気がなかったら外をずーと見ているしかないと思います。もちろん売りに来るものはお店で購入するよりもかなり高いです。
ホテルから昼食付き１日ツアーを予約、参加しました。瀬戸内海育ちなもので、どうもスケール感も合わないし、 船のコースもあったのかもしれませんが、茫洋としすぎていました。
周りが開発で土砂等が流出していて　湾の奥ですし　濁っていて残念。景観は素晴らしく　船内でのランチも　美味しかったです。途中立ち寄った　鍾乳洞も　良い風情でした。

Stayed on a boat overnight on Halong bay as part of a 10 day tour of Vietnam. Such a lovely place but so overcrowded with boats everywhere! Also sad to see plastic bottles floating in the water and on the beaches. The development on the way into Halong Bay is ridiculous - so many big buildings going up - I would hate to see this place in another 5-10 years! It is so commercialised already - we were taken to a small beach on one of the many little islands to swim but there were hundreds of other people off cruise boats also there crammed into a small “fenced-off” swimming area. There was nowhere to sit unless you paid to use a deck chair. Glad I’ve been to Halong Bay but won’t be going back again.

Beautiful place. However there are many stairs and steps to climb into the caves therefore not recommended for elderly who has weak knees. My parents enjoyed themselves in the air-con cruise and the delicious meal.

To start off you need to know I live in New Zealand...land of clean green rivers and lakes.So I spent two nights in ha long bay to get to really see the area and the sites , this first thing to note is it is really touristy,  a bit Ike Niagara Falls,  a natural wonder surrounded by high rises etc..., ha long is boarded by alot of shipping docks and massive city construction all pushed to tourism.  You don't actually travel far into the bay . And all the other boats are there with you each day. The big problem is pollution...it is really bad , oil slicks , floating debris in the water and not a clean beach anywhere.  So again I am from nz so this took me back and I was disappointed,  but talking to others that come from different parts , they loved the area. So different strokes for different folks

ベトナム ハノイ にて 日本人宿？が、あるとの、クチコミで、宿を訪問。送迎バス(ツアー参加者をピックアップ方式)で その宿近くに迎えがきて、クルージングとランチと、カヤック、洞窟見学のツアーを体験してきました。1,780,000ドン(二人で)行きは、一般道路。途中トイレ休憩あって、三時間ちょっと。ランチは何品も出来立て料理が出て大満足。(飲み物は別途有料←あとで徴収)洞窟への階段は急でご年配には辛そうでした。カヤックも女性のみでは少し苦難？しかしとても、コスパよく楽しいツアーが体験経験出来ました。ちなみに帰りは乗客の意見で？高速道路で帰れました(一人、つ、別途有料徴収)。日本人宿があって助かりました。(大家さんは日本人です)
Too Commercialized . If you like being doing a trip led by a tour guide to standard places, this is a trip for you

Such a shame that this place is becoming very developed. Not only kilometers of new building complexes, shops and apartments, the whole beach area is grazed with new sand and palm trees. Nothing wrong with is if you want a holiday place like Phuket but for us this place has lost it’s flair and nature.

C’est le passage obligé pour les voyages au Vietnam. Mais ,il y a un mais. Trop de bateaux en file indienne, le soir toutes les jonques sont au même endroit, alors c’est pas très intime même si comme nous vous êtes sur une petite jonque, vous entendrez les moteurs, la musique.... et le lendemain, la mer est sale , pleine de déchets surtout du plastique, c’est désolant et ça m’a gâché le plaisir d’etre là dans ce paysage somptueux que personne ne respecte. Nous avons fait escale sur une plage qu’un monsieur nettoyait, une goutte d’eau dans un océan ! J’ai vu Halong mais je pense 10 ans trop tard.
宿泊先ホテルのロビーが集合場所で便利でした。日本語を話すガイドが添乗し、車中でベトナムの歴史、経済、文化などを聞くことが出来きてツアー体験以上に多くに触れる機会になりました。バスは時間通りに管理され、全てがスケジュール通りでした。最後のディナーでは食べきれないローカルフードに舌鼓を打ちました。

ハロン湾というと湾だけかと思ったけど、一周ツアーで連れていかれたいくつかの鍾乳洞は幻想的でよかったです。ハロン湾クルーズは個人でシーズンオフだと、舟がいっぱいになるまで待たされるとは聞いていたけど、2時間待たされて出発。周囲は中国人観光客ばかりでした。小舟に乗るところがあって、船頭さんが舟歌を歌ってよかったです。ニワトリ型とかイヌ型とかの巨岩が珍しいとかいってたけど、ベトナム人と私とでは感動の差があるかも。一度いったらいいかも。理由は有名だから。

We travelled to Ha Long bay on a small cruise ship in Nov 2018. It was very exciting pulling into the bay but the shear volume of tanker traffic was surprising and the hundreds of "junks" was not what we expected to see. I understand that to see a bit of the tranquility represented in the photographs of Ha Long Bay you must travel to Cat Ba or other islands. The immediate area of Ha Long Bay has  population that exceeds 2 million and according to wikipedia is  a major industrial city, the second largest city in the northern part of Vietnam, and third largest city overall in Vietnam. On the morning we left Ha Long bay the air pollution was shocking.

The cruise boat and crew were wonderful. The meals were great despite having seen the kitchen. The boat and quarters adequate, not as nice as “sail Croatia” boats. The bay itself is truly a treasure. It’s unfortunate that the whole experience is overshadowed by poor air quality, litter everywhere in the bay and on the van ride from Hanoi.  The water did not look inviting due to contamination by litter.  Our crew actually elected to paint our boat while anchored for the night with oil base paint and left an oil slick in the water for hundreds of yards. The odor irritated some guests. I personally wasn’t bothered because I’ve lost olfactory sense.  My opinion is there is no concept of preservation.  I’m glad I saw the bay but I’ll have reservations recommending it to friends.  My comrades are old kayaking river rats where the philosophy is leave no trace behind.

First, the water in the bay was dark and murky. Next the scenery was not as colorful as you see in websites and brochures. Lunch that was served in boat was good though. The tour was a good experience but I will not do it again.

This was my second time in Halong Bay. It really is a beautiful area however I was disgusted with all of the garbage in the water. How could people lack such respect? There was non-stop floating bottles, wrappers, plastic bags etc If I were one of the many cruise lines I would get together with the other cruise lines and fund a clean up. The government also needs to impose strict punishment for littering. Shame! I would not recommend Halong Bay unless they clean it and respect the environment.

Yes it is beautiful but it really has become an industry that makes too much of its reputation for being the must-see place in Vietnam. Given that you spend quite a lot of time travelling and the cost is high, the overall experience it adds to your holiday isn't that great. There are plenty of other beautiful places in Vietnam (if it's a choice between this and Ninh Binh I would definitely choose the latter).

Halong Bay is a beautiful place with spectacular limestone rock formations, but as for many of these places in the world, it is suffering from too many tourists. Staying there overnight is therefore a better option to avoid the typical tourist crowds coming for a day trip from Hanoi. Hopefully they will find a system to manage the crowds, but money talks, also in Vietnam.

le site est magnifique.....sur les cartes postales mais la réalité est très différente , les bateaux sont trop nombreux , ils font tous à peu près le même circuit car il faut bien protéger la population locale qui vit là depuis des millénaires et n'accepte pas d'être envahie ,même si elle vient vendre sa pêche aux bateaux .Certains sampans sont magnifiques , le personnel est charmant et dévoué mais n'est presque pas payé . Et maintenant pour ne pas payer la taxe au port d'Hallong certains bateaux font appel à des vedettes rapides qui vous débarquent n'importe ou avec la complicité des agences , le capitaine du sampan vous fait descendre dans la vedette en prétextant un problème et voilà !!! situation vécue en février 2019.

We had a morning sail for 3 hours direct from our cruise ship.Unfortunately our weather was foggy although it did clear up enough for us to see the views for a short time.We got a cup of free tea on the boat and you could buy refreshments and souveneers.Staff were very friendly and they live on the boat.Visited a cave during the trip which I have reviewed.We love boats and did enjoy the trip.

Wir waren vor 6 Jahren da und da war es schon touristisch, jetzt ist es übertrieben. Wäre es nicht als natürliches Weltwunder erkoren worden, wären wohl nur die Hälfte da. Es ist schön, ja, aber etwas überbewertet... wenn man schon mal da ist, muss man fast gehen, aber einmal reicht glaub ich...

This place is so incredibly beautiful, however I couldn’t believe how much rubbish there was in the water. It’s such a shame as it’s been spoilt. Also there are so many boats- which I kind of was expecting, but I couldn’t believe the amount there was. We got offered to swim in the water, however none of us did as it looked so dirty . I preferred Tam Coc as it has limestone mountains but is much cleaner and there are less tourists in this area.

Es war ein genialer Ausflug, anstrengender Aufstieg mit super Überblick über die Halongbucht bei Sonnenuntergang, nach dem Abstieg ein kühles Hanoibier. Nur der Bierverkäufer hat uns betrogen, er kassierte vorher, brachte das Bier, und bestand drauf dass wir nochmal zahlen, fing an unwirsch zu werden, die verschüchterte Käuferin zahlte nochmals, das sind Betrüger, lieber woanders kaufen, nie vorher zahlen !!!!!

The place is So beautiful! I love the rocks the calm water and our great tour guide and boat crew. The rubbish was incredible to witness, the sheer amount was staggering. We went for a swim on a small beach even though there was rubbish, and that was when we found even more around our feet floating past our faces so very upsetting for a stunning area. I'm afraid I am part of the problem, I'm a tourist wanting to see this place. Too many tourists, the water is covered in boats. The building going on at the Dock side indicates more traffic, I'm afraid Vietnam will need to take a close look at this area and make changes before its too late.Still a stunning place.

Get on boat...eat lunch...turn left to take photo...choose kayaking or rafting...turn right to get another photo...and so on and so forth, you get the idea.There is no question Hạ Long Bay is worthy of it’s UNESCO World Heritage Site status, we have all seen the pictures. It’s just these places are a twenty-four-seven, three-sixty-five tourist cash cows and you are herded in and out only to be replaced by the next lot.The sad thing is is that it’s only going to get worse. So I would say go see it ASAP if you ever plan to because this is the best it’s going to be from here on in.Five star scenery, just far too many people (me included) 👍🏼☹️
We visit bus tour from Hanoi city.It takes 3hours for one way.View is good but many people in there. If you hane enough budget, better to join praivate tour

1. 하롱베이는 너무 예쁘다. 신이 만든 예술품.2. 베트남 정부는 여행 상품에 대해 국가적으로 시스템 개편이 필요하다. 하롱베이로 출발하는 교통 상품, 하롱베이에서 섬까지 가는 배 상품, 도착해서 구경하는 보트 상품, 보트에서 다시 갈아타고 구경하는 나룻배 상품 등등 내부에 세부 상품이 너무 많고 각각이 모두 유료여서 너무 좋은거 구경하면서도 기분이 상쾌하진 않았다.3. 끝 없는 팁 문화는 베트남이 고쳐야 할 고질적인 문제다. 뭐 조금만 하면 팁을 바라는 문화는 대체 어디에서 생긴지 모르겠으나, 지금은 베트남이 싸니까 가는거지 팁 문화를 없애지 않으면 베트남 경제 발전에 비례하여 관광객은 없어질 것이다. 4. 하노이에서 5000~10000동하는 생수가 하롱베이에서는 25000~30000이다. 그 외 다른 물가들도 마찬가지다. 관광지라 비싸야 하는건 알겠으나 바가지 정도가 심하다. 베트남은 현지인 물가 따로, 관광객 물가 따로여서 여행을 다녀왔다기 보다 삥뜯긴 느낌이다.결론 : 베트남은 아직 후진국이다. 근데 하롱 베이는 정말 대박 예쁘다. 한 번은 가봐야 할 곳.

This place was stunning, amazing and spectacular- and compared to the chaos of Hanoi and Saigon, sitting on the top deck of a cruise boat in the middle of the bay was relatively peaceful. BUT looking at the smog, the rubbish floating by in the water, the hundreds of boats in the bay belching smoke, the hordes of tourists being herded from one place to another and the excess of concrete commercialised construction happening, I wonder how long this place can survive? As much as I was awed by the wonders of nature I was saddened by how humans can exploit such a beautiful area to the extent that they are likely to ruin it.

北ベトナム一番の観光スポットとも言えるハロン湾。3,000もの奇岩や島々が存在し、世界遺産(自然遺産)に登録されている。非常に多くの観光客が訪れており、港からはクルーズ船が次から次へと出航していた。クルーズ船は定員が40名くらい。木材を使用した内装はキレイで、トイレにはウォシュレットが付いていた。食事の際には地元漁師が捕ってきたという牡蠣・大あさり・シャコをたらいに入れてオプション販売しており、買うと食事の際に調理して出してくれる。ただし、標準で付いてくる食事の量が結構多いのでオプションを追加すると食べきれないかもしれない。食事の後はお土産タイム。2人の女性スタッフが小物入れやスカーフ、真珠のアクセサリーなどを次から次へと持ってくる。ツアーの中で一番期待していた場所であったが、霧雨の降る天気であったこともあり、残念ながら思ったほどの感動は得られなかった。

日本語ガイド付きの日帰りツアーで行って来ました。42人乗り程度のクルーズ船にのり3時間程度のハロン湾クルーズでした。ちょうどこの日は天気が悪く、バスに乗っているときは曇りだったのですが、ハロン湾で船に乗る時には既に雨。雨の為クルーズ船の船首や屋根の上に出ることもできず、ほとんどテーブルに座ったままだったので、遠くの景色もあまり見えず。空はどんより曇って青空はなし、水の色もエメラルドグリーンを期待していたのが、濁った緑色で綺麗さがありませんでした。船の窓からではあまり“美しい”と言われる景観は見ることができませんでした。夫婦岩（闘鶏岩）は船が近くに停まって綺麗な写真を撮れるようにしてくれたので良い写真が撮れました。ハロン湾は雨の時にはお勧めしません。

Every Vietnam visitor should go to Halong Bay. It is quiet, tranquil, a place to soak in the fresh air and do some self reflection. But that was all for me. If your budget allows you, do book a private car so that the ride to Halong is more bearable. Just stay one night. The Hanoi night market and street food are more enjoyable for me.

We went to Halong Bay with high expectations having been told how good it was. Sadly those expectations were not met. The whole area was clearly once a beautiful place but is now spoilt by hundreds of tourist boats, many in poor repair and not maintained. The boat we stayed on overnight was particularly old and poor and was in need of full refurbishment. Sad that what clearly had been a lovely wooden Junk is now a neglected and dirty piece of Junk. The sea and many of the shore lines are dirty and contaminated. The caves we visited were quite impressive but the long queue and crowds detracted from them. The Vietnam government really need to get to grips with tourism in the whole area before the place is totally ruined.

Leider völlig verdreckt und kein schöner Anblick, wenn man ins Wasser schaut. Ansonsten sieht man leider nur Schiffe☹️. Wir hatten auch keine gute Sicht, vielleicht ist es bei schönem Wetter interessanter.

je suis allé au Vietnam, croyant qu'il y aurait authenticité et calme.  Quel ne fut pas ma surprise de constater les plastiques et les klaxons qui y règnent en maître.  Le flot constant des motos et des conducteurs qui foncent littéralement sur les touristes, et qui ne respectent pas les passages pour piétons est ahurissant.   Ce que j'ai constaté c'est un Vietnam complètement dénaturé avec vocation première, le capitalisme dirigé vers les touristes.  Le plastique est omniprésent partout, les égoûts à ciel ouvert aussi, dans lesquels ont déverse tout ce qui peut y entrer, y compris les huiles moteurs usagés.  Si quelqu'un me demanderais si je lui conseille d'y aller, ce serait non.

We visited Ha Long Bay on a 2-day, one night 5 Star Cruise. This is a must see along with other wonders of the world. Our boat was supposed to be the best... We had paid top dollar, The staff were great, cabin with balcony good and the food 3-4 star. The attractions with cave walks, kayaking and fishing village all good. As we looked around the bay at the 20+ other boats we noticed many visited and moored at the same spots, and several had only a handful of people on board. Our tip... look for a lower rated boat and negotiate for the best room possible. Would I  recommend a visit to Ha Long Bay.... You've got to go.

UNESCO World Heritage Ha Long Bay – an area ‘known for its emerald waters and thousands of towering limestone islands topped by rainforests’. If you see promotional images of Vietnam, this location is bound to be among them, if not the primary image. However, marketing & reality was vastly different. The smog, despite being 3 hours from Hanoi was incredibly bad and the water was littered with rubbish. Thousands of wooden boats in various states of disrepair meander around the limestone islands, all the while pumping black smoke across the water and in to the air. Any stop made on foot is a torturous experience as your need to fight your way through the hordes of Chinese tourists who visit due to the location’s close proximity to China. There is simply no kind of reverence, care or responsibility given to this incredible, natural area. It’s just treated like a consumable tourist trap where guides and locals try their best to scam the tourists and in return, the tourists damage the rocks, litter on the beaches and in the ocean and think little of the future.A real shame...
Scenery is average. If you’ve a budget and would like to go out of city life to experience fisherman village life than this is the place.

Affascinato dalle recensioni lette e dalle immagini postate, nel mio viaggio in Vietnam ho previsto due giorni a Halong Bay con relativa escursione in barca. Avendo al seguito un bambino di due anni ho evitato di dormire in barca ma optato per un’escursione in giornata. La baia è bella ma non bellissima, intasatissima di imbarcazioni di ogni tipo, sporca e con un fondale perennemente grigio. La città è fantasma con tantissimi alberghi, prevalentemente vuoti, tantissime costruzioni ... avere informazioni, anche solo su cosa vedere o dove mangiare è un’impresa. Il consiglio è di evitare la tappa nei vostri giri, per qualche foto non vale la pena spendere soldi e tempo!

partis pour s'en mettre plein les yeux, nous sommes rentrés bien decus de ce sejour , rien a dire de l'hotel paradise suite . mais la baie d'halong n'a rien a voir avec les tres  belles cartes postales que tout le monde a vues , de la crasse partout, des detritus le long des routes et trottoirs, le quai de la baie , une arnaque a touriste, des prix super gonflés et a la tete du client. l'eau de la baie brunatre, une polution  a couper au couteau, des gens pas tres  polis , plus des trois quarts des etablissements fermes, ou a l'abandons, des poussieres partout et sur tout .

Ενδιαφέρον αν και το περίμενα πιο τροπικό και η θάλασσα να ηταν καθαρή.Πολυ βρώμικα νερα.Σαν εμπειρία ηταν καλή.Απλα για να πει κάποιος οτι πήγε στο halong bay.Δεν νομιζω οτι θα πήγαινα και δεύτερη φορα.Απλα για την εμπειρία ειναι καλό.Δεν με εντυπωσίασε.Το περίμενα αλλιώς.Οι ψαράδες και οι άνθρωποι που ζουν εκει,πολυ φιλικοί.5 ωρες απο το Hanoi για να φτάσουμε στο halong bay και 2.5 ωρες για να γυρίσουμε.

Has become very polluted with garbage floating around. The over saturation of tourist boats has taken its role on Halong Bay. I would suggest doing a 2 night cruise and going to Cat Bay. The water is much cleaner with fewer boats. We stayed at bungalow island as an extension of our cruise and it was amazing!

Halong Bay, çà fait rêver! Un endroit magnifique ! Où vous verrez énormément de bateaux de touristiques!Vous pourrez vous baigner dans une mer pollué par le fuel ( c'est vraiment dommage!) La grotte à touristiques !Le petit séjour sur un bateau ? A touristes (bien qu'ils étaient très sympas)Les repas sur le bateau ? Ne vous attendez pas à des bons plats vietnamiens ! Piémontaise revisitée ( et pas dans le bon sens), cari de poulet (beurk!)Ce que j'ai préféré? Le petit tour en canoë dans un lieu magnifique !
ハノイから4時間くらいかかりました。船着場の雰囲気は、想像していたものと変わり、やたら商業的なものの雰囲気でした。ハロン湾を走行し、島々の雰囲気は、雑誌や、テレビとは違い、はるばる来たのに、という感じでした。

We did a “luxury” 2-day cruise around Halong Bay, Lan Ha Bay and the Cat Ba island. It was pretty expensive for what you actually get. In reality, it’s only a 24-hr cruise, not 2 days, and we spent more on 1-night cruise than on all of our accommodation in Vietnam for 3-weeks we were there. Was it worth it?! Definitely not!!! Halong Bay is not as pretty as you see in magazines - photos that are heavily edited to make it look amazing. The place is overcrowded by tourists, as a result it is extremely polluted. There are plastic bags floating in the water, and a permanent smog surrounds the mountains preventing any clear views. No, it’s not clouds, it’s pollution from all of the boats around the area. I was unpleasantly surprised by how polluted this place was as I was expecting a natural beauty (which was undoubtedly there maybe 10-20 years ago but is now being destroyed by excessive tourism).If I had to do it all over again, I wouldn’t.If you, however, want a great alternative to Halong Bay, authentic and uncrowded beauty with equally impressive limestone cliffs, then you should head to Ninh Binh or Phong Nha - the places that still retain a lot of charm and authenticity of Vietnam. I wish someone would have advised me against coming to Halong Bay as I would have gladly extended my stays in both aforementioned places or gone to Sapa. If you are desperate to see it, then go to Cat Ba island and book a tour from there. It will be much cheaper, and the bay around that area is much quieter. The problem with overnight cruises is that they are super expensive for a relatively average experience. The rooms are nice, but would we really pay several hundreds of dollars for a nice hotel room for one night in Vietnam, where we were able to get a lovely room (of the same standard) in the center of Hanoi old town for $25?! Even 5-star cruises are still tourist factories - they get you in, they get you out as quickly as possible, because they are readying the boat for new guests the next day. There is no personalised experience that many of them claim they offer. If you want a truly magnificent views with limestone cliffs and crystal clear waters - go to Coron Town or El Nido in Philippines instead. Now that’s a view I will never forget!

Es duro ver como uno de los rincones más bellos del planeta están siendo destruidos por el hombre.Montón de basura flotando y exceso de barcos. Todos quieren sacar el mayor dinero posible pero no se dan cuenta de que o ponen un poco de freno y sentido común o la gallina de los huevos de oro desaparecerá.

Hallo.Das Erlebnis und damit auch die Bewertung ist bestimmt stark vom Wetter abhängig.Bei klarer Sicht kann es bestimmt schon in der Bucht sein.Wir hatten leider Pech und viel Nebel und Nieselregen 🌧 Das hat diesen Ausflug etwas kaputt gemacht.Bei der Buchung des Schiffes: Achtet darauf, das euer Zimmer nicht direkt neben den Maschinenraum ist! Diese Zimmer sind lauter (z.B. Zimmer 111). Um so weiter vorn ihr seit, um so besser 😊

I’m sure many of the Day tours to Ha Long Bay are quite the same. I shopped around through many before picking one. Besides the fact that Ha Long is so beautiful, it is not worth taking 6-8 hour day tour. We were picked up at 8:20, most tours start at this time, which is pretty late considering how INSANELY busy it is at Ha Long Bay. The whole tour was basically a hop on hop off bus tour. They brought you to 3 different places in the bay that are packed with thousands of tourists, let you look around for “30 minutes,” then back on the boat. I recommend going in summer time, taking a cruise where you can actually swim in the bay, and on a cruise boat that brings you to the islands without thousands of tourists. The “less visited” islands. Or a tour that starts much earlier in the day before the crowds. (I couldn’t find one like this) Because you simply cannot enjoy a trip like this.

Was there 5 years ago for a 1 day trip, was so hard with the all Staff that this time I choose the luxus 1 night trip in s small boat thinking that will be better, but unfortunately not! We were on Prince 3.We paid more than 500 USD for 1 night, we felt like in a prison because they act and talk like in the old Soviet-system. Eating : one the most boring food we had in Vietnam, everything came in a small plate for 5 persons. The person who host us was very nice when we bought wine, and beside talk to guests like we were animals ! They should all learn how to talk to people. They just don’t care because all the world comes to Halong Bay.We just decided to enjoy this amazing landscape and forget the Staff. But quiet expensif, 500 USD per person. Recommend you to choose a cheaper trip there because there is no difference in the way they act.

Wow love the beauty of this place, amazing, serene but ruined by pollution.  Waters are definitely not clear and emerald and pristine but totally the opposite, filthy water, dead animals floating by, heaps of plastic bags and general rubbish, Was so looking forward to snorkelling and swimming, yuk no way.  went to the swimming beach and raw sewerage was coming out in brown washes from the toilets, no way.  What are you people thinking are you living in a dream world ? this place is a unesco disgrace. shame shame.... Does anyone have the same feelings as me or am I the mad one?
Spectacular but be warned . . . The winter can be cold and rainy!  Our boat had no plan B for the chilly weather. I guess the summer would be great.

Wanted to go for a full-day Halong Bay cruise on Christmas eve but we arrived late and were told that it was only left the half-day cruise which would start at 1 p.m. The prices between the full and half-day cruises were not much of the difference, half-day tour being 440,000 VND and full-day tour being 490,000 VND. Hence, we decided to do the full-day tour the next day. Since we had some time to spare on Christmas eve, we came to know about Cat Ba island and decided to go, rather than staying in the Halong City which would be boring for my husband and myself. At the ticket counter to Cat Ba island, we were told that it was low-season and the ferry to Cat Ba would leave at 3p.m. and the last ferry back to Halong city would be at 4p.m. We took the fast boat which cost us 230,000 VND each. Ferry ticket would only cost 80,000 VND each way.  So if you have the time to spare, you can consider either taking the morning ferry to Cat Ba and leave Cat Ba on the last ferry or you can stay a night at Cat Ba island. My husband and I love Cat Ba, feeling it is quiet and there are many nature sights for us to explore. We took the last ferry at around 4p.m. back to the Halong City.It was an hour ride and during the ride, it was beautiful sights of karsts in water as backdrop with beautiful sunset colours. We love the ride and enjoyed all the way, silently watching the sky colours changing with the sun setting. The next day, we went for the full-day cruise. Maybe after having experiencing the ride back from Cat Ba, we felt it was the same. There were 3 stops (cave, island and a pearl museum) along the cruise routes and there were many people, mainly from China, as many ferries also did their stops there. The sights (especially the cave) were beautiful but there were too many people, which caused us not to enjoy as much as we did the previous day. In short, if you are up to it, you can consider having a day at Cat Ba island and take the slow boat to and fro the island. (It is just an hour each way and you will not get bored by the karsts scenery.)There are caves which are quite impressive in CatBa. We hope to have more time there. It may be a cheaper option to experience Halong Bay. You will not lose out anything without taking cruises. That's our opinion after two days at Halong.

Couple of my friends and our kids went to Hanoi for a short Christmas trip and booked a day tour for Halong Bay. It was on a weekend which prolly is part of the reason that ruined the fun. Just from the port itself, it was crowded with boats and cruises, which was pretty amazing in a way in how they pushed themselves in and out of the dock. The package tour was not too bad, as meals were included during our boat ride. It was a little hazy that day but I can imagine how beautiful that place once has been. We had couple of stops which included a cave, a bamboo ride, and a quick beach stop. We sat on the beach while the 2 kids are on their swimsuit playing sand castle. I could only imagine how beautiful that place used to be as it is now flooded with tourists and I could barely had a moment of silence to myself. Tourists coming in and out like buzzing bees and instead of sitting there in admiration of the place, I sat there thinking how humans abused nature for money. There are just too many tourists to really appreciate the place and it was impossible to take a picture of the scenery without people in the background. I would say Halong Bay is now overrated.

Arrived at the new cruise docking port although the cruise terminal is not operational yet, their is a small train that takes you too the port gates witch is about 800m away.Had a day tour of Halon bay the museum was not to be missed a fantastic place with all the history definitely one not to be missed. Visited the market cheap bargins to be had, once again be aware of your surrounding and stay safe.The shops and restaurants just beyond the gates are not normally open during the day but at night they come alive. Their is still this smell whitch I could not put my finger on not pleasant.

We were waiting to see clear water and we see dirty water , ok cruise interesting but nothing special ? They sale it over rating from Hanoi 170 km 4,5 hours never again road bad . Small rocking islands nice views but Greece is nicer and blue !!!!

The trip from Hanoi to Halong Bay takes about 4 hrs with a 30 minute stop each way in a Workshop for Disabled People where overpriced Vietnamese crafts like lacquer ware, scarves, limestone carvings and knick knacks as well as Vietnamese food items are being sold.After the long 4 hours trip you finally reach Halong Bay by noon and you are suddenly assaulted by a swarm of buses carrying tourists most of them from the Chinese mainland.You have to wait for your tour guide to buy your tickets and thereafter follow her to the ship parked alongside other ships in the marina that is surrounded by hotels and condominiums that looked like they have mushroomed from somewhere.I was a bit disappointed,First of all even before the start of the cruise there is no safety briefing like the use of life vests that we have not seen until later we noticed they were under our tables.Most ships also have no fear of bumping into each other just to gain a good view of the Kissing Rocks.Halong Bay is beautiful but I am afraid that if the government don't control the development of the area around the Marina ,address the lack of safety onboard ships and over all the influx of too many tourists, this will affect the ecosystem of the lake.

Getting to Ha Long bay for my overnight trip on a fancy cruiser, the natural scenery was absolutely amazing, service on the boat was incredible but then all place I visit on the bay full of Chinese tourists it made me fell like I am traveling in China but not in Viet nam and I felt a bit disappointed, I went to Ti Top beach and see all Chinese tourist having fun by taking photo of or with western travelers, ridiculous.

１度は行ってみたい世界遺産のハロン湾に行って来ました。ハノイのホテルからの往復、片道途中休憩を含めて約３時間３０分一般道ですよ。前から気が向かなかった行程にチャレンジしました。４名の日本人観光客でワンボックスなので車内はゆったり、車の移動中は天気も晴れ間があったりしましたが、ハロン湾に到着したら曇り、３時間コースの昼食付クルージングに出発です。出発して直ぐに海鮮料理の昼食で、酒蒸し海老・揚げ春巻き・紋甲イカの野菜炒め・白身魚の甘煮・空心菜炒めなどを頂きました。昼食後にデッキに上がると雨模様、折角のハロン湾が・・・一方、ガイドさんによるとかなり空いていたので３時間コースの一番のポイントでゆっくり船が進みました。天気の性もありますが、雨模様のハロン湾は残念、薄日でも晴れていればと思う次第でした。ガイドさんによれば、６時間コースがお勧めで、できれば１泊クルージングが良いそうです。次に機会があったら、できれば１泊クルージング、せめてハロン湾１泊の６時間コースに参加したいと思います。

In any photo or magazine I ever seen about Ha Long bay it looks great but When I came it was not as pretty as I expected. The view was nice but then the cave and the beach was full of Chinese tourists very loud and rude. I expected alot before I came but a little disappointed now

Absolutely beautiful, the sailing experience and the scenery worth every penny. Service is a different story though. Beware of drinks sold in the big ships that take you around the Bay. They will secrectly charge you a lot more than what's listed price on the menu, always check price first for everything you buy around this area. It's not about the price, but rather the principle. Next part: Unless you can kayak yourselves, if you choose the bamboo boats, these boat ladies will keep asking you for big tips (10 US or 200kVND) continously and shamelessly as they take you into the caves where surveillance are limited. I accept that they should be tipped for the physical work they do, but they make it feel like it's your obligation to give them the amount they demand. These people will screaming, yelling and dancing like crazy on these bamboo boats until they get the tips. Very stupid! Btw, I am native Vietnamese so no bias on my part. Just be aware that tourism in Ha Long still needs a lot of attention and improvement and don't be fooled. 3 star experience after all.
I came here for the second time, the scenery was really nice but the local people is not nice too much.

The bay is stunning but so overpacked with boats and people that you don’t really get to enjoy it. The city itself got nothing really to see, very bad transportation and people spoke little English which made it hard for me as foreigner to get around

Our second spot on the way through Vietnam from north to south. The bay itself is georgeous of course. Because it was created by nature not human.The rest of Ha Long Bay is just awful. Ugly harbor full with half-built oversized concrete hotel complexes. A lot of them is empty and never in use. The part of bay is disgusting dolphin arena for animals to be tortured to please dumbheads. Also a classical lunapark lies nearby. The motivation was clear - make this place as much money-sucking as possible. But it failed as the visitors can see on every corner.Again, the islands themselves are amazing, Surprise cave is magnificent and the views can never wore our from your mind. But the people factor here destroyd this places spirit. Nature is free and here it is prisoner.

The scenery is amazing, shame for how dirty the water/air is. Plenty of floating pieces of plastic, plastic bags, bottles and styrofoam, the air is polluted with the exhaust from hundreds of motorboats running all day long. It's an absolute must-see when visiting Vietnam but I can see people stop going there because of how dirty it is. Also, swimming there was banned 2 years ago by government, I guess must be because of pollution?
J'ai toujours entendu dire que c'était la 8eme merveille du monde,  ben désolé mais je ne l'ai pas ressenti, est ce que c'est car il pleuvait ?Je m attendait à qqch de grandisose...

Bien évidemment les paysages sont magnifiques. Bien évidemment on en repart avec de merveilleux souvenirs pleins la tête.Mais il y a tellement de bateaux autours de nous, tellement de touristes partout, tout est chronométré, et il est en train de se construire des hotels énormes et des parc d'attractions avec toboggans à l'entrée du site ... Cela dénature tout.La Baie d'Halong est l'un des sites les plus majestueux sur Terre et le tourisme de masse en train de tout détruire ...
I loved this, but it was hazy, and we couldn't see more than perhaps a kilometre.  If it was sunny and clear, it would be absolutely breath-taking.

ＪＡＬの現地発着ツアーでの参加。前日にハロン湾近郊ホテルに泊まり、昼ごろからクルーズ出発です。この日は日本人の様々なツアーが20名程集まっていました。船の中は思ったよりキレイでトイレも2つあります。食事が沢山でるのでそれを平らげた後、２階や甲板での記念撮影。正直同じ風景が広がり、ちょっと飽きてしまいました。途中鍾乳洞の洞窟へ途中下車し、そちらはとてもキレイでした。アップダウンがあるので靴はスニーカーがオススメです。全体で３時間程のクルーズですが、世界遺産なので一度は経験してもいいのかなという感想です。

My LP guide book stated that this was an absolute must see. I was a bit under whelmed however. meh, just a bunch of rocks. However, we did only a 1 day stay and it was winter. I didn't see any chinese junk boats. Perhaps those take off from a different port for overnight cruise launches. The guide book did strongly suggest the overnight cruise.  Perhaps it is more colorful in spring and summer, but in November it is brown water and brown rocks. The cruise will likely bring you to a cave - also meh, being inside a rock with a sidewalk. I suggest seeing it if you have time to fill, otherwise look into a different excursion like Hue or Sapa.

A UNESCO world heritage site in northern Vietnam and about 170km away from Hanoi, or 4 hours by car/coach.A beautiful sea panorama with thousands over limestone islands and islets covering 1,500 sqkm.The boat is Halong Legeng by Legacy Legend Cruises and has 18 cabins. It starts to cruise out of HaLong and later follow with lunch on boat with quite spread of pork, fish, vegetable and seafood. First activity is swimming and hiking 245 steps to the mid of the hill on Ti Top Island. After that followby afternoon tea with fruits. Learn Viet food cooking about 6:45 pm and follow by dinner.Next morning the first activity is 6:20am for 15min Taichi excerise by the ship captain. 7:00am is simple breakfast with fruits salad, bread with butter, strawberry and egg, coffee or tea. 7:40am go for cave visit and has to walk up to the cave with 3 chambers. The last chamber is the biggest and the most ineresting up to your own imagination. Later back to the ship and cruise another 2 hours with lunch on board near the port. The cruise end by noon.
We expected a little more from this. Nature is beautiful, but we only went to one cave and one beach, just for a short time. We hoped Halong could offer more

Zdecydowanie można sobie odpuścić. Byłem w prawdzie na jednodniowym rejsie, lecz podobne widoki można zobaczyć płynąć po prostu promem na Cat Ba i działając na owej wyspie. Być może dłuższe i jeszcze droższe są imponujące.Rejs jest bardzo komercyjny, w większości atrakcji, jak jaskinie, spacer na szczyt, pływanie kajakiem po zatoce jest naprawdę dziki tłum turystów. Atrakcje nie są jakieś porywające. Do tego najtańsze rejsy są powyżej 200 zł, większość za ok. 250. Po prostu nie jest to warte takiej ceny.Dobra opcja dla rodziny czy bardzo wygodnych, kurortowych turystów.

We have spontaneously decided to spend a night in the downtown of Halong, and realised beside day trips package and it doesn’t have much to see in the town. The town is divided into many areas by linked of the giant bridges. Transportation is not convenient and the GrabCar is not efficient like other city does, we booked few but end up we need to cancel it whereas the cars are not moving at all or couldn’t find us. We are quite amazed by a lot of new hotels are developing for opening, every single lane has new hotel to be built and wonder what are they catering to... If you are not a big fan of the scenic view and probably this place is not a first choice. Food, tourist scenic view, markets are not as happening as other city, just join the day tour will be more fun.

I visited Halong Bay the last year, I took a tour from Hanoi that started in the morning, taking a bus from the hostal to the first port, ge got in in to the ferry to cross to cross the water, then we keep in the bus to the next port where we got in to the ship. To visit an specific island. I did not like the rubbish that was in the sea, I'm not sure if the tourist did that or the local people but I was expecting more of the place, because so many people talk to me about it.The weather was tropical and damp, it was not a problem for me because after a week you get use to.I recommend to bring cash because in most of the island you will not find an ATM.The whole country is really cheap and the food is really good.

Beautiful bay with lovely scenery, beautiful Islands. Very peaceful and tranquil both sailing through the bay and exploring the surrounds, however, It was disappointing to see the bay, the docks and the surrounding islands had been polluted with rubbish/litter and debris. When given the opportunity our groups didn’t want to swim around the boat because we didn’t want to get in the water. People need to be more mindful and stop polluting the waters and surrounding islands and do more to protect this area, be cleaner and keep it beautiful so many more people can enjoy it for years to come I was disappointed by the rubbish floating in the bay and scattered about on the surrounding islands that we visited

We booked a one day tour to see Ha Long Bay during our stay in Vietnam. While the sites were interesting to see, a one day tour only gives you a small glimpse into the massive site. As the one day tours go to the same locstions, be ready for very large crowds everywhere you go - think Disney Land queues to hike up a cave. Not sure if the multi-day tours might allow you to see more of the remote islands, but the one day tour was definitely more regimented and meant for the "highlights only, taste of" tourists.
If you want clean pristine waters and limestone monoliths go to west coast of Thailand - not the Gulf side eg Koh Samui. This is what's happening to Halong Bay!
Did a day trip to Halong bay and overall was ok, it is being made into a very touristy attraction, very crowded.

Don't get me wrong, the nature itself is indeed breathtaking. But the whole "atmosphere" around the trip is just bad. Crowds are packed on a boat, served meals and beer for an hour before departing from the harbor just to "milk" more money from you. You wait in a queue and have a "time limit" literally everywhere along the trip. Halong Bay is a must see, but perhaps it was completely different before mass tourism mostly from China.

I found Halong Bay to be very commercial, basically a tourist trap. I did choose to do a tour that could have assisted in it feeling like a money pitt so unsure if you did it by yourself you would have the same experience.There are multiple boats sailing round the bay that drop you off at different points and you visit caves/kayak. It's a major production shuffling that many tourist around. Everything felt overcrowded and rushed. It's also expensive the currency used there is not Dong but US dollars so expect to pay up for your evening cocktail. Alot of the tours stop at a "rest stop" along the way which is literally a giant ssouvenir shop. I wouldn't do this again. There are also alot more beautiful places to visit. I would choose Sapa over Halong Bay

Natuurlijk hoort een bezoek aan Halong Bay bij een reis door Vietnam, maar houd er wel rekening mee dat het altijd druk is. Er varen heel veel boten en deze varen allemaal ongeveer dezelfde route.Het bezoek aan de grotten was heel erg mooi, maar ook hier weer erg druk.Wij hebben op een boot overnacht, het eten was prima, de drank behoorlijk duur en de cocktails van zeer slechte kwaliteit.colonne bezoek je de eilandjes met stranden, de grotten en genoet je van de geweldige omgeving. Drankjes zijn wel duur op de boten, iets waar je eventueel vooraf rekening mee kunt houden.

If your not on an excursion there is very little to do and finding decent places to eat was tricky. Lots of bars churning out popular music but for the older generation it was for us. Obviously a lot of redevelopment and investment is going on but it seems their target market is for the younger tourist.It is a UNESCO heritage site but I am sure that is for the surrounding Islands and natural habitat. The beach although pleasant enough is artificial man made resort.
ジャルパックを利用して参加しました。ハロン湾クルーズ三時間でしたが昼食もついて満喫できました。港周辺が再開発されていてびっくりしました。
Thought the kayak ride would be fun. Overall average experience. You spend more time on the road then Halong bay.
ハロン湾クルーズに行きました。昼食付きのクルーズで日本人専用の船だったようで私たちを入れて3組しかいないゆっくりしたクルーズでした。

We did a 2 days/1 night cruise in Halong Bay. This area is amazing, with scenic views. The only problem is the pollution... everywhere you look you will find plastics, oil, garbage floating in the bay and a really bad smell (from boats) in the air.  Most of the waste of our boat was discharged directly into the bay:( we could not believe what we were seeing :(It’s a pity what the local companies are doing to this place that is still a World Heritage Site.

Halong Bay has always been on my bucketlist because of the photos I have seen online and so I finally decided to join an overnight cruise with my husband last April. We cruised along Halong Bay and glad that we chose a cruise that stayed in Lan Ha Bay as it was so disappointing to see garbage in the water. Personally,I'm happy I was able to see it but it was not how I expected it to be (too many boats and smell of garbage) in Halong Bay area. If you are really curious about this place,I suggest you join a cruise that stays in Lan Ha Bay longer. It is much cleaner and has less boats so you get better views.

Halong bay is a fascinating place, ok. We did a 1N 2D cruise with Vega Travel from Hanoi, that was very well organized, transport OK, cruise ok. First day nice, with kayaking and a little swimming till something before sunset. Then we moved to another bay to sleep and there was something like 10 boats (and they told us that it was a quite one). Second day beautiful caves but still many boats, and a beach with not so much people but a landscape of boats and obviously a not clean water. In this sense I don't know if I would do it again. A guy told me that he did a longer cruise and he saw very beautiful and clean places and it was great. So if you have more days and some money more to spend it worth, if you stay one night consider what I wrote before.

La baie d'Halong est exceptionnel de par son relief et les méandres entre les iles/rochers où l'on peut complètement se perdre, malheureusement elle subit une pollution visuelle et de l'eau qui risque de mettre ce joyau en péril. Nous avons préféré Tam Coc avec la "baie d'Halong terrestre" (août 2017)

Bei panorami, caratteristici della geomorfologia carsica ma mi aspettavo di più da questo posto, che è considerato una delle 7 meraviglie del mondo naturale.Comunque è da vedere almeno una volta nella vita.

I frequently come to Halong and enjoying quietness on the sea is really fantastic. You go whether by boat with other tourists or beside the season you also can rent a boat for yourself on cheap basis. Be careful with the food costs on board while the serve you lunch. During boat trip you can swim or /and take a Kanu boat. It’s a bit shocking about the development of Halong. Since I’m going there for 5 years now everything is rebuilt. New apartments where the harbor was before, new city area and most shocking... a theme park and a cable car going to the hill next to the bridge where another theme park is located. It’s a pity against the wonderful nature and the natural wonder of Halong Bay.

朝夕の景色は世界遺産にふさわしく素晴らしい。この季節は日中は曇りが多くパッとしない上、蒸し暑いので着替えは多目に準備が必要です。ここはクルーズ船での移動が基本です。ただ過ごすと昼間は空もかすみ単調な地形が続くだけで飽きてくるでしょう。水上生活している村の周辺の海はゴミ溜めの様に汚いのがとても残念。絶景を楽しみに訪れるなら二泊して朝焼け夕焼けのチャンスを逃さず狙うのがオススメ。クルーズ船なら色々催し物が用意されているので参加しましょう。ハノイから4時間程の距離にあり何回も行き来するのは得策ではないです。海水浴やカヤックだけならクルーズ一泊で十分でしょう。カネがあるならノイバイからセスナで移動するツアーもある様なのでそれも面白いかもしれません。
ハノイの喧騒からハロン湾を訪ねるので海風が心地いいです。ただ世界遺産にも関わらず保護されている感じがせず、周辺地域も保護より開発に力を入れている感じが強いので心配になりました。いつかまた行くとしたら船舶したいです。
旅行会社のオプショナルツアーでハロン湾に行きました。片道3時半位、途中バッチャン村とお土産屋に寄りながらのツアーです。お昼過ぎに到着。それから3時間のクルージング。昼食を取りながら素晴らしい景気を眺めました。しかしクルーズ船にはエアコンは無く扇風機だけ…。ちょっとキツかったかなぁ…。

Sarebbe bellissima, almeno dal punto di vista scenografico, siamo stati sulla parte più esterna per 2 giorni ed una notte  di crociera con Cristina Cruise. Ci hanno aggiunto in una crociera di classe superiore (4*) perché avevano poca gente.La nave è decente e la camera pulita, anche se non nuovissima e un po'di manutenzione non guasterebbe.Per i servizi via a bordo a parte 2 bottigliette di acqua in cuccetta il resto te lo dovevi pagare, acqua compresa (a mio avviso inaccettabile su un 4*) va bene gli alcolici ma l'acqua a bordo no!Per quanto riguarda il cibo, buffet all'arrivo, cena al tavolo ok, prima di questa un pseudo aperitivo con vino Vietnamita un bicchiere a testa, pochissimi stuzzichinie un piattino di frutta, colazione scarsa e altrettanto il pranzo che è stato fatto in porto invece che nella baia.Da una 4 stelle mi aspettavo decisamente di più.Quello che sicuramente mi è rimasto é la quantità impressionante di plastica che galleggia nella baia, bottigliette di plastica, polistirolo, sacchetti è vero che è la stagione Delle piogge ma la quantità di rifiuti è notevole.Pur essendo nella parte esterna e meno sporca, anche a detta degli operatori turistici, quando abbiamo visitato il villaggio dei pescatori, carina la location, la ragazza che ci accompagnava in barca non faceva che raccogliere plastica che galleggiavano sull'acqua.Operazione meritevole, ma giusto per capire la quantità che c'era.Come dicevo la baia di per sé e bella, non ci hanno fare il bagno ma solo una camminata sulla spiaggia, credo per i motivi sopra indicati.Mi é rimasta la voglia di ritornare? No, decisamente no, mi ricordo invece con piacere la baia di Phan Ngha in Tailandia, molto simile come morfologia, ma con un mare che nonostante siano passati molti anni me lo sogno ancora.
천개의 섬사이에서 즐기는 짧은항해와 경치감상 다양한 나라의 사람들과 대화할수있는 기회 등을 경험할수있다 해수욕과 카약 스프링롤체험등이 기본액티비티코스다

Ja, also die Gegend ist schon speziell und gehört definitiv zu einer Vietnam-Reise. Für uns hat eine Tagestour genügt, auch weil es ein wenig bewölkt war. Wenn das Wetter schön ist, könnten wir uns vorstellen, auch eine 2-Tagestour zu machen.Den besten Aussichtspunkt fanden wir den Bai Tho mountain auf dem Festland in Halong City auf der Seite mit dem Riesenrad. Ist allerdings schon eine sehr schweisstreibende Angelegenheit und man muss durch das Loch im Gitter klettern und da sein, wenn die Anwohner für 20k pp das Tor öffnen.
