Lots of cruises and people which makes visits to the attractions less enjoyable. No doubt a beautiful area and worth a visit but you won't get much value from the stops along the way. Lots of pollution too which is sad to see.
ハノイから日帰りのツアーで参加しました。かなりの数の船がでているので、人気の世界遺産です。クルーズランチ付きみたいな感じでしたが、お味やサービスには全く期待しないほうがいいです…3時間は非常に長く、早く陸に戻りたいと思ってしまいました。途中で降り立つ鍾乳洞も、すでに進化は終わっているもののようですごく神秘的な日本の鍾乳洞のような感動はありません。

Massen von kleinen und mittelgrossen Schiffen, teils eher sehr marode aussehend, befahren die Halong Bay und man bekommt einen Überblick über Bucht und Geschichte.Mit Übernachtung lohnt es sich nicht, da man am nächsten Morgen feststellt, dass die Schiffe im Kreis fahren und das Gesamterlebnis den hohen Preis nicht rechtfertigt.

Visita imprescindible si viajas a Vietnam, realizar el crucero con una noche a bordo, vale la pena, la comida y el servicio son excelentes. Las vistas son increíbles, la única pena es lo sucia que está el agua y la cantidad de basura que tiran al agua, muy triste.

Peeps,If you're old enough to know the Bali song, you'll know the next line that follows my title. Don't get me wrong, Halong Bay is very picturesque but really, how many rocks can you look at.Not to mention the fact we were stuck on a boat in the hot heat and you're not allowed to swim in the water.We travelled 4 hours by car to get there from Hanoi, transferred to the boat ( Which by the way the staff and food was top notch ) spent the afternoon looking at rocks and then slept the night, transferred back to the dock to face another 4 hours thrill a minute ride the way they drive back to Hanoi. Give me a break, that's a whole day sitting in a car.Not my cup of tea but as the old saying goes and pardon the pun, "Whatever floats your boat".Start singing that second line peeps.Made Wijaya :(

The scenery is really stunning. Unfortunately this can be overshadowed by the overwhelming amounts of trash in the bay, obnoxious party boats that have no regard for anyone else, and how overcrowded it is by tourists. We did it out of Cat Ba island which I've heard is less touristy than from the mainland but its hard to tell. If you have to pick between here and Phong Nha-Ke Bang N.P., pick the national park!

I wanted to visit Halong bay for such a long time. The limestone islands and the cave are pretty amazing, have to admit. But the entire experience left me a bit disappointed. Especially the swimming part of the trip. Ninh Binh province is much more worth a visit!

Don’t get me wrong. The place is beautiful and relaxing. It’s just because I can’t help but compare it to other places I’ve visited which are more majestic in my opinion - Shenyang Province, China. However, Vietnamese hospitality is 110% greater.

A tour in Hanlong bay is not what you expect. The water is very dirty and not what it looks like in the brochures or pictures you see. The boats are old and antiquated constantly jostling for space. There are some good photo opportunities of the area but to many tourists loving it to death. It is packed with tourists doing the tours. Only did a day tour which was enough. The food was ok but nothing special. Go for the experience to see a UNESCO site but nothing more. In Vietnam the option for gluten free is squid or egg no matter where you go so be prepared.
Mooi om te zien met cruise, prachtig gebied met vele rots partijen. Jammer dat er vele cruiseschepen zijn en er veel plastic in het water drijft, de vervuiling slaat toe, maar zeker het zien waard.

Username: コトラコマメハナキララ
Comment: 世界遺産なので一度は行っておいたほうがよいかもしれませんが、一度行けば充分です。海は綺麗でリゾートという感じですが、ダナンやニャチャンのほうがよいですね。

I am not going to take away from the natural beauty of this 'new 7 natural wonder' it is incredible.That being said it is strewn with rubbish; beaches, in the water, on the islets just every where.I am not a card carrying environmentalist, however it made me sad to see the way that tourists and local alike abuse this once absolute beauty. The tour operators, local community and authorities need to act now before it is to late

Très mitigée de notre nuit dans la baie d’Halong sur un bateau. Nous n’avons pas eu de chance avec la météo très brumeuse le matin . Mais le soleil a fini par arrivé en fin d’après midi. Bien sûr que le site est magnifique mais lorsque l’on voit ces centaines de bateaux qui sont loin d’être aux normes, que l’on voit la pollution des lieux, le plastique, le gazole, pas un poisson à la surface et une eau très opaque... on se pose la question de ci c’était vraiment nécessaire de venir participer à cette pollution. Il y a d’autres sorties plus au sud de la baie d’halong qui permettent de profiter de la beauté du site sans pour autant être avec plein d’autres bateaux.Je pense qu’il y a plein d’endroits magnifique à voir au Vietnam et que ce n’est pas grave si l’on a pas vu la Baie d’halong

We spent a couple of days here at the start of March. It was extremely busy, the weather was grim (just like the UK) and the water was filthy.  This was the main place in Vietnam that I wanted to visit but even if the weather had been beautiful, the amount of rubbish floating around in the water would have spoiled it for me. It was a disappointment and work needs to be done to clean the place up!!!
Disappointing
its down to the weather
Such a lovely place but not well protected
お天気次第
クルーズのランチは注意して下さい！食中毒になりました！
Ruining a World Heritage Area
halong bay tour
Exceptional Beauty but so overly busy
Great beauty so spoilt by little
Beautiful Nature, lot of pollution

Die Bucht könnte so schön sein. Die Felsen sind teilweise atemberaubend und das Wechselspiel von Wasser und Licht ist traumhaft. Aber leider ist man nie alleine. Alle 270 Boote fahren ungefähr die gleiche Strecke, auch wenn behauptet wird, man fahre abseits. Alle Anbieter fahren in ein Fischerdorf (Ansammlung von 4-5 Schwimmenden Häusern) wo von einem Ponton aus in kleinere Fischerboot umgestiegen wird, die einem im Konvoi einmal im Kreis paddeln. Danach geht es zu einer "Einmaligen Felsenhöhle", die sich als mittelmäßige Tropfsteinhöhle entpuppt.Und Morgens dann im Gänsemarasch auf  Titop Island, wo 20 Boote und mehr gleichzeitig anlegen und ihre Passagiere abladen. Wenn man das alles ausblendet, ist die Halongbucht wunderschön. Aber eben nur dann.

Die Halongbucht wird gnadenlos vermarktet und erstickt in einem Riesenandrang lauter, sich ständig gegenseitig fotografierender asiatischer Touristen. Man sollte sich hier eine Schiffstour mit Übernachtung auf einem wirklich guten Schiff gönnen.Das Ufer ist eine Großbaustelle.

It is a pity how is managed this Unesco world heritage! 500 boats per day going in a very close space polluting the water: it is not allowed to have a bath in the sea and in any case I would have not done it because there is waste everywhere I'm the water.  The natural beauty of Ha Long Bay is amazing ,charming and unbelievable but the human management of this lanscape will distroy this natural beauty very soon if human being will continue to act in such way. One nights/two days trip is not recommended because you see very few things instead two nights/three days is highly recommended because you will go out of the massive touristic part of the bay : you will enjoy more calm part of bay by visiting the floating village and the pearl factory

The whole place has become one big construction site..i can see the poor locals being driven out by the greedy developers..The tour around the islands was nothing to right home about..A lot of slow timing by the organisers.The sea looks somewhat polluted with plastic and fuel from the hoards of tour boats..Give it 50 years and it will have died a sorry deathShame really

Nach ca. 4 Stunden Fahrt von Hanoi mit dem Bus sind wir in der Halongbucht angekommen! Leider sind dort zu viele Boote unterwegs. Die Landschaft ist wirklich schön jedoch zerstört der Müll und das ölige Meer den Charme der Bucht! Wir waren 2 Tage und eine Nacht, das hat völlig gereicht! Auf der Tour konnte man Kajakfahren, Baden im Meer, Höhlenbesichtigung, Kleiner Kochkurs etc. Leider war es im März noch ziemlich kalt und das Wetter trüb! Insgesamt waren wir eine nette Gruppe und es hat trotzdem Spaß gemacht!

Just returned from my “bucket list” vacation that took us to Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam and ending in Hong Kong.  One of the trips offered through Norwegian Cruise Line was a junk trip to Ha Long Bay.What a beautiful place to visit.  I am really glad that my wife and I had the opportunity to visit this world treasure.  A great place to visit for the first time visit to Vietnam.I have only one major complaint.  The local fisherman have used the bay as there personal trash can for years.  Trash all over the place.  From plastic bags, cans old fishing lines and anything else you can think of.  Very bad and something that the local government and the government of Vietnam must fix.Also the bay has been overfished for years resulting in very meager catches for the local fishermen.  Again, something for the local and nations governments need to fix.Hopefully the next time I visit there will be an emphasis in these two problem areas.DDSMPRET

We went for a day tour to halong bay due to tight schedule. Journey is about 3.5hrs from hanoi to the pier. At halong bay, there were too many other boats/cruises around. Upon reaching the attraction points, there were so many tourists that we have to queue up to visit the caves. The tourists were mainly from China, and they kept pushing us away just to go in front! It was so disappointing!

公式のシンツアーを879,000VNDで予約していきました。お年寄りのフランス人夫婦・韓国人ファミリー・日本人おばさま方・ローカルっぽいファミリーたちがいて、学生は自分たちだけでした。ハノイ市から片道バス4時間+船1時間。石灰岩の景色と鍾乳洞を楽しみました。凄く感動するほどでもないけれど、見る価値はあると思います。暑くも寒くもないので、過ごしやすい気温のなかで大自然の景色を眺める感じです。解説をしっかりしてくれるツアーを予約することがポイントになりそうです。フランス人のご夫婦は通訳を雇われていました。

The bay was like a traffic jam with too many boats in a very small area . Why they have  not developed other islands and areas so the boats go to other parts and ease the numbers this area is being ruined by too many boats visiting too few sights, this will in the end ruin the area.

A UNESCO listed World Heritage area because of it's outstanding beauty and 1600 islands supposedly "unaffected by a human presence" we were expecting to see relatively uncompromised natural beauty. Indeed the islands and bay are uniquely lovely, but.......  Rubbish in the water is disappointingly abundant. Plastic bags, surgical gloves, straws - all manner of plastic waste can be seen floating past your boat. Even more evident when you are kayaking. When you beach on the islands the amount of rubbish is confronting. We found literally mounds of it at one beach in particular. We would not risk swimming in the water. The air pollution, euphemistically labelled mist by the locals, means there is never proper blue sky and sunlight (despite our tour guide saying we had a perfect sunny day) so the colours are washed out and only the closest islands are clearly visible with the rest in the background in a smoky haze. We had a good time on a 2 night cruise and I think it is worth doing, but the level of pollution mars the experience. That is essentially true about our whole trip in Vietnam. The waste management is absolutely appalling and air pollution evident everywhere. A once beautiful country drowning in a sea of plastic and gasping for clean air. Sad to see.

Halong Bay er unik. Og flot. Og imponerende. MEN, desværre er det blevet alt for overrendt af turister. Vi var på et 2 dags cruise, som egentligt var helt ok, dog dyrt. Men sammen med vores båd var også 100 andre. 1 dags turister, speedbåde, og kinesere i hobetal. Vandet flyder med skrald og plastik. Gad vide hvor bådende dumper toiletaffald? Hvis det fortsætter er der ikke meget af det naturlige tilbage. Derfor, tag til Bai Tu Long Bay, hvor kun 4+ stjernede både må sejle, og dermed langt færre turister. Eller tag til Ninh Binh som er Halong Bay, bare uden vand

Superbe site,c'est magnifique ,magique.A voir absolument. Le côté désagréable c'est le tourisme de masse.Et le pire c'est de voir autant de déchets flotté sur l'eau. Vous trouvez tout et n'importe quoi du plastique, du polystyrène des casiers à poissons sans oublié les sacs en plastique. Pour moi c'est inconcevable de souillé un tel lieu.Pourquoi les locaux ne préservent pas ce lieu ,quel dommage

A beautiful picturesque area so worth the visit. Due to the sheer number of tourists and boats out on the bay doing the same tour it was disappointing. Take miss on Ti Top island tiny beach packed with people, people pushing and shoving to reach the peak, the smell of diesel from boats and tenders, people smoking even though clearly non smoking area. Spoils the atmosphere of the bay itself. I would also miss Sat Song or Surprise cave.  Was obviously a beautiful cave but along with Ha Long bay poorly managed. Rubbish everywhere in the water. I would recommend a more expensive tour that doesn’t provide the standard. Getting away from the crowds would be gorgeous. The kayaking was fantastic.

Went on an overnight boat, which was fine, the marina is so crowded but nicer when you get out in the water a bit further as although there are a lot of boats it is still quiet and eerie sailing past the cliffs. Could not believe the floating sea of rubbish, mainly plastic, that went by the boat or that was washed up on the bottom of the cliffs. The cave is totally crowded but still awesome and  enjoyable.  The climb up the 400 steps of the cliff is totally crowded but worth it for the view. About 50 boats anchored for the night in same area but was quiet. Other tourists told me it was great to stay at the resort there. Weather was mild and didn't use a jumper. A bit awkward in dining room if you are a solo traveller who likes their own space, but most others chat to you. The drive there in a small bus was very tedious and rough with the obligatory stop at a tourist shop.

Abbiamo alla fine fatto il tour due gg una notte! La baia forse complice il cattivo tempo non mi ha meravigliato! Bella ma non eccezionale! Belle le grotte e buon servizio nel caso vi fermate fuori a dormire! In un giorno è dura

Hice un crucero de una noche en el Moon Lithg cruise. Un barco viejo las cabinas viejas incomodas baño c ducha incomodo.La comida vietnamita mas pollo frito y papas siempre repetida en 2 almuerz y 1 cena. No me gusto. Me parecio caro 100 dol para lo q es. La empresa ahora se llama Fanta Cruise. Tenia 8 ptos de calific en booking.com. las fotos eran falsas...y para mi los comentarios tambien.Nos toco nublado. No se aprecia nada. El agua sucia. La cueva enorme fue lo mejor. El barco esta parado en la noche como todossss los q van. El guia te entretiene c karaoke nda mas. Y vender tragos. En la mañ vuelven lentam para apreciar el paisaje...van a la casa de perlas cultiv y media hora de kayak. No vale la pena. La mayoria de estos barcos chicos y viejos son iguales un grupo de 12 personas. Yo haria el paseo en el dia . Lo peor q son 4 horas de traslado en bus de ida y vuelta...es lejos..y el bus incomodo.

It had an interesting story behind which is kind of legendary dragon (something like that). We have been to quite a few lime caves along the bay and visited inside there. Very crowded and not really interesting though. We were on the small day cruise called Hailong Dream which was ok for price and the lunch on the cruise was very basic. Did not have any special experience after this trip.
It's naturally wonderful but full of organised tours doing the same thing. Would love to do it without an organised tour!

The bay is beautiful but very congested.  What I mean is that the view of the Islands are beautiful but the boat traffic is bad.  I don't think that the country organizes this or really cares about how many boats are aloud on the bay.  You are always surround by dozens of other boats and I feel that in a few years this bay will become polluted if it isn't already.  The government has to start controlling the traffic or it will lose a beautiful place.

Go if you are looking for great photos but if you want to 'get away' and 'escape for a while' you aren't in luck. It is very touristy, no matter what kind of cruise you go on. I wouldn't suggest it if you don't like crowds. I'm sure private cruises are available but the price is probably 4x as expensive.

Esta claro que es una gran atracción turística, pero en mi opinión lo están destrozando. Sucio, cientos de barcos y te enseñan apenas un trocito de bahía y rodeado de barcos a todas horas. Aun así vale la pena verlo, pero daros prisa si pensáis ir porque en unos años creo que ya no será lo mismo

i'm not into nature adventure, but i will not recommend halong bay as the one for nature adventure, i know there are other better places to do that, i am quite dissapointed because in reality it's not that attractive
パッケージツアーで行きましたが、ハノイ市から片道4時間はちょっと疲れました。チャーター船でのクルージングだったので気楽でしたが、中国の桂林と比べると見るべきものが少なく、観光船ばかりで帆船は全くいない。帆船は正月などの何かの行事がある時だけとか、ちょっと寂しい。もう少し観光に力をいれてほしい。途中に寄った鍾乳洞だけが素晴らしかった。

It was absolutely stunning! Such a wonderful part of the world. However, the pollution was so bad. We kayaked through plastic bags, water bottles, dead fish. The amount of waste that has been thrown into the bay is very disappointing. More needs to be done to protect this area. If you go, just don't look down into the water.

Jeli jsme z náboje přes agenturu z hostelu, cesta je velmi nezáživná, bohužel autobus nás vyhodil na ostrově, odkud odjíždějí výletní lodě a trajekty a odtamtud nám po našich protestech ne zrovna mile vysvětlili, že do města si můžeme vzít taxík. Venku už čekala lovkyně turistů a začala s nabídkou, kterou jsme po delším handlování dotáhli na na kompletní odvoz do a z hotelu a jednodenní výlet. Zaplatili jsme 55$ za osobu, což je podle recenzí běžné, ale asi bychom v hotelu dostali i lepší nabídku. Boat trip nás jednoznačně zklamal, ostrovy jsme viděli spíš z dálky, všechno bylo hrozně turistické a zinscenované. Druhý den jsme si daleko více užili cestu trajektem na cat ba. Jediné pozitivum bylo pro nás kajakování, i když poměrně krátké. V průběhu platby nám vnucovali suvenýry a pak ještě chtěli "tip for crew", to jsme ale absolutně odmítli. V Halong city můžu doporučit projet na motorce pobřeží a zastavit se, kde se vám líbí, ale jinak nic moc.

Habiendo visitado la Bahía de Halong cinco años atrás, en este visita nos llevamos un enorme desengaño.Desgraciadamente Vietnam se ha convertido en un destino de turismo de masas y, consecuentemente, Halong se ha transformado en miles de embarcaciones navegando por los mismos pasillos marítimos entre los característicos peñones del imaginario colectivo de Indochina.Hordas de turistas asaltan una gruta aquí, un mirador allá, un paseo en piragua con otros cientos de improvisados canoistas.Una auténtica pena que un lugar tan mágico y especial ya no guste de la calma y el encanto de antaño.

We heard a lot about Halong Bay, so naturally, we decided to visit it. The ride from Hanoi with a van took us more than 4 hours, but there is no faster way (except with a smaller plane). We boarded one of many boats that left the harbor and there was about 50 of us on board. We cruised around for about 4,5 hours, with 3 stops (the cave, kayaking, and a short hike) and returned back to the harbor. The water was dark green, with no visibility at all, there was garbage floating and we weren't impressed at all. The islands are nice and I guess worth seeing, but overall it's way too touristic and overrated.

The nature is exquisite witch exceptional rocks but the bay  is over crowded and a real tourist trap. Boats full of tourists are jamming and cramming around a 'kissing rock', visiting the cave is an ordeal, standing in line with Chinese tourists who elbow their way in. No time to stand still if you even wanted too. Our boat was hit by another, while 'parking', but fixed with a hammer right away. Don't go if you want rest.
日本の松島遊覧船とよく似ている。天気に左右されやすく、晴れや雨に関わらず霧がないことが最大の条件。よく目にする上空から写した写真ほどの絶景さはなくちょっとガッカリ。途中で立ち寄るティエンクン鍾乳洞が救い。ティエンクン鍾乳洞の入場券は乗船券売り場にしか売ってないので忘れずに買っておこう！

We went on a one night two day tour and I was so excited to get going! They do bring you to a load of lovely places but the views were awful with the fog! We went to a beach and walked up to a view point! They didn’t let us know there’d be a bar to buy drinks at so we didn’t bring our wallets! Can only imagine how fantastic it would look in good weather!

Typical challenges  can be expected from a 18 hole golf course  in a mountain we visited and played for the first time. Fairway conditions were excellent, but the green turfings were not trimmed for proper putting. Seems it was intentional to let the green matures . The green speed was somewhat similar to the tight fairways.The changing facilities were excellent.The male caddies could hardly speak English, and they were necessary to provide directions from hole to hole. Certain holes were far apart like Hole 8 & 9.

Le site est toujours beau mais cela devait être vraiment fabuleux avant..Des objets, plastiques divers flottent régulièrement...Des hordes de bateaux ! Même la nuit tous les bateaux mouillent ensemble... L’ascension sur le « Rocher » le lendemain permet de voir un joli panoramas mais un peu gâché par les mêmes bateaux..Une petite plage permet de se baigner.. mais peu de monde s’y risque

La Baie d'Halong est l'ascension que j'ai la moins aimée au Nord du Viêtnam. Très réputé pour sa beauté, c'était l'un des principaux buts de mon voyage. À mon arrivée sur celle-ci et après avoir passé quelques jours loin de la vie touristique, grosse déception... Oui, le site est beau (Si vous avez déjà été en Thaïlande vous retrouvez sur la baie certains endroits avec beaucoup plus d'iles évidemment...), mais pollué, beaucoup de déchets dans certaines zones, énormément de bateaux. J'avais pris l'option 2 jours 1 nuit et j'ai regretté une journée étant suffisant.

miejsce piekne bo "z natury" ale nie przesadzaj my z uwielbieniem dla tych wapiennych skał. Na pewno styczniowa pogoda odejmuje wiele uroku przez panujące 8 stopni i mgłę. Wydaje mi się że tajlandzkie zatoki zrobią większe wrażenie
ハロン湾の観光と美味しい海鮮料理を食べて楽しめました。料金もリーズナブルでした。洞窟探検は世界遺産にもかかわらず人工の手が入りすぎて良くなかったです。ライティングなどが過剰でした。

11年前曾造訪過一次，當時的下龍灣的渡船碼頭很陽春，不像現在有豪華的大廳，行程中當然也沒有換搭小船或獨木舟的體活動，而登島參觀鐘乳石洞讓我很痛心，以前雖然是很簡單的碼頭，和木板橋及可能是棕櫊葉屋頂的涼亭，和周邊的風景是很協調的。現在卻是用高級石材搭建，感覺就是用金錢堆疊起來俗不可耐的怪物。入洞後更加揪心。以前或許要步步為營才不致於跌倒，現在完全沒這困擾，因為走道也鋪上高級的石板地磚，空間大到不行，也亮到不行，可見裡面不知被砍掉了多少的鐘乳石，而每多一盞燈都會加速石洞的崩壞，應該剩沒多少年光景了！

この時期雨が多いのではの予感的中の中ツアーに参加、英語ガイドの方だったので16人中日本人は私一人、片道3時間はかかるというわけで、最初に皆んな自己紹介をする（拙いカタカナ英語で日本語は得意だが英語は残念なんでゆっくり話してと皆んなにお願いしました）、という感じで雨の中ハロン湾到着、遊覧船に乗りランチをする 値段の割りには美味い 満足、そうこうしてるとお約束のカヌー、足コギボート、欧米人はカヌーアジアンはボートときれいに分かれる。この頃にはツアーの皆んなでワイワイして写真の取り合いとか結構盛り上がってます。ガイドブック通りの岩に近づくと皆んなで歓声。帰りのバスはお休み状態。天気が良ければ100点なのに少し残念だったけど 見る価値はあります。

I spent two days on the Phoenix Cruise Boat in Halong Bay. I traveled alone and felt extremely safe. the best way to see Halong Bay is by boat but be sure to research your ship. I stayed on the Phoenix Ship which was three star (very different standard than American rating). The advertisement made the ship look a lot nicer than it really was. the bed was hard, the ship appeared to be very old and although I was skeptical I decided to stay and give it a try. In the end everything was fine. The other guest were all friendly and we all bonded by the end of the trip.  The food was great and they accommodated my dietary needs.   They also gave us really nice things to do like the cooking class and Kayaking and the cave tour. My rating would have been higher if the accommodations had been as nice as the advertisements.

I'm torn over Halong Bay.  While one must visit Halong Bay if you are ever in Hanoi, it was not an enjoyable day for me due to the overcrowding of loud tourists.I am sure without the massive amount of boats and tourists, it is a serene and beautiful area.  However, having been pushed into the limelight, Halong Bay is no longer serene.  In fact, we started out convinced we needed to overnight on one of the boats but after reading about the trash in the water and overcrowding, opted for a day cruise.  This turned out to be a wise decision.  I don't feel like I missed much by not spending a night there.I don't see the volume of tourists diminishing in Halong Bay so I guess one must just imagine how it was like 20 years ago without the huge influx of visitors.

What a shame.  We had a one night tour that was thoroughly enjoyable and I would have no hesitation in recommending BUT, my God, there is so much litter in the sea.  I set the alarm so I could get up early and see the sunrise but as I opened the window blinds all I could see outside the boat was a sea of litter that completely spoilt the whole thing.  If the authorities don't do something soon I think it will be a disaster.

We booked a one night cruise to Halong Bay. We were picked up from our hotel at about 0800. The coach would have been  fine apart from the leather seats that resulted in us slipping forward each time the driver hit the brakes. If you know Hanoi traffic, this was a lot. So we had a four hour coach journey with a 30 minute break on the way. Once at the port boarding was straightforward. Our guide and the crew in the boat gave us a safety briefing and we were shown to our cabin. It’s fair to say the boat had seen better days, but we were sort of prepared for that. What we were not prepared for was the seven or eight dead cockroaches in the wardrobe. It’s fair to say that once informed the crew cleaned them up quickly. The food on board is plentiful but it comes thick and fast and was mediocre.  There is a fair selection and they catered for meat eaters and veggies, including vegans. We then set sail and had fun kayaking under the guidance of our guide and then swimming off the boat. We were expecting turquoise water as shown in the brochure. This has clearly been photoshopped as the Bay is tidal and murky brown. We then headed to our anchor point expecting to be alone in tranquil surroundings. Not so. There may have been 20 other boats anchored nearby. Next morning we headed to some caves before heading back to shore before another 4 hour drive to Hanoi. We would have given this a higher score if not for the uncleanliness of the cabin, the photoshopped pictures and lack of acknowledgement of the numerous surrounding boats. Would we go again?  No. But f you are aware of these facts you can make up your own mind

Travelled with Flamingo cruises booked through Citrus travel don’t use this company. You can book this yourself through many outlets in Hanoi around 80 usd pp for an overnight cruise with full board and return shuttle to Hanoi.Four hours plus each way from Hanoi. Cruise starts 1 pm and finishes 12 noon the following day.The actual cruise with Flamingo or the scores of companies based there was good. Food excellent accommodation good beer four times what you would pay in town wine over 40 usd a bottle.... ouchThe area is vastly over crowded you are never alone scores of boats follow the same route. Does not smell of the sea but diesel. You would not want to swim in this bay and it’s not emerald. The island with summit view is a procession of tourists from bottom to top and back on one path. Surprise caves are excellent. Well worth a look but don’t be surprised if you expectations are dashed.

Forced because you go to all the tourist spots. You get off at the cave and there is 20-30 boats already there. And 300+ tourists in the cave at the same time. If I had a do over I would do some more research and try find a way to have an experience that others haven’t had. Like a private beach. Or a private cave. With a smaller boat that isn’t full of strangers (Probably would be easier/cheaper with a group of friends).You could try going straight to a local who owned a boat. In saying all that as a solo traveler I did enjoy meeting new people, and we did have fun.

Dieses wunderschöne einzigartige Paradies wird mehr und mehr Opfer des Massentourismus. Wer eine Fahrt auf einem Schiff nicht, sollte unbedingt schauen, ob es eine Tour abseits der Hauptrouten gibt. Andernfalls fährt man in einem Pulk von hunderten von Schiffen, umwabert vom Tuckern der Dieselmotoren und dem entsprechenden Gestank. Hier lohnt es sich, mehr Geld auszugeben - und zB auf einen Ausflug zum Ti Top Island oder dem Kajaking zu verzichten (ist eh beides nicht so lohnenswert) - und durch ruhigere Gewässer zu shippern.

Whether you love Halong Bay or not will depend on weather, duration of your stay and your expectation. My plan was to miss this place and spend more time exploring the city. But locals and fellow travellers kept telling me "you must go or you'll regret it". So I thought it would be a shame if I didn't go and I should find out if it's really one of those places you must visit before you die. I chose a day trip as I didn't have enough time to spend more. The weather was cloudy but Halong Bay still looked nice,,, Yes it was "nice" but not "amazing!" as many people said. My rating would probably be higher if it was sunny. But what I didn't like about the world heritage listed site was this "Disneyland" feeling that you get from the tour. Unfortunately, it's not possible for you to go to Halong Bay on your own. And you don't have time to explore the site by yourself, especially if you booked a day tour. You will be seeing people everywhere you go. You will see lots of tour buses when you arrive at Halong Bay. You'll see many gift shops on your way. Kayaking was the best part as that's the only time I could be on my own. Wish I could spend more time on this. Looking back, I enjoyed more walking around the city, talking to locals in Old Quarter and visiting temples in Hanoi. So if you visited Vietnam and missed this place, don't feel bad or regret it. I can assure you that there are many other places that will impress you in the world. This place just wasn't for me.
ベトナム屈指の観光地で、近くの港から船でクルーズに出発。穏やかな湾で突き出たような島が点在しています。特に夕景はモヤがかかり幻想的でとてもすばらしいと思います。ただ、水が汚いのにはガッカリでした。

After a bone shaking 4 hr car ride, in a shared coach( incl a half hour comfort stop at a warehouse of trinkets for tourists), we arrived at our overnight Chinese style Junk. We set sail and drifted leisurely between the huge Karstic outcrops just as the pictures show. Unfortunately our boat was one of 30-40 similar sized vessels littering the bay in all directions. On board the service and quality of cabin and food was excellent. Heritage Line. JasmineBut outside is a pea green soup of littered water. Some of the colour may have been due to the prevailing atmospherics at the time, overcast and poor air quality that really followed us all the way from Hanoi City. On day 2 we awake early to a voluntary Tai Chi and then a visit to a local beach for a swim(?) and a climb to a view point. Unfortunately every other boat offloads it’s passengers at the same time..Returned to ship, excellent brunch then back ashore about noon for the 4hr trip back. Visiting the floating village on day one was interesting. The limestone cave, less so. Seen better in many othe countries. Shop around for prices for Halong Bay trip. We organised it from Uk and paid quite a lot more

I understand why Halong Bay is considered as one of the natural wonders of the world. This place is beautiful but also very crowded and polluted. There are around 400 boats cruising every day. We took overnight cruise and it was ok but the overall experience was underwhelming. Each cruise does more less the same set of the activities/locations so trillions of people everywhere. Try to organize this trip by yourself if you have more time. I think it may be better experience and much cheaper. Or go to Ninh Binh for few days instead (not just as a day trip from Hanoi) and find local homestay outside of the town. This location has very similar landscape to Halong Bay but it's on the mainland. It's much less crowded and absolutely amazing. It was our favorite place in Vietnam

Like the pictures you see, the bay and rocks from afar was a beautiful sight. I enjoyed getting up early and watching the sunrise. Part of my cruise included a walk through the cave, kayak and stopped at a beach to swim or walk the 400 steps to get breath taking views. It was hard to get up and down those 400 steps but the views were worth it. I enjoyed the relaxed feel on the cruise and just seeing darkness in the night-serenity. I didn't think it was 100% beautiful as the water was murky green, you couldn't see to the bottom. You would see rubbish floating by as you cruised around. The water also smelt and others in the group went for a dip and the water wasn't clear and they came out with a layer of oil which is probably petrol from the boats. People who live on smaller boats throw their rubbish into the water, so not being crystal clear water I wouldn't recommend swimming in it.Yes do a cruise but don't expect too much from the water. Also choose a cruise that's 4-5 stars it'll probably make your stay better.

Posto molto bello, il modo per visitarlo un po' meno. Ho fatto la crociera 1 notte. Si sta quasi tutto il tempo sulla nave, le attività che si fanno sono mediocri. Il cibo niente di ché. 40 minuti di kayak, 40 minuti su una spiaggia, mezzora in una grotta, il resto sulla nave ferma poco lontano dal porto. Ho viaggiato sulla viola cruise ma penso che siano più o meno tutte uguali. Ovviamente, ma questo si sa pieno di turisti. Peccato la baia è bella, ma la gita ha un prossimo rapporto qualità prezzo.

Veramente un peccato. Il posto è assolutamente meraviglioso, ma non potere (o meglio volere) fare il bagno per via dell’acqua piena di rifiuti, fa sicuramente arrabbiare. Dategli una pulita e le 5 stelle non ve le toglie nessuno

So overrated. Was so disappointed! There are boats everywhere so views aren't too great and it just isn't anything special especially for the price you pay for the boat packages which are terrible and you pretty much wait around on the boat the full day/night with nothing to entertain you. The experience would be better if you were with a fun group of people but the bay itself is very overrated and I would say don't spend the money doing it! We done the 2 nights 3 days packaged with 1 night on the boat and 1 night on Catba. Was okay but I wouldn't do it again or recommend. There are better things to do and spend your money on!

My partner and I visited Halong Bay for one night. I have been before in 2010 but it was his first time. Unfortunately I didn’t enjoy this experience nearly as much as the first trip. The Bay itself has grown massively in popularity, has rubbish floating everywhere and the cave which everyone visits is not protected in any way from damage by people. Parts of the formations have been pulled off, the limestone is damaged from people touching it and parts of the cave have been drained to get rid of water. It’s a sad sight. New regulations for Halong Bay also mean there is no swimming from the side of any boat and only kayaking in one area which is highly controlled and very busy.  The limestone islands still look beautiful, but as soon as you look a little closer you’ll notice the damage we are causing to this environment. A little bit of a sad and disappointing trip.

Wir haben eine 3Tage/2Nächte Tour auf einem 3-Sterne-Boot in Hanoi gebucht und sind im Nachhinein recht glücklich mit der Entschedung die längste der angebotenen Varianten zu wählen, da die allermeisten Touristen für maximal eine Nacht oder einen Tagesausflug in die Halong Bucht kommen. Die Kalkfelsen sind ohne Zweifel beeindruckend und sehr sehenswert. Schade ist jedoch, dass vor allem der westliche Teil der Halongbucht stark verschmutzt ist. Im Zuge der längeren Bootstour kommt man auch in den östlichen, sauberen und daher viel schöneren Bereich der Bucht.Die Fotos der Schiffe und Zimmer, die man beim Buchen sieht entsprechen generell nicht der Wirklichkeit und man darf sich keinen großen Luxus erwarten, auch wenn man die etwas teurere Tour ausgewählt hat. Das Tourenprogramm ist prinzipiell sehr straff, es bleibt dabei kaum Zeit für individuelle Aktivitäten auf dem Schiff oder auf den Inseln. Das Essen auf unserem Boot war gut, die Getränke sind jedoch für vietnamesische Verhältnisse relativ teuer. In der Regenzeit sollte man auch das Wetter ein bisschen im Auge haben und gegebenenfalls den ärgsten Regen abwarten.Die Halong-Bucht ist jedenfalls sehenswert und man sollte sie sich auf keinen Fall entgehen lassen. Unserer Erfahrung nach wäre es wahrscheinlich am Besten gewesen, die Kalkfelsen von Cat Ba aus zu besichtigen, so entgeht man dem Müll, dem schmutzigen Wasser und den Touristenmassen die vorwiegend im westlichen Teil der Halong-Bucht zu finden sind...

The title says it all, the scenery is amazing, however what spoils it immensely is the floating rubbish around the many bays and waterways. If this problem is not solved there will be no tourism whatsoever.
對下龍灣其實不了解，去到之後才知道，原來是很多的群島在海灣內，最令人驚艷的就是鐘乳石洞窟，裡面有各種顏色的燈，搭配鐘乳石真的很美，結束後還有人力划船帶你晃晃附近洞窟的美，是個可以留下好回憶的地方

A very much anticipated Halong Bay overnight trip was short lived due to a forecast storm that night. All cruises had to head back to shore. My tour group of 6 travelled 4 hrs from Hanoi to Halong Bay, tour with the cruise for only 4 hrs( indeed an awesome and beautiful place) and travelled another 4 hrs back to Hanoi .We all had to find a last minute accommodation for that night.I felt the tour agency should have been more diligent to check on the weather forecast and give the tourists the option to continue a day tour, change of day of tour or opt out and have a refund. Unfortunately we were only told of the change when we board the cruiser at Halong Bay.To the tour agent this is business but to us as tourists, it's time and money that we need to manage when we travel to a foreign land.I would strongly encourage tourists to check with the tour agent on the weather status before setting off for Halong Bay.

Die Halong Bay ist einer der schönsten und mystischsten Flecken in Vietnam und immer einen Besuch wert. Die Bootstouren bieten einen Vielfallt an Touren auch über Nacht und auch ansonsten hat der Ort viel zu bieten. Unsere Tour haben wir mit der Alova Touristik ab Hanoi gebucht und durchgeführt und waren rundum zufrieden. Aber Natur muss auch geschützt bleiben und daher muss ich auch Kritik loswerden, denn wie viele Orte in Asien, hat auch Vietnam ein grundlegendes Müllproblem. An den vor allem touristisch erschlossenen Punkten der Bucht, könnte man dieses Problem spüren.

We went to Halong bay after Catba as we have done a boat trip since in Catba already and it's nothing to do there we only went for a sightseeing. When we were there, it was a big builing almost around the island. In the night we went to the night club which a can of beer is 170K  VND or you have to buy a bottle which is very expensive, so I decided to just stay there only for 2 nights then we left.

El entorno natural merece un diez pero el entorno humano deja mucho que desear , suciedad , ausencia de fauna , no hay vida animal excepto en criaderos de pescado y rarísimo ver alguna ave , aguas sucias , timadores en agencias de viajes como en el hotel agencia de viajes "Four seasons" en Halong bay ( no tiene nada que ver con la famosa cadena hotelera). Hay que ver este lugar pero está muy degradado como gran parte de Vietnam.

Shin Touristのツアーを申し込み、$45程で昼食付のツアーに参加。ホテルのピックアップに手間取ったのか、スタートが30分遅れていました。早く帰ってきて、Ionahのショーを見たかったのですが、この時点で断念することに。ばっちりお土産屋さんでは1時間滞在（往復途中）。みんな早く帰りたがっているのに、決して早く出してはくれませんでした。契約しているから仕方ないのでしょうが、暑い中待つのは辛かったです。ツアーガイドさんの英語は個人的には聞き取りやすく、わかりやすかったです。途中で下船して、バンブーボートかカヌーに乗れる点が良かった点。ただ、奇石群としては、ニンビンの方が圧倒的。どうせハノイから長時間移動していくのであれば、ニンビンの方がおすすめです。
Paisajes maravillosos pero mucha demanda turística, lo que hace que no puedas disfrutar al máximo estas bondades de la naturaleza

Halong Bay is wonderful and the vertiginous mountains a joy to behold. Staying there on a boat for 3 days and 2 nights (see elsewhere) was wonderful but and it's a big BUT the sea is dreadfully polluted with everything from carrier bags plastic bottles cardboard boxes and general rubbish! Not sure if it's he locals or the tourists but such a beautiful stretch of water deserves to be better looked after
我们报了一日游，从河内到那里路上比较花时间。因为路况不是很好。到了那里后我们买了游船，这包含一顿午餐。量很大很多都浪费了。游船到了以后还需要另外花钱换小船才能进到里面的景区的。整个景区的风景挺美的，有点像海上桂林的感觉。然后再坐几个小时的车回到城市。个人感觉整体感觉没有想象中的那么好，花半天时间在路上去看这样的景色，有点......

We went on overnight cruise. Got picked up by the cruise company from the hotel. The boat we were to cruise on didn't look anything like the brochures. Was much older. Life jackets were ill fitting and no safety evacuation instructions given. Be mindful that the stairs were very steep and the back of the boat has no safety nets were someone to stumble from the stairs they will end up in the water. Rooms and shower was small. Food for vegetarians was awful only , deep fried stuff and cucumber. Just be careful when you booking your tour that you get the cruise you see in your brochure. We were shown swan tours 4stars and sold apricot 3stars. Not worth the 205 usd per person we paid for. Holong bay is littered with boats some very nice ones. This may be a world heritage place but not looked after. The whole trip was rushed. I didn't feel it was worth paying the money and staying overnight. I would advice people to go for day trip to see the caves. That was what I found was special. Water in the bay was quite dirty.

Acabo de bajar del barco en un crucero de 2 noches. Aunque la experiencia ha sido buena, no puedo evitar llamar la atención sobre el deplorable estado de la Bahía. Hay una gran capa de aceite en el agua y basura amontonandose entre piedras karsticas. Desaconsejaria el baño a cualquiera. Para ser un espacio patrimonio de la humanidad y una de las maravillas naturales del mundo ¿Dónde está la protección que este lugar merece? Hace que reflexionar sobre el turismo responsable

I and my boyfriend went there in the middle of the raining season. The only perk was only a few boats around, so it was quiet and peaceful. Unfortunately, enjoying the trip fully was impossible because of the pouring rain.On one hand it ruined the experience but on the other I can't imagine visiting Ha Long Bay in high season, when it's full of tourists... Anyway, took a one-day trip and have to say it wasn't worth to spend 8 hrs in the bus from and to Hanoi, just to sit in the boat for four hours. Additionally, consider going to Krabi in Thailand. It's way more impressive.
햇빛이 강해서 따가움.. 바로전날 짱안갖다왔는데 개인적으로 짱안이 더 좋았음. 뱀부보트 30분타는데 20분도 안탄듯. 같은 팀들 배는 좀 넓게 도는데 우리배 아주머니는 최단거리 루트만 이용.. 짱안은 3명씩탔지만 여긴 6명 한번에 배탔음. 가이드에게 말해도 안된다고. 팁 주기싫었는데 그래도 힘들어보이시니까 드렸음. 강매는 없었고 동굴은 매우 더움.

There's no disputing that Halong bay is one of the most beautiful places that we've ever visited. The consequence of such an attraction is that around 1000 visitors PER DAY visit the area. Unfortunately, everywhere is ridiculously over crowded and disappointingly, the water is strewn with litter. I would have expected, that with the amount of cash that the tourist industry is ploughing into the area, the authorities would consider tidying the waters up somewhat.
The bay is stunning, but some of the tour operators aren't. Best piece of advice is research the company thoroughly. I had a poor experience with Alova Cruises, so be weary before booking.

I have to say that I was Really skeptical to come to Halong Bay initially because I read so many reviews on all the floating garbage. I sadly have to admit that there was ALOT and it broke my heart to see such a beautiful place of nature get overtaken and abused by mankind - yet again. The local people are incredibly warm and friendly, and draw their living off the seafood - and I can't Help but feel the sea life is also polluted from all the garbage. With this being said, halong bay is still  worth going to see & experience and it is my hope that things will change on the side of the locals and the Vietnamese government to protect their coveted environment in the years ahead. This place is a natural gift and should be treated as such. If the tourists are throwing trash, their should be strong fines to the boats they come from , and there should an effort to educate all tourists. It is also my hope that clean up efforts will occur. I almost did not come because of all the reviews of the trash.. and wonder if others feel the same. Vietnam- it's on you to change this !

We started our stay in Halong bay on a cruise around the islands and the other activities that tourists do, the kayaking and the caves etc. it was great! Such an adventure and we really enjoyed ourselves. We elected not to return to Hanoi or stay on one of the islands and found accomodation in Halong bay instead. This was a mistake. We were in Halong bay for three days and found it to be very isolated. Most tourists came in on the bus to sun world and left straight away, there weren't a lot of good bars or a lot of people. We found the the local people didn't speak a lot of English and that many of the men were quite rude and objectified my girlfriend and I. We were very uncomfortable. We will not be returning to ha long bay in a hurry.
ツアーでハノイから片道4時間かけて行ってきました。船に乗って昼食をいただきますが、どれもまぁまぁ。途中で地元の漁師さんが船をつけて魚を売りに来ました。購入して調理してもらいましたが、味は値段ほどではありません。船にクーラーはついておらず暑い乳洞の中はもっと熱いです！水をかぶったように汗をかきました。帰りは渋滞で4時間半かかりました。
Beaucoup trop touristiques à mon goût. Lieu qui perd tout son charme avec le nombre de jonques au m2. Et que dire de la sortie en kayak !! Une blague rien d'autre

A place of extreme beauty which looks like it has been been damaged by both tourists and locals. Litter can be seen floating about the water including plastic bottles, polystyrene and lighters. Visited a monkey island where Vietnamese tourists where taunting and acting aggressively towards the monkeys. The monkeys looked completely imtimidated and ready to attack at any moment to their amusement. Disgraceful behaviour that somewhat epitomizes the effect of mankind on this area.
世界遺産なので、一度見て見たいと思いツアーに参加しました。しかし、タイのクラビと余り変わらない様な気がしました。7月はとても暑いので、涼しいシーズンに行く方が、体にも楽ですし、景色も雲がかかっていて良いのかなと思います。

The views are great, however chances are you'll be sharing the views with a whole lot of other boats (we heard: until 50 at a time!). We visited both Halong Bay, Lan Ha Bay and Bai Tu Long Bay and if you like the same view with less tourists, then definitely go to one of the other places.

It was beautiful but disappointed that the waters were polluted with rubbish and oil from the cruise! Hope that the tourism management team will do something and preserve this natural beauty before it turn to a sea of rubbish!
Cảnh đẹp, thơ mộng. Nhưng dịch vụ ăn uống quá chặt chém. Nên hỏi giá cần thận trước khi sử dụng.

Một trong những vịnh đẹp nhất thế giới, các bạn nên thử 1 lần trong đời trải nghiệm. Dịch vụ tàu tham quan tốt nhưng cần lưu ý tàu sẽ dẫn chúng ta vào các bè nuôi cá. Chỉ nên tham quan hoặc hỏi khi có nhu cầu, tranh tình trạng hỏi han chỉ chỏ họ mang lên đập đầu cá ngay khi mình chưa có quyết định chính xác.
Nước biển không được xanh có lẽ do thời tiết hôm đó ko nắng. Đi thuyền ra vịnh thì bị bỏ qua vài nơi trên vé, lý do là mấy nơi đó ko đẹp nên bỏ qua. Mình không hài lòng về dịch vụ thuyền ở đây.
Cảnh đẹp nhưng quá đông du khách người trung Quốc,họ rất ồn ào
Hạ long thì đẹp rồi. Nhưng dịch vụ xung quanh chưa được ok cho lắm
đi tàu dạo đảo nhớ mặc cả giá máy anh cò nha....
Bãi cát đẹp nhưng biển quá bẩn
Trải nghiệm mới với King Kong park rất ổn, tuy nhiên 30/4/2021 vẫn chưa hoàn thành nên chưa xem đc trọn vẹn

Ngắm kỳ quan thiên nhiên là quá ngon rồi. Mát giời, biển lặng, chan hòa nắng, ngắm hoàng hôn hay xế chiều đều đẹp. Đi bộ vào hang mỏi chân tí, nhưng bù lại ăn uống trên thuyền với nhau là quá vui.  Xem giá vé̉ trước để tiện chọn mua, say sóng thì chọn hôm biển lặng, chú ý chụp choẹt ko rớt cmn điện thoại xuống biển.  Mình là người có tiền hãy ăn trên thuyền. Có tầu khá khinh khinh thường khách, ra vẻ này nọ.

Vịnh thì đẹp nhưng bãi biển ở Hạ Long  thì tồi tệ, vô cùng tồi tệ. Tôi có cảm giác không yên tâm và bất bình về dịch vụ thay đồ tắm ở đây, người dân không thân thiện lắm (Họ có tư tưởng chèn ép khách, không biết chính quyền có quản lý hay là dv tự phát)

Cảnh đẹp nhưng những người lái thuyền hơi thiếu ý thức. Thiết nghĩ các thuyền nên nhường nhau, từ từ từng thuyền một tiến gần tới hòn đôi cho khách chụp hình cái tờ tiền 200k chứ không nên bu lại, thuyền này đụng thuyền kia, khách vừa sợ vừa không chụp được

Khung cảnh đẹp, dịch vụ và nhiều tiện ích giảm xuống do dịch covid và hạn chế người hoa sang quảng ninh giao thương. Khách sạn và hoạt động du lịch chỉ có người việt hoặc người nước ngoài đang định cư tại việt nam. Hoạt động ở mức cầm chừng, thăm dò.

Mình ở hưng yên nên có cơ hội tham quan nơi này khá nhiều. Cảm nhận của mình cảnh quan đẹp. Riêng về phần ăn uống cảm thấy bị chém khá nhiệt tình dù đã đi nhiều lần. Khách sạn 1l đặt và bỏ vì không đúng như ý mình. 100m là tới biển mà mình nhìn xung quanh toàn nhà. Khá thất vọng về các dịch vụ ăn uống nghỉ ngơi

nằm ở bờ Tây của Vịnh Bắc Bộ, bao gồm vùng biển đảo thuộc thành phố Hạ Long, Thành phố Cẩm Phả và một phần của huyện đảo Vân Đồn, tỉnh Quảng Ninh. Vịnh Hạ Long có bờ biển dài 120km, diện tích khoảng 1.553km vuông bao gồm 1.969 hòn đảo lớn nhỏ, phần lớn là đảo đá vôi, trong đó vùng lõi của vịnh có diện tích 335km vuông quần tụ dày đặc 775 hòn đảo lớn nhỏ. Không chỉ đẹp bởi cảnh sắc “mây trời sóng nước”, vẻ đẹp nên thơ hay vô số những đảo đá vôi nổi trên mặt nước mà Hạ Long còn đem lại cho du khách cảm giác bình yên khi hòa mình vào cảnh vật nơi đây. Được đánh giá là một trong 29 Vịnh đẹp nhất thế giới, cuối tháng 3/2012, tổ chức New Open World cũng đã chính thức công nhận vịnh Hạ Long là một trong bảy kỳ quan thiên nhiên mới của thế giới. Không chỉ đẹp bởi cảnh sắc “mây trời sóng nước”, vẻ đẹp nên thơ hay vô số những đảo đá vôi nổi trên mặt nước mà Hạ Long còn đem lại cho du khách cảm giác bình yên khi hòa mình vào cảnh vật nơi đây. Được đánh giá là một trong 29 Vịnh đẹp nhất thế giới, cuối tháng 3/2012, tổ chức New Open World cũng đã chính thức công nhận vịnh Hạ Long là một trong bảy kỳ quan thiên nhiên mới của thế giới.
Đẹp, nhưng con người làm du lịch hơi chán, đến đây nên mua tour 5sao đi thà mắc.

Giá cả cho 1 tour khá ổn 200k 1 người nếu bạn chỉ muốn lênh đênh trên biển vì muốn xuống các hang tham quan thì xẽ tốn thêm chi phí mình nhớ không nhầm là 70k cho người lớn và 50k cho trẻ em lên thuyền xẽ có 2 người 1 người chụp ảnh (50k 1 tấm nhận vào cuối tour )và giới thiệu xung quanh chẳng hạn như hòn đảo này , kia tên dì ( họ giới thiệu cho mình đúng 1 hòn đảo in trên tờ 200k và 1 hòn đảo hình đầu chó ) còn người kia bán các loại quà lưu niệm về cảnh vật thì.thật sự cũng không quá đẹp như quảng cáo dù mình đi lần đầu tiên trên biển có ít rác ngay bến xuống hang cũng có bên trong hang động cũng không quá đẹp như quảng cáo và 1 số chỗ người ta vứt tiền xuống khá nhiều trông không đẹp cho lắm còn về phần chiếc thuyền thì khá to có 2 lầu nhưng hơi ồn chắc do dùng động cơ cũ mình ngồi trên lầu đôi khi ngược gió bị khói đen phả vào chạy xuống gấp thuyền khá chòng chành ai xay xe cần chú ý
Vịnh đẹp đúng nghĩa di sản thiên nhiên, xong công tác quản lý, trật tự, và dịch vụ cần quan tâm hơn!
Đi tàu cẩn thận nha. Mua 2 dĩa mì xào dở ẹc mà 1tr (đoàn mình bị lừa)
Tuyệt vời
Cảnh quan bị thay đổi nhiều, lấn biển tự nhiên
Bãi nhân tạo không hề có sóng. Nước thì bẩn nhé.
Vẫn còn hạn chế và thiếu sót về phục vụ

Hạ long park chán lắm
Quá đẹp 😊😊😊😊 …
Nguyễn đình hữu.
2/2、2/3造訪，可惜冬日空汙嚴重，除了毛毛細雨也沒有美景，怎麼拍都是灰濛濛一片，過年期間團費又貴的可怕，強烈建議大家看好季節再來。

De los lugares más bonitos del mundo que he visitado en mi vida. Ahora eso sí, sigo pensando que los vietnamitas quieren hacer de su entorno un parque de atracciones para los turistas cuando lo bonito es mantenerlo natural y real.

Ha Long Bay is undoubtedly a breathtaking destination, and visiting it was a memorable one-time experience. The **panoramic views of the mountains and ocean** were truly mesmerizing, offering stunning scenery at every turn. Exploring the **hills, taking a bamboo boat ride, and visiting the caves** made the trip even more special, leaving us with unforgettable moments.  However, there were a few aspects that could be improved. I noticed that **guides seemed to give more preference to American and European tourists**, which was a bit disappointing. Additionally, the **boat service could use some improvements**, particularly in terms of **food quality and seating arrangements**, which were not up to the mark.  Overall, Ha Long Bay is a must-visit for its natural beauty and unique experiences, but a few enhancements in service would make it even better.
Nice day tour experience. Nice view of giant limestone, but water is not that clean compare to Elnido Coron, Phi Phi islands. There’s also a long queue (crowded) going to the cave.

Visto con il sole è molto bella, ma negli ultimi anni è sovraffollate. L'aveva vista nel 2019 a marzo, era nuvoloso freschino ma godibile. Per fortuna stanno ripulendo l'acqua che era veramente inquinata. Le attività da fare sono poche (non c'è più il villaggio dei pescatori, caratteristico ma assurdo allevare pesci in acque così inquinate), bisogna quindi affidarsi ad una buona nave da crociera che riesca a intrattenere gli ospiti.

Definitivamente es un lugar maravilloso, los atardeceres increíbles! Es una verdadera lastima la situación de la polución en el mar! La cantidad de plásticos hace que pierda la magia. Las autoridades locales y los empresarios del turismo deberían tomar cartas en el asunto y lograr un propósito especial para limpiar la bahía!

I booked tour through Getyourguid. With tour operator VIETNAM TRAVEL PACKAGE Looks like they deleted my review from there I didn’t like the organization. There was almost no interesting or meaningful info about place provided during our trip. Just like I ordered transportation from place to place. The guid was talking too much about himself. Several times he was pretty arrogant talking to me. It was so little time to enjoy nature and places. They don’t let you to take plastic bottles of water from bus to ship. But when you look into the water from ship, you can observe a lot of rubbish around. And plastic is included. They sell drinks for 30k+. Too touristy place. Traffic of crowds at each point. I felt like everybody here are just doing what they have, no personal attitude no real attention. Also they pick you up only from Old Quarter.  The only great thing is nature that is amazing. I think it’s possible and much better to go by your own for several days.
道中の移動時間などを考えたら一回行けば十分です。  エメラルドグリーンの海とネットには書いてありますが、盛りすぎです。 ただの濁った海です。  想像より絶景ではなかった。 ネットにのってる写真は色合いをだいぶ加工してると思うので注意。現実はそんなに甘くないです。笑

This place is overrun by tourists! It's a 2 hr coach ride from Hanoi city plus another 1 hr boat ride out. Personally it's a once only trip for me as I don't think it's worth the effort especially on a foggy day. I prefer the same setting in a neighbouring country with better weather conditions.  Do note this trip is not suitable for those on wheelchair or unable to climb flights of stairs unaided. I took a tour that had 3 drop off points to see a beach and climb up a mountain, to take a bamboo boat ride out, and a cave climbing experience. It would be fun if you are nimble footed. Otherwise, you are left to enjoy the experience of hanging out on the boat while waiting for the rest.
대한민국  다도해 바다가 잔잔하고 평화롭습니다. 유명해서 왔지만  두번은 오지 않을거구요 그냥 바다에 작은 섬이 몇개 있습니다

A bit disappointing with the view as I might be expecting too much (as it's the world heritage). The cave is wonderful, the bay is so so, nothing WOW 😅  The port is nice and very big but it's very crowded with tourists.
比起陸龍灣的閒靜，下龍灣是另一種美，不推薦坐快艇，漲潮的時候，什麼都看不到，只是花了一個人500塊，在海上曬太陽，被海風狠狠的刮了30分， 導遊一句，因為漲潮所以 沒辦法看，這不是你們要事先查好的嗎？越南地陪真的要挑人😑😑😑

Nous avons fait la baie de Lan Ha juste à côté. Temps maussade et pluvieux nous n'avons pas pu faire de belles photos de coucher de soleil !  Sinon croisière au top 3 jours et 2 nuits. Activités de Kayak, pêche de nuit du calamar et karaoké le soir.  Seul problème la pollution de la baie avec tout type de déchets sont du plastique !! Quel dommage

Krásne prostredie ,ktoré dokázali zničiť svojou nezodpovednosť a otrasný prístupom k prírode jediné čo ich zaujíma je,čo najviac obrať turistov o peniaze toto miesto navštívi ročne 12 milonov ľudí a niesu schopný si urobiť poriadok a takto ničia prírodu je smutné ,dúfam že sa zamyslia a začnú s tím niečo robiť.
El lugar es bonito pero muy parecido a Ninh Bình. Te cobran muy caro por los cruceros y si no hay sol no vale tanto la pena. Los guías son malos, hablan muy mal inglés no se entiende nada.

Trångt massor av människor. Blir behandlad som en skock får. Grottan är vacker.  Tyvärr är det svårt att stanna och stå still. Man får inte ta med vattenflaska ombord på båten. Plastpåsar med diverse saker går bra. Kaffemuggar i plast går bra.

Beautiful bay, crazy sunrise and sunsets, the Dau Go cave has a crazy big cavern (other caves aren’t really worth it). BUT…truth must be told here. As soon as you arrive in Tau Chau to get on your cruise, you realize how many boats are there (around 700 in total). The consequences of this are that to manage all these cruises and people, strict schedules are required and infrastructure has been built on islands such as on Ti Top island. Where you go kayaking, you have to queue and feel like an ant commanded to go kayaking within a certain timeframe. And at night, all the cruises are doing karaoke and you can hear music throughout the bay. For me…this takes away the magic. If you want to avoid this, book a more expensive 3 day 2 night cruise where they don’t switch you on different boats (they have a main boat, and then a boat that takes you on day 2 together with other people from other boats, losing you time in the process).

This place was a fishing village before tsunami. The residents have been relocated and given opportunity by the government to continue earning a living at sea by becoming canoe paddlers. We can meet former residents of fishing villages when the boat leaning on a pier near the caves.  It is not as we we expected. The distance, the view of the cave is not meet our expectations. If you look closely, you will turns out that the dirty water -even though the color is still green-. There was some kind of oil layer, floating at top of the water, some sort of waste that might have come from our boats. With this water conditions, I don't know if it's safe to swim here. It's a shame the world heritage is not well maintained even though it has entered UNESCO protection.

Este sitio es realmente mágico y lo disfrutarás mucho. Lo recomiendo al 100%. Le quité dos estrellas a mi review porque noté que toda la basura de Hanoi llega a la bahia, el plástico está por todos lados e incluso se forman islotes de basura. No se cuida la bahia como debería ser.  Es una pena visitar un sitio así, declarado patrimonio de la humanidad por la UNESCO, un lugar tan bello y completo de naturaleza, y encontrarse que no lo protegen😢 En general, deberíamos todos tomar acciones para cuidar nuestro medio ambiente.  Si visitate este lugar y notaste lo mismo, puedes hacer lo mismo. Quizás si somos muchos, nos escuchan.

El paisaje es hermoso pero el agua está mugrienta por dónde vayas. Muchísima basura, plásticos y demás porquerías. Imposible darse un baño sin pensar que te enfermarás o te saldrá algún eccema en la piel. Una lástima el poco respeto a la naturaleza de parte del ser humano. Para ver la geografía solo es necesario hacer un tour de un día. No vale la pena dormir en algún crucero allí.

This place is incredible but damn, it’s really hard to look past the thousands of styrofoam pieces, shoes, plastic bottles… and did I mention styrofoam? So.Much.Styrofoam. The magic of this natural wonder is covered in a layer of styrofoam. Bummer dude

Overall a must visit destination in your Vietnam travel itinerary. Allow 2-3 days at least to unwind and explore this magical place. A one day overnight cruise is strongly recommended which cost around USD$150 all inclusive except drinks.  Swimming is great here in the warmer months however water is heavily polluted. Some can’t resist the opportunity however if you have skin conditions and/or weak stomach DON’T  Must do: sunrise🌅 sunset  Reason for 3 stars you may ask. The amount of rubbish on the water is unfathomable. Completely trashed world heritage asset. Lots of Tourists but most will respect and cherish the place. The people living on the water throw everything into the water that feed them. The locals do not respect the beauty which brings tourists to this place. Cẩm Phả’s main economic driver. Shame on the local government’s for not doing enough to educate the public.

Il panorama nella baia è incredibile: le miriadi di isolette con pareti rocciose piene di vegetazione che costellano la baia sono di una bellezza incredibile e vale la pena passarci attraverso con una barca. Tuttavia questo è l'unico aspetto davvero bello di Halong Bay: il mare è molto sporco sia per l'enorme numero di navi da crociera che ci transitano senza controllo, sia per i rifiuti che galleggiano un po ovunque. Se pensate di fare una crociera nella baia per godervi un bagno in acque cristalline, vi sbagliate: l'unico punto balneabile in cui sarete portati è Titop Island, ed avrete a disposizione solamente due metri dalla costa di acqua verdastra e maleodorante in cui sguazzare. L'insieme è estremamente turistico e sfruttato all'osso, purtroppo Halong Bay è uno splendido patrimonio dell' UNESCO che si sta deteriorando a causa della sua massiccia mercificazione.
初四，忘了泡咖啡叫醒自己 棉被裡的溫度，讓人想著賴床的幸福！ 旁邊睡著的人兒，還抱在懷裡熟睡.... 我要讓心享受這一刻.... 我讓手臂享受酥麻..... 這一年， 一樣小河陪伴， 這一年， 一樣一起起床， 這是徐志摩式的浪漫🌹 當夜幕低垂， 輕輕數著心跳入睡， 當曙光撥雲， 靜靜守候妳睜開雙眼， 無論星月無際， 我心恬靜， 隨處結祥雲. 隨遇而安心⋯⋯
Prachtig maar Bai Tu Long Bay is veel rustiger en minder toeristisch. Cruiseterminal is erg hectisch en druk.
Un peu de déception en arrivant sur la baie très construite et encore en travaux. Croisière en bateau sympa mais perte d'authenticité lié à un afflux de touriste trop important.
Very beautiful place but too many people and boats

Views are great but the pollution, garbage, crowds are terrible. We liked our ship as it was calm there but all the attractions outside of the boat were very disappointing. It's a pitty to see such a beautiful place so dirty and packed.

Beautiful but full of boat and people. If you can go to New Zealand visit Doubtful sound. Same kind of place but you will be alone in the wild with same beautiful landscape and even higher peak than in Halong Bay. But Halong bay is also awesome it's just that there is too many tourists. But there is kind of solution to meet less people it is to buy an expensive tour. More you will pay more private and special the visit of Halong Bay will be. I hope I helped you. Enjoy.
Un bellissimo luogo creato dalla natura e rovinato dall uomo e dal turismo sfrenato
A lot of debris and pollution in the water. Hundreds of thousands of Styrofoam blocks and garbage floating. Stunning views and scenery but a lot of work needed to clean up the bay
夏の雨季は航海中に雨が降ることもしばしば。 隆起した岩が面白い形で観られました。 ハノイに来たら一度は見てみるのもいいかも。

Ha long is indeed an exotic place, when you're in Vietnam you must come and visit this place...  Usually tourists come here by joining a tour package sold in Hanoi, but please be careful when choosing the tour operator. Some of them aren't professional at all.  They usually promise you heaven but when you arrive in Ha long and you're already on the boat your dream will mean disaster. The seafood they offer can be very different...just be careful.  As for Ha long bay, when you've already had your lunch you can do water sports such as rowing/kayaking or swimming or you can hike up the hill and enter the cave.
天気によって、、と言う感じです。 鍾乳洞にもいけるのですが、 東南アジア特有のライトアップがうーんって感じでした。

Overall a nice experience. Booking it from Hanoi is very expensive. Takes up a whole day. Should check the weather before. It was too touristy for me. Not recommended to do on weekends (gets very busy with tourists)
確かに景色はいいものの、行くには相当時間がかかりました、ほとんどバスで1日観光になります。。。。
To many tourists and uneducated guides are destructing this natural beauty. Please help it protect for future visitors.

Absolutely beautiful, the sailing experience and the scenery worth every penny. Service is a different story though. Beware of drinks sold in the big ships that take you around the Bay. They will secrectly charge you a lot more than what's listed on the menu, always check price first for everything you buy around this area. It's not about the price difference, but rather the principle. Next part: Unless you can do kayaking yourselves, if you choose the bamboo boats, these boat ladies will keep asking you for big tips (10 US or 200kVND) continously and shamelessly as they take you into the caves where surveillance are limited. I accept that they should be tipped for the physical work they do, but they make it feel like it's your obligation to give them the amount they demand. These people will screaming, yelling and dancing like crazy on these bamboo boats until they get the tips. Very stupid! Btw, I am native Vietnamese so no bias on my part. Just be aware that tourism in Ha Long still needs a lot of attention and improvement and don't be fooled. 3 star experience after all, if not worse.

Spektakuläre Aussicht, tolle Gesteinsformationen, es hätte ein sehr schöner Ausflug werden können wenn nicht von Touristen überlaufen und das Meer entsetzlich verdreckt vom Müll der Besucher, schade für ein Weltkulturerbe!
Very scenic, but I'll recommend the day cruise instead of overnight stay.

All overnight boats big or small anchor in one bay although reasonably spaced apart. Of the 1,000 plus Islands the vast majority of the overnight boats see a few Islands close up. it’s not a tour of all of the islands. However, the mystery Cave is defo worth doing. Avoid the tourist trap beach, way too cramped for me

El lugar sin duda es precioso pero asegúrate de encontrar un buen tour. Es lamentable que uno no logra disfrutar de los puntos de atracción de Ha long por la cantidad de turistas que hay en todas partes, resulta agobiante. Además, tuvimos mala experiencia con un tour en crucero que reservamos por get your guide, en el que no nos dejaron entrar con botellas de agua, para vendernos el agua dentro del bote.
天空不算很蓝，游客有点多
天気のせいもあるでしょうが、あまり感動はなかったです。
ドラゴンボールの世界感ですね。 船上で食べる海鮮料理もなかなかです。

La baie est très belle mais son exploitation est à l'heure actuelle un désastre écologique, il faut durcir les règles pour la préserver  !!!  On vous empêche d'embarquer avec des bouteilles d'eau pour préserver la baie mais le personnel des croisières jette ses mégots dans l'eau. Les petits bateaux qui déposent à partir des bateaux principaux fonctionnent à l'essence et déversent des kg de gasoil sur les gens qui sont sur la plage. D'ailleurs tout le monde va à la même grotte, à la même plage qui sont bondées de monde, ce n'est pas très agréable, c'est dommage.
Good Place, but lot of Garbage in the sea which government should clean. If the weather is good and not hot then it's a good trip. Caves are wonderful. Beach is also nice.
穏やかな気分になりました。  少しツアーは長かったです。

당일치기 투어는 비추... 다시 가게된다면 깟빠섬 1박을 하거나 1박2일 투어를 이용할것 같다
Very busy lots and lots of boats .. tourist hot spot We stayed over the bridge and it was great no tourists clean sandy beaches friendly local non tourist prices
Too busy and polluted. The cruises are also really expensive so I think they can do a better job.

Beautiful scenery,but it's so sad it's getting slowly ruined by tourists.... it's not tourists' fault tho,as everyone wants to see it. It's the government not looking after it and not limiting the numbers. Also since it's a Unesco world heritage its disgusting how much plastic and rubbish you can see in water. Can they not pay some poor local to clear it?? Also saw 3 different men sailing the boats,smoking and chucking cigarettes straight into water,as well as rubbish!!!!! While they kept telling us tourists about the smoking areas and that they do not accept any plastic on board. While they keep chucking all the trash over the boat! DISGUSTING!!!! What's all this about "let's protect this area and keep it clean?" Its not tourists that trash the place,but locals who have no respect for their own land. It became just like phi phi island in Thailand, sadly..

Mixed feelings. The place is absolutely gorgeous, I agree with everyone else but it is just so crowded and overrun by tourism. There should be a limit as to how many people are allowed in into the bay. It was a one-and-done experience for me. Definitely not going to come back.

Although we had a great time with Elite of the Seas cruise, the waters here were strewn with litter! The experience would have been much better with cleaner waters. 3 stars as there is room for improvement!

Tightly regulated with 500+ plus tourist boats. Stunning scenery with degraded environment and increasing mass tourism. No place for solitude. If you like kayaking in swarms of tourists and  don't mind plastic garbage along the shore and in the water this is for you...
한번 가볼만 한듯. 와일드한 자연을 상상했지만 베이 옆으로 고층 빌딩도 보이고 선착장 주변으로도 개발이 많이 되어있어 개인적으로 살짝 감흥이 떨어졌음. 여행사들 호객행위도 있고. 왕복 차량이랑 배삯 따로 어레인지 갈수있음 그렇게 하세요. 하노이 여행사에서 패키지로 받은 값의 1/3 가격으로 가능함.
Um local lindo, classificado como património Unesco mas infelizmente muito negligenciado. Há barcos a mais, muito lixo na água, nada atraente para dar um mergulho.
Stunning place, although overturism (wich i am part of it) is destroying it, a lot of contamination. If you can avoid going here you're doing a good thing to the planet

This was one of the places we were looking forward to the most on our 3 months Asia trip. Unfortunately it didn't live up to the hype. The photos you see are highly edited and aren't a accurate representation of the place.  Its been plague with litter, so much so that the boat drivers think nothing of throwing empty cigarette packages etc into the sea.  We took a cruise around the bay for a couple of days so got to see alot of it. Its quite repetitive and very touristy.  Don't feel like you have to do this if you're in Vietnam, I'd highly recommend spending more time in Sapa or doing a multi day caving experience in Phong Na.

Ha Long Bay can be really pretty on a clear day. With caves, kayaking, trekking, monkeys to see and cool scenery, there can be quiet a few things to do. However, be prepared for a slew of Chinese tourists and it's wise to book tours to save you from all the chaos and haggling. Besides I believe some areas would be extremely difficult to get to as a solo traveler as it really seems to run off tours.

Hạ Long ist laut UNESCO eins der natürlichen Weltwunder der Erde und tatsächlich sehr schön. Die Karst Gebirge kennt man von vielen anderen Orten auch aber wie sie hier zu Tausenden als kleine Inseln aus dem Wasser ragen und sich über hunderte, sogar über 1000e Quadratkilometer erstrecken ist schon Wahnsinn. Die schiere Dimension der Bucht ist kaum fassbar.  "Hạ Long" ist Vietnamesisch und bedeutet vermutlich "Styropor", denn das ist die häufigste Sicht die man hat. Polyethylen ist auch reichlich vorhanden aber sehr oft auch unter Wasser, zu finden beispielsweise an den Stränden wo man zum Kajak fahren oder Baden hingefahren wird wenn man eine Tour macht.  Es ist eine Schande, wie die Vietnamesen mit ihrer Natur und unserem Planeten umgehen.
Overrated.  Nice islands, nice history but you're on a boat. If you plan on going here for an extended stay, try and get a kayak and solo it instead of doing it with a boat tour.

We all see the beautiful idyllic photos of Ha Log Bay with one junk navigating this amazing seascape. Well in November 2018 the landscape is still gorgeous and of course unique but that single junk is a dream from the past. All the 50 plus tourist boats must anchor for the night in the same small cove and leave the next morning to the port of Ha Long in a long procession. The Grotto which is also classified is quite impressive, well manned and perhaps we were lucky but with all the voluminous foot traffic we were relatively isolated. Like a lot of World Heritage Sites they are maximized to yield financial gain. Thanks Michel from Alaska

Eine atemberaubende Kulisse, die leider durch Massentourismus und den damit verbunden Dingen, ziemlich zerstört wird. Hunderte Schiffe und Boote täglich. Dieselgestank und tausende Touristen werden durch die Bucht, auf die Kajaks und die Höhlen geschoben. Die Natur ist wahnsinn, aber das drumrum ist für mich einfach nur traurig. Trotzdem sollte man es sich anschauen finde ich. Die 3 Sterne sind nicht für die Bucht, sondern für die übermäßige Vermarktung.

잔잔한 물결에 배 멀리를 걱정하지 않아도 됩니다. 이곳은 여행사를 통해 예약을 하고 갈수 있으나 현지에서도 직접 티켓팅이 가능합니다. 현장발권이 되지 않고 중간에 에이전트 같은 분(여성)들이 있습니다. 간단한 영어로 배 탑승권을 구입할수 있습니다. 풍광이 좋고 굴에 들어가는 코스가 있습니다. 중간에 소형 배(노를 젓는)를 타는 코스도 있습니다. 직접 몰아 볼수 있습니다. 중간 중간 코스별로 소소한 재미 있는 코스가 더 있다고 들었습니다. 한번은 가볼만한 곳입니다.
Sehr schöner Ort. Sollte man besichtigen. Jedoch nicht zu den Saisonspitzenzeiten. Wir waren im Dezember hier. Absolut mainstream und überlaufen (Höhlenbesichtigung dauert dadurch 1 Stunde länger).

Me pareció un poco sobrevalorado. Esta masificado, el agua es muy sucia con basura flotando y manchas de gasolina encima. Ninh binh nos ha impresionado mucho mas

UNESCO World Heritage Ha Long Bay – an area ‘known for its emerald waters and thousands of towering limestone islands topped by rainforests’. If you see promotional images of Vietnam, this location is bound to be among them, if not the primary image. However, marketing & reality was vastly different. The smog, despite being 3 hours from Hanoi was incredibly bad and the water was littered with rubbish. Thousands of wooden boats in various states of disrepair meander around the limestone islands, all the while pumping black smoke across the water and in to the air. Any stop made on foot is a torturous experience as your need to fight your way through the hordes of Chinese tourists who visit due to the location’s close proximity to China. There is simply no kind of reverence, care or responsibility given to this incredible, natural area. It’s just treated like a consumable tourist trap where guides and locals try their best to scam the tourists and in return, the tourists damage the rocks, litter on the beaches and in the ocean and think little of the future. A real shame...
Wonderful place. However the water is so dirty and full of plastic. Too many boats and tourists.

Unfortunately, the beauty of the islands in Halong Bay is overshadowed by the amount of different trash and  oil slicks now very visible in the waters as one cruises about. It was bad enough that I opted to neither swim nor kayak.
Should be good if not so many trash and party boat. Party boat was the worst. So loud and noisy. And our cruise cheated us. Bad experience for us for halong bay.
Nice view, but very crowded. I would prefer to spend time elsewhere

Intressant natur med fantastiska bergsformationer. Området är dock överetablerat. Massor av djonker som alla ska vara i samma område. Det är trångt med alla båtar. "Grottan" som är den stora sevärdigheten är kraftigt överreklamerad. Mycket folk, trångt och med dålig säkerhet eftersom folk skall fram och tillbaka i samma trappa och ingång/utgång.  Vill man åka till Ha Long ska man nog göra det omgående. Om några år kommer området att vara "överbelalastat" pga all turism. Exploateringen i området är för närvarande kraftig.  Men kul och intressant att ha varit där.

Hạ Long Bay is a breathtaking natural wonder, with thousands of limestone karsts rising dramatically from emerald waters. For the best experience, it’s worth choosing at least a 4-star junk boat, as comfort and service can vary greatly. Programs may change depending on weather conditions, and some areas can feel crowded with boats. Despite this, the overall experience remains unforgettable and absolutely worth it.
Prices aboard the ship are fairly steep. On the day we visited, the weather was overcast and dreary. If you’ve already been to Guilin, this destination likely won’t offer anything new or compelling.

A Ha Long Bay cruise is a must-do: stunning limestone karsts, emerald waters, and well-organized itineraries with cave visits, kayaking, and island stops. Enjoy fresh Vietnamese meals, relaxing pacing, and unforgettable scenery—perfect for nature lovers and first-time visitors alike.

Non metto 5 solo perché purtroppo l ho trovata molto sporca con plastica che galleggia ed in alcuni punti anche pesci morti. È un peccato vedere un posto così incantevole contornato da palazzi e continue costruzioni che rendono la baia un "americanata". Naturalmente vale la pena vederla e come abbiamo fatto noi godersela con una crociera di almeno una notte  nella baia di Tu Long, più tranquilla e defilata.
It’s stunning—but be prepared for crowds, as most tours tend to visit the same spots. If you can find a more unique or off-the-beaten-path tour, that would be ideal!
Absolutely stunning—but swarming with tourists, even during the off-season! The boats were packed so tightly against the jetties, there was barely any space between them.
Didn’t expect much, but skipping Halong Bay in Vietnam feels wrong. No need to overspend on a luxury overnight cruise—a well-planned day trip lets you see this stunning wonder.

a good destination for a day visit, there you can enjoy the view from the cruise, dinner on the cruise, ride a boat and visit the cave, but unfortunately in the cruise the dining area is combined with other people which makes eating less comfortable, and in the Sung Sot cave it is very hot and there are many people, especially many impatient Indians.
Kayaking through limestone towers, swimming in hidden lagoons, and sunset cocktails on deck? Absolute magic. Nature showed off—and we happily cheered along!

Una de los mejores visitas que hemos realizado en Vietnam es a la Bahía de Halong (Patrimonio de la Humanidad) y una de las Siete Maravillas Naturales del Mundo .Siendo un conjunto de Islas e Islotes de piedra caliza (nosotros elegimos un crucero para pasar la noche y desde él  hicimos diferentes expediciones a diversos sitios y actividades) es importante estudiar antes de ir él qué ver y el qué hacer .El paisaje tantas veces visto en reportajes o fotos no es comparable con la realidad  de los miles de islotes y formaciones cársticas .Un paseo con Kayak hará falta para comprobar toda esta maravilla de la Naturaleza,que lamentablemente y como no la cuiden desaparecerá por sobreexplotación turistica y malas prácticas por parte de algunos  locales y de algunos los turistas

The cruise was gorgeous—but we visited in low season, so all beach facilities were closed by day (yet two loud clubs lit up at night!). Hotels cater almost exclusively to East Asian tourists, ignoring the many Western visitors desperately seeking snacks, drinks, or toilets—only to give up and leave. A missed opportunity!
ハロン湾は本当に美しい海と山の風景で、子どもも大はしゃぎ。家族全員にとって最高の休日になりました。クルーズもゆったりで感動もの。ただ、観光客が多くて少し混雑していたのが残念。それでもまた訪れたいと思う素敵な場所です。

it is a nice, amazing palce, but sad to see rubbish and dirt in the ocean. Lots of boats on teh water and i think it could be better if they are less than what they are now.The trip is nice, i loved it and wife enjoyed it too. Halong is definiytely a gem.
私たちはハロン湾で信じられないほどの岩の造形を目の当たりに！船から見る景観はまるでファンタジーの世界。友達と洞窟探検やカヤックも楽しく、思い出に残る旅になった。自然の芸術に全員感動！

Cruising through the limestone islands of Halong Bay felt like entering a postcard. Kayaking together and watching the sunset from the deck brought us closer. It’s serene, magical, and the perfect romantic getaway.
