We did a Halong Bay day trip from the Peridot Gallery Classic Hotel in Hanoi, and it was stunning! The limestone cliffs and peaceful waters are unforgettable. We visited a pearl farm first, then boarded the cruise, and explored one of the caves—busy but beautiful.One lesson learned: don’t skip breakfast! I felt a bit seasick early on because I didn’t eat before the long bus ride and boat. The hotel serves a nice breakfast—take advantage of it!We chose a 5-star day cruise, and I highly recommend doing the same. It made a big difference in comfort—spacious seating, better food, and clean restrooms. It’s only a little more, but totally worth it.✅ Tip:
vịnh hạ long là một địa điểm thu hút rất nhiều du khu khách trong nước và khách nước ngoài ,khi du khách có kế hoạch đi du lịch ,địa điểm đầu tiên nghĩ đến là vịnh hạ long.
We did 3-day overnight cruise. The beautiful scenery.  Very crowded season.  We were disappointed when it's not so clean to swim. But it's till a must to do if you come to Vietnam.

We did Halong Bay. The limestone things are definitely majestic, no doubt. It was surprisingly peaceful out on the boat, just us two. You can sort of see where people get their cultural and artistic inspiration from, even if we mostly just pointed at stuff. Decent spot for a couple's trip, not gonna lie.
Wow it is very good place for sightseeing, kayak, climbing, the requested is clean up the trash by organizers. Stunning graphics
Halong Bay has many spectacular views with its unique mountain ranges and many islands for boat navigation. Be prepared to take many pictures.
Está todo muy bonito pero muy sucio el agua … es una llamada urgente al gobierno de Vietnam que cuiden más la zona y cobren tasa a los turistas y con ese dinero poner barcos de limpieza !!!

It's a beautiful and unmissable place to visit, a true wonder that sadly is highly polluted, which is why I gave it 4 points because we tourists need to speak out to save this ecosystem, which at first glance already looks polluted. The trash and oil in the water is the least of its problems, because the waters are heavily polluted by other types of substances. Truly, all of us who visit this place owe it to ourselves to improve it; otherwise, this wonder of nature could disappear.While we were kayaking, we bumped into pieces of plastic, Styrofoam that was disintegrating and polluted, even diapers floating. We saw very few fish and a lot of oil on the water...- - -Es un lugar hermoso e imperdible de visita, totalmente una maravilla que tristemente está muy contaminada por eso le puse 4 puntitos porque nosotros los turistas tenemos que alzar la voz para que salven a este ecosistema que a simple vista ya se ve contaminado, y eso que se ve de basura y aceites en el agua es el menor de sus problemas porque las aguas están muy contaminas por otro tipo de sustancias, de verdad todos los que visitamos este lugar debemos hacer algo por mejorarlo, si no esta maravilla de la naturaleza puede desaparecer.Mientras hacíamos kayak chocábamos con los trozos de plástico, unicel que se iba deshaciendo y contaminando, hasta pañales flotando, observamos muy pocos peces y muchos aceites sobre el agua…
世界遺産の環境関係から、缶、ペットボトルの飲み物の持ち込みは禁止されているとのこと。なので、ツアーのバスの中に置いていくことに。でも船着場にチラホラ発見。そして飲み物がめちゃ高いのにサービスのように持ってくるオバサン。メニュー表はその日のレートによるからないのだと...後の精算で腰抜けた笑笑

De Ha Longbaai (Vietnamees: Vịnh Hạ Long) is een baai bij de stad Hạ Long in het noorden van Vietnam in de Golf van Tonkin, nabij de grens met China. Vịnh Hạ Long betekent in het Vietnamees 'Baai van de dalende draak'. De baai is sinds 1994 werelderfgoed. Het gebied is een archipel van eilanden die hoog verticaal boven de zee uitkomen, dicht bij elkaar staan en door de zee van elkaar worden gescheiden. De baai heeft een kustlijn van 120 kilometer en het gebied beslaat zo'n 1500 vierkante kilometer. Wij genoten van de rust helaas was het bewolkt en mistig weer. Leuke plaatjes schieten was er niet bij hoewel wij nu mystieke foto's hebben.
特に夕日と朝日の瞬間は忘れられません。夕日が海に沈むとき、空と海がオレンジとピンクに染まり、まるで絵画のようでした。朝日も同様に素晴らしく、静かな湾に光が差し込む様子は幻想的でした。クルーズ船でのんびりと過ごし、石灰岩の島々を眺めながら、ゆったりとした時間を楽しみました。観光地として人気ですが、自然の美しさを存分に感じられる場所です。また訪れたいと思える体験でした。

Wir waren für zwei Tage an Board der Dragon Legend 2 und hatten ein positives und gutes Erlebnis. Es lohnt sich weiter rauszufahren und dort zu übernachten, so erfährt man das Gebiet weniger Massentouristisch.
9 em merveille du monde à voir absolumentFaite le de préférence en octobre car pour nous c’était l’hiver la chance a été avec nous avec un super soleil c’est absolument magnifique

Our time at Halong Bay was peaceful, with its stunning seascape leaving a lasting impression. We woke up early to catch the beautiful sunrise—it was serene, though a bit crowded with other tourists. The limestone karsts jutting out of the water created a dramatic view, and the sunset later that day felt equally calming. While some areas felt commercialized, it was still a memorable experience for us. Just don’t expect total solitude amidst the beauty.

The bay greeted us with its majestic karst formations rising like ancient sentinels from emerald waters. It felt peaceful, almost surreal—though the boat’s engine hum broke the spell now and then. We cruised past limestone giants cloaked in greenery, their reflections shimmering lazily below. Sure, it was touristy at spots, but there were moments when we had stretches of quiet to ourselves. Together, we kayaked through hidden caves, giggling as paddles splashed awkwardly. The sunset? Beautiful, yes, but not life-altering. Still, this place has a charm that whispers rather than shouts—a gentle reminder of nature's quiet artistry.
可以看到地标岛礁和许多岛屿的风光，值得一来
A spectacular destination
Exploring Caves and Coves
Ha Long Bay is so beautiful
Worth Seeing
Rocks & Relaxation
3 days of tranquility
海上奇观的平凡体验
Surreale nel bene e nel male
Where rocks reach for the sky

IMHO, if you have been travelling the region, it looks like any other bays, with more limestone and more caves. To make full use of your time, you have to stay overnight to probably kayak and do fishing. it is not worth boarding a day board for a spin.

Fomos em um cruzeiro em que a gente dorme no barco. Paisagens bem bonitas e uma experiencia diferente. Barcos menores saem para praias e cavernas - bonito. Comida boa e alguma diversão a bordo. Bom para descansar com o lçeve balanço do barco. Antes de embarque visitamos um local onde conta a questao das perolas e ostras. Interessante para quem curte
我们选了豪华游轮，感觉还不错。下龙湾的石灰岩岛屿确实很壮观，但人太多了。船上的服务一般般，景色还可以，但有点商业化。体验一次就够了，下次可能不会再来。

The unique karst formations are undeniably scenic, and learning about its fascinating history was a highlight. However, the area was more crowded than we expected. If you don't mind the tourist bustle, it's worth a visit for the natural beauty.

We joined the crowd of tourist boats cruising around. Yes, it's busy and commercialized, but those limestone karsts are still breathtaking. The water wasn't as clean as in photos, and some areas felt overcrowded. Still, our overnight cruise gave us some peaceful moments.

Despite being touristy, we're glad we visited Halong Bay. The misty morning views were magical, even with many boats around. Service was hit-or-miss, but the cave tours were interesting. Watch out for extra charges on drinks. The sunset was a highlight despite the hazy weather.
A wonderful experience at Ha Long Bay. The wonderful scenery along with the fresh air gave me and my family a wonderful trip

The towering karsts and tranquil waters offered a romantic setting. Exploring the bay sparked some cultural and artistic inspiration. However, it was a bit crowded at times. Overall, an enjoyable trip but not entirely unforgettable.

Halong Bay is undeniably stunning. Cruising past thousands of limestone karsts and islets rising from the emerald water was pretty surreal.  It's definitely touristy, but the sheer scale of the landscape makes it feel serene and majestic.

We did the overnight cruise thing like everyone else. The karst formations are stunning, but the bay is quite crowded with boats. Food on board was decent, not amazing. Cave tours felt rushed and packed. Water wasn't as clean as photos suggest. Still, the sunset views made up for the tourist chaos.

La baie d'Halong est une merveille rocheuse dans le ciel, avec des paysages pittoresques. Nous avons apprécié la croisière, mais la foule était un peu dérangeante. Les formations rocheuses sont impressionnantes, mais il y a tant de touristes qu'on ne se sent pas toujours en phase avec la nature. Un bel endroit, sans plus.
Kayaking through the emerald waters, surrounded by towering limestone pillars, felt like stepping into another world. An unforgettable experience we'll cherish forever!

Die majestätischen Kalksteinfelsen, die aus dem smaragdgrünen Wasser ragen, sind einfach überwältigend. Wir haben auf dem Deck unseres traditionellen Bootes den Sonnenuntergang genossen und uns wie im Paradies gefühlt.

La baia di Halong è un complesso di isole (quasi duemila) di origine calcarea, il paesaggio che offre è surreale. Merita una visita almeno una volta nella vita.Metto 4 e non 5 stelle solamente perché, essendo un complesso unico e, tra l'altro, patrimonio Unesco, dovrebbe essere immune da ogni sporcizia e invece ci sono sporadicamente rifuti che galleggiano.

Halong bay is a place of unique beauty. It is better than anyone can imagine. I am giving 4 stars because of all the dirt that is floating up in the bay along the islands. Full of polystyrene pieces. which i guess is used by fishermen. But it is so much,  which if no measures are taken for cleaning the bay, i donot think that visiting and cruising will be sustaibable for a long time.  This is a place where  i would not recommend swimming.
乘船游览下龙湾时，我被那壮观的石灰岩岛屿和碧蓝的海水震撼了。独自一人，享受着宁静的时光，海风轻拂，仿佛时间都停滞了。每个岛屿都有自己的魅力，探索神秘的洞穴让我感到无比兴奋。尽管没有伙伴，但这份孤独让我更加专注于大自然的美丽，心灵得到了真正的放松。

A visit to Halong Bay was part of our tour.  We stayed overnight on a boat called Oriental Sails.  It was a good trip.  The Bay was beautiful. There were monkey climbing on the rocks and the scenery was great.  However, the Bay was very crowded but everyone still had a memorable experience
传说中的巨龙从天而降，形成了这片壮观的喀斯特地貌群。近2000座石灰岩岛屿矗立在碧绿的海水中，确实令人叹为观止。船上设施还算舒适，但服务质量略有参差不齐。建议选择春秋季节前往，夏季游客较多且天气炎热。洞穴探索和皮划艇活动很有趣，但部分景点略显商业化。
Questo luogo fa sognare ad occhi aperti,peccato solo che il mare sia molto sporco,pieno di immondizia date da alto inquinamento di infinite navi presenti.

Halong Bay, near the Chinese border, and it was an unforgettable experience. The scenery was absolutely stunning, with towering limestone cliffs and crystal-clear waters that seemed almost magical. We spent our days kayaking and exploring hidden caves, each discovery more exciting than the last. The kids loved spotting the diverse marine life, and we all enjoyed the peaceful atmosphere.
壮大な石灰岩カルスト地形は息をのむほど美しく、まさに自然の芸術作品でした。しかし、その美しさとは対照的に、水面や岩場にゴミが散乱している光景も目にしました。観光客の増加に伴う環境問題は深刻で、保全活動の必要性を強く感じました。それでも、カヤックや洞窟探検を通じて、ハロン湾の魅力を十分に堪能することができました。持続可能な観光の在り方について考えさせられる、印象深い旅となりました。
Cruising through the emerald waters, surounded by magnificent limestone islands, felt sureal.  Kayaking, swimming, and exploring caves made for an unforgettable adventure!

The stunning scenery of limestone karsts rising from emerald waters left us all in awe. Our family found tranquil moments kayaking through hidden caves and secluded lagoons. The little ones loved spotting monkeys on the islands and learning about the bay's legends from our guide. Despite the occasional tourist crowd, we managed to find quiet spots for picnics and swimming.
Cảnh rất đẹp, du thuyền đẹp nhưng thực sự giá rất đắt, đi gia đình ngủ trên vịnh thì phải tầm 10 củ mới đủ đc. (4 sao). Giá cả trên bờ đắt đỏ cũng không kém.

Our visit to Halong Bay was a mix of  tranquility and romance,despite the crowds. The stunning limestone karsts and emerald waters created a picturesque backdrop for our getaway.while popular, the beauty of the bay still allowed us to find serene moments together.

Quite an outstanding location and very beautiful. Can well see the UNESCO heritage status. Just a pity about the sheer numbers coming through. They are going to need to cap it or it will be destroyed in the future.

The cruise is a must as it's a once in a lifetime experience, highly recommend to do the 3D/2N as the 2D/1N is practically 24 hours and didn't feel enough. The city itself is weird... many high rise buildings, many buildings are empty and look as if they are empty for a long time, the hotels are OK, I'd recommend staying in the city center as other areas barely have restaurants within walking distance. Prices of restaurants are very high comparing to the rest of Vietnam. We've spend here 6 nights and its clear to me why most tours start and end in Hanoi. If you do stay here Sun World is a great experience,  the water park, amusement park and cable ride combo is cheap and good value for money as it's valid for 2 days. The night market is pretty nice and the smaller one (closes at 10pm) is also cool and prices are barely cheap, don't forget to bargain 😀

We did 3 days in Halong before getting home. It's all amazing except pollution. We were shocked by the aount of garbage floating in Halong bay. Maybe, it was because of the heavy rain fall earlier. It's a must place you should not miss if visiting Vietnam.

Garden bay cruise, un crucero no muy grande pero perfecto, éramos 7 personas.  Tattoo muy amable, la cocina rica y el desayuno básico.  Los canarios anticuerpos pero tengáis y muy bonitos con balcón y vistas desde los dos lados del camarote.  Actividades muy entretenidas, clase de cocina, paseo en kayak,Tai chi, pesca de calamar... Las vistas son espectaculares, la únicas decepción es que el mar está tremendamente sucio y lleno de desperdicios, botellas, plásticos... Es la única decepción del viaje

It's worth doing 3 days. We had 2 full day on the cruise and about 3 hour - drive each way from Hanoi - Halong. Halong bay was too touristic. The scenery was impressive. But it was crowded any place we visited. There were too many ships.
Beautiful bay in a sunny day. the weather was not hot like we expected. Our family had a lunch on a boat and visit some cave and island. Its a pity that some areas are polluted by waste.
Halong rất đẹp, rất ấn tượng nhưng giá cả cao quá. Vé thăm vịnh đã mấy trăm một người, cộng hết lại mà ngủ đêm là mất chục củ luôn. Đồ ăn sứ quảng này cũng đắt dị thường :(
Địa điểm rất đẹp cho chuyến tour mùa hè cùng gia đình Sạch sẽ và hùng vĩ.Du thuyền sang trọng và đẹp Rất chuyên nghiệp Chỉ có 1 điều là mùa hè nóng.
Nous avons adoré l'atmosphère paisible et le paysage extraordinaire de la baie d'Halong. Les formations rocheuses spectaculaires nous ont émerveillés. Une escapade romantique inoubliable.
Kayaking through the emerald waters, surrounded by towering limestone cliffs, was pure magic.Sunset cruises and candlelit on the desk made it the most romantic trip ever.

De omgeving is zo mooi. Je wordt er echt stil van. We hebben gevaren met een boot, Emeraude Mini. Mooie boot, niet te groot, aardige mensen en heerlijke lunch. De boottocht was met bezoek aan de’surprising cave’ en Kayak toch in een cave. De laatste twee zijn zonde van de beleving. Het barst er van de mensen (het is geen hoogseizoen) waardoor je achter elkaar loopt in de prachtige grot of op elkaar vaart met kayakking. Kookdemonstratie aan boord stelt niets voor. Samen viskoekjes maken terwijl het deeg al klaar was en volgens de gids zat er koriander in het deeg.  Volgens ons was het dille. We hebben alleen het deeg gekneed eigenlijk. Het is zo mooi op de boot dat het eigenlijk mooi genoeg is of mooier als je alleen de boottocht doet en geniet van de lunch, uitzicht en het gezelschap.

Halong Bay is undeniably stunning, a sight to behold. But honestly, the amount of rubbish floating around was a major buzzkill. And the constant swarm of boats kinda ruins the peaceful vibe. It's a shame, because the natural beauty is incredible. Just be prepared for a bit of a crowded, messy experience.

'Get Your Guide' tour. Amazing. I had been advised not to go because of the weight of tourists, but went against that advice and was pleased to have done so: With expectations well managed, this was a fantastic day trip from Hanoi. Yes, there were lots of other boats / people, but managed to look past them. Special highlight was canoeing in a lagoon completely surrounded by lushly forested cliffs, save for the small cave we paddled through to get there.The boat we were on was very functional, with air-conditioning (plus food, drinks aplenty) on the ground level and open air upstairs.Our guide (Mr Phong) was very good, allowing plenty of time at each location.Overall, a very memorable experience.

We took a two-day cruise, enjoying the breathtaking views and exploring hidden coves and caves.  It is a true testament to the power of nature and a UNESCO World Heritage Site for good reason.Unfortunately, our experience was marred by the significant amount of trash we observed throughout the bay.  Plastic bottles, food wrappers, and other debris detracted from the otherwise serene environment. It highlights the urgent need for better waste management practices to protect this natural wonder.

The majestic karst formations were a sight to behold, like nature’s skyscrapers. But oh boy, the trash! It was like playing ‘I Spy’ with litter. Despite the unexpected ‘decor’, the bay had a unique charm that left us with unforgettable memories and a strong desire to start a recycling campaign.
An overnight cruise would be best way to explore this amazing place. Lot of beautiful small island. Lot of cruises. Very croded but nice and must visit.
The bay's limestone formations and tranquil waters create stunning scenery.  Even with its tropical wet climate,  a cruise through Halong Bay is a must-do for any visitor to Vietnam.

What a stunning place to see.  Over 600 commercial ships offering overnight cruises in the area - an overnight cruise is enough to glimpse the beauty of the landscape. Can be confronting to see the pollution here and only imagine the possibilities.

It's like those giant limestone islands just...grew straight out of the sea. We took a totally fancy cruise, so we lived like kings for 2 days.We kayaked around, and explored some caves with crazy rock formations.  Halong Bay made us feel tiny (in a good way) and definitely spoiled us with the luxury side of things.

El crucero por la Bahia de Halong, nos gusto mucho por lo espectacular del paisaje. El camarote correcto a pesar que la decoración necesitaria renovación, pero se compensa con la gran gastronomia y un magnífico trato de todo el personal del barco y mencion especial para Belle que es imposible mejorarla. Como negativo : es que no hay apenas navegacion estas muy cerca del muelle y rodeados de barcos de otras navieras

Come ci aspettavamo la località  è  molto molto affollata, ma grazie  alla bravura del capitano siamo riusciti a godere appieno le bellezze della baia. La Peony Cruise ha cabine molto confortevoli e pulite. Ia ristorazione è  di buon livello.Le varie attività  a bordo organizzate e condotte direttamente da Tom, sono   state tutte coinvolgenti. Tutto il personale a bordo è  stato sempre molto cortese e disponibile. Esperienza sicuramente consigliabile.
We had an overnight cruise of this beautiful bay, but left a little disappointed due the high level of floating garbage/rubbish which impacted on the beauty of the general environment.

Visited whilst on a cruise and Halong Bay really is very beautiful.  You'll notice that the water is polluted and I can't imagine that anybody would ever want to swim in it.  A short boat tour around the bay and a stop off at a market selling tourist trinkets was about enough for us in such heat and humidity.  Take a hat and cover yourself with sun cream.

Bellissima baia, con un colpo d'occhio fantastico e una visita guidata nelle grotte presenti. Unico neo, troppi turisti e tantissime barche, che stanno rovinando e inquinando il tutto; dovrebbe esserci una regolamentazione incisiva.

Naviguer à travers les eaux tranquilles de la baie est une expérience magique. En admirant les formations rocheuses spectaculaires, on se sent véritablement transporté dans un autre monde. La sérénité qui émane de cet endroit est apaisante pour l'âme.Les multiples activités proposées, telles que la visite des grottes mystérieuses ou une balade en kayak entre les îles, ajoutent une touche d'aventure et de découverte à cette oasis de paix.
意外と岸から近くてびっくりしました。よく考えれば「湾」なので岸に近くて当然なのですが、観光用の写真を見る限り、離島の湾のような秘境を想像していたので（笑）クルーズしながら食事をしたり、奇岩で写真を撮ったり、鍾乳洞に立ち寄ったりと、とても楽しかったかったです。景色もとても良かったので、大満足でした。
Ha Long Bay it's a wonderful place, but overrated, in my opinion. I visited about 4 attractions in this area. I won't come the second time here. At least not for this money.

La Bahía de Halong es un tesoro nacional de Vietnam, un paisaje espectacular, lleno de encantadores paisajes marinos. Esta área es famosa por sus impresionantes formaciones de islas y cuevas, que han sido preservadas durante siglos. Está llena de una magia única, con sus aguas turquesas y sus montañas cubiertas de vegetación. Puedes admirar la belleza de la bahía desde un barco, o disfrutar de una caminata por sus senderos. ¡Es una experiencia única que no te puedes perder! ¡Es una vista que te dejará sin palabras!
La baia di Halong è bella, molto più bella e affascinante di quanto non si veda in foto e video. Questi isolotti a promotorio sono molto belli e imponenti. Gita consigliatissima.

The seascape is stunning - dream like - and the food, crew and activities on our boat - Le Journey- were lovely. We had a great time. However, the bay is a mass tourist destination and we learned that, whilst the sea looks pristine, it is dying beneath the surface because of pollution. Fingers crossed that Vietnam honours its commitments to conserve this UNESCO world heritage site.
Halong Bay is a breathtaking seascape of emerald waters, limestone islands, and towering rock formations. A visit to this tranquil wonder in the sky is an unforgettable experience!

If you are visiting Vietnam and Hanoi, highly unlikely that you would skip Halong Bay. We booked our trip via local tour operator(Hanoi), which was expensive than Klook, but was available for next day (Klook didn't take the booking post 5pm on the previous day).We were picked at around 8am from our hotel and took us 2-2:30 hrs to reach the port, with a mandatory stop in between for washroom/meals and shopping. We had taken the route which had the biggest cave and luxury cruise which had 3 floors. We were treated to a good spread of lunch(limited options for Vegetarian but can't complain) and there were paid drinks available with happy hours in afternoon.Cave: We spent around 45 mins to 1 hour at this spot as the area was huge and very very crowded. It was decent experience but nothing that would make you jump out of your seat. Kayaking/Wooden Boat ride: We had to choose one and we choose Boat ride, if you are not confident of Kayaking, better to skip and go for boat ride as the place is crowded and we saw the folks doing Kayak getting squeezed multiple times. But if you are confident and used to doing Kayak, it would be a great experience too (as we checked with other folks who did Kayak).The views are breathtaking and difficult to keep your hands off mobile to capture the raw beauty of the place. The place consumes you and was the highlight of the day. We were also told by our tour guide to give atleast 1$ per head as tip to the women who had taken us around.Optional Speed Boat: There was an option to do speed boat by paying 10$/head and it was around 10-15 mins ride which was breathtaking as well. Recommended to opt for this option if you are looking for some fun and picturesque around the multiple caves and mountains of Halong BayBeach and Climb to view point: We stayed around 30 mins here and there are stairs to go to the top for view point. This is crowded too and there is also a mid point where you can go to, if not to the top. Top has 360 degree view vs one sided view at the checkpoint. This was the last point and we boarded the cruise for juices and fruits (which was earlier planned at the top of the cruise, but due to high wind speeds) was conducted in closed space on the 1st floor.We reached the port post sunset and took some good photos during our cruise time and took similar 2-2:30hrs to reach back to Hotel in Hanoi. A tiring and worthy day. Recommend to take limousine transport for to and fro as you would spend ~5 hrs on road.The luxury cruise have fewer people on board and better interiors and facilities vs the general ones which looked very vanilla and had bunch of people. There would not be any difference in the points covered from either cruise.Enjoy and have a great Halong Day.

We were here ten years agoThe landscape its scenery essentially remains unchanged…….charming,mysterious,serene..But it is getting commercialized,the Sampans the junk with the large sails have all vanished,,Too many boats creating cutting waves which ruin the serenity of this charming placeBesides this…still worth visiting,,thumbs UP!,
We had a over night stay on a boat, the first day was misty and the second day I was wet. The scenery was great, it would have been fantastic on a sunny day
O cruzeiro foi muito giro, valeu o passeio, o primeiro restaurante que fomos, foi caríssimo, não valeu o que pagamos
We booked a trip to Ha long bay full day, everything was excellent, staff was friendly, english tour guide was knowledagable!

It is about a 2 to 2.5 hour bus drive to Halong Bay from Hanoi. It is possible to do Halong Bay in one day but it will be a long day. We decided to stay overnight. The Cabin, Toilet Facilities, Shower and even a spa Bath were great.  We did two activities off boat - Kayaking and a Cave Tour. The kayaking was great. We paddled in two man Kayaks (they were really stable) around a couple of small islands. The government has recently moved to restrict plastics to protect Halong Bay so as we paddled we picked up any litter we saw. If everyone like me picked up some litter the place would benefit. The boats could even make it a competition, There would have been 10 - 15 boats with people paddling in different areas so a lot of litter could be picked up. There was some litter but on a whole it was a nice paddle. With the Cave Trip we went by Tender to a loading area where local wooden boats were used (all piloted by locals) to take us through a large cave, It was a very nice tour. Halong Bay is worth seeing and any efforts to protect it are well worth it.

Ha Long Bay’s natural beauty is beyond words, and this trip offered breathtaking views of the islands. Cruising through the landscapes was an enchanting experience, and the photo opportunities were endless. The caves were fascinating to explore, and the kayaking, though brief, was enjoyable.

We did 2 days and regretted that we should have done for 3 days. We had only nearly 1 full day to enjoy the scenery and services on cruise. It's rush. We had to spend much time for transfers from/to Hanoi. So do 3 days if you can. It's worth visiting!

Awesome scenery and amazing exp kayaking into Ha Luong cape and seeing Surprise Cabe the day after .. huge and amazing .Cruise we went on ok , but ship a bit tired but still good food .The beach drop off on Titop Island was disappointing .. man made and full of folk .. not the deserted beach I was expecting .Great sunsets . The newer boats go to the small group of karsts next door  Lab Ha .. less tourist and much better I suspect

Located in Northern Vietnam, this stunning area is home to friendly locals, unique floating villages, delicious seafood, and crystal-clear waters. The locals here are incredibly friendly, and you can always find someone willing to help you out. Whether you need directions or advice on where to eat, they are always more than happy to assist. The floating villages are a unique sight to behold. You can take a boat tour to explore the villages and get an up-close and personal look at the local way of life. The seafood in Halong Bay is simply delicious. You can find everything from fresh seafood caught right in the bay to more traditional dishes like Pho. Finally, the crystal-clear waters of Halong Bay are a sight to behold. The stunning blue waters will take your breath away and make you want to stay forever. Halong Bay is a must-visit destination for any traveler. With friendly locals, unique floating villages, delicious seafood, and crystal-clear waters, you can't go wrong when visiting this incredible area.

นอกจากที่เรารู้จัก Halong Bay เพราะว่ามันเป็นที่ท่องเที่ยวที่น่าทึ่งมากในปัจจุบัน มันยังมีประวัติศาสตร์ที่น่าทึ่ง ชีวิตป่าที่หลากหลาย และธรรมชาติที่ยังไม่ถูกทำลาย ใน Halong Bay คุณจะพบวิวที่น่าประทับใจ และคุณจะพบกับแม่น้ำที่สวยงาม และยังมีแหล่งท่องเที่ยวที่น่าสนใจอื่น ๆ ที่คุณสามารถไปทัวร์ชมวิวและทำกิจกรรมที่น่าสนใจ ทั้งนี้ Halong Bay เป็นที่ท่องเที่ยวที่น่าสนใจที่สุด ที่จะทำให้คุณรู้สึกสนุกและมีความสุข!

La baie est connue pour ses paysages à couper le souffle, ses couchers de soleil spectaculaires et ses vues imprenables. Les eaux turquoise calmes et les îles karstiques qui s'y trouvent sont une véritable merveille à voir. Les habitants de la région sont également très accueillants et serviables. Vous pourrez donc passer un très bon moment à explorer la région et à interagir avec les locaux. Vous pourrez également profiter des activités de plein air, comme le kayak, la pêche et le snorkeling, et admirer la vue depuis les bateaux de croisière. Les couchers de soleil sont absolument spectaculaires et vous offriront une expérience inoubliable. La baie est une destination incontournable pour les amoureux de la nature et les voyageurs qui recherchent des paysages à couper le souffle et des couchers de soleil spectaculaires.

The serene beauty of the bay with its thousands of limestone islands and emerald waters will take your breath away. The area is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and offers many activities such as kayaking, sailing, and exploring the caves and grottos. There are plenty of opportunities to spot wildlife, from monkeys to seabirds to fish. Halong Bay is an unforgettable experiennce the beauty and culture of Vietnam.

Esta bahía es famosa por sus majestuosas formaciones de karst, aguas cristalinas y vistas impresionantes. La bahía está llena de islas y islotes cubiertos de vegetación y formaciones rocosas, que la hacen una experiencia única. Además, los visitantes pueden disfrutar de la deliciosa comida local, como el marisco fresco y las verduras frescas. Los turistas también pueden disfrutar de actividades como el kayak, el buceo y el crucero en barco para explorar la bahía. Las vistas desde el barco son increíbles, con montañas que se elevan por encima de la bahía.
Breathtaking scene at the location. It’s something that you never seen before. Beautiful natural beauty in a somewhat calm environment to relax.

C'est un endroit magnifique, class au patrimoine mondial de l'UNESCO, o vous pourrez admirer des paysages. couper le souffle, des grottes mystrieuses et des les verdoyantes. Et pour profiter pleinement de votre sjour, rien de tel qu'une croisire de luxe sur un bateau traditionnel en bois. Vous serez choy par un personnel attentionn, vous dgusterez une cuisine raffine et vous dormirez dans des cabines confortables et lgantes. Une croisire dans la baie d'Halong est une exprience inoubliable que vous ne regretterez pas !
Not as good as my visit 5 years ago. Halong bay now has turned to a mess with many bad service. Really tired of what happen

This beautiful bay is known for its unique floating villages, abundant activities, majestic karst formations, and rich cultural heritage. On a boat tour, you can visit the picturesque floating villages and explore the stunning limestone karst formations. There are plenty of activities to enjoy, such as kayaking, swimming, and rock-climbing. You can even visit nearby caves and explore the rich cultural heritage of the area.

Les spectaculaires couchers de soleil, les luxueuses croisières, le paysage époustouflant et l'histoire fascinante en font un lieu unique en son genre. Les eaux turquoise et les îles karstiques sont uniques et offrent une expérience inoubliable. Les visiteurs peuvent profiter des activités aquatiques, des excursions en bateau et de la beauté de la baie. La faune et la flore sont exceptionnelles et offrent une vue magnifique. Les visiteurs peuvent également découvrir l'histoire et la culture des populations locales. Halong Bay est un endroit magique qui mérite d'être visité.

We took the 2 days 1 night Alisa Premium Cruise to Halong Bay in April 2023.  It was an enjoyable trip with my family.A big thank you to Nelly Pham, Operation Manager.  Nelly has arranged everything for us  before our arrival, from transfer to the cruise and the itinerary during the cruise.  Nelly is very efficient and responded to my messages via WhatsApp very quickly. It was a most memorable trip for our first time in Hanoi.

Sus misteriosas cuevas, cruceros lujosos y fascinante historia te dejarán sin aliento. Las vistas impresionantes son una maravilla para la vista. Los cruceros ofrecen una experiencia única para explorar la bahía. Hay muchas actividades para disfrutar, desde kayak hasta excursiones en barco. La historia de la bahía es interesante y hay muchas leyendas y mitos que se pueden descubrir. Si buscas un destino único y emocionante, Halong Bay es el lugar perfecto.

Halong Bay is one of the most beautiful places on earth. Its unspoiled nature and picturesque seascapes make it a must-see destination. Luxurious cruises offer a unique way to explore the bay and experience its stunning beauty. The scenery is breathtaking and the activities are endless. Whether you're looking for a romantic getaway or an adventure-filled trip, Halong Bay is the perfect place to visit.

Best place to go if you love to watch sunrise and sunset. By taking the cruise you will get the experience to watch it closely. Just make sure you bring small amounts of cash as everywhere you go, they will asked you for a tips… 🤭🤭🤭…

Extraordinary place that will not leave you indifferent. Went on a cruise for 2 nights and the scenery was breathtaking at any given time. Kayaking, far from the big boats, and exploring by yourself is a unique experience.My only constructive critique is the amount of plastic, floating or not, that you will see. Unfortunately this will not leave you indifferent either. Local authorities should put attention to this problem that may be close to be out of control (if not yet).

Went on the standard 2 days 1 night cruise with Halong aquamarine. Was more than sufficient time to take in the views of this UNESCO world heritage site.The scenery was exquisite (although we were fortunate with good weather). It did feel overcrowded at times but this didn’t detract from the overall beauty of the bay. The pollution isn’t as bad as some have suggested.Our itinerary included kayaking around Luon cave, a view point at Tiptop island, and exploring Sung Sot cave. The activities were excellent and let you really appreciate Halong Bay from different perspectives.

Nous avons fait un tour en bateau pour regarder des îles et la mer. Après, nous avons visité des grottes. C'était magnifique ! Nous avons été surpris par la nature sauvage.Son, notre guide, est impressionnant. Nous aimons ses explications simples mais intéressantes.C'était super ! À faire absolument !
Beautiful place with scenic views. The beach is clean and picturesque... The boat rides which originate from near the port, to the islands (and caves) is a must...

This is definitely one of the most beautiful places I have ever been and a must do if you're in Vietnam because it genuinely is stunning. We did a 1 night cruise (forgive me, I can't remember the name of it) and the views were astonishing, and it was great going to a little island for a swim and some boat jumping. We also did kayaking and again, the best views I've ever experienced when kayaking. The only downside is, as some other reviewers have mentioned, the rubbish. I wouldn't go as far as to say it ruined the experience but there was a bit more pollution than I thought. I only really noticed it when kayaking though and it wasn't ALL OVER it was just in certain bits. Thing is, though, that's the reality of our world isn't it. Humans have managed to damage even the most beautiful places.

Ha Long bay is a bit like Krabi & Pha Nga bay but more beautiful with a much bigger archipelago. Perfect place for couples to go on a boat tour for a night or two.A shame though to see how much garbage there are in the sea nowadays.

Beautiful scenery from a cruise ship. The bay near Ha Long is busy and has rubbish in it so we didnt swim, but if you go out further near Cat Ba island and beyond then you can swim and the water is clean

We visited here on a 1 night Cruise, the bay is simply stunning! We kayaked around a part of it and enjoyed getting close to the impressive limestone. Where our boat operator arranged the kayaking next to a pearl farm however you could see tons of rubbish has been dumped at various points along the shores which was very disappointing to see. I would think that with all of the money that the different operators bring in revenue to the area that it could be spent on keeping the area clean and protected from rubbish dumping. Otherwise it was a lovely visit to the bay which was impressive even on a rainy day!

We joined 2 hour cruise during rainy day but it was awesomeThe walking to one of the cave was ok, many steps, good for exerciseHowever we wish halal foods are easier to find here in Halong Bay, Hanoi and Sapa
Đã mấy năm ko đi Hạ Lồng, lần này đi thấy khác quá, bến đón thì chuyển vào bến của Sun, tuy đông nhưng không lộn xộn. Cảnh thì vẫn đẹp nhưng bãi tắm thì quá là đông.

Giá đình tổ chức đi cuối tuần, kiếm mãi mới đc con tầu còn phòng family, còn toàn kín chỗ hết. Khách thì quá đông, toàn khách việt ồn ào và sô bồ. Cảnh thì rất đẹp, chỉ tiếc tầu quá đông nên nhiều khi cũng thấy phiền.

The halong bay basically comes along with a stay at small sized cruises which are luxurious and keeps you engage for whole 2-3 days of your stay.The bay is beautiful and silent to have a peaceful relaxing time. The water is clean and fun to do water activities.

ベトナム北部に行った際には2日を要して行くと良いと思います。一泊2日がおすすめです。日帰りではハノイ近隣ホテル滞在だと車に乗る時間の方が長いです。現地で予め登録してももちろん良いしそれでも行けますが、少し高い。滞在先のホテルやハノイ旧市街でも申し込めて、安価な事が多いです。ただし当たり外れあり、そこが醍醐味と感じられれば。様々なプランがあるので、色々悩んで思い出になる旅にして下さい。船内で一泊いい思い出になりますように。
The weather was beautiful and we had a picnic on the local beach. There are lots of water sports for children and adults and it is very safe.  The palm trees provide protection from the sun.

Une agréable croisière avec Vietnam escapade,réceptionniste francophone . Bateau confortable,restaurant excellent . Paysage calme et magnifique,mais il est préférable de choisir la sortie sur au moins deux jours.

Patrimonio de la Humanidad de la Unesco.Antes de la contrucción de una nueva carretera no era posible visitar Halong Bay en un día desde Hanoi, pero ahora sí. Se ha convertido en el tour de un día más solicitado en la capital.No sé si es por ese motivo, pero a finales de noviembre de 2019 la masificación era absoluta.Se toma un barco en grupo, con el almuezo incluido, y se van visitando los principales atractivos de la zona durante la jornada.El entorno natural es una maravilla, pero tiene todos los números para morir de éxito, por la saturación y por la contaminación.Quizá haciendo la visita en dos dias y una noche la percepción sea diferente.

The cruising and boat during through Halong Bay is a magical, scenic and picturesque experience. Photos honestly don't do this place justice! A sunset here was an experience I'll remember for a life time.The only negative is in some parts the water was populated and dirty was was a downer.... We need to preserve this place!!

Le covid 19 a frappé et malheureusement la croisière et la nuit dans la baie d' Halong furent annulées...Tous les navires sont confinés au port, plus de croisières, plus d'excursions...Notre guide nous disait en début de séjour, "qui n'a pas vu la baie d'Halong n'a pas été au Vietnam" et bien nous dirons que l'on a vu la baie, mais de très  loin...Bien sur notre note ne reflète surement pas la beauté que doit receler ce site, on n'a du se contenter d'en voir qu'un petit bout de très loin , depuis la cote, un jour sans soleil. Quand ça veut pas, ça veut pas, c'est bien connu!on reviendra et on la découvrira pour y mettre 5 étoiles!  Seul le très bon repas aux fruits de mer , crustacés et poissons nous a remis le moral !

Awesome views. Two day trip on Emeraude Classic Cruise Ship. A visit to the caves involved a fair amount of walking but well worth the effort. Relaxing cruise over two days. Cabin on ship a little cramped and lacking in basic comforts. Enjoyable though.
コロナウイルスの影響で中韓国人の入国制限があり凄い空いていました。日帰りでしたが、日帰りで充分かと思います。
武漢コロナウィルスの影響で、中国人はほとんどいなかったためか、日本人4人で貸切の船船内で食事しながらの奇岩クルーズ　鍾乳洞見学して港へ戻る3～4時間コースおおむね満足　お土産の押し売りに注意

We had a vermicelli with grilled pork and stir fried flat rice noodles dishes.Both dishes are good.The pork in vermicelli was grilled to perfection and the stir fried noodles was one of the best we had.Atmosphere is ok and we had to wait for our check.

L'un des plus beaux sites du monde, donc immanquable lors d'un séjour au Vietnam.Évidemment on ne sera jamais seul...Les locaux doivent faire attention à préserver ce site car on retrouve pas mal de déchets flottants, ce qui est dommage.A cette époque du coronavirus, peut être un peu moins de monde.En tout cas nous n'avons pas vu le soleil comme attendu...en février.

Ne vale la pena sicuramente, due ore abbondanti di pullman da Hanoi, fatele con il VIP Bus, costa una sciocchezza e arrivi in un luogo veramente bello.Si può discutere a proposito dell‘acqua non proprio limpida... siamo gente di mondo suvvia... resta indiscutibile la bellezza del luogo.Io consiglio il barcone, comodo e pulito, da la possibilità di fare molte foto in tranquillità.

ハノイ3回目にして初ハロン湾です。思った以上に観光地観光地していて驚きました。季節的には冬なので船の上は寒く曇っていました。次々と現れる奇岩にテンション上がりましたが結構すぐにその景色に慣れてしまったかな。途中小舟に乗り換えて鍾乳洞へ上陸します。日本の秋芳洞などに比べて随分大規模で明るい洞内は見応えありました。階段やら登り降りが大変だとガイドさんから聞かされていましたが79歳の母も全く問題なく普通に歩ける行程でした。ちなみにハノイから行く世界遺産なら私はチャンアンの方が断然好きでした。
It was cold but still beautiful. Next time i would go in a warmer season, because a lot of the activities are wateractivities like kayak and swimming.

My wife and I took a 3 day/ 2 night trip in Ha Long bay mid January 2020.  I spent a lot of time reading reviews before I booked this and I wanted to provide some thoughts now that we completed the tour.General:  The cruise boats are parked in a waiting area and you are take a tender for about 10 minutes to reach it.  It's extremely organized and obvious that they do this many times per month.  Once on the boat, you go to another area that's about 2 hours away.  All boats park there and you take another tender to do the daily activities.  I envisioned our cruiser boat weaving in and out of the islands but it only does that for the couple of hours to get to the second parking lot of other boats.1 night vs 2 nights:  Our boat had several people who only did one night.  If you are short on time, then this is a limited option in my opinion because they offer just short trips like kayaking and visiting a cave in Ha Long Bay.  I honestly found the kayaking to be of poor quality because you do it where the boat park and it's fairly polluted with bottles and trash floating in the bay.  Since we did the 2 night stay, we left Ha Long Bay and took a tender to Cat Bay island where we biked, visited a small village, tasted rice wine, and kayaked in very clear water with unpopulated beaches we could explore.  It was excellent and I felt bad for the 1 nighters who missed this adventure.Bus vs Private Transportation From and To Hanoi:I specifically looked for cruises that included transportation in the booking fee but quickly regretted doing so the day of the cruise.  We were supposed to be picked up at 9:10-9:20 but were called and told we needed to be ready by 8:10.  I was in the shower at 7:45 so we had to scramble to pack and check out but we made it.  We were in the bus at 8:10 only to loop through Hanoi for 2 hours getting other guests ( there were only 15 people in total).  Our final pickup at 10:00AM was right next to our hotel so needless to say, this started out bad.  I'd recommend spending the money for a private ride. Overall Impression of Boat, Staff and Food:We stayed on the Pandora Cruise ship.  The room was comfortable, and the people were extremely nice.  The food was abundant and well prepared.  They had karaoke at night which was a lot of fun and some of the staff were really good singers. We got a room with a balcony but honestly didn't use it. Anyway, hope this helps if you are considering a trip...

Tomamos el crucero ROYAL PALACE. hermoso viaje. te reciben tocando unos tambores y tirandote petalos de rosa por las escaleras. Comida super abundante. hago hincapie en esto porque previo a mi viaje vi que habia gente que se quejaba de la comida en sus cruceros. las mesas eran compartidas, y facil trajeron 7 platos con comidas diferentes para que nos sirvieramos. llegó un ponto que ya nadie comía y seguian trayendo cosas. las excursiones fueron adecuadas. El Tai chi 6 am fue una experiencia nueva.
we did a overnite boat cruise,  nice spot, relaxing with interesting formations, caves and floating villages

We did an overnight cruise from Hanoi to Halong Bay, and i'm so glad we did!The ship we sailed on was lovely along with the beautiful views and great food!  We also had the opportunity to go on a bamboo boat trip through caves which was an experience!

The bay was beautiful. I had an incredible time on the boat tour I took despite the day being a bit overcast. Nice spots to hike and kayak. I was a little bummed at how overdeveloped Ha Long City was vs. what I was expecting from personal recos from people who have visited 5-10+ years ago. It’s all in what you’re looking for though.
私が行った時は曇りでした。晴れてたら良かったーと言っていたらガイドさんが曇りがちょうどいいと言っていました。雨は霧が出るし、晴れたら暑いとのこと。

Baia di una bellezza incredibile. La natura ha creato qualcosa di meraviglioso. Vale la pena fermarsi per la notte in una crociera per gustare l'ambiente suggestivo. Purtroppo la presenza di numerose imbarcazioni rovina un po', così come la presenza di spazzatura in acqua. L'uomo sta davvero rovinando tutto.

However you spell it, Ha Long Bay is one of the main reasons to travel to Vietnam!  The limestone karsts number in the thousands and are picturesque. It was peaceful and very beautiful being out on the water.  The only downfall was that the tour group I was on only had one scheduled overnight on a junk boat out on the bay.  The area warrants additional time, however prioritize your time as how you see fit.
Tolle Organisation, sympatischer Guide, wunderschöne, bizarre Landschaft, geschmackvolle Kabine, gute Verpflegung!

Obviously one of the most beautiful places in the world. Although it is stunning it is also very polluted and plenty of dead fish everywhere. We stayed on a private is all and which was kept clean but ever morning/afternoon they would have to clean the beach again due to all the rubbish.

We enjoyed our 2 day / 1 night junk cruise around Halong bay. The islands are spectacular and watching them pass whilst enjoying drinks and good food on the junk was stunning. Our visit to impressive cave systems was also very good although,sadly,due to bad weather kayaking and swimming were out. On the down side the number of people trying to enjoy the "serenity" of Halong Bay makes it very busy, in particular on the islands for viewpoint climbs etc. Associated litter and fuel residue from boats is also evident. Unfortunately extensive construction work at the mainland port suggests that these problems are going too be magnified in the near future, so get there soon !!
Halong Bay is a beautiful  destination..spent 3 days..one too many as it's not a very big place..not too much to do aside from the bay itself

最近になり、ハロン湾への使い捨てプラスチックの持ち込みが禁止されるようになりました。クルーズ船に乗船する船着き場へ入るためにチケットを見せるゲートがありますが、そちらでカバンチェックがあり、監視員から見えるところに持っていたペットボトル飲料やビニール袋を没収されました。船内、洞窟のある島などでビール、コーラなど缶入り飲料の販売はありましたが、水に関しては水筒？に詰められた水（安全な水かはわかりません・・）が常温で売られているのを見かけただけです。ウォーターサーバーなどもありません。砂糖入り飲料が飲めない小さいお子様連れの方、マイボトルでのドリンク持参をお勧めします。

ハロンワンクルーズ(3時間程度のコンパクト版)に参加しました。日本からの8人でのツアーで一艘貸切の贅沢な船旅です。見事な景色のハロン湾を回遊し、途中鍾乳洞に見学後、船に戻り海鮮料理を頂きました。周囲で漁をしている小舟がシャコなどの魚介類を運んでくれて、購入し(ex.巨大シャコ1尾で5＄)船で調理してもらうことも出来ます。船に乗っている現地の方がお土産を執拗に売ってくるのが、少し鬱陶しかったですね。こちらは逃げ場がないものですから。
Rispecchia realmente il concetto di “Ottava meraviglia del mondo”.Luogo ameno, incantevole e più o meno incontaminato.Peccato per la presenza di tante, troppe barche (navi) da crociera.
Nous avons pris le bateau pour passer une nuit dans cette baie. Malheureusement, il faisait brumeux et pluivieux en décembre. Mais cela reste un des beaux endroits à voir au Vietnam.

There are many notable international locations that look exactly like the pictures. There are even places in this world, like the power of Iceland’s Gullfoss Falls, or the smell of Japan’s cherry blossoms, that simply cannot be captured with even the best photograph.Sadly, there are many points of interest that simply don’t measure up to the photos, shot with special lenses, edited in Photoshop, and disseminated for the masses to use as screensavers. Halong Bay is one of those.There is nothing wrong with visiting Vietnam’s best-known natural attraction, and I’m glad we did it. Sailing amongst the endless rocky protrusions on smooth waters, spending the night on our boat with 5-star meals, it’s truly a once-in-a-lifetime experience. But those glorious pictures of this majestic bay turn out to be a real-life hazy, foggy maze of colorless rock, dirty-colored water, and little greenery.Don’t worry, it’s worth it. But don’t expect the famous pictures. With the grand finale being the bay in which King Kong was filmed, and a fabulous cave tour, it’s a fun excursion.
We we went for a one day trip to Halong Bay which was a very good experience. We went by boat where we had lunch while sailing around. We considered sleeping there, but one day was fine by us.

Ha Long is a beautiful natural wonder. The rock formations are stunning and the scenery mesmerizing. The downside is that it’s overcrowded by boats and people. Hundreds of boats go in out all day long. We took a tour of some caves and it was a single file all the way. Thankfully the caves were large and that made it less claustrophobic. We took an overnight cruise boat. Where we anchored in the evening there must have been another 100 boats around us.The day we went the weather was a bit overcast. Even then it looked beautiful. It’s a gorgeous place to see, but just be aware you won’t have your own piece of paradise here.
One of the highlights in our trip is overnight cruise in Halong Bay. There is some rubbish here and there, but still worth a visit

Used local tour operator to visit Halong Bay. Was approached at the Cruise terminal and agreed to go unescorted to see the sights. The minibus took about 1/2 hour to arrive at the harbour where the junks were situated. Had to wait another 20 mins for another minibus with tourists to arrive. We were escoted to our junk after the tickets were purchased, complete tour cost including transport was about $35. Did not have a guide on the trip, however it was not really required. The only thing to be aware of is the entrance to the cave. To access the cave you have to reasonably fit as there are a lot of steep steps to negotiate (100 +).  Remember to take note of the number of your junk as they sail round to the exit of the cave which is a fair journey and the at the berth there could be about 15 similar boats. Enjoyed the special scenery and the occasion,
沒有去過下龍灣，就不能說去過河內了，不論你覺得它美不美，也是要去一趟。下龍灣的節目，船夫載你水上遊，蠻有趣的，船夫很賣力說幫我們拍照，導遊建議每人給小費20000船夫。另外看乳石真的很震撼，有的石在電筒照射下，看到閃閃發亮的。

si, due giorni da sogno in questa baia spettacolare in mezzo ad isolotti incontaminati, una sola notte con un tramont ed un alba indimenticabile, peccato solo per le numerose barche che girano nel golfo, sono moltissime ma l'esperienza vale la pensa di fare una visita
A pretty place but is a bit gloomy in December. Also low season for squid fishing. But still is worth to go onto sung sot cave and tipov island.
Very tranquil until the touts come, but highly recommend that you take this tour once in your lifetime ;) Nice to stay on the cruise boat , it is clean and a good concept for tourists.

Went on a one day tour (forgotten which) at Halong Bay. The guide introduced us to 3 activities, exploring the largest cave, short Kayaking trip and trekking to the peak of another small island.The views were stunning and worth the trip, the only thing that is worth complaining about would be the number of tourists. We can’t stop to take a few more photos without them staring impatiently at us :(Do note that another boat option is available as the kayak is 2 seater and prepare a change of clothes. There is a time limit of 30mins for exploring if you choose the Kayaking experience.

I saw all the pictures I knew I want to visit  Halong Bay, which is a UNESCO world heritage site, The bay has emerald green water and just under 2000 limestone islands dotting the landscape. The only way to experience the bay is by boat. Here is where things change. The islands are beautiful, but all the tours are very controlled. They are making Halong Bay into a amusement park...with a lot more hotels on they way. There are 2000 cruise ships..... all going to the same place which seems like a parking lot in the bay.  I hope at some point they create different tours.....like one just to see more of the islands

We caught a tour bus that catered for 34 people at 8.45am for Halong Bay. It took approximately 2 and a half hours to reach our destination. There was a toilet stop about 10mins before boarding the boat at an oyster shucking farm. We then proceeded to the embarking terminal. We sailed accros the bay and were served local delicious dishes. Our tour guide was comedic and did his best to make us all feel very comfortable. There were 3 activities we partook in. The trek up and down Surprise Cave; kayaking and swimming on 3 different limestone islands. Beer, soft drinks and snacks are offered on the tour also. Be warned that it is a government regulation that you do not litter or take open plastic drinks or snacks onto the islands. Cost of the trip by bus plus tour is about  $AUD70.00 person. There are options for smaller tour groups but at a slightly higher price.
美しく穏やかな海と多くの島との組み合わせがきれいです。シーフードも豊富で美味しい。まだまだ開発が進んでおり、今の美しい景色の維持と今後の便利さの進化を期待したいです。
zurecht Weltkulturerbe, man sollte nur eine ganz begrenzte Anzahlung von Schiffen täglich in die Inselgruppe fahren lassen !!!

言わずと知れた世界遺産ハロン湾。ハノイからの１日ツアーに参加してここに行きました。天気も晴れていて海も穏やかだったのでのんびりとクルーズを楽しむ事ができました。ただツアーはぼっていました。ボートに乗る前はペットボトルの水は持ち込み禁止なのでバスにおいていくようにとか（ボートの中で高い飲み物を売りつけるため）．．私はこっそりもっていきましたが..あとでツアーコンダクターをしていてしょっちゅうここに来ている友人に聞くと”水の持ち込み禁止なんてない”とのことでした。

Only in Ha Long Bay for two days after 3 nights in Hanoi. A local friend arranged the tickets for a 6 hour cruise at 250,000 dong each (about 10 US $) which included two 1 hour stops one at Titop island and the other at the Sung Sot cave which had several individual caverns. Personally 6 hours was enough as it would have got a bit repetitive and we preferred to make the 5 Star Wyndham hotel our base which was just a 15 minute limo ride from the docks. But our friend did say they the overnight trip was relaxing and romantic though not a lot to do (other than it being romantic!)

The bay itself was awesome, especially the cave! We also visited Ti Top and did the kayak thing, which frankly wasn’t impressive. Note that there are many boats to choose from, but not all credible. The difference I think is the included lunch. We’ve been warned on buffet not being fresh. Our seafood lunch was excellent. The negative on our  one day 4 hours tour was the long travel time by bus. It took 3 n half hours to 4 hours one way. I think most tours take the longer route because they stop at a rest area which is a large souvenir area. On the way back they should have taken the faster route because we got back to the hotel at about 9:30 pm, left Hanoi Old Quarters at 8:30 am.

Abbiamo acquistato in una agenzia il viaggio alla baia di Ha long pagandolo circa 35 euro, e comprendeva il trasporto in autobus dal proprio hotel fino alla baia, la navigazione su un battello per circa 6 ore, il pranzo ed il rientro in autobus all'hotel.La baia di Ha long e' molto spettacolare con queste piccole isole ricoperte dalla vegetazione. Se siete ad Hanoi dovete  assolutamente andarci. E' patrimonio dell'Unesco dal 1994.

The Halong Bay cruise over a weekend let’s you experience the serene bay with the emerald rocks on them ! Vegetarian food is a challenge but the cruise team did their best to make us some veg food. While Exploring such places food is never a priority..
We went with tour group on 1/2 day. The morning cruise was peaceful.  Not too many people.  Lunch on board was good.  Halong Bay is truly awesome.  Would love to come again for the 2 nights cruise.

We enjoyed the Golden Cruise tour of Halong Bay (See my review) and this is such a beautiful and peaceful area. It seems crowded with tourist cruises but we had an overnight stay which is the best to do. Waking up to silence and serene views of this area is well worth it.
We are family ~GoodI will forever remember ^All staff kind of them^Restaurant clean and delicious 😋 설레이는 가족의 휴가이다기대에 실망하지 않았다 맛있는 음식과물위에서 1박을 영원히 기억할것이다하롱베이는 너무 멋진곳이다강력추천한다
Though there are lots of tourist boats & visitors, one can find peace & beauty in this bay, bewitched among 2000 limestone islands at this UNESCO world heritage site!

Not as stunning as I’d imagined. Too many boats. Too many tourists. Too much rubbish in the water. Too close to the city!  When moored up at night you could constantly see the city lights. The boats hardly make any impact into this UNESCO area. Very soon area will be overrun with tourists once the resort in Halong has been completedOMG

Una vez te ves allí empiezas a respirar el ambiente mágico del lugar,aunque con muchos barcos alrededor,nunca te sientes agobiado por la multitud,paseo en kayak entretenido y después el barco en sí,con servicio calidad-precio bastante justito,solo buscaban suplementos extra. Pero nada que el entorno no pudiese suavizar!!!!
下龙湾是越南北部湾的海湾，山海秀丽，为旅游胜地，这里山奇水秀，风景如画，是典型的喀斯特地形地区，1994年，联合国教科文组织将下龙湾作为自然遗产列入《世界遗产名录》。下龙湾是越南北方广宁省的一个海湾，风光秀丽迷人，闻名遐迩，2011年11月12日“世界新七大自然奇观”公布，下龙湾榜上有名。

Beautiful views of Halong Bay our tour guide Peter was very helpful regarding history of Halong Bay. The only disappointment was when we visited a swimming beach it was overcrowded with tourists such a shame for a beautiful place. Overall enjoyed our overnight cruise. Food on board was lovely
only one thing I do not like is noisy when visiting cave. So many Chinese there. So many cruise around.
日本には、ないような景色で、とても楽しかったです。前回の旅行が雨でハロン湾に来られなかったのでリベンジという方もいらっしゃいました。

The scenery is stunning and the overnight cruise was a good way to enjoy it all. We hiked up top-top, visited the pearl farm, toured the cave and loved the cruising. I was on Paradise Cruise and found the ship itself to be very good, the food was good, the service was impersonal and lacking. But the real star was the scenery.

Let's start with the bay is beautiful. I have mixed feelings about recommending it. The natural setting is spoiled by the construction and the garbage. It is very to focus on the beauty when you see the garbage and specifically the water bottles floating around the bay. The fishermen get paid to collect garbage but obviously not enough. We saw this all over the coast of Vietnam. The beaches were clean in the morning but early afternoon the garbage appeared.We arranged a tour of the bay and most of the boats offered similar packages with TipTop mountain (very crowded but a nice view), the caves, a visit to the pearl factory (overpriced) and a lunch. The boat was very comfortable since we were only about 16. The tour picked us at the Pier (we came on a cruise ship) and took us to the island where all the tour boats depart. There was so wait time but it was all in all pretty efficient. When done we took a tour of Halong bay on land. Nothing to see except ugly buildings and an amusement park that I hope has seen better days. I would not recommend staying in Halong Bay overnight.The construction in Halong bay is surreal: a lot of empty buildings waiting for the big boom of some sort.  I would say just go before it is too late and it is totally taken over by mass tourism. If you have been to Phuket and wonder how it compares: I would say that Phuket was not as dramatic in terms of landscape but it was more beautiful.  Enjoy while it is enjoyable!

The boat trip was great lasted 4 hours with lunch. Food was OK but not great . However the superb scenery made up for all that. could not believe just how many boats were touring the Bay there must have been hundreds.It was a very hot and humid day thank god the boat had good air-conditioning.A must do trip with great views.
One of the most beautiful place in Vietnam, to bad that the water is very dirty and smells very bad.

HaLong Bay is one of the most beautiful places in Asia. as one of the world heritage site, HaLong bay truly one of many destination that every travelers must come and see.From my own personal experience it is better that you stay in Hanoi then buy a tour like 3D 2N with 4 star Ship on Halong Bay. IT IS SO MUCH BETTER.If you stay and book your Hotel in Halong Bay, one the price they will charge you as a customer when buying food is outrageous almost 2-3 times the price they set on the menu.They dont speak any english at all, unless you eat on a very specific restaurant. There is a lot like A LOT of mainland china staying on guest house and hotel in Halong Bay that will ruin your experience. The food is also not that great, Hanoi food is far greater then Halong Bay. Except on the ship.You will be abundant of food on the ship you stay and its taste better then the food on land especially the seafood yum. Some of the ship also have Vegan meal if you requested before hand.Overall it was a very enjoyable experience.. But waste a lot of money staying on land while you can save abundant amount when staying and sleeping on ship. Cheers!

We enjoyed Halong Bay very much - we were in a small group so our boat ride was quite enjoyable and well operated, we had a chance to canoe in the Bay which was great and we also visited an impressive cave. The overall site is stunning and a must do when in Vietnam.
從河內出發，坐著3小時的巴士抵達下龍灣，接著再搭著遊艇出海，在遊艇上用餐及帶著啤酒在甲板上欣賞壯闊的風景，接著走訪鐘乳石洞感受別有洞天的奇異景色，這趟是很值得的旅行。

Went on a day tour to Halong Bay. It took 3 hours by a bus to get to the ferry terminal from the core of Hanoi. The water there wasn't so crystal clear but the view was great. We got on a kayak and that help amazing! The cave is so mystical!! It's worth it to go to Halong Bay to see the cave. It was a full day tour with lunch and I had such a great time!

I really liked the visit in Halong Bay. Beautiful view at the top of the Island. We also went to a glourious old cave the next morning.I was there in the winter, making it a little cold, about 8 degress Celsius, but that isn't really the locations fault. I would recommend to go there some other time during the year though.The reason why i gave this place a four out of a five is because it's really crowded. I understand why, but still not as enjoyable with that many tourists and boats.

Dubbed a UNESCO World Heritage Site, Ha Long Bay is a popular travel destination about 170 kms east of Hanoi. It provides a picturesque view as hundreds of isles sit atop lime green waters. While much of the water is not swimmable, there are many ports throughout the area that offer everything from party cruises to multiday excursion cruises. In addition, many of the islands are open for exploration. I personally did the day cruise which consisted of visiting a few of the islands as well as lunch. There was also an oppurtunity to snorkel in a certain area of the Bay but not many people took it.
絶壁が海に落ち込む島々が点在、とても美しい。日本にはない風景で、遊覧船を楽しめる。鍾乳洞洞窟は、日本にもあるが、ガイドさんが動物やモノになぞらえて教えてくれるのが、「なるほど」と思える。ハノイから遠いので、高速道路利用のバスがよい。

We traveled to Halong bay in the month of Feb. The closest airport is Hanoi. The most affordable way to reach Halong bay is by bus. Once you get down at the Noi Bai International airport walk to the bus stop and wait for bus 109 to take you to My Dinh Bus Stand, fare 8K VND. This is the only direct bus to My Dinh bus stand from the airport. At My Dinh there are plenty of options public and private buses that'll take you to Bai Chay bus stand in Halong bay. The journey is about 3-3.5 hrs, fare 0.1M VND. The day trip of Halong bay was arranged for us by the backpackers, right opposite Sun World Amusement Park. The day trip costs 1M VND and is a must do. It included the cruise, lunch and snacks on the cruise. It takes you around the UNESCO World Heritage site i.e the islands in Halong Bay and stops at a few of them for you to explore it. The return back to Noi Bai airport was arranged by the backpackers through a private van that charged us 0.15M VND but took the faster route, 2 hrs to Hanoi.

A landscape like from the avatar movie. The views are spectacular hence tourist flock to this place in thousands. Two day, one over night stay felt about the right amount of time to spend here. You can enjoy swimming in a secluded bay - if that’s even possible to find such a thing, exploring caves, kayaking around the floating oyster farms. The water pollution and overcrowding are going to destroy this place, so hopefully the local government will regulate the number of tourists to preserve this beautiful place.
제네시스 투어로 갔다왔는데하루 정도 갔다오기 좋은것 같아요바위와 케이브 절경도 너무 예쁘고하노이 시내 투어 중 하루 잘 갔다 온것 같아요

Recommend to Visit to Ha Long Bay First and then plan for Sapa.In our case we planed for Sapa first and then Ha Long Bay But in comparison to Sapa the Ha Long Bay experience just very good but not awesome.I would say go for 2 days 1 night trip plan only. Otherwise we might get feel bored. Of course it’s dependence on the individual.If you are looking for vegetarian food, while cruise booking itself you can mention the same and they will serve while on board. But don’t expect too much variety.Hanoi to Ha Long Bay is 3 hrs Journey.You can explore for double deckers Cable cars 🚠 ride and water world
Excellent boat and food beautiful scenery boat staff very helpful and tour guide excellent.transport from Hanoi to halong bay very good

La baia di Halong va vista. Sperando nel tempo migliore, come è capitato a noi, si può navigare tra innumerevoli isolotti carsici, con la tipica forma a "gobba di drago" che credo dia anche il nome alla Baia. Il paesaggio è meraviglioso anche al tramonto oltre che naturalmente con la luce del sole e magari qualche bianca nuvoletta all'orizzonte. E' possibile effettuare crociere di 2gg o più. A mio avviso 2gg sono sufficienti, capiterà anche di fare brevi escursioni in kayak (attenzione l'acqua è inquinatissima!). Non ho avuto tempo di soggiornare sulla terra ferma, ma va bene così. Un posto solamente secondo me compete con questo: gli arcipelaghi di isolotti nei pressi di El Nido a Palawan, Filippine, una specie di Halong Bay con un mare da favola (mi scuso con il Vietnam, ma le Filippine sono top!)

It is world-renowned for a reason. The best bits were when cruising the bay by boat and when there was dead silence with no one else around; I had not been expecting that and it was incredible to enjoy the scenery in that setting. When visiting any of the sites though (the caves, the islands etc.) do expect it to be incredibly busy. They should try and reduce the numbers, but I am still very glad to have seen it.
在河內參加一日遊，到下龍灣搭船出海，欣賞自然造就的景色，還有機會在海灣裡自己划小船，雖然比搭竹筏累，但覺得對景色感受也因此不同。

ベトナムツアーの一環として訪問。世界遺産として有名だったので楽しみだったのですが期待に違わぬ素晴らしい光景を楽しめました。クルーズにはランチも含まれていたのですがベトナムはエアコンが嫌いなのか船は付いておらず、海の上だから爽やかな風が吹いてくるという訳でもないので、非常に暑いツアーになりました。途中、鍾乳洞にも入ったのですがここも涼しくなく暑さで死にそうでした。でもこの風景は一度は見る価値があるものだと思います。

Erg mooi, dit moet je gezien hebben als je in het noorden van Vietnam bent. Er zijn talloze (meerdaagse) opties om boottochtjes te maken, vanuit Halong Bay City, maar ook als dagtripje vanuit Hanoi. Kies wat het beste bij je past, maar sla het vooral niet over.

ハイフォンのホテルでクルーズを頼んで参加しました。マイクロバスで拾ってもらい、別の一組の家族と一緒でした。途中、真珠の養殖場にある販売店に休憩兼ねて寄ります。かのミキモトさんに技術を学んだとかなんとか。ミキモトさんの写真が飾ってありました。どこまで良品なのかは不明ですが。ハロン湾まで一時間、船に乗って観光、ランチ、ミニボートに乗り換え洞窟をくぐると、大きな岩にポッコリ空いた半径50メートルほどの空間にでました。感動です。船頭のお姉さんが写真を撮ってくれます。またクルーズ船に乗り直し、今度は歩いて洞窟に移動。ここでは日本人もたくさんいました。ここも感動的な空間。ものすごい大きな洞窟です。不思議な感じ。でもここはものすごい湿度です。必ず飲み物持参で。5時間ほどの工程でした。楽しかったです。
Halong bay is gorgeous and a must visit but what a pity: there were plastics & rubbish in the water, too crowded tourists & many boat. Need improve to protect amazing bay!

We did an overnight stay visit. Apricot tours, budget operation, well organised, friendly and helpful staff. Daniel great. Halong Bay itself is beautiful. It cannot cope with tourist volume unfortunately. Apricot organised a fun kayaking paddle into a sheltered lagoon... complete with acrobatic monkeys. Worth doing but don't be dissapointed by the 100's of boats and 1000's of tourists.

Alors oui l'endroit est magique, tellement connu qu'on ne peut pas aller au Vietnam sans voire cette baie d'halong. Les croisières sont très sympas et on revient avec de beaux souvenirs en tête.Le seul hic est l'odeur de kérosène omniprésente, faut dire qu'il y'a tellement de bateau dans cette baie que le moindre air pur se fait rare. De plus les déchets plastiques et autres sont également omniprésent dans l'eau, cela gâche un peu cette magie.

It's one of those 'must do' places for most tourists in Vietnam and for good reason: the scenery is special and stunning. BUT (and yes its a big but), be prepared for filthy water full of plastic flotsam. We thought we might escape it by going further out on a 2-night cruise but sadly not. As for swimming...well most people (90%) on the cruise chose not to, despite the heat. So, be prepared. Oh and did I mention the seabirds? Well, there are none. Not a single one in 3 days. In an area like that with plenty of baitfish, that is unnatural and rather spooky.So a very mixed experience.
The scenery and views are great. However, the tour we took felt very touristy. If I were to do it again I would take a day trip or with a better boat

Going to keep this review just about the place. See my review on Vietnam open tours for an insight into cruises 🙄 Ha Long bay should be and could be the perfect place. The scenery is spectacular. However at any given time you can count something about 50 other cruise boats. There is plastic and other rubbish everywhere and I seen tour guides just casually throw rubbish in the water. I think the boats also throw food waste over Board to attract squid for an “on board activity”, squid fishing. All the tours seem to go to this one beach I think it’s call Ti top and it’s the dirtiest beach I’ve every been on. I certainly wouldn’t getting in the water here. It had a diesel slick on top 🤢. You’ll certain see if that typhoid jab did the trick!! Don’t be fooled by the “pearl museum” it’s basically an ice cream shop. Do a massive amount of research on the cruise company you book with. Get them to send you an itinerary and ensure you don’t get shoved into a day boat if your considering a three day cruise.
Tour companies need to take responsibility to clean up the rubbish seen floating in the water while we were kayaking. Not great for the environment or tourism. Other than that, an excellent trip.
ハノイから行きは1時間45分、帰りは2時間15分でした。奇岩は最初なかなかの絶景でしたが、写真撮影が一段落すると、冷房の効いた部屋でお酒を飲みながらリラックスして観賞しました。珍しいと思った風景も、時間が経てば見飽きた感じはしました。一度見られれば良いかなと思います。
Boat trip was OK , where was not so good , we had a good views , but too much boats and so on , a lot of asian tourists , noisy and not well educated , these small island must be protected

Halong Bay Information:We took a short, 2 day, 1 night cruise through the bay. Unfortunately, when we came in July, it was incredibly overcast and foggy/cloudy during the first day. Yes, there is trash in the water, but it's not as much as people are pointing out here. This is not an excuse to not be good stewards of the earth and to clean up and be conscious of the nature reserve, especially as a UNESCO site, but it definitely wasn't as bad as I thought it was going to be.The cruise that we went on also didn't have that many boats around us - we only had a handful of other cruise ships that we could see, so it didn't feel like we were being overcrowded or too touristy in the area that we went to, which was nice.The second day, early in the morning when it became more clear, the bay was much more stunning and spectacular than the first. Definitely, a clear day makes the bay an incredible site to see, and definitely a must-do if you're able to get there.Swimming in the water was nice - the water is incredibly warm, which I would think poses a problem to the wildlife there, but it wasn't as gross as other reviewers make it out to be. It isn't as clear as a tropical island, but what do you expect? The size of the bay is bigger than Singapore, and with so much tourism + industrial boats coming through, it's not going to be a pristine lake. Kayaking was nice - you don't need too long, maybe a couple of hours, to fully grasp the feeling of the place. Unless there are specific islands to see or places to go, seeing 2 or 3 of the islands felt like enough.Cruise Tour Information:We booked a cruise tour with Halong Bay Cruise Hunters, who seemed to arrange the tour through VietnamOpenTour. We were picked up from our hotel in Hanoi for our (2 people) 2 day, 1 night cruise.It took about 2 hours to get from Hanoi to the harbor, where we transferred our stuff to a smaller boat. The smaller boat took us to our ship, the Sapphire Cruise. It was a 3-star cruise, with a clean room with working AC and a generously sized bathroom. We also received a small balcony to look out, which was nice.We arrived on the boat around 1 PM, where we were served lunch. We went out kayaking for an hour and swimming in the bay for an hour, both of which were nice. Kayaking for an hour seemed to be OK - once you've seen one or two of the islands, that's really enough, unless you're specifically looking for something else. Swimming for an hour was also about enough - we were able to jump off the boat a few times, get some great pictures, and enjoy the water, which was quite warmer than the California ocean waters I'm used to.The dinner was fantastic, and we were able to go squid fishing (which was just fishing with a bamboo rod and a bare lure) off the side. Pro Tip: turn off the lights to have a better chance of catching squid.The next morning, we got an early start (activity start at 7:30 AM) where we sat in a boat while some locals rowed the boat through a cave and around a small cove. We returned back to the main cruise ship for some brunch, and then headed back to the harbor.The cruise was quick, but felt like just enough time. I think that if we had done one more day, we would've experienced more of the same.

一度は来て見たかったハロン湾です。湾内には３０００もの奇岩や島々が点在しています。私達はツアーでハノイから約２００㎞４時間掛けてきました。湾内には大小さまざまな船がありましたが、私達が湾内をクルーズする船は貸切です。船に乗るや否や景色を楽しむ間もなくランチタイムです。エビの酒蒸しから始まる海鮮料理で持てなしてくれました。食事が終わると船内でベトナム土産品の販売です。高価な珊瑚や真珠のアクセサリーから始まりバラまき用の刺繍用品、はては子供の玩具までです。値引き交渉も楽しいのですが、ハロン湾の景観を楽しむ時間がありませんでした。次回来る機会がある時は、船内で一泊優雅に時の移ろいを感じてみたいです。

Visited Halong Bay, a beautiful seascape of thousands of limestone mountains, on a 2 days / 1 night cruise in July 2019. Visited the Sung Sot Cave & Titop Island for swimming along its sandy beach as well as climbed atop the mountain on 400 Plus steps to enjoy the birds eye view of scenic beauty of the surroundings. Kayaking/boating was another interesting activity with visit to Oyster Farm. The Sunset views & the tranquility overall, was mesmerizing. Approx cost of 2Days / 1 night Cruise from Hanoi is 75 USD for 2 Star package, 85 USD for 3 Star package, 100 USD for 4 Star package & 120 USD for 5 Star package. Pl choose the cruise carefully after evaluating options as most claims in the brochure ( pics & amenities & activities etc on itinerary ) are misleading.

Beautiful bay and islands, somehow reminds me Thai, but so different.Lots of travel agencies or your hotel will have numerous tours for you to choose from.Nature of Halong bay is magnificent and green. You can stay there (hotels in Marina or on the cruise ship) and chill for a few days or take a day tour (as we did).We had a cloudy day and were happy about it, because June is very hot in Hanoi.Took a 6 hours tour including boat transportation between the islands/activities. Three activities: exploring caves on foot, small bay by kayak or boat (your choice), Tip Top island with the small and crowded beach or climb up the hill.Totally worth it. One day was enough, I believe. I wish we could start a tour earlier not to rush on the sights.

Halong bay was beautiful & a must do when travelling in Hanoi. There are lots of islands, islets with amazing shapes. The best way is book a package tour with cruise. But rubbish in the bay was a big problem, need to improve!

Visita imperdibile se si fa un tour del Vietnam. Per ammirala in tutta la su bellezza è obbligatorio partecipare ad una minicrociera di uno o due giorni densa di attività. Senza dubbio una esperienza da fare anche se l'affollamento turistico della baia è veramente importante e lo scenario naturale ne risente pesantemente.

I went on a cozy bay cruise, my room was nice enough. The food wasn't very nice but the views in Halong bay are amazing ! Absolutely gorgeous and unlike anywhere else. Would recommend anyone visiting Vietnam to go there. Went to amazing cave also it was beautiful and worth the visit.

Soñado e inmenso! Me encantaría poder poner excelente pero lamentablemente hay mucha basura en el agua. Espero que esto mejore pronto porque realmente es una belleza que no tiene comparación. Espero poder volver pronto.

Wir haben die Ha Long Bay Impressive Cruise gemacht. Die Tour ging zirka 5 Stunden. Auf dem Boot gab es zu Beginn ein Mittagessen. Man saß mit sechs Personen an einem Tisch und es wurden viele Leckereien aufgetischt, die man sich nach Belieben zusammenstellen konnte.Der erste Stopp war eine riesengroße Höhle, durch die man mindestens 10 Minuten gelaufen ist.Beim nächsten Halt gab es die Möglichkeit Kayak zu fahren. Leider nur 20 Minuten, da die Aktivitäten recht eng getacktet waren.Unser letzter Stopp war an einem Strand. Dieser war absolut überlaufen von Menschenmassen. Deswegen haben wir uns dafür entschieden auf den TiTov Mountain zu steigen und die Aussicht zu bewundern. Das war eine gute Entscheidung!Zurück auf dem Boot gab es einen kleinen „Kochkurs“. Wir haben Glücksrollen gemacht und gegessen. Nach dem Essen konnte man auf dem Dach des Boots den Sonnenuntergang anschauen. Zwischen den Bergen war das ein unglaublich schöner Anblick.Zusammenfassend kann man sagen, dass sich die Tour lohnt. Allerdings ist sie sehr kommerziell. Viele Menschen, wenig Zeit.
Halong bay rất đẹp, có nhiều tour du lịch ở đây, tuy nhiên không nên đi vào cuối tuần vì khách trung quốc sang quá nhiều

To fully appreciate what is an outstanding natural phenomena you really need to go on a 3 day cruise.as this is quite a large area.
