You just get a taste on a one day or two day 1 night cruise as they stay relatively close in and visit very busy anchorages. Chilling out and appreciating the incredible scenery in one of the more remote spots especially at sunset is amazing. All cruises offer a number of stops ranging from pleasant beaches to caves and pearl farms. A quite amazing place.

Best cruise in Ha Long Bay!  Executive rooms are the best, presidential room is double the size but not necessary as the balcony is even more narrow and only have one more sofa and a treadmill...water quality not good due to pollution by the tourists but as a mini-cruise this boat is the best in the whole area.

I visited sunworld as the cruise ship was moored near there and the cruise ship provided complimentary buses to the resort. It had a theme park, cinema complex, various cafes. Gardens with fountains for photo ops. There were live fish being sold on stalls. I felt a little bit intimidating walking around even though I was with other people of the cruise ship. Taxis drivers wanting to take you places and taking no for an answer. Just stayed for a few pictures and went back to the ship.

Ha Long Bay is beautiful.   We did an over night junk boat cruise and loved seeing the scenery.  Unfortunately, there is a lot of trash along the shores.  I hope in the future it will be cleaner since it is a UNESCO Heritage sight.  However, it is not to be missed on a trip to Vietnam.
一泊のツアーで行ったのですが、現地のコンダクターやほかの参加者と仲良くなれるので、場所もきれいですがその思い出が楽しいです。

I was really looking forward to seeing Halong Bay and having my treasured photo of the holiday amongst the karst scenery.  Not to be.  It was dull and overcast the whole time.  Not cold but not enticing.What was the main memory ?   Cruising alongside Kim Jon Un's launch with Security buzzing arounjd.  He and his entourage were taking time out for a cruise and lunch on board during his trip to Vietnam to meet up with Donald Trump (cut short). . Would I come again.  Yes, if circumstances meant I was in Halong Bay anyway.  Otherwise, I will satisfy myself with memories of the Li River in China.
Ha Long Bay is a must-visit attraction in Vietnam, it is absolutely beautiful. I would recommend people to join day tour from Hanoi, as overnight cruise is a bit overpriced.
We arrived Hanoi and plan to see Ha Log Bay.It 's so beautiful and bargee is friendly (she take photo our friend).

It was a very nice cruise. There were several boats, but it didn't feel "crowded", having said that, Bai Tu was much calmer and no less prettier.For those who want to swim ; forget it! The water was super dirty, again in Bai Tu the water was better, but still it was not very inviting.

🙋‍♀️ Vịnh Hạ Long (vịnh nơi rồng đáp xuống) là một vịnh nhỏ thuộc phần bờ tây vịnh Bắc Bộ tại khu vực biển Đông Bắc Việt Nam, bao gồm vùng biển đảo thuộc thành phố Hạ Long, thành phố Cẩm Phả và một phần huyện đảo Vân Đồn của tỉnh Quảng Ninh.🎒👒🕶🙋‍♀️ Giá cả ăn uống ở địa phương hơi đắt đỏ so với nhiều địa điểm khác. Không chỉ đẹp bởi cảnh sắc “mây trời sóng nước”, vẻ đẹp nên thơ hay vô số những đảo đá vôi nổi trên mặt nước, Hạ Long còn đem lại cho du khách cảm giác bình yên khi hòa mình vào cảnh vật nơi đây. Được đánh giá là một trong 29 Vịnh đẹp nhất thế giới, cuối tháng 3/2012, tổ chức New Open World cũng đã chính thức công nhận vịnh Hạ Long là một trong bảy kỳ quan thiên nhiên mới của thế giới.Nên trải nghiệm thử 1 lần để biết Việt Nam còn nhiều điều khám phá lắm.

Ha Long Bay is worth at least an overnight stay.  If it wasn't for the fact that our stay was marred by fog and light rain I would have given it a 5 star rating.  We were with a tour group and stayed one night only, arriving in the afternoon one day and left in the next afternoon.  Don't think you're the only boat in these waters because you're not.  This is specially evident once night falls.  Then everything is black except the lights shining from the other boats witch are all anchored around yours.  Great opportunity for photos.  These overnight tours usually include a visit to one of the many caves dispersed throughout the various islands.  We visited the Hang Sung Sot (Cave of Surprises) Cave.  It sits 25 metres above ground level and approximately 50 steps must be climbed before entering the cave which is quite large and tactfully lit. And yes there are a lot of people waiting to visit the cave but it's well worth it.  We were also treated to a small row boat tour of an inland bay accessible only through a hole cut in the face of the mountain.  Some of our folks saw a few large monkeys on the steep hillsides but I missed out. Halong Bay must not be missed.

ノイバイ空港で格安ツアーに申し込みました。大型観光バスに参加者が詰め込まれた感じです。ハノイを朝出発して休憩を取りながら昼間に到着しそのままクルーズ船で昼食という行程です。1人参加の人も多くてその人たちは1つのテーブルにあつまりました。韓国オーストラリアタイドイツからの旅行者です。昼食が終わったら洞窟の島に上陸、そのあと小舟に乗り換えてのカヌーみたいな体験と盛りだくさん。結局ハノイに着いたのは渋滞があったので6時間かかり夜11時でした。

My wife and I went on a Halong Bay Cruise when we were recently in Vietnam. We were picked up at our hotel in Hanoi and were taken to Halong Bay, with a gift shop stop along the way-- my wife ended up buying some lovely silk bath robes etc.It is a fairly long journey to get to Halong Bay, but once there you realize the volume of cruise boat traffic.  We went on a smaller cruise and spent 3 days & 2 nights. There are some pros and some cons, just be ready to get moved from one boat to another when you go for a caving expedition, which  was  quite nice.  We connected with two other couples, which we hung out with quite a bit, and this made the trip.  The scenery is quite nice.  Unfortunately they need to address the plastic litter issue in Vietnam. The water looks nice, but the amount of plastic litter is staggering. From an environmental standpoint, I feel that this is something that needs to be addressed.  When you kayak in the Halong Bay area you see the issue first hand, which is very sad.That being said, we did enjoy our Halong Bay trip. The food on our cruise was excellent and learning how to make Vietnamese spring rolls is something most cruises offer.  It can be a very eye opening trip, just go with your eyes wide open and have a positive attitude and I'm sure you'll enjoy it.

My last visit was in 2010; my recollections are of a chaotic fishing village lacking the infrastructure to deliver the thousands of tourists to the world heritage listed location.Now, the infrastructure is like a cancer, growing into the bay, and with an almost glutinous expansion of hotels and hi-rise developments to cope with the obvious natural attractions of the bay.But, too much is crass and ugly, and one can only image the place during high tourist seasons.My overnight cruise was very comfortable - the beds in Vietnam are very firm, to hard, and displease too many overweight or pampered punters, but for the well travelled - especially those who know the joys of India - the beds and the food added to the breathtaking wonder of the bay’s population of limestone islands.Sadly, it is not too much of an imagination to suggest Ha Long Bay is Vietnamese for, ‘you are surrounded’...

Beaucoup  de touristes, nuitée maison de pêcheur sur pilotis, très sympa, guide huy très gentil , professionnel, sommes passés par agence cap vision, repas bateau copieux et délicieux, prévoir longtemps à l'avance si vous souhaitez une nuit en privé sur jonque, nous n'avons pas pu le faire, plus de place

Sure, the water and islands are beautiful- but the endless number of boats are what I remember. And if you think that you have escaped the street sales people of Hanoi, think again - they will come at you by boat with fresh produce to sell. The stop at the caves is a quiet and cool diversion.
ハノイ市内から3時間ほどかかりました。ツアーてなく車をチャーターしたのですがツアーもあります。途中お土産やさんで休憩。美味しいコーヒーを飲むところもあり、麝香猫の糞のコーヒー豆を買いました。ハロン湾の船はいろいろなサイズがあるみたいで食事付きでした。私達は貸切だったので夫婦2人でゆっくり食事もでき、甲板にも出て撮影もし放題でした。到着すると鍾乳洞や筏などもあり十分楽しめます。

บรรยากาศฟินๆ อากาศสบายๆ ...วิวหลักล้าน..เหมาะกับคู่รักเชียวจ้ายามค่ำคืน ..จิบไวน์..นอนดูดาวบนดาดฟ้าเรือ โอ้ว!! โรแมนติค....การเดินทางสะดวก ..มีเรือให้เลือกเป็นร้อยๆลำ ..ราคาใกล้เคียงกัน ..เรือมีเก่า มีใหม่ ..เลือกให้ดีๆ ก่อนที่จะตัดสินใจซื้อทริป !!!
ハロン湾を見たくてベトナムに来ました。たくさんの島が連なり、すごく幻想的でした。いろんな形の岩があって面白いですよ。

This was a trip taken by a big group of us around 10.The place is definitely breathtaking once the boat moves away from the  mainland, but the best part is the trek up the mountain (caves).from here you can see the entire boats or tiny ships and it looks like a scene from a medieval oriental movie.The caves inside are huge!well lighted and with sign post and safety all around.While on the boat,you will also encounter Vietnamese boats (with their traditional straw hats) selling a variety of fresh fruits .its a must buy and also makes great photography. This bay is magical and anyone visiting Vietnam must visit Ha-long bay.Weather in September was also perfect.
Địa điểm du lịch thú vị lắm, đi trên tàu có cảm giác rất bình yên, thoải mái để ngắm cảnh và chụp hình
가족으로 크루저 투어왔어요 좋습니다이런 투어 처음해봐서 기대이상 동굴도 갔다왔고요 슬슬 올려볼게요크루즈투어 강추처음에 아쉬운점 몇개있었지만

Nous avons eu la chance de découvrir cette baie, de nous y baigner et d'y passer la nuit et enfin de nous réveiller avec un jolie soleil. Quelle beauté cette baie !!! Nous y retournerons sans hésiter.
Die Halong Bucht ist ein echtes Highlight auf einer Vietnam Rundreise. Wir würden eine Tagestour empfehlen.Eine Übernachtung in der Bucht würden wir nicht unbedingt empfehlen.

There are so many options to see the bay.  You could do a day trip but you would miss out on a lot as you would spend 4+ hours of that day just driving to and from Hanoi.   We did 2 nights which was perfect; one night is a must for the best experience.  We were on a very luxurious boat but you don’t have to do it that way, any boat with air conditioning would work.   The bay is amazing but the water is gross and there is trash everywhere... the people who live here are not taking care of their homeland as they mostly live day to day, which is unfortunate.   But the beauty of the islands and the terrain is a must see!
날씨의 영향을 많이 받아서 제가 갔을 땐 멋진 경관을 제대로 감상하기 어려웠습니다. 그래도 맑은 날씨보단 적당히 구름이 있는게 오히려 더 멋질 듯 합니다.아침에 선상에서 동 트는 모습을 감상하는 것을 추천합니다.
The karst mountains are just gorgeous. If you take a cruise you'll see a lot of them. Unfortunately, those cruise ships generate much of the pollution and smog that obscures the mountains at times.

天下にとどろくハロン湾の奇岩はハノイ観光に欠かせない。我々は出発前に、旅行代理店で１日ツアー・コース（１人約8500円）を申し込んで、これを利用した。だが、ハノイ市内からハロン湾までの行きと帰りの行程が長くて疲れてしまう。第一、バスの運行時間が片道５時間もかかる。その間には「お便所の休憩」と称する土産物屋への強制的立ち寄りが往復１回ずつある。往路の途中でバッチャン焼きの「村」へ寄ったが、これは良い。だが各種の土産物屋では、我々観光客の財布を何としてもこじ開けようとする「売り込み合戦」がすごくて、ヘキエキする。　肝心のハロン湾巡りは、遊覧船に乗って、まず平凡なランチを食べながらゆるゆると湾内を進み、スポットをちょっと見て回るだけ。１カ所、鍾乳洞の見学があった。湾内の滞在は４時間足らず、コスト・パフォーマンスは良くない。しかしこうしたパターンは、どんなコースに申し込んでも同じようなものだろう。湾内の宿泊付きコースを利用したら話は違うだろうが、値段がバカ高い。ベトナム政府は観光立国をうたう以上、ハロン湾までの高速道路（「高速鉄道」も含む）の整備を急ぐべきである。しかしハノイ市内の地下鉄もまだ通っていない。ハロン湾はいつまで「遠い場所」であり続けるのか。
Cruise was efficiently organized.The itinerary was well planned.The tour guide, Tony, was arranging activities effectively.My wife and I are very pleased with the tour.We thank you for your efforts.

I was so excited about going on a cruise here. I was so shocked regarding the amount of people to be fair, there’s busy and then there is chaos! This was chaos around the area where you embarked and disembarked. Keep sight on the people that you are with as you can easily loose sight of them. It is definitely worth doing at least once. I would not want to do it again as once you have seen it you have seen it. It is a fabulous sight to see and to wake up on the water in kind of magical. I wished we had chosen a better boat and paid a little more. Saw some magnificent hawks whilst there too, which was my highlight! I would not go again, like other places I have visited due to the massive amount of people there at the same time. But if you have not seen it you really should.

Halong bay is known for its picturesque landscape of turquoise blue sea water and the thousands of jutting rock formations that stand out from the sea. It's a sight that holds you in awe. The beauty of mother nature is there before you to love and enjoy. It is an experience of a life time. Seeing this from a cruise boat provides added charm as you see the sun rise and sun set from the deck. I think no visit to Vietnam can be complete without a visit to Halong bay.

This is the iconic of Hanoi! It even appears in movies like the King Kong! However, it was quite foggy when I was there. Otherwise, the view would have been even better. Worth checking it out but I would recommend you to book at least one night stay on cruise to fully explore Halong Bay.
Around 3 to 4 hours from Hanoi. We took a cruise and we stayed on it for a night. This place can also be visited just as a day trip from Hanoi. It was a great experience altogether.

We did the bay in a day 🎼...and felt that was ok. We chose Heylong bay tours who seemed one f the best. After a long 4 hour drive from hanoi we arrived...ticket purchased we got on our boat. Nice old tub with a pretty good dining deck and an open top deck for the spectacular seascapes. We were greeted with a wonderful selection of plates to share and could only just fit them on the table! Drinks apparently are always extra.We stopped at Ti Top island and enjoyed a refreshing swim and relax on the lovely sandy beach. Then on to the famous ( once ) subterranean cave complex, which was a long initial climb, yet with breathtaking stalagmites and tites. Very spectacular. You emerge from the caves about 100 foot up with , again, spectacular views across the bay, from on high! Our driver then took us to a large island with an internal lagged, accessed through a hole in the cliff where swifts were darting in and out. We chose to do this in a 2 person kayak, but you could also jump in a 10 man round boat thing and be rowed! Many macaque monkeys came down to greet us ( see what food they could get !!😊 ) as we got close to he lagoon wall. Another hour or so cruising and returning to port.All in all a very unique place and well worth the effort and expense to see.

We have arrived Hanoi one day earlier to arrange for a day tour to Halong Bay. There are many prices and packages but make sure you choose the right company that's it.  Program and schedule wise all about the same. It takes about 3.5 hours to arrive the harbor. Unfortunately we choose the wrong date it's national holidays it is so crowded with all nationality. The package covers three places mainly the cave exploring   kayaking and hiking up the view point at Titoc island.   Ours started with a simple lunch once the boat starts leaving. It's a 6 hour trip. You must be fit enough to do all these in six hours. If not you still can enjoy the sea breeze on the deck or inside the boat

We went for a half day cruise , had a sumptuous lunch on board a boat. It was a cloudy and foggy day and it made the surrounding limestone islands felt surreal. Visit Song Sot cave, did a wooden boat ride at luon cave then ended with a hike up to a hill at titop island. Recommended to be reasonably fit as there are steep staircases.
Kaya king was very great with beautiful sightseeings. Seafood is really delicious and fresh. Wonderful!

A must-do-once cruise. Scenic and idyllic. Bookings for Basic category do not reveal if or not your room has a balcony as it is randomly assigned. Food on board is plenty and reasonably tasty. Activities like fishing village visit & kayaking were enjoyable. Emerald Bay water beautiful but not clean.

took a boat trip to the caves at Halong Bay, the views are stunning and the caves even though there are some steps are well worth the effort, its a very laid back trip especially on the boat, and its great seeing all the boats jostling for position trying to pick the tourists up , lots of little shops dotted about near the caves, great day

Ha Long Bay has a dramatic landscape which can be seen from shore but is best seen close up, from one of the many little tour boats which set off from the harbour. These trips can be arrange from Hanoi and include transport to and from Ha Long but can also be arranged locally if, like us, you are staying at Ha Long Bay. Essentially you visit three key places:Sung Sot Cave, the large cave with stalactites/mites; monkey island and Tito island. The stalactite caves are interlinked and worth seeing, being much bigger than expected. At Monkey island, you move to smaller craft called bamboo boats, but which are metal and of low height to get passengers through the low cave opening into the lagoon behind. Here you can spot monkeys in the trees. At the same spot you can opt to row yourselves in kayaks. Life jackets are provided for either option.The third, Tito island, has a small beach where you are told you can swim, but beware as it is swarming with people and is not the quiet idyll you imagine. You can also climb the rather steep steps to the top for a spectacular view, well worth the effort. The sea on the day we went was like a mill pond so even though I am not a very good sailor, I felt no sickness.Lunch, which was far better than expected, was served on board. A word of warning, this did not include drinks so you need to take plenty of water or select to buy it from the boat operator at an inflated cost.The whole trip takes around 4 - 5 hours. On the way back to port our trip offered sun downer food and a local wine. The trip is well worth doing, even though there are many boats on exactly the same route, so it is not the serene landscape you expect but rather noisy, busy and full of other tourist groups, making the place rather crowded.

Thought it’s yet another touristy island trip but this trip ended as a memorable one . The package which consisted of a Pleasant cruise trip , a short boat ride, kayaking, the cave visit and good food is a must do. The limestone caves are intriguing . The cruise between the rocks seem very surreal with the guide narrating the story of the place and the local practice of naming the rockets scattered  after their shapes . A place to remember .
방문한 날이 흐리고 안개가 끼어서 분위기가 좋았어요 약간은 촌스럽고 낡은 영화관 느낌의 크루즈이었지만 값은 싸고 사람들은 친절했습니다. 풍경으로 말하자면,  대단히 놀랍도록 아름다운 풍경까지는 못 되었어요. 하노이에서 여기말고  딱히 갈 곳이 없어요..;;

Turquoise waters, beautiful islands. Only problem was that when kayaking you realise how dirty the water is which put me off swimming. This needs urgent attention before the bay is completely polluted

We were lucky on our day there, a cloudy day with light fogs rolling it, so some of the cliffs and rock formation was lightly covered in fog to give it that magical feel as ships and mountains appear and disappear behind the fogs. From our photos I can easily see how past painters view this bay and it's monochrome mystic qualities. If I can remove tourists like myself and some of the pollution on the water Ha Long Bay would have been perfect.

Like so many other tourists doing the standard itinerary of Vietnam, I did a 2 day Halong Bay  cruise with Le Theatre Cruises. The scenery is beautiful, despite the fact that the weather was clouded over and grey. My biggest disappointment however, was all the garbage that was floating in the bay. When our group went kayaking, some of us picked up the garbage on the way and came back with virtually full kayaks - with everything from rusted fishing nets, blocks of disintegrating Styrofoam, and, of course, the ubiquitous plastic items. The bay itself is becoming very crowded with cruise ships, and if the problem of throwing garbage into the bay is not addressed, I don’t think it will be as wonderful a tourist experience anymore.On the plus side, the virtually new cruise ship that I was on from Le Theatre Cruises was gorgeous.  The decor is reminiscent of a high end ocean liner and everything is five-star here. The rooms are beautifully outfitted with a large bathroom and balcony, the staff is exceptional - from the waiters to the spa service - and the food was among the best I had during my entire time in Vietnam. It’s just a shame that most of the cruises on Halong Bay are only one night because I would have  loved to have stayed on this ship.

Halong Bay is so beautiful but I believe Coron Philippines is much more beautiful. The limestone islands are incredible and so worth the visit. Peak season can be a zoo!!! Literally thousands of people visit this place every day and you can see what this does to its environment. We booked the tour from our hotel in Hanoi and got the full day trip. If you want a relaxing tour go for two days if you have the time. I saw no need to pay any extra charges for a tour as everyone will basically see the main sites. It’s a good 4 hours drive with a pit stop at a factory that sells marble statues to beautiful hand crafted art pieces. Our boat was quite nice with an upper deck. Food was not bad but if you’re a big eater you won’t get full. Drinks are for sale on the boat. Sad to see garbage floating in the bay while sailing through the islands. First stop you can kayak or ride a paddled boat into a secluded cove entering through a big gap in the limestone. Cool to see the monkeys. The next stop is a huge and I mean massive cave! I’ve been to many caves but this was impressive. Easy steps to get upto and a wooded floor that guides you around the cave. The view upon exiting the cave is fabulous as you see the bay from high up. The last stop is a small island where you can actually swim or climb the island peak. Then the boat ride back is nice as sunset photo ops are great. It’s quite a long day and you arrive back in Hanoi around 9ish. Well worth the visit. Please don’t litter 😀

Une très belle croisière sur un beau bateau (jonque de luxe). Cabines spacieuses et bien équipées. Équipage très sympathique et cuisine excellente à bord.Visites proposées et activités sur le bateau pour la croisière de 2 jours, 1 nuit très agréables. Très bons moments passés. Seul bémol, odeur d'essence dans la cabine et à l'arrière du bateau car proche des moteurs.

Fomos em um dia com intensa nebulosidade e uma discreta garoa. O barco navegando calmamente, com ares de Piratas do Caribe. O lugar é muito bonito, linda a natureza. Dormimos por lá, e com a proteção da Marinha.

Kayaking & Swimming at the pristine lagoon was very good experience after Titop island tour. All boats in Halong bay do the same same route that why some places were too busy. We enjoyed our Legacy legend cruise trip which offered visiting Lan Ha bay  for kayaking & swimming!

ハノイからクルマで片道4時間、200km超の長旅。ツアーで行ったため途中の休憩(お土産スポット)含めストレスなく往復。船着場は広大で無数の船が停泊しており、個人で見つけることは困難。ツアーでありながら、当日は50人超は乗れようかという船一艘を妻と2人(プラス添乗員さん)で貸し切り。曇天で肌寒い天気だったがハロン湾の絶景は見事で、まさに水墨画の中を旅するような感覚。食事は可もなく不可もなく、お酒も高くなく安心して飲めるが飲み過ぎは禁物。途中大きな鍾乳洞巡りが組まれており、具合が悪くなっては台無し。総じて満喫できたが、近隣あるいは船中で一泊してゆったりと景色のうつろいと食を楽しむべきでしょう。

Es uno de los paisaje más bonitos que he visto nunca.Es el típico destino conocido por todos y, porque no decirlo, un poco masificado. Pero os aseguro que la visita merece la pena.Imponentes rocas altísimas con vegetación frondosa que nace y crece en la misma roca. Hay mucho tráfico de barcas, pero se respeta el silencio que le da (todavía) más encanto en el escenario.Imprescindible visitar el pueblo flotante "Cua Van" y la cueva "Sung Sot".Raconets.

Elbette ki görmeliyiz. Adacıkların arasında gezerken daha önce gezdiğiniz yerlerden farklı hissedeceksiniz. Adacıklara verilen adlar, görünümleri sizi farklı bir evrende hissettiriyor. Ayrıca tekne turu sırasında uğradığınız mağara, maymunlar sayesinde bulunmuş yeni keşfedilmiş bir mağara. Sizi içeride oluşumu bitmiş harika bir güzellik karşılıyor. Ancak denizin üzerindeki akıntılarla gelmiş çöpler bizi üzdü. İnşallah bunun çözümünü kısa zamanda bulabilirler.

The natural beauty of this place is simply incredible. We had overcast weather on both days of our overnight boat stay, but it didn't matter. It is simply breathtakingly beautiful. The downside is the intensive tourism and pollution, and you can read more than enough negative reviews on those aspects elsewhere on this page, and the reality is that every one of us who has visited has contributed to that, but that doesn't mean you don't enjoy one of the most beautiful places on earth. We were in a group and had a boat to ourselves so our guide timed the visits to Ti Top (just before the ticket offices closes) and the caves (very late and very early) and kayaking to perfection so we didn't really encounter huge crowds anywhere, so it can be done with a bit of savvy. Yes there are hundreds of boats and the day trip ones are real kamikaze pilots and very annoying, but it is such a privilege to see this beautiful place you just look past those things. Go soon though, the character of the place is about to change rapidly with all the development going on.

We did 2 days/ 1 night on Legacy legend cruise & had amazing experience. The boat was a bit older than picture but clean & safe. Services were all very friendly. Halong bay was very beautiful, Titop island was too busy but Kayaking & swimming in Lan Ha was brilliant. Highly recommend!
効率重視でVeltraで高速道路を通るツアーを予約。前夜遅くのハノイ到着であったので、出発時間に余裕をもって向かうことが出来て、結果的に良かった。参加者の一人が無断で離断してしまい、出航が遅れるハプニングがあったが、日本語ガイドのランさんの機転が利いた対応や丁寧な説明で、充実した時間を過ごせた。湾は水墨画の世界観で、鍾乳洞も楽しめる。昼食も美味しかったので、日帰りであれば、ツアーがオススメ。
Un imprescindible si viajas a Vietnam, el sitio es espectacular aunque el agua y las playas sucias, deberían cuidar el entorno algo mas.
訪れた日、乗船当初は小雨で霧がかかってましたが、雨は直ぐに止み、曇り空となって、幻想的な景色のハロン湾も魅力的でした。今後は晴天のハロン湾を再訪したいです。

Not doubt it is one of the UNESCO World Heritage sites and the scenery around the area is indeed wonderful and fantastic. Very nice in the arms of our mother's nature. There are a total 1969 islands around the area, big and small, tall and short, "fat and thin" and each is unique and nice in its own way!We toured the Halong Bay via the Escape Sails (please refer to my earlier review of the company here too) cruise and we spent about 5~6 hours cruising around the bay. Although it was a little foggy initially, the weather turned for the better afternoon which we could enjoy a better and "heavenly paradise" scenery.We got up and closed when we took a bamboo boat ride around the area. It was really amazing and nice! We could hear the birds' singing, the water sound and it was so peaceful and calm and totally all stresses were gone. The limestone caves were awesome too and indeed it took hundreds, if not thousands of years to have such a lovely formations.Along the way, we noticed that some parts of the water were littered with many used items like plastic bottles and cans, plastic bags floating around. We were sad and anguish to see how inconsiderate these small groups of "bad hats" that tried to destroy our nature and environment. We enjoyed our tour here and liked the scenery of course!

Comment: ハロン湾の日帰りツアーで参加。 期待していませんでしたが、お天気も良く、素晴らしい景色に大満足。  船からの景色も素晴らしく、世界遺産にも納得。 鍾乳洞は日本の鍾乳洞と比べてもすごいものでしたが、ガイドさんは岩がどんな形に見えるかということに焦点を当てていて、どんなに長い年月でこの素晴らしい空間ができたかには関心がないようで(^ ^)。  バス、船、ランチ含め、とても素晴らしい日帰り旅行ができました。

さすが人気観光地、とても心が洗われる美しい景色で大満足でした。 潮風を浴びながら遠くを眺めるだけでも十分価値がありそうです。 強い日差しが苦手なので、個人的には日帰りクルーズで十分でした。 雨季でしたが天気はよく、夕陽を拝むこともできました。 カヌーやバンブーボートでのアクティビティ中には猿を観察することもできたり、鍾乳洞を歩き回ったりとただクルーズする以外の楽しみ方もたくさんあります。 ただ人気なだけあって観光客は多く、カヌーは少し漕ぎづらそうでした。 そのため行く場合は平日をおすすめします。 電波はところどころ弱い気がしました。  しかしながら、エリアによっては水がとっても汚く、ゴミも大量に浮いているのを見かけました。 私たち観光客のせいで汚染されているのだと考えると、手放しで褒め称えるのもなと。 少なくともプラごみは必ず持ち帰りましょう。

Halong Bay is a breathtaking natural wonder and a UNESCO World Heritage Site in northern Vietnam. Known for its emerald waters and thousands of limestone karsts and islets, it offers a surreal seascape that feels like stepping into a dream.  Activities like cruising, kayaking, and exploring hidden caves or floating villages make the experience unforgettable. The sunsets and sunrises over the bay are particularly magical.  Whether you're seeking relaxation or adventure, Halong Bay’s serene beauty and unique charm make it a must-visit destination for any traveler.

A must do when coming to  Hanoi. The caves were really busy but well worth the wait in line. I’d also recommend hiking the Ti Top mountain for a great view, if able. We chose kayaking over the bamboo boats, great choice!

Ha Long Bay's ethereal beauty is unrivaled, with its emerald waters and towering limestone karsts creating a dreamlike landscape. Exploring hidden caves and floating villages reveals a rich cultural tapestry. The bay’s serene ambiance at dawn is enchanting, making it a must-visit for nature lovers and tranquility seekers.

Unesco world heritage must visit place if you come to Hanoi, Book a boat include lunch or dinner, enjoy the great view of the sea and the island and busy traffic along the way to the cave. Every convenient and easy to walk to see the different shape of the rock inside

Ha Long Bay is one of the most beautiful places in Vietnam. I was so impressed by the islands even when our cruise ship entered the bay. So many beautiful rocks and islands seem to pop up from the ocean. It is even better when we have a local cruise boat that can bring us up close to those islands, and our activities are delightful and fun.

Tôi đã có một chuyến du ngoạn Vịnh Hạ Long!  Ban đầu tôi đặt vé qua Trip.com, nhưng vé hết nên tôi nhanh chóng đặt qua …

We took one day tour to Ha Long Bay. After getting on board of cruise we had lunch which was good and after that we stopped at one island where there was option of hiking or swimming. We did hiking which was tough and excruciating but view from above was worth it. After that second stop was there where there was option of Kayaking or Bamboo boat and we chose Kayaking which was awesome experience and last stop was the caves. Caves were fabulous and worth seeing.

Ha Long Bay is a breathtaking natural wonder located in northeastern Vietnam. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the country’s most popular tourist destinations.  Here are some key features and activities you can experience in Ha Long Bay: -scenic view -cruise tours -islands and caves -kayaking and swimming -sunsets and sunrises

La Baia è di una bellezza sconsiderata e con duemila isolotti da visitare. Va sottolineato purtroppo la sporcizia del mare! Troppo polistirolo e plastica probabilmente proveniente dal villaggio di pescatori.

Crews booked even 5 star are like normal ships , clean safe but not so much of luxury . Main it is for natural scenery and not for comfort but the staff was trying to put there wfforts to make it worth but still it was no value for money in terms of food and other activities. Only natural sightseeing was worth visiting this place. It can become much better and organized for the good tourism
ベトナムハノイにある世界遺産。 ハノイの南部にある風光明媚な湾。 ハノイ市街地からは車で２時間ほどかかる。  ハロン湾のクルーズは人気ツアー。 3000もの島や岩を臨みながらシーフードを食べる日帰りツアー。 ホテルや船上に泊まるツアーもある。  ティエンクン鍾乳洞は幻想的で特に人気が高い。

旧市街よりクルーズツアーに参加。 英語がニガテなので日本人ガイド付ツアーにしました。 流暢な日本語でベトナムの歴史や習慣など 色々説明してもらえて為になります。 1人11500円、リムジンバスなので現地までは快適。船も豪華？のランクらしくエアコン付で ランチはブッフェ形式でした。 ブッフェではないツアーは4人くらいで食事をシェアしたりするスタイルなので ブッフェにしました。 夜は街に帰る途中のサービスエリアでフォーも食べられます♡ 1日たっぷり遊べるのでコスパはいいかな。 今回は天気が悪かったので景色は全く見えませんでたが、鍾乳洞は絶景でした。 各国からの観光客で埋め尽くされている感じでしたが、異国の人と交流できたり 楽しい1日を過ごせました。 次回は晴天の時に参加したいです😃

遊歷越南下龍灣的最佳方式，相較於一日遊船行程，搭乘遊輪在海上住一宿，不僅可欣賞海上桂林般的群峰、體驗水上活動，船上供應三餐，還有酒吧宵夜，夜晚天氣好時可仰望星空，更為愜意。  Peony Cruise（牡丹遊輪）是下龍灣眾遊船業者中偏高檔的遊輪，二層樓的Peony Cruise共有20間客房，每間豪華客房都有私人陽台，約7-8坪大的空間，以及獨立衛浴和浴缸，還有靠窗檯的貴妃沙發椅，基本上和陸上酒店無差別，且海景更近、更美！  訂了二天一夜的方案，雙人價格約一萬元。一早就派大巴從酒店接送至碼頭，c碼頭Lounge辦理登記後，先搭小船往下龍灣內海，再登上二層樓的大船 Peony Cruise，開啟了特別的遊輪體驗。  行程豐富、照三餐餵食：自助午餐、海上立槳或快艇、越式春捲體驗、西式排餐晚宴、雞尾酒 happy hours、自助早餐、登島遊石灰岩洞。  由於大船緩行於內海，風平浪靜，完全無搖晃，窗外景色不斷轉換，有時還會有兜售零食的小船，篝著長網竹竿和船艙客人進行買賣，相當有趣！  遊船的餐食也是頗豐盛，晚上還有幫三對新婚夫婦慶祝的燭光秀、以及導遊小哥的橫笛表演，也可以在甲板泳池玩水或躺椅耍廢。  其實不在船上住宿也可體驗這類行程，但就無法感受下龍灣的寧靜與星夜，愜意程度和爽感還是有差。  #越南自由行 #下龍灣 #peonycruise

A beautiful paradise on earth.. it has got 1969 limestone mountains that you can visit... Kayaking can be done.. cruise on the ship. May be stay overnight... Visit the amazing caves, it has got 3 chambers.. 3rd one is one of the biggest in the world.. you got uphill to trek.. a nice beach to take a swim... One thing i didn't like is the commercialization and the rude behaviour of the boys who hand over the photos taken inside the cave.. you see a sign board stating a free gift available (A small key chain with your photo on it). Towards your exit, you'll have few boys trying to sell u a A5 photo of yours and charge 10$. Pure scam !!! Avoid that and you can still collect you Free Key chain photo. ..

Một nơi tuyệt đẹp để tham quan bằng du thuyền. Có rất nhiều địa điểm tham quan và hoạt động để trải nghiệm (chèo thuyền kayak hoặc tàu cao tốc, tham quan hang động và đi bộ đường dài). …

Ha Long Bay, a UNESCO World Heritage site in Vietnam, is a mesmerizing natural wonder that leaves visitors awe-inspired. The bay is a breathtaking tableau of emerald waters dotted with over 1,600 limestone karsts and islets, creating a surreal landscape that seems plucked from a fantasy. The sheer scale of these towering limestone formations, some reaching heights of 200 meters, is nothing short of awe-inspiring.  Cruising through the bay unveils a cinematic panorama of lush greenery clinging to the karsts, while traditional Vietnamese fishing boats glide gracefully on the tranquil waters. The bay's mystical ambiance is further heightened by ethereal mists that shroud the karsts, adding an air of mystery to the scene.  Exploring the numerous caves and grottoes that pockmark the karsts reveals a subterranean world adorned with stunning stalactite formations. Sung Sot Cave, with its cathedral-like chambers, stands out as a testament to the region's geological and cultural significance.  While the bay's beauty is undeniable, its popularity has led to concerns about environmental impact. Responsible tourism is crucial to preserving Ha Long Bay's delicate ecosystem and ensuring that future generations can marvel at its splendor.  In conclusion, Ha Long Bay is a must-visit destination for nature enthusiasts and adventure seekers alike. Its otherworldly beauty and cultural significance make it a gem in Vietnam's tourism crown, inviting travelers to embark on a journey through a landscape that feels like a living masterpiece.

We embarked on a day trip via Phoenix cruise. The bay is gorgeous, and it certainly earns its spot as one of the world's wonders.  But the cruise itself was pretty dissapointing when it came to the food selection. It certainly did not reflect the region's best cuisines.

It was nice. It was cold, though, when we visited this place around the last week of March. You can do kayaking if you like. It was a good experience for sure. Enjoy. We took the guided tour so we don't know the prices here. We took an overnight cruise called Renea.

Ha Long Bay has some beautiful scenery, with dramatic limestone crags jutting out of the water. Unfortunately there’s a lot of trash and debris in the water, almost everywhere you look you can see some junk floating around. I wouldn’t recommend swimming in the water for this reason. All the trash doesn’t ruin the beauty, but made it just a little bit sad compared to something like Khao Sok National Park in Thailand, which has very similar and gorgeous scenery but more pristine waters.  Many different tour companies operate boat cruises in the bay. Most cruises within a given price bracket have almost identical activities / itineraries, and very similar ships. So pick your price range based on how much luxury you want, and don’t worry too much about which specific company to go with.

I booked a tour through the hotel I stayed at in Hanoi. The drive there was calm, and not too long, just about 2 hours. We took a boat out to the islands. There was a pearl shop tour before the boat, which has a lot of nice jewelry. Lunch was served on the boat, and then they took us kayaking. I would recommend this for sure.
Leuke dag cruise gedaan over de Ha Longbaai, heel mooi en leuk om te zien maar wel enorm toeristisch en druk op de tussenstops.

Beautiful scenery. The places to explore around the bay are worth it. Most people have to do the tour to get around the bay. For me personally, there are times there are too many people in certain sites that you have to be in line to experience the sights such as going inside the cave.

So với gần 10 năm trước, Vịnh Hạ Long đã thay đổi đáng kể. Có một tuyến đường cao tốc từ Hà Nội, chỉ mất hơn hai giờ để đến đó. Các khách sạn ven vịnh cũng được cải thiện đáng kể với nhiều khách sạn mới. Cảng du thuyền Vịnh Hạ Long đã được …

Vịnh Hạ Long là một điểm du lịch vô cùng nổi tiếng. Chúng tôi phải đi thuyền từ đất liền đến đó. Có hàng trăm hòn đảo đá vôi với nhiều hình dạng khác nhau nằm rải rác khắp vịnh. Chúng tôi cũng được đưa đến một hòn đảo lớn để xem nhũ đá và măng đá.

A great place to visit and do a cruise. The boat will take you to a kayak location in a scenic bay, an overcrowded, not really too special beech and an impressive cave. We enjoyed the boat, the great food and the funny guide, the kayaking would have been perfect, if it wasn’t for a lot (!) of styrofoam trash (used for buoyancy of the pearl farms) floating around. I hope the cruise companies will realize the impact of tidiness on their business (if not the environment) in the future as it would not take much to clean the bay up.

Una delle tappe obbligatorie in Vietnam è la Baia di Halong costellata da un arcipelago di più di 2000 isole. Il posto è sicuramente meraviglioso ma ci sono delle cose a cui essere preparati per non rimanere delusi rispetto alle aspettative. Primo fra tutti è sapere che siamo in un posto altamente turistico; la quantità di barche e persone è incalcolabile, le rotte che le barche percorrono sono di fatto le stesse per cui non è sicuramente un’esperienza di distacco dal mondo nonostante la bellezza della natura. Vengono proposti giri in kajak e bamboo boat e l’attracco è anche qui molto affollato e per ultimo il clima - che si sa nel paese essere molto variabile. Senza sole ovviamente i colori non esistono. Se si investe in una crociera di una notte il tutto è fatto con più tranquillità e nella nostra (Orchid cruise) ad esempio il secondo giorno il kayak lo hanno calato dalla barca in un punto dove c’erano solo le nostre due navi, quindi un’esperienza più immersiva nella natura. Se venite in inverno considerate anche le temperature .. un piumino leggero non da fastidio 😊. Un posto sicuramente nella lista di quelli da vedere nella vita, ma non nelle top ten per quanto descritto sopra!

Amazing place, but WAY TOOO MANY cruise ships allowed to be there, and the impact on the environment is not a pretty one. I hope something will be put in place to control more that. As with most places in Vietbam, they are amazing, but mass tourism will end up deteriorating them and it’s a real pitty.

U can opt for day trip, 2D1N or 3D2N cruise. The activities are the same for these 3, the major differences gotta be how rush or chill you want to be putting aside the cost.  Personally 2D1N was nice blend given the travelling time I have. Note that the attractions such as Surprising Cave and tiptop island can be really crowded cause everyone that visits HLB goes to those spots, once u are done with the sightseeing, then u are with Ur cruise mates.  What's a little turn off to be is how the cruise offers karaoke in such an environment. This is a very subjective opinion. I don't think it gels well.

Un lugar impresionante. Imprescindible si visitar Vietnam. Algo negativo es que poco a poco se esta masificando por lo que yo recomendaría optar por la bahia de Lang Ha o la isla CatBa que están alado y aún no son tan turisticas. Lo ideal es dormir en un barco una noche. Hicimos una excursión de kayak con este mismo barco y paramos en un playa que desgraciadamente estaba llena de plástico... Es de reflexionar, ya que los turistas no llevamos plástico, no se nos permite bajar con botellas de agua por ejemplo... Por lo que entiendo que serán los locales quienes ensucian así un paraje tan espectacular. Aún así, es uno de los mejores recuerdos de nuestro viaje.
3月のツアーで行きました。いちばんのメインイベントでしたが、天気があんまりよくなく、それだけが残念。 3時間のショートコースのクルージングですが、時間があればお泊まりするコースもいいかも。途中の洞窟探検も楽しかった。 とにかく天気！　天気が評価を左右します。

I travelled from India, and must recommend place if you visit North Vietnam. We took one day tour package from Honai and started at 8.30am. The place is definitely Breathtaking and Marvellous 🤩 But little crowded during the weekend as it’s tourist attraction. Choose your cruise carrier carefully due to lot of spams. I would recommend a 2days/1 night cruise. Lot of good sea food options in cruise

Took a 2 hour chartered bus ride from Hanoi to Halang Bay for a day cruise.  Paid Vnd 700k per person which includes entrance to Halong Bay, Boat Cruise, 7 course lunch and Bamboo Boat / Kayak ride to explore the caves under Route 1.  Bring lots of sunblock, shades and caps to shelter from the sun.  Afternoon took the same chartered bus for a 2 hour ride back to Hanoi.

Ha Long Bay: necessary evil?  I've conflicting thoughts about Ha Long Bay. Ecologically speaking, it's marvelous, breathtaking beautiful, and magnificent, and that's me seeing it on a cloudy day speaking.  Tourism industry, me included, have worked towards it's decline. New highway cuts travel time from 5 to 2.5 hours. Harbor is dotted with new hotel construction. Single use plastic is banned. Food served on junks is meh. Thiên Cũng grotto is carefully curated. Why is the pearl farm pit stop mandatory? What steps are being taken for Oyster conservation?  It's indeed a marvel worth seeing, but how can a country get its tourism dollars without taking away the essence from it? There's no correct answer I guess, only thoughtfulness required.

Salah satu yg diakui unesco. Tjakep sih. Pemandangannya biru2 adem cerah gitu. Kesananya naek kapal terus poto2 nikmatin pemadamgan di kapal sambil disebraning untuk masuk ke goa nya. Goanya juga unik bebatuannya. Ada sejarahnya.  Salah satu tempat wisata yg setidaknya sekali harus dikunjungin yak.
5回目くらい 20年前と比較して人工物が増えた。 鍾乳洞も変なカラー照明もなくなり すっきりした。 観光地として進化している。良いよ。
It's fine? Maybe an overnight cruise might be an over hyped idea. But overall it is beautiful

Le site a perdu de sa superbe. Trop de bateaux ,trop de touristes. La pollution de l'eau est importante , de nombreux déchets jonchent la surface de l'eau trouble. Certains endroits sont définitivement dégradés. Je l'ai visitée pour la première fois il y a quarante ans ,on en est loin malheureusement.

Not a picturesque as expected as shown on brochures and tv. A lot of rubbish in the water. Our tender to the main ship kept having to dodge the floating debris/rubbish. Had to stop and clear the propeller at one point. But still a must visit and an experience to have. Something to tick off the bucket list. Just try and do it in style in one of the luxury cruisers out there for 5 star service and comfort
Prachtige omgeving. 2 dagen doorgebracht in de Ha Longbaai.  Vietnam 🇻🇳 moet zuinig zijn op de schoonheid van het gebied. Laat plastic, een grote vervuiler, de schoonheid niet verpesten.
Một địa điểm tuyệt đẹp để tham quan. Hang động rất tuyệt vời và có nhiều nơi để chụp ảnh đẹp. Chuyến đi thuyền sẽ mất từ ​​3-5 giờ hoặc hơn tùy thuộc vào gói dịch vụ bạn chọn. …

Đây là lần đầu tiên tôi đến đó, và tất nhiên cảnh quan ở đó rất hùng vĩ. Tuy nhiên, những tàn phá của du lịch cũng rõ ràng không kém. Bạn có thể cập bến ở một bãi biển gần đó, tham quan một hang động và chèo thuyền …

Nhìn kìa. Nơi này tự nhiên đẹp đến ngỡ ngàng - những khối đá vôi, cây cối, màu nước dưới bầu trời, những hang động - tất cả đều tuyệt đẹp! Tuy nhiên, đối với một di sản thế giới, tình trạng ô nhiễm thực sự đã làm mất đi vẻ đẹp tự nhiên của …
有機會到此一遊，包船是不錯的選擇，雖然貴一點，但不受其他遊客干擾，比較自由自在

À faire! De très beaux paysages, de belles activités comme balade en kayak ou visite de grottes. Néanmoins ça reste très touristique et surtout très polluée (avec les bateaux et platisque flottants dans l'eau).
It was a great tour. Except knee pain on climbing hundreds of steps, it was amazing
北越下龍灣位於下龍市,街上建築色彩繽紛,爭奇鬥艷十分熱鬧,沿岸有近二千座島,大多無人居住,但是水上倒有一些水上人家,住在木造船屋上,其中一些大島有大型鐘乳岩洞,值得參觀~~

Great place to visit. Indochina Sail is one of the 700 boats/ships in the area. It is usually 2 days 1 night or 3 days 2 nights. This time I choose 2 days 1 night, and it was pretty relaxing. We went to to different small island in total. Ha long bay's view is breath taking, and even better from the top of the TI TOP mountain.

Die Halongbucht ist definitiv sehenswert. Man sollte dazu wissen dass das ziemlich touristisch ist und es ist einfach viel zu viel los, man kann es weniger in Ruhe genießen. Wir waren auf einem großen Schiff mit essen, unser Guide war sehr freundlich und hat uns über den Tag sehr gut begleitet. Die Firmen bzw. die Guides von den Minibooten mit ca. 10-15 Leuten auf dem Boot sind sehr unfreundlich, sie schupsen andere Urlauber die nicht auf deren Booten sind z.B. in der Höhle rum damit ihre eigenen Leute in Ruhe Bilder machen können. Ich finde das eine Tagesreise komplett ausreichend ist.

Ładnie i na pewno zasługuje na listę UNESCO. W tych skałach na morzu znajdują się wewnątrz piękne jaskinie, lub te ostance skalne są puste w środku. Na minus ogromne zanieczyszczenie powietrza i bardzo duży ruch turystyczny. Mieszkańcy są bardzo mili i pomocni.

Một nơi tuyệt vời. Phong cảnh ngoạn mục, biển lặng, và rất đông người trên nhiều thuyền. Nơi đây rất phát triển và hoàn toàn tập trung vào du lịch. Đó là điều làm giảm giá trị của nó, nhưng cuối cùng, tất cả chúng tôi đều là một …
Thật sự hùng vĩ!  Tôi rất thích hang động đầy bất ngờ này! Điểm trừ duy nhất là có quá nhiều khách …
Vẻ đẹp thiên nhiên ngoạn mục của Việt Nam, một Di sản Thế giới. Tiếc là nơi đây quá đông khách du lịch. Những tảng đá vẫn giữ được vẻ đẹp tuyệt vời.

Ha Long Bay is without a doubt a beautiful feature of different isles and it is quite breathtaking. Unfortunately the area is overly exploited with boats and tourism. Nevertheless it was a stunning and once in a life time experience.

昼食は美味しかった。 売り子が売りにくる5ドルのシャコ海老も美味しい、食べ終わってテーブルクロスを回収した途端にお土産販売が始まる、かなり安くしてくれるが上手に交渉が必要コレも楽しまないとイライラしちゃうので値切る事を楽しんでね、船で飲みすぎると鍾乳洞の階段がしんどいのでほどほどにね〜 ♪(´ε｀ )ハノイからはバスで往復8時間（休憩あり）💦 Lunch was delicious. Souvenirs are on sale The $ 5 shrimp prawns are also delicious, souvenir sales will start as soon as we finish eating and collecting the tablecloth, but it will be quite cheap but negotiations are necessary. If you do not enjoy it, it will get irritated Enjoy having a price cut, if you drink too much on a ship the staircase of the limestone cave is too tired so moderately ~ From Hanoi 8 hours back and forth by bus

Halong Bay has amazing views and beautiful water and islands. It has crystal clear water when you go up north (only select cruise liners have permission to go up there). In central and southern Halong Bay it is crowded and the waters are polluted. The north is beautiful and there aren’t that many boats up there. There were amazing views and it was spectacular and out of this world when we got there. We could also climb onto selected islands and canoe around islands as well.
World class bay. Nice cave, sunset at the beach. Good to cruising it.
the most gorgeous bay in the world.1 day tour about 6 hour to visit booked via tourist Company

Great cruise and experience around here, spent 2 days and 1 night on a cruise and I think this was perfect. All cruises seem of high standard and really want guests to enjoy themselves. There is still a pollution problem in the bay but I think this is being worked on and slowly improving.

The long ride from Hanoi is worthy it. You will arrive to a very beautiful place, away from the busy streets of Hanoi. In the cruise you will enjoy authentic Vietnamese cuisine, a great way to enjoy the stunning view of Halong Bay. A stopover in a cave is also an awesome experience. The cave is beautiful and clean. You just have to take a short trek. There are also other activities like kayaking.

We visited during June, probably the hottest and most humid period of the year. It’s a unique experience cruising through the bay. It’s pretty crowded with cruise ship and certain location seems to be contaminated by tourism (guilty)  The view was magnificent, it’s truly one of a kind.
Magic place but over crowded. The sea water dirty and at some places full of oil. The government has to do something quickly or else this paradise will die...

Did the 5 start cruise to see the bay and its a natural wonder. However there are several bays and the main hay long bay is very crowded with boats and ships all over the place.  It can only be compared to the M25 on a Friday evening.  Also did swimming and kayaking.  Its a big shame that I could see plastic waste all over the place and stuck between the rocks etc.   It's time we cut plastic  out and started to look after our  waters.
45 USD for one day trip for Halong bay. 2.5 hours drive from Hanoi. You can do kayaking and visit t-top island. Wonderful view of Halong bay from T-top island. You also see surprising caves.
One of the best experiences! A must visit for anybody traveling to this beautiful country ! One must definitely prefer the night stay over the day trip.

ハノイからkkdayのツアーを利用して出かけてきました。バスで片道3時間。途中トイレ休憩1回あり。 到着してお昼ごろだったので、まずは船内でランチ。 どれもおいしくて、しかもボリューミー。 おいしいランチとベトナムビールを飲みながらハロン湾の風景を窓から楽しみました。 その後はバンブーボートで湾をめぐりました。 ココナッツジュースもおいしかったですが、マンゴーがめちゃめちゃおいしかったです！！ バンブーボートのお兄さんには降りるときひとり20kずつチップを渡しました。

An absolute dream destination that's been on my bucket list for years. I was a little saddened to see - though such is the way with the world these days - just quite how busy and touristy it is. Having seen ha long bay on TV, i expected more tranquility and exclusivity from such a far flung place, but perhaps that was a little naive. Regardless, this was still a very fond memory i absolutely recommend - totally worth the 4 hour drive we had to do!
Trước đây tôi đã nghe rất nhiều điều tuyệt vời về Vịnh Hạ Long, vì vậy tôi vô cùng tò mò khi được đến đây bằng tàu du lịch. …
Cảnh quan đẹp, tuy nhiên hơi đông khách du lịch như hầu hết các địa điểm nổi tiếng khác. Thích hợp cho chuyến đi của các cặp đôi hoặc nhóm bạn, nhưng không thực sự phù hợp cho gia đình có trẻ nhỏ.

Một trải nghiệm không thể bỏ qua nếu bạn đang du lịch đến Việt Nam! Bạn có nhiều lựa chọn: một chuyến đi trong ngày quanh vịnh hoặc một chuyến du ngoạn qua đêm hoặc dài hơn để khám phá toàn bộ vịnh. …
อากาศ บรรยากาศดีสุดๆ (หักดาวไปหนึ่งเพราะตกกลางคืนหาของกินยากมาก) แต่ล่องเรือลมดีมาก เหมือนอยู่ในห้องแอร์24องศา ใครมาเวียดนามเหนือควรจัดทัวร์มาเที่ยวแบบไปกลับนะคะ แนะนำๆ

Ha Long Bay, located in the Gulf of Tonkin, within Quang Ninh Province, in the northeast of Vietnam, is 165 km from the capital of Hà Nội  The scenary stretches across 1553 Sq. Km. & contains 1969 megalithic islets.  I was there with my family, we had taken a one day trip from Hanoi to Ha Long Bay, but you have many more options.  Highly recommend to spend at least one day in this magical place.

日本人が少ない観光エリア、日本語は殆ど通じない。英語が話せる現地のスタッフがいて不自由はない。食事は中国とタイに挟まれている湾という事もありどこなくミックスした食に感じる。全般的にベトナムは香草が多いので苦手でなければ、日本人の味覚には合うと思う。現地でのアクティビティは船で巡る洞窟やカヌーなど幾つかある。どちらかと言えば何もしないでゆっくり過ごしした方がおススメ。クルーズ一泊だと比較的イベントが多くあっという間に終わる印象。
Super schöner Ort aber extrem voll mit Touristen und mit Booten 😅 …
ランチ付きの半日ツアーに参加。空は霞んでいてきれいな景色でした。  I took a half day trip tour with lunch on the ship. Sky is hazy with beautiful view.
세계7대 비경중하나인 하롱베이로 지구의 융기작용으로 변한지형으로 배를타고 돌아다니면 절경이다. 부모님들이 좋아하시는것같다.

Hicimos el crucero de una noche por esta bahia, es un espectáculo para la vista 😍 además pudimos disfutar de una actividad en kayak por la bahia, si hay algo que no me gustó... Fue la gran masificación de cruceros que se llegan a juntar, por la noche llegué a contar mas de 20, pero bueno el turismo es inevitable, y lamentablemente hay mucha basura, esta un poco descuidada. Tambien hay opcion mas económica de coger un barco de un dia y pasear por la bahia y hacer kayak.

二回目のハノイ、前回行かなかったハロン湾クルーズを体験してきました。昨年高速道路が開通したとの事ですが、高速を使っているツアーは数少ないようです。 でも少々価格高めでも其方を選んだ方が楽だと思います。行きはトイレ休憩挟んで4時間弱、帰りは2時間半くらいでハノイに到着します。 松島より沢山の島々があり、犬やニワトリに見立てた岩はガイドさんが漏れなく教えてくれます。船内でのシーフードのランチも量がたっぷりで美味しかったです。 ただ次々と土産物を繰り出してくるのには閉口しますが。

Beautiful scenery! We did a one day tour through Halong Bay. The boats are safe and comfortable. The food on the boat was delicious and the kayaking was also fun. The best part was definitely the cave. I've never seen a cave this big! The whole place is full of tourists so be aware of that.
Es war sehr schön aber es ist völlig von Touristen überlaufen. Wirklich jede Attraktion ist voll mit Touristen. Hat sich trotzdem gelohnt.
2018年訪問。ベトナム世界遺産のハロン湾。中国の桂林と少し雰囲気が似ていますが、洞窟内は自然光でなくてライトアップされているのがお国柄もあるのかなと思いました。

An sich traumhaft und unglaublich und unbeschreiblich schön. Doch es wird dabei nicht lange bleiben. Ein hoher Grad des Tourismus macht die Natur kaputt. Im Wasser findet man sehr viel Müll und Schmutz. Traurig
Beautiful place - I went while Vietnam was experiencing poor weather. I’m sure it looks even more fantastic when it’s sunnier
ずいぶん前になりますが、 ハロン湾クルージング　洞窟観光に 来ました。　ランチは船内で食べ 途中　海上生活している人が小さな船で 貝、エビ、カニを売りに来て それを買って　船内で焼いてもらって 食べていた人もいました。  洞窟は色々な色のライトが 照らされて神秘的でしたが湿度が高く 暑かったです。
ハロン湾へは個人でも行くことができますが、都市部からは離れているので、ツアーに参加するのが便利です。 ランチと洞窟にも行くツアーに参加しました。  日本でいうと、松島のようなイメージでしょうか。奇岩はどれも異なる形で、犬やゴリラのような島もありました。  ツアー含まれているランチは、魚介がメイン。青菜炒め、アサリのワイン炒め、ガーリックシュリンプなどが並びます。

Halong Bay is a must-see when visiting Vietnam, but don't expect crystal-clear water or exciting activities. The cruises all offer very similar itineraries, typically including a cave hike, short swim, cooking class, Tai chi class, and canoeing.  Overall, we enjoyed the experience of being on the cruise and seeing the bay, but I would recommend a day cruise or one-night cruise only. Longer cruises may be too boring.
we took a day cruise to this place , it was very hot , one should avoid going in June to October , but otherwise its very nice place , good view and about 2000 islands !!
It is still a beautiful place to visit, but overcrowded, dirty water and expensive. The new buildings they prepare will distroy what once was an amazing place to visit.

Username: やっちゃん（ヤッチャン）
Comment: 日本に帰る前に 一泊ハロン湾クルーズに 大きな鍾乳洞や魚やゴリラに似た奇岩など 見所満載 船上の食事も美味しく 朝は太極拳の教室有り 二日間充分に楽しめました
ฮาลอง เบย์ ถือที่ท่องเที่ยวสำคัญที่ไม่ควรพลาดเมื่อมีไปเที่ยวที่เวียดนาม เป็นแหล่งท่องเที่ยวที่สำคัญ สวยงาม วิว บรรยากาศดี มีกิจกรรมเยอะ พายเรือ เป็นต้น
這次參加北約旅遊 來到下龍灣景色還算不錯.我們搭公主輪中午還在裡面用自助餐 .菜色還可以

Un posto molto bello da esplorare, tra case galleggianti, velieri, barchette e villaggi di pescatori, per non parlare di questi centinai di isolette sparse per la baia tutte da esplorare, con spiaggette e caverne.

Due to time constraint, we did a day trip from Hanoi and enough to catch a glimpse of the picturesque Halong Bay + sunset view. Of course, as recommended by most travellers, an overnight cruise can be amazing too, and not be in any hurry.  A day trip would normally cost around USD 50 from a decent and reputable tour operator, with professional guide. You may get cheaper one but be sure to check and compare what is included in the package before booking.
Beautiful views and nice activities. A bit too touristy for my liking, immense amounts of boats and people.
Belle expérience dans un lieu incroyable, de belles rencontres et une nuit parfaite sur le bateau.  Point négatif : eau fortement polluée, trop de bateau, énormément de touristes, prix assez élevé.

Un viaje que lo tienes que hacer una vez en la vida, poder disfrutar de las vistas de lo que esconden las islas del continente asiático. Si tienes la ocasión de poder hacer una travesía con barco y contemplar la tranquila bahía
Beautiful chain of islands, the only problem is the very high volume of tourists and cruise ships.

There's no place quite like Ha Long Bay to make you feel so small among the towering mountain forests and incredibly mysterious network of caves. Sure to make anyone either appreciate the world a bit more or enough to get sea sick via the boat ride.

가능하다면 4성급 이상 대형 크루즈를 타고 즐길 것 강추합니다. 호텔급의 식사와 공연 그리고 서비스를 받을 수 있습니다. 밤엔 밤하늘의 수많은 별과 함께.. 시원한 바람. 배에서의 수면. 둥실대는 풍경. 아침에는 작은 섬의 정상을 구경하고, 그 아래에 위치한 해안서의 해수욕은 큰 선물입니다. 일정이 끝나면 너무도 아쉬워.. 맘이 아파요. 하루에 다녀 올 그런 곳이 아닙니다. 패키지로 점만 찍고 오는 우를 범하지 마세요. 충분히 시간 투자할 가치가 있는 곳 입니다.
Amazing place very scenic and serene the cruise was awsome . The food could have been better. But awsome
就坐船，三座小島，嗯，好山、好水@@

Beautiful landscape, a must to visit in Vietnam. Long distance from Hanoi, around 3hrs by bus. Get a better deal, book your tour at any of the tour agency near the walking street. VIP day tour is around US$60. Value for money: $$$$•
Paysage magnifique. Mais faites la baie de lan ha aussi. Taliya Cruise une très bonne compagnie.
사람마다 보는 시각과 느끼는 감성이 틀리니 강추다 할수는 없지만 자연경치 즐기는거 좋아하는 사람들은 다 좋아할꺼같다. 탁트인 바다에 오랜 세월 자연에 의해 만들어진 크고 작은 바위로 만들어진 섬들, 동굴 들이 많아  감동적이다~

Die Halong Bucht ist wunderschön und definitiv zu empfehlen! Leider sieht man nur oft die Umweltverschmutzung, vor allem in den kleinen Buchten zu Beginn. Es sind sehr viele Touristen unterwegs und super viele Kreuzfahrtschiffe, nur anders kommt man hier leider nicht hin
It was great weather when we went but the local authority have not done their best to clean up the environment! They should be aware of it!
Great cruise and amazing activities and we had wonderful time to join kayaking time, bamboo boat trip. Titop island tour and sung sot cave tour were beautiful. Will be back next summer

The cruise gent we booked Halong Bay Cruise Hunters on Tripadvisor was great. Everything was well planned and the most exciting activity was kayaking Cave tour and Ti top island tour we enjoyed so amazing..

La baie d’Halong incarne l'emblème du voyage au Vietnam. Ce site prestigieux figure en tête des destinations incontournables du pays en forme de S. Une fois dans sa vie il faut y aller pour voir la beauté des paysages et prendre un bateau croisière pour se perdre dans les méandres.

Nel periodo giusto è un luogo incantevole e imperdibile. In alta stagione navigano circa 250/270 navi da crociera, in bassa circa 50/70... fate i vostri conti.Rimane senza dubbio una delle bellezze naturali del nostro pianeta

We enjoyed a 2-day, 1-night cruise aboard Scarlet Pearl Cruises—what a wonderful experience! Each meal featured an abundance of courses, making it a true paradise for seafood lovers. J’ai été profondément impressionnée par la baie d’Ha Long, c'est tres formidable un site naturel d’une beauté exceptionnelle. Booked a cruise from HALONG BAY CRUISE tripadvisor and we did enjoyed our great time for kayaking. Cave and island tour. People were friendly and  helped me and my wife lots of things during the trip.

Our day trip included setting sail in the morning, when it was still a bit chilly and overcast (in December 2024). We visited the cave complex at Tuan Chau, which none of us knew about beforehand, so that was an unexpected highlight. Otherwise, just sailing past the beautiful islands and taking in the scenery is what it's all about.

We visited Hạ Long Bay as part of a cruise stop on Holland America’s Westerdam, cruising into and out of the bay rather than doing a separate Hạ Long Bay boat or day cruise. Even without a traditional tour among the limestone karsts, the sail-in itself was a highlight, with calm waters and scenic views as the ship approached port.After docking, we took the cruise shuttle into town, which dropped us near Vincom Plaza Hạ Long. From there, it was easy to explore on foot. We visited Starbucks Vincom Hạ Long (perfect for festive Christmas photos), wandered through the nearby parks and waterfront areas, including Cầu đi bộ, Công viên Lán Bè Skywalk, and Công viên Hoa Hạ Long, and stopped at WinMart for a look at local groceries.This felt like a relaxed, everyday view of Hạ Long city life, rather than a sightseeing-heavy experience. It was flat, walkable, and easy to do at your own pace, making it a good option for families, anyone wanting a low-effort port day, or those who have already done a traditional Hạ Long Bay cruise in the past.While you won’t see the famous limestone formations up close without a dedicated tour, this is still a pleasant and stress-free way to experience Hạ Long as a cruise port, with cafes, parks, shopping, and waterfront views all close together.

Aiyoh, Ha Long Bay is seriously beautiful, power! When you see those huge limestone islands sticking out from the water, you know why it's a UNESCO site—the scenery is next level, talaga. Must go, lah! Okay, okay, it’s super crowded, especially at the pier and when you enter the caves, but you have to accept it, mah. All the famous places in the world are like that, right? But the best part is getting a luxury cruise ship. The rooms are so nice and comfortable, the food is top-notch, and they take you to quieter spots sometimes. It really makes the trip feel very exclusive and syok (enjoyable). If you invest in the cruise, the Ha Long experience is best!

A visit to the bay is an absolute must during your trip to Vietnam—it’s nothing short of magical! I highly recommend spending at least one unforgettable night cruising its emerald waters, surrounded by towering limestone karsts and serene beauty that will stay with you long after you leave.

My trip to Ha Long Bay was genuinely enjoyable and felt very real from start to finish. Being on the cruise and slowly moving through the limestone islands was the kind of experience that stays with you long after the trip ends.The bay looks exactly the way people describe it. The air feels clean, the water is calm, and the islands rise up around you in a way that makes the whole place feel peaceful. Nothing felt overly touristy or forced. It was just easy to relax and take everything in.Some of my favorite moments were having meals on the deck, watching the scenery change as the boat moved, and waking up early to see the morning light over the water. Those small, simple moments were what made the trip special.If you’re coming to Vietnam, I think Ha Long Bay is worth the time. It’s the kind of place you understand best when you’re actually there, just sitting on the boat and looking out at the view

We’ve spent two nights in Haling Bay (Bua Chay Ward) as part of a cruise. Lots of trips are advertised from the cruise to Hanoi and of course out into Halong Bay on all the charter boats. But there is very little promoted for the town of Haling itself. It has some great spots to visit - a major museum, a flower garden, a really local market (not many LV copies!). And it has Sun world which is a quite thoughtfully designed play park for kids and adults. It has a cable car, spanning two islands, a big wheel, a toboggan ride, beautiful gardens. It is magnificent. But there’s no integrated literature or promotion of the town for new tourists. The most disappointing thing for myself and quite a few other cruisers hoping to visit the town was the lack of reliability of the Hop On Hop Off bus.  It either didn’t come on time to the limited no of stops there were or it came overcrowded and then we couldn’t get on it. Apart from the terrible Hop On Hop Off bus, the town has so much potential to realise.
3 jours 2 nuits c'est juste parfait. Un endroit magnifique. Seul bémol tous ces navires et cette pollution. Un réajustement touristique et des bateaux plus propres seraient nécessaires.

Very nice cruise food serve as buffet.Our first stop was on a beach where we just stayed not more than couple hours.Next the bamboo boat ride where you go under the water cave and its very strange feeling when you stop in the middle of those enormous rock hills.For the last one is going to massive caves but before you get in its massive hike to go there but surely its rewarding when you reach the cave.Finish the whole trip sun down already.By the way pick up point for the tour is on the Opera house in the city of Hanoi via a coach.Book it on online there's plenty of website offers the trip.

Very interesting and relaxing experience in cruises towards the end of our Vietnam trip. Once we settled down in cruises, We had very finest buffet arrangement for lunch with healthy and unique food options to enjoy in waters. It took 2 to 3 hours to travel from Hanoi city to reach here. This place is very beautiful with scenic view of mountains at distance. After our lunch, we had activities to get down and enjoy. First thing is to climb 400 steps up in little bit rough & tough pathway to the maximum point which is a great achievement to have in the beautiful landscape. We were also allowed to relax by swimming in the beach. Post this, cruise took us to Sung Sot cave point which is so nice to explore by walk and inside cave was very awesome with excellent view. We were allowed to capture picture and frame it and take it as a memory. Inside the cave experience is something very unique and so memorable. Then, finally cruise took us for boating. We had multiple options to sail in waters like coconut boating with group of people or speed boating or Kayaking. HaLong Bay is truly a must-see destination in Vietnam. Recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, the scenery is breathtaking — thousands of limestone cliffs rising dramatically from calm, emerald-green waters. Cruising through this natural wonder feels like gliding through a dream.We joined a tour with Diamond Cruises. While the service wasn’t the best, it didn’t take away from the beauty and magic of the place. One of the highlights of our trip was visiting Titop Island — climbing to the top rewarded us with a spectacular panoramic view of the bay. The sight from above, with boats scattered across the emerald water and limestone peaks all around, was absolutely worth the climb.The peaceful atmosphere, hidden caves, and stunning sunsets made the entire experience truly unforgettable.

We recently visited the Ha Long Bay both by seaplane and cruise. I will say that this is an unbelievably beautiful place with lush landscapes and stunning visuals. If you are in Viet Nam, this is a don’t miss adventure.

Halong Bay is simply magical, a world-class destination that is perfect for a romantic getaway.​The emerald waters and thousands of towering limestone karsts create a breathtaking, almost ethereal backdrop for couples looking for an unforgettable experience. The sheer scale and raw nature of the bay, a UNESCO World Heritage site, will leave you speechless—it truly feels like stepping into a painting.​But it’s not just about the view; there are countless activities to enjoy together. Cruising on a traditional junk boat is an adventure in itself, especially waking up to the sunrise over the bay. You can spend your days kayaking through hidden lagoons, exploring stunning caves like Sung Sot (Surprise Cave), and trekking to the top of Titop Island for spectacular panoramic views. For a deeper dive into culture, you can visit floating fishing villages and see a way of life that has existed for centuries.​Whether you're relaxing on the sundeck with a cocktail, sharing a delicious Vietnamese meal on board, or simply watching the sunset paint the cliffs in gold, Halong Bay offers endless moments of intimacy and discovery. It's a journey filled with beauty, adventure, and a rich blend of nature and culture that will bring you closer. Highly recommended for any couple seeking a memorable escape.

Đó là kì nghỉ hè thú vị khi tôi đến Hạ Long.  Đảo Tuần Châu như 1 hòn ngọc nhỏ có bãi cát trắng thơ mộng. We spent the day on a cruise kayaking and swimming in the emerald waters. The limestone karsts are even more stunning in person. Sharing this natural wonder with our best mates made it an epic, memory-filled day. 100% worth it.

I guided a one-day tour to Ha Long Bay from Hanoi and it was absolutely worth it. The drive was comfortable, and once we reached the bay, the scenery was stunning – thousands of limestone islands rising from emerald green waters. Lunch on board was delicious with fresh seafood.It’s a long day, but very well-organized by Vietnam Xplorer and a great option if you don’t have time for an overnight cruise. Highly recommend for first-time visitors to Vietnam!
We went on a quite a luxuries boat to do a 2-day trip to Ha Long bay. We had perfect, calm weather and it is very very nice to see. One picture to share which is the raising of the sun at 6 AM.

Visiting Ha Long Bay is truly magical. We booked a cruise on the Amethyst cruise through our Travel Agent. It was a very good choice and we were extremely happy with the services. Also, Ha Long Bay is a journey not to be missed. Thousands of mountain peaks jutting out of the ocean and a biodiversity is truly magical. We also visited the caves in Ha Long bay and were informed that they were formed between 400-500 million years ago.

The advantage of visiting during this time is the vibrant, lush greenery that flourishes after the rainy season. Additionally, there tends to be fewer international tourists compared to the peak season, making it an ideal time to visit. To fully enjoy the beauty of Ha Long Bay, taking a cruise is highly recommended, although it is important to stay updated on weather forecasts in case of any potential storm cancellations.
Bucket list! We only had a day, but it was well worth the schlep from Hanoi. Stay longer if you can, but even a day trip is memorable.

Một trong những điểm đến thiên nhiên đẹp nhất mình từng tham quan. Cảnh quan vịnh tuyệt đẹp, nước xanh, núi đá vôi dựng đứng tạo nên khung cảnh hùng vĩ . Đi tàu tham quan các hang động, làng chài rất thú vị

Our overnight cruise in Halong Bay was the romantic highlight of our Vietnam trip. Waking up surrounded by the limestone karsts was simply breathtaking. We loved kayaking together through the quiet lagoons. While the boat was a bit crowded, the stunning, otherworldly scenery made it all worth it.

Fiz o passeio em que se dorme uma noite na baía. Foi muito bom mas fiquei com a sensação que só vale a pena se for em navio de bom nível, já que a grande parte do tempo a gente fica embarcado. O passeio tem somente 3 paradas de 1:30 hora cada. Na primeira, existe a opção de ficar em uma praia ou subir em um mirante com vista para a baía. Só dá tempo para uma dessas opções. Fiz o passeio da montanha porque a praia é lotada (todos os barcos param ali) e me pareceu não valer a pena. A segunda parada é a mais bonita porque se visita uma parte da baía que só tem acesso através de uma fenda na rocha e a entrada só e permitida com barco a remo (opção de caiaque paga a parte).  A terceira parada é em uma caverna, no segundo dia. Bonita e vale a pena. No navio vai ser fornecido almoço, jantar e café da manhã, nessa ordem. Então tem que ser um navio com bom padrão para o atendimento ser satisfatório. Fiz no navio Paradise Elegance e recomendo. Quarto e serviço de bordo de primeira. No local em que os barcos pernoitam é possível ver embarcações de vários níveis. Algumas delas me pareceram precárias.
Lovely city with so beautiful surroundings.  This is my 4th visit to Halong bay and I still amaze with city and the islands around the bay

It was declared UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1994. It is also home to many fishermen who live here since years. We were about 25 of us and we had our own boat for this tour. The boat journey was very enjoyable watching many islands and mother nature. All the boats drop the passengers below limestone mountain where one has to climb steep steps to see the geological wonder in caves. Few of us did not attempt due to our mobility issue. This point was very crowded and touristy. Those who went up, liked it immensely.

Es un lugar imperdible si vas a Vietnam, hay muchos tours que salen de Hanoi y te recogen del hotel. No te olvides de llevar una casaca porque hay bastante viento.

來到《下龍灣》玩。最主要既活動，當然是出海啦。在下龍灣的第二朝早，大伙兒在酒店吃好早餐之後，就來到 《Cảng tàu khách Quốc tế Hạ Long》 (Halong International Cruise Port) 了。下龍灣的國際港口，也是國際郵輪的停泊點啊。而我們的下龍灣海上遊，也是從這一個碼頭出發。單是碼頭的內部，已經可以說是一個景點。在等導游安排小船時，我們已經不停在古色古香的門口，同一個完全不是越南風格的候船大堂拍照。然後，當我們拿了票，入到碼頭的內部，原來是一個小小形的商場啊。船準備好了。我們大伙兒就踏出碼頭的廣場。果然，是一個國際級的碼頭。一邊是給郵輪停泊，一邊是給進入下龍灣的小船停泊。導遊說，若是有郵輪泊岸，遊客不需要步出碼頭就可以轉小船了。上船了。我們一團人的小船，絕不細小。內室是四人一檯的餐座。開船後，想乘風的，就可以上去船頂的甲板。怕風大把人及頭髮都吹散的，就可以坐在船艙內，隔著窗戶，欣賞下龍灣的奇人奇石。然而，船慢慢地駛入那些奇山奇石之中。我雖然未去陽朔，但我覺得這兒的確是異工同曲啊。這兒，絕對有一個令人覺得身處異世界的感覺。在此，我同我太太，也許拍了超過100張的照片。而且我可以說，船隻每前進30秒，你我所看到的，都是不一樣的景色。回程，也是我們享用午餐的時候了。船家就在回程的時間，為我們一團人，泡製一頓精美的下龍灣海鮮午餐。首先，他們要照顧我的素食太太。而她的第一道菜，就是一個香口的煎蛋角。之後，也有一個炸玉米粒，炸豆腐同一個紅白蘿蔔製成的素湯。而我們的海鮮餐，就魚蝦蟹盡有。有墨魚餅，有簡單的蒸蟹、蒸蜆、蒸蝦。而最受大家歡迎的，就是香口的炸魷魚。雞肉串也是我兩個小朋友愛吃的東西。而我，就也那兩片煎魚 KO 掉。午餐完了，我們亦慢慢地回到下龍灣的碼頭了。下龍灣的海港，不只是為遊客而設。同時，這兒也是越南北部的一個重要貨運港。在我們回程時，就遇到一部巨形的連油輪出港。這一個《下龍灣》海上遊，絕對是一個精彩及會令人留下深刻印象的旅程。來越南北部玩，無論是自由行或是跟團來。都是值得體驗的一個行程。如在河內自由行的話，在酒店或在舊城區找 local tour 來一遊，也是推介的。

We spent almost three days sailing through Halong Bay as part of a tour group.  Lots of activities undertaken including checking out caves, a floating village, and pearl place.The hero, though, was the majestic islands rising into the sky in a variety of shapes and sizes.  I can see why this is a UNESCO World Heritage site.  There were lots of tourist boats around but it was actually very peaceful.  We had days when it was foggy and misty and mystical and a fa6 when the sun shone and gave a different slant on the islands.Photo ops galore.  Point and click and you've got a great photo.

Ha Long Bay is why you come to Hanoi.  As part of our Viking Mekong river cruise, we added on an extension so that we could travel to Ha Long Bay - we spent a glorious afternoon on the Bay in December 2024.  Truly lucky to have a sunny day with temperatures in the '70's.  Guide told us often it is cloudy, overcast or rainy.  What was also nice was that the Bay was not crowded, there were other boats in the Bay but it was very calming and relaxing.
還暦過ぎの一人旅で英語力も乏しいので、ちょっと割高ですがkkdayの日本語ガイド付きの日帰りツアーにしました。船上からは切り立った奇岩の島々が無数に眺望できました。また、手漕ぎボートでの遊覧、鍾乳洞の散策など貴重な体験ができました。ハロン湾は波がなく揺れも少なかったので、船酔いすることもなく楽しく観光することができました。

We arrived on the Celebrity Solstice and were staying overnight in Hanoi.Many of the advertised cruises did not fit what we wanted and so decided to go into the Cruise Port building.At Desk 5 we spoke to the staff and we agreed that we could hire a boat the following afternoon at 1400 hrs for 4 hours or shorter if we wanted so this gave us the flexibility to cut the cruise short It worked out at $180 for the boat plus a ticket for entry to the caves we had to buy..which was one of those that had gone out in the morning. We had 6 on the boat and did the cruise around the bay stopping off at the caves..Worth considering if you are doing Hanoi overnight and then want to do a cruise in the afternoon

许多同学造访下龙湾时，常选择从河内参加一日游行程，但这类行程往往只能走马观花地打卡"海上桂林"，行程仓促难以深入体验。若想真正领略下龙湾的神韵，建议在此留宿一夜，方能完整感受其二十四时辰的流转。✅晨起后推荐前往下龙集市（Halong Market），最能体味本土风情与市井气息的场所莫过于传统集市，这里汇集着各色热带鲜果、时令菜蔬、馥郁花束与地道小吃。周边分布着多家汤粉老店，逛罢集市后，择一家本地食客聚集的粉店享用早餐，往往能收获惊喜。✅毗邻集市的诗歌山（Poem Mountain）是俯瞰海湾全景的绝佳观景台。导航至86 Hàng Nồi时会途经一座古寺，可依据兴趣选择是否入内参访。到达导航位置以后蓝色墙壁的就是有上山通道的人家，塞给老板5w越南盾/人即可上山。✅自诗歌山信步而下，便可前往下龙湾国际邮轮码头安排出海行程。各类定时发船的套餐可供选择，若同行人数较多更可包船定制，自由度更高。行程中必访的两处溶洞略显平淡，但是必须包含在项目内。✅返航后莫急于离开候船大厅，待多数游客散去之时，这座充满法式风情的建筑，恰是拍摄氛围感大片的绝佳取景地。✅不远处的太阳世界主题乐园（Sun World），可搭乘全球规模最大的双层跨海缆车饱览落日熔金与群岛剪影，成人票价36万越南盾，儿童26万越南盾。山顶乐园内的过山车与蜡像馆可选择性体验，摩天轮因台风侵袭损坏尚未恢复运营。
