Nobody likes to be scammed, so 1 star. Do something good and improve the experience guys. Better go to Trang Am - they are much better organized and there is no pressure to spend anything more than the ticket price.

Initially this was a very cool experience, stunning views and  exploring caves. However, like so many other reviews, and also the experience of one of the families in our tour group, it was ruined by rude and aggressive boat driver. Firstly, he asked my mum for her bottle of water??? And she gave it to him out of politeness. Then half way in, he started asking to turn back. It was a very hot day and everyone was feeling it so in the end we agreed because admittedly, I was starting to burn and we felt bad for him.  Although I did have a desire to see another cave. On our way back, he pulls us into to this bit, and starts pressuring us to spend £5 on "homemade" embroidery. My mum only had 80k on her but he didn't want that and insisted on 150k . We felt vulnerable at that moment as we were still far from land. Then as we were about to Dock he stopped and starting demanding a tip, we gave him 40k (considering that he only took us half way and drank our water) he said this wasn't enough and wanted 50k. I then stood up and tried to get off the boat, the boat master shouted at him and us for me to sit down. Overall, as two women we felt very vulnerable in both these situations. The whole thing left a very sour taste in our mouths.
自然は素晴らしいですが、お土産物、写真の押し売りと、チップの要求がひどい。チップが少ないと喚き始めてうんざり。お勧めしません

Very beatifull but they will try to sell you paintings in the middle of the visit and if you say no they get mad. Also, they demand you a tip and if it’s not okay with them, they will demand you badly, before you can get out of the boat. So either way you will spend more money than you thought.

They'll scam you. So be very careful. They'll ask for tips, they'll ask you to buy photos, they'll ask you to buy some artcraft. And i mean not buy but  force you to buy. They'll stop paddeling at the middle of the river and force you to buy. If u dont buy, they wont go
A fuir, un vrai parc d'attraction..les bateaux sont a touche touche .....heureusement notre rameuse était sympathique a l'inverse de l'accueil au Guichet ! !!!

I don’t know how to explain this 1-star review. The view is amazing, the hard work of the workers is admirable, but the worker on the boat was so aggressive, demanding a tip and even specifying an amount. I felt totally scammed and sad.
Pan który kierował łódką wyłudził od nas pieniądze, byliśmy pewni że płacimy za dodatkową atrakcję, a okazało się, że jest to chamsko wymuszony napiwek, słabe

After reading all the reviews on aggressive boat operators we opted for Trang An instead where we gladly showered our boat operator with drinks, food and tips as she didn’t ask for anything.  Such a wholesome experience, would rather that than risk what others have experienced with Tam Coc. I took the opportunity to row with her most of the trip to get a bit of a workout—even got a few blisters! Worth it.
要搭船的千萬不要理會划船員用各種不同藉口額外要你給的小費，因為沒必要，一艘小船收費150000越盾已是他們的收入。你額外給的少還可能被罵，被當白癡，何況根本沒必要給。越南人搭，他們就不會要，外國人就厚臉的硬開口。
Turisty tady berou jako chodící peněženky. Za špatné služby chtějí z lidí vytáhnout co nejvíc peněz

La balade vaut 5 étoiles mais le harcèlement pour nous vendre des choses sur le parcours et l insistance de la rameuse pour les tips gâche l'expérience. Je rejoins la plupart des commentaires et j'espère que faire baisser la note permettra de mettre fin à ces pratiques.
Absolute Frechheit… aufdringlich Trinkgeld verlangen fast schon zwingen. Rechnet damit das ihr alle unterwegs zurück noch Schwarzarbeit unterstützt.  Nie wieder!
SCAM! We were 3 boats and got scammed! RUDE rowers who REFUSED TO RETURN unless you PAID EXTRA! THE WORST EXPERIENCE IN VIETNAM!  NOT WORTH IT! (Nature is also less beautiful then Lang An)

超级垃圾的地方，不要去。这里的工作人员，从上到下各种欺骗和坑人，非常恶心。 今天我们去买票。我们2个人，说要3-4个人才能够一座船，叫我们等15分钟，不等的话2个人60万，我们选60万然后给钱售票员给了我们一张单据，我们以为就是票然后去河边。 到河边检票的说这是发票不是门票，不给我们进去，我们说卖票的就是给我们这一张发票，什么门票我们没见到，他们让我们回去处理，我们说我们赶时间，让他们电话问卖票那边，等了蛮久说卖票的说票给我们了，不是他们的问题，态度很凶。我们和他们吵了一下回去买票的地方，我们说也不想扯给不给我们票的问题，如果没有打票出来就帮我们打出来，如果说弄丢了他们检查一下系统如果显示还没有用就再打给我们，他们销售的敷衍说在处理，磨磨蹭蹭过了半小时，和我们说不是他们的错，我说现在需要的是处理情况，而不是谁的责任，如果票没有用就重新打给我们，卖票的说现在确定票还没有用，能不能打要问，问了一下说 ，我们的票被别人捡到转卖给别人了，不能打（转卖给别人没有用她怎么知道？）。很气愤，我们决定不玩回去了，要赶飞机。 坐摆渡电车回河边的时候时候那司机（免费的，我们第一次去河边的时候就是他带的）一脸讥笑的故意问我们买到票没有，问了两次，那架势貌似好像不买就不给我们坐车，我直接举发票给他看，他和旁边的一个工作人员都一脸嘲笑和轻蔑的表情，到了河边我们离开的时候，还有一个人跑过来喊我们叫我们不要走他有票只需要给一点钱就能进去玩，我们不理他就走了。 所以去买票一定记得看清楚发票和门票有没有给齐全，否则他们卖票的不给票转手就卖给外面的倒票的，他们工作人员和景区人员都上下勾结，欺骗和勒索游客。

de mensen behandelden ons als oud vuil, we konden niet in 1 boot met onze 2 kleine kinderen, Vietnamese mogen met 5 in 1 boot, blanken met 2 zonder veilig vestje voor kinderen, alleen voor volwassene en zonder te informeren toen we de tickets kochten, nu we dit horen  bij de boten. We hebben 1,5 gewacht terwijl we boos aangekeken werden door de medewerkers voordat we na 1,5uur ons geld terug kregen. Er was 1 medewerker die heel hard haar best deed ons te helpen  maar geen bevoegdheid had om ons geld terug te geven.
Nothing special to see and they will strand you in the water until you tip them minimum 100,000. Dangerous game to play considering they likely cant swim if they piss the wrong person off.

Très déçu. Nous ne voulions faire que le tour en bateau (pas le tour avec les voiturettes pour aller voir les temples). Mais ils ne vendent pas les billets sur place. Il faut aller à un autre bureau de vente plus loin dans la ville. La qualité de la balade est très aléatoire et dépend du pilote que vous avez. Le nôtre avait l’air pressé et ramait très vite, on a doublé des dizaines d’autres barques et avons atteint la dernière grotte très rapidement. Une fois là bas le pilote ne s’est pas arrêté aux temples comme cela était prévu, il n’a pas non plus fait le tour du petit îlot. Il a juste badgé à la machine et est reparti direct. Sur le retour nous lui avons demandé de s’arrêter au point de vue (gioi ravine) et il nous a demandé de l’argent (en plus du prix du billet qu’on avait déjà payé) sinon il ne s’arrête pas… nous avons donc dû payer! Les autres visiteurs présents au point de vue nous ont dit qu’ils n’avaient pas eu à payer de supplément. Clairement on s’est fait racketer par le pilote! Une fois reparti, tout au long du trajet retour le pilote a essayé de nous vendre des souvenirs et n’arrêtait pas de nous demander un pourboire. Rendant ainsi la balade très pénible, on ne peut même pas profiter tranquillement du paysage. Enfin, c’est clairement l’usine, mais ça nous le savions avant d’acheter les billets.

Komplette Touristenabzocke, der Ort ist völlig überlaufen (Massenabfertigung) und es ist alles darauf ausgelegt Touristen das Geld aus der Tasche zu ziehen. Die Höhlen überhaupt nicht spannend/sehenswert und wenn man nicht genug Trinkgeld gibt, kann es passieren, dass man nicht zurück zum Hafen gefahren wird.
Visité il y a quelques années, c'était moins touristique. Il y a trop de monde sur la rivière. Les barques se touchent tellement le site est fréquenté. Il faut aimer la foule.

Bel endroit, le tarif est de 120k par personne pour entrer dans le parc puis 150k pour un bateau avec 2 personnes, le paysage est magnifique! Le point négatif est que dès le début des vietnamiens sur des barques proposent de vous prendre en photo en échange d’argent, puis après la 2 eme grotte d’autres personnes sur des barques proposent de vous vendre de la bière, des fruits puis vous font sentir coupable de ne pas acheter à boire pour la personne qui pagaie! A la fin on se fait demander du tips par la personne qui pagaie, chose que j’aurais fait par moi même si elle n’avait pas demander, ensuite la dame compte le tips et dit que ce n’est pas assez et qu’elle veut plus! Le gouvernement devrait faire vérifier cet endroit car le fait de devoir constamment payer est énervant, je ne suis pas riche et ce n’est pas parce que je voyage que je peux donner mon argent comme ça. Très déçu du comportement des locaux

The boat ride is a beautiful experience however I didn’t like the boat riders approach where in they force you to buy eatables from the floating market and by the end of the trip they pressurise you to tip them a large amount.

The scenery is beautiful and during our two weeks in Vietnam all the Vietnamese people were very welcoming, kind and fare. However, during this trip everyone was forcefully asking for a tip and selling us scam after scam on every corner. It was not a pleasant experience. Trang An we visited the day before was amazing and we decided to stay one more day in Ninh Binh because of it. Pity we got this. I love supporting the locals by buying fair priced products and services and giving tip when i wish to give it not when I'm asked for it 100 times.
Normal scenery, cheap boat rides but kept asking for tips even though fare has been paid. Could’ve increased base fare price instead. Boat ride is 1 hour and 30 mins.

D'une tristesse incommensurable ! Venus à Tam Coc pour cette balade en barque au milieu des rizières, nous en sommes ressortis extrèmement déçus ! La balade consiste en un simple aller-retour en barque sur une eau verdâtre, opaque et pleine d'algues. Sur les rives, des rizières et c'est tout ! Le spectacle est peut-être joli vu d'en haut (comme sur les photos), mais là il est ridicule. À pied ça n'aurait pas été plus mal... Il y a d'ailleurs des endroits où l'on aperçoit des gens venus en deux roues. Et ce spectacle pathétique dure 2h ! Enfin, entre 1h20 et 2h suivant la vigueur de votre conducteur... Nous avons d'ailleurs eu de la peine pour le vieux monsieur qui ramait derrière nous : il devait au moins avoir 80 ans ! Bref, au bout une petite cave ridicule et demi-tour !  - "Pffff... il reste encore le retour. Tu ne veux pas l'aider qu'on aille plus vite !"  Et c'est à ce moment là, alors que vous êtes déjà blasés de l'aller, que vous êtes alpagués façon "pirate" par des vendeuses ambulantes aux barques chargées de boissons, de chips et de bananes... leurs prix sont eux aussi très "chargés" et elles sont plus qu'insistantes ! Si vous ne voulez rien soyez fermes et catégoriques car si elles percoivent une once d'hésitation elles ne vous lacheront plus et vous auront à l'usure !  - "Allez chauffeur, c'est tout droit !"  À la fin, alors que vous apercevez enfin l'embarcadère, c'est là que votre conducteur, fermé et silencieux jusque là, décide de l'ouvrir. Et c'est seulement pour réclamer un pourboire... À vous de voir, mais c'est déjà inclus dans le prix des billets.  Moralité, si vous voulez voir les rizières allez plutôt grimper les 486 marches de Hang Mua, de là-bas le spectacle est largement plus impressionnant ! Et si vous voulez faire un super tour en barque allez plutôt le faire à Trang An, le circuit 1 vaut le coup et fait de la balade de Tam Coc une expérience complètement inutile.

Touristenabzocke in schöner Umgebung  Die 3 Höhlen zu durchfahren mit 2 Personen für 16 € ist für vitnamesische Verhältnisse schon relativ teuer. Das zusätzliche Anbetteln um Trinkgeld und Versuche billigen Schund einem zu überteuerten Preisen anzuregen ist völlig nervig. Habe mich geweigert da mitzumachen und würde dann ganz heftig und aggressiv bearbeitet.  Miese Art und Weise die dem Ort viel an Schönheit nimmt!
This tour is trash. Instead of telling us the pagoda closes at 4:30 pm they let us buy tickets. So needless to say we did not get to see what we paid for at the end. The boat ride is beautiful.

Eger Vietnamca bilmiyorsanız anca  bu tur sizin için iyi olabilir. Eşim vietnamli ve vietnami komple geziyoruz birlikte burası da gezimizin bir şehri  olarak buraya geldik bu sabah  kişi başı 350dong bilet aldık ve teknemize yerleştik burda ki abla resmen kafayı yemiş yol boyunca kadın hiç susmadı ve rahatsız etti ve bahşiş istedi eşim 500000 dong verdi ama hanımefendi parayı az buldu beğenmedi  ve o resim gibi şeylerden satmaya kalktı ama biz istemiyoruz dediğimiz halde ısrar edildi   ve diğer teknedeki ablalarla konuşup burda tip yok vermiyorlar demiş eşim bana bunları tekneden indikten sonra söyledi bunlar aynı dilenci gibi böyle davranıp başka insanların gününü mahvetmek size ne kazandırıyor bilmiyorum kimse buraya size para dağıtmaya gelmiyor bütün günümüzü mahvettiniz Ninh Bình güzel olsa ne olur insanları kötü tüm güzellikleri sildi yetkililerden rica ediyorum lütfen bu dilencilik sistemini  düzeltiniz Gelecek olanlarda bunları bilsin de gelsin (bence gelmeyin Vietnam’ın çok daha güzel yerleri var )Vietnamca bilmiyorsanız güzel olur çok İngilizce bilmedikleri için konuşamazlar

Allez plutôt chez leurs concurrents à Tràng An, à seulement 10 minutes de là : ce sera dix fois mieux pour votre argent et pour une plus belle visite. Ici, c’est un vrai attrape-touristes : 25 € pour deux personnes pour 30 minutes de tour,alors que trang an 18€ pour deux pour 3h de tour avec des temples, magnifiques. Chez eux il n’y a pas grand-chose à voir. Les rameurs vous pressent pour acheter des chose sur le bateau en plus de demandé des pourboires, Franchement, ça ne vaut pas le coup.

Was nice at first she took us on a hike and was very chatty and nice. But she tried to sell us a “homemade” picture which were the same ones we saw earlier and was very pushy once we declined she became silent. At the end she demanded a tip which equated to what the tour cost when we didn’t give her her desired amount she started yelling at us.
免费的，进门阿婆忽悠你返回去坐她们的小小船，晃晃悠悠穿过黑隆隆的溶洞，还没有任何救生衣，差评

Nothing but a huge scam. The big trip costs 350.000VND per Person - foreigners only 2 people/boat. If you want to make the smaller one you ought to take a taxi to a different ticket counter you don't know the exact location about?!  The boat trip itself lasts about 1 hour with no stops. The boat drivers are selling their souvenirs and at the end are strongly and unpolitely reclaiming a big tip. If you give them less than they expect they're actively and rudely claiming more.  The bus brings you to a couple small temples with lots of natives selling their stuff, actively and loudly annoying you with their offers.  The cave was small in comparison to the many others in Vietnam.  So all in all: take a bike / roller and go yourself. There are roads which lead you to the temples and even to the water. And go in the morning because of the many tourists here.  Or go to Trang An - apparently way less people there. We wished we would have gone there

Pongo una estrella no por el lugar, sino por las personas que trabajan aquí. Su único afán es engañar y sacarte dinero. IMPORTANTE!! NO COMPREIS lo que os ofrecen en la barca, te dicen que es artesanal, que lo hace su familia y te da pena... Pagamos 300vdn.. dos días después lo vemos en Hanoi a 50vdn!!!! Ni es artesanal, ni lo hace la familia, luego te exigen una propina, le dimos 30vdn y nos dijo que eso no, que más... Y se enfada encima después de habernos estafado con el souvenirs... Una vergüenza Otra cosa a tener en cuenta, hay personas haciendo fotos, NO LO HAGAIS!! La calidad es malísima,tipo foto del Nokia 3310.. sin bromas.. y te obligan a comprar las fotod que hacen porque las imprime todas.... Nos dejamos una pasta ... A base de engaños. NO CAIGAIS!!!

중간에 음료수를 강매하는 행위 그리고 과도한 팁을 요구하는 행위. 그리고 나의 뜻을 묻지도 않고 뱃사공에 음료와 간식을 전달하고 나에게 지불을 요구하는 행위  그것만 제외하면 좋습니다.  일부 무례한 사람들의 무리한 요구에 No.라고 말하세요  그들이 하는 불쾌한 행동에 긴장하지 마세요 불안해 하지마세요. 웃으면서  no라고 하세요  일부 예의없는 사람들의 행동이 성실하고 친절한 사람들의 이미지를 망칩니다.
Brutální turistická past.  Jsou tu v okolí hezčí místa

Unless you are pressed on time, then go to the other boat site (approx 20 min away by car).  This one only has one route of approx. 1-1.5 hours and costs 250K, which is the same as the 3 other routes at the other site, which are 3 hours long.  The other site is way more beautiful than this and has more untouched nature and always views.  Additionally they ask for tips without having done anything special, we didn't even get to stop at any temple. We still decided to give a decent tip, which the sailor was saying was to little and almost demanded that we gave 10 times more.  Overall just a disappointing and slightly uncomfortable trip

Une horreur côté tourisme de masse. Tam Coc  n'est (plus) un village traditionnel. Tous les villageois sont des hommes et des femmes d'affaires. Chaque maison est soit un homestay, soit un resto, soit un café, soit un centre de massage, soit une agence de voyage, soit un magasin. La nature majestueuse et défiguré par des constructions n'importe où et n'importe comment et peu respectueuses de l'environnement. Comme par tout au Vietnam c'est bruyant, pollué et salle. Il y a des déchets dans les rivières, dans les rues, dans les arbres, dans les grottes, sur les sentiers. Des tas de sacs poubelles le long des magnifiques rizières et dans le village. Des immeubles dont la construction a été abandonnée. Je dis ça avec beaucoup d'amertume car ça fait mal au cœur de voir comment les humains, dans leur quête de faire du pognon,  détruisent la nature.

Pire arnaque. Des vrais voleurs. Les prix sont abusés. Ils vous forcent a prendre le packagé avec les temples sinon il faut aller a 400m plus loin pour prendre un autre ticket spécial et très mal indiqué. Ensuite ils vous font revenir au même point d'embarquement. Ailleurs le prix est a 120.000/pers et la bas c'est 250.000/pers sans le pourboire pour le pagayeur. Impossible de prendre un bateau pour 4 car nous sommes des étrangers et non des vietnamiens. Donc obligé de prendre 2 bateaux. Vraiment très déçu et gros foutage de gueule.
팁 강요 그 나라 수준 알 수 있음 절대 추천 안함 기분 좋았다가도 더럽게 끝남 쩔떄 갸즷맏쎼요

Guides will hand you items to buy mid tour, didn’t get to get off and walk around at the river side pagoda, guide demanded a tip, asked for more when it wasn’t enough. Bich Dong/land based pagoda tour was disappointing, vendors pushing trinkets on the way into the sites. You will be offered photos and then be harassed to purchase them. Save yourself the coin and go do Trang An instead, cheaper and much more beautiful (they do a loop instead of a back and forth, vistas are more impressive too), less touristy, more and larger pagodas along the river that you can actually go and walk around, you won’t be harassed for a tip/to buy their trinkets (at least we weren’t). If you have to choose one, definitely do Trang An instead.

Do Trang An instead! For the same cost (250k/pers), you will have a 3h ride instead of 1.5h. Furthermore, you won’t be harassed by your rower trying to sell you something and at the end complaining that you haven’t tipped them enough. The place is majestic but ruined by such practices

Tourist trap: we got an old lady who was so slow we did 2,5 hr over a tour that was 1,5 hr, and she didn't even stop anywhere. When we were on our way back she tried to sell us painting that were largely overpriced. When we said no she was really annoyed with us. When we got back we even wanted to tip her, and when we gave her the money, she even said it wasnt enough. We paid 250k pp to do this boatride and then she got annoyed for not giving enough tip. The scenery is nice, but not worth the bad feeling they give you afterwards. Would not recommend, spend your money on the people who are greatfull for it and deserve it. Dont be a moneymule, lots of other nice places in this area to spend your money on.

Am Ende sehr enttäuschend - nicht zu empfehlen. Ein Ticket für 1,5h Bootstour für 2 Personen kosten 500VND, was ok ist, wenn man bedenkt, dass man mit Muskelkraft gefahren wird. Allerdings war unsere Bootsfrau nicht sehr freundlich und hat nur 5-6 Worte englisch gesprochen. Was ok ist, dann hat sie uns Bilder angeboten die wir kaufen sollten, als wir ablehnten wurden Trinkgeld gefordert. Ich hab mich so unwohl gefühlt, dachte sie bringt uns einfach nicht zum Hafen zurück. Eigentlich war niemand von gesamten Personal freundlich zu uns. Die Höhlen sind keine Besichtigung wert, ansonsten ist die Umgebung schön, aber nicht wenn man bedenkt, was im Boot passiert. Ich empfehle die Region mit dem Fahrrad zu erkunden. Diese Tour ist eine Touristenabzocke - leider.
Tourist trap. Although beautiful scene but forced to tip and photography by lady in boat randomly and asked to pay at end of boat ride. Horrible experience!! Dont come here

I just do not like this kind of business where you pay for the trip and later on are forced to buy something in the middle at the floating market. Furthermore, the boat driver knows only one word in english - tip.

Une balade en bateau chère qui ne valait pas le coup. Le paysage est gâché par les 1500 bateaux qui font continuellement le tour. La rameuse gâche aussi le voyage en demandant tous le temps un pourboire (à la limite du harcèlement) Je déconseille, et je ne comprends vraiment pas l’engouement pour cette balade

J ai trouvé la ballade fort sympathique pour ma part. bon il est vrai que la rameuse insiste pour le pourboire mais peut être a juste titre car je ne suis pas sur qu elle profite de l’augmentation des prix et du système vicieux de la billetterie. 1 etoile Google afin de vous avertir. La billetterie proche du quai ne propose que le combo a 350 k avec bateau et randonnée. Pour effectuer seulement la sortie bateau a 250 k, déjà il faut le savoir (merci les avis Google) et de plus il faut marcher à 300 m Adresse Google map : 6W8R+R3H, ĐT491C, Van Lam, Hoa Lư, Ninh Bình, Viêt Nam  Bizarre, de ne pas proposer cette offre directement à la billetterie sur le quai…

The boat ride was nice and scenic, but was ruined by the boat driver pressuring customers to buy overpriced mass-produced "handmade" embroideries (the same ones you'll find at any local market for much cheaper) and still expecting a tip on top of selling you their overpriced souvenir.

ATTENTION : on nous prend pour des vaches à lait ! Très déçus avec le sentiment de s'être fait arnaqué ! Le paysage est beau mais le rameur vous vend des tableaux que vous trouvez juste en face l'embarcadère 3 fois moins cher. Après avoir payé 250 000 vnd par personne, on vous réclame encore un pourboire. Le paysage est joli mais on a préféré le viewpoint.
Overcrowded , no rice as in the photo. It is not worth it!

Not only is it more expensive than the listed price, but you have to give the boat keeper 75k VDN (3€) even BEFORE hopping on the boat itself. Once you reach the end point with snacks and drinks vending boats, they will nearly force you to buy something. If you don't, they will also nearly force you to buy some drinks for the person bringing you on the tour. Once we reached back the "harbour", she acted as if she was very tired and almost begged for a tip AGAIN !! we didn't giver her anything as we felt nearly harassed for money and it seems like that's all they care about. While the scenery is fantastic, the people in charge are so money minded that it simply spoils the experience. Don't waste your time here, there's a lot more to see around just a few miles away!
Bad experience. A family with three children can not go in one boat just because they are not Vietnamese. Incredible!

El paisaje del paseo en barco es maravilloso. Se ven los campos de arroz a cada costado del agua y se tiene una vista más panorámica del valle. Está más masificado que Trang An (chocábamos permanentemente con el barco de adelante), pero pongo una estrella porque el trato con los turistas es bastante desagradable. Nos presionaron desde que llegamos primero con las fotos que te proponen, luego la señora que nos llevaba nos quiso hacerle comprar unos cuadros que no nos interesaban, para después reclamar una propina antes de llegar y poner mala cara cuando se la dimos. En comparación, el paseo en Trang An es mucho más disfrutable. Por el mismo precio de 250k te llevan el doble de tiempo y el trato es mucho más amable. La señora que remaba en Trang An nos trató muchísimo mejor, no nos exigió nada y al recibir una propina de nuestra parte (la misma que dimos en Tam Coc) sin pedirla previamente, nos agradeció con una gran sonrisa. Es una lástima que el paseo en Tam Coc se vea opacado por ese trato que solo consigue espantar a los turistas.
유튜브를 봤을때는 혼자가도 된다고 했는데 이번에 표를 사려하니 배에 2명이 타야한다고 한다 매표원이 기다리라고 하는데 혼자오는 여행객이 언제올줄 알고 기다리나....

The scenery was beautiful but the experience was tainted at the mid-point, where there were locals selling snacks on boats. I was guilt-tripped and forced into buying overpriced snacks for the rower on my boat. It's a total scam. At the end of the ride, the boatwoman explicitly asked for 100,000 dong in tips. I mean, we were planning to give tips anyway but being demanded of it left a bad taste in my mouth. So go for this boat ride, but absolutely say NO to the snacks at the mid-point.

Widoki są piękne, ale wymuszają kupno napojów na końcu trasy za ceny z kosmosu, osoby które płyną z wami łódką chcą wcisnąć obrazy czy inne rzeczy własnych wyrobów z tekstem że nie mają na jedzenie, po powrocie kobieta wolała o napiwek, którego nie dostała :)

I arrived with my 5-year-old daughter, but they declined to sell us tickets, citing insufficient passengers for the boat. They mentioned needing at least four people for the boat to depart. However, from what I observed, every boat seemed to carry only two passengers and a boatman. I’m deeply disappointed and saddened by this experience. Phân biệt rõ rệt người Việt và người nước ngoài. Phải chăng vì người nước ngoài có thể charge cao hơn???

Die Landschaft ist ganz in Ordnung, für 16€ aber eine Frechheit. Das Ganze wird nur dafür genutzt um einem weiter Geld aus der Tasche zu ziehen, man soll Fotos kaufen, Süßigkeiten die einem auf Booten angeboten werden und diese Boote verschwinden nicht einfach! Es wird einem fast schon was ins Boot gelegt um danach Geld zu fordern, im immer böser werdenden Ton! Am Ende verlangt die Fahrerin noch penetrant Trinkgeld, wozu wir uns erst gegen entschieden haben, weil sie uns absolut gar nichts zu der Landschaft erzählt hat usw. Da sie dann aber leicht aggressiv wurde haben wir ihr was geben wollen was sie aber wütend abgelehnt hat weil es ihr zu wenig war. Schade, dass einem erneut an einem weiteren Spot der Urlaub vermiest wurde aufgrund des unfreundlichen passiv aggressiven schnorrenden Verhaltens der VietnamesInnen.
ボートで奥まで行くと食べ物を売ってますが、そこでボラれました。多少高いのは覚悟の上、ボートを漕いでるおばちゃんにも買ってあげようと全部払ったら、かなり高額を請求されました。 さらに降りる時にチップを要求され、安いと文句も言われて非常に気分が悪かったです。 行かなければ良かったです。
景色はとても良いです。でも、三つ目の洞窟を出ると、商売のボートがたくさんいて、 船頭さんの為のチップだから、ってただの缶ジュースを約250円で売ってきます。 そして船着場に戻る直前は今度は船頭さんが、チップくれって催促して来ます。 それがなければ満点なんだけどね。 全て興醒めです。

Fermé au public depuis le 08 juillet, on a été déçu d'apprendre ça à notre arrivée sur place. Les touristes sont redirigés vers les autres embarcadères de la zone. Le lieux est fermé pour réaménager les espaces, protéger l'écosystème et les travailleurs.
Very bad app when you want to change from car driving to work

The boat ride is a rip off. It's way too expensive and you get rushed through the boat ride. You are not allowed to hop off for longer than 3 minutes. The ride is not worth it. It's adversity for two hours but actually it's half of it.  Boater was also very rude, didn't speak a word English and pressurized us to tip which is a cheek for the service.  Just skip this "experience".

Widok piękny i na tym się kończy, wyłudzanie napiwku pod pretekstem że nie ma na jedzenie. Na końcu trasy natarczywie wciskają żeby coś od nich kupić za ceny z kosmosu. Płynąłem tam zadowolony, ale wróciłem z zepsutym humorem, ponieważ zepsuli całą otoczkę pięknego miejsca.

Very nice ride, but (!!) this is a “tourist trap”, from the worst kind. (Just look on how many “local guides” gave their very high review…)  Examples - After starting the ride we were pictured by a local photographer. We refused to cooperate but they waited to us in the end, making us feel very “guilty” not to buy their work in 1$ per picture and worst of that - they were following us until we agreed to pay them to disappear…  More example - in the ‘turn around’ point waited some locals with on-boat drinks and snacks. For 2 fruits (mango and pineapple) and 2 coca-cola they asked for 500000 vnd (more than 20$).  Sorry but this (and more examples) ruined the whole visit. My recommendation - If you are looking for authentic atmosphere, Look for other places to visit

Venir en pleine journée, se retrouver face à une barrière et un garde qui ne veux pas que l'on rentre. Finalement, devoir se faire emmener par un locaux pour consommer.. C'est décevant, on en perds le charme se cette rue.

Avoid this boat ride and take a boat ride at Trang An instead. Trang An is much beautiful and well organised compared to here.  Boat lady was rude and expected 100000VND tips from 2 person. She was trying to sell painting and other stuff during the ride.  Beware of parking. There is no official parking for 2 wheelers. Negotiate with the shops and park your 2 wheeler safely next to there shop. I paid 10k for parking after the negotiation.  In my personal opinion, Trang An much much better than Tam Coc

Malheureusement comme partout au Vietnam c'est L'argent l'argent L'argent.. Cela devient fatiguant.. Vous payez pour tout ici, bientôt pour respiré. Ne vous faire pas avoir vous allez payé pour la balade mais les dames qui pagaie avec leurs "pieds" ne récolte rien de cet argent c'est une compagnie privée qui encaisse donc elle vont vous demandez de leurs donner un pourboire ou leur acheté de la nourriture ou des boisson et cela devient de plus en plus insistant asseyez vous sur le bords des marches de la rivière et regardez les vous allez voir par vous mêmes. bref cela devient juste une usine ou les touristes sont pressée comme leurs porte monnaie.

Stunning nature worth 5 stars. Downside are the people trying to sell stuff during the boat trip, cursing at you if you don’t want to buy overpriced drinks and the boatmen who shout at you if you don’t give minimum of 100k Dong tip at the end of the ride. He even tried to negotiate a higher tip after I offered them something. Would not recommend. Same thing happens in Trang An btw.

Only for tourist, there are a lot of boats with drivers in them. Feels Like You enslave them. the vie is very stunning and at the end there are other boats with food you can buy, and if you do not buy it your rider  will get angry at you and they also force you to tip them at the end. I did not liked it at all.

Had a really bad experience at Tam Coc boat ride. The journey was nice until the turning point where there are food/drinks sellers on boats. I bought a can of coke for myself and some snacks/drinks for my boat rower and was charged 200k!!! Then the boat lady would ask us to buy some souvenir which we are not interested. At the end, the boat lady would asked for a tip and when I give her 30k, she demanded for 100k!!! I simply refused and told her that I already paid 200k for a coke. This place seems to be ripping tourists off. Instead, I would recommend Trang An as it is very professionally run and less touristy. The boat lady did not even ask for any tips but I give her some anyway.
Die aussicht ist ok. Wenn man aber nicht darauf aus ist sich andere touristen anzuschauen und zu WARTEN um die treppe hoch zu steigen eher nicht empfehlenswert.
ภาพไม่ตรงปก ไม่มีการทำนาริมแม่น้ำ  บังคับซื้อของ และขอทิปเพิ่มที่สูงเป็น 2 เท่าของค่าบัตร ค่าบัตร 120k ขอทิป 200 k  แต่เราให้ 50 k และถูกต่อว่าด้วย

Una decepción total, no por el sitio, si no por la persona que remaba, esta reseña es para que no les pase y no los engañen como a nosotros, no se si está persona no tenia ganas de trabajar pero hay que ser malo, uno paga una entrada y espera hacer el recorrido que toca! La persona que remaba a menos de la mitad del camino empezó a preguntarnos si ya queríamos volver y nosotros le dijimos que un poco más, pero insistió tanto que nos dio pena y accedimos y gracias a eso nos ha hecho perdernos lo más bonito del paseo en barca, además que se acercan otras barcas a ofrecerte cosas y te hacen sentir lástima que le compres cosas al conductor de la barca que uno dice “bueno” pero a ellos no les da pena haberte cobrado la entrada y hacer que te pierdas las mitad de los templos del recorrido, y además te ofrecen que le compres souvenirs y te enseñan fotos de la familia para que te de pena y cuando acaba el tour no se cortan en pedirte propina ojo con esto. Que  ellos te ponen el precio ( le compramos 200 dang en productos para que comiera durante el camino, que más encima nos la ha jugado y nos hizo perdernos lo mejor ) y además al final del viaje nos pide propina que dijimos le dejamos 50 por lo que nos hizo y casi nos pega que quería que le pagáramos 100 + cada uno, me parece que te intentan de dar pena con que son pobres pero al final lo que son todos unos ladrones! Una pena pero todos son asi ( en todo tam coc hacen cosas similares, no ha sido solo ella) con esta actitud que han tenido nos han hecho marcharnos antes de este maravilloso lugar. Nos han hecho sentir como un euro con patas.
Not worth it. Too touristical, river and environment are very dirty. People who pedal boats are real hustlers, they don't have a shame to ask for a tip even when you paid regular entry ticket.

Pemandangannya lumayan bagus Pendayung kapalnya memeras kami dengan membeli banyak barang di tengah perjalanan dan menyuruh kami membayar semuanya, dan kami pun membayarnya karena sudah di tengah danau Harga tiket masuk per orang 120.000 dong, tp kami harus membayar makanan yang dibeli oleh pendayung kapal sebesar 200.000 dong Setelah dia beli banyak barang, dia menawarkan barang jualannya lagi kepada kami, dan di akhir perjalanan dia meminta tips dengan mematok harga 100.000 dong  Kami merasa diperas Mohon pengelola pariwisata dapat memperhatikan hal ini

Veníamos predispuestos a conocer un lugar maravilloso, recomendados en todas las guías y foros pero nuestra decepción ha sido bastante grande. El paseo en barca ha sido muy corriente si lo comparamos con TRANG AN que es ESPECTACULAR. Haces el mismo recorrido de ida y de vuelta. Te paran en una zona para que consumas bebidas de los lugareños en su barca y encima tienes que pagar el almuerzo al señor de la barca. Y para colmo al terminar te vuelve a pedir propina. No lo recomiendo.  Si queréis ver paisajes espectaculares hacer el tour en canoa pero desde Trang An. No os decepcionará como tan coc

If I could give this zero stars I would. At the half way point we were set to turn around and head back towards where we came from. At this point the boat rower gestured for us to get off the boat.. in the complete darkness in the middle of nowhere, Or to return to Tam Coc where the tour is meant to end he was to charge us another 200,000vnd. No phone reception, and my partner being a diabetic. This put us in a potentially life threatening position. Do not take this tour! we paid the 200k and returned to Tam coc. police have been notified of this incident. Beware of these scammers

帶著家人去玩, 先是被船夫要求外國人2人開一條船,越南人則無此要求, 所以加開一條船另花15萬越盾,船划到返回點, 會遇到許多會說中文的划船販子兜售水果飲料紀念品,要遊客買給划船的船夫吃或喝, 回程途中船夫會強索小費,小費本就已包含在門票中, 船夫還是向遊客要, 甚至於停船強索, 每人2萬盾, 沒零錢給多了也不找零,. 以此處和長安相比,長安水道比較長,風景也漂亮多了, 山底下的水溶洞也有更多看頭, 可能因一般旅行社和陸龍灣定有較優惠合約, 一般遊客會被帶到陸龍灣, 就一天內遊這兩處比較後,建議不要去陸龍灣,兩地相差不遠, 花費相差不多, 極力推薦區去長安遊河,去長安遊河你不會後悔的!

Die Tour kostet pro Erwachsenen 120k Eintritt in den Nationalpark und mind. 150k für eine Person für das Boot, d.h. bei einer Person 120+150, bei zwei 2 * 120 + 150 und bei drei Personen 3 * 120 + 150 + 75. Also pro Person 75k mehr. Man sieht all die Reisefelder und Gebirge wie auf den typischen Ninh Binh Fotos. Leider kommt gleich zu Beginn ein Boot mit einer Fotografin und will Bilder von der Tour verkaufen. Die Tour fährt bis zu einem kleinen Bereich bei dem dann gewartet wird, bis vom Steg ein anderes Boot zu einem herrudert und dann einem etwas verkaufen will (Blumen, Trinken, Früchte). Wenn man Nein sagt, sagt die Verkäuferin "For your driver/her" und zeigt auf eine Cola und Früchte. Wir haben abgelehnt. Hier wird dann gedreht und man fährt zum Steg zurück. Auf der Fahrt zieht die/der BootsfahrerIn Bilder und Postkarten heraus und will diese dann auch verkaufen. Kurz vor Ende sagt die/der BootsfahrerIn dann "Trinkgeld" oder "Tip for me". Wir haben 30k gegeben, was schon viel zu viel für diese Frechheit war. Beim Aussteigen kam eine andere Vietnamesin und sagt "Tip for her, tip for her". Zusammenfassend ist die Tour echt schön aber wenn man schon mind. 200k bezahlt, muss man nicht 4-5 mal noch belästigt werden.  Fahrt lieber die andere Bootstour bei Trang An, den die ist viel schöner und ruhiger.

Mais quelle arnaque...  Payer si cher pour qu'à la fin le bonhomme te demande vulgairement "tips tips tips" mdrrr  J'ai vu une dame refuser un pourboire car c'était pas assez !!!  Durant le long parcours, des gens en bateau eux aussi vous harcèlent pour vous vendre de la malbouffe ou bien des photos.  Un vrai piège à touriste !

Not worth, avoid  The boat tour was ok, you get to go through 4 caves and enjoy the views. However, I would not recommend it as you get forced to buy things and are asked to provide a tip. The Trang An boat tour is much better, with more to see and no annoying staff to deal with.  When we went through the last of 4 caves, there are half a dozen boats waiting there and will grab onto your boat to sell you refreshments. They don't take no for an answer and will overcharge you for junk. They ask you to buy it for the rower, but why don't I just give money to the rower instead? I would advise you to ask the boat operator to turn around after the 3rd cave as there isn't really anything else worth seeing to avoid these people.  Also, at the end of our trip, we were asked to provide a tip and after giving them a good tip they didnt even say anything.  The government should really step in to enforce better customer service at these places. Otherwise they will lose customers.  Avoid this place. Trang An boat tours were much better.

A lot of tourists, a line of boats, paddler was not nice (rude maybe), talking to other paddlers in vietnamese. Probably for a long time (more years) there is no rice fields just grass, no maintanence or they changed the profession to tourism. At the end they ask for a tip. You pay already enough. Foreigners can be only 2 in a boat = tourist scam if you ask me. 1h30min ride almost racing, not time to walk around. Selling snacks at the end (dont buy anything and do not tip and they wont do this things- simple). No future if you ask me. Bad managing of the place for sure.

Einige hier gelesene Bewertungen kann ich nicht ganz nachvollziehen. Das Zimmer war soweit sauber, allerdings fehlten Handtücher, Toilettenpapier etc. Dies wurde dann auf zweimalige Nachfrage umgehend nachgeliefert. Dummerweise war das Fenster am Kopfende des Bettes indicht und wurde als akzeptabel angesehen. Aufgrund der seit kurzem herrschenden Kälte war es eine äußerst frostige Nacht im Zimmer. Das Wasser war nur für kurze Zeit lauwarm. Das Homestay liegt in der Nähe von Minimärkten und booking offices. Alles in allem kam man sich wie ein ungebetener Gast vor.
วิวสวย บรรยากาศลงเรือดีมาก แต่มีพวกรีดไถเอาขนมน้ำมาบังคับขายให้เราซื้อให้คนพายเรือ บอกว่าเป็นทิป ทั้งๆ ที่เราจ่ายเงินจ้าง และเราให้ทิปเป็นเงินสดตอนกลับมาถึงฝั่งอยู่แล้ว แย่มากๆ
🤷‍♂️. Too touristy. Beautiful scenery in the area but not for me. …
谷歌地图导航是错误的，河内这个车站我是没找到
The lady boat forced you to pay 20000 dong per person for tip. Never give tip until you landed. They will threating you. They even not give help taking picture. …

Touristenfalle in schöner Landschaft. Tam Coc bzw. besonders die Bootstour ist total touristisch. Wer Mallorca vermisst ist hier allerdings genau richtig. Was sich mehr lohnt ist einen Roller oder Fahrräder zu mieten und die Landschaft zu erkunden. Von Land aus kann man die Landschaft genauso gut betrachten und wird dazu nicht von unfreundlichen Ruderern abgezockt.

하루 코스로써 좋기는 하지만 팁을 비롯해서 수상에서 먹거리 파는 행위 말과 달리 사진 강매에 한국사람에게 집요한 장사 배려해서 맥주와 과일도 사주고 팁도 주고 시진도 사주고도 여행기분을 상하게 하는 유일한 날입니다. 가시게 되면 수상에서 찍는 사진도 외면 하시고 음료수 과일도  뱃사공에게 팁도 많이 주려고 하지마세요. 배려해서 챙겨줬지만 더 달라고 요구하는 어이 없는 일이 발생 합니다. 물론 한국돈으로 따지면 얼마 안되는 금액이기는 하지만 기본적으로 바가지 씌우려 하는게 눈에 보입니다.

Today we took a boat ride to see the sunset.  Our rower threatened us, told us to pay 200,000 else he would kick us off the boat in the pitch black dark.  It was scary :( photo of man in question.  Police being contacted, boat ride was picked up from the centre of Tam Coc.

Tourist trap tour, worthless attitude of boat drivers and staff, overpriced. Only two 'foreigners' allowed on one boat, Vietnamees or other asians for that mater easily share a boat with 4 or more. Instead of a relaxing sight seeing tour with some stops to enjoy and take photos this is what happens. It's a race to get from start to finish as quick as possible. Offcourse when you almost reach the end he slows down and starts begging for a tip of minimum 100k. During the trip boatdriver shouts rudely in Vietnamees to other boats. Involuntary stops are made at boat vendors who try and sell you anything you can think of. If you kindly decline they start pushing you to buy drinks and snacks for the boat driver. My conclusion, the views are nice, the total experience slightly disappointing. If you take a walk along the river or rent a bike the experience is far more enjoyable.

Ich würde definitiv empfehlen die Tour bei An Trang zu machen. Sie ist deutlich schöner und man wird nicht von penetranten Verkäufern belästigt! Dafür lohnt sich die 15-20 min Scooter fahrt nach An Trang
Bateliers trop insistants pour avoir un pour boire. De plus ils réclament leur somme minimale!
À fuir. Trang An est beaucoup plus sympa. Des scams avec tips obligatoires partout et aucun merci ni rien après avoir donné (je parle Viet...). Honteux.
Boat trip is beautiful but not a good value for money. Furthermore, all the people are trying to sell you things and make you feel guilty if you don't buy.

Don't make my mistake and visit here. You'd better off going to Trang An instead. Trang An costs only 1-2 USD more than here but you get so much more value for your money and much better service (I find this odd since both are owned by the same company). After I have visited Trang An, going here felt like a fraud. The best way to see Tam Coc is actually going to Hang Mua (Mua cave) and climb to the view point where there's a really great view of Tam Coc, especially when it's sunny.
注意攝影人跟停車小夥 會大量跟你索取金錢 不給不行
Nul. Une porte de 3 mètres avec trop de monde déjà début avril

Avoid! Go to Trang An instead! It's a 100 times more beautiful, better organized and the same price, without the scams and asking for tips. The Tam Coc boats really have no reason to exist next to Trang An!

절대 가지마세요. 땀콕 갈바에 짱안 2번 가는 게 낫습니다. 보트 타고 가다보면 선상 노점이 길막고 "너네 뱃사공 저렇게 고생하는데 맥주 하나도 안 사주냐 이 쪼잔한 놈들아?"하면서 이것저것 막 강매시킴. 안 사면 자기 혼자 화내면서 감. 뱃사공은 뒤에서 못 본척 하고 있음. 출발점으로 돌아오면 뱃사공이 먼저 팁 안 내놓을 거냐고 큰소리 침. (응? 팁은 주는 사람이 주고 싶을 때 주는 거 아니었나?) 그래도 고생했다 싶어서 2만동 줬더니 막 화내면서 더 내놓으라고 함. 어이가 없어서 나도 무시하고 그냥 옴. 주변 경관도, 코스 프로그램도 짱안이 훨씬 멋있고 알참. 땀콕 갈바에 그냥 짱안 루트 3개 있으니까 짱안 두번 타세요. 땀콕 절대로 가지마세요 여행 기분 잡칩니다

WARNING! Be careful where you park your motorbike here as they will hide it and tell you it has been ‘stolen’!! The security and all people working at the ticket office are in on it and want to get money from you to give you the bike back!
מלכודת תיירים מבטיחים לכם סיור שעתיים ובפועל שעה

I might went to this place in wrong season ( Jan). There was not full of green color same as the advertising. The oarsmem even arked us for more tips after service and force us to bought some pictures. It's not impress us at all.
The scenery is lovely. The price is too high and the service is poor yet they try and sell you something half way through and ask for a tip at the end.
Overrun by tourism. Same as most of this country. Pity. Also extremely trashed with rubbish everywhere.
great place but be aware of scam, I was charged double the price that should be, buy ticket from the right place.
Harassed to pay $40 for two bags of fruit, woops. Couldn’t tip the rower who did all the work 😂 …
Very touristy. Hate the way rower asking for tipping even they deserve to have tip
المكان بعيد ولا انصح بالزيارة
The high pressure sales nonsense and demands for tips... It's not going to happen .
경치 아름다웠어요
Site fermé pour le moment. On ne sait pas pour combien de temps.
Avoided this boat ride based on review and went for the Trang An boat ride
Overpriced compare to Trang an and people are not nice and asked for a high tips
This place was closed due to some unresolved issue between the locals and the government.
Never come to this restaurant, food very bad and  Rid off
expensive worst tour in Ninh Binh
I can't take the boat because no boat man.
Fermé jusqu'à nouvel ordre!
Very bad staff
N'y allez pas. Merci
Really rude receptionists
Nada que ver

initially, I actually quite enjoyed it and thought, I'd do this again or recommend it to others. while the cave was impressive and memorable, there was something even more memorable, with a sour taste. the boat 'driver''s incessant want for money. at the start, she was nice and all, pointing out parts of scenery and smiling, even complimenting us. now i wonder if that's out of the goodness of her heart or to make us open our wallets more. at a point in the tour, she stopped at what seemed like a dead end to try and sell us souviners. initially me and my dad said no, since we didn't want nor need any, but she kept pressuring us and there was no where to run, so we gave in and bought a magnet for 50k dong, which is really expensive. after that, she still wasnt satisfied, still trying to sell us other stuff, which were all terribly overpriced. at that point, i was already quite irritated, but tried to sympathise, but it left me upset for the remainder of the ride. just when i thought the money-making tricks were over, at the end of the boat ride she asked for a tip. well, its not uncommon in vietnam, but we had already bought overpriced souviners, and tried refusing, but she was adamant. we caved in and passed her 20k dong, which to out shock, she insisted on 100k dong. i was so appalled, never had i seen someone blatantly ask for so much. at that point, i was already looking forward to getting off, so much as to half standing up before she even stopped at the end. i was genuinely angry and if not for the boat 'driver', this would have been a great memory. the 2 out of 5 was for the scenery. it really was nice, with the mountains and caves, which i enjoyed. i even saw a few ducks and goats.

After we came from the beautiful authentic Pu Luong, Tam Coc was a completely different story. We thought it was very touristy and the area we explored on the scooter was not spectacular. The boat trip in Tam Coc is very overrated because you are in a traffic jam with hundreds of other boats. We left earlier than planned. Vietnam has so many more beautiful places than Tam Coc.

Ban quản lý cần tập huấn quán triệt thái độ của lái đò với du khách, nay t cùng gia đình đi tham quan tam cốc, gia đình có 6 ng trong đó có mẹ già, 2 ng lớn và 3 cháu. Chúng tôi xếp mẹ ngồi cùng cháu lớn và 1 cháu nhỏ, tuy nhiên 1 cháu nhỏ lại muốn ngồi cùng anh em nên 1 thuyền có 4 ng và 1 thuyền có 2 ng, theo quy định 1 đò chở 4 ng việt nam, nhưng ng lái đò tức tối vì trong nhóm có 6 ng mà đò ông ấy phải trèo 4, rồi thái độ khó chịu vs chúng tôi, đi 1 đoạn rời bến r vẫn nói đi nói lại với thái độ khó chịu nên chúng tôi cho thuyền quay vào bến để đổi đò. Thật sự vừa mất tiền vừa mang bực vào ng. Tôi đề nghị ban quản lý cần tập huấn và quán triệt về thái độ giao tiếp với du khách đến tam cốc đừng để khách đến và có ấn tượng xấu về ninh bình nói riêng và ng việt nam nói chung.

Tam coc has majestic landscapes and pagodas.However tam coc municipality support scammers.They do nothing to prevent scam parkings. Never park anywhere where they yell at you, whistle, tell you road is closed or that you cannot entry, etc.. these are just SC*mb*GS trying to rip you off.For the boat trip I advise you to do it in Trang An (they have it very organized, rowers are very polite and friendly). They will not ask you for a tip. But most people will give them at the end because you realize they did a great job during three hours.  In Tam Coc, is a different story, they will try to scam you: asking for tips consistently, and they will tell you to give them more... Other "partners in crime" will approach your boat asking to buy something for you. If you refuse they will ask you to buy something for rower. And will not let you return until you buy something or you spend several minutes arguing.

Very disappointing walk on the river Tam Cuoc. Certainly their way of rowing is fun, but here everything is trade and if you do not buy the sellers become aggressive. The landscape is not extraordinary, this site does not reflect the terrestrial Ha Long Bay. Choose the Tran An boat ride, a little more expensive but so much more faithful to the clichés of the terrestrial Ha Long Bay and there no forced sale.

The boat tour was ok, you get to go through 4 caves and enjoy the views. However, I would not recommend it as you get forced to buy things and are asked to provide a tip.The Trang An boat tour is much better, with more to see and no annoying staff to deal with.When we went through the last of 4 caves, there are half a dozen boats waiting there and will grab onto your boat to sell you refreshments. They don't take no for an answer and will overcharge you for junk. They ask you to buy it for the rower, but why don't I just give money to the rower instead? I would advise you to ask the boat operator to turn around after the 3rd cave as there isn't really anything else worth seeing to avoid these people.Also, at the end of our trip, we were asked to provide a tip and after giving them a good tip they didnt even say anything.The government should really step in to enforce better customer service at these places. Otherwise they will lose customers.Avoid this place. Trang An boat tours were much better.

Nog voordat we in de boot stapten was het personeel al niet vriendelijk. Je moet werkelijk voor alles betalen: je fiets stallen, je entree tot de boot, toeslag als je met meer dan twee personen bent, kosten voor de boot zelf, etc. Dit staat natuurlijk niet op het bord aangegeven maar kom je pas achter bij de kassa.Eenmaal in de boot een leuk tochtje, maar het was wel file varen. Onderweg kregen we een peddel om mee te helpen. Ik vond het zielig als die oude vrouw alleen moest peddelen het hele eind, dus heb ik de hele weg mee gepeddeld. Onderweg zijn er meerdere punten waar ze je proberen op te dringen wat te kopen: fotos van iemand die langsvaart, een snack uit een bootje die aan jou boot blijft hangen. (Als je zegt dat je niks wil proberen ze je te pushen iets voor je peddelaar te kopen). Aan het eind gaven we onze mevrouw een fooi, omdat we het sneu vonden dat ze daar waarschijnlijk zo weinig betaald krijgen van de toegangsprijs, maar toen vroeg ze om meer!  Erg jammer dat alles weer draait om geld en dingen verkopen, want het was wel een leuk tochtje.

In tutta sincerità l'escursione non é nulla di ché, un giro sul fiume con l'omino che rema con i piedi lascia proprio a desiderare, senza contare che dopo che acquisti il biglietto per l'imbarcazione, per la coppia o il singolo, ti fanno comprare cibo e bevande e al termine ti chiede an he la mancia... Veramente troppo commerciale per un nulla di ché
von Touristen überrant. Üble Abzocke. Laut und lärmig.Schön ist allerdings eine Fahrrad-Tour in die Umgebung.

If in need of a taxi from Ninh Binh City to Tam Coc or vice versa the price should be 85-90vnd. As we all know too well taxi drivers try it on and charge more money. These sneaky sods have another trick. All the Grab taxis will turn offline but still park in the rank (normally just before 9pm), then between themselves and the other local companies they quote prices from 150-190vnd. Grab taxis in Tam Coc will also not drive to the collection location. Instead, they confirm arrival on the app whilst sitting with their feet up by the coach park. You then walk 200-300yards down the road to try and find the car searching all the number plates in the rank. You can rent a scooter for 90-120vnd per day, which we had but wanted a night without driving. Just wear the helmet and two max to a bike and that’s all you need.

Its not worth it. They asked for 150k for boat and 120k for entry fee and at the end the guy asked very rudly for 100k for tip. We gave him 50k. I would recomend to take tour in Trang An. Absolutely different you pay 200k and it was worth it

Nadat we een dag ervoor een boottocht zijn gaan voeren in Trang Ang, ook een boottocht gedaan in Tam Coc. De prijs scheelt nauwelijks van elkaar en de boottocht in Trang An duurt 3 uur en in Tam Coc 1,5 uur. Daarnaast heb je bij Trang An de keuze uit 3 routes. Wij hebben voor route 1 gekozen met wel 9 grotten. Veel indrukwekkender dan Tam Coc al vraag je het ons. Toch is het de moeite waard als je niet in de buurt van Trang An komt of minder tijd heb.Wel wil ik nog aangeven dat de roeier een fooi 'afgetroggeld'. Op de terugweg ging hij langs wat verkoopsters die drinken en snacks op een boot verkopen. Zij gaven ons aan dat we dat voor de roeier moesten kopen en vroegen 100.000 dong. Dit vonden wij te veel voor hetgeen ze aanboden. Ik vroeg de roeier of hij misschien wat wilde drinken of liever een fooi wilde. Dit leek hij niet te begrijpen. Vlak voordat we terug waren vroeg hij om zijn fooi. Ik gaf hem 45.000 dong. Dit vond hij niet genoeg en bleef maar vragen om 100.000 dong. Echt een smet op deze trip. Bij Trang An moeten de roeiers aan het einde een klanttevredenheidformulier geven waarin o.a. gevraagd wordt of de roeier om fooi heeft gevraagd. Dit was daar niet het geval en naar mijn mening een mooie oplossing om dit te voorkomen.

Like some reviews already stated here... All went great untill the last few meters. I went at 7 am to avoid the crowd, which was perfect, i was the first! The lady seemed very nice, she talked a little, showed me some birds. I thought she was genuine but thats me being naive. The last meters first she stopped beside another boat selling fruit and water. I said no, I don't need anything. Then the seller started pushing to buy something for the rowing lady... Again I said no because I wanted to tip her later because she was nice. But then right before the end she asked for the tip herself, so I was less fond of giving her something because now it all felt fake. I offered her less than I first intended and she said it was to little. So then I gave her nothing, even after her nagging. Because I was early I read the 'rules' outside the ticket office. It states they can't ask for money, if you really have a pushy person just tell him or her to come and read the sign. My relaxed state after the calming boat ride was completely ruined. I recommend you just go sightseeing by yourself... Save the money, which i find overpriced anyway. 270.000 VND for 1 person...

Ich habe beide Bootsfahrten ausprobiert, einmal in Trang An und hier in Tam Coc. Trang An ist absolut mehr zu empfehlen! Von Tam Coc bin ich absolut nicht begeistert. Für die Bootstour zahlt man pro Boot und nicht pro Person, somit ist es als Alleinreisender teurer als in Trang An. Aus den versprochenen 2 Std. sind knapp 1 Std. 15 Min. geworden und der Bootsfahrer war mit dem Tipp auch nicht zufrieden, wie auch das ich ihm beim Verkaufsboot nochts gekauft habe. Ich fand das nicht in Ordnung. Als Ausgangspunkt ist Tam Coc super, Bootstour aber definitiv in Trang An empfohlen!

We had been looking forward to our boat ride was quite some time, but felt sadly let down comparable to the last season of GOT, and film ‘ the last Jedi. The scenery was beautiful and the lady who took us around was pleasant enough. Unfortunately once we arrived at the midway point, we were pounced upon by sellers offering us over inflated snacks. They were quite pushy and rude, and made the whole experience quite unpleasant. It is no wonder that so many tourists never return to Vietnam.

Sehr schöne Bootsfahrt durch tolle Landschaften und Grotten.Die Boote sind leider nicht sonderlich bequem, für 1 1/2 Stunden aber auszuhalten.Unterwegs wird man von Verkaufsbooten genötigt, Getränke und Obst abzukaufen. Wenn  man verneint, wirft die Verkäuferin dem Bootsfahrer trotzdem Snacks und ein Getränk zu und fordert erneut die Bezahlung hierfür. Wenn man verneint oder ablehnt tut sie so, als ob sie kein Englisch verstünde. Absolute Unverschämtheit, hatten hier bereits 100.000 VND für das Getränk und die Snacks für den Fahrer bezahlt. Zum Abschluss der Fahrt wird man vom Fahrer selbst nochmal dazu aufgefordert ein Tip zu geben.Dieser hat allerdings von uns nichts mehr erhalten, da wir bereits die Snacks und das Getränk für ihn gekauft hatten.An sich ist die Tour insgesamt ganz schön und in Ordnung, wenn die genannten Minuspunkte nicht wären.
Lamentable exploitation d'un endroit qui se voudrait pourtant magique, j'ai bien peur qu'il n'en reste plus rien d'ici quelques années !

This is a typical tourist attraction. While the scenery is beautiful, it is no where near Trang An in terms of beauty. The trip is supposed to last 2hrs but really only lasts about 1hr. They make you pay for entry through the caves despite the fact that the caves aren't avoidable and you have to go through these to do the route. Then they add the cost of the boat on top. There is only really one waterway so it's crowded and it's difficult to take a picture without any other boats around. At the half way point you are asked to buy your boat man/lady a drink. They insist until you do and sell the drinks to you twice as much as they would be anywhere else.Then at the end, our boat lady asked for a tip. We were always going to tip her but the fact that she asked really put us off. She also refused the original tip we offered her and asked for more. I wouldn't bother with Tam Coc, spend money at Trang An instead, it's very special.

Le site est magique ,mais il y a une forme de surexploitation. Trop de barques sur l eau et forcément l idee du paradis terrestre prend un coup !!!lLa ballade est aussi ponctuée de pauses payantes pour le rameurs ....C est pas la meilleure visite de mon séjour.

Landskabet er utrolig smukt men hele roturen er en turistmaskine designet til at få flest penge ud af dig! Forvent at blive bedt om at købe alt for dyre drikkevarer undervejs til roeren + at give drikkepenge til sidst. Personalet er meget aggresive, hvis man ikke giver noget (vi havde allerede betalt entrepris). Turen er meget lang (1,5 time) og vi anbefaler man cykler rundt (meget sjovere og billigere) i stedet for at tage en båd!

The boat ride is 2 hours, and beautiful, through rice fields and mountains and 3 caves. Stunning surroundings. However, I visited Trang An the day before and did Route 1.First of all the scenery is even better, and the caves are absolutely awesome - 9 in total! But also, there were no boat vendors overcharging or hassling you to buy drinks and the lady that rows your boat does not ask you fit any tips or try to sell you crafts.At tam Coc, we bought a drink from a boat vendor and one for the lady who was on our boat as it was a hot day and it cost 150000 dong!Secondly, she then stopped to try to get us to buy her crafts. And finally at the end she constantly asked for a tip - even suggesting how much - 200000 dong which is the same cost as the trip!!I wouldn’t go there again and I wouldn’t recommend it to anyone either.

Un très beau site qui perd de sa superbe du fait du trop grand nombre de bateaux qui y naviguent et des conditions de travail des rameurs...Fin de parcours avec arrêt obligatoire chez les vendeurs ambulants, trop insistants. Un très joli site mais au final pas une expérience aussi majestueuse qu’elle ne devrait l’être.

Después de hacer el recorrido por Trang An, el recorrido en bote por Tam Coc es muy normalito, por no decir malo, la vista es bonita, pero la atención en la taquilla y el embarque es mala, a mitad del recorrido te intentan vender comida para ti o la remera casi que obligado, después ella misma te intenta vender productos y al final la propina “voluntaria”, nada de esto pasó en el recorrido por Trang An. Ahh y el precio es el mismo! Por lo que mi recomendación es: si tienes tiempo y 200.000 Dong escoge hacer alguno de los otros dos recorridos que ofrece Trang An.
Not ready for prime time but ok if you're in to the hostel and homestay thing. Trang An is a better boat trip.
Not nice ,na trang much better,tam coc is worthy visiting in april/may when rice fields are actually green.And also people trying to lure You into their private parkings.

Les paysages pourraient être magnifiques mais le site est dans un nuage de pollution à cause  des usines de ciment et cela gâche complètement la vue, on ne voit pas grand chose à plus de 100m... tellement dommage

Went on this boat ride during a bike ride set up by the place we were staying. On one hand, this is a way to provide a source of income to some very poor women in the area. They provide a service which is very physically taxing. However, the experience is not super pleasant. First, the views are beautiful, but nothing like Trang An. Second, the rower gets to a turn around point and spends 5-10 minutes going through tons of buckets of things to sell. It feels so awkward, you are in the middle of nowhere with her, and she refuses to take no for an answer. Finally, she rows back and right before she drops you off she stops rowing and asks for her tip. So, I understand that this is her livelihood and that this is her business- however, it is simply uncomfortable. Trang An does not have the selling on the boat and you can choose to tip (which we of course did) without pressure. Also, it is 100 times more beautiful. Overall- I think you must balance the pros and cons of this operation.

I would really not recommend this boat trip.. First of all, the price for the tickets was much more expensive than it said on the sign, and they could not explain why.. The lifewests was old and none of them fittet us! Second, was we rushed into the boat and no one seemed very welcomming! So the trip started out a bit bad, and the atmosphere was not good... But in the chill and pretty surroundings, we could not stay mad for long! And the trip was nice, until we came to the end, where people in boats tried to sell us things, we could give to the boatrider.. We said we would tip later instead, and they seemed satisfied with that. My sister and I had agreed about how much we would give, but in the end of the trip, we couldn’t decide it ourself, as she ASKED for the tip and said that she wanted 50.000 from each of us.. After how we was treated, and that we allready had paid way more than we though we should, this was just too much, but we gave the money and left the place very dissapointed...

Poor mans Hau Long Bay. Overcharged for the Sanpan ride. No Life Jackets that would fit and all others broken. The water is polluted. The scenery is good but better value elsewhere. Not wot he the two hour each way from Hanoi.

Not recommended if you have only 1 day tour at Ninh Binh. & there are people come sell drinks & foods, & take photos (approach you to buy after tour) felt quite annoying because the motive I went there were to enjoy the quiet nature.

Beautiful scenery however the man who  was rowing on our boat stopped just before we got back to shore and changed over to an old lady. He asked for a tip which was expected because he rowed us probably 5.5km but we had also bought his a drink, fruit and snacks at half way. The older lady that he swapped with then asked us for another tip before she would let us off the boat!! I feel this was extremely rude of her and also of him at the complete disregard that we had already paid 150 000d for his snacks and red bull drink. It is such a shame that such a beautiful experience was then tainted by the incredible rudeness of these locals. Incredibly disappointed and unfortunately I will not be recommending friends to take this boat ride. They would be better off going to Halong Bay to see the rock formations.

Parto col dirvi che circa 100/150 metri prima potrebbe esserci una persona con un gabbiotto in mezzo alla strada che esce e vi ferma dicendo che i turisti non possono passare con il motorino ma devono parcheggiare lì...Assolutamente NON VERO! Tra l'altro vi sparerà un prezzo triplicato rispetto al solito e se farete finta di andarvene vi proporrà il prezzo "giusto". In realtà potete arrivare con il motorino vicino alla biglietteria e parcheggiare lungo la strada o se pagherete un parcheggio almeno sarete vicini.- se andate in orari di punta le barche saranno molte quindi non aspettatevi niente di romantico.- il giro in barca è lungo il fiume, arriva in uno snodo dove sarete assaliti da venditori di cibo e bevande, se dite che non volete niente cercheranno di farvi comprare cmq qualcosa da bere per la rematrice. - ed eccoci alla rematrice....che pretenderà una mancia, con fare sgarbato ed aggressivo se non lasciate quanto vuole lei.- lungo il percorso sarete affiancati anche da barchette con fotografo che cercherà di vedervi una foto scattata senza che lo abbiate chiesto.Insomma il posto sarebbe anche carino, ma l'aggressività commerciale rovina assolutamente tutto.Molto meglio Trang An, sia come paesaggio sia come atteggiamenti

Started off ok, guide was rowing with his feet which was impressive plus pointed out where to take photos and took some of us. Went through three caves and the got to the end of the river. Several women in sampans selling drinks, snacks and lotus flowers. One blocked us in and was very pushy so felt pressured to buy our guide a drink and snack. But then he asked for a tip at the end. Had he not been so persistent,  along with the boat ladies we would have given it to him. Completely different experience to Trang An, wasn’t very enjoyable after that. If you get to pick one dont do this one. We only really did it as had time to do it as been here three days.

I took a stroll through the village which I hadn't visited for some months. I noticed the result of extensive building activities consisting in numerous new tourist premises. Restaurants, accommodations and shops. Tam Coc is now considered to be the tourist center of the Ninh Bình area and so overtook the city of Ninh Bình. There is almost nothing left which reminds of the former Vietnamese village, all is dedicated to tourism business. Different little super markets offer all the foreign products the tourist wishes for. An ATM is finally to be found to supply the guests with the money to spend in Tam Coc. Many restaurants try to attract the passers by. The merchant attitude in Tam Coc has overwhelmed the usual Vietnames hospitality. One example of many: I called in a place  along the main street with outside chairs which offered popular local beer on draught. Disappointed I was, when the glass, filled 2/3 arrived after 20 minutes although the place was not busy. The owner took it back to fill it up as I requested. The price which the Tam Coc publicans have all in common was 12.000 VND, compared to usual 5.000 VND in the city and all places frequented by locals.

Le site de Tam Coc est sur fréquenté par des rabatteurs. Ici on va essayer de vous faire payer tout et n'importe quoi : le parking à vélo (qui n'est pas un vrai parking), les vêtements, les boissons.. On est abordé de partout. Le tour en bateau est agréable et avec un jolie paysage mais un peu gâché par le reste. On vous propose de payer pour se protéger du soleil, pour prendre une photo, enfin bref partout et tout le temps et de manière peu commode... Dommage

We came to this canoe tour by accident. We were actually looking for Bich Dong Pagoda which we were not sure whether it included a boat ride to reach the pagoda or not. We got to this area and saw a lot of commotion going on so we thought this may have been where the pagoda was. We turned into a parking lot to try to figure out where we were and some ladies were yelling at us to park over by them. Everything happened so fast and we got sucked into it. The ladies charged us for parking and went on to insist that we leave our belongings on our motorbike because "it is safe and no one will take anything"-- obviously we did not leave anything behind... I would never leave my belongings anywhere I thought was safe out in the public at home so why would I do it here? After we paid for parking, the lady insisted we pay for the boat ride with her. I refused because we were nowhere near boats. Why would I pay someone who is across the street in a parking lot for a boat ride on the other side of the road? After I refused, she led me to the boats and I paid the lady who rowed the boat. It was 200k for each of us, 400k total. We gave her 500k and the parking lady and the boat lady both insisted that they keep the 100k for tip WELL BEFORE WE EVEN GOT INTO THE BOAT. A tip is earned, not expected. I demanded that I receive my change. I always plan to tip if the person has given me great service, especially the ladies who have to row you in a boat for hours with their own two arms--it looks very tiring! I thought it was incredibly inappropriate to suggest they keep the rest of my money like that before we ever set foot in the boat.At this point, I felt really uncomfortable the whole boat ride. It was just the 2 of us and the lady rowing the boat. My mom (a native of Vietnam) warned me about people trying to scam me and warned me of the dangers of getting on a boat in the middle of nowhere with a non-reputable tour service as they might try to scheme and scam you for money or they will not take you back to the dock. Although the danger part was not 100% true, I will go on to tell you what happens as the boat tour continued.With what my mom said on my mind, I felt unsafe for a good 20-30 minutes as I had just told both these ladies I did not want them to keep the change they so desperately tried to keep from me--not off to a good start. A while in, the scenery was beautiful (Tam Coc experience somewhat redeemed) and I saw many ducks and house structures along the way. Towards the end, we enter a cave that was completely dark and only lit by the rower's head light (not as legitimate of an operation as Trang An). Rowing into any cave is an amazing experience and this was only one of the good parts of this trip. On the other side of the cave was a round about rest stop surrounded by rice paddies, mountains and water buffalos with herons/cranes on their back. An amazing view... ruined by many other boats of tourists who were all getting massages forced on them--it was clear it was another ploy to trick money out of the tourists. You know, offer up something nice so the tourist thinks you're being friendly but at the end demand money for your services. It was an awkward sight to see and I knew exactly what was going on there which made me immediately fear that our rower would do the same--she did not. I was surprised! I feel like possibly we were given a pass on this scheme because I spoke Vietnamese and had an idea of what was going on. I didn't get off scott free though. The lady had a bag full of goods with her and proceeded to ask me to buy some items from her. When I said that I did not need anything, she kept pushing and pushing me to buy something. She let off forcing the goods on me and we sat and enjoyed the scenery for a few minutes. We headed back through the cave and started our journey back. (The whole trip was about 1.5 hours by the way). On the way back, the lady became more friendly with me and started talking to me about her family and asked me about mine. I conversed with her and finally felt comfortable being on the boat ride. My boyfriend (who does not speak Vietnamese) decided to tip her 70k so that the weird lingering tension of not receiving a tip would ease up--also he did not want the ladies at the dock involved with this tip. The lady thanked him. She continues talking to me and as we got closer she started up again about buying items from her. I never gave in. Then when we finally were in the last 5-10 minutes of our boat ride, she slowed down almost not rowing anymore and said to me "the american gave me 70k for a tip, what are you going to give me?" E X C U S E M E? what did she just say? I proceeded to tell her that that tip was from both my boyfriend and I and she said that I needed to give her money also. I could not believe the nerve of this lady! I thought we were having a nice time after all but this was deemed to be untrue! She kept insisting that I gave her more money and that the 70K was not from me and that it was from "the american (AKA my boyfriend)". She told me if I gave her the 100k change from earlier, she would give me back the 70K that my boyfriend had given her and that would be OK. I do not need someone telling me what an acceptable tip is and whether I need to give them more or not. I was livid by the time we docked and she is chasing us off the boat insisting I give her more money. At this point I did not give her the time of day anymore and continued walking--of course it doesn't end here. We were chased down by a man who took our photo in the beginning (who stands IN the river) and is pushing photos on us and demanding we buy them. We kept saying no and he chased us all the way across the street to our motorbike and is shoving them onto my boyfriend--granted they were not that expensive but by the time you have been asked to tip someone and buy things from them for the past 1.5 hours we were not having it.OVERALL, the scenery is beautiful and the boat ride would have been nice if all of this didn't happen but it did and therefore the two stars is for the great location but not for the trip itself. We had a terrible experience and it really put a damper on our day--this was the first time we were schemed like this and we had been in Vietnam for about a week and a half by this time so it was just a different side of Vietnam to experience. The previous day we went to Trang An and went on a pleasant 3 hour boat ride with a woman who was much nicer and never requested a tip from us--of course we tipped her and she was incredibly gracious. We have no problem spending a little money or tipping but when someone decides those things for us, it really leaves a sour taste in your mouth. PLEASE SAVE YOUR MONEY AND GO TO TRANG AN INSTEAD--similar scenery and more to see/stops on the route and pleasant rowers. If you want to see Tam Coc, I believe you can see an aerial view from the Mua Caves stairs. Plus, the Mua Cave view is spectacular.

The scenery is breathtaking and really enjoyed the boat ride and it was super neat how they rowed the boats however it was completely ruined by some ladies on the boat rowing and using, playing or whatever on their phones and especially the ladies selling snacks when you reach then end. You get super pressured and feel very uncomfortable that your rower needs a snack and she's super tired so you must pay for her. The lady rowing the boat was friendly until we didn't  go for the pressured snacks, then she completely stopped talking to us and then started asking for her tip before we were even finished the tour. I gave her a decent tip and she wanted more which is unreasonable. I get it they are trying to make as much as they can but we paid a lot for this tour which includes the boat ride and we are trying to enjoy ourselves.We are going to tip regardless, don't make us fell bad and uncomfortable in a tour that we are trying to enjoy. The area is exploited and very touristy, it's a shame what it has become.

Just finished the tour and i feel the urge to complain now:Although the landscape was really nice, we felt that the price we paid at the ticket office was  more or less ok.But at the end of the tour, there were 5 or more women waiting on their boats to sell snacks and drinks. We politely said we dont need anyting but the old lady asker me to handover something to our boat driver which i did. Then she wanted me to pay 100k dong for a can of soda, some peanuts and some slices of pinapple.I complained but she told me thats the tipp everyone buys for their driver so after some discussions i gave her the money and we returned home.After we returned she asked for more money but we told her we tipped 100k in food already and left.Also there were women waiting on the way asking to sell pictures of us. Better take a bike and visit the area yourself.

Ab Tam Coc hunderte von Booten in Einerkolonne. Abzocke. Beim Pier in Trang An auch viele (vor allem asiatische) Touristen. Aber keine Abzocke. Es werden keine Waren verkauft, Trinkgeld ist absolut freiwillig.Wer weg vom Massentourismus eine Bootstour machen will, sollte in Linh Coc (ca. 7 km ausserhalb von Tam Coc) ein Boot besteigen. Einfach wunderbar und so ruhig. Ganz wenige Boote fahren durch eine wilde, ursprüngliche Landschaft und u.a. durch eine sehenswerte, grosse Grotte. Es hat dort keine Lichter, der Bootsführer gab uns eine Taschenlampe. Sicher nicht so spektakulär wie andere Touren und Reisfelder gibts dort auch nicht. Uns wars egal, wir haben diese Idylle genossen. 1,5 Std. für 200'000 Dong für 2 Personen.

I will start by saying that, in my opinion, Tam Coc is nowhere near as spectacular as Trang An, and if I was going to choose between the two, I would definitely do Trang An.Our rower was a friendly older gentleman, who happily posed for photos, which turned out to be most fortunate (more about this later).  We were accosted by the lady trying to sell us overpriced hot beer and snacks for our parched and starving rower, and we politely and repeated declined and she eventually gave up. We enjoyed the rest of our trip until we got back to the steps/dock.  As we pulled up, the rower fell as he attempted to get out of the boat.  My husband stepped out of the boat to assist him regain his footing.  There was also a lady waiting there for us to collect the life jackets, as we were one of the last boats for the day and she was in a rush to get out of there.  In the confusion my husband left his phone on the seat (it was out of his pocket as he was taking a photo when the rower fell).  The rower quickly took off in the boat and made no attempt to return the phone to us, even though it was sitting on the seat in front of him.  Once back at our hotel, the staff attempted to contact the organisation that does the boat tours, but without success.  Fortunately the next morning my husband was able to go down and see them, show them the photo and demand to see the rower.  He turned up, pretended to know nothing until police were mention, then miraculously had the phone in his pocket and demanded a ridiculous tip for returning the phone.  While I realise it was our error leaving the phone on the seat, it was a very negative experience.

El paseo en barca dura hora y poco. Reman locales con los brazos o con las piernas y da un poco de pena porque estan destrozados. Encima intentan dar lastima con eso y pedirte propina (la palabra TIP es la q mejor se saben).El paseo en si esta bien, las vistas son bonitas.Hacia el final del recorrido, antes de dar la vuelta y volver al punto de salida (el recorrido no es circular sino de ida y vuelta), hay varias barcas llenas de comida con señoras que intentan venderte. De hecho, alguna te intenta forzar a que le compres algo como el tip para la persona que te esta llevando en barca y esa situacion es bastante desagradable. Lo mejor que puedes hacer es regatear, porque te intentan colar varios productos y es casi imposible decirle que no. Nosotros nos quedamos solo con un paquete de galletas para nuestra “barquera”.
