Y aun asi, al final del trayecto nos volvio a pedir propina (ahi si que nos negamos rotundamente).

First of all, you are charged for 3 different things just to go take the tour. The landscape looked a bit dirty. I had done Trang An the day before, and Trang An was better looked after that Tam Coc.What made the experience disappointing was the boat driver that we were assigned. He seemed nice at the beginning until we understood it was all about the money - literally. He started rowing fast passing other boats. It looked like a joke until we realized he was rowing fast because the faster he went, the faster he would finish the tour and take other people in; so it wasn't the peaceful ride we expected at all, he even was a risk for other boats because he was going too close and sometimes hitting other  boats in the run. At the furthest point of the ride, we stopped in front of some women who were selling food at their boats and who openly suggested to buy food and a drink for the driver (called by his name) as a tip (?). Next, while rowing, he opened a box and tried to sell us some souvenirs he had hidden. Finally, when we were arriving to the end, he ASKED for 5$ tip. We told him we didn't have dollars, we would leave him a tip in the local currency and he INSISTED in giving him dollars, what at this point was rude and a bit abusive fro him side. When we got off the boat we offered a tip in VND that he rejected with bad manners. What could have been a nice experience, turned to be awkward first and then disappointing for one person (but I don;t think it was the only one)I wouldn't recommend Tam Coc for a boat ride. I did Trang An the day before and it was a much better experience.

Attention aux arnaques à l'addition au restaurant. Très touristique et on se demande pourquoi. Balade en bateau à la queue leu leu comme à la sécu... Marché pauvre. Beaucoup de français. Bref on ne se croirait pas au Vietnam à part les ordures.
short river cruise (approx 1 hour), not much scenery, water is dirty and the caves is dark (total darkness) Trang An is at least 3x better at almost the same price.

This is not a boat tour, it's just a boat ride, as the person rowing doesn't tell you anything about Tam Coc Grottoes. Don't go in Winter. The photos you see of the rice field lined river don't exist in Winter there all flooded. There is also mist everywhere so you can't get any good photos. If you do decide to go in Winter go in the afternoon, so the mist is less. The three grottoes, Ca, Hai and Ba are just tunnels through the small hills. I wasn't very impressed, either with the boat ride or price. Cost 270,000₫. Feb.2018. Trang An Boat Ride is much better.

Je précise avant tout que nous avons fait la visite en début janvier...clairement pas le temps de voir des rizières d'un vert éclatant ! Mais c'était sympa aussi avec une légère brume. Nous avons commencés la visite vers 15h40, du coup on a vu tous les bateaux des touristes revenir au port et avoir la visite pour nous seules quasiment. Visite qui a duré 1h30. Honnêtement... C'était moyen. Je tiens à préciser que le passage sous les 3 grottes n'a rien d'extraordinaire, ça ne dure que quelques secondes et il n'y a rien à voir. Excepté le magnifique décors des collines et la capitaine ramer avec ses pieds. Qui nous a d'ailleurs répété le mot 'tips' un nombre incalculable de fois... Elle a même stoppé le bâteau à une 20taines de mètres du port on a cru qu'elle voulait son pourboire sinon elle ne bougerait pas..finalement non, on est rentré sans accros et n'avons rien laissé, elle ne nous a pas embêté plus que ça.Nous n'y avons sûrement pas été au meilleur moment.Tarif: 150000d bateau pour deux, 120000d pour l'entrée.

Let it be clear, it really IS beautiful. The scenery is stunning. But ...If you can share the boat with a few people (we were 3) then it's a fair price and worth it. Until you reach the turning point where you meet some vendors in boat that trick you into buying some stuff (drink, snack) for your rower. You don't want to be rude to the rower by refusing, right ? Of course too expensive.And at that point it's not worth the money anymore.But that's not enough ... then when you get back the rower insists on a tip ... And not a 20000 dong tip (what we tried to give to get rid of her), but it has to be at least 50000 dong and not one single dong less ...So in the end you leave with a very bad feeling ...Along the way they also make some pictures that you can buy when you return, but fortunately there they don't insist on buying and you can even bargain a little.

J'ai mis 2 pour les paysages, bien que Lan Ha et Ha Long soient nettement plus spectaculaires. Nous savions où nous allions mettre nos pieds. Nous n'avons pas été déçu. On arrive à 6h45 pour partir les premiers au départ à 7h. Bonne option. Personne devant nous avant que le "train touristique" n'entre en fonction. Au guichet, une personne nous informe des prix. Ils sont plus chers pour les étrangers. Cela ne me choque pas car cela se fait dans beaucoup de pays. L'organisation mafieuse impose 2 étrangers par barque quand les Vietnamiens peuvent être à 4 ou 5. Là, cela devient gênant, car en plus de payer "l'entrée", il faut payer la barque. (120 000 VND/adulte, 60 000/enfant -1m40, 150 000 VND/barque). Nous sommes à 2 adultes et 2 enfants de 7 et 9 ans. On demande à avoir 1 seul barque pour ne pas dispatcher la famille. Réponse : OK mais il faut quand même payer la seconde que nous n'utiliserons pas!!! Inflexible, nous n'avons pas le choix sinon celui d'y renoncer. Comme nous y étions tôt, les vendeuses agressives n'avaient pas encore pris possession des lieux. On en croise sur le chemin du retour qui se mettent en travers pour nous empêcher de passer. Inflexible à notre tour , elles nous jettent des regards noirs. À l'arrivée notre rameur nous demande un pourboire. Nous refusons d'abord, mais comme il a été plutôt sympathique et qu'il n'a pas essayé de nous vendre quoique ce soit, on lui donne 8 000 VND. Certains réclament 100 000 VND!!! Bref, prenez un vélo et arpentez les rizières. Les paysages sont identiques avec la tranquillité en plus.

We visited Tam Coc with a tour group, and had read several reviews before hand. We were prepared for some things that occurred. Life jackets are required before entering the boat. Most boats had 1-3 people depending on size and weight. Very impressed that the boat rowers could row with their feet, probably to conserve energy. The views of the river, rocks and small caves were very peaceful. We noticed our rower and others had difficulty with more people or heavier loads and some were helping their rowers. Our boat seemed slower and we could tell she was working hard rowing in the heat. My boyfriend and I both offered to help her row together. There was a boat that came by the take our picture and we said no several times. She took it anyways. Then we got to the boats with drinks  and treats. We said no several times and the vendor asked us to buy for the rower. I offered her a bottle of water there, and she refused. Several minutes returning back, she accepted it. We also offered her another one. We continued to help her road most of the way back. Once we arrived, we got out and offered her a very generous tip (more than 50k dong.) She asked for double since it was a difficult row in beginning. We wavered, but gave in since it was hot. Then the photo person badgered us to  buy two photos we did not want. (I was literally waiving no in one). It ended up being 2/40k dong, so we just paid it to get away. The sales person only gave me part of my change, and I stood and waited for remaining, and she finally gave me correct amount. It was an interesting experience but the last part with tip and photos left a bad taste in our mouth. Would recommend going but beware of scams and stay firm.  It is similar to Halong Bay, but glad we did both.

I joined a local tour and paid usd30 for non buffet lunch. 1 day tour. We had cycling and boat cruise.Markwtimg photos are definitely deceiving. My toue was very disappointing. 1) fields are not as green2) boat ladies asked for tips repeatedly for more than 20 times, so disturbing and annoying. She asked for 50k dong each. A tot of 100k dong for 2 of us. Ridiculous!Overall i did not enjoy  tam coc as much as i expected. If u had limited time, is much better do halomg bay istead of tam coc.

This place is about taking money from the tourists. Short (though nice) loop ride on the river, where you are forced to buy something from boat sellers (even if you need nothing). Though you pay a ticket for boat and service, the oarsman asks for a high tip. We were really disappointed.I really advice to go to Trang An, which is much bigger, nicer and less agressive on tourists.

die gegend ist schön, aber anstatt einer bootsfahrt besser mit dem fahrrad zwischen den felsen fahren!der ort ist sehr auf tourismus ausgelegt, die leute dort haben uns eher als wandelndes portemonnaie wahrgenommen als als menschen. besonders auf dem boot wird man abgezogen, da verkaufen sie aus anderen booten heraus getränke, bei denen sich der preis kurzerhand ändert und die bootsfahrerin hat kurz vor ende der tour ein horrendes trinkgeld eingefordert.

I'll start off by saying either doing this before trang an or not at all and go looking down from mua viewpoint. After what was an unbelievable experience at trang an, myself and my girlfriend decided to give tam coc a go as well. It didn't start great with not just us paying per person but paying for an "entry fee" as well. It was going well for a while but the traffic on the water even at 9 am was quite busy, but I can't really fault that since it is a tourist attraction. Having people on boats taking pictures of us to try sell to us after was off putting, again, nothing like that in trang an. The kicker came at the end off the first leg We were unavoidably brought over to the ladies selling food and drink on their boats to buy stuff off them but we couldn't move, which really annoyed me. We declined many times but she then insisted we should buy something for the lady rowing which I had less of a problem as they do work very hard. Anyway, we got her a drink and peanuts for around 50k, which was far too much for what it was. But as we were turning to leave, I glanced back to our rower HANDING EVERYTHING BACK to the food lady. I was just finished after that. Myself and my girlfriend were no longer enjoying the trip and were miserable and pissed off with 3km left. We got back eventually and off course the request for a tip, I just signalled it was the "lunch" we bought her earlier and got off. If it hadn't been for her greed it would've been more profitable for her. Again, the trang an rower was phenomenal and wished we could go back and give her more after this experience. It was just a far more enjoyable experience in trang an without people trying to sell you something at every corner and far longer too. The scenery was beautiful of course but far better from the mua lookout.

닌빈에서 옛 건축물들과 사원을 보고 땀콕으로 이동 후 보트투어, 자전거투어를 진행했습니다. 원조인지 확신할수는 없지만 신투어리스트에서 예약했고 일인 4만원정도 하는 디럭스(중간급)을 예약했급니다 밥은 맛없고 버스는 그저 그런 버스였지만 가이드는 영어실력이 좋았고 설명을 잘해줬습니다.그러나 땀콕투어를 비추천하는 이유는 첫번째로 보트투어에서의 과도한 팁 요구와 두번째로 더위때문입니다.보트투어에서의 경치는 정말 아름다웠지만 사진 강매, 음식 강매와 뱃사공 아주머니의 과한 팁 요구와 기념품 판매로 아주 기분이 찝찝하고 안좋은 상태로 투어를 끝낼수밖에 없었습니다.  원래 사진과 음식을 구매하지 않는대신 팁을 넉넉히 드리려고 했던 생각과 달리 사진과 음식을 구매하지 않으려고 하자 뒤에서 계속 강요하시는 뱃사공 아주머니 때문에 사진과 음식에 5달러 가량을, 아주머니의 기념품 판매가 팁 대신인줄 알았던 덕분에 기념품 구매에 5달러를 지출했습니다.  필요없었던 기념품을 비싸게 주고 구입한것은 팁 개념으로 생각했기 때문이고 끝나갈 때쯤 좋은 마음으로 2.5달러를 더 드렸더니 강가의 마을주민에게 조롱하듯이 지폐를 흔들며 뭐라 외치시더니 바로 이건 한사람분 팁이니 2.5달러를 더달라고 하시는 터에 정말 어이가 없었습니다. 결론은 자전거밖에는 좋은 기억이 안남아서 2명이 지불했던 한화 8만원 가량의 돈이 참 아까운 투어였습니다

We were on a day trip with some vietnamese friends. We payed 4 times as much for a boat ride than they did! There are different prices for foreigners and vietnamese people. We have been in Vietnam for 6 month and this was a scam😣

Uno de los lugares más turísticos de Vietnam. Comparado con Ha Long Bay no es nada especial. Intentan sacarte dinero en todo momento. La duración del trayecto es de 1.5h. Sería perfecto si te dejasen tranquilo, pero están encima tuya todo el tiempo para que compres cosas o des propinas. Una experiencia no muy agradable. Si estáis considerando hacerlo, id a Trang An. El paisaje es muy parecido pero es muchísimo más tranquilo. Eso sí, dura entre 3 y 4 horas.

Found the river in a sorry state..unfortunately smog from surrounding cement factories dulls the atmosphere. The boat trip is a bit long(90 minutes) and can get boring. The saddest part is the vendors at the end of the river almost forcing you to buy things. Luckily our tour guide made us aware of them. They will ask you to buy water for the person rowing the boat and will later resell. There will also be people along the river trying to take photos of you and then give you an album at the end and abuse you to buy it so don't smile for any photos. The person rowing the boat will also harass you for a tip. Considering all the other beautiful spots in Vietnam I wouldn't consider this a must-see owing to the harassment by the locals and sorry state of the environment (we saw dead fish floating in the river). The surrounding rice fields nearby and temples are beautiful though and worth seeing.

We DID NOT do the Tam Coc boat tour because it was very crowded at that time (Tet holiday) but we did find a very good alternative... If you drive 2/3 km further on the road towards the Bich Dong temple and you will find a little river at your right hand side with the same boats as in Tam Coc. No waiting queues at the time and we paid 300000VND for a boat for the 5 of us (2adults/3kids). On our almost 2 hour trip we came across 6 (!) other boats, wonderful views of the typical karst scenery and we went through 2 caves... It was great, very friendly boatwoman and so much peace and quiet! We had a great time and we would like to recommend this place before it gets as crowdy as Tam Coc or Trang An...

We took a day trip to Ninh Binh from Hanoi, one of the stops was this Tam Coc floating boat attraction. It's a 2 hour ride.The sights were beautiful, the mountains, water and caves. It was very interesting to see the people row with their feet, amazing. It's a huge tourist attraction, nothing really authentic about it (other than having locals row for you). This isn't their way of living, it's really is a long ride for tourists. Along the ride, you can see some lodging, and a lot of big hotels in construction. This will become more westernized than it already is. Our guy was the slowest rower of all, though he was very young. Everyone from our group passed us, and when we arrived back, everyone was already ready to go. At the half way point, when everyone turns around, there are a dozen floating vendors who force you to buy from them. We had just eaten lunch before so we didn't really care for anything. When we refused to buy, the lady put a bunch of stuff on our boat and said "you buy for him for tip". We said ok, he smiled and said ok. Just before arriving, he slowed down even more and kept saying "tip sir! tip! tip!". We knew we had to tip and were ready for it, but it was our first time in our trip that someone demanded tip.This 2 hour ride was too long. I guess it's worth seeing because the scenery is so unique, but the experience in itself wasn't pleasant. We wouldn't do it again.

Wij vroegen voor 1 boot met 4 personen. Was niet mogelijk werd ons gezegd en we moesten 2 boten betalen!! Onderweg kwamen we bootjes tegen met 4 personen en zelfs 1 met 5...Toen we naderhand verhaal gingen halen, werd ons verteld dat dat Vietnamese mensen waren. In mijn ogen, mag dat geen verschil zijn...
We did Trang An first and it was amazing. Tam Coc boat ride was poor with them making you food and asking for a tip. The water was dirty and brown. Do Trang An it was beautiful.

On a tous vu les photos superbes sur internet. Je pensais vraiment vivre une experience unique j'ai ete decu. Tout d'abord le prix 250000 dongs (environ 10 euros) ce qui parrait rien mais vous pouvez faire enormement de choses pour ce prix la. Ensuite arrivé au bout une horde de vendeuses sur barques vous assaillent.  Le calme laisse place a la tempete et si vous refusez vous vous faites incendier. De plus en revenant la femme a la barre du bateau ne cessait de nous repeter le mot tips. Je comptait donner plus a la base mais son incistence m'a fatigué et finalment j'ai diviser la somme initiale par deux. Pour la photo finish qu'on essaye de vous vendre en moyenne 30 000 je l'ai eu a 10 000 apres avoir demandé a un local combien il avait payé. En gros payer un peu plus oui servire de pompe a fric et etre vu comme un porte feuilles moyen ... Le paysage reste superbe neanmoins. (Ne pas y aller a partir d'aout le riz est coupé et c'est vachement moins beau. Voila

Worst experience we have had in 10 years of month-long visits to Vietnam; we do not recommend this unless you are not bothered by pestering personnel on and off the river.  The ticket sellers insisted that we foreigners ride in a boat by ourselves, NOT accompanied by a Vietnamese-speaking person, and not given a boat driver who spoke English.  .Our boat paddler actually stopped in the middle of the river and demanded "tip, tip, tip"; we signaled to take us to shore.  When we got there and I  got out, he physically held my husband's wrist until he handed him some dong.  Onshore I was hounded by a photo seller who demanded that I had asked her to take photos (which I hadn't), and also that we buy all the photos she had taken; we walked away, she followed.  We finally threatened to call the police and she left us alone.  Any natural tranquil beauty we enjoyed on the boat trip was negated by this treatment.

Compared to Trang An, Tam Coc is expensive, you pay an entrance fee per person and a separate ticket for a boatride.The caves are not spectacular, compared to Trang An. At the end, a tourist trap awaits, it's good to buy a drink, but your rower will have a wishlist too and the salesperson forces that on you. It took a bit of trouble to put half of it back. And at the end of the boatride, he still dares to ask for a tip and complain that we on't tip well enough.When you think you';ve had it all, they already printed a photo taken of you and it takes quite a bit of effort to say no to that.If you don't have time for Trang An, this might be fun I guess.

Un conseil à tous les voyageurs tam coc est moins agréable que Trang an qui contient plus de 9 grottes alors que tam coc n'en possède que 3!Le trajet dure plus longtemps et il y a même des endroits assez frais en haute saison. A tam coc on se fait harceler dans les vendeurs ce qui rend la visite désagréable.

aparte de ser un dia lluvioso, no nos dejaban tranquilos, hay complot entre las demas barcas, el señor nso hiba diciendo que tenia calor pero el dia era fresco, te pedia agua para que le scompres y luego las devuelven y se reparten el dinero, al finalizar el trayecto querian una propina y nos estuvieron siguiendo hasta el restaurante, fué imposible, les dimos però les parecia poco despues de haber pagado el trayecto que no fue barato, el dia no ayudó a las vistas, lástima que lo estropeasen de esa manera, fue una pena por que me lo esperaba con entusiasmo y no me quedaron ganas de volver.

Very disappointing! Not worth the price or the time. Though the scenery is pretty you don't get a chance to get off the boat. When you get to the turnaround point you are swamped with boats trying to sell you water, soda, excetera and the drivers don't leave until you buy something.   There will be a man near a bridge taking photographs, when you return you will have someone hound you until you buy them. We ended up buying them because they were very good photos. If you want to do a boat ride the better choice is Trang An.

We hebben hier een boottocht gedaan. Intree 120.000D per persoon Ga er vroeg naar toe dan is het heerlijk rustig en vreedzaam. Deel een boot (150.000D per boot) met anderen Ze zeggen NO maar dat wordt alleen gedaan om meer geld te verdienen.Boottocht was erg mooi. Jammer dat gids om geld vroeg 100.000 voor mij en 100.000D voor mijn man. Onderweg komen ze met bootjes naar je toe met bloemen (heel mooi) en dan de vraag :Drinken? Nee ...ohh maar de gids wil wel!!Nee te commercieel ! Ze ziet hier 3 grottenWe hebben van diverse mensen gehoord dat de Trang an grotten 9 stuks! Mooier waren minder toeristisch en een stuk minder duur. Ook hier weer sharing boats!

De tocht per roeiboot op zich is schitterend en indrukwekkend,let alleen op als je op het punt aankomt waar de boot omdraait want daar worden woekerprijzen gevraagd en sommige kunnen best onbeschoft zijn.Maar laat dat je niet weerhouden om de dure boottocht te doen
Comparing to trang Ann that delivers the same experience ( butt better) tam coc offered us a drunk (but cheerful ) boat driver that never stopped laughing while mumbling the points along.

Es mucho mejor ir a Trang An que a este lugar. Aquí te cobran 120.000 por la entrada y ADEMÁS 150.000 por el bote .. Una barbaridad de caro si lo comparas con todo lo que se hace por la mitad en Trang An.. Aquí es solo un paseo de hora y media, dos Cuevas, ningún templo y la persona que lleva el bote se para al lado de otros botes que venden cosas de 1 dlrs y te presionan a comprar. No se mueven hasta que compres. Nosotros no compramos dijimos que habíamos gastado todo en la entrada. Cuando estás por regresar la persona del bote deja de remar y te pide tip de manera bastante agresiva. Si no le das no se mueve y no termina el viaje. No lo recomiendo para nada.

J avais prévu une balade en barque à Than An mais comme il avait beaucoup plu pendant la nuit, le centre était fermé, le niveau de l eau trop haut ne permettant pas d accéder aux grottes. Je me suis donc rabattue sur Tam Coc.Mon avis est partagé.Tam Coc peut être atteint très facilement à vélo au départ de Ninh Binh. Le cadre est, comme dans toute cette baie d'Ha Long terrestre, exceptionnel mais le touriste est plus qu'attendu. Si vous voyagez seule, attendez quelques minutes au bureau de vente des tickets pour partager les frais de location de la barque. Moi, j ai trouvé deux jeunes et charmantes françaises qui m'ont accueillie sur leur embarcation. Les barques chargées de touristes, parfois bruyants, se suivent à la queue-le-leu. Nous traversons trois grottes. Une fois parvenus au bout du trajet, nous sommes entourés par des barques de vendeuses qui vous demandent, de façon un peu virulente, d acheter des fruits ou des boissons pour revigorer votre rameuse. Notre refus ne fut pas apprécié.Si vous le pouvez, choisissez plutôt Van Lan, plus loin de Ninh Binh mais tellement plus paisible .

Although some parts of Tam Coc are pretty it is over priced for what it is compared with Trang An which is definitely better value. You are hassled by people selling food and drinks and photos while you are trying to enjoy the experience which is completely annoying. It also travels the same route to the caves and back and you only see 3 caves in total.

Have been pretty amazed with the beauty that is Vietnam but incredibly disappointed in what tourism has done to its natural allure. Save yourself the trouble and skip Tam Coc... second biggest tourist trap next to Halong Bay. Locals here are more interested in swindling you than making you feel welcomed. Save yourself the trip and cherish the pictures you see online. You won't have the same opinion once you see it for yourself and look back at your photos only to see herds of Asian tourists with their 30 ft long selfie sticks.

Tam Coc boat tour is the reason I'be know Ninh Binh and decided to come here.I did enjoy the boat tour, passing through 3 caves however I feel that is overated and the floating drink seller can be very persistent.  At the end of the trip before turning back she is asking to buy drink ans fruit for the boat rower and suggest to pay 300,000vnd more if I would like to continue 6km further.

Echt een prachtig gebied en je moet het dan ook zeker gaan bekijken. Houdt er echter rekening mee dat je de verkeerde lui als roeier of roeister kan treffen. Na jou met de hand of de voet anderhalf uur te hebben rond geroeid kun je die mensen best een fooi geven. Maar als er zo nadrukkelijk om gevraagd wordt als in mijn geval; en na even rondgevraagd te hebben was ik zeker niet de enige, dan kunnen ze mij de plomp in. Ik was er toevallig op een dag dat er niets te doen was en misschien lag het daar wel aan. Als dit echter gemeengoed wordt net als het opdringen van foto's en andere rotzooi dan raakt Tam Coc in notime zijn aantrekkingskracht kwijt. Dus mooi gebied en voor de rest moet er eens gesproken worden met die rondvarende zeurpieten.

It seems like the town of Tam Coc are in a scam together, or support other people's scams. We booked a bus/boat/bus from Cat Ba island to Ninh Binh. In fact we paid extra to go to Ninh Binh rather than Tam Coc, however the bus driver who drived like a maniac in heavy rainfall, took us to Tam Coc and hid in someone's kitchen until we went away as we were asking to be taken to Ninh Binh (there were 5 of us all separate travellers having this problem). The other people in the town were either laughing at us or were trying to make money out of the situation by asking if we wanted a taxi or hotel to stay. This was infuriating, and when we finally walked off the bus driver ran out of the restaurant and drove off like a coward! The hotel who he was affiliated with was 'The long hotel', which had the most obnoxious and horrible staff working there who refused to speak English (said they couldn't), until I asked how much a taxi was. All of sudden their English was fluent! The 5 of us then walked 9km to Ninh Binh with all of our luggage. We did have a saving grace of a dinner at Trung Tuyet, and it was plentiful and the family were lovely. The following day we went to Tam Coc for the boat cruise. It was nice, but after a sunset cruise in Halong Bay it didn't match up. We had a male boat driver who didn't speak any English but tried to indicate when there were birds about. Before turning the boat around you go through a final cave where there are lots of people trying to sell you food and drinks. I think it cheapens the whole experience, and the women are persistent so just ignore them. At the end of the trip the boat driver started demanding a tip of 100,000vnd. This annoyed us as you should be able to tip what you like. Due to his demanding, I gave a tip of 15,000vnd instead!All in all we felt that it was nice but not worth the effort to get to. Go to Halong bay instead.

The scenery is great but they try to ripp you off wherever they can. It takes a little more than 30 minutes to reach the end. You can go further but have to pay an extra 200k dong. On top of the 150k for the boat and (in our case) 2x 120k per passenger. At the end they try to sell you food and drinks for a disgraceful amount of money. 200k for 3 sodas and a pack of nuts. "Negotiated" to have just the drinks (one for the driver) for 70k... Then she (the driver) tried to hardsell us her ugly goods (no thanks, no, no, no we said) At the end (same route back) she not asked but demanded a tip... Which we didn't give her, obviously. Rather go to Trang An. Much longer route (2,5 hours), less ricefields but with several pagodas and more (some half-artificial) caves. And a lot cheaper.

Contratamos la excursion y pagamos un dineral por un viaje de porqueria. una vuelta en un bote, una vuelta en bicileta, comida libre de pesima calidad. mejor olvidar. Recomiendo ir al verdadero Vietnam Sinh Tourist (ex sinh cafe) y reservar con ellos son realmente buenos

Die Landschaft bei Tam Coc ist auf jeden Fall sehr sehenswert und einer der Gründe, weshalb ich nach Ninh Binh gereist bin. Am Tag zuvor hatte ich bereits das Höhlensystem Trang An besucht und war sehr beeindruckt.Ich kam morgens früh mit meinem Guide in Tam Coc an. Ich kaufte mir ein Ticket für die Bootsfahrt und war erst einmal irritiert, dass diese 270.000 Dong kostete. (150.000 + 120.000) Es waren zwei Tickets. Laut Auskunft meines Guides eines für die Bootsfahrt und eines für den Blick auf die Landschaft. Für vietnamesische Verhältnisse ist das eine Menge Geld. Mein Guide konnte über diesen Preis nur ungläubig den Kopf schütteln.Die Landschaft war dann wirklich sehenswert. Wir fuhren auch immer wieder durch Höhlen. Diese sind jedoch bei Trang An spektakulärer. Die Fahrt dauerte bis zum Endpunkt dauerte ungefähr 30 Minuten. Hier lagen dann mehrere kleine Boote aus denen Frauen alle möglichen Waren verkauften. Eines von diesen Booten kam sofort auf mein Boot zugerudert. Nun wurde ich auf eine wirklich sehr vietnamuntypische Weise gedrängt irgendwelche Getränke oder Kekse zu kaufen. Meine ersten Verneinungen wurden ignoriert. Mehr noch, die Verkäuferin kam mit ihrem Boot direkt an meines ran. Da ich schon nichts für mich kaufen wollte, schaltete die Verkäuferin jetzt einen psychologischen Gang höher. Ich könnte doch meiner Ruderin jetzt ein Getränk kaufen. Auf solche Spielchen hatte ich schon mal gar keine Lust. Sie zog dann ab und redetet noch ein oder zwei Minuten mit meiner Ruderin. Die beiden kannten sich offensichtlich. Dann fuhren wir wieder zurück. Keine Ahnung, ob die Fahrt wirklich an dieser Stelle schon zu Ende war, oder nur aufgrund meines unwilligen Verhaltens. Ich habe zumindest einige Boote noch weiter fahren sehen.Am Ende der Fahrt erfolgte dann auch noch die Frage nach einem Trinkgeld. Ich habe ihr etwas gegeben, obwohl ich im Nachhinein erfahren habe, dass man dies nicht tuen sollte.Ich möchte an der Stelle betonen, dass ich den Leuten ihr Geld gönne. Aber ich entscheide immer noch selbst, wann ich etwas kaufe bzw. wem ich etwas kaufe.Die Fahrt kann ich deshalb nur bedingt empfehlen. Sicher, die Landschaft ist schön, aber die Fahrt bei Trang An fand ich deutlich spektakulärer. Die Höhlen durch die man dort mit dem Boot fährt, sind niedriger und es gibt viele Stalaktiten. Und die Fahrt kostete zudem nur ungefähr die Hälfte (150.000 Dong) und es wird doch nicht aggressiv versucht, etwas zu verkaufen.

Tam Coc is polluted in the month of February and we had to wear mask throughout the visit. Not as beautiful as the photo which attracted us to visit.Still nice overall but do keep the expectation lower! Maybe the area will clear up in other months but this is just my positive guess!

El paseo en barca es muy bonito, las cuevas nada especial. Lo lamentable es que la magia del lugar se ve destruida por la presión (en dos ocasiones) de que compres cosas con precios desorbitados. Al llegar al final la barquera te pide propina, pero jo cualquier cosa no, ella quiere mínimo 100.000d. Dado que la excursión no es barata por lo que ofrece, si le añadimos el intento de timo, no vale la pena. Hay otros lugares próximos menos explotados y igual o más bellos.

Our rower was boring and slow. She did not engage with us and on multiple occasions, yawned and sighed really loudly. The scenery was nice but after 15mins, it gets old. I guess due to winter, there was no padi fields alongside. Also, we get harassed by photographers trying to take pictures of us and selling us multiple copies. And drink peddlers trying to get us to buy expensive drinks from them for the rowers. We were told the rowers prefers tips and apparently they share the profit of the drinks with those peddlers! Before reaching our destination, our rower even demanded for her tips! Probably something to do once but never again.

We had a great trip on the sampan, however pestered by touts to take photos. Upon return they try to sell you a dollar a pic. They were poor quality so we didn't want them. They dropped price to dollar for all. We still said no. One of the vendors then stopped our car leaving and the guide Ended up buying the pictures. The vendor mumbled something at us and left in a bad mood. Very bad attitude. Over pushy vendors.

The scenery here is very nice, it is definitely worth the trip to see.A massive downside is the fact that the boat rowers are very pushy for tips and money for food.My advice is to definitely break your notes down before you go to smaller amount to save being forced to tip 200,000 dong or more.I can imagine in a few years this will be a horrible place to visit considering the pushy rowers. I wouldn't go back.

Went to Tam Coc today for a boat ride... With two people or ends up at 195000vnd per person with some crazy math that made no sense, plus 10000vnd to park scooters. It was really nice, but the boat ride was probably only 40 minutes... although the scenery was gorgeous, it was marred by boat vendors trying first to get you to buy refreshments, then trying to got you into buying refreshments for your boat rowers because they work so hard. Our rower took a few phone calls while rowing, too. Then they also expect you to tip them at the end so be prepared for that. It was worth doing, but a pricy endeavor.
Trop touristique et ventes abusives de souvenirs ou autresDemandes abusives de pourboiresOn a l'impression d'etre dans le metro, les barques se suivent

Die Bootsfahrt führt durch ein wunderschönes Gebiet. Allerdings trübt der sehr überteuerte Preis den Spaß dann doch ziemlich. 390.000 Dong halte ich für 1,5 Stunden auf einem Boot doch ziemlich unverschämt. Ich dachte eigentlich, dass man für diesen Preis wenigstens noch ein paar Stopps unterwegs einlegt, um sich Grotten anzuschauen. Unser Fahrer hat aber so schnell wie möglich gerudert, um die Fahrt schnell wieder zum Ende zu bringen. Englisch hat er auch nicht gesprochen. Die Forderung nach Trinkgeld am Ende der Fahrt fand ich deshalb lächerlich.

Disappointed at the pushyness of the local people down here. We paid for a tour, and boat ride, felt bullied into having to purchase there products out in the row boat and buy them drinks from there selling friends, The they asking for a tip. Worked out expensive, paid for the tour, the boat trip, then over priced products and tips. The scenery was lovely but the expectations and attitudes did lower the standard of this tour. the guide did not keep very good idea on where people were, some got left off some partsdue to language communication.

The lake was really pretty (though not as spectacular as Halong Bay, it was still very serene and beautiful), and the man rowing our boat seemed nice. But as we paddled along the lake, we were accosted by photographers who insisted on taking our photo. At the turnaround point in the lake, there were people in canoes selling drinks and food. We were basically forced into buying things for our paddler, which he didn't drink, and presumably had a deal to give it back to the sellers for their profit. On the way back, he stopped the boat and tried to sell us souvenirs, insisting on showing us every single one, even though we made it clear we were not interested in buying anything. When we got back to the dock, he demanded a tip out of us (we had already paid for the trip, and tipping is not standard in Vietnam). Other tourists nearby were aggressively forced into giving tips by paddlers standing around them, yelling and shaking their fists at them. After this, we were followed for about 10 minutes by people trying to sell us the photos they had taken of us. They followed us into a restaurant, and we really didn't know how to get rid of them (saying no, and ignoring them didn't seem to work). This trip would have been nice were it not for the locals trying to squeeze every penny out of us.
Giretto in barca carino ma niente di che, in Vietnam ci sono luoghi molto più belli, le signore che renana le barche alla fine sono molto ma dico molto insistenti sulle mance

Beautiful scenery and lovely to be on the boat. But there were very pushy women trying to sell you things and make you buy things for the person rowing your boat, and then making you feel guilty when you don't buy things! Also, when we were nearly back, our driver asked for a tip, which I hate. We had already decided what we would give him and when we gave him 100,000 dong he didn't even seem that grateful. To be fair to him, it is a long journey (about 1.5 hours), but I honestly think the workers here are ruining this attraction.

So first of all, I had to park my motorbike for 5,000 VND, then buy tickets, one for the boat and one for the temple. In total this was 270,000 VND. The 1,5 hour boat trip started off pretty nice, we came across three caves and the scenery was lovely. But then there were a lot of boats with locals selling their drinks and so on. I had my own water but they sort of made me buy drinks for the two ladies that took me on the boat. And at the end of the trip they asked for a tip. Normally I would definitely give a tip if I'm satisfied and think the trip was worth more then the fee they ask for it.Then I had to walk back and get my motorbike from the parking lot and drive up to the temple, which wasn't anything special, really. It wasn't really clear which way I had to drive and the lady at the counter wasn't interested in explaining how the get there. The other temple she told me, which was included in the price, I couldn't even find at all. So I took a road to the bird park, which would have cost me another 50,000VND if I wanted to visit this park.I had a whole day trip on a boat in Ha Long Bay for 370,000 VND (incl lunch, snorkeling, kayaking and go to an island). I think this attraction is way too expensive and the fact that they are so keen on selling things and getting tips made the experience a lot less nicer.

It's not ha long bay but for some reason calls itself the halong bay of the rice fields. A boat will take you on some basic tour which is nothing more then nice. The most exciting thing you will experience, is the non stop demands for tips from the lady "guide" on the boat.

Tourist trap. We were aware of pushy sales at the end of the trip and ladies asking for tips. But none of these reviews mentioned strange ticketing policy towards foreigners. Cash desk informs you that max. 2 foreigners per boat are accepted. I originally thought that this is because we were on average havier than locals, but I was with two kids, so hope they would make an exception. Nothing like thi! The accepted us on one boate on condition that we pay equivalent of two! So everything is about money! Avoid it – go to Trang An instead, where only Vietnamese and Chinese go.

Although nice to see, ita way to tourist minded, haggling, wanting more money, expedting tips,  foreigners are only allowed 2  pwr boar, so you have to rent another one .... Even though you have two children. Locals go with4 to 5 per boat. Service is rip off! Too bad i i very pretty, but Trang Ann is more beautiful and less  tourist unfriendly

สถานที่ตรงนี้จะใช้การพายเรือล่องไปตามแม่น้ำที่รายล้อมด้วยภูเขาสองข้างทาง แต่ไม่แน่ใจว่ามาเที่ยวผิดช่วงหรือไม่เพราะบรรยากาศสองข้างทางที่ผ่านไม่ได้สวยงามมากนัก ในบางช่วงของเส้นทางจะมีลอดเข้าชมถ้ำซึ่งก็ไม่มีอะไรน่าตื่นตาตื่นใจนัก ด้วยการซื้อแพคเกจทัวร์แบบเที่ยวรายวันมา ทำให้ตารางเวลาของสถานที่นี้ถูกจัดอยู่ในช่วงบ่ายต้นๆ บวกกับหน้าร้อน ซึ่งร้อนแผดเผามากก็เลยไม่แน่ใจว่ามีอะไรน่าประทับไจที่นี่

Wir sind mit dem Motorroller in den Nationalpark gefahren und haben, wie von der Guesthouse Besitzerin angekündigt, 100.000 dong pro person bezahlt. Da war Parken und eine 40-minütige Bootstour mit inbegriffen. Im Falle des Parkens traf die Information zu, bei der Ruderbootstour wollte die Bootsführerin beim Verlassen der Höhle aber ein "tip" in Höhe von 100.000 dong haben, sonst würde sie uns nicht aus der Höhle zurückrudern. Auch nach erstem Widerstand bestand sie weiterhin auf die 100.000 dong. Zudem hat die Bootsfahrt maximal 20 Minuten, anstatt der versprochenen 40 gedauert. Fazit: super schöne Landschaft, aber keine wirklich kostenlose Ruderboottour durch die Buddha Cave (im Naturpark). Die Erfahrung bleibt uns leider schlecht in Erinnerung.

Tam Coc is every bit as beautiful as other reviewers will tell you. But it has a problem, one that management needs to address. Our ride went well until the last 100 yards, when our (until then) friendly rower tapped me on the shoulder and asked for a tip. I then gave her the amount our hotel manager had said was customary, the same amount that had elicited a grateful response at Trang An -- 50,000 dong or $2.50. She then started pestering us, saying "Not enough,not enough..." It may not have been, but at that point there was no way we were going to cave in to what felt like "ransom" demands. The badgering continued as we walked away from the boat. Tam Coc, I suspect you need to pay these skillful rowers a respectable wage so we tourists don't have to carry the load.

There are only three caves on this tour and while the landscape is nice, there is a serious problem with most rowers (chatting with others). Basically, you hassled to give a tip shortly after leaving the dock. Photographers were at two places taking pictures to sell you upon your return; they insist on you buying food and drinks not only for you, but also for your rower when it is time to turn around, after the 3rd cave, and the rower tries to sell you her knitted wares on the return trip. All in all a very annoying experience.

The scenery is breathtakingly beautful but after a while you want to turn around an come back. Its a long way on a slow boat in the hot sun and high humidity.  The families who row the boats (in our case a 60+ year old woman) are so fit and strong I felt emabrrased at our western style laziness.  Take something to sit on becasue your bottom will get really sore after an hour or so on a tiny wooden seat.  Wear suncreen and take a hat or umbrella for shade because you cannot escape the sun once you are out there.  Also take plenty to drink we took two small bottles - not enough - I also poured water over my head to help me cool down.  The heat is overwhelming with very little breeze so a fan is also a good idea.  This was the only day I felt affected by the heat and thought I would pass out.  Ask your rower to stop in the shade or in the cave grottos from time to time if you feel too hot.Be aware of the scam 'vendors' selling food and drink at the midway turning point.  Dont feel forced into buying things for you or your rower.  Our rower didnt touch theirs but gave them back to the vendor  to resell - after we paid for them.  Not happy!!  if you want something fine but bargain well.  Also my husband was charged $1 USD to use the toilet at the midway point.We noticed many people passing by us on their return journey who did not look happy and we thought they might be bored or heat affected also.  Some were rowing themselves back.  We soon learned why.  Your rower will stop and try to force you to give them money or to buy their 'hidden' souveniers. They may stop for a long time until you buy them.  We saw one couple sitting for more than 20 minutes with their back to the rower and not moving - just stuck there in the little sampan by the side of the water in the hot sun thoroughly miserable.  Our rower clearly told us "you no buy - I no row" and left us also sitting there while she ignored us.  We were basically 'pirated' - she kept demanding money from us = $10 USD (more than 200,000 dong) when we would not buy her souveniers.  We would gladly offer her a large tip at the end of the journey but we were determined we were not going to be pirated.  We sat there for a long time and she would not row.  My husband didnt know whether to try and take the oars and row us the hour journey back or not.  After about half an hour and with me almost passed out from dehydration and heat exhaustion we gave in and bargained to $6 USD for a few lousy souveniers then she happily started rowing again...all the way flashing her CASH to passing rowers as if she had scored big time.  Talk about furious!!  and she was so friendly and innocent on the way there too... after all what can one little olf lady do .... HUH!!Still we had to TIP her at the end of our journey as her 'male mider' was watching carefully and our tour guide who was waiting at the waters edge informed us we had to.  We justed wanted to get in the car and get out of Nin Binh ASAP.  We later told him of our experience and he was also upset and apologised profusely.  He reported it to his tour company.We spent much of our time in northern Vietnam being too wary to go off with any locals in sampans or xich lo without our guide - it was a bad expereince.We kind of laugh about it now because we were pirated by a 60+ year old woman who only wanted to support her family living.  We still have the souveniers - gathering dust - not sure what to do with them .

Wir hatten einen Tag in dem kleinen Städtchen Ninh Binh Aufenthalt und wollten uns wenigstens die Gegend etwas anschauen. Auf unserer kleinen Rollertour sind wir dann auf "Tam cok" gestoßen. für 260k wurde man auf einem kleinen Bötchen über den Fluss gefahren. Die Landschaft ist nett anzuschauen, man fährt durch 2 unspektakuläre Höhlen. Am Ende der Flussfahrt sammeln sich auch schon die Boote und man wird quasi von fliegenden Händlern dazu gezwungen überteuerte Getränke etc zu kaufen. Wenn man jedoch nichts für sich kaufen möchte, soll man dem Bootsführer ein Getränk als Trinkgeld kaufen. Am Ende der Fahrt verlangt dieser jedoch nochmal Trinkgeld. Achtung: die Bootsführer können schnell sauer werden.Wir empfehlen eher eine Rollertour Richtung National Park bei der man eine tolle Aussicht genießen kann und wovon man wahrscheinlich viel mehr hat, als von dieser Bootstour!

바다위의 하롱베이, 멋진 카르스트 지형을 기대하고 갔습니다.가는중에 그런 지형이 많아서 더더욱 기대를 했었습니다.코스대로 베트남 최초수도의 사원들른 후 점심 먹은 식당 주변을 보니 땀꼭 투어 보트가 보이길래 여기가 시작인가 했는데, 차로 다시 이동해서 다른 출발 보트 포인트로 가더군요.좋긴 했습니다만, 기대했던 그런 육지의 하롱베이의 모습은 보지 못했습니다. 동굴을 3번 거치는 코스인데 이후 풍경은 볼만한 산수 정도?.... 제가 여행사의 설명을 정확히 듣지 못했을수도 있지만, 하여튼 땀꼭 투어 예약할때 정확한 코스 가 어디인지 확인하는게 좋으실듯 합니다.  거기다 보트를 몰던 아주머니니는 대놓구 과한 팁을 요구하시더군요.... --;제가 갔던 보트 투어 코스가 나쁜건 아니었지만, 기대를 충족켜주진 못했어요. 잘 알아보고 가시길 바랍니다.

Don't get me wrong Tam Coc it self is a great place to visit, after 4 weeks in Vietnam the local people are only trying to take you for everything you have but in a more aggressive way in which I did not  appreciate you have read all the scams they are up to which spoils this trip 40 minutes down stream and back the same way traveling inside three caves and to watch them paddling with there feet is amazing. A long drive from Hanoi 3 hours each way for just a short boat ride.Halong bay is a better option if you are pressed for time

It is the Halong Bay on land but a lot of practices up there are not so good.   For future travellers I will let you know what happens up there.   You will have your photo taken and they will ask for 100,000 VND.   But believe me, you can get them down to 20,000 VND without even trying too hard.   Also where the canoes turn around at the end of the river, do not pull out your wallet to buy a drink.   They will get you to buy for the paddlers as well.  (Chips, lollies and drinks). They are very persistent and very annoying.   Also the people paddling the canoes are very annoying on the way back.   They will try to sell you table cloths, pencil cases, hats, t-shirts etc.   They also bring out the photo album of their children and families to make you buy more.   In the restaurant that the tour group takes you to, they ask you do you want a drink.   The price in the menu and the price that you have to pay is totally different.   It appears they are deliberately overcharging the tour groups.   The group we were with was an ATP bus tour and also the tour guide was carrying an ATP flag.   The only advice I can give future travellers, you are expected to tip the paddlers, even though I totally disagree with this, as we paid $100 for 2 people for this tour, just hand over 20000 VND per person.   I hope this helps future travellers.

We visited this beautiful area a few years ago but unfortunately the experience was spoilt by the very pushy tactics of the local people in trying to sell their wares. The peaceful surrounds and the serene boat trip should have been magical but was memorable for all the wrong reasons. We are returning to Vietnam and I would love to take some other friends to this area, however, after reading recent reviews nothing seems to have changed. I can understand the locals need for the tourist dollar but I feel sure they would earn far more if the tourist was allowed to enjoy the experience and then buy from vendors/stalls selling their goods on land around the docking area. Officials need to consider these practices as current harassment is turning people away. It goes beyond a joke that you can laugh about later and instead becomes an experience to avoid which is a real shame for both locals and tourists alike.

Just for the beauty of the area and the serenity of the boat ride, this attraction is clearly a five.  However, the high pressure of the rowers and food hawkers ruined the experience.We visited on a misty day which made the area seem somewhat mystical.  Our boat ride started mellow enough with our tour guide setting us up with one of the boats and off we went.  The boat trip lasted about 1 1/2 hours for the ride out and back.  About 15 minutes after setting off, our rower pulled into shore and let on a woman. News to us.  She didn't really make any small talk, just picked up an oar and started rowing.  This is truly a beautiful area with limestone cliffs, wildlife, and calm waters.  The caves are also amazing.After the 3rd grotto, the fun starts.  We just pulled into to some open area to turn around, and there to greet us was a lineup of locals in boats with lots of goods to sell.  They are set up to take turns at the next boat to come through.  The first pitch is to buy something to eat and drink for the hardworking boat rowers.  Our sales lady actually put pop and snacks in our boat and asked us to pay for it.  This is obviously set up with the rowers, but since they did do a good job we everntually relented and purchased each a pop - but not at their prices.  On the way back, the rower who jumped into our boat started pulling out souveniors to sell us.  We said we didn't want anything, but that didn't stop her.  It must have gone on for 10 to 15 minutes as she tried one tactic after another.  Finally, she gave up, but was none too happy.As we approached the dock on the way back, she then asked for a tip.  She actually said we should give her 100,000 Dong ($5).  I said I would give her a tip, of my choosing.  This also went on until we landed.  I was not about to give her anything until we were on shore.  She accepted my $2 tip, but was again not happy and with no thanks.If you decide to visit this area and do the boat trip, make sure you are willing to put up with the hassle to enjoy the beauty.  If the "overhead" doesn't bother you, it will be great.  If this type of behavior turns you off, it will ruin the experience.
Heard of this place from the Tripadvisor prior the tripsomewhat enjoyed the boat ride till the staff pressured us to foods or drinks for them besides selling us the souvenirs

Not really a must see. I mean if you have already visited Halong Bay.You can skip this. Bascially it is the same as Halong Bay just that you be on a sampan.I visited Tam Coc during Feb, It was cold while on the open sampan.The tour guide told me there was 2 types of Tam Coc tour.one of them is human made while the other was natural.The tour guide said the human made was for local ( just to scam ppls for a short visit)Of course i choose the nature.Basically you will put on sampan and passed through some dark cave.Get ready a touch light on your own so you can see what in the cave.Nothing impressive. maybe i have already visited halong bay.

We visited Tam Coc on a day tour with a driver and guide furnished through a travel agency.  The guide did all of the arrangements with the rowers at Tam Coc, and overall, enjoyed the history told by the guide but disliked Tam Coc. The caves that we went through while on the river were interesting and the scenery is beautiful.  However, beware the industry that has developed around tourism.  As soon as we got near to the river where we were going to board a small boat, photographers started hounding us to buy photographs that they had already taken as we talked through the square.  Then while we were on the river, other boats came up to us and their rowers held onto our boat and *told us* to buy drinks for our rower, which I did knowing that she wouldn't drink them but suddenly feeling guilty that she was rowing us around.  No one takes no for an answer.   The rower then tried to sell us shirts, and she was incredibly insistent.  When the trip was over, our guide told me how much to tip the rower, and I followed his advice.  Then he talked for a while to the rower, then told me that her whole family had died from Agent Orange (I'm from the US, and this was clearly a guilt trip), and then asked me to give her a bigger tip than the one he had suggested.  Even he seemed embarrassed by what he was doing.  It all felt like a scam, and in the end I regretted going there because as a tourist, I was cast as either the bad guy or the fool.

The only reason I'm rating this attraction as "poor" rather than "terrible" is because of the scenery. There are some pretty nice caves (three to be exact) and hills, although you'll quickly get bored and wish the two-and-a-half hour boat ride would end sooner. What truly irritates me about this place is the number of people trying to get their hands on my wallet (figuratively, of course). First off, I had to pay 5000 dong (per person) just to use the water closet at one end of the designated course, and the exceedingly poor quality of it definitely didn't help justify the amount I paid to use it. Moreover, there will be paddlers trying to get you to buy drinks for your rower, although this is entirely up to you. For me, this happened on the way back. As if all that wasn't enough, the rower herself (or himself) will open up a box of goodies (surprise, surprise!) and ask you to purchase souvenirs. Even before you reach the shore, expect your rower to be already asking you for tips.Finally, just as you've gotten out of the boat, and sighed a breath of relief, feeling glad that everything is finally over, a familiar face will appear before you. This is the woman who took photographs of you and your hubby (or whoever you fancy) while you were happily posing on the boat an hour ago, and now she's here to haunt you - 9 US dollars for 20 photos. Reject her and she will persistently pester you, following you wherever you go. The feeling that everyone was trying to suck money out of me effectively ruined the trip for me. I strongly recommend that you skip this place and go to Trang An instead; it's heaps more beautiful, and definitely less touristy.

Un train de barques promenant les touristes à la queue leu leu, des rameuses qui vous prennent en otage au mileu de la balade pour vous vendre des broderies maladroites et insistent tellement  que l'ambiance devient lourde. un pourboire exigé à la descente.. Autant d'ingrédients qui jouent en défaveur de la balade en bateau présentée comme le point fort du lieu. Quand les rizières sont vertes, allez plutôt vous promener en vélo le long des digues, vous perdre en suivant les circonvolutions de la rivière à votre rythme, découvrir seul les temples minuscules ou les très grandes pagodes, flâner sur les marchés et admirer le paysage depuis Hang Mua, là vous sentirez vraiment l'atmosphère de cette magnifique petite région. Pas besoin de guide, pas besoin d'organisation. Achetez une carte et demandez un vélo à votre hôtel.

Wir haben den Ausflug nach Tam Coc über einen Tagesausflug von Hanoi aus mit Vietnamopentour unternommen. Vom Veranstalter war alles super organisiert, Guide und Fahrer waren sehr gut. Tam Coc machte leider gleich am Hafen den Eindruck einer großen Touristenfalle auf uns. Die Boote fuhren im gefühlten Sekundentakt ab. Alleine ist man in Tam Coc am frühen Nachmittag definitiv nicht- eventuell zu einer anderen Tageszeit. Unterwegs dann die unvermeidbaren Fotografen. Nichtdestotrotz : Eine umwerfend schöne Landschaft und ein kleiner Einblick in das Leben der Fischer hier vor Ort. Nach dem Passieren der letzen Grotte wurden wir regelrecht genötigt, Getränke und Kekse zu kaufen. Wenn nicht für uns, dann für die Bootsführerin. Der Preis war noch ok (3 $ für 2 Getränke und eine Keksrolle). Frech war dann schon die Frage nach 1 $ als Tipp für die Verkäuferin. Auf der Rückfahrt versuchte uns die Bootsführerin noch recht massiv, gestickte Sachen zu verkaufen. Die zwar schön waren, ein Einkauf uns unter diesen Umständen jedoch nicht wirklich Spass machte. Nach mehrfachem massiven Ablehnen wurden wir endlich in Ruhe gelassen. Ca. 20m vor der Anlegestelle verlangte sie recht nachdrücklich einen Tip und war mit 2 $ (Empfehlung vom Guide) nicht zufrieden. Ein Dankeschön haben wir jedenfalls nicht vernommen. Glücklicherweise war dies der einzige Ausflug während unserer gesamten Vietnamreise, bei dem wir uns so unwohl gefühlt haben.
i like the atmostphere there,too bad i don't like the hawkers surrounding there and keep asking you to buy some drinks from them.

Visite effectué en milieu d'aprés-midi avec ma petite amie. Ciel gris. Notre rameuse, malgré une entame de dialogue sympathique en début de parcours, décide subitement de faire demi-tour après la première grotte (sur les 3 comprises en tout dans le ticket acheté auparavant), prétextant l'arrivée de la pluie et qu'elle était "fatiguée"...Voyant une barque revenir de la 2e grotte, je lui montre et lui demande pourquoi au même tarif nous n'aurions que le 1/3 de la visite. Je la rassure également sur le fait que la pluie, qui n'est finalement jamais tombée, ne me ferait pas fondre.Une ambiance de plomb s'est installée dans la barque gâchant totalement le plaisir. Nous décidons au retour de prendre les 2 petites rames supplémentaires pour soulager notre "pauvre" rameuse qui a déjà essuyé un échec dans sa stratégie. Mais le meilleur reste à venir...Nous ramons donc assez vigoureusement sur tout le chemin du retour pour écourter ce supplice.À 20 mètres du quai de débarquement, elle s'arrête nette et, accrochons-nous, nous réclame (en français) et sans l'ombre d'une quelconque gène "Monsieur madame, pour papa maman, 2 euros pourboire" ! Le pigeon standard doit sortir la monnaie ou rentrer à la nage ? Lui rappelant la manoeuvre manquée de demi-tour après la grotte n°1, ma petite amie lui tend 8000 dongs en petite monnaie, ce que la rameuse ne méritait même pas, mais elle nous a finalement déposé à quai voyant que je commençais à la regarder fixement et silencieusement (ce qui est en général chez moi l'étape préliminaire avant le pétage de plomb...).Inutile de vous préciser qu'il n'y a pas eu de bise ni de merci une fois à terre. Juste un "you can see it has not rained".Bilan : endroit impressionnant, mais exploité par une bande organisée. À éviter.

Let me start by saying that on our visits to Viet Nam we have been very happy with just about everywhere we have gone and everything we have seen. We consider ourselves to be enthusiastic advocates for Viet Nam as a tourist destination.Our Tam Coc experience was a major disappointment. It was sold to us as the Inland Ha Long Bay. We didn’t think it was.You start by boarding a small and rather uncomfortable boat, to be rowed down a small lake between limestone outcrops that give the area its nickname of Inland Ha Long Bay. In terms of scenic beauty, there is absolutely no comparison with Ha Long Bay. Tam Coc is vastly inferior.Our guide advised us that the first priority after boarding your boat is to turn your head away when one or more of the photographers, positioned on the left hand side of the dock, attempts to photograph you.  If you don’t, you can look forward to being badgered to buy from them on your return. There are more photographers at further vantage points along the way, so constant vigilance is advisable.After about forty minutes of rowing, you pass through a couple of grottos and eventually arrive at the end of the long lake.  Here you are besieged by pedlars trying to sell you, at extortionate prices, cans of soft drink, candy bars, potato chips and other items you don’t want. They are extremely insistent and most annoying.When your boat lady finally decides it is a waste of time sitting there any longer, as you are not going to buy a can of warm Coke for a lot more than you would buy it on shore, you then head back.About half way back, she stops and opens up a tin box. Out of this she produces embroidered tablecloths and other softgoods, none of particularly great quality and doubtless more expensive than better quality goods would be in the Old Quarter.After about ten minutes of trying to persuade you to buy something, she eventually starts rowing again.As you approach the dock, she then starts asking loudly and repeatedly for a tip. Although we saw no real reason to give her one, we did so.Verdict: Don’t bother. It takes about two hours to get there from Hanoi and the countryside  through which you pass is not particularly attractive. PS: The food at the restaurant in the village next to the dock area was not that great.

This is another tourist hell hole hyped up in a place with not much better to do. Phong Nha Caves are Halong on land this place is just plain horrid. The Karsts are fairly attractive but the number of people, touts and general vibe are far from peaceful, it's  nice but in a country with far better hardly worth getting of a train/bus for.

The scenery was lovely, a bit like Oxford Street though!  Too busy, too smelly, sewerage river and the hassle of people wanting cash from you spoiled it.A typical trip is this - the boat person paddles for a while then you are asked to help, that's fine.Photographers surround your boat and take pictures of you.When you get to the end of the trip, where you turn around, you are ambushed by ladies wanting you to purchase drinks/treats for the boat person.  You are pressured into buying and your boat is held hostage.  It cost us $1.When begin to return the boat is stopped at the right hand side and goods are brought out from the large tin box.  Again you are pressured into buying with the assumption that you won't be returning until you do!As you approach the end you are again pressured for money by the boat person who yarps on about giving her more money.When you arrive on the steps to leave, you are shown the pictures that were taken by the photographers, cost $4.You end up with the boat person, people trying to sell you pictures, beggars in your face.  It's like a feeding frenzy.  I just walked away. Of course the photographers follow you and you pay $1!I just got sick of the filth and every person I ever talked to in Vietnam wanting money from me.

The water is murky and the water is crowded with boats and people harassing you. Go to Trang An instead. It is the same idea, only less crowded. The water was emerald green and clear and no one bothered us the whole time.
Go to Trang An. Same scenery also lot of caves. Less boats and no selling souvenirs on the boat.

Tam Cốc Bích Động bao gồm  Đi thuyền trên sông Ngô Đồng Xuyên qua 3 hang (Hang Cả – Hai – Ba) Hai bên là ruộng lúa + núi đá vôi  Bích Động 👉 Chùa cổ – tâm linh – leo núi Đặc điểm chính Chùa cổ xây từ thế kỷ 15 Gồm: Chùa Hạ Chùa Trung Chùa Thượng (trong hang) Leo bậc đá, ngắm cảnh từ trên cao  Tui chia sẽ thẳng lần đầu đi thì tui thấy sông nước hữu tình yên bình lắm,thuyền thì nhẹ nhàng lướt đi Chán sáng đi tam cốc, xuống thuyền cô hỏi nhà các cháu ở đâu bọn mình cũng là người con đất nb và bọn mình lần đầu đi tam cốc , thấy cô cứ nói bóng gió là ng nb chắc đi nhiều rồi chắc chán đi ngắm hang rồi hay cô đi một hang thui nhé +1 lý do khó chịu ( nhắc đi nhắc lại rất nhiều nha ) xong cả thuyền mình Ko hề vui và đi qua ngay cửa hang một mình bảo cô cho bọn mình về , lúc chèo về cô cứ nài nỉ mọi mỗi ng mua một bức tranh thuê cho cô với giá 200ng/1 bức bọn mình quyết định thôi mua một bức ủng hộ cô +1 lý do khó chịu ( mình thấy thuyền nào cũng xảy ra vấn đề này ) cô còn bảo mấy đứa mà mua được có một bức à . Xong lúc đưa tiền bọn mình có tip cho cô thêm một chút mà lúc đấy cô nói thẳng bọn mình ( sao cho gì mà cho ít v ít nhất phải cho đôi ba trăm chứ ) bọn mình xịt keo và chỉ biết giải thích là bọn cháu mới còn học cấp 3 nên Ko có nhiều ạ+1 lý do thất vọng , mình thấy cô Ko nói j và chở bọn mình về bến. Về bến mình gặp một chú để lấy ảnh bọn mình đã chụp trên thuyền ,nhưng lúc trên thuyền mình đã nhắc cô chụp ảnh chỉ lấy 3 tấm thôi nhá ( chuyện j quan trọng nhắc lại 3 lần ) ấy mà lên bến bọn mình nhận đc 6 bức ảnh 30k/1bức với lý do chắc cô thấy xinh quá nên mới chụp nhiều bọn mình lại ngậm ngùi xoè tiền ra trả . Và bọn mình quyết định đi về luôn chứ Ko đi tiếp theo combo nữa, chứ mình để lại một ấn tượng Ko tốt thì chắc sẽ Ko bgio quay lại

First of all, we feel like being scam as there is no option to choose boat trip only when we purchase the ticket. All banner and notice board showing it is combo package of 350K VND /pax for boat trip + 3 stop spots by electrical shutter car. This is combo 2. Combo 1 with boat trip only never show anywhere and never being ask which combo you want during purchase. The package start with boat trip. We was told to split to 2 boats as we are 4 pax. The scenary is nice but boat trip is consider short compare to Trang An boat trip. It bring us up to a point and then U-turn back. After U-turn, the boat lady start to try selling souvenirs to us. Before the drop off point to a small temple, she asked for tips. The funniest experience was I don't hv small changes and she said she can exchange after tips. I pass her 200 VND and she return me 100 VND. Later I found out that another boat took by my family member was asked for 100 VND tips as well. They gave 50 VND but boat man said need to gave  100 VND.  Next on the 3 stop by spots. It actually can be access by road. Should be can reach by taxi or grab. That's make us feel being scam again.  Thai Vi Temple-->fairy cave - - >Bich Dong Pagoda. Fairy Cave and Bich Dong Pagoda quite interesting but can just  go by land vechicle.  OVERALL experience for us are Trang An Boat trip ( it cost 250k for 3 - 3.5hrs and 4 pax per boat)  recomended compare to this!

Nous avons fait l’excursion à Tam Coc comprenant la balade en barque puis les déplacements en voiturette de golf avec plusieurs arrêts. Le lieu en lui-même est très joli, les paysages sont vraiment beaux et pourraient offrir un moment paisible et magique… malheureusement, l’expérience a été largement gâchée par l’organisation et le manque de professionnalisme. Le principal problème a été la personne qui conduisait la barque. Dès qu’elle voyait quelqu’un, elle criait et parlait très fort. Pire encore, son téléphone n’a pas arrêté de sonner pendant toute la balade, et elle passait ses appels en parlant à voix haute. Nous lui avons demandé à plusieurs reprises si elle pouvait appeler après la prestation, car nous étions là pour profiter du paysage, du calme, du clapotis de l’eau… sans succès. C’était vraiment inadmissible et très peu respectueux des visiteurs. De plus, nous avions payé pour nous arrêter au temple situé à la fin du parcours. Au départ, elle ne voulait pas s’y arrêter et nous avons dû insister. Elle a finalement accepté, mais en nous disant “cinq minutes”, ce que nous avons trouvé très moyen. Pour couronner le tout, à la fin de la balade, elle a insisté pour avoir un pourboire. Nous avons refusé, car un pourboire se mérite. Avec un tel manque de professionnalisme, c’était hors de question. Concernant les voiturettes de golf, l’organisation laisse également à désirer. Nous avons attendu très longtemps à chaque arrêt. À la sortie de la Fairy Cave, aucune voiturette ne nous attendait, et nous avons encore dû patienter. Vu le nombre de voiturettes disponibles, une meilleure gestion et rotation seraient clairement possibles. En résumé, Tam Coc est un endroit magnifique, mais l’expérience touristique n’est pas encore au point. Le service client et le professionnalisme doivent vraiment être améliorés pour que la prestation soit à la hauteur du lieu.

Die Aussicht war wirklich wunderschön aber der Fahrer war katastrophal. Ich bin nicht pingelig und ich verstehe das hier die Menschen anders sind als ich es gewohnt bin aber während der 1 1/2h Fahrt durchgehend ins Wasser zu spucken und furzen geht gar nicht. Vor allem wurde dann noch Trinkgeld verlangt und das was wir ihm geben wollten war ihm nicht genug, woraufhin er angefangen hat zu schreien. Leider unprofessionell und unsympathisch. Schade

Unser Bootsfahrer war extrem unfreundlich, hat die ganze Zeit irgendwas auf vietnamesisch geredet. Er konnte gar kein Wort englisch. Google Übersetzer brachte meistens nur irgendwelche Beleidigungen hervor… An dem Ticketschalter am See kann man nur Tickets für die Bootsfahrt inklusive Busfahrt zu einer zusätzlichen Höhle und 2 Pagoden kaufen. Wir wollten nur die Bootsfahrt, da hieß es man muss weiter laufen ca 300 Meter, unausgeschildert. Es hieß zu uns es kostet dann 250k/Person. Vor Ort musste man aber 270k/Person zahlen. Außerdem mussten wir zu 4 auf 2 Boote aufgeteilt werden, haben während der Fahrt aber viele Boote mit 4-6 Leuten gesehen…  Die Landschaft ist dafür aber sehr schön und man fährt durch 3 Höhlen hindurch. Am Besten zwischen 7-10 Uhr vorbeikommen, da blühen die Seerosen mehr.

We did this boat ride yesterday evening and although the scenery was beautiful, the experience with the rower was uncomfortable. He was nice and neutral at first, then around halfway through the tour asked for a tip. We said “after the tour” as we had not yet even reached halfway and he became verbally aggressive and shouted at us in Vietnamese. We tried to ignore it and enjoy the rest of the boat ride - still polite and respectful with our rower. Also note around this time he picked up a bag from another boat full of “locally made pictures” and tried to force us to buy some. Immediately we said no, as there was a posted sign at the ticket office warning tourists of this. All other boats we saw had the same “souvenirs” being sold. This is supposed to be about the nature and scenery, not banging into the other boats to trade pictures for sale. He began to row almost double the speed from before, making our boat ride around 30 mins less than what was advertised. Then, when reaching the end point, he started demanding a tip again quite aggressively. At this point, we felt extremely uncomfortable and were trying to get off the boat, when another woman working for the tour came towards the docking boat also demanding tips for our rower. We did not give a tip due to this. This is not the way to ask for tips, and honestly took away from the whole experience. Please be aware of this happening at this boat tour, but also others in the area. Note: I added the photo of the notice to tourists from the ticket office.

The scenery was amazing but our rower was quite off putting. He was in a mad rush - zoomed passed all other boats as if we were competing in some kind of race. He turned his radio on with loud talking- completely ruining the serenity. Obviously he was fully aware he was not meant to as he quickly turned it off when near other boats. We eventually asked him to turn it down and he did turn it off while demanding his tip - repeatedly. We were turfed off at some place, way before all the other boats, that had started before us. As others have said we also got the hard push to buy his embroidered pictures for 250,000 - seen them in Hanoi for 80,000 🤣. He really ruined our experience so cannot recommend. After the boat ordeal, we were dropped off by a series of random people driving electric wagons around at the other places included in the ticket. No information was given at any time - we literally had to google where we were what to see there eg the fairy caves, the pagoda and a temple. Very odd all round.

The boat ride itself was very beautiful and enjoyable, with stunning scenery.  Unfortunately, the experience is overshadowed by the behavior of the boat operators. At the end of the tour, they very insistently ask for tips, sometimes in a rather pushy way. During the trip, photographers also approach you to sell photos, additionally the drivers want to sell you more images, supposedly taken by themselves, but in reality, every boat operator seems to sell the same pictures from a small bucket.  It’s disappointing that the service allows this practice. It gives the impression that the boatmen are not paid fairly, which turns what could be a relaxing activity into an uncomfortable situation for visitors. A more professional approach would make this a much more enjoyable and memorable experience overall.

I definitely do not recommend this place to visit in December. There are no flowers here, the water is muddy, there are a lot of tourists, along the way you will be offered souvenirs, and the spoon maker will ask for a tip. I took the spoon+bus combo tour. Nothing impressed me in 2 hours. It is a very tourist place. It is better to choose Trang An - it is much more beautiful there now

This place is truly beautiful, but unfortunately, I encountered ALL of the unsavory practices mentioned by other reviewers.  Unlike Trang An, the rower will not stop at the temple without you asking her to do so. We only stopped at one temple/shrine (the one at the end before the boat turns around). I asked if we could see this other temple which was listed on the flyer, but she said if we decided to stop there, she would just drop us off and we would have to walk the ca. 1,5km way back to the beginning point, so we decided against it. At the start of the tour, we encountered the people trying to take photos, but thanks to the tips of other reviewers, we did not make eye contact or engage them, so they left us alone. During the tour, the rower was constantly talking loudly on her phone, so it was hard to enjoy the beautiful surroundings. On the way back, she tried selling us some "home made" pictures/handicrafts, and at the end, she asked us for tips. This place could be 5 stars because of its stunning natural beauty, but our experience with the rower ruined it for us. Hopefully the tourism department educates the rowers to be more professional, in this way they wouldn't have to ask for tips, because tourists usually give good tips when they are happy with the service (we happily gave 100,000 to our rower in Trang An because she truly deserved it) In the end, I would recommend doing the Trang An boat ride instead, it's more beautiful and the rowers there are more professional.

Beautiful ride but half of our tour was ruined by our boat rower asking incessantly for a tip that we would have given anyway at the end. You cannot fully enjoy the nature around, you are reminded every 2 minutes that it is a commercial ride and that a tip is REQUIRED at the end

The views are beautiful, but let down by rowers trying to sell stuff and pushing for tips. I gave her a generous tip but still pushed for more. Ruined the experience for me, bit of a tourist trap! Trang An soo much better

ベトナムを代表する景色を味わえるボートツアー。 同じルートを往復するだけの1コースのみ。 2時間近くのツアーで日傘必須、途中でトイレ休憩出来る場所はありませんでした。  日本人にはメディアに取り上げられる頻度が多かったり、周辺宿泊施設の多さ、アクセスのしやすさから人気のようですが、チップの要求があったり、写真やドリンク、お土産の売り子が寄ってきたり品のなさでがっかりする事になりました。  ニンビン周辺にはボートツアーが体験出来る場所が複数あり、混同した書き込みがこちらには多いように思います。 (書き込み内容や写真から、特に高評価されている方にチャアアンと間違って投稿されている方が多いと思います。)  ツアーのボリュームや見所、複数コースを選択出来たり、統制の取れた船頭さん、こちらからチップを支払いたくなるような品の良さなど、同じ料金を支払うならタムコックよりチャアアン(Trang An .Tràng An)の方がお勧めです。

It was the worst thing I've done. A complete  tourist trap. Mass tourism. Thousands of people with about 500 boats bumper to bumper. People were loud it was Wful.i do not recommend it. Such a pity as the scenery was spectacular but Laos has the same and not bombed by tourists

Don’t get me wrong the landscape is worth to be seen, but my today experience it’s awful! We arrived with an organised tour. Queue for the attraction took ages under the rain, but it’s something workers can’t influence, fine. Already during queuing some staff members pushed me few times, because apparently I didn’t queue the way they wanted me to. Anyway, the river ride stared fine the lady driver was lovely despite the horrible weather conditions and then just when we turned back she become an absolute devil. Well, I kept in mind whole ride to tip her hard work at the end, but she unfortunately preferred to remind us rudely about it on the way back. So first she offered some painting to purchase as we didn’t feel the need to buy it my bf just tipped her instead for both of us. It unleashed hell, whole the way back she was screaming on me “ Madame tip, Madame tip” over and over. We tried to explained her that my bf is holding our cash and I don’t have any additional one. She couldn’t understand it. At the end of the ride she looked at me and said I own her 60 vnd. I decided to ignore her and walk away. It’s absolutely unprociate  way to treat tourist!

Wish I checked the reviews before doing the boat tour. As everyone else said, the view was beautiful, but as soon as you rejected purchasing goods from the boat driver, their attitude started to change. We voluntarily gave him some tips at the end of the boat tour, but then he demanded more. What a shame. Go to Trang A boat instead. We took it the day before. The view was more gorgeous, there were many stops of interesting sites, and the tour was very well regulated.

Solo merece la pena visitarlo si pillas los nenúfares en flor. Las escaleras para subir arriba de la montaña están tan masificadas que es peligroso. Y suerte si intentas aparcar la bicicleta o la moto dentro del complejo, te empiezan a perseguir gorrillas para que dejes la bici en su "parking". La parte de la entrada tiene algún bar y está bien.

The boat ride goes through beautiful valley with caves that you go through. It takes about 1.5hr, no stops on the way. Trang An is honestly more beautiful and slightly longer. The key difference however is that noone tries to sell you anything nor asks for tips in Trang An. Tam Coc, we were offered (and actually bought one) painting from the driver, but then she started demanding further tip for the ride, when we refused she kept asking for one for the remnant of the trip which was very annoying.

ツアーの一行程としてタムコックのボートに乗りました。ボート料金（一人250,000ドン）はツアー代に含まれていますが、ボートを漕いでくれる人達のお土産売りつけ、チップ要求が厳しいので覚悟の上乗ってください。チップ相場わからず、現地の物価を考えて60.000ドン（360円）渡すも、少ない、もっとよこせとベトナム語で激しく要求し（言葉はわからないけど怒って数字をワーワー言っていたので察した）チップ受け取りを拒否され、仕方なく100,000ドン（600円）を渡すとサッサと胸ポケットにしまいました。景観も良く、おばあちゃんが足で漕いでくれるボート乗船は楽しかったですが、最後にこんな後味の悪い思いをしました。ほかのツアー参加者（ドイツ人）に聞いたら、30,000ドン（180円）を渡したら激怒されたけど、押し売りが酷かったのでそれだけ渡して逃げてきたと言ってました。嫌な気分になったと話していました。 チップは心付けであり、感謝の気持ちをあらわす行為であって、いくらじゃなきゃダメと押し付けられるものではないと思います。ボート漕ぎの方も普段は農家で、農業が暇な時はボートを漕ぐらしいので、生活がかかっているのでしょうが、こんな事が続くとお客さんもいずれ離れていってしまいます。ボート料金も決して安くはない料金を払っているので、ボート漕ぐ方たちが法外なチップを要求したりお土産をしつこく売りつけたりせずとも良いように、ちゃんとした報酬が支払われるように制度を見直していただければ良いなと思います。

Boat ride at Trang An is much better. In this one they asked for tips after the ride and try to sell you souvenirs on the boat. Very pushy.  We also expected we would stop at the temple at the end, but the boat woman just turned around and kept going.  They sell a discovery tour where you hop on and off an electric car, but most of the time you are just waiting for them to fill up the car with enough people. You can easily do this yourself instead.
