Giving a review to a place like this is quite difficult. The scenery is absolutely stunning and quite different than Trang An. In that sense, it is worth coming here. Unfortunately, there is pressure to give tips and to buy crap souvenirs. I felt uncomfortable with the boat driver because of that.

50% Betrug. Leute vor Ort haben auf den falschen Patkplatz verwiesen wo es dreimal so viel kostete wie auf dem Offiziellen. Und auf dem Boot sind wir an allen Atraktionen vorbeigefahren ohne anzuhalten, obwohl an der Kasse 6 Stops genannt wurden. Doch der Bootsfahrer kann kein Englisch oder Deutsch. Mehr als ein Fluss zwischen Bergen gab es auch nicht zu sehen.
風景は言わずもがなとても美しく一見の価値あり。一方オーバーツーリズム気味でかなり混んでいる。さらに詐欺まがいの売り子が大勢おり、いい体験だったのに、その人たちのせいで嫌な思い出になった。

可以去，推荐去，但是一定要注意！小心！不要被骗！！！  第一，他们在转折点让你买喝的，吃的， 我们太傻了，没有问价格就买了还给船夫买了，结果收了我们400，000don，这是什么价格，气的直接无语了，甚至后悔坐上船 但是想一想还是自己的错，没有好好提前看看谷歌评价，没有好好问价格，还同情船夫，想他很累给他也买的喝的，姐妹们，兄弟们千万不要太好心，好心喂狗吃，没有必要，这是他们的工作，如果可怜或者同情，还是感谢他们就给一点点小费就好了，要不然好不容易来享受旅游的心情都被破坏了。  第二，中途有位阿姨一直给拍照，教你摆各种姿势，下了船之后追着你让买她拍的照片，价格是20张600.000don，不买就一直追一直追，还摆出一副很亏，好像是我们骗了她的样子，还要哭（我心里一直想，妈的，被骗的我们才想哭呢），结果实在她不走，我们就买了5张，100.000don  诶，这么好的体验，风景就被他们肮脏的手法给败了，但我们也学到了，希望大家一定要小心！！拒绝他们！

Les points positifs + : - tres jolie tres calme et reposant . Les points negatifs : pour le tour 1 a 6 ( juste le bateau 250 000 ) il ne faut pas se rendre ici (pourtant il ya un comptoir qui vend les tickets mais que les tours 1 a 12 ici ) ont doit prendre navette jaune electrique gratuite et rouler 5 min a la station de bus  et le guichet pour acheyez il est tout au fond la personne qui conduit ne pas dit c ete ou ma juste laissez a l entree . Arriver au comptoir la personne me dit que 1 personne ce n est pas possible minimum 2 . c est nul heuresement vous pouvez attendre 1 ou 2 autre personne et leur demender. Et apres reprendre la navette et revenir . J ai dit que je suis tout seul j ai montre mon ticket et c ete ok ici . Pour le vietnam toujours vouloir plus d argent ?? .

We did the shorter tour for 250k/pp. Trang An was way better for the same price. No stops compared to 3 at Tran An, and way less time going through caves. Altogether shorter and at Trang An we didn't have our boat operator hawking trinkets at us. The ticket booth system is a joke. Just do Trang An this place is a big scam.
Beautiful boat ride but the person in the boat was really pushy about giver her a tip that ruined our experience. We gave her tip just to ask her to stop and she was asking for more

Spart euch das Geld und fahrt lieber mit den Booten in Trang An! Die Landschaft ist zwar schön aber auf der anderen Route in Trang An um einiges besser, man sieht auch das King Kong Movie Set. Außerdem möchten die Bootsfahrer am Ende ihr „handmade“ Ramsch verkaufen sowie ein „ordentliches“ Trinkgeld. Zahlt den Bootsfahrern mehr Gehalt! Fazit: keine Empfehlung!

The views and experience are amazing. However, the boat person stopped and ask for a tip. I was going to give a tip anyway but dislike being manipulated. The boat person also refused to continue rowing as they didn’t like the amount I tipped which was 50k dong and asked for much more. I understand it isn’t a lot of money to us Westerners but this isn’t how you encourage positive tourism. This experience ruined our view of Tam Coc boat tours. They should pay their drivers more or discourage manipulation.

Kurze Infos: Preis: 390k VND für 2 Personen Dauer: ca. 2 h  Begründung: Der Grund für die schlechte Bewertung ist, dass die BootsfahrerInnen Vorort einen das Geld aus der Tasche ziehen wollen: die ganze Fahrt wurde nicht mit uns geredet, bis zufällig bei der dritten Höhle VerkäuferInnen auftauchen. Die VerkäuferInnen versuchen zwanghaft Essen und Trinken zu verkaufen. Wir hatten kein Interesse und dann haben sie versucht für den/die BootsfahrerInn Essen & Trinken für (100 k VND) zu ergattern. Wir haben das abgelehnt, wir haben zwanghaft ihr ein Getränk gekauft, welches sie hinter unseren Rücken umgetauscht hat. Am Ende bevor wir aussteigen wollten hat die BootsfahrerInn begonnen wie ein Kind „Tip Tip Tip“ zu sagen, bis wir ausgestiegen sind.  Wir hätten der/dem BootsfahrerInn gerne freiwillig trinken gekauft und Trinkgeld gegeben, aber mit der Art wie das gehandhabt worden ist haben sie nichts verdient!  Fazit: Die Umgebung ist schön, ähnelt Ha Long Bay. Also wer davor schon dort war muss diese Fahrt nicht unbedingt machen.  Wir hätten uns die Fahrt sparen können, da dieser Umgang bei den Preis nicht in Ordnung ist.

We had a blast until our boats person tried to force us to buy his paintings, which we sternly refused. When we were almost docked he then requested a tip, I obliged and checked my wallet, I had maybe 20k in there and I went to give him the money and he said 'no, not enough' I showed him my empty wallet and said sorry that's all we have, he replied 'I need 100k' which we didn't have so I apolagized. He then tried to stop us from leaving the boat while forcefully demanding the money. Absolute disgusting behavior. We had a great afternoon until then. I'm sure not every worker is the same but it really affected our afternoon.

닌빈에서 뱃놀를 즐기시려는거면 땀꼭 보다는 짱안을 추천드립니다. 우선 땀꼭은 구명조끼가 오래되어 깔끔하지못하고 다 곰팡이가 슬었습니다. 그리고 배 타는도중 호객행위가 너무 심합니다. 옆에와서 음료수 구입을 권하거나 그림을 사라고 하거나 자기 멋데로 사진을 찍고 나중에 강매를 시키거나 하는 행위가 너무 심해요. 마치고 나서도 너무 노골적으로 팁을 요구합니다. 짱안은 정 반대에요 깔끔하고 강매나 호객행위도 없으며 뱃사공도 젠틀합니다. 땀꼭이 다른게 있다면 발로 노를 젓는다는거? (짱안은 손으로 저으십니다)

Ce temple troglodyte, ancré dans la montagne, serait charmant, s'il n'y avait autour et dedans le site une ribambelle de vendeuses, de toutes sortes d'objets, insistantes et aux méthodes agressives allant jusqu'à vous agripper le bras. Dommage
Veel te veel mensen. Zo toeristisch dat je niet meer de landschap ziet maar alleen boten en de  mensen die je roeien waar je al intree voor betaald  dan ook nog om geld vragen. Voor hun werk

Les paysages sont magnifiques mais malheureusement l'organisation est à revoir. Déjà il faut aller acheter les billets plus loin (perte de temps et cela n'était pas claire). Au moment de monter dans une barque (nous sommes 5 : 2 adultes 2enfants en bas âge et 1bébé) ils ne veulent pas et nous font monter sur 2 barques différentes. Nous avons pourtant insisté. Au bout de 10min de barque nous croisons des familles vietnamiennes à 4 voir 5 personnes par barque... De pas passer ce moment en famille à gâchait cette expérience
Muy buenos los cócteles. Pero no nos sentaron bien en el estómago.

Als je net uit Phong Nha komt, zoals wij deden, wil je hier zo snel mogelijk weg! Hoorde al verhalen over agressieve tour medewerkers die meer fooi wilde hebben. Als je internet moet geloven moet je hier zijn geweest, wij zijn van mening dat je deze plaats kunt overslaan.
발로 노젓는 사공이 신기함 코스가 너무 길고 느림. 그나마 겨울에 가서 덜 더웠기에 다행이라고 생각되었음 코스 반환점을 지나고 수상매점(?)강매가 짜증났음. 코스 끝나고 노골적으로 팁을 요구하는 사공, 팁을 적게 줬다고 인상 찌푸리는 사공 때문에 기분이 별로 좋지 못했음 그나마 경치가 좋아서 조금은 위안이 되었음
Chtěli po nás parkovné 40000 dongů za 2 kola, přestože se tam platit nemělo.

Les paysages sont très beau mais la balade est beaucoup moins bien que celle de Trang An, (qui dure 2 fois plus longtemps avec 3 fois plus de grottes et temples), pour le même prix... Énormément de touriste croisé également à Tam Coc, vu qu'on ne fait pas une boucle mais un aller retour  J'aurais pu mettre 3 étoiles si le conducteur de bateau n'avait pas arraché une fleure (lotus) pour la garder.. très déçue de cet acte.

This boat tour is around 1 hour long. It costs 390,000 dong for 2 people, plus you get pressured to tip the boat driver 100,000 dong at the end. If you choose to buy any of the items off the boat (fruits, beer, candies) you will be charged ultra tourist tax, so don't buy any of that! We made the mistake of doing so and ended up paying 250,000 for 2 pieces of fruit plus we got told to buy food for our driver. No explanation of what you are seeing along the way. Very little English on the boat rides. At the end I would say it was scenic but I wouldn't do it again and I wouldn't necessarily suggest people go. We found it be quite the tourist trap. Which is unfortunate, the tourist trap aspect took away from the experience.

Projížďka na lodi. Má trvat 90 minut, naše loď to stihla za hodinu a kousek. Projíždí se tři jeskyně, stejnou cestou se jede zpátky. Cestu otravují nabídky focení, vnucování nákupu občerstvení (i pro veslaře, který případné koupené pití uloží do lodi a pravděpodobně pak dal zase prodá). Veslař sám vnucuje nějaké obrázky a není mu trapné si ještě před koncem cesty sám říct o tip. Cena 120 tisíc na osobu (údajný vstup do jeskyní + 150 tisíc za loď) je postavena naprosto nelogicky (do jeskyně se bez lodi nedostanete). Za parkování vybírali 15 tisíc (scooter). Celkově drahé v porovnání cena/výkon. Pokud natě možnost navštívit Trang An, jeďte tam, dostanete vyšší kvalitu za nižší cenu.

Très joli mais le conducteur du bateau nous a jamais souri et n'a jamais interagi avec nous, mais pour nous demander des pourboires à la fin, là ya aucun problème.  Nous sommes considérés comme des vaches à lait. Quel dommage!

Je ne recommande pas. Nous avons fait le tour en bateau de 1h30. La promenade était plutôt sympathique, mais a été largement gâchée par l'attitude de la batelière. Celle-ci s'est arrêtée une première fois pour tenter de nous vendre des tableaux, avec insistance, et une seconde fois pour nous réclamer un pourboire (je n'avais jamais vu ça). Nous nous étions renseignés sur les montants recommandés de pourboires et avions prévu de lui laisser 150.000 dong. Une fois le pourboire donné, celle-ci nous dit que le pourboire est de minimum 200.000 dong, et attend donc que nous lui donnions plus d'argent. Je n'ai vraiment pas apprécié son comportement qui a gâché la promenade, qui n'était déjà pas extraordinaire. En somme, l'expérience a été totalement gâchée, nous avons eu l'impression de nous faire racketter et il y a des choses beaucoup plus intéressantes à faire aux alentours de Tam Coc.

Landschaftlich schön, aber was hier abgeht ist einfach nicht mehr angenehm. Dieses Abzockeverhalten mancher Vietnamesen kennt mal wieder keine Grenzen.  Unterwegs werden von den Bootsführern familiäre handgemachte Bilder oder andere Sachen angedreht - wenn man nichts kauft ist die freundliche Art auch sehr schnell vorbei - und am Ende der Tour wird man AUFGEFORDERT Trinkgeld zu geben. Unfassbar schlecht! 🙅🏼‍♂️  Übrigens wenn man nur die Boottour haben möchte, muss man extra zum Busbahnhof laufen. Wo anders werden die Tickets nicht verkauft.
Sympa, mais ressemble à Trang an. Donc en choisir une des deux. De plus le rameur, nous a jamais déposé au différents endroits. Et demande ouvertement des tips …
這邊很貴，入門票一個人120,000VND，一艘船150,000VND，所以如果自己一個人搭船要付270,000VND，而陸龍灣一個人就只要付200,000VND。這裡範圍很小，只是看山水而已，沒有任何建設，一路上也沒看見範圍廣闊美麗的農田。很快就覺得無聊了，而且會有小船推銷買飲料水果，最後下船前還要被收小費。陸龍灣範圍廣闊，有建設，比這裡有趣多了，沒有推銷，也不用付小費。把錢花在這邊，不值得。
I was ok just chilling in the boat, but you need to pay extra tip on top of the ticket you bought. They'll yell at you if you pay to little.

Una maravilla de paisaje y recorrido. La señora que remaba  según llegamos al final del recorrido dijo de dar la vuelta. Sin embargo, otros paraban y bajaban en los templos del final para visitarlos unos minutos, a nosotros ni nos lo ofreció. Además 5 minutos antes de acabar el recorrido empezaba a pedirnos propina descaradamente, le dimos porque la situación se hacía muy incómoda y encima se quejaba. Deben de estar explotados y hartos de los turistas, una pena.

Katastrophal organisiert. Ticket muss an anderer Stelle erworben werden, dann mussten wir uns eine Stunde im Gedränge nach Rechts und Links durchboxen, um nicht selbst immer wieder nach hinten gedrängt zu werden, um zum Boot zu kommen. Jeder Freizeitpark ist ein Kindergarten dagegen. Anscheinend ist dort niemand auf Touristenmassen vorbereitet. Die Bootsfahrt selbst war dann schön, allerdings recht kurz und ohne geplante Stopps. Auf Nachfrage ist unser Bootsfahrer dann aber angehalten und wir konnten einen kleinen Aussichtspunkt besteigen. Klarer Pluspunkt für den Fahrer. Den hat er sich dann aber direkt wieder kaputt gemacht, da er danach nach Trinkgeld gefragt hat. Scheint dort ja zum Standardprogramm zu gehören. An Tagen ohne viel Personenaufkommen vielleicht insgesamt etwas schöner und entspannter, allgemein aber eher Touristenfalle.

Déçu, nous avons fait Trang An juste avant qui a été un coup de cœur ! Les paysages, temples et grottes sont bien plus jolie, de plus le côté touristique ressort encore plus, on nous propose d’acheter des tableau sur le bateau, puis on nous demande un pourboire pour 1h de barque à peine  alors qu’on a payé déjà 25 à deux pour le tour en bateau + le tour en voiturette. Notre rameuse est partie énervé car nous avons refusé de donner un pourboire. On a vraiment l’impression d’être dans une attraction à touriste. Si vous hésitez entre Trang An et Tam coc n’hésitez plus.
자연 경관은  좋으나   강매하는  것에서  정떨어지네요. 주변   공사 중이라  분진도   심하네요. 사진 강매에 팁 강제  추가 요구.. 물건 강매 ...   두번 다시 오고 싶지  않은곳
겨울이라 그런지 별로에요.너무 비싸요

Sinceramente il giro in barca è stato deludente, il paesaggio è bello, ma se fai un giro in bici nei dintorni hai un panorama migliore e senza pagare 250VND, le grotte rappresentano poco interesse, in più il rematore è stato super scortese.

Hvis jeg skulle vælge mellem denne tur og turen i Trang An, ville jeg vælge turen i Trang An. Der vil du få en billigere og længere tur gennem de smukke klipper og grotter + ingen kræver drikkepenge af dig som de gør her. Dog meget sjovt de ror med deres fødder på dette sted

The views were wonderful and the boat tour quite nice. What was disappointing was at the middle of the tour, the river sellers force you merchandise that you have to buy for your boat driver... It's not like you can just walk away... Also at the end of the tour, the boat driver refused our tip saying it was too low and demanded more... All in all the views are spectacular, but in the end it all felt a bit like a scam ...

The landscape is stunning - highly recommended. However, the operators are charging high amounts. The boat drivers are working with sellers on the water to make you buy from them. Our one also laughed at our tip - I guess it was too low for her... go at 4pm after tourists leave on buses.

Pour commencer, l'achat des billets est des plus compliqué. Rien n'est indiqué, il faut prendre un minibus jusqu'à l'entrée de la ville puis revenir à l'embarcadère. Le personnel n'est pas très sympathique. Circuit peu attrayant, et pas d'explication pendant la balade. Prix cher pour ce que c'est. Je ne recommande pas.

Les paysages sont très beaux ainsi que le cadre. Cependant, la rameuse nous a demandé un pourboire avant la fin, nous lui avons donné 100000 Dong pour deux personnes. La rameuse n'était pas contente et nous a demandé 200000 Dong, nous n'avons pas voulu lui donner et cette dernière était très insistante et n'a pas voulu repartir. Nous avons attendu qu'elle lâche l'affaire pour de nouveau continuer notre balade. En plus, nous étions l'une des seules barques sans parapluie ...

Beautiful place and a great experience overall. You get scammed a bit during it which is a shame. They try to scam you into buying tonnes of photos and over charge you at the end forcing you to buy drinks. This is a bit of a shock for westerners like myself and not what you expect when you're not used to it. We tipped the boat driver who did a great job but were a bit sad to get forced into buying over charged drinks and hassled into buying photos. In hindsight you can obviously avoid this by being impolite yourself and just ignoring everyone which I guess we will have to do next time this happens!

Il posto è anche bello, però le barche quando siamo stati noi,  21-22-23 agosto, non partivano. Quindi l'escursione non siamo riusciti a farla, non ho capito quale fosse il problema. Per fortuna che siamo andati a fare quella a Trang An, peraltro molto bella.

Wirklich atemberaubend schöne Landschaft und eine der schönsten Bootsfahrten die ich bis jetzt gemacht habe. Allein dafür würde ich die Tour weiter empfehlen. Allerdings sind die Menschen die hier arbeiten extrem dreist. Zuerst war die Frau die unser Boot gefahren hat sehr freundlich. Ich habe ihr als Dankeschön kurz vor Ende 30.000 VND Trinkgeld gegeben (hatte die Rezensionen vorher nicht gelesen). Ich dachte sie würde sich darüber freuen da es sehr harte Arbeit ist. Statt sich zu bedanken hat sie aber angefangen über die Höhe des Geldes zu diskutieren und wollte mehr haben. Bin unendlich enttäuscht.

Nicht zu empfehlen.  Wir haben zu zweit 390k bezahlt. Relativ am Anfang kam ein anderes Boot auf uns zu und die Fahrerin machte ein Foto von uns und wollte es uns verkaufen. Nach der dritten Höhle wird aus Booten heraus Snacks, Trinken und Blumen verkauft. Es wird einem regelrecht aufgedrängt und man wird auch dazu „gezwungen“ etwas für die Fahrerin zu kaufen. Zurück an Land forderte unsere Fahrerin 50k Trinkgeld.  Ich finde es unverschämt, dass man hier Eintritt bezahlt und dann die ganze Zeit über belästigt wird und am Ende sogar noch die Höhe des Trinkgeldes vorgeschrieben bekommt. Landschaftlich war es ganz nett.

Très bel endroit, la balade en barque est vraiment agréable. Cependant, expérience gâchée par les arnaques similaires aux autres commentaires : à mi chemin, une femme nous demande avec insistance de lui acheter à manger et à boire et ajoute également pour celui qui conduit la barque, le tout pour un prix vraiment très élevé. A la fin de la balade, demande avec insistance un pourboire et nous dit que ce que nous lui donnons n’est pas suffisant. Ne vous faites pas avoir par les barques à mi-chemin, dites leurs un « non » franc. Je recommande tout de même la balade en barque vraiment sympathique

I recommend not doing this. The people on the boat are constantly trying to make money from you. Therefore we could not enjoy the sailing. They ask/beg for tips while sailing and try to sell you things. A German couple in front of us was afterwards even chased by the woman who had sailed them who thought she hadn't gotten enough tip. I recommend cycling along the water instead of paying for a boat trip where you constantly are being asked to pay.
Toristen falle und nicht wirklich schön und viel Zuviel los
A little deceptive. Very little cave and temple, nothing really worth the 250,000 VDN entrance fee. Beautiful landscape but you can see everything by bike (50 000 VDN/day)

Astonishing view but the tour takes way too long. You drive along the river but also back which takes 2 hours all in all. Along the route vendors try to guilt you into buying snacks for your rower and towards the end he even stopped the boat and demanded a tip.

Nature is beautiful, but the rest is just a tourist trap. We paid 390 for two people. During the ride, a lady took photos of us. We did not know why. At the end of the ride, a salesman was forcing us to buy an overpriced drink for our captain. Coming back, my wife fainted, and we had to leave the boat early. I was helping my wife while the captain was aggressively asking me for a tip. I gave him 30, and he was very mad asking for at least 100. Later, when we were coming back to our hotel, a lady caught up with us, showing us the photos taken on the boat. She asked for 400. So, all together, we paid 1000 for two people, mostly for stuff we did not really want. Very disappointing.

Schöne Tour und wunderschöne Landschaft aber können das mit der Touristen Abzocke leider nur bestätigen… Am Anfang war unser Bootsführer echt nett, hat uns noch auf verschiedene Sehenswürdigkeiten während der Fahrt hingewiesen. Kurz vor Ende der Tour wollte er unbedingt Trinkgeld von uns. Da wir das eh schon geplant hatten, haben wir ihm 30.000 VND gegeben. Das waren ungefähr 10% des Preises der Tour, die ja sowieso schon sehr teuer ist - zumindest für vietnamesische Verhältnisse. Als wir ihm das Geld gegeben haben, hat er nur ungläubig gelacht und wollte 50.000. Das wollten wir ihm nicht geben also war er für den Rest der Fahrt super unfreundlich zu uns. Echt schade, das gibt der Tour leider einen faden Beigeschmack.

Turen er for lang. Damen der sejlede os ville have drikkepenge, hvilket er fair nok, men da vi tilbød hende drikkepenge, ville hun forhandle, og krævede flere penge, da hun synes at hun havde sejlet en lang tur.

2h de trajet absolument magnifique pour 390 000 VND à deux. Je ne mets que 2 étoiles car vous êtes limite forcé a payer 100 000 VND de plus pour une boisson, fruits et autre chose pour la conductrice du bateau, vous pouvez refuser. A la fin demande un pourboire également, je n'en ai pas laissé car j'avais déjà payé pour le bateau.
Sortie en bateau sympa. Cependant ce n’est clairement pas la plus belle affaire il y en a des beaucoup mieux et c’est un autoroute de touriste

Très beau paysage... décors sublime mais super touristique,  et il faut tomber sur la bonne rameuse  la notre était avec son portable ,  ne nous a pas adressé la parole de la balade mais à essayé de nous vendre des tableaux du Vietnam...et à l arrivée,n a pas oublié de nous réclamer le pour boire avec insistance.... Si vous tombez sur la bonne cela peut être une superbe expérience....
짱안보다 별로? 새들이 많아서 재밌긴 함

The tour itself was nice but not as good as the Trang An River Tour. Nice scenery and interesting tour, spoilt by hard sell from boat sellers and constant demand for a tip from the lady rower. The difference between the two tours is like chalk and cheese, yes a bit more expensive for the latter but out of the two, 100 times better.

Die Tour an sich ist ganz okay, die in trang an ist allerdings wesentlich schöner. Hinzu kommt, dass von Anfang bis Ende der Tour sehr vehement versucht wird einem noch mehr Geld aus der Tasche zu ziehen. Der Gipfel war, möchte man am Ende dem Bootsfahrer Trinkgeld geben, verlangt er dass man ihm noch mehr gibt.

It's beautiful and all but honestly too long. We tried to ask our driver to turn around after the first cave but he insisted we go all the way. Once we were ate the turn around Point he stopped and a bunch of boats came over to sell us stuff. They instead we buy a drink and a drink and food for the driver. No wonder they didn't want to turn us around so early. Be prepared to tip they constantly ask.  And the photo you get they will also charge. If you do decide to go, take some water, and  an umbrella for shade.

Sympa et beau mais très cher. Il faut compter 120.000 par personne + 150 000 par bateau. Un bateau prend maximum 2 personnes. Il faut aussi donner un pourboire au rameur (visiblement 20 000 n'est pas assez vu sa tête alors que nous n'avions eu aucune explication ni discussion pendant la visite)

Une très jolie balade autours des montagnes. La vue est spectaculaire. Dommage que ce soit aussi touristique. Les bateaux se suivent à la chaîne. Dès le début, une femme nous tombe dessus pour nous prendre en photo et ensuite nous les vendre.  Au bout du chemin, des bateaux boutiques nous attendent pour nous vendre des boissons ou de la nourriture pour 3fois le prix. Si tu ne prends rien ils te poussent à offrir quelque chose  à ton batelier. Si c'est une boisson, il te remercierai, la posera sur le côté probablement pour la leurs revendre après où se repartager les gains.  À la fin du tour, le batelier exige un pourboire. Et c'est lui qui choisi le montant, si le montant que vous voulez lui donner ne lui convient pas, il vous suivra et s'énèrvera contre vous.

Check if it’s open before you come!! We came on Aug 24, 2023 & Tam Coc was still not yet open (seems the place was opened for some days then closed again amidst some unresolved dispute between peddlers & the company). Some mid-aged ladies kept hovering on motorbike asking to take us to their own boat tour (somewhere nearby), which was really annoying. There were lots of men on security guard suit sitting at the entrance but did not give us any guidance, until a kind lady came to suggest we visit Trang An instead (we went there though). Hope things will be better next time, including the tipping & floating market issue we’ve been hearing about.

The views were stunning  spoilt a bit by the weather. There are 2 prices on the board, one for the boat ride the other for entry to caves, you can't really do one without the other. The price you pay at the desk is different from the board. There is an expectation you buy fruit and drink from women selling these on a boat. The rowers of the boats also expect a  substantial tip. Also there is an option for a photograph to be taken. They tell you the price then when you get off the boat want to charge you more and won't accept you just buying one photograph. Shame really and they are very insistent following you in the hope you will change your mind
충경은 조으나.강제적인 팁요구에 짱안으로 손님을 다 뺏겨서 한산해요..보트요금도 인당 25만동으로 많니 올랏고..먼저 탑주니 적다고 더 달라고 해서 그럼 안준다니까 받음..대신 배에서 내릴때까지 팁더 달라고햐서 짜증..그냥 짱안으로 가세요.관광객이 끊긴 이유가 다 있어요.

Beautiful Landscape, but a bit a scam if you take a ride with the boats - the driver forces to give them a tip, and on the end of the road, there is a floating shop, where this ladies give the driver some drinks and food and you have to pay for this a high price (drink and snack over 300 k VND). Also a photographer took a picture from us, as suggested 1 USD for a pic - when coming back, he wants you to buy full album for about 500 k VND

Se non avete tempo preferite trang an, più bello ed economico, barcaioli simpatici. Qui ci hanno costretto a fare la mancia e ci hanno imbrogliato facendoci comprare da mangiare e da bere per il barcaiolo spendendo quanto avremmo speso una notte in hotel

Un vrai piège à touristes! Des bateaux "nourriture" s'accrochent à vos barques, donnent de la nourriture à votre rameuse et vous demande de payer, cette même rameuse vous crie dessus pour avoir un pourboire. On vous bombarde de photos et on vous suit pour que vous les achetiez. Du racket non stop, je ne recommande pas car au-delà de ça, cela ne s'apparente pas à une balade mais à une course, les rameurs se foncent dessus, se crient dessus et vous subissez tout cela alors que l'endroit est splendide (sale quand même). Extrêmement décevant.

Paysages très beaux mais ce lieu est devenu un vrai attrape touristes, où l’on vous force à sortir de l’argent du début à la fin. Il y a évidement le prix du tour qui s’élève déjà à environ 350K pour deux personnes (270 si vous êtes solo). Au début de la promenade, une barque s’approche de la vôtre pour prendre une photo puis vous réclame de l’argent et se montrera très insistante si vous refusez. A mi chemin, après avoir fait demi tour, votre batelier vous amènera au milieu d’autres barques qui vous forceront à acheter à boire ou à manger. Si vous déclinez, on vous fera culpabiliser de ne même pas accepter d’acheter à boire à votre batelier (surtout refusez, ils travaillent tous pour la même compagnie et peuvent tout à fait se fournir des boissons entre eux gratuitement). Puis sur le retour, votre batelier vous déballera probablement tout un tas de produits souvenirs à bord en vous demandant d’en acheter. Et enfin, si vous avez eu l’audace de résister à tout ça, le batelier vous réclamera un tip avant même de vous ramener au point de départ. Ce tip me semble être la seule chose acceptable et j’aurais de toute façon eu l’initiative d’en donner un, mais après tant de tentatives d’arnaques et réclamé de manière aussi insistante, ça laisse un goût amer.

The view is good. But I really hate the boat vendors and the boat rower(asking too much tip) They are very greedy, pushing, and rude. I don't know why we have pay so much for the entrance and boat tickets(120k+150k), but we still have to stand for this bad experience.

Sehr schöne Landschaft und tolle Atmosphäre. Das ganze Erlebnis wird allerdings stark von dem Thema „Trinkgeld“ bzw. „Zusatzverkäufe“ getrübt. Wir waren relativ früh da und ab dem Zeitpunkt des Starts hat uns unser Guide erklärt wie schwer die Arbeit ist bzw. wie müde er ist. Die Verkäuferinnen beim Wendepunkt waren sehr aufdringlich. Als wir auf dem Rückweg waren hat er uns mehrmals sein Handy gegeben mit einem Text wie schlecht es ihm geht und auch auf dem Rückweg hielt er ständig an. Ich bin echt kein Unmensch und mir ist durchaus bewusst, dass es kein leichter Job ist aber man hat leider gemerkt, dass es stark gespielt war.

Der Ort an sich ist schön und sehenswert, die Landschaft beeindruckend. Aber die Bootsfahrt ist insgesamt ziemlich teuer. Am Umkehrpunkt haben schon die Händler in booten auf uns gewartet und man wird quasi zum kaufen gezwungen. Am Ende der Tour hat uns die Bootsfahrerin noch frech nach Trinkgeld gefragt. Ein Mann in einem Boot, wollte Bilder von uns machen. Nach seiner Aussage 20k vnd und wir kaufen nur das schönste Bild. Am Ende hat er 6 Bilder gemacht und wollte 120k vnd haben. Nach seiner Aussage: weil ich Ausländer bin und es mir leisten kann.  Abgesehen von der schönen Landschaft hat mich die Tour sehr verärgert.

Pretty place but entire river is dirty. Don't forget to bring sun cream and hat. Be careful the rowing ladies ask you for tips and to buy their hand crafts, I found that Very annoying. And they will ask you to buy them beer as they are rowing for 2 hours...

Thoạt nhìn, nơi này rất thú vị. Tiếc là bạn phải thuê thuyền chèo để tự chèo, và họ có những người nông dân nghèo, già nua chèo thuyền đưa bạn đi tham quan ba hang động (Tam Cốc). Dọc đường, bạn sẽ bị ép mua ảnh, nước ngọt, khoai tây chiên, kẹo, v.v. Sau khoảng 90 phút, một số người nông dân già chèo thuyền đã thấm mệt. Thật đáng buồn.

Nette Bootstour die meiner Meinung nach etwas zu langen dauert. Hinzu kommen recht penetrante Verkäufer auf Booten, die teilweise ziemlich anstandslos sind. Wir wurden klassisch abgezogen, indem die gute Verkaufsdame unserem ärmlichen Bootsführer was angedreht hat und wir dafür zahlen mussten, um aus der Sache wieder rauszukommen. Am Ende wird fürs anlegen und für den Bootsführer auch noch nervig nach den obligatorischen „Tips“ gebeten. Das ist also mehr als ausbaufähig, die Fahrt an sich ist aber schon schön.

짱안 보다는 못합니다. 밤이 되면 지역 주민들이 호수 연안에 세제 가루를 풀어놓고 그 위에서 빨래를 합니다. 주변은 항상 관광 차랑들로 번잡하고 먼지가 많습니다.  위로 좀 올라가면 작은 로컬 시장이 있는데 간혹 옆에서 죽은개 대가리랑 사체를 해부해서 파는 것도 볼 수 있습니다. 개고기 문화가 있습니다.  근처 음식점들은 기본적인 수준이라는 게 없고 대부분 형편 없고 가성비 편차가 매우 극심하기 때문에 세심한 주의가 필요합니다. 분류가 확실한 펍이나 바베큐를 제외한 특색 없는 식당은 지역 주민들끼리 담합을 했는지 모든 메뉴가 동일 합니다. 메뉴판의 디자인도 똑같습니다. 냉장고에 있는 시원한aquafina 500ml 생수의 가격은 보통 5천동 합니다. 그러나 여기를 걷다보면 1만동을 흔하게 접할 수 있을 것입니다. 몇 가게만 거쳐가도 5천동에 파는 곳이 나오니 절대 1만동에 구입하지 마세요.  제가 다시 간다면(그럴리 없겠지만) 여기서는 배를 타지 않습니다. 배를 꼭 탄다면 짱안에서 탑니다.

The scenery is amazing and the driver was very friendly at first. But the driver got annoyed when we wouldn’t buy her peanuts at the turning point. Later she asked for a tip. We planned to giver her 20.000 first so gave it to her. She than asked for 40.000. We told her no.  It’s already a pretty expensive activity. People shouldn’t ask for tips.

Really lovely nature, and impressive to see people row with their legs. However, they see dollar signs in you as a tourist. Go to Trang An instead.  First a very annoying guy with the scooter parking, asking 10k. Then they charge decent money for Vietnamese standards, 390k to enter with two people. But then it comes. 1st, an annoying photographer asking for money. 2nd, halfway you make a turn and locals try to sell you stuff for crazy amounts of money, beating prices they charge in Amsterdam! You then supposed to buy something for the rowman/woman too. (Just refuse politely) 3rd, at the end you will be asked for a tip, which we tried to give 20k. She shook her head as she found it too little, so we took it back. This is just a very VERY nice piece of nature, which is made less nice by the "in your face" quest for making money.

Die nagtiven Reviews über die Botsfahrt sind zutreffend. Aufdringliche Verkaufsgespräche und das verlangen und verhandeln von Trinkgeld (50k für 2 Personen gegeben, sie hat 100k verlangt) am Ende der Tour macht die schönen landschaftlichen Eindrücke der Tour leider etwas kaputt.
Chaotic scene at the stand where you buy tix for a boat. The river and scenery is beautiful but be prepared to get aggressively hit up for money by various folks along your journey.
Recomiendo hacer mejor Trang An Grottoes tour, es el triple de tiempo (este dura 1 hora) por el mismo precio. Estuvieron todo el viaje intentándome vender cosas y después piden propina.
Te toeristisch. Hebben hierna de Trang An boottocht ook gedaan (voor dezelfde prijs) en deze was echt stukken beter.

beautiful scenery but a tourist trap. be prepared to be harassed to buy snacks for your rower half way through the journey and then harassed for tips by your rower 100m before the end. they will even stop rowing unless you  tip. avoid all this and go straight to trang an. no tips required and no harassment to buy snacks.  much more beautiful scenery in trang Ang as well.  trang an is amazing. go there.
Điều duy nhất bạn cần làm ở đây là đến và đi thuyền sampan. Đó là một trải nghiệm tuyệt vời, khi bạn lướt trên mặt nước giữa một vùng thiên nhiên tươi đẹp! …

Cher ! Mais plutôt joli. Dommage que l'on vous harcèle avec des ventes en plein milieu du parcours et surtout que le rameur vous fait bien comprendre que votre pourboire ne suffit pas. Je précise qu'il ne parle pas un mot d'anglais et que bien sûr, nous n'avons eu aucune explication durant le trajet.
Giro in piccole barche con remi azionati con i  piedi costo 200000 dong non lo consiglierei , meglio a Trang An costo 25000 dong ma di gran lunga migliore molto più suggestivo è caratteristico!

The ride itself was really nice - peaceful and picturesque. At the end of the last cave, boat turns back and you will face ladies selling different stuff on their boat. One lady grabbed our boat and started to offer different items. We didn’t show any interest, then she started to talk with the woman rowing our boat.  She didnt want to take us back until we buy something so we had to buy a coke for 100 dong and pay tip for our lady another 50 dong. IT was very unpleasant. The only word they know in english is TIP. IT felt weird since we would have tipped her anyway but with this kind of aggressive approach we totally didn’t feel like it. With such approach towards tourists vietnam will not be a nice destination soon..
경관은 너무 좋습니다 ~ 그러나 보트투어를 했을때 1시간30분~2시간 너무 긴 시간이었고, 좌석이불편해 끝에는 엉덩이가 저립니다. 그리고 뱃사공의 팁강요와 상인들의 물건강매는 너무 심한편이라 짜증이날정도였습니다. 자전거투어에선 고물자전거가 많아서 이동하기너무 힘들었고, 도로가 모두 비포장도로라 자전거를 잘못타는분들은 힘드실수도 있습니다.

Wenn man zu Zweit kommt, muss man einen Aufpreis für ein Boot zahlen. Während der Fahrt kommen Verkäufer auf Booten zu einem, auch die Fahrer selbst versuchen etwas zu verkaufen und wollen Trinkgeld. Das trübt die Fahrt leider.
Carino. ma meglio andare a Trang an allo stesso prezzo giro più lungo e panoramica mogliori.
小費文化非常煞風景，還會推銷買手工藝⋯
The landscape is boring. Nothing much to photograph. But if you have never on a boat, you should try once.
A "can do" not a "must do" as it is flooded with tourists only interested in taking pictures.
Cũng hay. Nhưng họ bắt bạn phải boa tiền và mua ảnh họ chụp trong chuyến đi. Không ổn chút nào.
Balade magnifique, mais ne vous attendez pas à aller aux points de vue .. et attendez plutôt à un "tips tips" en guise d'au revoir ..
Don't go for the boat ride  if you are going to trang an . Over priced and the boatmen try to sell stuff and ask for tips . Trang an is much better than this

It was nice but..... unfortunately, we did this and in the afternoon went to Trang An, make that one totally useless and redondant.  If you plan on doing both , save yourself time andmoney and skip that one :)
Unfrienly staff, weak organization. One of that travel places, where you are just a wallet with money, ATM, not a person. A foreigner who should pay, pay and pay. You better pay of f××k off :) Next!
建議大家去長安,三谷的船家小販會向遊客不斷索取小費.推銷，大大破壞旅遊興致
소문보다는 실망입니다..
Mini detour, historical. But nothing special, not for me.
Hemos entrado para alquilar 4 bicis y ni nos atendieron. Nos dijeron el precio con una mala cara y mirando el móvil.Al final no alquilamos las bicis allí.Me parece muy mal!
Muy poca seguridad con las barcas....es peligroso y falta simpatía.,..
Tốt nhất là nên đi theo tuyến đường Trang An số 3...rất tuyệt vời.  Trên tuyến đường này, họ cứ hai phút lại xin tiền boa (cho tiền boa, cho tiền boa).
A poor replica of Trang An. Water is dirty, not scenic and people will try to hassle you to buy food n drinks for your rower and take pictures at high price

Ich bewerte hier nur die Bootstour auf Tam Coc und diese war für mich die reinste Touristen abzocke. Der Fluss war stark besucht und Verkäuferinnen die einen auf dem Fluss penetrant versuchen was zu verkaufen.
Great location and views, however we dont like that they try at all times to get the money to the tourist
Good environment, nature. The place is good but they must standardize the prices. Everyone has different prices what you buy.
Not recommended. Ugly town, boring restaurant selling all the same average stuff and the boats tour is also not very exciting at all.
Viel Müll überall....
배타고 왔다갔다. 3번째 동굴후에 벅을꺼 사라고 달라붙음. 뱃사공이 처음에는 말시키다가 도착할때 되면 표정 바꿔서 팁달라고 함. 5만동 주니 일인당 5만동이라며 더 달라고 함.
Dissapointing, kinda just a 60mim boat trip with no options to explore
Pendayung sampan memaksa meminta tips sebelum perjalanan selesai, sangat disayangkan.
Khu vực này khá đẹp nhưng những người lái thuyền lại rất hay đòi tiền boa, điều này làm giảm đi phần nào trải nghiệm tổng thể.
Đừng đến đó. Họ không hỏi xin tiền boa, mà họ đòi hỏi tiền boa. Thay vào đó, hãy thử Tràng An, cách đó vài km về phía bắc.
Con sông kia trên đường vào chùa Bái Đính thú vị hơn nhiều.
No se enteran. Les falta rodaje. Buena voluntad.
Much worse than Trang an. Lady asked for tip
호객행위..최악..절대 사주지말고 팁은적절히
Accueil à l'embarcadere très froid.
รสชาติไม่ได้เรื่อง
Không có nhiều ở đó
Nothing special...

A beautiful and interesting experience, with stunning limestone landscapes and a unique boat ride through caves and rice fields.  However, there is far too much overtourism, which makes it hard to enjoy the peaceful atmosphere. On the way out you constantly meet boats that are already returning, creating a lot of traffic on the river. On the way back, the rower tries to sell paintings quite insistently, which makes it difficult to relax and fully enjoy the scenery

We took the combo at 350,000 per person. Do not do after Trang An otherwise you will be disappointed. 1h30 walk with a round trip and not a loop, less caves, just a temple (we had to wait because our rower had a drink laughing with his friends) and we also had to ask the rower to stop at the "viewpoint" where ascending a staircase you have a magnificent view, otherwise he continued straight like 90% of the other rowers. Then we disembark you, you wait for a yellow shuttle that takes you to see two pagodas including the famous Bich Dong.

For those visiting the area and wanting to do the boat trips of Tam Coc, Trang An, the visit to the Hoa Lu Citadel, the pagoda and Mua Cave, Tam Coc is the ideal base because full of homestays, restaurants, cafes, has everything you need, including ATM, mini market, pharmacy. We stayed in Trang An (which is 6km away) and was too isolated to eat for dinner.Only my surprise, and I still do not explain why, it was all rather deserted, lots of locals but no people around, yet in 3 weeks of Vietnam we always found a large crowd of tourists everywhere

Save your money and go to Trang An. The scenery is great, and the people rowing their boats row with their feet which is a nice novelty, but you don't stop anywhere and the route back is just a reverse, so you'll see the same things twice. Lastly, and the real downside, is that the lady REPEATEDLY asked for a tip! Never had this anywhere, and prior to her asking one would have been set in stone, as it's a hard job, but that put the money back in my pocket and left a sour taste.

Boat rides can be boring if you are not fond of nature.  1 hour for 2 ways ride. The ride was okay, don't meet any persistence seller on the ride. The view in December was not great but still good, not many boats so you can enjoy nature more.Some captains will volunteer to take your photos so tip them well. YES !! TIP THEM WELL.  I hate giving tip but this one. IT IS A MUST.
Very nice to do. We even saw some monkey from afar. Their are more options to go. Did others as well. They sell combination tickets. Be aware of that. If you get them, use them.

We left from the main pier, early around 9 a.m. to avoid the crowds. The walk is magnificent, rocky peaks, rice fields, water lily fields, three caves under which the boat passes. everything invites you to daydream along the water, unfortunately, the boats follow each other in procession and our boat was accompanied throughout the journey by another boat and the two rowers were particularly happy to meet again because they chatted without interruption during the one and a half hour walk, so for the peace of mind, the reverie and the stroll, it was a bit of a miss. On the other hand, we escaped the forced purchases, we were not solicited. Our rower nevertheless expressed her dissatisfaction with the tip given.

Beautiful scenery, greedy peopleThe landscape along the way is beautiful, the boat is also hard, but forcing spending is actually too disappointing and disgusting.The cruise boat will start with a camera boat, if you agree to buy a photo he will start screaming around the sky for 500,000Don ten photos, waiting for you to hit the price, I later bought eight photos for half the price of 250,000 but I still feel losing.At the end of the boat, there is a boat that sells snacks, and she will ask you to help the hard boat to buy some comfort (but how can she buy more than us), this trip we spent 200,000 (remember to discuss the price)On the way back the boat will also stop to sell the paintings (2 300,000) but we didn't buy, my patience is running out.Finally I gave the boat to the little fee she was not satisfied, and asked if I could give the whole number to the multi-point.Really too greedy. However, the scenery is beautiful, but for the first time I wanted this trip to end soon 🙂

Went with my hubby and 3 year old with a tour. It’s very easy to get to the ticket office and book your own ticket. No queues at all. The boat trip was safe at took approximately an hour. The scenery is beautiful. However it’s a tourist trap. The boat purposely takes you and stops at some ladies selling snacks. They are very pushy. They guilt you into buying for the boat man. Anyway after a wonderful ride we were cornered  and rudely asked for a tip. Also they demand for a specific tip amount and are not satisfied with small amounts. Besides rowing the boat, we were not even given a smile. Anyway maybe it’s their culture. I recommend tam foc if you can tolerate the rudeness

We were a family of 4 doing the tam coc boat trip, and as westerners we had two boats of two, unlike Trang An which allow 4 per boat.It was easy enough to buy our tickets and there was no waiting round for the boat, we opted to set off at 10am to avoid the hot port of the day. Our boat driver was certainly a chatty one! The tour is about 1.30hr which I think is about right seeing as there are no stops. The boat was marginally more comfortable than the trang an boats but both would not go amiss cushions! You go up and down the same stretch and through some small caves.The turning point has boat sellers, watch out here, we said we didn't want to buy anything having just had breakfast and she said what about a drink for your driver, then proceeded to pack up a bundle of drinks and snacks for him and tried to charge us a 100k, even with a mark up those items in a shop would cost about 20k, plus we suspected that the driver probably wouldn't even consume them and they would get handed back to the seller later on. Therefore we declined and told the driver he could have the 100k as a tip instead which he clearly preferred.

Endroit très touristique, la balade est sympathique, les vues sont très bien. Le point négatif se situe à la mi parcours avec quasiment l'obligation d'acheter à manger et à boire pour le pagayeur...certe cela est dur, mais a la fin le pagayeur n'oublie pas de vous réclamer un pourboire. Dommage de prendre les touristes pour des portes monnaie sur jambes.

The scenery is nice but it’s heavily polluted by infrastructure. Don’t go for a boat tour here. Trang An is 100 times better, there’s a reason this isn’t part of the UNESCO site. It’s a decent place to walk around however.

Wij zijn 2 nachten in Tam Coc gebleven. We hoorden goede verhalen hierover. De eerste dag besloten we om de 1 uur durende boottocht midden in Tam Coc te doen. Deze zou ik alleen niet aanraden. De omgeving waar je doorheen vaart is prachtig maar het wordt verpest door het onvriendelijke personeel die jou alleen als bron van inkomsten ziet. Degene die onze boot bestuurde deed voor zijn doen zijn best om leuk over te komen. Het enige wat hij steeds zei was 'goodmorning' en 'good afternoon' en dat bijna de hele bootrit. Onderweg wilden verschillende vrouwen in bootjes iets verkopen aan ons. Een vrouw met een camera die foto's van ons wilde maken en we werden meerdere keren tegengehouden om de prachtige lotussen te kopen, wat ik zonde vind dat ze die uit het water daar halen. Toen we zeiden dat we niks wilden kopen, moesten we wat voor de bestuurder wat kopen of een tip geven van haar. 1 keer nee werd niet naar geluisterd. Alleen naar onze boze blikken, omdat onze bestuurder waarschijnlijk hoopte op een tip, waar hij na de rit bij ons om vroeg. Dit allemaal verpeste de ervaring om rustig te genieten van de omgeving. De bootrit was met zijn 2en al 15 euro en het was ondanks de prachtige omgeving geen tip waard. We hebben de andere boottocht van 3 uur niet gedaan, maar ik denk dat die leuker was geweest.De volgende dag was het beter weer en hebben we fietsen gehuurd voor maar 2 euro. Dit was erg leuk en de omgeving was erg mooi! Zeker waard om rondom Tam Coc te fietsen of een scooter te huren In de avond stonden er 2 karaoke bussen. Dit was erg gezellig. Veel mensen van omliggende hostels kwamen hier waar we gezellig een drankje konden drinken en feesten.

Im Oktober passen die Bilder mit den Reisfeldern nicht. Das hat uns sehr irritiert. Die Fahrt ist sehr schön. Doch andere Touristen machen diese Tour mit lauter Musik, was dem Naturereignis sehr schadet. Am Ende dachten wir der Bootsfahrerin etwas Gutes zu tun und ihr Tip zu geben. Doch die fing an zu verhandeln und wollte das Doppelte. Hier gab es auch keinen Fragebogen am Ende, wie in Tran An, wo explizit gefragt wurde, ob nach Tip gebettelt wurde.

C’est un endroit sympa mais très touristique les barques se suivent les unes derrières les autresQuelque chose qui est un peu déplaisant à mon goût c’est que les étrangers doivent payer une barque pour deux alors que les locaux peuvent être quatre sur La Barque bien sûre cela double le prixOn ferait ça en France on serait critiqué

I guess the only silver lining to this dreadful Covid affair is that we were the only boat when we went "foot rowing" through the "three caves valley" in Tam Coc. In normal times there are dozens at the time. It is a peaceful experience through a very picturesque landscape. Worth the excursion.
Đúng là Tam Cốc đẹp và nổi tiếng thật nhưng con người làm dịch vụ ở đây thật quá tệ. Chỉ nhăm nhăn xin tiền tip. Làm cho cả chuyến đi mất cả hay.

Cảnh đẹp phải nói là hiếm có và có lẽ đây là top 3 địa điểm bạn phải đi nếu đến với Ninh Bình. Tuy nhiên,....1. Lái đò chưa gì đã có ý tip, toàn ăn nói bóng gió để khách tip nên mình khó chịu vô cùng.2. Quá đông, lộn xộn

>>Tam Coc c'est 1h de barque sur un trajet aller-retour pour 120k+150k+10k*=280k>>Trang An c'est 3h de barque sur 1 boucle au choix parmi 3 circuits pour 250k+15k*=265kSi vous devez en choisir qu'1 allez à Trâng An et si vous souhaitez faire les 2, commencez par Tâm Coc qui est bien moins impressionnant.J'y suis allée pour voir les champs jaunes de riz vu dans Google image... C'était vert, pas la bonne période ! >_<Ha et je déconseille également la fin de journée, j'ai fait de 16h30 à 17h30 et c'était tout à l'ombre ! *parking moto

We arrived here around 2pm. It was starting to get a little grey out but was the perfect temperature for going down the river. We were told to grab lifejackets and get in line to get on a boat. It was just the 2 of us plus the rower. Which was nice now they are small. Some boats had 3-4 people in them. There was a lot of people here but the line up moved very quickly. The ride is about 2 hours and the rower rows most of the time with their feet. Pretty incredible to see. You will pass threw 3 water caves complexes Sang Cave, Toi Cave & Ba Goit Cave. The river runs along side other limestone mountains and rice fields. The tour is not a loop but has a turn around point. So you pretty much see everything twice. The way back can be a little boring and very uncomfortable. There is people along the way taking pictures if you want to purchase them later as well as boats selling food & beverages. Our boat guide didn’t say 2 words to us. After trying many time we actually assumed she couldn't speak English. Then in last 5mins she just repeated tip for me? Then when we tried to give her a $5usd tip  she got mad (in English) that it wasn’t more and demanded $10usd. On the bus before we arrival we asked our Tour guide what a good tip would be he said to tip $2usd per boat. He also told us that they would give us information along the way and point things out. Our boat guide talked on her phone quite a bit and if she was not on the phone she would talk to other paddlers passing by. Other guides would allow the people on the boat to attempt paddling with either their arms or feet. So we really $5usd was generous. back on the bus others on our tour said their guide talked to them the whole time. Others on our tour were threatened at the halfway/ turn around mark that she wanted $5usd a person and she wanted it now or she was going to leave them there (they had 3 people onboard).Even with it being a little boring and uncomfortable it is worth doing. So much stunning beauty. It was such a highlight of Ninh Binh.
Beau site mais les locaux essaient d’arnaquer en forçant beaucoup l’achat de produits à mi parcours, et en refusant de terminer le parcours s’ils n’ont pas eu de pourboire !
Good place and beautiful Nature. Unfortunately we went there while it was cloudy. But still a Nice and cozy place.

Tam Coc Garden hotel is indeed an invitation to dream; and indeed you will have to “fill in the “gaps” because this fabulous location has so much potential. However, when you read reviews and replies from the administration that go way back to 2018, and still see many of the same problems going on... it feels a bit discouraging.The area is so visually idilic that it sells by itself. Rooms are right on top of the rice paddies, with their aquatic life of frogs and dragon flies... We are used to frogs, so we do not mind the loud late night chatter, but if you are a light sleeper, or city dweller may be bring earplugs. Cooler nights are more silent. And of course it will be extremely humid. Even the toilet paper. It comes with the location. Nothing can be done.The staff is delightful, Vietnamese people are mostly sweet and smilie. But unless you speak Vietnamese, communication is a great challenge. Also because gestures seem different too. At Tam Coc, some dishes were very tasty, but the photos you see on the web site and menu are there for “marketing purposes” so I was explained. That food is not what you will get. Breakfast is not very good, in my opinion, and I shall tell you why:There are thousands of tiny black flies all over the hotel, food is not “net-dome” protected. We heard complains of this much before... The person in charge of making your eggs, sometimes someone with a chef hat, sometimes a boy... unluckily a simple omelette seems to be a huge mystery.  Do not worry, a kind of weird tortilla will keep you fixed. In a country famous for its coffee, hotels should keep some of the good stuff. Not so much here, where coffee is rather bad. Close by in Ninh Binh milk flows, but, may be I am wrong, in Tom Coc milk does not seem splendid, but rather “long life” stuff. Do not take this point particular from me, as I do not have proof of this.Hygiene, masks to prevent spraying food in the times of Corona, well... it took time to convince the young manager that it was pertinent to maximize precautions. But I wonder what happens behind doors in the kitchen. We did not see staff rubbing alcohol on their hand. Teapots and plates held by the rim... and you wonder... In any case after we talked to the manager a bottle of alcohol and masks were on the front desk... The fridge in the room was noisy, sometimes disconnected. The hot water finishes in a jiffy.We found the toilet, every day filled with little ants.This hotel claims to be in the “vein” of ecology awareness, but small plastic bottles is the container of your water. We found dozens of fish floating on the water on the rice paddy, just a few meters from the room and pieces rubbish everywhere. It was not windy. It is possible that this has nothing to do with the hotel, but still, water is always water, right? We believe that we are always on a learning process, so about certain things we also have to allow time to mature. However the guest does not care about their learning process. We are here to enjoy, feel pampered, find coherence, honesty and quality of produce and service. Now, if you are not a foodie, you do not care so much about this nonsense of masks, you sleep like a log, and do not mind to see some dead fish floating near by, this is definitely the place for you!

We went during early Feb, which meant it was more foggy and cold. Due to the poor food choices and the poor weather, we left a night early and it was the correct decision in hindsight. Pros:- Tam Coc Boat ride was beautiful and peaceful when we went - during off season, and also during the coronavirus outbreak. - Cycling around Tam Coc was nice, with not much traffic and a pleasant countryside view. - Hang Mua Pagoda: the steps are uneven, some of them very high. If you have any mobility issues be wary, especially in the rain. The view on top was foggy for us but I can imagine it being beautiful on a sunny day. Even on this day of poor weather, there were quite a few people who were smoking (?!?!) on the way up, climbing on places where it was signed no climbing, and the bugs at the top were quite impressive. Cons:- Food: After being to Hanoi and being amazed by street food at cheap prices, Tam Coc food was a shock. It is entirely catered to Western tourists: extensive menu, expensive prices. Usually, in a city or town, there would be at least a few local joints that serve popular local food and specialised in 1-2 meals maximum and would be freshly made and delicious. I did not understand the stunning TripAdvisor ratings for some of these restaurants. One of the banh mi we had at a supposedly highly rated restaurant used old bread, unseasoned pork mince, no sauce, at double the price of excellent banh mi places in other areas. My vegetable stir fry at another place was unseasoned again. Rice was old. A roast duck was overcooked, chewy, and the glaze had colour but no flavour. I ordered a pork noodle soup dish at yet another place and was served packet ramen with a few pieces of meat. I noticed other diners ordered "Stir fry" which also used ramen packet noodles. - The exploitation of boat workers: rowers in Tam Coc were paid minimally and quotas of boat rowing were imposed on them so they cannot rely on this for full-time income. One of the rowers I spoke to was a lovely gentleman whose family was living in Tam Coc for 9 generations. It really broke my heart to see the corporations who own the land profiting off the locals who do the leg work (literally, as rowers use their legs to row). - Weather: Do not go in winter if you plan to climb Hang Mua, you will not see anything and will be attacked by mosquitoes the entire way. The boat ride was was nice, only a little foggy, but absolutely freezing. - Extremely touristy: Natural attractions in Vietnam which attract tourists seem to also attract tacky plastic decorations in an attempt to make the place more bright and colourful. I do not understand the need to create more plastic waste in a country that already has issues with waste removal.- Scams: I understand that this is a way of earning just that bit of extra income for struggling families, however, I do not think being dishonest is the right way to do it. In our hotel at a buffet breakfast, we were approached by a lady who started off which the usual "Hi, where are you from? How long you plan to stay in Vietnam" before telling us that she rents out excellent scooters for VND 150K and any scooter anyone else rents for 100K is of poor quality. Anyway, this was obviously a scam, but I would have thought I would be safe in my hotel buffet breakfast to not be approached. Shame on the hotel for letting her do this to guests.  Other scams to be aware: parking scams, where you cycle towards a touristy attraction and attempt to be stopped by locals who charge you a fee to park your bike (just keep cycling until you reach your destination), boat ride scam - where the rower turns you directly into the "floating market" while on the boat ride and you are heavily "encouraged" to buy goods. You will need to say no repeatedly. Then you are asked to buy a beer for your rower (the beer is obviously not drunk by the rower and the money is split). If you want to help your rower, you can tip him/her. My opinion is that you only need to do 1 boat ride, as the scenery is pretty much the same.

We didn’t have to queue long for the tam coc boat ride. It was D390,000 for the two of us (about £13) and the boat ride was just us and the chap rowing. We went along the river with lots of other people in boats under caves and saw lots of karst mountains and birds feeding in the rice paddies. There must have been a hundred or more other boats though it is tet when a lot of Vietnamese take their holidays. It was restful and fairly interesting. There were hawkers on boats selling stuff quite insistently and we have our rower a tip. It was ok but not amazing.

Caratteristica la gita in barchetta con la signora che remavon le gambe, ma veramente fanno di tutto per spillarti soldi. Arrivi in piazza a Tam Coc e ti fanno pagare il parcheggio per la bici, poi vai alla biglietteria e fai il biglietto di circa 5€ a persona per entrare + 6€ per la barca. Ok è finita qua dici...e invece no. La tipa ti porta sotto tre e tornando indietro si ferma da alcune signore che hanno sulla barchetta roba da mangiare e ti dice spudoratamente di comprargli qualcosa che la venditrice gli ha gia preparato (sicuramente è una prassi) poi arrivi alla fine del tour e scendi salutandola e lei ti dice senza pudore give me a tip e sono altri 5-6€. Il tutto per un semplice giretto in barchetta che non ha nulla di speciale
Nonostante il panorama non consiglio il tour in barca. Si viene condotti con queste barche lungo il fiume passando sotto alcune cave. Nulla di eccezionale. Consiglio un bel giro in bici nei dintorni.
En décembre, c'est idéal :pas trop de monde mais déjà suffisant.. Tour en vélo avec aurora, temps en barque à rames avec les pieds : pas mal..

The views throughout the boat tour are exquisite and our rower was initially very friendly.Halfway through the ride, your rower will take you towards several boats with merchants trying to sell you cups of fruit, beers and other small snacks at extortionate prices. They will then try to force you to buy snacks for your rower by throwing them into your boat. Their guilt-trip worked and we ended up buying several things for our rower (which she didn't even touch).At the the end of the ride, our rower began yelling at us for a tip and became quite aggressive. Such a shame as it would have been a lovely experience without the scams and abuse.
我們在 Trang An 坐完船後, 搭車 15分鍾左右來TAM COC 吃午餐休息,接著爬上山頂, 下望 TAM COC 的遊船.上下山階梯很陡, 不適合年齡小或年齡大者. 非常耗體力.(搭船建議到 Trang An, 管理較完善, 景色也較漂亮)

If you have to choose between the boat trips at Trang An and Tam Coc, choose Trang An. If you want to do both, do Tam Coc first, or you'll find it an anticlimax as I did.Tam Coc has two pluses. First, the wider vistas make for better photographs. Second, the novelty of the boat(wo)men's rowing technique - plying the oars with their feet.Tam Coc is a full-on tourist hustle: touts everywhere and pressure for tips. There is only one route, returning the same way. Views are spectacular, including the ridgetop dragon of Hang Mua, and it's a pleasant relaxing trip.

A very hard sight to review. The views are really nice, but it's not cat ba by any mean. You get on a rowing boat with aady rowing for you. On the way to you stop to buy some drinks and the selling ladies push you to buy some drinks and snacks for the poor rowing lady - found it really discomforting. On top they try to hard sell you everything both before and after you get on the boat. It would be 4/5 without that sales push, but it really leaves bad taste.
À découvrir si vous venez vers Hanoï Vietnam du NordBalade en vélo Jolie petite ville calme , certains restaurants ont du personnels qui parlent le français Très pratique pour mieux comprendre...😊

Hoa Lu is more like a temple than a castle. As a foreigner, I don't know how to enjoy this place because Vietnam's history is complicated...it's difficult.Tamcock was reasonably funny. If you're going to midsummer, don't forget to take precautions because the sun is so great. For nearly two hours, there is no escape (^^;

Stunning area to visit. But I felt uncomfortable throughout the boat tour. I'd heard you could be dropped off far away from your starting point depending on how the boat tower rated your tip. Ours must have been appropriate but it was nearly the cost of one ticket again. We are hassled to buy drinks at the half way point, for the rower once we said we didn't want them...just uncomfortable. Ruined a beautiful view.

Well luckily this was our last day and tour in Vietnam.  Tam coc was absolutely stunning and was lovely to experience it by boat but the constant demand for more more more leaves a bad taste in your mouth.  What could have been a lovely boat ride admiring the scenery turns into a fleecing the tourist.  The pressure applied to buy something for your rower to eat and drink.  They are just so pushy and aggressive.  We didn’t get anything and our rower turned from being very friendly on the way up the river to not speaking on the way back until she requested a tip.  We gave 100,000 but she said that she wanted 200,000.  I think the whole experience would be better if it was just included in the price and then everyone could just enjoy themselves without this ending your trip with feeling that you are just constantly being preyed upon.  Would still recommend going here as it is so beautiful but just be pre warned that pressure is going to be applied.  Also a photographer who even if you say you don’t want a photo want their piece of you too.

De boottrip die vanuit Tam Coc gaat is wel een verplichting. De beste tijd is overigens mei, dan is de begroeiing het mooiste. Ik was er in oktober, maar dan is het nog steeds de moeite waard. De opdringerige verkopers, zeuren om fooi en de verkoop door de roeister moet je maar voor lief nemen. Ze kunnen wel wat leren van de organisatie in Trang An. Daar gaat het een stuk beter.

Ngo Dong River meanders through the limestone mountains, through 3 caves including Ca, Hai, and Ba Cave, seems like another mini Halong Bay.We took a boat at Vung Tram boat landing. Each boat can only take 4 passengers and leaves the wharf when filled, most of the boat rowers are women, gently use the two paddles that control the small boat. I suspect their is no rudder. The two paddles were tied to the boat, the rower leans back, using their legs to control the boat. Do note that when you wish to take photos, it may be awkward for your photo to have the sculler with both legs spread open.... 🤣.The boat moves slowly through the waters for visitors to admire all the beauty here. There are also many small boats to following the visitors to sell there are lotus buds, bananas, pineapples, drinks, candy, snacks etc. Also, there is a photographer who rows his own boat and taking potshots at the passing visitors. He will then wait at the boat landing to sell the printed photos. The river does not have fishes as it is mostly alluvia from the upper reaches and with my action camera under water, there were only weeds and milky green water. Riverbanks does have lotus and hyacinth growing.And, the lady rower does demand a tip at the end of the trip. 🤔 Like 100,000vnd per pax. Wow. I needed to get off the boat, so.... Enjoy.

Beau site et belle balade en barque mais prévoir d’y aller tôt car beaucoup de monde. Nous avons eu de la chance car peu de touristes quand nous y étions. Le rameur avec les pieds très gentil au départ puis dès qu’il a vu que nous n’achèterons pas les souvenirs qu’il vendait et bien très étonnamment il était beaucoup moins sympa...je voulais lui donner 100 000vdn de pourboire et lui ai donné 50 000 qu’il a pris sans le moindre remerciement Des vendeuses de souvenir et boisson en barque vous attendent à la sortie d’une grotte pour essayer de vous faire acheter qq chose pour le rameur...Trop / très touristique Bref à faire mais privilégier trang an
Paseo muy tranquilo por el río bajo un sol de justicia. Las cuevas no valen mucho la pena, pero la zona es muy bonita

Wij hebben vandaag met zijn vieren de boottocht van ongeveer anderhalf uur gemaakt. De omgeving is prachtig, maar het was érg druk. Honderden boten roeien achter elkaar in een rij door de bergen. Bij het keerpunt zijn er opdringerige verkopers en de roeier vroeg voordat we terug waren al om een fooi. 50.000vnd gegeven, maar dat vond hij te weinig...

ツアーの行程でとても楽しみにしていたタムコックなのですが、真夏の暑い時期には長すぎるボートクルーズになりました。景色は良くのんびりしていたのですが、最後の折り返し地点で物売りのおばさんが待ち受けていて、船頭が欲しがっているから買えと無理やりお菓子を買わされ、帰りには写真を撮っていた女性にも無理やり写真を２０００円で売りつけられました。今後行く方は気をつけて！買う意思がなかったら要らないと必ず断ることをお勧めします。景色、洞窟は良かったのですが、なんとも残念な場所になってしまいました。

Attrazione molto turistica e il giro in barca risente molto dell’affollamento. Si attende sulla banchina dopo aver fatto il biglietto, fino a quando non arriva una delle piccole imbarcazioni su cui ci può imbarcare (due per barca). La caratteristica principale è che il rematore (per la maggior parte donne) manovra i remi con i piedi e la forza delle gambe. L’imbarcazione non è molto comoda, bisogna tenerne conto visto che il giro dura quasi 2 ore e si è sempre sotto il sole meno i 3 brevi tragitti nelle grotte. Offrono ombrellino ma è scomodo. Il paesaggio intorno è bello e forse unico, con formazioni rocciose che puntano verso l’alto, una sorta di baia di Halong sulla terra ferma. Tanti turisti, forse meriterebbe, avendo tempo a disposizione, un giro sulle cime per ammirare i templi che si scorgono appena dal basso.

Beautiful views. Maybe not worth the ~2 h drive from Hanoi, though. Boat tour took about 2 h, same route back and forth. Not too many tourists. Price of the tour was 150 000 for the boat, plus 120 000 for each passenger. So 390 000 in total for the two of us. This price would have been decent for the experience. However, we felt very irritated for the behaviour of the rower and at the end, were not happy. We do not recommend the boat tour. You can apparently also rent a bike and cycle around, seeing pretty much the same things.As mentioned by many in tripadvisor, at the midpoint of the tour, there are vendors trying to sell overpriced stuff. Rower stopped there, and they tried quite loudly to make us buy "tip" for the rower. But, as we had learned before the trip, rowers do have their own beverages with them and these vendors ask for extremely high prices. So we stayed strong and did not buy. At the end of the tour, before going to ashore, rower quite rudely asked for tip. We actually were going to give her a tip, but after her rudely asking, we really felt a bit sour. She stopped rowing, asked more loudly and it seemed like she wasn't going to row ashore before we tip her. We then, feeling irritated, tried to give her a small tip. Then she sniggered, and asked for bigger tip. We had to give her the money, then she row ashore.Considering tourism in the future, attention should really be paid for the behaviour of the rowers. Tourists or not, it is not nice to get fooled and face such a rude behaviour. After all, tourists must be important for the whole business of tam coc...For the views, 3.

Place scenically is great... But hard to get a photo without a tourist in it. The river cruise is OK... Spoiled by another paddler with her tourists about 2 metres from you all around.They take you to the end point where heaps of Viet women await in THEIR boats to sell you crap you don't need or want.Simply keep saying no. Or khong.Later on the way back your paddler will become peddler. Sighs. Give them a tip at the end. Our guide suggested 10000 dong per person.1 1/2 hours on the water... Too long in fact.

This boat ride is incredibly beautiful and is worth it. The landscape is breathtaking. However, at the end of the journey, other boats come to dock you to offer you something to eat/drink. Our rower was in cahoots and forced us to pay 6$ of peanuts for her, which is a huge amount of money for Vietnam. And she asked for an extra tip at the end.... Just say you have no money with you for the peanuts and give a tip at the end if you want to.They also take pictures of you during the trip and try to sell it when you come back to the dock. They do not insist but it’s not pleasant that we always try to get money from us because we are tourists ...

The limestone formations seen as your boat cruises along the river are beautiful though not so impressive for me anymore since I have seen the same landscape numerous times in my home country (PH). What made it unique for me was passing along 3 short caves. I believe it would have been more beautiful if the sky showed some blue since all the clouds made it look gloomy. I was also put off by the snack vendors forcing us to buy food for the rower. I would have volunteered to buy but the food was overpriced (100K VND ~ 2 USD for a small can of Red Bull, a small bag of junk food, sliced green mangoes). I was able to haggle the price down to 50K VND after removing the sliced green mangoes. After the boat trip, the rower asked for a tip of 150K VND. I am willing to give a tip but I don't like it when they put a price to it. But since the work they did was also physically tiring, I gave a tip of 100K VND. I just hope they are properly compensated by the tour operators.

La visite sur les barques est très agréables car les paysages sont magnifiques. Mais notre rameuse nous a bien fait comprendre que le trajet était fatiguant et à la fin elle nous a carrément demandé le montant du pourboire en nous disant que c'était fatiguant... Alors qu'on allait lui donner. Bref le fait de se plaindre pendant le trajet rend la visite moins agréable, c'est dommage
Không thật sự đẹp nhưng cũng là 1 trải nghiệm tốt . Khung cảnh thiên nhiên sông nước . Vào hang thì tối quá nên ko thấy đc gì

Je m’attendais à mieux après avoir parcouru la campagne environnante en vélo, très reposant sous un ciel bien gris. Pour la barque, rien de bien original . La rameuse rame avec les pieds . Le paysage est joli mais pas très impressionnant, les grottes sont des passages intéressants et rafraîchissant mais à la sortie on vous arrête et là, c’est le grand déballage de souvenirs à 2 balles et même si vous refusez poliment, on vous déballe tout le sac quand même ! Ça gâche un peu et casse l’ambiance avec la rameuse. Celle-ci vous réclamera un pourboire bien avant l’arrivée . On lui a donné bien sûr mais toujours en se sentant un peu forcé.

We found our own way to Tam Coc rather than organising a group tour as we prefer our own company. Transportation and buying tickets was super easy. We got in the cue to line up for the boats and were given our life jackets. A tour group was in front of us and two behind us. When we got to the front of the line, the boat operator grabbed my partner by the arm when we moved a step forward for the next boat and pushed her saying no. He then went to the group guide in the line behind us, looked at their tickets, pocketed some cash and proceeded to let that whole group get on the next series of boats. We challenged him and the group guide and were ignored by both. He then did the same with the next group behind us, again pocketing more cash. We challenged again, this time a bit more forcefully and were finally allowed on a boat. The boat trip itself was spectacular and fun, but spoilt by that experience. At the half way point, there are boats trying to sell you for and drinks. They can be quite forceful and try to guilt you into buying something for the person rowing you. We made some enquiries, and these rowers would much prefer a tip and will normally ask for one. The going rate seems to be 20,000 (approximately $1), but given the work they do and how far they row, we think that a more generous tip would be warranted. (Given our experience, we don't think the operators would pay them very well!)

C'est très beau oui, mais rien à voir avec trang an. Noud avons fait les 2 et selo nous il n'y a pas photo. Ici c'est plus petit, les grottes ne sont que des souterrains, et le côté sauvage des panoramas est moins important.Nous l'avons fait à 7h30 le matin et nous étions seul, mais 5 minutes après c'était déjà la foule, donc si vous venez ici pour éviter la foule de trang an ce n'est pas forcément le bon plan.Également, ici l'achat de soda/bonbon/fruit est quasi obligatoire. Un arrêt pour le point de vu terrestre sera payant et impossible de rejoindre le quai sans un dernier tips. Alors ok, tout est fait dans une relative bonne humeur mais le sentiment qui reste à la fin n'est pas des pus agréable. A trang an, pas de pause achat à la moitié du parcours et le pourboire est selon vos désires (du coup nous avons laissé plus).Bref pour nous c'est trang an, même si il fait impérativement éviter les heures de pointes.

Per sampan met als bemanning vrouwelijke roeiers een tocht van ongeveer 1,5 uur gemaakt langs Karst formaties.Het is een mooi tochtje ,lekker ontspannend. De vrouwen bedienen de roeipeddels met hun voeten. Let wel op dat bij het keerpunt aan het eind van de eerste helft de dames hun koopwaar soms vrij agressief aan de man/vrouw proberen te brengen.

Very beautiful scenery. Can find lots of restaurants and bar here.. But the boat ride in Tam Coc can be very touristy and expensive. Those who love more peaceful and calm environment can try boat ride in Van Long Nature Reserve.:)

Wandered down last night around 4:30, very few tourists around, nice trip with only a few boats on river. Bloke was quite insistent on a tip which spoiled it as cost wise was similar to Trang An (much better experience in my opinion)

The mountains and rivers are beautiful trang an is much better with better boat rides and scenery better,the thing i cannot stand is tam coc is much too touristy they are everywhere,all the shops and restaurants just for the tourists calling you over as you walk by,and the boats hundreds of them as the crowds,prices more expensive here so go to Trang An and enjoy yourself more.

Lovely scenery and most people are kind and accomodating.Grab drivers trying to charge us twice. Difficult to get around if not hiring a bike.Ancient capital was a waste of time and prepare to be harassed of you go there.

We did this boat trip after doing Route no. 3 of Trang An.  In comparison we found the Tam Coc trip to be 'average'.  We found the caves and the scenery better by far in Trang An.  This Tam Coc trip was an hour and a half long.  The trip takes you through lovely emerald green rice fields and past the hang mua caves (500 steps up to the dragon).  It was late Feb when we did it and even though the rice fields were short, they were still bright green.  The trip had you coming back the same way whereas in Trang An you do loops.  The intriguing, unique and ingenious way of rowing is something I have not witnessed elsewhere.  I wonder if that is where Team NZ in the America's Cup got their idea from!  We voluntarily assisted rowing with the extra oars in the boat.  Our oarswoman respected our decision not to stop at the river hawkers who were selling food & drinks to other boats.  She rowed right past them, which we were thankful of.  We were going to give our rower a tip, when just before we got back, she did ask for one.  We gave her a tip of course.    If you only had time to one or the other I would recommend Trang An.  Although in saying that if you want to paddle through rice patties, then our Trang An Route 3. trip did not go past any.  I don't know if any of the other routes go past them.

最初はタムコック行きたかったのですが、ツアー会社の人がMua cavesの方が季節的にも良くて、今推してる観光地だよーと言っていたのでそっちのツアー予約したのですが、いざ行ってみると、タムコックのボートにも乗れるという事で、乗ることに。   景色は美しい。でもやはり1番いい季節ではなかったんだな。って感じ。稲の成長的に。   折り返しの場所あたりで水上のお店があるんですが、そこのおばちゃんが、チップチップと言って、この船の船頭さんにビールとお菓子を買ってあげなさいよと強めに言われ、まあチップでいいだろうと購入して、その船頭さんにあげた。   そして降りる時に、さっき食べ物飲み物渡したにも関わらず、チップを要求してきて、これまた、強めに要求してきたので、少額ですが二万ドン渡すと、何を言っているのかはわかりませんが大声で不満そうなことを言っていました。  チャンアン、ハロン湾行きましたが、チャンアンはそんなことが全くなく、かつ、やはり景色、洞窟が美しいので、どうしてもタムコックでボート乗りたいってわけではないのなら、チャンアン、すごくおすすめです。

El paisaje es un espectáculo, pero lo empañan el precio de la entrada, la antipatía de las taquilleras y las barcas/kiosco que te paran durante el recorrido para que compres comida o bebida y si dices que no, te engañan diciendo que se la pases a tu remero, se la pasas, ¡y luego te piden que lo pagues tú! Encima antes de desembarcar el remero te "pide" propina.

Dans le cadre exceptionnel de la baie d'Halong, une organisation bien rôdée en machine à cash ... si vous êtes en manque de rencontres avec vos compatriotes ou si la solitude vous angoisse, n'hésitez pas !

Die Landschaft um Ninh Hai und die Tam Toc Reisefelder sind wunderschön. Auch die Bootsfahrt an sich hat uns aufgrund der schönen Natur inklusive Reisfelder, Felsen und Höhlen sehr gut gefallen. Leider warteten am Wendepunkt Verkaufsboote, die nicht abzuwimmeln waren. Wir ließen uns letztlich auf den Kauf von ein bisschen Obst ein. Daraufhin schmiss die Verkäuferin jedoch noch weitere Dinge zur Bootsfahrerin, die wir dann gerne für sie mitbezahlten. Am Ende unserer Fahrt wollten wir der Bootsfahrerin trotzdem noch ein bisschen Trinkgeld geben, aber sie lies nicht locker und forderte mehr als das Doppelte. Nachdem wir ihr von dem Verkaufsboot bereits was mitkauften, war dies eine absolute Unverschämtheit. Diese Verkaufsboote waren leider reine Masche und Touristen-Abzocke und machten das ganze Bild der schönen Tour kaputt. Nachdem danach jedoch noch die Dreistigkeit der Fahrerin hinzukam, würden wir die Bootsfahrt eher überdenken und eventuell lieber die Trang An-Tour machen, obwohl wir diese nicht bewerten können.

nothing special, only the lake view with the big rocks.. people who love view the rock mountain realy love this place.. just enjoy the holiday in tam coc, because is cheap and the food before going this place is delicious, its called goats soup ...

Leider war es sehr bedeckt , als wir die Tour gebucht hatten , gab aber auch keine andere Möglichkeit da wir nur 1 1/2 in Ninh Binh waren , Tour als solches schön , ruhig , tolle Berge , ein paar Vögel .
