Nerven tut tatsächlich die Abfertigung, mein Tip so gegen 16 Uhr buchen ( Tour dauert in etwa 1.5 Stunden ), dann sind die meisten Reisebusse wieder unterwegs die die Leute hergecart  haben . Wer aber in Ninh Bình ist , sollte auf jeden Fall dieses oder auch gerne eine andere Bootstour , allein schon wegen den Natureindrücken , machen .

Roddtur, var mycket fin, men tyvärr förstördes denna mycket av att personen som rodde krävde dricks innan hon släppte av mig.De har även båtar längsmed vägen som pressar mycket hårt på att man ska handla, först till sig själv och sen till föraren.Tråkigt på en så vacker plats.

יצאנו 4 חברים לשייט לאחר שקיבלנו המלצות ממטילים אחרים. לצערנו הרגשנו שהמקום פחות נועד למטיילים בטיול הגדול. אלא, יותר לזוגות בני 40+. המקום ללא ספק יפה מאוד, אך התחושה שמקבלים היא סוג של ספין למטיילים.

ハノイに3泊した際のうちの1日、日帰り現地ツアーで行きました。サイクリングもしましたがメインは手こぎ（足も使います）ボートに乗るクルーズです。風景は素敵ですが、少し所要時間が長すぎるのではと思いました。折り返し地点で漕手へのチップとしてドリンクを売りつける船が寄ってきますが、無視すれば買う必要はありません。また降りるときにもチップを要求されますが、チップを上げるのは嫌だと思ったなら断れば問題ありません。チップは漕手に全額渡るシステムではないようです。

Nous avons fait le tour en bateau proposé (390K dongs pour deux, prix officiel). Le paysage est magique et les grottes sympas (moins impressionantes que Trang An). On est entouré de pains de sucre et c'est assez magique. On l'a fait en décembre, en semaine et aux alentours de 15h30. Pas trop de monde. En revanche, le tour est un peu gâché à la fin par la rameuse qui réclame à plusieurs reprises "tip tip tip" puis qui affiche grandement son mécontentement du pourboire qu'on lui donne. C'est dommage.

We had 2 days in Ninh Binh and stayed in Tam Coc as a base. Thankfully our homestay host gave us good advice on the places to visit first and we swopped the itinerary as the activities for day 1 (Esp Pagoda and Mua Cave climb) were more tiring. We were travelling with 2 kids and their 2 grandparents, no guide but a driver who spoke little English. Itinerary:DAY 1  Trang An (2.5-3hrs)-200K dong/pax for boat rideBai Dinh Pagoda - free admission but electric car and guide are optional and payableMua Cave - 100K dong/paxBoat ride at Trang An was enjoyable. Depending on the route you choose, we chose route 2 and visited fewer caves compared to route 1. Had a few stops, including the King Kong movie setting. More variety, more scenic and organised compared to Tam Coc boat ride. There was even a small pagoda in the middle of the water catchment with people performing. Rowers had uniforms and gave you a feedback form at the end. There were oars in the boat so it was fun to do our own rowing too.When we reached Bai Dinh Pagoda, we realised the area was massive. We bought 2-way tickets for the electric car as we didn't want to spend too much time walking. Electric car stopped only at entrance to the temple and the return ride was at the same place or further uphill (looked so from map). We didn't opt for English guide and so was rather lost. We followed the directions and walked past impressive buddha statues (many lining the walkway towards the hill top pagoda). Lady selling tickets for entry to pagoda was unfriendly and unhelpful. We figured we didn't want to walk down past the same statues again to the electric car station. So we ended up walking back to the entrance. It was downhill, unsheltered and not scenic (same route if you walk up instead of taking car). But we still thought it made more sense to not walk back to the electric car drop off point at the entrance.We next visited the Mua Cave area. By the time we reached there, we quickly made the climb (quite tiring and not for the grandparents) and managed to enjoy the lovely sunset view and Tam Coc valley (location of the next day's boat ride) from the peak. Didn't have time and energy for the cave, eatery or paddling boat at the bottom after the climb. Day 2Tam Coc boat ride (1.5-2hrs, 195K/pax)Bich Dong pagoda (free, 1.5hrs)Hoa Lu capital (payable) Return to HanoiBoat ride charges consisted of per person entry fee and two-to-share boat. We (4 adults and 2 kids) had to pay for 3 boats though we opted to go on 2 boats. In early December when we visited, no lush greenery or yellow fields like the photos which we have seen. Lotus plants have also withered. Was hot and somewhat boring. Do note that there are many eateries to the right of the area when you return from your boat ride. Bich Dong pagoda was an easy to manage climb up to a small temple. A small area and quick stop over. Hoa Lu Ancient Capital - area was big and map was not clear. Was a pity as we wondered about and didn't manage to find the palace.. Just the Kings' temples.

Si vas a hacer el crucero por la Bahía de Ha Long, quizá no valga la pena hacer además este Ha Long terrestre, que es como denominan las agencias a este recorrido por un pequeño río. No obstante, he de reconocer que el paisaje es bucólico. Es una actividad solo para turistas, y quizá la distancia y el tiempo empleado, no sean proporcionales al resultado. Normalmente se puede combinar esta excursión con una visita a los templos de Dinh y de Le en Hoa Lu, pero sobre todo lo que vale la pena ver por esa zona es la pagoda de Bai Dinh.

Al llegar a Tam Coc es de lo primero que ves, y según lo que vi en internet, había que ir si o si, así que fue lo primero que hicimos. Pasas por 3 cuevas muy chulas y las vistas son espectaculares pero un par de cosas que no me gustaron.... Primero que la señora qie va remando te pide propina al acabar y segundo, a mitad del recorrido se acerca a una de las barquitas que de por allí en medio que venden bebidas, etc y te medio obligan a comprarle algo a la señora que va remando tu barca, si, es muy barato, pero no me gustó nada, luego ella te da unas galletas o algo pero no me gustó. Trang An tiene el mismo precio, el recorrido es mucho mas espectacular y no te piden ni propinas ni nada.

Pick up at the hotel at around 0830 for a 2 hour drive to the destination. One stop on the way there for the ‘happy room’ and the opportunity to buy local crafts.  We visited some pagodas on the way also, had a buffet lunch which wasn’t too bad - big variety of food to suit most tastes, then onto the river for a boat ride.  It’s all been said by others - the ladies (and some men) who row with their feet, the caves and the hasssling to buy products.  Just resist the touts, and tip the rower directly at the end.  After the boat ride went for a short cycle tour which was fun, Although the bikes left a lot to be desired.  The first one I jumped on didn’t even have a chain. It was a pretty hot day, but the bus was air conditioned, the boat ride was breezy and in the shade at times, and the bike ride was in the cool of the afternoon.  Didn’t get back to the hotel until about 8pm. Long and tiring day, but well worth the experience.  The river did not look like the pictures in November, and it would be nice to see it with the yellow fields on both sides, but pretty non the less. Andy was our guide, and she was eager to please all, and was certainly enthusiastic.

Tour de 1h30Jolis paysages et très jolie région mais le tour est un peu gâché par les arrêts forcés aux bateaux marchands et par les sollicitations de la rameuse pour son tips.Nous avons préféré le tour de Trang An, plus long, aussi beau et plus "calme"
beau circuit en sampam, mais beaucoup de monde, alors préferez la visite en fin d'après midi  vers 16H ou il y a moins de touristes et où le soleil couchant rend la nature si belle.

La visite de cette ville est incontournable pour voir la baie d'Ha long terrestres, cependant la ville est extrêmement touristique et la moindre chose se paie. Nous sommes vus comme des portes monnaies sur pâtes ! Attention au parking dans les endroits qui sont gratuits mais où de nombreux vietnamiens veulent vous faire payer...Les activistes en bateau, certaines ne valent vraiment pas le coup... Et le prix est quand même pas donné.

Leuke/mooie boottocht gemaakt, helaas bij iets minder weer. Je gaat helaas geen rondje maar een stuk heen en weer terug. Onder de rotsen door gaan is erg leuk. Er wordt door de dames gepeddeld zowel met de armen als met de benen. Bij aankomst is het wel erg druk... je bent er niet alleen.

We did the Tam Coc boat ride after we’d already done Trang An. I’d 100% recommend skipping Tam Coc and just doing Trang An. Tam Coc cost twice as much, and the entire experience was spoilt by touts. It’s ~400k dong for a couple to get on the boat. The trip up the river is beautiful, but on your way back your boat is held ‘hostage’ by touts with little floating shops who insist you buy something at 100k dong and won’t let your boat go until you do. When you say no thank you, they try to make you buy something for your boat rower instead which didn’t bother me at all except he didn’t even eat it. Then when you’re almost back at the pier, the boat rower pulls over to the side and insists you pay 100k dong tip; we haggled and dragged it out whilst he rowed back to the pier and then paid 40k dong as we got off the boat, which we were told was generous. The entire experience was unpleasant and not worth it.
Super balade mais beaucoup de monde et traquenard des vendeuses en barque que j'ai encore un peu de mal à digérer. J'ai refuser d'acheter quelque chose et c'est mal passé !

I wish l had read the reviews here beforehand. Our trip was fine until the halfway turn around point. Then as others have said we received aggressive hassling by the boat women selling food and drink, almost demanding we buy something for our rower. We declined as we intended to tip anyway. Atmosphere soured already our rower then brought  out some purses asking for money for them. I handed over what l intended to give as a tip then to try and shut them up. They carried on rowing but it was too late and the whole thing was ruined. Also worth noting if you’ve been to Halong Bay previously you won’t be as impressed as everyone else.

This boat ride took us through a few caves amidst peaceful rural scenery. There were no rice fields here, only karst mountains and lotus blooms in the river. The rower used his feet to hold the oars and paddled with his legs. Going through the low ceiling caves in the dark was quite an exciting experience. The whole ride took more than an hour and before returning to dock, the boat man hinted that he was tired out and needed tips. The boat ride was interesting at the beginning but towards the end, it became a bit boring. The water in the river was not the cleanest unlike the crystal clear waters in Yangshou, China. The crowds of tourists also turned the quiet serene atmosphere into one noisy experience.
떰콕을 방문했는데.. 장안을 보고 땀콕을 봐서 그런지 그닥..추천하고 싶진 않은곳. 풍경은 장안이 더 좋을듯하고요.. 여행으로 베트남 오시는 분들은 땀콕보다는 장안을 보시는게 더 나을듯합니다. 땀콕하고 장안하고 차로 30분(?)거리니 시간되시면 두 군데 다 둘러 보실수도 있어요
Pretty river view and walkway around. Street is lined with redundant shops of souvenir junk. Many restaurants, higher prices. Lots of street vendors. Nice area to walk. Take some photos.

After a wonderful 3 hours at Trang An, on the same kind of boats, in more beautiful settings, with more caves, and the opportunity to explore temples at the same time, we were left wondering why anyone bothered with Tam Coc? It was so much more touristy and we got hit with silly tourist scams (making you buy drinks and snacks for the rower etc). If you've got a limited time in Ninh Binh, do yourself a favour and go to Trang An instead!
장점: 경치는 아름답다. 베트남스럽고 이국적임단점: 한시간반의 보트투어가 더운 날씨 때문에 너무 힘들었고, 뱃사공 아주머니가 과도한 팁을 강압적으로 요구함 (5불). 중간 중간 물건을 사라는 호객행위있음. 아무것도 안 사자 노를 다른배에 비해 느리게 저어가서 더욱 힘들었다.초반엔 아름다우나 계속 같은 풍경을 보다보니 한국의 동강같은 느낌이 없지않아 있다

Mình khuyên các bạn nên đi Tràng An hơn là Tam Cốc vì các lý do sau:1. Tam Cốc bị thương mại hoá quá nhiều. 2. Dịch vụ bát nháo và nhếch nhác.3. Những điểm thăm thú không hấp dẫn4. Cảnh quan không đẹp và được bảo tồn sạch sẽ như Tràng An

It’s very nice boat trip , but much commercial and less interesting then the Trang An boat trip. It’s the same way back (not like the An Trang boat) and in the middle they try hardly to convience you to buy fruits and drink. In the end the driver insisted of being tipped and also demand minimum tip . So it’s nice, but if you don’t have time here, it’s much better to do the Trang An

When you arrive, there will be a lot of chaos at the boat launch.  Make sure you coordinate with your party prior to boarding as it can happen pretty quickly.  The views are spectacular and the rowers are nice.  Please be aware that at the apex of the ride (before turning back), there are people selling food.  So manage expectations with kids if you do not want to buy it, as it happens pretty quickly.  Also, note that they will sell you food for your boat rower.  Kind of a tourist trap.  I'd rather save the money and give the rower a bigger tip, than spend it on food.  There will also be someone who is taking  your picture and will try to sell this to you.  Just have a plan in place prior to starting.  Again, it is a great place.  Just know that it is a tourist trap.

Het verblijf in Tam coc is prima. Een mooi vertrekpunt om excursies te doen. Aardige mensen en plenty restaurants, hotels, homestay’s etc. Voor iedere prijs wat wils.Check je prijs wel even met booking.com.Dat kan nog wel eens wat schelen in de kosten.

The boat cruise was pleasant and scenery is amazing, however, I'm taking off 2 stars: - The price for the cruise as written on the board next to booking office is 120 / pax and 150 / boat. We thought that we should pay one or the other. Instead we were charged 3 x 120 and 2 x 150 as we were 3 and we were told that we had to take 2 boats. There were Vietnamese people buying tickets next to us and they only paid per person so we felt cheated. - In the middle of the cruise when the boats turn around there are several boats selling food and beverages. We were asked to buy beverages for our rowers! They stopped there and didn't want to go back until we bought something! It was a very bad experience. It all feels like a scam as the rowers didn't even open the beverages and they probably resell them. We liked the rowers and wanted to leave a nice tip (it's also expected) but after all the haggling we only left absolute minimum of 1 USD per boat!If you have time to visit either Tam Coc or Trang An, go for the boat at Trang An.

Perhaps it’s because I arrived to tam coc just after the rice harvest, so the paddy fields were just beginning to undergo clearing, so there was less lush greenery? I’d excepted to be wowed by Mother Nature but that didn’t quite come to pass. Village life was a little more fun- we witnessed snails being fished out from the paddy field, followed a herd of mountain goats and watched local boys hurl themselves somersault into the river. I wouldn’t have stayed for than a day in Ninh Binh. Go straight to Trang An grottoes for the boat ride and give tam coc a miss

The scenery and boat ride was marvelous- peaceful, scenic and relaxing. 90 mins all in and you can get up close to the paddy plants. Spectacular views of the many limestone rocks. However, the trip is spoiled by over-zealous vendors and even your boat-person to buy stuff ( fruits, souvenirs etc) . If you resist- they will persist you to buy for the boat- person. I feel that the Vietnam Tourism Board ( or whatever authority) there should educate these people as in the long run these practices will give this place a bad name. In addition there are these pesky photographers ( who is in their separate boats) who will pester you to buy photos ( its not worth it AT USD1 per photo-lousy quality) . I would have given either 4 or 5 stars if not for this negatives
여기보다 짱안을 추천해 드리고 싶고 여긴 굳이 배 안 타도 될 것 같아요. 그냥 자전거나 오토바이로 주변 마을들 돌아보는 게 훨씬 더 좋습니다.

We did Tam Coc before Trang an Grotto as we had been advised that it was quieter and less touristy.  We thought it was pretty good.  Nice boat ride through caves and lovely scenery but then the next day we did Tran an which was a longer ride and better caves.

Beau paysage. On ressent la sérénité les endroits visites sont paisibles. Par contre en fin de visites on se sent pièges par des vendeuses de friandises qui vous  disent de prendre une boisson et des gâteaux pour la rameuse. Celle ci vous propose des articles de broderies cartes sacs et veut vous forcer a acheter c est assez désagréable et en toute fin de visite elle vous réclame un pourboire.

We paid nearly 300,000 between us for our trip which was fine. You went past rice fields,  the viewpoint at the top of Mau caves and we went through 3 caves. We stopped on the way where a vendor tried to sell us a beer and crisps for 80,000 which we declined. For some reason our guide smirked at us for this. We didn't get hassled any more. On the way back we had 40,000 VND to give her as a tip. She stopped us in tbe middle of the lake and demanded 100,000.  After a few minutes of sitting there we handed it over to go back to shore. Once again she sat there smirking at us. I wish I had just waded back through the water.Completely ruined a great journey.

My girlfriend and I went later in the day to avoid the heat and crowds, the scenery is beautiful and looks exactly like the postcards. Unlike other travellers we weren't really harassed by vendors the whole way down the river, just once. But, before we got back to shore, our rower demanded a tip of 100k, I already had the tip I was going to give her in my hand before she said this but it wasn't enough. It's not a tip if there is a set demanded price, we already paid 390k to do the boat tour in the first place and I want to tip for service not because it's demanded. We were sat in the middle of the lake until I gave in and gave her the tip, making sure to receive my reluctant change. It ruined a wonderful trip.

Lieux magnifiques à voir, à certaines heures seulement, sinon c’est une autoroute française un 15 août, et le seul but est effectivement de faire «cracher » le touriste , les photos , les boissons, les biscuits , le rameur qui réclame son pourboire en demandant une somme précise ... ça gâche franchement la visite .Dommage car dans de bonnes conditions c’est un lieu inoubliable

Nous avons réalisé la ballade en barque. Les paysages sont beaux mais c'est ultra touristique. En plus du prix de la ballade on nous pousse à acheter de la nourriture lors d'arrêts prévus et les rameuses exigent des pourboires d'une façon assez agressive. De plus les étrangers ne peuvent monter à plus de 2 sur les barques ce qui n'est pas le cas des Vietnamiens...
Très déçu par le prix que j'ai trouvé excessif pour la ballade proposée.Le site est très beau mais cela ne mérite pas le tarif demandé!!!!

Site très beau à visiter mais très touristique. De plus on voit certes les trois grottes mais on reste sur notre fin. Nous pensions vraiment faire plus vu le prix. Je préfère à choisir prendre des petits bateaux à côté et donner de l’argent au locaux et moins être entouré de touristes plus insolite pour nous. Après on ne voit pas les grottes mais personnellement ce n’est pas trop grave c’est le paysage alentour pour nous qui vaut vraiment le détour.

The indication at the ticket booth states 120,000 VND per individual + 150,000 VND per motorboat, though they are going to charge you for two people in case you are a solo traveler, earning the cost 390,000 complete (plus tip, often 50,000-100,000). When you do not wish paying this much, you are able to attempt to locate various other travelers to reveal the boat with.It is advisable to go at the start of the early morning (before 9am) or even late in the evening (after 3pm), when there're much less folks as well as the tour buses are not there. Only some rowers are pushy or dishonest. My rower was a really good forty year old female that did not request any tips (so I provided her 100,000).You will not get this view since you will be at water amount. The valley is entirely around mountains as well as the sole method to get into it's through Mua cave by motorboat (see: google maps satellite view).In addition, wear sunscreen and also bring a hat/umbrella when the sunshine is out. Also remember that by late March, the grain fields is greening up nicely.General I'd suggest it, particularly if this's the sole time you will actually be in Tam Coc in the life of yours, twenty dolars USD is not very much cash within the grand pattern of things.

We had been accosted by the person attempting to market us overpriced hot snacks and beer for our parched and also starving rower, as well as we tactfully & repeated declined and she ultimately gave up. We enjoyed the majority of our journey until we returned to the steps/dock. In the misunderstandings my husband left the phone of his on the hold (it was out of the pocket of his as he was taking a picture when the rower fell). When again at the hotel of ours, the staff attempted to get in touch with the organisation which does the boat trips, but with no success. Luckily the following morning my husband managed going down and see them, demonstrate to them the picture and need to find the rower. He switched up, pretended to find out nothing until police were note, and then miraculously had the telephone in the pocket of his and demanded a preposterous idea for going back the phone.

Okay, i enjoyed the lazy river boat ride add to that the "captain" of the boat is usually an old woman using her feet to paddle. the scenery is breathtaking, just the way i imagined it to be based on what i saw at some cooking shows featuring Vietnam. it was a pleasant experience because the weather that day is pleasantly cool. but at the end of the river comes the most irritating part for me, the boat lady is demanding me to buy her snacks, i just gave her the supposed tip and told her to buy some for her but that is already your tip. she smirked on me and from then on, kept on complaining until we reached end of the tour.and the photgraphers are iritating as well, they would snap a picture of you and will compel you to pay for it. if you see one just tell them no.

I would say this is NOT a must go and the 3 stars are purely for the scenery. Scenery was nice but the people working/operating the boats are all out to get tourists. Firstly, the person rowing your boat is eagerly awaiting for you to tip them. I gave 30,000 dong to him at the end of the trip and he was constantly pestering me to up it to 50,000 dong. I refused and he was grumbling. Awful ! Half way thru the trip, you will see plenty of boats selling snacks and drinks and they will all be pestering you to buy or to buy for the person rowing your boat. Feels like a Tourists trap! Ruined the trip in many ways.

We arrived early at 8am, and paid for our tickets at the small kiosk. The price was 120 000VND per person, and then an extra 150 000VND per boat. Foreign tourists are only allowed to be 2 per boat, while we saw up to 5 local tourists per boat. The tour guides work hard and row with their feet, and the scenery is beautiful, although not what you see in all the posters and brochures - the promotional photos are all taken from above, while you will be seeing the rice paddies at eye level, often edged with tatty pieces of plastic.Be prepared to be harassed - people in boats taking photos of you as you come past, which they print and try to sell to you when you return. Also hawkers in boats trying to convince you to buy over-priced food and snacks for you and your guide. The trip takes you through 3 caves, and before we were even through the final caves, our guide turned the boat around and rowed us back. There is nowhere for you to get out and explore or take photos from a higher vantage point on the tour. I agree with previous posts suggesting renting bikes to explore, and viewing the valley from Hang Mua peak.We did the Trang An boat trip the day before, which worked out cheaper, was better organised, no harrassment and a beautiful round trip with nicer scenery - all in all better value for money than Tam Coc.

Nice scenery! But experience spoil by the boat lady who sell stuff in the boat, half way photographer who takes photographs with bad quality of print and high price (the more you bargain the better price), after the cafe, boat with shopping items in high price as sold there. If nothing being bought, the boat people will pass bad remarks on the tourist!

짱안과 땀꼭 둘 중에 저는 짱안을 추천합니다. 다낭으로 이동중 어쩔 수 없이 들르게 된 곳인데요. 입장료와 보트비를 따로받아 가격이 비쌉니다. 27만동이구요. 보트 운전하는 분이 여러가지 설명을 하는데 관광객 입장을 배려하지 않은 설명이 주로 이루어졌습니다. 그리고 보트를 타면서 여러가지 강매도 이루어지고 가격이 조금 비쌉니다. 나중에 팁으로 5달러 요구하는데 1달러만 주세요.

Die Bootstour dauerte ca 90 Minuten. Man sieht die schöne Landschaft vom Boot aus, aber leider sind sehr viele Boote auf dem Wasser unterwegs.Preis/Leistung stimmt auch nicht ganz. Die Tour war mit 195.000 Dong recht teuer.Wenn man die Trang An Bootstour macht kann man diese hier gut weg lassen.

Nice little quaint town, if not a little crowded out by loads of tourists. It's an area that should be explored either by motorbike or a bicycle. Loads to see, do and eat. Food prices are extremely reasonable. Area needs a couple of upmarket accommodation places though.Air & dust pollution is extremely bad and I would not recommend spending a long time in the area. Not if you have asthma and other respiratory ailments. SERIOUSLY............

De natuur is heel mooi en er doorheen varen met de bootje is wel leuk voor een tijdje.Maar het is er op gericht om zoveel mogelijk geld uit de toeristen te halen. Je betaald voor het bootje en ook nog eens inkom per persoon. Onderweg op het water komen andere bootjes langs je varen om vanalles te verkopen.Als de roeister dan ook nog eens 500meter voor het einde om een fooi begint te vragen is de pret er wel vanaf.

The sightseeing is mostly rice fields at water level surrounded by big mountains. You also cross some grutes (3 in total) being the first the only where you stop seeing daylight. In the way back you are approached by boats selling stuff for you to buy and offer to your boat paddler + the tip in the end of the journey. Honestly I think that if you go to Tràng An you can skip this trip
All they want is your money, stay far away as possible.  I walked up a single and they wanted to charge me twice and wouldn't help me at all.  Horrible, horrible people, thanks so much for you help.

We go together with friends.. i really disappoint in this place because when we go to ticket counter it say 1 boat for 2 foreigner ppl.. however i am only 1 and 3 Vietnamese friends.. still they force use 2 rent 2 boats.. when we ask why they charge for 2 boats.. the counter lady say it is policy.. but in policy it say if 2 foreigner people need 1 boat.. however we rent 2.. water was not so deep and dirty.. also we just sit and see.. did not get anywhere to get off to see more things.. and the way person take our pic also they force us to buy it.. we do not even ask for it.. i hope the vietnam tourism must improve service over here..

Balade d’1h30 en barque sympathique. La rameuse utilise ses pieds avec une certaine falicité. Le passage des grottes est surprenant, comme les discussions sur les bizarreries à droite et à gauche (œufs rose d’escargots d’eau...). Avant de faire demi tour, un petit massage est proposé, puis les fameux petits souvenirs... et après retour au point de départ ou on vous proposera votre photo dans la barque. Vous pouvez pagayer si vous le souhaitez. Pourboire de 30k dong. Les paysages de calcaire sont très beau, le jour où nous l’avons fait le ciel était nuageux et la pluie toute proche. À faire pour un moment de détente si vous passez dans le coin.

Tam Coc is super nice and all the sights around are defintely worth seeing it. But do mit visit it on weekends!!! It is horrible overcrowded by local tourists. If you go there from Monday till Friday, it is nice, beautiful and calm. So avoid the weekends.

This was one of the first palces my wife and I visited on our recent tour of Vietnam and Cambodia. It was a drizzly day and quite cool, and we had made the mistake of not wearing any waterproofs or carrying brollies. The lady rower lent me her had part way through our tour of the lakes and caves, to stop me getting quite as wet!They call this place "Halong Bay on land" and having visited both, I can see why, but this isn't as extensive as Halong Bay, and I think if you are going to visit Halong Bay there is no need to visit this place. It's over a 2 hour drive from Hanoi (and 2 hours or more back), so think carefully before you committ to this tour.
Trop touristique, du coup pas dépaysant mais néanmoins beau.Restaurants trop touristiques. Allez vous perdre dans un peu dans la campagne, plus calme et plus joyeux.

This is a mayor tourist area in Ninh Binh. You will discover the the nature park with small boat that accept only 2 people. Round tour takes about 2,30- 3 hours. On the tour you will see some caves and beautifull rice paddies. However you have to visit this place between end of May and end of Jun because this is the time when the rice fields on the Ngo Dong river start to change the colour from green to yellow.

Die Landschaft die man mit dem Boot durchfährt ist wirklich sehenswert. Wenn der Reis grünt ist es sicher noch schöner, ebenso intressant sind die Höhlen die man durchfährt.Leider gibts es einen kleinen Wehrmuts Tropfen. Die Bootsführerinnen sind sehr lästig und aufdringlich. Am Ende der Route wird gewendet und sie halten bei Bootsverkäuferinnen an. Als wir der Verkäuferin sagten das wir nichts kaufen wollen drängte sie uns dazu wenigstens der Bootsführerin was zu kaufen. Wir verneinten weil wir ihr am Ende Trinkgeld geben wollten. Mit dieser Aussage brachten wir sie dann endlich auch zum weiterfahren. Während der Rückfahrt fragte sie alle 2 Minuten wieviel Trinkgeld wir ihr geben sie hat zwei Kinder die sie versorgen muss. Als ob das nicht genug wäre wollte sie uns dann noch Taschen verkaufen. Die ganze Rückfahrt wechselte sich die Frage nach dem Trinkgeld und buy something from me ab. Das war sehr lästig und unangenehm, wir fühlten uns nicht mehr wohl und konhten so die Aussicht auch nicht wirklich genießen. Als wir ihr am Ende mehr als 10 Prozent des Ticketpreises Trinkgeld gaben war sie immer noch nicht zufrieden und verlangte nach mehr.Wir würden anstelle dieser Tour die Bootstour in Trang An empfehlen, die ist mindestens genauso schön wenn nicht sogar schöner ist und die Bootsführerinnen sind überhaupt nicht aufdringlich !

The boat ride is beautiful, like an inland Ha Long Bay but the experience is diminished by the non stop badgering of boat vendors. We made the mistake of interacting with the woman taking photos, who guioted us into buying all of them. A bit of a tourist trap, be warned but do it.

Si les paysages sont superbes, l'affluence est au rendez vous et des centaines de touristes arrivent par bus entier. C'est aussi le rendez vous de toutes les grandes agences de voyages d'Hanoi et des tours opérateurs internationaux.Le mieux à mon avis est d'aller à Bich Động, tout près, ou Thung Nang (bateau en bambou) ou il n'y a généralement que quelques visiteurs dans un décors paradisiaque.
you experience  wooden boat to visit 3 caves ,  natural around is so nice and quite peaceful. I come there 3 times already

This is my second visit to tam coc and the scenery is stunning even without the rice fields. The bitter taste is when you pay a fair price then get asked one at the turn around point to buy off a local seller a tip for the person rowing our boat. Which I thought he deserved so I bought him a drink, cookies and peanuts. 100k dong. Then at the end asking for another tip. I didn't give one. I'm sure I would have if I hadn't been pushed into buying something from the vendor for him. Apart from that great trip

Pros: scenery is beautiful, exciting to go through grottos, the ladies row with their legs which is incredible to see, our rower was a lovely lady Cons: Almost everyone is scammed by either being charged huge amounts for drinks for their rower, or photography, it takes a long time to row out to the grottos and other sites, there is a real discord between the beauty of the scenery and the icky feeling you get from being yelled at for not tipping enough, or not paying enough for drinks which is a real shame.
Overdag genoeg te doen, ‘s avonds niets te beleven. Wij hebben de omgeving per fiets verkend. Erg mooie omgeving. Aanrader om een paar dagen te verblijven als je genoeg tijd hebt

Balade en barque pour de beaux paysages. Prendre le temps de regarder autour de soi, les filets à anguilles dans l'eau, les nénuphars, les oiseaux. Passage dans quelques grottes éclairés par la lampe frontales des rameuses. Plutôt Sympa. Ne pas oublier le petit pourboire à la fin de la visite.
Ordinary boat ride. Too many people on the river. Feels like an attraction in a country fair. Boat operator expects a tip equivalent to 50% of tour fee.

You walk inside the rice fields so you don't see them and they charge for tickets and for boat and in the middle you have to buy food from boat sellers, after allyou can do it but too many boats going around and you will not enjoy as much as trang an.

Das meiste ist gesagt. Paparazzi Bildern und Verkaufsbooten kann man ganz gut durch selbstbewusstes wegschauen und Neinsagen ausweichen. Wir hatten eine sehr freundliche ältere Fahrerin. Selbst unser Guide wusste keinen guten Rat bezüglich eines angemessenen Trinkgeldes und sagte uns, dass in der Regel das doppelte von dem gefordert wird was man geben wollte. Die Fahrt ist ganz nett, uns fehlt der Vergleich zu den anderen Bootsfahrmöglichkeiten.

First of all before i start, it is a beautiful place to visit and definitely worth to be fitted into your itinerary. Just take note on certain things. 1) Be careful of the camera woman/man on the sampan boat. First they will coy you sweetly into taking A PICTURE with an exorbitant price. Do not even negotiate with them since the beginning because they will follow you all the way just to beg you to take their services. After that, it will not be A PICTURE. They will take tons of pictures and you are expected to take all. Before they say anything, just say no and look away. We saw the couple next to us get shucked into it innocently. 2) DO NOT BUY ANYTHING FROM THE BOATMEN/WOMEN. They will approach you asking you to buy a drink after when eventually you do buy a drink at an exorbitant price. They will proceed to say buy for your rower and they will start passing a lot of tidbits over to your rower and telling you that you need to pay an ridiculous amount for all the things they have pass over to your rower. They will then shame you by saying buy food for your rower, they work very hard to row you. That really pisses me off and i say in that case, forget it. I am not even going to buy anything and i was quite loud saying that this is cheating. Only after that, they began to back down and say ok then just pay for your drink then. I paid for my drink and bought another drink for the rower. That is all. I know that the drink that i had bought for the rower is definitely just a scam as our rower doesn't even open the drink. But it is ok. At least i bought you a drink. 3) Be ready with small change as the rower will bug you for a tip before you even arrive on shore. I gave up and yes i did tip the rower due to his efforts.  Lastly, do be vigilant when visiting tam coc. Tipping is entirely up to you but i do not enjoy being threaten or shamed into paying things that i dont feel like paying. It is a very beautiful place but I am truly disappointed with the experience there. I understand that they are trying to earn some money but they should never use our safety as a way to extort more money.

Die Landschaft ist sehr schön. Allerdings wurden wir hier, entgegen der Infos in Reiseführern, häufig "genötigt" Essen oder ähnliches zu kaufen. Das trübte das Erlebnis für uns leider. Wir haben auch die Fahrt in Trang An gemacht und fanden diese wesentlich angenehmer, hatten jedoch wahrscheinlich auch Glück, dass wir dort kaum Betrieb antrafen.

I found his tour very relaxing viewing the majestic natural landscape of  Tam Coc, Ninh Binh and yet I was humbled by how the Vietnamese work very hard rowing us back and forth from one end to the other with their feet and hands, the paradox of traveling,

Price ticket was 195,000 Đồng. When we went there, the places got flooded a week ago hence the rice field is not as pretty as the photo. The boat ride was about 2hrs , during the ride, there will be people selling some fruits and drinks to you. Overall it was just an average boat ride compare to Trang An.

While we were certainly impressed with the beautiful surroundings, our experience was dampened by some of the locals whose sole purpose is to rip off the visiting tourists at any cost. One such example was halfway through our boat ride, a scam in a lady on another boat is aggressively trying to sell overpriced snacks to feed the boat rower (who plays along by overacting sighs and groans of tiredness). Of course if you buy the rower just gives the snacks back to the lady after the ride. Towards the end of our ride, we tipped the rower with what felt like a generous tip. What happens next? He tries to negotiate the tip to a higher amount.. And this on top of the usual pushy attempts to sell you photos, hats and everything else that's at hand. It's a shame that the locals haven't learned how to be tourist-friendly and leave a bitter aftertaste in what should be one of the biggest natural highlights of North Vietnam.My recommendation is to visit Tam Coc, rent a bicycle to get around yourself and go on the boat, but to be prepared for the various inevitable scams. OR, consider skipping altogether if you don't have much time and are already doing a trip to the even more dramatic Halong Bay.

J'ai fait le tour en barque que j'ai partagé avec 2 autres touristes pour diviser le prix. Sachez qu'il faut payer 120 dongs par tête + le prix du bateau, soit 75 supp par tête. Le prix du bateau est le même que vous soyez 2 ou + ... On a pas été tant harcelés que ça. On a dit non ferme à la vendeuse qui n'a pas insisté et pareil pour les tips à la fin. Je trouve que le prix est déjà cher pour payer quoi que ce soit en plus.La balade dure 1h30. C'est sympa mais pas exceptionnel. Je vous recommande par contre de faire le temple du dragon (Mua Cave)qui offre une vue exceptionnelle sur les environs.

Tam Coc (means 3 caves in Vietnamese) is a beautiful experience with scenery all around. It was a very relaxing boat ride where I saw green fields, mountains, caves, ... but that it was relaxing because I am Vietnamese and I can reject huge pressure to buy things, pictures, trinkets, etc.  For you, non local visitors, a little preparation will make the tour more enjoyable for you. Get your hotel to know how much is the ticket price. Have water bottles with you before you start. Say No, and firmly no to anything you do not want to buy! Be prepare to RAISE your voice and firmly state NO!  Learn some Vietnamese to say NO in Vietnamese! When you board the little boat, do notice that for "Vietnamese" there is only the rower on the boat. For non-locals, there will be a second person to "help"; ALL THE SECOND PERSON WILL DO is TO PRESSURE YOU TO BUY STUFF! Insist no second helper be allowed on the boat. At the start of the boat ride, be friendly to the rower and ask for a paddle of your own so that you can "pretend" to help row; only paddle if you want to. So when the rower stops at the far end so that sellers can ruin your day, tell the rower to go, and if necessary you start to paddle yourself. Do not be scammed to buy things for the rower; the rower will give them back for part of the cash; give tip only at the end!  Finally a tip is a reward; only reward if your trip is worth the reward. Now for my part, I have had great experiences at Tam Coc and Trang An and I rewarded the rower with big tip (such as 500,000 Dong) where they went out the way to show me more than the usual route. Some locals do not understand what it means to be tourist friendly and politeness for some is still a long way to go. When you encounter unfriendliness and less than politeness that you can tolerate, be firm in your dealings and with your voice. I am sorry to say, it may take that firmness to get through.

Ballade sympathique de 2h en barque dans un paysage très beau.Les 3 grottes traversées ne sont pas exceptionnelles (surtout si vous arrivez du centre, en ayant vu les grottes dans la région de Phung Nha) et le site est très fréquenté. A faire tôt le matin !Vendeuses de fruits très (trop) insistantes.

Het is een prachtig landschap maar Ha Long Bay heeft nog een veeel mooier uitzicht. Eigenlijk wordt je contonue gechanteerd met fooien die ze echt vragen en ze worden bijna afgressief. Nadat je door de grotten bent geweest leggen ze je bootje stil en proberen ze je allerlei souvenirs te verkopen, het kost je veel moeite om daar vanaf te komen en te zorgen dat je terug gaat. Daarnaast staat er papparazi langs het water en zeg duidelijk NO PHOTO, anders maken ze er zeker 5 en moet je ze betalen want ook dan worden ze aggressief. Verder zijn ze echt onvriendelijk en zeggen ze geen woord en een glimlach kan er ook niet vanaf. Als je gaat, bedenk je dan goed dat dit echt wel heel mooi is maar lang niet zo bijzonder als Ha Long Bay (waar je niet gechanteerd wordt). Kortom genieten van het landschap wordt je erg moeilijk gemaakt en je krijgt erg een akelige nasmaak van. Op 2 personen vroegen ze om 150.000 dong (veel te veel dus). Dus "tip" 150.000, souvenir (houten bootje) 200.000 en foto 5x 20.000 en nog een drankje. Dan is de lol er af. Bij ons niet zo geweest maar je moet dus wel volhardend zijn, de tourleader kondigt het wel aan maar het lijkt een spelletje.

No nos informamos de forma correcta (culpa nuestra) y pensamos que Tam Coc incluiría el recorrido completo de las cuevas y de los arrozales con las montañas (que luego descubrimos que era otra zona, Trang An). La cuestión es que no te lo dejan nada claro cuando compras la entrada y te dan el folleto (adjunto imagen). Claramente la imagen de la portada es Trang An y no Tam Coc. Pagamos por el billete y disfrutamos de las cuevas, pensando que luego venía lo mejor. La decepción fue que en cierto punto la Barquera se dió la vuelta y comenzó a remar de nuevo hacía el puerto. Si lo que buscas es la imagen de las montañas con los arrozales, es Trang An!!!

Leuke korte (2u) privé boottocht door een paar grotten. Zorg dat je er vroeg bent dan is het nog rustig. Vanaf het middaguur is het file varen. Bij het keerpunt word je bijna agressief vanuit verkoopbootjes benaderd om fruit en drank te kopen. Een stukje heerlijk fruit gaat er hier altijd in maar vervolgens beginnen ze in hun eigen winkel te graaien om de ons roeiende dame ook op onze rekening te laten eten en drinken. Dat gaat werkelijk veel te ver. Alles terug gegeven. Vergeet onderweg vooral niet op de ooievaars, ijsvogels en geiten te letten.

Joli et calme mais la personne qui devait ramer pour nous était une personne âgée qui nous a demandé de ramer, on a été plus ou moins forcé de payer une boisson et des petits snacks pour elle, la balade était un aller retour et l'endroit est beau mais pas autant que je l'espérais. Le montant demandé lors du paiement était incompréhensible (480 000 pour 4 + 300 000 pour une raison inconnue) et il faut encore payer (fortement demandé du moins) 50 000 de pourboires minimum.A noter que nos amis sur l'autre barque n'ont pas ramer et ont passé un bon moment.

Dépaysement assuré avec une ballade en barque pour passer sous les cavités naturelles. Trois passages pour arriver finalement à un refuge type marché fllttant avec son incontournable lieu de culte local. Chèvres et boucs accueillants.

Los tours normales hacen ruta por varias pagodas, 45 minutos de bicicleta y finalmente ruta de 2 horas por el lago Tam Coc.Lo malo de la experiencia son las vendedoras súper insistentes que estropean la experiencia, y hasta los remeros de la barca que te llevan a donde se vende comida y cosas a mitad del recorrido e incluso ellos mismos paran la barca para venderte cosas lo cual es bastante violento. Estropean una fantástica experiencia y un magnífico paisaje con tanto agobio.

The view was nice but not that wow. The boat ride was too long as there was not much to see. The rower tried to sell things during the ride and would stop to row the boat if you did not buy them. Even my friends told them that they would gave them the tip in cash, the rower still insisted us to buy. This was really annoying. There were photographers to take your photos along the way. Just make sure to buy photos on the river bank, so you could walk away in case they were too pricey.

Definitely worth a visit. We hired a 7 seat car for $60 and a guide for$30 and made our own itinerary which included here and trang an. Boat rowers row with their feet which is interesting. Scenery nice. We shortened our boat ride to one hour because we had kids 1.5yo n 3yo.

Beautiful place, nice scenic relaxing boat ride.The sad thing is the woman on a boat half way through the journey trying to sell you water/snacks for the boatman which are ridiculously priced, and also the woman at the end screaming TIP, TIP in your face.Expect incredibly rude people at this attraction, who have no shame in screaming at you. A similar theme throughout our trip unfortunately, beautiful country, shame about the greedy Vietnamese people involved in the tourist industry.

The scenery was beautiful but really crowded and touristy. The boat ride was pleasant and relaxing. The really bad thing about Tam Coc is the shops and sellers before/during/after the ride...They are there to rip you off and it hurts the atmosphere a lot...Overpriced water bottle, no change, I wasn't happy at all.

คนพายบางทีก็ใช้เท้าพายทำให้เป็นเอกลักษณ์ของการล่องเรือที่นิงห์บิงห์ น้ำใส มองเห็นสาหร่ายด้านล่างชัดเจนเลย ระยะทางค่อยข้างไกลสำหรับการพายเรือ ข้างทางเต็มไปด้วยธรรมชาติ แต่ไม่น่าตื่นเต้นเท่าทีควร ตอนไปอากาศเย็นพอดี มีฝนตกนิดหน่อย ปลายทางขายผลไม้และขนมราคาแพงมาก ไม่อร่อยด้วย และมีคนท้องถิ่นพายเรือเพื่อมาถ่ายรูปแล้วเสนอขาย
Se ha masificado y hay demasiada gente todos haciendo lo mismo. El recorrido en barca es bonito pero se explota demasiado al turista y quieren cobrar por todo.

-Tour agencies offer this tour at USD$70/person, just take a train from Hanoi to Ninh Binh and take an Uber to the Tam Coc Jetty. It should cost no more than USD$30.-Bring a good camera, lots of Instagram worthy shots available.- Would advice to rent a scooter (just outside the train station, no need motorbike Licence, Ninh Binh is generally light traffic) for 100,000VND as there's more to explore around the area e.g. Pagoda-Boat ride was about 90 mins. -Just get the boat ticket from the counter, a boat for 2 costs 390,000 VND. -Make friends with the boatmen/women and he/she will let you paddle the boat in the traditional Viet technique.-Boatmen/women expect tips after the rowing. Generally USD$1/ passenger will appease them. There's also a floating tidbit market at the end of the river, you can get him/Her a drink there as a gesture of good will.

Nous sommes allés faire la balade en bateau. Nous avons eu de je chance car il n'y avait pas beaucoup de monde. Et nous avons pu profiter de ce merveilleux paysage. S'il s du monde c'est un serpent de bateau. Sur l'eau.Au cours de il y'a des vendeuses d'eau et de fruits a des prix exorbitants et de plus elles sont agressives...  ce qui a gâché notre parcours

Paysage magnifique malgré un temps grincheux. Belle balade en barque gaché par l'insistance de la dame de la barque pour avoir un tips de 50 000vnd (pas moins). Elle a passer tout le fin de circuit à raler derriere nous.. en sachant qu'on lui avait deja acheter des boissons et a manger. Le ticket pour 1 adulte est de 120 000 vnd. Mauvaise surprise encore une fois,il faut ajouter 150 000k pour la location de la barque..

Aside from being scammed at the ticket booth, the view is amazing. You can see locals in their rice fields at the sides of the river. The boat ride is one hour and again, aside from locals insisting on you buying their products at the stop (before turning back) and them asking for a tip after the ride, it was an amazing experience nonetheless. In Trang An, you won't see any of these, and my friend and I gladly gave a tip to our rower without her asking for it.

I stayed 200m from here so that I could easily access nearby attractions. Arrived around 8am and probably waited around 40 minutes for someone to share a boat with as it works out cheaper.The boat ride is fairly straight forward, going under a few karsts. I turned my face away when I saw the boat person with camera.At the end where you turn around a boat lady with drinks came over but she did not hassle after I said no thank you.You then return the way you came.If you have limited time I would recommend doing An Trang over this one.

Agradable paseo en barca pero muy caro. Galaxy gottoes y Trang An son mucho más baratos y mucho mejores.  El paseo es corto, las cuevas no son nada especial y las vendedoras demasiado insistentes en que compres cosas y bebidas para ti y tu remera a precios muy altos. Galaxy grottoes es mucho mejor y Trang An también.

Uns hat zwar Trang an besser gefallen, aber auch Tam Coc ist sehenswert. Die Landschaft ist anders, da Tam Coc von Reisfeldern umzingelt ist. Dies macht sie sehr schön grün. Aber leider sind dort schon sehr viele Touristen und man wird gedrängt etwas von den Booten für seinen Fahrer zum trinken zu kaufen. Unser Fahrer hatte leider starken Husten und hat uns dauernd in den Rücken gehustet. Leider schon sehr unangenehm.

Promenade en barque à rame en matinée au milieu des rizières La rameuse actionne les rames avec ses pieds. La rivière traverse 3 grottes sans concrétions mais il faut parfois se baisser pour ne pas toucher la voûte. Promenade calme à cette saison( fin février) on peut voir des oiseaux dans les rizières.Hélas au milieu du parcours à l'endroit du demi tour, vous serez rattrapés par des marchands qui veulent vendre des boissons et biscuits en insistant lourdement et vous demandant de les acheter pour la rameuse!! Avez vous le cœur de refuser?? (J'ai bien vu que la dame de la barque voisine reposait sa canette dans le bateau de la vendeuse)Oui! en promettant un pourboire à la rameuse qui vous le réclamera à plusieurs reprises sur le chemin du retour.À l'arrivée essayer d'éviter les vendeur de nappes brodée !!
Fairly nice town with a few restaurants, cafes and shops, stopped here for a drink after biking to a nearby temple

The boat trip is highlight of the tour, you will fall in love with the scenery from sides of the rivers. The landscape looks similar as Ha Long Bay with hundred mountains cover around the river. The caves are beautiful too but compare to Caves in Quang Binh or Ha Long Bay are less colorful. What i was really unhappy were boat drivers, they always show that they are poor and they non stop asking to buy things from their boxes, you just really fell annoying and accept to give them a tip to let them stop asking and enjoy the quite and peaceful landscape.

Lovely area to visit but takes a long time to do the boat ride with the return over the same route- better if somehow the trip was changed & you could get of at the turnaround area. Our boat rower never hassled us at all but others on the trip said when they offered a tip at the end the rower said it wasn't enough & demanded double & was abusive. Also best to turn your back on the irritating photographers -seemed to solve the problem & sellers at the half way point are very insistent. Bike ride around the rice fields was much better.

I had read ahead of time about this place being touristy, but was still shocked at how busy it was. I think it's best to go in the morning before the crowds get there or later when most have gone. Our second half of the boat ride was later than most everyone, and it was much much nicer. The view is partly dependent on the rice season. Ours was only recently planted, so it was pretty, but nicer to go when the rice is closer to harvest I think.I know it's not avoidable, but the trip is too long, and would be 5 stars for me if it was one way only, instead of going over the same area twice.

Nothing spectacular if you have seen halong bay. Although we went on a day with near rain which may dampen my review. You sit in a small boat with quite a hard seat and see limestone formations similar to halong. When you reach the bottom of the river they try to sell food and novelties. If you take out any money they give the person rowing food and drinks and you pay a highly bumped up price while they say "tip". Once you reach the end he stops and asks for another tip. It is few hours out. Personally after seeing halong it was nothing special.

Its REALLY REALLY BUSY. Maybe because it is still consider Tet month but there are a lot of tourists. If you have the chance a bike ride along the rice patty fields and the limestone mountains. The boats are kind of neat to go on but our rower was unpleasant, pushy and kept on harassing us for a tip (we were told by the tour guide to only give them a tip when we get off so that they don't ask for more). If it wasn't for him, our trip would've been nicer.

A fun way to spend the day if you have the time, but don't expect much frills.Main Points: Very touristy (no locals), get to see two temples (which are very run down and not overly interesting), many hours spent on a bus, but very beautifully scenic. Things to pack/bring for the day: If it's not summer, a light sweater or pants (we went in Feb), the 1.45 min boat ride was chilly, most people seemed un-prepared for weather. Bug spray, a must. Comfortable shoes / athletic wear (if doing the boat ride and bike ride), small cash for tips (boat towers expect minimum of 50,000 Dong), and SNACKS!!! You make 1-stop too and from Tam Coc for a bathroom/pit stop and chocolate bars are $5, among hundreds of other very overpriced snacks. Got picked up in Hanoi, our bus was full (half young people and half older) - a good mix. Visited temples first, was a little redundant, both temples are almost identical and very small. For lunch, we were brought to a big hall-style room for buffet-style lunch, we were told it was "local" food but it was definitely not - your typical rice, French fries, greasy noodles and pork skewers - drinks not included. People poked fun at how mediocre the food was, truthfully. Made our way to Tam Coc, boat ride was beautiful however you are surrounded by tourists from all parts of the world, there is no interacting and exploring with local culture. You're expected to buy snacks and/or photos during your boat ride and also pay for the boat rowers snack. Very sour attitude if you don't. Bike ride was very short, only 40 minutes and very beginner level. The bikes weren't in great shape, 2 people had to swap theirs half-way through tour because they broke. Got great views of locals from afar working in rice fields. Overall, most people on our bus seemed either content or un-impressed, would recommend a day trip to Halong Bay or getting lost in downtown Hanoi eating your way through the streets of Banh Mi's and Pho for some real culture. Hope this helps, Enjoy!

Le problème avec les excursions organisées c'est que tout est réglé à la minute près.Le décore est très joli cependant pour nous la forte pluie a un peu gâché le moment même si ça reste un joli souvenir et que vu les mauvaises conditions il n'y avait pas trop de touristes prêts à se mouiller!!La magie retombe complètement à la fin où la rameuse demande un pourboire d'une façon tellement élégante...et en plus ce n'est pas assez

Would have been more beautiful during spring & summer when the paddy fields are green next to the river. The surrounding limestone hills & outcrops tower over the river.Beautiful place to visit. Note - probably only spoilt by trinket sellers and boat person trying to cheat with change and asking for more tips.

Nice enough boat ride, scenery amazing but spoilt by staff. Ticket office girl tried to short change us, nice little earner for her if she does this to everyone. Trapped by sellers halfway round who won't take no for an answer. Tipped the rower then she asked for more. When are the Vietnamese going to learn that this sort of thing puts people off

Tam Coc has beautiful scenery. The town itself isn't particularly nice but the surrounding area is nice. We visited in December and the rice paddies had been cut so it wasn't green like you see in the pictures. The karst landscape is nice but if you have seen Halong Bay this may not be an essential stop.

Already done Thang an which was lovely but thought we could do this boat ride just to see the ladies use there feet to row. All reviews are true, the boat ladies talk all the time either on there mobiles or to other boat ladies( to be honest this did not bother us, as it is there job and get to talk to my work colleagues during my working day) it cost us just short of €20 for my husband and I in a boat, maybe took about 90 min. Scenery amazing like all of Vietnam.At half way point as we turned around there are about 15 boat ladies waiting loaded up with fruit, water and numerous other items to sell us, our lady made our boat float about in there area till she realised we weren't going to buy anything.As we approached the finish my husband gave her a €2 tip to which she asked for €5!!! All the other rowers were asking there passengers for money. I would not do this trip again, such a shame as it does make you feel embarrassed & uncomfortable by the pushy ladies.
Globalement assez cher, il y a d'autres promenades en bâteau moins cher aux alentours.Le pourboire est réclamé d'une telle façon qu'on ne le donne pas de bon coeur.

I can't comment on the 'real' Tam Coc experience as we got tricked into a poor version by some rouge outfit. A bit lost, we managed to get coerced onto a boat that was not the main Tam Coc river trip. Although the scenery was still great (fortunately Mother Nature does not scam you), we were disappointed to learn that the real Tam Coc trip was only a few hundred meters away. By the time we got there, it was too late for us to spend another 2 hours on a tour so my dreams based on pictures of Tam Coc will have to remain in my head until I get a chance to go back there. It probably wasn't the ideal time to go anyway as the rice paddies weren't in their bright green and yellow glory. Note to others... when arriving in Tam Coc, go to what seems to be a central concreted area to get on the proper trip. It should, at time of writing, cost 120,000 vnd pp and not 45,000 - if you are unsure then check as you are probably, like us, on the wrong jetty!And yes, the old lady rowing the boat (with her legs) tried (and did) sell us handicrafts that we didn't want, which spoiled the trip a bit but I can't blame her when you see how little she has and how hard she is working. That, plus a tip, hopefully gave her something to help for the rest of the week.

Unless you're really struggling for things to do in Hanoi or aren't going to see the actual Halong Bay, I would give this a miss. All the nuances you read on the reviews are true. Everything from the 3 hour bus ride, the average looking temples, the average buffet food and the boat lady demanding more money and spoiling our ride by talking to the other boats or on her phone. All for 40 dollars. However, if you can look past all this, the river and the limestone landscape was actually very beautiful and our tour guide was superb (company was called Alova) and made the day more enjoyable. It was a bit of a 50/50 day.

El entorno es bonito.Lo que no me gustó es la suciedad que había en el agua... Y al final del trayecto de ida, llegas a donde estan el resto de barcas y todos te piden que les compres o des dinero.En nuestro caso, compramos un refresco a la mujer que remaba porque se pegan un buen rato.... Esta bien... Pero no volvería como si haría a Halong Bay por ejemplo.

Viajamos a Nim Binh con la intención de disfrutar de Tam Coc y de Trang An, habíamos leído sobre los 2 lugares...pero la gente recomendaba Tam Coc. En mi opinión es muchísimo mejor disfrutar de Trang An.Tam Coc esta muy bien, el paisaje es espectacular..pero los barqueros...aquí vienen los suvenirer...el precio para realizar el turismo es bastante elevado y durante el recorrido te intentan vender refrescos para la mujer que rema...te piden propina...nos pasó todo lo que la gente había advertido...El recorrido se hace entre 1h30  o 2 horas...pero unos amigos lo hicieron en 45 minutos...el remero decidió que podía llover...y hizo un tur rapidísimo a pesar de las quejas de mis amigos para que fuese más despacio.Mi conclusión es que Trang An es muchísimo mejor..pero ara empezar mucho más barato en un recorrido mucho las largo con unas cuevas espectaculares!! Los remeros o barqueros en ningún momento te intentan vender nada..ni te piden propina...Si tienes que elegir entre uno de los 2..te recomiendo sin lugar a dudas Trang An.
...rice paddies are finished. Scenery and caves, while nice, are probably not worth a dedicated trip without the paddies.

We headed early (7am) to the boats to see the sights in serenity and gain some nice photos and it worked a treat. The scenery is beautiful throughout the 2 hour paddle. BUT: As you turn around to head back into Tam Coc township, your boat stops and allows another boat with a vendor selling junk food, fruit, drinks and beer to attach themselves until you buy their food. Our guy helped himself to a red bull and biscuits while the vendor started handing us all this food we hadn't asked for then demanded we pay before we moved any further. Then when you get back to the town, our guy and his friends surrounded the boat and hounded us for a tip as well! Bit of a disappointing way to end the trip!

Nice to go and see for the day, take a hat and sunscreen.   Quite a long ride to the caves and then back again (get comfortable, you are gone for a couple of hours).  the boat rowers (often older vietnamese) will mix it up rowing with both feet or hands - truly amazing.  We had already paid for the boat ride and were then pressured to pay another US$5 to the rower.  It is an extremely busy tourist spot.

c'est tres jolie mais trop poluer par l'argent et les touristes , les rameuses te demande un pour boire apres la balade alors que tu a deja payer la loc de la barque et la balade environ 8euros pour 1h aller retours , elles s'arrete sur la riviere devant leurs copines vendeuses en barque trop insistantes , si tu veu aller un peu plus loin elles demande des tunes, elles discutent te parle de leurs enfant juste pour grater un bon pour boire en arrivant et encore te dise que c'est pas asser. en ville tout les resto une rabateuse devant se qui est vraiment enervant ils comprenne pas que sa donne pas envie. faut juste sortir des chemeins battue , prendre des velo ou scooter et aller se balader pour profiter et sans guide ça ne sert a rien , decouvrir pas soit meme , comme tout le vietnam. 1 mois sans guide c'est bien plus agreable a decouvrir :-)

We went to Tam Coc first and we thought this was one of our highlights, because of the views. But two days later we went to Trang An which was much much better! In Tam Coc there are a lot of boats who want to sell stuff to you on the water and the ladies are quite busy talking to each other, which is a bit annoying because it kills the nice silence in the area. The views are great, but the rest isn't, sadly. And remember this one: When you are half way they ask if you want to buy a coca cola for the lady on you boat, we thought it will be nice to do, but she took pineapple and cookies as well. So we had to pay 100000 vnd, it wasn't the end of the world but really annoying because she didn't even drink the coca cola!!

We took the boat trip and it was just OK. The views are not as stunning as we expected and the boats are not comfortabel for the 1,5 - 2 hour trip. Also there is a lot of hassling on the water by people who try to sell you stuff.

3 dagen in Tam Coc gebleven. Let op: geen pinautomaat in dit kleine gehucht. Boottocht van ongeveer 1,5 uur gedaan. Was heel erg mooi maar je merkt ook duidelijk dat dit de meest toeristische in de omgeving is. Het duurst van allemaal en aan het eind schreeuwde onze roeister om fooi. Na het geven van 50000d eiste ze dat we meer zouden geven. Hier hebben we uiteraard niet aan meegewerkt. Dit gaf een nare nasmaak aan een verder mooie boottocht. Als je een boottocht in deze omgeving doet zou ik die bij Trang An willen adviseren. Hier zit je bijna 3 uur op een boot voor minder geld en er werd niet om fooi gevraagd maar uiteraard wel gewaardeerd.

Gegen Trang An ist Tam Coc kein Vergleich. Klar es ist zentral aber von der Natur her nicht vergleichbar mit Trang An. Und zudem diese Touristenabzocke. Hier geht die Tour auf gleichem Weg zurück wie man gekommen ist.Trang An dagegen ist eine tolle Rundfahrt.

Nous avons fait la balade en sampan .. cela aurait pu être idyllique si la rameuse n'avait cesser de se plaindre  "moi ,très fatiguée "( ce qui est sans doute vrai )  .. puis elle  m'a arraché le pourboire ( conforme à ce que notre guide avait conseillé ) des mains en  réclamant davantage !!!
Nothing you have never seen before, we went for a bike ride for a couple of hours and did the boat thing which wasnt the best experince

Nous avions prévu de passer une nuit en baie de Tu Long (moins touristique que Along), mais l'ouragan  se dirigeant vers cette baie le 17 octobre 2016 à eu pour conséquence que toutes les navigations n'ont pas été autorisées. Nous nous sommes alors rabattus sur Tam Coc.La promenade d'environ 2 heures sur cette rivière est certes agréable mais que de barques à la queue leu leu! Comme dans beaucoup d'endroits touristiques, le mercantile, malheureusement, prend le dessus: photographes, vendeurs de produits sur barques...Néanmoins, le paysage reste joli, dommage de ne pas avoir vu plus de couleurs car le riz était déjà récolté.Louer un vélo et se 'perdre' sur les petits chemins au travers des rizières est également une ballade recommandée.

Au cours d'un périple de 15 jours dans le nord du Vietnam, nous avons bien sur effectué la "traditionnelle" ballade en bateau sur la baie d'Halong terrestre à Tam Coc: la promenade d'environ une heure est agréable mais nous suivons une file de bateaux; ce sont les occidentaux qui, en majorité, visitent Tam Coc; à signaler qu'il n'y a plus de rizières mais des champs de lotus, qui, en octobre, sont fannés.
I love enjoying scenery here, but after the boat trip, the rower asked for more tips if they don't satisfy with what we gave. this is why I gave only 3 ***

Die Natur auf dem Fluss und die schönen Felsen drumherum sind sehr schön.Das entspannte Schippern ist super..trotz der anderen Touristen,ist nunmal beliebt und man kann auch den ein oder anderen Spass mit den Leuten haben.Bei sonnigem Wetter unbedingt Sonnenschutz auftragen oder einen Schirm mitnehmen.2std braten können ganz schön krass werden.Das was uns sehr ärgerte waren die nervigen Verkäufe die ab der Rückfahrt losgingen..teilweise echt penetrant.Man zahlt definitiv genug..Und ich denke nicht das ich für die Versorgung der Bootsfahrer aufkommen muss,das habe ich schon mit meinem üppigen Eintrittsgeld!Also entspannt bleiben und nein sagen..

Nice scenery but after coming from Sapa and Harlong Bay, it wasn't that great. Very touristy and lots of aggressive hassling. Perhaps if it was the first place we'd visited rather than the last, may have rated differently. Time again, we'd spend the day elsewhere - but you don't know until after the fact:)

Had combined this visit with Hoa Lu trip which is probably the best way to do it as a day tour. The area rises out of a flat plain and the hills are like that of Halong Bay. The area is on a flat plain and we could see the hills as we drove along the road rising up in front of us. It is eerie that you can have a flat plain and suddenly hills popping out of the ground. Would love to have learnt a little about the geology behind this but no information on offer. We did learn about the history given its ties to Hoa Lu and were offered the chance to do a bike ride around the area but the day was stinking hot and humid so past on that. We had lunch opposite the park at the side of the water area which was nice and could see people walking around and taking off on small boats. We walked over and sat in little two person( plus oarsgirl) and set off. The local girls were rowing almost all the boats and it was very interesting to see how the sit down and row with their feet. Did notice a couple of males rowing but they used their arms. The ride takes you along the waterway and there are still families living in pockets in a traditional manner. WE cruised past very slowly and could see fish traps, rice fields and more though noticed that there were several places that had been let go. The guide/rower did not speak English so could not obtain any information about the people still living here, how many etc. pity as that would have been very interesting. We wound around the waterway which appears to cover a circular route but does not go all the way around and we had to turn around at a point just near where I thought the end would be and go back. At a point near the turnaround point you travel thru a cave/tunnel. There are stalactites hanging down and the boat had to wind its way though. You could see tracks leading off through the grass areas and I would have liked to know how high the water rises in the wet season, how people then get around and more. This was a good trip to do but the bus trip there and back takes a longer( about 2 1/2 to 3 hours each way)  than the time you spend looking around. Also be very aware of the weather forecast as on a hot day it is very humid and sweaty. It makes doing any bike trip difficult.

Described as the inland Ha Long bay I enjoyed it but Ha Long bay is much better IMHO, scenery is nice - yes people do try to sell you things but as everywhere else just politely say no and you won't have any issues. Caves where interesting but not some spectacular must see natural beauty. by the end of my boat ride I was definitely ready to leave.

The scenery in ninh binh is always amazing but you get the same with a bike. Nice passages through the caves. 150000 a boat if you didn't book a tour. What troubled us is that everywhere there are people selling you pics if yourself, beer in whatever...pretty persistent. They don't really let you enjoy the trip. And also they way the paddler asks for tip...like you have no choice. But you do so we didn't pay cause he was a bit annoying. Hadn't he done that he'd probably have his tip now...

Trang An is cheaper and the duration of the trip is longer. This cost around $10 per person and was over within an hour. The rower earned herself a small tip and then had the cheek to ask for more.Honestly, give it a swerve.Go to Trang An

Camino obligatorio para cruzar a Hue por tierra, existe un paseo en barco para cruzar las tres cuevas, una cordillera montañosa enorme y con la forma reconocida de los montes de Vietnam, además de un par de complejos Budistas.Existen multitud de restaurantes para comer cabra, típico por lo visto en Ninh Binh, alquilar una bici o moto es ideal.

Sur le papier tout est idyllique : une balade en barque sur une rivière au milieu de rizières entourées de montagne. La réalité est beaucoup moins glamour : ça commence par un shooting photos un peu insistant après 10 minutes de navigation, puis l'assaut de marchandes très insistantes, elles aussi en barque, au moment du demi-tour. Mais la palme revient tout de même à la seconde rameuse qui est montée en catimini lors de l'embarquement et qui s'est assise à côté de vous. Mais pour quoi faire ? Pour assister la rameuse principale ? C'est ce que l'on peut croire ... jusqu'au moment du fameux demi-tour. Après avoir été littéralement harcelés par les marchandes qu'il aura presque fallu insulter pour qu'elles nous fichent la paix c'est au tour de la seconde rameuse. Elle déballe alors un tas de camelote qu'elle va essayer de vous refourguer avec insistance pendant de longues minutes. Si on ajoute à ça le fait qu'elle aura passé la quasi totalité du trajet (soit 1 heure 30) à parler très fort avec sa collègue, on peut résumer en disant que la balade idyllique et zen se transforme en épreuve pleine de stress.Sachant qu'il faut faire 2 heures de route pour arriver depuis Hanoi et autant pour rentrer on peut se demander si cette étape est indispensable.

Well worth doing, but be aware you a pressured into buying drinks and a drink for the boat driver. On the way back I handed the boat driver 40,000dong as a tip and she immediately demand me to increase it to 50,000. I genuinely didn't have another 10,000 in my pocket to give her. It got really awkward!

... mehr auch nicht. Die Bootsfahrt dauert rund 1,5 Stunden und kostet für 2 Personen etwa 400.000 Dong. Man fährt hin und wieder zurück, wobei man am Wendepunkt nahezu genötigt wird, dem "Kapitän" Verpflegung zu kaufen. Unserer Meinung nach macht es mehr Sinn, die schöne Landschaft mit dem Rad zu erkunden, hierfür einfach am "Hafen" nach rechts fahren und dem Feldweg folgen...
Very commercialized location though. It would have been nicer if we re not bombarded with photographers and sellers

I like the scenery but the flood of tourists really kills the mood. Plus, they trick you into tipping them at the end because they reminded during the whole journey how tiring it was way to row with their feet. At the end, their friends are waiting for you with snacks and drinks to buy for your rower. A bit cheap.

This area is lovely but not big enough to visit multiple days. It's a good stop for between Hué and Hanoi/Cat Ba/Halong Bay.We rented a bike and explored the area. That was lovely but very hot. Renting a motorbike seems a better idea. Costed us about $3The boot tours are the largest attraction. But be aware that you have to buy a ticket and then have to rent the boot. $15 is a little much for what you get.All the shops and restaurants are really try to scam you big time. More then in other cities. Make sure you make everything very clear befitting handing money. One of the store owners asked 55k dong for a bottle of water and a pack of cigarets (a little over priced), but after I handed him 205k he didn't want to give any change. I had to became mad and start making a scene before he was willing to return the change.

We had an organized tour from Hanoi, so the price for the boatride was included.We arrived just after lunch at the hottest hour. We knew this, so no problem. Just be prepared with an umbrella, sunblock and of course a lot of water.The trip over the water was nice and not many people at all, probably way too hot at this hour. This might also be the reason we lacked the experience of locals selling extremely overpriced stuff :-). The boat ride takes you to a nice cave with bats where he turns around to go back. The landscape is very impressive. The water is green and polluted. The guide told us to tip the driver "a little bit". At the end we offered him a very decent tip, but he started showing/demanding big bills. We were completely not amused by this tactic and made this very clear to him.We would rate this experience 4 stars, but the rudeness demanding more tip made us believe this location became a tourist trap.

Mit dem vorher zu bezahlenden Boot geht es 2 Stunden durch drei Grotten auf dem Fluss an den Felsen entlang und nach der 3. Grotte sofort die Strecke zurück zur Anlegestelle.Die Bootsfrauen rudern mit den Füßen.Wehrmutstropfen sind die vielen aufdringlichen Souvenirverkäufer und die Bootsfrauen selber, die während der Fahrt versuchen erstens ihre eigenen Souvenirs an den Mann zu bringen, zweitens zu anderen Verkäuern rudern um deren Geschäfte zu unterstützen und drittens kurz vor Ende ganz unverhohlen nach hohem Tipp verlangen und  hinterher noch schimpfen, dass es zu wenig sei, obwohl die Fahrt vorher bezahlt wurde. Kein schönes Erlebnis für uns.

I do not understand why the boatride here is shorter than Trang An but more pricey. I decide to climb to Mua Cave to have an overall look. Do not try boatride. Instead try in trang an is enough for me
For people who likes natural scenery  this might be a good place to go. Boats are rowed by the locals using legs, which is unique.

Heard bad reviews of the boat ride overcharging tourists. Hence only rented a  scooter and took a left all the way in to the Bich Dong Pagoda. A lot of food options along the way in, so don't worry being hungry or if you need wifi.

The scenery around Tam Coc is beautiful and well worth the visit.  In my opinion it is on par with, if not better than, Ha Long Bay.  Unfortunately the rowboat experience was spoiled by the persistent badgering from vendors and the even more persistent badgering from the rower of our boat to give her a tip.  We are more than happy to tip but this was over the top and she was ungrateful no matter what tip we gave her.  Here's a tip lady; shut up, do your job and gratefully accept the tip you're given!!

A group of three of us paid 195,000 dong each. It was definitely expensive, which gave us the impression that there would be a lot to see. Sure the view was lovely and very picturesque, but the photographers at the beginning and the floating food and drink vendors at the end made it a little less enjoyable. When we got off the boat we were forced to pay a tip to the rower, which we didn't really mind, but then we were scolded that our tip wasn't big enough. We were then pestered by a man selling the picture taken of us, which had already been printed and laminated, which we didn't want and didn't buy. This reinforced our opinions that Tam Coc is just a tourist trap and those who work there are not selling you an experience, and only see dollar signs above your head.

Tam Coc is nice in the summer with rice field. But when we finish the boat tour the rower ask us the tip. On the tour they always invite us to buy something on the boat. This area is not friendly people where only business not nature. Trang An is the best place to see in Ninh Binh.

When I did Tam Coc I thought it was great, but the next day I did Trang An which is waay better. The style is the same, the locals paddel you around, but views are greater and the caves also really cool. Tam Coc is still more touristy, but for me I think not the best option.

Tam Coc is een fraaie vallei en een boottochtje op een traditionele sampan is een leuke manier om die te bekijken. Langs de oever is soms wat activiteit, maar verder zie je vooral groen onderweg. Het hoogtepunt zijn de lage grotten waar je doorheen vaart. Aan het einde gaven we onze roeisters een nette fooi (we hadden gecheckt bij onze gids wat een redelijk bedrag was), maar daar deden ze zeer ontevreden over. Zoiets doet toch afbreuk aan het verder aardige tochtje.

Did a two day tour from Hanoi staying in Ninh Binh for the night. First day went to Tam Coc it took nearly 4 hours to get there as it was a public holiday and traffic out of Hanoi was ridiculous! Went to the temple first which was incredible when you think of how old it is. Then luch in the town which actually pretty horrible. All the bus tours come here and it was buffet style and hundreds of people.The food was mostly cold and unapetising compared to other food we've eaten here in Vietnam. Then a bike ride whihc we paid extra for but was really good - biked through the quiet lanes enabling us to be part of the countryside.
