Then the boat trip - we had a 52 year lady and they do amazingly well with driving the boat using their feet. It was a bit long though - very crowded waterway and we were a little disappointed when we realised how we had been ripped off by the food sellers at the end of the third cave - they sell you stuff then ask you to buy something for your driver and we found out afterwards they don't even eat or drink it but onsell to other unsuspecting tourists. She then asked us for a tip towards the end even though we had already planned to tip her. We gave her 80,000 but now wish we had made it 50 instead. Then got hassled by photographers and ended up paying more than we should have for photos. That put a different spin on the whole experience. However - forewarned is forearmed so go with your eyes open. The countryside is absolutely stunning and they call it Halong Bay on land whihc is exactly what it looks like.
Paid $32 for a day trip from Hanoi. Staff were amazing but the tour itself was only average. Would of preferred more time cycling around Tam Coc rather than being on the boat for so long-1.5 hours.

The setting at Tam Coc is very beautiful, and we enjoyed our small boat trip up the river.  Although it was overcast while we were there, that only added to the unreal beauty.  We photographed another boat going upriver, only to discover we were expected to purchase souvenirs from both the other boatman and our boatman before we were returned to the dock at Ninh Binh.  Enjoy the natural beauty but be ready to be firm in rebuffing the repeated requests to buy souvenirs at inflated prices.

While I was here it was a bit cold and very overcast. The scenery was nice and it was pretty impressive to see someone paddle a boat with their feet, but I feel like this kind of outdoor activity would be better suited for the warmer and sunnier parts of the year.

Hicimos el paseo en barca de Tam Coc a las 15h para quitarnos todos los turistas. Nos montamos cuatro en dos barquitas y a nosotras nos tocó un remero bastante serio y mayor. A mí me dió cosilla pq el hombre parecía cansado pero intenté disfrutar de las impresionantes vistas desde el río. Además pasamos por 3 cuevas totalmente a oscuras con frontal.En general buena impresión.

Hum... comment dire que c'est pire qu'un nez à touristes où vous serez harcelés par des photographes pendant votre visite.Le pire est d'arriver à la fin des 7 km et de voir que plein de vendeuses en barques vont vous proposer nourriture et eau à acheter de manières très oppressantes. Ca en est tellement oppressant que cela vous déçoit de la visite.Retour sur le même chemin qui enlève donc du charme à la balade, même si elle reste sympa.Une visite très sympa est celle qui est plus loin, Van Long, mais nécessite de prendre un petit transport.Hors mis cela Ninh Binh est juste sublime mais évitez de faire la balade en barque de Tam Coc. Il y en a au moins 10 différentes et beaucoup plus charmantes que nous avons aperçu sur le chemin mais n'avons pas eu le temps de faire.

J'y suis allé dans le cadre d'un circuit organisé et pour le coup, je ne pense que cela soit la meilleure façon de découvrir la baie d'Halong terrestre ! Depuis les barques, on ne voit pas grand chose car la végétation est assez dense et les grottes n’apportent pas grand chose !

Nous avons aimé : paysage , la tranquillité des lieux tôt le matin.Nous n'avons pas aimé : plusieurs fois arrêté par des vendeurs ambulants sur des barques pour vendre de la nourriture , rameuse elle même s’arrêtant en cours de route pour nous vendre de la broderie et nous réclamant un pourboire à la fin. Pour couronner le tout une personne nous ayant prit en photos sans notre accord au départ nous attendait à notre arrivé pour nous vendre la photo. Le seul moment au Vietnam ou nous nous sommes senti harcelé pour dépenser notre argent.Nous avons des paysages similaires voir plus beau sans être dérangé Trang An

Ini tempat wisata yang mirip suasana Halong Bay. Tempatnya bagus tetapi hal yang patut disayangkan sewaktu kita tengah asyik-asyik menikmati suasana eehh ada-ada saja scam :( dan tak jarang banyak yang maksa juga. jadi gregetan saya. Senang menikmati pemandangan tapi hufff banyak scam....

The Tam Coc excursion seemed to us like one of the highlights of the trip. However, the trips are so "turistified" that they are quite aggressive in the sense that the boat ladies and everybody around this business want to milk your money out somehow. The views from the boats are nice, but not as awesome as if they had taken us up the mountain, which is the classical postcard of Tam Coc. The caves at the end are just waiting points for merchants to try to board your boat and sell you anything.

Very scenic boat ride but quite touristy. We went at around 3.30pm which was a good time as we saw hoards of boats coming back the other way but we were one of the only boats heading out at that time so it was really quiet on the way back, and the vendors selling snacks were all rowing home so didn't bother us much either. Have some small notes ready to tip your rower at the end (they will ask for it!).   For two people on one boat the total price came to 390,000 dong (not including tip) and you can't really haggle it down as the prices are written up on the board at the ticket office. It was also 10,000 dong extra to park our motorbike. It was a relaxing ride but we preferred some of the other attractions in the region like climbing to the top of Mua Caves and Bich Dong pagoda (better views from up high!).

When I went to this river, the weather was not so good. The sky was all white, and the rice paddy field was all covered with water. I don't like some sellers who are a bit persistent in offering their goods, with exorbitant price (100K dong for 3 pack of snacks).

Dit kan heel leuk zijn maar hou zeker rekening met het weer of neem voldoende warme kledij mee als je dit in deze tijd van het jaar wil doen, wij waren bijna onderkoeld na 1 en half uur in een open bootje, nu ook geen rijst op de velden dus beetje dooie boel allemaal, je merkt dat dit duidelijk een toeristisch trekpleister is, wat zeker mooi kan zijn maar nogmaals doe dt enkel bij goed weer en in de zomer maanden, let wel dat het er dan druk kan zijn. Laat je ook niet imponeren door de aanhoudende verkoopsters in de bootjes onderweg.

The scenery when on the boat ride was very pretty but they always seem to have people asking for money.  Other boats come up to your boat trying to sell you food and then they told us we need to buy food and drinks for our boat driver.  Then a woman takes your photos and then tries to sell those to you even though you didnt ask for them.  Then our boat driver kept asking us for a tip after we had paid over 400,000 for our "Tickets" and then "Boat Fees".  If they don't treat the tourists better they are going to lose their revenue stream.  I wouldn't recommend going here unless you are really interested in scenery or you have another reason to be in the area.

Tam Coc is mooi om er geweest te zijn. Boottocht is leuk, vooral met goed weer. Wij hebben een dag tour geboekt bij Rose Dagtours in Hanoi. Bij haar was het mogelijk om de dagtour te boeken en pas de volgende dag met de bus opgepikt te worden om terug te gaan naar Hanoi. Zo kan je zelf een hotel zoeken en heb je de volgende dag genoeg tijd om een scooter te boeken om heerlijk Tam Coc te verkennen. Ik zeg doen! Het was erg leuk. Tam Coc zelf is een klein plaatsje met wat winkeltjes een aantal hotels en restaurants. Omgeving er omheen is erg mooi.

Nice experience, but a bit annoyed with our paddler that chat loudly with two other paddlers which kind of ruined the peace & beautiful scenery experience. Get ready to give tips, as it is the only thing the paddlers care about in the last 10 minutes of the ride!The photographers kept chasing & asking to take photos, that as another annoying thing. If they took a few photos of you without consent and try to sell the photos to you after the boat ride, don't be afraid to formly say 'NO!'.... Otherwise they keep following you everywhere.
私は３回ほど行ったことがありますが、洞窟を抜けた所にいるおばちゃん達の物売り攻勢がすごいので閉口しました。現在では、近く公開されるようになったTran Anの方がはるかにきれいだと思います。
陸のハロン湾といわれているTam Cocです。ちょっと期待が大き過ぎたせいか、あまり感動しませんでした。しかしながら１時間にもわたるボートトリップを楽しめました。自分がベトナム語を少し話せたせいか、漕ぎ手には好印象でした。（笑）途中、もの売りが来ますが買ってはいけません。また、漕ぎ手へのチップは２ドル(4万ドン)/名が相場ですので、気持ちよく払いましょう。(笑)お互い不愉快な思いをしません。

Compared to Tran An,  disappointed. Water wasn't clean, life jackets were not provided, prices were cheated, boat pilots ask too much tips... I don't recommend to visit. All these destroy image of Vietnamese tourism.

Firstly, it's really worth visiting.  The landscape is amazing, it is very unlike any other place on the map. However, you get the impression it's beginning to be a victim of its own success. You are hawked at the boats.  Then at the end turn around point, you are ambushed by touts, in clear cahoots with the boats person for you to buy drinks. Really annoying after a tranquil trip through the caves. After you are forced into a terse no, they start employing you to buy a drink for the boatman....On the return about 300 meters to shore, the boatman started to say pip rather loudly and had stopped rowing. Until he corrected it to tip - which we had no trouble with.  After a generous tip he aggressively demanded double that, for each person...Got of boat, swarmed by people selling the same unique hat The management of the area you pay for a permit really needed to lay down some ground rules.

Boat hire with lady rowing was D150,000 for a couple. Plus entry to the 3 caves via the boat was D120,000 person. Total 390,00 Dong for a couple. Supposed to be 2 hours but our trip was 1hr 40mins. On the way our lady rowing stopped to let her buddies try and sell us drinks etc from another boat, even telling us to buy one for our lady. We said no but our lady did not continue untill I told her to. It was a quiet trip after that until 20 metres from shore when she kept demanding "tip". I think not, even though I originally planned to. The scenery was lovely, but not spectacular. At the furthurest point she offered to progress further but only for another D200,000. A bit of a rip off. The locals in town even said so.

Es una de las cosas que se venden como obligadas de la visita por tam coc, vale 120.000 dongs y después de leer a tantas y tantas personas teniendo problemas con las propinas decidimos hacer el recorrido a pie y en moto por mi parte merece mucho más la pena que el mal rato y el cabreo, preciosas vistas desde dentro y fuera del río

The lake looks very serene. There are three limestone caves and it takes an hour and a half to go n return. The boat person rows the boat with their legs. Impressive but they r very tourisy. Once u offer them a hot drink they ask for more n push their produce to u. Pushing you to buy from them n they come on hard.

This boat tour was less impressive than we imagined. But it still was a nice boat trip and the scenery is beautiful and serene. A lot of other people on Tripadvisor complain about the crowds. We saw loads and loads of empty boats at the starting point, so we're pretty sure it can get very crowded here. But we arrived early (around 8) and there was not a soul to be seen. Also, there were only a few vendors at this early hour. We saw some of them at the turning point halfway, but they weren't pushy at all. A stern 'no' did the trick.

Eine wunderschöne Landschaft ,traumhafte Berge und alles in allem ein muss .Wird nur durch sehr überteuerte Preise geschmälert .Die verkaufsmaschen und Tricks muss man einfach ignorieren und massiv ausschlagen die anschließende Fahrradtour kann man sich schenken, die Räder sind meist defekt.Dennoch war es mit Einschränkungen ein wunderschöner Ausflug .

Les grottes de Tam Coc se trouvent assez loin de Hanoi, et peuvent se visiter dans la journée sans problème. Je conseille de les visiter avant la baie d'Halong. L'entrée est payante et la ballade en barque avec une rameuse qui rame avec ses pieds dure environ 45 minutes aller retour. Après avoir traversé 3 grottes et après avoir été pris en photo,   quelques vendeuses essaieront de vous vendre à boire pour vous , et si vous ne souhaitez pas boire, elles essaieront de vous convaince d'acheter à boire pour votre rameuse !! par contre je n'ai pas vu de vendeuses de nappes brodées. Après la ballade assez paisible certes, mais vous ne serez pas seuls mais entourés de barques avec d'autres touristes, vous débarquez et on vous vend la photo que l'on vous a prise aupraravant pour un prix dérisoire. Laissez un pouboire à la rameuse (environ 2 euros) de toute façon elle le réclamera (bizarrement les touristes vietnamiens ne donnent rien, et rien ne leur est réclamé, petit constat)Il faut savoir qu'il fait très chaud, le soleil tape (creme solaire et chapeau sont recommandés), en tout cas au mois d'octobre -novembre.Je me pose toujours la question si cela vaut le coup de faire toute cette route au départ de Hanoi , je ne suis pas convaincue que cette excursion soit indispensable dans un circuit Vietnam comme beaucoup le disent ! Mais il en faut pour tous les gouts, en tout cas je n'ai pas été époustouflée par la beauté du site, que j'ai trouvé moyen.
promenade en bateau amusante surtout pour la technique des rames maniées par les femmes à l'aide des pieds.
Was a good day tour. no much to do in this town. The boat are not very safe and the water very cold. Visite of the cave is ok as some cave was not naturally build.

This place is beautiful and worth seeing but on the other hand be prepared for the hard sell. Drinks and junk food for your rower, ( over priced )the stuff they try to sell you then the tip. Be prepared to be seen as "a rich tourist". Ignore all that and take in the scenery.

Creo que lo visite... Los guoas hablaban en vietnamita así q nunca entendo el nombre del río...Durante la navegación ves unas cuevas y la guía canta unas canciones folclóricas...Lo más lindo fue ver los camalotes florecidos...
個人旅行でハノイにいき、ホテル近くの旅行社でツアー申し込みました。２～４名乗りの小舟で行きます。　　湿地帯を１時間ほどゆっくりと景色を楽しむツアーです。　おばちゃん一人でしたので、私の漕ぐのを手伝いました。　途中で物売りが来てうるさいです。　　それに、チップを要求するので困りましたよ。　気分を悪くするので行かなくてもいいとこかもね。

Een tripje aan Tam Coc is het waard voor de views en de mooie plaatsjes als begin van Halong Bay. Een 2 uur durend tochtje is een verrijking van de vakantie aan Vietnam. Storend is wél de verkopers en het onbeschoft vragen om fooi na afloop.

Very nice and interesting place to visit. We organised a daily tour and no regret at all. We took many interesting and scenic photos so liked it. Very quite and tranquil place... If you have 1 day extra, you can visit Tam Coc and the nice river.
Tam coc streek is mooi, ha long bay on land, veel hotels en restaurantjes, je kan er goed relaxen. Wij vonden trang an grottes leuker, minder druk, maar daar waren niet zo veel plekken om te eten....

Bonitos paisajes, aunque si ya has viajado por el sudeste asiático, quizás no te impresionen tanto. Se tarda unas 3 horas en llegar por carretera desde Hanoi, y una vez allí, coges unos barcos que te llevan a dar la vuelta que dura aproximadamente una hora.

We made a day trip from Hanoi. We left at 8 and we were back at 6:30. We probably didn't go on the best time of the year, but contrary to other places we have visited in North Vietnam (Sapa, Halong Bay) we were not impressed.The pictures looked much better than reality.

The scenery here is spectacular but the boat trip is horrendously overpriced and slightly ruined by being constantly harassed for more money. The entrance fee and boat fee to start with is ridiculously high. Then there are locals on boats who harass you to buy drinks and souvenirs. We bought ourselves drinks as we'd been riding all day and were parched but then we were made to feel guilty and buy drinks for the rowers - we didn't. Then at the end of the trip our rower lady pleaded for a tip and followed us. A great natural landscape that is ruined by the scamming.

The Tam Coc area was very touristy.We were in the area for several days and saw plenty of beautiful places. We went on 4 different boat trips and the Tam Coc one came 4th. It was also the most expensive.

La balade est certes sympa mais il y a eu de la pluie donc difficile d'apprécier à sa juste valeur. Un peu déçue car je m'attendais à voir les mêmes photos de paysage que sur internet, or ce fut un peu fade.

The river boat trip is nice but nowhere near as beautiful and eerie as the grottoes boat trip 5km from Tam Coc, which not many people seem to know since it is not in the guides. To make things worse, the trip was twice the price of the other one and half the time.

We went to Ninh Binh because our trip to Halong Bay was cancelled due to poor weather conditions. Our hotel told us Ninh Binh is like Halong Bay but in a river. We were not at all disappointed by the landscape and its beauty, but by locals trying to rip you off, especially on the boat trip. Right after the third cave when the boat takes a turn to go back, we were assaulted by sellers on boats. An old lady grasped our boat and almost forced us to buy a drink. She pointed at the rower and said this could be "a tip for her". So after asking the rower if she wanted anything, we bought one Coke for her. She didn't drink it though. Our guess is that she gave back the Coke afterwards to be reselled again and again. After a few minutes, our rower displayed a whole bag of purses and hangbags. We kindly said we weren't interested but she was pushy and we thought, okay, we buy something from her and that's the tip. But once we arrived in the shore, she was begging again for a tip!! Which we didn't give. Once on land, a guy approached us with a picture of us on the boat. And so on an on for the whole day.

We were a little bit disappointed about this trip. We feeled there ripping-off. The boat drivers seemed to work with the market boats. They tryed to sell watee and other stuff for much money. Disgisting. Trang An was much better!

Nous avons fais toutes les ballades en barque de la région. Celle ci n offre rien de plus ni de moins que les autres. 120 000 dôngs adultes. 60000 enfants. Le problème c est Qu ils risquent de tuer la poule aux œufs d or car cette ballade est très commerciale avec les photographes , les boutiques sur l eau et les souvenirs de la rameuse. Curieusement nous avions 2 rameuses donc 2 pourboires. Il pleuvait à verse et nous avons donc trouvé le temps un peu long.

Avons visité le tres celebre site de Tam Coc (baie d'halong terrestre). Impression mitigée. Balade en petite pirogue avec aux rames (pour nous en tout cas) une vieille femme quj souffrait de la chaleur (nous aussi mais on ne ramait pas !). Moins d'eau à cette époque. Paysage certes envoutant mais nous l'avions visité en hiver il y a 20 ans et on en gardait un bien meilleur souvenir. Si vous hésitez entre Tam Coc et Baie d'Halong, n'hésitez pas, allez à la baie d'halong !

J'ai apprécié ce moment en barque malgré son prix (270 000 pour moi seule). Je pense que je l'ai d'autant plus apprécié car je n'avais pas encore fais la baie d'ha long (je l'ai faite le lendemain). Je peux comprendre que bon nombre de personnes soient déçus ! Donc un conseil: visitez d'abord Tam Coc puis Hà Long pour profitez de ce voyage en barque, tout de même joli et reposant. Faites mine de ne pas parler anglais, dites non calmement mais fermement lorsque les femmes essayent de vous vendre leurs produits.

ช่วงที่ไปคือช่วงปีใหม่ บอกเลยว่าอากาศค่อนข้างหนาว ยิ่งลงเรือแล้วหนาวเข้าไปอีกทัวร์ที่ซื้อไปเป็นเดย์ทริปจากฮานอยรวมอาหารแบบบุฟเฟที่ไม่ค่อยจะอร่อยเท่าไหร่ เรือที่นั่ง นั่ง2 คน มีคนพาย2คนหรือ1คนแล้วแต่ลำ แต่ที่นี่จะใช้เท้าพาย พายไปสุดทาง หรือ กลางทาง จะมีการขนของออกมาขาย บอกเลยว่าราคาแพงกว่าในเมืองฮานอยเยอะ ต่อเยอะๆ ถ้าจะซื้อ พอจบทริปก็ขอทิป ให้แล้วก็ไม่เลิกจะเอาเยอะๆ ดึงมือดึงขา ทำให้คนในทริปเซ็งไปตามๆกัน

Tam Coc meaning 3 caves is one of the must see Northern Vietnam attractions. If go by tour, it is normally bundled together with Hoa Lu Ancient capital tour to see the temples. Tam Coc is a really beautiful boat ride about 1.5 hours long on the paddy fields among the limestone cliffs. The novelty is truly seeing the boat paddlers using their legs to paddle.Ways to enjoy yourself better-a) See the title above. Put plenty of sunblock, wear a cap and bring a huge umbrella along as well or rent from the wharf if possible. Even the boat paddler has a gigantic umbrella to protect himself from the relentless sun. Sizzling to a crisp on 1.5 hours on the boat is not my idea of a good time.  b) Have small change of USD or 20K dong bills. Tam Coc is really about getting money from tourists. An recommended USD1/passenger tip for the rower, drink sellers at the end of the 3rd cave guilt-tripping us to buy drinks for the paddler, and USD2 for one photo taken by the hordes of photographers on the river. It's disappointing to feel that tourists have been transformed into a commodity. Even the poor paddler is trying to sell handicraft. The trip could have been better if we were not made to feel guilty for not buying things to support these people.

The scenery is absolutely gorgeous, and the boat we were rowed out to the caves by a chatty lady with fairly good English. Unfortunately, (and this is just the way Vietnam is so you cannot expect any different), the boat company runs a monopoly here, you are lead to believe that you must take their boats to get to the caves. I think you can probably row it yourself if you found a boat to rent, however it isn't particularly expensive to rent from the company, especially for us westerners and I'm all for supporting the locals. The problem lies in the fact that they only pay the rowers a very low percentage of what you pay them for a 'ticket' they are poor farmers in the local area, often waiting days at a time to have the opportunity to take some tourists out. Therefore when they have you in their clutches they will ask you for tips outright and expect a certain amount, some will almost force you to buy the souvenirs they hide on the boat until you're out in the open water with them, and they will also row you to an inlet where local ladies have floating shops, allow them to attach themselves to the boat and badger you to buy drinks and snacks. We were guilt tripped into buying our rower a red bull because she was tired, we are not nasty people and don't want to see people suffer, but as young backpackers we aren't made of money. We still tipped at the end but she said this wasn't enough and tried to get more but we can only give what our budget allows so she was forced to take us back to land, but she wasn't happy. And I can see why, they are desperate, it is their only opportunity to supplement their already low income from farming and they have come to rely on generous westerners. This moral and ethical debate lies with the government, all I say is be aware, be friendly, please don't lose your temper and be rude, but be firm with them. Try and make them laugh and be interested in their lives, if you don't have the money to give, at least try and make them smile. Above all, enjoy what you came to see, the scenery and relax.
Beautiful location but overpriced. Went from 130k to 195k VND per person in less than a year. And I suspect that the rowers did not get their share in this...

Stunning scenery which is just as beautiful if not more than Halong Bay.  Truly amazing.  Really enjoyed this aspect of the trip.  However, the pushy touts demands for tips and being almost held to ransom eg: felt like she wouldn't row the boat back to shore unless we bought drinks and gave a tip to the woman rowing the boat ruined the trip. A lady on our tour was harassed by a man taking a photo.  A scam.  They take your picture then you have to buy the picture.  She said no, he didn't take the hint and carried on taking her picture she said no! again and our tour guide told her off for yelling at him!  O M G seriously??!!!  She was so upset she cried.  Shocking experience of what should have been a pleasant day. These locals need to back off.  It's very sad that it's their only way of making a living but if they're that pushy and word gets out the tourist numbers that go there won't want to.  If the locals demand such tips etc maybe the tours need to charge a lesser price and inform tourists that they have to "buy" from the locals.

venant de la baie d'halong et ayant fait un détour d'une journée juste pour ça,  je fus un peu decu.surnommé la baie d'along terrestre j'attendais à quelque chose de grandiose.Au final j'ai découvert un endroit joli et mignon.Je ne suis pas déçu d'y avoir été mais je n'en garderais pas non plus un souvenir impérissable.
Les paysages sont beaux,rizières, grottes, montagnes, la ballade est tranquille mais un peu longue...il ne se passe rien! Si le ciel est gris, passez à autre chose.

พวกเราไปกับทัวร์แบบวันเดย์ทริปจากฮานอย ระหว่างทางรถทัวร์พาแวะเมืองฮวาลือ  แล้วไปทานข้าวเที่ยงกันที่ Tam coc จากนั้นลงเรือที่พายด้วยเท้า เราได้คนพายเป็นผู้หญิง ระหว่างที่นั่งเรือไปมีการจอดเปลี่ยนคนพายด้วย สองข้างทางวิวสวยมากอยู่ท่ามกลางภูเขาสลับกันไปมา ที่น่าเสียดายคือไม่มีทุ่งข้าวให้ดูเหมือนที่เคยเห็นในภาพโฆษณา และเรือบางลำก็มีการขายสินค้าให้นักท่องเที่ยวบนเรือซึ่งเหมือนจะทำให้นักท่องเที่ยวลำบากใจ ส่วนเรือที่เรานั่งไม่โดนขายสินค้า

We came here with some trepidation having read previous TA reports of intimidation and harassment. We found neither. This may be because we were there on a Tuesday and there were very few sampans on the water. Also we had the company of a Vietnamese guide. I find it hard to believe some of the tales told by previous reviewers. The trip is just an hour and a half, the scenery is pleasant but not spectacular.In short if you are in the area already it would be a five star but if you are travelling down from Hanoi on a day trip then it is just the three awarded - four hours there and back detract from the days enjoyment.

We did a tour that included this and Hoa Lua.  While it is scenic this is disrupted by line of boats in front of you, behind you and coming at you from the other direction.  I do not think it would be worth making the trip out here just to do this boat tour but combined with something else it is a worthwhile addition.  Someone said that they were told that if they did this they did not need to do Halong Bay.  While I understand where this comment came from as both are large rock mounds in the water they are vastly different and both worthy of a visit.  If only visiting one, Halong Bay would be my choice hands down.Perhaps I'm spoiled as I have recently visited caves in Capri and Barbados and thus these caves did little to impress me.  What did floor us though was watching the boat rowers row with their feet instead of their hands ...wish I had gotten a picture of this.  True talent.
Aucun interet,mais la région est magnifique,surtout de voir ces étendues de rizières!!!!!Des images inoubliables typiques du Vietnam!!!!

This place is what I would imagine Halong Bay to be like on land. Overall the natural beauty is amazing. However, my experience was spoiled by the dishonesty and greed of the local boat vendors. I admit it was my fault. At the end of the 1 hr boat ride ( our rower was slower than normal), there are ladies who sell snacks, fruit and drinks on the turn around point. I am vietnamese American so I have no problem telling them no but our rower lady asked us to buy her a drink for 20k which should normally cost no more than 15g. Out of the goodness of our hearts we felt bad for her having to row for 1 hr in the heat even though we know that we already paid $45/person for our day tour and her pay was already included when I saw our tour guide handing the rower' "handler" money. It's my second day in Vietnam and I was not yet acquainted to the currency here. I neglectful lly handed the seller $200g which is equivalent to $10usd. She didn't even bother to give me change back and quickly rowed away. On our way back we gave our rower 20g which was the suggested tip by our tour guide. She still shamelessly asked for more,  saying that it's not enough in vietnamese. I told her back that we already gave her the drink and she knows that I paid 200g for that so she should just ask the seller lady to split the profit.

We loved the scenery but  too many people around (tourists and local people trying to sell things) spoil the experienceTake a boat early in the morning or in the afternoon, to avoid the tourists coming from Hanoi and  experience the magnificent nature far from the crowds We preferred the visit to Trang An river which is also crowded by Vietnamese people,at least the experience is more genuine and not so touristy

The boat ride was awesome despite the rainy season. Amazed on how they can paddle the boat with both hands and sometimes with their legs. Half way the boat ride, there will be people selling food/souvenir on other boat and they will try to approach you to buy, just said no if you don't want to. Coz they are a bit pushy. and there will also be a lot of people with DSLR wants to take your photo and they will also try to sell it to you with a very expensive price. be very careful on that since they are a bit manipulative. If u really wanna buy the photo they took and printed out for you, before you make the bargaining, try to record on video what they offer you, since when they came back to you at the end of the boat ride, they will say the different thing.

the river and scenery are great, reminding a lot of the views in Chinese Lee river and north of Thailand. The rowers, quite old ladies, are well trained and can row with their feet while using their hands for texting with their smartphones. The caves are nice, but nothing to write home about. On half way, after the third cave when returning back - a fleet of "floating market" boats with all kinds of souvenirs and tourist traps block the way and do not let you pass until you buy something or succeed to convince them you are not going to buy anything. Photographer take  photos of you along the way and sell them to you after disembrking your boat. Nice experience but not more than that

Tam Coc merupakan salah satu wisata di daerah ninh binh yang patut Anda kunjungi. Anda hanya memerlukan waktu sekitar 2-3 jam dari Hanoi untuk ke daerah ini. Untuk lebih mudahnya, Anda dapat membeli one day tour ke Tam Coc yang banyak ditawarkan hotel maupun agen tour di sekitar old quarter. Tam Coc sebenarnya sangat indah dan dikatakan Halong Bay in Land tetapi keindahan itu dirusak oleh oknum-oknum mereka sendiri.Pertama, satu kapal hanya boleh memuat 2 orang. Saya tidak tahu kenapa mereka menerapkan peraturan seperti ini, mungkin awalnya Anda mengira untuk alasan keselamatan tetapi di kapal tertentu Anda dapat melihat ada 3 orang di satu kapal. Ketika saya bertanya kepada guide saya kenapa di kapal tersebut diperbolehkan 3 orang, dia menjawab karena mereka orang Vietnam. Sungguh diskriminasi karena ada satu keluarga yang membawa 3 anak kecil harus dibagi dalam 2 kapal.Kedua, pengayuh kapal memaksa kita untuk berbelanja barang bawaan mereka. Saya tidak mengalami peristiwa ini. Yang mengalami peristiwa ini adalah teman saya. Teman saya diberhentikan di tengah-tengah perjalanan balik karena si pengayuh menawarkan barang jualan mereka. Teman saya telah menolak untuk membeli tetapi si pengayuh tetap memaksa. Teman saya yang mencoba mendayung untuk menggerakan kapal pun dilarang untuk mendayung karena dia sedang berjualan. Karena hal ini teman saya pun jadi tidak mau memberikan tips, tetapi pada akhirnya dia hanya memberikan tips 10.000 VND tetapi sang pengayuh kapal menolak. Sungguh tidak tau terima kasih.Sedangkan pengayuh kapal saya tidak malu secara terang-terangan meminta tips kepada saya. Itu yang membuat saya ilfill. Kenapa mereka tidak memberikan service yang memuaskan sehingga dengan hal tersebut kita tidak akan segan untuk memberikan tips. Sepanjang dia mendayung, dia hanya diam dan tidak menjelaskan apa-apa kepada saya, satu kalimat yang keluar dari mulutnya hanyalah meminta tips.Berbeda lagi dengan kapal yang dinaiki orang tua saya, sebenarnya pengayuh mereka cukup baik karena menjelaskan mengenai hal-hal penting sepanjang perjalanan tetapi tetap saja pengayuh mereka berjualan.Saya tidak tahu apakah semua pengayuh disini memiliki sikap yang sama tetapi itulah yang dialami oleh saya dan keluarga saya.Sehari sebelumnya, saya mengikuti one day tour ke bai dinh-trang an, menurut saya bai dihn trang an lebih bagus 100x lipat dari tam coc. Di trang an Anda dapat menikmati pengalaman yang sama, menyusuri sungai untuk melihat pemandangan seperti halong bay dan melewati 7 gua, lebih banyak dari tam coc yang hanya melewati 3 gua.Ya, tidak salahnya Anda mengunjungi tempat ini tetapi waspadalah dengan oknum yang tidak bertanggung jawab.
Sur les barques, nous sommes tombés sur une rameuse qui a stoppé sa barque jusqu'à ce que nous achetions de la nourriture à l'une de ses amis. Un peu trop insistant à mon goût.
In short, better to go to Trang An if you can. It may be a bit more expensive but more beautiful than Tam Coc.

The landscape is pretty nice, but the Caves are a joke. Also the trip takes very long and the boat is uncomfortable. In the end a bunch of ladies is trying to sell you stuff. I'm not sure if it's worth the money. Maybe it's better, just to cycle around the area.

We went on a group tour and whilst we were looking forward to our trip, we were most upset by the rower's attitude. At mid way of our river trip, He just told us to wait , tied the boat and went to sit and have a drink! Because i was alone in the boat with my son, i did not feel reassured and asked him to go back. He went off anyway. No other boats around stopped and i was getting worried. Lucky i saw another person on the tour and shouted for her to come to us and then her rower had to call for the rower to come back. Also he stooped rowing on our way back trying to sell items. The way they act is far from encouraging tourists. I wouldnt go back and instead concentrate on halong bay

Fizemos o passeio de barco pelo rio, as paisagens são lindas, passar pelas grutas/cavernas na canoa/barquinho foi bem diferente, vimos alguns campos de arroz e vários trabalhadores. Interessante. Mas o que nos irritou é que todo o caminho digamos "de volta" para o embarcadouro a senhora que  estava conosco no barco ficou tentando vender coisas. Ok oferecer, mas à partir do momento de que falamos não, acho que basta, afinal estávamos pagando o passeio, não foi de graça. Mas ela ficava nos puxando e oferecendo, isto irritou um pouco afinal queríamos ver a paisagem e tirar fotos.

We paid 120,000 dong per person for the entrance fee and 150,000 dong for a boat (for 2 people) - a total of 195,000 dong per person. The boat ride was about 2 hrs and I got bored halfway. Don't get me wrong, the view was very nice but I guess the ride was just too long. Our rower was a lady with her toddler and they sang children songs on the way I think, which was very cute. It was also a plus that she did not have goods to sell in the boat, though she demanded a 20,000 dong/person tip at the end of the ride (which we were happy to give for her rowing effort).We arrived at about 130PM, just right before all the tour groups come in. Tip: this is a good place to buy the Vietnamese hats, they go for 20,000 dong only. Very helpful too to protect yourself from the sun while on the boat ride.

We loved the view on this trip. We took a boat along with many others tourists it was very busy! The mountains and caves are beautiful. The woman who try to sell us food at the turn in the harbour were annoying. We bought drinks and fruit for our guides but that wasent enough. They tried to sell us stuff they made and before the tour ended they asked for a tip! They kept on asking for money for about 20 minutes it kinda ruind our boattrip.You can also rent a bike in that area and drive by the mountains ans caves to enjoy the view, we would advice people to do that instead of the boattrip.

We took a private tour to tam coc and ancient capital for the day. When we arrined at tam coc, we were put into small boats, 3 in 1 boat, 2 in the other.our tour guide told us we had to tip the rowers at the end of the trip and also that we would be harassed to buy things from other boats and our rowers.in our boat we had no trouble at all except for right at the end before he let us out he asked for money, which we already had there waiting for him.our friends in the other boat were harrassed from  start to finish. He even pulled over at one stage to  show them how deep the water was then asked them for money, pure intimidation. Saying all that, they work mighty hard rowing with their hands and feet up and down the river. Just be aware.

Maybe it was my fault because I went to Trang Anh first before this one. I had a bit of heightened expectations since this one's more famous. However I was disappointed with the new prices imposed and the rules for foreigners where you cannot share your boat with more than two people. Not friendly at all. Plus they cut the tour and closed the farther side of the lake. What a trap. The selling and tip solicitation didn't improve my experience in the end,

Unas vistas preciosas, pero está masificado por cientos de turistas. Las barqueras intentan venderte todo tipo de artículos, refrescos, comida, fotografias, llegando a importunar y molestar. Hay otras alternativas a Tam Coc mejores y más cercanas.

Wat mij een paar nachten geleden is overkomen wens ik niemand toe. Het was mijn derde nacht in dit hostel en de 2 nachten ervoor sliepen de mensen van het hostel ook al in de vrije bedden in dezelfde dorm. Deze nacht sliep de hosteleigenaar in het bed dat haaks op die van mij stond. 's Nachts werd hij wakker, ging op z'n knieën zitten en maakte aanstalten om te gaan plassen richting mijn hoofd. Hij slaapwandelde omdat hij had gedronken die avond. Ik sprong op tijd uit m'n bed om niet vies te worden en ik weet niet of hij uiteindelijk heeft geplast of niet. De volgende ochtend ontkende de beste man, zei dat ik loog en bood niet z'n excuses aan. Ik had meteen het licht aangedaan en de rest van de kamer heeft ook gezien wat er gebeurde. Ik kreeg met moeite 1/3 van m'n geld terug.Het jammere is dat de omgeving prachtig is en het leuk is om de omgeving vanuit Tam Coc ipv Ninh Bin te bezoeken. In Tam Coc is dit het enige hostel jammer genoeg, probeer niet in de kamer beneden te slapen, maar de dorm boven of slaap in een hotel. Bovendien is de beste man als hij niet dronken is best aardig en behulpzaam. En de prijs kwaliteit van dit hostel is ook in orde.

We chose to visit Tam Coc by hiring a driver to take us directly to the river boat. We're not a fan of temples and didn't see the value in wasting half a day looking at an old relic. We weren't interested in lunch at the restaurant (serving goat) that most tour companies want to take you to as all reports were less than positive. Plenty of other food options available at the dock. We left Hanoi at7:30am and the trip took 2 hours ( company told us us 1hr 30). Boat trip took 1.5 hrs only. No hawkers annoyed us at all. At turn around point, we bought a drink for our boat pilot (US$1) and everyone happy. Scenery was nice, smog/ haze was evident so no bright sunshine. No rice fields were seen during the three cave trip, just water and limestone mountains.The car trip was boring, no nice scenery along the way, just concrete buildings, some rice fields (not attractive though) and endless traffic.We paid US$40 per person for car trip, 80,000Dong ea for boat ticket plus 100,000 for boat (paid at the site).Car driver stopped for toilet break but it was a con to have us spend money at outlet for souvenirs to support handicapped children. He tried it again on way back, this time wanting a coffee. We said no and he kept driving. Lovely items for sale but very inflated prices. Some handy food available.

Довольно раскрученное туристическое место! Если вы попадете, когда приезжают туристические автобусы, бывает очередь на пристани. Поездка по реке среди гор и природных пещер занимает около двух часов. Места вокруг красивые, пещеры небольшие, посмотреть интересно. Но под конец становится скучновато. Напрягает развод в конце с продуктами и наглое вымогательство чаевых гребцами, поаккуратнее.

Cette balade est plaisante quoi qu'un peu longue. Un conseil, faites la avant d'aller sur la vraie baie d'Halong sinon vous ne serez certainement pas aussi époustouflés. Attention au soleil car les barques ne sont pas couvertes.

We visited this on a day trip with one of the many excersion companies, it wasn't run brilliantly, the lunch was terrible in a big buffet place, that you wouldn't touch! There seemed to be a lot of tours all doing exactly the same.The pagoda was impressive but very much like all of the others. The boat trip however was worth the long drive there and back, a lady (or man) took you down the river, with the most stunning scenery, scenery that you don't experience from the road, past rice crops where people are picking, the down to a cave, it really is beautiful.Be warned there are ALOT of people that want your money here, they'll try and sell you anything, just say no politely and you might have to repeat it more than several times!

The setting is very tranquil and beautiful, a photographer's delight.  We went at a time when it wasn't so crowded, so at times we felt like the only people on the river.  The guide who drove us to this place had fore warned us of the sales pitches that we would encounter on the actual tour.First they will insist on taking pictures of you as you get underway at the beginning of the tour and then try to sell you those pictures at the end of the tour at an elevated price.  I guess that's not so unusual of any touristy place.Secondly the person rowing boat (typically a woman but not always) will keep a box with various embroideries which they will try to sell you on the way back.  They claim to have hand made everything themselves, but some of the designs look like common cheap factory works. They can be somewhat pushy with the sales.The most annoying sales pitch came at the turn around point in the tour.  A bunch of ladies will be waiting in boats or on shore if you go onshore, trying to sell you snacks and soft drinks.  They are quite pushy, and after refusing several times, they changed tactics and appealed to us to buy a drink for the rower.  It's a total scam.  Even though I speak Vietnamese, they still tried to pull this on me.  It was apparent to me that they lady selling the drinks and the one rowing the boat were actually related and colluding with each other.In any case the pushy sales really spoiled this tour for us.  It's a shame because the setting is just post card perfect, and this was off putting even though we had been warned about the sales.

We were picked up from our hotel promptly by Nikki (English name) from Galaxy Halong tours. It is a 2.5 hour bus trip to and from Tam Coc. During the length of this trip we were provided with little to no information on the surrounding area. We stopped for a break on the way down at an over priced gift shop where the staff stalk you around the store. When arriving at the temple our tour guide stayed on the bus while we joined another tour! The boat ride was lovely with great views but our boat rowers spent more time trying to sell us bags/ wallets than they did rowing!! They then demanded a tip at the end. This put a dampened the experience. Our your guide spent more time on Facebook/ sleeping than he did giving us any information hence the glorified taxi ride.

I visited Van Long Nature Reserve and Tam Coc and vastly preferred Van Long. I had read reviews before visiting Tam Coc and was prepared for some hassle, but found it to be even more absurd than I'd expected. You pay for the row boat, but you are basically held captive while you are guilted into buying crap the entire way. Half way through you have to buy food for your rower, and then there is a good chance that your rower will try to sell their own stuff on the way back. It's pretty, but the whole set up is so annoying you can't really enjoy it. Van Long is just as beautiful without any of the hassle.

We went on a rainy day but that did not spoil the fun for us.  As what was reviewed by others, mid stream we were asked to buy drinks for the rowers (we had both sisters) but declined as I wanted to give them a bigger tip.  However instead we were sold souvenirs, that was still fine though we knew it was dearer than the price at markets but wanted to tip the rowers anyway.  However when we came on shore the photographer wanted 500,000 for the photos she took of us.  We refused but she chased after us and lowered the price to 250,000 and she followed us all the way and I felt a bit sorry for her so bought and ended up throwing away those terrible shots as we looked terrible drenched in the raincoat.  Would have been a nicer experience if there was no 'forced' selling or buying.  I won't have bought those photos had I read the review earlier.  I felt emotional and I bought something I did not even wanted.

The main feature of Tam Coc is that it such a different experience. Being rowed through the shallow water in a 'sampan' is pleasant enough and the scenery is beautiful but I would advise you not to do it in the middle of the day as it is dreadfully hot and there is no shade apart from when you are in the caves. Our trip was ruined by the lady rowing our boat as she kept asking for a big tip then when we reached the turn-around at the end she greedily bought several other items along with the cold drink we bought for her. I preferred Trang Anh. We'll be going back in October so it will be interesting to compare with the last trip.

we paid 180.000 dong for each included the 80.000-entrance fee and 100.000-boat trip. so expensive in compare to the other places, the boat trip was valued but the entrance fee was ridiculous. we prefer Tran An

I enjoy the great view of tam coc.. We was going with the boat and with bicycle.. In the boat, they force us to buy something, first some lady in other boat force us to buy a drink, not for me but for the woman who drive my boat.. And then the woman force us to buy something from her, if we didn't buy something she didn't want to go back to the check point.. But the price didn't make sense, it 10 times more expensive than in Hanoi.. After that she also ask for the tips.. And my friend in the other boat had a same condition.. I also buy a sugar cane, after i pay for it, they ask me to pay for it twice.. What a crazy people..

Visited Tam Coc on an organised tour, it was a nice day out and the scenery along the boat ride is lovely, but the constant pressure to buy stuff gets abit tiring and annoying. You almost have no choice - during the boat and the bike ride vendors were set up with large SLR Nikon cameras taking photos of you, whether you want them too or not! As soon as you get off the boat/bike they are in your face trying to sell you the photos which they have already printed out. Bearing in mind that they can hardly sell your photo, already in hardcopy, to anyone else they offer you it for 10,000 dong whether you want it or not, literally not giving in until you pay them at least that for it. Which is quite frustrating because you would think that trying to avoid a photo being taken of you would be enough to warrant them not printing it - but no they are persistent. And when it comes down to it you feel like its better just to give in and to pay 30p for them to leave you alone. I understand that this isn't alot of money to me and maybe to them, but you feel cheated into buying something you didn't even consider wanting and when there are lots of them and this happens with everything you physically do it becomes abit over bearing! I went recently during the summer, (not peak season) and from other photos (and amount of parking and large restraunt) I have seen prior to the trip it was much quieter than it could be - but still felt touristy - so my advice would be to go out of season or at least be prepared to fork out for photos, drinks for the rowers and tips!

I would definitely rate this place excellent, however, the overall experience about the place is not only about scenery you see, but also about local people you meet, and this really left us disappointed.We spent around 12 days in Vietnam (Halong, Sapa, Hanoi) , and found people very friendly, honest, caring, helping. However, Ninh Binh area ruined all this positive view just in one day. It's partly our fault because my wife had big notes of money, and as appears you need to be very attentive with "0" in the notes as they look very similar. In one case, my wife gave a bigger note than she thought for the drinks in the restaurant across the street from the tourists boats, and of course she didn't get back the change only to realize this later. Another woman sold Vietnamese hat to my wife, and had been asked 3 or 4 times to give correct change and not to cheat. The biggest scam happened in the boat. At the furthest point of boat trip (where boats turn around) there were several boats selling fruits, water, and other kind of snacks. we decided to get a couple of bananas and peace of pineapple. when we got to the seller boat, she asked to pass some stuff (I remember it was redbull can, oreo cookies, smth else) to the rower which I did. only later I realized what is all about when she asked to pay for everything, and that I am buying treats for the rower. Of course, she already had our big note, and we end up paying. Probably we could refuse, but this happened very quickly, and all the good decisions come after the fact :) besides that, the seller like in the other cases tried to cheat on change as well, and wife had to ask several times to give correct change. forgot to mention, all those rower treats cost us $15! on the way back the rower itself still wasn't shy to offer hers souvenirs, and even ask for the tip which I, of course, refused feeling already ripped off.Even after such unpleasant experience, I still suggest to visit Ninh Binh area, just be careful with local vendors, and watch "0" on the notes. The boat trip is really relaxing, but stay away from the boats which sells goods. We also had a short bike tour, which I really enjoyed, but felt it could be much more interesting if we did it on our own, not with a guide and group. In this case you would be able to stop and take photos and relax at any time, and views are really amazing.Some people say it's like Halong on land, and not worth the trip if you been or planning to visit Halong, however, I reckon it still worth the trip as it does provide some different views and feelings. Have a safe trip!!!

Ich stimme mit der Bewertung vom 1. Mai absolut überein und schliesse mich in allen Punkten dieser Bewertung an. Was ich noch hinzufügen möchte, istan positivem als aller erstes die wahnsinnige aussicht. Tam Coc wird nicht umsonst als Halong Bay auf dem Land bezeichnet, es ist einmalig schön und sehr sehenswert wenn man in Hanoi ist!!!!Unser guide war freundlich und gut drauf, hat super englisch gesprochen. Was nicht gut war, ist-dass man genötigt wird einen festen Betrag Trinkgeld (20.000 VND pro Person) für die Bootsfahrer zu zahlen, obwohl die Bootsfahrt bereits bezahlt ist (wie auch schon richtig in der Bewertung vom 1. Mai angegeben ist). -auf dem hin- und rückweg haben wir 20-30 min. an derselben raststätte gehalten, was bei einer langen busfahrt von 3h unnötig ist.-es gab eine kakerlake beim buffet im restaurant, die da munter ihres weges kletterte.-der fahrer ist auf dem rückweg nicht besonders sicher gefahren. -als wir von der bootstour wiederkamen und alle mitglieder unserer gruppe wieder am vereinbarten treffpunkt waren, mussten wir eine stunde warten, bis wir endlich fahren konnten!!!!!!!-insgesamt muss ich leider sagen, dass die vielen aufdringlichen verkäufer und nötigungen, trinkgeld zu zahlen, die wunderbare tam coc erfahrung stark trüben. Fazit: wenn ihr es euch leisten könnt, bucht euch ein auto mit fahrer und guide dorthin und kommt vor 12h oder nach 16h (da sind die reisegruppen dann durch und die idylle mehr, was sie eigentlich ist).

We a group of 5 couples arrived at Hanoi airport in the morning and we planned to take night train to Sapa. We make arrangement with Ethnic Travel Agency to visit Tam Coc since we have plenty time before our night train. It was quite a distance from Hanoi town to Tam Coc. We had our nice homecooked lunch in a village.The boat ride at Tam Coc was a disappointment, nothing much along the way. After the ride we were harassed by the people who took our photos and tried to sell to us.

Tot le matin, jusqu'a 10 heure, il n'y a pas grand monde donc la ballade en bateau est agréable. Prévoir 130 000 VND par personne pour un bateau de 2 personnes. 1 heure de ballade en fonction de la rapidite du rameur qui vous demandera de lui donnez un petit pourboire ou d'acheter de l'artisanat. Et si vous refusez il ne sera pas content, ce qui gache un peu la ballade, somme toute rapide et touristique... L'excursion au site de Mua cave permet d'avantage d'apprecier la splendeur de ces pitons rocheux. A preferer donc.

ニンビンと言えばタムコックということで、土産物などの押し売りの悪評も頭に入れて赴きました。ぼられないことばかり考えていては楽しい旅も委縮してしまいそうですが、一応、ぼったくりと押し売り対策について書いておきます。まず最初に気をつけたいのはチケット売り場です。チケットを買うのはなるだけ現地ガイドにお願いした方がいいと思います。私のお願いしたベトナム人のバイク運転手兼ガイドによると外国人がチケットボックスに行くと大抵チケット代を上乗せされてしまうとのことで、私は彼にお願いしてチケットを買ってきてもらいました。以下、舟に乗ってからの注意点です。景色を眺めながら小舟に乗っての1時間半の遊覧は確かに楽しいですが、後半は同じ道を下ってくるだけなので当然ながら飽きます。そして、Uターン地点で、ものすごい数の”売店ボート”を目にした瞬間、かなりげんなりとします。「あなたの疲れ切った漕ぎ手の女性にこの水とお菓子を買ってあげて！」と、決まり文句でしょうが激しく声を掛けられると、本人がいる手前、「いやです!」とはなかなか言いにくかったです。でも良く考えると漕ぎ手の女性に頼まれているわけでもないので、「あとで直接チップを払いますから結構です」と断るとすんなりと引き下がりました。（ニコニコしていると、どこまでも押し売りしてきますので、きっぱり断るのが大切だと思います）そして次の関門は、復路にもうひとつあります。それは、これまで静かに舟を漕いでいた中年女性（もしくは若い女性）が、突如として押しの強い売り子になることです。漕ぎ手たちの中には「漕ぎ手・兼・売り子」の人もいて、その人の舟に乗ると、帰り道でボートを止め、突然段ボール箱大の大きな箱を目の前に出してきます。中には、刺繍など布製品がびっしり入っていて、私の時は漕ぎ手の女性は家族写真のアルバムを見せ、「この子たちのために働いている。買ってください」と言ってきました。もちろん必要なら買ってもいいですが、私は不要だったので、「あとであなたにチップを払いますから、ボートを漕いで元の場所に戻ってください」と断りました。すると、突如として漕ぎ手はチップの値段交渉をし始めるのですが、最初にベトナム人のガイドから、「3万ドン～5万ドンが相場です。とても良くしてもらったと思ったら5万ドン払ってください」と言われていたので、ボートを降りる時に5万ドンを渡しました。舟の上では、しきりに「チップとして20万ドン渡してくれないと嫌だ」というようなことをしつこく言ってきましたが、実際舟を降りるときに5万ドンを手渡すと、まったく怒った素振りもなく「ありがとう」と笑顔で受け取りました。つまり、20万ドンとは、「相場を知らない客だったらひょっとして払うかな？」とふっかけられた金額なので、舟の上で決して支払ってはいけません。きっと日本人に対する相場をまた吊り上げますので・・・。いろいろと書きましたが、もしこのようなやりとりが面倒だという人は、男性の漕ぎ手だとほぼこういうわずらわしさはないと思います。漕ぐのもものすごく速く、何グループもごぼう抜きして私たちが帰るまでに2往復くらいしている男性の漕ぎ手が何人もいました。押し売りに豹変するなどの煩わしさもなく、サクサクと景観を楽しんで帰っているようでした。「ちょっと急いでいる、男性の漕ぎ手を」と舟に乗る前に言ってみるのも手かもしれません。

tam Coc is the town where the best boat trip through the caves and cast ,limestone hills originates. There are some nice walks and rides to be had from tam Coc but other than the Viet bamboo restaurant it has nothing going for it as a town. We stayed here so that we could walk in the countryside and get on the river early and it did that. The hotels generally disappoint in tam Coc but hey life is not perfect:-)

Tam Coc’s evocative image is seen on most Vietnam tourist brochures featuring tall karst rock towers covered in jungle foliage, like crumbling ancient temples they are an unusual sight. The karsts lie in amongst lush green rice paddies while tourists can float along a snaking river on a private sampan, admiring the views.  Unfortunately, in my opinion much of Tam Coc’s serenity is spoilt by tourism & the shameless greed of boat rowers, drink-hawkers, souvenir sellers & tour guides who no longer grow rice & raise cattle in the area but flock to day-tripping tourists like flies on a warm day. The tour bus pulls up at the small river jetty where we are assigned a sampan boat & rower by our guide. It’s great to see many of the rowers are women who can earn a living in rural Vietnam, they wear the iconic conical hats while working in this small piece of heaven. The scenery of the surrounding country is truly peaceful, bamboo shoots grow along the riverbank which look like rice, lotus flowers bloom everywhere & are naturally beautiful in the wild. We rowed into the shade of caves, which offered a cool break from the blazing sun & saw dead stalagmites, stalactites & examples of water erosion which had carved out the cave over thousands of years. Once we reached the end, turned around & came back out of the caves it all began….another boat approached our bow like a pirate ship trying to board our vessel. The most common scenario is a woman rowing her sampan selling drinks.Using a well well-practised line she urged us to buy our rower a drink. Out of courtesy, I turned to my rower & offered him a drink from my own bottle. They were both horrified & shocked, I wonder why... It is well known here that rowers sometimes sell the drinks back to the women later for money to complement their income, this is not a problem for me, when its business its survival of the fittest, however she wouldn’t leave us in peace until another boat appeared, she quickly put her pirate hat back on & off she went. Before disembarking, our rower then put out his hand & rubbed his fingers for a tip. I know that our rower is paid a fee from the tour company. Knowing it wasn’t necessary I offered him a 20k VND tip anyway. I was surprised by his nasty scowl! I kept my money but his whinging all the way back to the jetty was so unpleasant that it would’ve been worth paying him twice as much just to shut up. Maybe I offended him & 20k VND was way too much as I know their fee for each trip down the river is approx. 30k VND. Then on dry land, an amateur  photographer came running out holding pictures he took of me earlier from a distance….it’s like a Viet Cong ambush! 3 assaults in five minutes, no wonder America didn’t want to fight on.I think our tour & most others are poorly run. Most are exactly the same & rush people in & out like a production line. In hindsight I would have found more satisfaction doing it all by myself, in particular I’d chose the Trang An Grottoes over Tam Coc, especially if I had the time. Trang An is the alternate river nearby. Paying $30 for a standard tour sounds cheap but it’s even cheaper by yourself. If I could go back in time I’d catch a bus from the My Dinh bus terminal in Hanoi or pay the money & hire a driver for the day. I can imagine capturing more scenic photos in the early morning or late afternoons. More importantly it avoids the mid-morning & afternoon lunch-time period where day tours from Hanoi arrive & crowd the river, contributing to the feeding frenzy for foreign dollars to the once simple rural people who have lost much of their innocence & charm. What used to be something simple, that beautiful natural setting much like from Pearl S. Buck’s, The Good Earth, is more & more like a modern day tourist trap.

En janvier il y a peu de monde . Nous avons démarré la ballade en barque à midi , avec un temps magnifique , nous étions seuls ou presque . Un rameur souriant au départ , qui s'arrête au bout de 5 mn pour prendre une rameuse , qui n'arrête pas de causer avec son collègue , impossible de profiter de la sérénité du lieu . Le retour se fait par le même endroit , même si le paysage est très beau cela parait long d'autant que l'assise est très inconfortable . Et puis la rameuse déballe tous ses napperons made in China  , il faut acheter quelque chose sinon elle ne vous lâche plus jusqu'à l'arrivée . Même en achetant 2 napperons cela ne suffit pas , on vous demande de payer une boisson et on vous demande un pourboire plus gros que celui donné à l'arrivée . Plus le moindre sourire quand vous débarquez , cette balade laisse un sentiment désagréable hélas . Ce qui est super à Tam Coc , ce sont les ballades en vélo et à pied le long de la rivière , là on profite pleinement de ce superbe environnement , il faut y dormir 2 nuits , c'est un vrai plaisir sous le soleil bien sûr .

Den Ausflug hatten wir in hanoi über eine Agentur gebucht. Leider hatten wir festgestellt, dass die Preise je nach Agentur bei der gleichen Leistung variieren. Das Buffet zum Lunch war mittelmäßig bis schlecht.. Die Bootsfahrt mit den Rudern war auch nicht so spannend, da wir Reisfelder erwartet hatten, die nicht vorhanden waren. Die Höhlen waren ganz interessant zu sehen. Der Ruderin haben wir ein sehr üppiges Trinkgeld (200.000VND) gegeben, da es eine alte Frau war und dies wirklich ein anstrengender Job ist. Eine Frau in diesem alter wäre in westlichen Ländern in Rente. Wir verstehen nicht diese Diskussion um Trinkgeld. Abends beim Diner wird auch ordentlich Geld oder bei der buchung der flüge ausgegeben aber bei dem Tip regen sich die Urlauber auf! Ein oder zwei Euro müssten doch drin sein?!Es stimmt dass es eine touristenabzocke ist, aber das Geld landet bei den Reichen in Hanoi. Die Ruderinnen kassieren nur einen Hungerlohn.Am besten haben uns anschließend die Reisfelder gefallen. Es war wirklich sehr interessant die Pflanzer beim Anbau zu beobachten! Daher 3 Punkte.

I wrote a similar review for Halong Bay yesterday and the same goes for Tam Coc. We booked one day tour from Hanoi with Joy Travel, a middle range on fir 27 USD.  On this tour we were pcked up the last so it was supposed to be our turn to get the additional seats. I am not twenty years old any more and this is not my first trip over the world, so Naturaly I don't like it if somebody is making a full of me and Certainly I want to get what I paid for. And in this case  I didn't get the proper seat and of course I was not willing to take an additional one unless I get a discount. The guide was only able to tell that this tour was very full. And they tell you this all the time. They just like to take the money, but they don't produce the service. They don't have this in their dictionary yet and I doubt they will ever get it. At the end some young guys gave us teir seats, because they just wanted to start the trip. I mean ok and thanks(we took the seats), but as long as there are people who just don't care it won't get better.And again there on the bus there were people who booked three different categories of the tour and at the end we all got the same again.And Tam Coc itself - very nice scenery, but or tour started in some part of the river where noone else was. In a way this is good, because there was no hassle with paddlers and buying drinks and other stuff, but on the oher hand I can not loose the feeling, that we didn't get what we were supposed to.Our paddler insisted at he end on a tip of course. She got t, but to be onest I would have gave her more, if she woldn't be so shamless.In short - my three points go to the scenery, everything else is a big big scam. But this is like it is here up to the north and I am just wondering if other cities will be like this as well. I was four years ago inSaigon and Mekong Delta and it wasn't so bad.Don't book anythig with Joy Travel!

Tam Coc is a beautiful ride and we went first thing in the morning which was not so busy by reading other reviews.  Our guide told us to not buy the drink that is pressured half way through ride.  The pressure was so intense we gave in to the $1USD for our rower's drink who had become friendly with us.  There are other boats trying to sell souvenirs but we just kept saying no and then they move on.  But the most aggressive on the water was the photographer who immediately started taking photos of us, I agreed on $1USD for a photo but was talked into 10 photos as we had our sons on another boat and decided it would be nice to have some photos of them.  We asked to see them at the end of the ride......WRONG.  The photographer rows back during your ride and hands you the photo album (all photos are in enclosed plastic sleeves and this makes it harder to discard unwanted photos) and everything becomes very hasty and rushed and the pressure is back on.  Somehow the English is not so good now but there are now 20 photos in the sleeves and bam it's more money.  The photos are over exposed but you feel a little more vulnerable on a boat and don't feel like pushing the point in case you end up a lot cooler than you anticipated.  Of course our rower wants more money as a tip, we could live with this as she had just rowed for nearly 2hrs and the scenery is so worthwhile.  Just when you think its all done and dusted after disembarking from boat a stranger approaches us with more photos of us on our boat trying to sell the 'better' photos that were taken expecting of course that I would want these. This photographer has every trick up her sleeve.  There was no way I was buying one more photo and was harrassed for some time before we left.  It is unfortunate as we did love the scenery and would have given this trip a higher rating if it had not been for the aggressive photographer.

Tam coc is a lovely place but can get VERY busy. I would suggest a boat trip in the morning or late afternoon/eve. This way you can avoid the crowds of tourists that arrive around lunch who have come on day trips from Hanoi. I would recommend renting a bike to cycle to see Bich Dong pagoda and the beautiful scenery to and from there.
If you want a sampan ride through karst outcrops and through caves choose Trang An.  We did both and enjoyed Trang An much more.  It is not as commercial as Tam Coc.

Wir waren in TamCok und haben über das Hotel eine Ruderpartie von ca. 1 1/2 Stunden gemacht. Die Landschaft ist wunderschön und die Ruderin war sehr fleissig und geschickt. So dass wir uns entschlossen haben ihr am Ende der Ruderpartie 1 Dollar Trinkgeld zu geben. Zu unserem Entsetzen hat sie sich daraufhin lauthals und wütend beschwert, dass wir ihr so wenig geben.So etwas unverschämtes habe ich auf meinen ganzen Reisen noch nicht erlebt.

A lot less interesting that Trang An, too many tourists, very short trip compare to Trang An. I went to Tam Coc after Trang An which I did first thing in the morning, when there was no people at all. Maybe that is the reason I was not that impressed with the place.

More for the geology and geomorphology enthusiast to really appreciate the wonder. The lay tourists will just be awed by some of the rock formations and the peaceful tranquility of the river. However, if there are crowds. it will mar the experience. Go either very early in the morning or late in the afternoon.

The trip to Tam Coc was long but the Alova Gold Cruises bus was comfortable, the driver was good and our guide Sunny was very interesting. First stop was to visit a couple of temples. Would have been more interesting if the guide had given the tour round the temples and shared some information. This also may have lessened the irritation of the very pushy locals trying to sell their goods. Lunch was good with a large selection on the buffet menu. Boat trip down the river through the limestone caves and mountains was beautiful. Very unfortunate that our boat rower stopped half way to try and persuade us to buy their goods. Rather spoilt the tranquility of the trip as it was the most beautiful scenery. The bike ride was great fun. Beautiful views and interesting going through the villages. Long day with lots of sitting but overall a good trip and great to experience the beautiful Vietnam scenery.

Nous avions dormi à l'hôtel Yen Nhi, à 5 min à pieds pour pouvoir être dès 9h30 sur les barques. La balade a duré presque 2 heures, à mon avis un peu trop long, car c'est un aller retour, et au retour on a déjà vu les paysages à l'aller.....en plus on est très mal installé sur les barques, mal de dos assuré!!! seul point positif, mon fils de 8 ans a pu ramer ce qui lui a permis de trouver le temps moins long...Le ou les rameurs ( nous ils étaient 2, le fils et la mère) sont un peu insistants pour leur acheter des objets. de plus, la mère rameuse n'a pa cessé de bavarder avec la rameuse de l'autre barque à côté ( barque avec nos amis), ce qui a gaché la visite, car c'est site calme et reposant.....Par contre à la fin de la balade, nous avons donné aux rameurs des produits récupérés dans les hôtels (brosses à dents, peignes, savons..) ainsi que des stylos que nous avions apporté de France exprès.L'après-midi nous avons fait une balade de 2 h en vélo avec notre guide autour des rizières : bien plus sympa!!!!!

Nulla di particolare. Un bel giretto in barca con signora che nel mentre cerca anche di vederti qualche cosa. Forse perché sono giunto qui con una escursione da Hanoi ma tante ore forse non ne valgono la pena. Certo lo scenario e' carino con tre grotte da attraversare ma nulla più.

This is where you get taken when Halong Bay is closed, and it is very pretty and peaceful along the clear river, covered with lotus flowers.  You take a 2 hour boat ride and going under the mountains is novel and adds to the experience.  At the turnaround point however, they pull up to other boats that want to sell you drinks, when you say no they ask you to buy the rowers something - they will pull a sad face and say "they very thirsty" - THIS IS A CON.  You will be asked to pay way over the odds for drinks and snacks they grab and do not want.  All 14 in our group had they same experience, once you've been fleeced, they head back and offer you the refreshments and do not touch it themselves.  If you are pulled in take the drinks, they just sit in the boat for them to repeat the trick later.  They will then spend 15 minutes heckling you to buy bags, T-shirts, table clothes and claim to have embroidered them themselves.  One of our group did buy something, but then the rest of the stock was thrown to the next boat for selling and she realised again this is not sewn by the nice lady on your boat.  Final scam - your guide will tell you to tip 20,000 VND to each rower, which is fine, but if you give it too early they will DEMAND more - ours wanted 100,000 VND each.  My advice, play deaf and only hand it over as you get out.  It's a real shame they are like this as it is otherwise a lovely thing to do, but everyone was left felling annoyed and ripped off.  We then hired bikes and cycled to the temple, this was worth it and the slight breeze as you went along was lovely (no need to upgrade from the basic road bike - it's completely flat and not that far).  At the temple again it's very pretty and the scramble up to the top of the mountain is rewarded with fantastic views (but no breeze!).  Carry water and you camera with you and wear trainers or sports sandals, and some bug spray  - I got eaten.  The latter really made the day.  The trip would have been very good but for the rowers behaviour.

We were unlucky on our trip here as the weather was atrocious, it rained all day. The place itself is lovely and scenic, not the best ever or the most beautiful place in the world but very nice with opportunities to take great photos. Our experience was marred however by the short sightedness and greed of the hawkers. Whilst I understand they are poor and its only a few dollars here and there, I still believe their behaviour to be unacceptable. We, like everyone, were met at the halfway point by a woman hawking drinks we should buy for the guide, we said no and then no again (our guide also brought along her 12 year old daughter for additional guilt trip, I imagine). I then turned away and pretended I couldn't hear them. Then 5 minutes later the boat came to a stop and they wanted us to buy some bags. Again we refused. And refused again.Finally she asked for a tip of 100,000 - irritated beyond belief we reduced our original tip to less than half. About 1km from shore she asked us to handover the tip and I refused and said she wouldn't get anything until we were returned and it would be small and that's it. I got off the boat and begrudgingly gave her 2 dollars for myself and my partner. No thank you or any mark of gratitude other than looking like she had sucked a lemon.We went here as our cruise on Halong Bay was cancelled - more's the pity. Go and enjoy but beware of these scams and be firm.
The sampan tour is ok but nothing spectacular apart from the 30 to 60m long caves you cross. Surroundings ok but not really a feeling like in Halong Bay.

Restaurants echt heel matig. Roeien wel ok, je wordt wel 2 keer geacht om iets te kopen bij je roeier en een soort verkopers op andere boten. Je voelt je schuldig en koopt toch iets.. Ik zou in tamcoc zeker niet langer dan 2 dagen blijven. Fietsen is opzich wel grappig! Is een schooltje en een tempel in de buurt.

We've traveled around SE Asia a lot, but the hawkers here are of a special breed. There is a an agreement between the boat drivers and the boat hawkers. Part way through the journey, the hawkers pull up to your boat and try to get you to buy a drink for your boat driver. If you do, the driver may just give the drink right back, and split your cash (we saw it happen to a boat near us)! If you say no, the boat driver just sits there and lets the hawkers go at you, even if you ask to leave! The second attempt to get your money is when the boat driver stops paddling and tries to sell you stuff. They then bombard you with guilt and questions. They act like you didn't already PAY them to take the boat in the first place! We had fully planned to give our boat driver a tip, but after this treatment, we thought twice.This horrible business practice almost wrecked the beauty of Tam Coc. We had to really concentrate on keeping calm and enjoying the natural beauty.

I went to both Tam Coc and Trang An for boat rides on the same day (since boat rides around the limestone caves were almost about all you could do in Ninh Binh). In comparison, Tam Coc was quite a tourist trap. We arrived at about 10 am and were one of the earlier tourists, so we strolled around a little while before we saw many tour buses arriving, after which we hurried onto one of the boats to prevent getting trapped in the tourist mass.The first half of the boat-ride was scenic, but as everyone else would say, the boats will arrive at this dead-end where other boats selling drinks / snacks will sail up to you and push you to buy something. The worst was when they started to guilt-trip you into buying drinks for the rowers on your boat. It wasn't the best experience, and following that on the way back, the old man on our boat started pulling out souvenirs of woven cloth, little keychains etc which we politely turned down on multiple occasions. On the way back, he also made an elaborate show of coughing and massaging his arms as though he was extremely tired, and as we expected, he requested for a tip at the end of the trip. We gave him a 50,000 VND note and he pointed to the other girl on the boat (possibly his daughter) and asked for some tip for her as well. We walked off telling him that it was for both of them.The sights make the trip worth, but honestly I'd say that Tam Coc has comparable sights, without all the tourist business.

Better than our previous trip to Halong Bay, Temples and historic sites worth the visit on way to Tam coc, great serene beauty whilst you are on the river been paddled by some local lady, unfortunately great big industrial sites are just a stone throw away and very visible. Be warned, and the tour guide told us this as we left Hanoi in the morning, the same ladies that row you down the river expect or try to con you into tipping them half way and then again at the end of the journey, the cold drink and ice cream sellers ,are all in with them on the deal, its a little annoying that all this has been spoiled by greed.

I visited Tam Coc in May, 2013. It is very beautiful. There are green rice fields mixed with gold, makes a pristine beauty and incredibly poetic. I saw the farmers harvesting, really interesting. But not as some info i read that it takes 2 hours for sailing, the truth is just 1 hour.One more things, do not forget to visit Thai Vi temple. You will wide bicycle through a small winding road among fields, mountains. Like a landscape painting!!!

I visited Tam Coc in early April with 12 other visitors and again in early June with 6 visitors.  The place was beautiful as always.  The attraction here was to ride on a boat to pass through green hills on the side of the river and also 3 caves and then returned the same way.  Being foreigners, only 2 were allowed to board 1 boat.  There was a boatman/lady giving us a ride.  As we were on the boat ride, some locals took photos for us.  Seeing that they may be able to take pictures that we could not take ourselves, we welcomed their service.  And then at the final caves, there were many boats selling snacks and soft drinks.  The hawkers asked us to buy soft drinks for the boatlady saying that she was tired.  This was clearly a manipulation tactic to exploit our empathy.  We had to buy a can of soft drink for the boat lady.  And then mid-way back, the boat lady stopped to ask us to buy from her embroidered cotton products.  If they be good products, we would have bought something.  However all we were offered were low quality products that we would be happy to keep at home.  Fortunately we were not forced to buy as some one team members were.   After landing, those photographers popped up to show us the pictures they took.  They did not charge high for the photos and they also put all photos in plastics.  Problem was, in general they did not take good pictures.  I tried to buy some of the pictures but they were not satisfied.  It's until we had bought all the photos that they stopped following us wherever we went.  It's really annoying.  At my second visit, the feeling that this was the most commercial tourist area became even stronger.  The six of us were in 3 boats.  We tried to ask the boatmen to keep together so that we could talk with each other and took photos for each other.  However, one of the boat man did not listen and always insisted to go as fast as he could.  He just stopped where he pleased and then went back to shore in the shortest time.  I asked my friend on that boat to tip him in advance to see if he would be more co-operative.  However he just took the money and continued to do as he pleased.  There's just nothing we could do.  This reduced our interest in this place.Tam Coc was a pleasant place but those people working there were not.  I think I may not go to this place anymore unless situation turns better.

Did this as a day trip from Hanoi. The scenery was lovely but didnt really meet expectations. Go to Halong instead.  Our boat guide was down right rude and the touts are more annoying than most other places.

Maybe we expected too much on our way to Tam Coc. But a little bit disappointed when we found the spot. The picture in advertisement was too beautiful than the reality. But luckly we could still enjoy the trip.During sailing through the grains, we enjoyed the nice view and landscape.The people on boat also nice in the begining, offering help to take pics for us. But unfortunately we go there in the afternoon. With a sweltering sun we sailed around 2 hours.Better prepare sunblock etc if you dont want to get your skin burned.Overall that was a good place to visit.

Une expérience décevante. En période de vacances vietnamiennes, c'était la cohue sur le site. La procession de barques gâche le paysage et la magie du lieu. Nous nous attendions à 2 heures au fil de l'eau mais en à peine une heure, nous avions fait l'aller-retour fissa !
