Le harcèlement commercial est partout : au point de retour où les barques des vendeuses vous encerclent, sur l'eau (notre rameur avait une caisse pleine de souvenirs à nous proposer mais il n'a pas été insistant) et encore à l'arrivée, une vieille dame exigeant qu'on donne un pourboire au rameur !Suivez les conseils des guides : à faire tôt le matin ou en fin de journée, quand c'est plus calme.

A pleasant boat ride down the river between some pretty impressive limestone rocks and through some caves.  Not been to Halong Bay yet so can't compare.Was quite busy when we went (2pm), but I don't think that detracted too much from the experience because it was quite fun people watching - the other tourists and the boat paddlers, many of whom paddled with their feet.Be prepared for people trying to sell you drinks and snacks (for your paddler as well as yourselves, which made me give in.  sucker!) and also souvenirs from your paddler.  Just keep smiling and saying no and they give up eventually... and start asking for a tip instead!We were told to sit one behind each other in the boat, which made conversation difficult, but there were plenty of Vietnamese tourists sitting side by side, so I'm sure its fine to do that.  We made no stops and the scenery although nice is pretty repetitive, so two hours was quite a long trip without being able to easily chat.After the boat trip we took our bikes along the river to explore some of the fields and cemeteries we had seen from the boat and we also found a temple and a shrine in a cave.  We also met some local people working in the fields, and this really added to the experience. Even if you don't have bikes it would be nice to walk this way.

Go before 11am to avoid crowds. Nice scenery on the boat ride, it's about an hour long. Some of the rowers row with the feet which is a great sight. At the end of the river, women in boats will guilt you into buying your rowers some drinks and food (for their hard work!) - but don't be scammed - the rowers just give the food back or swap it for something cheaper. If you do buy them food/drinks, open it immediately and share it with them so they can't give it back. The rowers will try to sell you clothes and fabric on the boat ride too. Towards the end of the boat ride they will ask you for a tip and become very unfriendly if you don't tip them.
We went early , a mist on the water and no other boats .It was magic .The vendors on the water were barely ready for us so it was peaceful ,serene , and highly photogenic .

We did the boat ride through the three caves of Tam Coc and I must admit I was surprised how busy the place was. There was a circus of people getting put onto boats paddled by the locals. There are over 1500 boats and our guide said he only got to take 4 rides a month! not too sure about that. The ride itself was good and our guide was friendly. We got hassled by the photographers a bit but that was to be expected. Unfortunately at the turn around point, each boat was paddled to a vendors boat and we were pressured to buy something, if not for ourselves, then our hard worked boat guide. It was very unpleasant and tainted the whole trip. As if that wasn't enough, our guide later stopped the boat and asked us to buy a souvenir from him- he said we would regret it if we didn't... not sure exactly how he meant this statement but we were not impressed. Nearer to the end of the ride he hassled us for tips. We hadn't even docked the boat yet. The idea was good and the trip would have been very enjoyable if it wasn't for the pressure/tourist scene.

Oui c'est beau à voir, mais le jeux des rameuses de vous extorquer de l'argent à chaque virage, gâche vraiment tout. Je penses que ça ne vaut pas le détour, c'est l'attrape touristes.Je n'y retournerai pas.

Baie d'Halong terrestre, comme beaucoup de site au Vietnam, il est exploité par une société privée qui vous emmènera faire un tour d'~ 7 km si une barque.Malheureusement, et malgré l'achat des tickets( bon ok pas cher)Le rameur va certainement essayer de vous vendre des broderies, t-shirt et autre souvenir... Avant d'entamer les traditionnels plaintes;je suis beaucoup fatigué, très dur, beaucoup fatigué moi... Tout ça pour s'arrêter un peu avant de vous débarquer et supplier un pourboire. Normalement, mon amie et moi donnons un petit quelques choses (tout le monde doit bien essayer de s'en sortir...) mais demandé de la sorte nous a révolté! Nous avons refuse de lui donner quoique ce soit, du coup on s'est fait copieusement engueuler par les femmes  venues faire leur lessive sur le quai...Suite de quoi, il a fallu dire au photographe venu prendre des photos (non souhaitées)sur le trajet que nous ne voulions pas les lui acheter..Si c'était à refaire nous éviterions Tam Coc!Et je vous parle même pas des hôtels...

The trip started out so well since our paddlers offered to take our picture and the scenery was very beautiful. Because Tet was nearing there weren't many boats on the river. At the turn-around point however we were approached by a lady selling drinks from here boat and when we declined buying a drink for ourselves she awkwardly asked us if we wanted to buy a drink for our paddlers. On the way back out paddlers then tried to sell us things and just before reaching the end asked us for a tip! My advice is to read the page on Tam Coc scams to make sure you are well prepared before going here, as it really does ruin the trip. Come full, have extra sealed water bottles and snacks in your bag, and don't show any interest in souvenirs.

Tam Coc is stunningly beautiful, the scenery is spectacular but we opted out of the boat trip and decided to cycle the route. Granted you can't see the entire route but what you do see is fantastic! There are small shrines and temples along the way which you would miss on the boat and plenty of places to stop and dip your feet in the river. We noticed that the boats are something of a rip-off anyway for foreign tourists (Foreign: 2 people per boat Vietnamese: 4 people per boat) which was a bit annoying anyway so we decided to do it our own way! Would recommed Tam Coc and Ninh Binh as a cycling experience as a whole, I am certainly not an experienced cyclist so the mostly flat land was a giant bonus!

洞窟をボートでくぐっていくのと船頭さんが足を使って漕いで行くのが面白い。往復2時間。水は意外にもきれいで藻のような水草がいっぱい生えていました。船頭さんはほとんど英語が話せません。私のときは船頭さんのお母さんが一緒に乗ってきてほとんど漕がなかったのにしきりにチップを請求されたのには閉口しました。ベトナム人に聞いたらチップを払う必要はないと言っていたけど。。。ベトナムはなかなか後味良く去れないですね。

Het was leuk om er geweest te zijn. Het is een bijzonder stukje natuur waar je doorheen vaart. (je gaat met een bootje) Mijn advies is om zo vroeg mogelijk daar naar toe te gaan, omdat er dan nog niet zoveel toeristen zijn. De mensen die de bootjes varen proberen je onderweg van alles te verkopen. En ze varen naar een keerpunt waar ook een heleboel bootjes zijn met mensen die je wat willen verkopen. Als je daar doorheen kan kijken, dan heb je een leuk tripje.

we bought ticket to be on a sampan rowed by a lady, the trip took 2 hour with returning, on the boat, they tried to sell us some souvenirs as well as cakes and candy. We did not mind it. After finishing we stepped up to the wharf and she asked for tips. 20.000vnd is enough.

Nous avons d'abord fait van Long, qui est une réserve pure et encore préservée, dans des sampans en bambous. Ici à Tam coc, vous embarquez dans une des centaines dee barques en métal vert, en voyant des affiches publicitaires pour des pizzas et des hamburgers. Le site est magnifique, ensuite. Mais après avoir fait la Réserve de Van Long, on a difficile à être charmé autant...

Tam Coc is located three hours at thesouth of Hanoi. The tour is an enjoyable boat ride of a river that meanders through caves. There is also a small rice field, in the middle because it is a boat skimmed the surface of the water, while being comfortable to face the wind, I enjoy relaxing. Frustrating being bothered by hawkers. They do everything to be able to sell anything to us. Beautiful scenery, but we do not have much time to enjoy because of the carrier as scrambling to finish the journey as soon as possible...

as many others said: this was some years ago a really nice Vietnamese experience - 2012 it isn't yet a scam only because the authorities don't allow to charge more from the tourists (boat 80k, entry fee 30k/person).almost 2 hours in the sun (don#t forget you suncream or umbrella), yes the landscape but why by boat? you can do this with you bikes or hiking (if not too hot)Mixed feelings about the new hotel they build at the wharf - restaurants are empty already today - who should travel there? Chinese?

No photographs can prepare you for the beauty of coursing down meandering waterways flanked by majestic craggy formations .Lilies , cat tails and feathery reeds frame the waterways gracefully .Limestone karsts covered with dense vegetation and the steady dips of the oars sending ripples through green waters crowded with submerged swaying plants made us gasp at the majesty of nature .The crowds of noisy locals splashing each other and numerous boats to the left and right of you cannot be avoided as this is a very popular destination but if you are lucky , you may be able to escape the density if you choose the right time early in the mornings or later in the evening .Dart the photographers by pointing your camera or iPhone at them and ignore the boat lady when she asks you to buy embroidered tablecloths.. But they are not pushy and  if you say , "tip after "finish they seem to understand and leave you be . You should tip 40000 or 50000  dong as they do work hard with their arms and feet .  Like Trang An , avoid weekends and local holidays . Look out for the mountain goats and ducks and darting fish .Relax and take in the views and even though the vendors try tomake you buy a drink , just smile at your boatman and repeat the " tip later " promise and they will just go on without a word ,

Enjoy a serene river trip and a traditional meal in a local village, really? There is no doubt this tourist attraction was once a very beautiful and quiet part of the world. And I imagine, revered by the local inhabitants in times gone by. What a pity 'progress' can actually ruin the very thing that tourists are attracted to. We made the journey to Tam Coc as we didn't have time to get to Halong Bay, but it is not worth the trip from Hanoi – either in time or money. The sales pitch from our boat rowers to buy rubbish before, halfway through the boat trip and at the end ruined what we were expecting to be a peaceful water journey and left us with a bad feeling and a true sense of loss. Added to that was the significant over development on the shore of an unfinished resort (the village owner has run out of money), considerable amount of rubbish in the water, rock music blaring from a house up the river and a tasteless meal as part of our 'tour' confirmed our thoughts that this is now, just a complete money grab. Of course we realise that these village people are poor and any extra is a financial help but the tour operators are accountable and should pay better rates to the rowers. The local operators need to maintain the river and its environs in pristine condition, stop any further development on the shores and cut out the high pressure selling because ultimately the word will spread and tourists will stay away – perhaps then something will be done and Tam Coc can get back its mojo. Postscript: We gave our rowers a tip which was completely undeserved.

Well.... We red that this place is sort of "must see" when you are in Ha Noi and nearby but if you plan to visit JUST this place, than there is no sense. You will spend 1-2 hours on the boat rowboat with a lady who will take you to the place, where few locals will try to sell you overpriced water, fruit, chips, postcards... and if you don't want to, they will try to convince you to buy it for you rower... When we are coming back to the start point, our senior rower started to beg like:"...money tip sir, money tip lady....". It took 10 minutes to make 50 meters because of this. Doesn't matter you obviously paid for a trip before.If you can live with it, go there, enjoy and take really nice pictures!

We decided to do a day tour from Hanoi to the region of Nihn as we didn't have time to get to Halong Bay. Our tour guide was pleasant as was the driver who was amazing dealing with traffic and road conditions. The morning went fairly well with the usual stop off at souvineer sellers along the way. Arriving at Tam Coc we were loaded onto our boat in this very picturesque valley in between the limestone mountains. It was bliss as the rower skilfully manoeuvred our boat along the waterway passing through the naturally water carved tunnels, enjoying the peace and quiet after the noise of Hanoi. BUT..I had read the reviews of being coerced to buy drinks etc at the turn around point of the trip but certainly didn't expect to be bombarded for the next 10 minutes. I had indicated that I would prefer to give a larger tip then pay for drink or food that we didn't want. She then proceeded to push tshirts, caps, paintings. It just when on and on, it is very well to keep saying 'no' but guess what we are in a boat, you can't get away! Nearing the end of the boat trip you are asked for a tip, yes we did give more than expected only because we appreciated the effort required to row us.

My impression is too crowded. The boats feeling like as racing with each other to see who would finish first. I worried for his safety and the loss of desire for beautiful scenery. Too busy, too smelly, sewerage river and the hassle of people wanting cash from us spoiled it. Our Tam Coc experience was a major disappointment. Maybe next time we will try to Trang An Grottoes......

Excursion agréable mais sans plus... Une balade en barque de 1H30 vous fait traverser 3 petites grottes, dans le beau paysage de la baie d'Halong terrestre. Le site est toutefois hyper touristique, et on est sollicité en permanence (achat de souvenirs, de boissons pour vous ou pour la rameuse si vous n'en voulez pas, pourboire à la fin...) ce qui gâche un peu l'expérience.Mieux vaut aller voir les grottes voisines de Trang An, sans comparaison plus belles et impressionnantes.

This place is beautiful, You would need to take a bus tour to this place as you would need an English guide to explain to you about this place. The ride from Hanoi was a bout 3 hours. Once we reached we were asked by our guide to sit in a boat . The boat tour through the lake was an hour with beautiful scenes from either sides, just enjoy the the ride and enjoy the calmness of nature specially tourists coming from busy city life.After which you will need to walk up the hill to see different temples ( Beware lot of walking required). Lunch at this place may not be to your liking so remember to carry some dry snacks. ( Chips, fizzy drinks, and water available at small shacks).You can either walk or take a cable car to the main Cave. Take care if you afraid of heights! This tour is a whole day tour.Beware of:- Rowers demanding for more cash ( The ideal amount you can tip them if you wish is 1 dollar, sadly they are happier when you pay them a dollar rather than give them more in dongs)- Ladies selling postcards -

Our visit to Tam Coc was part of a customized private tour of 5 days. The area of tam Coc is lovely with a,l the water lilies and beautiful mountains. We did a bike ride in the area which was absolutely lovely.We also took the boat tour. I guess I have to agree with all the other reviews on this site: very nice relaxing tour with beautiful sights but that is partly ruined by the aggressive selling and hassling by the locals on the boats. It's hard work, rowing those tourists around for a couple of hours, but they don't give you any information n what you see ( they don't speak English) and they try to sell you s much and demand tips at the end.Its a shame but I fear Tam Coc will harm itself as a popular tourist destination this way.

We did a day trip toTam Coc from hanoi. I've been on all the day trips out of Hanoi  and HCM, and whilst the Tam Coc boat trip is worthwhile and something special, the blatant way that the trips are designed to rob you blind at every opportunity always detracts from the day's pleasure.First off, they always stop the bus for 20-30 minutes at a massively overpriced warehouse selling standard souvenirs at 5x the price they are in the market, and bottles of water for a similar 500% markup. The AC on our bus was broken, despite us having it confirmed by the agent Sinh Cafe that the bus would be AC. We all sat in the bus on plastic seats and sweated on each other for 3 hours each way.When on the boat trip, there are several attempts to sell more banal souvenirs whilst on the boat. Another boat then rows up to you offerring expensive drinks (20,000 small water when normally 3000 in shop or 5000 in a restaurant) - and trying to guilt-trip you into buying drinks and food at a hugely inflated price for the woman paddling the boat.  When 5 minutes away from the end of the trip, the paddling woman starts bleating on about a tip. I fully intended to give her a 20,000 tip, but having her moan on and on made me feel like hanging on to it. So - a great scenic boat trip, but with all the usual tiresome money-extraction exercises that seem to go with any organised tour in Vietnam.

While in Ninh Binh, I visited Tam Coc as part of a DIY day tour, which was fantastic, though Tam Coc was easily the most touristy part of this day, with the floating markets harassing us to buy things, and the boat guide begging for tips... Aside from that it really is quite lovely.Go early in the day, or later in the afternoon, to escape the blazing heat, and the hordes of tourists, as you will be largely exposed to the unrelenting sun at other times during the day. AMAZING photo ops,  incredibly scenic place, very peaceful for the most part, going into caverns, through mountains, and past huge karst formations along the way. Our guide was rowing with her feet for part of the trip, very novel approach, added to the whole experience, though again, a touristy one at that. I hired a guide to drive me around on a motorcycle for the day, $10, took me to many, many attractions along the way, Tam Coc being one of the last ones. We also checked out many temples, did a 2-hour boat cruise around the karst formations, stopped for lunch at a local place, took well over 250 pix on this tour, GREAT way to see what Ninh Binh has to offer. Entrance fees and food were extra, though with tips, the whole day was under $18USD, and well worth it. With your own motorcycle driver, you see what you want to see, for as long as you'd like to see it, no pressure, no HUGE groups to contend with either. LOTS of freedom to explore at your own pace, and my guide spoke ok English, enough to get by.Ninh Binh is not on the radar as yet, great place to check out without having to deal with tons of tourists - except at Tam Coc - very affordable, chill place to spend a day or 2. Highly recommended.

I should have taken more notice of previous reviews on TA.  This started off to be a beautiful day, being rowed through the rice paddies and through the caves.  It was then we were approached by sellers in boats wanting us to buy things, then as we went through the last cave another boat approached us and suggested we buy drink and biscuits for our rowers.  We declined and offered our rowers fresh unopened water bottles.  From there the atmosphere changed and I felt we were "hurried" back.  The lady paddling behind me kept saying something, which I couldnt really hear very well, but it turned out she was asking for a tip, all the way back.  During this time she also presented us with t-shirts and other souvenirs, pressuring us to buy them.  We were a captive audience!  It is worth the trip for the scenery and the serenity of just paddling though the rice paddies, but I think I read about another area where there is no pressure, so maybe investigate that and avoid Tam Coc.

We visited Tam Coc on a day trip from Hanoi with Go Asia Travels (GAT).  The heat was brutal, and the 4.5 hours spent in a bus really detracted from our experience.  By the time we got to the river, we were already pretty zonked.  The caves themselves are quite lovely, and so are the limestone rock formations that protrude from the river.  The boat-ride is very touristy, and the boatman, along with no shortage of other peddlers, will inevitably try to sell you some napkins or other souvenirs.  If you can get over all that, the trip is an interesting way to spend a couple of hours in the Vietnamese countryside (including some views of the rice paddies); but be forewarned that the entire boat ride is uncovered and exposed to the sun.  On a hot day like the one we went on, the exertion is just not worth it.

We gave this place an average rating, we can see how it can be beautiful but....Its overrun by tourist and the people taking you on the boat will get angry if you do not purchase anything from them.We would have totally passed this location up for the many other things we did with few tourist and peacefulness.If you like peaceful nature sight seeing, avoid this place.  If you want a tourist mill... enjoy!

At Tam Coc a shallow river winds through paddy fields with dabbling ducks and grazing cows, through limestone caves. The boat trip is a pleasant relaxing experience but Tam Coc itself like many other attractions is spoiled for a lot of Westerners by the aggressive persistence of the concession owners and hawkers determined to extract every last dollar they can out of you. I would prefer paying a higher tariff for the boat trip to avoid being nagged incessantly by the crew to buy goods they have for sale and (in the last few hundred metres) for a tip. But the countryside is beautiful in the middle of the rugged outcrops of the limestone karst country and it was interesting to see how Vietnamese farming families live on the water margin.

C'est correct mais sans plus. Ça vaut quand même le détour! Ne vous attendez pas à ce que vous voyez sur les images, c'est que qui déçoit sinon!C'est tout de même charmant et c'est tellement agréable ensuite de regarder des films et de tomber sur les lieux qu'on a déjà visités!Attention aux vendeuses ambulantes à la fin du parcours, elles sont insistantes et vendent 20 fois le prix d'ailleurs!!!!!

Tous les bus se sont donné rendez vous ici. Vous ne serez certainement pas seul, mais cela ne gâche rien. Au contraire ces centaines de barques à la queue leuleu sur la rivière qui serpente au milieu des pitons et des rizières a un certain charme. Bon à s avoir : éviter de prendre une embarcation gérée par deux rameurs. Ils vont plus vite, pour revenir chercher une cargaison de touristes. cela écourte la ballade prévue de 2 heures en 1h30...prenez le temps ce sont vos vacances...et si vous pouvez éviter Tam Coc c'est encore mieux : Trang Anh est plus authentique et pas encore envahie de bus...

Le site est pas mal on ne peut pas dire mieux car ce jour la malheureusement il ne faisait pas beau et vraiment tres froid. En fait, ce qui gache tout cest que comme tous les sites touristiques quon a fait jusqua present cest quon nous sollicite constamment. Donc ce qui devrait etre une belle balade tranquille et decontractee devient un peu stressante lorsquune myriade de bateaux charges de souvenirs vous accostent en vous pressant dacheter.Petit conseil pour eviter ce desagrement cest de proposer a la bateliere deviter de sarreter devant ces barques parce quon est pas interesses et quon prefere lui donner cet argent a la fin de cette promenade qui fait quand meme 6km

I visited Tam Coc as part of a tour group. Summer is really too hot to visit and we were melting even under our umbrellas! The boats made out of aluminum seem to reflect the heat, too. The boat ride itself takes about an hour and a half and it's a pleasant ride along shallow waters surrounded by limestone hills. The interesting thing is many of the boat rowers were ladies who rowed with their feet. The ride was somewhat spoiled at the turn-back point by the many boat vendors, selling drinks and food. They surrounded our boat and were very pushy.

There are a number of scams beware of if you do this trip.One as you get on the boat, if there is a lady in your  party, then they will provide another Lady on the boat. This other lady will be there to sell you items of cloth craft and textiles. The lady in question will begin the hard sell at the return trip. The best strategy is not to touch the cloth or to show any interest in it. This is not being rude, it's just refusing their sale. They may act as though you're being rude, but you're not. Because we have two men on the boat we were given a man as ROI and no additional woman. We made best use of the ores he gave us by panelling on when he tried to call us aside to get Lone of the ladies from another boat to give us the hard  sell   Two at the halfway point you will approach a number of boats selling drinks and crisps. One of the boats with a lady selling crisps drinks will immediately thrust of beer into the hand of any male boats man. Then she will also pass a packet of biscuits and say"You buy a drink for him". I consider that we are savvy   to scams but this one happened so  quickly that it called us out!Again, the best thing to do is do not entertain any discussion about their drink. Remember this transaction has got nothing to do with you so don't feel rude about ignoring it. To  be fair, however you should tip the person who rows your boat at the end of the journey by two or three US dollars or the equivalent.  3. If you take this trip, you should  definitely do the bike ride which should not cost more than three dollars extra. At the end of the short bike journey your guide will say that the trip up the hill to see the Temple's not free. This is not true.   There is no signage to show that there is a charge and another tourist who became independently confirmed that he knew from the hotel that there was no charge for this. when we questioned our guide about this he said "I'm not making money this is to pay to go up to look after the bikes". Basically, this is a straightforward scam between your tour guide and the people at the bottom of the Mountain who supposedly take part of that charge  to look after the tour operators bikes. For this reason, I did not tip the tour guide at the end of the trip, which I would normally do. Overall it is a trip worthwhile, if you do the trip however you must do the bike ride and you must got the mountain otherwise you will miss out on a large part of the trip, even if you are about rescanned for a few dollars.Unfortunately be interesting scenery and nice boat trip are tainted by the  fact that you are being fleeced and are about to be fleeced.. I appreciate that these people are poorly paid, and as a rule you should always tip and consider the actual cost of the trip. However, if  the tourist authorities do not get a grip on the scams that are occurring here, they may well destroy the very thing that brings the revenue and jobs to the area. I hope the tour operators start to take notice of all the comments on trip advisor and begin to put some measures in place to protect unworn travellers going to this part of beautiful Beermann.

I would agree with a number of others that the hard sell and not being thankful for the tip tends to spoil the trip. Avoid having your picture taken just another hassle. If people stopped buying stuff under pressure or going there at all the situation should improve.

Địa điểm đẹp. K được đầu tư. Nhà nước chỉ tập trung thu tiền.
Không sánh được với Tràng An
Đẹp tuyệt vời
長州不能遊船時的替代方案  大巴下車處..有廁所 轉電瓶車 到乘船處 穿救生衣排隊上船 2人一艘（小費20000/人） 約1.5小時

Die Bootsfahrt selber ist sehr schön und auch empfehlenswert. Zu gewissen Zeiten sehr stark überfüllt, ca 12-13:30 kommen alle Touren mit den grossen Bussen an. Also diese Zeit vermeiden. Wollten am Ende unserm Fahrer einen Tipp geben. Dieser verweigerte diesen und verlangte das fünffache des Trinkgeldes. Ticketpreis pro Person 250‘000 für nur die Bootsfahrt.

Visited this UNESCO World Heritage site, known for its limestone karsts and tranquil river. The boat ride through the caves was scenic, though the experience felt somewhat commercialized. The temples at Bích Động offered a peaceful retreat, but the overall visit lacked the authenticity expected from such a renowned location. While the natural beauty is undeniable, the heavy tourist presence detracted from the experience.

Nous avions fait les barques de Trang An la veille et franchement il n’y a pas photo. Si vous n’avez pas le temps ou l’envie de faire les 2 faites Trang an. Certes le faits que les bateliers rament avec leur pieds amènent du pittoresque mais certains points dérangent. Nous avons dû aller obligatoirement à 2 par barque alors que beaucoup de groupes vietnamien vont à 4 voir 5 personnes? 🤔🤔 (plus de barques plus de pourboires ???) L’itinéraire n’est qu’un aller-retour par la même voie (contrairement à Trang An où ce sont des boucles) résultat vous croisez toutes les barques en sens opposé ce qui ajoute encore du traffic. Les bateliers essaient de vous vendre des cartes brodées en cours de route et à la fin vous êtes sommés de donner un pourboires (on comptait le faire de toute façon mais là on ne vous laisse absolument pas le choix) Donc clairement pas une top expérience ATTENTION les tickets ne sont pas en ventes à l’embarcadère mais plus loin en ville. Soit les acheter avant soit prendre les voiturettes électriques jaunes qui vous y emmèneront.

Je mets ce commentaire pour aider les gens qui viennent par leur propre moyen : A cet endroit, il y a un guichet où ils vendent le tour de bateau avec les arrêts à plusieurs pagodes + plusieurs trajets en voiture électrique puis vous êtes ramenés au point de départ. En août 2025, le prix était de 350 000 VND par adulte. Si vous souhaitez acheter le billet avec le tour de bateau uniquement, il faut aller à l'autre guichet à 500m (chercher "Dong Gung Boat Tour ticket sales point" ou "Điểm bán vé đi thuyền Tam Cốc- Bích Động". En gros, c'est à l'est de ce POI et légèrement au nord). Le départ se trouve ici : vous prenez un gilet de sauvetage et on vous dirige vers un bateau. Notre groupe a pu monter à 3 personnes (le mari fait 85kg + femme + 1 fille de 12 ans) ou 2 personnes. Je ne sais pas si c'est parce que nous sommes d'origine vietnamienne mais le bateau qui prend les photos n'a pas insisté plus d'une fois et notre batelier n'a même pas essayé de nous vendre sa camelote. Par contre, il allait vraiment vite et on avait l'impression qu'il voulait en finir vite avec nous... Bref, par rapport à ma première visite ici il y a 20 ans, il y a du mieux car on s'est pas fait harceler mais notre batelier a fait le minimum et n'a pas fait le petit tour au bout du parcours et n'était pas hyper sympa. On lui a laissé qu'en même 100 000 VND de pourboire mais il n'avait pas l'air super content mais n'a pas réclamé plus. J'ai vu un groupe d'espagnol qui n'ont rien laissé et leur batelier était très mécontent. A mon avis, vous pouvez donner 50 000 VND et ils ne diront rien. Si vous préparez votre voyage au Vietnam, que vous lisez ce commentaire et que vous comptez faire la Ha Giang loop, je vous conseille de faire Ninh Binh avant Ha Giang sinon, vous serez déçus. J'ai oublié de dire que la balade en bateau nous a pris 1h30 et la visite du reste 1h30 également. Il y a des WCs accessible au fond de la rivière (au bout d'1h) puis à tous les stops toutes les 30 minutes. Nous y sommes allés à 8h30 et ça commençait à être assez plein à certains endroits. J'ai essayé de payer en carte mais ça n'a pas fonctionné (ça marche 1 fois sur 2 au Vietnam). Pour conclure, c'est à faire une fois dans sa vie mais ne vous attendez pas à être bien reçu... c'est l'un des pires endroits où le touriste est pris pour un portefeuille ambulant...

La balade est superbe et le lieu magnifique. Les rameurs rament avec les pieds c’est assez drôle à voir. Par contre : le billet est très cher comparé à la balade de Ninh Binh (même prix mais 2 fois plus court !), on a été fortement pressés d’acheter une sorte de broderie à bord et la rameuse nous a demandé un pourboire de manière très insistante avant de nous ramener sur la berge. Ça donnait l’impression qu’elle nous ramènerait pas si on ne lui en laissait pas ou pas assez à son goût. Sachez aussi que le gilet de sauvetage obligatoire gâche un peu la photo et qu’il y a beaucoup de traffic à certaines heures. La billetterie n’est pas sur place mais on vous y amène en voiturette (ce qui est totalement idiot).

Sehr schöne Bootsfahrt durch die Hügellandschaft. Das Personal bis auf den Ruderer sehr freundlich und Service orientiert. Leider hatte ich genau den Ruderer von dem hier schon einige negativ berichtet haben. Zahnloser alter man, ständig Radio an (wenn keine Kollegen/Aufseher in der Nähe) Dann irgendwann beginnt die Masche mit dem Verkauf ranziger alter Bildchen..... Und zum Schluss sagt er einem was für einen Tip er erwartet... Hab ihm 50' gegeben, dann hat er Ständig bestimmt 30x 100' lamentiert. Wofür bitte? Die anderen Guides machen Fotos mit den Gästen und bemühen sich zumindest.

Activité assez décevante par temps couvert.  Il y a deux options : 1- seulement la balade en bateau (190.000). Pendant plus d’une heure vous suivrez un flot de touristes 2- la balade en bateau + la visite de 3 pagodes (350.000) Honnêtement la visite des pagodes est inutile. Elles sont très moches et le prix est très élevé. N’oubliez pas de donner un tips au rameur entre 50.000 et 100.000 pour les aider à vivre décemment car l’embarcadère les paye très mal…

Beautiful landscapes and views, water lilies and ducks and limestone mountains. The price is very expensive tho, and the employees, whether it’s at the queue or the ticket counter or the boat, sometimes not so nice. If you have time for only one boat tour, I would advise the one of Trang A Cave a little further away. If you have time and money to do several boat tours, then this one is also nice ! To know: - tickets must be bought near the bus station - go before 9 AM, it takes around 1h30 - tips are not included but will be asked for repeatedly during the boat

Nous voulions seulement faire la balade en barque et non faire le tour avec la voiture électrique mais nous n’avons pas compris où aller pour acheter ces billets. Au final les arrêts faits par la voiture électrique étaient sympas à faire. Concernant le tour en barque, tout dépend du rameur. Je pense que nous n’avons pas vu ce que d’autres ont pu voir d’après ce que je lis dans les commentaires. Aussi, alors que nous voulions lui laisser un pourboire, à la fin du tour en barque le rameur nous a imposé de lui en donner un... c’est un peu dommage. Les paysages sont très beaux. Cela reste bel et bien une activité très touristique, nous le savions avant d’y aller, le faire nous l’a confirmé.

コース2に回りました。 あまりこのコースを選ぶ方がいないようで、同じコースの方が来るまで１０分くらい待ちました。  ３時間乗って途中途中、お寺のようなところに降りてみることができました。  洞窟などもありとてもすごかったです。景色も綺麗でした。 漕ぎ手の方が３時間も漕いでおりすごいなぁと思いました。  ただ、３時間と長いのですがあまり景色は変わらずでだんだん飽きてきてしまいました。 ショートコースあったらなぁと思いました。

Nice place. Unsure of dress code on arrival due to signs there so didn't go into temples etc out of respect. Bike and scooter parking is free so be careful of people yelling at you to park in spots to charge you. Be prepared to be harassed every 10 metres to buy a fan, beads, elephant etc. Vendors stand on street to try to make you stop to buy food drink etc at the shops on way in. We did the boat ride in late afternoon.  Similar experience with ladies yelling at us, demanding we buy rubbish items, demanding tips( we were going to tip but once she demanded and was yelling I didnt). Got back to our rented  scooter which had the key stuck in ignition upon pick up to find they had somehow removed it and taken the key away. After they told me "scooter man" had taken it I simply took the ladies key out of her scooter and told them I was ringing police. Miracles happened and my key was returned by a lady from the house next door after 5 minutes. Overall ruining the beautiful scenery experience.  We all are aware of the culture but there is boundaries I believe that were definitely crossed.

J'ai fait les 2 sites pour faire la barque sur la rivière,Tam coc et Trang An.  Sans aucun doute et de très loin faire Trang An.  C'est le même prix pour les deux, 250 000 dg soit 10 €.  Sauf que Trang An c'est 3 heures et il y a une traversée d'une grotte de 1 km et la fameuse montagne du film king kong se trouve là.  De plus le complex de Trang An est super et très professionnel.  La Rameuse de Tam coc va vous crier pourboire (en Français) et pas d'un air joyeux alors qu'elle aura ramé que 1:30 heure.  Ah j'oubliais la couleur jaune c'est la fleur du riz uniquement de Juin à Septembre, vous ne verrez pas ces couleurs à une autre période.  Vous pouvez faire en randonnée le début de la rivière par un petit chemin derrière le lac vers le cimetière et c'est très sympa.  Merci de mettre un like si vous trouvez ces informations utiles pour les autres.
正直、可もなく不可もなくと言う感じです。1時間40分オバサンの漕ぐボートに揺られ、終点まで行って帰ってくるだけで、これといった見所は特にないです。2~3箇所鍾乳洞のような所をくぐり抜けますが、変化というとそれ位。後はずーっと景色見てるだけです。何よりもボートをひたすらこぎ続けるオバチャンに感動しました。

Super mooie natuur.  Maar het is wel echt op massa toerisme gemaakt. De roeier die wij hadden kon ook alleen maar grommen ipv ook maar een paar woordjes Engels of vriendelijk zijn.. dat was wel wat jammer.

Kind of a cool area, but got constantly pestored by vendors. The boat trip is probably slower than it needs to be, our boat person was on the phone the whole time and then harassed us for a tip at the end

L'endroit est fabuleux. Par contre, une fois payé les 580 000, vous serez harcelés jusqu'à ce que vous preniez les photos prises depuis une autre barque (250 000) et 100 000 de plus de tips pour la dame du bateau, malgré le fait quelle a déjà été payée par la coopérative ! C'est limite une mafia !

Une balade de 1h30 en bateau. Les paysages sont très beaux mais il y a beaucoup de monde sur l’eau, j’avais l’impression d’être à Disneyland c’est dommage. Gros point négatif et je l’ai vu à plusieurs reprises dans les commentaires, c’est le batelier qui demande un pourboire à l’arrivée, je ne trouve pas cela normal et ça n’arrive jamais ailleurs au Vietnam. Ce n’est pas la meilleure expérience bateau de la région, allez plutôt faire celle de Trang An

I would highly recommend Trang An more than this. However, you can choose this if you have plenty of time and don't know what to do. You can drop by. No problem. Just avoid buying things from other boats. Very expensive.

Comment: เป็นการนั่งเรือที่เป็นนวัตกรรมใหม่จริงๆ ฝีพายใช้เท้าครับไม่ใช่มืออืมนี่ เก่งจริง เป็นการล่องไปตามแม่น้ำ มีลอดเขาบ้าง ระหว่างทางก็จะมีช่างภาพคอยถ่ายรูป ถ้าไม่ถ่ายก็บอกเขานะครับ และมี 7-11ลอยน้ำด้วย ก็ค่อนข้างจะยัดเยียดสินค้าหน่อยๆยุนะครับ

Some nice views but recommend just cycling the area instead for better views. Boat ride costs around £17 for 2 people and after an hour you’ve seen it all. The boat ride went on for around 1hr 45 and by the end we was pretty bored.

C'est un lieu magnifique....néanmoins on ne peut pas ignorer les usines juste à côté des sites.  Certains locaux sont très sympathiques, d'autres abusent grassement du système touristique....c'est dommage...

We went here for a boat tour. Do not buy the stitch pictures from the boat  rowers. They are produced at other places and you can buy them for much cheaper. After we bought some, he also asked us for a 100k tip which we declined, he was unfriendly afterwards. Boat tour was nice though.

Ho visitato questo luogo, dopo aver visitato Mua Cave, percorso lungo il fiume con vista risaie, l'unica cosa carina era il fatto che i vogatori usassero i piedi anziché le braccia. Consiglio di visitare Mua Cave che, secondo il mio personale giudizio, merita un 10. Noi ci siamo auto organizzati in modo da essere slegati da orari. Con Grab è facile.
The ticket price is expensive imo. Feels boring as it takes too long

The water lilies were in full bloom, and the boat ride went through a beautiful part of the river. Our rower, a middle-aged lady, seemed to be in a hurry to finish our boat ride, and quickly turned the boat after emerging from a cave, instead of parking the boat to let us disembark at one of the very beautiful stop-overs. I protested, and she reluctantly turned the boat again. I was disgusted and furious, as we had paid for the whole of this trip. She later tried to sell us a picture album, then demanded a tip-- I refused all her efforts, as she had tried to cheat us. The area passed was truly beautiful, the electric car ride was less exciting. And, yes, the Tamcoc ride is prettier than the Trang An ride, though Trang An is much better organised.

This is a magical area with a scenic boat trip, a stiff 500 step climb to top of Dragon Mountain as well as a cycle trip along very flat, varied and very attractive countryside.The boat trip was serene though wonderful scenery. Well worth it and breath-taking which included a series of cave passageways. The trips are well ordered and regulated. However many of the boat-people expected their passengers to buy pictures / souvenirs and provide tips. They can be persistent but can be resisted.Climbing Dragon Mountain is a challenge. Most of the steps were smooth, well maintained and safe to climb but there were some uneven spots which were a risk especially coming down and in the wet. As well good climbing footwear should be mandatory.There seems little regulation of the numbers climbing the Mountain which must be a real risk at time.At the top there is a pleasant viewing spot but the dragon is on a separate craggy, rocky outcrop. This can get over-congested and must be potentially very dangerous especially in wet weather with people wearing sandals, crocs and other inappropriate footwear. This need much stronger regulation.

Boat ride was so beautiful & peaceful.Quite long at about 1.5 hours but very much enjoyed it; stunning scenery & wildlife.  Boat itself was well maintained & very stable; driver very good also.They did try to sell items to us, and my friends did report some hard tactics; also very direct about tip at the end. Photographer also available, but my friends reported changing prices & hard tactics again - so be prepared.
We glided through Tam Coc’s emerald-green rivers, surrounded by towering limestone peaks and swaying rice fields. The quiet rhythm of the boat, the gentle breeze—it felt like time stood still.

We enjoyed this spot as part of a tour of the Dry Bay - Tam Coc and Hoa Lu within our tour of Vietnam. The rowing boats that run along the river are not entirely comfortable but the scenery is wonderful crossing various caves, a wonderful experience. The Bich Dong Pagoda, with its three levels, is a very interesting place.The citadel of Hoa Lu is very worthwhile too.

It is a very peaceful and immersive experience, away from the noise of the city. You will have time to admire the natural beauty and take great photos.- the boat ride (in 1,5-2 hours) -Bike ride in the countryside (45 minutes to 1 hour or more, depending on your pace)Mua Cave (Hang Mua) with 500 steps for panoramic viewsVisit the peaceful Bich Dong Pagoda nearby.Cost: Admission for the boat ride in Tam Coc is about 120,000 VND per person, plus the cost of the boat (often 150,000 VND per boat for 2 or 3 people)

Pleasant relaxing boat ride. Scenery is not mind blowing but the rowing-with-one's-feet used is definitely an impressive must-see.Affordable ticket to which an unannouced tip for the rower must be added.Fun though.

It was an interesting trip out to Tam Coc. Spent the day peeking into river life amid the weathered karsts. We left the area in the afternoon; though, I would imagine sunsets against the jagged peaks in Tam Coc would be spectacular!Alas, it was a warm day, but the hazy sky (smog?) cast a weird film on photos.
Great backpacker town altho many people of all ages. Amazing cliffs surround the town. Very busy tours so either go early or late on boat trip and early or late (or late morning) to Hoa lu

Really nice place the river went up with flat boats with oarsmen with feet, shallow water and sides with lotus plants and birds pecking in the water, then the various passages in the caves. Then the temple and the staircase to the temple and the dragon with many steps to go to the top, and the fascinating 360-degree panorama, on one side the waters covered with lotus flowers and on the other the river in the middle of the mountains in rock overlooking. That's nice.

nice boat trip, of course super touristy. I found it quite crowded on the river, but we knew that from the outset. The scenery during the ride is quite nice, but nothing unforgettablely spectacular. However, it's kind of part of being around Ninh Binh.

Tam coc was potentially my favourite place in Vietnam. It has a town feel to it, which makes a change from the hustle and bustle of the big cities, but still has everything accessible to it. There were so many amazing restaurants, supermarkets, cafes, etc. Banana Tree Hostel provides a great hang out and chill spot, Sisterfields serves an exceptional lunch and Trang An National Park boat tour was a fantastic experience. Strongly recommend visiting if you have the chance!

We started our tour at 4:15pm , therefore when we arrived for the temple, the furthest point and only temple to visit on this tour, it was closed. So we directly turn around and went back to the boat station.Other than that, this tour is very very beautiful ! You won't regret doing it.
You can make many different excursions and all very pleasant: the classic boat ride passing through caves, visit the ancient capital Hoa Lu, climb to see the view at Mua Cave

The scenery is beautiful, although not very wild, but come on, this is a planned tourist area. The water in the second and third caves is clearer and you can see the bottom as well as small fish swimming below, which is very interesting. When you go deep inside, you feel like you leave the civilized human world behind and calm down. However, I was quite uncomfortable when the boatman invited me to buy a painting
We took the trip on bamboo boats. The landscape is very beautiful. It's interesting to see how the boats are driven. It lasts around 1h30min.

Beautiful area of outstanding beauty..easy to get around. We didn’t do a tour..just went independent from Hanoi via limo van. Do the trang a boat tour..it’s fabulous and well organised. Route 3 is great with the right amount of caves and temples, we loved it and stayed 4 days. Chilling, walking, cycling and relaxing. Book a place with a pool!! Highly recommend if you have time. 3 days about right. Mua  cave is fab but phew…very tough and hot!  Tam Coc is touristy but small..fab massages and some good food.

So, for us this was a day trip from Hanoi, taking in the ancient capital of Truong Yen, followed by lunch and a cycle ride, a rowing boat tour down the river, finishing off with a climb up Hoa Lu.All fairly crowded with fellow tourists, but very well worth the effort.Ancient capital: underwhelming.River boat tour: too long in that day's heat (40+, with no wind: draining), but the most amazing scenery.Hoa Lu climb: again, busy, but so well worth the views from the top. Stunning.

The boat ride along Halong Bay on land is a must in the Tam Coc region. Extremely touristy site, but the scenery (karst sugar loaves, rice fields, etc.) is definitely worth it, even if the views from the sky (by drone) are more impressive than the feeling on the river itself.Long procession lasting around 1h30 of the boats (the rowers handle their oars with their feet!), passing under three quite impressive caves. A beautiful walk!

Very relaxing area of Vietnam, boat trip was long but enjoyable, our paddler was sweet and paddled mostly with her feet, the boats are small but very stable. I recommend taking a cushion as the seats are metal and uncomfortable for older people or bad backs. The river way is clean and tranquil

You can take quite comfortable boats driven by elderly ladies who row with their feet. You pay 250,000 dong each. There are two people per boat.During the tour you pass through incredible karst formations and under three caves. In some places, herons are seen eating rice in the fields. Interesting.
More than an hour rowing boat ride along Ngo Dong River. Magnifique. Also needs to keep your head down when going dark, under caves.
We did both the boat ride here and Trang An, and this was definitely quieter and more peaceful. The scenery of limestone cliffs might be better at Trang An, but I liked this boat experience better.

Boat trip scenery is beautiful and despite all the boats we found it enjoyable and serene. Like other reviewers we got asked to buy photos (no hassle) pictures (boat driver) and then our driver kept demanding we tip him! He was so aggressive we took his photo and complained. He was brought back in to speak to us (translated obviously as he only knew 2 words in English - money and tip). We thought we had got a bad driver but it seems to be a regular thing. Shame as it is a fantastic trip and worth the hefty (for Vietnam) price.

The boat trip is set in  beautiful surroundings. The limestone mountains are stunning. We were lucky to go at a time when it was relatively quiet. It was overcast but warm and I think it was the perfect weather as two hours in hot sun doesn’t appeal to me. The only thing that spoilt it for us was the pressure to tip & buy photos. It states on the regulations that rowers shouldn’t sell things or ask for tips. We would have been more likely to tip if not constantly asked for one. We bought one book of photos but the vendor literally followed us on her bike trying to get us to buy more. Don’t let this put you off though as it really is quite amazing scenery.

Tam Coc is a nice, but touristy village. You can go boating from the center (on the lake) and there are plenty of bicycles (50 dong) and scooters (100 dong) for rent. We did the boat trip in Trang An after the negative reviews on Tripadvisor. Was busy, but fine. Route 2 is the most popular, then you go in a procession across the river. We chose route 1: 9 caves and 3 temples and the most quiet route. Your boat must have at least 3 people, so we had to wait a while for more people who wanted to do that route. All in all, it took 3 hours to sail on the river and through the caves. No sellers along the way and the rower was not fussy about the time, so you could visit and view the temples in peace.Cycling from Tam Coc to Trang An is about 12 km inland. And you will pass the rice fields if you set Google Maps to walking. Bin Dinh is a bit further away so we did that by scooter. Partly the same way. Also doable.Beautiful mountains here.Tam Coc itself has many homestays, hotels and restaurants. It is also a real backpacking destination. Delicious coffee available at Duc's Coffee, near the lake in the quiet part (just after the entrance to Emeralda resort). Food looked good there too.

Nice place for 2-3 days. Came here for boat trips but they were closed - which ended up being a good thing as we did boat trip at Trans An which was better by most accounts (very busy with Vietnamese tourists but that added to the experience). I expect Tam Coc village would have been a lot busier with busses etc had the boat trips ben running there. TC good place to hire bike and just explore. Trang An was about 15 minutes away on a scooter, and the hill with the dragon on about 10 minutes.Enjoyed eating at the TPub for a fix of great western food for a change from local food.

On a day trip that included the ruins of Hoa Lu we also did a 2 hour boat trip of Tam Coc described as the Ha Long Bay on land.  Tickets must be bought before boarding the boats which can take up to 4 passengers.  There are three route options and our guide recommended route 3 to avoid periods bent over as boats travelled through long caves.  Along the river there are several temples and each has a small market offering food/drinks, toilets and souvenirs. Note July is very hot and humid and the best cover is an umbrella. Fans are also essential.  Seats are hard and can be comfortable towards the end of the trip so take a small extra cushions if you have a back issue.Boarding and leaving the boat is tricky so this may not suit people with mobility or balance problems but it is a beautiful experience and the temples are stunning.
his is a beautiful ride.  The most interesting thing about this trip is the rowers use their feet to paddle.  You get your own boat-2 people.  If you only have time to do one, do the Trang an.

The real scene is even more magnificent than what I saw in films. Last March, I went to Tam Coc, Ninh Binh with my friends and we took a lot of pretty pictures of nature. Hope to come back in one day^^

I have spend there 3 days and it's probably the part of Vietnam I enjoyed the most. Nature there is spectacular, hills, rivers, caves pagodas and overall religious places. Everyone could there what he wants to. I encourage everyone to spend there few days. I was accommodated in family which did perfect service for me. Renting bike, motorbike, doing the laundry and even finding bus to leave..

Afin d'éviter le monde, nous avons fait cette promenade le matin vers 9 h 00. Nous sommes partis du principal embarcadère situé dans le centre ville. En principe, il ne peut y avoir que deux passagers par barque en plus de la rameuse mais nous avons pu négocier de monter à 3. Nous avons payé 585 000 dongs (120 000 dongs par personne, 150 000 pour le bateau et 75 000 pour la rameuse). La guichetière nous a redonné les 75 000 dongs afin que nous les donnions à l'homme qui se trouvait au tourniquet d'accès. Il nous a désigné notre barque et nous avons commencé notre balade sur la rivière. C'était très agréable et paisible de naviguer parmi les rizières et les rochers. En chemin, des photographes en bateau nous attendaient mais il a suffit de les ignorer. La promenade dure un peu plus de 1 h 30 aller-retour. A la fin du parcours, avant le demi-tour, des vendeuses en barque nous attendaient. Au courant de cette pratique, nous avons informé la rameuse qu'on lui donnerait un pourboire supplémentaire, ce qui a mis fins aux sollicitations. Nous lui avons donné 100 000 dongs de retour à l'embarcadère. Peu de temps après, nous l'avons vu courir vers l'homme qui était au tourniquet et avons assisté à un échange d'argent. Nous espérons qu'elle a bien reçu la totalité du pourboire qui nous a été facturé car les rameuses sont très peu payées.

wir haben die Bootstour gemacht, die Tickets gibts direkt beim Einstieg um VND 195.000,00 Die Fahrt dauert ca 90 Minuten. Sollte man in Ninh Binh schon gesehen haben. Negativ war, dass die Verkäuferinnen auf dem Fluss ziemlich genervt waren wenn man nichts kaufen wollte und das der Fahrer 5 Minuten vor Endstation ein paar Mal erwähnte dass er Trinkgeld will.

You are taken by boat along the river and next to it you see different houses and at the end you pass through some limestone rock caves until again you go outside and there they turn around and go to other caves. The best is the scenery. King Kon's film was shot there. in October you don't see rice fields because it's already picked

Lunga escursione in barchetta con le signore che per ben due ore remano senza sosta, spesso è utile aiutarle visto che il peso della barca piu altre 5 persone può essere gravoso. Lo scenario dopo un po diventa ripetitivo e tolto un bel tempio che affiora dall'acqua ad un certo punto ci si annoia velocemente.
Tam coc is so beautiful. Boat trip was very nice and the local people were friendly. Cycling is very interesting
I understand why people name it Halong Bay territory. Impressing site. The boat take 90min so a bit long as it is not super comfortable if tou have long legs and back issue.

Dank der COVID-19-Pandemie und des Ukraine-Kriegs war es relativ leer. Die Landschaft ist wirklich beeindruckend und man muss es halt mal gemacht haben. Das golden-Rice-Festival ist leider ausgefallen, da der Reis noch grün war.
Mùa này nước hơi đục ,nước trong xanh mát có rong ở đáy sông thì đẹp tuyệt vời hơn. Giá mà được bay trên cao để ngắm cảnh sông nước thì sẽ thú vị hơn.

I did a boat tour in Tam Coc during my 1 day private tour with Chookies tours. They recommended Tam Coc because it is shorter (1,5h - private 2 person boat) than Trang An (3-4h <ith 5 people on the boat) and the views are the same. I enjoyed it, I think it's a good tour when you're only in Ninh Bin for one day. There are a lot of tourists and people try to sell you food, flowers and pictures, but I declined and they went away quickly. The tour was 120k and you're expected to give a small (50k) tip to your rower.
Điểm đến nổi bật khi bạn đến với Ninh Bình. Phong cảnh đẹp, giá cả cũng hợp lý. Có thể đi thuyền nhiều rồi nên đến đây lại đi thuyền tiếp nên mất hết cảm xúc 😐

Tam cốc là một trong những điểm thăm quan đẹp nhất ở Ninh Bình. Bạn về Ninh Bình thì không nên bỏ qua địa chỉ này. Cảnh đẹp hữu tình. Chỉ có duy nhất một điểm mình không ưng là lái đò ở đây rất lôm côm, vừa mới chèo đò là đã thấy gợi ý tiền bo rồi.
Phải công nhận là Tam Cốc rất đẹp. Đi thuyền chèo ngang qua các điểm, nối với nhau bởi những đoạn rất đẹp. Mùa này nhiều cây cối cỏ dại, nhiều hoa mặt nước nên càng đẹp.

Là quần thể hang động ở vùng đất xã Ninh Hải huyện Hoa Lư tỉnh Ninh Bình, Việt Nam . Tam Cốc – Bích Động còn được biết đến với những cái tên nổi tiếng như “vịnh Hạ Long trên cạn” hay “Nam thiên đệ nhị động” là một khu du lịch trọng điểm quốc gia Việt Nam. Toàn khu vực bao gồm hệ thống các hang động núi đá vôi và các di tích lịch sử liên quan đến hành cung Vũ Lâm của triều đại nhà Trần. Quần thể danh thắng Tràng An – Tam Cốc được Thủ tướng chính phủ Việt Nam xếp hạng là di tích quốc gia đặc biệt và đã được tổ chức UNESCO xếp hạng di sản thế giới.

About two hours by car from Hanoi, you will discover this very nice site with boat ride, walk (instead of bike) to have more time to stroll and take pictures. The limestone studs covered with vegetation, the rice fields, and the Mood are perfect to forget the hectic pace of the capital.
手漕ぎボートを楽しむことができました。ボートなので水面が近く、洞窟もあり周囲の景観もよかったです。ただ乗船時間が長く、最後は少し飽きてしまいました。

We really liked this part of Vietnam. Very nice site. The boat ride with the rower was perfect... at first! Beautiful setting, no one smiling at us much, very nice with us and our daughters until halfway through where she takes out a basket of things to buy, insisting that we buy. We end up buying some souvenirs, not enough according to her, the whole return is done in silence, dissatisfied, then on arrival, we give a tip that is not yet enough! So we got a little disenchanted with that, too bad...
Full day from Hanoi, Rice, small boat , bike ride . Beautiful scenery. Definitely very touristy.

We called in here as part of a day long trip to the region from Hanoi. There's plenty to see around here, in fact we wished that we'd booked an overnight stay and would recommend others to consider the same.The town itself has a number of restaurants and places to stay. The attraction is the surrounding countryside and, if you want, you can hire bicycles (we did) and I believe scooters.About a mile beyond the town centre is a river where you can hire a boat and lady who rows. They'll take you on a wonderful two hour river trip, past incredible scenery and through two or three low level caves.I'd certainly recommend a visit if you have time and inclination.

Autant Trang An m avait pensé à la Baie d Halong avec les pitons rocheux qui sortent directement de la mer (du lac pour Trang An), autant j ai préférer Tam Coc avec ses rizieres qui bordent le parcours en barque et offrent plus de perspectives entre ces espaces d eau et ces petites montagnes rocheuses. Moins de grottes à traverser (ce qui a rassuré ma fille de 10ans).Attention, pour les "étrangers" (foreigners), c est 2personnes maximum par bateau, alors que les Vietnamiens peuvent aller jusqu'à 4. A noter qu au moment de faire demi tour, des barques remplies de boissons de bonbons et des gateaux viennent vous se coller à la votre vous proposer leurs produits ou si vous ne voulez pas, d au moins acheter une boisson pour votre rameuse émérite. A votre conscience Messieurs, Dames.

Ninh Binh is an unattractive city sprawled over quite a large area, so Tam Coc is definitely the better place to base a stay in this area. It has a main town centre full of bars, shops and restaurants and maintains a villagey feel to it. It’s no longer a hidden gem or secret tip as it’s full of tourists of all ages and nationalities but it never felt crowded nor overwhelming.
To przejażdżka, wśród gór pokrytych zielenią. Z łódki, można podziwiać widoki, oraz płynąc pod skałami. Wszystko to tworzy magiczny klimat.

Had a wonderful time there. With the hike on the two peaks, the cycle across that beautiful landscape and little boat trip with local. You just need to experience it. Also the local food at lunch time was nice.Things I still would like to change was the tip on the boat service and the local selling stuff at half way. Feel like being forced to buy stuff for the sailor + had to give them a minimum of asking tips. Seems it was well planned.
Als je houdt van landschappen en kan scooteren is dit top.Boottocht was leuk, fijn dorpje, goede Indisch restaurant.

Un giro in barca tra grotte e rocce calcaree, da godersi mentre il rematore o la rematrice conducono (con i piedi, nel senso letterale del termine) e chiacchierano tra di loro. Il giro richiede più di un'ora perciò ci si può tranquillamente rilassare mentre si osserva il panorama.Non è obbligatorio comprare dalla signora che si avvicina alla barca né lasciare la mancia, anche se ritengo quest'ultima meritata data la lunghezza del tragitto.

We enjoyed this boat trip of about an hour and a half. The scenaries are pleasant as it is such a nice area. It is fun to go through caves. We did not experience any scam. Only one boat tried to sell us things and we said to our rower we were not interested and he kept rowing straight away. He did not ask for tips ever. We paid 150k vnd for the boat, plus a ticket of 120k vnd per person. A bit expensive but still a nice experience.

Wij zijn met de fiets ipv een bootje door de ntuur van tam coc gereden. Het werd ons aangeraden om trang an met de boot te doen wat een mooiere en langere route is (en net zo duur) als tam coc. Met de fiets (of scooter) door tam coc raad ik je aan om ook vab de weg af te rijden en de onbekende paadjes te nemen. Zo kom je mooie dingen tegen!

Bellissima escursione in una giornata di sole.Costo per due persone : 390.000 vndParcheggio per le biciclette : 10.000 vnd a biciclettaSiamo stati a fare il giro alle 7.30 di mattina e non c' era nessuno durante il percorso, e' stato fantastico.Durante il giro si attraversano 3 grotte, e nel mentre la nostra "guida" cantava e rendeva il tutto ancora piu' suggestivo!!Abbiamo dato alla fine 100.000 vnd di mancia ( che e' stata comunque richiesta, anche durante il percorso si e' avvicinata una barca che vendeva frutta e bibite e voleva che le acquistassimo per la nostra guida, noi non lo abbiamo fatto essendo gia consapevoli che avremmo dato la mancia)

ボートは2人乗りで所要時間は1時間40分でした。田園地帯をのんびりクルーズと思ったら、チャンアンと同じような風景（と感じた）。1月だったので単に稲がなかったということか？あるいは以前と比べてコースが短くなった？個人的にはここが一番気になったところ。良く言われる折り返し点の押し売りは、今では客用ボートの方が多いし無視すれば寄り付かなくなるので問題なし。この場所で止まるのは漕ぎ手を休ませるため。チップ問題は2人で50000ドン渡したら明らかに不満そうなお顔。100000ドンで満足といった印象。客がどんどん吊り上げることもありそうで、チャンアンのような管理システムが必要と感じた。漕ぎ手はほとんど休めないのでチャンアンよりきついと思うけど。開発が急ピッチで進められていて、もうすでにオーバーツーリズムが目前といったところで、牧歌的でのんびりの風情はなくなりつつあります

The site and fabulous, we navigate in the village, through the rice fields then we pass in 3 caves. Impressive! The only negative point: before returning to the starting point we get to a point where we are almost forced to buy food for us and the rower with a price worthy of the best rackets (150,000 Dong for 1 mango 1 drink and crakers!) And just before arriving the piroguier asking for his tip!We had planned to give before starting the walk but there was a little too much! It was a shame because the site really deserves the detour! To conclude do not take food on the way and tip the piroguier and that is all!

I went here in the off season knowing that the rice fields had already been harvested, however the scenery was still beautiful.  I think it would be more spectacular if you go before the harvesting of the rice to see something even more picturesque.  The row boat ride was very interesting as we went into three separate caves.
ハノイから約2時間、途中の景観も楽しめました。古都ホアルーでは、ベトナムの初代王朝がおかれていたところで歴史が感じられました。英語ガイドの説明もわかりやすく楽しめました。タムコックのボートでは素晴らしい景色でしたが、ボートの漕ぎ手のおばさんのチップ要求が執拗で、2人で4万ドン渡したら足りないと言われ、追加で2万ドン渡すことになりました。それでもまだ不満そうでした。星1つ下げました。
I took a boat trip to Tam Coc and bike ride around the area and totally loved it. Amazing landscapes with mountains and river (not the right time for rice paddies).

ボート折り返し地点での食べ物の売り込みも値段がわからず、ふっかけられて、交渉、というのが、こういう観光地での定番とわかっていても面倒。手持ちのドンが少なかったし、結局断った。ボートの漕ぎ手へのチップは一人２万ドンという説明だったので、家族３人分として６万ドン渡したら、おじさんが「足りない！」と。無視したが、気分が悪かった。買い物、チップはあくまで、自分の目安をしっかり持たなければ、と思った。昼食のバフェは他の口コミにあるほど悪くないと思う。特に美味しいわけではないが、種類は多い。ビール等、飲み物は町の倍の金額。泊まってみたいと思うようなホテルや、あちこちでホテルが建設中で、これからどんどん開発されるのでしょう。

Tam Coc staat op het punt ( 2020 ) ten prooi te vallen aan plat toerisme. Prachtige omgeving, leuke hotels en homestays maar je merkt dat gemiddelde publiek ver onder de 30 is ( of reist 50+ niet naar Vietnam? ) Restaurants hebben bijna allemaal te harde muziek, ( van het type ‘playlist samengesteld door 15-jarig kind’) . Op het dorpsplein staan soms 3 bussen met bar op het dak, alle drie met eigen takke-herrie de sfeer van het dorp te verknallen.  Als je hier mee kan leven: de hotels liggen, ondanks op loopafstand, bijna allemaal buiten deze herrie en de omgeving is ongelofelijk mooi!  Ga vooral fietsen in de omgeving ( kan zonder gids ).Over de roeiboot tour: een echte aanrader, neem die in het meer in het centrum. Tip de roeier vooraf, hiermee voorkom je de truc op het keerpunt van de tocht: je wordt belaagd door verkopers in bootjes die -als je zelf niets moet- wel iets in de handen van je roeier stoppen ( soms wel 3 producten van 50.000 dong per stuk) je durft het bijna niet uit de handen van de roeier te halen maar je moet er wel voor betalen! Vanzelfsprekend retourneert de roeier het ongeopend bij de verkopers. Indien je de roeier al getipt hebt kan je alles wat makkelijker weigeren. De roeitocht blijft ongelofelijk mooi! Eten moet je in restaurant  ‘family’, 1 familie in de keuken, lieve bediening, geen muziek (!) , lekker eten

I like particularly the visit to Mua caves , the view is fantastic there and there is also a pagoda to visit. Lodging is basic here and can’t expect city standard but good enough for foreign tourists .

Booked the trip from Klook. The tour including the lunch and it was really good. After lunch walked to the river bank to start the boat trip. The trip took 90 min but may be longer. The mountain scene is beautiful. Our tour guide told us to give tips usd$1 to the boatman. The boat woman asked for more tips when I already passed her usd$2 (we are 2 ppl) so I just ignore her. Visited on Dec 2019.

Just take note that after you paid for the standard fare, the counter staff will pass you 75,000VND (from our fare) to be passed to the boatman.  Guess this was the boatman’s “salary”.  The ride was scene & we even spotted goats on the mountain.  Just as other tourists who had commented, the locals on the boats really hard sell us drinks but we declined & they were not happy. There were photographers on their boats to take many pictures for tourists. One such photographers kept taking many pictures of us & we have to tell her politely it was enough.  The boatman stopped the boat before we reached the jetty & asked directly for tip. They expected nothing lesser than 100,000VND.  Once we reached the land we were handed our 8 photos & cost about 16,800VND per piece. We just bought it since we wanted a happy trip.  But, the Vietnam tourism board should do something about the attitude & service of the boatman. On another note, the boatman at Trang An didn’t ask for the tip & we were more than happy to tip the boatman willingly.

El entorno es precioso, te deja sin respiración! La visita la tienen totalmente organizada, y bastante caro para lo que cuestan las cosas en Vietnam. Pero por ver este sitio tan increíble, merece la pena. Nos dijeron que Trang An merecía más la pena pero no pudimos ir por lluvia.
Situated in the Ninh Binh province one of the most beautiful scenic view.We took a boat and covered 3 caves .It was a 1 and half hour ride and pretty enjoyable
景色は最高の星5です。写真を撮って売られるのも、折り返し地点の物売りがしつこいのも周知していました。それを含めても星5でしたが、一度乗ると途中にトイレがない、、、奥さんがオシッコしたくて、最後は楽しめなかったので星3.5、四捨五入で星4にさせていただきました。

Stunning scenarios, sailing down the water in the boat, with the boat man paddling the oars with their feet. The sun was scorching despite being winter. Good to bring your sunhat or umbrella to prevent sunburn.On the round trip, they were selling snacks and were rather pushy. Kept shoving the stuff to us even when we had shown that we weren't keen.The boat man did not hesitate to ask for tip at the end of the ride.
The scenery was great and spectacular scenery.  But the people at Tam coc, I am a bit......  take a step back

Escursione suggestiva con le barchette tipiche del luogo che ti portano a visitare le grotte e se si fa di pomeriggio poco prima del tramonto si può assistere al rientro ti tantissimi uccelli che portano il cibo ai piccoli.

At the ruins of Hoa Lu, the small but still emperor's compound, sculptures and tiled roofs. The first and second generations are treated differently.On Tam Coc boat tours, women paddle a simple boat for 90 minutes to admire the landscape.I was able to enjoy the nice scenery like the one in the photo, which was not in the rice fields (it seems January is good).After going through the cave, there was a boat for sale, but I enjoyed it as a memory of my trip.An hour of cycling is also interesting because it is a road that you don't usually ride. Seeing the boat tour from the top of the land was different.The trip takes about 3 hours. On the way back, we took the bus for about two hours, but there was a break in the middle, so it didn't bother me.
Tam coc is beautiful. We did boat tour on river through 3 caves. Amazing landscape with moutains, rice fields and river

Spent a great day in Ninh Binh and the highlight was the row boat at Tam Coc . Peaceful , beautiful , loved ducking through the low dark caves and out the other side to see lush forests . Boatman skilful , rowing with his feet and smoking at the same time . Well worth the long drive from Hanoi .

Not a stop always included in the classic North to South Vietnam adventure but so worth a visit after the craziness of Hanoi. Hire a bike and cycle through rice fields to caves and temples, take a tiny paddle boat through the caves to magical open lakes or stroll through the bars/cafes sampling the local food and coffee specialities - all aimed at the backpackers so don’t expect ‘haute cuisine’ but that doesn’t detract from Tam Coc charm.

I didn’t enjoy this as much as Trang An, but this could be be because my boat man was not as much fun as the one at Trang An.At the halfway they have people selling drinks and fruit, they did not seem happy that I didn’t want any. There are also people to take photos, but these were less pushy so it was not a problem.At the end a woman was telling me how much of a tip I should give.The scenery is lovely and it is well worth doing.

Stayed in Tam Coc and did a motorbike tour of the area with Tommy's tours. Was amazing. Love the area and so glad we stayed locally to enjoy how beautiful the place is. An inland Halong Bay.....There are plenty of restaurants and found a great massage place on tbe main street.Would recommend staying locally rather than a day tour from Hanoi.
Beuatiful place where go on a boat on the river to see caves and rocks. Less crowded than simila Trang An

Basically you sit in a boat whilst being propelled by a rower who rows with their feet through fairly interesting scenery - not a trip that you want to do when it is raining. I think it takes about 90 minutes there and back.It's quite relaxing sitting there as the river banks pass by.The rower expects a tip at the end of the expedition.

It is fascinating to see the boat rower row their boats using legs... But quite boring for us to sit on the boat for too long... Our tour guide Peter told us only to give tips to the rower at the end of the trip, because otherwise there might be a different rower to fetch us back & we might to give the tips again! ... So we expected to alight from the boat at some point of time to view the scenaries around... But alas, no... Throughout we were seated on the boat...After passing by 3 rocks and the ending point will see some rowers selling foods & drinks... They quite persistent to sell their products, but we can always tell our boat rower to bring us out from there...Only then, we realised there's no alighting point... The rocks we passed by are actually Tam Coc, which they mean the 3 caves...Well, the caves are beautiful...
Le cadre est reposant, on est en pleine nature.Traversée de 3 grottes, cueillette de nénuphars...Attention les rameurs réclament si le pourboire n'est pas assez important.

Une belle promenade en barque qui dure 2 heures. - ticket d’entrée à Tam Coc : 120.000 VND- barque à rames : 150.000 VND / barque ( 2 personnes ) si vous êtes en solo vous payer le prix intégral de la barque. - navigation sur la rivière Ngo Dong et pendant la balade on traverse 3 grottes.Le paysage est vraiment magnifique et beau. Si on ajoute la particularité de Tam Coc à savoir que les rameuses rament avec les pieds et non les mains, ça fait une expérience inédite. Dommage que le charme soit un peu gâché par les vendeuses qui presque vous obligent à acheter quelque chose et si vous refuser elles vous disent de prendre pour la rameuse( on vous coince à la sortie de la dernière grotte avant le trajet du retour ).Malgré cet incident le site est très beau. J’ai beaucoup apprécié cette balade que j’ai faite en début de matinée où il y avait moins de monde. Toujours prendre avec soit un parapluie ( subitement il s’est mis à pleuvoir pendant 10 min et ça s’est arrêté) sinon chapeau et protection solaire car le soleil tape fort.
La visita merece mucho la pena,subir los 500 escalones de Mua Caves,imperdibles. El viaje en barca,bonito pero nada del otro mundo

A relaxing two hours it was. I was hesitant to do the Tam Coc boat ride after reading some of the negative reviews. I understand some of the negativity. Half way through the ride they pull up alongside boats who try to sell your food and drinks. They hassle you quite a bit. My fiancee speaks Vietnamese so we just told the driver we would tip them at the end and she seemed to steer away from those boats selling you stuff and continued on. If you only had a short amount of time in Ninh Binh I recommend doing Trang An Boat Ride. If time permits consider doing both.

Well it’s a tough decision trang an or tam coc. Both are amazing with a great scenery except the fact that you must be bit careful in Tam coc as the local sellers may try to sell you lot of unwanted stuffs.

We had a great day with our guide ‘John Snow’. The temple visit was good & just the right amount of time & history shared. The boat ride nice but could have been cut in half, & the experience marred a bit by the local rowers trying to earn as much from the tourists as possible. Cant blame them in taking advantage I guess but spoiled the enjoyment a bit. Highlight came from the mountain walk. 500 stairs took a bit of effort but well worth it. The views were spectacular!

We zijn op het laatst van de dag gegaan (let op: staat open tot 17.30, maar om 17.00u gingen ze dicht) en daarom was het erg rustig op het water. Onze roeier sprak twee woorden Frans (madame, monsieur), maar wees ons daarbij wel op plekken die we anders niet hadden gezien. We zijn met hem op de foto geweest en naar de King Kong opname plek geweest (aan het einde van de derde grot). Na een tip van 50k pp moest ie huilen van geluk.Aanrader!

Somigliante alla baia di Halong per il fatto che le piccole colline sono simili agli isolotti di halong, bello il giro in barca con la rematrice che rema con i piedi, belle le tre grotte che si attraversano e belle le risaie che circondano questo laghetto. veramente piacevole farci un giro.

Very nice boat ride, the rowers row mostly with their feet and already this is to be seen also you make a path in which you cross three very beautiful limestone caves, one of which is about 400 meters long, there are many limestone rocks even more than 100 meters high that closely resemble the islets of Halong Bay!! also beautiful the typical rice paddies around the lake! It's funny.

Pasamos dos días en este lugar, nos sorprendió muchísimo, muy acogedor, la gente súper amable y atenta. Pudimos disfrutar de varios de sus restaurantes y pasear alrededor de su lago y arrozales. Lo más interesante es el paseo en canoa entre arrozales, tienes la sensación de estar en Halong Bay pero en tierra, el rio que recorre las montañas, ofrece un paisaje increíble, las canoas son remadas por locales que utilizan tanto las manos como los pies, es muy curioso lo fácil que lo hacen. Hace bastante calor, por lo que recomiendo comprar un paraguas en los puestos de la entrada, por algo menos de 3€ harás que el  trayecto sea mucho más agradable. Las cuevas que se atraviesan son realmente increíbles, una experiencia que no olvidaremos.

It costs 120000₫ per person (plus 150000₫ for one boat, which can carry 2-3 ppl). They take you along the river, through 3 caves. The view is stunning, although personally I prefer Trang An. All in all, it was a relaxing experience.
Locais lindíssimos, paisagens arrebatadoras, mas se pensas que vais encontrar um lugar calmo e livre de turistas enganaste

Tam Coc boat ride This is labelled as one of the must do things in this area and the natural beauty that you see along the ride justifies this. The sign at the desk when you buy your ticket says only two foreigners per boat but we had a group of three and this was allowed. The boat ride was really lovely as it meanders through the water through the  rice fields. Going through the three natural caves was gorgeous (and at times very dark). We got to see mountain goats perched up on the rock surfaces which was really interesting. We braved the weather and went during a short break in the rain and were lucky for most of the way. The rain we did encounter even though it was quite heavy did not ruin the trip at all (it was almost refreshing after dealing with the heat for the past few weeks). This was possibly one of the reasons also there were not hoards of other groups going when we were out which made the trip quieter and nicer. It would be very hot normally along this route so be prepared for full sun exposure. The boats are not covered.The lady rowing the boat (with her feet!!!) was friendly during the trip and offered to take a photo of our group, although at the end she said we didn’t tip her enough and dropped us off further away than when we started. Not the best end of the trip but it didn’t take away from the beauty of the landscape we were able to see.
