Life jackets are also provided however we were told to take them off once we got on the boat, these did serve as good covers from the rain!This trip was lovely and definitely worth it for those who want to see one the “inland Halong bay”.
Für vietnamesische Verhältnisse sehr ruhig, schöne Restaurants und Bars. Die Lage direkt am See macht eine tolle Atmosphäre (Autan sollte aber auf jeden Fall griffbereit sein)

Mooie vaartocht door een prachtige omgeving. Jammer is wel de bootjes op het keerpunt die je van alles willen aansmeren.  Aan het eind gaven we een tip,  maar dat vond de roeier niet genoeg en bleef drammen. Gewoon weggelopen en genegeerd.

Great to see but, as other say, the boat lady does demand a tip before you get back. Also watch when you get off as it's very slippy. Route itself takes 45 mins each way with a stop at boats full of salespeople half way which we firmly declined. We also declined paying for food and drink for rower. She clearly wasnt too upset as she saw us in town two days later and smiled and said hi! It us more of a tourist trap than Trang An.
짱안을 선호하기는 하지만 더운 날씨에는 비교적 짧은 땀꼭이 좋을 수도 있겠더군요. 보트 및 입장료는 매표소에 가격이 정해져 있으니, 팁만 고민하시면 될 듯합니다. 대부분 관광객들이 팁으로 보트당 기본 5만동 정도로 줍니다만 한국사람들만 인당 5만동 주는 듯..

A stunning place and had a great time there. The people are very friendly and helpful and the scenery is beautiful. We hired bikes and scooters to see al the major attractions and the majority of them were well worth the effort and money. However some were not. I guess you get that anywhere but do your due diligence and you should be fine. The four star rating is because we got fleeced a couple of time and that left a bit of a sour taste in my mouth but other than that this place is definitely worth coming to.

Passeggiata in canoa di due ore sul fiume, durante le quali si attraversano tre grotte, di cui una lunghissima ed una breve, sotto le quali si gode una piacevole frescura. È indispensabile proteggersi dal sole perché, dopo la traversata, avevo i piedi a strisce secondo l’andamento dei sandali. I rematori si difendono dal sole mantenendo un ombrello e remano con i piedi con grande agilità. Fra cimiteri a pelo d’acqua, piante infinite di fiori di loto e coppie in luna di miele, il fiume scorre in grande silenzio.Alla fine della terza grotta aspettano al varco delle donne che vendono bibite gelate e piccoli snack ed insistono perché li compriate al rematore. Non cadete nella trappola perché questo non vi sbarca fino a quando non gli avete dato la mancia.Il nostro rematore ha sputato nel fiume per tutta l’andata; al ritorno si è astenuto forse perché dai miei commenti ha intuito che non era proprio gradevole

Nous avons beaucoup aimé les paysages qui sont différents de Trang An et c’est impressionnant de voir les rameuses ramer avec leurs pieds. Toutefois, nous n’avons pas apprécié les vendeuses sur l’eau qui vous arnaquent à moitié et vous forcent à acheter une boisson pour la rameuse et les rameuses qui vous réclament un pourboire à la fin de la promenade. Nous avions prévu de lui en laisser un mais le fait qu’elle réclame nous a gâché un peu la visite. Mais il faut y aller car le site est vraiment superbe.

It was quite pleasant boat ride. Although you cant get any extra info from vietnamese since they doesnt speak any english whatsoever. The nature is really beautiful there, but the water is dirty, garbage (especially plastic bags and bottles) were floating everywhere. Rovers expect to get a tip, if you buy them refreshments during the boat ride at the floating market, they ask for the tip anyways. Rude!
Mooie rondvaart met lokale roeier (voeten). We hadden het geluk een stomme (niet kunnen praten) roeier te hebben. Maar hij had de grootste verhalen... Lachen!

Another UNESCO heritage site which is popularly called Halong Bay on Land. Boating for a 90 minute return trip passing 3 caves/grottos, alongside limestones.Prior to this was a visit to Hoa Lu ancient kingdom. Learned vietnam history.Overall fun and exciting. Better to fo it in the afternoon during summer because the heat was unbearable.
Careful in the hot days as the 2 hour boat trip might be too much. It would have been great if we could have a bit more of history from the guide. And the lunch was quite disappointing

We saw this in a tour booklet and decided we wanted to go. We had the boat trip up and down the river and it was spectacular. Apparently it's known as the Ha Long Bay on land and i can see why! The only thing that dampened on our experience was once we got to the end where the boat turns around, there are local vendors trying to sell drinks and food. We were thirsty so thought we'd get a drink and ended up getting fruit and items for the person operating the boat, which in itself i don't mind as they're rowing) however the price was through the roof. So unless you're in need of refreshments, don't engage the vendors and if you do, haggle hard!  However, don't let this put you off going, it is worth it.
가는 길의 경치는 trang an보다 별로였다. 하지만 배 타는 코스는 역시 오리지널이 더 낫더라.배는 구름낀 날에 타자. 맑은 날엔 전기구이 오징어가 될 수 있다.

Tam Coc is really beautiful and nice. They're lots of things to do here. Boattrips, going around by bike, Mua Cave,...The people though are not always very friendly. Be careful for pickpockets and rip-off.

The "Ha Long Bay of the land" was very beautiful and the boat ride was very relaxing.  However, since we did chose to go during very hot weather, we found it very helpful to bring an umbrella to cover yourself while in the boat.  At the end of the boat ride, there were many vendors selling drinks and basically boxed us in and wanted us to buy a drink... we refused and then they basically guilt tripped us into buying a drink for our driver.  It wasn't expensive but a little bit annoying. It was a hot day and she did deserve a cold drink.  As other people have said, the driver "reminded" us that we needed to tip her which we knew already because our guide had already told us.  The whole boat ride was about 1hr 45 min.  The biking portion was very short - just about 30-45 minutes.  Don't expect to meet with any locals.. you'll just be riding right past some of their farmland.  The bikes are very old and I saw a few bikers have to carry their bikes back because something broke/went wrong half way through their ride...

Tam Coc, also known as the dry Ha Long, is beautiful. Besides the King Kong Island, which was a waste of my time, Tam Coc offers great scenery. The boat ride was tiring because we went at the wrong time (it was 36 degree celcius!!!). However we still enjoyed the caves and the occasional breeze. I believe Tam Coc is lovely in Autumn and Spring so choose the right time to go. And as a local etiquette, remember to tip the local who rowed your boat (about USD3). It was hard 3-hour work and they do not get paid alot for this task.  Between Tam Coc and Ha Long, I would visit Tan Coc again.

Magnifique mais trop de touristes et encore nous commençons la saison creuse. Vaut malgré tout le déplacement. À tester les bungalows au début de la ballade en bateau. Couleur locale au calme,, nous aurons sans doute mieux  fait de loger là plutôt qu'à Hanoï bruyant et sale. Sans parler des pick pocket( un ce matin près du lac Hoan Kiem)

Paddle up the rice paddies in long boats driven by ladies using their feet! The scenery is amazing, especially dragon mountain, but you need a little bit of fittness, its quite steep and alot of stairs.

Omgeving tam toc is mooi, scooter gehuurd en heerlijk rondgereden. Boottocht in tam coc is kwa natuur heel mooi, maar let op bij het keerpunt zijn ze erg opdringerig met verkopen van drankjes aan jou en om ook iets voor je roeister te kopen. We hebben ook de boottocht van trang an gedaan, deze was iets langer en veel minder commercieel. Hier vroegen ze ook niet om fooi of iets te kopen. Wij verbleven in tam coc westlake homestay en deze kunnen we zeker aanraden.

We enjoyed a lovely boat trip through beautiful countryside. Interesting way of rowing using feet.We did buy fruit from one of the ladies and were persuaded by her to buy for the boatman, didn’t have a problem with this. However when we came to the end of our journey he stopped rowing and prodded my son in the back and said ‘tip’. We had intended to give him a tip and looked up before we left the recommended  rate. The rudeness of the demand just made us want to leave less, then he suggested what he would like. We gave him a tip which was more than recommended but less than intended as left rather sour end to journey, I think we were a bit unlucky with our boatman as the one earlier in Trang An was delightful.Apart from this I would recommend trip

We had a great trip down the river on board a small boat rowed by a local woman. It was very impressive to see them row with their feet down the river through some amazing scenery.The whole trip was spoilt somewhat by forced tourism and the women expecting you to pay extortionate prices for drinks when on the boat as well as buying nuts and drinks for the driver themselves.Tam Coc is a lovely town with breathtaking scenery but we felt that local people are ripping off tourists
Leuke toer door het achterland van Ninh Binh en door grotten en langs tempels. kan zeer druk zijn op de dag
Es ist eine tolle Anlage, eingebettet in viele Reisfelder. Personal freundlich und hilfsbereit. Ausflüge sehr gut. Essen und trinken gut.

Tam Coc is a terrific full day excursion from Hanoi. We opted for a tour that included a visit to the temples of Hoa Lu, buffet lunch,  “private” boat trip along the Ngo Dong River and bicycle ride. The tour was well organized and good value. The “private” boat trip entailed a 90-minute paddle up and down a stretch of the Ngo Dong River with beautiful karst scenery on either side and included the negotiation of three caves where the river has carved a path under karst outcrops. “Private” refers to the fact that my wife, son and I had a row boat (the rower worked the oars using her feet) to ourselves. But we certainly didn’t have the river to ourselves...as there was a flotilla of other “private” boats all around us. Having said that, it wasn’t as bad as I expected (or the pictures I had seen beforehand). The only disappointing aspect for us was the hard-sell vendors awaiting us at the half-way mark/ turning point on the river. The short (30-minute) bicycle ride after the boat trip was also pleasant. If you’re looking for a wilderness experience, this is not it. It is nevertheless an enjoyable, scenic and relatively serene break from the chaos of Hanoi.

Had a lovely time visiting Tam Coc. Beautiful scenery and interesting caves to look at. However don’t be fooled by the food and drink offered as it’s very pricey and they slyly make you buy food and drink for the rower.

Après avoir fait les grottes de Trang An, nous avons décidé de se laisser tenter par les grottes de Tam Coc.Cela est intéressant car totalement différent! Alors qu'à Trang An, on pagaye avec les mains, ici on le fait avec les pieds. Le paysage est moins impressionnant en terme de pitons kartistiques mais beaucoup plus calme et reposant que le site de Trang An qui est pris d'assaut.Il est recommandé de le faire en début ou fin de journée pour éviter les touristes de masse et le samedi.
La navigazione alla scoperta delle tre grotte è rilassante per la scelta di barchette a remi condotte dai locali. Bei panorami di risaie, montagne e boschetti.

Tam Cốc, có nghĩa là "ba hang", gồm hang Cả, hang Hai và hang Ba. Cả ba hang đều được tạo thành bởi dòng sông Ngô Đồng đâm xuyên qua núi. Tam Cốc là tuyến du thuyền được khai thác đầu tiên ở khu du lịch Tam Cốc - Bích Động.
Paseo muy agradable en barca conducida en su mayoría por mujeres que reman con los pies. Precioso el paisaje, lástima que haya demasiados turistas, pero nosotros también contribuimos a ello.

Some people stay for a few days around Tam Coc in honestay or bungalow on the bank of a river in Ninh Binh province. In my opinion one day tour is more than enough to explore this region. You can visit two temples of the ancient capital, have a boat ride and cycle around on a bicycle. Sometimes you might be unlucky and you won't get a bike. Anyhow, walking is also a good option.

Tam Coc is a buzzing little backpacker town near Ninh Binh. It's the ideal place to use a base for exploring the region and fun to walk around the town as well. Overall it was my favourite part of my holiday in Vietnam.  I stayed 3.5 days and could happily have stayed longer. A couple of points to note. Tam Coc is packed with very cheap restaurants and for those of us who are past backpacker style eating, doesn't offer anything half way acceptable.  Don't take a boat tour from Tam Coc. Go instead to Trang An.

We really enjoyed the boat trip with such stunning views. We knew it would probably be the case but we're still a little disappointed to be made split up into 2 boats as our boys are young, especially since there were some boats with 5 much bigger Asian families.. We were overcharged by the lady with the calculator at the ticket desk by 50% on each boat but I guess its our fault for not double checking it! In contrast the boat ladies on both our boats asked respectfully for a tip just before our return & were very happy with US4 each we gave them. The scenery was breathtaking, even our kids were amazed.. Not to be missed!

Tam Coc vaut deux, trois jours pour visiter et prendre le pouls de la campagne Vietnamienne. Une petite balade en bateau au fil de l'eau en passant par des grottes. Plutôt à faire en fin de journée,  Il y a moins de monde.

This was a very pleasant boat ride, some compare it to Trang An but I think they are a bit different, on this one you paddle through rice fields and 2 or 3 caves and in Trang An there are more options and you get off the boat to walk to temples and cliffs.I loved the scenery and the perfect green rice fields…is just as you see in videos or pics. The ride did feel a bit rushed…right after the third cave the lady simply turned the boat and returned to make sure we made it back “in time” (probably for the next ride). You will see vendors in boats along the way…so as in any market, just assertively and politely say no and they will stop.I got a tip from someone we met at the hotel to drive to the top of the cliffs if you have a motorbike to see the amazing views and take the perfect shot…unfortunately we could not make it since it was raining and really foggy but I am sure it is an amazing view.The lady rower gave us a survey and one of the questions was “Were you requested a tip?” to which we answered “no” but 30 seconds after we returned the survey forms she requested the tip…Smart and not cool but on the other hand they are making a living.Overall this one felt more touristy than Trang An and for similar price…honestly I felt I got more in Trang An…we rowed more time and saw much more things. The good thing here is that you see the boat operator row with their feet which is part of the attraction in a way. Also, here you will have the boat for yourself (if you are going in a couple), which is cool.

Se hace en barca de 2 personas y el paisaje es espectacular.Lo malo la parada que hacen a mitad camino para q compres, casi x obligación, alguna bebida y también para la señora de la barca. Aquí abusan del precio y encima luego cuando llegas te pide la propina. El paseo en barca x Trang An (4 personas por bote) es igual de bonito y con paradas por templos. Aquí no te agobian con comprar o con la propina.

O passeio é feito pelo rio, passando pelas 3 grutas. Os remadores utilizam os pés, e não as mãos para remarem. O único inconveniente é quando passamos a última gruta, e somos abordados por vendedores em botes.

Tam Coc is absolutely stunning, the boat ride was so scenic and was so relaxing. The only negative was that half way through we were taken to another boat that sold fruit/drinks etc. The lady was very pushy and wouldn’t take no for an answer, she then gave it to the lady rowing the boat and demanded money, it was very overpriced. We paid it and used that as the boat lady’s tip. The food in the area is average.

We did both Trang An and Tam Coc during our stay in Ninh Binh.  Having done two routes (1 and 2) in Trang An, I can’t see any reason why anyone would choose Tam Coc over either of those routes.  We did not have any problems with aggressive peddlers in Tam Coc (just people on boats asking us to buy stuff, like photographs or snacks).  Yes, someone did try to get us to buy something for our boat driver, but we said ‘no’ (3 times) and she went away.  Although we did buy some mangoes from her for a dollar.  We said no to the photo lady, but when she told us it would be one dollar per photo, we decided to pose for a photo and see how it came out.  Considering what you pay at amusement parks and other attractions, a dollar didn’t seem so bad, and we got a great photo of our family that we couldn’t have gotten otherwise.  But even with this pleasant experience in Tam Coc, the scenery and experience simply can’t compare to Trang An.  In Trang An, we had route 1 pretty much all to ourselves (done at 8:30 am on a weekday), although we shared route 2 with a fair number of Vietnamese tourists (8:30 am on a Saturday).  It seems local tourists come on weekends, and route 3 was the most crowded route.  But when compared to Tam Coc, which we did during a Friday evening, even the most crowded points in Trang An weren’t so bad.  Tam Coc felt like a lazy river, with one boat after another all in a line.  If Trang An weren’t right in the same area, I’d happily endorse Tam Coc.  Tam Coc is beautiful, and we didn’t find the peddlers too bad, but if you only have one place to visit, Trang An is much better.  There are more caves, the scenery is just as good, and you can get out of the boat a few times to see temples and other sites of significance.  Overall, it’s just a much better experience.On the tipping and other ways that people are trying to make money, my advice is just go in with 10 dollars worth of Vietnamese money, and view it as the cost of enjoying your day.  Like I said, the photos cost a dollar.  A bag of fresh sliced mangoes cost a dollar (and was delicious).  A ‘good’ tip is four dollars.  A very nice tip is eight dollars.  If they give you a survey asking whether you were asked for a tip, check ‘no,’ and give them a tip when you return the survey, so you don’t have the awkward post-survey tip request.  Don’t let a few dollars ruin your day.  If you are lucky enough to be able to afford to be generous, be generous.

Très bel endroit, juste sublime !!!Mais .... au départ de la ville, nous sommes déçus par l'eau polluée et surtout, à la fin, les personnes sur leurs barques qui insistent pour vous vendre quelque chose à manger ou boire .... si vous dites non,  ça insiste encore et encore puis elles vous disent d'en prendre pour le / la rameur .... ça a tendance à être énervant bien que ce soit régulier dans le pays.Mais le paysage est ce qu'il est :)

L'escursione in sampang attraverso le tre lagune di Tam Coc è veramente assai bella anche se un po' lunga e se va affrontata con un minimo di accorgimenti.Le tre lagune sono molto simili ad un fiume dall'acqua praticamente immobile e circondate da un paesaggio incredibile dai caratteristici colli, tanto simili alle isolette della baia di Halong, dalle ripide pareti di rocce calcaree ricoperte di vegetazione.La prima laguna attraversa in parte il centro abitato, poi affianca una distesa di sepolture e poi si inoltra nell'aperto paesaggio, ma ben presto si giunge ad una ripida parete di rocce nella quale si apre una grotta/galleria in cui la barca entra e, dopo un breve tratto nella quasi oscurità, si esce nella seconda laguna, la più ampia e, a mio parere, la più suggestiva. E' una lunga conca oblunga completamente circondata dai caratteristici colli e, fra l'acqua e le alture, tante verdissime risaie in cui si aggirano candidi volatili: veramente idilliaco. Dopo un lungo tratto, una seconda galleria  porta alla terza laguna: poco più di un laghetto con le pareti quasi a picco e qui la barca gira per rifare il medesimo percorso in senso opposto. Soprattutto qui sono in attesa diverse barche-banchetti, che si accosteranno alla vostra per vendervi bibite ed altri prodotti. Noi ce ne siamo astenuti più che altro per paura di scarso igiene, quindi non abbiamo un'idea dei prezzi; comunque, nel caso, informatevi prima di accettare qualsiasi cosa e contrattate!Come dicevo il giro è molto bello, però va affrontato avendo ben chiare alcune cose: innanzitutto la barca è molto piccola, con i bordi quasi a pelo dell'acqua e con il fondo piatto: quindi risente sensibilmente di ogni vostro minimo movimento. Una volta occupato il vostro posto dovrete restarvi quasi senza potervi muovere per poco meno di due ore (tanto dura il percorso), soprattutto senza poter distendere le gambe. Tutto il tragitto è in pieno sole (salvo, ovviamente le gallerie) quindi è importante, prima d'imbarcarsi, spalmarsi di abbondante crema solare, disporre di un cappello (ottimi quelli conici vietnamiti che potrete acquistare quasi ovunque) ed essere ben provvisti d'acqua. Infine tenete presente che il movimento è dato da un rematore (quasi sempre una rematrice) posto a poppa che stranamente muove i remi con i piedi; purtroppo la nostra accompagnava ogni spinta con un colpo secco contro la struttura che generava un rumore che alla fine ha finito per essere piuttosto ossessivo.Comunque, a parte questi inconvenienti, è un'esperienza molto bella, specie quando ci si inoltra nelle gallerie, quasi al buio (l'unica luce proviene dalle estremità), con il basso soffitto che a tratti pare toccarci e poi quando, all'esterno, ci si sente davvero immersi nella natura.

The view on the top is amazing and beautiful. Just be aware of the parking scam on the road before you enter. There are lots of people trying to stop you and ask you to park inside. Some of them even blow a whistle to stop you. We were ask for money immediately after we get off the bike. Just keep going to the end and there are free parking in front of the entrance.

Went as a privat tour, because you can not go to these 2 places together as a group.Costed us 160 USD, pic upfrom the old quarter.Beautiful area, with a stunning view when you get to the top.The climing to the top can be a little hard, but If at normal health you should be okay. Be carefull If hot and the sun is hard.Can be very crowded.

Nous venons de faire la balade en bateau à Tam Coc. C’est joli et reposant après Hanoï! Par contre, premier racket au bout où une vendeuse ambulante nous a « forcés » à acheter plusieurs choses à manger et boire pour la rameuse! Nous avons décidé que ce serait une partie de son pourboire! A  l’arrivée, notre rameuse a réclamé son tip, et nous courait après car elle voulait au minimum 50000 dôngs, ce qu’on a refusé d’atteindre, vu ce qu’on lui avait déjà payé avant, par principe! Cette fin est un peu désagréable!

We were planned to visit Trang An. However, on sunday many local peeps also go to Trang An and visit temples and Bhudist pagoda... so the line was very long (several hours). So, we decided to go to Tam coc in stead. Since it is a new crop season, the party is not quite mature or lush. The boat ride in Tam coc is operated by the Vietnamese government. Ticket is sold at the counter for 200,000 dongs per person or less than $10 usd. The boat operators are all different ages, the older one seems to be crumpy. The entire boat ride is about 2 hours with the view of lying dragon mountain, caves, etc.At the end of the boat ride, the boat operator will ask you for tips, buying food or drinks for them. You don't have to do it if you don't want to... just say no and keep walking away.We did tip our boat operator extra 200,000 d for $10 usd. They did try to ask for more but we just said "no".There are some local people hanging out on their own boat and trying to sell your photos at the end of your boat ride. Again, you don't have to buy it. We ended up buying our photos , 3 for 20,000d or about $1USD. Trang An is operated by private company. Therefore, the boat operators there will not ask you for extra tips or buying drinks. However, it is very popular with local peeps. So, if you are planning to visit Trang An, you should avoid sundays.

We zijn hier drie dagen geweest en heerlijk vermaakt! Met een scootertje of fiets ben je overal snel, mooie plekken gezien en je moet zeker even naar bird valley! Aangenaam verrast hierdoor.De rijstvelden zijn overigens ook prachtig, mooi groen en heel uitgestrekt! De Mua Cave ook leuk om te zien, wordt wel toeristisch allemaal maar goed je moet wat! Zeker een aanrader!
We spent an afternoon on a boat heading up the river to Tam Coc. It’s a great way to experience the river and the rice terraces close up. Worth a visit if you are in the area.

Bonito paseo en Barça empujado por mujeres remando con los pies, similar al de trang an, siendo este más corto con menos cuevas y con arrozales a los laterales, para mi gusto si solo se va a hacer uno más recomendable trang an.Recomiendo hacerlo al atardecer ya que hay bastante menos barcas y hay mucho más silencio, ten preparado algo de propina porque llegando no pararan de recordártelo.
Le site moins connu est tout de meme tres.visite. le.prix proche pour deux de 500000 vnd avec le pourboire est prohibitif, mais vaut le coup d oeil.

Es muy espectacular ver como las remeras riman con los pies mientras con una mano llevan la sombrilla y en la otra el teléfono.Es un paseo que dura unas 2 horas que tiene un precio un poco alto en comparación a otro cercano mucho más espectacular y bien organizado.
Boat trip across ricefields and rocks is an interesting & nice experience. The nature is great, quiet, relaxing. Not a "must" but worth experiencing.

À Tam Coc se trouvent de magnifiques paysages avec des montagnes karstiques sortant de l'eau. De nombreux tours en barque s'effectuent depuis l'embarcadère de Tam Coc. Ce sont majoritairement des femmes qui vous emmènent faire un tour sur l'eau pendant environ 1h30 aller retour en ramant avec leurs pieds. La ballade est magnifique. C'est cependant relativement cher, depuis l'embarcadère de Tam Coc il faut compter environ 15 euros pour 2 à payer juste à côté de l'embarcadère, dans le minuscule bâtiment où sont vendus les tickets. Il est visiblement d'usage de laisser un pourboire à la rameuse car elle ne perçoit qu'une maigre partie du prix du billet.Il existe également un autre embarcadère sur la route qui mène à la pagode Bich Dong, sur la droite dans un virage quelques centaines de mètres avant d'arriver sur le site de la pagode. Ici il y a moins de monde, on paye directement le billet à une femme qui semble centraliser tout ça car il n'y a pas de bureau de vente des tickets. Il m'en à coûté moins de 7e pour 2. Je ne sais pas si c'est négociable car je n'ai pas essayé. J'ai également laissé un pourboire. La ballade en elle même est très jolie également, peut être un peu moins impressionnante mais j'ai apprécié le fait qu'il y ait eu moins de monde.Je conseille de faire la ou les ballades soit tôt le matin soit en fin d'après midi vers 16h ou 17h pour éviter la foule et la violence d'un soleil ardent.

There were two factors that made the visit to Tam Coc a little disappointing. The first was the smog that was persistent through the entire visit to Hanoi and the second was that the rice paddies were not yet sprouting (which is just timing). This meant the area was more of a dirty grey and brown, rather than blue skies and bright green rice paddies. But, despite the smog, lack of sprouting rice and an over zealous guide for the day, Tam Coc was still a great experience and a unique place to visit. The boat ride was enjoyable despite the locals trying their best to make a sale by offering snack whilst out on the water, then when declined, pressuring us to buy for our rower. Good experience all in all.

The scenery and tour down the river were amazing and well worth doing. Just be warned that at the end there is a little floating market where you are expected to buy drinks. Once brought you will find the prices have been inflated 1000% so be strong and say no. Also your tour guide will ask for a tip before he gets you back. Again be strong and tell him/her that you will tip at the end. Otherwise if they are unhappy with the amount they won’t take you back ashore. But please don’t let this put you off. The scenery is well worth it.

Since it is lunar new year, we were told that Sapa valley tour was not available. So instead we went for this Hua Lu and Tam Coc day trip. Hua Lu tour was mainly to visit 2 temples and was nothing to scream about but the afternoon trip to Tam Coc made this a worthwhile day trip. In Tam Coc we were the taken on a Sampan  ride along the river. It’s close to two hours of rowing by a lady who used her two legs to row instead of her upper limbs.  The boat ride went through three caves along a small river, and along the way, the scenery was beautiful with limestone mountains dotted here and there on both sides of the river. We went through some rice fields too. This is a tour not to be missed if you visit Hanoi.
Well worth the day trip and travel. Very scenic boat ride but marred by the somewhat aggressive haggling for selling of photos and food/drinks/souvenirs along the way.

Magnifique endroit je recommande ! Nous y sommes allés en fin de journée pour qu’il y ait moins de monde. Sinon c’est l’enfer.Un truc que je trouve pas très correct c’est les marchands ambulants qui t’accoste avec leurs barques pour te vendre à boire ou a manger et qui insistent jusqu’à nous demander d’acheter quelque chose pour notre rameuses... qui d’ailleurs a la fin n’était pas contente du pourboire qu’on lui a donné.

We rented a boat for the canal/cave tour thing down the road from The Temple of the Cave, roughly west of Tam Coc. We paid 200,000 dong / $8 for a boat for 3 people. The lady whom we paid for the service kept reminding us not to forget to give the lady who will be rowing our boat her tip at the end of the boat ride. We said yes and off we went. After 2 two caves and a whole lot of rowing, we stopped at a dead end. Suddenly our boat lady started to pull stuff out from her plastic bag and wanted to sell gifts to us. She was very persistent but we politely said no, thank you. After about 10 minutes, she started rowing and off we went again back to the starting point. Towards the end of the journey, we decided to give her 40,000 dong as a tip for her hardwork. So, we happily handed the money to her. The next we knew she threw the money on the ground and started screaming something in Vietnamese and pulled out 100,000 dong to show to us. It was only then we realized that she wanted 100,000 dong for tip which for us was quite ridiculous because 100,000 dong is half of what we paid for the boat tour. How is that considered as a tip? We took the money from the ground and gave it to her again. And for the second she threw it back to the ground. At then point, we see no point arguing with her. So my friend picked up the money and started to walk away. If she didn't want the money, we figured we might as well give it to somebody else. There were no other people at the pier at that moment because it was at the end of the day and everybody else had left including the boss lady. The boat lady was screaming and yelling as we were heading towards the road. One lady from across the street ran over to speak with her. We let the boat lady talked to her and then we explained to her our situation. Luckily, she was able to understand us in English and counted the money. We explained that what we were giving the boat lady more than 15% of what we would normally give as tip in the US. From the sound of her voice when she was talking to the boat lady, she seemed to explain to the boat lady to just take the money.So, moral of the story, just be careful when you want to rent a boat at this location. I guess you can verify with others what the correct of tipping should be before you get on the boat. However, I somewhat agreed with my travel companion that they should just charge a standard 300,000 dong for the boat ride and be done with it. No more tip etc. All in all, the whole boat ride was interesting. The view was fantastic at least for me anyway. The only reason why I went to Tam Coc was because I had seen cliffs like Halong Bay in Thailand and in parts of Malaysia. So Tam Coc would be a different ball game. You should do it at least once in your lifetime if you get the chance.

My experience of Tam Coc in January was that it was quite peaceful. Reviews do talk about crowds of people, but that was not my experience. We biked from our hotel (Tam Coc Garden Resort -- a fabulous boutique hotel with lovely views) to the boating area. It was actually a really nice bike ride and only about 5 km from our hotel. We were warned to get there early, and we were there by 8 am and were one of the first boats on the water. I think this was key, as when we arrived back after our 1.5 hour boat ride, there were many more boats heading down the river. Some of the caves that the boats go through are quite narrow so I am not sure how they manage that when there are a lot more visitors. I am a bit claustrophobic, and so I was worried about the caves. But it was not a problem for me. The caves are not high, but they are not very long either and you soon literally see the light at the end of the cave. I thought the trip was very beautiful. It was a grey, misty day, so not hot out on the water. The women who row, use only their feet. They are extraordinarily dexterous. Another woman was rowing her boat with her feet while peeling fruit that she was preparing to sell to boat passengers. It was amazing to watch her. Other people have talked about aggressive selling. I had heard about this as well before we visited. Again, this was not our experience at all. Only someone taking photos approached us at all and we just said no. But we went as part of a small group with a guide, and perhaps when he arranged for the boats he made clear what the arrangement should be. I thought this was a very pleasant way to see the scenery in Ninh Binh. We also got to see men at work preparing parts of the river area for rice planting. I will say that in January the rice fields are just brown mud. So while you may not have the crowds, you also don't have the dramatic green of the rice fields. Bring your camera.

We arrived very tired after a long bus journey but were warmly greeted with a long cool drink and fresh fruit.Our room was large with two King size beds, there was a fridge with cold drinks and water.  The bathroom was large with a shower and toiletries.  We were given advice as to where we should eat that night and were given bicycles to ride to the village.  The next day we were so pleased with our stay and accommodation that we asked for another night's stay but this wasn't possible but we were told that the bungalow Homestay next door could accommodate us and so our luggage was transferred.  Breakfast was included in our tariff.  Everything was perfectly clean.We were given help as to what we should do whilst in the vicinity and were introduced to another member of the family who was willing to drive us around and guide us generally.  We shared the car with another couple and this worked well.

Tam Coc is a fantastic place to stay for your time in Ninh Binh (much nicer than Ninh Binh itself), but the boat trip is probably the worse of the three (the others being Trang An and Van Long). It's the busiest, as it's the one most day-trippers from Hanoi and tour busses stop at, and it also has the least interesting scenery. I'd still recommend it, but if you only wanted to do one - don't do this one.
Rustige boottocht in mooie omgeving! Alleen opletten als je terug komt, bij ons probeerde de kerel die onze boot roeide 100k als entree ook al hadden we dit al bij de entree betaald.
We are spend two day for visited at Tam cok area, absolutely enjoy out time there, our childs very happy when they are went out with a lot break taking views. Will be back if have opportunity.

Stayed here for 3 days. Has got all you need: rice fields, hiking places, tours by boat, tourists to meet and enjoy or to hate...Has got the center town with lots of restaurants, bikes for rent, coffee shops etc
Prima om even te zijn. Zeker buiten de toeristische attracties om kan je prima wandelen en lekker dwalen. Alle gratis tempels zijn de moeite waard om langs te wandelen.

2016年にチャンアンを観光し、今回はタムコックに行ってみました。双方共にボートでの遊覧ではありますが、見えてくるもの、感じるものは違います。タムコックは周囲の環境が素朴で牧歌的、田舎の風景の中を進むといった様。チャンアンはやや冒険的というか頭の当たりそうな洞窟を潜ります。タムコックを選ぶのなら、多少、体力が必要ですがハンムアに登ることを薦めます。ボートで進んだ水路を上から眺めることが出来、より立体的に楽しむことが出来ると思います。

Après une journée passée à Hanoï ou nous avons été désagréablement surpris par le nuage de pollution permanent qui entoure la ville nous étions à la recherche de verdure et de nature. Outre la traditionnelle croisière sur la baie d'Ha Long, que je vous conseille vivement, nous avons vu sur des blogs le site de Tam Coc (surnommé Ha Long terrestre en raison de la similitude des formations rocheuses). Gros coup de coeur ! La balade de 2h sur le lac dans un bateau avec une rameuse le matin est simplement splendide (120k par personne + 150k pour un bateau pour 3) puis nous avons loué des vélos à un prix modique (50k par personne pour 4h) et nous nous sommes baladés autour du lac. Le panorama de rizière est magnifique et les chemins sont très praticables.Conseil : organisez le trip vous même sans passer par un tour opérateur ça vous coûtera bien moins cher et vous éviterez le monde (par exemple la balade sur le lac le matin au lieu du début d'aprem comme toutes les agences le proposent). Certains blogs conseillent d'aller d'Hanoi à Ninh Binh en train, nous avons opté pour un taxi car de 8 personnes à 200k par personne (site internet de Baolau). Il vous amène directement à l'embarcadère de Tam Coc. Seul (gros) bémol de cette sortie : tous les locaux vous voient comme un portefeuille géant et cherchent à vous vendre des trucs parfois à la limite de la vente forcée (accostage par un bateau durant notre balade sur le lac qui ne voulait plus nous lâcher).

Definitely recommend going to the Tam Coc area, as it is a stunning place. I agree, however, with the other reviews: when you do a boat tour don’t buy anything (unless of course you just want to spend money) and give the rower a tip that you think is fair. They will ask for more anyway and try to make you feel bad. Touring around the area by yourself on a motorbike is also a lot of fun but again they make you pay for everything, from parking the bike to entrance fees. Just be prepared for this and you’ll have a good time ;)

The Tam Coc trip is surely a must do when you are in the area. Spectacular scenery and surprisingly clear and clean water. I do agree with some of the other reviews, The ladies at the turn around point are very pushy and you have to be firm. Don’t get caught in there trap. They will ask you to buy a drink for the rower as she might be thirsty after the long paddle. Lol. The asking or rather demanding of a tip is also a bit annoying. The worst of all, the ladies selling the tickets. They are simply just rude. However, this is still a great trip and don’t be discouraged by some reviews. A definite must see

A short drive from Hanoi will take you to this place where you could sit on a small boat with 2 or 3 others and enjoy a tour for over 90 minutes while it is ridden by a man or a women using legs. We never saw them using their hands during the entire ride. The most exciting part of the ride is when you sail through 3 pitch dark tunnels with the help of a torch. We saw in pictures beautiful greenery of paddy fields in both sides of the river/canal, but when we took the ride it was just after the harvest and we missed that. It could be advisable to check the proper time before visiting the place. The large number of small mountains in the area has named this as the Ha Long Bay in the land. Our excellent guide Quy ( Cookie Monster) explained these mountains are younger as they are only 2 and 1/2 Million years old compared to the 5 million years old mountains in Ha Long Bay.

We did a small group day tour from Hanoi which included a visit to Hoa Lu temple, bike tour & the boat ride on the tam coc river. There were a lot of tourist & boats on the river but the scenery was still gorgeous. A very relaxing  90 minute ride along the river passing through 3 caves & then returning the same way. The boat trip is very relaxing, but on a hot day make sure you have a hat & a drink. Yes there are the ladies on the boats trying to sell you snacks then trying to make you feel guilty & buy drinks & food for your boat paddler as a tip, but just politely say no & look away. The boat paddlers on this tour are accustomed to demanding a tip before the end of the tour. It’s rude, but unfortunately this seems to be the way they operate so go with the expectation you’re going to be haggled for a trip, smile & try not to be too bothered by it. I waited till I was just about to get off & handed over 50,000. My friend got very angry with the whole “scam” and the demand for a tip and unfortunately this seems to be all she remembers of the day.I don’t let it bother me, we are in Vietnam, the culture is different, there are a lot of scenarios where you might feel ripped off. We don’t want to encourage this but at the same time as a traveller you’re always gonna to find yourself in these situations. We are their livelihoods. I just remind myself that I’m a foreigner & I have to try & do as the locals do, smile, treat everyone with respect regardless of the way they treat you & don’t let it ruin your experience.

Spent a night in tam coc. Would be best if you could ride a motorcycle to get around else by bicycle is fine though you will be limited to the nearer attractions. Very beautiful country side with nice scenery. A small downside was that the area blacked out for about 2 hours in the night. But I believe this should improve in time to come.

Magnifique site, l’entrée est assez cher, les rameurs vont harcèleront pour des pourboires et des arnaques sont mises en place entre les rameurs et les vendeurs ambulants qui vous demanderont d’acheter une boisson pour le rameur.Le site vaut largement le détour malgré qu’il soit entaché par la pollution et cette arnaque
Εξαιρετικα ομορφο μεροςΚαταπράσινα Αρκετά τουριστικο αλλα αξιζει να το δειτε Θελει προσοχη στις συναλλαγές οπως παντού στο Βιετνάμ

Tam Coc Si trova circa 90 km a sud di Hanoi: è un villaggio accanto ad un laghetto e ad un fiume che gira calmo e tortuoso  per vallette tra le solite colline calcaree.Scopro che ha il nick name di Halong terrestre.La navigazione del fiume è diventata un business. Dal 2014 è inserito nel Wold Heritage Unesco.Buon buffet al ristorante The Long, poi gita in barca sul fiume con tinozze metalliche portate da indigeni che remano pedalando! Sono 6 km tra andata e ritorno.Il giro si dipana in un paesaggio di colline e risaie passando sotto tre grotte: Hang Ca - Hai - Ba, lunghe ciascuna un centinaio di metri.Al termine dell'andata, sono in agguato alcune barche colme di ogni genere di ristoro che vengono all'abbordaggio, proponendo bevande, frutta, snack … e i più audaci, se tu ringrazi e rifiuti … ti chiedono di offrire almeno un drink al tuo vogatore!
Mooi landschap, wij hadden helaas wat nevel wat het uitzicht belemmerde. Maar als de zon schijnt extra indrukwekkend

There is no doubt that this is one of the most beautiful landscapes on planet Earth. You will want to capture the sights with every tool at your disposal and remember the journey when you are sat back at your office desk breathing in wafts of poorly imitated Chinese food back in your home town.The only problem with this 1.5hr boat ride is that after paying a hefty price (relative to local salaries) you will encounter two scenarios in which your money is the target. Firstly, half way along your journey an associate of the boat rower will latch on to your boat and begin one of the hardest sells I've witnessed to date, quoting random numbers for drinks and snacks which you most likely don't need. If you do, of course that's great, go ahead and purchase them. The problem is when you go prepared for the journey like we did, the sell turns into begging, turning into sympathy for the boat rower to buy her a drink as she may be thirsty. Secondly, towards the end of your journey your rower will start shouting tip repeatedly, point at a barrel with the ticket you provided them in the first place and continue to shout tip, followed by random numbers. Ours liked the sound of 150,000 followed by 20,000 when I had laughed. Although she didn't understand, I told her that it was rude to ask for a tip, we would have tipped 100k, but instead gave her 20k. Unfortunately I fear that even this amount we gave encourages future bad behaviour. Therefore I would suggest for those that don't wish to purchase anything along their journey to have a small bottle of water to provide the rower when they pretend to be thirsty and be firm in telling the sellers you don't wish to buy peanuts and a curious green drink for 100k. Secondly, it would be amazing to use google translate to let the rower know that their hard work is appreciated and they would overall receive more tips by not asking for tips, and when given a tip, asking for more. It's odd as the rest of the people in Tam Coc are actually the friendliest we have encountered in Vietnam!Be prepared and enjoy one of the most beautiful sights this planet has to offer.

This was my second time in Tam Coc and as my previous time I wanted to do the boat trip again with my girlfriend. To be honest I love it, it's really relaxing and it takes quite a long time, around 1 hour and half.Our rower was really nice and when we were near the end of the trip, other boats approached us in which there were women trying to sell stuff to us for her. I personally felt inclined to give her something to drink, for the tiring job, but obviously not the "entire package" of 100k of fruits, drink and crisps they tried to sell us. I haggled to 20k for a red bull and we continued our trip. In the middle of the return ride I gave her another 20k as a tip. She tried to ask for more when we where almost at the end, but we declined saying that we already gave her a tip. She didn't complain and we arrived safe at the end. Reading others' reviews I feel we've been lucky, but it's totally true you feel you have to give something to them.

The 1 and a half boat trip on the river was relaxing and invigorating. The rower was very skillful with her feet. Maybe I was lucky as not so many boats were out there. Going inside the caves was awesome. The downside was the ladies trying to sell you food and drinks on the way back. If you say you don't want anything they will insist you to by something to your lady paddler. Hard to get rid of them. I was not so impressed by the scenery since I had gone to Halong Bay the day before. So plan to go to Tam Coc before you head to Halong. I hired the services of a tour company in Hanoi for this trip and it included a bike ride in the surroundings that was fun.

From the look of it the start point looks commercial and unappealing. But go ahead and take that boat ride. For you will see - these women who peddle boats with their feet and use hands to hold umbrella/ fan themselves, caves on the Tom Coc river and the going through them on a 45min ride. Worth the time!
The view are breathtaking. The boat ride is long, and it is not very comfortable (to the least). Try taking a life vest and use it as a cushion.

The attraction in Tam Coc is the two hours bamboo boat ride on the Hoàng Long river to explore the Tam Coc caves. If you like nature where there is water and sky and mountains, you will enjoy the bamboo boat ride which can be quite monotonous since it’s not a luxury ride and on a very lowly seated shallow boat which is quite uncomfortable for most adults.

Just back from one and a half hour boat trip in Tam Coc. Nice scenery coupled with a cool weather today made our boat ride very enjoyable. But I must admit it take too long and was very uncomfortable sitting on the hard bamboo sheet toward the hour mark. The Trip was also ruined by the "scam" at the end of the river before the boat lady making a U turn. A boat peddler offers us some fruit and drink which we refused. But she immediately hand over a drink and some fruit to our boat lady telling us we should pay for it and was expensive too.Bicycle trip around the villages in he backdrops of limestone hill was good. My son enjoyed the ride very much albeit was a short 45minutes.My suggestion is maybe boat trip could be halved rather then full one and half hour. May be tourist be offer a mixture of bicycles ride into the lime stone area follows by boat trip out or vice versa.
在11月造訪陸龍灣該說是不會太熱的天氣，但還是被曬紅了皮膚，整體來說這是個舒適的行程，除了貪得無厭的要小費的船夫及拍照賣相片的人。

Relax and take in the mystic-like nature that surrounds you  ...That's the experience I felt when rowing around Tam Coc River. It's a 1hr 30min boat ride which cost 390 000vnd for a couple. Along the way, there are local people who sell refreshments and snacks on the river. Also, expect to a tip after the tour. Apart from that,  a sight worth checking out👌

Este lugar se conoce como Bahia de Halong de interior. Es una paseo en unos barcos de remos llamados sampan. El pasaje es precioso, entre arrozales, espectaculares formaciones rocosas y varias cuevas. Me ha encantado!
Le site est magnifique et la balade agréable et reposante. Nous avons moins apprécié de voir notre rameur quémander un pourboire alors que nous avions payé au départ. Un vrai traquenard!!!!

Die Stadt ist klein und ab 10:00 Uhr voll mit asiatischen Touristen. Am besten startest Du die Tour mit dem Ruderboot vor 09:00 Uhr. Dann ist es noch ruhig auf dem Fluss. Du kannst entspannt die Fahrt genießen und hast gute Chancen, ein paar beeindrucken schöne Bilder zu machen.Vorsicht, kein Trinkgeld (Tip) an die Bootsfahrer geben. Die werden schnell unverschämt.

Having seen the beautiful brochure photographs of Tam Coc we had dropped the temples in our standard sugested 1 day itinerary of Ninh Binh Motor bike tour and instead included Tam Coc boat ride along with Trang An Grottoes. We were warned by Tommy (who arranged the 1 day motor bike tour) that the Tam Coc was not nearly as impressive or beautiful as Trang An- but we did not listen and ended up going to both boat rides to conclude at the end of the tour that he was right. Yes- it is beautiful, calm and pleasant. However if you have time for only 1 boat ride in Ninh Binh we would strongly suggest Trang An and giving Tam Coc a miss. We waited till the end of the long boat ride hoping that the yellow colored (or at least green colored) paddy fields shown in brochures would show up- however we were disappointed.The boat riders of Tam Coc have a novel way of paddling with their legs- interesting to watch. They are supposedly more aggressive in asking for tips as compared to Trang An boat riders- but our boat lady was not and she seemed to be happy with the tip we gave. Unlike Trang An , Tam Coc boat ride does not have any stops and no impressive grottoes - altogether a disappointing experience. However if you do not have time for Trang An and you have already included this in your itinerary , go ahed and enjoy. Try to avoid the hot sun and apply lots of sunscreen for your motorbike tour and boat rides in Ninh Binh
Vriendelijke bevolking, leuke winkeltjes en goede restaurantjes, voor elke beurs wat. Leuk om de omgeving te verkennen op fiets of scooter

It is a rather scenic ride along the Ngo Dong River .. the weather, whilst it was sunny, was cool .. It's been said tt Tam Coc & its spectacular canals, karstic rock formations, rice fields stretching along the Ngo Dong River, became popular after "Indochine" won the Oscar in 1993, and word spread tt some scenes were shot in Tam Coc .. whatever it may be, it does provide tourists w an option to Halong Bay .. by comparison, it is a less known natural site ...We marvel at the formation of the limestone karst as we enter the 3 limestone grottoes .. Hang Ca (first cave) to Hang Hai (second cave) and finally, Hang Ba (third cave) ... Some interesting facts .. :Hang Ca (1st cave) .. is the largest of the 3, with 127 meters long and an entry of 20 meters wide, and a height of 2 meters. We had to "duck" as some parts were very low .. it was quite an interesting experience .. Hang Giua (2nd cave).. also called Hang Hai, is 70 meters long, w multitude of estalagtitas on their roofs .. Hang Cuoi.. also called Hang Ba, is the smallest of the 3 with 45 meters in length .. We enjoyed this experience, as there were only the occasional  tourists in sight .. at least when we were there, tt autumn afternoon .. :)) .

Tra Tam Coc e Trang An preferite quest'ultima. A Tam Coc il giro in barca è carino, ma dura pochissimo (la nostra guida andava velocissima e abbiamo impiegato poco più di un'ora). Alla fine del giro c'erano donne che vendevano cibo e bevande e praticamente ci ha obbligati a comprare qualcosa (a caro prezzo) anche per la guida (che alla fine del viaggio ha preteso anche la mancia)

We visited Tam Coc River during our time in Hanoi as a day trip. Nature is definitely a must see! The tone of the green is so bright, it feels almost photoshopped. I advise going there before the rice harvest cause at the time we were there (in October), the crop was already done so the picturesque rice fields were not there anymore. Even with that, being in that nature was liberating. Quietness, green, yellow, blue... All together.However, in the middle of your journey, we came to a floating market where 10 ladies in 10 boats were trying to sell us the most ridiculous things; cola, sprite, peanuts, potato chips... If you say no, they point out your paddler (the guy or the girl paddling the boat) and tell you to buy it for her cause she is hungry or thirsty. If you say "no" to that, then they immediately unload their stuff to your paddler and ask you the money of the 100 things she got... We had to fight hard for that and in the end, there was an uncomfortable situation between our paddler and the paddler from another boat for an entirely different reason. We understand that the locals are poor and they can use whatever help they can get but giving each other a bag full of biscuits, cokes, gummy bears and asking us for the money was too much! Our paddler's attitude also didn't help the whole situation... Overall, it's a 50 50... If you're luck with your paddler, it's an amazing journey. If not, the 2nd half of it is full of uncomfortableness.
Tam Coc is absolutely gorgeous. Way less pushy and touristy than Trang An. Definitely a must!!The boat trip caterer’s for couples and not cramming 4 up on each boat. Absolutely beautiful.

Om 10 uur begonnen wij aan de tour over de Tam Coc rivier. De dames die voor ons peddelden deden dat zowel met hun armen als benen, erg grappig. We waren niet de enigen op het water maar hadden weinig last van de drukte. De tocht die ongeveer 1,5 uur duurde was mooi, en ging langs karstbergen (vergelijkbaar met Halong Bay maar dan op land) en rijstvelden, die meer onderwater stonden dan zichtbaar waren door de hevige regen. Onderweg zagen we nog aapjes door de bomen slingeren. Aan het einde van de heenweg varen bootjes met drinken etc. voor de toeristen.  Teruggekomen vroegen de dames een tip, en ook gelijk hoeveel het moest zijn. 50.000 dong dat is 2 euro. Zorg dus dat je dit cash bij je hebt.
ツアーを組んでいたハロン湾が前日台風のためにキャンセルになり、ホテルの人に相談したところ、こちらも湾があり、その中をボートに乗って通って行ける、かつ湾のある田畑の周辺を自転車で回ることもできるということで、タムコックに変更を決めました。聞くところによると第二のハロン湾と言われているそうです。市内からバスで1時間程度で行けるので終日かかってしまうハロン湾よりサクッと行けていいかも？と感じました。

Balade en barque très reposante et agréable. Petit bémol sur le fait que malgré que l’on ait payés l’activité, la conductrice de la barque est mécontente de son pourboire que nous lui avons donné gracieusement.

Si tratta di un luogo caratterizzato da enormi formazioni calcaree che si stagliano tra piccoli fiumi e risaie; si visita facendo un’escursione sul fiume Ngo Dong con una barca a remi tradizionalmente condotta da donne, lungo il percorso si attraversano tre grotte (Tam Coc significa, appunto, 3 grotte).Questo posto, la cui bellezza per alcuni supera quella della baia di Halong, è detto " Halong terrestre", in effetti il percorso è molto suggestivo e vale la pena effettuarlo.Purtroppo quando siamo andati noi, a fine gennaio, il tempo non era granchè, essendo inverno anche in Vietnam.

We travelled here and then went on to go boating. I loved the fields and the idea that people are truely self sufficient. We visited a farm where rice wine was made and saw corn drying on the veranda has.  There are lots of little animals, pigs chickens etc.,  All very interesting as we live a fairly insular life.  Here its all in together, home and work in same home.   The people were as always kind and welcoming.

Nice small village in Ninh Bin region. Not as crowded as Ninh Bin city. It's good for recovering from Hanoi. I can recommend Tam Coc Rocky Bungalow. Nice Accommodation that is run by locals. They just started their business and are doing quite well. The bungalows are really nice. Absolutely breath taking is the view when you walk up Hang Mua. You can easily go there by bicycle (Nice ride through the rice fields). You should also visit Tran An. You can go there by motorbikes , bus or bicycle. We rented a motorbikes to go there. If you visit Tran An you can leave out the Boot tour, which starts in Tam Coc City centre. (Unfriendly, very crowed, rower begging for tip).

this place is the main attraction of Ninh Binh, it looks beautiful when the rice fields across the river or water way are yellow, it is a real painting of the nature.we visited in Nov, the rice fields were already under water and we missed the beauty of rice fields but i really enjoyed the place, really worth to visit. perfect nature
Good way to unwind. Safe and reliable boats with life jackets provided. Makes for a nice break and great way to enjoy the limestone mountains. Take a hat and sit back and enjoy....

3 days ago  Quite nice to visit.  Obviously in the early evening is best when all the birds come back to nest. Heaps of birdlife and you're going to need a good zoom lens to get the prime shot.Boat ride along the lake was relaxing. Also check out mermaid cave which is also there

Cũng như các nơi khác ở miền Bắc, điểm tham quan tương tự như đi suối Yến chùa Hương, xếp hàng lên thuyền để vào, hai bên có núi, ruộng lúa hoặc sen/súng tuỳ mùa, cảm giác rất nên thơ nhẹ nhàng, mát mẻ xen tý hùng vĩ, tuy không đặc sắc nhưng cũng là lựa chọn hay của vùng miền.

Sắp xếp khu vực bán vé không phù hợp,có 2 loại vé,thăm quan 3 hang 250k và vé combo thăm quan 3 hang + xe điện đi chùa 350k.Không thông báo trước,xếp hàng đến lượt báo mua hạng vé 250k thì báo chỉ bán vé 350k, vé kia quay ra ngoài mua,có xe …
Cách làm du lịch bền vững cho các địa phương khác học hỏi.
Bên cạnh địa điểm Tràng An thì đây cũng là 1 nơi du lịch trên thuyền và tham quan phong cảnh. Cảnh đẹp và nên thơ nhưng chỉ ngồi và nhìn cảnh quan chứ không có dừng lại tham …
Khung cảnh đẹp nhưng chưa thật sự xuất sắc, cảnh quan được bảo tồn tốt, việc tham quan được tổ chức qui củ.
Nếu không muốn đi Tràng An thì có thể đi Tam Cốc tuy nhiên không có điểm dừng là các đền chùa đổi lại thì trần hang cao hơn không phải cúi đầu

Ở đây khác Tràng An vì là khu phố tây, cảnh sẽ nhiều núi đá nên chụp hình đc nhiều ảnh khá ổn, một vài hang nhưng cũng bình thường và ngắn, hang ko đc như tràng an, nước cũng rất trong, đặc biệt nhất ở đây là mùa súng, nhưng nên đi tầm 8h …
ở Nình Bình mình chỉ đi được Tam Cốc & Tràng An thôi không kịp tgian đi Hang Múa huhu, với giá vé 250k & cảnh vật tương đối giống nhau thì mình prefer Tràng An ơi. …

Khá đẹp nhưng không bằng Tràng An, giá vé bằng nhau (250k) mà thời gian chuyến đi chỉ có 1h30', lái thuyền chỉ giục đi nhanh cho hết chuyến, thuyền thì nhồi nhét 5 người nên rất chật. Điểm nhấn là view chỗ khe giời rất đẹp.

Khung cảnh thiên nhiên đẹp, đáng để ghé thăm. Về con người thì do chưa tốt, bạn sẽ bị mời chào các món hàng tranh thêu, giá cả tùy theo người bán nhìn mặt bạn từ 100.000- 250.000đ và thêm tiền tip chèo thuyền. Tiền tip không bắt buộc nhưng …

Đây là 1 địa điểm trong quần thể danh thắng Tràng An được Unesco công nhận, bao gồm: Tam Cốc - Bích Động, Tràng An, Cố Đô Hoa Lư, Chùa Bái Đính ... Về cảnh vật ở đây cũng giống như Tràng An nhưng sẽ không đẹp, rộng lớn và hùng …

Tam Cốc nhỏ và ngắn hơn Tràng An, Nên đi vào tháng 6 có lúa chín rất đẹp 1 năm chỉ có 1 vụ. Mùa hè thí sáng lên đi sớm hoặc chiều muộn sẽ đỡ mệt vì nắng nóng.Ở đây mọi thứ còn hoang sơ, phong cảnh thiên nhiên tuyệt vời.
Điểm du lịch có từ những năm 90 (theo lời kể của bác lái đò). Nơi đây thu hút nhiều khách quốc tế. Mình đến trước dịp Lễ hội sắc vàng Tam Cốc, là thời điểm tháng 5, 6 mùa lúa chín …
Ở đây có hồ sâu hơn 1m, khá rộng và mát, đi thuyền nhỏ qua 3 động, cả đi và về hơn 1 tiếng, nên đi mùa thu đông mát mẻ, tránh nắng nhé. Rất thư giãn. Bé nhà mình rất thích.

Được ví như “Vịnh hạ long trên cạn” hay “Nam thiên đệ nhị động”, nơi núi đá vôi Ninh Bình được thiên nhiên thật ưu ái khi ban tặng Tam Cốc Bích Động Ngồi trên đò xuôi dòng con sông Ngô Đồng vào ngày đầu xuân với cơn mưa phùn nhẹ …

Cảnh đẹp thì tháng 5 6 mùa lúa chín mới đẹp nhé. Còn các dịp khác thì bình thường, không đẹp bằng Tràng An. Giá 1 ng 120k vé tham quan và thuyền 150k 4ng VN hoặc 2 ng nc ngoài. …
Đi xuồng qua 3 hang. Cũng ko có dài lắm. Đi vô rồi đi ra tầm 2 tiếng. Giá ve 250k 1 nguoi

Mình ở phòng gần đây. Khu này khách tây hay ở. Đồ ăn uống cũng khó chọn. Đang sửa đường nên bụi bặm. Nắng. Dễ tắc đường. Khó đi bộ. Mình thấy nắng nóng nên không thăm quan mà lên tràng an tham qua Luôn
Đẹp người dân xếp hàng chờ đồ rất cực khổ phải chờ từ chiều hôm qua đến trưa mới đi được mn nên trò chuyện để hiểu thêm về cuộc sống nơi này

Mọi người thân thiện, khá mát mẻ, đi mùa lúa nữa thì rất ok, xung quanh có nhiều homstay, KS, tiệm tiện lợi. Giá vé ngang với đi Tràng An. Có một điểm trừ đó là người lái thuyền hỏi tip. ( nếu chọn giữa Tràng An Và Tam Cốc mình sẽ chọn đi Tràng An- ý kiến riêng thôi ạ)
Khá đẹp và mát mẻ nếu căn đúng giờ chiều. Nên tránh các ngày lễ hội vì trải nghiệm quá đông đi qua hang sẽ rất khó chịu, lại còn phải nhịn đi vệ sinh nữa. …

Đi Ninh Bình mà không ghé Tam Cốc thiệt là lãng phí, núi non thực sự đẹp và rất hùng vĩ, mình đi theo tour nên được mua vé ngôi thuyền được chính các cô chú nông dân chở đi, được nghe các cô chú kể chuyện nữa, thật tuyệt vời, Tam Cốc thực …

Tiếc 1 điểm trừ là vẫn có bạn phản ảnh có lái đò xin thêm tiền. Và có chị chụp ảnh dịch vụ, dù mình đã nói rất dứt khoát là mình ko lấy ảnh, ko chụp ảnh nhưng vẫn in ra, cho 1 đứa trẻ, bằng 1 cách nào đó mang đến tận homestay mình ở.

Ngày 04 Tết 🧧 tập thể lớp TD K35 Trường ĐH TDTT Bắc Ninh đã có buổi gặp mặt 20 năm, đầu Xuân năm mới rất ấm áp, vui và có ý nghĩa tại Ninh Bình. Nơi đây có nhiều địa điểm du lịch nổi tiếng để lưu lại những kỉ niệm đáng nhớ 🥰👍🏻☘️🧧🎇 Hẹn gặp lại vào thời gian gần nhất 😁 …
So với Tràng An thì không bằng Lái đò có xin thêm công chở 1 thuyền chở được 2ng VN …

Phong cảnh rất đẹp, tự nhiên. Ở đây chèo xuồng bằng chân rất riêng và độc đáo. Kinh nghiệm của mình là các bạn nên cẩn thận khi được mời chụp ảnh, thỏa thuận rõ ràng số lượng ảnh chụp và báo trước không nhận nếu chụp nhiều hơn. Đoàn mình …
So với Tràng An thì Tam Cốc thực tình là không bằng, hoạt động du lịch cũng hơi xô bồ & lộn xộn. Nhưng cũng có vẻ đẹp riêng, đi hết Tràng An rồi đi Tam Cốc vẫn không sao cả.

Tam Cốc thuộc huyện Hoa Lư Ninh Bình là một danh lam thắng cảnh rất kỳ vĩ mà thiên nhiên ban tặng cho mảnh đất và con người nơi đây. Đến Tam Cốc Bích Động du khách được chiêm ngưỡng vẻ đẹp của những dãy Núi hùng vĩ chạy quanh con sông êm …

Đi không đúng mùa nhưng vẫn rất thơ mộng

Tâm cốc Ninh Bình là một doanh lam thắng cảnh, nôn nước hữu tinh, rất đẹp. Đến với tam cốc du khách được ngồi thuyền dọc theo hành trình khoảng 7km đi qua ba hang đông  .Du  khách được thả hồn  Hòa  mình cùng với thiên nhiên xưa tan đi những lo nghỉ mệt nhọc  trong cuộc sống, và lưu lại cho mình những khoảnh khắc tuyệt đẹp
Đi thuyền trên sông rất là tuyệt vời,  có điều khu vực bán vé và khu vực quanh bến đò còn nhiều rác và quy hoạch chưa bài bản
Cảnh đẹp, giá vé đò và phí tham quan 150.000d/ người  Beautiful landscapes

Cảnh Tam Cốc rất đẹp, đặc biệt đi vào sâu bên trong có thảm rong dưới nước đẹp như trong tranh. Tuy nhiên, so với Tràng An thì cách làm việc bên Tam Cốc không chuyên nghiệp bằng. Ở Tràng An quy định một thuyền là 4 khách không phân biệt …
Du lịch trải nghiệm khá thú vị với sông nước, hang động khá đẹp, gần gũi thiên nhiên. Thời gian đi tầm 1 tiếng 30 phút, trong 1 buổi là đẹp
Theo cảm nhận thì Tam Cốc không đẹp như Tràng An, nghe nói đi vào mùa lúa chín thì tuyệt đẹp, nếu có thời gian sẽ ghé lại và cảm nhận

Tam Cốc vốn nổi tiếng cả vùng Ninh Bình. Vốn được coi là Hạ Long trên cạn. Từ trên cao nhìn xuống cảnh quan rất đẹp. Chèo thuyền ở đây không quá lâu (1.30'-1.45') như bên Tràng An(2.5-3h). 3 hang động về độ đẹp thì chỉ được điểm trung bình. TC sẽ đẹp và đáng tham quan nhất là vào mùa lúa chín (t4-t5).
Khá đẹp vào ngày nắng , dịch vụ hơi mắc xíu , các cô chú lái đò thân thiện , giá vé 120k / ng , muốn đi 2 ng/ đò thì mất 390k , nên đến vào mùa lúa chín tháng 4 5 thì đẹp nhất !!!

Cảnh đẹp, tự nhiên, nước trong nhìn thấy đáy, đúng với cái tên Hạ Long trên cạn. Kiến nghị nên đi thăm vào tháng 6 đến tháng 8 sẽ có đồng lúa 2 bên. Nếu đi từ tháng 11 đến tháng 1 thì nên đi xe đạp thay vì đi thuyền bởi gió lạnh và sẽ thăm được nhiều điểm hơn.

Tam cốc Bích động ở Ninh Bình thật nên thơ và đẹp, tuy nhiên địa phương cố gắng giữ được cảnh quang, không nên để xây dựng lấn chiếm và tạo 1 phong cách du lịch thật văn minh lịch sự, nhất là không để nạn mua bán chèo kéo khách cũng như công bố rõ giá giữ xe tránh tình trạng mỗi nơi lấy 1 giá.
Điểm đến thú vị khi tới Ninh Bình, bến đò ngăn nắp quy củ không chèo kéo. Có thể đưa gia đình đi khám phá khi tới Ninh Bình.
Cảnh quan thiên nhiên tuyệt vời, nếu cung cấp thêm dịch vụ cho thuê thuyền tự chèo thì trải nghiệm của du khách hoàn hảo hơn

Tam cốc - ba hang - hang này là hang xuyên qua núi. Mất khoảng 1h40-2h cho quãng đường đi về bằng thuyền. Rất đẹp nhất là mùa lúa chín tháng 4-5. Đi sẽ nắng và siêu nóng trong mùa hè tháng 6-7-8. Những tháng hè nên đi vào sáng sớm hoặc …

So với Tràng An thì Tam Cốc đẹp hơn nếu đi vào thời điểm lúa chín, 2 bên thuyền đi sẽ đẹp hơn là khi đi vào những lúc họ đã gặt. Vấn đề đi thuyền cũng thoáng hơn so với Tràng An, có thể bao thuyền để đi 2 …
Một trải nghiệm thú vị, ngắm nhìn núi non hùng vĩ. Cảm giác mình nhỏ bé giữa thiên nhiên, thật sự tuyệt vời.

Tam Cốc là du thuyền trên sông đi qua 3 hang. Dịch vụ ở Tam Cốc ko nhiều như Tràng An, ngồi thuyền mùa nắng này cũng khá là phê ạ… vừa phê cảnh, vừa phê nắng. Các bác chèo thuyền cũng nhiệt tình, mọi người có thể nhờ bác chụp ảnh. Nếu đi 2 …
