Una excursión muy bonita dos horas y media desde la ciudad de Hanoi. Un valle entre las montañas que puedes hacer excursión con canoa. Bonita experiencia por un embalse gigantesco entre montañas, unos paisajes muy pintorescos donde disfrutas haciendo fotografías increíbles.

Très cool, la balade en barque est de 1h30 pour 250000dong par personne. Seul chose un peu incomprehensible est qu'il faut aller acheter les billets à 5min de voiturette (gratuit et il y en a tout le temps), pas de vente direct là où est l'entrée des barques.  Le paysage est magnifique, c'est bien un décor de film !

Eine sehr schöne Bootfahrt. Ich habe die Tour im Dezember gemacht, dementsprechend Farblos war die Fahrt aufgrund fehlender Vegetation. Die Verkaufsshow (Stickgemälde/Taschen) unterwegs fand ich weniger schön, diese war aber nicht aufdringlich. Mir hat die Bootsfahrt in Trang An wesentlich besser gefallen.

Experiencia y paisajes increíbles! Merece mucho la pens aunque aconsejo ir por libre si es posible. Los tiempos en circuito guiado son limitantes y tienes que seguir las indicaciones sin dilación. Algo a destacar es que son MUY puntuales, positivo para ser organizados y que te dé tiempo a todo pero no deja opción para nada a la improvisación. Hay opción para dormir allí, pero al estar a menos de tres horas de Hanoi, en mi caso, es preferible ida y vuelta. En caso de ir con niños, puede ser opción pasar una noche, pero hay poco donde escoger más que tranquilidad y paseos.eso sí, rodeados de paisajes especiales y únicos. Si quieres alejarte del ruido y la multitud, es muy buena opción

Bella visita nella natura anche se secondo me il giro in barca è un pó troppo lungo essendo che dopo un pó quello che vedi è sempre uguale,col fatto che la barca è a remi il giro dura un bel pó e la seduta non è comodissima. Peró per il prezzo che si paga lo consiglio vivamente.

Also an sich eine wunderschöne Tour, viele gute Aussichten. Das einzige ist dass die Bootsfahrer dort gegen Ende penetrant nach Trinkgeld fragen, und wenn es Ihnen zu wenig ist dann leicht sauer werden (unsere wollte 100.000D pro Person...)

Schaut in der früh vorbei - dann habt ihr das Erlebnis für euch alleine. Und lasst euch nicht von der netten Omi vor Ort 50k fürs Parken abluchsen. Die Geschäfte auf dem Weg dorthin lassen euch gratis parken ;)

Tam Coc ou Trang An ? Faites les deux si vous pouvez,  mais si votre temps est compté, Trang An est franchement plus intéressant. Pour un prix inférieur (250.000 dongs) vous aurez droit à une promenade plus longue ( environ 3h), et surtout,  en choisissant le parcours n°3, vous traverserez "Dot cave". Cette grotte, longue d'un kilomètre (!) fait forte impression et nécessite de souvent baisser la tête car la barque passe assez près du plafond. Tam Coc reste néanmoins intéressant,  surtout en période de floraison

ハノイに行ったらボートツアーに行ってみたいと知り合いに頼みニンビンに行って来ました。 事前に旅行社に予約を入れて市内の集合場所から７時頃出発 途中で朝食と休憩を挟んでニンビンに向かいます。 自分がタムコックを指定したのですがチャンアンのボートツアーになってました（笑） パンフとどうも違うと帰国してから調べたらチャンアンのボートツアーになってました（笑） ４人と船頭の５人乗りのボートで２時間以上の観光です。 岩山の下を頭を下げてくぐり抜けたり、川の中央に古い仏教の建造物が有ったり素晴らしい景色でした。 ハノイに出掛けたらハロン湾も素晴らしいがここも優劣付け難いほど素晴らしかった。 生憎の小雨模様でしたが知り合いと楽しく行けたので満足な旅でした。 ６月にハノイに行念願のタムコックボートツアーも体験 ここは舟が一回り小さいので船頭と３人乗りが基本のようで、変わってるのは何と手漕ぎボートでなく足漕ぎです（笑） 足を使うので両手が空くので便利な様子でした（笑） 田んぼの中をボートで川を遡上する姿は何とも癒されました。

Attrazione molto turistica ma molto rilassante che sembrerebbe dar da lavorare a molte famiglie in paese. Si tratta di un giro di un ora e mezza in un barchino a remi, guidato sapientemente con i piedi da persone del posto. Il paesaggio è molto carino. Adatto a coppie, perché permettono anche di salire solo in due, a differenza della stessa attrazione a Trang An, che è ancora più turistica e organizzata, e tende a riempire tutte le barche con 4 persone. La persona che guida normalmente non conosce l’inglese quindi ci saranno poche interazioni. Alla fine bisogna dare una mancia che sui 200.000 VND è più che sufficiente in due.

The landscape from the top of the mountains is just amazing, I managed to get to the top climbing 500 steps around sunset time. The pictures I got were just amazing. If you can't climb the step, you can go to the right site of Little Lake to enjoy the view of Louts.
Nice experience. In my opinion the best moment is a couple of hours before sunset, there are less people and you can enjoy better the views

コースは３種類あるらしく、私は一番短い90分のコースにしました。 船代とは別に船頭さんに1人2USD相当を渡す必要があります。このチップが船頭さんの給料となるみたいなので、船代と思った方が良いです。  船頭さんと一緒に漕いでも良いし、お任せでも良いです。途中から漕ぎましたが、結構疲れました。  3箇所位の洞窟は頭をかすめる低さで、面白いです。 途中には、キングコングの撮影ポイントもあり、映画好きな人には良いと思いました。 日差しがきついので、帽子を用意された方が良いですが、船には傘もあるので日傘として利用しました。  疲れましたが意外と楽しかったです。
三古碧洞的套票行程。150000盾的只有單程。要自己走路回來。我們買了350000盾行程。包含大部分景點約2-3個小時。來寧平一定要體驗一下。覺得不輸下龍灣。有另一種美。

ツアーで2時間のコースに参加。250,000ドンでした。 3月末で暑すぎず良い感じでした。直射日光照りつける真夏日だったらきついのかもしれません。  大人4人とおばちゃん1人でおばちゃんが手漕ぎでこいでくれます。混む時は2000艘も船が出るとか  中には大人5人のボートもいました。 稀に水に落ちる人もいるそうです。  水墨画の風景のようなところ、鳥の声を聞きながら、静寂に包まれて進むので自然を感じられてよかったです。 鳥の雛がボート近くで泳いで、時々潜って餌を取ったりしていました。 先が見えない洞窟をくぐり抜けていくのは、テーマパークのようなわくわく感がありました。洞窟の入り口に300メートル200メートルなどと距離が書いてあります。  陸に上陸してトイレもありますし、お寺のような所に金ぴかの閻魔様みたいなのが祀られて、地元の人はお参りをしていました。  2時間もあって暇すぎるので、ボートに乗っているオールをこいで帰りました。 ツアーガイドさんに1人1ドルチップを渡すように言われたので渡しました。  海外だけでなく、ベトナムの方にも人気の観光スポットだそうです。

Luogo davvero bello il fiume risalito con le barchetta piatte con i rematori con i piedi, l'acqua bassina e ai lati con le piante di loto e gli uccelli che beccano nell'acqua, poi i vari passaggi nele grotte. Poi il tempio e la scalinata fino al tempietto e il drago con tanti scalini da fare per raggiungere la sommità, e il panorama affascinante a 360 gradi, da un lato le acque ricoperte di fiori di loto e dall'altra il fiume in mezzo alla montagne in roccia a picco. Bello bello.

Tam Cốc offers a beautiful and peaceful boat ride through stunning limestone karsts and lush landscapes, making for an overall enjoyable experience. However, what really spoiled the trip for me was the constant pressure from the boat rowers to buy items and tip them, even though we had already paid for the entrance ticket. This felt intrusive and took away from the otherwise relaxing atmosphere.  Another downside is the intense heat on sunny days, which can be quite dangerous for skin burns. Thankfully, the boats provide umbrellas, which help to protect you from the sun, but it’s something to be aware of if you’re planning to visit during the hotter months.  Despite these issues, the natural beauty of Tam Cốc is still worth experiencing, but be prepared for a bit of hassle along the way.

Da wir am Tag zuvor berwits mit dem Boot die trockene HaLongBay besichtigen konnten, haben wir dieses mal die meiste Zeit der Wasserroute mit dem Fahrrad erkundet. Cool hier ist die andere Art der Fortbewegung durch die Bootskapitäne, die allein mit ihren Füßen die Boote bewegen. TamCoc hat für mich, besonders die ersten Meter, den Eindruck erweckt, hier wurde viel erschaffen um Touristen anzulocken, Trang An fand ich persönlich attracktiver (wobei natürlich auch hier alles um den Ticketschalter und den Parkplatz voll von Verkaufsbuden ist)
Leuke boot tocht door 3 grotten en langs een pagode. Wel een echte toeristische attractie gezien de hoeveelheid bootjes die er varen.

Cena za bilet na miejscu 250tys od osoby lub 180tys kupując przez internet. Wycieczka trwa do 2 godzin. Do Łodzi wchodzi od 2 do 4 turystów. W trakcie wycieczki można podziwiać porośnięte roślinnością,ogromne masywy wapiennych skał. Przeplywamy przez 3 niewielkie jaskinie. W paru miejscach dostrzegłem dzikie kozice, czaple czy niewielkie niebieskie ptaki. Podróż bardzo relaksująca, polecam każdemu. Uwaga - każdy wioslarz ma na pokładzie wiadro z różnymi tkackimi wyrobami i w pewnym momencie będzie próbował wam coś opchnąć. Najlepiej w ogóle się tym nie interesować albo wziąść do ręki i trzymać przez całą podróż. Absolutnie nie pytać o cenę bo się zaczną długie męczące negocjację 😜 Wracając do przystani, nasza wioslarka poprosiła o napiwek (to również standard) mieliśmy przyszykowane 20tys co jej nie usatysfakcjonowalo 😂 Zapytała o 100tys. Przetłumaczyłem jej translatorem ze jesteśmy biednymi turystami, zaśmiała się i wszyscy wróciliśmy do przystani w dobrych humaorach😉 Polecam😉

Tam Coc Boat Ride: A peaceful and scenic journey through rice fields and limestone karsts. The slow paddle boat ride along the Ngo Dong River lets you glide under caves and between towering cliffs. Especially magical in the early morning or late afternoon. A tranquil, almost surreal experience—like drifting through a painting. Bich Dong Pagoda: Nestled into the mountainside, this ancient pagoda complex blends nature and spirituality. Climb through mossy stone steps to reach hidden temples built into the rock. The view from the upper pagoda is serene. The ornate three-tiered gate, framed by karsts and lotus ponds, is picture-perfect. Quiet and atmospheric.

Tam Coc - Bich Dong  Paseo en barca con paisajes preciosos. Duracion de 1h30. Se pasa por 3 cuevas. Recomendable ir pronto por la mañana para evitar colas y por el calor que hace. Llevad paraguas para el sol y crema solar. Es curiosa su forma de remar. No utilizan los remos con las manos, sino con los pies.

搭船的景觀不錯，我是參加klook的寧平一日遊行程，下午坐船有兩個選擇，一個是長安，另一個是三古碧洞（Tam Coc），我選擇三古碧洞遊船，時間約50分鐘，記得帶傘，防雨又防曬。船一出發不到5分鐘，就有人要幫你拍照，直接無視即可，如果想拍，一張20k越南幣，比這個價格高，直接說不要買。小費可以不用給（他會給你“面腔”看），網路上說每位客人給船夫20k越南幣，但是，我們給的時候，導遊說“too more”，不知道是不是在暗示我們給太多了！？🤔😅自己斟酌吧。
went up to thebtop.of the limestone mt, very nice Paramount view of Bich Dong lotus pond

250.000 dong pour 1h30. Le paysage est très beau. Les fleurs de lotus sont ouvertes de 5h à 9h du matin. On s’arrête au bout du circuit et après quelques marches, ont a un point de vue magnifique. On ne fait pas une boucle mais un aller/retour. Si vous allez à pied ou en 2 roues jusqu’au temple Thaï Vi, vous pouvez aussi faire le même circuit pour je pense beaucoup moins cher. Si vous avez peu de temps, il faut mieux faire le tour en bateau de Trang An. Meme prix mais 3h en faisant une boucle. Les paysages sont époustouflants. On fait 3 arrêts à des temples. Pourboire recommandé 50.000 dong par personne. De préférence tôt le matin pour les couleurs. Éviter l’affluence du dimanche
ミニ山登り(ドラゴンの山)の後に、この川下りでしたが、距離が長い。基本4人乗りの舟を、女性な船頭さんが1人で漕いてる。一時間以上漕いでたと思う。スゴイ。景色はセリ立った山が圧巻！また、山の下に穴があって3回ぐらい、その洞窟の中をくぐります。まあ、一度は行った方がいい。 文句があるとすれば、木の上のゴザに一時間座るとおしりが痛い。厚手の座布団持参がベスト。あと、ハノイから片道2時間はちと遠い？

Everything you need to know: First, you must buy tickets at the ticket office which is 300m away. The ticket price is 250k per person or 500k for a private boat if you are a single. Otherwise you can have up to 4 people (I think) on a boat but it’s still 250k per person. The ticket price includes an electric shuttle from the ticket office to the starting point and back.  The ticket price also includes as many stops along the river as you like BUT if you don’t tell the boat driver, they won’t make any stops. Without stops the total time up and down the river is a little less than 1.5 hours.  There are about 4 potentially interesting stops along the river. I suggest stopping at the end for a short walk, as it’s nice to stretch the legs and walk over a few beautiful bridges. The water is also cleanest there. The other stop you ABSOLUTELY MUST make is Gioi Ravine. You’ll see a sign for it along the river, before entering the second cave. It’s a short 5-10 minute walk through narrow rocks but it’s vertical and up stairs the entire way. At the top is the most beautiful viewpoint, it’s top 3 best places we’ve seen in Vietnam. So many boats passed this stop and it’s a shame because this is a MUST do.  There is only one route up and back, you’ll pass beautiful rice paddies and go through 3 caves in a nice looking boat as you’re towed along the river by the drivers feet. I strongly suggest you bring an umbrella to protect from the sun and water because it gets hot! There was only shade in the caves and at stopping points along the river. We went around 10am in May and it wasn’t too crowded on the river, maybe a bit on the way back.  It’s also important to point out the boat drivers are paid with your ticket price, so I don’t understand why people are suggesting you tip. Most boat drivers will try to sell you artwork or similar towards the end and they will strongly suggest you tip them. This was the only negative for us, as our driver demanded we tip her for the last 5 minutes of the trip. Tipping is not customary in Vietnam so we did not tip, and I’m from the US. Of course feel free to tip if you like but you definitely shouldn’t feel required to and it definitely shouldn’t be demanded by the drivers. Hope this helps.

Tam Coc loses a start because of the terrible air quality. Piles of debris burning on the sidewalk at night.  Result is that the sky was always hazy. Ruins the view of the majestic karst mountains. There are two main boat river tours. Price is the same for both. If you have only one day, Trang An is much better. But the tour is regimented. The most beautiful scenes were at Bich Dong pagoda. I

In spite of the hoards of tourists, we quite enjoyed this trip in beautiful surroundings and relative calm. The recurring theme of the ladies trying to sell you things and asking for a tip also happened to us. Whoever is in charge of the boats ought to put a stop to it because it does create an unpleasant feeling and that's a real shame.

The trip was very nice. The boat ride cost 250k vnd/person and lasted around 90 minutes. From the ticket office we were transferred via an electric car to the pier. At the end of the ride the guide asked us for a tip and was not satisfied with the amount we gave her.
Nice trip, but a bit too long, expect some pressure to give tips and if you refuse they will hassle you.
Eine atemberaubende Landschaft. In der Nähe ein Höhlentempel und etwas entfernt ein weiterer Tempel der einen Besuch wert ist. Am besten man sucht diese Orte mit dem Fahrrad auf.

Schöne Pagode am Berg, mit einer tollen Umgebung in der Natur. Auf jeden Fall den Ausflug von Tam Coc oder Ninh Binh wert.  Leider werden Touris hier gerne abgezogen, so hieß es bei uns, wir müssten 50k VND zahlen, als Parkgebühr für den Roller und den Eintritt. Stellt sich heraus: Der Eintritt ist kostenlos. Also verhandeln oder woanders parken. Sehr unschön, Hanoi und Hoi An waren hier sehr viel angenehmer für Touris.

This was overall a nice experience. The slow pace was just right to glide past rice paddies, limestone cliffs and through the  limestone caves. The rowers using their feet was pretty cool too. BUT there are touts trying to take photos and even the rower can be a little full on with asking for a drink/food and to buy postcards. Our definitive “no thank you” worked each time, but it may be because we were with a Vietnamese tour guide who arranged the tickets and tip. Also our rower was very slow, every boat overtook us, a few times I was concerned the rower might not get us back.

The area is not wide but surrounding by beautiful limestone mountains, caves, and lush rice paddies on the two banks of Ngo Dong River. Take a boat riding along the river or hire a bike cycling around the village. The weather from May to June is ideal. The rice fields turn golden, and the lagoon outside the entrance to Bich Dong Cave is filled with blooming lotuses. However, its weather will be very hot during summer days, and there is a likelihood of rainfall.
Très touristique, j’avais fait la balade il y a 10 ans et cela me semblait plus authentique. Lorsqu’il y a trop de monde, cela perd de son charme

What a beautiful scenic lil cruise was all fun till the person rowing our boat demanded a tip at the end I was already planning to since it cant have been easy rowing over an hour I was just pulling some notes out as my mate pulled out the last of his cash In his wallet and passes it to him then he goes “not enough at least 100k” so i put my wallet back in my pocket. Asking for a certain amount when its not obligated is wild

Spanish: 🇨🇴 Cuidado: Se enojan si no les das propina de $50.000 Đồng, el ticket de $250.000 incluye el recorrido en estos botes, la vista genial, los paisajes hermosos, una experiencia que recomiendo hacer; lo único incómodo es que intentan venderte artículos en el bote durante el recorrido e iba a dar propina de $20.000 Đồng, se enojó & tiró los chalecos de mala gana. Deberían tener más control de esto ya que viendo otros comentarios no es la primera vez  English: ❌ Caution: They get angry if you don't tip them $50,000 Dong, the $250,000 ticket includes the tour on these boats, the view is great, the landscapes are beautiful, an experience that I recommend doing; the only uncomfortable thing is that they try to sell you items on the boat during the tour and I was going to tip $20,000 Dong, he got angry and reluctantly threw the vests away. They should have more control of this since seeing other comments it is not the first time
So beautiful. But paddle tours up there in Trang An are way better. This is 1,5 hours long and the tours in Trang An is 3 hours for the same price and the scenery is way better there

We took this tour (Nihn Binh) beacuse of Kong movie. I was curious on what the place looks like. In the movie it looks so majestic (tho i understand some enhancement were added) but still the inspiration of Skull island was based here. It was exciting to ride the boat especially when you see how the boatmen row the boat with their feet, yes they use their feet to row the boat. It was simply amazing. The scenery tho is just average although i like it when we pass trough inside a cave. The mountain formation is good and well... worth it. Its a long ride back and forth probably around 45mins. Put on a sunscreen and wear comfortable clothes, for men probably shorts is better. We took this ride in the middle of the day around 2pm so its scorching hot.  Its a fun ride. Worth the experience, but i wont probably be back.  P.s. we took a bike ride after the boat it was fun.

Diese Bootstour hat mehr gekostet als auf dem Trang An und als Ausländer durfte man nur zu 2. auf das Boot. Es gibt nur eine Route und es wurden auch keine Stops bei Tempeln gemacht. Die Fahrt dauert nur knapp 2 Stunden. Die Aussicht ist zwar schön, aber von oben (Hang Mua) ist sie noch beeindruckender.  Die Bootsfahrer versuchen während der Fahrt etwas zu verkaufen und erwarten am Ende der Tour ein Trinkgeld.

Eine gut beworbene Attraktion, mit kleineren Abstrichen. Wir konnten die Tour aufgrund von Hochwasser nicht komplett nutzen, wofür nach den Taifunen aber wirklich keiner was kann. Die Fahrt auf dem Fluss war dennoch sehr schön und eindrucksvoll. Auf eine andere Weise sieht man diese schöne Landschaft sonst nicht. Dies ist also absolut zu empfehlen. Unabhängig ob komplette oder nur teilweise Tour schließt sich am Ende eine Fahrt mit dem Electricity Car an und man bekommt noch einige kleinere Sehenswürdigkeiten der Umgebung gezeigt. Leider waren wir nur zu 2 und so sah sich am ersten Stopp keiner veranlasst uns weiter zu fahren. Vermutlich hielten sich die Fahrer in einem der Stände auf, um zu warten, bis genügend Leute zusammen kommen. Da der Weg zurück zur Anlegestelle nur 1,3 km betrug, entschieden wir uns, zu laufen. Auf etwa 2/3 der Strecke hielt ein vollbesetztes Electricity Car vor uns an und bot uns an, einzusteigen. Wir lehnten ab. Der Fahrer wurde aggressiv und nötigte uns, die Besuchermarken ihm zu geben. Das lehnten wir ab und liefen weiter. Der Fahrer wurde lauter,  doch wir liefen weiter. Am Serviceschalter erläuterte ich die Situation. Das tat dem Personal zwar leid und man bot uns an, einen Fahrer zu rufen, der uns zu den verpassten Stationen fährt, doch das lehnten wir ab. Wir hatten uns diese bereits zuvor selbst angesehen. Geld gab es keines wieder. Man kann diese Tour durchaus machen, doch man muss aufpassen. Das penetrante Fragen nach Trinkgeld können wir bestätigen. Man sollte dem nicht nachgeben.

Had a really nice time here yesterday. Most of the paddlers here use their feet to paddle instead of the arms, which was pretty interesting to see! The ride itself is longer than I was expecting, but it's a pretty relaxing ride with some beautiful views, and passing under a few very low passages under the caves was pretty cool. Oh, you'll probably want to sit on your life vest at some point as the seats get pretty uncomfortable.  The only negative I'd say is that we got accosted by our paddler for a tip multiple times just before the end, like while there was still a good 5 minutes of paddling left. It was annoying and made us really not want to tip her.  Other than that though I really enjoyed it.
כניסה ללא עלות, נמצא קרוב ל tam coc ניתן להגיע למקום ברגל או באופניים, מקדש מקסים לצד נוף מגוון ומיוחד

È molto bella la zona in generale, da girare in bici oppure in scooter, un po' deludente invece il giro in barca che risulta un po' noioso, probabilmente da fare quando c'è ancora il riso per apprezzarlo di più perché senza non è niente di speciale (bello ma non entusiasmante)

On a fait le tour de Trang An et de Tam Coc et nous avons aimé les 2 qui sont différents. Beaucoup moins de monde dans celui de Tam Coc et plus de nature, moins de temple, Il est plus court (1h30 contre 3h) mais le belvédère de May Peak est incroyable, ça vaut le coup. Je vous conseille d’y aller tôt tout de même, Le (gros) point négatif : le rameur qui insiste pour qu’on lui achète des souvenirs et donne plus de tips (on avait donné 100k), ce qui est assez lourd. On avait prévu de donner des tips dans tout les cas mais avec cette insistance on se sentait moins à l’aise, à Trang An ils n’ont pas dutout insisté et ont été plus agréable. Il faut aller à ce point pour acheter des tickets : Dong Gung Boat Tour ticket sales point

Die Bootstour auf dem Fluss war sehr schön und landschaftlich beeindruckend. Leider gab es nur wenige Informationen vom Guide, was das Erlebnis ein wenig geschmälert hat. Trotzdem eine angenehme Tour mit schöner Atmosphäre.

Ese paisaje es una maravilla. Los campos de arroz, cuevas, montañas… Precioso.  Ahora si preparaos para el bochorno y las casi 3 horas en barquita que se hacen bastante pesadas. Y como te encuentres un día con muchos visitantes, más aún.

120k per person entrance. 150k per boat. They say 2 passengers per boat, but I saw boats with up to 5. 1hr 45min total. Great ride. Beautiful. Definitely worth it to see karsts this way.  Boat woman tried to sell me awful embroidery pieces for 100k. At the end, I went to give her a 20k tip, and she said "NO! 50!" So I turned around and left with no tip. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

Très jolie balade et 1h30 c'est largement suffisant. Mais il faut faire attention aux arnaques. Par exemple, au moment où on fait demi-tour, des bateaux vous proposent des fruits et boissons, seulement vous payez pour vous et le batelier qui se sert en même temps (vous payez pour 2 en fait). Et le batelier ma demandé en plus un pourboire. Non, certainement pas.
Pretty, though be prepared to make new friends if you're a solo traveler, as it's two persons per boat minimum! So they'll pair you up with another loner

A ride in the river is around 150k. It takes a pretty long time (1h to 1.5h) and is very hot. Recommend getting an umbrella and putting on lots of sunscreen. The river boat ride takes you through and under 3 caves as you look at the scenary and the rock formations of Ninh Binh.  The boat rowers are all locals and don't speak too much English. It's interesting watching them paddle with their feet.  Once you reach the end, there are other locals on boats trying to sell you food and drinks. They'll also use some emotional tactics of saying you can buy for your hardworking rower. We chose just to directly tip the rower and they pocketed the money instead.  Rowers will ask you for tips, you don't need to tip them. If you tip too little, they'll also ask you for more.

Balade sympa en bateau, un arrêt à un point de vue pour une magnifique photo !  Le seul problème c'est qu'on ne peut pas acheter le billet "uniquement pour le bateau" sur place, il fait allez 200m plus loin...
好玩的洞穴探險 非常推薦體驗+自助旅行過來 這次跟一日遊團來的 導遊都會要我們給船夫小費 一人25k 但是越南根本沒有小費文化啊 建議帶傘 晴天會很曬 外國人兩人一船 越南人可以四人一船 這是歧視嗎？而且只用越南文寫 剛好我看得懂

schönes Dörfchen 4.5km entfernt von Ninh Binh, wir haben uns für die Unterkunft hier entschieden was absolut der Hammer war. mit dem Fahrrad kann man in 2min zwischen den Reisfeldern und Berge fahren, problemlos auch zum Mua Cave/Aussichtspunkt. die meisten Unterkünfte bieten gratis Fahrradverleih an. Am See kann man nen wunderschönen Sonnenuntergang geniessen. Restaurants und Bars hats auch reichlich
Am Wochenende morgens super-voll🙄 Bootsfahrt und Höhlen kommen nicht an die Galaxycave ran

very scenic part of Vietnam, often likened to ha long bay. The 90 minute boatride is about 100000 dong but they will try and upsell you to a package with Bich Dong and the fairie cave (both interesting). Well worth a visit from Hanoi

Nettes Ausflugsziel in der Umgebung von Ninh Binh. Da sich dort ein Buddhistischer Tempel befindet, bitte an die richtige Kleidung denken ( nichts schulterfreies und lange Hosen). Wenn man beim Zweiradparkplatz den Weg weiter geht, kommt …

Nous avons commence par une balade en vélo puis un balade en barque. C est très joli mais attention notre balade sur l eau s est mal fini nous avons été attaques par des guêpes noires resultat 8 piqures pour moi 14 pour mon mari. La bateliere a voulu ramasser une Narcisse d eau

2 tours possibles. 250000vnd par personne. J'ai pris celui de 1h30 et c'est parfois un peu long. Il faut laisser un pourboire pour les rameurs. 50000vnd. Allez y avant 11h sinon plein de monde. Prenez de l'eau avec vous
非常值得來看的經典，走一些階梯。可以看到三個不同的寺廟，還有古洞穴！

You should come here for a day trip when you‘re in Hanoi. It‘s a really really beautiful and stunning landscape. Be aware of the scamming photo- and fruit-ladies on the other boats though. Always ask for the price first and don‘t settle for more. They try to trick you and can be extremely annoying - sometimes ruining this beautiful experience with their unmannered behaviour. Also some of the boat captains are pretty straightforward with demanding tip ~ 50.000 VND will do the trick and they‘ll be happy.

현지 Tour 예약을 통해, Tam coc에서 1시간 30분의 보트투어를 했다. 하노이 의 현지 투어 가이드는 한 보트당 50,000동 이상의 팁을 주지 말고, 중간지점에서 먹는 것 등 물건 강매에도 응하지 말 것을 알려주어 큰 부담없이 Tam coc의 아름다운 자연을 느낄 수 있었다. 강 옆에서 농부가 농사를 짓는 것으로 보아 수심은 깊지 않은 듯 했다. 보트투어 도중 항무아의 꼭대기의 용으로 이루어진 통상 밑을 통과한다.

Great scenery, relaxing and quiet (despite many tourists). Boat company has a rule that 4 Vietnamese people or 2 foreign people are allowed on one boat. This is based purely on looks however as we had a Cambodian man that was not questioned in our tour group. While this could be related to body size, the rule should be stated as a weight limit or balance problem rather than nationality

Estuve hace 15 años y madre mía como ha degrnerado esto. Antes te pedían propina de manera tímida, y así no cuesta soltarla. Ahora nos ven como un cajero con patas. En fin, supongo que yo haría lo mismo
Is wel even leuk om te doen duurt ongeveer 1uur en 15 minuten Met z’n tweeën in een bootje De kapitein of hoe noem je zo’n roeier die roeide met zijn voeten Wel weer een schitterend uitzicht
Quite nice pagoda laid against the mountains. However the architecture is a mixture of old statues (1400s) and new pagoda buildings which were only built in 1992. That dilutes the experience a bit.
Naturmäßig sehr schön. Haben sogar paar Affen bei der Kanutour gesehen. Die Leute sollten aber ihr Müllproblem in Angriff nehmen.
Great boat ride. Idyllic and peaceful loved going through the caves. Worth the ride
Một địa điểm du lịch có phong cảnh đẹp, thích hợp cho các Photo chụp ảnh phong cảnh. Tam Cốc có nhiều góc chụp lên hình rất đẹp.
Giao hàng uy tín nhân viên gọi giao hàng hiện thị tên viettel post để người nhận dễ nhận dạng
