Tôi thật sự rất ấn tượng với tour “Farmer Experience” tại Ninh Bình. Đây không chỉ là một chuyến tham quan, mà là một trải nghiệm sống đúng nghĩa giữa làng quê Việt Nam.Ngay từ khi đến nơi, tôi được chào đón rất thân thiện bởi người dân địa phương và hướng dẫn viên. Không khí trong lành, cánh đồng lúa xanh mát và khung cảnh núi đá vôi hùng vĩ khiến tôi cảm thấy vô cùng thư giãn.Chúng tôi được trực tiếp:Cấy lúa, gặt lúaBắt cá ruộng, trồng rauĐi xe trâu, đội nón lá, mặc đồ nông dânNấu bữa cơm truyền thống cùng gia đình địa phươngMọi hoạt động đều rất vui, gần gũi và phù hợp cho cả người lớn lẫn trẻ em. Hướng dẫn viên nhiệt tình, nói tiếng Anh tốt, luôn giải thích về đời sống và văn hoá địa phương một cách dễ hiểu.Bữa ăn quê do chính người dân nấu cực kỳ ngon và sạch sẽ. Tôi cảm nhận được sự chân thành và hiếu khách của người Ninh Bình.Nếu bạn muốn tìm một trải nghiệm khác biệt, tránh xa du lịch đông đúc, thì tour làm nông dân ở Ninh Bình chắc chắn là lựa chọn tuyệt vời. Tôi sẽ giới thiệu tour này cho bạn bè quốc tế của mình!Rất đáng tiền và đáng nhớ!

My wife and I stayed at Tam Coc 4 nights and I must say that the choice was spot on, it is located in the most convenient spot to visit the main attractions of the area and its small town offers great tranquility, but also some tourist services that make the holiday more relaxing and nice

Taking a tam side boat is probably the most popular attraction 2 on a boat and you leave for 1h30 of ride.the particularities of men and women sail with their legs.I've never seen this before it's very sporty really and they do it with great ease The landscapes are beautiful limestone karst rocks and water everywhere it is the terrestrial Ninh Binh for taste of Along Bay.You will see water lilies pass under tunnels with beautiful colors.So yes it is very touristic but it supports whole families.They will propose on the return of very pretty embroidered photo frames, you are not obliged of course but it is handmade and handmade. May this nature remain intact
Absolutely breathtaking—this place left us utterly spellbound! Without a doubt, one of the most stunning gems in all of Vietnam. A must-see that will steal your heart!

The moment you step through a limestone tunnel on a hand-made boat, you feel like you’re lost in a secret world. Surrounded by the countryside and tranquility, only their smiles echoed. The best dating course spun by nature!

The boat ride, driven by a woman (rather older) who rows with her feet, extraordinary. Spectacular viewsWhat I don't like so much is the way he has it organized, like a triviathat makes you lose your charm a bitEveryone's trying to sell you something when they don't demand a tip.Anyway, essential and recommendedSingle entry 250000 VDN per person. Ticket with electric car tour, with visit to Bich Dong Pagoda, and another, VND 350,000

The tour is beautiful, and although it is ultra touristic we found it quite authentic, within what we can fit.A huge catch is that it is very difficult to understand how tickets are taken out. Where supposedly the ticket office is only sell you a tour with stops included, if you only want the tour by the river you have to board an electric bus that takes you to another ticket office three minutes away where they sell you only the tour. But as much as we asked, no one explained how we had what to do and for a moment they were taken away from us. I think they should improve this point quite a bit. The people carrying the boats continue to paddle with their feet, and the surroundings are beautiful, in some parts it is impressive. The boatwoman was quite friendly, at the last moment she offered to buy some sheets but it was nothing heavy, and asked us for the tip, but nothing happens, enters into the experience and we thought we would give it to her. We also expected everything to be full of street vendors, but just one person offered us a photo at first, we said no and he didn’t insist. Maybe this was because we did it very early in the morning, maybe later there are more vendors. The duration is 1h 30min long or so, it is worth it. We liked it better than Trang An. If I had to do one of them again, it would be this one, although I know that people prefer the other one because they visit more things.

We purchased only the boat ride.  The boat ride was a very good and without any of the negative experiences in old reviews.  The price is standardized now, with a ticket office some distance away from the boats.  The rower did not stop halfway through and try to negotiate a higher fare, as warned in older reviews.  A boat approached to take our picture for money, but it was very soft sell and easy to refuse.  Be sure to wear a protective hat, possibly bring a sun umbrella, and wear plenty of mosquito repellent.  Use sun block, because the river is not in the shade.  Expect to give your rower a tip when dropped off at the dock.  I asked at our hotel beforehand to know what was an appropriate amount.     Halfway through the experience the boat turns back and retraces the first half of the journey.  There’s one particular section where the summit of Mua Cave / Lying Dragon Mountain is visible.  On another section going through a valley with steep limestone walls monkeys were calling out to each other.  During our visit in early May, the vegetation along the river was just starting to turn yellow green, as seen in the famous promotional photos.     One stop along the journey is allowed and included in the price, but that is not communicated well.  We stopped at Dinh May (Cloud Peak).  There is a sign for Dinh May (Cloud Peak) after passing through the first cave.  Once we knew the location, we waited until the return journey to request the stop.  It is a hike up some stairs to an elevated look out over Tam Coc river and very scenic.   Reading reviews, I was really torn between the Tam Coc and Tràng An boat rides.  For our short overnight visit to Ninh Binh, I has very happy with my choice of the Tam Coc boat ride.
đồ ăn ngon, sạch sẽ, lên món nhanh, không gian thoáng mát. nhân viên thân thiện lễ phép. tối đã cảm thấy rất thoải mái ở đây..
Gliding through the rice fields and limestone caves on a rowboat was an incredibly romantic and peaceful experience!!!

Wonderful escape with a boat ride driven by someone who paddles using their feet, bringing you on a serene River cruise going to a cave and limestone cliffs. Expect the talented rowers to offer you some pictures , paintings , whatever . If you’re lucky they will even take your photos using your camera phone. Enjoy the adventure.  Even though near the end it would be dragging.

In Tam Coc you come mainly for the boat ride on the river. Excursion that definitely deserves. Those who carry the boat row with their legs and those on board preferably cover themselves from the sun with the umbrella. Unquestionably very suggestive landscapes with passages even through caves. We suggest an intermediate stop on the way back to reach a very interesting vantage point, the same from which the presentation photos for this excursion are taken. We found it less interesting to visit one of the two temples proposed in thePackage purchased at the marina included in the boat trip. Very helpful and appreciated the trips with electric cars.
Touring the river in the local rower’s boat for three hours has been fantastic. The rice paddock around you is full of life and you will also stop to see the temples.

I went on a very cheap 4,000 yen tour and it was really good.The boat went around the cave for about an hour, forgetting the days and the hustle and bustle of the city.It was a relaxing and refreshing feeling and the view was amazing.

We were scheduled for an overnight cruise on Halong Bay but it was cancelled due to Typhoon Wipha. We were diverted to Halong Bay (on land), Tam Coc which was similar. Let me say, it was great if not better than Halong Bay. So beautiful seeing nature in its pure splendor. Mountain goats, lily pads, lotus plants and the boat ride. I would highly recommend doing this tour.

Trải nghiệm rất đáng giá. Bạn có thể dễ dàng tham quan Tam Cốc, thăm chùa Bích Động hoặc leo Hang Múa để ngắm cảnh từ trên cao. Nếu đi sớm hay cuối ngày, tránh được đông người và trải nghiệm sẽ rất thư thái và chân thực.

Tam Coc was an amazing place and we could have spent so much longer here enjoying the unique beauty of the landscapes.  Being on an organised trip though this wasn't possible but next time would stay in accommodation at Tam Coc and spend the time exploring the beautiful little town that it is. We did the boat trip through the caves and up the river for a way but this is where the disadvantage of being on an organised tour was a downside in that the majority rules what happens.  Sadly most tour participants only wanted a 1 hour ride which didn't get us into the iconic open river plain area at all and we turned around and went back.  If you do get there do any boat tours and walking on your own so you can make your own arrangements!

Tamcok's boat trip was an unforgettable memory for the family. It was a truly amazing experience to take in the majestic natural scenery as we slowly made our way through the quiet river. The children were also very interested in the scenery around them and the whole family was able to relax.

Boat tour was fabulous. Such great value for 3+ hours on the boat and side trip to Bich Dong Pagoda. Just sit back and watch the amazing scenery go by and marvel and the people rowing the boats (by hand and by feet).

Really a beautiful landscape.  We made the boat + electric car tour that passes through Hoa Lu and the pagoda, offered at the Tam Coc pier. That's more than enough. The boat tour is enough 1 hour and then you go to Hoa Lu by electric car and to the pagoda. Hoa Lu is disappointing. You are assailed by people who want to sell you something, there are steps to go up and down so they help you but in exchange you have to give them something. They're chasing you down the aisles. That's terrible. I come back to Tam Coc, nice little town with many restaurants. I recommend this tour

Great day with a new experience. We boarded a rowing boat for a beautiful walk on the river, along the water, in the middle of the rice fields, we admired extraordinary landscapes with numerous limestone peaks with twisted shapes covered with lush vegetation, we went up the river to the three beautiful caves (Tam Coc).

At first you are a bit shocked when you arrive at the boat docks as it is very mass tourism. Everything but perfectly organized and made very pretty. From the boat trip tourism stretches over the river ;-) and the ride is very nice and the large cave that is passed through phenomenal. Temples are controlled from the water on the tour which are worth visiting. Nice trip.

Rowing through the limestone karsts and emerald rice paddies felt like stepping into a painting. The boat ride was peaceful, and the locals were warm and welcoming. A perfect romantic getaway.Highly recommend!
Một trong những điểm du lịch phải đi khi du lịch Ninh Bình. Bạn sẽ được ngắm thiên nhiên núi rừng hùng vĩ trong khi được thưởng ngoại trên những chiếc thuyền nhỏ
1,5 hours on the boat went through the romantic river and caves. Tam Coc boat tour is one place you can't miss once you come to visit Ninh Binh.

The tranquil boat ride through limestone karsts and lush rice paddies felt like stepping into a painting. Our rower’s rhythmic paddling and the peaceful surroundings created a magical atmosphere. We even explored nearby caves, adding a touch of adventure to our romantic day.

Recognized as the land Halong Bay and it is.The walk runs on the Ngo Dong River between mountains with rice paddies on both sides although in January it cannot be seen.The typical sampan boat ride in which the gentleman paddles with his feet and lasts about an hour.During the tour you cross three caves but beware of the head, bend down to enter them.It seems to me a must-see place and very beautiful.I recommend it.

The unique geological formations and tranquil river cruises made for a mesmerizing experience. We drifted through stunning limestone karsts and lush rice paddies, feeling truly connected to nature. The peaceful ambiance was perfect for a romantic escape, and the local boat guides added a touch of charm with their warm hospitality.

Very nice landscape, the boat ride was amazing passing thru the limestone caves.A UNESCO heritage site.Enjoyed watching the localpeople planting rice alongside the river.It was one of the greatest trip for me.

Absolutely loved our time in Tam Coc! It has a chill vibe and the perfect place to relax and adventure the surrounding landscapes for 3 or 4 days.We took the overnight train from Da Nang to Ninh Binh and arrived around 4am. Definitely set an alarm as it’s wasn’t super clear we were arriving. We easily got a taxi to Tam Coc at that time. Found it helpful to check with our local hotel via WhatsApp what the estimated fee would be to avoid being overcharged (we paid 100k VND).Stayed at Tam Coc Victoria Hotel in the centre of town. The rooms were great (comfy beds) and the owners were lovely and accommodating.Highly recommend hiring bicycles from your local hotel to explore the surrounding areas. We paid 50k for a full day hire and 25k for a half day the following day. We explored a lot on bike and loved the freedom of going down random roads to see where it leads - pretty safe as two females. We did take a grab taxi to Trang An as it was a little further from our accommodation.For an amazing viewpoint, make sure you climb Mua Caves (it wasn’t as hard as we expected). You can easily take a grab or it took us around 20-25 minutes to bike from the centre of town. To avoid the crowds we decided to climb at sunrise. The park opens at 6am, but we arrived around 5:40am and could get in earlier. Entrance fee was 100k and paid 5k to park the bikes. We didn’t get hassled at this time with parking scammers (apparently a thing).Food recommendations:- Hoang Viet Restaurant (across from the Tam Coc boats) has the best rotisserie duck and good + cheap draft beer that costs around 7k.- Madam Loan does a great Banh xeo (a local savoury pancake).

It starts from a pond that seems artificial inside a Resort and at first it leaves a bit 'disoriented but as you go up the river nature takes over, you are in a limestone canyon covered with vegetation and on the sides the rice cultivation. The boats are actually aluminum and not bamboo but are max for 3 people and therefore reduces crowding at least on the boat. As for the presence of tourists depends on the period and time. I was lucky enough to have a few moments when we were alone sailing the river and the lady rowing had a chat with the boats that occasionally met. You also cross caves on the water which makes the trip suggestive, we were about an hour and a half sailing but there is also the possibility to go ashore, depending on the tour you choose. A bad thing I noticed here in northern Vietnam is the request for tips and the sale of photos and souvenirs (directly on the boat) this habit came with mass tourism. Many criticize this tour but we really enjoyed it, we saw herons, ducks, turtles, fish, fishermen and farmers preparing rice fields. The peculiarity here is that they row with their feet seated while in other places of excursions they rowed standing and using their hands.

The walk on the Ngo Dong River is truly a magical boat moment and a dream on the meanders of the NGO Dong River. Go very early between 7 and 9am because after groups can spoil the visual tranquility of the place. No rice in December, it is rather from February to April but the water lilies dot pink spots throughout the walk. The price is 250,000 Vnd and for Western tourists a boat for 2..... "they are heavier"“The skipper” earns half theprices and sometimes only does it once every 3 days ... too many boats ( 1000) for the number of tourists in this season. Finally it was unforgettable even if the sun god was not on the date.

This is a very pleasant place to spend 3 or 4 days, the village itself is a bit of a backpackers hang out so can be lively at times. But that all adds to the charm. It's especially vibrant at the weekend.A few tipsYou don't need a tour or a guide for most attractions, the best of which is Trang An with its spectacularly beautiful waterways, caves, and immense towering landscapes. It offers 4 routes the best of which in my humble opinion is route 1, 9 caves and 4 temples (there only so many temples you can visit with enthusiasm) all routes take circa 3 hours ( you won't notice it go by) with the exception of route 4 at 1 and 1/2 hours. All for $ 10, it's a steal. Skip the tour, get a grab, and save yourself some money and aggregation.The bird reserve is nice, two boat trips included in the ticket. It's not as good as Trang An, but it's good for a morning. Be aware that the ticket office is  circa a a mile from the park, so if you arrive by taxi, make sure they wait for you to get your ticket and drive you the rest of the way. It would be an arduous walk.We also visited the bear sanctuary, which is a worthy cause and worth a visit, albeit that it is a bit away from Tan Coc.Lots of temples and pagodas if that's what you're into, and a mountain viewpoint with 500 uneven steps to a seriously overcrowded photo opportunity,  if that's what you like.People everywhere selling tours and convincing you you need them ! Honestly, you don't, and with a tour, you are always on someone else's agenda and timeline.We stayed in Tam Coc Golden Sky homestay which was very good and centrally located, and the best food we found was directly across from the Tam Coc boating area and next to the karaoke bus that serves really really good rotisserie duck and really cheap but good draft beer.Well worth a visit enjoy.
Good, mọi thứ đểu tuyệt với , khung cảnh rất thơ mộng, giống như đang được hòa mình vào thiên nhiên, rất nhiều thứ đáng để trải nghiệm

I totally recommend checking out this awesome place. You can see the rice fields, ducks, goats, and monkeys while you're on the boat. The water was super clear, so I could see right through it. The natural beauty was off the charts. While on the boat, we can soak in the views of rock peaks, rice paddies, and caves. It's a must-see experience, for real!
great foods, awsome drinkswelcome to Drink& foods Traditional in Tam Cocnice place, friendly staff,  good services ...

Du lịch Tam Cốc, Bích Động những ngày cuối tháng 9. Thời tiết khá đẹp. Nếu có thể nên chọn đi vào dịp đầu năm, mùa Xuân hoặc khi mới chớm lạnh sẽ phù hợp. Không nên đi vào thời điểm nắng nóng. Tuy nhiên khoảng thời gian đẹp nhất trong năm là tháng 5 và tháng 6. Nếu đến Tam Cốc thời điểm này sẽ phải đi thuyền dưới cái nắng nóng nguy hiểm

This place is so beautiful. You just want to jump into the emerald waters and swim. We couldn’t visit all the caves because of storm Yagi, but the trip down the river did not disappoint. We took a late trip and were therefore the last people on the river… it was so peaceful.

We arrived and had a 15 minute wait for a boat, it was busy as it was being shut due to typhoon We were Road down the river through the caves and back again it took over an hour but  really enjoyable.Watch out for photographers half way down, they will take your photo and pressure you to by them, just say no thank you if you dont want them.They also try and sell you pictures we liked them and bought two.Remember to tip the rower, we were suggested 100dong, but it is an optional thing.

As all places, it is a bit touristic. Spending 2 days should do the trick. We did not do the river boats as it looked ridiculous. Way too busy, too expensive and lots of bad reviews.If you have been to Cat Ba en done a boat tour including canoeing then scrap this river tour. Car ba is better and less touristic. We hired a private car for 1,2 million a day to go to three different locations. It was worth it but it’s better to rent directly. The market up of the hotel is between 20% en 40%.Rent bikes or take a hotel that has bikes. Bike riding is nice and easy. Go off track!Tip for when you have kids, Buffalo cave. For 100/150k including a drink you can feed the ducks, fish with a bamboo stick, etc. We spend 1,5 hours but could have spend more time there!
boat ride, view point of Heaven, Cloud Peak is so amazing view...You should come here to enjoy the most peaceful, natural and comfortable atmosphere.

We went on the 2 hours boat trip through the Trang An Landscape. It was very hot. We were two couples in the boat, plus the rower. It was the most interesting part of what we experienced in Vietnam, the view changed constantly during the trip, we visited several temples and went through several caves. It was a fascinating experience.

I recently enjoyed a fantastic one-day trip to Tam Coc in Ninh Binh. The boat ride along the Ngo Dong River was mesmerizing, with stunning limestone karsts and serene waters. Passing through the three caves was a unique experience. The visit to Bich Dong Pagoda offered incredible views and a peaceful atmosphere. Cycling around the village allowed us to immerse ourselves in the local culture, surrounded by lush rice paddies and friendly locals. A perfect blend of nature and culture – highly recommended for a day trip!

This is one for the rice fields serenity mountains and caves . Be prepared for a bit uncomfortable seating for 1.5 hour but the views compensate for that. Stable water and boat and the wonderful boat rowers make the trip so pleasant . You go underneath the caves ( three ) not inside them . So expectations beware . Not sure what Trang ang tours route offers . The river is stable and the views are of cliffs and trees surrounding . Not one to be missed . I went there as a part of a guided tour by local mint travel WhatsApp +84 94 925 96 75 ) . They were very helpful and professional and no complains at all . This is a nice relaxing place and the boat tour is a must
The highlight of the tour was the mesmerizing boat ride through the picturesque Tam Coc waterways, surrounded by towering limestone karsts and lush greenery.

Lovely being rowed through the rice fields, very peaceful just the sound of the oars in the water. We saw goats 🐐 scrambling in the limestone hills on either side and plenty of bird life. The trip lasted about an hour and was “ there and back” rather than a circular tour like Trang An. Very enjoyable in a lovely setting and well worth doing.

The Nihn Bihn area is very rich in things to do and in my opinion deserves a visit of at least two full days. We slept in Tam Coc, a small town with many hotels and many restaurants and we had a very good time. In Tam Coc the main activity is boat tours. We bought a ticket which allowed us to do not only the boat tour but also to visit all the attractions in the Tam Coc area with an electric shuttle. It takes about three hours and in my opinion it's absolutely worth it! Of course the Trang An boat tour is more beautiful, but if you have enough time Tam Coc is also worth a visit! The cost of the ticket is 340 dong per person and you also visit the Thai Vi Temple, Dong Tien, Bich Dong Pagoda. Not to be missed!

The landscape is beautiful and worth it, but you are actually supposed to buy something (pictures). And at the end of the boat trip we were asked for a tip, which we would have given willingly. It's a shame, it spoils the mood a bit.
The boat ride at Tam Coc through hills and cave grottoes is just lovely. The scenery is beautiful and the boat ride itself really enjoyable.

Everything that surrounds it is beautiful and characteristic. The trip on the river will be an indelible memory. Never felt such peace immersed in a surreal environment seen until now only in TV documentaries.To be tried without ifs or buts.SURREAL.

We chose to stay in Tam Coc for three nights rather than do a day tour from Hanoi. So glad we did. We organized a 'limo' transfer from Hanoi thro the hotel we booked with in Tam Coc. They run every hour.Day one we arranged a driver who took us to Trang An, amazing,  we were rowed through the waterways and caves and stopped at various temples. Tip...find other people share a boat with, they take 4 at a time. There are three tour options, we choose 2. It was great. Arriving early meant we missed the hoards arriving on the day tours from Hanoi.Next stop was Bai Dinh. Electric buggies to Wizz you around, ancient Buddist temples and modern temples. Huge gold leaf covered bronze Buddha's and great views from the top of the pergola (elevator!) By that stage we were exhausted! Lots of walking plus high humidity. Fabulous day.Staying in Tam Coc meant we could walk to the Tam Coc row boats.  Arriving shortly after 9am meant we missed the crowds again. Glad we did this as well as Trang An as different experience. Loved the foot rowing technique!

A stunningly beautiful place that needs at least three days to explore, incredible views but also full of local life and culture.The boat tour ie a must but hire q bike and get out into the paddyfields, cannot wait to return/
The only real reason you would visitTam coc is to do the row boat cruise down the river past all the rice paddies through the caves and beautiful scenery. The restaurants are fairly good.

The boat tour was really beautiful! Contrary to what was complained about in previous reviews, we were not pressured to buy anything and no tip was expected from us. Our foot rower spoke a little English and was very friendly. We went on a Saturday morning, didn't have to queue and were able to enjoy the tour to the fullest!

Such a peaceful and beautiful place to visit. Drifting on the river amongst the rice fields between the breathtaking mountains. Was definitely an experience and one not to be missed when visiting Ninh Bính province.

We visited Tam Coc on our couple's trip and were blown away by the stunning beauty of this place. The karst formations rising out of the water looked like something out of a fairy tale. Paddling down the river in a small boat, we felt like we were in our own private paradise. Plus, no need for sea sickness pills! Definitely a must-visit if you're in Ninh Binh.

You get on small boats and are rowed  up the river. There are rice paddy’s along the way and the karst mountains that rise up. You go through three caves and then turn around and go back down the river. The people rowing often use their feet to row. This was a fun experience

It is wonderful to take a rowing boat ride, certainly moved with the feet of the rowers, for an hour and a half, with rice fields on both sides, and crossing two caves along the way, which make the journey grandiose, ending in a cove in which to visit a small Buddhist temple, and return to the exit.It is very worth enjoying this show
Nice and amazing place in Vietnam. It is one of beautiful destinations in Vietnam. Maybe better than Trang An

Incredible,  absolutely loved it!! Breathtaking scenery!! Busy but we still found it serene & tranquil. Pain being asked for a tip, asked to buy souvenirs,  asked to pay to get photo taken but all part of it.

The Tam Cốc area was a once-in-a-lifetime experience. Being surrounded by the limestone cliffs we were in awe. The inside of the caves was gigantic, and felt like they went for miles. The talented boat rowers added more to this experience as we were amazed by their rowing skills. 2 hours - all by their feet! Their leg muscles must be crazy.Make sure to tip the rowers. This is how they make their living. Our driver was a sweetheart, and kept us smiling - despite the language barrier. Although this attraction was touristy it was relaxing and beautiful.
We loved the boat ride. Super relaxing, beautiful views, travelling under the caves. A must do. Best part of Ninh Binh in our opinion.

Take the 90 minute row boat ride on the river and admire the gorgeous view of the lime stone hills and caves. The scenery here is just as pretty as Halong Bay. The difference is that Tam Coc boat ride allows you to be up close to the formation whereas Halong allows you to see the formations in a larger scale.A must do in Hanoi

Fab, great, beautiful, quieter, local, nice, wonderful place where you can set your base from where to explore many places. Suggest Amanda Villas as a terrific place to stay, which is a 10minute walk into the touristy centre. Amanda Villas can kit you out with moped &/cycles. Loved Tam Coc area.

Most people seem to visit the area on day trips from Hanoi but my advice would be to catch the train or get a Grab and come and stay for a few days. There's loads to see and lots to do. We stayed at a homestay for 4 nights and like most of them in the area they provided free push bikes. You can safely cycle to many of the local sites but I'd recommend getting a driver for the day (about $60 dollars) and visiting 3 or 4 of the main sites. We went to Trang An for our boat trip and took route 3. It was stunning and absolutely amazing, there was no hassle and no one trying to sell you anything.5 ⭐Tam Coc Horizon bungalows for accommodation.Trang An for boat trip.Cycle to Mua CaveCycle to various temples and pagodas.

I didn't do the classic boat excursion because it was closed for holidays, but I stayed one night and Vincenzo visited the surroundings which I review separately. However, the country is very beautiful and hospitable. I slept in a Homestay which was the best quality accommodation of my trip. The mountain landscapes that overlook the rice fields, visible even with a short walk, are fabulous

This is one of the most beautiful places I have visited . The natural beauty of this area is stunning and the river boat ride was so relaxing. This is a must do if you love nature and beautiful nature landscapes.

We did not arrange a tour. We made our way there and bought the ticket there. It is very easy to do so and there is no need for pre-booking.Tam Coc is one of the most beautiful places I have seen. I highly recommend it!There is a boat tour that is very nice. You can chose to stop and see a small pagoda and go up a king kong looking 'mountain' to see the views from above. These options are not always offered, so ask to do so!We also went to the Mua cave and the dragon Mountain that is in the same area. It is beautiful and I highly recommend to visit all in the same day.

My family loved Tam Coc.  We did the boat ride at Trang An mainly due to reviews and don't regret that decision.  Tam Coc area is just so lovely and scenic.  Its not hard to find a bicycle and ride around and enjoy at your own pace without being harassed.  Do your homework and enjoy. Lots of little shops and homestays.  A group of women were practicing a dance or maybe it was just as exercise class, but we enjoyed watching them.  Didn't have the co ordination in join in.  A highlight in Vietnam and I really hope they don't over tourist this place and destroy the natural beauty.

Beautiful time we had! The boat ride was 1.5 hours and our Boat lady was an expert one. We were led to  the 3 different caves which is part of the  long stretch of the  river Ngo Dong while our lady  gently rowed for us.  We were in awe enjoying this  beautiful site and nature's  wonder. Though we had the full whole day trip, i think we'll go back again 🥰 It helped us to  relax our mind and allowed us to inhale the pure air around the pollution free site.

Un lugar mágico, increíble!!! Hiper recomendable. Hay infinidad de sitios para conocer y recorrer, con la libertad que da hacerlo alquilando un scooter. En todos los lugares se come muy bien y barato. Nosotros estuvimos 3 noches y da para quedarse 4 ó 5 ya que hay muchas cosas para recorrer y conocer.

This was part of our tour itinerary so there was no haggling for price or tipping at the end of the boat trip. The river gets very crowded with sampans, but the scenery is fantastic. You go through 3 caves, and then turn around to come back. However, at the turn around point there are boats selling drinks and trinkets, and they won't let you go unless you buy something. So, I suggest you take some small denomination notes with you as they won't have change and buy a drink. it is interesting watching them row with their feet.A great trip well worth it. Remember only one gets in and out of the boat at a time, unless you want to get very wet.

It was one of my most beautiful excursions around the world! Among the rice fields, in a surreal silence .. boating between the spectacle of nature. It deserves once in a lifetime to live an emotion like this.

Tam Coc est le "centre" des lieux à voir dans la province de Ninh Binh.Il s'agit d'un joli village traversé d'une grande rue où se trouvent, de part et d'autres, un grand choix de restaurants, de cafés et de bars. La ville s'anime le soir. Une balade à vélo y est très agréable.

Tam Coc is surrounded by exceptional beauty, best experienced by bicycle.  Explore the roads leading out of town in multiple directions.  Surprises abound, in the fields, mountains and waterways.  No need to sign up for an organized tour or guide.  This is a place for serendipity.
Nice placeNice river but I had my tour guide said time nicest on May or September:(But this time still nice trip with me :)
đến đây rất nhiều lần nhưng không thấy chán , cảnh đẹp , thơ mộng, yên bình , đi bẵng xe máy rất thú vị , mình thấy tam côc đẹp
Very nice landscape and nace People. We really e enjoyed the stay.Going by bien enjoying the nice views or going by boat was really worth it.
È stata un'esperienza straordinaria. Abbiamo fatto un giro in barca lungo il bellissimo fiume e le meravigliose grotte. Sightseeing, montagne, paesaggio...

One of the best attraction we had in Vietnam. We took the boat riding between the gorge and trough the caves, it was outstanding experience. It was also nice to see how so many woman and old people from the comminute get income from this attraction and how it nicely combine with the surrounded nature

Very helping staff, our guide Bao was extremely nice and funny, and managed for us to still have a beautiful day despite a violent storm! The journey is rather well planned, we get to see and do a lot of stuff in just one day. Would definitely recommend for short-stay people.
Tháng 6 là tháng diễn ra festival mùa vàng Tam Cốc, một bức tranh quá đỗi tuyệt vời từ thiên nhiên và con người.

This is a beautiful boat ride. We went in July so it looked not like rice fields at all, everything was green. There are lots of tourists doing this one. We were a couple and got our own boat. Luckily we had 3 days in Ninh Binh so could do several things. Don't go too late as there will be shadows everywhere from the setting sun.  I loved this boat ride but liked the one at Trang An even better.
Agréable promenade en barque. Nous étions peu nombreux (peut-être est-ce parce que le pays a ouvert depuis peu ses frontières), contrairement à ce que laissent entendre les avis précédents

I was part of a tour which had the two hour boat ride preselected.  I was so glad it was the last activity of the trip since I used up quite a bit of energy climbing up the mountains by Mua Caves.  Our group was split into groups of threes but the boat could fit up to four (maybe five or six depending on the size of the child).  Everyone was required to wear life vests but as we cruised the river not everyone had them on after they left the dock.These were metal boats and my only complaint was that my butt was tired from all that sitting.   Nicer if some padding was available.  There was a pattern in how we had to sit in the boat and our rower got a little excited when we shifted out of our position for any length of time.  Apply lots of sunscreen, use sunglasses and wear a hat or take an umbrella if you are on the afternoon boat rides as the sun got pretty intense.Be amazed by the feet rowing prowess of your guide.  It was a lot of effort to row three grown men around.The scenery was quite amazing and beautiful.  I could see why Kong Skull Island had chosen this area as one of it shooting locations.  Snack boats were available at the turnaround point.  Prices were not cheap but a cold drink and fruit was good after the first hour in the sun.Remember to tip generously at the end.

Tam Coc is so beautiful with nature like Halong Bay but there are nice local people and daily life.2 days in Tam Coc will be really nice to stay overnight in Tam Coc.i have been to Tam Coc many times but i still want to go there when i have time to come back.Just come back to Tam Coc after reopening. There are most Vietnamese now.This period is good time for rice field.

We did the Tam Coc Boat Tour through the three caves. The scenery was beautiful and the boat driver was very kind. The tour for 2 of us in a boat was 390,000 VNDBeware of the “market” after the last cave. They try to sell snacks and drinks, and if you refuse they will suggest you buy one as a tip for your driver. Although we were happy to do so, we are not sure this was actually beneficial to our driver as he did not pick what he wanted nor did he drink the drink. Also note that the drivers expect a tip on this tour. We had no problem with this, but tipping is not customary in most places in Vietnam so this is something to keep in mind.
Khung cảnh núi, sông và cánh đồng lúa vàng óng hai bên tạo cảm giác yên bình. Các bạn nên đến vào mùa lúa chín để có những bức ảnh đẹp nhất và thưởng thức được vẻ đẹp trọn vẹn nhất của Tam Cốc

I love this tour though the traveler is very far but you were able to see beautiful mountains go biking in the rice field with tourist the lunch is also good it has lots of vegetarian options and the Tour is well organized
Tôi rất thích nơi này, đi thuyền chậm rãi cùng ngắm phong cảnh rất đẹp. Long dong trên con sông, thư thái và an nhiên.
Cảnh sắc thật tuyệt vời. Mặc dù đã xem phim King Kông nhưng thực sự là cũng rất ngạc nhiên khi xem trực tiếp cảnh sắc ở đây.

We've done the caves at Trang An several times now but this was our first visit to Tam Coc. It was truly a spectacular experience. Due to COVID, they will charge a "surcharge" if you want more than two adults and two small children in a boat. (They seem to charge both Vietnamese and foreigners an extra fee. No, it makes no sense...but what can you do?)  The two hour trip was lots of fun. The really sweet and kind woman paddled with her feet which I'd heard of but never seen. (This was featured on one of the seasons of the US version of The Amazing Race.)  I would highly recommend this tour. But, in high season, Trang An might "feel" less crowded as it's more spread out.
Khung  cảnh đẹpKhông khí trong lànhNgười dân thân thiệnRất phù hợp cho các gia đình,các cặp đôi,team phượtGiá vé là 150k/người khi đi thuyền

Beautiful!!! Absolutely Gorgeous!! Pictures don’t do it justice! This place is awesome!! The sweet ladies who paddle you in the canoes are the kindest and are very hard working- please be generous to them and consider tipping! This place offers multiple options of the canoe ride you would like to take and all of them are gorgeous views! They let you out along the way as well to view and walk around the beautiful and interesting temples (there are also restrooms- if you do a long tour as well) it goes fast though as your immersed in the beauty of Ninh Binh! Highly highly recommend doing this tour- it’s a must and main thing you should seek to do while in Nihn Binh! Price is excellent and well worth the experience!! Follow @travelingypsey on Instagram for more travel tips & pictures on GoogleMaps as well! Tam Coc-Bich Dong (Picture below with info)
Tam Coc is beautiful! I stayed in Chezbeo Valley Bungalow and it was such a wonderful place! So close to the small but lovely town centre as well as Hang Mua where you can easily spend a day!
Sehr sehenswert eine echte Perle !!!Leider sind die Bootstouren oft mit Abzocke verbunden Vorsicht und gut überlegen was man dort kauft

If you can deal with the buses of Chinese tourists you will have a blast at this place. The scenery and water blew me away at Tam Coc and once you get out onto the water is surprisingly peaceful. This was a place and travel experience that really exceeded my expectations!!

Increible zona de Vietnam  (como vietnam en general),  puedes hacer el Tour en bote por los rios, pasando bajo cuevas y vistando pagodas a  la vez que es una zona muy buena para recorrerla en moto por tu cuenta.
We took a boat trip alog the river to the three caves. Nature here is stunning. It is recommended to take some kind of protection against the sun. Beautiful rice fileds along the way.
Literally standing on a pier looking at the view and I couldn't believe my eyes. Spectacular place to hang out and explore on a scooter.

I had a local boat with my boyfriend, we are so excited to go there.The locals took us along the river,  the two sides are rice fields. I was like going back to the village, everything was peaceful. I love it.It just unfortunately cloudy weather. We will be back again.
Jolie balade en barque, agréable promenade à vélo, sympathique animation au coeur de la ville. Un arrêt mémorable dans notre parcours de deux semaines au Vietnam.

I have done this tour before in another year in January and it was cold and the rice just planted, thus brown but it was still a must do. Now I have come back in late February and the rice is growing and showing a bit green. I’d say March would be perfect. You can pay for a boat as a solo and pay the extra or this time 2 persons and boat. Cost 350,000d. I usually do not tip as it is not a Vietnamese custom (or my country)but I thought I would tip our boat girl because of the physical effort they need. I gave her 50,000d before reaching the end of the trip so that other women would not see. Then as we returned and were getting out of the boat, the woman who met the boat on the steps (not knowing I spoke Vietnamese) asked her ‘did you get a tip?’ And the young boat girl said ‘yes, 50,000d’. So I’m guessing that the young girl didn’t get to keep the tip and rather they must collect all the tips and I’m not sure how they distribute it but I now know the money doesn’t go to the rower. Hence I’m back to my “no tip’ rule. Anyways definitely a must do and totally different from Trang An.
מקום עם נופים מדהימים, שיט לתוך הנהרחובה ביקור, אנשים נחמדים, דוכנים.אווירה טובה. ממליצה בחום על מקום זה

Perched between mountains and a lake. Healthy happy ambiance. Locals do laps around the lake at sunrise, everyone greets you good morning. Nice slow pace, clean air (except for little pockets of burnings which sadly seem everywhere in Asia.I wish there comes water refill stations and bamboo straws soon. 🙏 You can take a tranquil boat tour on river.We enjoyed heaps of local foods, walking around town and getting massages.Great way to break up the trip from Phong Nha to Halong BayWe stay at Tam Coc Mountain Lake Bungalow and loved it. Very hospitable and cheerful family run business. Some of the best pillow top mattresses in VietnamLove the warm hospitality, eye contact and greetings everywhere. Such a warm friendly little town.

Kurze Erklärung: Ninh Binh ist ein Provinzort in der Nähe der beiden Weltkulturstätten: Tam Coc und Trang An.Tam Coc ist ein kleiner Ort voll mit Restaurants, Pubs, Homestays.....und der Abfahrort für eine Bootstour.das Boot fasst 2 Personen, die Ruderin rudert mit den Füßen. Der Spaß kostet 390 000.dong ( schlappe 14euro). Dauer: 1,5stdenAbsolut sehenswert. Ein kleiner Kanal ( teilweise von Reisfeldern gesäumt), nach ca.40min hat man das Ende erreicht und wendet. An diesem Punkt warten einige Frauen in Booten und versuchen Snacks  Getränke und Andenken zu verkaufen.Man sagt " Nein" und gut ists.Das Besondere ist einfach die atemberaubende Landschaft - muss/ sollte man gesehen haben. Der Begriff " trockene Halongbucht" ist wirklich zutreffend.

I believe is one of the most beautiful places that I’ve ever visited and I’ve traveled quite a lot... is just heavenly to see the limestone mountains while you’re on the boat... the locals row with their feet!! You should definitely add this place to your “day trip” from Ha Noi or at least stay for 1 night. I liked it more than Halong Bay actually... is just breathtaking...
It seems to me that Trang An is less touristy than Tam Coc. Many caves, rice fields, mountains and beautiful pagoda! To enjoy the scenery, you can rent a bike or take a boat trip.
Who would have thought to cover, or uncover, three caves in a river? This was a perfect getaway amid drizzles!

The hotel is out in the countryside, surrounded by rice fields full of noisy frogs. Bird watchers will enjoy the storks and herons chasing the frogs. It's a delightful setting and the gardens are beautiful. The swimming pool was cold in mid February but happily there were no mosquitoes.

Tiene merecido el nombre de Ha Long Bay de interior.Los paisajes son igual de maravillosos.  Yo recomiendo ambos, pero si el precio de los cruceros en la bahía te parecen prohibitivos, no renuncies al sabor del paisaje mas auténtico vietnamita y dedica 2-3 días a esta región.
It’s beautiful. Surrounded by mountains, not too busy, easy to get to places (by scooter or bike). If you’re staying in Ninh Binh, would recommend staying here instead :)

We really enjoyed our boat trip along the river. There are amazing views of the mountains and rice fields,  and we saw kingfishers,  egrets,  storks and mountain goats. There were half a dozen boats on the river when we arrived at 10.30 am but this did not detract from the peace and beauty of the place. Yes there were boat vendors but you can say "no", and yes we did tip our rower but seeing as he had just spent 1.5 hours rowing us along the river,  a couple of dollars didn't seem unreasonable. Happy to recommend.

A visiter absolument si vous passez dans le coin. Cette petite ville se trouve au centre des sites à visiter et possède  de très beaux coins. Elle regorge de restaurants et bars pour des soirées sympas.Louer un véhicule avec chauffeur est pour moi la meilleure solution.

하노이에서 리무진을 타고 호아루, 항무아 및 땀콕을 돌아보는 하루 코스의 투어를 다녀왔습니다.  땀콕(Tom Coc)의 뜻이 3개의 동굴이라고 하는데, 약2시간 동안 보트를 타고 강을 거슬러 올라 동굴 3개를 지나 다시 돌아 오는 코스였습니다. 강 양옆으로 펼쳐지는 신비한 자연 풍경이 너무 멋있었습니다. 하롱베이와 닮았다고 해서 육지의 하롱베이로 불리는데, 개인적으로는 멀리 있는 하롱베이의 섬들 보다는 손에 잡힐 듯 펼쳐지는 땀콕의 풍경이 더 멋있었고 그 절경에 푹 빠졌습니다.  강 양옆에 펼쳐진 논의 벼가 푸르게 자란 계절에는 더 멋있는 풍광을 선사할 것 같습니다.  보트에서 내릴 때 2시간 동안 수고한 뱃사공에게 적당한 팁을 잊지마시기를...

I did this on a self made day trip from Hanoi, with Mua Caves as well, I rented a motorbike. I went on a rainy day and there were no other boats in the water, it was so nice and beautiful. Paid 400k for 2 people, 1 boat and a parking fee for the bike. (1.5 hours) Like others, they do try to sell you stuff but just say no if you want or bargain like crazy and you can get things for super cheap. They also demand a tip at the end (they reminded me at the beginning and end), but only give if you want.
Tanto por las vistas de de la cima de Mua Cave, como por los maravillosos paseos en barca en Trang An. Reserva un par de días mínimo aquí en tu ruta por Vietnam, vale mucho la pena.

If you want to do all the activities in Ninh Binh, I suggest staying in Tam coc! Definitely better cause you’ll be close to a lot! A very cozy little town and even if you have a day you don’t do anything it’s very nice lite stroll around the town! Rent a scooter and drive around from tam coc is definitely the beat option! A lot more peaceful that Ninh Binh centre!
I see the scenery here so great the air is also fresh, I also shoot quite a lot of photos here really beautiful

Simplemente bellísimo. Estuvimos en Enero (cuando no había arrozales) y tuvimos que sumar días de la tranquilidad que ofrece el lugar. Hay varias actividades que hacer alrededor, pero recomiendo alquilarse una bici o moto (5usd al día sin nafta) y poder recorrer el lugar al ritmo propio. El camino entre Tam Coc y Trang An es un encanto y muy sencillo de hacer. También recomiendo ir al complejo de templos de Bai Dinh, a 22km de Tam Coc.

We went there before 8 am (open from 7 am) and the experience was amazing: no other boots on the way there and the nature was waking up in front of our eyes (many birds were hunting for breakfast). When we entered a cave we did not see anything and did not hear anything. Purely amazing.HOWEVER: on the way back roaring people were loudly chatting, many boats came close to each other. Boats with the purpose of selling some snacks and drinks also arrived (they are colleagues so your roaring person will stop for a couple of minutes letting them pushing their products to you).Therefore tourists starting after us lacked the true nature experience. Believe me: it is not the same.TAM COC VS TRANG ANWe did both, both were beautiful and exciting. Of course there are some slight differences between the routes, you can find great blog posts around the topic of which to choose based on your preferences.Trang An:They focus on a definitely better service compared to Tam Coc, they try to avoid the annoying factors of Tam Coc: more transparent pricing, no selling people on the way and there is even a paper based feedback form at the end of the route with the question of whether the roaring person asked for a tip. So they control them not to ask for a tip. As a result, it is a more relaxing place, I think popularity is high similarly to Tam Coc.Especially if you cannot make it before 8 am in Tam coc, I would definitely recommend Trang An. We did that starting at 10 am and due to the fact that they have 3 routes the crowd split in 3 ways.

Ik las een beoordeling dat de 3 partybussen veel takkenherrie muziek voor overlast zouden zorgen maar dat slaat echt nergens op. Deze 50ers genoten van de leuke sfeer die hier heerst. Zorg dat je de app Maps.me op je telefoon staat, huur een scooter en rij zoveel mogelijk over de stippellijnen dit geeft je een fantastisch Vietnam ervaring! Veel hotels en restaurants zijn hier te vinden.
This was a short boat ride but the oarsman stopped when we wanted so we could take photos. It was lovely scenery and charming people.

The 6 kilometres 90 minutes return boat ride has your jaw dragging along by the shear beauty of the landscape. Very peaceful and the perfect way to try and take in what Mother Nature has created. Very easy to find a boat once you have purchased the official fixed price ticket.Thanks for the vote if you found this review helpful.

אני חושב שכל הביקורות הרעות קצת מגזימות, באמת תלוי עם איזה נהג סירה אתה בשייט.אנחנו ראינו אחת שנראית נחמדה מאוד ופשוט מיקמנו את עצמו בתור כדי שנהיה איתה, הנופים מדהימים, השייט עצמו שעה וחצי שזה בול מספיק. פשוט להגיד למוכרים ״לא״ ישר ולא להתלבט אחרת לא עוזבים אותך חחחח והנהג של הסירה מבקש טיפ בסוף כי זה שייט לא פשוט בשבילו. אנחנו ממליצים.

L'établissement Tam Coc est très bien, tout près de tout, embarcadère, resto.Personnel très sympathique et accueillant.Très bon petit-déjeuner.Chambre très agréable et propre.Environnement apaisant.Prêt de vélos et conseils de balade
Amazing view, it’s a great walk. Take some water with you, and go for it! :) especially the sunset is amazing. You can go for a boat trip as well, which is better in the morning.

We cycled around and had a good boat trip. Views are amazing. You can easily spend a couple of hours looking at the nice views. Highly recommended if you have a day to spare in Hanoi. The boat trip will take about an hour and it is hard work for the rowers. They appreciate a tip at the end. Some of the photos do not do justice to this place. You have to be there and see it and appreciate how beautiful the place is.
Tam Coc is really beautiful! I am glad we went there during during our trip to Hanoi, I thought it won't like it because I already went to Halong Bay.

It was a day trip from Hanoi which includes both Hao Lu and Tam Coc. In Tam Coc, we cycled as well as took the small sampan boat to enjoy the scenery. It is a really beautiful place with amazing scenery of the limestone mountain. The boat ride was extremely relaxing. Highly recommended!
It's a wonderful destination ,very peaceful and quite . The boat ride under the caves was amazing and very thrilling . We claimed a mountain there and did a lot of shopping

Tam coc ( tre grotte) è una gita in barca fuori dal tempo. Le signore guidano queste imbarcazioni con i piedi mentre con le mani raccolgono fiori di loto con i quali fanno piccole collane. L'ambiente è suggestivo, silenzioso lontano dalla frenesia di Hanoi

we went on a clody day it was cool and perfect and very relaxing. was an hour long boat ride surrounded by mountains covered with green trees. it was amazing to see them row with their legs never seen that. at the end before they take a u turn beware of the people selling overpriced things they try to sell stuff to u or prey on your sympathies to convince u to buy for your boatman. try putting your legs in the cool water if u are sitting the front. highly recommend this.

I´ve stayed for 2 nights in this hotel and it has amazing mountains around it! One of my favorites places in Vietnam. You can cruise around the area with the bikes they provide and after you return you can enjoy the scenic pool! Highly recommended!

Visitable with a day trip from Hanoi. You cross a river that in some periods is bordered by flowering plantations. The boats are driven almost all by women who row with their feet and wear the typical hats.The view on a sunny day is spectacular

Why is it so good:-1. Landscapes are stunning. Mountains, river, the cold wind, peace, caves....makes it for a surreal experience.2. Boat ride lasts nearly 1.5 hours...giving you ample time to soak in the scenery and appreciate the nature's beauty3. A single oarman/woman rows the boat non-stop for nearly 1.5 hours and their stamina is just 'wow'What could have been better:-1. If you have taken the bus tour from a travel agent or through Klook...they would waste your time with 2-3 unnecessary stops, with not so good lunch and a visit to a pagoda. Frankly, you can do this tour in half a day then a normal full day event.2. You can hire a private car (from Klook or travel agents) and come directly to Tam Coc and buy a boat ride directly through the ticket counter. That would easily save you 3-4 hours. 3. Lunch was ok but not great...they sell it by saying authentic Vietnamese food but in my opinion...it can be easily skipped.

Even though it was misty this morning, the scenery was amazing.  The sun tried to get through the mist - worth coming even when the weather isn't really sunny.  It is very peaceful to be rowed down the river and listen to the birds and duck when you go into the river caves - and of course gaze at the mountains in the distance

Bella escursione durata un paio di ore in cui si può ammirare la natura di questo magnifico luogo standosene seduti sulla barchetta in compagnia di una locale signora addetta a remare ( anche con i piedi ) . Lungo il percorso si possono incontrare vari tipo di uccelli . Da non perdere
Caving by Pagoda was truely magnificent experience - should be a seven wonder of the world. Great break from a busy city
Klein, niet al te toeristisch (naar Vietnam normen) dorpje. Ideale uitvalsbasis voor allerlei bezoeken in de buurt. Gemoedelijke sfeer. Prachtige omgeving.

Great little touristy village with loads of home stays, hotels etc.  We decided to stay in the village so we had options for dining and the odd luxury of massages etc.  Surrounding landscape is spectacular.  Even in December low season, it's still humid but no rain and it's still warm, not nasty and cold as we feared.

Wij hebben door Tam Coc met onze brommer gereden. Dit was echt fantastisch! Ga op zoek naar de kleine paadjes en blijf niet op de grote weg! Deze zijn soms wel niet zo gemakkelijk om met de brommer door te rijden aangezien er veel modder is maar het is echt zo mooi! Wij hebben hier geen boottochtje gedaan en hebben gekozen voor een boottochtje in Trang An.

Besides being one of the incredibly beautiful sites that Ninh Binh has to offer, It has Tam Coc town built right up to it and the cultural attraction of the local rowers. What makes it unique is that they row with their feet. It is 2 people per small rowboat and you pass through a few caves. Bring or buy a Vietnamese hat, (great for pictures) and sunscreen. Also, unless you want to buy drinks and food from the boat venders in the river, which is fun and part of the experience, bring your own snacks and drinks. Not, all of the rowers speak English, but ours did, she was able to double as a guide and explained a lot of what we were seeing. We were with friends, a group of 5 so we had 3 rowboats, which was very practical for getting great photos of each other. We were in Ninh Binh for 5 days and we visited all the attractions. This area is definitely a must see when visiting Vietnam. We highly recommend it!

This is a short drive from Hanoi. Ninh Binh is an amazing area. We really enjoyed our boat ride through 4 different caves. Extremely relaxing. We weren't offered an option to stop during our boat ride to visit the islands. Some boats were stopping.

Worth a stopover for a night or five. Great chilled Village in which to relax and unwind. Plenty to see and do. Great for cycling and walking. The boat trips are worth doing and well organised. Plenty of accommodation and dining choices of good quality.

Beautiful small down in Ninh Bình, and a relaxing place for those stressed out tourist travellers. Lots of activities such as boat rides, pagodas and temples to see and stunning view points for the sunset. Hire a scooter here and they are probably the safest roads in Vietnam due to the low traffic density and everyone’s relaxed way of life. Helpful and polite people with all your normal sites of market stalls and North Face knock-offs, but somewhere I’d recommend to family and friends to visit in future!
I will visit this amazing place again. Lavender Homestay was absolutely amazing, the family treated us like long lost relatives and they knew what we needed before we did. It is a must...

We caught the train from Hanoi to Ninh Bình and it took around 2 hours. We got a taxi to Tam Coc town and it was easy to get tickets for the boat ride. The boat ride lasted around 1.45 minutes and was very enjoyable and peaceful! It’s about 10km in total. Nice to take photos!
Descrever este local é impossível, pois quando vc passa pela primeira caverna, entramos em outro mundo uma maravilha!!!

Interesting excursion to historical region of Vietnam. Guide gives us interesting information about history during the visiting Hoa Lu ancient complex. After that we enjoyed a good lunch. Second part of excursion was a pictures boat trip by the river and also bike ride in rise field. We really enjoyed.

Stayed 2 nights in the Hidden Charme Hotel, an escape from the busy town.  You can experience some Vietnamese rural life, an increasingly popular place with tourists though.  Hire a bicycle to explore the countryside and beautiful ‘Dry Halong Bay’ where some famous films were staged.  A short drive to Van Long first thing in the morning for a boat trip will not disappoint.
Piękne miejsce, zapierające dech w piersi. Byliśmy w marcu, pogoda była pochmurna, ale i tak warto. Polecam.
Wonderful place to visit Well worth going. We really enjoyed this little side trip. Great for any age group.
As the locals say it is like the Ha Long bay inland. Even greater variety of spots to visit around. River-like channels and water ponds. It was amazing to see the river floating on both directions.
Ambiance village, avec des bons restos.Les gens sont sympathiques.Il faut découvrir les alentours en vélo ! Magnifique !

Amazing,is the one word to describe this beautiful place near Hanoi. This is must see place while you are in Hanoi. Limestone hills with spectacular shapes,a beautiful river, winding through paddy fields, boating through river caves, enjoying the natural beauty, I would say this was the best day in my Vietnam tripWe joined the sinh tours, who collected us from our hotel in Hanoi, the tour was well arranged. On way we visited the old temples,the Vietnamese lunch they offered was excellent.The best part of the tour,was the energetic guid, Kimi,who made the long trip fun.We had a chance to biking through the fields also. The weather was good too,All together an unforgettable experience.

You must visit Tam Coc whilst in Vietnam! The countryside is stunning, the little town is a lot of fun! There is so much to see and do around Tam Coc, but the best thing is possibly to rent a bike and just explore the spectacular surroundings.Make sure you do the hike to Hang Mua, the view is amazing!!
Magnifique ballade extrêmement touristique. A faire tout de même malgré le flux de touristes que nous sommes.A partir de 07h00 AM.

We went Tam coc from Hanoi and it tool 2.5 hours driving. There are some temple to look around since most of the ancient monument are destroyed there ain't much to see. but if you have time and want to know about their history it's good to visit these places as well. After the lunch we cycled around and it was great experience with beautiful scenery. be careful with the paparazzi :D. then boat trip to visit Tam Coc to see the beauty of the karst formations as you pass along their base and row into one of the many caves carved through the limestone. i have not seen these kind of caves before so it is a beautiful sight. end of the trip you have to give a tip to the paddler and our guides' advice was minimum 50,000 dong or no tip at all.

Sehr touristisch aber für den Anblick kann man das in Kauf nehmen. Spätestens auf dem Boot ist man ein wenig für sich. Man kann am Anfang zwischen 3 Touren wählen, wir haben die Tour 3 gewählt. Pro Person kostet eine Fahrt 200.000vdEs müssen immer 4 Leute in einem Boot fahren, außer man kauft vorne 4 Tickets und sagt man will alleine bzw zu zweit in einem Boot fahren. Dafür haben wir uns auch entschieden, weil wir keine schnatterenden Chinesen mit im Boot haben wollten.Man fährt bei der Tour 3 durch eine 1km lange Höhle die wirklich faszinierenden ist.Wir würden es aufjedenfall wieder machen.

Wi j zijn niet lang in Tam Coc geweest. Wij hebben er een middag gefiets en tevens de Pagode Bich Dong bezocht. Schitterende omgeving. Aanrader om deze omgeving per fiets te doen. Deze activiteit geef ik aan te doen in 2-3 uur maar dit is natuurlijk geheel afhankelijk hoe lang de fiets tocht is en hoelang je de Pagode bezoekt. Zelf de duur van deze activiteit erg goed te bepalen
Simplesmente a paisagem é linda e experiência de navegar pelos pés da vietnamita é extraordinário. Vale muito a experiência, não perca essa oportunidade!

Leaving from the main harbour you set off in a canoe type boat ($10). Rowing with their feet the trip is very peaceful. The scenery is stunning. You travel through three caves, turn and head back. There are people selling drinks and crisps so bring some extra money to buy something. Also provide a tip to the rower at the end. 90minute trip. Well worth it
Le site est vraiment magique. Les montagnes karstiques et les grottes sont a voir.Le point négatif le pourboire OBLIGATOIRE des rameuses.
Yes, we will be asked to buy something for your rower and they will try and sell you some linen. This is Vietnam and thats how they operate. Sit back, relax and enjoy the breathtaking scenery

Wenn man sich aus Zeitgründen zwischen Halong Bay und der Region um Tam Coc entscheiden muss, ist Tam Coc absolute Empfehlung: tolle Natur, schöne Tempelanlagen und nicht (ganz) so überlaufen wie die Halong Bay.
Fomos no final da tarde. Não estava tão quente e a luz do sol estava linda com sombras sobre os campos de arroz.

最寄りのホテルに到着し、免許不要の「スクーター」（？）を借りて訪問しました。入場料：VND120K／人、ボート料：VND150K／ボートでした。また、１つのボートに外国人が2名以上は乗船できないとありました。「スクーター」は近所の「バイク・スクーター専用駐車場」に駐車します。VND15K／日でした。午後に訪問しました。日差しが強く空気が澄んでいたため、景色は大変すばらしかったですが、その分日焼けと日射病が懸念されます。帽子を持った行った方がいいです。ボートにはボートをこぐ人が1名同乗します。私たちのボートは男性でした。チャンアンと異なるのは、チャンアンでは手漕ぎでボートを漕いでいましたが、こちらは足で漕ぎ、手は使いません。３つの洞窟を潜り抜けますが、天井が大変低いので、ボートの椅子に座ったままでは頭があたるため、ボートの底に座って伏せながら潜り抜けました。途中、川の上で物を売っている船が出てきます。ボートの漕ぎ手にプレゼントしろとかうるさいです。頂けなかったのは、下船時にボートの漕ぎ手が自らチップを要求してくるところ。チャンアンは要求などしませんでしたから、こちらの方が、商業的でガメツイ感がありますいずれにせよ天気に大変依存しますが、よく晴れていたこともあって、景色は最高でした。途中に見える山の頂上にはお寺らしき展望台があって、のちにMua Caveから登れる展望台であったころがわかりました。こことMua Caveはセットで見た方がおすすめですが、両者の移動は徒歩だと非現実的なので、是非、「スクーター」を借りられて移動することをお勧めします。Tam Cocでボートに乗ってる時間は結局2.5時間程度でした。なお、おなかが空いたのでTam Cocの乗船場横のレストランで「Pho」を食べましたがコクのあるダシが素晴らしくおいしかったです。Tam Coc真正面の2階建てレストランではなく、Ninh Binhの方に向かって左手横の、薬局の右側、入口でホットドックを売っているレストランです。
We did the boat tour with my friends, I really enjoyed the landscapes. I have travelled quite a lot around the world, and these landscapes are ones of the most amazing I have seen so far!

It's called the land-version of the Halong Bay, and located about 2 hours from Hanoi by bus, The best way to see the beautiful scenery -- many steep and isolated hills alongside a nice river - is to take a 1~1.5hr boat ride (usually 4 tourists and a roller on each boat). The hills are much smaller than the islands of Halong Bay, yet they are no less beautiful. Definitely worth the trip !

Warto zobaczyć i doświadczyć Tam Coc. Mimo średniej pogody w październiku, na którą trafiliśmy-deszcz z przerwami, krajobrazy są niezwykłe. W odróżnieniu do Trang An tu należy się nastawić na próby wyłudzenia pieniędzy - na napoje dla wioślarzy- są one potem przez nich oddawane do koczujących w łódkach sprzedawców i tak w kółko. Przewodnicy informują, żeby nie dać się wciągnąć w ten proceder, bo to deprawujące. Średni, sugerowany napiwek to 1-2 $.
Tam Coc is called Halong Bay on Land by the locals. It is absolutely beautiful and worthy if you stay in Hanoi. If you have more than one day you can also spend one night close by. Recommended.

If you are in Hanoi, chances are you'd plan to visit Sapa and Halong Bay. As great as they are, I would suggest that you make time for Tam Coc as well. 4 days 3 nights would be about right. So much to see here. Rent a bicycle and slowly explore all the village paths. It has both therapeutic and calming effects on your mind and body. Do not rush anything, just relax and enjoy the moment. You won't regret it.

Что бы избежать потока туристов, отправляйтесь рано утром , позднее  становиться очень много туристов, в пути будет рынок на котором вас будут заставлять купить напиток для вашей сопровождающей , мы от этого отказались , дали ей свою воду и в конце поездки , чаевые .

Definitely worth the boat ride theough the caves. Three haves im all and the boatmen/women row the boat with their feet. Don’t get your photo taken unless you plan to buy it. Super pushy photo sales.

Die Bootsfahrt war super und es war sehr entspannend. Zudem ist die ganze Gegend total toll und viel relaxter als in den großen Städten.

Only about 100km away from Hanoi, Ninh Binh is a touristy destination that attracts both domestic and foreign tourists thanks to its beautiful landscapes and many famous cultural and spiritual tourist sites.Tam Coc - Bich Dong is considered one of the most attractive places to visit Ninh Binh. This place is known for its poetic and peaceful scenery. The best time is in January to March of the lunar calendar, because at this time, the weather is not too cold, the sky is clear and there is little rain so it is convenient for sitting on the boat and visiting scenic spots. This season is also the season with many festivals taking place here, which can be combined with visiting festivals or going to pagodas.In June, the weather is quite hot but the sky is clear and there is little rain, and this is the time when the lotus season flourishes, which is also very suitable for sightseeing. July to September is the season of ripe rice, for those who love sailing and watching the golden rice fields, it is impossible to ignore this moment.Transportation: Ninh Binh is just over 100km from Hanoi so there are many facilities you can choose to move. You can choose a passenger car and depart at any time because the North-South bus runs continuously. You can also take a train or motorbike, the road is very wide and easy to go. Or if you go with your family and have small children, you can reserve your own taxi for convenience.In addition, the boat is the main means of transportation when visiting Trang An, Tam Coc - Bich Dong and most selected by tourists, the boat will take you to visit the entire ecological area of Trang An, giving you the feeling of floating on the water, admire the majestic nature and beautiful caves. In order for the trip to be convenient, you should agree on the means, travel time and respect for punctuality.Touristy places you can't ignore when coming to Trang An - Ninh Binh.Tam Coc:Tam Coc, which means "three caves", consists of Ca cave, Hai and Hang caves. All three caves are formed by the piercing through the mountain of the Ngo Dong river. Coming to Tam Coc, you have the opportunity to sail around the Ngo Dong river, crept through the cliffs, explore mysterious caves, watch the golden rice fields ... all create a "fairy place" captivates many guests whenever they come and visit. Bich Dong:Bich Dong is located 2 km away from Tam Coc wharf, meaning "green cave", the name given by Nguyen Nghiem, the grandfather of the great poet Nguyen Du in 1773. Bich Dong consists of a dry cave lying on the middle of the mountain (making Bich Dong pagoda) and a water cave piercing through the mountain (called Xuyen Thuy Dong). In front of the cave is a branch of Ngo Dong river winding on the mountain side, across the river is a rice field.Trang An:This place is a UNESCO world heritage site and a world cultural and natural heritage. With pristine and charming natural landscapes created from rivers running through limestone mountains, intermingling are fanciful, mysterious caves. Just like Tam Coc - Bich Dong, to Trang An you have the opportunity to glimpse the boat through the mountains, watch the water here, and explore the famous caves here: Bright Cave, Dark Cave, Cave of Wine,... In particular, if you come here in the fall, you will have oppoturnity to comtemplate water flowers blooming on the river, making the space more poetic and romantic.Delicious dishes should be tried:Mountain goat meat: The mountain goat meat is characterized by firm, low fat and aromatic taste due to being raised on cliffs, and eating herbs. There are nearly 20 ways to process different goat meat such as goat sausage, steamed goat meat, grilled goat meat, goat raw blood pudding, but tai de (rare goat meat) is still the first choice of us.Kim Son raw Nhech fish salad:Nhech fish salad (or cá nhệch) is a special dish in Ninh Binh but you have to try this salad in Kim Son where considered as best place to enjoy it. Nhech fish has curved shapes of eels but larger and longer. Nhech has much meat, less bone, lots of protein. The first bite of Nhech salad will bring you the attractive aroma of roasted sticky rice, light sour of vinegar interjected to the spicy of ginger, garlic, pepper, chili and lemongrass. You will never forget this taste.Some other notes:- Trang An eco-tourism area is very wide and the journey is long, if you go in the dry season, you should bring umbrella or leaf hat and sunscreen to use while sitting on boat on the river and some simple appliances like a thin coat when sailing into a low temperature cave. Going during the rainy season, a raincoat is extremely necessary to not disrupt your tour.- When sitting on a boat to travel through caves, visitors should pay attention to the instructions of the boatman to avoid hitting rocks in the cave or sinking boats causing accidents; You are required to wear a life jacket and follow the regulations of the resort.- You should not wear clothes that are too thin or too short when visiting temples and pagodas.

Calm, amazing view, clean water, eco-tourism. This was one of good memory in the Vietnam. You will see authentic Vietnamese landscape. Highly recommended tour.조용하고 환상적인 뷰, 깨끗한 물과 에코 투어리즘. 정겹고 열심히 노를 젓는 로컬분들과의 시간. 팁과 음료수 및 음식에 너무 돈을 아끼지 않을 것을 추천함.

We had a 1.5 hour boat trip with stunning views. We left at around 11.15 Am and was surprised to see how calm it was. Also on the river, we only passed a few boats. We bought enough cold water at the mini market, since I read about the scams of vendors half way. I didn't buy anything, didn't show any interest and they left us alone. I gave the lady driver one of my cold waters and she was happy with that. I also read about the drivers asking for a tip at the end. In fact I asked the lady at the ticket counter what a fair tip would be, and she said 100.000 VND should really be the max for One boat. Since the lady driver did a good job and was not cheecky,  I gave her  100.000 Vnd  for me and my daughter, she asked for 200.000 but I said no with a smile and she smiled back  and she thanked me for the tip anyway. The good thing about TripAdvisor reviews is that I read many reviews in advance so I know what to expect and it also prepares you for scams. So being well informed, we enjoyed every minute of this boat ride but it was very hot and we were happy to get off the boat to find some air-conditioned restaurant to cool off.
Most beautiful place on earth. No hassle or pestering. Get there early aroun 7:30amForget Tam Coc. Will take you breath away.

No dudes en ir, es cierto que tuvimos mucha suerte y estábamos con muy poca gente. El remero encantador y no nos pidió propina aunque se la dimos después. El precio son 120000 por persona y la barca 150000. El paisaje simplemente precioso y súper verde!!! No dudeis!!

Alojarse en Tam Coc y alquilar una moto para poder visitar toda la zona durante 2-3 días mínimo es uno de los mejores planes que se deben hacer en Vietnam. Mucho que ver, desde los paseos en barca (donde las propinas obligadas están a la orden del día..., quizá de lo que menos me gustó, en fin), subir los desiguales escalones hasta alcanzar el dragón, cenar en cualquiera de los restaurantes de la zona, visitar la pagoda...

Non c’è bisogno di guida, si parte su piccole barchette a remi guidate anche da bambini e donne abilissime a manovrare i mezzi con i piedi e non possono salire più di due persone x ogni imbarcazione. Il giro dura circa un’ora e mezza, in più punti si passa sotto a delle grotte da cui scendono rivoli d’acqua che a volte ti fanno la doccia e i paesaggi sono mozzafiato. Lungo il percorso si trovano anche altre barchette con persone locali che vendono bibite, frutta o ammennicoli molto caratteristici. È una delle attrazioni maggiori della zona anche se la stessa esperienza si può fare in altri punti non lontani da qui ma la nostra guida ci ha consigliato questo come più suggestivo. Per questa ragione non si corre il rischio di trovarsi da soli ma con parecchio traffico di barchette che salgono e ridiscendono il fiume.

The sight seeing is fantastic and we recommend to rent a scooter; it's beautiful. Also the people are very friendly and service minded.

Tam Coc, or three caves, is a nice little getaway from Hanoi. Tam Coc is touted as Ha Long Bay on land, and while we chose not to visit Ha Long Bay, we were quite happy with the Tam Coc visit. Lush green rice fields, towering karst mountains and river caves made for a nice afternoon. We took the boat ride through the caves and then cycled through the countryside and rice fields. Our tour guide was very informative and introduced us to Vietnamese culture and way of life, and also the traditional bamboo flute
