Tam Cốc - Bích Động là một trong những danh thắng nổi tiếng nhất của Ninh Bình được mệnh danh là "Nam Thiên đệ nhị động" (động đẹp thứ nhì ở trời Nam), nằm trong dãy núi Ngũ Nhạc Sơn thuộc địa phận thôn Ðam Khê, xã Ninh Hải, huyện Hoa Lư.  Khu du lịch Tam Cốc - Bích Động là một quần thể du lịch, một địa danh nổi tiếng từ xa xưa, thuộc địa phận xã Ninh Hải, huyện Hoa Lư, tỉnh Ninh Bình; cách thủ đô Hà Nội 100 km về phía Nam. Nơi đây vẫn giữ được nét nguyên sơ, thiên tạo, với nhiều hang động, di tích lịch sử, văn hóa nổi tiếng trong và ngoài nước như: đền Thái Vi, Tam Cốc, chùa Bích Động, động Tiên, chùa Linh Cốc…  Tam Cốc nghĩa là ba hang: hang Cả, hang Hai và hang Ba. Lúc thuyền luồn vào ba hang, du khách sẽ cảm thấy mát lạnh. Thạch nhũ từ trần hang rủ xuống lô nhô óng ánh như những khối châu ngọc kỳ ảo. Bích Động nằm cách bến Tam Cốc 2km, có nghĩa là “động xanh”, là tên do tể tướng Nguyễn Nghiễm, thân phụ của đại thi hào Nguyễn Du đặt cho động năm 1773.  Tam Cốc - Bích Động là một quần thể kiến trúc đậm nét thiên nhiên, phong cảnh hữu tình, chùa chiền mang nhiều dấu ấn của nền văn hóa dân tộc... đã và đang thu hút ngày càng nhiều khách du lịch trong và ngoài nước đến đây chiêm ngưỡng.
Không gian thoáng đãng và dễ chịu
Đẹp như vịnh hạ long trên cạn
Điểm vui chơi du lich thú vị
Đến một lần đã bị quyến rủ bởi non nước hữu tình ,bình yên, thơ mộng .
Phong cảnh rất đẹp và nên thơ
Phong cảnh rất đẹp và yên bình
Phòng cảnh bình yên đẹp tự nhiên

Trải nghiệm loại hình du lịch trên thuyền vô cùng mới lạ. Ngồi khoảng 2 tiếng qua 3 hang động, hai bên là cánh đồng lúa và thiên nhiên kì vĩ. Giá vé đâu đó 250k mình quên mất, típ cho người chèo thuyền khoảng 50k - 100k là hợp lý. Tam Cốc và Tràng An cũng tương tự nhau, nếu quỹ thời gian ít có thể đi Tràng An thôi.
phong cảnh hữu tình. độc đáo nếu đi du lịch ở đây các bạn nên chọn tuar 3ngay 2đêm tuyệ tình cốc cực đẹp như chốn bồng lai tiên cảnh nhưng rất tiếc trong này không cho nghỉ lại qua đêm.

Tam Coc in Ninh Binh is absolutely breathtaking! 🌾🚣 From the moment I started the boat ride, I felt completely immersed in nature. The limestone karsts rising dramatically from the river, the peaceful water, and the lush green fields all around—it’s like stepping into another world. I loved every second of drifting along the river, taking in the stunning scenery and the calm atmosphere. It felt so relaxing and almost magical, a perfect escape from the busy world. I could have stayed there for hours, just enjoying the beauty and serenity. Definitely a must-visit spot if you want to experience the true charm of Ninh Binh! 🌿💛

Visiting Tam Coc – Bich Dong was one of the most beautiful and peaceful experiences of my Vietnam trip. The entire place feels like a painting—lush green rice fields, calm rivers, and majestic limestone mountains all around.  The boat ride through the caves was slow, serene, and absolutely mesmerizing. Gliding through the river while passing under natural caves and surrounded by nature was truly relaxing. Bich Dong Pagoda, set against the hills, added a spiritual and calming touch to the visit, with lovely views from the top.  Everything here feels unspoiled and close to nature. The atmosphere is quiet, refreshing, and perfect for anyone who wants to step away from city life and just breathe. Tam Coc – Bich Dong is a place that stays in your heart long after you leave. I would highly recommend it to anyone visiting Vietnam—it’s peaceful, scenic, and simply unforgettable. 🌿✨

Tras visitar los Templos de las dinastías Dinh y Le, y la Pagoda de Bich Dong, realizamos el paseo en barca a lo largo del Río Ngo Dong, acompañados siempre por un espectacular paisaje; en este trayecto de unos 2 kilómetros cruzas tres cuevas (Hang Ca, Hang Giua y Hang Cuoi); Tam Coc quiere decir “tres cuevas”. La gran curiosidad de este paseo en barca, es que reman con los pies. Fue un paseo muy tranquilo y agradable; duró algo más de una hora.

Tam Coc–Bich Dong felt like Vietnam slowing down just for me. The boat ride through the river was calm and beautiful, with limestone cliffs rising on both sides and green fields stretching endlessly—nature showing off, quietly. The rowers using their feet was fascinating to watch, skill and tradition in perfect sync. Bich Dong Pagoda added a peaceful, spiritual touch. Climbing up through the caves and steps, surrounded by old stone and silence, gave a lovely sense of calm. It’s simple, soulful, and deeply scenic. Overall, Tam Coc–Bich Dong is pure serenity—no rush, no noise, just water, mountains, and moments that stay with you long after the ride ends. A gentle reminder that beauty doesn’t shout; it whispers.

A must do to experience the beauty and tranquility of Mother Earth.  This absolutely awesome boat ride was manned by amazing women who use their feet to row the boat. The entire experience takes about 2 hours - be prepared with sun block, hat or umbrella and water because it gets hot.  Soak in the majestic landscape of hills and mountains well known as the “inland ha long bay” amidst the tranquility of gliding along the calm waterways lush with water lilies in bloom if you get there early.  The ride takes us into narrow caves with very low ceilings. We had to lower our heads to avoid bumping into the cave structures which are centimeters within reach. The expertise of our females rowers navigating through the extremely narrow passages were nothing more than astounding.  Before the return journey starts we are offered aplenty local handicrafts to buy. Do bring cash along with you if you want to support the local community and also to tip them at the end of the ride.  We took a day trip from Hanoi to Tam Coc; We left Hanoi at 645 and arrived at 830am, perfect timing as the weather was still cool. The cost per pax for the boat ride is 100k VND which was bought when we arrived. There were no queues and was very quick. We had to then choose our life jackets and straight to the boat from there.

Super pleasant tour! Totally worth it, we didn't wait at all to get on, there weren't too many people on the river, the gentleman who took us on the boat was super friendly and although he didn't speak English, he taught us the Vietnamese names of the animals we saw. Unfortunately, there aren't many water lilies at this time of year, but it's still super beautiful and I highly recommend it. And after the boat trip, we also took a tuk-tuk to visit 3 temples.

We were on an organised tour from Hanoi. We loved the day to Ninh Binh. The ride on the boat where the driver used his feet to operate the oars was amazing. He rowed us using this method for up to an hour to an hour and a half passing lots of boats doing the same. The landscape enroute was lovely. Passing by paddy fields and through caves. It was such a lovely day out with a very nice buffet lunch.

Une superbe activité à faire à Tam Coc ! Les paysages sont superbes, il y a beau avoir du monde sur l’eau vous verrez que c’est très très calme, relaxant et apaisant. C’est très bien organisé vous faites le bateau puis une navette vous prend directement et vous ramène c’est très fluide. Le Monsieur qui nous transportait en barque était incroyable 🤩

Tam Cốc – Bích Động is a very beautiful and relaxing place. The boat ride takes you through caves, rivers, and rice fields surrounded by limestone mountains. It’s peaceful, great for photos, and the pagoda visit adds a nice cultural element. Definitely a must visiting spot in Ninh Binh, especially on a clear day.  You can see why this was a chosen spot to film Kong Skull Island Monkey

This a beautiful boat ride after a long day trekking. Breathtaking scenery. Very peaceful. Must do if your visiting Ninh Binh. It was the first time I see uncles and aunties rowing boats with their feet

One of the most peaceful and memorable places in Ninh Binh. Bich Dong Pagoda sits quietly between limestone mountains, creating a stunning and almost surreal atmosphere.  The path leading up through the pagoda, caves, and viewpoints is beautiful and easy to walk. The mix of temples, natural rock formations, and greenery makes it a perfect spot for photos and quiet moments.  If you're visiting Tam Coc, this is a must-see. It’s calm, scenic, and feels far away from the busy tourist areas. Highly recommend combining it with the Tam Coc boat tour for the best experience.  Highlights:  Beautiful scenery with lotus ponds and limestone cliffs  Unique cave-temple structure  Peaceful, quiet atmosphere  Great photo spots throughout the path

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️  Tam Cốc – Bích Động National Park is an absolutely breathtaking place and one of the most beautiful natural areas I visited in Vietnam. Often called “Ha Long Bay on land,” the landscape is truly magical, with dramatic limestone karsts rising from endless rice fields, calm rivers, and hidden lakes.  The canoeing (rowing) cruise was the highlight of the experience. Gliding slowly along the river, surrounded by towering cliffs and lush greenery, felt incredibly peaceful and almost surreal. The route takes you through several caves, where the light fades and the sound of water echoes off the stone, creating a quiet, almost mystical atmosphere. Emerging from the caves into open valleys and lakes feels like discovering secret worlds hidden within the mountains.  One particularly fascinating historical anecdote is that this area was once chosen as a strategic natural refuge during times of conflict, thanks to its caves and waterways offering protection and secrecy. This adds an extra layer of meaning to the journey, knowing that these tranquil landscapes once played an important role in Vietnam’s history.  The boat rowers are incredibly skilled, navigating narrow passages and shallow waters with ease, often using their feet to row—an impressive and uniquely local technique. The entire experience is slow, immersive, and deeply connected to nature.  Tam Cốc – Bích Động is a must-see destination, and the canoeing cruise is an unforgettable way to explore its lakes, caves, and timeless beauty.

Visiting Tam Cốc - Bích Động was truly a magical experience. The boat ride along the river, surrounded by stunning scenery, becomes even more breathtaking as you glide through fields of vibrant pink water lilies. It feels like stepping into a painting : peaceful, colorful, and absolutely unforgettable.  Tourist Tip: Go early in the morning to avoid the crowds and, most importantly, to see the water lilies in full bloom. Once the sun rises higher, they begin to close… so the earlier, the better for the most magical view!  The calm atmosphere, the reflections of the limestone mountains on the water, and the charm of the traditional rowing boats all contribute to a moment of pure serenity. If you’re in the Ninh Bình area, this is an absolute must-do.  A beautiful, authentic, and picture-perfect experience that stays with you long after you leave.

wundervolles Panorama und definitiv eine Tolle Erfahrung, sollte man besucht haben! 250.000d p. P. ca 1,5h sehr freundlich Fahrer, auch wenn man nichts von ihnen kauft 😅😉 die schönste Zeit um blühende Landschaften und die Reisfelder zu erleben ist ab April/Mai. wir waren anfan dez hier und es war tro atemberaubend schön.

Magnifique expérience à Tam Coc – Bich Dong, un lieu absolument incontournable au Vietnam. Le paysage est à couper le souffle, avec ses rizières verdoyantes, ses formations karstiques impressionnantes et cette atmosphère paisible qui invite à la déconnexion totale. La balade en barque sur la rivière est un moment magique, rythmée par le silence, la nature et le sourire des rameurs. Bich Dong, avec ses pagodes nichées dans la roche, apporte une touche spirituelle et authentique au site. L’ensemble est très bien préservé et permet de découvrir un Vietnam naturel, calme et profondément dépaysant. Un endroit parfait pour prendre son temps, admirer la beauté des lieux et repartir avec des souvenirs inoubliables. Je recommande sans hésitation.

Magnifique site qui vaut bien son nom de baie d'ha Long terrestre. Facile d'accès en voiture, possibilité de stationner avec quelques places autours du site gratuitement. Plusieurs offres possible avec en plus de la typique balade en barque, un tour en voiturette des 3 sites /temples. Surtout ne pas hésiter à gravir les 300 marches des grottes et monts a proximité, la vue est magnifique.

One of the best boat rides I've ever been on. It takes you through several caves and the scenery throughout the ride is simply amazing. It takes about 1.5hrs and each boat can fit 2 people. Highly recommend to anybody visiting the area.

I was positively surprised! 350k VND per person A long scenic boat tour, a stop off to walk up to a view point (steep steps) a bus tour of a few different temples and the fairy cave! I would recommend!

Super balade. Rendez-vous devant l’embarcadère pour prendre ensuite une navette jaune qui vous emmènera au « ticket information ». 250k Dong / personne pour la simple balade classique. Puis reprenez la navette en direction de l’embarcadère et vous pourrez monter pour faire une balade.  À la fin, il vous demande soit un tips soit d’acheter un cadre ( j’ai pu négocier à 110k Dong au lieu de 200k Dong ) au lieu de faire un tips à 150K Dong.

Even though it's quite busy and touristy, it's highly worth doing and great value for money.  Many of boats are paddled with their feet which is good fun to watch. Expect the locals to try to sell you bouquets of flowers and other items along the way. There are also boats with photogs in them offering to take your picture for extra $. Our boat paddler took photos for us and expected tips for doing so. Many boats have buckets of embroidered pictures to sell while you are taking in the scenery.

Ik verbleef hier vier dagen en heb het echt fijn gehad. Tam Coc is zeker toeristisch, maar voor mij totaal niet op een vervelende manier. Ik zat net buiten de drukte en kon daardoor volop genieten van de prachtige omgeving. Er zijn hele leuke plekjes om koffie te drinken, gezellige plekjes aan het water waar je naar de bootjes kunt kijken, en heel veel mogelijkheden om mooie dagtochten te maken met de scooter of fiets. Je zit dichtbij prachtige tempels en indrukwekkende natuur. Wat mij betreft absoluut de moeite waard om naartoe te gaan!
Beautiful place to visit. In some ways I like this area more than ha long bay. Very peaceful and scenic. I suggest anyone to visit if they can

Bardzo fajna atrakcja, choć ciut za długa, ok 1,5h powolnej jazdy lodka, natomiast fajne, zeby sie zdrzemnąć. Widoki pięknie, wioslarze wsioslujacy nogami. Mielismy okazję zobaczyc ludzi pracujących na polach ryżowych.

Fuimos por libre, compramos las entradas en el lugar, te explican las opciones y esperas a que se llene la barca con más usuarios que hayan elegido el mismo tour. Elegimos el de 9 cuevas, y estuvo muy bien. Uno de los templos estaba cerrado por remodelación así que vimos dos. Son templos pequeños. Lo que más nos gustó fue el paseo en barca por las cuevas. Parece que la mayoría elige menos cuevas, nosotros nos quedamos contentos con nuestra elección

Boating in Ninh Bình is a peaceful and unforgettable experience. As you glide along the winding river, you’re surrounded by stunning limestone towers, lush rice fields, and quiet caves. The boat rowers, known for their unique skill, often paddle using their feet, adding to the charm and tradition of the journey. It’s one of the most beautiful landscapes in Vietnam.

Tam Coc - Bich Dong ist ein atemberaubendes Naturparadies, das für seine spektakulären Karstlandschaften und ruhigen Wasserwege bekannt ist. Die Bootsfahrt durch die üppigen Reisfelder und majestätischen Felsen ist ein unvergessliches Erlebnis, das die Schönheit der vietnamesischen Landschaft in all ihren Facetten zeigt. Die Atmosphäre wirkt friedlich und einladend, und man kann die lokale Flora und Fauna hautnah erleben. Die Tempelanlage von Bich Dong, die in die Hügel eingebettet ist, bietet eine interessante kulturelle Ergänzung zum Naturerlebnis. Es gibt reichlich Möglichkeiten zum Wandern und zur Erkundung der Umgebung, was den Besuch noch bereichert. Ein Nachteil könnte jedoch die zunehmende touristische Erschließung sein, die in Stoßzeiten zu Menschenmengen führen kann. Dennoch bleibt Tam Coc - Bich Dong ein absolut empfehlenswertes Ziel, das sowohl Naturliebhaber als auch Geschichtsinteressierte anspricht und viele schöne Erinnerungen bietet.

جولة نهرية متميزة ومتعة لا توصف ، جميل ان تستقل قارب وتتجول في النهر لمدة ساعتين إلى ثلاث ساعات، كل قارب يأخذ عدد أربعة اشخاص، تمر بعدد من المناظر الطبيعية وتخترق الجبال الشاهقة عبر الممرات المائية ، والمرور من تحت الجبال عبر عدد من الكهوف يمتد بعضها لما يزيد عن 300 متر تحت الجبال ، انصح بتجربتها. هناك ثلاث مسارات الاول والثاني والثالث، اخترت أنا المسار الثاني، وحسب معلوماتي فإن المسارات تتشابه إلى حداً كبير مع اختلاف بسيط والسعر واحد ، حقيقة لا اعرف هذه المسارات ولا اين تمر لانه لا يوجد وضوح في مساراتها، فقط مجرد يخيرونك بين المسارات .

Definitely go and enjoy this incredible experience! Super relaxing and great way to see Vietnam countryside and traditional villages. Restaurants are very good and people are very friendly. Boat ride is definitely worth it!

오후3시쯤 방문 표사려면 어디로 가냐고 물으니 콤보 코스 티켓 구매하면 바로 탈수 있다고함 난 혼자에 처음이라 완전히 땡큐(솔직히 갔다가 다시오기 귀찮아서)350,000vnd 지불 검색해보니 보트만 250,000vnd 인듯함 주황색 구명조끼 입고 맨 앞자리 혼자 탑승 뱃사공에게 베트남어로 인사하니 살짝 놀라심 (하지만 몇가지 단어만 알고있을뿐) 출발하고 다리 몇개 지났나 나를 부르시더니 구명조끼 벗어도 된다고 하신다 (뭐지? 괜찮은 건가?) 일단 벗으면 편하고 좋지(빠지면 살려주시겠지) 조금더 가다가 엉덩이가 아파 중간 좌석에 손을 짚고 뒤로 살짝 누웠더니 나를 부르시며 첫번째 좌석과 중간좌석 사이에 앉으라고 바닥에 뭘 깔아주시고는 벗어놓은 구명조끼로 등받이 하라고 해주신다 ㅋㅋㅋㅋ(되는건가?) 너무 편하고 좋다 거의 욕조에 반신욕 하듯 반 누워서 투어했다 ㅋㅋㅋㅋ 다른 사람들이 나를 쳐다보고 사진도 찍는다 ㅋㅋㅋ (따봉 날려줬다) 혹시나 문제 생길수도 있으니 뱃사공 이름은 언급하지 않겠다 여러 구간을 지나 정말 작은 사원에 도착했는데 편하게 해주고 재밌게 해주셔서 소정의 팁을 드렸다 (좋아하시는거같다) 사원 옆에 테이블에 앉아서 차를 드시길래 옆에 갔더니 차를 주신다 그런후 산책로가 보여 한바퀴 돌았는데 처음 내린 곳 반대편이 나오는것이 아닌가 뱃사공과 눈이 마주쳤는데 늦었다고 빨리오라고 하신다  이런 언제 다시 돌아가지 생각하는데 다행히 뱃사공이 직접 배를 끌고 와주셨다 돌아가는길 풍경이 너무 이쁘다고 하시며 나에게 사진을 찍어준다고 하신다 처음에는 거절했는데 뒷모습이면 괜찮겠다 생각해서 찍어달라고 했다 (거의 누워있다) 보트 투어 중간에 지나쳤던 곳에 내려주신다 (콤보 코스) 뱃사공 폰으로 내 뒷모습 찍은게 있었는데 너무 잘나왔었다 나도 뱃사공 사진 찍은게 하나 있어서 교환 할겸 잘로 교환 하였다 (고마운 마음도 전할겸) 사실 수제자수 권유하고 팁 요구 한다는 리뷰들을 미리 많이 보고 탔는데 보통 되돌아가는 길에 그러는것 같다 나는 자수들이 다 맘에 들어서 모두 사고 싶었지만 한개만 사기로 했다 (200,000vnd) 그후에 노란색 버스? 카트? 를 타고 나머지 두곳을 방문했는데 처음이라면 올라가보길 추천한다 (허탈함을 느낄수도) 베트남 커플과 다른나라 사람들의 탄식이 들린다 난 땀을 뻘뻘 흘리며 올라갔다왔는데 나름 괜찮았다 콤보 투어를 다녀오면서 느낀것은 지금도 좋지만 추수 시즌이나 연꽃이 필때쯤 오면 정말 좋겠다는 생각이 든다 콤보투어 강추!!!

El paseo en barca, impulsada por una mujer (bastante mayor) que rema con los pies, extraordinario. Unas vistas espectaculares Lo que no me gusta tanto es como lo tiene organizado, como una turistada que hace que pierda un poco su encanto Todos intentan venderte algo, cuando no te exigen una propina De todas formas, imprescindible y recomendable Entrada simple 250000 VDN por persona. Entrada con tour en coche eléctrico, con visita a Bich Dong Pagoda, y otra, 350.000 VND

Visiting Tam Cốc – Bích Động is truly like stepping into another world. The beauty here is breathtaking and carries a sense of peace that is very hard to find elsewhere. The landscape of limestone mountains rising beside the calm Ngo Dong River creates a picture that feels both dramatic and serene. Taking a boat trip through the caves, with the gentle sound of paddles moving through the water, makes you feel as if time slows down. It is one of those rare places where you can completely relax and reconnect with nature.  For Vietnamese visitors, the experience feels almost like traveling abroad. There are so many international tourists here that sometimes you might think you are in a foreign country. This unique atmosphere makes Tam Cốc Bích Động stand out compared to other destinations in Vietnam. You can see people from all over the world enjoying the scenery, taking boat rides, and cycling around the rice fields. It gives the place a very international vibe while still keeping the authentic charm of the Vietnamese countryside.  The food scene here is also quite interesting. Because of the large number of Western visitors, many restaurants and homestays serve mainly Western-style dishes such as pizza, pasta, and grilled meat. This is convenient for foreign travelers, but for Vietnamese people who want to enjoy local cuisine, the options can feel a bit limited. There are still some traditional Vietnamese dishes available like cơm niêu, goat meat, or simple rice with local vegetables, but the variety is not as rich as in other regions. If you are specifically looking for authentic Vietnamese flavors, you might have to spend some time searching.  Overall, Tam Cốc Bích Động is absolutely worth visiting. It is a perfect place if you love peaceful landscapes, slow travel, and cultural exchange. The blend of natural beauty, the feeling of being part of an international travel hub, and the calm countryside atmosphere make it unforgettable. Whether you come here for a day trip or stay longer, the memories will stay with you for a long time.  Highly recommended for anyone visiting Vietnam!

Une ballade sur l'eau calme au milieu d'un paysage magnifique, à la recherche des singes en short blanc qui ne se sont pas montrés mais se sont néanmoins manifestés par leur cris particuliers. A faire, comme nous idéal semaine et plutôt en fin d'après-midi pour éviter la cohue.

La guida dell'agenzia turistica VIETNAM EASY GO TRAVEL è stata molto gentile,  professionale e ci ha dato tutte le informazioni del luogo visitato.  Il posto è incantevole e molto suggestivi e diversi uno dall'altro i tre diversi templi  opzionali in vari livelli, dal più basso al più alto lungo la montagna. Il primo più grande alla base, il secondo letteralmente incastonato nella roccia, nella quale vivono molti pipistrelli e l'ultimo,  più piccolo dal quale su può ammirare il panorama sottostante. Tour super consigliato,  grazie alla nostra entusiasta guida. Consigliatissimo!!!!!

Recomiendo la primera ruta, pasas por todas las cuevas y esta super bien, paisaje muy bonito. Lo único malo las escaleras que hay que subir a mitad de trayecto jajaja pero queda en una anécdota por los bonitos paisajes, totalmente relajante si vas solo. Recomiendo ponerse delante en la barca es impresionante

A 💯% must see and is mandatory to be on every bucket list. Fantastic row on a flat bottomed punt through magnificent vistas and through watery tunnels carved by the water over millennia. It is spectacular to watch the boats men and women propel the punt along using oars controlled by their feet. One can only imagine the stress and strain in the hips.

Schöner Punkt für eine entspannte Bootstour. Wenn man nur Bootfahren möchte, dann ist das ein anderes Office. Einfach mit den E Buggy mitfahren, die bringen einen dorthin und wieder zurück. Preis 500.000 für 2 Personen eigenes Boot. Die Fahrt dauert ca. 1,5 Stunden und ist der schön. Man durchfährt 3 Höhlen. Alles freundlich. Es werden Bilder zum Kauf angeboten, wenn man die nicht möchte gibt man sie einfach zurück.

티켓이 배만 타는 것과 전동카를 추가로 타는 것 두가지가 있는데 전자는 1시간30분. 후자는 2시간 30분 걸립니다 주의할 점은 두가지 티켓의 판매장소가 수백미터 떨어져 있고 두곳을 오가는 버기카가 있습니다 모르고 가면 당황하여 저처럼 걸어서 가게 될 수도 있습니다 저는 시간여유가 없어서 배투어만 했으며 강 앙쪽으로 붉은 연꽃이 피어있어 더 좋았습니다 이른 아침 권장, 양산 또는 모자 필수, 생수지참

It was a beautiful boat ride of around 1.5 hrs.When I went there was a typhoon so cave 3 & 4 were not functional otherwise it is a 2.5 hr boat ride.The view and the scenery is breathtaking.The lake is filled with roses and water lilies.They paddle the baot with their legs so that's fascinating. If you are planning to go for a baot ride at tam coc plan in the morning or evening otherwise it becomes very sunny and hot.carry loads of sunscreen,water and your camera

I did this around 3 PM and it was very chill and not crowded. The views are beautiful. I have also done Trang An and I like that slightly more, they offer a bit different views though. Worth a visit for sure

This discovery tour is truly amazing and will leave you with a lasting first impression. We took the tour in the week so it was less busy. A very peaceful and awesome experience

Super rameurs qui chante! Qui vous explique pas bcp mais pas trop ! juste parfait! Ils vous propose des arrêts photos! D'ailleurs celle ci pour l'arrêt photo où on a un super spot vu d'en haut! On lui a acheter une broderie a 100 k !  On à été vers 11h30! Pas trop de monde juste parfait!

O passeio de bote em Tam coc é muito legal! Recomendo ir enquanto estiver sol mas não tão forte, tipo 3 da tarde mas não tão tarde pois pode esfriar. Fui no início de novembro então peguei as flores abertas, cobraram 500k o casal. Incrível passar pelas cavernas e ter uma senhora remando com os pés.
以前までは1時間コースがあったようですが、現在は2時間コースしか開催していないようですので、ゆとりを持っての乗船をおすすめします。 小さなボートがいくつかの洞窟内を上手に通り抜けていく様は、少しだけスリルがあって楽しかったです。

Très belle expérience à Tam Coc. La descente en rivière est superbe, paisible et vraiment immersive. Tam Coc–Bich Dong is a beautiful and peaceful experience. The hike offers great views over rice fields and limestone cliffs, while the boat ride through caves and rivers is calm and scenic. It’s a relaxing contrast to busy cities and well worth the time for nature lovers.

Beautiful but busy - Here is how to find the "real" Ninh Binh ​Tam Coc is undeniably iconic, but honestly, the mid-day crowds and the "tips pressure" can be a bit overwhelming for some. If you want those postcard views but crave silence, I suggest coming here at the very first boat (7:30 AM). ​However, for those who feel Tam Coc is becoming too commercial, I found much more peace in the northern part of the province. I spent an afternoon in Van Long and Yen Mo villages—it felt like stepping back 50 years. No engines, just the sound of oars and the chance to see the wild Delacour's Langurs (one of the rarest primates on earth). ​Pro tip for explorers: Look for local groups that focus on "Responsible Travel" or "Wildlife Conservation" in Ninh Binh. There are a few small, native-run teams like Vietnam Charm that specialize in these quiet routes rather than the mass-market tours. They really helped me see the "Charm" of my hometown without the tourist traps. ​Safe travels and please keep Ninh Binh green!
닌빈 투어 일정 중 제일 좋았습니다.자연이 너무 이쁩니다. 배 노 저어주시는 분을 잘 만나야 하는것 같습니다. 저희는 역사랑 여러 재미있는 이야기를 해줘서 너무 만족 했습니다 팁은 인당 5만동 줬습니다.

Tam Cốc – Bích Động in Ninh Bình is one of Vietnam’s most breathtaking destinations, often called “Ha Long Bay on land.” The area is famous for its dramatic limestone karst mountains, winding rivers, and lush rice fields that change colors with the seasons. A boat trip along the Ngô Đồng River is the highlight, where local rowers—often using their feet—guide visitors through tranquil waterways and caves while surrounded by stunning natural scenery.  The three caves of Tam Cốc (Hang Cả, Hang Hai, and Hang Ba) are carved naturally into the limestone cliffs and offer a unique sense of adventure as boats pass through their cool, echoing chambers. Nearby, Bích Động Pagoda adds a cultural touch, with its beautiful temples nestled on a mountainside and views stretching across the countryside.  For photographers, this region is a dream. The reflections of the cliffs in the calm river, the vibrant green or golden tones of the rice fields depending on the season, and the interplay of light and shadow inside the caves all provide endless creative opportunities. Sunrise and sunset are especially magical, casting warm tones over the karst peaks.  Tam Cốc – Bích Động is both peaceful and awe-inspiring, making it a destination where natural beauty and cultural heritage come together perfectly.

Mi az esti órákban, találtuk ki,hogy ezt is megnézzük 😁 utolsóként indulunk ki a vízre, előnye az volt hogy nem volt sok csónak  ,és hallottuk a majmok bőgèsèt visszafelé akkor már sötét volt. Una experiencia única que sí o sí hay que hacer en Tam Coc o si estás por la zona de Ninh Binh. Este paseo en barca es diferente al de Trang An. Está mucho menos masificado y es más personal, vas solo con tu pareja en la barca. Fuimos cerca del atardecer y fue un acierto, ya que la luz era la ideal y no había prácticamente nadie! Los paisajes son una maravilla. Dura aproximadamente hora y media, y el barquero que nos tocó era un encanto, por lo que decidimos darle algo de propina y comprarle un cuadro hecho a mano, que al cambio a lo mejor fueron como 6€, que a ellos eso les supone mucho y a nosotros muy poco. A tener en cuenta!!: los tickets no sé compran aquí, sino que hay que subirse a un bus amarillo que te lleva calle abajo a la zona de taquillas, y después te vuelve a dejar en el embarcadero. Eso sí, si comparas el paseo en barca de Trang An, los paisajes de Trang An son más espectaculares, sin embargo, está mucho más masificado, por lo que si buscas algo más personal, mejor el de Tam Coc, aunque yo te recomiendo probar los dos, merecen la pena ambos.

Une magnifique aventure partagée avec mes deux enfants, pleine de rires, de découvertes et de complicité. La balade, tout simplement splendide, nous a offert des paysages à couper le souffle, entre sentiers ombragés et points de vue majestueux. Le passage entre les grottes mystérieuses et les impressionnantes formations de granit ajoute une touche d’émerveillement à l’ensemble du parcours. La durée est idéale, ni trop courte ni trop longue — parfaite pour profiter pleinement de chaque instant sans se presser. Seul petit conseil : prévoyez de quoi vous protéger du soleil, qui peut se montrer ardent par moments. Une expérience inoubliable que nous garderons longtemps en mémoire.
Nice ride. Very prity. Guides will wabt a tip abd try sell you drawn pictures and other will try get photos and sell them to you. Bring a hat there isn't much shade.
Very peaceful and scenic through the caves. The paddlers use their feet to row the boats ...very different

三古碧洞：越南北部的隱世仙境與千年人文交響 位於越南寧平省的三古碧洞（Tam Cốc Bích Động），距離河內約100公里，因其石灰岩山脈、蜿蜒河道與隱秘洞穴的壯麗景觀，被譽為「陸上下龍灣」。這裡不僅是自然奇觀的集大成者，更承載著千年歷史與宗教文化，吸引無數旅人探尋其山水與人文交融的獨特魅力。 自然奇景：山水畫卷中的洞穴與稻田 三古碧洞的核心景觀由「三谷」與「碧洞」兩部分組成，其名源於三座天然洞穴——Hang Cả（第一洞）、Hang Hai（第二洞）、Hang Ba（第三洞）。遊客可搭乘傳統竹筏沿吳東河（Ngô Đồng River）漫遊，船程約2小時，穿梭於洞穴與石灰岩山脈之間，感受河道忽寬忽窄的驚喜。 Hang Cả（長洞）：全長127米，入口處可見鐘乳石如雲霧垂落，洞內陽光穿透縫隙，形成光影交錯的奇幻景象。 Hang Hai與Hang Ba：較短且狹窄，船行其間需低頭避開低矮岩壁，增添探險趣味。 人文瑰寶：千年寺廟與信仰聖地 三古碧洞的人文底蘊集中於碧洞寺（Bich Dong Pagoda），這座建於15世紀黎朝的寺廟群，依山而築，分為下寺、中寺、上寺三層，被稱為「南天第二洞」。 下寺（Ha Pagoda）：入口處的木造建築未使用釘子，展現古代工藝智慧，寺內保存10世紀的藝術遺跡。 中寺（Trung Pagoda）：鑲嵌於岩壁中，寺後隱藏洞穴，需彎腰穿行，洞內供奉佛像，氣氛幽靜神秘。 上寺（Thuong Pagoda）：位於山頂，需攀爬數十級陡峭石階，登頂後可俯瞰吳東河蜿蜒與石灰岩群峰的壯闊全景。 碧洞寺不僅是禮佛之地，更被視為「心靈洗滌之所」。清晨或黃昏時分，陽光透過岩縫灑落，寺廟與自然融為一體，宛如水墨畫中的隱世仙境。 攝影建議：下午2-3點光線最佳，Hang Gioi（天堂洞）山頂是拍攝全景的絕佳位置，需攀爬250級陡峭階梯。 三谷船票：成人250,000越南盾（若包船需另加100,000盾）。 三古碧洞的獨特之處，在於其將大自然的鬼斧神工與人類的虔誠信仰完美融合。無論是乘船穿越碧綠洞穴，或是攀登千年古寺俯瞰山水，這裡都能讓人暫時忘卻塵囂，沉浸於「畫中世界」的靜謐與詩意。正如瑞士遊客比諾特所言：「這裡的居民、船伕的笑容，與風景一樣令人難忘」。若想深入體驗越南的自然與文化，三古碧洞無疑是不可錯過的朝聖之地。

A very nice river boat tour with some interesting points of view on the rice fields and the surrounding karstic mountains. Avoid the mid-day crowds and choose a visit in the morning when the water lily flowers are opened or in the evening. The discovery tour, including the climbing to the highest viewpoints and temples are no longer available by mid-afternoons. Also, you have to know that the tip to the rowers is not an option (approximately 50K dongs per person).

To be honest, the natural scenery here is truly beautiful and unspoiled. Taking a boat ride along the river is a wonderful experience each boat can carry three adults and one rower. The route is quite long, taking around 1.5 hours for a round trip. I visited during Tet 2025. There were not many Vietnamese tourists at the time, so the wait was short. However, there were many international visitors, and they had to queue in long lines (there is a separate area for domestic and international guests). One downside is the parking area for visitors there are no clear signs, so I saw many foreign tourists looking confused. The parking area for boat rowers is not clearly marked, which leads to misunderstandings. At the time I was there, I witnessed security staff and some boat rowers showing rather unpleasant attitudes, even driving away foreigners who parked their motorbikes near the internal staff area. This is something the local management should improve, so as not to tarnish the image of Vietnamese people especially in a destination like Ninh Binh, which receives so many international visitors.

Mooie tocht over het water. Je vaart naar een kleine pagoda en dan weer terug. Wij waren bijna als laatste gegaan, eind van de middag, rond half 5. Hierdoor al veel minder druk en temperatuur aangenaam. Je vaart over de rivier waarbij er geen schaduw is, dus overdag met zon moet het wel bloedheet zijn. Je gaat door twee grotten. In vergelijking met de boottocht in Trang An vond ik met deze tour dat je wat meer in de natuur zit. Er heerst ondanks dat het druk kan zijn, toch een stilte. Trang An wordt snel te toeristisch. De bergen en de rijstvelden geven een mooi panorama. Onze dame was aardig, wees af en toe wat plekken of dieren aan. Ze vroeg of wij nog wat prenten van haar wilde kopen maar hebben haar liever een goede fooi (300k) gegeven. Ze is toch een aardig eind aan het varen voor je. Tickets zijn te verkrijgen door met de gele golfkar naar een ander punt te rijden, dan weer terug naar het haventje. Het gaat een beetje op zn Vietnamees. Zeker aan te raden, ondanks dat het best toeristische is.

Tam Coc - Bich Dong offers a beautiful and peaceful boat ride through some of the most scenic landscapes in Ninh Binh. As you drift through open rice fields and between limestone cliffs, the atmosphere feels calm and immersive. The journey also passes through several caves, adding a unique touch to the experience. The whole ride takes around 1 hour and 40 minutes at a gentle, relaxing pace.  One unique detail is the way the rowers use their feet to steer the boat — it’s a traditional technique that’s fascinating to watch. You may also see local photographers offering souvenir photos along the way, which adds a personal element to the trip. Even on a cloudy day, the views are stunning and memorable. If you’re visiting Ninh Binh, this is definitely worth including on your holiday.

Tam Cốc – Bích Động is truly one of the most picturesque places I visited during my time in Vietnam. Often referred to as “Ha Long Bay on land,” this destination delivered a peaceful and breathtaking experience that I won’t forget.  We took a boat ride through winding rivers surrounded by towering limestone karsts and lush rice fields. Along the way, we passed through several natural caves carved into the mountains — an incredible experience as the boat glided smoothly through the cool, dark interiors and back out into sunlit scenery.  The boat ride was relaxing and immersive, with the rhythmic sound of paddles echoing off the cave walls. It’s worth noting that many of the boat rowers use their feet to row, which was fascinating to watch.  ⸻  ☀️ Tips for Visitors: •	Wear sunblock and bring a hat or umbrella — it gets hot under the midday sun, especially during the boat ride. •	Bring water and sunglasses for extra comfort. •	Have your camera ready — there are plenty of photo-worthy moments throughout the journey!  ⸻  ✅ Final Verdict:  If you’re visiting Ninh Bình, Tam Cốc – Bích Động is a must-do. The combination of natural beauty, gentle boat rides, and peaceful surroundings makes it a perfect half-day escape from the city.

Ti consiglio di andare lì presto, apre alle 7, per goderti al massimo l’esperienza.  La bisietteria non è nello stesso punto dove partono le parche (vicino al fiume) ma è circa 200 mt distante sulla strada. Ti scrivo l’indirizzo da mettere in google maps: 6W8R+R3H, ĐT491C, Van Lam, Hoa Lư, Ninh Bình, Vietnam  Noi siamo andati un sabato mattina a novembre intorno alle 9:30 ed è stato super super rilassante e tranquillo. La signora che stava guidando la barca ha permesso al mio ragazzo di provare a guidare la barca (è stato molto divertente, anche per i turisti che ci superavano) 😂  Siamo passati attraverso 3 grotte e abbiamo visto molti fiori di loto aperti ed è stato fantastico perché non ce li aspettavamo! 🪷 ci si può fermare a lato circa tre volte per delle pause fotografiche, una al un piccolo tempio, la seconda accanto a una distesa di fiori di loto e la terza per salire sulla cima di una vetta da cui si gode di un’ottima vista! Sulla strada incontri anche un paio di punti dove poter comprare una noce di cocco. La signora alla fine ti chiede una mancia, le abbiamo dato 100k, non so se è molto o no, ci ha ringraziato e se n’è andata comunque!  Consiglio di andarci presto, prima delle 9, meno persone, temperatura più fresca e fiori di loto aperti (dopo le 10 chiudono).

三谷(三古)碧洞其實就是旅遊業俗稱的「陸龍灣」，又分為水上遊船的「三谷」和旁邊爬山看「碧洞寺」，也是有生之年必來的世界遺產！ 這次跟團前來，陸龍灣遊船約一個半小時，雖然天氣不是很好沒有出太陽，但景色還是很美，穿過洞穴和兩岸有稻田的河道更是讓人印象深刻，不愧是有陸上下龍灣之稱，遊船結束後記得給辛苦的船夫小費！碧洞古寺約40分鐘來回，千年全木造古剎也讓人印象深刻。 另外，三谷碧洞其實僅是世界遺產長安風景區的一部分，長安風景區除了三谷碧洞外，其他比較出名的景點還有北邊的華閭古都和沛嵿寺，下次有機會再來！

ANIMAL CRUELTY. Chickens chained to trees outside, horses chained to bridges where the can’t move from, and goats in tiny enclosures. THIS IS NOT A VILLAGE, ITS A THEME PARK. There is trash everywhere, it’s falling apart and the first part of it is stalls with miserable and angry people all selling the same thing. The Chinese seem to love it because they can get dressed up in traditional clothing and have photoshoots with sad and abused animals. I hated every minute of this experience and am very angry I was lied to about what the place is. A theme park posing as a village with way too many selfie spots. This has been built for the chinese ridiculous obsession with photoshoots. They will do anything to get pictures of themselves looking ‘perfect’ at anything and anyone’s expense. Totally ruined what was once a beautiful spot. I will always tell people to avoid this. Nothing Vietnamese about it at all.

I had to be indignant with one of the “trainers” in order to obtain that one of his frames, obviously at a loss of strength, be attached more lightly, which immediately collapsed to the ground of exhaustion, which did not seem to surprise more than that its owner, who was obviously used to the blows of the Westerners.After a small investigation around the village, it would seem that horses and dogs executed by drowning are commonplace despite the repeated actions of animal protection organizations.No authenticity here, just a traumatic and vulgar horror. Very bad!Never comeback for this tourist trap.Nothing traditional All is shopping and photos, that's it1 star for staffs as well.
As others may have mentioned, this village is just a fake decoration with most buildings being tourist shops that are ready to scam you if given the chance.

English translation belowNGƯỢC ĐÃI ĐỘNG VẬT! Khu du lịch này vì lợi ích cá nhân mà đánh đập, bạo hành ngựa một cách dã man. Em ngựa trong hình bị đánh, đạp tới mức nằm ra đất. Giữa một thời đại văn minh, mà đối xử với động vật như vầy thì quá là tàn nhẫn và vô nhân tính. Động vật cũng biết đau và có cảm xúc, xin hỏi những người đánh ngựa và những người quản lý khu vực này, nếu bị đánh đập ngược đãi như vậy thì mấy người sẽ cảm thấy thế nào?Please boycott this place until they stop ANIMAL ABUSE! The horses in these photos was beaten severely and repeatedly by two men. The horse lying on the floor was in such a terrible pain that it couldn't even stand up. I had the video but it was too much to watch, and it made me cry each time This place cared about their profit more than the well-being of the animals. This whole region is known for eating horses and dogs in Vietnam, so if you are an animal lover, please avoid this place at all cost and report any disturbing behaviors like this.
A lot of tourist shops — not a great experience.The horses in the park are used for taking pictures with tourists, and they don’t look well.Hard to see.

A tourist catch. It’s just a giant photo spot. It is very beautiful visually but it is not authentic and full of visitors. After going on a trek and visiting the surrounding villages, there was really nothing to see for me there

CAT CAT is pretty much the most artificial village (if you can even speak of village) that we have seen in North Vietnam. Even the fruits and flowers are artificial and have been attached to the trees. In addition, you will find almost only Insta. and co. Poser. Too bad, actually, because the valley itself is worth seeing.

ANIMAL ABUSE – DO NOT go here!They hit the horses. This place is a complete tourist trap. The animals are treated terribly  the horses were exhausted, barely able to stand. The owner beats horses with hands and sticks. People were paying just to take pretty pictures while the horses were suffering. Absolutely disgusting and heartbreaking to see.

The guidebooks and even some reviews on trip advisor say this is a traditional village where you can see local villagers and their crafts - selling outside their home. I didn’t experience that at all. First, to get to the village it is an extremely steep walk down the hill to the ticket office (10 usd per person) and then another steep walk to the village. It was extremely hot and humid especially when you get to the village.  The main items for sale are the traditional clothing for women. There are a lot of tourists (young women and couples)  getting their pictures made by the river on horses and at the love waterfall. I didn’t see anyone demonstrating their handicraft or a lot of shoppers. After the heat and walk in you will gladly pay the additional 5 usd to ride up the hill to the ticket office and then pay for a taxis ride back to Sa Pa.
Too many tourist, very high prices! It is a cosplay place for pictures but not really local. I wouldn’t recommend the 30 minute walk downstairs to take a very expensive taxi upstairs!

To avoid, no authenticity, than tourists who take pictures in “traditional” outfits, exploitation of children.The houses are new like a Puy du Fou AliExpress.The only fun activities are extra (rainbow slide)

Rolled up at ticket office, paid for 2 adult, was told to walk the 400m to cat village, when I noticed there were golf buggies taking people somewhere, I asked where are they going?, ticket office person said you will have to pay to use them, well how about telling me that first, instead of telling me to walk 400m, totally unacceptable, nice place to visit but there are a lot of stairs. Just ruined by rude and racist ticket office staff. Only say that as I saw a Chinese or Vietnamese person go to ticket office and could see staff member pointing to the golf buggies, says it all, also had to ask for a map, even tho there was a whole stack of them there.

Saw a number of animals in very poor conditions during our visit including starving kittens & over-worked horses being used for tourist pictures.Do NOT go if you want an authentic experience or care about animal welfare.

Would not recommend. During our time visiting the village we saw a number of animals in very poor conditions including 2 kittens sitting on the main path of the village that were clearly very ill & near death. We purchased some food & water for the kittens, however they needed medical help. When we raised the issue with local shop owners & the tourist office no one seemed to care. Given this is a paid family tourist attraction I think those selling tickets & profiting from this village should have a duty of care to the animals within it. Really ruined the experience, alongside the rampant over-tourism & inauthenticity of the village which is now clearly just an Instagram location.

This place is a pure tourist trap. You get a taste of how the local people love but beyond that is quite chaotic. The bottom of the climb is overcrowded with people trying to complete photoshoots. The funny part which is also genius is that some of the waterfalls are fake.

Awful. It feels like a budget Vietnamese Disneyland. There was nothing authentic there, just a tourist trap where people pay to dress up in traditional attire and have photos taken, you could barely move for all the ladies pretending to be h’moang and having professional photo shoots all over the place. No original buildings or infrastructure there just gift shops and cafes. We spent one hour there after spending 150000 per person. Rubbish.

This was easily the worst place I visited during my time in Vietnam. It felt more like a staged tourist trap than a genuine cultural experience—essentially a glorified gift shop. Everything looked newly built and lacked any sense of authenticity. The atmosphere was bizarre, with people dressed in traditional attire from other cultures, almost as if they were playing pretend.If you're looking for a real insight into local life, I strongly recommend visiting the surrounding villages instead. They offer a far more authentic and meaningful experience.

We were looking for something enjoyable on our last day in Sapa and chose Cat Cat Village — big mistake. You’re charged 150,000 VND per person just to enter, only to walk down a steep path crammed with souvenir shops. No views, no atmosphere — just a tourist funnel.Even the waterfall at the bottom felt soulless. Whatever magic this place once had is gone. Overcrowded, overpriced, and utterly forgettable.Even the salt coffee was bad — and that’s saying something after all the great street coffee we’ve had. They try to milk more money by routing you through different exits, probably to force you into taking overpriced taxis. We walked back the same way. Two thumbs down. I would willingly pay 300k dong to not have my time wasted with this and have a nice day.
Spending 150,000 VND for this tourist village is not worth it. After entering, they try to sell you more products. The experience doesn't feel authentic—it's more about making money from tourists.

Don't go here.  First, you have to walk down a billion uneven stairs.  There are vendors on both sides of the stairs trying to sell you something.  You can't look up because you're too busy watching your steps so you down tumble down the stairs.  There's also a million tourists.  Once you've reached Cat Cat Village at the bottom, it's a weird Disneyland of sorts where people rent local clothes and take fotos.  It's bizarre.  The dancing show inside is sub-par.  Then to get back to the top, you either walk up a steep hill, or you pay to take the cart.  We paid.  It was the best money I ever spent in Viet Nam.  Cat Cat Village is a waste of time, energy, and money.  There's soooo many other wonderful things to do and see in Sapa.  This is not one of them.

Tourist scam, don't come here! Certainly not authentic. If you pay to come in here, you support animal cruelty. Stood 3 horses at the waterfall that looked completely worn and tourists sat on them to take pictures.

The biggest scrap I've seen in all my travels. Pure tourist rip-off (admission 150,000 VND pP) and you can only see junk. Just more shops, countless scooter riders who want to drive you back to Sapa). The viewpoints, waterfalls are absolutely laughable. Don't do this to yourself.
This village is only nice if you want to take photos. The horses there are also treated very badly and looked very overworked.

Very bad experience with Cat Cat Village. Tourist trap and not so good staffs who is work there. Will never come back here. 150,000 is too much for this experience. Poor of this stop on my journey around South Asia

This is nothing but an artificial and commercial tourist trap. It can of course be an experience in itself, but when you read about how the locals have been moved to create space for the “town”, and about the background of the city’s name, it leaves one with a bad taste in the mouth.Then choose something that will benefit the locals. For example, a hike with a local guide.

Chinese junk (sound bad, but it’s just the way it is) selled as traditional. Nothing authentic, just a bunch of shops.Inrgwret visiting such a venue and would definetly recommend taking a nap instead of visiting it.I was so “impressed” that I didn’t take any pictures there.

Lamentable experience; avoid this village. We refused to pay admission, a vigil from the most rebarbative asks you 150,000 D per adult, for children the price to pay is a matter of size, new marketing probably! Is this really a H'mong village or an amusement park, with extras? In any case the hike ends there, you can go no further, no way to cross and follow its path. Nice example of the mercantile artificialization of the world.

Run away! This place is no more or less a Disney Land for tourists. We regret having paid for access to this site, because it is condoning this overrated tourism, which uses children and animals to satisfy the narcissism of certain tourists... the smile on the face of the inhabitants disappeared, prisoner of tourism... In short, try not to condone this type of tourism!

We tried to find a bit of info on cat cat but could find much. Being in Sapa Cat Cat being close by we decided to go and visit it. First of all the walk begins along a busy road. After 1 km we found a short cut and ended up in a parking lot were we had to buy a entrance fee ticket. I live in Bali so nothing new here we have the same kind of villages. Then the walk to the village is surrounded by sellers from top to bottom. The whole way its steps with on flat area its down all the way. Ones arriving at the village its hard to distinguish were is the village. Has its a few dirty huts turned into souvenirs shops. The waterfall are not impressive and the place has sillions of tourist you could hardly walk. Imagine being stuck on steep step in a subway kind of crowd in a hot environment. And after all this effort finding yourself surrounded by shop on the bottom of a ravine. Thanks god after getting back up we hired 2 bikes for us to be brought back into Sapa. The bike ride was the best thing from the whole trip that got us as far away from there has possible.
Terrible, don't waste your time and money, really. They just sell cheap souvenirs, for quite high prices. There is absolutely nothing authentic about this place.
What a tourist trap this is, I hadn't read the reviews but should have. A commercial business ... that's all it is.... You better spend your time on a nice walk through Moa valley.

So its not touristy season so some of the attractions are closed and there were no performances. It's the same clothing sold in every shop and it's more expensive at the top of you want something they tell it's it's only 1 way but you can always go back up the steps to get something you might have missed out on. The views from the white cloud coffee shop are exactly the same as you see in the village stop there have a coffee save yourself 150 000 and go to the Sapa market and buy goods there instead exactly the same stuff. Not worth your time.

What a scam , only stitching going on in this village is on the tourist, visited 2 September and most of it was closed with no entertainment nothing. You just pay 150,000 dong to walk up and down a set of stairs and get hassled to but crap made in china. It was all the same designs and materials come on we not stupid. Reviews spoke about amazing views, you can get them from a near by coffee shop for free. Do not waste your time and money.

Place built specifically to draw money from tourists. You pay a ticket to end up in a fake village made of shops and restaurants in fake structures and with fake bridges. Nothing authentic if you wanted to see a real ethnic village. It's a shopping mall. I would add that we arrived at the peremptory indication of a place in pseudouniform in that street, as if it were the only village that could be visited, only to discover that he worked there, and we were charged for the ticket even though it was now evening and all the shops closed, so a useless visit. To go up from the valley a shuttle loaded us and eventually asked us for more money.
Total commerce. Actually only one shopping street for which you have to pay. Partly dangerous. Platform boards above the waterfall had rotted and can easily break through. Not recommended
Pay to go through some kind of shopping mall of small shops to walk. One big traffic jam of tourists. Waterfall is one big fair you save the trouble and money. It's really ridiculous.
Mass tourism, just stalls with small things. Lots of crowds. A place rather just for a photo on Instagram. You feel nothing of an idyll.

To avoid ! Accessible on foot about 20 minutes walk from the city center depending on your hotel.I arrived without knowing that there was a charge. I arrive at the top of a staircase, the entrance is manned by a guard, you have to pay 220 or 250D I don't remember well.But don't go there! It's a total tourist trap. For me it's like going to a human zoo, I totally refuse to pay to see people in their place of residence and see them working etc.... And who does the money go to? The women of the villages harass you in the street to make us buy souvenirs but especially THE CHILDREN! if the money is not for the children and to help them avoid forced labor....where then does this money go?!?! Lots of children in the evening, especially under 10 years old, probably who carry babies on their backs and try to sell key rings while the mothers look on. Sometimes sitting around doing nothing or selling too. Total boycott of these places and boycott of sales by women in the street because they are the ones who make their children work.
The path is a disaster and did not encourage us to pay the entrance fee! So dirty... Carefree! And steep! Taxi up is of course expensive
Pure souvenir stuff, nothing that is nice in any way, everyone says buy something, which makes it annoying, it costs 150,000VDN, although you can get in there for free, a little further down
An entrance fee to a non authentic village set up to provide yet another avenue to sell products to tourists. I guess the waterfall was ok, the rest of it is shops.

If I could leave no stars I would. Most unauthentic place in all of Vietnam. Rude locals (if they even are), everything is extremely overpriced and also does not seem authentic. They yell at your to buy stuff and if you don’t you also get yelled at and called names. LOTS and LOTS of child labour.

The place is basically tourist trap, there’s better option for nature village nearby and it wasted time and effort to climb and back onto this village. Some of places is asking you 10,000 VND just to take photo of the scenery.
No views, only mist. That would have been fine if not for the fact that this whole village and surroundings is under construction. The magic is gone.

A complete waste of time, it’s not a real village (maybe it used to be once) but a purpose built set of shops and cafes. Most people come there to so a professional photo session against the fake surroundings - I recommend to avoid it
Tourist trap, completely commercialized and as it's free for locals, you have to pay 150,000 per person as a tourist. 150,000 to let you spend money in the souvenir shops.  Waste of money and time.

A scam and a tourist trap.This place is absolutely of no interest. It is a huge maze of stone stairs lined on the right and left with souvenir shops; the steps are high and slippery. There is nothing to see or discover. When you arrive at the bottom of this endless journey, scooter drivers will offer to take you back to your starting point for a few dongs. We didn't hesitate for a minute because we were in such a hurry to leave this place, which I should point out that there is a fee to visit.

Worst Tourist Trap , We have ever visited in our life ...nothing to visit for .. no way to take your car ..local goons run mo bike service...dirty place ...parking far away ...only retail shops ...fellows wanting to overpriced crappy products  .

Not up our street at all. Tourist trap. Every shop sells the same thing claiming it's homemade... if you want posey photographs it's probably for you. Don't think it's fair to describe this village as traditional... the main activity is tourists renting traditional dress and posing in every location. You can also pay to sit on a horse with a bow and arrow for a photo...... Nothing traditional about the place. Expensive to get in and not worth it.

Cat cat village in sapa is overrated place I have ever been to. 150000 vnd ticket per person is just too much to see just a waterfall. Honestly, it was not worth a visit too. Its highly commercialized.People in Sapa are Little rude than people in other cities of Vietnam.

Don't Do It - Cat Cat is a total tourist rip off.A short walk away from our Hotel we thought we'd do it despite rhe reviews. It has a gang feel to it with the men using standoger tactics to get $$ from you. They charge 150,000 vnd per person to let you walk through their village., then badger you constantly to buy some tourist tat under the guise 'home made'. It wasn't a nice experience.Don't waste your money. Muong Hoa Valley, Ta Phin far superior experiences.

In een woord: verschrikkelijk. De grootste toerist trap ever!Je betaald veel te veel geld om langs kraampjes te lopen. Eenmaal beneden moet je zelf maar uitzoeken hoe je bovenkomt.Twijfel niet, ga niet!

I know that tourism is a way for many poor people to gain income and a better life. But wow. I have seen nothing like Cat Cat village. Actually there is no village or community anymore. From start to finish it is only tourism (clothing shops, Instagram photo ops, restaurants). While I was there I saw more selfie sticks than locals. If you look for an authentic village life you won‘t find it here. If you wanna take pics this place might make you happy.

This place is a tourist trap. To get to this place, you have to go down a long narrow street full of street vendors and dogs. Be prepared to get exhausted to say "No" and refuse people that try to sell you stuff and stop you. The village itself is not an authentic village, it's a fake one, made for tourists, and it's full with tourists only dressed up in traditional clothes that came there to take photos. Go on a hike in the rice fields instead, this place is a tourist trap and the few hours it takes to get there and see it are just not worth it.

We are two seniors that visited Cat Cat Village this Saturday morning. Bought the entrance tickets with our hotel and booked two way taxi in front of Sun Plaza. The steps down to the waterfall were not easy, luckily I have brought my hiking stick. There were many people in costumes that will push and shove their way down. After the waterfall and water wheels place, it is better to back track till you see motorcycles waiting.  If you follow the 'come back' sign to exit, the ascending part is tiring, as most energy was already used during the descend. Please take the first motorbike you see to get to your waiting taxi. We passed the phone to the motorcyclist to communicate with the taxi driver.Not sure if I have chose to do trekking with the tribe girls, will the route be easier.
It's all fake, nothing real. No authentic village.Entrance fee 150000 d. not 70000 like announced..

Had to pay for entrance to the village which was just all shops. It’s really not worth the money paid. Just look at the shops outside the main entrance. Even using the toilet was a paid thing. I’m really not sure why people still go there. Don’t bother to go there.

Went for a walk through the rice paddies and coming back along road unexpectedly got hit with 150,000 Dong charge just to walk through this village. What?!!! I have no idea why anyone would go there deliberately, it certainly isn’t authentic but appeals to the same tourists who like dressing in hilltribe costumes for posed photos.
Absoluut géén aanrader. Veel te touristisch. Je betaald 150.000 dong per persoon om souvenirs te bekijken. Na de trip heb ik absoluut niet 't idee dat ik iets van de cultuur heb gezien.

No!! Non ci andate è una vera trappola per turisti. I soldi spesi male di tutta la vacanza. Ingresso 5€ a persona in più bisogna considerare di arrivare e tornare in centro soprattutto se non si ha tanto tempo per fare la strada a piedi.Varcato l’ingresso è un susseguirsi di bancarelle e punti “panoramici” per scattare foto per i social. Noi siamo scappati nel giro di 20 minuti, fate qualsiasi altra cosa ma non sprecate soldi e tempo per questo FINTO villaggio.

From all villages that we visited around Sa Pa, Cat Cat Village was the least authentic one (and the only one that required an entry fee). Worst part was our lunch at Cat Cat. We got served the worst tea we drank in a while and both ordered sone North Vietnamese dish that is supposed to be eaten with rice. The food was served cold and we did not get the rice, so it was the worst meal we eat on our whole stay in Vietnam and still paid 18 dollars for 2 dishes. The little boy that served us did not understand english and the older people downstairs did not care. Retrospectively we should nor have paid for the food, since it was cold and incomplete.I would recommend to cut the Sa Pa trip short. It is definitely an experience  (like the Village of the Black Hmong was interesting and far more authentic), but there is not much more to do in the area.
Not worth the time or the money. There was absolutely nothing authentic about this place. Such a waste of time. Do not visit.
Ce village est une reconstitution pour touriste, un mini parc où l’on suit un circuit piétonnier qui conduit à des boutiques et quelques constructions qui sont tout sauf authentiques.

Tôi đã đi bản vào năm 2015, thời đó bản vẫn còn đẹp, còn nhiều mái nhà của người dân tộc địa phương, những ruộng bậc thang, đàn lợn bản.Tuy nhiên vừa rồi tôi mới đi lại và cảm thấy thất vọng toàn tập, con đường đi xuống bị các hàng quán che hết tầm nhìn, các quán xây những địa điểm bằng xốp và nhựa để mọi người chụp ảnh. Không thể thấy được một người dân bản địa nào, bạn chỉ có thể nhìn thấy nhưng cô gái trẻ người thành phố mặc những bộ đồ như người Trung Quốc và tạo kiểu ở khắp mọi ngóc ngách. Sẽ không bao giờ quay lại.

To jest najgorsza atrakcja turystyczna jaką kiedykolwiek odwiedziłam. Więcej tu Chińczyków przebranych w Wietnamskie stroje i ganiających za nimi fotografów niż prawdziwych Wietnamczyków. Aż przykro było patrzeć jak z wioski mającej pokazać życie lokalnych mniejszości ktoś zrobił cyrk i targowisko.Na każdym kroku wywieszone są kartki by nie dawać dzieciom cukierków. Turyści, zwłaszcza Chińscy, oczywiście wiedzą lepiej.W całym swoim życiu nie wyszłam z żadnego miejsca na świecie tak bardzo poirytowana jak z Cat Cat.Wstęp płatny, więc tym bardziej nie warto.Omijać z daleka. Wokół Sapy można zobaczyć wiele piękniejszych miejsc, bez  kiczu i komerchy,

I am local from Sapa I work here in Sapa everyday I Take tourism to going to this village  yesterday I went down to village and they ask me to pay they ticket because I am from different village I have to pay to get down there if i not guides i would don't want to going down there nothing to see  I am very disappoint from this people this is who ask for the ticket nothing to see and they charge me like visitor this is nothing to see and no long tradition waster time

It should be avoided. Too crowed with people thronging from all sides for pics. Shops on both sides of the road. You won't feel like having an authentic Vietnamese village experience. Visit Lao Chai -Ta Van trail instead.

Nuestra idea era ver un pueblo tradicional del norte de Vietnam, que es lo que la agencia nos informó. Ahora mismo es un gran shopping center que parece más un parque de atracciones. Si lo hubiesemos sabido no hubieramos desperdiciado una mañana yendo, porque hay cosas mucho más interesantes en el valle. Si por otro lado, el interes es en hacer compras de productos locales, es el lugar adecuado.
Village surfait, le disneyland des japonais amoureux de selfies et de kitch. Le cadre est tres beau quel dommage !!

Es handelt sich hier einfach nur um eine Straße am Hang, die keinesfalls als Village bezeichnet werden kann. Es reihen sich hier vielleicht 10 Verkaufsstände mit dem gleichen Zeug. Am Ende soll man dann (zumindest wenn man mit dem Roller fährt) eine Gebühr für die richtig schlecht ausgebaute Straße zahlen. Absolute Abzocke und die schlechteste Erfahrung während unser ganzen Vietnamreise!!

als je de echte toerist wil uithangen moet je naar cat cat. de weg ernaar toe is saai de watervallen en het hele dorp is gemaakt voor toeristen. 10 jaar geleden had het misschien de moeite geweest. "lokale"mensen verkopen je souvenirs en bieden je de hele weg vanalles aan. ben je rond en wandel je terug naar boven moet je eerst 30 taxi chauffeurs weigeren en dan dezelfde weg terug omhoog die saaie baan. het enige positief puntje is dat je een idee krijgt van hoe mooi het hier geweest is
מקום תיירותי עייף מאוד. זה לא ממש כפר, יותר חנויות של תיירים עם פריטים מכוערים במיוחד ומאוסים. ממש לא ויאטנם של פעם עם מבט על התרבות של המקומיים. חבל
Turistada máxima donde hacer tus posados y poco más! No queda nada del pueblo que en algun momento fue!

I don’t get why I went to Vietnam. I guess the idea of cheap travel to a beautiful destination, culture, and a unique experience... We did the Cat Cat trekking tour with a guide. Su our guide was adorable. She spoke excellent English and knew everything there was to know about Sapa Village and Cat Cat. I just cannot get over the pollution, the dog s*+t, the smells.... the wares are not cheap, (and each shop sells the same stuff), the tour is noisy and dangerous with traffic whizzing by, narrow steep winding walk to the village at the bottom. If you can’t walk back up it’s a 30 minute wait for a taxi for the treacherous drive back up with trucks attempting to pass tourist buses on the narrow steep cliff edge roads.... We arrived day after a torrential downpour so lots of roads had collapsed, landslides everywhere with the rain so even more harrowing getting around in a car or even walking.... We ended up getting a taxi to the 🚠 cable car. That was amazing.... but if you’re not good with heights and you can’t walk too far it’s not for you. Even if you take the cable car and the train to the top, you still have to walk back down several steps (100?) to get the cable car back. The weather was clear when we went but this is rare. If you can do it Fansipan at 3143m is unbelievable, how did they build that!

Wer nach Sehenswürdigkeiten in Sa Pa sucht, wird vermutlich binnen der ersten Minuten Recherche auf das berühmte Cat Cat Village stoßen. Was einige als Einblick in ein authentisches Dorf der Black Hmong mit pittoresker Szenerie (Wasserfall, Windmühle, etc.) beschreiben, würde ich eher als zu groß geratenen Souvenirshop bezeichnen. Überwältigend war für uns somit leider nicht die Schönheit des Dorfes, sondern die Schattenseiten des Tourismus, der sich hier unter Anderem auch in der Ausnutzung der Kinder, die in jüngstem Alter bereits arbeiten und/oder um Geld betteln, äußert. Es zerreißt einem praktisch das Herz, diesen bedürftigen Kindern kein Geld zu geben. Aber eine solche Geste kann letztendlich zu mehr Schaden führen. Die Kinder werden oft von Erwachsenen (nicht immer den Eltern) ausgenutzt, die das verdiente Geld dann für sich behalten. Man kann sich daher nie sicher sein, wessen Geldbörse man mit dieser kleinen Tat füllt. So unmenschlich dieser Rat auch klingen mag: Gebt kein Geld an die bettelnden Kinder! Obwohl Tourismus in vielerlei Hinsicht, den Vorteil mit sich bringt, eine Wirtschaft voranzutreiben, hat es auch seine Schattenseiten. Im Falle des Cat Cat Village sieht man sehr genau, wie Tourismus Abhängigkeiten schafft und Orten ihre einst so einmalige Authentizität nehmen kann.

Ce village fake n’a rien à offrir ! certes le paysage est relativement beau (et encore y’a plein d’autres spots gratuits qui sont aussi bien voire mieux !), mais quel crève cœur de voir des enfants d’à peine 5 ans costumés « « traditionnellement » » (dans le meilleur des cas) qui servent d’attraction touristique et qui désertent l’école parce que des pigeons de touristes trouvent ça cool de les photographier et de leur donner de l’argent ce qui alimente la machine !Surtout que c’est écrit NOIR SUR BLANC partout de ne pas le faire. Mais des gens continuent d’encourager la mendicité des enfants...🤦🏼‍♀️🤦🏼‍♀️🤦🏼‍♀️

Se pensavate di venire a Sapa per fare un po' di trekking VERO, e vedere posti incontaminati o comunque veri, avete sbagliato proprio tutto. Magari cercavate quella tipica atmosfera alla Chiang Mai anni '90, o la fuga dal turista "lonely planet" (citazione "the beach"). Ecco, Sapa non avrebbe potuto essere più distante. Ma il fondo del pozzo lo si tocca col giro al villaggio di Cat Cat, dove sembra di stare a Gardaland.tutto finto, tutto strutturato per i turisti. Tutto a pagamento. Ci sono effettivamente persone che ci vivono, ma più che nativi del posto sono imprenditori alternativi. Se potete, evitate sapa. Ma se ormai siete qui, andate a xin chiao... Finché sarà ancora minimamente "vera"
Touristisch ausgebeutet an der Strecke sind zu viele Shops. Die Touristen laufen mit den Einheimischen Gewändern rum. Dadurch ist die eigentliche Attraktion nicht mehr sehenswert.
Un villaggio ricostruito per i turisti. Lungo la scalinata solo negozietti di souvenir. Poco autentico.

A visit to Cat Cat village is horrible. It doesn't feel authentic at all. It's a big tourist trap. And worst of all, there are small children (with even smaller children on there back) selling things... It's so sad to see

Liebe Leute erspart euch bitte CatCat! Da gibt es nichts zu sehen!!! Nur ein künstlich aufgebautes Dorf mit x gleichen (Souvenire)-Ständen!!! Nur ein Touri-Falle! Hat nichts authentisches! Nur ein Massengedränge.
Skanzen pro jihovietnamské turisty, budiž jim přáno, ale rozhodně nemá cenu tenhle cirkus navštěvovat. Neřekne nic o životě horských etnik. Smutné, kam se Sapa ubírá.

Do not go to this village. This village is filled poor people trying to sell souvenirs. Nothing special to view. Locals will try to make you pay for parking motorbike and sell you anything they can. Once they see a westerner they try to make money. Also we have seen kids playing in the mud with pigs. A man running with chaser in the middle of the road and Hmong people eye balling us. Not recommended.

Ich war dort an einem Sonntag, vielleicht war das der große Fehler und es ist sonst nicht ganz so schlimm: eine unendliche Touristenhorde ergoss sich über den Ort (Tausende! Überwiegend Vietnamesen), man ging nicht, man schob und man wurde geschoben. Das ganze ist aber eh nur ein großer Marktplatz, wo das übliche Zeug angeboten wird. Eintritt dafür 70000 vnd, nicht viel, aber zu viel! Tipp : Da haben Vietnam und Sa Pa viel besseres zu bieten, meiden.

If you come to Sa Pa to see the local tribes and villages, just skip Cat Cat all together. Instead use your money to buy a local guide (many to recommend), and trek out a little bit. Cat Cat caters for massive groups of Chinese tourists who buy tribal dress and roam the village. It has lost all its previous beauty and is just a tourist trap. Avoid!

Einen schlimmeren Touristenort habe ich während meiner 8-monatigen Weltreise nicht erlebt. Alles ist künstlich für Touristen erstellt. Der Wasserfall kommt aus Rohren! Und alle 2 m sollst du kaufen, kaufen, kaufen. Besonders schlimm waren die Kinder, die bereits ab 3 Jahren den Touristen Dinge verkaufen müssen. Ein sehr seelenloser, hochgradig kommerzialisierter Ort!Sparen Sie Geld und vor allem Zeit und besichtigen Sie das „Dorf“ nicht.

I hate that kind of experience. It’s like an amusement park: you pay the entrance, it is overcrowded, there is more souvenirs shops than attractions, and the attractions are all fake: traditional dances all day long, water wheels that bring water nowhere, it’s just for pictures, bamboo bridge so instagrammable, and fake typical houses where nobody actually lives plus they exploit children. They put rag clothes on them and they request them to convince tourists to buy cheap souvenirs. It’s a shame and it’s a scam. Keep your money and go trekking in remote authentic villages. Except if you’re in a desperate quest of more likes on your Instagram feed this place is useless
It was a nice walk however the path is full of mud and you have one shop stands after another very far from being authentic

Plus rien d’authentique, tout est fait pour les touristes. La descente de ce ‘’village’’ n’est que petits magasins où les locaux vous stresse pour acheter leurs soit disant produits artisanaux... Des qu’on s’arrête on vient nous parler et sur tout nous vendre des petits articles aussi soit disant artisanaux... Bref, intéressant de voir comment le tourisme a transformé ce village en un faux-village de shopping et de commerces. Dommage...

We just wanted to go for a short hike so the hotel reception sent us there. You pay 100.000 vnd to get into an "authentic" village, but its just a kilometre of steps with souvenir shops. Just terrible.
Went expecting locals and a cozy atmosphere but were met with a lot of tourists and locals trying to sell goods and trick us. Very money hungry. Totally not worth the entrance fee.

We went there to see the authentic local people and instead we got stairs full of shops, most of them with overpriced items. We asked to buy a coffee filter, only to find out that this was several times more expensive than the one that we bought from a shop in Sa Pa Center. I would recommend to skip this.
Only shops and fast foods, nothing really there to be seen in Cat Cat.Better save your time and avoid this!
On finance un décor attrape touristes. Des cascades fausses et des roues à eau pour les photos. Je regrette d'y être passée.

A very busy touristy walk through many stalls selling a few real hand crafts but mostly contemporary touristy stuff. A few old homes and craft demonstrations and we did see a little dancing but mostly a very sad reconstruction of village life. Too many locals trying to sell goods. Tourists getting dressed up to pose for photos. I would not bother going to Cat Cat, better to go walking to see real villages.
Drive 10 minutes more to the next village and get the real experience (free of cost). This is a dumb tourist attraction that does not provide any added value (except for the view of the waterfall).

Went to Sapa and had part of tour the Cat Cat village. It is very touristic and filled with shops selling same items. It is not worth with a tour and neither if you have limited time in Sapa. Better a hike in villages than this touristic area. Very disappointed.
Lo único que hay es una cascada muy normalita el resto todo puestos ambulantes. Una auténtica pérdida de tiempo

Es una aldea completamente preparada para el turista, y abarrotada. El camino de bajada está repleto de tiendecitas, las vistas merecen la pena, el pueblo no tanto. Puede ser una buena opción si vas con niños pequeños.
나는 소수민족 마을의 살아가는 모습을 보고싶어서 방문한것이었는데, 그냥 관광객 대상 물건팔이 마을이 따로없었다입장료는 7만동/인 을 받고 오르락 내리락하는 수고에 비해 볼것 하나 없는 이상한 관광지다비추다 비추 핵비추

One of the worst tourist traps I have ever seen. It just feels fake all the way. I also felt really sorry for the kids and youth at this place. Small kids dressed up to please the tourists and youth dancing for the tourists, trying to look happy but you do not have to know many teenagers to see that they really hated it.

It's a joke. They charge 70000 dong to enter the village. It's hardly a village. They have like 2-3 old houses and a 100 shops selling things, and a few restaurants. There is a waterfall which is again surrounded by restaurants. The walk is all steps or cement road.

Not for nature lovers, not for hikers, not for authentic culture. Simply one long tourist market.The first 2/3 of the walk is down a rubbish strewn pathway with hard selling shops all selling the same tourist junk. The waterfall is beautiful and gives you a moments peace before you continue amongst the never ending shops. The rest of the path is covered by motorbikes trying to take you back to Sa Pa. You will be hounded the whole way by sellers and you cannot see much of the beautiful landscape as the shops overhang the street.Plus you have to pay entry to this market.Definitely worth a miss.

스위스에서 마테호른과 아이가빙벽을 바라보며 하이킹했던 기억을 떠올렸으나 완전히 상업화된 관광지에 불과하였음.시멘트 포장된 도로위로 자동차와 오토바이가 무질서하게 오가고, 곳곳에서 호객행위하는 사람들, 구걸하는 어린이들....7만동 입장료 내고 입장하면 보이는 상점들... 오던 길에 보였던 카페나 레스토랑에서 내려다 보는 풍경이 오히려 더 아름다움...돌아오는 길 모터바이크 이용하라고 하길래 되었다고 했더니 영어로 욕하는 사람까지... 최악이었음. 다시 가고 싶지 않은 곳.

Mais est-ce encore un village. Non.C’est une succession de boutiques avec des gens en costume, et noir de monde. Toute la difficulté de concilier tourisme et préservation des modes de vie locaux...Si vous avez envie de shopping et que vous avez de bons mollets (c’est tout en escaliers), pourquoi pas. On peut au moins supposer que ce qui est acheté ici revient directement aux Hmong.

Please do not go. Horrible. Horrible walk. Tacky tourist stuff. No sense of community. I am sure the people in the village do not want to be there and I certainly didn’t. Hordes of tourist turn up dressed in “ traditional dress” bought on the way and pose for photographs. Sordid and tacky the whole thing. The walk to the village - awful.While I am here and I have never written a review about a town before - Sapa is awful. It’s just awful - like a badly managed construction site. Building works everywhere- roads dug up everywhere. It’s like you are going through the trenches. I am unsure why people would come here as I know there are far more beautiful places in Vietnam where people show that they care for the places they live in - feel very sad to write such a review about a place. I know it is about the hiking here but what surrounds that needs to be cared for too and the infrastructure created in a way that supports the area. So sad to see what is happening here.
Not sure whether I cover the entire place, as what I mostly saw are shops selling souvenirs, not the village that I expected to see.

Um zum Dorf und zum Wasserfall zu gelangen muss man eine Art Wegezoll in Höhe von 70.000 Dong bezahlen. Dieser Wegezoll ist in keiner Karte beschrieben und auch auf dem Weg selber nicht ersichtlich. Als wir die Straße Richtung Cat Cat entlang gelaufen sind wurden uns sehr unfreundlich hinterher geschrien, wir wurden regelrecht angepöbelt, das wir die Gebühr bezahlen müssen. Wir haben uns dann dagegen entschieden das Dorf und den Wasserfall zu besuchen. So unfreundliche Vietnamesen sind uns auf unserer ganzen Reise nicht begegnet. Es zeigt aber auch wie touristisch die Sa Pa Region ist. Ständig wird man angesprochen etwas zu kaufen, ein Motorbike oder Guide (für Wege die man sehr gut alleine laufen kann) zu mieten. Für uns war es die größte Enttäuschung unserer Vietnamreise.

Kalt. Matschig. Nur kommerziell. Fühlten uns wie in einem Freizeitpark. Handwerk Stickereien usw.  Aber Webstühle kennt man auch aus Deutschland. Unser Resume für Sapa: muss man nicht hin. Zumindest nicht im November! Nur kalt (10grad) nebelig. Nass. Sehr matschig. Schmutzig. Hotels besitzen keine Heizungen (3*hotel) Schimmel und Feuchtigkeit in den Zimmern ist das Resultat

Un "village" commerçant avec très peu d'intérêt, on se demande d'où vient cet engouement. Sans parler du prix d'entrée, 70000vnd, abusif. Faire le trajet à pied du centre de Sapa à l'entrée du village suffit pour admirer la vue, nul besoin d'y rentrer.Nous sommes arrivés très tôt pour éviter le trop plein de touristes. C'est donc un village mort que l'on a visité, quelques commerçants ouvraient leurs boutiques, ça n'avait rien d'un village habité, grosse déception...
Niet 'echt', alleen maar gefocust op touristen en zelfs de omgeving is niet te vergelijken met die in bijvoorbeeld Lao Chai. Daarvoor betaal je dan ook nog 70000 VND per persoon.
