Se encuentra a unos 30 min caminando desde Sapa. El paseo es agradable.Una vez llegas se tiene que pagar una entrada de 70k por persona. Entras una especie de circuito que parece un parque temático sin ningún interes.Lo que en teoria es el pueblo se halla en muy mal estado. Los niños estan vendiendo souvenirs o preparados para una foto a cambio de dinero. No tendrían que estar en el cole? Me pregunto donde se invierten los recursos generados por las entradas.No lo recomiendo.

De omgeving is erg mooi, maar het dorp zelf is enkel opgezet voor toeristen: overal souvenirs (ik betwijfel zeer sterk of die daadwerkelijk handgemaakt zijn) en 'optredens'; niets authentieks aan.  Ook staan overal kleine kinderen die souvenirs proberen te verkopen, onder andere een ongelukkig ogende kleuter met een pasgeboren baby op z'n ruggetje gebonden, zodat toeristen uit medelijden iets zouden kopen. Al met al een droevige bedoening!

Walking through Cat Cat Village near Sa Pa was my worst experience in Vietnam. It's not an original village, everything you see is especially made for tourists. Also bridges and mills don't seem to be "real". After paying entrance (50k Dong) you walk stairs up and down through the village - but you just walk by souvenir shops. The worst disgusting thing was that the children were dressed up in traditional clothing, sometimes also carrying a baby, forced to sell bracelets or some stuff. They are treated not like humans, more like a nice decoration for your photo book, because you could also pose with some girls for a picture and afterwards they wanted money. Believe it or not, many tourists wanted a photo with them! I felt extremely uncomfortable walking through the village - no visit worth! Everything is just made up.

En quête d'authenticité, nous avons posé nos sacs à dos 2 jours à Sapa pour découvrir la vallée de Muong Hua.En arrivant à Cat Cat, quelle déception !!!À peine arrivés sur la route menant à Cat Cat, la route vous est barrée pour déposer votre moto, et surtout payer le parking (10.000vnd).Ensuite entrée dans le village s'élevant à 40.000vnd.À peine entrés dans le village, vous serez harcelés par des femmes et des enfants vous vendant des produits "artisanaux" et "authentiques" tels que des sacoches Made in China, des t-shirt Reine des Neiges, ou encore des perches à selfie.Vous descendez alors des escaliers sur quelques centaines de mètres, afin de descendre vers une seconde zone, au décor très joli, mais sentant le carton pâte et la peinture fraîche (cascade artificielle, grandes roues à eau, inutiles mais jolies, spectacle de danse traditionnelle avec jeux de lumière et de fumée), puis passerez devant des stands ou des personnes (des minorités ?) sont comme "parquées" et procèdent à des démonstrations de tissage et tressage...On en vient même à se demander si des personnes vivent réellement dans ce village, tant les bâtiments sur place sont consacrés à la vente d'objets.Si le travail des enfants vous dérange, et que vous cherchez de l'authenticité, préférez une journée de trekking dans la vallée, renseignez vous au Tourism Information Center, près de l'église, ou demandez aux femmes venant des villages de vous guider.

Fuimos a visitar el pueblo y no es nada original. Te obligan a bajar por un sendero con todo tiendas, la gente del lugar te pone malas caras. Nos obligaron a dejar la moto en la entrada, te cobran para luego cuando ya estas abajo te ofrecen subirte en moto y te cobran cuando en realidad puedes bajar con la moto y dejarla gratis en el parking de abajo y pagar el ticket en la parte baja. Decepcion total, hay otros pueblos mucho mas bonitos que merecen mucho mas la pena visitar.

Ich habe eine längere sapa wanderung über die dörfer unternommen und in homestays gewohnt. Cat cat village habe ich am letzten tag vor der abreise besucht und war ehrlich gesagt schockiert. Es handelt sich dabei um ein künstlich angelegten park der eintritt kostet (50.000 dong) und aus einer reihe von verkaufsständen besteht. Vom leben der menschen sieht man nicht wirklich etwas, das einzige was ganz nett ist, ist der wasserfall! Wer tatsächlich das leben der einheimischen sehen möchte sollte eine geführte wanderung über die dörfer buchen und sich diesen teil sparen. Hier sind vorallem die menschen gut aufgehoben die es lieben die souveniershops zu wandern :).

Cat cat Village, ca. 1,5 km von Sapa entfernt, geriert sich als einheimisches Dorf zwischen malerischen Reisterrassen. Auf den ersten Blick dürfte allerdings jedem klar werden, dass es sich dabei um die reinste Farce handelt. Wer tatsächlich Wandern und die Landschaft bestaunen möchte, sollte eine Tour in die umliegenden, wahrhaft schönen Täler machen. Das geht im übrigen auch sehr gut ohne Guide :)

Das "Dorf" ist eine reine Touri Attraktion mit einem gefaktem Wasserfall und unzähligen Verkaufsständen (die alle dasselbe Verkaufen.)Die ersten 10 Minuten geht man kleine Gassen hinunter wo man überall dasselbe kaufen kann. Standard Touri zeug nichts Besonderes.Wer darauf steht ist hier perfekt aufgehoben allen anderen würde ich davon abraten.

We chose cat cat village to visit as we were short on time and it's only a 2km downhill walk from sapa centre. It is purely a very slippy, rocky, crowded path between lots of stalls/shops selling the same old tourist tat. The place was full of Asian tourists and was very busy. The local village people were just sat in there shops in Nike clothing trying to entice you in. There were also the typical children selling stuff, like you see in Sapa, also a few begging too. One thing I witnessed that really shocked me was a young, Vietnamese tourist boy offer some sort of food packet to a very scruffy looking village boy, who then snatched it from him, threw it on the floor and bashed it with a big stick he was carrying. There is a stream with large water wheels which is pretty and a few flower gardens too. There is also what appears to be a man made waterfall however that is pretty too. We also managed to catch the end of a traditional dance show which was lovely and at the end we could get involved on the stage too - favourite bit of the experience. Overall this is not at all an authentic village experience, it is purely just for tourism.
Hätte man sich sparen können. Das Dorf ist nur für Touristen und wirkt wenig authentisch. Selbst der Wasserfall ist zur Hälfte künstlich.

Typical tourist trap, everyone is selling the same stuff and renting traditional clothes. Entrance fees of 150k VND to only get to see about 3 waterfalls & that's pretty much it.  Bought my tickets at ticket checkpoint 1 & was approached by local motorbike riders soliciting to fetch us to the main entrance of cat cat village for 150k VND to and fro as they claimed it takes about 1-2 hours to reach the entrance by foot & there is nothing much to see on the way. Do haggle with them if you intend to take up their offer.  You should expect a lot of hiking for this place, you will be greeted by a long flight of stairs down from the entrance. This village is built in a loop of stairs (in a sense) that you have to exit the same way you came in. They also exploited a few horses for tourists' photo-taking, mostly looked malnourished & exhausted; heartbreaking to see.  If you are looking for the rainbow slide at cat cat village, you have to walk your way up to the top; it's hidden behind the trees at the back. Do not get duped by the many wannabe influencers who said the slide is "around" cat cat village. You still have to pay the entrance fees and walk your way up, the entrance of the slide is on top of a steep road.  They have buggy rides which charge 10k VND to take you up that short steep road if you're too tired to walk up the slope. One ride on the slide costs 40k VND per pax, drone video will cost you 100k VND (no sound included).  All in all, i do not recommend spending your time and money to visit this place.

Thứ nhất, t mới trải nghiệm cái chỗ nhà trình tường đất có con mẹ bán bạc người Kinh, không phải chủ nhà đâu mà ăn nói với khách rất chợ búa. Thứ hai, có biển bảng cấm mặc đồ Mông cổ rồi các cửa hàng vẫn cho thuê ầm ầm. Đề nghị chính quyền làm việc lại nhé.

We had a great time at Cat Cat Village. A long walk. Not recommended for senior (old age people), you have to hike and walk up and down stairs. Everything was good except for the rude lady on the ticket booth, she didn't want my 8 yr old child to pay child rate fee even if she is 130cm only, she said when she put on the shoes, she not 130cm anymore. 😂 My first time to rate a spot this low.

Cảnh thì nhân tạo, chắp vá hết cái này cái nọ mất hết vẻ đẹp thiên nhiên. Lối vào thì bị chặn bới một đống xe ôm cò mồi chỉ muốn ăn tiền gửi xe, thuê trang phục và xe ôm 50k 1 người. Tổ chức quá kém cho một địa điểm mới nổi. Tóm lại là không bao giờ quay lại!

SCAM SCAM SCAM ‼️  You pay an entry fee, and then once you’re inside there are still so many extra things you’re expected to pay for. It honestly feels like a scam, especially with so many shops and cafés closed.  It’s also pretty dirty in a lot of areas. If you’re only going to take photos because you’re an influencer or something similar, I’d say it’s worth it only when the sun is out - otherwise it’s really not that pretty.  Souvenir shops have the same stuff as in the city.

I didn't like this place.  The natural beauty is really astonishing but all the other constructions and buildings of the local people are dangerous and terrible.  They have a ticket entrance and inside there are small shops for selling (suppose) local goods. The hygiene conditions of the restaurants are dangerous. The there is fee for the toilet use.  The waterfalls are beautiful but still the would be without all that constructions.  Overall is a cheap copy of the locals of fancipal mountain.

Only beautiful on photos. This is a fake village made for tourists, that you have to pay to enter (tickets + parking). There is not much to see and do, besides sit in overpriced restaurants and watch people walk around like actors in traditional clothes. The only good thing is nature, but you can hike and see waterfalls elsewhere for free.

A village that mistreats horses – very disappointing experience  We visited Cat Cat Village in August, as recommended by several travel guides. The entrance fee was nearly €15 for two people, which already seemed excessive. Despite some hesitation, we decided to go ahead based on our guide’s advice. Unfortunately, the visit turned out to be very disappointing. Cat Cat felt more like a tourist trap than an authentic village, with streets lined with shops selling items that appeared to be mass-produced, most likely in China. The only highlight was the central square with its waterfall, but even this did not justify the admission price.  What we found most troubling, however, was the condition of the horses used for tourist photos inside the park. They were left standing all day under poor conditions, and one horse in particular was in very bad shape, lying on the ground while visitors continued to take pictures around it. We approached the handler to express our concern. Although he did not speak English, he attempted to get the horse back on its feet, but it was clearly struggling to stand.  This was a very distressing sight, and we felt it important to share our experience here since we were unable to contact a local animal welfare organization.
This Village is just to take Photos there. Nothing authentic or real sadly. 150k only for entrance is a bit pricey.

I went to catcat on National holiday and The place is very quiet, there are only a few locals who open their store there. I didn’t see a good view, Dry rivers, poorly maintained places and scenery are only limited to plants that have mostly withered. You have to walk quite far and very tiring, but not presented with a beautiful view. Btw We didn't bring dong and haven't bought an entrance ticket, so we bought an on the spot ticket using USD. The rate they gave was so low that we were very at a loss. The ticket seller is also not friendly and the staff there can't speak English

If you're into crowds of tourists, an $8 entrance fee, Disneyland-style setups, and plastic fruit hanging from real trees, then Cat Cat village is perfect for you. But if you're looking for authentic local culture, traditional villages, and a genuine experience, I'd suggest skipping it. I found it incredibly disappointing—a total waste of time and money.
Lái xe 24HC 003-07 lái xe cẩu thả, khách chưa lên đã nhấn ga, kết quả là ngã chảy máu chân thay vì xin lỗi thì lại quay ra chửi tục thoá mạ khách hàng. Khốn nạn hết mức !
A super touristy destination with only artificial scenery. You literally pay 150k VND to enter a huge shopping center. No cultural heritages. No authentic locals. A stage for Instagram wannabes.

Thái độ nói chuyện của nhân viên bán chỗ này rất khinh thường khách du lịch , mình chỉ ngồi chỗ mình đánh dấu ( rõ ràng kh có biển cấm ngồi nào ) và mình chỉ ngồi lau chân , mang giày , giấy mình để ngay chỗ sát bên người và mình chưa hề bỏ đi ) bên góc trái có thể thấy ly của người nào đó đã bỏ trước . Tại sao không dẹp đi mà lại mặt khó chịu chạy ra nói như kiểu mình xả rác xong bỏ đi vậy . Một chỗ nổi vậy mà trainning nhân viên TỆ vậy ? Mong quản lý check cam xem thái độ và làm việc lại với nhân viên . TÔI KHÔNG XẢ RÁC, và có ý thức bảo vệ môi trường , tại sao lại dùng thái độ cha chết mẹ bệnh mặt khó chịu nói chuyện khách mất dạy vậy? Nếu hư hại chỗ đó tôi sẽ đền chứ không có trốn . Thái độ như vậy làm tôi chả muốn giới thiệu bất kì ai tới KDL này quá!!rất thất vọng

The village itself is pretty nice. Pretty good vibe with locals in their natural habitats. But… We arrived 5:40pm . I thought it would be like any other villages. I didn’t expect it to be like a theme park. And it closes at 6pm only realised that after buying the tickets. The ticket receptionist didn’t bother to inform us at all. If she is actually caring and have a slight sense of honesty and hospitality, she would have informed us that “it’s pretty late to explore, come back tomorrow”. But no, she speak very good English yet said nothing. The same with the ticket checking guy at the gate. Wasted the ticket fee for nothing. It’s all so lame. Is it all about money? Where is the basic human sympathy… What a shame.

This place is 100% fake. It has nothing to do with the reality - you pay expensive ticket and there is even no one to tell you something about traditional vietnamese lifestyle. There are only shops and people that rented traditional costume to make picture with it. A lot of noisy and running children that are probably working there, because they were in the traditional costume. Not worth any money.

Giá vé rất cao 150k/ người lớn, áp dụng vé trẻ em thì yêu cầu dưới 1.3’m là điều vô lý, nhiều nơi họ tính dưới 1.4m là vé trẻ em. BQL và sở du lịch làm ăn kém, buôn bán đầy đường, chèo kéo làm khách đi 1 lần ko muốn quay lại. Ý thức từ người kinh doanh cho thuê trang phục chụp ảnh cần cấm trang phục mông cổ đi vào bản. Du lịch đi 1 lần sẽ ko ai muốn quay lại. Du khách muốn ngắm cảnh đẹp tự nhiên ko nên đi điểm này, đi bộ rất nhiều, mỏi chân mà ko có gì hay.

Noisy, dirty, lacking authenticity. If this is your reason for coming to Sa Pa, better try other destinations. The vistas, rice fields and waterfalls are nice but they are insufficient vs. the rest of the experience

This place is completely artificial and feels like a scam. I initially agreed to 300k VND for a dress and photography, but later they changed the deal to 600k. The photographer took only a few pictures in limited spots and said the session was done. The entrance ticket is also a scam. I strongly recommend not visiting this place.

Nice place to get photos if you are so into Instagram. Have to pay 5Euro to get in. Was not happy when I saw a YOUNG PUP tied up with an electrical cable around it's neck that was WAY TOO TIGHT. I said this to a girl next to the puppy who untangled it and retied it. I said to them that the puppy is too young to be tied up and sitting on the edge of a concrete wall. The girl brought me to the owner who laughed at what the girl said. I said this is animal cruelty and she walked over and untied the puppy, the puppy just walked over to where the grills were and laid down, calm and cute. No doubt she will tie the puppy up again.  If their products were "handmade", why can't they make a lead for the dog with material, or give it a blanket from behind them. They care more about money than animals well being, as they also allow people of any size to sit on and take photos with ponies.  Do not buy their goods or support this behaviour. Please speak up if you see this and tell them this is not good.

Trải nghiệm quá tệ - Đã thanh toán QR từ 15:38 nhưng đến nơi 16:15 vẫn không nhận được vé - Bắt phải đợi Sếp xong việc mới xuất vé - Vé 150k nhưng không bao gồm xe điện (10k/lượt),  cầu trượt (40k) Mọi thứ trông rất giả tạo Tổng kết: không khuyên thăm quan ở đây

You have to pay to get in, and then you walk ~20 minutes through vendor stalls before you can actually see anything of interest, with vendors continually trying to shill you fake product. The vendor stalls block any type of view of the rice fields or the mountains.  The only thing of interest is a small path that takes you OUT of the village and through the rice fields. You can access that trail without having to deal with Cat Cat.  The “village” is full of souvenirs stores, places to dress up and have a photoshop, and touristy restaurants. They even have a giant slide that you can pay to ride on that cuts through a lot of the nature.  Nothing authentic at all, it’s the definition of a tourist trap. Avoid and go elsewhere.
This place is so trash!!! Because that not a village. You to spent  150000vnd /person and you need rent a motorbike

I dont know what the essence Of this Village. Came here last saturday with 3 Of My friends. Yes we did not use tour.  I Did not find any cultural activity exept lot Of chinese tourist wore tradisional cloths and take a foto.  We have to pay enterence fee 150.000 vnd and 40.000 vnd for motorcyle parking.  After get The ticket, no Maps with historical description was given.  We enter The auto gate after scanning The barcode, and down to stair crossing only souvenir store untill we reach The waterfall.  Hopefully They made improvement at least put some Information what this Village offer other than souvenir store.

Cát cát đẹp, vé 150k 1 người, chuyến đi của mình mọi thứ ok đến lúc về. Lúc về mình bắt xe ôm 1 chú người dân tộc về Silk path. Giá thỏa thuận là 80k, về chú đòi 100k. Mình ok trả cho nhanh còn về, đi cả ngày mệt rồi. Xuống xe được 10 bước, mình nhớ ra gửi chú 1kg trâu treo ở xe. Gọi nhưng chú không dừng lại. Mình ở resort mấy ngày đều hỏi lễ tân có ai đem trả không. Nhưng đều không có. Từ trước đến nay suy nghĩ mình về người dân tộc rất chất phác, đáng tin. Nhưng từ nay sẽ khác. Mình không có số điện thoại, cũng mệt nên không chụp biển số xe lên không tìm được. Thiết nghĩ, một chỗ thu vé đến 150k 1 lượt thì cũng nên quản lý đội xe ôm, ít ra mỗi xe chạy phải có số thứ tự, đến lúc mất đồ còn chỉ đích danh người lấy.

for the price u pay the service was not good they are very adamant on having u wait till 2pm for a checkin. when they are so particular on timing u expect them to give u the room at 2 the rooms we still not ready till 2:20pm there was just one buggy to take u to ur room, with so many ppl checkin at the same time there is a huge line for ur turn. the luggages had to be carried ourselves till the room,  the buggy guy was not able to help us with ur suitcases so we had to climb all the stairs with it. this could have been managed better. luggage’s could have been tagged with room numbers and have it delivered separately before hand or later.  while most of the ppl come to sapa by train and bus they reach early there should have atleast had some welcome drink  the hotel has much more to improve on the hospitality aspect this is expected when you pay a premium to stay at such resorts  the reception staff is pretty rude as well.

Có 1 vài chỗ chụp ảnh đẹp vài chỗ thì phải mất tiền để chụp ảnh (không hiểu kiểu gì), đi khá xa (ít nhất là mất 2 tiếng ở trong đấy), không có điểm nhấn, thác mới mưa xong nên đục ngàu. Chỉ nên đi 1 lần trong đời để trải nghiệm, giá vé thì tận 150k/người lớn, hơi thất vọng với số tiền mình bỏ ra

not worth it, just a huge gift shop with an exorbitant entry fee. the waterfall was drained while they were doing construction which is fine but having to pay 150,000 just to walk through hundreds of tacky gift shops to then see a tarp waterfall with the sound of construction is ridiculous. just do some trekking if your in sa pa and want to see a waterfall there are plenty others that are free and much nicer!
Tourist trap: 150k entrance fees. You can do a day trek for 500k with locals and visit other villages during the trek, much better and real.
Thái độ nhân viên thu vé rất tệ. Vào trong như một khu chợ. Cảnh nhân tạo ko thật. Tóm lại giống như mua vé vào chợ
Don’t bother. You can see waterfalls just on the drive/ride up. So over being asked if I want to buy some junk ffs.
Massive tourist trap wouldn't reccomend. Waterfalls are fake can see water pipes at the top, felt like a big gift shop selling the same stuff.

ANIMAL ABUSE – DO NOT go here! They hit the horses. This place is a complete tourist trap. The animals are treated terribly — the horses were exhausted, barely able to stand. People were paying just to take pretty pictures while the horses were suffering. Absolutely disgusting and heartbreaking to see.
Everything there is fake and touristy. Hundreds of the same thing that you can find everywhere in Vietnam. I didn't enjoy it at all...

The traditional village experience is a great experience. But all the shops are expensive. No change. Also, taxi-motorcycle at the entrance of the village sets a high fare. I paid 180,000 dong (Vietnam-dong) to the hotel 5 minutes away. It's crazy

Very Unsafe and Dishonest Experience  We were rushed into buying tickets without being told it was past closing time. We went down the steep downhill path with our 2 kids and a baby, navigating lots of stairs, only to find that everything was already closed and the lights were going out.  We had to climb back up in complete darkness, which was extremely unsafe, especially with children. By the time we returned to the car park and ticket office to ask for a refund, it was also closed.  We left feeling tired, stressed, and robbed. If you’re visiting with family, avoid this place — safety and honesty are clearly not their priority.
You have to pay to even walk through the village to get back to Sapa from the hill paths. The ‘village’ is full of tat.

Purely touristic with lots of souvenir shops and shops selling local foodstuff and other things that are not unique to any other shops you find in town. Also at 150,000 VND per adult, it’s pretty pricey.  Walked down from town while backpacking a toddler because there was no actual path to walk on. It was basically an unpaved road with loose gravel. The view was pretty nice though!  Honestly WOULD NOT recommend if you’re looking for some authentic H’mong culture. It’s a bit too made up here. Also it’s a not so fun to walk all the way back up.
Disappointing. Just a giant souvenir shop and tourist center with a river running through. Nothing authentic to see and hardly any real nature. Also need to pay 150k just to walk through.
Nice waterfall RUINED by Plenty of shops and cafes surrounding the waterfall. Too touristy. Dont bother coming.

This was easily the worst place I visited during my time in Vietnam. It felt more like a staged tourist trap than a genuine cultural experience—essentially a glorified gift shop. Everything looked newly built and lacked any sense of authenticity. The atmosphere was bizarre, with people dressed in traditional attire from other cultures, almost as if they were playing pretend.  If you're looking for a real insight into local life, I strongly recommend visiting the surrounding villages instead. They offer a far more authentic and meaningful experience.

Ngày 30/6 cuối tuần vừa qua. Tôi có đi du lịch sapa. Trải nghiệm của tôi rất thất vọng. Là người miền Nam từ Đồng Nai ra đây chơi mà có 1 số ng tôi thấy rất thất vọng. Như gia đình tôi 2 vợ chồng, 2 đứa con có đi vào Bản Cát Cát chơi, khi đi lên đoạn đường nhỏ đi lên để về. Thì xe khách biển số 24B-009.21 có 1 anh người mập mập lái xe rất ẩu. Và lái xe suýt tông gia đình tôi khi đi bên trái. Anh ta đã xuống xe rất hung dữ định hành hung gia đình tôi. Vợ tôi rất sợ. Khi đi lên trên đường gia đình tôi đã báo chính quyền địa phương về thái độ và cách lái xe rất nguy hiểm của anh ấy. Tôi mong rằng sapa nên thây đổi và có sự kiểm soát chặt chẽ hơn.

Walked down the hill to see Cat Cat village. The walk down is not pleasant to say the least. The road is completely torn up and it’s quite a long walk down. We arrived only to discover that they charge 150,000 VND per person to enter the village. Based on what we could see from the road, it didn’t look like the village is worth paying that much to visit, and after reading other reviews we feel like we definitely made the right decision. Then we had to hike all the way back up the hill to Sapa town…. You’re better off paying for a half-day guided tour through 3 villages where you can see and walk through the rice fields and get lunch included for just a little bit more money.
Look at the pictures. Pretty self explanatory. People mainly go to wear the cultural clothes and take pics for social media.

Tourist trap. Stay away.  Extremely overcrowded. Filled to the brim with vendors trying their hardest to sell you things. Extremely fake feelings "cultural experience". And to top it all off the entrance is expensive too. But it's somewhat pretty it you can look past that

A complete tourist trap! Avoid. More than half the trek is past shops. Everything is paved and did not see a single rice field. Luckily we had a lovely trek the day before to Lao Chai and Ta Van which was amazing. Also the village feels artificial, the waterwheels don't seem to have any function and the scenery is not too impressive.
You pay entrace to enter a village. U walk through same looking shops selling the same things. There is nothing unique nothing cute. Vietnam has more than this :(

Would not recommend. During our time visiting the village we saw a number of animals in very poor conditions including 2 kittens sitting on the main path of the village that were clearly very ill & near death. We purchased some food & water for the kittens, however they needed medical help. When we raised the issue with local shop owners & the tourist office no one seemed to care.  Given this is a paid family tourist attraction I think those selling tickets & profiting from this village should have a duty of care to the animals within it. The same extends to overworked horses by the river.  Really ruined the experience, alongside the rampant over-tourism & inauthenticity of the village which is now clearly just a location for getting instagram photos.

If you enjoy paying 150k to go shopping while experiencing empty representations of traditional cultures then this is the place for you. Most of the village is downhill through stalls selling replicas or variations of the same products. Some of these are handmade, many appear to be mass produced and factory made.  The historical or traditional appeal of Cat Cat has long disappeared. There is no information about the minority cultures or traditional ways of life. The entire place feels like a cheaply made movie set to buy cheap and generic products.  I feel for the people who work there attempting to entice tourists all day long. Especially in a place that essentially rebuilt the village that was once there, and left standing a sad and hollow simulation of a once beautiful and historically valuable place.

So sad coming to this place to enjoy the beauty of the Cat Cat Village and its rice fields and ending up seeing an horrorific escene of a local woman beating his son cruelty and badly. A kid of no more than 4 years old desesperetly crying and screaming on the ground while this bit*** was out of control hitting him with a broom. I wish I was writing this review about the place, which is beautiful but overcrowded tho. Instead, I’m making this review because apart from what I could do at that moment, I want to make sure everyone can see the face of this animal. Maybe it’s nothing we can do about it, but even if one person recognises her and don’t buy at her store, it’s gonna be enough for me. The place is located at the bottom of the path you take down from the entrance. Before turning right towards the river. On the right side (where the blue chairs are on the picture)
This is a man made amusement valley, not really the place for nature. Also charges 150k for entrance.

They’ve commercialized the heck out of this village. It lacks authenticity and is a huge tourist trap. Before coming in, we were asked many times to hop on a scooter despite saying no. There are many stalls selling the same souvenirs and shops renting out traditional Hmong clothes (which don’t get me wrong, they are beautiful). However, they tie up horses to stand there all day for photo shoot purposes. Also we saw many kids running around on a Monday which we thought maybe they should be in school?  There are some areas where the view is nice but not nice enough that you cannot enjoy from Sapa. If you are coming for the muddy waterfalls, there are many other places in the world that offer better views for no entrance fee.  Overall, it is honestly such a bizarre experience because people of all different nationalities will come here to dress up in traditional Hmong clothes/ have photo shoots/ pretend to be Hmong for a day, but will come back to Sapa and react quite rudely to the Hmong kids (who are clearly being exploited) OR will even participate in the exploitation of the Hmong children ( videoing the children who are forced to dance on the street all night or having their own kids pose with them for photos as if they are performers).  Witnessing these things didn’t sit right with us and tainted the whole experience.

Nice to visit but extremely tourism trap almost everything needs to be paid, want to take a pic with a horse need to pay, want to take a pic with the bow need to pay so prepare a lot of small demonization notes to pay for everything

There are a couple spots to take cool pictures of the valley here but I would recommend to avoid it at all costs. Walking/hiking around the valley itself that surrounds the "village" is gorgeous, free, and far more enjoyable than this tourist trap.  Shortly after entering we witnessed someone dragging a struggling and already-injured puppy by its tail across the road. We watched him kick the puppy as it tried to limp away. It looked like it had been abused for a while. We stopped him from continuing but saw him return after we had to leave. Also when we were leaving, people kept harassing us to take a ride with them to Sapa. Was not a pleasant experience.

You have to pay to get in to the village to be pushed to buy everything in site, it’s just for Instagram pictures that they’re capitalizing on which is unfortunate. 150,000 dong for entrance fee. Felt way too touristy.
The most beautiful village of Vietnam
The biggest tourist trap I have ever been to!
Thấy đội nhân viên soái cổng
The nature is amazing, but do not pay to get in. Not worth it at all, tourist trap. Only the trekking from Sapa to there is worth it, but once you reach Cat Cat, turn back 😉

150.000 dong entrance and what you get is only souvenir shops... And when you get here from the east (trekking) and you just want to pass to get to sapa, no chance to avoid it. 150.000 dong just to pass the "village".
This guy treats very not nice to the animals!! Forcing them to act as he wants, using a stick. And egoistic people are using them to get likes on Instagram, shame on you.

It’s a fake village, not worth it. The winding roads that take you to the village entrance has a spectacular view though. I’d recommend to just go for a coffee shop nearby and enjoy the view instead of entering the village.

Có đoạn đường 2km đào lên lấp xuống hàng năm trời không xong, lúc thấp điểm không làm, ban đêm không làm, cứ phải nhè lúc thật đông xe cộ lưu thông thì chặn đường để làm. Quản lý ở đây không biết đầu óc tầm cỡ nào chứ người thường khó ai làm được kiểu này !

Ở bản cát cát này bình thuòng ko có gi là đẹp Toàn dân tộc  con thổ nó mổ con kinh  thue 2 bô quần áo măc vào để chup ảnh lấy 500k 1 bộ  ỏ đây ko có gi là đẹp chỉ có ko khí mát mẻ sáng sớm thoi còn từ 8h sáng Nóng đến chieu khi hết ánh Nắng măt trời

The most fake boring place I've been to in vietnam - essentially an expensive picture of a waterfall with the added hassarssmemt of several shop vendors trying to feel you mass produced items pretending it's locally made and coffee shops trying to get you in the door  Very little to do but buy crap or walk up and down the river 150k per person is a rip off plus the added cost of the 10k per person eletric buggy

I would not recommend to see this village, rather enjoying the nature around Sa Pa. We were on a hike and were forced to buy tickets (150.000 per person) as it was on our route and the people refused to tell us any other way around the village, even turned away. We've met so many helpful and kind Vietnamese people, but here they just focus on money. However, it is a compilation of expensive souvenir shops and instagram spots which were in really bad and even dangerous condition.

Waste of time. 150k VND entry to see some fake houses and expensive souvenir shops. You also can't walk through the village to continue on the sapa walking trail to Ty Van so you have to walk all the way back up to sapa and along a different route

Would only recommend going there if you want to spice up your IG with some semi authentic pics. It is way too overpriced with 150k per person (and some extra entrance fees for fancy photo spots).  What you would expect is to see a small village on the hill and maybe some locals. What you get is a souvenir store in each hut.

If you like a theme park, please go and enjoy. Enjoy the 150K entrance fee, waiting at the ticket boot and only seeing fake dressed up people trying to act authentic to sell you more stuff. If you want to see truly how these people live, please walk the countryside with any colourful mama that talks to you in Sa Pa town.

Honestly it's a nice place but too touristy. The entrance fee is really uncalled for just to walk steps and walk past tourist souvenir shops. You still have to pay to use the washroom after paying entrance fee.  Just avoid unless you wanna take photos inside. It's not expensive but the concept is ridiculous.
Mình là người yêu vẻ đẹp hoang sơ. Nơi này h giả quá rồi 🙄 …
Không bao giờ quay lại. Toàn là hàng quán và xe ôm chèo kéo

150k VND to enter what is essentially an open air market filled with cheap goods with large mark up prices. There isn’t a village at all to be seen. Just a few Instagram prepared spots. It’s essentially a shake down to stop you from being able to hike in the valley. You either pay 150k to take an easy route or are forced to walk through wild routes down the mountain or pay for a guide. It’s a bit of a scummy move really
Tourist trap. Everything is built for tourists to take photos

We sold me a truck in an authentic north Vietnamese village 😍...but it is not a truck, it is a tourist attraction. More it is JUST a tourist trap. A big supermarket where you have to pay 6$ just to visit vietnamese minority shops, but not owner by minority people. Everything is fake : plastic flowers, plastic plants, concret woods, imported clothes and stuff everywhere (made in China is not far). It is just a big joke .. but I will stop there : mass crowed tourists like this kind of place.
Even if it would be free, it would still not be worth visiting. Just two touristic streets, nothing authentic. Now 150.000 VND entry. Not worth it, avoid and go for a walk to Lao Chai
Terrible tourist trap. Skip it completely.

Our tour guide Dimana Peneva, from Varna Bulgaria, brought us here for a day long walking tour. Unfortunately this place ia s tourist scam. Nothing authentic, nothing real, just a bunch of shops and Instagram spots. If you want real authentic hmong villages, just drive QL32 road to Yen Bai.

150 000 VND entrance ticket. Too expensive for what it is. For 70 000 would do it once, untill I saw the animal cruelty down the road. The best similarity is too compare it with an attraction park. With sad attractions though.  Shops with all the same products. Animal cruelty like: Paying to sit on a horse to take a picture. A stressed owl being tightened up. Neglected sheeps in a small space that can't hide from tourists. Especially the goats were fed up.  The route of the waterfall downstairs was a short walk we enjoyed the most of this village.  Go for a walk in the rice fields through original villages for a more authentic feeling 💚

Quá tệ, đường đi xuống bát nháo, oto và xe ôm chạy loạn xạ, tới Cát Cát mua vé tham quan 150k/người nhưng đi vào không có trải nghiệm nào hấp dẫn ngoại trừ đầy hàng quán buôn bán mọc ra rồi thu phí và tiền khách du lịch, vệ sinh 5-10k nữa, mất hơn 2km leo bộ các bậc thang lên và xuống tới cuối bản chứng kiến cảnh thiên nhiên bị phá huỷ thay cho các dịch vụ tư nhân khai thác khác. Ai có ý định đi thì ngưng ngay nhé

Reading the reviews I was left disappointed with Cat Cat village. Did not give me a village feel. Was built of selfies spots and restaurants and shops lined all along the walkway. It is more of a market than a village. They continue to expand the tourism village. Was completely artificial.

Nice place, but overly touristy. What made the trip terrible was the animals by the river. The people standing with the horses were hurting the horses because of the smallest movements they made. It was awful to see. It overshadowed our whole trip. It was torture of animals. We do not recommend this place to anyone!

Don‘t go. Tourist trap! It was highly recommended by our hotel owner, so we went there and got disappointed. You have to pay an entrance fee of 150.000d and you will walk your way down into the village alongside a lot of tourism shops. Also there are a few photo stops that cost extra 10.000d. The whole town is built for tourists. There is a waterfall inside the village that might be the best thing about Cat Cat. The way up is the same way as the way down so it can get very crowded. Also the way from and back to Sapa is a big road with a lot of traffic.

Phí vào Bản quá cao nhưng trong Bản không có nhiều thứ để xem ,toàn là bán  hàng lưu niệm. Khu vực thác ko còn vẻ đẹp tự nhiên nữa thay vào đó rất nhiều thứ dựng lên cho việc chụp hình sống ảo.Những trang trí hoa mai hoa đào giả từ tết đến giờ vẫn cứ để như vậy dù là mùa hè...nhìn những bông hoa giả ấy thấy lem luốc dơ lắm luôn.  Mình đi cùng các bạn nước ngoài...các bạn ấy nói thất vọng Sapa quá...kế hoạch ở đây 5 hôm thế mà các bạn ấy phải hủy .Mình đã đến Sapa năm 2008 lúc ấy Sapa cực đẹp chứ ko như bây giờ...hơi buồn
Thấy giống kiểu tận thu quá. Bản thì cũng không phải nhiều chỗ chơi. Vé 150k. Dịch vụ xe điện với xe ôn nữa hơn 200k

Ein Dorf, was lediglich für Touristen gebaut worden ist. Ich wollte eine Runde wandern gehen, bis ich plötzlich vor einem Ticketschalter angehalten wurde und 5€ Eintritt zahlen musste für einen Ort, der gestellter nicht sein könnte. Mich haben Kinder um Geld angebettelt, die Wasserfälle wurden einfach künstlich geschaffen und alles ist eher wie in einer Fake-Welt. Einen Besuch kann man sich definitiv sparen und man sollte eher die echten Dörfer und Reisterrassen besuchen.

Schrecklich. Gehen Sie nicht hin! Wie der Name schon sagt: TOURISM Village. Sie zahlen Eintritt um im Gänsemarsch mit 1000 anderen Touristen einen ursprünglich mal schönen Weg ins Tal zu einem Wasserfall hinab zu stolpern. Der ganze Weg auf beiden Seiten voller Verkaufsstände, die zu 90 % China-Ware verkaufen, die Sie genauso von Ho Chi Minh bis Hanoi überall finden. Es sind auch nur teilweise Einheimische Minderheiten, die etwas verkaufen: hier versucht jeder sein Business. (Positiv: es wurde uns nichts aufgedrängt). Die Masse der Touristen, die dann auch noch um den besten Preis feilschen, macht es unmöglich die Landschaft und Aussicht zu genießen. Es ist ein Geschiebe und Gedränge bis Sie unten ankommen. Dort können Sie vermeintlich traditionelle Kleidung (das meiste ist hier auch aus China) für Foto Shootings ausleihen. Ob es Ihnen gelingt ein Foto vom schönen Wasserfall ohne Touristen oder den zahlreichen Cafés/Bars mit englischer Schrift, zu machen? Jedenfalls lernen Sie nichts über die lokale Kultur, Sprache oder Lebensweise, es sei denn Sie haben einen einheimischen Guide, die Ihnen Ihre Fragen beantwortet (danke Mao Ca, du warst großartig). Die meisten, die hier herkommen, wollen konsumieren. Echtes Interesse für die indigene Bevölkerung hat hier niemand: ohne Nachfrage kein Angebot.
不要购买门票，沿着下坡路可以免票。整个🐱🐱村，我只看到一只猫。看到🐶🐶的只数，却超过了 20 只🥲，里面商业化太严重了

距離市區騎機車約15分鐘 我們租機車騎下去是大斜坡 路面稍微顛簸需要小心 抵達售票入口 門票費用150K VTD 據說是最靠近市區當地苗族人的村子 完全被觀光客佔領 看不出居住的痕跡 他是一條樓梯一直往下走 樓梯兩側都是類似的商品 有些小商店有設置觀景台，必須消費他們還讓你進去拍照 不知道這樣買門票的意義是什麼 裡面有一些美麗的風景 可是其他地方也能找到 回程可以請摩多車載 價錢約40KVTD/3km 可以試試看殺價
Toeristenval, het enige de moeite waard is de waterval aan het einde, de uitzichten en een paar winkeltjes met traditionele kledij.

Village sympathique cependant l'entrée est très chère pour le Vietnam et nous sommes sans cesse importuné par les vendeurs de souvenirs. De plus les toilettes sont payantes malgré qu'on est payé l'entrée et si on souhaite remonter au village de Sapa il faut payer le taxi. Je ne recommande pas forcément cette activité

A fuir !! Sur le papier, on pensait visiter un village Hmong ; il s'agit en réalité de boutiques de souvenirs et spots Instagram. Tout est fait pour le touriste asiatique (location de costumes, chevaux et colombes pour se prendre en photos...), qui s'en donne à coeur joie. De plus, selon l'entrée par laquelle vous accédez, le balisage n'est pas du tout clair (un groupe de touristes chinois avec guide a marché pendant 35 minutes dans la mauvaise direction avant de rebrousser chemin). Le chemin secondaire n'est d'ailleurs pas très bien entretenu. Aucun intérêt ! Payer 150 000 VND par personne pour ça est une honte.

Village fait uniquement pour les touristes, si vous voulez prendre des photos insta foncez, tout est fait pour Dommage que la maltraitance animale soit banalisée, avec des rapaces attachés au bout de cordes pour prendre des photos avec par exemple Je vous recommande plutôt d’aller sur les petits villages à côté
15min scendere i gradini, 15 min per risalire. Pieno di souvenir scadenti, una trappola per turisti. Non so perchè le agenzie lo definiscono "trekking": ci si va in infradito.
風景很漂亮，但是超級無敵商業化。每家店都賣同樣的商品，很像是大量批發而來。若不喜歡太商業的旅客可略過此景點。 貓貓村外頭很多租服飾的店，一件喊價50k，有興趣可嘗試。 風景的確很美，但可從sapa其他地方欣賞。

소수민족 마을이라 해서 갔는데 전통옷 대여마을이라 고치셔야 됩니다 소수민족에 생활이나 전통은 볼수 없었고 옷 대여점과 오토바이  호객꾼만 잔득있네요 완전실망 기대 이하에 마을 입니다 지나가던 소수민족 할머니 발견해서 겨우 겨우 사진한장 남겼습니다 찍고나서 돈달라 해서 20000동 드렸네요 우리나라 민속촌 10%수준 입니다 사진은 좋와 보여도 권하고 싶지 않습니다

Ich kann die ganzen positiven Bewertungen absolut nicht nachvollziehen! Dieses Dorf hat nichts mit traditionell zu tun, sondern ist ein komplett erbautes Touristendorf und eine einzige große Fotoshooting Bühne! Überall sind Menschen die Bilder machen, keine Chance einfach gemütlich herumzuspazieren. Ebenso werden Pferde für die Shootings benutzt, die mit Stöcken geschlagen werden um zu gehorchen. Bitte spart euch die 150k und macht lieber einen Spaziergang durch die richtigen Dörfer.

Koszmar! Odradzam to miejsce jeżeli jesteście wrażliwi na cierpienie zwierząt! Komercja na pełnej. Człowiek na filmie zmuszał siłą to biedne umęczone zwierzę by pozowało z wysuniętą nogą do zdjęcia. Gdy tylko odsuwał nogę z całej siły uderzał go w pyszczek. Nie mogłam na to patrzeć jak się nad nim ten człowiek znęca i go bije! To było okropne doświadczenie! Modelka i fotograf zupełnie się tym nie przejmowali! Skandal, że coś takiego się tam dzieje. Niszczy to odbiór tego miejsca. Już nigdy tam nie wrócę. Nie chce patrzeć na ten obraz okrucieństwa i wyzysku zwierząt!
就是非常商業化的地方，不需要來。 畢竟一張門票150,000不算便宜，階梯很多，長者要小心。 要去看當地文化，去Ta Van就好了。

Che tristezza, una tourist trap in tutto e per tutto. Il paese è costruito ad arte per il turista, con cattivo gusto e senz’anima. L’ingresso è a pagamento e si entra in questo percorso obbligato fino all’uscita fatto di travi che traballano e in alcuni punti piuttosto pericolose dove si attraversano attrazioni turistiche costruite per intrattenere (come una casa sull’albero finita tipo parco divertimenti) Il percorso è tra l’altro parecchio lungo, e all’uscita ci si ritrova a 500 metri da dove si è entrati, in modo da dover usufruire della navetta, di nuovo a pagamento e non inclusa nel biglietto, per ritornare al punto di partenza. Iper iper iper sconsigliato in tutto e per tutto, a meno che non siate fan del kitsch fatto male. Peccato perché avrebbero potuto renderlo un posto autentico e grazioso. Si esce con una grande amarezza per la denaturalizzazione di un villaggio

Это уже рынок, а не деревня. Жаль потраченных денег за билет. Фото зоны устарели, доски прогнили. К сожалению у нас было мало времени в Сапе, поэтому решили посетить хотя бы ближайшую деревню. Зря, очень зря.

Le village est une mise en scène et non un village typique. Si vous souhaitez voir des boutiques, des restaurants et vous faire alpaguer constamment c'est le lieu parfait. Par contre, si vous souhaitez visiter des villages typiques, le mieux est encore d'aller dans les villages autour de Sapa et ça ne vous coûtera pas 150k Dong par personne. En plus, si l'on regarde bien, on peut admirer de jolis tuyaux en plastique noir qui permettent d'alimenter ces balles cascades instagrammables. Le cas est quand même très intéressant pour des étudiants en tourisme pour voir et comprendre ce qu'il ne faut pas faire en terme d'aménagements touristiques si l'on souhaite être en respect de la culture. On nous avait prévenu que c'était Disneyland et on avait vu tous les avis négatifs notamment sur Trip, on a voulu se faire notre propre avis, erreur !!!

A fuir !!! Ce lieu n’a aucun intérêt, je ne sais pas qui a eu l’idée de créer une chose pareil. Ce n’est pas un village local mais un endroit où on veut vous faire acheter tout et n’importe quoi aux touristes. On paie 150k pour voir des magasins, une fois arrivés en bas on s’aperçoit que rien est authentique tout simplement. C’est uniquement pleins de spots pour faire des photos instagramable. Je ne suis pas là plus grande militante de la cause animale mais il y a aussi de nombreux chevaux maltraités, on voit qu’ils sont tristes et frappés, on a pu le voir de nos propres yeux. On regrette vraiment d’y être allés.
Pseudo village completement artificiel. Des centaines de boutiques avec les memes objets made in china Perte de temps et d argent.

Village touristique mignon. Il n’y a plus rien d’authentique, on se croirait plutôt à Disney avec tous les décors. Tout tourne autour du commerce. Très très gros point négatif: les chevaux près de la rivière. On leur monte dessus toute la journée pour prendre des photos. Ils sont accrochés à des cordes si courtes, qu’ils ne peuvent même pas étendre leur tête. C’est triste! Ils sont figés, pas le droit de bouger d’un millimètre.  En plus c’est un village payant, je ne conseille pas.
150.000 VND für ein künstlich angelegtes Dorf mit zahlreichen Touristenshops, definitiv nicht das Geld wert. Wir haben den Eintritt bezahlt, da es auf unserer Wanderroute lag. Wirklich schade!

Je suis allé à Cat Cat Village et franchement, j’ai été déçu. L’endroit manque cruellement d’authenticité, tout semble très touristique et monté pour plaire aux visiteurs plutôt qu’un vrai aperçu de la culture locale. Ce qui m’a le plus dérangé, c’est la maltraitance visible envers les chevaux. Certains étaient attachés en plein soleil, et clairement pas en bon état. Je ne recommande pas.

Это не деревня. Это торговые ряды бесполезной чепухи, не идите сюда ! Это стоит 150 000 но я бы не дал за это и доллара и вообще бы пропустил это место. На протяжении всего пути продают одну и ту же ерунду   Смотреть тут нечего. От деревни одно только название.  типичный туристический капкан для массовки: кафешки, мостики, сценки с хламом. Настоящая Сапа — это за её пределами  *** если вы дошли до низу и вы молоды  проигнорируйте местных бомбил таксистов, которые берут бешеные 100к за подьем на верх на своем драндулете. И сделайте небольшой хайк минут на 40  пройдя дальше. Уверяю вы не пожалеете. Очень красивые виды и действительн настоящая красота. Но это находится за этим всем беспределом с рынком

Sehr stark kommerzialisiertes Dorf mit hohem Eintrittspreis (150k). Nach dem Weg durch ca. 50 Verkaufsstände kommt ein recht schön angelegtes Dorf, dass hauptsächlich für Fotoshootings mit geliehenen Gewändern benutzt wird. Leider kommt es dabei auch zu Tierquälerei, da u. a. Pferde angekettet, ohne Bewegungsfreiheit, in der prallen Sonne stehen und für Shootings missbraucht werden. Wir würden es nicht unbedingt weiterempfehlen.

깟깟마을이 과연 마을인지도 모르겠다. 마을은 온통 관광상품 판매점이다. 그들이 어땋게 사는지 알수조차 없다. 전통복장은 모두 관광객이다. 오토바이 운임으로 돈을 벌게 위해 관광객은 오토바이를 금지시키고 지신들은 타고 다닌다. 상술의 끝판왕이다. 관람코스는 단방향이고 경사가 가파른 계단이라 오토바이 탑승을 노골적으로 유도한다. 폭포 말고는 볼기 하나도 앖다. 굳이 깟깟이 아니여도 볼 수 있는 폭포가 있다. 돈내고 갈 이유가 별로 없다.

Ein fürchterlicher Reinfall! 150k Eintritt, nur um Souvenirshops zu sehen. Erwarten Sie nicht dass, man irgendwelchen authentischen Minderheiten sieht. Alles ist durch und durch Kommerzialisiert. Daher besser einen großen Bogen drum herum machen!

Tout est faux de haut en bas du village. Même les "cascades" sont alimentées par des tuyaux en plastique ! Un peu de décence svp... Bref, faites autre chose mais pas ça. Et svp arrêtez de prendre les enfants, les adultes en photos.... Respectez les.

Horrible, el precio es exagerado, lo han subido hace poco de 70.000 a 150.000, un robo totalmente, después de pagar eso te esperas algo impresionante y la verdad es que pagas y luego entras para pasear entre tiendas, es ridículo. Se aprovechan totalmente, una cosa es que te vendan sus productos locales y artesanales un poco más caros para ayudar al pueblo y otra que se rían en tu cara cobrándote esa entrada a ese precio sin nisiquiera comprar nada. Simplemente decepcionante. Luego es un paraíso del postureo vietnamita, que eso en parte tiene su gracia porque son muy graciosos pero la aldea en si es totalmente un parque temático sin nada de autenticidad. Por favor que no se promueva esto.

Un circo. Teatro total y absoluto donde ves a cien mil personas disfrazadas posando para Instagram, cero encanto y cero autenticidad, no ves el pueblo, el circuito está marcado y no te puedes salir de una calle de un solo Sentido con tiendas de souvenirs a ambos lados, de los souvenirs el 90% son importaciones chinas de copias del batik tradicional, queda muy poco ya de los tejidos y tintes tradicionales. Para mi gusto una pérdida de tiempo y de dinero, un día perdido allí.

Fæl opplevelse. Hesteeiere slo hestene i tennene hver gang de rørte for mye på seg når turistene tok bilde. Endte med at hestene sto tilnærmet helt stille gjennom alt for lang tid. Ingen pause. Dyreplageri absolutt. Kan ikke forstå at folk ville komme og ta bilder med disse stakkars skapningene. For all del, det er vakkert landskap og organisert men hele greierne er laget for turister og kun det. ikke dra dit. Ikke støtt opp under dyreplageri. Bruk stemmene for å si fra om at slik fortjener ingen å ha det.

Ein lauter, hektischer, von Touristen völlig überlaufener und überformter Ort. Aufdringliche Straßenverkäuferinnen und penetrante Männer, die auf Mopeds Taxidienste anbieten. Architektur: nichts von authentischer vietnamesischer Dörflichkeit. Im Grunde erschütternd. Man muss Eintritt zahlen (das war für mich überraschend), danach wieder für "spezielle Aussichtspunkte". Die Strategie ist klar: Möglichst viele Leute herlocken und an ihr Geld herankommen. Eigentlich wollte ich in ruhigem Ambiente die wunderschön in den Prospekten abgebildeten Reisterrassen erwandern (solche Phantasien entwickelte ich zu Hause beim Studium des Angebots der Reise-Agentur) und viel Zeit haben fürs Fotografieren. Ich flüchtete bald und versuchte mich im Bett des Hotels irgendwie zu erholen.

사파에 왔으면 여기는 꼭 가야지 하고 왔다가 후회만 하는곳.. 대자연 속의 소수민족의 삶은 온데간데 없고 중국어로 물건 사라고 외치는 소리와 끝도 없이 이어지는 상점가만 있다 '관광'마을 맞구나..  물레방아니 소수민족 집이니 폭포니 정말 볼것도 없고 입장료 15만동인데 베트남에서 돈 쓰고 후회한건 여기가 처음이다.  거기다 계단들이 너무 많아 볼것도 없는 주제에 체력소모까지 심한 건 덤..  사파에선 그냥 함롱산 등반하고 판시판 케이블카만 타자.
