Không còn vẻ đẹp hoang sơ, chỉ còn là những khu du lịch thương mại

Great experience for the cultural side of Hmong people. Beware it's very somewhat commercialized. It can be very full with tourists. You will be walking down the hill to the waterfall from street level then go back again on the other side …

Visit this place only if you have surplus time at hand while you're at Sapa and have nothing else to do. If you're a social media influencer then it's pretty good to take photos. That's it. There's nothing about the village, its people or …
Good day trip.  Not ideal for parents or people with knee issues as its a very long walk down. …
A village put up for tourists. Tons of Vietnamese tourists dressed up in local outfits taking pictures and videos.
Đi đễ biết chứ không có gì hấp dẫn. Tất cả đã thương mại hóa đễ thu tiền từ khách du lịch. Giá vé 150.000 đồng/ người không xứng đáng với những gì họ đầu tư. …

You have to pay 150.000D to enter. It’s a village produced for tourists so don’t expect authenticity. I also dislike their use of horses for photos sitting outside in the hot sun for tourists to sit on and get an Instagram photo

The place is too crowded, and there are no waterfalls as expected. However, I enjoyed being in nature—the scenery is beautiful, but there’s nothing extraordinary. If you visit, there’s no need to take a tour guide; they just follow the last person in the group without adding much value.

I went at night when it wasn’t crowded so the experience was quite pleasant though the main area is really small and it took me 30-40mins to enjoy the environment and take some pictures before leaving. …

This place is underwhelming to me. It's okay if you genuinely like to know the local culture.  But most people here just go to try the local clothing and take pictures. If you like doing it then it gets enjoyable. But if you just go for sightseeing, there's really nothing much to see.
lâu rồi k đi, tới đây mới biết giá vé đã lên 150k 1 người, giá quá cao cho 1 khu du lịch bên trong không có gì đặc sắc.

OK I Guess - although I was a quite disappointed as 'tourism' has certainly started to take away from the authenticity of the original village. I am sure that it used to be a lovely old village just outside is Sapa town - but now it seems …

Well, I don’t know if it’s worth the time and the money. We walked all the way from sapa to the village, it was an easy hike to do but the way to Sapa again was extremely difficult for us. Grab takes 55,000 for the drive but the cab drivers …

We ventured out to Cat Cat Village, we took a taxi as it was rainy and the hotel suggested walking with kids wasnt a good idea. We were dropped at the ticket booth and got tickets for 2 adults and 2 kids - it was 70k dong for adults and 30k for kids. All along the roads leading into Cat Cat was costume shop after costume shop of traditional clothes you could wear to have photos whilst hiking through the village. The village was nothing as I expected walking into it as there was shop after shop after shop either renting costumes, selling souveniers or food shops as you head down hundreds of stairs. The village was beautiful none the less, it would have been nicer if it was less touristy as we went on a Sunday - apparently its advised to not go to Sapa on a weekend. The most beautiful part was once you hear the waterfalls when you are at the top of the stairs and look out and see its beauty. In this part of Cat Cat there is a cultural dance show which is performed many times throughout the day for about 40 minute sessions. The worst part was the wet and muddy climb back up the stairs to get to where the taxis are waiting. Our kids faired quite well considering all of the stairs that needed climbing up and down on the whole trek. This village is well worth the small entrance fee to see its beauty.
We visited Cat Cat on day 2 of the package tour. it was 3 hours tour, good village, beautiful scenery, nice waterfall and local dancing show but avoid the children who try to follow u to get money.

And of course plenty of tribes people pushing the usual stuff they push. But at least you only have an easy walk down the hill to get to it. Take one of the bike taxis to get back up. It’s only a couple of bucks.

The place lets you get a feel of the local village culture with a very natural and beautiful setting. The valley is awesome and the river and water clean. The view going down is amazing. The walking trail is easy to use and not hard. A must see place to visit with a lot of photo opportunities. The only negative comment is the presence of uncollected garbage like plastics starting to be noticeable. The government should be stricter in preserving the beauty of the area.

Normally I hate this kinda place and we only went because the weather wasn't great.  But I have to say that it really is a beautiful spot.  Ok it's fake but you can't take away from the natural beauty of the area.We might have been lucky.  Only a few Chinese tourists dressed in local clothes taking selfies but I would imagine on a busy day it could be very frustrating

We joined a half day trek to Cat Cat village, a lovely walk, many tourist shops for those wanting to shop. We had a great guide, viewed the beautiful waterfall, walked across the river watched local children fishing, a very enjoyable afternoon.

I chose to do this tour with my partner. The scenery and villages were beautiful. Any one with mobility issues would not be able to do this tour is there and steps, steep hills and wet areas that make slipping easy.We walked 3km overall and it was a great experience with a local guide.Local villagers make all the souvenirs and items sold at the shops.
Nice Palace to visit. Waterfall you can visit and you can watch local dancers with happy music😁. You can walk around and make Nice pictures.
Place is very nice but became highly touristic with two much people just coming to take pictures....

Cách Sapa khoảng 3km, bản Cát Cát (Sapa, Lào Cai) là làng dân tộc người Mông, nằm bình yên bên thác nước Cát Cát (thác Tiên Sa) trong thung lũng ngay chân núi Hoàng Liên Sơn với ba bề là núi non hùng vĩ.

사파는 기대와는 달리 이미 도시화 되어가고 있었다. 곳곳에 공사현장과 수많은 인파...깟깟마을은 다른 세상처럼 보였으나 실제는 어떨 지 알 수 없다. 아직은 전통을 지켜나가는 소수 민족과 아름다운 자연을 보며 천천히 걷다보면 마치 시간여행을 온 듯한 느낌을 준다. 중간 중간 상점과 호객행위는 너무 비난하지 말고, 있는 그대로 받아들이면 즐거운 산책이 될 것 같다. 사파에 오면 반드시 충분한 시간을 가지고 천천히 그들의 문화속에 들어가보면 좋을 것 같다.

If you are visiting Cat Cat village without any tour, keep in mind that you do not have to go via official entry with stairs. If you turn left to a tiny path approximately 300 meters before the "toll booth", you can enjoy nice scenery of rice fields and avoid payment (70.000 dong). If you go the official way, it may take a while to get to the village, because the path is always full of people, who walk very slow. Moreover, locals cell different stuff on the same stairs. From Cat Cat village you can continue walking to Lao Chai and Giang Ta Chai. Take a lot of water with you and prepare to sweat! Comfortable sportive shoes are the must!

What a cute place to visit. Definitely designed for the tourist in mind but well worth the visit. We enjoyed the traditional show and demonstrations of local culture and crafts and lunch looking down on the waterfall.

Jag är ambivalent. Bodde några dagar på ett hotell nära marknadsplatsen. Gick till och från denna by, det var varmt soligt och uppförsbacke på hemvägen.Är glad att ha sett och besökt denna by, samtidigt som det är mycket turistiskt och jag såg många möjligheter för förändringar till det bättre!Det finns en bra toalett i mitten av byn, nere vid broarna.Vackert på många sätt, samtidigt som byn skulle behöva hjälp med logistik, informationsmaterial mm.HälsningarLena från Sverige

After being stuck in our hotel for a full 24 hrs staring at a white back drop because of the heavy fog. We asked out hotel to arrange a short trek. We were taken the back way and we're able to see for the first time the beauty of the region. As we made our way down the hill the fog cleared and we took some great photos.  Not so many tourists when we went. But definitely made for a great half day tour and gave us something to do close to the hotel. Spent 3 days in Sapa. Dense fog all three.  We were told the day before we arrived was sunny and clear. It's all your luck.

The visit to this destination is so interesting. It’s one way in and the other way out. Better to ensure you can walk up hill and down hill for about 3hours. For those we have problem walking, please bring a walking stick. Travel light and wear shoes with good grips. Along the walk, you will be hawk by peddlers selling their goods. It’s a fun trip seeing all terrace  padi planted on the . The air is so fresh and the sweat are so real. The climb will take you to the waterfall and a short toilet break. The coffee at this place is so good. Their pineapple juice is pure too. Cost us VND40,000 per cup each. Tried their famous bamboo rice at VND10,000 but not my taste so I gave to the children there. If you have any food give it to the children there. They like it. It can be a slippery journey so take care.
Great village with a lot of culture on offer. Wet markets food stands and a lot of history to see. Nice friendly people and the local dance theatre was worth a look.
I rented the motorbike and drove through the village.   Past all the tourist peeps and souvenir shop, across a dodgy small bridge, and drive was beautiful.

באזור סאפה מצויין מספר כפרים קטנים השוכנים בעמק מרהיב בנוף הררי. בני המקום משמרים מנהגים ומסורות ולכל שבט תלבושת מסורתית וססגונית. האווירה המיוחדת וההליכה בין הכפרים מחזירה אותנו לפחות ב100 שנה אחורה. מומלץ.

Still a nice walk.  Many, many steps. Along the way some shopping. Especially the Hmong and Indigo fabrics are great. (You need to bargsin, first prices are too high).  Many Asian girls/woman rent, and dress up in Hmong dresses.  At Cat Cat village a nice waterfall and bridge.

เดินเที่ยวหมู่บ้านcatcat อากาศหนาวเย็นสบายๆเป็นเพราะการเดินลงเขาของเราทำให้ไม่รู้สึกหนาวเดินเที่ยวชมสินค้าของชาวม้งในหมู่บ้านแทบทุกร้านขายของเหมือนๆกัน และพวกม้งที่หอบลูกมาช่วยขายของตามข้างทางเห็นแล้วก็ดูน่ารักเพราะเด็กประมาณ2-3ขวบก็หัดขายของแบบแม่และเก่งมากๆ ความเป็นธรรมชาติของหมู่บ้านก็ดูสวยงามแบบชาวเขามีไร่และนาข้าวเป็นแบบขั้นบันไดได้เห็นความเขียวของต้นข้าวดูสวยงามมาก
Ingresso al villaggio a pagamento, ma la visita n vale veramente la pena. Si può vedere come vivono tutt'ora queste povere popolazioni vietnamite campando di lavori manuali, bestiame e ortaggi

We enjoyed visiting the village. Maybe a little fake in my opinion and nothing like the many other villages we visited in Sapa on our moped. People were lovely and not pushy at all. Small service charge but definitely worth it.

We are extremely surprising with many old architectural houses. Amazing designs and charming place have made us to have more knowledge about the culture in Sa pa. We have make friends & exchanges information with the citizens there, they are very hospitality & friendly.However, some things need to improve such as: small children still try to ask traveler to buy souvenirs or beg money...Some danger areas, we have not seen the notices or security staff there.

Cat cat village is about 20 minutes' walk from SaPa. The road isn't steep, making it an easy path to traverse. Buy the ticket and then take the narrow stairway path towards Cat Cat village with shops lined up on either side. There's a bunch of stuff you might be tempted into buying, but bargaining is a must! The village itself is a sight to savour with the water wheels and the waterfalls. I don't really think you need a guided tour of cat cat village since you can enjoy a stroll by yourself (unless you plan to hike to a nearby village and stay there overnight and go through Cat Cat).If you want to buy stuff or eat food it's best to stop at a shop when you go up towards the Cat Cat bridge. The prices are lesser there for the same stuff. Tried Vietnamese coffee at a small joint and it was delicious!

Igényesen kialakított skanzen szerű falucska. Szép volt az idő,jót sétáltunk,klassz szoknyákat vettünk:)Gyönyörű a táj,meseszép a vízesés. Be lehet öltözni népviseleti ruhákba és szuper fotókat készíteni. Mindenki árul valamit,néhányan nagyon tolakodóak és erőszakosak,kicsit sok volt már az út végére.

it is a relatively downhill walk from sapa town to cat cat. taking a car or a bike wld be a lot easier as the road is narrow and constructions going on here and there. we went on a weekday. it seems better than reported crowd we read for weekends. the water village is pleasant and good to hang around and relax.

We had a very nice trek to the village from our hotel in Sapa, about 3km each way. We had a local h’mong guide show us the way down to the village from our hotel as part of a trekking tour. The views of the waterfalls are beautiful. The village had a lot of personality, it felt like the Hmong version of a hobbit village. I’m sure many of the buildings are not authentic and much of it has been recently built, but it’s really beautiful to look at.The only negative was all of the locals trying to sell us Knick knacks which diluted the experience after a while. But I understand that these activities are their bread winner. The path is touristy, but worth the detour to enjoy the sights.
사파를 방문한 여행자라면 한번은 들르게 되는 가깝고 멋진   풍경을 마을하지만 거주민들의 주택은 거의 볼 수가 없고, 상점만 가득한..조금은 괴이한 마을.다음날 택시를 대절해 방문한 타반마을과 그 이웃한 마을이 원래 모습을 더 간직하고 있어 좋았다.하지만 이곳도 도로확장 중이어서 어떻게 변할지?

Good to visit but better to go with a guide that can explain the culture to you. The wet weather is really a spoiler. To note that this is not really a very natural village as compared to Muong Village trekking.
Хорошо пройтись прогуляются по “Traditional village”. Красиво, но не оставляет ощущение бутафории и сделанного specially for tourists!

OK- so on balance this was a lovely place to visit, great snap shot into life in Sapa.It is commercial- with an entrance fee, loads of craft stalls to buy things and very busy.The scenery is stunning, the steps up and down to the waterfall are steep so if you have any mobility issues I suggest you walk the winding road-as we did...still requires a level of fitness to do this...never fear a trusting scooter is not far away to charge you for a ride back to the top.Very interesting to see how the traditional H'Mong people live and create there craft items.I think I would have preferred to visit a local village and see the real thing...but this is not so easy

The area is a little touristy but that doesn’t detract from all the fun features. There are plenty of vendors offering goods and food on your walk down to the waterfall. You could grab some bites and eat by the water and then explore the climbs on the far side or break off from the main trails and see something from a different perspective.

Staying in Sapa one must visit Cat Cat Village as it’s close and walkable from Sapa central. A colourful spectacle though very touristy. There’s a small rose garden with a view over the valley. A beautiful waterfall with a small theme-park built around it. To get to the waterfall you have to walk past lots of craft stalls. A popular thing to do for South Vietnamese tourists is to hire a local traditional costumes and pose for photos everywhere. The selfie culture is alive here too. Cat Cat is small, picturesque and worth a visit even though it’s really all set up for tourists. You also need to pay a small fee to enter the village.

Not easy to trek to this place. Visitors have to go walk and also go down many flights of steps (of unequal height because the steps are made out of stones). Some descends/steps were quite steep and young children may have difficulty or needed support.After the visit, visitors will have to climbed up the same flights of steps. Down seems more painful than up.
경지가 좋고 가갑도 싸다 👌🏻👌🏻👌🏻친구와 같이 가세요 가을에는 가면 좋고 겨울에는 가지 마세요🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈🌈

A typical tourist place, little bit crowded with tourist during the peak season. Worth going through the village. Must be able to walk like 8000-9000 steps (down hill, flat & uphill a little bit). The river at the end is quite nice. However, must see if you are in Sapa.

This trip was recommended to us by the hotel reception as an afternoon trek, but it's just a visit to an ethno village that has been arranged to show tourists the lifestyle and crafts of H'Mong tribespeople (100,000 dong entry). It's less authentic for that reason but very conveniently close to Sapa, beautifully arranged in bamboo wood and next to a lovely waterfall. The bamboo rafts are very cool, to try but also to watch local children play on them. There is also a small dance group performing by the waterfall.

Our trip to cat cat village was very enjoyable. We were taken with a guide who talked through many of the sites/ traditions etc. At the bottom make sure you go on the bamboo rafts which are a lot of fun. It was very touristy so be prepared for lots of stalls selling their wares. Overall a nice day trip.
Has a pretty view of a waterfall and lots of tourist shops. Your best experiences will probably come from hikes taking you a bit further from center of Sapa but this is a nice low key adventure.

Had a great time exploring Cat Cat village with my tour the other day. Our tour guide was very knowledgeable and it was a lot of fun. It was extremely wet and foggy (of course because it’s winter) so the views from the top were obscured, but once we got down to the waterfalls it was absolutely worth it!

Nice hills on sapa town area, the culture and the people still nature, but unfortunately too many children exploitation for tourism. It’s winter drop to 5C and many children at street at 9pm still around to selling the souvenir while the parents look away from 15-20m.The road to catcat so messy with many trash in anywhere. The weather still fresh and clean. No more local price while everything already tourism price.

A pretty little "village".  Lots of photo opportunities here!  It's an easy walk to the village, a bit challenging on the way back up to Sapa, for 100,000 VND take advantage of the moto drivers offering rides up the hill!

Wir waren während unserer Rundreise in Sapa und sind natürlich auch ins Cat Cat Village. Wir hatten zum Glück eine super sympatische Guide die selbst in dem Dorf aufgewachsen ist. Von Sapa ist man in ca. 15 Minuten ins Dorf gelaufen. Dort muss man erstmal Eintritt zahlen. Dann gehts vorbei an den Souvenirläden runter ins Dorf wo einen Aussichtsplattformen und Blumengärten erwarten.Von dort aus gelangt man zum Zentrum, dem Fluss mit anschließendem Wasserfall. Auf dem Weg dorthin sind Wohnhäuser und Webstühle zu besichtigen, an den man frisch gefärbte Artikel kaufen kann.Die Landschaft ist atemberaubend schön und außerhalb der touristischen Gebiete sehr idyllisch.Alles sehr hübsch aber man merkt es ist für die Touristen aufgemöbelt.Wir sind noch weiter in ein kleineres Dorf gelaufen, welches auch sehr schön und weniger touristisch war.Einen halben Tag sollte man einplanen und ein Guide ist von Vorteil.

This is a short trek of maybe 3km down the hill from our hotel (Sapa Vista). We went in a small group with a Moung guide, a 22yo girl who spoke perfect English. The walk down to the village and then to the river at the bottom of the valley is steep but easy as it is mostly paved pathway and some stairs. Halfway down there is a gate where you must buy a ticket to enter the village. The guide bought our ticket but the cost is $70,000 Dong. As we walked through the village the guide explained the culture and their way of life including housing, farming and about the animals they raise for food. At the river there is a small hydro electricity generator that supplies power to the town. Here we saw villagers perform a traditional dance. All the villagers (women anyway) wore traditional colourful dress. At the end of the trek, still at river level, we were met by a van which took us back up the hill to the hotel. The trek and village visit took about 2 hours. You can visit the village without a guide, so long as you buy the ticket, but if you want to learn about the culture take a guide. If you are on your own, I believe you can catch a taxi back up the hill or else you better be really fit as it is a very steep climb out of the valley. There are plenty of vendors along the way selling food and drinks at reasonable prices. They also sell (or rent if you want to dress like a villager for photos) the traditional clothing of the Cat Cat village. Definitely recommend. Only reason for 4* instead of 5 is that it is a bit crowded and touristy in places.
Được hình thành từ giữa thế kỷ 19, đây là điểm tham quan hấp dẫn của du lịch Sa Pa nói riêng và Lào Cai nói chung.

We walked to Cat Cat with our tour guide Chau one of the Black H'mong ladies and she was very well spoken and knowledgeable.Cat Cat is worth a visit especially as a prelude to visiting the tour guide's local village as they are very different. This village is subsidized by the government and tends to be a bit more touristy but it's still an amazing place to see. It's also not a long track to visit

Long walk down the hill to Cat Cat Village (and back up again afterwards). When I arrived the ticket sellers were friendly, and gave me a map showing me where to go. But walking through the village itself felt extremely intrusive. People going about their everyday lives, and me just wandering around taking pictures. The scenery is great, but there are probably better places to visit in Sapa.
Sad to see a series of shops built to exploit tourists in such a beautiful setting. The local people are friendly and happy but they have so little.

Rather commercialized with shops along the road to the village, it is still quite nice. When you reached the village, you will be greeted by a few huge flywheel. I don't think it's serving any purpose but just to give it a hint of village life.

Wer nach Sapa reist sollte unbedingt eins der Dörfer besichtigen. Sei es bei einer geführten Tour oder auf eigene Faust. Cat Cat Village ist sehr leicht zu Fuss von Sapa aus zu erreichen. Der Eintritt hat 70.000 vietnamesische Dollar gekostet und dann gehts auch schon über etliche Stufen herab in den unteren Teil. Die Besichtigung ist recht anstrengend weil viele Stufen zu bewältigen sind und es danach fast ständig aufwärts geht. Es gobt haufenweise Verkaufstände auf denen man angesprochen wird. Wer dankend ablehnt wird dann auch nicht weiter bedrängt. Die Aussicht ist absolut toll und wer den Weg ab beginn von Cat Cat Village nach Sapa nicht mühevoll hochwandern will, kann auf eins der vielen Taxis zurückgreifen. Vorsicht allerdings beim Preis. Da wird gerne mal mehr draufgeschlagen, deshalb handeln und den Oreis sofort ausmachen.

C'est décevant quand on y est déjà aller il y a plus de 15 ans car c'est bien triste de voir ce que le village par lui-même est devenue par contre si l'on y va pour les vues on y est jamais déçu et il y règne une atmosphère plutôt agréable malgré le cinéma des locaux "déguisés" en costume traditionnels.A faire malgré tout, il faut le voir, la nature y est splendide

Sollte man gesehen haben wenn man in Sapa ist da es zu Fuß gut zu erreichen ist.  Klar gibt es woder Tourismus Abzocke aber das ist ja irgendwie überall in Vietnam  ;)Empfehlen kann ich ein kleines Kaffee mit tollen Ausblick ziemlich am Ende des Rundgangs. Leider ist uns der Name entfallen aber es ist alles in blau gefärbt ;)PS:  früh wie möglich hingehen. Um 11 am war es knallevoll

Well , Cat Cat village is very touristic/commercialized place . Every intention is made to make you by some souvenirs  (made in China), and/or some real authentic , ethnic local hand made crafts . But , you are already in Sapa , so do what all other tourists like yourselves do :  Pay the 70 K (Under 3.00 USD) entry fee , have your cameras ready , and walk down the shopping path , all the way down to the reconstructed/renovated spinning wheels and the nice waterfall , and you may catch a glimpse of a local dancing troupe in action (It's not a "LUNE Production , but it's nice) .The following is a quote from a review by Seon B : "....While some may feel that the entrance fees are exhorbitant, if you pause for a moment and think carefully, every park or cultural village needs maintenance and just think about where the funding is coming from? I can easily point anyone to the fees charged for some of European and China's attractions and the entrance fee for this village will be viewed in a better perspective....." .For a more authenticity , take a visit at Lao Chai and Ta Van villages . That's what we did the next day with our guide Hoang Minh , who actually lives in Ta Van village , and runs with his wife a homestay named "Po Mu House" there (See separate review) .

This tour was the first one our group of 6 took when we got to Sapa. Highly commercialized but once you pay the park entrance fee, the local people are real according to our local Hmong guide. You can rent some Hmong outfits to go around and take picture in for 50,000 dong. The natural beauty all around is what caught my eyes.

Большой совет тем кто хочет посетить данную деревню из Сапы, не берите такси за 80 000-100 000 донгов. Потому что ехать на машине 2минуты. Лучше пройдитесь пешком до деревни, а уже на обратном пути решите нужно ли вам такси. В самой деревне на выходе есть мото такси, подвезут с ветерком.Вход в деревню стоит 70 000 донгов. По первым улочкам складывается впечатление, что это не деревня, а рынок традиционной одежды. Но пройдя вглубь деревни вы окунетесь в красоту природы и посмотрите на старые постройки местных. Вроде как местные устраивают показные представления для туристов, но мы на них не попали.В общем нам понравилось, единственное о чем пожалели - это путь до деревни на такси.

I just came back from the village this afternoon. Entrance fee is 70,000VND from a ticket booth on the right hand side of the road about 20m before the entrance to the village on the left. The signage to the village is clearly marked. Ethnic tribe costumes are rentable from shops on the way down to the village.I recommend taking the route down first from the road from Sun Plaza, which is also the place for you to buy tickets to the train and cable car ride up to Fansipan, up in the mountains.The trek is all the way down and you will come across many shops selling local ethnic tribal handicraft items as well as some trekking accessories. While some may feel that the entrance fees are exhorbitant, if you pause for a moment and think carefully, every park or cultural village needs maintenance and just think about where the funding is coming from? I can easily point anyone to the fees charged for some of European and China's attractions and the entrance fee for this village will be viewed in a better perspective.It is the closest to an authentic ethnic tribal village as you can get within Sapa city without having to join a tour that requires 3 hours and a 110km road journey through bumpy roads and a few hundred bends (enough to make a person with motion sickness to throw up) to visit an ethnic village with a few houses and domesticated pigs, chickens, buffalos. In Cat Cat you can also see the windmills and several waterfalls (not very big ones) plus a short live cultural dance programme featuring several dance items.Besides handicraft stalls, you can also get local food from the food stalls selling local food.Overall, in my own personal opinion, it is a good experience from this village. I enjoyed the trip to this village.

Breathtaking view of the waterfalls and village between furious rivers. Bamboo / wood bridges and steep stairs in the rock.  But you pay an entry ticket and get harassed by pedlars of more or less local goods.

We went on a Saturday, but probably better to avoid the weekend many short trip tourist, in any case they have built a great development Nice show inside the little theatreYou can go down from one side and up on the other side of the river to avoid the same route

A good place to explore especially for avid photographers. The village sits on a hill slope with many tiny shops and houses. Animals such as black pigs, buffalo, dogs roam freely around the village. There are few model houses and windmills for us to explore. However, there are many steep steps that one needs to negotiate in this village. If you have difficulty in climbing steps, then this place is not advisable for you.

Our lovely guide Ly took us down through the meandering stairs and explained how life, society and work functioned in this small community. The village itself is more made for tourists but the surrounding landscape of rice fields and homes are authentic. After descending many stairs and stopping to barter with the market folk and take pictures of the beautiful surrounds, we found ourselves in a gorgeous clearing complete with winding pathways edged with flowers in hanging baskets and a beautiful waterfall. After taking more photos and enjoying a rest, we began to climb up the many stairs to another section above, where a shuttle picked us up and drove us back to the hotel.Well worth the trek, however I wouldn’t recommend it if you have issues with climbing a lot of stairs, which wasn’t mentioned prior to undertaking the tour.

It was a bit touristy but a nice way to see some of views and offers places for photos. Would not recommend for people with mobility issues as there quite a few steps to go down and then up to get the bottom.  Admission was 70,000 Dong per person which I found was very cheap for what you get.
관광지로 너무 유명해져서 기대안하고 가볍게 감. 호객 행위 심하지 않고 소수민족 대부분 친절~사람 많은건 어쩔 수 없으니 오후 늦게가니 사람들 관광 끝난 분위기~~~~~~여서 잘보고 왔어요

It was a good place to see how the village function but it was quite sad that they sell sweets and junk to visitors to give to the children. Many children will surround you. I was shocked, I asked if they sell real food and they offered me instant noodles. If you are going there, I urge you to bring real food. The children are under nourish
ค่าเข้าหมู่บ้าน 70000 ด่อง ในหมู่บ้านมีของขายของชาวเขา เห็นวิวนาขั้นบันได มีสวนดอกไม้ มีน้ำตก มีกังหันน้ำ มีลำธารเดินลงไปเล่นน้ำได้

Join a trekking tour for the best view. The stunning view is breathtaking. If you like greener fields, visit in mid year sometime between June to end of AugustFor a golden touch from the harvesting months - best to visit mid September till October (shoulder season) might rain a little.

See the daily life activities of the locals. A nice waterfalls at the bottom of the valley which was built by French with a hydraulic power station. You can even swim to under the water falls. It is so refreshing after a long hot day.

We visited on a sunny day and was greeted with lovely blooming morning glory at the entrance. It was a nice day for a walk around this village. Even though some reviews call this a manufactured village, it was lived in with real houses, villagers, dogs, cats and other farm animals like pigs, chickens and ducks. They do receive their fair share of tourists and have a daily performance. The scenery was beautiful with lots of greenery and jungle foliage, river, waterfalls, bridges and water wheels. It was an interesting morning walk and took us about 3 hours to complete the whole circuit.

An amazing village!! its 3 km from sapa. you can walk or take taxi (dont need a guide to arrive, its really simple to get there). when you arrive you need to pay 70.000 vnd per person for entrance. there is a long trail or short one. we picked the short one that is 3 km walking. you will see beautiful view and a waterfall. ther is alot of small local stores on the way. important to know that there is many steps and the way isn't easy.  in the end of the trail there is taxi that waiting.

It was a cute little village.Me and my friend went there without the tour but we could see them come and go. If you go with the tours it can be quite crowded and maybe it's harder to take a picture. but at one stage we were alone and it was a very nice place to relax. They had a little performance which they were more than happy for you to try some of their traditional dances on stage can be a bit difficult though but fun! try it.! The village also has a beautiful little waterfall. You can also watch the ladies being creative with their homemade things. interesting! definitely worth a visit while in Sapa. Stunning veiw on the way
리뷰가 안좋은게 많아서 걱정했는데 아이랑 같이가긴 무난하니 괜찮았습니다 무리한호객행위 없었구요 아이들이 자연스럽게 여기저기서 관광객신경안쓰고 노는모습 많이 보였어요
We liepen de trappen naar beneden naar de watervallen. Door het Cat Cat dorpje. Veel verschillende winkeltjes en restaurantjes. Een aanrader. De entrée komt ten goede aan het dorp.

Definitely set up for tourists, with bus loads coming in and out everyday, and village life showcased next to each other. If you take all that with a pinch of salt, you’ll see a unique culture in North-Western Vietnam. Nice short trek from SaPa around 3-5km round trip, up and down hill, a mixture of dancing, agriculture, weaving from hemp/dyeing from indigo plants, there’s a great sense and feel of the culture. Best done with a local H’mong tour guide but can be done solo.
The local guide led the group over challenging terrain and shared a look at farming, how people live and their homes and customs. It was a very humbling experience but totally captivating.

We really enjoyed walking the village, the views were stunning.It might feel sometimes like a one big show with the locals.Visiting the villages requires going down and climbing many steps, but definitely worth it.
ちょうどよい広さです。サクサク観光でき雰囲気を味わうことができます。近くのカフェヘブンも近いので一緒にサパ棚田を満喫できます。
사파에서 걸어서 다녀 올 수 있는 트래킹 코스. 경사가 급하니 더운날 트레킹 하기엔 힘들수도 있다. 적당한 개울과, 폭포, 마을, 음식점들을 즐기기 좋은 곳이었다.

Just few minutes ride from sapa it’s a interesting small villages.very touristique.if you are on your feet lots climbing.There a small lovely water fall,,stream.Can relax and enjoy coffee by the waterfall many restaurants too.A good place to enjoy the natural landscapes,,rice fields Hmong houses as well traditional Hmong .

Nice little village! What a pity was that it was mainly made for tourists, and you can't really see the local families, how they're really living. Otherwise it was very nicely made up, so it was worth to see. To visit the Cat Cat village, you need to buy ticket which is 70 000 VND.Please prepare to go up and down many stairs! You can find little shops/ cafe's very often during the route. Cat Cat is about 1,5 km from Sapa village, we rented scooters from our hotel, which was quite nice option, as you need to walk in the village as well. Be prepared that the roads are not in very good shape..

El pueblo de Cat Cat está muy cerca de Sa Pa, por lo que se puede llegar paseando tranquilamente sin problema. Para entrar al poblado hay que pagar una pequeña cantidad a la entrada. Desde allí se tienen unas bonitas vistas a las terrazas de arroz y se llega hasta una pequeña catarata. Los habitantes van vestidos con sus ropas tradicionales y resula muy colorido; sin embargo, todo está enfocado al turista y todas las casas tienen en sus bajos tiendas de souvenirs, restaurantes, etc, por lo que, desde mi punto de vista, da la sensación de pérdida de autencidad.

It's a long walk but it is worth it. There are couple of water falls and they are ravishing. You will pass through the village and see their life style. I will record if you can walk than don't hire a guide just follow the Google map. Its very easy

After paying the entrance fee, you'll be given a map of the area, much like entering an amusement park (that's how touristy it feels). Nonetheless it was still nice to walk around the village visiting each of the attractions, like some show houses to see how the locals live and also the waterfall.

Un tour au total d’environ 8 kms accessible sans guide depuis Sapa. (Possibilité de rentrer à Sapa en moto pour le retour)Le village en soi demande un billet d’entrée dans la vallée (70 000 Vnd) et est très attrape-touristes (boutiques partout, prises à partie...) mais la rivière et les environs sont magnifiques.Une belle balade facile pour les enfants / personnes ayant plus de difficultés à marcher longtemps ou sur terrain difficile. Petite photo en prime.

We spent the day walking down to Cat cat village from our hostel on the edge of Sapa. It felt reasonably touristy but was still interesting to see everything, the waterfall at the bottom of the valley is beautiful with water wheels turning up stream (we couldn't work out what they were for, apart from to look pretty) It's all very hilly but we had the whole day so took our time looking around. We were approached a lot of times by pretty persistent locals offering to take us on hikes or attempting to sell us things but you can't blame them for trying!

상업화되었다고 실망스러워 하는 사람이 많아 보입니다만 어차피 관광지라는 곳은 다 상업화되어 있는 곳일수밖에 없습니다. 사파 시내 근교에 있어 접근의 편의성도 뛰어나고 소수민족마을 테마파크와 같은 역할을 충분히 하고 있다고 생각합니다. 진짜 현지인들의 삶을 들여다보고 싶으면 차라리 현지마을 홈스테이를 추천합니다. 말씀드리지만 이곳은 잘 정돈된 상업화된 관광지입니다.
Enjoy the waterfall and traditional water wheels and cloth making process . Photographer please bring along ur tripod

Gerade unter Sapa beginnt der Weg, zu welchem man Eintritt zahlt. Er führt steil hinunter. Vorbei an kleinen Läden und Restaurants. Die Ladenbesitzer waren sehr zurückhaltend und bedrängten uns wenig. Trotzdem ist es natürlich sehr touristisch.

We were met at the hotel by our guide, Soa. She is such an amazing person and spoke brilliant English. She was kind, sensitive and very informative. The scenery is truly beautiful and we were fortunate enough that there were people that rented local costumes... the brilliant colours of the costumes added to the beauty of the experience. The moss covered roofs and brilliant green of the rice fields added to our photographic experience. A few tips: Make sure to wear comfortable shoes... Make sure that you are relatively fit as the climb down and especially up van be very draining. Also, please take some extra cash with as there are many many people that would just want to sell you something. I found this very disturbing as you would want to help everybody but this is just too much... when the children started to come with their little bracelets it really got to me! I had an amount of money and just gave them all my money that I had on me. For the rest it is a MUST!

עשינו טיול ערב והיה נחמד מאוד ומעניין. הרבה חיות מתוקות וילדים בדרך. נופים עוצרי נשימה. קחו בחשבון למי שמתקשה שבהתחלה יורדים מדרגות ואחכ צריך לעלות הכל (כ15 דק בערך בקצב רגיל) אלא אם כן לוקחים אופנוע שגובה לא מעט.
We walked from Sapa to Cat Cat and then to Ta Van. TOTALLY amazing walk all that 17km walk. The kids loved it!

A 2km stroll downhill from Sapa to Cat Cat Village. You must pay a small entrance fee (70,000VNDpp) which also gives you a map to guide you on a circuit of the village. It was lovely to see the water wheels in action, theres a pretty bridge and waterfall in the village and the surrounds are very beautiful. Also plenty of markets (a little touristy now but still a lovely half day out).If you dont want to walk back up the hill to Sapa, you can get a taxi or motorbike. Allow 2-3hours.

I debated between 4 and 5 and kept a 4 due to a few things to be wary of. 1) this is an amazing trip! A must do! It is about 5km with mostly down hill - so worth it! 2) know you will be harassed to buy trinkets and the guilt is tough - hard to avoid. 3) show only ok, don’t  worry about getting there on time.

We walked to Cat Cat from Sapa, downhill on the way there but hard going on the way back. The village is very touristy but worth a visit, it has a very pretty waterfall and some interesting scenery, including raised wooden walkways and waterwheels. An easy afternoon out if you have some free time but there is much nicer scenery a bit further afield

Ugh I hate touristy tat so much and walking down Cat Cat is just a tat explosion. Everywhere you look: tat, tat and more tat. But! You get some nice views and the tat seems to stop when you reach the river, waterful and turbine things. We enjoyed the walk regardless of the tat. At 70,000 pp it wasn't the worst way to spend a small amount of money.

Il villaggio di cat cat di per se è carino, anche se sta diventando anch’esso un po’ turistico ... Comunque se vi trovate a Sapa consiglio di passarci , magari con un tour organizzato e proseguire per vedere le valli sottostanti

Vi var uppe i Sapa 3 dagar/2 nätter. Tyvärr hade vi en väldig dimma och såg inte så mycket som vi ville se. Rundturen med guiden var bra. Byborna som följer med dig är väldigt trevliga till en början, och när man inte vill handla något från dem blir de lite griniga.

If you are reasonably fit, walk there and back yourself, it is a fair distance but won't kill you. You have to buy tickets for about 70,000V. Make sure you stop to watch the dancers in the performance building at the end of the village by the waterfall. If I was to do it again I would wear sports sandals (tevas of the like) - my runners got wet and smelt bad for the rest of the trip :( Wear a hat because it can get hot. Watch out for helpful Hmong people who want to show you the way - you will have to pay them. Treat it as tourist tax because they look to have a tough life!

Quite a fair bit of hike from Sapa town to this place with the altitude. Very touristy place with stalls haggling people to buy things.Wear proper footwear. It was rainy when we went in December. There are plastic bags on sale to cover track shoes but I felt that it would be worst with the muds along the track.
Cat Cat Village is a great hike ... awesome way to see the H'Mong lives.  Walikng distance from Sa Pa

That was an amazing and also a very long walk. We started from the center of Sa Pa, went down to the valley. You will notice many local ladies trying to sell you handmade things and they also provide different kind of tours. We wanted to do one they walk, so at the end we got tired explaining that we don’t want to do long tour. Be aware that foe some villages you will need to buy an entrance permission. You can do it in the information center in the Sa Pa central square or right before entering in the village. We payed 70,000 VND per person for Cat Cat village enter. There is an amazing waterfall you can enjoy, rice fields, tracking paths. We really enjoyed our walk. However if you plan to do it around the 11:00-15:30, take care of some head cover and shirt or jacket with long sleeves, as the sun at this time is right above the head and you can easily get a sunburn. I did it unfortunately and also got a bad sunburns on my head, so at the evening I was dealing with terrible headaches and couldn’t do any activities for few days more. It is dangerous, the sun is tricky. Otherwise everything was brilliant!
You need to be prepared to climb down many steps & then be prepared to climb back up. It is worth the effort.

Miejsce bardzo turystyczne; prawie cały czas idzie się między straganami. Ale jednocześnie centrum jest urokliwe, oferuje ciekawe pokazy tańca, wnętrz domów itp. Bardzo miło relaksowało się nam w hamakach.
Buena caminata.. Mucho turismo pero es interesante para visitar.. Nada del otro mundo pero si estas en SaPa.. Hazlo si tienes el tiempo.

This is an easy downhill walk from Sapa town. There are great views to be seen going both up and down. There are lots of stalls selling the same kind of products the whole way down. Make sure you make it all the way to the bottom to the waterfall. There is a 70,000 dong entry fee to the town. You can do this hike without a guide, just look up the route on google maps. If you aren't too keen for the walk back up the hill there are several people offering a motorbike ride back up for a few dollars.

Chuyến du lịch đến thành phố mờ sương 3 ngày 2 đêm là chuyến đi vô cùng đáng nhớ. Bản Cát Cát khiến mình thấy ấn tượng nhất. Lần đầu tiên mình được thấy nhà sàn, thấy cuộc sống và sinh hoạt của đồng bào dân tộc ít người vùng cao Tây Bắc. Tuy nhiên có nhiều trẻ em bán hàng.

The village is a long way down, and a very steep climb back up, but well worth the visit. It's in a pretty valley with the main attractions being a waterfall.  Sapa gets lots of rain so the falls were spectacular.  There is a cultural show and other attractions to enjoy in the village.
ačkoliv se nejedná o žádnou super atrakci je přesto zajímavé Cat Cat vidět. Život ve zdejších horách není jednoduchý o čemž právě tato vesnička svědčí.

1番の目的は黒モン族の布製品だったが、ツアーのためゆっくり吟味できず、ブランケット1枚だけしか購入できませんでしたが、翌日立ち寄ったサパマーケットでは品揃えも品質も劣っており、価格もかなりの交渉が必要だったため、もっと村でゆっくり買い物したかったと後悔しきりでした。土産物屋以外にも滝や簡単なアトラクションなどあり、なかなか楽しめます。ただかなりの谷を下り、帰りは駐車場までは20分ほど登るため足腰に不自由のある方は大変です！

In Sapa kun je best een trekking met gids doen, maar dit dorpje kun je gemakkelijk op eigen houtje bezoeken. Het is wel zeer commercieel gemaakt, maar uiteindelijk vond ik het toch zeker de moeite, met onderweg mooie uitzichten op de rijst terrassen.

Realmente muy bueno, veras la realidad  y la forma de vivir en las villas de vietnam. Parte de varias villas y pueblos. Consejo: HOMESTAY 1 DREGULAR? si esperas tener las comodides de occidente... no vayas.

È senza dubbio molto affascinante, soprattutto la parte con le vecchie ruote e le CascateSu questo non si può dire niente, diverso è se si pensa che sia davvero un villaggio ancora autentico perché purtroppo non è più così, è dilaniato e snaturato dal turismoNel giro da fare, consiglio passare dall'Old Village, non va nessuno da quella parte, e proprio per questo ne vale la pena

We walked around Cat Cat village without knowing what to expect. It is indeed a very nice walk and the scenery is really beautiful especially in the waterfall and hydroelectric plant areas. However there were so many touristic shops, which are ruining a bit the scenery. But since it’s the only village you can see without a tour guide it makes sense it would be so touristy. In addition, there was not a lot of information about what was around. Lastly, I just read at the back of the ticket that an art performance by h’mong people is also included. They did not inform us at the ticket booth so we did not look out for the schedule. Apart from these points that could get improved, overall it’s a village good to see for everyone that visits Sapa and don’t want to spend a lot to trek around other villages.

nice walk from the entry to the village until the river and waterfall, very touristic route, seems like dictating from the ministry of tourism, or the local minority office, hence, everybody is doing that, we did it in bad weather so it wasn't crowded,
Đến Đà lạt k nên bỏ qua những điểm như thế này . Đẹp và ấn tượng nhưng trẻ em theo xin đồ quá nhìu như vậy k hay lắm .  Nói chung vẫn rất tuyệt.
posiblemente de las mejores caminatas por libre en sapa. aunque está en el límite de ser protegido/teatralizado por el turismo vale la pena. es tranquilo y agradable!

It was a good walk from Sa Pa, especially they way down the hill. We had to pay 70.000d p.p to enter the village, which we didn’t know but it didn’t surprise us. The village is nice, you get a bit of a feeling when you are walking over there. But it is very touristic, children walking with children on their back and children selling stuff. Enough to make tourists feel bad so they give money or sell things. We never sell anything because we don’t want to encourage this. The way back is a climb, but you can take a motorbike.

We took a 4 km walk down the road from Sapa to the village and a motor cycle ride (100,000 dong) back as quite steep and still aching form the day before's trek. Once in th evillage there are still many steps to encounter. It costs 70,000 dong to go into the village which is interesting with a nice cultural show. Some lovely scenery with waterfalls. We were lucky to be invited to a local home where they were enjoying a new year's feast.
내리막길이 가팔라서 다칠위험 크구요폭포근처라 습기가 많아서 미끄러지기도 쉬워요기념품점 호객행위는 아침에가서 그런지 생각보다 많지는 않았고 폭포랑 대나무다리도 볼만했어요

サパの中心街から徒歩で行ってきました。Tシャツにパーカーを着て、その上に薄手のジャンバーを羽織って行ったのですが、けっこう寒かったです。村までの道はとにかく霧が濃くて、数メートル先も見えない状態。それでも、時折日がさすと霧が晴れ、周囲の山々や棚田を見ることができました。村は鶏や豚や水牛が多数おり、水量の多い渓谷と滝もあるのでとてもよいです。霧に包まれながら歩くのも素敵な体験でした。ただ、霧のためにガイドブックの写真のような景色は見れませんでした。１月は大体こんな感じらしいです。

El sitio es bonito y a mí me gustó, pero a dia de hoy es una “turistada”. En su momento si que debió ser un rincón auténtico, pero a dia de hoy está todo montado para el turismo. Para hacer el recorrido se tienen que bajar muchísimas escaleras y una vez abajo se cruza el río a través de un puente y se atraviesa el pueblo en unos minutos. Si vas sin prisa recomendaría tomarse un té o comer algo de lo que venden en los puestos ambulantes. Quedan algunas artesanas a las que puedes ver trabajando y poco más. Diría que es uno de esos sitios que se puede visitar pero sin grandes expectativas. No se si volvería. Cuando llegas a la salida puede ser una miniaventura contratar a algún motorista para que te suba hasta la puerta de acceso. Lo hicimos y fue una experiencia.

Nous avons vraiment aimé la balade au travers les maisons et les rizières bien qu'il pleuvait toujours et que c'était nuageux. Nous avions un guide privé avec nous et c'était très agréable. Effectivement, il y a plusieurs marches et c'est parfois glissant. Il faut passer par cet endroit pour mieux comprendre comment les gens vivaient.
Văn hóa của người vùng cao. Nhiều cảnh đẹp. Nhưng có rất nhiều trẻ con bán hàng. Người dân thân thiện. Nên đến đó.
離Sapa市中心不遠，可步行到達，也因為如此遊客很多，票價70000元，村裝內很多賣原住民服飾或配件等的小店，也有可以喝飲料吃小吃的地方可以休息，村內的景觀還好，如想欣賞壯闊風景如梯田，建議申請一個嚮導往其他村落健行，如無太多時間的話來這裡看看也可以。目前市區往cat cat village的道路狀況不佳，很多泥土和灰塵石頭等。

Beaucoup d'échoppes de souvenirs,( you buy something) où l'on vous invite à l' achat, et cela n est pas vraiment moins cher qu'ailleurs ! Le prix du village 70 000 vnd par personneIl est quand même sympa de faire le trajet de la visite pour y voir les cascades, les moulins et le pont suspendu, Une bonne alternative si vous n'avez pas fait d'excursion pour voir les ethnies locales autre part.

This is the closest Hmong village near Sa Pa town. They still preserve few traditional houses, from mud and wood, as well as the Shaman's house. The hilly, up and down steps, are good for exercise, and give a glimpse of the traditional ways of Hmong live. The waterfall and hydro power are excellent. Love the clean water in the streams - very well done to the local people for keeping them clean, despite of all tourists (who sometimes, need to be mindful about littering). The usage of traditional clothing and the rental is a good idea to raise funds in preserving the village.
Bei schönen Wetter ist es dort sehr schön, man kann sehr gut zu Fuss von Sapa hinlaufen, es gibt dort einen kleinen Wasserfall und man kann auch eine Folklore Show sehen.

This is a small village near Sapa. You can walk from Sapa and you reach the place in 30 minutes. Is placed in a valley so is all the way down. That means when you come back is all the way up to Sapa again. The landscape is awesome so it doesn't matter the effort. There are a lot a small villages where you can buy souvenirs. I have read that they look at you badly if you dont buy anything. That didn't happened to us. People were quite friendly actually. It's true that it focused to tourism but you don't have to buy anything if don't want to. In the way to Cat Cat you can see the rice terraces sorrounding all the valley and it is really beautiful. In Cat Cat there's a waterfall (brown water because of the mud) and a few water mills. Beautiful place to spend the morning. If the weather is good is a great experience.

หมู่บ้านน่ารัก เล็กๆ ในหุบเขาลึก เราต้องเดินเท้าเข้าไปในตัวหมู่บ้าน โดยต้องซื้อบัตรเข้าหมู่บ้านเดินตามทางผ่านบ้าน โรงเรียน ร้านค้า ตลอดทางมีสินค้ามากมายหลากหลาย เมื่อเข้าไปถึงกลางหมู่บ้านจะพบน้ำตกสวยงาม มีโซนแสดงการผลิตสินค้าชุมชน มีร้านกาแฟที่รสชาดดี มีมุมให้ถ่ายภาพมากมาย ได้ออกกำลังกายจากการเดินไปในตัว

Interessante visita in un villaggio-esposizione dove si mescolano aspetti antropologici, culturali e artigianato locale. Un buon riassunto per turisti della vita delle comunità nelle montagne. Bello spettacolo di musica e balletti. Possibilità di acquisto manufatti artigianali a buon prezzo

Nơi đây thật sự đẹp nhưng có 1 điều tôi không thích đó là có quá nhiều trẻ em đi theo và xin tiền của khách du lịch. Nó thực sự ảnh hưởng đến địa điểm du lịch và ấn tượng của khách du lịch về Sapa nói riêng và về du lịch Việt Nam nói chung. Nhưng có một điều đó là chúng nói tiếng Anh khá tốt. Điều này là một điều tốt cho du lịch Sapa. Nên tới Sapa để chiêm ngưỡng vẻ đẹp nơi đây.

Walked here from Sa Pa, lovely views off the rice fields . You do go down so eventually you have to go up. Cat cat its self is small with lots of stuff for you to look at, nice waterfall. To get back to Sa Pa or get up the hill to the coach park there are men on scooters who for a small fee will take, game for a laugh gotta be done

The village itself is beautiful and you probably get more out of the visit with a tour guide who will explain it better than just going on your own. (Entry 70,000vnd)It is incredibly over commercialised, on your walk in with as many souvenir shops tucked into the approx. 3 km walk in and out of the village.It is sad that it has lost its charm but that's progress.At this time of year there are constant fogs in Sapa but the village it situated lower down so there is more visabitity.

Oui c'est touristique. Oui le site est à 70000VND et 30000VND pour les enfants (un moyen comme un autre de gagner de l'argent) Oui le début est une succession d'échoppes (déception). Non, les Hmongs noirs ne vivent sûrement pas ici. On ravale notre déception.et on arrive sur le village fabriqué pour touristes. Simplement magnifique. Ma fille de 7 ans nous a pris dans les bras avec des yeux remplis de joie en nous disant " C'est comme ça que j'imaginais le Vietnam". Rangez vos yeux DC adultes dans la poche et regardez avec une âme d'enfant.  Ce village est bien entretenu et fait penser à un musée vivant. Pour ceux qui se plaignent de la pression touristique, rien n'empêche de faire un tour   vers Y Lonh Ho à 1h de marche tranquille de Cat Cat. Authenticité garantie. Rencontre géniale. 2 touristes en 2h. La vraie arnaque de Sapa  ce sont les tours organisés qui vont vous convaincre qu'il est très difficile de se passer d'eux et qui vous promettent l'authenticité à des tarifs prohibitifs en suivant finalement les mêmes traces que tous les autres.

Trek down to Cat Cat to see interesting people and great views. Many local shops along the way selling handicraft items but the vendors are not pushy, they are just trying to make a living and the items they sell are interesting and not at all expensive.

Our guide Ben seemed to know everyone and took us into a number of the locals homes. The views are stunning and the water wheels a real throw back. Literally there are cottage industries everywhere. Be warned, some physical exertion is needed, but we are in our 60s and coped ok

I was very skeptical about visiting this place initially because of the "very touristy!!" reviews - which it was. It was too pretty to be a functional village - the fields are laid out in heart shaped patches (haha), tons of shops lining the street, children trying to sell you things. Look beyond it and ignore all the shops, you see the huts build into the side of a mountain, livestock roaming free, and great views - especially towards the waterfall. Definitely make the walk out to the other side of the town where the river and bridges are - it looks like it could be straight out of Tropic Thunder (pictures included in this review!). Lots of steps - may be tough on those with knee problems - but doable. Do this if like us, you're short on time in Sa Pa and want to see a village, great views that you can do in half a day. Head for the other villages and skip this one if you want a really authentic experience and would be offended by anything less. I would definitely come back to Sapa to experience the other villages and bike to the mountain pass but I was happy with the 70kVND (quite steep) I paid as entrance fees. My top tips for visiting the place would be:1. you can bike from town (off Fansipan street) to Cat Cat in about 10-15 minutes if you're short on time/ keen on saving energy for the steps).2. Entrance fee 70kVND bought at the village entrance (not in town as quoted in some reviews I've read); bike parking in the shops near the entrance for 10kVND3. Go early to avoid the crowds and unobstructed views (we were there by 8am and the trickle of tourists already was coming in). I'd imagine we'd have a much less enjoyable time fighting our way through a crowd and people photobombing all our photos - one of my greatest pet peeves.4. Always. Check. The. Weather. It was pretty rainy when we were there - 9th November but we got a glorious 3 hour gap between the light showers and made full use of it.5. The roads in Sa Pa are ... hazardous. What seems like constant hillside development, rain, narrow winding roads makes the whole place really muddy, wet and slippery. Take necessary precautions especially in places they're excavating into the hillside or if you're riding a bike.6. Unrelated to the village itself but, great food places near the marketplace in Sa Pa! Not within the market itself, but the shops surrounding it which seem to cater more for the locals than tourists. You can get amazing salmon bun cha for 25kVND (cheaper than everywhere else) and great spring rolls (3 for 10kVND) that we're still dreaming of today.

The visit to Cat Cat village is one of the shorter trek we went while in Sapa. It was a 3km trek mainly downhill from the start with a shorter uphill trek before reaching our bus. The view is nice as with the many treks in the area. The waterfall makes a nice place to take pictures. There were some cultural performance and weaving showcase for the tourist visitors at the main waterfall area while we were there. Do bring a walking stick if stairs is a problem. As usual with attractions in Sapa, there was a group of villagers following us as we alight from our bus. They did not follow us too far (probably half a km) asking us to buy souvenirs from them. I enjoyed the place as it was not too long (like some 7km trek) and more suitable of older and less fit visitors.

Our bus arrived at 1pm and as mentioned in other review this small trekking fitted our afternoon very well. Beautiful view in general! Downside is the constant little shop selling useless goodies that kind of remove the atmosphere of the town.

The trek to Cat cat village took us one hour from the entry point. Although the village was like any other one but the quaint cafe's with picturesque views and the waterfall were the highlights of the trek. On the way, we had local green tea at a cafe and tried the traditional dress as well. The locals were extremely friendly.The waterfall and the hydroelectric power station were a sight to watch. It forms a perfect backdrop for a beautiful picture. The way up is a bit steep and tiring and a walking stick is recommended

We enjoyed our walk from Sapa to Cat Cat Village. It's a fairly easy walk down hill to the village with some lovely views along the way.  Entrance was 70000d per person which is probably worth it. There are a lot of steps down to the base of the waterfall and remember "what goes down must come back up" so bit of a hike back up again . We opted for the motorbike taxi back to Sapa which was 50000d and well worth it. Overall very interesting and not too many tourists the day we were there. I did not find the vendors too agressive either as mentioned in some reviews.

We were really looking forward to this part of our walk around Sapa- and we loved it.. You pay to get in to the village (no problem) and you get to explore their village! You can visit local houses, see what they are like inside, you can see how they make clothes and even rent clothes to dress up and take photos looking like a local.. The views are spectacular and there are animals everywhere..
The waterfall, the bridge and water mills are nice, but the whole path among small butics and houses was boring. Plenty of poor quality souvenirs and not handmade items eventhough they say so.

I took a slow walk from town and took about 3hours in all. Not for the elderly definitely as there are stone paths and plenty of steps. Beautiful views despite the fog. Harvest is over but still awesome views. I found a little coffee shop with an amazing view. Had a drip coffee and an egg for about 3 dollars. See water bufflos, boars, chicken, ducks and loads of dogs along the way. I didn't get poached at all by the locals. Very little tourists when I went perhaps coz it was raining. Be sure to have good shoes on as it gets slippery in wet weather. Perhaps not very safe in anything more  than a  drizzle. Walking back, some part of a tree fell from the side and landed seconds before me. Plenty of motorcycle taxis at the end of the trail to bring you back to town. I bargained from 50k to 40k.

Cat Cat Village is close to Sapa and is a great place to explore.  You can walk down the hill fairly easily but I would recommend getting a taxi back up when you finish.  Along the way you can see traditional houses, some interesting shops, beautiful views and a lovely waterfall.  You can easily spend a few hours exploring this area.
You still see ethnic people, their villages, and rice terraces.... along with all the other hordes of tourists.Makes me wonder of the impact on their village life...
Bản nằm gần trung tâmHầu như bao gồm hết mọi đặc trưng và khung cảnh của bản khácĐi bộ hơi mỏi chânCon gái ko nên đi giày cao Điểm nên đi khi đi sapa

Cat Cat Village is a short trek downhill from Sapa.  We had a wonderful guide named "Sue" escort us, but it could be done without an escort.  The views walking down to Cat Cat Village are amazing and there are several stops along the way with fantastic overlooks.  Cat Cat Village itself is a bit "commercial", but interesting.  They have a show with traditional dance and demonstrations on how they make hemp fabric.  There are many little shops and a great rooftop restaurant/bar overlooking the waterfall.  An easy trek down to Cat Cat Village, but you may want to take one of the readily available taxis back up to Sapa.

After the visit up the Fansipan Mountain .. I decided to go explore Cat Cat Village by taxi.It offer a different setting where ones can see up close the Padi field and the local harvesting the Padi as well as how the living life style of the folks there.This period is Autumn so it is nice .. apart from the Padi field there was also waterfalls village Worth a visit but beware a lot of stairs to climb up and down .. ones will also see many Vietnamese ladies donning traditional tribal dresses that ones could rent for 50,000VND to roam around the village and have their photos taken or selfie.Entrance fee to the village is 50,000VND per head, if you don't feel like walking a lot .. ones can catch a ride from the motorbike guys for 40,000 VND down to the village from the entrance of the village

It is unique experience. It doesn't seem like original village as they tried to show everything about culture and lifestyle. But the visit is worth the time and money. It is organised tour. Surrounded by mountains and fountains , lot of places to click good pictures. Umbrella or raincoat is must. It might be physically exhausting for oldies. There is hydro station at bottom of village built by French.

Se puede ir andando desde Sapa, es la aldea mas cercana al pueblo, se puede hacer un sendero circular bastante bonito donde se va bajando cruzando la aldea hasta una cascada y un arroyo que hay bajo del todo con unas norias de agua muy chulas. Luego se vuelve a subir hasta la aldea y de alli a Sapa, recomendable 100%.

We travelled by cab and got dropped at the entrance of the village. The climate was plesant and not raining. Most of the trek was downhill in between stone houses which were all turned into shops selling handmade items and few restaurants. The village had a small dance troupe performing local dance and they were entertaining. My kids and wife took their turn to dance with them across bamboo poles and it was fun. The water wheel  and the waterfall is nice to see. Little sad that the place is losing its authenticity and more touristy. The steps are quite steep and you have to be careful especially if you are travelling with children. Overall a plesant experience.

Took a taxi from Sapa town. Apparently they charge a standard 200k VND. The driver will even wait for you at the exit and you only pay when you are back in the town. It is set up for tourist but I went there around 4pm so most of the tourist were gone and I had a good time walking the path free from haggling as other reviewers mentioned. It offers a lot of good photo ops. But if you are doing the one day trek and don't have enough time for this, you can give it a miss. The day trek will give you much better scenery than Cat Cat Village.

Passeggiando durante la nostra escursione tra i terrazamenti delle risaie siamo capitati in questo villaggio creato per i turisti in riva ad un torrente e con una cascatella che si può vedere arrivando dal ponticello.Siamo stati fortunati perchè 10 minuti dopo il nostro arrivo è iniziato uno spettacolo con danzatori in costume che eseguivano balli caratteristici di questa minoranza etnica.Bello spettacolo inatteso.

Certes il y a beaucoup de touristes, certes, on vous attend un peu partout sur le chemin avec toujours plein de choses à acheter. Néanmoins, la visite vaut le détour (même si la balade, surtout s'il a plu, peu s'avérer un rien sportive !). En prime, arrivés au village, vous découvrirez certaines activités artisanales ainsi qu'une démonstration des danses traditionnelles locales. Evidemment, là aussi, les touristes ont investi les lieux, il n'empêche, les paysages sont somptueux. Pour info, ce parcours nous était proposé par Sapa Pathfinder Travel qui organise de nombreuses excursions (nous avons également découvert les rizières sur une autre journée avec eux) : nickel, rien à redire.

Op wandelafstand (circa 3 km) van het centrum van Sapa.Een mooie wandeling en leuk om te zien. Ook hier is het bezoek aan het stadje zeer toeristisch opgesteld (overal kraampjes met souveniers), waardoor je je de vraag kan stellen in hoeveer je nog te maken hebt met een 'authentiek' stadje.Wij hebben het bezocht met een privé gids, wat zeker een aanrader is gezien de bijkomende uitleg die je krijgt. Gids was zeer vriendelijk en behulpzaam!

Es ist zwar etwas touristisch und kommerziell aufgezogen, doch die hier angebotenen Handwerkswaren sind authentisch. Die Gegend ist sehr schön und es werden einem doch auch Einblicke in die Kultur der Bergvölker (hier v.a. die H'mong) geboten.

Took a short hike from SAPA town to the Cat Cat Village and the first thing that greeted us were the valley view of the extending paddy fields and lush greenery. Visit to the village will cost a small entrance fee of 40,000 VND and you can explore the village houses and stop for some souvenir shopping.

When other traditional villages don't have much to see except the concrete roads and sellers, Govt made sure this village got all traditional things in one place, beautiful setup in an original village. River and the waterfalls in it give a good view. Must visit..!!

This village offer a nice view on paddy field. You can also see a waterfall and a representation of a traditional dance. The entry is less than 10$. I went there when it was cloudy and it was still really nice. With the sun this village might even more beautiful.

הגענו לביקור בכפר בליווי מדריכה.הירידה למפל ולמעיין שבמרכז הכפר הינה במדרגות מסודרות כאשר לאורך כל הדרך בכפר התיירותי לגמרי חנויות של מזכרות, תיקים , בגדים וגם בתי קפה. בסוף הירידה גשר מעל המים ומול המפל הנחמד. הכפר הוא של בני שבט ההמונג השחור.הדרך חזרה לחניה,למרות   שהיא בעלייה ובטיפוס בשביל במדרגות , איננה קשה מידי.בסך הכל כפר נחמד , למרות שהוא מאד תיירותי וטיול מהנה.

Visiting the cat cat village was an awesome experience. We had a private tour guide which allowed us to visit peoples homes and get an extra insight as to life in the small village. Lots of things to buy along the way and you have the ability to see the women weaving and making scarves, bags and multiple other things. However there are a lot of stairs and steep walks however after the bridge you can get a motorbike back to sapa :)
Điểm du lịch thú vị khi được trải nghiệm, tham quan bản làng của người dân.  Tuy nhiên vì phải đi bộ khá nhiều và xa nên phải sử dụng dịch vụ vận chuyển tại đây nếu ko đi bộ rất xa và nhiều.

My first piece of advice is not to take a guide to Cat Cat Village so you can actually enjoy the walk down - stopping when you want to. The guide we had through our tour almost ran from the top to the bottom so we would be at the bottom in time for the local cultural folk singing. It would have been much more enjoyable if we had actually had the time to take in the shops, stalls and their offerrings on the way down. Would also have been nice to linger and take in the view. The steps down are many and of varying heights so it is not an easy walk but enjoyable all the same. You could actually spend quite a while here both on the downward and upward journey with stops along the way. The water wheels are interesting as is the working display of preparing hemp for production into woven garments. I agree early morning would be the best time to visit.
사파 지역 소수민족이 사는 마을을 그대로 관광지로 개방한 곳입니다. 그 지역 특유의 수공예품도 팔고, 계단식 논, 그리고 커다란 물레방아를 볼 수 있는 곳입니다. 사파에서 먼 곳까지 트래킹을 하고 싶은 생각이 없으신 분들이라면 근처에서 사파를 맛보기는 가장 좋은 장소가 아닌가 합니다.
หมู่บ้านกั๊ตกั๊ต หรือแคทแคท อยู่ห่างจากเมืองซาปาประมาณ 4-5 กิโลเมตร เดินไปได้ แต่ทางขึ้นๆ ลงๆ จะเหนื่อยก่อนเข้าถึงหมู่บ้าน เสียค่าเข้าชม 50,000 ดอง ประมาณ 75 บาท มีน้ำตกในหมู่บ้านด้วย

The fun, colour and atmosphere of this village is excellent and worth the effort of climbing up and down. We did it early morning and believe it the best time. Not too hot and the mist hadn't rolled in.

We had a nice wander for an hour or so. Not been to any other villages yet so can't compare. The way you walk is lined with shops but we had far less hassle to buy stuff than I expected. If you just have a little time to go somewhere from Sapa I'd recommend it, 15 minutes and you're in the village. The entrance fee is 50k for adults and they check tickets. We got a motorbike taxi back up the hill for 30k.

Es una bonita excursión que conlleva la bajada de más de 600 escalones desde donde te deja el microbus, hasta el río y la cascada. Las casas del poblado son todas tiendas de recuerdos o bares. La cascada, aunque no muy grande, tiene bastante caudal y es bonita. El regreso se hace por la otra punta del poblado a fin de recorrerlo completo y de evitar los 600 peldaños.

サパの中心から西へ２キロほどらしいです。道に案内があるので迷いません。ずっと下りだからなのか、中心部から２キロなのか、街はずれからは２キロと感じず、もっと近く感じました。村の入口で料金を払うと、回る順路が決まっており、迷うことなく現地の空気を満喫する事が出来ます。両側に商店やカフェがある細い石段を下ると、川辺に出ます。ここが一番の見どころかと。そこを過ぎると帰りはひたすら上りです。ここにバイクタクシーが沢山いて客引きをしてきます。ただし、時間に余裕があればゆっくり歩いてサパまで戻ってもそんなに大変ではないかと思います。余談：石段で少年に連れられた牛の集団とすれ違いましたが、その際に歩いてきた一頭の角と衝突、大した速度ではないにも拘らず凄い衝撃で、傍の商店に飛ばされ、体勢を崩して石段に残った自分の足の外踝をその牛に踏まれました。凄く痛かった・・・出血もした・・・でも足の甲だったらもっと痛かったかも。こんなこともありますので、ご注意下さい。

If you want a to visit one of the villages, but need a village that's more convenient due to time restraints, etc.  then it's perfect. I'm sure that you could get a more 'authentic' village, but cat cat gives you a flavour. And as it's a tourist attraction, there is quite a bit to do there. You can have a nice walk around and it's quite big. There are the water attractions and the waterfall and we followed the path to the golden stream, which NO ONE does, as it was completely quiet even though there were loads of people in the village. So we had a lovely hour long walk along the river and then took a path going steeply up the side, which then flattened out along paddle fields and offered lovely views. We enjoyed it and it was a good compromise as we didn't have much time and it's cheap. We arrived around 3pm. Between 12-4 it's very busy and before and after it's quieter.

Se você tiver pouquíssimo tempo em Sapa e puder ir a apenas um lugar, vá ao Cat Cat Village. Você poderá ver os terraços de arroz, visitar instalações das tribos locais e fazer o passeio totalmente sozinho, sem a necessidade de um guia!

We decided to wander down to Cat Cat Village as husband wasn't feeling 100% so any major trekking was off the agenda, also the weather was quite a deterrent for anything too far off the beaten track. A nice walk from Sapa Village and all downhill. The weather was completely changeable and very misty when we first set out. However we were very lucky to have a couple of clear hours in the middle of the day to enjoy the village, inspect the water wheels, drink some ginger tea and take photos. Yes it's a bit contrived, but it's a pretty spot and you get to see a bit of how things are done in the real hill tribes. We weren't hassled at all by anyone trying to sell, just friendly hellos and 'no thanks' when asked to buy anything... and we were left at peace to enjoy the place and the scenery. Possibly not everyones cup of tea if you're up for some hard-core trekking and experiencing 'the real thing'... but we enjoyed it and I would recommend it to anybody wanting a pleasant stroll through the valley. We taxied back up as the journey back to Sapa was all uphill and we chose 'easy'.
Bonito poblado que se ha convertido en un parque temático y de shopping... A pesar de ello vale la pena.

由於為客製化的觀光村落，沿途有許多的商家，讓一開始覺得索然無味，但是在繞過一個山頭後，山後的溪流儼然成為當地孩童的消暑之處，瀑布、水車和竹搭的溪上道路，都值得遊客駐足欣賞與拍照，在入口處會擋住汽機車通行，兩側會有人員指引停車方位，但建議與入口人員溝通，沿著路向下前往道路尾端的停車場與第二售票點(2nd check point)，這樣可以省去最後一大段的上坡路段！門票：50.000/人，機車停車10.000. 建議平日前往，更加清幽！

I was there with my wife when it started to drizzle. A poncho is a must as it rains here periodically. If you are just interested in the terraced fields as your photo backdrop, the first 400 metres of the route will satisfy you and you don't have to bother walking through the 2 hour route. As for me, I was interested to spot as many fauna as possible, so I took 3 hours.
Ở đây được cái mua hàng an tâm, ko bị chặt chém nhiều. Mua hay không mua thì người ta cũng vui vẻ, cảnh đẹp

Très belle promenade d'une matinée à Cat Cat en partant de SaPa à pieds. Le site est très joli mais malheureusement beaucoup trop touristique. Le prix pour rentrer et correct, n'hésitez pas à tenter les petits chemins en terre qui permettent de s'éloigner de l'ambiance touristique pour un instant!

We visited Cat Cat Village on a sunny day. It takes 15 minutes to walk from Sapa center to the village. There is not to much things to learn and explore. You will walk in down the steps and buy sweets for the H'mong kids. It good for their business but bad for the kids. There are so many tourists and shop fronds flogging of souvenirs.

Visited Cat Cat Village as part of the half day trekAs you walk down the steep walkways, you will pass by rows of shops selling all kinds of tourist stuff from T-Shirts, Woven bags and hats to Stone carved boxes and Buffalo horns.Along the way down, there are some places where you can take some nice pictures of the mountains and beautiful flowers.At the bottom where you arrive at the waterfall, there is a beautiful stream where waterwheels show you how the villages had used to harnessed the energy of the river to pound the rice.There is also a wooden bamboo bridge and row boats for you to try as well.As you walk towards the beautiful waterfall, along the sides you will see how the villages make the weaved clothes and other traditional clothing.There is also a cultural theater showcasing traditional music, songs and dance.After which , a long up up hill awaits and can be challenging for those that are less fit...but have no fear, as there are many riders on motorcycles available for hire to bring you back to town without walk up all the way.

Giá vé 50k/người. Đi taxi vào đây giá chung là 200k/2 chiều.Theo mình đây là điểm đáng đi, xứng đáng vs giá vé =))Mua vé xong mọi người sẽ đi bộ vào tham quan bản. Cả đi bộ + chụp ảnh cũng mất khoảng 2 -3 tiếng đó mọi người ạ. Đi vào đến chỗ có cái guồng, thác Tiên Sa í. Mình thấy thác nước chỗ í còn to vs đẹp hơn thác tình yêu =)), đi xong khỏi đi thác ty làm j, đỡ mệt :vĐến đây hầu hết mọi người đều thuê trang phục dân tộc để chụp ảnh, đa dạng lắm luôn, đầu bản đến cuối bản đều bán, tha hồ mà lựa chọn, chụp chán chê quay về trả =))Giá thuê 30k/bộ.Vào bản cũng đông vui lắm, có cả văn nghệ, nhảy sạp các kiểu =))

This tour was arranged as apart of our package through AsiaView Tours and so we had a guide.We enjoyed visiting this village. It is a working village so people live there too but it is also a tourist attraction and so there is a lot of things that are for show. For example how life used to be before technology came to the area, how they used to harvest rice etc. However having said that it is a working village and the locals were planting their rice and corn and they work really hard from such a young age.There is a lot of walking involved which is fine but there are also a lot of stairs that at times are very steep both up and down and very slippery as it was quite wet when we were there. It was also deliciously cool after the heat of Hanoi.There are beautiful waterfalls and clusters of shops selling traditional clothing, medicines etc.We were there a good 2 hours and really enjoyed it.

Hay que hacer la excursión a este poblado porque así se puede ver como viven la gente de allá con sus "problemas" de no tener los lujos que tenemos nosotros.Es un sitio que no se puede imaginar sin verlo. Parece de estar en un otro mundo.
A short walk (3KM) from Sa Pa town, great scenery.  A bit tourisy: lots of tourists and local trying to sell you something.Good place for photo but not the 'real thing' we are looking for.

We walked from our hotel in Sapa using an online map to the Catcat hotel about 20mins from Sapa itself. The View from the road are very good and you are walking on a concrete road all the way.Then just follow the road downhill to Catcat town, on the way expect to be asked by the local women if you want shopping, if you don't just be firm but polite, what ever you say don't say 'maybe' unless you mean it or she will be your best friend for the rest of the day.. At Catcat town you buy your entry ticket and get a map, walk to the left, down steep steps past loads of local stalls all selling much the same stuff. Near the bottom take a right to the waterfall and display water wheels, then follow the path back to town marked 'come back', Motorbikes will take you from the bridge at the end of the walk to the top of the hill for 1usd /20,000dong per person if you don't fancy the walk, it is about 5km mostly uphill. Overall a very nice walk, very scenic and with great photo opportunities.Our phones clocked the walk at 22000 steps about 15miles if you walk the whole way.

It was a nice visit, there is not a whole lot to see but it's still a good walk and scenery. There is a fair amount of uphill walking so be prepared for that! The village is pretty and the stalls were nice, the waterfall is nice too but not sure if it's natural - seemed like there were taps where the water was pouring down from (??). They charge you 50000d per adult to enter, parking is free.

午前中にサパに到着し、サパの町はずれをフラフラ歩いていると、ほんの２０分ほどで着きます（1本道で迷いません）。たまたま混んでいたのか、バイクは駐輪場が混んでいてタクシーも渋滞していました。（歩行者はマスクが必要でした）村の中は傾斜がきつく（主に下りのルートになっています）思ったより広いです。徒歩だったのでそのまま村のはずれまで行き、バイクタクシーでサパまで戻ってきました。村はずれにはバイクタクシーは沢山いますので、あまり手前で決めてしまわず、じっくり値交渉しましょう。バイクを駐輪場に止めると、途中で戻らないといけないかもしれません。バイクルートもあるようですが、サパ中心から徒歩圏内です。翌日はTa Vanまでのトレッキングツアーに参加しました。
