Very nice views of the terraced mountainsides and a nice look at some Hmong tribal culture. There are opportunities to rent tribal dresses. Even if the tribal stuff isnt your thing, the walk down and up the mountainside was a pleasant walk for us and well worth the visit.

Das Dorf ist nur noch für Touristen. Überall auf dem Weg befinden sich Geschäfte. Die Wasserräder und Werkschaften erfüllen keinen Zweck, sind aber schön anzusehen und der Wasserfall ist sehr schön. Hinter dem Café am Wasserfall kann man noch etwas weiterwandern und findet den zugehörigen Flussverlauf, in dem man auch baden kann.

Cat Cat Village is an interesting downhill walk along the road from Sapa (there are no paths until you get into the village itself) with a few good viewing points and bars/cafes nearer the top looking down across the valley. The village is perhaps more touristy than we expected, with local Hmong benefiting from the 50,000 VND pp entrance fee to the village and a number of shops offering souvenirs for sale to supplement their farming incomes. There is a path that takes you on a circuit through and around the village and it's a pleasant way to see the local countryside for the morning if it's dry. The road back up to Sapa is fairrly steep,  but you can haggle with a taxi for a cheap ride up the hill when you're done.

Cat Cat is an old village with a few old houses, local souvenir shops and restaurants. Old watermills and bamboo paths makes photo perfect view.  Easy to reach, takes about 40 min on foot downhill from Sapa. On the way up you can take a taxi or Scooter driver that will cost 3-5 eur. No need to take a guide or buy a tour, you can easily visit it by your self.

Les paysages dans la vallée et au niveau de la cascade avec les moulins à eau sont très beaux.La descente est assez raide avec beaucoup de marches mais elle est surtout bordée de magasins à touristes qui offrent tous la même chose... Cela enlève le charme de l'endroit.Je vous conseille de faire la remontée en scooter ( environ 40 000 VND) car la remontée de 5 km est rude.Attention, l'accès au village est payant!

It's the closest village to Sapa, so it's worth considering on a wet day, the path is paved all the way and if the weather deteriorates, it is easy to get a taxi or a lift on a motorbike back to Sapa. The entrance fee is 50,000 VND per person, in the ticket office you get a map that shows a very scenic 2.5 km loop to follow with all the attractions marked. There are some nice restaurants with beautiful views to stop for lunch too. Touristy and less authentic - yes, but still highly recommended and worth visiting. And you do not need a guide!
nice village on way to the waterfall, good cheap clothing shops and souvenirs and artisan stores as well. lots to look at

We decided to visit Cat Cat tourist village in the afternoon, although this is not really my type of thing because i intend to stay away from tourist sites like this. We managed to find a taxi there for a few dollars but we should have asked him to wait for us or arranged a later pick up as we had to walk the 3 or 4 kms back to Sapa in the drizzle. There was a small entrance fee to pay and we were given a map showing the walking trails. It was possible to make a loop but most tourists were making the short t=hike down to the theater and back up again. During the walk we saw Weavers, stone carvers, silversmiths, the shaman's house and plenty of souvenir stalls. When we reached the bottom of the hill there were some water wheels, a waterfall and a fast flowing river. The best part of the walk was seeing the cultural dancing which takes place in the small theater every hour. This was free but as usual there was a donation box here we dropped a few notes in. Afterwards we slowly continued along the path that led us to the end of the road.This is well worth doing if you have not experienced much of Vietnam.
Tourist setup. Very scenic. Waterwheels. Lots of shops of course. Bamboo platform  local dance performance

Cat cat village jest urokliwa wioska z pieknymi widokami i wodospadem.W wiosce mozna obejrzec popis lokalnych tancow.Wbrew pozorom ceny pamiatek nie sa zbyt wygorowane(kwestia umiejetnego targowania)Miejscowi bardzo sympatyczni,udostepniaja mozliwosc zobaczenie nietypowego sposobu wyrabiania i farbowania tkanin.Na goracy dzien polecam napoj z trzciny cukrowej wyciskany na poczekaniu 15.000dongow

ביקור בקאט קאט הוא מעניין ולמה? הוא כפר תיירותי מאוד. כמה תיירותי? לעבור בו עולה כסף. מהכניסה ולאורך המסלול כולו שפע של חנויות תיירותיות. מביגוד, בתי קפה, מזכרות, עשבי מרפא ומה לא? הנוף ( ביום שאין ערפל מדהים ביופיו. המסלול המובנה חוצה את הכפר, יורד למטה לערוץ הנחל, ועולה לצד השני. סך הכל זה חביב רק מה הכל שם זה תיירותי ולפעמים יותר מדי תיירותי.
Cute little village, very modernised for the tourists. Nice steep walk into it with picturesque views.

반나절 정도 다녀올 만한 곳입니다. 자연도 아름답고 현지인의 삶을 좀 더 자세히 볼 수 있는 곳입니다. 내려갈때는 내리막길이라 걸어서 가고 올라올 때는 오토바이 타고 올라왔어요. 35000VND로 네고해서 올라왔습니다. 내려갈때 삐끼가 따라 붙어서 물건을 샀는데 네고해서 70000VND으로 팔찌를 샀습니다.(한 30분 따라오는데 안사면 계속 따라올 듯 ㅠㅠ) 은색 팔찌는 사지 마세요. 하롱베이에서 수영한번 했더니 노래져서 버렸어요.계단 내려가다 보면 갈래길이 있는데 old village 쪽으로 가보시면 현지인들 그냥 사는 모습 그대로를 보실 수 있습니다.
Beautiful local village <3There is a huge water fall at the end of the village, and lovely stream where local kids come to play before school :)
It would be better if there were balustrade and handrails along the path.  Cat Cat Village does have nice views.

มีของกินของขายตลอดทางทำให้การเดินไม่น่าเบื่อและเมื่อเดินไปถึงน้ำตกก็จะรู้สึกเย็นสบาย หายเหนื่อยแต่ถ้าผู้สูงอายุอาจลำบากเพราะทางเดินไม่เรียบ ทางเดินเป็นแบบบันไดหิน พอพ้นทางเข้ามีร้านกาแฟสามารถนั่งคอยได้ เจ้าของใจดี แม้จะสื่อสารกันไม่ค่อยรู้เรื่องแต่ก็พยายามชวนคุย

The trek down into Cat Cat village was an easy walk from our hotel and it was amazing to see how traditional Vietnamese families used to live, our guide told us that today they cook on gas not an open fire. The waterfall was beautiful and the bamboo bridges lovely to walk across. I do wander how the villagers make any money from what they sell as none of the tourists seemed to stop and buy anything!

The village is within easy reach of Sapa so very touristy but very interesting especially for the children . The steps down into the village are steep and long and not for the elderly or anyone with walking problems. There are wayside activities for children such as swings, rocks to climb and rafts to paddle. There are also good examples of village life.

You can walk to this village from town. It is downhill to the village but is quite a steep long walk back you can get a ride back up to town on the back of a motor bike for $2 Aus or less if you bargain.It was a very cloudy and misty day on our visit so visability down the mountain was bad. The water wheels and waterfall were a great surprise. There were quite a few shops selling wares a little touristy but worth a visit

Ein Ausflug in das Dorf Cat Cat ist auf jeden Fall empfehlenswert. Es ist nicht sehr weit von Sapa entfernt und kann man schön zu Fuß gehen. Es sind sehr viele Touristen unterwegs, was leider ein Nachteil ist. Aber der Wasserfall, der am Ende der Tour zu erwarten ist, ist es eindeutig wert. Hier werden auch von einheimischen die Herstellung von versch. Sachen demonstriert z. B. weben oder Korb flechten.

The village is really close from Sapa, just a short walk of 2 km and you are there. You can go around and see a lot of souvenier shops, and how the locale live, there is nice walk around the village, along the hanging bridge and passing the waterfall,  but compared to other villages, the atmosphere was a bit impersonal, not so authentic. We prefererend Ta Phin so much more

It takes you two hours to walk to Cat Cat Village and come back to Sapa center but you will find the interesting things along the way (streams, steps, giant water wheels, local people in colorful and delicate traditional clothes. Although sometimes, I feel it looks like a little bit commercial place, I am sure you still find, buy somethings fantastic there. Enjoy!!!
เป็นหมู่บ้านเล็ก ๆ ที่น่ารักมากบรรยากาศดีมาก ถ้าเดินตามเสน้ทางจะค่อนข้างไกล ตอนไปซื้อส้มๆจากหมู่บ้านทานสดชื่นมาก

It is one of the places you should visit during your stay in Sapa.it is not far from center but consider you should climb from several stairs and it may be hard for some one.you will have a beautiful view and the live of local people and a small waterfall at end .

Ein Besuch im Cat Cat Village ist ganz sehenswert. Dafür benötigt man keinen Führer. Wir haben das Village an unserm Anreisetag besucht. Leider ist es jedoch sehr touristisch und nicht sehr traditionel.
기대보다는 아주 볼만하고괜찮악어요마을구경에 소수민족보는 코스로많이 힘들지않게 택시 대절해서 다녀왔어요. 가격운 20만동으로 택시비내고 편히 봣어요

Gode della vicinanza con Sapa 3/4km quindi molto accessibile. paghi ( come in tante località una tassa d' ingresso) questi soldi hanno permesso la ristrutturazione di molte costruzioni nel villaggio (alcune riuscite altre meno) e dato la possibilità di un lavoro ad un po' di persone. la visita si svolge a senso unico su un percorso prestabilito fino al fiume dove c'è una cascata e delle ruote in legno che potevano servire come macine o mortai. Comunque nel complesso interessante e piacevole..  .

The walk is hilly and there are many steps. A walking stick is very useful. The villagers offer ,without pressure, to sell you something all along the way. The 11am culture show was difficult to understand but visually reasonable. There are good photo opportunities all the way if the weather is clear.

A nice downhill walk from the town with a waterfall and waterwheels turning at the bottom. There are local H'moung people selling clothes, jewellery etc.  We walked back up to the town and the whole excursion can be done in a few hours. No need to book a tour as you can do it yourself.

Bạn phải mua vé tham quan với mức giá 50k/ ngườiQuãng đường di chuyển khá xa, nên đi giày thể thao, tránh các loại giày cao gótBạn sẽ được tham quan ngôi nhà truyền thống của người dân tộc Mông và mua các sản vật nơi đây như vải dệt, thịt khô, bạc, tinh dầu và tìm hiểu cách thức vận hành của cối giã gạo bằng sức nướcKhu trung tâm của Cát Cát có Thác và các cối giã gạo bằng sức nước. Nước suốt ở đây rất trong, bạn có thể nhìn rõ đáyNên đi vào dịp mùa xuân, thời tiết và phong cảnh rất tuyệt vờid
This is one of the most beautiful villages we've been. Scenery was very beautiful & peaceful with corn fields, rice fields, flowers..Local people was very friendly, you should do it!

Tolles H'mong Dorf, welches sich einfach von sapa aus erreichen lässt. Man kann viel über das traditionelle leben erfahren und hat bei gutem wetter beim abstieg sogar Sicht auf den fansipan. Sehenswert sind auch der wasserfall mit der bambusbrücke im Tal.  Manko sind die Touristenströme.

Cat Cat village is a short (1-2 mile) trail through Sapa that gives you a quick look at the typical village life. First, it is a walk by many small merchant shops lining the descending and slightly steep hill. I recommend picking up a walking pole sold for about 80k Dong as it can be slippery when wet. At the bottom, you will explore about 3-4 huts that show the daily lives of these villagers. Walk another 5 mins paste the giant water wheels and you will see a giant waterfall (photo opt location). It is definitely good to check out this village at some point in your Sapa journey. Preferably in the early afternoon to work out a good appetite for dinner.
Pour aller à cat cat pas besoin de guide . Accessible à partir de sapa à pied , joli a visité , vente de produits du pays , dégustation de sucre de canne ect...

Username: เด็กหญิง น
Comment: เป็นหมู่บ้านที่ไม่ไกลจากตัวเมือง ส่วนใหญ่คนจะซื้อทัวร์เป็น One day trip หรือไม่ก็ขี่มอไซต์ไป แต่เราเลือกวิธีที่จะเดินไปและกลับเอง แอบเหนื่อยแต่สนุก ทางไปก็มีร้านขายของ ร้านกาแฟ คาเฟ่ ตลอดทาง ทางเข้าเสียค่าเข้าแล้วเริ่มตะลุยไปตามเชิงเขา คิดว่าถ้าไม่ได้มาหน้าหนาว คงเห็นตัวหมู่บ้านเขียวๆที่สวยกว่านี้ วันที่เราไป หมอก แอบเยอะไปนิดส์ ช่วงที่เดินไปถึงน้ำตก กับ สะพาน สวยมาก แต่เสียดาย หมอกเยอะ บวกกับอากาศชื้นๆ เดินขึ้นๆลงๆ เหนื่อยเอาการ ถ้าจะพาผู้สูงอายุมาเที่ยวที่นี่อาจจะไม่ค่อยเหมาะเท่าไหร่ เพราะต้องใช้กำลังขาเดินขึ้นๆลงๆ พอสมควร ทางออก จิงๆจะมีมอไซต์สามารถต่อรองราคาให้ไปส่งที่ในตัวเมืองได้ แต่เราเลือกที่จะเดินกลับ เดินไปเรื่อย ชมธรรมชาติ 55 เหนื่อยเอาการแต่สนุก คิดว่าทัวร์ one day ที่ถามราคามา 10$ US  เทียบกับมีคนมารับมาส่งบวกค่าเข้า สมเหตุสมผลดี ไม่แพง

After trekking ta phin village, cat cat pale in comparison. You have to pay and entered and all you see are shops and shops. nothing really special. The view is just so so. It is just too touristy. Forget about going to Cat Cat.

Le village se caractérise par des petites chutes d'eau, un système de récupération d'eau à base de roue à eau, des sentiers de promenade en bambou, un spectacle de danse folklorique et les traditionnelles échoppes d'artisanat Hmong.La route qui va de Sapa à Cat Cat permet d'apprècier le spectacle des rizières en terrasses.

หมู่บ้านม้ง ห่างจากตัวเมืองซาปาประมาณ 6 กิโล ที่นี่มีนาขั้นบันไดที่ใครๆมาซาปาต้องไม่พลาดที่นี่ด้วย ช่วงที่นักท่องเที่ยวจะมาที่นี่คือช่วงเดือนก.ค-ต.ค. จะเป็นช่วงที่ชาวม้งปลูกข้าวเต็มภูเขาเลย เเละอีกช่วงนึงคือหลังการเก็บเกี่ยว จะเข้าสู่ฤดูหนาวที่นี่อากาศจะหนาวเย็น บางปีถ้าอุณหภูมิลดต่ำอาจมีหิมะให้เห็นด้วยนะ ชาวม้งที่นี่สังเกตุเค้าจะต้องปลูกผักสวนครัวไว้ทุกบ้านเลย

หมู่บ้านนี้ได้รับความนิยมเป็นอย่างมาก เนื่องจากนักท่องเที่ยวสามารถเดินมาเที่ยวเองได้จากเมืองซาปา (จะขับมอเตอร์ไซค์มาก็ได้) โดยการเดินทางจะต้องเดินลงเขามาจากตัวเมือง เพื่อเข้าไปเที่ยวชมในหมู่บ้าน และเดินขึ้นเขาเพื่อกลับไปยังที่พัก ใช้แรงในระดับหนึ่งเหมือนกัน

It takes 3-4 hours to wander around Cat Cat village, especially on sunny days as more chance to take beautiful photos. H'mong people here are quite friendly. The nice waterfall and lush green trekking routes make it ideal place to explore

The confusing map meant we almost gave up before even reaching the village (something I think some of the earlier reviewers must have done). Definitely worth the walk for our first view of terraced paddy field and beautifully/ exotically dressed villagers.Even the touristy trinkets were better value than many other places in Northern Vietnam.
It's nice because of its proximity to Sapa, but that also means that it's more "commercial" than the other nearby villages. It makes for a good 2-3 hour walk/wander/exploration.

All I'd heard about Cat Cat was that is was made for tourists and was very busy. Luckily for us the time of year was on our side and it was pleasantly quiet and we could wander round at our own pace. There's some beautiful scenery and I recommend the short trek up to the gold stream for more breathtaking views.
The village is very scenery, nice water fall, nice view. However the kids keep going along and ask to buy things which is irritating

We were on a small tour through the village. To see how these people work and live, is both amazing and sad.  It's basically a farming village where they try to sell items when it's not rice season. The local outfits are unique, shorts and leggings with sandals. Watch out for chicken, pigs, and piles while walking.  It's a good walk to the falls, hope you have a ride back up.

This walk was built for tourists, but we found it very interesting.  There were vendors along the way, but we did not get a hard sell.  There were examples of Hmong homes, culture, and arts.  The waterwheels and waterfalls at the village were interesting as was the dance show.  This took several hours and we found it an interesting look at Hmong cultural group.

The Cat Cat Village, belonging to the bigger tribe - the Black H'Mong, is most organised of all the villages in Sapa. Somehow, it has been commercialised. There are rows of shops for tourists and even organised traditional shows for visitors. Less authentic for those keen to see the "real" villages but a better transition for city dwellers.

We visited Cat Cat Villiage on our 1st afternoon in Sapa, it was quite a pleasant & interesting walk to this village. We were lucky to get a cultural programme at the centre, the traditional dance act was quite interesting. We also got to see the old power generator that was used back in the day.We spent 2 nights here at Sapa. The hotel has really clean and well kept rooms, the view from the side we were staying at was breathtaking.. Just wake up to the mountains & nothing in front to obstruct the view. Plenty of restaurants and shopping places nearby

Located just 3km from Sa Pa central so walking there is an option.When you pay your entry fee you will start walking down the steps and be encouraged by stall holders to buy sweets for the kids.Its good for their business but bad for the kids as the increase in dental issues and diabetes has really become an issue since this village became one of the "go to" places in this area. If you have to buy something then fruit is available.You will walk in a loop, firstly down a lot of stairs with plenty of shops and cafes and things like the water driven plant traditionally used for de-husking and processing rice.Cat Cat waterfall is just below the village and there's a building here where a cultural show is presented regularly throughout the day. From here it's the start of your uphill trek.then up quite a few stairs followed by a long pathway up above the river. After walking over the suspension bridge you'll be offered a lift on the back of a motorbike for 20,000 VND.It's either that or a 1 & 1/2 km uphill slog.Also at this point there is a road to the left that leads to Shin Chai Village. The reason I mention this is that Cat Cat is the "Hollywood" presentation of the local ethnic minority, the Hmong, and the experience feels less authentic because there are so many tourists and shop fronts flogging off souvenirs. If you have the chance then pay the small fee to go to see a real Hmong village and find out what "dirt poor" really means. There have been big changes since I was last at Cat Cat a few years ago with several rice paddies abandoned and a souvenir shop or cafe now located along the path.

De afdaling naar beneden is best stevig door de ongelijke treden. Onderweg overal stalletjes met zelfgemaakte tassen, sjaals, knuffels enz. Je wordt beloond met een geweldig uitzicht. Op het einde van de afdaling een waterval en een theater waar men een dansvoorstelling geeft. Wanneer je weer naar de uitgang loopt is het leuk om achterop de motor je weer naar het begin van het dorp te laten vervoeren.

Very touristic. And a lot of climbing. If you want to save steps and energy, go to exit ( checking point 2 on grab) then visit town then go out from the same entrance / exit. And use 10k for the golf cart. There is sliding down activity …

I came very early so the shops weren't open yet. But I enjoy my time there. I took many pictures. The buildings are nicely decorated and very ethnic. If you're not into the costume, just skip the Hmong dress-up. Dress light, it can get …

Bản Cát Cát là một bản của người dân tộc Hmong nằm cách trung tâm Sapa khoảng 2km . Nơi đây còn lưu giữ nhiều nhà truyền thống . Các nghành nghề làm nông nghiệp , dệt vải chàm chưa bị mai một . Bản làm du lịch giá thăm quan 150k / người .

Cat Cat Tourism Village, Sa Pa is a beautiful place to visit and experience local culture. The village has scenic mountain views, traditional houses, waterfalls, and cultural performances. Walking through the village is enjoyable, and there …

Đây là một khu du lịch ngay trung tâm Sapa, dưới chân núi Fansipan cũng khá đẹp, tuy nhiên cũng không được đầu tư bài bản cho lắm, đa số là các quán xá bán đồ lưu niệm, nhà hàng, cà phê phục vụ cho việc hoá trang chụp hình, ở đây giống một phim trường chụp ảnh hơn là một khu điểm du lịch.

Lối vào chật hẹp, vé vào cổng hơi cao, du khách không nên gửi xe phía ngoài vì di chuyển vào trong rất xa, phía trong có bãi xe ngay cổng soát vé, mua vé ngay ngã tư trước cổng soát vé. Cat Cat Village, Sapa City, Vietnam is a tourist destination for the place of the M'hong Ethnic. However, it's cover by souvenir and food sellers are lined up along the road so the original ethnic's houses are not exposed. …

Bản du lịch Cát Cát nằm sâu dưới thung lũng, trên đường xuống bản mình chọn đường đi qua bản, ở đây 2 bên bày rất nhiều sạp mua bán hàng hóa, cho khách du lịch vừa xuống bản vừa mua sắm. Ở dưới bản có bán đồ ăn, nhưng mình không ăn ở đây, …

Cách Sapa khoảng 3km, bản Cát Cát (Sapa, Lào Cai) là làng dân tộc người Mông, nằm bình yên bên thác nước Cát Cát (thác Tiên Sa) trong thung lũng ngay chân núi Hoàng Liên Sơn với ba bề là núi non hùng vĩ. …

Cát Cát Ngày Mưa Đã không còn bản sắc của người dân tộc nữa, k còn những ngôi nhà dân tộc thay bằng những nhà gỗ đẹp hơn nhiều, chúc mừng cho cuộc sống người dân tộc HMong tốt hơn nhiều.

Rất thú vị. Bạn có thể thưởng thức ẩm thực bản địa, gà đồi nướng... Thưởng thức những điệu múa của người dân tộc Dao, mường... Hoặc thưởng thức giai điệu kèn lá...bên dòng thác chảy. Nước suối chảy thì trong vắt in hình hoa hồng cổ Sapa xuống lòng suối!
Địa điểm ngắm cảnh, khám phá, trải nghiệm nổi tiếng của sapa. Đi vào cuối tuần, ngày lễ chắc chắn sẽ đông đúc chờ đợi, nhưng bù lại các bạn có thể ngắm quang cảnh núi rừng từ đây …
A cute little village in Sapa, Vietnam. But, be ready to face a long trek down the valley. One can also shop for trinkets and stuff and souvenirs here.
Khu du lịch ngắm cảnh thiên nhiên với nhiều nơi chụp hình đẹp. Cảnh sắc bản làng nên thơ.

Khu vực bản đã đc xây dựng phục vụ thương mại nên ko giữ đc nét bản địa như trước. Có khu nhà gỗ gần suối rất đẹp, nhìn cứ như bên Tàu . Đi lên nên thuê xe máy vì rất xa và dốc, lúc đó mệt nên ko đi nổi.
Good for shopping items produced by local ethnic communities & experience a local village

Rebuild last year after big flood. Very nice virus although you have to walk up and down

Ciekawe miejsce, kiedy już przejdziesz przez kilkaset metrów straganów, zaczyna się główna atrakcja, urokliwa wioska pod zrobienie ładnego zdjęcia. Kilka pięknych miejsc, kilka sztucznych wodospadów, przedstawienie taneczne i muzyczne - bardzo dużo chińczyków i hindusów. Miałem wrażenie, że ta atrakcja jest zrobiona właśnie pod nich. Piękne scenerie, mało autentyczności.
전통옷을 입고 사진찍기좋은곳. 그러나 현지인의 생활과는.전혀 거리가 먼곳 관광지100% 한번쯤 갓다오기에 좋은곳 2.3시간이면 짧게방문하니.괜찮습니다

깟깟마을은 소수민족의 삶을 체험한다기보다는 이미 상업화되고 인스타 사진과 민족의상 체험이 주를 이루는 곳이 된듯 하지만 나름 자연의 경치와 건물, 상점들이 잘 어울러져 풍경과 자연을 만끽하기에는 괜찮았었습니다. 입장료는 성인 1인당 150,000동이었고 계단을 따라 내려가다보면 계곡과 폭포등 다양한 포토 스팟들이 있고 물소리를 들으며 음료한잔하는 여유를 느껴보는것도 좋을듯합니다. 하울의 움직이는성은 입장료를 따로 10,000동 내며 입장시 생수한개 공짜로 가져갈 수 있으니 생수도 챙기시기 바랍니다. 그러나 시설 상태는 조금 청소가 필요해 보였습니다. 깟깟마을 내려갈땐 좋았는데 올라갈땐 운동좀 됩니다. ^^ 사파에 오셨다면 잠시 둘러보셔도 좋을듯하며, 둘러보는데는 2시간정도 잡으시면 될듯합니다.

越南最高峰番西邦峰山腳下的夢幻梯田、蒼綠山谷、潺潺流水中，孕育出黑苗族的神秘文化，曾被選為世界最美的30個景點之一，它就是貓貓村，又稱為吉吉村，遺世獨立的村落，你一定要來造訪他。 從售票口往下走，一路長長的下坡階梯，兩旁都是販賣伴手禮的店家，走了約20、30分鐘，就到溪邊，跨過橋之後左手邊就是瀑布，再往前走還有一座橋，跨過橋右手邊還有兩個大水車，風景秀麗，最後再往前走，往上要爬長長的階梯，到了停車場，有接駁車接到大停車場。很值得遊覽喔！

Beautiful village, very typical.It takes around 20minutes walk to arrive in Cat Cat village, close to the river. Air is fresh and atmosphere is relax. If you are in Sapa I strongly recommend to spend at least half a day there.Electric buses can take you to the parking for 10k vnd per person.

August is a highly coveted month for experiencing the breathtaking beauty of Sapa, a charming mountainous area nestled in the northern region of Vietnam. This picturesque paradise boasts of its mesmerizing terraced rice fields, enchanting valley vistas, and rich cultural diversity of its ethnic groups. Despite occasional rainfall, the misty weather adds a mystical touch to the already captivating mountain landscape. Moreover, the drizzle brings respite from the scorching heat, making it the perfect weather for indulging in invigorating hikes.

Tell us, how was your visit?      "My visit was fantastic — friendly staff, clean space, and quick service. I felt welcomed the whole time and will definitely return. Highly recommended to anyone looking for a pleasant and hassle-free experience."

We really enjoyed Cat Cat. At first it seemed to be a lot of locals asking you to buy from them but they were not rude or pushy about any of it and as we walked deeper into the village we really enjoyed all Cat Cat had to offer. There is a lot of touristy things but it is also very clearly a traditional village and the people were very kind.
The views is very amazing the culture are amazing we got more pretty picture. The people here are friendly. The culture of the people are good and amazing.
The cat cat vallage was absolutely amazing. Thier culture, Thier tribe, how they show us Thier way of living. The tour guide Dao she is good guiding us. Definitely we recommend her to our friend

We had a great time, the views were amazing. Cat cat village was beautiful with lots of fun things to see, dancing, shopping, lovely waterfalls.Dao was a great guide and did a good job keeping track of everyone while we wandered around! Would recommend to others!
Village tour is a necessary tour while visiting SaPa, Lao Cai province. Our guide Dao was very polite, shared all the details and clicked our amazing pictures too. Must visit.

I visited Cat Cat Village in Sapa, Vietnam, and absolutely loved the experience! The village is surrounded by lush greenery, beautiful waterfalls, and a sense of calm that comes with the pure mountain breeze.Along the way, you'll find several selfie spots — some of which are chargeable — placed at scenic intervals, making it a perfect place for photos and nature lovers.Keep in mind that visiting Cat Cat Village involves walking down a long path and climbing back up via stairs, so I would not recommend it for those with knee problems, heart conditions, blood pressure issues, or heavy-weight concerns.There’s also a lovely cultural performance for tourists that adds a traditional touch to the visit. I suggest keeping at least 2–3 hours aside to fully enjoy and explore the village.A peaceful and photogenic destination that’s definitely worth the visit!
We really enjoyed the hike and Dao was an amazing guide. It was nice learning about local traditions and crafts, highly recommended
The most beautiful village in Vietnam Amazing landscape with stream and waterfall .. We passed Hmong village to get to centre of Cat cat ..It' so interesting trip !

I truly hate tourist traps and by rights should have hated Cat Cat - yea there are a million stalls trying to sell you stuff all the way down to the village but the village itself was a lovely surprise - we went on a weekday in Feb so may be totally different on weekends and in high season but it’s was very quiet and 80% Vietnamese in national dress come to take photos in a stunning location.I acted photographer for a few couples to allow them to have photos together and they were so appreciative - it’s a very photogenic spot with lots of waterfalls and cool bridges - well worth a visit just ignore the haters.Rob

Beyond amazing! Cat Cat village was the closet to being in a real life fairy tale I’ve ever felt! The tree house as you come in was well worth the very small fee (10k). We have 2 small children (2 & 5) the steps where a bit much for our 2 year old but it was worth carrying him down to the waterfalls, the most magical scenery & a cultural show every hour we where there for 3 of the shows, my 5 year old wanted to watch them over & over & join in with the dancing at the end! We jumped on motorbikes for a very reasonable fee (100k each bike) back to Sapa town to save the kids walking back up all the steps (totally doable by ourselves but would have been too much for the kids). I’m really glad we didn’t read the trip advisor reviews before going to Cat Cat because maybe we wouldn’t have went and would have missed out on seeing one of the most beautiful parts of earth!

Just go! If you're already planning a trip to Sa Pa, then make sure to stop at Cat Cat village. I was a little hesitant because of the negative reviews, but we enjoyed ourselves. I think we spent about three hours here. It has a little bit of a fake Disneyland feel/parody vibe of a real culture(s) but just enjoy it for what it is. A couple of us paid extra for the photo opportunity thing where you climb treehouse like stuff. It was cute and affordable. Plus admission includes a water bottle. We never dined at a restaurant here but we did stop for drinks and fresh coconut at one point. It was kind of hot and dusty that day, and we dipped our feet in the cool river at the bottom and it was very nice. I bought a real leather card holder that I thought was affordable. If you have the time, then maybe it would be fun to rent traditional outfits and get photos taken?Lots of stone stairs fyi! I don't know how accessible it is for the elderly or people in wheelchairs :(

We walked downhill to the village which took an easy 45 minutes from Sapa.  It’s confusing, but look for the ticket office.  It’s easy to miss if you don’t have a guide, but people are all so helpful and nice, you don’t need a guide and may enjoy exploring on your own like we do. There is a lot to see. Waterfalls are amazing.  A scary tower to climb for awesome views and lots of photography going on of people in traditional clothing.  Plenty of great views and hiking trails.  Plan on taking a taxi back to Sapa unless you really like hiking uphill.  Candace is very reasonable and admission tickets are close to nothing.
Although this is a manufactured experience, we had a great experience, visiting at a weekend all the local families came dressed up, what a wonderful array of costumes and a photographers paradise .
The village is clearly a tourist Attraction however it is inserted really well into the landscape which is truly unique. We made the visit on a rainy day and in my opinion made it even more special

During our family’s unforgettable journey to Sa Pa in June 2024, we had the pleasure of crossing the iconic Cầu treo Cát Cát. The panoramic views from the viewpoint were breathtaking; lush terraced fields unfolded before us.Visiting Cat Cat viewpoint in Sa Pa during the third week of June 2024 was an unforgettable experience. The weather was perfect, with just enough sunshine peeking through the clouds to illuminate the verdant rice terraces that Sa Pa is renowned for. The bamboo structure at the viewpoint provided a unique frame to view and capture photos of the stunning mountainous landscape. It’s a serene spot that invites reflection and appreciation for nature’s beauty.As my journey in Sa Pa came to a close, I found myself at this very spot, soaking in every last detail—the cool breeze and tranquility. It was more than just a visual treat; it was a moment that engaged all senses and left me with a profound sense of peace.

Yes it’s a touristy place but it didn’t make it unenjoyable for us anyway. It’s popular for a reason cus it’s pretty so the locals have made money from it and why not. You can easily walk to the start of the village from sapa town, not too far down the bank. Easy to find following the main road out. You buy at ticket at the kiosk just off the road then it’s quite a steep downhill trial but it’s steps all the way and way enough. Took us about 30 minutes maybe. Shops and sellers along the way but didn’t find them pushy at all.When you make it down to the village it’s so pretty, wooden bridges, paths and flowers all around it’s so picturesque. Deep in the valley with green hills all around and the river running though it’s a lovely place. Lots of local tourists having photoshoots in costumes. Busy but easy to find some more peaceful spots. We walked back up which is steep but easy enough taking your time. There are also bikes and taxis that intersect the path in parts of you prefer a lift up. Really enjoyable morning spent here, glad we ignored the negative reviews.

My first suggestion - go with a private tour guide . This will ensure you enough time to go slow and enjoy the terrain without feeling rushed . There are steep walk downhil and uphill involved however it is doable for all ages if you go SLOW . Hence this is where a good tour guide comes in place . I took a tour with “ local mint travel “ group  ( WhatsApp +84 94 925 96 75 ) who then connect with local guides . I did the mistake of going with a group tour which was my fault as I did have an option to go private tour too - kicking myself for it . However my tour guide was equally wonderful and had the patience to keep up with my slow pace . I can recommend my tour guide absolutely however I think all local guides are equally wonderful and patient . This is a beautiful place and the experience to reach cat cat village is a wonderful experience on its own . You walk downhill to the village then through meandering narrow path of steps lined with shops on either side - so magical . There are outlooks overlooking the rice paddies and that’s like from a fairy tale , then you enter the village which is filled with colorful people who rented the local costumes - so ethereal and beautiful. This is not a place to be missed
Its amazing place to visit. But if you need a real nature place, go further village. If you just need to check in, then come to cat cat

It was my first day in Sapa and I was wondering where to go. I read many reviews saying Cat Cat Village is crowded and a tourist trap but I went anyway. Cat Cat village is extremely beautiful and the waterfall is amazing. You should 100% visit if you are in Sapa. Yes it is for tourist, and you are probably one.  Prices are not unreasonable at all! Especially with the number of staff they have on the ground to care for tourists. You usually wouldn’t notice them but thanks to my broken foot, I met a whole bunch of them who helped me and cared for me. Yes, an unfortunate accident happened to me there (totally my fault, I tripped) but at the same time because of it, i am 100% certain that faith in humanity has restored. Thanks to the amazing and caring tourists, Cat Cat Village staffs and every other Vietnamese I met in Sapa. I have never felt so safe in a foreign country.I truly wish to give the Cat Cat village staff a big shout out for taking turns to piggyback me out of Cat Cat Village. I was literally at the lowest part of Cat Cat Village! It is a crazy hike of stairs all the way up. Once at the top, they even took care of me by making sure I got a buggy ride and then a motor ride back out to a main street, got me a taxi to the nearest medical center. They also rejected my tip.. 😭💖 (Even the taxi driver waited for me to be done at the clinic, sent me to have dinner at a restaurant and sent me back to my hotel) 🥹To all the Vietnamese tourists who assisted me to a seat and took a look at my foot, thank you so much. To the American tourist and his tour guide who helped translated for me and activated the Cat Cat Village Staff to make sure I will be taken care of, thank you so much.  Among the chaos of tears and pain, I didn’t manage to get any one’s name. So for anyone who happened to be reading this and helped the solo travelling girl in a white dress with a broken foot by Cat Cat village’s waterfall on 19th May, truly thank you so much. It is a shame my trip here will be cut short. But i will definitely come back in the future!
Village of the Hmoong ethnic group in Sapa. The beautiful scenery is very suitable for walking, learning about Hmong ethnic culture, and taking photos. Natural landscape

This was a unique experience. The village they have built down there is amazing and beautiful and would be an instagrammers dream. They have set up so many different beautiful locations/scenes and themes to take gorgeous photos if that is what you are after. The waterfall was lovely. The walk down there are many small stalls selling local products, and many different small bars and cafes to stop into. We had a beautiful clear and warm day here away from the usual foggy and colder Sapa weather so could see the gorgeous views. There are A LOT of stairs involved, so maybe don’t head down there if you are not good with stairs. Otherwise. Highly recommend you take a look at this individual and quaint little village in sapa.
Beautiful village! We hike for about 2km from sapa towards cat cat village.The only downside is, villagers selling the same stuffs at each of their stalls.

Cat Cat Village in Sapa is a charming destination known for its ethnic Hmong culture and scenic landscapes. Visitors often appreciate the traditional handicrafts, cultural performances, and trekking opportunities. The terraced rice fields and cascading waterfalls add to the picturesque setting. Keep in mind that it can get crowded, but the unique cultural experience and stunning views make it a worthwhile visit.

Everything we read said that Cat Cat Village is very touristy, not authentic, crowded & not worth going to. We say: none of the above is true & we are so glad that we went! If you imagine that his created village is more than just that then you, too, will enjoy it. Yes, you have to first walk through a long line of small scale vendors who sell everything from buffalo jerky (it was quite good!), to silver jewelry, woven bags, belts, blankets, food, drinks, you name it. Once in the valley area, you will see TONS of people, mostly Vietnamese tourists who rent traditional clothes to have their pictures taken alongside one of the waterfalls, on horses, next to one of the many water wheels, near the river...it's all so colorful. We happened to walk into one of the small theaters in time to see dancers practicing their routines as their leader beat out the rhythms & perfected their movements. Suddenly, a local vendor came in with baskets of greens & all the dancers stopped their practice so that they could buy some herbs & veggies for their families. We saw beautiful waterfalls, huge birdhouses, gardens of flowers & fruits trees, people coming & going everywhere, taking in all of the sights. We enjoyed the rural feeling, the stone stairways, trees hugging the hillside, the slow-moving river, & we, too, took one of the best pictures here. A good memory.
Was hesitant to go because other places were rated better, but this place surpassed my expectations. This is a must go for tourists.

Cat Cat village is the oldest village in the Northwest region located right at the foot of the majestic Hoang Lien Son mountain range. The village belongs to San Sa Ho commune, Sapa district, Lao Cai province. Cat Cat village is located 1500 to 1800 meters above sea level, so the climate is cool all year round. This place is the home of the H'Mong ethnic people. Cat Cat village is located very close to Sapa town at a distance of 3 km. It is no coincidence that Cat Cat village is considered the most beautiful ancient village in the Northwest. When you come here, you will witness the simple, rustic wooden roofs located along the clear streams covered by the green of the mountains and forests or discover firsthand the culture of the H'Mong ethnic group through daily living activities.

なかなか良かったです。サパ市街から歩こうと思えば歩ける距離。ただし長い坂道なので、特に帰りはタクシーを拾うことをおすすめします。市街から近く歩いて訪問できる点では貴重な、少数民族のエリア。入場に料金がかかるということからもわかるとおり観光客向けに整備されている感はもちろん否めませんが、谷沿いの家々や遠くにみえる棚田、そして川沿いの雰囲気など、少数民族の方々のくらしが保全・演出されていて安心して歩いて楽しむことができます。川沿いでは（あるいは川の中では）ベトナムの方々が思い思いにポーズをとって写真を撮っており、そんなベトナムらしい雰囲気を楽しむことができました。半日程度以内でさっと少数民族の生活の様子にふれてみたいという場合には、おすすめです。

ベトナムの少数民族の生活をら垣間見れます。こちらは有料観光地なので、とてもきれいに整備されています。フォトスポットも多数有り、写真映えします。この村に、行くまでにレンタル衣装屋さんも沢山あって、そこで衣装をレンタルしてから行きました。とても楽しかったです。谷に向かって降りて行くと、水車小屋や歌やダンスを披露している所があります。また、滝も有り景色はとても美しいく、別世界に来た感じです。サパ駅までの帰り道はとにかく登り道なので、バイクタクシーが沢山、待ち構えています。安いので、それを使いました。アバターの映画の中のような村でした。

Visited Cat Cat Village in the afternoon with a knowledgeable guide. It is a 5km walk to and back. Very touristy with shops on the way catering only for Tourist. However, its natural beauty and scenery is stunning. It's a walk down, full of steps and pathways and fortunately my wife and I were fit and enjoyed the exercise. Entering the village was busy with lots of tourist. Lots of photo opportunity with the waterfalls in the background. Had the opportunity to participate with the locals in a musical show.Well worth the visit and the walk. Take advise from the guide or the hotel if you are unfit.

It's a nice village. You can do trekking from Sapa Town to this village because you'll have a chance to enjoy the great scenery along the way with local houses, terrace -rice fields, locals, streams...But we think the way to go down the village is a bit hard for elderly people.
Dont miss this place if u r in sapa....my words was ** better than heven!!***......sapa is so beautiful....we was there fo only a nice...enjoyed a mong stay resort stay....
Nice village in Sapa for visiting. It is not so far from Sapa and there is a dancing show there. By the way, for elderly it is not for becuase it is hard for walking. It is crowed also

We enjoyed this magical day in Sapa at the Cat Cat Village. It is in an absolutely beautiful and picturesque valley. It is, of course, for tourists, but this was our first trip to Sapa and we thoroughly enjoyed this village, but cannot compare it to the more authentic villages that we hope to visit in the future. I did want to warn though that it is a very steep walk down very uneven stairs and the walk out the other end traverses some very old and rickety wood platforms and a swinging wood bridge. This was not a problem for us, but could be if you are unsteady on your feet. There was a musical program going on when we were there that added to the magical atmosphere. The entrance to start down to the village was a little tricky to find, but we felt it was a very fun and enjoyable experience.

Cat cat village is not so far from Sapa and it is easy to get there with a taxi. We visited cat cat village for about more than 2 hours. You need to walk and not for all. You have to walk for about an hours and it is steep so if your legs are not good and you need to consider.

So great view for visit Cat Cat with natural view. We took more two hour to visit Cat Cat, everything here is so cute. If you visist Spa, should check in @Cat Cat. It is so beautiful place to discovery and enjoy food there.

Amazing place to visit. People are so friendly and amazing. Market stalls are fantastic. This Village has so much to offer. So much more than I could have imagined. A must do when in Sapa. The walk is easy with a few steps and inclines but nothing too much. You could literally spend as little or as long as you like. Markets stall, food, cafes, waterfalls, music, dancing, amazing views of the tiered rice fields, so many ‘selfie’ photo opportunities and  so much more
Beautiful place but the stairs were abit daunting. Worth the trip! Many food choice. Washroom was clean.
Its better than heven!!! I was speechless!!! So so beautiful..wonderful...feel like that i was in onother world...visit there sp in evening time....like it much in my sapa trip

Cat Cat is a village a bit East and a touch South of Sa Pa up near the big mountain. It is so surreal, it most reminds me of Disneyland, except the main attractions are of a natural variety, the goods local whenever possible and environmental impact kept to a minimum. Commercialized about as much as it could be and keep the charm, admittedly, it does seem to achieve it.Stands are myriad along the trail down, but without major lighting nor even much that requires any power at all. Products are a mix of local crafts and delectables with basic anticipated tourist and local's needs filling the gaps. See the pics for the basics. Lots of repeats. Its really the part that most reminds me of Disneyland, these stands with sellers about.The trail down is steep. And stone. Strong legs are helpful. Wear something with good traction on stone and expect it to get wet at some point without care. Its a nice walk taken as a tour. We paid to get up onto the “Love Nest”. A beautiful piece of art with the motif of hearts that you'll see everywhere here. Seems purposeful and a perfect branding for Sa Pa in general. And it'll never sink like Venice. 🙂 The “Nest” is three stories and made for picture taking. What Amerikans would call a “Vista Point”. People from all over take pics of themselves and others and just the view, terraces included.Many here like to dress up for the occasion in local attire. I see Vietnamese from all over the country dressed for the day trip (Mostly a girl thing) and hard to blame them. Its beautiful. Back in the states many emulate the style as well, most never knowing where it came from. There's stores and stands all about that specialize in it.Keep walking down and there's more food, less clothes. Guess people get hungry after some walking. Lots of places to sit and order something. Lots of buffalo-to-go jerky. They seem to have a knack for it and sell it in various flavours. Lots of foods half-familiar. Never seen that many colors of sticky rice before and I eat it daily. The ways they use bamboo to cook I'd never seen before either. Found a restaurant at the bottom with Horse. I was game, but the tour group was bound upstairs for chicken. Foods seemed basic from local ingredients. Not fancy, but likely more healthy than most. We all enjoyed it. Roasted moist and tender. Lots of local meat about. Chicken, pork, goat and buffalo seemed to be their mainstays and you'd see them farming them locally. Everything was so fresh.Mind, back in Sa Pa, they do have about anything if you're hankerin' for something not local. The last thing in the world I'd expected to find, much less eat here, was seafood, yet we got stoked by the hotel manager who whipped out a stash of the finest roasted tuna I've ever had and followed it up with salmon so fresh, we wrapped it up in rice paper raw with local veggies for what I can only call “Vietnamese Sushi”. Something I hadn't even known existed. Mighta been the best meal of my life. But I digress.Reaching the bottom of the canyon, you enter a paradise. Again, commercialized, but not with the metallic hands of Disney or some corporation, but very carefully places so as not to adversely effect the natural environment. All wood exterior on the restaurants. Huge water wheels more out of artisitic than pragmatic need. Old buildings redone. A river down the middle, wide, shallow and slow. People waling about in it playing in the water, taking pics and just enjoying the place. Here, it is particularly nice with all the girls enjoying looking pretty in their local clothes brought for the occasion. Their beauty matching the natural environment so perfectly.From here, there's plenty to explore. The trail going up the river goes a good ways along a gorgeous little canyon with lots of spots to stop and cool as you go. Could spend a day there easy.Eventually though, its back up the hill. Cheat and you can skip the largest part of it, grab a moto at the crossroad that can take you back to the top or straight back to your motel.Cat Cat was incredible. I would strongly suggest it to anyone coming to Vietnam.

Con đường đến với Cát Cát khá đẹp, bạn sẽ được đi qua những đoạn đường cua tay áo uốn lượn mà hai bên là những thửa ruộng bậc thang cùng những ngôi nhà nhỏ lấp ló. Khi đi bộ xuống bản, qua đoạn đường dốc lát bê tông sẽ tới những bậc thang lát đá. Tiếp tục đi qua cây cầu Si , du khách sẽ tới trung tâm Cát Cát, nơi hội tụ 3 dòng suối: suối Tiên Sa, suối Vàng và suối Bạc.
Bản catcat cách trung tâm tầm 2km. Vé vào cổng giá 80,000 tại thời điểm mình đi. Nói chung bản xây dựng mô hình theo kiểu check in cho giới trẻ. Nên đi

Cat cat village offered a stunning view to the mountain. We have the chance to visit one of the local families who were making handcrafts. We visited the house they stay and the conditions inside the house is not good although the space is well organized. Most of the villagers are poor and they are selling handcrafts to earn their living. The village is growing and connected to the other part which has a river crossed. The view is almost similar to the south western part of China where those aboriginal live. Although they are artificial, they really presented the local culture to the world.

Cat cat village is the must visited place if you are in Sapa. From the lovely waterfall to the gardens which you'll see on most of your ways everything so close to the nature and makes you feel so grounded.

Fantastic place! Stunning views! Loved it here on a clear afternoon in winter. Lots of steps and a beautiful, happy cultural performance at the waterfall. You can buy some beautiful local handicraft we didn’t see in other places. We have the free cultural performance a tip in the box but didn’t see other tourists do this. Maybe they didn’t see the box. Performers looked so happy and proud. Thank you for opening your beautiful village to us.

קט קט נמצאת במרחק 15 ד- מסאפה.אתר מדהים עם נוף מטריף,שביל לא קל אלא שהפיצוי שנקבל בדמות נופים עוצרי נשימה והכרות את חיי שבט המונג.למרות שהכפר הפך,מזה זמן רב,לממוסחר מדי הביקור עדיין מדיף ריח אותנטיות. הבגדים שהמקומיים שוכרים בזמן הביקור בצבעים מרהיבים מביאים אותך להתחרט שלא שכרת גם אתה.הירידה מעט קשה והעלייה חזרה קשה לכאלו שאינם מיומנים בהליכה אבל המאמץ שווה.חייבים לבקר,לא לפספס.....

The scenery there is breathtaking. Impressive! At first we were wondering should we pay for the entrance fees as we probably will explore the culture by walking around the nearby area. We had made the right decision, is worth of value.

Great little village not far from sapa centre. Got a taxi there. Entry is cheap 70,000vnd. Lots of  stairs down to village passing heaps of little stalls. Slightly touristy, but not pushy.  If you're able walk down to waterfall area. Lots of great photo opportunities and little cafes with fresh bbq good as well. Take at least 3 hours here. Once you make your way back up from waterfall, take a tandem bike ride back into sapa town. Only 100000vnd ($6-$7au). Honestly a great 10 minute ride that saves hundreds of stairs and offers great views of this gorgeous place.

Best way to see this place is to get a taxi to drop you there and then pick you up later in afternoon. You will need plenty of time i you want to really experience everything and sample some of the local food. I think easily you should allow 5 Hours or more.

Tuy đã bị bê tông hoá, nhưng vẫn mang một vẻ đẹp rất riêng của núi rừng Tây Bắc. Mặc dù có một điểm trừ là đường đi dốc, làm mỏi chân nhưng bù lại được chiêm ngưỡng cảnh sắc nơi đây, được gặp gỡ những bạn nhỏ trong Bản thì thật là thú vị!

Visited yesterday and have to say it was the highlight of our trip to Sa Pa - if not the best thing we have done since arriving in Vietnam some 12 days ago. Astoundingly beautiful scenery. A quite easy and gentle walk downhill from Sa Pa town. Once in Cat Cat Village itself there are loads of craft stalls, small cafe/bars etc., the centre of the village with its waterfalls and terraces is breathtaking. There is even a local show put on with dancers and musicians staged right in front of the main waterfalls. Everywhere you look there is another photo opportunity. Although the walk down is ok I must say that for me personally the walk back up would have been challenging to say the least ! So I bottled it and got a motorbike ride back up for 100,000 VD - about £3- well worth it.
We had a great trip. Your tour guide has led us to an interesting place. We experience the folk activities. The scenery is very beautiful, we had great photos. I am very satisfied with this trip.

Highly recommend renting the local costumes before going. I joined one of those 2 days 1 night trekking tour and went to Cat Cat Village on Day 1.  I noticed that many Vietnamese tourists were dressed in local costumes and the place is excellent for photo shooting especially if dressed in the traditional clothings. I was very regret that I didn’t know this earlier and hence would like to remind all who is planning to go. Don’t miss it!!
Nice place to hike, the waterfall was beautiful and the place was well kept. It was a crowded place with tourist and locals taking photos. Still 5 stars.
我们在这里看到了白色的水牛，美丽的玫瑰。看黑苗族跳舞，织布，整个村子我们花了2小时走完。门票70千越币不算贵。有美丽的梯田，大自然的风景，瀑布＋上新鲜的客气😘

Spend all day at this attraction. It is like a Cultural Theme Park in a beautiful natural setting. Go for the whole day, eat there, climb up and down all the stairs, hangout in a hammock, visit the theatre, watch the show, have lunch, take lots of pictures and throughly enjoy the scenery complete with running river and a huge waterfall right in the middle of the Village. It is a must-visit attraction while you are in Sapa.

Don’t go on a tour -we got a taxi that dropped us at the entry and picked us up at the end. It cost. $7 each way. You then walk down a path after paying a cheap entry fee and pass many many stores of the cat cat village people. The scenery is beautiful as is the bottom of the valley. Can’t say I enjoyed walking up the mountain track but lucky the taxi was waiting half way up. It was fantastic and you don’t need a tour todo it.

The walk from our hotel in Sapa town to Cat Cat village is 3.7 km downhill. The return journey of a climb of more than 1000 ft gives a very good workout. Had a coffee break at a cafe with a splendid view.
Bản cát cát này rất đẹpĐịa điểm thích hợp cho người việt là chínhDân tộc ở đây đều là người H'MongGiá vé vào cổng là 70k/ người
Interesting to see how the local ethic tribe lives. There are many souvenirs shops lined the trail. You can rent their ethic costumes and walk the trail.
穿過樹林斜坡階梯一路走下去, 遠遠望去, 有如走進一座山谷中的世外桃園, 山寨沿溪谷而蓋, 有小橋穿越溪谷與大小瀑布. 可靜靜坐下來欣賞或沿溪谷來回走走. 到Sapa 別忘了去這個地方.

Очень аутентичное место, красиво. Много туристов (даже несмотря на то, что во время нашего посещения стоял плотный туман). Плата за вход символичная, есть кафешки, сувениры. Но главное - природа и местное население

Cat Cat cultural village belongs to some ethnic groups living in mountainous areas of northern Vietnam, especially Black H’Mong.when i visited North Vietnam last year in Nov/Dec.,i visited Sapa two days,and joined an half day tour.cat cat village very close to Sapa center,we walked to this village.some said it is too tourism,but i think it is okay,no problem,the villagers need money too.so visit here is to support them.you can have a glimpse into the daily life of local ethnic people here, as well as take part in traditional activities such as weaving fabric. It is also a good opportunity to purchase some  souvenirs and handicrafts from the craftsmen.i bought a leather wallet,which is a nice product,not expensive for me.there is a small waterfall inside,many people taking pics here.the scene is beautiful.before leaving here, I watched a minority people dance show,impressive! a nice trekking experience.if you stay in Sapa town,do come here and have a look.

หมู่บ้านCat Cat อยู่ไม่ไกลจากเมือง สามารถเดินไปได้ (ทางเดินลาดชันเป็นเขา)​ หากร่างกายฟิตพอ เมื่อถึงหน้าหมู่บ้านแล้วต้องชำระค่าเข้าก่อน จากนั้นจะผ่านร้านค้าชาวเขา หากต้องการซื้องานแฮนด์เมดให้เดินลึกเข้าไปค่ะจะมีชาวม้งคอยแนะนำ จากนั้นจะถึงน้ำตกซึ่งสวยงามมากค่ะ แต่ระหว่างทางเดินมาน้ำตก เค้าจะมีสถานที่ให้พักถ่ายรูป 2 แห่งค่ะ
The village is located at a walking distance to the town centre and provides an excellent opportunity for interacting with the local Mong hill tribe
Wonderful day out in Cat Cat with a free traditional show. Definitely a place to visit. The staircases are steep and gets you at times out of breath :)

So this is really the Vietnam Disney world. It’s how the South Koreans and Japanese imagine Vietnam. They all get dressed up in the local national dress and parade around the village posing for pictures. We saw the included show which was sweet. Plenty of places to grab a meal.

Leuk dorpje maar heel toeristisch. Overal zijn er Aziaten een fotoshoot aan t houden in traditionele kleding. Je kan een rondje lopen van ongeveer anderhalf uur rond het dorp. Daar kwamen we gek genoeg weer bijna niemand tegen. Loop dus snel door het dorp naar de wandelroute er omheen en geniet van het schitterende uitzicht.

No matter what others think about this place, me n my gf enjoyed our lunch there beside the stream.  Waterfall are amazing.  It was just 5 mins drive from our hotel.  Not a bad place though. :D

Lovely village. Nice walk around the small shops and waterfall, stop for some tea, sweet potatos and corn. Lots of places to sit and enjoy the view.

I and my friends have been here for the very first time. It took us some unforgotten memories at Cat Cat Village, Sapa, Lao Cai. I was being fell in love with the natural landscapes, the atmosphere, and nice people. We had some souvenir photos that keep us with this.

Je moet vroeg gaan, dan merk je echt niet veel van het toerisme. Wij waren er voor 9 uur en toen was het super leuk. Veel te zien, misschien wel niet meer zo authentiek, maar je kijkt er wel je ogen uit.
