כפר נחמד קרוב לסאפה, מי שלא רוצה להתרחק יותר מדי מהעיר ניתן לעשות טיול של כמה שעות לקאטקאט, איזור יפה מאוד.
Great walk lots to see on the way - like a living museum really.  Great dancing show included in price and waterfall spectacular

Địa điểm đẹp, phong phú. Không khí trong lành. Đi hơi mệt nhưng xứng đáng để đi xuống đó. Khi đến đây không mang giày cai gót. Nên đi giày thể thao, hoặc dép lê. Không nên cho tiền hay bánh kẹo trẻ em trong bản, bởi vì khiến trẻ không muốn đến trường.

There are quite a few villages you can visit when you are in Sapa.  Cat Cat Village I would personally recommend, because You can easily spent a couple of hours walking and seeing amazing scenic and also It’s close to Sapa downtown (it cost around 100K for a taxi ride each way)The only thing you need to prepare yourself is a few miles of walking up and down the stairs and it is worth the effort.

Het is een flinke afdaling, lopend vanaf Sapa (zeker met kleine kinderen), maar het is de wandeling dubbel en dwars waard. Als je helemaal beneden aankomt kan je heerlijk pootje baden in het riviertje of bij de kleine waterval. Voor een klein bedrag brengen de locals je met een scooter weer terug naar Sapa. Echt een aanrader!

It's location makes this old fashion village pretty convenient to visit, and the local know it. Going in car would take you not even five minutes from where the descend to the village starts, and easy troll down hill not recommendable to take on your way back to town. Don't know how difficult might be to hire a car/motorcycle to go back, so plan ahead coordinating with your hotel. Your can walk back to town or arrange for your transport to wait for you when they dropped you. To do it will require to go all the way up to the entrance and walk down the road where all the vehicles park (the road is pretty narrow with no parking allow it).This village is an old fashion one kept that way for touristic purposes but still in operation in some extend. The village have restaurants, restrooms, and more shops among its beautiful old fashion features.Use comfortable shoes, umbrellas,  and a bottle of water expecting to buy more on the way for sure. It's a relaxing full day experience, so don't hurry planning other activities that would ruin the mood this lay back place will invite you to embrace. Eating there and relaxing after is something you must do, giving you time to get ready for your way back to reality from this nice trip to the past!!

Ci siamo recati in visita ( da soli) in questo villaggio a pichi metri dal centro di Sapa. Ingresso a pagamento, ma ne vale assolutamente la pena. Villaggio caratteristico, con tanti negozietti carini dove fare shopping. Presenza di spettacoli ad orari definiti.

Cat Cat village is very near Sapa Center. You can go there on foot or rent a motorbike when you are a little bit further. The entrance price was 70,000 and was worth it. In the first part, you have a lot of shops (a bitt to many shops). But the more you decent the more amazing it gets. Along the way down, you have an amazing view of the valley and there are enough spots to get a drink or a nice shot The nicest part is al the way down by the river and waterfall. The whole tour takes around half a day and has some steep steps. You need to be able to do a lot of steps and be prepared for a hike. I was able to fly my drone (DJI Spark) along the way at several spots and also did a  short fly inside the village. All without any problem.

This place is like 1km from Sapa, you need to pay an entrance fee of 70.000 VND but it is worth it. You need to walk a lot of stairs in order to visit this small town but it is very beautiful, full of small stores with Hmong and Vietnamese hand made products. There is also a beautiful garden and cascade. Honestly if I would have seen this place from day one I would have avoid Bac Ha market, which is 2 1/2 hours from Sapa and you need to take a bus or rent a car.
Đến với bản Cát Cát - Sapa nhất định phải thuê trang phục người dân tộc và đi khắp bản để sống ảo nhé 💪💪💪Giá vé vào bản Cát Cát : 70k Giá thuê đồ : 50k ( bao gồm váy, nón và luôn cả ô )

Ne manquer absolument pas ce rendez-vous! Il faut descendre à pied de Sapa jusqu'à ce menu village, mais arrivé, on se croirai dans les films de guerre américano-vietnamienne, c'est poignant et superbe à la foie, on se remémore les scène les plus dure de la guerre mais franchement que c'est beau et authentique, rien que de repenser à cela, je ressent de la joie par la beauté de ces paysage, et de la tristesse de savoir le malheur qui s'est abattu de ce coin du vietnam.
Ok this place is designed for tourists, so you are not really getting away from it all.  However easy to get too from Sapa (although lots of steps to negotiate) and some great photo opportunities.
כפר קטן וחמוד שנמצא בסאפא. יורדים במדרגות ועוברים דרך הבתים של המקומיים. הם מציעים המון מוצרים למכירה. בסוף המסלול היתה הופעת פולקלור של בני המקום. מקסים

It is located on the Muong Hoa Valley which is the village of the H’Mong hill tribe. It was only 300 mtrs away from our hotel. The village was on the slope of the hill and easy walking, rice field and corn were grown here. The village was rich with its distinctive customs and practices which have been lost in almost ethnic villages. We visited a big waterfall on the stream and the old Hydro Electric Power Station built by the French here. Took us almost 3 hours exploring the enchanted village and it's people. The entrance fee was 70k vnd. There were a lot of small stalls through the valley selling souvenir and their unique clothing. We have to step on steep stairs and could be quite slippery. We had a brief stop at one of the rooftop cafe and had a pancake and coconut water. The road leading to Cat Cat Village was an easy and interesting route for easy trekking. The spot was only within 20-30m minutes walking from Sapa center and the hike through mountains took us to the wonderful picture of rice paddies, water fall, or even water buffaloes grazing leisurely. Both of us were impressed by the peacefully picturesque scenery. We caught the sight of women sitting in front of the loom with colorful pieces of brocade, while children playing around with pet

Beautiful village! A bucket list item since I have been visiting the country for the past decade. It was everything I imagined and more. Beautiful people, customs, nature, and overall visit. Please tourists, keep it clean and untainted!

You have to take a walking tour through the villages if you go to Sapa they are amazing and the rice fields breathtaking. Even though it rained we still did it and it was eerie but still beautiful. It’s a long hike but mostly downhill and well worth it.
Cat cat is so beautiful , the local people is very friendly and helpful. I learnt very much about culture of H'mong people. Very nice place !

山間にある少数民族の集落です。入り口手前のチケット売り場で入場券を購入して、入り口でチェックを受けます。村の中は起伏がありますが、入場料を徴収するだけあって、それなりに道や娯楽施設が整っています。お土産屋さんや食べ物屋さんのある路地を抜けると轟音を響かせる滝があるのでそこへ向かって谷を降ります。滝の前はちょっとした劇場があり、定期的にここに暮らす少数民族である黒モン族の伝統舞踏や演奏を見る事ができました。周囲では竹籠を編んだり、糸車を回す人など、集落の伝統的な暮らしぶりを紹介するコーナーもありました。お花が綺麗に植えられていたり、観光地化されていて、まるで日光江戸村みたいですが、少数民族の暮らしを覗くにはとてもいい場所です。お土産もじっくり探すと本当に素晴らしい物に出会えます。アップダウンが激しいので、できれば運動靴かトレッキングシューズで行くといいと思います。

We took local taxi to village then walked down through shops to the gardens.. selfies being taken 😃We then walked the road a mistake 🙀 it’s much better to go down the steps than walk back up them. The village along the river was the highlight amazing bamboo structures. We were saved by the motorbike taxis who took us all back to the village ticket office and our taxi home😎

Cat cat village is a great starting point to begin your Sa Pa Valley Hikes.  It is an easy 1.5 hr to 2 hr circle hike to take in the scenery.  The waterfalls are cool to walk over via suspension bridge and it gives you a taste of the rest of the amazing sites, sounds and experiences of Sa Pa Valley.

Un petit parcours est prévu pour visiter le village à 70k. Certes des shop sont présents tout le long, mais aucune pression des vendeurs. La promenade est sereine et les spot très joli avec des ponts en bois. À faire sans problèmes en toute autonomie, sans guide etc.
Nơi để bạn có thể trải nghiệm tour trekking. Mất khoảng 2 tiếng để đi thăm quan hết làng Cát Cát. Thanks
Be ready for a long walk,  but the scenery is just amazing!; it is worth every step of the walk, the people is very friendly and a good taste of the vietnamese culture and food.

We had a great  time for trekking around Cat Cat village, it was located 2 km from Sapa town and we spent 15mins for walking and would love the waterfalls, village, people here and the senery was spectacular !
หมู่บ้านชาวเขาบริหารจัดการดี ห้องน้ำสะอาด ซื้อตั๋วเข้าไปชมหมู่บ้าน มีการแสดงให้ดู  แต่การแสดงยาวไปหน่อย สี่สิบนาที น่าจะตัดเหลือครึ่งนึง . เดินกันไปจากโรงแรม ขากลับจะมีคิวมอไซ แล้วแต่จะตกลงราคา

Tôi và gia đình đi Sapa, ghé ngang qua Bản Cát Cát, rồi sau đó thuê những bộ đồ dân tộc mang vào xinh lắm. Bản Cát cát như 1 bức tranh vậy, khung cảnh hài hoà, con người thân thiện, rừng núi, nhà cửa, cây cỏ , mọi thứ trở nên đẹp hơn nhờ những con người ngày ngày chăm sóc nó. Thật sự rất thích , đây là chuyến đi tuyệt vời nhất của tôi 🥰🥰🥰
It was a remarkable village, lots of stairs and wonderful little shops. Stairs are uneven so a walking stick is useful.

The walk down to Cat Cat is lined with Souvenir Shops etc.  It is very touristy but fun.  Once down there is a small theater with dance, lots of scenery, and fun. Long hard climb back up, but worth it.

A very nice and welcoming village we enjoyed it very much. We used the service of a local tour guide her name is Chai Lythi and she is wonderful and very recommended in the area of Sapa and villages around.

We visited here when we first arrived in Sapa, we walked from Sapa town and really enjoyed the village. We were a little hesitant based on the reviews but we had a really great time. We went around 4:30 so it was pretty quiet which was nice, the sun was just setting and the town is really beautiful. There’s lots of little walkways and a small waterfall at the end. The big wheels turning are really cool, there’s hammocks to relax and a stream running through the town. With it only being a short distance from Sapa there’s really no reason not to go. Might just be worth not going in the middle of the day when it’s busy

כפר קטן, לא רחוק מסאפה, שמור יחסית לכפרים האחרים שראינו, בכפר שבילים בנויים והרבה מדרגות, החיים בכפר בצידי השביל, בתי המלאכה, בתי המגורים, ובתי האוכל, העבודות החקלאיות  והנועה לכיוון המפגש עם מפל המים, גשרי העץ הבנויים, עצי המשאלות ושיטת הדיג בסלסלאות. יפה יפה

I came to sapa not sure what to expect. I had read mixed reviews so I thought I would share my experience so others can see it what I saw. The morning was SO densely foggy I could see almost nothing. There was thunder and lightning and a MASSIVE torrential downpour, which led me to retreat to a shopping area inside a large hotel. I was able to purchase a poncho and waterproof shoe covers for my feet and continue on my way. The hill tribe people around town would approach me and say ‘hello where you from? How old are you? What’s you name?’ And I would politely reply. Then they would ask if I wanted to buy some souvenirs from them. Most of the time you just politely decline and then walk away. On a few occasions, though, they would follow me and continue to try to sell me things which was somewhat annoying. I did, however, end up meeting a lovely girl who walked with me through town for nearly 2 hours. We talked about her life in her village, my life back home, etc. Her English was impeccable and she was just genuinely friendly and didn’t ask me to buy anything. After our walk I asked for her to show me some things and decided to buy a beautiful hand embroidered scarf from her. She was not pushy and we parted amicably. Later on I went out for lunch and met Bao, another Hmong lady, who followed me to lunch and promised to take me on a tour of cat cat village. I was skeptical at first but she seemed very friendly and I decided why not. She walked with me, holding my hand when the road was rough (I have a broken ankle so that was very kind of her) and warning me when trucks and cars were coming so I wouldn’t get hit. She showed me how to make indigo dye using plants (my palms are still blue from it 😂) and then helped me purchase my entrance ticket to the village. She then took me around, showing me places most tourists don’t get to see, then we sat by the waterfall and ate fresh bananas from a tree. She took many pictures of me which was kind of her, and then we hiked all the way back up the hill together. When I offered to pay her, she gave me a beautiful handmade silver necklace and a small purse and thanked me. I know I’ve read that it’s ‘best’ to go with a registered tour company, but I felt this was a very authentic and unique experience I will never forget. We shared laughs and she taught me how to say various words in her language, she taught me about her customs and things I would not likely have learned in a larger group tour. We shared a day together that was unforgettable and I can’t express how wonderful that experience was. The sun came out for our whole trek, making it a very pleasing day by all accounts. If you get the chance to go to sapa and only have a short time, I hope you will find someone like Bao to take you around. She really made my visit special and unforgettable. Tip-the more earrings they have, the more important they are, so they’re less likely to pester you about buying things and are more easily able to get you in places because they’re seen as highly regarded people amongst the hill tribe folks.

If you come to SaPa you should come to this place be cause it is very beautiful water fall and beautiful garden make you feeling like you on the paradise, take some food sitting near the water fall nice music plus the sounds of the water drop from up to down is make you very happy!

A must-see! Such simplicity and beauty. You can gain insight on how the Cat Cat people live and produce their indigo garments, we loved it. The path to get there is quite steep, with lots of stairs, but it is worth the effort.
Love this place.  So peaceful and a hidden gem.  After all the walking you will come to a very scenic waterfall.  Worth the walk.
We found this village just delightful. Little shops, gardens, waterfalls, dancing... it all made for a beautiful and enjoyable day. The walk itself wasn’t to strenuous either.
amazing to see, don't miss the hike and the people. lots to buy and experience a unique and ancient culture

Wir sind mit dem Roller dort hin gefahren, was sich als gute Entscheidung heraus stellte. Da es doch sehr bergig und steil ist. (Der Rückweg wäre sehr anstrengend geworden.) Man kommt problemlos mit dem Roller dort hin. (parken kostet 10000VD) Der Eintritt ins Dorf kostet 70000VD also alles sehr günstig. Es ist alles sehr beeindruckend wie die Menschen dort leben. Sehr interessant

Quay lại Cát Cát sau nhiều năm, tôi thật sự thấy ngợp khi lần ghê thăm này, Cát Cát thay đổi khá nhiều và hoành tráng hơn vào 5 năm trc đây. Một diện mạo mới, khung cảnh đẹp hơn và được dân bản bố trí khung cảnh rất thiên nhiên, tôi và gia đình đã có những giây phút rất tuyệt vời ở đây.
Although it is a bit of a tourist trap, it is a great wall through, with some beautiful places to take photos. Local people are also friendly
We were warned about the stairs, it is a hard slog down but worth it. Lovely scenery at the bottom. Also a small theatre which was entertaining. Worth the walk if your knees / legs are up to it.
Tuy có thay đổi khá nhiều theo thời gian nhưng mình thấy nơi đây vẫn là một điểm đáng đặt chân đến khi thăm quan sapa, hy vọng theo thời gian mọi thứ ko bị phai nhòa đi.

Did an organised trek to Cat Cat. 3 km from Sapa, lots of steps down to the village passing all the little eating shops, taking in the sights and sounds, plenty of souvenirs. Many people dressed in traditional costume on the way down. Beautiful at the waterfall and there is a small show to watch. If you have knee problems the climb down and up may be a problem. Well worth the trek.
We nearly didn’t go as we have been to other Black H’mong villages on our trip,this would have been a big mistake.Cat Cat village is stunning,we walked down and got a scooter Taxi back up.

Были с детьми в дождливый день. И несмотря на погоду - шёл проливной ливень - нам очень и очень понравилось!!! Народу практически не было:) так красиво вокруг! Зрелищно и интересно. Если дождь - резиновые сапоги возьмите в отеле;)

We went to visit Cat Cat village in the afternoon, the view is so beautiful and our tourguide is so knowledgeable and friendly. He escorted us from beginning till the end, told us everything about the culture, people in the village. His name is Ma A So, thank you so much for a wonderful trip to beautiful village. Highly recommended

Lots to occupy here, including water activities, while you catch your breath for the climb up out of the valley. The water wheels still functioning. It would be interesting to see the falls after the monsoon
Such a beautiful little village, ferl like you are in a fairytale. Definitely worth a visit,. Do the hike there through the jungle
Villaggio molto caratteristico a Sapa City. Ingresso a pagamento, ma ne vale la pena. Un sacco di scalini (in disceso e al ritorno i salita), per raggiungere il villaggio. Siate pronti a sudare!
A must visit when in this area. The scenery in these mountains is sublime and the villages full of interest.
J'hésitais à faire la visite car j'avais lu que les locaux étaient un peu 'collant' mais ça allait. Très mignon et pas loin de Saoa

Guidé par Trung d'Asiatica travel à Hanoï,moi et mes amis, nous avons  passé une bonne matinée à ce village. Le Cat Cat Village est une belle balade agréable à Sapa, nous avons l'impression de vivre au milieu de la nature,  c'était une bonne façon de passer une matinée tranquille.

Μόλις 2 - 3 χλμ από την Σάπα, μπορείτε να κατεβείτε με τα πόδια και να ανεβείτε με κάποιο σκούτερ.Το χωριουδάκι είναι πολύ τουριστικό, πρακτικά υπάρχει και συντηρείται από και για τους τουρίστες, αλλά αυτό δεν σημαίνει ότι στερείται ομορφιάς. Κυριολεκτικά κάθε δέκα μέτρα είναι και μια ευκαιρία για μια φωτογραφία!Έχει ακόμα και δύο τρία μικρά καφέ για να ξαποστάσετε λίγο από τον ποδαρόδρομο.Αν έχετε τον χρόνο μπορείτε να ξεφύγετε λίγο και να πάτε προς τα λιβάδια της κοιλάδας!
Có lẽ Cát Cát chính là cái mộc nhất của Sapa. Thuê 1 bộ váy của dân tộc Mường Hoa, tung tăng cầm ô đi dọc theo những con đường, gặp các em nhỏ vùng cao. Đều là những trải nghiệm đáng nhớ với mình.
Nice and fun to be there.Got culture performance and demo making  of local products.Love the waterfall there.
Một trải nghiệm tuyệt vời khi lần đầu tiên đặt chân đến nơi đây, con người thân thiện, cảnh đẹp không khí trong lành và yên tĩnh

Very nice view for take photo. Also a lot man make spot for take photo. A lot of Vietnamese visit there. Can conclude that place is quite popular among Vietnamese. Have hire a local tour guide during the time of visit.
This place was great!  Alot of things to buy and see.  Very colorful check out my videos on youtube amausa steve williams

Very colourful and picturesque village. Nice and easy walking. Beautiful place to relax at the bottom od valley close to waterfall. Lots of local people around. And of course lots of gift shops with hand made stuff.

We walked to here from Sapa town. A nice walk down but lots of steps. When we arrived at Cat Cat village it was a special place! I thought it was beautiful, my children loved the waterfalls, water wheels, bridges, river!

Place was great. I went there by taxi from center and end up paying 200k vnd for 2 ways. Not that expensive considered the road. Taxi was good enough to wait for us till we finished. Anyway going to Cat Cat Village, there will be 70k vnd per person as admission fee. Dont forget to take the map since the place is big and you might need it to find your way out. With great scenery, you will get a lot of photo shot. Remember not to gave the little children any money and if you feel want to give money, just buy anything they sell. While you in there, dont forget to eat a bamboo glutinous rice. Totally worth it.
Lovely walk trough the village, amazing scenery. Definitivly worth the visit when you stay in Sapa !!

The village is almost 4-5kms long one has to walk down the village the entry and exit both are placed in opposite directions. We had a 10 year old with us it was tiring for him to walk. The village has shops, it has some craftsmen making leather products. There r viewing zones from where you can get a beautiful view of the rice terraces there is a beautiful waterfall as well in that stretch of the village. If in Sapa you must visit this place.

Although it is a touristic spot, it is still much more authentic and alive then many other similar attraction.It is an actual village ready for tourists, go to the water fall if you can, it has many uneven stairs.Loved every minute there

6월에 여행한 곳입니다.오토바이를 타고 갔는데 돌아올 때도 예약했더니 오토바이 기사분께서 직접 호텔까지 데려다 주셨습니다.입장료를 받는데 마을 발전 기금으로 쓴다면 좋지 않을까 하는데 어떻게 쓰일지는 모르겠네요.여기 폭포와 주변 산에 둘러싸여있는 마을을 보는 것 만으로도 충분한 매력이 있다고 생각합니다. 사파 자체가 물론 엄청난 매력이고 더운 여름에 시원한 곳이라 더 매력적이게 느껴지지만 정말 동화 속 아름다운 마을과 같은 곳이 cat cat village입니다. 사파를 방문한다면 꼭 방문해보시길 추천합니다. 물론 판시판 산도 꼭 가보시고요!

Der Besuch hat sich gelohnt. Der renovierte alte Ortskern bietet einen guten Eindruck und der Wasserfall rundet das Bild ab. Man kann mit dem Motorbike bis ins Zentrum fahren. Am Ortseingang ist Eintritt zu bezahlen.

Wenn man sich darauf einlässt dass es sich um ein für die Touristen aufgehübschtes Dorf handelt,  lohnt sich der Ausflug. Man geht von Sa Pa immer bergab durch das Dorf. Am Ende des Rundgangs geht es dann 3 km bergauf nach Sa Pa. Hier empfiehlt es sich ein Taxi zu nehmen. Im Dorf selber kann man sehen wie die Bewohner wohnen und arbeiten. Auch eine Folklorevorstellung gibt es.Wer möchte kann sich auch mit Souvenirs eindecken.

Nos gustó mucho. Han subido los precios en relacion a la guia que habiamos consultado. La entrada son 70.000 dongs. Comimos en el primer restaurante que hay nada más entrar y aparte de las espectaculares vistas, muy buen servicio.

We hired a local guide, Me (spelling?), and she took on this amazing walk through the Cat Cat Village, offering information whenever we needed them, but also let us take in the impression. GREAT EXPERIENCE
It was a great walk through so much culture. Felt great to buy handicrafts since you could see women were making those while you were buying other things. Great experience!

A bit of a hike down from the town of Sapa but worth it.  I recommend hoofing it down and, if you can back up, but it's easy to find a taxi up from the bottom if you need.  The walk down is pretty tacky and touristy but the views are great and the village at the bottom is actually quite charming - the water wheels are cool and the waterfall is lovely.
We went there in late afternoon. Make sure you got time to walk. It's a bit of a walk up and down hill. Close to the city. Cute little village.

Aller jusqu’à Sapa et ne pas descendre à CatCat serait vraiment dommage! Balade à travers petit village et rizières pour arriver aux cascades. L endroit a été aménagé avec beaucoup de charme et sans nuire au paysage. Petit spectacle de danse traditionnelle vous attend en bas.
Ideal time to visit village in Sapa to admire theirs majestic with green terrace fields' view over the mountain. Let's rent motobike from hotel and discover how beautiful these landscapes are!!!!

Although it can be argued that the village is a little setup for tourists it still provides a great opportunity to meet some wonderful local people and see their fantastic goods.  The views down through the village and in the valley are beautiful and well worth the walk down and back out again.

you must pay the admission fee but it worth for you surethis village has many attractive points and many beautiful views you can see a traditional home and local Lifestylein the afternoon, you will get beautiful light in your pictures

70k/adult for the village permission and the ticket office provides a map of the village with scenery spots. Takes about two hours to explore this place. The children runs to people with snacks but it is prohibited by Sapa tourism. Cat Cat village is closest village to Sapa center. Wild dogs, pigs and chickens will accompany you.
Es la recreación de un pueblecito antiguo de la zona de Sapa. Interesante de visitar. Muy recomendable.

It's quite a walk down to the village from Sapa and of course it's a lot harder walking back up. You can get a taxi or scooter to the bottom of the valley, but the walk is best if you can do it as there's so much to see and explore on the way down.The Cat Cat village itself is located right at the base of the valley with a fast flowing creek flowing through it. The village straddles the creek and you get to walk aropund this small and genuinely see how the locals live, even though some aspects are getting a little touristy. The waterfalls are ther added bonus of visiting here. You'll need 3 hours or so if walking, but Cat Cat is one of the main reasons for visiting Sapa, so don't miss it.
Booked an overnight stay to visit Sa Pa and Cat Cat village. I was part of a small private tour guided by a local and would definitely recommend to all my friends that they visit Sa Pa.
There are many other more autenthic villages in Sa Pa area ,but Cat Cat is still a very nice place to visit. Recommended!!

We had been experienced journeys with Tour guiders of Go Asia from Bai Dinh-Trang An, Ha long bay to Sapa. Everyone in my family were massively excited. Particularly, Local guider Mr. A Trang - his cellphone: 097.568.9544. He was genuine enthusiasm. Can vote for him 100 marks.I'll introduce Go Asia Travel to my friends, colleagues because of this satisfaction.I'm from Ho Chi Minh city.Tram Nguyen

It's absolutely worth of visit. I don't really like to be loaded with tons of information on the trips, so I've decided to go by myself to discover the area. There is many agencies or just locals approaching on the streets and offer trekking around the villages, so it's up to individual what is more convenient. But if you decide to go by yourself don't believe to guys on the way who offer bike rides as the village is kind of far. It is very near to Sapa and you can walk to there with 20 minutes (depending on the start point location). Entry fee is 70K dongs ($3). As soon as you enter there will a street packed with shops and people that want to sell you something. Don't waste much time there as all beauty is further. However it's good place for buying souvenirs as you can bargain and even start price would be cheaper then in Hanoi. As well there is so many unique things that you won't see anywhere as it is made by local tribes. Nature around is stunning. You can easily turn on the side path and go to see nearby villages.

A bit touristy this is still a rather interesting place to visit. We had the misfortune of visiting on a rainy misty day which dampened proceedings a bit but nonetheless the scenery was still beautiful. A steep climb into the village with a variety of shops lining the paved pathway.Always something to look at, the trip down to the village was very interesting but you do have to wonder how much of this was built purely for the benefit of the tourist. However the waterfalls down the bottom are beautiful to look at, more so because of the rain on the day. A number of locals were demonstrating local arts and crafts in the area around the waterfall It’s a steep climb back up from the village and you will need a good 3-4 hours to do this properly. Well worth the effort.
You can see breathtaking scenes while getting down to the village. The local people with their traditional costumes is very friendly. So many interesting thing to check out.
Great view and mix feeling when you go there such as tired to walk at the bein until the end but all the stop point is great. You could see the different view and feel from this village.
I was very surprised by how neat and quaint this village was.All locals wear traditional colorful dress.Beautiful setting.
Precioso lugar en un entorno privilegiado, no dejes de disfrutar y pasear por esta maravillla natural a poco minutos andando de Sapa.

Очень красивое место. Помимо природы есть фотозоны (например, шалаш из кукурузы)и качели. Довольно много подъемов в гору и лестниц, но оно того стоит. Можно прокатиться на байке, если устанешь.Местные дети просто преследуют, пытаясь продать браслеты и прочую ерунду. А еще тут можно арендовать симпатичные традиционные костюмы. По дороге всегда можно купить воду или сок тростника.

Lou est juste une personne simple, gentille, qui prend soin de vous.Il guide les personnes depuis 4 ans à travers les rizières, ils contaient par cœur tous les coins de cette magnifique région. Lou s’adapte à votre niveau, il est vraiment avenant.Il parle très très bien anglais c’est très plaisant de se balader avec lui, il a de la conversation et connaît beaucoup de détails sur la vie des ethnies c’est vraiment un magnifique trek que je vous conseil de faire de tout cœur
Ci siamo recati in questo stupendo villaggio da soli. C era davvero tanto da vedere. Consiglio scarpe comode

This offers some interesting insights into the local H’mong culture.Ignore the silly rubes that say the village and costumes are fake. They’re not. The H’mong actually dress like this and they actually live here. Certainly there are also some tourists around wearing H’mong outfits for photo ops, but everything here is pretty realistic. One genius even said the waterfalls are fake, which is probably the most ignorant comment I’ve ever seen here. No. They’re not. The sellers also aren’t pushy. And finally, there weren’t even that many tourists around when I went in the early afternoon in August.

Very beautiful and cozy. The people there is so sweet and nicely decorated. It’s definitely worth a visit. But make sure you see it all and not just the touristy part. If it’s possible you should go with a local guide so that you can see the houses from the inside and hear about their story and beliefs 😃

Dejlig lille landsby med fantastisk stemning. De lokale er så rare og venlige. Super spændende at komme tæt på deres hverdagsliv. Der findes både restauranter, beboelsesområder, smuk natur og butikker.

Посетить интересно. Это деревня одной из народностей Вьетнама -черных хмонгов.У них очень веселая яркая одежда, которую можно купить и сразу надеть. (Некоторые туристы, особенно из Китая, так и делали). В деревне огромное количество сувенирных лавочек. Торговцы почти не пристают. Но их товары продаются везде во Вьетнаме, многое видели в Ханое, иногда намного дешевле.Довольно интересно. Есть красивый водопад горная речка, разные домики. Много удивительных бабочек. Есть зал, в котором периодически устраивается перформанс, в котором местная молодежь представляет народные танцы хмонгов.Не то, что уж очень впечатляет, но посетить можно.Да, по дороге много крутых спусков и подъемов (это же горы). Возможно, пожилым людям будет тяжело.
gemakkelijk te bereiken, zeker de moeite waard, wel al erg toeristisch.  De lokale bevolking biedt al hun handgemaakte artikelen aan.
El recorrido se puede hacer perfectamente. Es un pueblito muy pintoresco en el que hay cataratas, puentes y molinos de agua. La gente muy amable.
Easy walk and beautiful scenery of water falls. Local shops along the tracks. Recommended to pay the motorcycle man to go back to town.
Cat cat village is worth the visit. Walk to the waterfall. Keep in mind there are a lot of steps. We walked down and got a motors bike back up. Approx 50,000 Dong. It was worth it.  Loved it.
굳모닝베트남레스토랑에서밥먹고 천천히 사진 찍으면서내려갔어요내려가다보면 뷰좋은 레스토랑도있고산구경도 하고 다만 중간에 진훍밭이 있어신발이 좀 엉망됩니다세옴기사들이 타고 가라고 호객행위 엄청해요. 저흰 안타고 올라올때 택시 타고 왔어요 십만동주고특별한거는 없지만 그냥저냥 동네 산책한다 생각하면 좋아요
Cat Cat is nestled in a short trek from Sapa Town. The village is set up nice for interactions with villagers, and tourists too.
Zeer mooi en zeker de moeite. Zat er wel vol toeristen maar heeft ons niet tegen gehouden een leuke tijd te beleven.
If you visit Sapa, you should not miss this village since it is near the Sapa town so you can walk and see the very beautiful landscape!
This cost 70,000VND each to get into but we found it very well worth it...walked down from the township & back but you can take a taxi or motorbike ride

Great to walk down through all little stalls and markets, interested to go inside a display house of traditional people. Wear appropriate footwear, steps can be slippery in the rain. Beautiful rice fields and an awesome waterfall to see at the bottom of the hike

We walked there all the way from town, a lot of local food which you might not dare to try but some really taste good and special.  I was amazed by the "old time" children playground, very creative and we have fun there!

A lot of negative reviews of the “local” stuff for sale but we just breezed through that without even noticing to get to the waterfalls, bridge, river rapids, etc.  All of that is worth visit and we didn’t find any of the locals pushy with their wares at all.  In fact they mostly ignore us as much as we ignored them.  Also had a great meal at the Cat Cat Riverside.  I wouldn’t miss Cat Cat if I was in Sa Pa.

We enjoy the dance and songs perform by local people. The structure of the house and the view of stunning. However, local would need to consider to clean the area. There are a lots of steps to go down.
Lots of walking and lots of steps, so wear really comfortable walking shoes and have either an umbrella or poncho for the rain in the wet season.  An amazing village, amazing people.
民族衣装のレンタルが出来ます。Sapa girlになった気分になれました。あちこちで売っているスモモと水牛の燻製が美味しかったです。とても広いので、迷子にならないように、地図が必要です。もしくは、あちこちにバイクが止まっているので、お金を払って、おじさん達に乗せてもらうか… 外は暑いですし、アップダウンも激しいので、ぐるっと歩いて回ると、かなり体力を消耗します。

Di sini kita akan melihat rumah, cara hidup dan juga ada persembahan untuk tourist yg datang melawat kampung ini. Ada kedai disepanjang jalan disini menjual baju / disewa untuk tourist yg datang. Sebelum masuk harus membuat bayaran di pintu masuk. Just nice place to visit for us to see Hmong culture.
We went to the sandy beach on a nice day and there are still waterfalls and people living there. I am very excited that I will return to this place in the next vacation.

This village is about two kilometres from Sapa and you can walk along the road. The terrain is quite steep and good walking shoes are recommended. Unfortunately, the quiet village has become very touristy, however, there are many stalls displaying the local craft work. A walk to the waterfall and waterwheels is worth a visit and provide excellent photo oppportunities. The walk back to Sapa is all uphill and quite steep. Taxis are not readily available unless you are happy to ride back into town on the back of a motor bike.

Its a bit of steep walk down into the valley - but there are a lot of things to see down there. The water wheels look great and there are bamboo walkways so you can access them over the river.Great for taking pictures and also sitting back and watching everyone enjoy themselves.Definitely worth the trip and make sure you have your camera ready at all times, on the way down, there are multiple places to see some great scenery

Cat Cat village is at the bottom of the valley under SaPa so of course you need to walk down a very long way, but the walk is relatively easy with lots of stone steps, if you want there is a nice coffee shop half way down where you can rest and enjoy the scenery below. Once you get down to the village there is a street with shops on either side, and yes it's touristy but then you are in a tourist spot after all, the people in the shops will,ask you to buy, just be firm without being rude and they will leave you alone. Continue down to the waterfall it is lovely, we went in and saw a show of traditional dancing and music which was free. Surrounding the waterfall and further up you will see bamboo walkways suspended above the water, also some old French hydro electric wheels, they are lovely and the flowers around all the structures makes it a very nice spot well worth the trek. Continue on up the valley for some more views of the river and eventually you end up at another village, you can have some lunch here and get a car back to Sa Pa.

we enjoyed our walked in Cat-cat village but i have no idea also to get this Cat Cat Village in cheap way other than walking because Taxi have special Fare to Cat Cat village, no matter where you depart from they will still charge you 200.000VND.the way back from cat-cat to city center is going uphill so it's nice for exercising n sightseeing. but that day was raining all day the village was nice, need to paid 30.000VND/PAX for entrance. they have some show when you reach the check point.

We went for a guided tour with Sapa O Chau and were the only two on it. We had heard that Cat Cat was nice and we should visit but actually hadn't really looked into it much. We loved it! Helped that we got a really sunny day. It costs to get into the village but it was included in our tour so I'm not actually sure how much it is. You could easily go without a tour as well. Allow a couple of hours

Enorm genoten van deze voormiddag.Voor mij zeker de moeite om deze uitstap te doen. Ook perfect mogelijk met kinderen!We zagen een prachtig uitzicht, waterval ook de prachtige natuur en de locale mensen. Als je even wou ontspannen, kon dit perfect in de hangmatten die aanwezig waren boven het water. Als er kinderen bijzijn, of je wil jezelf weer even kind voelen, kan je jezelf uitleven op de bamboeschommels, vlot, etc.

There were many photo opportunities through this amazing village. The H'mong people were very welcoming people, with one woman even allowing us to peak inside her home. At the bottom of the hike a traditional dance was performed for all those who wished to watch.

Although it has been seen as an over commercialised attraction, but there are many interesting places of interest to explore within the village. Halfday is more than enough, and highly recommended to hire a taxi/bike for a two-way to reduce walking time which may take up to 45 mins max (to and fro).

Cat cat met een lokale gids bezocht.Zeer leuk  maar commercieel Zeker ook aan te rade met kindereEerst wandel je door de locale winkeltjes naar beneden. eens beneden aangekomen kan je er toffe activiteiten doen en naar een tradioneel dansoptrede kijken,de waterval bewonderen ...En dan is een kleine klim naar boven die je naar een hangbrug leid en dat is dan tevens het einde van je bezoek aan cat cat

The dull and somewhat misty weather didn't spoil this experience. With Tuan from excellent Tour Company, Buffalo Travel (highly recommended) we had an easy walk down from Sapo town to Cat Cat, learning about Vietnam in general and this area in particular. On the valley floor we were entertained by some Vietnamese dancing and some sightseeing. This was physically good for a couple of elderly but fit travellers who were spared the rigours of most of the uphill drag by a taxi ride. Buffalo Travel  and its canny Guides cater for the individuals!

One of the nicest attractions I visited during my trip to Vietnam. When we went in September, the weather was just right — breezy and not too hot.  In Cat Cat Village, there are several photo spots. Some are free, but others require payment to the individual owner (some let you use the spot after paying just to take photos, others require you to buy something from their shop first).  The free spots were okay but not very well maintained, while the paid spots were mostly nicer, well kept, and had better views. Prices varied around 10k–15k VND depending on the spot.  I rented a traditional costume for 200k VND — it was quite heavy, and since you walk down lots of stairs and up again, carrying the outfit felt tiring.  The walk from near the entrance down through the path and steps toward the waterfall and village is part of the experience — the trail loop can take around 1–2 hours depending on pace and stops, and the return uphill can be quite steep and requiring good stamina. Even so, the costume was worth it for the photos and memories.  There were many shops selling local products, food, and drinks. I bought an iced coffee for 40k VND — it was small but tasted good.  The place was nice, affordable, and worth the money, and generally clean. There was one free toilet near the hall where shows are performed.  We really enjoyed our time there. Just be ready for the stairs and uphill walk back to the entrance/parking area, as it requires good stamina.

Visiting Cat Cat Village was one of the most memorable experiences of our Sa Pa trip. It’s more than just a sightseeing stop — it’s a well-organized cultural experience that blends local traditions, natural beauty, and interaction in a very enjoyable way.  The walk into the village is scenic, with stone paths winding through terraced fields, wooden houses, flowing streams, and beautiful viewpoints. The waterfall area is especially picturesque and adds a calm, refreshing feel to the visit. There are plenty of spots along the way to pause, take photos, and enjoy the surroundings.  A major highlight was the local cultural show featuring traditional Hmong music and dance. What makes it special is that visitors are encouraged to participate — you can join the dances, try on traditional clothing, and interact with the performers. It’s fun, inclusive, and creates a memorable connection rather than just being a passive performance.  It’s worth noting that Cat Cat Village is quite tourist-oriented, with souvenir stalls and organized pathways throughout. However, it is well maintained, clean, and thoughtfully managed, and the cultural elements still feel genuine and engaging rather than rushed or artificial.  There is a fair amount of walking involved, including slopes and steps, so comfortable footwear is recommended. That said, the pace is relaxed and suitable for most visitors.  Overall, Cat Cat Village offers a beautiful mix of culture, scenery, and interaction. Despite being tourist-friendly, it delivers a rich and enjoyable experience and is absolutely worth visiting when in Sa Pa. Highly recommended for families, couples, and first-time visitors.

Chỉ tốn vỏn vẹn 70.000VND dành cho người lớn và 30.000VND dành cho trẻ em là bạn đã có thể bắt đầu hành trình khám phá trọn vẹn buôn làng của người dân tộc H’Mông. Ngoài ra, tại quầy bán vé, người dân địa phương cũng rất tâm lý và khéo léo khi chuẩn bị sẵn những tấm bản đồ chỉ dẫn cách đi đến những địa điểm tham quan nổi tiếng tại bản. Nếu như lần đầu tiên đến với Bản Cát Cát, bạn chắc chắn vẫn có thể dễ dàng khám phá trọn vẹn mảnh đất xinh đẹp nơi đây mà không sợ bỏ sót bất kì địa điểm nào cả. Không chỉ có cơ hội tham quan và trải nghiệm trọn vẹn một ngày để hiểu rõ hơn về cuộc sống sinh hoạt thường nhật của người dân địa phương, bạn cũng có thể hóa thân thành những cô sơn nữ xinh đẹp và những chàng trai sơn cước với những bộ trang phục dân tộc được cho thuê ngay tại quầy.

Beautiful place, many people complain that is touristic but Is very well done and we are tourist at the end. The dancing show Is great and the food Is also very authentic. Lleva ropa cómoda para caminar.
Worth seeing, even if many call it a tourist trap, it is a good one. A part of few shops with unique suvenirs, I liked the short show with local music, old power plant and coffey next to the river...
Traditional village but nice scenery but need to climb up and down. But anyway worth it. Remember to change to their traditional costumes, take photos.
Hơi nhiều dịch vụ mua bán, mọi thứ mua bán đều khá trùng nhau nên ko tạo ra điểm nhấn mua bán Nhưng bù lại cảnh rất đẹp, các cây cối đc chăm sóc ra hoa rất đẹp Một nơi đáng để ghé thăm
Beautiful village. Touristy as expected, but still so much natural beauty. There are waterfalls and lots of greenery.

It exceeded my expectations. It doesn't need a tour guide, the same goes for fansipan. We walked from Sa pa town centre downhill, about 20". We were a bit hesitant at first as it costs 120,000 per person and we didn't know if it was worth it. In the beginning you pass a big market of things people want to sell, and of course it has restaurant/cafe with a view of the rice fields and instagrammable places to take a photo (with or without payment).  Once you pass all these...we went with direction to the forest. Without realising we passed it as it was small and nothing special and ended up in rice fields with local people working. This was our highlight. We saw them harvest, passing the rice into a machine to separate the seed from the hay, they were burning the hay and putting the seeds in bags. We managed to see some rice fields that were not harvest yet, as 90% is already gone (unfortunately) and it was an amazing experience.  Then we headed towards the waterfalls which were more impressive than what we thought and we realised that it was full of people renting clothes from the tribe and doing the hair and make up and taking professional photos by the waterfall and bridges.  The trip finishes with going up some stairs which was a bit tiring and in the last 400m we decided to take the electric car for 10,000 to take us to the exit!  Overall it was a great experience.

It’s nice to visit and learn about the Hmong ethnic culture from the living experience here. If you visit on a weekday afternoon, it is not too crowded.  Pay a visit to the performance hall where you can watch a short traditional dance. It’s good to enjoy a coffee in one of the cafes here to support the locals too. There are many vendors who are selling souvenirs.  There are plenty of steps to walk, so come prepared for this. It’s also possible to ride a motorbike up and down this area for about 50K each way, just ask a local who will be selling his service on the path.  It is unfortunate to see how touristy this area is with many tourists disrespecting the culture by treating it as a photo spot. There are areas which are filled with trash due to littering too, more should be done to preserve this ethnic area.

It is true that catcat has been commercialised and designed for tourists.  Nevertheless, it still provides beautiful views of waterfalls. And if you have a guide, there's so much you can learn about the culture of the Hmong ethnicity. I thought it was really interesting and worth a visit. Picked up some beautiful indigo dye pieces too.

Beautiful village with cultural shows. We enjoyed walking around the village,exploring and supporting their local products.  The walk down to the village was ok but walking up was a little workout 🤣 anyway there's a vehicle driving up with a small fee back to the entrance.

Fantastic little place to spend a few hours. 150k per person entry. No map provided but there's one at the ticket booth, however it's easy to find your way around. Lots to see and plenty to buy, if souvenirs are your thing. Lots of fixed price goods that were the cheapest we've seen for a while.  Very picturesque and beautiful. Make sure to check out the various bridges, waterfalls and the cultural performances.  We found it a little tricky to find a coffee or drink, and another downside is the horses all out in the sun for photos all day.  Beautiful walk, a fair amount of stairs but nothing an averagely fit person can't manage.  We got a golf cart buggy for 10k up to the top once we were done. However it was only 400m so could have easily walked that part.  The whole village is a 1 way system, so you won't double back on yourself.  Would certainly recommend to all ages for something like 3-4 hours if you'd like to take your time and see everything, however it could be done in 2hrs easily. No need for a guide, it's all very self explanatory.  There's lots of construction going on at the moment, it's nice to see the ticket money going to good use.
Beautiful picturesque village. Attended a traditional dance in the auditorium. Must visit!

Một địa điểm tuyệt đẹp để đi du lịch. Giá các dịch vụ cũng vừa phải. Không có hiện tượng chèo kéo khách du lịch, chặt chém khách du lịch. Con người khá thân thiện và lịch sự. Rất đáng để có một trải nghiệm vui vẻ

It's one of the most picturesque places in the vicinity of Sapa. We visited during the weekend, and I suspected that many people dressed in the ethnic group's beautiful clothes made it a unique experience. The soaring waterfall runs through the village, uniquely between green mountains with luscious plants and flowers. The ethnic people, the Hmong, are very kind and friendly. Some walking is required; please be prepared for it, but the experience is excellent.

Great experience of old Vietnamese culture. There are the tickets which you need to purchase to enter the village. I wasn't super lucky with the weather but they put the lights on  I had a chance to take a few nice pictures. If you are in Sapa you can't skip that place. I recommend that one as it's very interesting to see.

The ticket is 150,000 VND and you can walk around and explore their culture and buy souvenirs. The lake is really pretty with some waterfalls and there is a free performance at every hour interval for about 40 minutes. There are cafes to sit around and enjoy the view as well, and even traditional costumes for rental (and professional photographers). Definitely worth a visit when you’re in Sapa!
As a perfunctory tourist visit to Sapa, cat cat village tour by walking would take slightly over four hours. Plenty of sights and 'shopping'.
Insane place, totally beautiful. I can see it would be a bit much in full season but in February it was perfect. We walked there from Sapa then a grab back to sapa was 200,000 . Perfect
Experience traditionall village, you can find buffallo meat. Or herbal tea here.

Cat Cat Village is a fantastic place to visit when in Sapa. A place that cannot be missed. The rich culture, the livelihood and the village itself worth the visit notwithstanding the entrance fee of VND150,000 per person. Sharing my personal experience, eating and drinking within this village are great. However, if you want to buy food (pre-pack nuts, and any food including dried food), it is strongly advised not to do so as these are at the stall for long and not fresh. Given it is a tourist village, the price is usually more expensive than getting the same products from Hanoi Dong Xuan market.

We visited Cat Cat in our first day at Sa Pa and honestly don’t understand why some reviews are so negative. Yes, it’s a touristic place—but that doesn’t mean it lacks charm or value. In fact, we found it to be a wonderful and memorable experience.  The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, and the entire setting was well put together. It’s a great opportunity to see how people interact, meet locals, and get a glimpse into cultural traditions in an engaging and accessible way. There is a lot of complaining regarding stairs - well, you have to try climbing in Tam Coc :) One of the best parts was the chance to watch great photo sessions of the local girls - people really enjoyed it and knew how to make great content :)  Of course, we understand it may not be 100% authentic in every detail, but that doesn’t take away from the effort, hospitality, and quality of the experience. For us, it was a true highlight of our trip, and we left feeling happy and inspired. We would absolutely recommend visiting —it’s a warm, enjoyable place that gives you something special to remember.

Mình đi buổi chiều nên thời tiết rất mát mẻ, khung cảnh đẹp rất nhiều chỗ sống ảo. Các bạn rút kinh nghiệm chạy thẳng vào trong luôn đừng gửi xe bên ngoài. Đi bộ rất xa và mệt. Bọn kình xem kĩ tiktok hướng dẫn nên ko bị mời dụ gửi xe bên ngoài.

Çok güzel bir köy. Tamamen turistik. Bu bölgenin asıl sahibi H'mong ve dao yerlileri biz Vietnamlı değiliz diyorlar. Etnik kabileler birbirinden; farklı giyim, saç bandı, eşarp lari, bacaklarina taktiklari tozluklarla ayrılıyorlar. Kadınları ve çocuklar genelde şehirde satıcılık yapıyor. Hemen hemen hepsinin sırtında çocuk bağlı. Kendi yaptıkları elişi hediyelik satıyorlar. Biz sapadan yaklaşık 1.1km. uzaklıktaki köye yürüyerek gittik. Araba ve motorlardan zor yürüdük. Biz tet bayramında oradaydik. Tatil olduğu için genelde yerli turist gördük. Kalabalıktı. Yol surekli dik bir yokuş halinde. Giderken sorun değil fakat dönüş zor. Köye gelince sürekli dik merdivenlerden iniyorsunuz. Sağlı sollu hediyelik esya, elbise, takı ve yemek yerleri var. Fiyatlar çok iyi, pahalı değil. Fakat yine de pazarlık yapın. Yağışlı olmasa bile sisten yerler kaygan olabiliyor. İyi bir ayakkabı giyin. Şelale en altta ve en güzel yer burası. Ormanın icinde3-4 yerde şelale ve akan bir dere var. Fotoğraf için suya bambu tekneler koymuşlar. Dönüşte hiç zahmet çekmeyin. Şelale çıkışında motorlar bekliyor ve bir ücret karşılığında sizi o dik yokuşlu ve virajlı yoldan çıkarıyorlar. Motor sürücülerinin ikiside kadındı. Birine eşim, birine ben bindim. 300.000 dong verdim. Su degirmenlerinin olduğu yerede gidin. Şelalede bir çok restoran ve kafe var. Uyarı yazmışlar, yukardan taş düşmesine karşı dikkatli olun.
我們搭計程車過來，需要一點路程。有先在路邊租當地的服飾來這裡拍美照體驗（服裝含妝髮約落在TWD500元）需要走下坡一段路程也有階梯才會下到村落裡(所以回程也要一直爬坡爬階梯😅)裡面很好拍，處處是美景（在對的季節來應該會更美）回程可以去坐他們的滑坡很好玩🤩PS:記得一定要空拍機拍影片超好看的👍

一開始看Google 貓貓村評價蠻兩極的 但我本人給很高的評價👍🏻 有人說景色不好或很商業，我倒覺得這裡的梯田景象更美，而且把後方的大小山一起拍進去更有大景之感 至於商店，雖然很多，但也不會抓著你推銷，價格都比河內市區便宜，小吊飾價格50k左右 Btw真的很大很大 建議預留一整個白天給他 弄好服飾拍照逛街三個小時才發現我們只逛了一半 下面的村莊跟瀑布大概還要一兩個小時才能完整的逛完&拍完，而且階梯超級多，要有健行的毅力 走到最下面基本上要爬回入口是很痛苦的 但也無法叫轎車，只能用機車的方式會到入口 一個人價格是50k 試過了無法砍價XD當時也只有這個離開的辦法了 租借衣服跟妝髮是在 Peaces house 這家也是老網紅了 女生價格是30k/人附從飯店來的打車費用 老闆語言能力不錯、溝通上沒問題、價格又透明 總之很喜歡貓貓村啦～滿足了女生們想逛想拍的慾望，1/10去的，天氣微涼霧也不會太大 但這裡真的沒什麼貓哈哈哈 唯一看到的是衣服店裡的🐱

Comment: ใครจะมาสามารถซื้อตั๋วเดินลงไปชมได้นะ คนล่ะ 150 ดอง ไม่รวมมอเตอร์ไซค์ เพราะถ้าเดินขึ้นมาไหวขากลับก็ไม่ต้องซื้อตามที่เค้าแนะนำ แต่ไม่ไหวก็แค่ตกลงกันตอนจะกลับขึ้นมา เราได้ที่ 40 ดอง ถือว่าคุ้มนะ เพราะชันเลยทีเดียว ด้านในมีน้ำตกหลายที่ เดินสบาย ทางเดินดีมาก วิวสวยตลอดทาง ใครจะแวะซื้ออะไร จำไว้ว่าให้เดินเปรียบเทียบราคาก่อน เพราะมักจะขายตัดราคากัน สนุกดี แต่ก็แอบรำคาญเพราะแม่ค้าถามแม่งตลอดทาง 55 ด้านล่างไม่ค่อยมีอะไรกินนะ อย่างมากก็แค่ไก่ย่างที่ มันออกจะไม่น่าอีสสักเท่าไหร่สำหรับเรา กับเครื่องดื่ม เบาๆ ใครหิวมา แนะนำว่ากินไรมาก่อนเลย ไม่จอยแน่ เพราะมีแต่ร้านขายของฝากกับร้านเช่าชุด และคนแต่งชุดพื้นเมือง ที่ยืนบังวิว 🤣

Es cierto que lo hemos visitado en 2025 y por los comentarios, ahora quizá algo turístico de más con bastante gente a cualquier hora del día, pero no deja de llamar la atención y, si consigues retrotraerte en el tiempo, te podrías imaginar cómo sería antaño este pueblo y la vida que allí se desarrollaría. A mi sinceramente me ha fascinado, no solo por la arquitectura y el enclave propio del pueblo, sino también por las maravillosas vistas del Valle y de los arrozales. Simplemente maravilloso!! Ahora bien, moverte por el pueblo no es del todo fácil y es bastante incómodo, por lo que hay que ir con las pilas cargadas y con calzado bueno y cómodo. Y encima para lo que cuesta la entrada, está muy pero que muy bien. Eso sí, dentro te van a intentar vender de todo.  Procura llevar efectivo!! No aceptan tarjeta!!

본인의 실패담으로 인한 깟깟 마을 관광 잘하는 법을 알려드리겟습니다. 썬플라자에서 그랩으로 깟깟 마을을 가니 저 밑의 입구에서 내려줍니다. 우리 애들 옷 입히고 사진 찍는게 버킷 리스트 였으므로 입구에서 옷을 빌려줍니다. 여자옷이 삼십만동 이였던것으로 기억합니다. 표를 구매하고 내려 갑니다. 튜브 미끄럼틀이 있습니다. 인당 이만동이었나? 이천월꼴 이였나? 기억이 잘 안납니다. 중간에 날라가는줄 알았습니다. 개 재밌습니다. 내려가서 사진 찍어줍니다. 인생샷 많이 남겻습니다. 커피 한잔 마셔줍니다. 커피 마시고 화장실 갔는데 냄새 심합니다. 위생 신경쓰시는 분들, 특히 여자분들은 많이 힘들것 같습니다. 하울의 움직이는 성 비슷한 조형물이 있답니다. 거기서 사진 찍어야 합니다. 아뿔사 다른 입구쪽으로 올라가야 한답니다. 주말이라 무지막지하게 내려오는 사람들을 뚫고 올라갑니다. 조낸 올라가야 합니다. 중간에 뭐 이상한게 보입니다. 저긴가? 하고 보면  아닙니다. 절반 올라온겁니다. 꾸역꾸역 올라오면 거의 입구쯤에 있습니다. 저기 갈려면 만동입니다. 괜찮습니다. 물도 주고 어짜피 돈쓰러온 관광객입니다. 사진 이쁘게 찍어주고, 이제 갈시간입니다. 아.... 옷 빌린곳이 다른 입구입니다. 오도바이타고 가면 되지만, 오토바이는 무섭습니다. 그래서 다시 조낸 내려갑니다. 우리가 왔던 길을 다시 갑니다. 다시 처음 왔던 출입구로 갑니다. 맞습니다. 올라가야합니다. 내려 올때 탔던 튜브가 거꾸로는 못올라 갑니다.. 그래서 전기 카트 타고 올라갑니다. 아들은 땀이 젖어 옷 반납하기가 챙피하답니다.  입구로 나와서 옷반납하고 보니. 주말이라 입구가 개판입니다. 겨우 그랩잡아 탈출했습니다.  가시는 분들을 위한 꿀팁  입구가 두개이지만 제일 위에 입구로 입장해서 다른쪽 입구로 나오는개 이득입니다. (좀 덜 올라갑니다.) 옷을 빌리실 분들은 깟깟마을 안에서 빌리셔도 될거 같습니다. 옷 반납하러 가는게 은근 신경쓰입니다. 내부 옷 빌려주는곳 가격은 잘 몰르겟습니다. 올라가실때 전기 카트 타고 가시는거 강추입니다. 하지만 카트를 타시면 튜브를 못타니. 좀만 걸어서 가셔서 튜브 한번 타시고 카트 타는걸 추천 드립니다. 우리 애들 사진 다 이쁘게 잘 나왔습니다. 깟깟 마을 가시는거 강추입니다. 진짜 이쁜 사진 많이 찍엇습니다.

來到沙巴，Cat Cat村絕對是個不容錯過的亮點！這條健行路線真的很棒，沿途風景美得像畫，梯田、竹林、小溪，處處都是拍照的好地方。如果不趕時間，慢慢走、慢慢感受當地的生活氣息，真的超療癒！村裡的街道雖然有點商業化，販售的手工藝品琳琅滿目，像是苗族的手織布和飾品，雖然沒找到特別想買的紀念品，但光是逛逛、看看當地人如何製作這些手工品，就已經很有趣。 從買票入口到谷底的瀑布景區，路程不算短，但沿途環境整理得算乾淨，走到谷底瀑布時，滿滿的苗服旅客等著拍照！回去的路上爬坡真的很累人，幸好有接駁機車可以搭，一趟5萬越盾，價格對我來說挺合理的，尤其是走得筋疲力盡時，坐上車的那一刻簡直像救星降臨！ 下次再來，我一定要留一整天在Cat Cat村，帶著相機慢慢走、慢慢拍，細細品味這慢活的鄉村氛圍。這裡的環境、絕美的自然景觀，真的值得五星好評！推薦給想體驗沙巴在地文化的朋友！

第一天到沙壩 先去了番西邦峰 回市中心在小沙壩吃了午餐 下一個行程就是去“Cat Cat Village"貓貓村  其實這裏已經非常觀光化了 下長長的石階 兩旁都是賣紀念品的商店 下到河谷 有瀑布也有當地少數民族表演 買了些少數民族的手染絲巾 便踏上了歸途  這時發生了一個小意外 道路坍方 接送的車無法到達 只得先坐電瓶車 再每人坐當地的登山摩托車 跨越泥濘 回到接送車的停車場  這趟旅行 飛機 汽車 遊輪 小艇 觀光遊河船 臥鋪火車 臥鋪巴士 電瓶車 摩托車 人力三輪車 可說是所有的交通工具都坐遍了 也是一種經驗

山谷里的原住民村莊也發展成景點了，大約10年前來過一次，當時難忘的瀑布、村莊、山上的少數民族，都讓人印象深刻

越南沙唄市的貓貓村（Cat Cat Village）是近年熱門的旅遊景點。 門票價格：成人約150,000越南盾（約台幣200元），兒童半價。 交通方式：從沙壩市中心搭計程車約10分鐘，或步行20分鐘抵達。 服飾體驗：租借苗族傳統服裝價格約200,000-500,000越南盾（台幣260-650元），含配件與簡單妝髮，被認為是拍照必備，貓貓村的自然景觀與少數民族文化 瀑布、梯田、吊腳樓、河谷等景點被反覆提及，尤其瀑布區被形容為「仙境」或「必拍地標」。 苗族（赫蒙族）的傳統服飾、手工藝品（如刺繡、銀飾）及歌舞表演，部分深入介紹村落的歷史背景（如法國殖民時期的命名由來）。 村內過度商業化，沿路充斥紀念品店與付費拍照點，甚至需「後製修掉背景人潮」，反映觀光發展的兩面性。居民販售手作商品或孩童兜售紀念品的現象，引發對觀光經濟與文化保存的反思。 貓貓村已失去原始部落風情，轉型為「人工民俗村」，例如商店街商品同質性高，部分景點需額外付費。 貓貓村成功凸顯其「自然與文化交融」的特色，但較少深入探討少數民族的當代生活樣貌。對於追求深度體驗的旅客，建議參考結合周邊村落的行程（如塔番村、老柴村），並避開連假人潮，以平衡觀光便利與文化真實性。
非常適合來這邊待半天以上的行程，有很多有趣的體驗

Tempatnya unik, cantik. Bisa sewa baju buat foto2 cantik. Bisa sewa di luar, bisa juga di dalam. Dari depan loket sudah ditawari jasa sewa. Sewa baju dan hairdo tanpa make up 250vnd. Kalo mau sewa fotografer ditawarin 500-400vnd tergantung kita nawarnya. Waktu aku kesana pas habis hujan jadi repot banget pake baju panjang,mana jalan licin dan becek. Tapi enaknya kita jadi bisa foto2 sepanjang jalurnya. Kalo mau ngga ribet, sewa di dalem sebelum jembatan di waterfall,  tapi kita cuman bisa foto2 di sekitar area waterfall. Tapi menurutku itu udah cukup banget buat dapet foto bagus karena spotnya juga lumayan banyak, daripada harus ribet pake baju panjang jalan kaki lumayan jauh dari pintu masuk sampe pintu keluar. Btw sebaiknya pastikan berapa harga sewa baju, hairdo, make up dan fotografer di awal sebelum ambil ya.

沙壩的貓貓村，說大不大，說小不小，要待上一時半會兒還是可以的 這次是跟著當地的小型旅遊團來的。 門票150k越南盾。 我與友人穿上了沙壩少數民族的服飾，在貓貓村拍了很多很好看的照片。我覺得有個導遊還是不錯的，不然不知道從何逛起，也比較好溝通。不過，靠近售票處的衣服租借處會比較貴，這次與友人都花費了500k越南盾來體驗，若要加上化妝便要再多上100k越南盾。前往瀑布的路上都還有其他租衣服的地方，若想來體驗的人未必需要於入口處便體驗，可以自行斟酌。 我挺喜歡這一處的，好拍、好看，導遊說貓貓村的烤肉特別有名，也興趣的朋友也可以嘗試一下～

사파시내에서 가까워보여도 갈때는 내리막길 올때는 오르막길이라 택시타고 이동 추천합니다 입장권 끊고 내려가다보면 하울의움직이는성 입구에서 돈을 받는데 1만동에 물하나 공짜니 구매하시는편이 좋아요 내려가다보면 기념품가격도 저렴해지니 아래쪽에서 구매하세요 사랑의 폭포가 끝인데 50m 올라가면 카트 있습니다 나머지 400m는 언덕이니 1인 1만동에 타시는걸 추천합니다

거시기 어 썬플라자에서 걸어올만함 내리막이라 문제없음 하지만 돌아올 때는 택시 또는 바이크를 이용해서 돌아오시길 바람 그리고 깟깟마을 입구가 두곳이니 이것두 하울에움직이는성 하위버전 있는쪽으로 들어가면 쭉 내려오면서 볼수있으며 쭉 둘러보고 아래주차장쪽으로 오심됨 그리고 버기카 전기차 있으니 그거타시고 출구오시고 만동이니깐 출구에서 그랩불러서 타시길 개인적으로 추천 중간에 이쁜 까페들 많으니 오실때 걸어오시는걸 추천 왜? 내리막이니깐 아 물론 썬플라자 기준임 끝

Cat Cat Village, berjarak sekitar 4 km dari Sa Pa, jalanan kecil, terjal, tebing curam. Apalagi kalau hujan licin dan rawan longsor. Karena habis hujan tadi pagi, maka air sungai menjadi keruh dan tidak jernih. Harga tiket masuk adalah 150.000 VND per orang, ikuti jalan kecil untuk masuk dan bawa saja motornya. Setelah sampai di pintu masuk ada 2 pilihan, turun pakai tangga atau jalan lurus lagi dan naik motor sehingga tinggal jalani tidak jauh lagi.  Aksesnya hanya tangga saja, jadi siapkan kaki yang kuat. Sepanjang turunan di kiri dan kanan warga berjualan cindera mata. Fokusnya ada di sungai bagian bawah yang banyak orang berfoto bahkan sampai sewa baju adat masyarakat sekitar. Overall menyenangkan asalkan fit badannya dan banyak tenaganya.

來的時候是下雨天，先買便利雨衣穿上，再購票入場，進入園區後是一路往下走的階梯，階梯兩旁藝品、土產店林立，前半段有種買票進來逛商店街的感覺，一直下雨心情不OK。  走完商店街後雨停了，中段是田園景觀，還有人工巢箱、涼亭、水車等造景。再來下切溪谷，欣賞瀑布美景，乾涸的河床上可以體驗騎馬的樂趣，具民俗風情的房屋，是拍網美照的絕佳場景。  最後順著溪谷兩旁的棧道直上停車場，步道口距離停車場還有400公尺，有付費的接駁車可以搭乘，滿座即發車。貓貓村的入、出口為A進B出，來往沙壩的道路也坑坑洞洞的，下雨或是濃霧路況更是糟糕，想騎摩托車遊覽要先做好風險評估。

Desa ini terletak tidak jauh dari Kota Sapa, hanya membutuhkan waktu perjalanan sekitar 10 menit. Untuk menuju ke desa ini anda lebih baik menyewa kendaraan motor, yang banyak ditawarkan di jalan utama kota Sapa, krena taxi/grab tidak diizinkan untuk masuk kesana. Desa ini menyuguhkan banyak pengalaman menarik mengenai budaya desa di vietnam. Anda dapat berswafoto dengan latar belakang pemandangan yang sangat menarik, menyewa baju baju masyarakat lokal dan berbelanja pernak pernik menarik khas Cat Cat.  Butuh stamina fisik yang kuat krena anda akan menuruni banyak anak tangga untuk menikmati desa cat cat. Tapi jangan khawatir untuk naek keatas lagi nanti anda dapat menyewa ojek motor lokal, bervariasi harga tergantung kelihaian anda menawar harganya.  akan banyak scam menawarkan parkir, saran saya abaikan saja hingga anda tiba di dekat loket masuk, anda dapat menyewa baju daerah agar anda mendapatkan akses free parking.
Cảnh đồng quê tươi đẹp của Sapa và người Mông. Sâu trong núi, bạn sẽ tìm thấy một thác nước hùng vĩ, nhỏ hơn sông nhưng lớn hơn suối.

cat cat 마을로 가는 동안 오토바이와 차들이 많이 다녀 조심히 지나가야했고 수로가 길(땅)과 통해 흘러 내리기 때문에 흰운동화는 비추천. 좀만 더 내려와 보면 상점들과 같으 전통길들어가는 입구와 마주치게되는데 못보고 그냥 지나칠 수 있다. 입구옆에 매표소가 있으며 어른1명당 7만동. 즉, 3,500원 내고 구경할 수 있다. 가운데 돌계단이 있으며 양옆으로 주민들이 만들어놓은 물건들을 팔고있으며 음료,과자,가방,빗,악세사리,미용도구 등등 여러가지가 있다. 중간중간에 모형물과 쉬는곳이 있는데 그 뒤로 자연이 돋보여 사진찍기 좋다.내가 갔을 때는 날씨가 좋아서 사진찍기 수월했다. 그후 중간지점에 오면 오토바이운전사가 사파까지 태워다 주겠다며 가격을 흥정한다. 거의 1인당 7만동 부른다. 흥정을하다 돌아서면 5만동까지는 해주는데 그랩이 훨 싸다 2명이서 5~6만동이 나왔으니까. 잘 생각해서 타면 좋다. 담에 또 들리면 cat cat 마을 끝까지 가보고싶다.

사파의 소수 민족 전통 마을로 사파의 관광 명소로 유명한 곳. 가장 사파스러운 분위기를 느껴볼 수 있는 작은 마을입니다. 여긴 대단한 무언가 관광 어트랙션을 가진 곳이 아니라 잔잔하게 1~2시간 정도 조성된 산길을 걸으며 소수 민족의 삶을 분위기만 느껴보는 곳 정도로 생각하고 방문하시면 될 것 같아요. 저희는 오토바이를 렌트해서 다녀왔고 사파 타운에서 걸어가기에는 조금 거리와 경사가 있어서 렌트 혹은 택시 이용하시길 권장합니다. 안에 뭐 이런저런 상품도 많이팔고 오토바이 호객도 많고 오롯이 순수한 전통 마을이라 할 순 없지만 자연속을 거닐며 보내는 시간은 평화롭고 아름다웠습니다. 저희는 번거로워서 안했는데 사진 남기기 좋아하시는 분이라면 의상 대여해서 이쁜 사진 남기시는 것도 좋은 추억이 될 것 같습니다!
한번쯤 가볼만한 장소. 아름다운 풍경과 잘 어우러지는 마을이다. 의상과 메이크업을 받을 수 있는데 좀 비싼것 같아 하지는 않았다. 마을 초입에서부터 중간까지 상점이 늘어서 있는데 전부 비슷한 제품만 판다. 수공예 제품이나 마을에서만 구매할 수 있는 제품을 판매하면 좋을것 같다.

Terrible, very noisy. Scooters everywhere. Locals rude and unfriendly, trying to sell you something or scam you. Can’t walk without constant fear of being run over. Pavement taken by scooters and small businesses. Terrible place

Horrible dirty unfriendly city.Don't speak english and very hard to communicate with these people.Never come back.The traffic is really bad.People at shops are very rude.Dirty street food places.We all got sick few times.Even in good hotels the staff is very rude.Not good hospitality.

If, however, you go like us to the old quarter of Hanoi on a scooter, beware of the police's practices which are dishonest to say the least.We parked near a restaurant and when we came out, our scooter was gone.In fact, this crooked police calls on touts in the street who alert them to tourists' vehicles parking and the latter proceeds to remove them.I would like to point out that we were in no way poorly parked.Then these touts, seeing you looking for your scooter, approach you and ask you what your problem is. Very kindly they tell you that it must have been the police who carried out his kidnapping. They will even accompany you to the station. Wasn't that adorable?Subsequently, these nice police officers ask you to pay a fine if you want to get your scooter back (200,000). That's what we did.These police officers, even if they are poorly paid, are a disgrace and dishonor the uniform they wear.So be careful if you ride a scooter in this very beautiful neighborhood!
It's big and noisy. Not very pleasant. Would rather recommend going south. Hello, for example. Their night market is only on weekends and was terrible.

I don’t know how people write that this area is easy to walk. The streets are very busy, the sidewalk is not very wide and is full of motorbikes parked. Half the time we were dodging cars and motorbikes forced to walk in the street. We didn’t really see any shops that were very interesting.

While Old Quarter is nice, the rest of the city is super dirty. We went for shopping near the water puppet theatre and they first lured us with a price and later when we said we buy 2 things they increased the price for the first too. When we tried to remind them what was initially agreed they were super rude and gestured us out of the shop. Hated the experience in Hanoi. Ho chi Mihn is so much better and people are much nice!
Chaotic impossible.For hotels and restaurants to avoid. Prefer other neighborhoods such as the French Quarter.It's worth a visit at most
Extremely busy, dirty area, you will be harassed at every step by people trying to sell you something. Sometimes they will also follow you, making it even more scary and unpleasant.

This was our second visit to Hanoi Old Quarter. First time was in 2017 and we enjoyed it so much we planned a return visit. The reason I'm giving it 1 star this time is because of an unprovoked dog attack. This happened on the sidewalk in Old Quarter, where a medium to large dog (~18kg) bit my leg while I was walking the street. Didn't even see it until it was too late. The dog was being kept by one of the shop owners. Long story short, upon doing some research, found that Vietnam still has a rabies problem and because of it I decided to go to a 24 hour medical centre in the middle of the night, get the wounds cleaned and start a series of rabies vaccinations and serum shots (I haven't had any prior rabies protection so needed it and this is what cost a lot) - total cost was US$515.Went to the FMP Hanoi - International Clinic. They spoke decent English and while expensive, if you have travel insurance, I'd definitely recommend them. I still need 3 more vaccinations to complete the set, which I'll be getting in Phu Quoc - I'll follow up with where you can get rabies vaccinations in Phu Quoc. This has caused problems with our itinerary and now have to take time out to visit clinics for rabies shots not to mention the pain and trauma my wife and I experienced.While we looked forward to visiting Hanoi for its food and massages, it will be a long time before we consider revisiting Hanoi again.As for the dog, we provided the hotel with the dog/shop details who said they'd follow up with the authorities.

As old quarters go this one is poor. There are a few “tourist tat” shops, and many “local” unfriendly restaurants. Independent stores or restaurants are few and far between. Maybe if you visit at a weekend when the streets are closed to the traffic for a night market it would be a more pleasant experience . Visiting midweek it’s basically an exercise in dodging traffic, walking past dumps of shops and cafes looking for the illusion Tripadvisor recommendation. It’s beyond me why Tripadvisor has rated it.
Can I give zero stars?This is the worst place I've visited in the world, and I've visited a LOT of places.This area is not fit for humans. Avoid, avoid, avoid.
The streets are full of rubbish, there is a different awful smell every 30 seconds. Huge disappointment, wouldn’t recommend Hanoi never mind things in it

I’ve been to most major cities in Vietnam and the old quarter has nothing special or unique about it.The only defining characteristic is being forced to constantly walk on the roads.The air is thick with pollution. The drivers around here are particular lunatics, and it is teeming with scams and hostile locals.Funnily enough, I would recommend going for the “experience”. You will also find that most recommended places to visit and eat are in the old quarter, so it is unavoidable.
Hanoi is boring period. Not as vibrant and exciting as HCMC Saigon. Only go here to go to Halong Bay or Sapa.

A lot of people may find this area fascinating but I hated it. I knew about the millions of scooters in Hanoi but was not prepared for them to be parked all over the pavements as well. You literally take your life in your hands when out and about. You cant stop to look at anything because of the chaos. I do not like the idea of playing chicken but that is what you have to do if you need to cross the road. On a weekend the roads around the old quarter are closed to traffic but guess what some of the scooters do not obey the rules which makes it more dangerous as you are not expecting one of these contraptions to come flying up to the pavement at speed. Make sure you have travel insurance if you go to Vietnam.

When I see its called the Old Quarter, I had an idea that I might see some original building, genuine Vietnamese architecture and nice sites. How wrong I was, this is a horrible dirty part of town.. advertising everywhere, facades covered any glimpse of the buildings. Dirt and filth, poorly designed traffic system, scooters blocking pavements force pedestrians into dangerous roads. Constant harassment from scooter sellers shoving dirty pictures into your face for "girls' or massage.Much racist attitude from locals.I imagine this was once a reasonable area but unless you like the worst a city has to offer, stay away. Better areas to stay only a short walk away and then you can visit the old part if you must.

Simply like d place so as we can walk all over Old quarters many street food n shop for any things u want but we dont shop soveiner for family n friends lots of travelled agent in one words u name it it's there.This trip we went to ning binh tran an a UNESCO site .We will go again hopefully 2 Hanoi

When i inquire at the front office they charge me USD15.00 only but after the service i availed when am about to get out of the room there were 2 men who threatened me to pay additional USD80.00 as they said for the facial mask and drinks they offered which i did not avail.
