Ha sido mi primera vez en Vietnam y por ello en Old Quarter. Por lo que había leído y me habían contado Hanói era una ciudad con encanto, pero más bien me ha desencantado. Se trata de un lugar sucio, muy sucio, decadente y muy ruidoso, diria que asqueroso. La gente deja toda la suciedad, basuras y restos de comida en medio de la calle, junto a restos de agua sucia, los escupitajos y mocos que incesantemente echan... no ayuda en un ambiente tropical dónde los olores, más bien el mal olor, se multiplican por tres, junto a los otros inquilinos de la ciudad, las cucarachas (enormes y voldoras) y las ratas. A todo esto, se debe sumar la mala educación y la falta de modales que me he ido encontrando, sobretodo por muchos conductores de ciclomotor que con su incansable “tocamiento” del claxon llegan a ponerte nervioso/a.
It’s crowded and lot of bars, maybe it’s ok for anyone who want a night life. The commercial spot which now lost their uniqueness.

Like on every Asien marke a lot of fakes an cheap stuff ist sold, but in Saigon or Bangkok the quality of the fakes are 100% better ! I have never seen before a Nike yeezy with Adidas Strips ! Funny and rediculus in one way. Spent your time with something else
맛있어요. 그리고 깨끗해요이틀연속 방문입니다힐튼옆이고 소피텔에어도 가깝습니다음식은 깔끔하고 가격도 저렴합니다

After reading rave reviews about Hanoi I was left gob smacked. Which planet are these people from who find this festering fetid decaying place a tourist attraction. Look below you and there is no pavement. Just a pile of rubble. Every road in the old quarter is just a rubble strewn uneven surface. Loom above you and all the electric and telephone cables are a mass of jumbled ugly wires. Everywhere you look it is the same. The lake area is the same. Totally unappealing. As for the tat that is sold in the souvenir shops....china is close by and they have dumped their wares here. As for the food. Its basically rice noodles in a broth that looks and tastes like dish water. The meat floating in it is indeterminable and could be dog meat which is consumed in vast quantities here. How can anyone think this food is good. Just adding fresh coriander or basil to a dish does not make it good. Hanoi has less that 5 percent of returning travellers. The back packing crowd kicked out of their parents homes might find this place endearing. Avoid like the plague. The people can also be nasty to visitors.

I get this is a developing city but omg what a dump. The stench and trash alone are enough to keep me away. Don’t even get me started on the hygiene, the revolting food, the traffic and the unfriendly ppl. Besides all the above there is absolutely nothing to do, we couldn’t wait to get out of there!

I am very sorry to have come here, it’s a very unfriendly city in terms of no one smiles and on every single road you are very lucky to not get run over by honking motorbikes and cars, and the Old Quarter the only place to see and after 1 day of being pestered to buy stale donuts from very aggressive sellers on the street, you just want to leave the place, if you have to come here don’t make the mistake we did and come for more than 1 day, as you will be so so bored.
오토바이고 차고 너무 많다. 인도도 잘 안되어 있고 빵빵거리는 차와 오토바이에 호객행위까지.. 차라리 스쿠터를 빌려서 돌아다님 좋을꼬 같다
This is fun from a bus or a taxi, but walking is dangerous. The area is disorganized with scooters on the pavement and drivers in a hurry and texting. This is a public health nightmare.

Stayed at Lo su street for 3 nights. Big mistake. Narrow dirty streets with thousands motorbikes and no traffic lights. Blackened squirrels covered with dirt running on electric wires. Dogs and babies riding along on scooters with no safety concerns. Bars filled with lost souls, people eating in garbages and traffic of rats. Air’s super polluted.Just drive through if you really want to see it. Or come down on a Sunday afternoon, families and young generation are hanging out by the lake. That will give you different views of the old town.

All reviews about the old quarter forget to mention one crucial detail: the maze of small streets and alleys, invariable described as “charming” are home to the same level of traffic as the other streets, but with even less space. So the battle between pedestrians and motorbikes here reaches fever pitch, and the whole thing just becomes a bit too much. It feels like one of those areas that people try hard to convince themselves that they are loving it, just to tick the box of the “real Asian experience”.The pleasantness of the city ends at the north end of Ho Hoan Kiem Lake, where the Old Quarter starts. I saw nothing in the Old Quarter remotely interesting. The same crazy traffic is crammed in streets half as wide as the rest of the city. That plus tourists perhaps looking for some “authenticity”. A place to be avoided. Nothing will be missed from your trip, guaranteed.
Don't go. This area if flilthy, dirty, crowded.  Smog and moter scooter infested.  I wold never go to Vietnam again or Thailand.  I spent amonth traveling.  A miserable place.
차가 너무 막혀요 ㅠㅠ 차 안에서 아들이 계속 불평불만, 차 안에서 계속 게임만 하게되더라고요 그나마 시티투어 버스를 타서 헌국어 서비스 이어폰으로 구석구석 설명은 들어서 다행.

Tiny streets, both sides full of tiny bars and shops. Very repetitive. Bars with plastic chairs and cheap beer. Some streets are traffic free, but scooters huge feature on most. Piles of rubbish. Traffic fumes terrible. Gift shops nearly all the same. Did love the propoganda poster shop, 3 hang bac. Not a place to stroll around relaxed. Hard to stop to look at anything of interest(shrines, burning offerings) because of traffic, other tourists and hassle. It makes you ashamed if this is what tourism has done to Hanoi.

I wish someone could give me an advise simply not to go there. There whole place is trash (kinda like city itself but more concentated). Hustle and bustle, crowd of people, so called "bars", garbage on streets and people on motorbikes who sneak there somehow. I felt only stress and disgust there. There is not much to see in Hanoi in general so Old Quarter is kinda a sightseeing.
더럽고 무례하고 위생은 안중에도 없는 세상 최악의 도시지만 그런거 상관 없으면 와도 됨ㅜㅜ50자 압박은 안니지 않나제발 미친 리뷰
Old quarter is dissapoinment, dirty streets, the street foods display not hygiene, shops are over priced, not recomended. Not interested to take any photo.

The whole area is extremely rundown. The buildings look as they are about to crumble. You can barely walk as there are bikes parked on the sidewalks. You have to walk on the street, skipping the cars and bikes. The smells are overwhelming. Everywhere smells like sewage and old oil. The area is very noisy and overrated. I wouldn’t stay here as the hotels are very ugly, small, and noisy.

En realidad creo que aunque siguen siendo experiencias diferentes para una persona de occidente como yo lo soy, creo que esto es un poco mucho. A pesar que la gente es muy amable, la limpieza que escasea y el exceso de ruido y caos es algo que me habría evitado y habría dedicado el tiempo a visitar lugares más interesantes. En mi experiencia, lo había evitado.
Possibility of a great atmosphere but the traffic and air pollution ruins the area. Would not recommend

Narrow streets, can not walk on the footpath due to scooters parked there, noisy, dirty, heavy traffic, polluted air and crowded, making sure your stuff doesn't get ripped off. It only gets worse in the evening.Full of tourist shops all selling the same stuff, you can be certain that you are being overcharged on everything - is it even made in Vietnam?It takes half an hour to walk a distance that can be covered in five minutes elsewhere.Definitely no fun here.
Rất tốt nhân viên vui vẻ nhiều mặt hàng văn phòng phẩm. Gian hàng bầy đẹp và là gian hàng tự chọn

I don't think I've ever found any vacation place quite as unrewarding and unpleasant. The traffic situation is beyond belief; even as a born and raised big city guy, and a veteran of many, many cities, all over the world, I found it to be incredibly difficult to negotiate the traffic. You basically are forced to cross through flowing traffic, trusting that nobody is going to hit you.  Almost all of the sidewalks are completely blocked by either countless scooters parked in your way, or street vendors, forcing you to walk out into the street constantly. It's jam-packed with tourist after tourist after wandering Street vendors, and stopping to even look at your phone for a moment means someone walking up to you to try and sell something. It's incredibly unattractive, and pretty much without redeeming architectural merits. Other than that though, it's a great place.
It's ok if you are not expecting too much. It is dirty and messy, not much to see or.buy Will not.come.back in a hurry for sure, maybe in abother ten years time and hope it will improve.

The place is crowded,smells terrible,the street food looks like it's going to develop into Ebola, rubbish all over,the sellers are just shouting to buy something,if you are a tourist they will ask for ridiculous prices

Maybe if you're young you can cope with this mad house of a place - cars driving over your feet, constant horns, carbon monoxide, sidewalks choca with motorbikes, constant pressure to buy buy buy from street vendors, heat and humidity adding to the mix (- it took ten years off me and I can't afford that!). Make for Ho Bai Mau lake in the French quarter to go into recovery mode briefly! If you are in a restaurant, make for a table well inside away from the street vendors who will interrupt your meal with all sorts of goodies.

Visiting Hanoi ou myust stroll around the Old Quarter.  It is a series of  alleyways and streets that retains lots of the old colonial French buildings and is now a thriving market area. Beware of the traffic has a different meaning here as hundreds of bikes compete with cars and people.  They pay no attention to traffic signs and run in any direction even on one-way streets.  Just crossing a narrow alleyway is hazardous. There are a few modern conversions but most of the old buildings are badly neglected. Lots of knock-off goods for sale as well as traditionally embroidered linens and cheap clothes.  Look out for the 3D cut-out cards which are a item to buy: birthday cakes with candles,  Ho Chi Min Notre Dame detailed boats etc. Coffee and food shops abound  and noise and smells, not all bad, are everywhere.Traditional Vietnamese coffee is strong and  tasty.Some beans are roasted in butter which give the coffee a Mocha flavour. Its easy to see how they lived in the past here but sad to see the garish conversions and lack of historical preservation.
보행자 신호등은 몇 개 없는데 그나마 오토바이와 차들은 신호를 하나도 안 지킵니다. 밤새도록 경적을 울려댑니다.보행자 도로는 폭이 좁은데 그나마 오토바이와 각종 물건, 의자, 테이블 등으로 막혀 있어 통행이 무척 힘듭니다. 유일하게 편안했던 건 토요일에 호안끼엠호수 주변 도로들이 차량이 통제되어 보행자거리로 이용할 수 있었다는 것입니다.

I know some people absolutely love the old quarter and Hanoi in general.  I'm not one of those people.  I find it hot (and I come from tropical North Queensland so I know about heat!) and I never feel safe in Hanoi, unlike HCMC. The vendors are relentless and downright annoying (a million times worse than you find in HCMC). I've visited Vietnam every year for the last 6 years and been to many different places.  I revisited Hanoi again this year as last year we were very sick and couldn't enjoy it so we were hoping for a great visit.  Didn't happen.  We forfeited flights and our accommodation (which was prepaid and non refundable) purchased new tickets and returned to HCMC early.Like I said, some people love Hanoi and some like us, don't.  I won't be back again.

2018年8月にハノイに初めて行きました。ちょっと大袈裟なタイトルですが、ハノイのバイク事情については、行く前に聴いてはいたもののバイクの想像を絶する多さと、交通ルールを守らない国民性？に驚きました。ベトナムは他の東南アジア諸国に比べ勤勉で真面目と思っていただけにがっかりしました。そもそも、地下鉄などの公共交通設備が貧弱なため、バイクによる通勤に頼らざるを得ないのは一昔前の台北も同じでしたが、一向に改善しようしている様子もなく、このような状態を放置しているとこの国に未来はないとさえ思えます。結論から言うと、ハノイには二度と行きません。極めて不快な町でした。ただし、２泊３日でしたが。
Les scooters ont tout envahi de maniere anarchique, impossible de marcher sur les trottoirs, pasages pietons non respectes, klaxons de partout. Cela donne envie de fuir.

Chaque ruelle a une activité spécifique. Cependant il ne reste pas grande chose de la veille ville. La visite en cyclotourisme sans commentaire n’était pas bénéfique.Par contre la chaleur, le bruit des scooters et surtout les klaxonnes étaient difficiles à supporter.

Achei o lugar horrível, quase não tem calçadas e as poucas que tem são ocupadas com mercadorias ou motos. Horrível andar por ali, você tem que dividir a rua com os carros e as milhões de motos que não tem regra para transitar. Fui comprar souvenir, mas nem deu vontade de ver o que tinha para vender e acho que nem tinha loja de souvenir lá.
그 유명하다는 분짜닥킴  .저희는두세젓가락들고 바로내려놓았습니다.베트남음식이저희에겐 넘을수없는벽이었습니다.ㅠ.저희만그런가요?
호털이 편하고. 직원분들도. 친절하고 식사도 맛있고 잠자리도. 불편하지도안고 두분여성도. 매우친절했다. 청소도잘되있어고. 모둔면이 좋다 감사합니다

I stayed a month in Hanoi and this was my least favourite area. I stayed a few days in the den of tangled streets of the Old Quarter and it was a terrible experience  overall that I dreaded each day. I have travelled the world for years but this is definitely my least favourite area.What is fun about the cacophany of constant noise, mayhem and chaos? I was glad to move to another hotel on the periphery of the Old Quarter where I could experience the real Vietnam and walk easily to meet my Vietnamese friends at small family run restaurants, visit Fair Trade shops, the Temple of Literature, the Womens' Museum, stroll "Walking street" on the weekends and enjoy the music as well as the peacefulness of the Lake. I lived in Vietnam for 3 months this year and the noisy den of the Old Quarter for "beer and fun" is not the real Vietnam.

Je n'ai pas du tout apprécié ce vieux quartier des 36 corporations et son marché  de nuit du WE. Impossible de marcher sur les trottoirs occupés  à 100 % par les cyclo en stationnement. Obligé  de ce frayer un chemin sur la route avec les scooter vous frôlant en klaxonnant frénétiquement, les pousses pousses qui vous harcèlent, tout en faisant attention ne pas se fracasser les pieds sur les obstacles divers qui jonchent le sol). Hanoi quitté  au bout de deux longues journées, sans aucun regret. Je pourrai  en rajouter avec les odeurs, les tentatives d'arnaque (white taxe, erreur rendu monnaie) les rats présents meme en plein jour). Non, la vieille ville n'est absolument pas représentative des vietnamiens, qui sont à  99 % d'honnêtes gens et rendent avec gentillesse les sourires distribués.

Filthy place.Lots of crooks trying to con tourists.Very dangerous due to no code of conduct on highways.Not a place for elderly or young or for those who value their life.Would NEVER visit this part of Vietnam again.

Surtout ne pas se faire avoir par un tour en pousse pousse: unique raison les conducteurs qui sont désagréables et qui réclament des pourboires en dollars se montrant menaçant si ils trouvent que c'est insuffisant.par contre j'ai bien aimé la ballade dans le vieux quartier, me perdre dans les rues; etc.Hanoï n'est pas très propre, c'est bruyant, il y a une circulation intense: c'est le Vietnam urbain.
Une horreur: bruits, impossible de marcher correctement entre les voitures et scooters, saleté,polution extrême , magasins cheaps l’enfer! Aucun intérêt à Hanoï
河内感觉我不会再来第二次，城市乱，人也不友善，路上各种摩托车，看到人也完全不会让路的。每次过马路都像打仗！城市大型超市也没有，整体感觉比岘港落后，不值得停留！

Extremely dangerous to walk along and cross the streets. No proper foot paths. Cars and bikes darting all over.  Noisy, messy, smelly, stuff on the ground, fire hazard. Children, elderly and the physically challenged must be guided to avoid mishaps. Properly charming only to westerners based on the large numbers of F&B handouts for them to do people watching.
Is zeker een bezoek waard. Vele smalle straten met een verscheidenheid aan winkeltjes. Het krioelt er van de scooters.

Narrow streets that make you feel like you are risking your life every time you cross them. It’s actually quite hard work to walk through this area as you have to constantly walk on the road and be alert. No motorbikes, scooters or cars stop at cross roads and thus you have to ‘risk it’ and attempt to find a gap in the traffic to be able to cross the street. Due to locals sitting on the pavement on small chairs and parked bikes you cannot walk safely on the pavement either. Streets are also relatively dirty and our hotel told us pickpocketing is rife in the old quarter too!

Das Old Quarter ist sicherlich der beste Platz in Hanoi. + Einige schöne Cafés, Restaurants und Bars- viel zu laut, alle Hupen durchgängig um sich einen Weg durch Hanoi zu bahnen- da die Gehwege mit Mopeds vollstehen, muss man sich jederzeit über die Straße einen Weg bahnen j d wird daher durchgängig angehupt. - um über die Straße zu kommen, muss man viel Mut aufbringen, da von allen Seiten Mopeds kommen, die wenig Rücksicht nehmen- dreckig - wenig individuelle Geschäfte 3 Std reichen vollkommen für eine Besichtigung, um froh zu sein, endlich die Stadt verlassen zu dürfen.
Un casino assoluto impossibile camminare senza rischiare di morire tra i motorini un vero incubo non vedevo L ora di scappare via sono veramente dei criminali con i motorini

Mon pire souvenir du séjour. Nous sommes arrivés dans ce quartier un vendredi soir et c’était notre premier contact avec cette ville. Se déplacer dans ces rues est un cauchemar, la pollution extrême, le bruit et les odeurs n’aident pas et le traffic est oppressant. C’est effectivement quelque chose à vivre lors d’un passage à Hanoi mais sans s’y attarder.

Pollution terrible, ville extrêmement bruyante et donc fatigante, la circulation est complètement anarchique, les trottoirs défoncés, le vieux quartier n’a décidément pas su me charmer. Déception totale.

Overcrowded, noisy, dirty and packed with peddlers. Quite a negative experience. Don’t come to Hanoi for the old quartier experience. Quite sad and high chance u will get run over. The rest of the city was splendid.

Hotel convenable avec tout confort nécessaire.  Rosie est une personne aimable souriante toujours à l'écoute des clients et à leur service. Très bonne organisatrice concernant les visites touristiques dans Hanoï. Je conseille fortement cet hôtel.
오토바이들이 무법지로 다니고 미친놈들마냥 빵빵거리면서 거의 사람 칠라하는 곳이에요 카미카제마냥 달려드는데 왠만하면 가지마세요

This is not my place definitely. Noisy, chaotic, dirty, shabby . I didn’t expect that, I really thought that the this was a pleasant place to visit. But the constant and dangerous traffic make it hard to enjoy. Moreover, it is not well kept and historical buildings are decadent. Not my place

1. 오토바이- 심각하게 많고, 스트레스 받아 몸과 정신이 매우 예민하고 짜증. 오토바이가 상전이고, 인도나 도로는 오토바이가 장악, 목숨을 내놓고 길을 걸어다녀야 함.클락션을 짜증나게 울려대서 성질 매우 더러워지는 자신을 발견. 절대 뛰지마세요, 차가 오는 방향을 항상 주시하세요.2. 더러움- 후진국이 후진국했다는데 문제될것은 없지만, 기본적인 위생관념이 현저히 부족.  길거리음식 절대 먹지마요. 저렴하다고 먹다가 병원비 털려요3. 호객행위 - 관광지 어딜가나 호객꾼, 사기꾼 천국이지만... 안산다고하면 그냥 좀 가라.
Our hotel, La Siesta, was so central to walk anywhere, food great, outstanding service and the staff , Nguyen,  and everyone were so helpful.....
오토바이로 횡당보도는 무실하고, 차와 오토바이의 소음과 상점의 바가지요금등이 여행내내 지치게 했습니다.

Впервую очередь бросается в глаза множество припаркованных на тротуаре мотороллеров, причем припаркованных так, что ходить не возможно. Когда идешь по дороге некто не уступает пешеходам путь, от этого приходится постоянно уворачиваться от едущих мотоциклистов. Переход дороги это аттракцион не для слабонервных, мало того, что не уступают дорогу на зебре, так еще и едут по встречке, не соблюдают рядность движения, в общем, никаких правил. Из ПДД только одно правило работает безотказно, чем больше автомобиль, тем он главнее. Старый квартал пестрит множеством лавок в которых продают всякие тряпки и сувениры, ассортимент исключительно для туристов и практически одинаков везде. Имеется множество кафешек в которых можно купить похлебку с лапшой и «удобно» расположиться на детском стульчике прямо на тротуаре. При этом возникает впечатление, что эти все заведения общепита соревнуются у кого меньше табуретка. Бросается в глаза отсутсвие доброжелательности при посещении большей части кафешек. Не говорю уже о лучезарной улыбке при вхождении меня в кафе, но не подают соусы, приправы, хотя на соседнем столике все это есть и местному населению это все приносили. Да и санитарные условия сомнительные, посуда моется на тротуаре рядом с кафе. Плюсом старого квартала могу назвать большое количество гостиниц на любой вкус.

Visit superfluous. Tourists annoying, hawkers desperate, stores arrogant. Feel sorry for anyone who needs to walk down this beaten path instead of exploring the real Hanoi and its people. Or checking out the country side, dog forbid.

This place has been destroyed by mass tourism - it used to be quiet and romantic - it now mad with people and full of shops selling fake brands and dodgy food to unsuspecting tourist who think this Vietnam.

Not much to see or do here. Streets are home for many shops of different kinds, but nothing of a special interest. The pavements are difficult to walk  because they are either broken or blocked by motorcycles. There are restaurants here and there but they lack the vivid atmosphere of their peers in Bangkok. Add to this the unberable heat and humidity and you have a recepie for a gloomy walk in the streets. This is the main attraction in Hanoi but you shouldn't plan spending more than a few hours here.
No real old town. Just narrow streets with excellent street food restaurants and imitation branded products maid Inn vietnam

Yo he estado en varios países asiáticos y para mí ésto no tiene ningún atractivo, un barrio absolutamente caótico, con una circulación colapsada lleno de coches y motos, con una dificultad enorme para andar por las calles porque las aceras están llenas de motos aparcadas o llenas de tenderetes y todo muy sucio. Realmente    esto gusta ? A mi en absoluto, hay muchos barrios en países asiáticos con mucho más encanto. A gustos colores. Y desde el punto de vista turístico poco que ver. Para mi de lo peor que he visto en Vietnam.

Great for the 18-25 crowd looking for cheap beers, cheap pizza and sweaty spotty new friends.  Its busy and awful - fake clothes and shoe shops, sleazy massage places - only fun if that's what you like.

Many tourist walking along with injuries caused either from motorbikes or the footpaths. Your forced to put your lives into a dangerous situation as motorbikes cover up all of the footpaths in most areas and if their not the locals sit on the street blocking your path. I was hit by a motorbike as getting out of a van after a tour, she knocked me over and kept on going, nice. I wouldn't walk around with kids sorry to valuable to lose over really bad road rules and their disrespect for tourists.

Queria informaros de lo que me acaba de pasar. Me he alojado en este establecimiento Paper Nest (en el barrio viejo) y dentro hay ratas y cucarachas.  No llegando a estar ni 24horas alojada, la policía llamó a la puerta con un intérprete. Nos comunicaron que el alojamiento era ilegal. No era la primera vez que había pasado lo que allí nos estaba sucediendo a nosotros . Acabamos en la comisaría como testigos, perdiendo el tiempo y con el consiguiente disgusto que eso provoca más cuando uno está en el extranjero y más cuando uno está en Asia. Tras la denuncia, la policía obligó al propietario a devolvernos el importe total de la reserva. El propietario tiene varios casos abiertos y se ve que no cumple los estándares del Gobierno de Vietnam. La entrada a la habitación es terrorífica, oscura, grotesca, suciedad por todos lados,con goteras y hasta la policía presenció una rata mientras hacíamos las maletas para irnos a la comisaría .Entran cucarachas del tamaño de un dedo.Me gustaría que se tomaran medidas contra este alojamiento para que no le pase a futuros huéspedes. No quiero que nadie pase por lo que yo pasé. Vengo de vacaciones a disfrutar, no a acabar en una comisaría porque el dueño del apartamento nos estafe.Las fotos no se corresponde con lo que es. Es todo un engaño. ¿Por qué no pones fotos de la entrada?Es el peor sitio donde jamas haya estado. La cama es una piedra. Muy dura. La puerta de acceso al apartamento es de cristal y se cierra con un candado. ¿Qué clase de seguridad es esa?Ya solo con las explicaciones que me dio la policia me vale. Nosotros tras el paso por la comisaría acabamos en otro hotel. Por favor, no reservéis si queréis que esto no puede pasar. La gente va de vacaciones no a una comisaría. Lo pasamos realmente mal, llorando...esto no es serio_I wanted to inform you of what has just happened to me. I have stayed in this establishment Paper Nest in Old quarter where there are rats and cockroaches. 24 hours later, the police knocked on the door with an interpreter. We were informed that the accommodation was illegal. It was not the first time said the police. We end up in the police station as witnesses, wasting time and with the consequent displeasure that causes more when one is abroad and in Asia.After the complaint, the police forced the owner to refund the full amount of the reservation.Pictures showed dont show how is the apt.The owner has several cases open and it is seen that it does not meet the standards of quality of the Government of Vietnam. The entrance to the hotel is terrifying, dark, leaking and even the police witnessed a rat while we packed to go to the police station.It's the worst place I've ever been. The bed is a stone. Very hard. The access door to the apartment is glass and is closed with a padlock. What kind of security is that?Already only with the explanations that the police gave meI would like  that does not happen again to any other guest.I do not want anyone to go through what I went through. I come on vacation to enjoy, not to end up in a police station because the owner of the apartment spoils us.We after the passage through the police station we ended up in another hotel. I do not recommend it. Really, look for another place. Seriously, guys, go to hotels. Vietnam is not a safe place and this Paper Nest is the example.
Shopping/souvenir haven. Can find almost everything in the old quarter. Bargainable prices, very authentic local vibes. Easily lost in the endless winding lanes.
Any type of motor would not stop for people and not really much of the walk way. There are no mid-ranging dining available here only hole in the wall.
Restaurants,shopping, bars. Close to main old city attractions,basically walking 🚶 distance. Night market, free Internet in the all places we visited.

Me ha decepcionado. Todo El Barrio es como un bazar gigante, imposible andar por la acera, llenas de motos y obstáculos. El tráfico es absolutamente caótico y ruidoso. El calor no ayuda para pasear por la calle. Algún restaurante agradable, pero la visita al barrio me defraudó.
Enjoyed exploring the various shops. Not too much pressure to buy and no begging which was a relief.

My initial response to this area was that I had made a grave error and missed the turn. I felt that I had travelled back in time. It is just so different to other parts of modern Hanoi. However i found that I had made a great choice in my error. I saw Hanoi as it was many rears ago, a place without the glitz and shine, the true life style of the Vietnamese people. It was indeed a wonderful experience.Not many spoke my language , but who cares, I had great fun exploring and took up the challenge of trying to establish some common verbal contact.Make sure you have plenty of space on your flash card, you will be in for some incredible sights
臭いがすごいのと雑然感がすごいです。売ってるものもイマイチだし、わざわざ訪れる意味がないと思います。価格交渉が好きなら、それ目的で行くのはいいと思います。

I had to risk my life walking around and my friend was pickpocketed once. I had a guy who just took my shoes off and glued it when I refused it. He repaired it and six other people surrounded me to sell me something.

Disgusting dirty place with millions of scooters that literally run over tourists, never stop and hit my friend when we tried to cross the street on the green light. The food in the local restaurants is terrible, I would not even feed pigs with it. The souvenirs are way overpriced and there are very little variety, we did not buy any at all, the biggest ladies shoe size is 37 when the Europeans mostly have 38-40, massage saloons look more but houses for prostitution with dirty dusty beds that my friend was coughing from for 3 days after and believed she got lice in her hair. Everyone is only concerned to rip you off money and beg for tips which they do not deserve. There is nothing to see in the old quarter and nothing to do. Worst city in the world !

Horrible place, dirty, disgusting and dangerous.  From the moment we arrived, we hated it and couldn't wait to leave!  Medieval conditions in the extreme.  Really surprised compared to Saigon - Hanoi is our last leg of a month long tour, wished we stayed longer in Saigon, Da Nang or Hoi An.  This is supposed to be the capital city but REALLY VERY unpleasant.  Good luck with the traffic if you decide to come - honestly, you're gonna need it!!!
Too crowd, too polluted, too noisy. Motorbikes drive over the sidewalks, park on the side of your table. Merchandises are cheep but quality is poor. It doesn't worth a visit.

A terrible place and neither picturesque nor romantic at all.Never seen so much filth and dirt  nor rats the size of a poodle like in this place.Probably I am not romantic enough but the old quarter is just DISGUSTING.No way even to walk, literally no way because the motorbikes are either all parked on the sidewalks or just run over you ,even coming from the wrong side.Ambulant peddlers,hawkers, they touch you, sell you anything, see you as a cash cow.I do imagine the gates if hell like this.What a corrupted place this was.....never again.Truly hated it. Looked like a movie set from "the life of brian"Terrible ...omg so bad

An amazing place to wander through, just watch out for traffic, mainly scooters. They weave round you so just walk on. Lots of restaurants and bars, mostly cater for locals, but there are more tourists ones. Friendly peopl.

Terrivel. Assim se imagina os portoes do inferno. Jamais vi um lugar mais sujo, cheio de lixo ,com pessoas mais ansiosas de te roubar ou te vender alguma coisa. Verdadeiramente Sodom e Gomorrha. Tanta sujeira nao e bucolico...e simplesmente uma vergonha para a raça humana. Sujeira, ratos, barulho, mochileiros, ladroes....TERRIVEL LUGAR

Super tasty beef Pho at the old quarter, and beautiful hoan kiem lake. BUT just that.Traffic is dangerously chaotic, and the streets are very dirty. Even though there were so many street cleaners working around the street, sticky substances(vomit,beverage spills,organic waste)were everywhere.

Il peggio dell'Oriente che, solitamente, piace tanto a noi occidentali. Sporco, caotico, con incroci dove si rischia ogni volta la vita, rumoroso, con autisti e motociclisti con la mano incollata al clacson ma privi del piede per frenare. I marciapiedi inservibili perché occupati da moto, attività, tavolini e gente che cucina e mangia tra la spazzatura e i topi morti. Qualche abitazione più antica delle altre preservata ma, nel complesso, una miriade di negozietti di pattume Made in China spacciato per artigianato, alberghi decadenti e fatiscenti, agenzie di viaggio che propongono tutte gli stessi tour, qualche ristorantino più chic e qualche altro qualità/prezzo decente, il night market da venerdì a domenica. Solo per un giro, tanto per capire tutto quello che una città non dovrebbe essere, a Est come a Ovest.

Старый квартал - один большой вокзальной рынок, с мусором и ужасными товарами.Покушать тоже нечего - они используют ужасное масло при готовке, чувство что на нём в мак даке тонну картошки прежде пережарили
Used to think Cairo and Manila were dirty, wait until you visit Hanoi Old Quarter! That's not "culture", it's filthy and uncivilized!

If you want get mauled by a horde of crazy moppets motorcycles this is the place to go. Otherwise the shopping is not worth the noise and the pollution. Yes you can find good deals but the same stuff than anywhere else.

We hated the Old Quarter. We stopped in Hanoi last after spending a couple of weeks exploring Vietnam's cities, starting in the south and ending here. This was by far the worst part of our trip. The quarter is very crowded, and the street vendors are extremely aggressive. If you are interested in seeing the "market" aspect of the quarter, it's honestly not very different than any other central city in the country, and the others are much more pleasant. At night, the streets are filled with tourists drinking, eating, and shopping on the streets they shut down for pedestrian access. Our hotel was nice, but it was still loud every night. I wish we had booked elsewhere in the city - the other quarters seemed quite nice.

Do not bother going into the Old Quarter there is just nothing to see and its expensive and not worth the time. Lots of hawkers and people trying to rip you off. Including a guy that tries to glue your shoe as you walk then demands money. Just be firm and tell them to go away. Thats if you can cross the road, good luck with that.

Motorcycles and some inconsiderate people block most pavements so you have to walk out into the roads where motorcyclists who don't give a damn come close to running you over. You find you spend less time looking in shops than passing people on bikes do. I would not go back to this area again.

I planned to go to the old quarter. Just the name of it makes me imagined differently. There were no authentic houses or any of Hanoi styles to visit. There are all souvenir shops where they overpriced. Since the rent was so high, which made the whole old quarter idea ruined. I strongly do not recommend any tourists to come here, unless they want some overpriced souvenirs.

Almost got hit by a scooter twice. The scooters owns the town and you need to look everywhere to not get hit. Not a comfortable walking. The air quality is horrifying!! You can't breathe without cough. There is fog in the air because of the air pollutions! I understand why they have breathing masks...... I visited blogger cities in China that was much better air beacuse all the scooters there were electrical. Something for Hanoi to think about! Not a nice town to visit.
在河內，真的要小心當地的的士司機，我們親身經歷在車上被搶了錢，他還睜大眼睛說我們付錯面額鈔票。如果可以避免，我覺得不太值得遊覽這個地方，沒有很多吸引的景點，只是某些食物還可以。在河內，電單車是很可怕的多，過路時很危險，很多電單車穿插身邊。
הרובע העתיק הוקם במטרה להעניק שרותים אך ורק לחצר הלכות והוא ניקרא שלשים ושש הרחובות כשלכל רחוב היה מישלח יד אחד.מקסים לטייל באזור

We had high hopes for Hanoi given all the terrific reviews. Saigon we liked, Bangkok and Phnom Pen we loved so we are used to the hassle of an Asian city...but omg is this smelly, dirty, noisy, crowded and honestly nothing that compensates... No nice temples, buildings, food, souvenirs...we wanted to stay longer in our old quater hotel but escaped to West Lake. Never again!

The quarter gets very active on the weekends and is worth a visit during those days! The night markets, lights on the lake, people frolicking around on the streets (where no vehicles are allowed on the weekend evenings) - all add to the charm of the place. And you can pick up great bargains from the night market as well.

We arrived in OQ and was trapped by an old trishaw rider. He followed us and tried to show us a 1-hour ride in OQ area. We ended up ok with his offer of 300,000 VND. At the end of the trip I realised that it's only 40-min ride. I was so generous enough not to mention to him because you will really get boring after 15 minutes. I also thought of tipping him some after the trip. When we finished our trip, we confirmed with him of 300,000 VND but he said that price was per person. We finally paid him 600,000 VND. When he saw my unhappy face he gave me 100,000 VND discount but I told him I don't want because it's a learning for me to know how bad the Vietnamese people are (only OQ area). Do not get trapped in OQ in everything, shopping was overpriced.
There are some good restaurants. But to busy to walk. very crowded.this Area might be more for backpackers.

It's impossible to walk on the side walk because motorcycles and locals eating block every foot. When you try to walk on the paved road, you have to dodge non-stop motor cycles and scooters. They completely ignore "walk" signs. Fortunately for us we avoided getting rundown although we had many close calls.

Cela fait partie de l'aventure dans les grandes villes du Vietnam j'imagine. Mais j'ai trouvé le vieux quartier laid. Son intérêt ne réside que dans son âge, mais il est clairement insupportable. On se fait en permanence alpaguer, en tant que dollars sur pattes occidentaux pour des tours en motorbikes. Hormis la fameuse frousse à traverser la rue, on ne peut marcher sur les trottoirs car ils sont pris par les restaurants de rue et les mobylettes qui s'y gare. Bref, cela fait partie de l'ambiance d'Hanoi, mais il n'y a rien à voir et c'est un quartier étouffant.

Simply hated this whole area.  It is unsafe to walk down the streets.  The sidewalks are used to park motorcycles, and are clogged a shops so you half to walk on the road the merchants are aggressively, the drivers are ignorant and have no respect for pedestrians or any traffic laws.

This place absolutely stinks and is filthy. The people are rude and want to run you over. People are constantly trying to rip you off just because you are white. People will literally take a huge bag of rubbish out of there shop and just dump it on the pavement. Maybe there is a serious culture clash here but as a westerner I found this place revolting.

I seldom give negative reviews, but i just had to this time to warn tourists visiting Hanoi Old Quarter to beware of conmen; shoe repair conmen and the trishaw riders. I have to admit that I did not do enough research going to Hanoi as I thought that it was just like any other ASEAN country. I have visited Indonesia, Malaysia, China, Hong Kong, Brunei and Thailand; none of these places prepared me for the nonchalant scammers in Hanoi old quarter. Please beware and do your research before visiting this place...google Hanoi Shoe Repair scam. this should give you information you need. I almost came to blows with the scammer, thankfully managed to get out of the scam.

Quelle déception!! Et dire qu'ils veulent le classer par l'Unesco!!!Bruyant comme toute la ville d'Hanoi d'ailleurs, les vieilles échoppes n'ont ni le charme des anciennes maisons boutiques chinoises ni d'intérêt architectural ou esthétique...Pour les belles échoppes chinoises dans le "jus" suranné de la période coloniale, Hoi An restera décidément beaucoup plus belle à découvrir et à y flâner...
오토바이가 진짜 극혐입니다. 노이로제 걸릴정도로 많아요. 길 건너기도 힘들고 길 걷는 거도 힘들고 공기도 너무 나빠요. 목이 아플정고로. 다시는 가고싶지 않네요.
Puede parecer una calle comercial pero en realidad es una calle con poca variedad y bastante sucia por más que regatees ellos no están por la labor y para comprar mucho mejor Bangkok
I arrived at lake area at 10:10pm, looking for some food. No way, no Domino, no Burger King, no Pizza. Everybody start to clean at 10:00pm even around is plenty of people.

I don't mind the crazy traffic. I don't mind the chaos. I don't mind having to walk on the street and facing being run over constantly. But, OMG, the never ending rip off was more draining than the awful heat! I bargained as suggested by other posts but I still felt the stuff is SUPER overpriced. I am Chinese-Vietnamese-Canadian who can still speak and understand a bit of Vietnamese so I can tell you that they don't just ripoff Westerners.For example, I forgot to bring my hat one day and decided to just get one at the OQ. The same sun visor that I bought in Walmart, in Canada, for $4.99 was offered to me for $14US! The same silk scarf, but in a different color, that I bought at a huge gift shop on my way to Halong Bay cost me $20US, but at Khai Silk it was $90US with no bargaining. The same scarf was $29.95US a couple of stores down from Khai. So, in the end, I ended up not buying anything in the OQ even though I had budgeted a very generous shopping allowance.The food is the next ripoff. To be honest, majority of the food/drink prices are still way cheaper compare to Canada. BUT, one has to do an apple to apple comparison not an apple to orange. For example, 2 cups of fresh squeezed sugar cane juice cost me 50,000d in OQ but only 20,000d near the Marriott hotel I stayed at, which is located in a pricier part of Hanoi. The plums, mangosteen, passion fruit, and jackfruit that I bought at BigC grocery store was 30% to 3 times cheaper than those at the OQ. The worst food ripoff that I experienced was when I ordered 6 bbq chicken wings. It was my fault because I didn't asked him for the price before I ordered because it was my first day there. The 6 wings cost me 150,000d which at that time was equivalent to $9CAD. OMG, even 6 wings ordered in Canada don't cost that much. To give a comparison, a few night after, my hubby and I went to the bbq place that everyone on TripAdvisor recommended at the end of "The BBQ Chicken Street". We ate a ton of wings, thighs, and honeyed bread with 2 sugar cane juice and they only cost us a bit over 200,000d.I did buy a few items of clothing in shops locals frequent and the prices were very fair. Unfortunately, I don't know the locations or stores I shopped at because they were often places I tell the taxi to stop on my way to the attraction of the day.Honestly, the OQ is an awesome place to explore and to take in the craziness but shopping is a strong CAVEAT EMPTOR.

Alleys are so narrow, having to constantly watch the traffic while walking, while being honked at.. many shops selling multiples of the same stuffs. Perhaps its better to do more cafe-hopping in Hanoi than shopping at the Old Quarter...
Really have no idea what the hype is about this place, dirty smelly felt like I was in the slums and crossing the roads is a death wish

You can't come to Hanoi & not visit the old quarter. It's complete hustle & bustle. You will find sellers selling everything from pictures, jewellery, to fake the North Face tshirts , jackets and bags. Haggling on price is a most if you want to get a bargain. There are plenty of restaurants & pubs here to stop & have a rest. Definitely worth a stroll through

Fare una recensione della vecchia Hanoi, la stessa vecchia città che vidi per la prima volta nel 1989 e che ho poi visitato regolarmente ogni 2 anni è doloroso. Piace il successo che ha e la ricchezza relativa di coloro che hanno un locale in questa area. Ma ogni magia se ne è andata, divorata da un turismo junk che chiede nulla o poco al posto. I negozi - che un tempo non avevano nulla - ora hanno 4 articoli in croce, sempre quelli. Adatti a un turismo che non chiede nulla a parte di essere economica. La domanda è se potrebbe essere fatto qualcosa. Forse basterebbe dare disposizione che le insegne dei negozi non coprano le belle facciate francesizzanti della città vecchia. Non ci vorrebbe molto. Ma cambierebbe poco per molto turismo che chiede solo wifi, musica e spendere poco. Perchè poi uno debba venire da tutto il mondo a Hanoi con richieste così modeste resta un mistero. Tristezza e turismo junk. Da evitare se possibile.

The place is awesome with lots of things to see.things we dont normally find in most country.The street food is also good steaming hot and watch people go by every minute with a hot cup of coffee..by the road side with so many lanes to walk  in the old quarters its amazing to see the local do their business, working , eating, shopping in their daily life !!

Love this part of the city - busy and hectic but also charming. Great market stalls, little historic buildings and great street food too!  Lovely lake. Nice to wander and get lost in! Just be careful of the scooters/ motor bikes (Hanoi is full of them and they will run you over, even when your on the pavement)
Many vendors around me. They are inconvenience and i feel uncomfortable when they follow me all time

Complete tourist trap.  More foreigners than locals. The local vendors are very polite and will give you a big smile while the rip you off and short change you! Keep your hand on your wallet and count your change CAREFULLY!

Our hotel was in the old quarter so couldn't aviod It, overcrowded, lots of traffic especially motor bikes, unable to walk on pavements due to parked motor bikes can't access many shops or stalls due to parked bikes very difficult to cross road. Very noisy, consent beeping of cars and motor bikes.Not a place I would revisit.

In my 4 months in Asia, here is the first time were almost run me over 5 times in les than 5 hours walking around. The street food... the worse in Vietnam. I ended the day very stress because of the people driving around everyone and by everywere. Caos.
Don't shop at HONG ANH shoe shop the staff is very dishonest !!! Check you note before you hand over the amount as their will give you less change.

All the guidebooks make Hanoi sound charming and beautiful, like a little European city. It's dirty, you can't even walk on the sidewalks there is so much stuff and motorbikes. Skip this city at all costs and head to sapa or elsewhere for better shopping and better views.

Narrow, crowded, dirty and full of grumpy unfriendly people. There is nothing positive to say. Lots of tourist tat shops, lovely pavements used as moped parks leaving pedestrians to walk in the road with all the crazy drivers.Lots of small plastic stools of people perched eating un-hygienic food that smells horrid.We did the walking route recommended in the lonely planet book and so I think we saw most of it and we just hated all of it. We had expected to love it as we have loved all the rest of Vietnam! Give this a miss and explore the rest of Vietnam instead
Wander until your hearts content and then look at the map to find your way back. The footpaths are full of motorbikes so you will need to walk on the road.
車道、歩道にはバイクが溢れ、生ごみが散乱し、お店の商品は古くいつの物かもわからないような感じでした。私は無理でした。
アジアンチックな雰囲気が色濃く残る旧市街は1人でも楽しめます。アジアン雑貨がかなり安くで手に入るのでここぞとばかり買いました。また最初はふっかけてくるので、ガンガン値切るのを勧めます。またマッサージも、かなり安いです^_^たくさん散策してベトナムコーヒーで喉を潤し、フォーと、生春巻きで空腹を満たし疲れた手足をマッサージで癒すといいですよ^_^

Sure, it has small streets and old looking buildings, but it's full of honking scooters, angry drivers, cheap accommodations, massage parlors, gift shops selling the same taudry things, all for foreign consumption only. If you love Khao San, Kuta, Jalan Sastrowijayan, you will love this Old Looking Quarter.  But then again, what else is there in Hanoi.

Great food. try the vegetables clay pot noodle soup. the resurant have a great waiter name Diep. she is very kind and speaking English very good. we came back day after day, and enjoy a lovely dinner. all and all the place has great food, kind and nice staff members and great background music. very recommended!

Couldn't wait to get out of this haggle city tourist trap!! Honestly, stop over an then leave ASAP unless u like a honking city an haggling!! Head north to sapa, much more relaxing an in winter is so magical..My partner an myself like more relaxing when on holidays..
There are trashes everywhere, no street lights, no traffic signals , no yield signs. Motor cycles rampage on the street. There are no buses, no metro station, they are 100 years behind time.

Kota ini. Boleh dibilang tempat kumpulnya para wisatawan .terlebih lagi backpacker. Sehingga ramai sekali. Tapi sayang. Lalu lintasnya kurang teratur. Kita harus extra hati2 jika hendak menyebrang jalan.

Can be annoying, it's over an over again.. As soon as u leave ur hotel it's all around u. Locals are nicer on the outer sides. Be careful of ur things also, pick pocketing!! Everything coast more here, we ended up walking out of here day an night to have dinner an meet locals, can sit back an jus watch rather then be haggled by the ladies walking around. Shopping is cheaper out of here to, nicer ppl an not so hectic!!!! Is honk honk honk honk. We stayed at Hanoi guest house, was a 10/10 for everything there, staff an rooms, location!! But jus noisy
交通非常混亂,很少紅綠燈, 走路要非常小心,喇叭聲此起彼落挺嚇人的.........商店街大多沒開冷氣,很悶熱,街道 到處都有垃圾很髒亂.
The worst room I have ever slept in. Shower is a pipe from the taps on the sink.air condition leaking down the wall.and it goes on. Just don't stay here. You have been warned.

Not the best place to visit as it is just  cater for the wholesaler community. The people of Hanoi has weaker english command than HCMC. When you try to buy stuff speaking in english, they might react in hostile manner as they have no idea what you are talking about

I have been to a lot of the big cities in SEA, but none of it is as crazy as the Old Quarter.Motorcycles would just speed by inches away from you; not to mention the incessant honking, which would ring in your ears for hours. Having said that, it's an incredible eye opening experience, and a humbling one as well, to see the Vietnamese people making do with what they have.

I found the Old Quarter to be dirty and crowded. Lots of shops selling items of dubious quality and origin. Local people sitting in the dirty streets eating and drinking. Impossibly crowded streets with lots of scooters, bikes and mopeds parked everywhere blocking your path as you attempt to walk around. Sleazy guys in your face trying to sell you wallets and lighters. Unfriendly and unhelpful faces everywhere. I have traveled the world and this place was not quaint or charming. I had been looking forward to seeing the so called charming French inspired architecture. Take care as my female friend was mugged in Hanoi by a sleazebag on a motorbike.

Crowded, hot and grubby (?normal for Hanoi). Prices for most things start v.high but can be bartered down to UK High Street prices but as most things are fake there doesn't seem much point. Watch out for the "Shoe Repair Scam" - a man will grab your shoe as you walk by and squirt liquid in the seam. This happens so fast you cannot stop it, he will then claim your shoe needs immediate repair and attempt to remove it from your foot. If you allow this to happen you cannot recover the situation without parting with cash to get it back. Apparently the liquid is a solvent, my sandals were made of leather stitched with cotton so no permanent harm done!
うるさい危ない臭い観光客値段で高い、人力車みたいなのに乗りましたが感動はいっさいなく、早くおりたかった。ベトナムの人の生活を垣間見ることはできますが、買い物は値段が高くハロン湾の船の中で買ったものと同じでした。殆どの店の物は同じところからの仕入れかも。

Nightmare of a city, was only here couple of days, visa run out of BKK. Dont want to judge vietnam on one quick visit but it seemed everyone was trying to scam and con you. Certainly avoid "The Sihn Cafe Tourist" we booked the worst tour iv ever been on with these guys. Old quater hard to walk around, million scooters everywhere, bit like bali. If you want to cross rd you literaly have to step in front of traffic and make them stop, dont worry they do stop.
Over-run with Ruperts and Camillas on their braying gap yahs. Pavements stuffed with mopeds. Roads jammed with motorbikes spewing out pretty nasty fumes. Forget it. It's gone.

There is nothing in this city that is worth visiting.  The city is dirty and noisy with the constant honking and no respect for pedestrian crossings.  All food signs are not in  English and the street vendors can't even tell you the cost of the food.  All the attractions are mediocre and uninteresting including the dirty, dark green looking Hoam Kiem Lake.  This will be the last city I will recommend to anyone other than a stopover to Halong Bay.

This was very typical of many SE Asian markets where you will be harassed by sellers into buying some mass produced in China and overpriced piece of junk. Garbage everywhere, so watch where you step and the scooters and motorcycles will deliberately try to knock you off your feet, so hopefully you're not past the age of forty. If you happen to dodge potholes in the side walk and bricks from dilapidated buildings falling on top of your head, you will certainly end up in the hospital after eating street food. Pots and pans sitting on the ground amid garbage that are later used to cook your food, mangy dogs and cats licking meat juice off knives and cutting boards that are later used to prepare your vegetables, again unwashed due to lack of running water. I watched one of the street food vendors wear blood and dirt stained cotton gloves to pick up freshly fried donuts and hand them to tourists. As a healthcare professional I don't know how anybody could eat this food and not get sick, even the basic hygiene requirements do not exist here. There is a very small nicer area to walk around the Sofitel Metropol and look at the architecture, they also have a few nice restaurants there, otherwise waste of time.

Hanoi might look beautiful and scenic but the people here are absolutely rude, atrocious and major thugs. We were conned by 2-3 different fruit sellers, the tuk tuk gyys and the money exchangers etc.. People in this city are super money minded and are extremely arrogant; they start yelling and screaming at tourists and after experiencing the same 3-4 times we finally got the hang of their behaviour. Having been to so many other countries , this was by far the most arrogant country visited. We were in Ho Chi min city and it was way more friendly, down to earth, friendly and hospitable. All I can say is be alert in the city or be ready to be tricked and fooled!

Feria americana horrible. Llena de cosas de mala calidad, nada que ver con los mercaditos nocturnos del resto de Asia. Encima no sé qué les pasa a la gente que vende que se enoja si no comprás.Las motos no te dejan caminar tranquilo, todo el tiempo tocando bocina y las tenés que dejar pasar, sino te atropellan.Hanoi es un caos, una ciudad realmente fea y con gente muy poco amable. Una pena

Aucun plaisir à s'y balader, il faut en permanence faire attention à la circulation dans un bruit ambiant épouvantable. Et je ne parle pas des vendeurs et moto-taxi qui harcèlent les touristes tout les 2 mètres...

Hanoi is a fascinating place to visit and we are glad we came. The terrible rating is because we were victims of a pickpocket (which could happen anywhere). Please heed this warning: WATCH YOUR WALLETS!!!!!!!!!!!!! We are staying in the old quarter and visited the night markets today, and my partner had his wallet pick pocketed. I say this to warn other people, as you do NOT want this experience happening to you. Please take heed. Wear backpacks on your front, keep handbags strung diagonally across your shoulders rather than on one side where it can easily be snatched, and in front of you where you can see it.

I was expecting something different. I never let my guard down nor could relax enough to enjoy any of it. I felt like frogged trying to cross the streets, the traffic was non-stop and little to no traffic lights. The mopeds where everywhere... Zipping from behind, left right and front. Then the hustlers were everywhere. Somebody took my shoe and attempted to resolve my shoe. I took my shoe from the young man who took it and he demanded that I pay him. I said no. He continued to yell swear words at me and in front of my children. Then the ladies selling the donuts were in our face non-stop. Don't take a sample or they will demand payment despite the, saying free samples. Avoid allowing them to place their hats on your head, as they will then offer to take picture with them, and then demand a payment. It was pure stress at every corner

J'ai détesté. Avant de m'y rendre, j'avais lu dans des guides touristiques que le vieux quartier était plein de charme et je le lis également sur cette page. Nous n'avons pas tous la même définition du mot "charme" et quelque part, c'est tant mieux. Notre hôtel était dans ce quartier et nous y avons donc passé du temps. Nous étions en permanence dans le bruit. Un conseil : n'y allez pas en robe avec de petites chaussures de ville à talons. Portez plutôt des chaussures de randonnée montantes et des pantalons de trekking. Les trottoirs (que dis-je, les rues - car les trottoirs sont réservés aux scooters et aux chaises et tables en plastique) sont tellement sales qu'il vaut mieux ne pas se découvrir. Vous êtes obligés de marcher sur les voies de circulation mais là, vous vous faites klaxonner par les scooters (ceci dit, que vous soyez sur la chaussée ou non, ils klaxonnent ! C'est leur manière de vivre. Attendez-vous à un parcours du combattant car traverser une rue est un défi. Ne croyez pas que parce que vous êtes sur un passage clouté, ils vont vous laisser passer. Non, ils vous klaxonnent, ils vous contournent mais ils ne s'arrêtent JAMAIS. N'y allez pas avec des enfants en bas âge. Ce quartier est et restera un mauvais souvenir pour moi.
disliked the whole place..would not recommend this hotel to anybody..i am not happy one little bit the only good thing is the reception staff smile as you walk in the door
We found this place grey and busy, smelly and depressing. Too close , too much traffic, depressed people . After our other Vietnam and Cambodian experience this was awful..hated the place
There is about 7 million inhibitant and there is more than 7 million motor bikes...Almost every bike rider wears a mask...that's how bad it is in Hanoi.

Dirty, noisy, chaotic.  Zero respect for pedestrians.  Cannot think of any reason to visit Hanoi except to pick up the transport for Halong Bay.  Impossible to walk on the pavement for parked scooters and crossing the road meant walking into the path of hundreds of scooters or stay where you are.
구시가지는 좋지만 이곳의 상인들은 아닙니다. 우리를 호구로 알아요ㅎㅎㅎ외국인에게 바가지가 심합니다. 의류의 경우 만약 25만동을 부른다면 십만동이면 될거에요. 가격흥정하실때 반값이하로 깍으세요!!!무조건!저희는 동쑤언시장에서 바가지썼네요....ㅎㅎㅎ베트남상인들한테 비웃음당하는기분이 참 더러우니 여러분은 이런 피해 안당하시길 바래요^^....... .

The place is losing its "antique look". The merchandises selling there is no longer from traditional workshops around Hanoi. Most of them are low quality/fake goods imported from China. The people there are no longer nice and welcoming. All they do is trying to rip off tourists and taking up place on the street to sell their goods.

The streets are dirty because street hawkers simply wash their bowls and cutleries in to the roadside drains. Motorbikes are plenty and rowdy, riding in any direction as they like although the roads are left-hand drive. Pedestrian walkways are difficult to walk as they are either blocked or cluttered with parked motorbikes, some shops even park their motorbikes to block off pedestrians from crossing their shop-front. Beware of pickpockets and muggers operating in pairs on motorbikes targeting solo backpackers on quiet streets, I encountered such attempts myself.

Everyone keeps saying "this is where the action is," but all we found here was honking, crappy bars, scams, and filth. The lake is nice-ish -if you don't look at it (water is murky-brown, with no bird-life...you know as in the simplest creatures on earth won't even go near it). The only good thing we found here was a tailor Duc Mihn who did an amazing job on a suit, several pants, and shirts.

Although old quarter has it's charm the merchandise sold there were not interesting at all. Most of the items were either fake goods or old fashion apparels. If you want shopping, this is not the place. Navigating the different streets were not too difficult with maps. Traffic can be nightmarish as there is no proper path for pedestrian to walk. Most of the times you felt the motorbike wheeling pass your feet by an inch while walking. I had my children, a pre-teen and a teen walking with us and thought it was an interesting experience for them but the high stimulation and noise pollution was a tad too overwhelming. Try to get away and go to the quieter streets near St Joseph Cathedral, such as Au Trieu, Na Tho of even further out to Hoi Vu street (quaint quiet alley). There are nice cafés, boutiques there for a respite away from the hustle bustle.

Trash dumped into the street and motor bikes parked on the sidewalk forces pedestrians into the street to walk as a dirty and dangerous situation.  The style of socializing sitting at small tables and chairs on the sidewalk listening tio the "beep-beep" of traffic and breathing the exhaust fumes is not our definition of a good time.

It's a must, fascinating architecture narrow streets full of street traders and amazing shops. Thousand's of mopeds some with the whole family on board. This is the place to learn how to cross the road, do it slowly and keep a eye ball on the person coming towards you. The locals are very calm people and you must remain the same. Hanoi has so much to see and do.
I have never visited a place like this and  wish I hadn't , I have never seen so many people on bikes in my life, you will find it very hard to breath as the air is so polluted.

Trip AdvisorBeautiful scenery but that's where it stops. From the crazy streets of Hanoi, called the BEEP BEEP CITY because of all the motorbike and car horns to the insanity of just trying to walk around or just trying to walk in Sa Pa,  you are bombarded with "you buy, you buy, you want, you buy, you buy,  where you from, you buy, you buy, you want, you buy, you buy, we walk with you, you buy"  it's sad when that's all it's about. I said to a young H'mong girl here in Sa Pa after the onslaught slowed down and I got her to smile, I said more important then "buy buy buy" was friendship with people and here was the punchline " you BUY my friendship" she said, then I heard the echo in my head, Robin Williams saying" GOOD MORNING VIETNAM "I will NEVER return! a note; the train from Hanoi to Sa Pa 10hrs + is a nightmare) beds made from styrofoam or cement, YES we booked Soft Seats as its called, toilets worse then an old dirty out house, and attitude worse than that) from what I understand the Orient Express Coaches on certain trains (2 per train ) are the way to go..which we were never told about… YIKES, I'm just being honest...Enjoy yourself !!!!

We had a 6 days trip to Hanoi and were staying in the Old Quarter area but we all couldn't wait to go back home! The traffic is crazy (worst than Bangkok!!!) and it felt as though we might die anytime on the road. The cars and bikes were out to kill and it's almost impossible to cross the roads each time. On our second night, one of our mates' phone also got stolen right from her front pocket while walking on the street in Old Quarter. The police did not bother to do anything and it took us 4 visits to the police station before we finally got our report for the insurance claim. In general, our lives felt endangered there and we feel very unsafe and uneasy walking on the streets there. It was a disappointing trip and I don't think we will be back in any time soon.

This is one of my worst holiday ever and the destination is called the Old Quarters in Hanoi. Rude drivers and motorbike riders are a plenty there. Definitely not for the faint hearted and crossing any streets in Hanoi is a challenge and your life will be at risk whenever you are walking near any streets. Food vendors and retailers there have a 'special price' list  for tourists which is jacked up by at least 50%. The place is filthy and smells bad and the hygiene levels there are just horrifying. My advise is to spend your hard earned money on other Asean destinations and trust me that you won't be missing anything if you do give Hanoi a pass. In short, this is my worst holiday experience ever!

People are rude, they see that ou are a tourist and prices go up triple. Bananas were $1 each because we took a photo with the stupid hanging baskets. We got street donuts and the lady took all my money, before even telling me how much even though I repeatedly aske her how much, yet she bagged them up and gave then to me. I couldn't afford a bottle of water that night- thanks lady. There are rats everywhere. It's dirty and unsafe. To cross a street you risk your life. To walk down a street, you risk your life... As there are no sidewalks available. The motorbikes take up all the space or the people drinking coffee take up all the room. There was absolutely nothing cultural or interesting to do in hanoi and I was there a week. I stayed in two different areas, by the "fancy " west lake area and the. By the old french quarter.  The highlight of my trip was halong bay. Don't waste your money. I heard sapa valley was nice except you will have to read up on all the scams that go on there. You would think the people would appreciate that you are spending money in their country but they were all a holes. I really wish I would've had a better time but I am so disappointed in this place I will never dream of going back. I enjoy french architecture, but it was ok. If you've ever even to New Orleans, savannah or France then You are all set. The french people are the ones who made hanoi a little prettier otherwise it's still the crappiest place on earth.

We had 4 nights in The Old Quarter.  Found it too crowded.  Could not even enter the hotel door without bikes being moved.  Just down the street a woman was cutting up a carcass on the pavement.  Turned us right off eating.  The restaurant nearby also has dog on the menu.  Very crowded and continual beeping.  Unable to walk on the footpath as too many people sitting or bikes blocking the way.

no good points at all, Vietnamese just threw everything that they used on the street, and it made the street very smelly and dirty. The air pollution in Hanoi was very serious because of fume from motorbikes.

Plenty of shops, cosy little restaurants, narrow streets, a blur of buildings built yesterday and buildings centuries old. It would've been a 4 or 5 stars review. But the pollution in Hanoi is now so bad, and the motorbike traffic so dense, that we could not walk in the Old Quarter for more than 15 minutes without getting nauseous. Hanoi has been crowned "most polluted city of South East Asia" with good reason.

This town is so over rated. Are we missing something? The locals are unfriendly (unlike the south of the country) the traffic is chaotic, the streets are dirty, the handicrafts are over priced with no negotiation allowed, the local points of interest are nothing special and not any better than HCMC. The traditional buildings in the old town are hard to spot and appreciate due to all the cheap make shift buildings which have been built in front of them. I was expecting this place to be more like Hoi An. How wrong was I. Whilst it is an experience to see the old quarter a 1 night stay in Hanoi is probably enough. There is a growing coffee shop culture which is good and if you are heading here as a group, there is a good selection of bars.

Most of the shop seller (99.9%) at old quarter were unfriendly & rudeness. For those tourist who like shopping....old quarter is not the suitable place cos the price they offer is much more expensive compare with shopping complex. So be aware & prepared that they will chase you out from the shop if you would like to ask for some discount from them.

Reading up about this area I was excited about the quirkiness, winding streets and history, but unfortunately disappointedly I have to say I just did nt like it. It is a frustrating area!! The winding streets are small, there are no pavements as parked motorbikes take up all the space- with no consideration so frequently you have to reverse your direction and go clambering everytime you want to go along a street. You walk in the road with scooters constantly beeping, and barely swerving past you and constant hassle as a westoner to 'buy my things' from shop owners. Everything you may want as a tourist is scattered apart so it is frustrating searches to find a restaurant, or shop, plus the street names merge and it is v difficult to navigate! Get a map out and you get pounced on! The one way system and restrictions mean getting a taxi is a nightmare. The general aesthetics of the area is not attractive- and whilst to see this is a site within itself the dirtiness and disrepair is unappealing after a few hours! Eating at street vendors while is great for an experience you get clambered over, knocked constantly and there is no escape from the noise. The streets named after the items they sell is geared towards locals (rightly so!) but you may find yourself wandering down gravestones, wood, metalware streets with no let up, and after seeing it once that is enough. With so much going on it is very pungent- which is great as part of the experience but can be overpowering.  I really wanted to love this area, I thought I would but just found the whole experience frustrating! Go for an hour or two, but don't stay here.
大した見所はありません。バイクが多すぎてあるきにくかったです。ハノイは、ハロン湾へのゲートウェイでしかないておもいます。また、そこら中に、シンカフェがあるので、どれが本物かわからないので注意した方がいいかも。

J'ai beau chercher, je ne vois pas ce que l'on peut trouver de spécialement intéressant à ce quartier où l'on se retrouve finalement les yeux rivés au sol à regarder où l'on marche et les sens en alerte car il faut surveiller sa gauche, sa droite, et partout à la fois de peur qu'un cyclo en tout genre (cycliste, pousse-pousse, scooter) ou pire une voiture ne vous écrase...C'est un quartier où les trottoirs sont occupés par les scooters qui s'y garent et les restos de rue, ainsi que par les poubelles. On erre ainsi à éviter les dangers et les obstacles, au son des klaxons incessants. Il n'y a pas de feux de circulation naturellement. C'est le grand bazard.  Le chaos. Paradoxalement, le piéton n'a pas sa place alors que ce quartier aux rues étroites se prêterait à la promenade tranquille et au shopping. Que nenni! Voyageant avec mes enfants, j'y ai pris une bonne dose de stress et je n'ai pas trouvé grand chose à acheter car il y a beaucoup de fripperie et de chinoiseries.

We stayed at a hotel in the old quarter thinking it would be great, it was shocking and disgusting. I'm not a delicate flower but this part of Hanoi is disgusting and I only wish we stayed somewhere else!! Visit here for interest sake but don't stay here or hang around

The Old Quarter was just too crowded and hectic.  There where motorbikes everywhere creating a dangerous situation for walking down the roads.  The shops are all just junk cheap shop stuff, nothing worth purchasing.  I attempted it a few times and gave up because it was too stressful.

ok this is a street food and street shops district, but you should be aware of lots cheap (chinese) shops, one after another every street has specialties (hardware, home facilities, mirror and frames, furniture, plastic bags..). people cooking and eating on the dirty street, you get sick of food smelling. Just to stay one hour or two and then go elsewhere. If you like to join locals in their tipical food, you find lot of people in a row for noodles breakfast. hygiene is not an opinion..

The Old Quarter is not worth much of your time.In most of the online sites the Old Quarter is dubbed the "hot spot" and the highlight of a visit to Hanoi.  The energy, the smells, the flavors, the over-priced food and drink, and the miles of touristy garbage quickly wore on us.  Because of the recommendations I read I booked a few nights for my family (with two small kids) in the Old Quarter- this was a horrible mistake. We live in China and are used to the craziness of what Asia has to offer, but the Old Quarter in Hanoi is just insane!  The sidewalks are clogged and blocked with parked motorbikes, there are more souvenir shops here than actual pieces of Vietnam, and everything is way to expensive- especially for Vietnam.  Now that I have done plenty of complaining, let me say that there are some redeeming parts of this area of Hanoi.  First, if you step a little into the areas that the locals hang out in you can find some awesome food and cheaper beer.  The Hanoi brew, the Saigon brew, and Tiger are everywhere and are fairly cheap.  There is great bread everywhere.  Overall the people are really very nice and helpful.  The architecture is really cool, especially comparing it to the average Chinese city where functionalism and commerce have destroyed most of their history.Do not spend more than one day in Hanoi- you wont need much more time.  Spend two hours in the Old Quarter, go see the Water Puppet Show, walk around Hoan Kiem Lake, get over early to see Ho Chi Mihn and his palace, then get some great, sugary coffee somewhere.  The whole day should be filled with eating great street food away from the Old Quarter- you'll save a load of money.  I am not critiquing Vietnam with this commentary, simply this specific area of Hanoi.  Our family will not ever have a need or a will to return here or to Hanoi at all.  I hope the rest of Vietnam has more to offer than what we have found so far.
Don't bother. It's just streets of stores selling wares (looks like from China instead of Vietnam) and street food.
The relentless traffic and tooting simply meant I ran out of tolerance for this area. Pity really. Had a more deliberate look around been possible, I would have been attracted.

Stayed in the Old Quarter for over a week - it would be nice without the motorbikes, but unfortunately they spoil it all to such a point that I actually hate this area. It's a pain to cross the street, even walking on the pavement is a struggle since they park the bloody bikes there ! Lots of nice shops though (mmm, well - lots of fake rubbish actually) but not many nice restaurants - they all eat on these dirty smelly streets. If you go for drinks on Beer Corner for example, you get a vietnamese lady every 2 minutes wanting to sell her stuff. So annoying.  Unfortunately most hotels are located in this area and it's close to that lake, which is nothing special either. Hanoi is a good hub if you want to visit Sapa and Ha Long Bay - but don't stay too long, it's total chaos and I guess that's not what you're looking for if you're on holiday.

Actually I quite liked the Old Quarter but I thought I'd join the carping brigade and put a one-star review in to balance things out. First, you've got to have your street smarts on in the Old Quarter. Trust no-one, when crossing the street assume there are no road-laws, count your change twice, beware of pickpockets, ask for the price of everything first (including food) and be very firm to the street vendors. Avoid taxis like the plague unless you've got your hotel to call one for you, you have GPS on your phone and you are constantly monitoring the meter. That's assuming of course you have a trust-worthy hotel. The people in the Old Quarter have the least friendly and hardest faces I have encountered in my travels through Asia. They have good reason. The Old Quarter is incredibly crowded, polluted and stressful. Streets are impossible to walk in and frequently gridlocked with motorcycles - and they cheat each other as much as they will cheat you.The area is very atmospheric however if you want to see how most mega-cities will look like when the world economy falls into a heap, oil-prices go through the roof and we all have to rely on motorcycles.On the plus side, you are not likely to be stabbed, shot or blown up. Also, the food is clean, the beer is cheap and the coffee is great.

I first saw the Old Quarter in 1995 when there were few motorbikes and aven fewer cars. Since then, the government has been mulling various plans to control traffic, to little avail. As a result, it is impossible to walk the sidewalks as they are blocked with parked bikes and vendors, the roads are continuously jammed and there is constant noise. If you insist on staying in the Old Quarter, make sure your hotel has double glazing and that it is not in the Night Market area as you won't be able to access the hotel by car at night.The only way to make the Old Quarter attractive again would be by making it a pedestrian area.

Lleno de suciedad muy guarros y sin educación no se puede caminar por ningún lado las aceras llenas de motos y venta ambulante y en la carretera no respetan ni paso de peatones ni nada.  Ruidosa estresante no no me gusta

Caótica, sucia, huele espantosa. Gente mal educada y bajo nivel de inglés. No hay que romantizar la mediocridad. No todo fue malo: un restaurante muy bueno con gente amable. Viajar en tren es un desastre: La orina salía por los filos de las puertas APESTA .. Estuvimos en Tam Coc y la situación mejoró. Aún así es gente sucia, sin respeto por las leyes, el hombre vietnamita es grotesco y grosero. Poco lugares que realmente vale la pena visitar, casi todo está descuidado y apesta. Sus paisajes es algo que puedo rescatar: bellísimos, deslumbrantes. Templos que me dejaron conmovida: Bellisimos  Un destino sobrevalorado, solo para ir una vez. No me lo repetiría así me regalaran el pasaje.  Es mi opinión, respeto la de ustedes..
Можно прогуляться ночью Но днём вообще ничего интересного
사람다니는 인도에 오토바이가 주차되어 있어서 인도로 다닐수가 없음. 30분동안 어머니 모시고 걸어다니는데 죽는줄 알았음. 차도는 좁은데다가 오토바이 차량까지 통행량이 많아서 굉장히 위험함. 거리에 쓰레기 냄새도 심함.  특별히 볼게,먹을게 많은 거리도 아님. 비추.

사람에 치이고 오토바이에 치이고 정말 다시는 가고 싶지 않은 거리 배트남은 왜 인도와 차도를 구분 안시키는지 거기다 오토바이 가 없다해도 인도에 가게들이 물건들을 놔둬서 올드타운 거리 다닐때마다 진짜 거의 목숨 걸고 다녀야 함 팔 부딪히고 손 부딪히고 뼈 안부러진게 다행 다시는 오고 싶지 않은 거리 옷도 다낭 나트랑 보다 비싸고 사이즈도 없고 쇼핑 거리라는데 살것도 없음 와서 실망만 하고감..
정말 너무 볼게없고 바가지 심합니다 야시장을 못가봐서 잘 모르겠는데 평일에 이곳은 절대 방문하지 마세요 물건파는곳도 별로없고 짝퉁 나이키 조거팬츠 65만동 부릅니다 저는 호치민에서 비슷한 제품을 20만동에 구매 했어요 시간낭비하지 마시고 하노이에선 쇼핑 안하는게 답인거 같아요
常に原付がクラクションをならしていてうるさい どの店もぼったくってやろうとしか思っていない

36거리 킹로티 "번" 판매하는 교차로 소매치기 주의. 번을 두손으로 찢어서 먹는 시점을 이용해서 종이를 접어서 펼치면 입체감이 생기는 상품을 3개정도 들고 접근하는 사람주의하세요. 구입의사 없다고 해도 계속 따라오는데 이상할 정도로 접근 후 종이 제품을 펼쳐서 시선을 빼앗고 상품 아래쪽으로 해서 크로스백이나 핸드백 털어갑니다. 스킬이 허접하지만 주의하지 않으면 털릴 수 있어요.
観光客の方々はくれぐれも深夜にここで客待ちをしている白色のタクシーを利用してはいけません メーターOKといいながら1桁増しつまり10倍の値段をふっかけられる被害が多発しています。 ハノイは緑色が1番安全、つぎがシルバー。 ホーチミンはビナサンに乗りましょう。 ベトナムは無名の白色のタクシーが1番ヤバイ。
horrible, lleno de turistas e ingleses borrachos, no tiene nada ke ver con vietnam, mas com benidorm
Agobiante y sucio
Ruidoso sucio muchísimo tráfico no se puede caminar por las aceras y los motoristas no respetan a los peatones
Super contaminado... definitivamente la zona que menos disfruté de Hanoi.
정신없이 시끄럽고 혼란스러움 ㅜ
소매치기 많다 조심해
Ощущение большой помойки
진입금지되어 방문할 이유가 없음

Just a lot of shops with cheap knockoff clothes.  Ton of people and scooters. Bad air and terrible noise. There isn't much to do unless you want to buy a knockoff Beckham jersey or want to get ran over by a scooter. You won't miss out if you don't visit.

On my second day in Hanoi, after work, I went to visit the Old Quarter. First of all, the weather here was extremely hot and dusty. I was wandering around wanting to buy gifts for my two young daughters when I saw two saleswomen arguing over customers. It was horrible!

I really wanted to love Hanoi’s Old Quarter, but honestly, it was a bit too chaotic for me. If you enjoy a fast-paced, noisy, and packed environment, you might love it. But if you prefer a more relaxed vibe, this place can be overwhelming.

Awful. As a solo man you are incessantly harassed by scammers, they follow you down the road and as soon as they finally leave another one rides up to bother you. Touts stand in your way and forcibly try to drag you into bars. Shops are very pushy and demand that you BUY SOMETHING RIGHT NOW NOW NOW! The liquor in the bars is mostly fake, made from fusel oil, flavorings and water. Large numbers of vietnamese regularly die from drinking it. Like the rest of Vietnam, everyone is smoking all the time and the stench is everywhere. You have to walk in the street with the gazillion motorcycles and you have to be prepared to leap out of the way.The lake is pretty and there a few decent restaurants. A few people are actually nice.

The Old Quarter night market was overwhelming in the worst way. The constant hawking, crowded streets, and cheap souvenirs gave us a headache. Not a romantic experience for couples. Save your energy for something more relaxing.
