The passion of the people for this place is incomprehensible - small shops full of rubbish, dirt and stink, motorbikes everywhere...after seeing 7 medinas in Tunisia I asked myself why I even go there...maybe I'm a masochist.Perhaps the only memorable experience is Train Street - at 8.20 we could see the Hanoi-Saigon train whizzing by a few centimeters from our faces (?)

The place is chaotic with drivers parking their cars anywhere. Taxi driver would wait for passengers at the corner of the street. Motorcycle drivers would traverse the street without any caution.One could have enjoyed the shopping along the streets but you would have to focus not to be hit by the motocycles.

There are many shops in the old town, which are very easy to browse, including many unique shops and old shops.Small vendors are also common on the sidewalks, and the shops are completely different day and night. You can even see small drink stalls setting up casually on the roadside in the middle of the night.In addition, you will see rickshaws recruiting customers in this area, but basically it is more cost-effective to use GRAB for transportation.However, the area is a mixed bag of people and vehicles, with almost no sidewalks, making it very time-consuming to actually visit.It is recommended that the old city should be properly planned as a pedestrian area or the existing sidewalks should be completely managed

Some may find the chaos and activity interesting and authentic, and perhaps it is. However I personally found the whole area pretty grim.Everywhere you go there is litter, mysterious liquids, and animal corpses (both vermin, and for preparation as food) all over the floor.Traffic is insane, but manageable once you get used to crossing the street.

This place is a mess. First day I was here, I felt like I was risking my life whenever I try to cross the street. I did get used to the traffic later so it didn't bother me much afterward. Overall it's dirty, expect some vendors ripping you off. For example I tried to buy sugar cane juice and the vendor wanted 50 when it should cost anywhere from 12-20.The food in the area is quite good if you pick the high rating ones from Google map.Not much to see in this area. Endless repetitive poor quality souvenirs. Also there is a lake in this area, I don't have much to say about it.

Very noisy area ! And definitely not a walkable district ! Too many street restaurants and motorbikes parked on sidewalks preventing the pedestrians walking . Hectic traffic ! Red light at traffic lights does not stop vehicles to go ahead ...

Un quartier qui pourrait être plein de charme mais une authenticité qui perd en valeur avec un désintérêt pour sauver le patrimoine. Comme dans chaque grande ville du Vietnam, une impossibilité de flâner dans les rues par manque de structure pour les piétons. Un vacarme et une pollution qui deviennent partie intégrante du paysage, des regards trop souvent appuyés, une insistance des commerçants à acheter et des arnaques qui gâchent le voyage. Malgré la demande du gouvernement envers sa population visant à interdire la viande de chien dans la capitale, vous ne pourrez éviter les stands avec vos animaux de compagnie rôtis.
It's noisy, crowded and doesn't have much of interest for us. We started on a Lonely Planet Walking Tour and then had a coffee and left.

Interesting  place, footpaths are used to park motorbikes and used for cleaning , cooking and whatever.So then when you walk on the street you have to avoid getting run over by this mad traffic coming from all directions.
Hanoi is a dump. Crowded with cars and scooters that have no regard for pedestrians. Massive smog. Mostly rude people. Great poverty.Few redeeming features. Give it a miss.

There’s a never ending stream of people trying to sell crap, that won’t take no for an answer!My friend did make a purchase in the hope that he’d be left alone and the opposite happened. He was then chase down by more street vendors that were outrageously aggressive!The streets are a health hazard they are so filthy with garbage, right next to the food, sidewalks have potholes and large cracks.We saw a young mother break her ankle right before us as her three young children watched in horror.

Het oude centrum van hanoi is erg leuk en gezellig maar de hoeveelheid scooters en het rijgedrag van de bevolking maakt het centrum erg voetganger onvriendelijk, ik snap de mondkapjes ook beter want doordat je constant door de grote hoeveelheid scooters loopt adem je veel uitlaat gassen in

I cannot recommend Hanoi as a destination.  It is choked with motorbikes which also park on all the sidewalks.  This and the sidewalk food vendors make it impossible to walk on the sidewalks so you are forced to do battle with the traffic.  Every 2nd bike or car honks at you and it is quite unnerving.  I was knocked over by a motorbike, there were people sitting on the sidewalk right beside me and not a person got up to help!!  It is worth your life to cross the street, the crosswalks mean absolutely nothing.  It  is so polluted we never saw the sun (even on sunny days).
Harsh yes but having spent 4 days here recently Hanoi's old quarter just did not grow on me. In fact I was glad to leave after the fourth day. Strongly suggest Halong Bay for at least a day.

Quand je pense que le Routard le classe en première position des incontournables du Vietnam... C'est un ramassis de tas de vêtements vraiment moches, des scooters dans tous les sens, rien de typique.. À faire le soir si vraiment vous voulez le voir.. Et levez les yeux pour voir les façades typiques si on veut avec la clim...

Good for what’s around (bars etc) but also full of shops selling the same rubbish. We didn’t see much interesting architecture and we walked around a lot of side streets. We did go for a cooking class so walking around the food market was interesting. Definitely worth visiting if in Hanoi as you will find good cafe/food/beer but don’t set expectations too high.
Too focused on tourists, lost the authentic Vietnamese culture. Everywhere is filled with pubs and bars with tourists. Noisy, crowded and definitely not recommended.

So, I finally went to Hanoi. Loved the food. Great food from the streets all the way to the restaurants street food. That’s why I didn’t give it a 1As far as the rest, I am not sure what the big deal is about Hanoi. I travel and I’m not new to getting ripped off in developing countries as I actually take it as a right of passage, but Hanoi was different. Their attitude was was that of entitlement and disdain. I try and feel out the street scenes, and people don’t smile at all here. When I wanted to ask someone a question this lady was looking at me all crazy. Had a few rude / bizarre experiences. One that stood out was as I’m walking back to my room with a friend late at bight and some guy from a group of people sitting in a street corner randomly starts yelling at me in Vietnamese and starts pushing me. I was wearing a shirt with the Vietnamese flag. If that offended him, I don’t know what to tell you. The only explanation was he probably didn’t like people that look like me and was playacting for his buddies( I’m not white or Vietnamese), or it was some precursor to starting some scrap that will end up with robbing me or something. I’ve lived in the hood and I’ve spent enough time in 3rd world countries. Either way, this left a bad taste in my mouth. As soon as I got to my room, I got out of the Vietnamese flag shirt I was wearing and left it at my air B and B. I tried Vietnam. I tried :) Never say never, but I got tons of places on my bucket list before I come back here. My best times were outside Hanoi. Nothing I dug about this city

Although highly rated, I found it far below my expectations given the dirt and grime lining the streets. Furthermore traffic was exceptionally bad, and so was the air quality with the numerous motorcycles around. Would not recommend.
혼돈의 chaos 그 상태. 길 건너기도, 하다못해 인도가 스쿠터의 주차장으로변질되어, 보행자의 안전은 go to 헬위생은 말할것도 없고, smog로 가득한 거리에서목이 칼칼하고, 눈이 따갑고, 숨조차 쉬기 어려움. 거리에선 눈치게임이 시작되니 최대한 민첩하시길.

We had a good time in Hanoi but beware of pickpockets I have unfortunately had my wallet stolen out of my back pocket while on tour to a temple. By the time we got back to our hotel, they had taken a large sum of money over the internet by buying up through a dummy outlet. The police were very difficult to deal with, and their knowledge of English was limited,  if it wasn't for our hotel porter we would still be there explaining our problem. When you travel there to make sure you have at least two credit cards one for your partner and your self. The other is always carry extra cash in case of problems like this arise. On a good note, we received all the money back from our banks and credit card company
Kein Altstadtbummel, wie man ihn sich vorstellt. Viele Läden mit Billigkram, schlechte Luft und lauter Verkehr.

Sinceramente esperaba otra cosa del barrio viejo de Hanoi. Es una zona abigarrada invadida por las motos que hacen imposible caminar o cruzar las calles. Aún con notas de  pintoresquismo local, es mejor visitar barrios similares en otras ciudades vietnamitas. Oportunidades de compra limitadas con precios para turistas realmente delirantes

Il vecchio quartiere di Hanoi è sicuramente caratteristico, ma le strade sono sporche, i cavi elettrici pendono dagli alberi e gli scooters sono ovunque, per strada e sui marciapiedi.Abbiamo passato il nostro tempo a schivare macchine e scooter; i vietnamiti sono sempre pronti a intimarti di spostarti a colpi di clacson , non si fermano mai anche se ti trovi sulle rare strisce pedonali che incontri!Il resto del tempo lo abbiamo impiegato a cercare dei ristoranti dove poter mangiare in tutta sicurezza, dove la carne e le verdure non fossero tagliare sul pavimento non lavato del ristorante o peggio, sul marciapiede !Un giorno ad Hanoi e i nostri jeans puzzavano terribilmente, ci fossimo rotolati in una discarica avrebbero avuto un odore meno pungente. Per riassumere , siamo contenti di aver visto Hanoi una volta nella vita, ma ci è bastato così.
There is nothing significant about this area. It's simply...old. Hence the name...The area is full of buildings that are old. Not the attractive,wonder provoking old. Just old..and ugly.
신호와 횡단보도가 없는 이곳. 있으나 그것은 장식일 뿐인도위에는 수많은 오토바이로 인해 사람들은 차도로 걸어다녀야 하고,오토바이와 차들의 무한 질주로 인해 걸어다니기 힘들다. 현지인들처럼 앞만보고 갈길 가야 길을 건널 수 있음. 거기다가 빵빵 소음도 장난아니고 매연도 장난아니다. 잠시 걸어다니면 머리가 아프다.

El centro es demasiado caótico, mucha gente caminando, demasiadas motos que se cruzan por todas partes, calles sucias, totalmente desordenado. Las veredas son usadas como estacionamientos de motos, así que los peatones tienen que caminar por las pistas Casi no hay semáforos y si los hay, son de adorno, porque nadie los respeta.
We were in Hanoi for 4 days.  The Old Quarter was very busy and loud.  Much street and foot traffic.  I ultimately stayed in the hotel to escape the chaos.

Hanoi has long been plagued by uncontrolled traffic - particularly motorcycles.  It is now completely lawless.  Sidewalks are for parking motorcycles, crossing the street is life-risking whether you are in a crosswalk or not.  The city has it's charm, but it is now difficult to see this through the pollution.  It's a shame, but it's no longer worth a visit.  We won't be back.

Arrived Sunday evening....some streets closed off, thousands of pedestrians, music and street food. Really fun place. Fast forward to weekdays. A tired collection of buildings which have combination of a nail/massage salon or travel agency or boutique hotel or a restaurant stacked on each other. Traffic is so so. Probably the best side of Hanoi.

Wszystko do potęgi n-tej. Chodniki zastawione skuterami i kuchenkami, więc trzeba iść po ulicy, po której pędzą skutery albo jest taki tłok, że poruszanie trwa nieskończoność. Do tego wizualnie nic specjalnego. Serdecznie radzę nie zostawać w Hanoi za długo.

1 afternoon is good enough to explore. Totally not kid friendly with the busy small streets and smokers everywhere. Even though everything is near each other, we had a hard time going around to explore, as we spent more time looking out for oncoming bikes and cars and puddles, then to look at the shops. Please DO NOT bring prams. Street food's sanitary conditions are terrible, ok for an adventurous couple, but don't try your luck with small kids.
El tráfico es un desastre, el asinamiento está a flor de piel y el olor de todo se curte en tu nariz
Roads are very busy. Honda & Motorcycle all over the place. Very tough to walk freely. Some places are dirty also.Specially I didn't enjoy.

We thought this would be a great time to stock up on souvenirs and clothes before trip home. It was as expensive as home, and the people would not barter. Which is fine, but not what you would expect.
Not many bargains and no-one will bargain. Put on for tourists but it's all a bit of a rip-off. Motor bikes are a pest thru the narrow markets. Beware of pickpockets.
Terrible, you have to walk on the road as the pavement is full with motorbikes. Motorbike going in all direction and the noise and pollution from same not good. Personally not my cup of tea.

Honestly, I feel like I’ve been conned by the thousands of reviewers on the Internet that has been selling the “charm” of Hanoi’s Old Quarter. This is the first time I’m on vacation that I just can’t wait to go home. Where is the charm of the Old Quarter? Maybe if you are from a developed country and the “authentic Asian experience” (aka poverty porn) excites you, then you will find the Old Quarter “charming”. But if you are like me, who is also from a developing country, then there is no charm at all. It’s just like being back home. The place is old and crumbling, crammed and rather dirty (not as dirty as Manila’s Binondo but I still wouldn’t call it clean). What I really hated the most was that they didn’t have any indoor restaurants or cafes apart from the ones in hotels and these were very few. We are here in the summer so this is extremely problematic. It was 35 degrees today and we just wanted to have coffee somewhere out of the sun, and there was no place like that at all. Well there was, but it was just one place and not surprisingly it was full. In the evening we walked for 30 minutes along different streets to look for a decent place to have dinner but all Old Quarter has to offer are eateries where they place you in low plastic tables outdoors, which would be okay if the place was squeaky clean. Which it’s not. I didn’t find it very appetizing to eat literally at the gutter while the city’s thousands of motorcycles spewed their pollution at me and my food. We weren’t even sure if the utensils we were using were clean; they sure didn’t look it.If you’re visiting Hanoi, maybe just drop by for half a day to walk around and check things out, but don’t make the same mistake we did and make Old Quarter your base of operations. I’m sure there are better places to experience “the real Hanoi”, not the Hanoi that is being sold to rich privileged tourists who enjoy poverty porn. Unfortunately, I will never be able to see the real “real Hanoi” because Old Quarter has honestly ruined this city for me and I won’t ever go back even if someone pays me.
Walk around a REAL neighbourhood- not this awful tourist trap. The streets south of the lake are much more charming and interesting.Don't mistake the old quarter as the real Hanoi.
Pedestrian unfriendly and unsafe. Best places to view the traffic and small street shops are from the outdoor balcony of Highland Coffee or inside an electric cab.

Visit it fast because it is “mandatory”, and avoid it when you are done already, unless you love stink, bad food and want to get a headache.You can bargain a bit the prices (20%) but not too much since it is very touristic. I won’t recommend eating in the poor street places, I saw very ill chicken, alive in some of the poor restaurants)

ハノイ中心街の通りは、ガイドブックで見ると緑の街路樹が続いてロマンチックに見えるが、現実はひどいものだ。排気ガスと交通渋滞、そして交通ルールなど何もないような通りが多い。特に単車とスクーターの洪水がものすごく、信号など誰も守らないから道路は簡単には渡れない（特に日本人には不可能に近い）。さらに通りの歩道には無断占拠の超簡易レストランが並んでいて、歩きにくいし非衛生的。ホアンキエム湖の周辺や大教会あたりだけを歩くだけならいいが、ハノイの旧市街を歩き回ろうとするには、相当に強い心臓と覚悟が必要になる。ベトナムの諸都市で、道路の問題は大きな頭痛のタネ、地元住人も、観光客も困っている。
하노이의 오토바이 메연은 진짜,,,여름엔 웬만하면 가시지 마시고, 구시가지도 나름 20대였으면 진짜 신났었을 텐데,,사람도 많고 그닥 볼건 없습니다.

To be very honest, it's an okayish place for bagpackers but pretty inconvenient for choosy travelers like myself. My friends and I got an airbnb here, however, the area around it was pretty shady and loud at night. Given an option- I'd shift to a nice place around Bao Wow considering the vibe of that area is way better and classy unlike old quarters. Disclaimer - It's just my personal opinion and I would recommend travelers to do some more research on what kind of accommodation they'd like and what's their budget.

Our view is that this part of Hanoi can be skipped.  Traffic and no sidewalks make it unmanageable - at least when we were there in the afternoon.  Maybe it is better at night, but not during the day.
交通量がすごくて、バイクも車もひっきりなしに通る。道路を横断するのも命がけだ。信号もないところも多く、建物をのんびり観光して楽しむ余裕なんてない。

ホテルから、タクシーで、旧市街を目指しました。ホテルのスタッフの方に、道が狭くて、交通渋滞が起きるので、充分に時間をみておいて下さい。というアドバイスがありました。ホテルから旧市街に近づくにつれて、だんだん、道が狭くなり。ちょうど小学校が終わる時間帯と重なってしまい。旧市街地にの中に数校ある小学校が一斉に終わって、50ccバイクで、子供を迎えに行く親のバイク群の中に入ってしまい。タクシーに乗ったまま、車は動かない状態が続きました。ハノイは市街地の中心部に、地下鉄や電車が見当たらないので、公共の交通機関が、バスくらいしか無い様ですが、旧市街は道が狭くて、バスも入って来れない位狭い道でした。50ccバイクは無免許で乗れるくらい、公共交通手段が無いようです。50ccバイクは、通勤通学の重要な交通手段の様でした。タクシーに乗っていても、旧市街の目的地直前で、排気ガスで、気持ちが悪くなり、タクシーを降りて、排気ガスの中を観光出来ないと判断しました。しかも、旧市街地に、空車のタクシーは一切見当たらない事にも気がつきました。大きなホテルや駅も無いので、帰りの交通手段が無いと、街を見ていて気がつきました。仕方なく、タクシーに乗ったままの旧市街観光となりました。建物は1軒1軒カラフルで、可愛くて。洋服屋さんの通りが有ったり、ちょっとオシャレなカフェが有ったりしていましたが。道幅が狭くて。そこにバイクの排気ガスが充満しているのが旧市街、と言う印象でした。池の周りの公園やベトナムらしい雰囲気の観光の中心が有りましたが。旧市街を見て回りたい場合は、旧市街地の中にある、昔からの小さな地元のホテルに泊まって旧市街を歩いて観光して、歩いてホテルに戻れる。という条件が、まず必要だとわかりました。旧市街以外の新しいホテルに滞在した場合は、ツアーに入るとかしないと帰って来ることが出来ないと思います。旧市街を歩いて観光するのであれば、旧市街の中に泊まらないと無理だと言うことが分かりました。旧市街を自分で歩いて観光しようと計画されている方は、旧市街の中に宿泊しないと旧市街を歩いて見て回る事は難しいと思います。ハノイで、旧市街を観光したいのであれば、旧市街の中で宿泊する事をお勧め致します。
Just plain gross! Rats and roaches EVERYWHERE!! Good if you’re 16 to 22 and looking to get drunk on cheap beer. I saw so many rats! Place is disgusting!
Not amazing and there's just to many motorbikes and extremely hard to crops the road, saw 3 motorbike accident in 1 day that's how bad it is here. Don't spend more than a day here.
I dunno why this is even an attraction. The place is dirty and cramped up. Full of over priced places.

It is chaos in the streets. Millions of motorbikes always honking to each other. Dirty and smelly city. Not many attractions except a walk in the lake, the prison museum and not much more...Bring money to do shopping. Here they imitate many brands, particularly Nike and North Face.Attention: bargain for all that doesn’t  have the prices marked. They see a € in every tourist.Try the food in the street: it is tasty.Nobody speaks English. Google translator became an important tool !

This is an interesting area, unfortunately marred by the millions of motorbikes on the streets and sidewalks. They are everywhere; sidewalks, pedestrian streets, and parks. The air is hard to breathe and you are likely to be hit, as the riders follow no rules, don’t obey any lights or signs, and go too fast. A real problem in what could be a prime tourist area.

Старый квартал это хаос. Для европейского туриста очень близко к визиту в ад. Хаотичное дорожное движение, узкие тротуары, огромное количество маленьких магазинчиков и лавочек со всевозможным ширпотребом... местами уличная еда - прямо на тротуаре... голова буквально взрывается от нахождения в этих местах. Ничего хорошего из вещей вы здесь не купите, сувениры тоже не очень. Каждый предмет (джинсы, очки, кофе...) продаются на отдельной улице. Кофе не советую брать в лавочках, он обжарен на масле и не отличается качеством. Для этого есть speciallity кофейни.
賑やか、華やか、夜も遊べますが、人多い、臭い、車多く空気が悪い、これを我慢すればぶらぶら、買い物は面白いでしょうね。私は1時間で退散し、スタバで肺の煤をクリーンアップしに行きました。
Old quater might sounds interesting, but unfortunately not at all.Very busy and very dirty. Very hard to walk, all sideways full of motorbikes. Lot’s of cheap restaurants and dirty stores. Avoid!

Le Old Quarter est tout simplement rendu un lieu trop touristique, où que les chaînes de restaurants et commerces américains se sont établis. Même la nourriture de rue n’est pas si bonne, en plus d’être cher. Vous êtes sollicité la plupart du temps par des vendeurs de beignets ou encore des vendeuses de cartes... si vous voulez de l’authenticité, déplacer dans l’ouest de la ville, près du musée B52, c’est là que nous avons le mieux manger et apprécier la vie à Hanoï. Cependant, le Lac Hoan Kiem à proximité est très jolie, et on peut y retrouver des attractions intéressantes comme le Théâtre de Marionnette à titre d’exemple. Nous n’avons pas vraiment profiter du Night Market, mais nous croyons qu’il s’agit d’une activité intéressante à faire dans ce quartier!
Es una ciudad segura con monumentos y sitios históricos interesantes desordenada debes tener calma con el pito de los carros y motos. Quedarte una noche es suficiente.

Hanoi was the last stop on our Vietnam trip.  I didn’t enjoy the old quarter, it was just too busy and the shops were just more of the same stuff shop after shop.  I recommend a quick visit here but thats it, spend more time out of the big city, thats where you see the real Vietnam.

Hanoi was not a terribly interesting place to visit, but the old quarter is worth walking through. A few hours is more than enough. Hard for me to like a place when the locals treat it like one big dumpster.

If you enjoy endless streets of replica North Face clothing, tacky souvenirs, rip off tour agents, massage bars, suspect street food with the odd nugent of passing interest, then five minutes will be sufficient to battle with impatient traffic-to-nowhere.There are a few old buildings up alleyways or lurking among the milling millions but it appears better 100 years ago.
The streets are cramped and dirty... The cafeteria takes up the walk. Everything is expensive but average quality, everything is very messy here

3 weken Vietnam !!!Beetje teleurstellend... Thailand en Indonesië zijn veel leuker en vooral  veel vriendelijker mensen en lekkerder eten.Hanoi zal ik niet aanraden.... niet veel te zien qua architectuur , geen musea en geen vriendelijke mensen.Als je van ENORME drukte  houdt en van viezigheid , dan zou ik terugkeren.
정말 오토바이가 많고 사람들도 많아서 복잡한 곳이었어요. 그래서 걷기가 정말 힘든 곳이었어요ㅠㅠ저처럼 사람많고 복잡한 곳 싫어하시는 분은 굳이 갈 필욘 없는 것 같아요.
Old Quarter is different than I imagine.  Very congested and streets needs repaired. Very hard to get around.

This area is very crowded and scooters everywhere. So beware where you are walking. Be careful of pick pockets. I got my phone stolen so not very impressed. I was being careful but obviously not enough. If you go here be very very careful.

Username: Travel and food are my best interest
Comment: Wonderful place to explore the shops with so many varieties. The worst is using life at death point when doing this exploring , the pathway was parked with all motorcycles，most times you are force to walk on the side of the road and with the super super heavy traffic of cars and motorbike horning that will not only give you stress but craziness. It almost can deaf any person by walking only 100m. Not advise for family with kids.
My hotel in old quarter is smallEnvironment is dirty and noisyThough central, too crowdedPlenty of food choices thoughPrices are really cheap
Dirty with lot of trash. It's a shame to compare with Hoi An ancient town in kiddle of Vietnam. This place is old not ancient

Si lo que vas buscando en Vietnam es tranquilidad, paisajes y conocer a su gente, las ciudades sin duda no son lo adecuado. El Old Quarter es el típico lugar lleno de turistas, occidentalizado, lleno de souvenirs y restaurantes donde la gente intenta que entres al suyo, te persiguen para que compres cosas etc,

You see one you’ve seen them all. Not impressed. Dirty, smelly and the sad part, the Pimps hustling young girls to foreigners. Depressing. Those are someone’s daughters! The negative experience robs the place of its potential charm.
Just traveling around with my fiancée and was not expecting the roads to be so unsafe and manic. Some good people but you have to search for them.

It’s a bit of a shock to be honest but like a lot of unusual places around the world, you tend to get used to it after a day or so. It’s not a place I would ever want to come back to and wouldn’t recommend it to anyone. Crossing the road becomes fun after you’ve sussed it. The locals make it look easy, bless em. We couldn’t find much to attract our interest historically or in any picturesque kind of way but this is written before we see outside the city. I’m told it’s lovely so here’s hoping.

街歩きを期待していたが、歩道はバイクの駐輪場かプラスティックのテーブルやイスが置かれていて歩くことができない。車道を歩くとひっきりなしにバイクの群れがギリギリに横をすり抜ける。危なくて街歩きを楽しむどころではない。こんな一画でも観光地になるほどハノイには、他に観光資源が無い。戦時中の戦闘機や戦車や大砲を置いただけの軍事博物館、囚人の人形を並べただけの監獄、外壁がうす汚ないハノイ大聖堂などが観光地とは余りに貧弱である。長期のベトナム戦争で街が破壊されたので仕方がない面があるにしても観光地としての価値はない。市内観光よりは郊外のニンビン、サパ、バックハーを勧める。

I'm not sure what I expected, but it wasn't a boisterous neighborhood of tiny streets filled with garish lights from raucous bars and restaurants--all serving essentially the same cheap food. Tourists of all ages abound in close quarters. Not my cup of tea. The only saving grace were the coffee sellers who stock the best Vietnamese coffee beans and blends.
אין מה לעשות בעיר, צפיפות נוראית, מסריח, שוק לילה לא משהו.היינו ב 2 מסעדות טובות מאוד שהומלצו עי המלון queen light hotel, מומלץ מאוד במחיר סביר עם צוות מדהים ונקי.

Si vous avez envie d'un bain de klaxon, scooter qui vous renverse, gens pas aimables et qui essayent par tout les moyens possible de vous escroqué c'est l'endroit idéal pour vous ! Sans doute la prière ville que j'ai pu découvrir en asie, les gens sont méprisant envers les touristes, on a l'impression d'être des portes monnaie vivant, les gens vous sautent dessus pour que vous leurs achetiez  quelque chose et si vous n'achetez pas presque il vous cacheraient dessus.
Những con đường nhỏ, hàng quán xen lẫn nhau, bụi bẩn, không còn lối cho người đi bộ, Cafe pha tạp không chất lượng,nhà cũ chứ không phải cổ

Traffic chaotic, old shops and some places smelled bad and dirtyCustomer services mostly excellent while others are mostly interested in their mobile phonesThe lake surrounds has plenty of seats under trees and can be cooling on a humid day.  It’s very crowded on weekend for familiesFood so far what I have tasted are all delicious and healthy unlike Szechuan cooking which are covered with oil and Thai food all sugary yuk and others too salty .. Currency Dong a nightmare to figure out and ended up having to trust getting my change back Hanoi, done it been there and won’t be back

Deux heures ok pas plus , à voir une fois dans sa vie ok alors pour moi deux heures de balades est un doliprane 1000 imagine 4 h de balade sera un paquet de doliorane!!! Trop bruyant trop étroit et les motos ou voitures sont les prioritaires, soit vous évitez et laisser passer ou soit vous vous fêtes engueuler ou vous faire écraser les pieds. C’est comme en Chine que j’ai connu en vacances!!! Ok deux heures!
旧市街のホテルでしたが、とにかく外の音がうるさかったです。そして、雑誌やガイドブックにもあるように、バイクと車がもの凄い量でした。信号が一切なく、小さいお子さん連れては危険です。何度もひかれそうになったのと、廃棄ガスで呼吸が苦しいのと、臭いのと・・・電線や崩れかけた建物、ゴミ・・・ディープな街です。

Stayed here 3 nights It always appears to rain heavily in the evening Wet, slushy, dirty and loads to 2 wheelers literally riding over you Note humans and vehicles share the road Pavements hijacked by food stalls Nothing out of the ordinary

Walking around the lake in the old quarter is actually very cool, as well as the stalls and shops along the way. Every weekend they close off the lake to moto bikes and cars and get ready for the giant weekend market ( also amazing). But why I’m giving this a 2 star is because I had to go to the bathroom while we were walking around the lake, I stumbled across a public restroom that said “free public toilet” I tried to enter but a lady yelled at me and told me it was 3000 just to go in. She was just sitting in a chair, not even selling toilet paper. If a sign says “free” is should probably mean “free” obviously i didn’t pay the woman but always go to the bathroom before walking around of buy something you need from a circle k and use the bathroom there.
This place is always full of people. You can really move and enjoyed this place. Food is great, but that is it.
This place is overcrowded, traffic chaos, hard to find good places for dining. Overall, nothing to do or see.

La vielle ville est à faire pour ces restaurants appelé "street foods", mais ne regardez ni l'hygiène, ni la propreté des lieux, les rue dégagent des odeurs acres et nauséabondes...mais faut voir et sentir au moins une fois ça dans sa vie...
老城區對外國人可能好新鮮好多嘢睇，但對普遍亞洲人嚟講可能會冇乜特別，只係一個貧窮人住嘅地方，街道狹窄危險，衛生有待改善。

Un vieux quartier dont la plupart des fameuses maisons tubes, ne sont pas entretenues et sont en très mauvais état.  Beaucoup de panneaux commerciaux les masquent. Le harcèlement des touristes par toutes sortes de vendeurs est permanent, certains sont très agressifs. La circulation dans les rues étroites est dangereuse pour le piéton, la pollution prend à la gorge. Finalement aux dires des locaux, les pickpockets sont nombreux. Heureusement dès que l'on s'eloigne du vieux quartier, la ville retrouve un certain charme.

This is a happening place with many streets catering to the visitor. Its a fun place and many food options but really it's just very touristic in nature. More commercial I guess. One can miss going here is you really are looking for authentic food and culture. But if you love the bars and the people and just looking around, its fun!
Will not go back as it is very crowded with motor bikes wizzing by close...lots of low end local restaurants, bars and people.

We stayed in the old quarter and I would not recommend. The place is like a maze , overcrowded, over whelming the sight and the senses. I saw live crab getting pestled into a paste with a mortar, fresh chickens chopped. The side walks have scooters parked or small side tables so as a pedestrian you have to share the road with scooters, car. It was stressful.

Could be a lot of fun if this is kind of environment you can tolerate. Dirty, dilapidated, full of people and motorbikes. We stayed in a hotel at the edge of the Old Quarter, and found it to be fine for the first few days, but eventually the chaos started to vet on our nerves. When feeling overwhelmed, a stroll along the lake or a visit to a museum helps restore the inner peace.
Maximum if 2 days. Needed in Hanoi.. The old quarter there is not much to do.. also very noisy very touristy very crowded

paraphrasing one previous view named as "Noisy mess!",  which I do agree completely with: noisy, chaotic, messy, shabby, constant and dangerous traffic with the motobikes blowing the horn all time, making the decibels unbearable  We will not come back nor recommend anyone just for the "noise pollution". Aside from this, "it is not well kept and historical buildings are decadent". So, for the "old stuffs", Hoi An is the perfect choice, and for shopping, cosmopolitan culture, modern asian life style, we will come back to Saigon. Hanoi is a nightmare definitely. And amazing no authorities care abt this!!!

Una decepción. Calles muy sucias. No hay por donde caminar. En las aceras parquean las motos y los locales tienen sus negocios, lo cual deja poco espacio para caminar. En las calles los autos y motocicletas van a toda velocidad sin respetar las leyes del tránsito ni los semáforos. Muy bajo atractivo turístico.
noise dirt terrible traffic-all about Hanoi. Street food that I wouldn't touch. A few vegan place where tiny and expensive dishes are served; tasty though. Only quick and short visit recommended.
車多擁擠，人車爭道...但有趣的是...感覺他們亂中有序，即便如此也很少聽見他們有事故發生，但空氣品質真的不好@@
5000동 주고 먹는 아이스크림도 좋고 상점도 많고 다 좋은데 너무 혼잡합니다.오토바이도 너무 많고 차량도 너무 많아서경적소리에 머리가 아플지경입니다.매연도 매연이지만, 마스크를 써도 목이 아프니ㅠㅠ 교통신호 체계가 너무 미비합니다. 계속 있고 싶은 곳은 아니네요
It is quite a dirty city with a lot food options relatively cheap. Not so much to do in Hanoi. I dont regret going there but as an advice i would first visit another city in Vietnam.

Where do I start the pavement have motorbikes parked all over them so you have to walk on the road taking your life in your hands the first day I got a bad throat because of the pollution the sad part is you could see at one time this place was amazing such a shame
Stayed here for 2 nights. Locals are not friendly, not helpful, and the language barrier made it worst. Expect to pay higher price for everything because you're a tourist!
原付バイクが多すぎて排気ガスが蔓延、とてもゆっくり買い物、食べ歩きな感じではなく、ただ歩き回った感じ。バイクの規制しないと買い物は出来ないなぁ。バイクに乗ったまま店先につけて買い物するのは当たり前、どこでもどちらからもバイクがやってくるので要注意です。

una città dove fare zig zag tra i motorini dove il cielo è sempre stato plumbeo. meno male che nel fine settimana creano una zona pedonaledivertente la mattina presto vedere tanta gente intorno al lago che fa movimento sport o tai chi

A nosotros nos par ció muy ruidoso, sucio y poco disfrutable. Tiene su cosa los edificios antiguos pero es desagradable para pasear y estar relajado. Demasiadas motos y tráfico, aire irrespirable. Si acabas de llegar a Vietnam o has viajado poco por asía puede que aguantes el caos, pero si es el final del viaje (como nos paso a nosotros) o ya conoces muchas ciudades asiáticas es más de lo mismo en cuanto a ruido y humos, por lo que es posible que quieras irte de allí a un sitio más relajado sobre todo si tienes pocos días. Volveré en 10-20 años cuando los vehículos sean eléctricos
Very crowded with shops and stalls. Not many good restaurants for dining. Hard to navigate through small streets/roads
Spoiled by fast food, loud bars, and cheap tourist shops - needs to be more like Hoi An that had actually improved

We were shocked how dirty is hanoi. The traffic is a chaos, we were afraid all time long if we will be hit by a scooter or a car in every minutes. Street food can be good if the streets wouldnt be so disgusting and dirty. The money changer almost tricked us, you need to be cautious. Streets are crowded, it is hard to enjoy walking. Overall Halong Bay is amazing, it is worth to visit Vietnam, but dont spend more than 2 hours in Hanoi.
バイクの群れと埃と排気ガス・・・これがこの地らしいですが、慣れないので歩くのが大変で疲れます。お店もたくさんありますが、購入意欲をそそられるものをみつけられませんでした。

A lot of motos and caos. Many many noise and a lot of pollution. Interesting to see for the culture but in 1 hour you are tired of all the noise. You have to walk in the street because all the sidewalks are covered with motos

Hanoi is perhaps one of the few remaining places in Asia where old buildings co-exist with a jumble of wires, polite folks hurrying about on two wheelers. If noise, chaos, street food, hidden history appeal to you, this is your kind of really cheap holiday place. Old quarter typifies all that Hanoi has to offer. You can see the profile of the tourists changing as day turns to night and low barstools come out. Day brings visits to a few scattered tourist sites punctuated by visits for food and foot massage.
雨で客足が少ないせいかあらゆる店で店員がやる気ゼロ。アパレルの店で洋服の下に顔をつっこんで寝てる店員までいて、ちょっと笑えた。趣があるというより、ただただ雑多なだけ、という印象。

Не дай бог попасть сюда в выходные или праздники. Весь ханойский люд приходит сюда и толчея такая, что не протолкнуться. А ещё через эти толпы недружелюбных и плохо воспитанных людей протискиваются такие же мотоциклисты. Жуть!
You can't walk on the street- everything is taken over by scooters;you can't cross the street without risking your life;there are no real attractions there.
개인적으로 베트남 여행이 즐거운 기억은 아님음식도 맞는게 별로 없었고 비위생적인 거리때문에 힘들었음날씨도 너무 더웠고 특히 오토바이때문에...소음과 매연때문에 고통스러웠음

The streets are narrow, filthy and polluted, with zillions of mopeds.Not being killed is an everyday risk!Nobody bothers with crossings.... you’ve just got to move purposely and confidently to cross!To add to the danger, the pavements have either parked mopeds on them or people cooking and eating....anything, I mean anything... noticed fresh chickens and dogs still alive ready for the pot!Every now and then a waft of something comes from a drain grill cover... smells like something horrible... just to add to the fumes from the mopeds!A massive shock.... an experience no less!
세계 어딜가나 맛볼 수 있는 흥청망청한 분위기 그 이상도 이하도 아님. 베트남만의 그 무언가가 느껴지는 거리라고는 생각되지 않음. 야시장도 야시장 특유의 매력이 있으려면 특색이란게 있어야 하는 법일진데. 그냥 쿵쿵대는 클럽 음악과 해피아워로 꼬시는 삐끼들밖에 기억에 남는게 없다.
좁고 씨끄럽고 정신없다.호치민 시티 보다 더 심하다.아주 좁은 길로도 스쿠터가 수없이 다니고 아침 저녁으로 씨끄럽다.베트남에 익숙하지 않다면 그것들을 피해 길건너기도 쉽지 않다.

Maybe this place is more geared up for teenage backpackers and middle aged sex tourists but I really didn't get it or understand all of the excellent reviews it gets. It's really grimy and polluted.This review is from the perspective of a couple in our late 20's/ early 30's.  We stayed here as a place to fly into/ out of and a connecting point between all of our trips in north vietnam- sapa, halong bay & tam coc, which were all excellent places to visit.. stayed in the old quarter 5 nights in total. Here is my summary:Positives:Cheapest drinks I have seen anywhere in the world: 50p a pintGreat restaraunts food at less than £10 per mealNight market atmosphere is alright if you are into a grimy festival vibe- lots of musicians playing in the street and people sitting on tiny plastic chairs eating street food that will probably make them illKnock off north face and Superdry stuff is pretty convincing and cheapUber: really cheap way to get around and always there within 5 minutes.Negatives:Crossing the road- hard to explain how  stressful this is. You just have to walk out into a stream of cars and scooters who are aggressively beeping their horns, texting, carrying huge, strangely shaped loads that must make it impossible to steer, chatting to each other... and hope that they part and let you through. No lights, no crossings just trust in the fact they will let you past. Never seen anything like it.Beeping- gets its own bullet point because the constant beep beep of scooters on a hot day is really annoying.Pavement: no concept of pavement. This is just an extension of the road.Pollution- did the 2 hour lonely planet walking tour of the old town and ended up coughing our lungs out as though we had just smoked 20 cigarettes. Best to limit exposure to the toxic scooter fumes. Go from air conditioned hotel to your uber to your restaraunt. Weekend night market is the best time to walk around as they close off the roads.Dirty run down streets- full of rubbish, saw a rat as large as a cat on my first day. No idea how anyone can stomach the street food as it all prepared on the dirty floor and left to marinade in toxic fumes.Summary:Overall hanoi's old town left me feeling anxious, ill and a little bit disappointed. Worth stopping 1 or 2 nights and not booking hotels in advance so you can move on the the much nicer rural locations  earlier if you want to.But then lots of people I talked to loved it... I think they drank a lot more heavily than we did and stayed for less nights.

Travelling through asia now for months and this is the first city (espacially the old quarter) which I didnt like. The people in shops, traffic, on the street and at the beer were so unfriendly. The place is getting really dirty btw. Maybe i was unlucky, but im not comming back.

As a tourist you have to have seen it but as a person who has lived in Hanoi a couple of years I'd advise against booking a hotel there unless you can't afford anything else. You can go for a few beers in the evening but do yourself a favor and don't go to the clubs. They are filthy and particularly westerners who hang out there are unpleasant to say the least.
メリアハノイホテルから徒歩１０分程度で旧市街の大聖堂に到着。バイク＆車が多すぎて横断するのに苦労しました。おしゃれなお店もいくつかありましたが、そのすぐ隣にローカル食堂があったり、買い物を楽しむという場所ではないですね。ベトナムらしさを実感できるところかも。。。

I loved Vietnam. Fabulous people, fascinating history and interesting culture BUT Hanoi old city is just a 3rd world slum. Incessant scooters blare at you while you walk in the street because the sidewalks are filled with people eating, hawking their stuff or rows upon rows of parked motorbiked. It is hot, noisy and filthy. The night market (when they shut off the street to all but maybe 20% of the motorbikes whol ride through anyway) is much more pleasant and worth a visit.

You lungs will bleed and your boogers black after a 15-minute stroll through its acrid, smog bathed narrow streets.  The continued proliferation of scooters are to blame and the air quality on track to only worsen: a proposed ban on scooters or motor free zones remains years away.  The first of two subways lines won't open until 2018 at the earliest while the public seems to prefer the  point-to-point convenience of personal transport while preliminary mass transit efforts (bus routes) have yet to catch on.
The street wasn't the worst compare to Manila but their traffic was definitely a nightmare for travelers. Drivers weren't friendly, horns everywhere.
Điạ điểm tuỵêt vời để chúng ta ăn ương à vui chơi mà giá bình dânAi chưa đi nên thử một lần để bíêt.

Dirty, disgusting, noisy, scooter hell, rude people. Nice hotel, cheap beer etc but save your money and go elsewhere. Full of white people on holiday, which we are too but this place is crazy tourist trap compared with other, nicer places. Guess it's a thing to do for young people on gap years to pretend to be in "real" Asia. Cambodia has far less money than here, but manage to be friendly and clean up after themselves. Disgusting, never again.

Na het lezen van alle positieve reviews op tripadvisor viel het oude centrum ons een beetje tegen.. Er is weinig te merken van een authentieke sfeer.. Het is gewoon een stadscentrum met winkeltjes en barretjes; niet persé heel bijzonder. De grote hallen met eten & kleding waren wel grappig om te zien.

Old quarter é um local bem antigo, sujo, apertado e barulhento. Com muitas motos e carros passando e buzinando o tempo todo. O lugar era sujo e super lotado. Só tinha produtos falsificados. Não indico este lugar, se você procura relaxar, lá com certeza não é o lugar ideal.
숙소로 이 곳 정하지마세요너무 복잡하고환경이 안  좋습니다저는 교통이 좋다는 웹을 보고 한호텔 에서 3일 다른 호텔에서 2일을 정하고 와보니난감했어요아주 어린 젊은이 들은 모르나 중년이랑 되신 분 들 절대 이곳에 숙소 정하시지마세요
とにかく、交通量が多く歩くのが困難でした。目的地が決まっている場合はタクシーをオススメします。散歩の場合は車道を歩き、車&バイクには負けない心で堂々と歩きましょう。道路沿いで売ってる食べ物、お菓子を食べるのは衛生的にオススメしません。とにかく、排気ガスが凄いので喉を痛めない対策をしましょう

Old Quarter is very dirty. Chickens getting their heads cut off on the side of the road, rats running riot, motorbikes flying thru the street, not caring about you or themselves and not to mention the mafia boys running the restaurants ect.I hated it and couldn't wait to leave. Go to Hoi An = beautiful

It can be wrong to find clean street in Hanoi. But most of environment in hanoi is dirty. What you confront in hanoi street is waste, dust, pollution from motorcycle, and dish washing beside road. Your throat will be offended by pollution. I don't recommend this place to someone who like clean environment.

Hot, hectic, crazy traffic and maddening motor scoters who even come up on the food path at speed. A few worthwhile attractions but not for us. Best to get out of the cities and enjoy the towers and country side.
Never go back again, did not like hanoi crayzy driving dangerous walking. Unpolite people and taxi drivers, and had pick pocket theft...never ever go hanoi again

We're 60 plus folks who like clean, orderly stuff, so you may wish to consider that.  We felt relatively safe coming and going, but it is a mess.  Motorbikes parked everywhere, forcing you to walk mostly in the street.  People washing on the walkway, occasional really nasty odors, and cheap stuff on stands.  Gave it 2 stars because of historical significance.

Stayed in the old quarter for three days and whilst there was lots of markets and shops and food there really was not much to see or do.Naturally their are scooters everywhere and they have priority on the pavement so you walk in the road.As a place to stay to see Hanoi nearby to food its fab as you won't go hungry, other than that wouldn't bother.There's maybe 2 bits of old wall that illustrates the 'old'.It's dirty in places and rubbish piled high
I have been visiting Hanoi for 25 years and sad to say the charm has gone... many of the old small businesses have been pushed out of town due to high costs of rent and real estate

This area doesn't really have side walks for pedestrians to walk on. A lot of motorbikes are parked on the so called sidewalks and people also sell fruits or food there.  The streets are narrow and they are packed with honking cars, tour buses, motorbikes, and people. The shops/stores in that area are very small.

I don't mean to sound rude but the food in these markets should not be able to be even sold, no hygiene, meat left out in the elements and then sold. ONLY drink bottled drinks and try and resist the temptation to eat any street food as only the locals have the stomachs to handle it.

I thought Old Quarter in Hanoi is full of history, but it's an old market where if you're not too careful you get scammed by sellers! Not a very friendly atmosphere and expects that you understand their language

On the outskirts of the old quarter is great street food that is more authentic. This place had lots to do in the night life - in terms of drinking and clubbing late into the night. But I was looking for a more local experience rather than getting pissed. It is called the backpackers area and it is okay to explore for a bit.... but there is a lot more outside the area.
Also mit Vietnam hat das hier nicht mehr viel zu tun. Restaurants und Unterkuenfte sind im vergleich sehr teuer und traditionelles Lebenn ist ist dann wohl eher mal Kommerz.
Too many travel agents!!!!more travel agents than backpackers..... over rated. Lots so usual souvenir shops!!!

Streets are very dirty, buildings are untidy and old and there are many street vendors along narrow streets selling raw meats, seafood (poorly kept), and motorbikes just come really close to you to buy food. Unless you enjoy these places, this is a place you can skip.
Wanted to buy sandals,... and they quoted 500,000vnd! Wouldn't be back to Hanoi if I have a choice! Bah
Such an amazing place that takes you back in time with tiny streets bustling with people and shopfronts selling their wares and making a living.

Many friends have told me how much they enjoyed old Hanoi, so I was somewhat surprised to discover that I found it rather uninteresting.  Whatever architectural merit it once had seems to have been buried by years of modification and neglect, not to mention the ubiquitous drapery of electric and telephone wires that front every street, nor the apparently unbridled business signage everywhere.The shops are not particularly interesting, either, largely overflowing with typical tourist merchandise or with clothing aimed at the local population.  And most of the restaurants and bars offer little obvious appeal.  The vendors are very aggressive here, too, which always turns me off a place.  All in all, it didn't seem to me to have much soul.  And if someone had told me it was all thrown together 10 years ago as a tourist entertainment district I might have half believed them. Around the edges of the old quarter there are some much more pleasant streets with the odd architectural gem and more interesting-looking shops, restaurants, temples, historic sites and local communities.  I'd recommend spending more time here and less in the old quarter itself.

We walked around here for a while at the end of our 3 week trip up Vietnam and there wasn't much to see. Super busy, full of tourists, not much activities and a bunch of people pestering you to buy cheap knockoffs, mass produced souvenirs, and drinks. Not an enjoyable experience.
Don't like this sort of place expensive bars restaurants, you pay two to three times more for food etc than anywhere else in Vietnam and not so nice, just a get money from foreigners place.
The architecture was nice, traffic is terrible,Shops around the area is tourist slaughter. Be very careful with snatch phone thieves.

Nous avions notre hôtel dans ce quartier... les rues sont bondées de monde, de mauvaises odeurs, de bruits etc... les locaux ne sont pas du tout agréables avec nous a moins de leur ouvrir nos portes monnaies ! Ceci dit, c'est dans ce quartier que se font les animations en soirée, qui d'un coup devient plus sympa malgré la surpopulation !

Not my cup of tea. Plenty of redeeming qualities I'm sure but every time I'm here I can't wait to leave. Incredibly busy and dangerous traffic, aggressive hawkers, and lots of rude and indifferent Hanoians. Plenty of nice people here that don't feel like they have to correct your southern viet accent but typically they do.  Living in Saigon it's pretty obvious how aloof and indifferent they are. They just aren't really friendly here overall, not just by my assessment.  By millions of saigonese too. I keep giving Hanoi a chance but Saigon has good food and great history too. And unpretentious people that respond in kind to a smile and hello. Too often here they just scowl and tilt their head back :) I speak the language. With a southern viet accent. I really hate to generalize because of all the great people and historical sites here.  I'm just giving my honest assessment to balance out other reviews.

I recently went to Hano Old Quarter and it was awful, I was staying in a hostel right in the centre of the quarter and absolutely hated the noise from all the other hostels/nightclubs that they have, also felt like I was putting my life at risk walking through the old quarter as all the paths are full of parked motorbikes so I was forced to walk in the hectic roads that they have in Hanoi. After 2 nights I left the old quarter and stayed in a hostel nearer the lake and it was much quieter and still just as easy to get to places

la guida ci ha raccomandato di attraversare le strade molto lentamente, infatti le centinaia di motorini non frenano ma si limitano a scansarti. Il vecchio quartiere e pieno zeppo di negozietti, molti ad uso e consumo dei turisti.
사람들은 불친절하고 매연은 심하고,,, 글쎄요. 다시 오고싶은 생각은 안들것같습니다. 길거리는 숨을 쉴수가 없네요. 그리고 왜 가게 직원들은 하나같이 비웃음을 달고 있을까요? 베트남 특징인가요?
Assez déçu, c'est très sale, et à mon goût pas spécialement pittoresque. Chaque rue a le même type de magasin: rue des casseroles ou de la mercerie...

Sans grand intérêt sauf pour celles atteintes de la fièvre acheteuse....Les couleurs des tissus sont inutilisables en Europe, sauf pour le Viet Minh "revival".Les vanneries sont de très piètre qualité.
O Old Quarter é onde as coisas acontecem em Hanoi. Entretanto é completamente caótico... atravessar a rua é uma aventura.
I love Vietnam but Hanoi especially Old Quarter is the place I wouldn't go back . It is very noisy, dirty, smelly etc. To cross a road you have to be very lucky.

Normally the old quarter in a city has character. Well, Hanoi old quarter has none. Its a street market area that is filthy and difficult to walk on the footpaths. This is due to the motor scooters parked right to the edge and forcing walkers onto the road. If in Hanoi , you probably will go anyway as there is not much else in Hanoi to do. This are is best at weekends when the night market is on north of the lake.
Some buildings may be old, but none are quaint. Do not expect to see French influence. This is a commercial district over run by rickshaws, motorbikes, cars, trucks and busses. Gasp!

First experience in Vietnam and you soon learn that their hygiene is not good and pavements are cluttered with street sellers or motorcycles. Opposite our hotel you see Vietnamese ladies squatting yet working on the pavement, preparing food, sometimes in hygienic rubber suits. The local butcher is a woman with a large tray on the back of a motorcycle, with meat on the tray uncovered!!Not a good image and following our transfer from Hanoi to HCMC where everything is much cleaner, the old quarter has a lot of work to do to improve that image.

We are well travelled, but at every step I felt I needed eyes in the back of my head for fear of being run over. The Vietnamese on bikes or in cars are not aware at all of pedestrians .... the challenge  is that they park their bikes on the pavement so you have to walk on the road for alot of the time . Felt it far dirtier than Bangkok .... not impressed . Some lovely temples and old buildings .... but won't be back

Imagine narrow roads with very dense traffic, which gets even worse due to an absence of traffic lights or rules. Pedestrians have to risk every time they cross the streets which happen every 3-5 minutes. It is totally normal for locals but for foreigners who come from places with strict traffic rules. The head starts paining after 30 minutes of that permanent noise of cars and scooters as well as from attempts to reach a destination. Food is delicious and cheap, but it is served right on the streets which unusual and interesting in the beginning but after some time, you will start missing normal table with a chair. Do not buy any clothes if you hunt for high-quality products, all branded are fake but reasonably cheap.
Same as every dirty, noisy, Asian market during the day. I am told is a lot more fun at night. Not worth visiting - unless you have never seen an Asian market area.

The streets and walkways in this part of town are narrow, which means there isn't as much room to negotiate, but there are all the same kinds of traffic as there are in any large Vietnamese city. It feels dangerous here (a lot of close calls), but ... at night (at least the night we were here) they close down some of the streets to motorized traffic, and the police do a fairly good job of keeping the law enforced. Not entirely, but at least most of the time. And when the roads turn into pedestrian only, the cafes spill out onto the street so that it becomes a very festive scene, with many Vietnamese young people gathering for a fun time and a good meal. Worth seeing, especially at night.
Seen one, seen them all. I hear it is buzzing at night but in the day it is like every other Asian shopping district.

The Old Quarter is one of Hanoi's favorite tourist areas. As you might expect from the name, the streets are narrow and the buildings old. The problem is the sidewalks are used for parking of motorcycles which means people are forced to walk on the very busy streets, where the motorcycle rides and car drivers would as happily run you over as slow down to let you get out of the way. All the streets are extremely busy , all the time. There are a great variety of shops, bars, restaurants and even temples to view. Just be very careful and mindful of the fact that in Vietnam pedestrians do not have right of way.

Hanoi's old quarter is the most touristy place in Hanoi. If you love being surrounded by other tourists, then the old quarter is the place for you. But if you prefer genuine places, then leave the quarter and explore the rest of Hanoi.

Not a nice place to stay. Horrible dirty and polluted lots of people will say differently using words like nostalgia but the reality is most local people struggle to live a decent life here in a polluted and overcrowded city area. One good thing is the traffic here is so bad it travels very slowly so there are few serous accidents. Take care if you can use the sidewalk as people will emerge full speed on a motorcycle from a side alley without warning I was nearly run over. While you will not see anyone starving there is very obvious poverty here it is not a museum for rich people to be romantic about. In general the local people are really nice and welcoming

By far the dirtiest city I've been to in se Asia. Trash everywhere and questionable hygiene. Good things about old quarter:Food varietyVery cheap hotels and restaurantsDecent shoppingStore owners aren't as aggressiveWalkable to many tourists destinationsBia ha noi local beer is 22 cents.Bad:Trash literally everywhere, no garbage cans.Sewage in streetsNo restrooms unless at restaurant.Not a single place I was in had hot water.Very difficult to get around, as too many motorbikes and cars.No such thing as a quiet restaurant.Rude and snobby UK college aged touristsThis isn't the real Vietnam experience...All together Hanoi is fine for a layover but you would be crazy to stay here for more than two days. I just can't figure out why this city seems to be YEARS behind others in Asia, it's really sad and not good for tourism.I noticed a lot of people saying they get scammed on food prices, I would say not true , all street vendor restaurants have little menus with clearly marked prices. If you are asking for special request, yeah you are going to pay extra. We are only talking 50 cents to a dollar more.. if that breaks your  budget you shouldn't be traveling because you know back home it'll cost you ten times more.As with all Asia travel, please know your exchange rate before complaining about getting scammed or paying too much.

Es gibt keine Wege auf denen Fußgänger laufen können. Auf den Gehwegen versperren Mopeds und Straßenbistros den Weg. Touristen sind gezwungen am Straßenrand zu laufen, wo jeder seinen Müll hinwirft. Viele Straßen haben gefährliche Löcher. Also Ausgangspunkt für Touren in die Umgebebung ist das Old Quater gut geeignet.

The Old Quarter is a crazy bustling district of fake North Face Jackets and never ending motorcyclists that won't stop for anyone or anything.  Because of the motorcycles, the pollution is unreal.  I literally had dirt stuck to my teeth from the amount of exhaust floating in the air. I would advise to eat meals at your hotel as the people of Hanoi are aggressive and will scam tourists for every dollar they have.  If I didn't fly into Hanoi I would've skipped this city entirely.
テトの一週間前のせいで、たぶんいつもよりバイクが多くて足をひかれました。混み合った商店街に普通にバイクが行き来している状態ですね。

Old Quarter is one of those tourist destinations that one need to visit and see in Hanoi, I guess.Good place to shop for knock-offs, souvenirs and sample local food.  Be prepared to navigate the chaotic and busy narrow roads and traffic. It was very hectic and the streets can get a bit confusing after a while.  Don't think this will not be one of those places I will want to re-visit.

The old quarter are the streets around the central lake.Regular streets, each street tends to specialise in one type of shop.  The sidewalks are generally covered with seller’s goods, so you have to walk in the street.  This is very hard work, dodging other pedestrians, cars, but worst of all the huge number of mopeds.  You start thinking the locals are bonded to their mopeds and never walk.  The mopeds are usually overloaded with goods, and you stand a high chance of being hit by what the carry.  The noise also very tiring as it is local procedure to toot your car/moped horn every few seconds.The goods for sale are not particularly different from those in other Asian cities, except for lacquerware, however this is to be seen much less than it used to be.So worth seeing when you are in town, but there is better in Saigon or Bangkok.
Too much noise, the traffic is crazy! it is not nice to walk in the street, dont spend more then one day in Hanoï , it is not worth it (it is better to spend your time alsowhere)
More or less like the rest of Hanoi, don't expect a different feel from other areas. But still alright. The lake is nice enough.
기대하고 갔는데 너무 더운데다가 오토바이가 사람도 안보고 다니는 통에 너무 짜증나서 다닐수가없었다. 그냥 휙 통과하고 말아버림.

Hanoi is just a large city, with old buildings. It's not clean, full of smog. It's noisy, crowded, and not much to do, unless you really like watching people on scooters, or shopping for overpriced junk on every street.
Atmospheric and worth visiting but genuine fear of getting mowed down. Shame as charming place but experience is stressful rather than enjoyable.

Cierto que descubres el casco antiguo y las calles más importantes de este, pero sólo puedo acordarme de la mala sensación de estar todo el tiempo respirando los tubos de escapes de las múltiples moros y coches.Es igual que estar en medio del tráfico parado y además temiendo por tu vida
요즘은 가격흥정을 해도 안깍아줍니다외국인한테는 3배 이상의 가격을 받습니다그리고 매연이 심해서 매일 목이 아프네요

Noisy, dirty, crazy traffic, you can't walk on the footpath because off all the motorbikes parked on them or the people siting eating.You risk your life trying to cross the road especially at rush hours.

This is basically the main area for tourists, not necessarily bad because its a large area with lots of locals. But the vendors here are more aggressive, make eye contact or look at their item for sale and they are on you. Here I had a lady basically shove a donut in my mouth saying no charge but when we ate them she then quickly put more in a bag and put it in my hand asking for money. I ended up paying 100,000 for a few donuts. Which is probably 3x more than you should. We also got into a cab that charged us 226,000 ($11) for a ride we took from the opposite direction (hotel) for 40,000. Lots of shops and restaurants, prices are 4x higher than other parts of the city.

First and last in Vietnam, full of backpackers ( even old ones, like me haha ) The food taste like bland Thai-food. Not really anything interesting to see either.  Been to all of south east Asia and love it, But never Hanoi again
ถ่ายรูปก้อจะเก็บเงิน ถามทางก้อจะให้ขึ้นtaxiอย่างเดียว ผู้คนไม่มีน้ำใจ เห็นแก่เงิน มารุมทึ้งนักทองเที่ยวเหมือนแร้ง ไม่ไหวๆ
Noisy, dirty, but worse of all is the caos with the motos. Walking is definitely difficult here. There are lots of small stores selling millions of stuff though. Mostly fakes, however.

We did a cyclo tour through the old quarter as part of an organized tour. Nervewrecking with all the people, scooters and cars, everyone hooting, shouting or making some noise. The smell is rather obnoxious, the shops much of a muchness to any other part of an Asian shopping area. Not much left of the "old" manufacturing or doing business. If you are a first timer in Asia, give it a go, otherwise skip - saving you quite a few nerves in the heat and humidity and noise and smells.

Our hotel was in the Old Quarter, something that we thought would be beneficial.On day one we were scammed by three young pastry sellers, and on most days have been hassled by shoe fixers, who sometimes literally grab your leg as you're passing. We also had people suspiciously following us down the streets, who disappeared the moment you make eye contact and they realise you've sussed them.The worst thing is the traffic. Bikes park all over the pavement forcing you onto the road, there are no manners, they just cut you up. in fact we felt that unless you are in a restaurant then people were in the main, quite rude, and saw Westerners as a source of money, and little else.There is the occasional good shop such as gingko for nice clothes, but mainly the Old Quarter is full of Chinese tat.Not recommended particularly.Only the lake and pagoda are particularly beautiful and worthy of a visit.
We were very unimpressed with the mess everywhere.Streets and even footpaths dangeres with motorbikes riding wild without any rules

Be prepared to be yelled at, scammed, nearly run over and touched constantly. You do have to see it, at least part of it, but it's not something I wanted to spend much time doing. Just more busy Asian streets, yes - kinda cooler cause they're older but essentially more condensed and hectic.
A little too noisy and chaotic for us. Safe but over crowded. Street food doesn't look too appetising.
교통 질서가 아주 엉망인 나라이며 목숨을 내놓고 다녀야 할정도로 수많은 오토바이때문에 무서웠습니다.

We walked for several hour through the Old Quarter and were a bit confused.  There wasn't much to do.  Many trinket and clothing shops but nothing of authenticity.  It's changed a lot over the last decade.  The people are more wealthy now and doesn't feel the same.  However it's much safer than it was 10 years ago.

La verdad es que al llegar a Hanoi nos encontramos una ciudad agobiante, un calor húmedo asfixiante, un olor a comida sin cocinar y en no buenas condiciones higiénicas, un tráfico caótico, aparecen motos por todas partes y ni siquiera se respetan los pasos de cebra. Sin embargo, si se viaja a Vietnam no se puede dejar de visitar, es verdaderamente auténtico. Recomiendo en cualquier caso alojarse en Old Quarter o alrededores (nosotros estuvimos en el hotel Essence Palace y fue una de las mejores decisiones de todo el viaje) puesto que el resto de ciudad (al menos la que nosotros visitamos) no tenía especial interés.Nos pareció interesante el hecho de que cada calle está dedicada al comercio concreto: la calle de las ferreterías, la calle de las toallas, etc).

The old quarter at night reminds me bit of kaon san road in bangkok, but much noisier and crowded. People will try and ask you to come in for food. Watch out for your personal belongings when you come here. it is full of bars and clubs with cheap drinks. its a good experience but unlikely to return.

I spent about and hour today looking for the old quarter only to be told that I was in it and that my hostel was also in it. I expected the streets that sell a certain thing on each street but I believe that these went long ago.

There are plenty of shops around but quality is often bad if you want to buy something or China made.This is not fun to visit because you have to walk on the street the sidewalk being full of motorbikes, tables, chairs, garbages, ... This is called "old quarter" so I was expected to find old houses but there are almost nothing left and the ones remaining are hidden between constructions, cables or other stuffs.Go to Hoi An if you want real historic and beautiful houses ! Very disappointed by this city.

I got the impression from Lonely Planet that this was a place with a lot of historic sites. It's pretty much entirely shops and hotels and swarming with tourists! Some of the more traditional shops are very interesting like the religious artefacts and metalworkers but in general its just hundreds of fake North Face shops.

We stayed at Hanoi Boutique Hotel. Having a long overnight flight decided to eat in at their  boutique Restaurant.It was a really dissapointing experience. Ordered spring rolls as a starter which we greasy and soggy.Main dish was crumbed prawns with accompaniments. The second main course was Norwegian Salmon with a runny mushroom sauce. The mushrooms were a shrivelled up affair. The pictures bear testimony to my gripes and dissatisfaction of dining in house.

I wondered round with a sunken feeling. Lots of bars with people trying to convince you in. The restaurants looked mediocre, you can even have fast food. Take a chance outside of the Old Quarter, you might like it!!!

Locura de trágico en esta ciudad. Abundan las motos e invaden las aceras. Una locura andar por sus calles. Pero es su cultura y están más que acostumbrados. Para cruzar siempre hacia adelante que ellos te esquivan,nunca atrás que les confundes.

Ambience of the Old Quarter is completely spoilt by the vehicle traffic that flows through it - pavements (sidewalks are blocked for pedestrians by parked mopeds meaning you have to walk on the road and are in constant danger) such a sad situation!
골목 곳곳을 누비는 오토바이의 소음과 매연은 걸어다니는 여행객에서 불편함을 줍니다. 스트레스 받고 싶지 않다면, 최소한으로 돌아다니는 것을 추천합니다.

The Old Quarter of Hanoi is a dusty and noisy neighborhood, full of motorcycles on dangerous driving, full of petite and informal business of any kind.  Yes, the Old Quarter has a couple of interesting places and is the place for some wonderful restaurants, but as a whole it is a bad preserved part of a city struggling hard to overcome the poverty and backwardness and the Quarter is its reflection.  Go to the Puppet Theatre, enjoy the vietnamese food in a clean restaurant but do not make praise of the Quarter itself as the good points some attractions could bring to Hanoi cannot whitewash the obvious uncleanliness of the place.  Hope the best for Vietnamese and surely with the coming economic development the Old Quarter will become a real attraction worth to show as a tourist target as there are many in Asia.
Not a lot of interesting items to buy and the food did not seem safe to eat in most places.  It is also HOT during the middle of the day.  Lots of people on tight streets and motorbikes with exhaust.

The hustle and bustle of it is what Vietnam is all about I guess but it was a bit dirty, noisy and crazy. I personally won't be rushing back here, but it is worth checking it out I guess if you are here.
Too much bikes and cars, making horns so loudly all the time. I couldn't enjoy walking at all. But there are some great cafes. If you find it in the building, it would be like a heaven.

was expecting much more French influence or at least some semblance of the french old quarter in Shanghai but it really is more a drink and club area with restaurants....not much of character one would expect
とにかく多くの商店や飲食店が並んでいますが、路上は汚くて原付や車両が遠慮なく突っ込んで来るので危険です。危険なので子連れにはオススメしません。さらに路上や移動販売をしている中には観光客狙いのボッタクリもあるので非常に注意です。客引きも多く、ただでさえ暑くてムシムシしているのでイライラしました笑
베트남 사람들 처음 만난  소감은 좋았읍니다, 순수하고 진실된면이매력이엿읍니다.그리고 애메랄드워터 호텔 근처에 호수가 너무나도인상적이엿읍니다
쇼핑거리에는 노스*이스 등을 저렴하게 살순 있지만(진퉁인지는 모르겠음...) 일단 시내거리 자체가 오토바이가 사람만큼 많고 같은거리로 지나가다보니 너무 혼잡함....하노이에서 있다가 한국오면 엄청난 평온함과 애국심을 느낄정도...

Phố cổ hà nội đối với tôi mà nói khá thú vị, dạo quanh với trà đá vĩ hè, các chú xích lô vòng bờ hồ... tuy nhiên ấn tượng xấu để lại quá lớn khiến tôi không còn lưu lại quá nhiều nét văn hóa đặc sắc của phố cổ, vd như bạn đừng quá ngạc nhiên khi banj phải trả tiền cho một bát bún gấp đôi người khác chỉ vì bạn nói giọng miền nam..
The streets are lovely with old houses, the motorcycles are parking on the footpath so we were forced to walk on the road where motorcycle are running everywhere.
First few days in Vietnam and not ready for all the scooters and traffic and lack of walking space on side walks
交通亂中有序,搭電動車或是人力黃包車要小心您的貴重物品,汽機車亂鳴喇叭, 一堆"號稱"越南制工廠外流的名牌包, 以及名牌外套..價錢隨人亂開,一件 the North face 外套可由100USD砍到40USD...街上的路邊攤,不建議亂吃..怕拉肚子,附近有些西方人是開的PIZZA店或咖啡店倒是可以進去光顧一下..
It's hard to believe there are so many bikes running around.  The air is terribly polluted and the streets are dirty.  But the people are (usually) very kind and the foods are amazing.

Unfortunately the old quarter is where backpackers hostels and cheap hotels are. So therefore it's also full of souvenir/paintings/spa shops, pretty average bars and average to ok restaurants. I get the feeling most tourists don't leave the old quarter so they don't know what else Hanoi has to offer, which is a pity. If you like the tourist feel then it's for you.
You are constantly being hassled by locals, trying to sell you overpriced crap or street food that can make you sick.

Old Quarter es conveniente por: el precio de los hoteles, la infinidad de tiendas para comprar de todo, la cercanía a mercados y puestos de comida, pero el continuo caos de las motos y el tránsito de taxis y camionetas es enloquecedor, y uno siente que su vida corre peligro de accidente.

Since here it's one of those must do things. It's tough to actually see what's in stores because u can't walk on the sidewalks barely, an constantly being aware of the traffic. Then again it's a lot of stuff I'm not interested in buying. How the shop keepers and locals can live like this day in and day out I have no idea but feel soooo blessed!
Our hotel was in the old quarter. If I ever go back it will be to soon. The streets are so crowded the hotel staff or guide has to get you across. It is a necessary evil due to the airport.

I'm not really a city person and places like this are why. You really only need a few hours here (although it is a great place to have a drink and meet people in the evening) then you should head for the lake

The streets are very hard to traverse. The sidewalks have motorbikes parked on them and restaurants set up on them leaving you no choice but to be forced to walk in the street at times. People are everywhere and the motorbikes don't seem to care if they hit you. I was hit by the handlebars of one of the bikes and witnessed another lady being hit also. The drivers of the motorbikes don't even say sorry or excuse me when they hit you either. It's a crazy place and I thought the vendors were rude. I had more than one person grab me. One person was trying to move me out of the way of her stand as I was looking at a map. Apparently, I was in the way of her wares she was trying to sell. Another lady grabbed me when I tried to ignore her as she was selling the fried doughnuts. The sellers walking the streets just did not take "no" for an answer. They were very aggressive and this went for the cyclo-tour drivers, the vendors selling doughnuts, the shoe-shiners and the guys selling watches. They targeted my kids when I said no to them hoping that the kids would want what they were selling. One lady put a hat on my son and tried to put a hat on me and this was AFTER I had told her "no" twice. A doughnut seller continued to follow us and put the doughnut on a stick in my seven year old son's face to the point where he had to run from her. It was ridiculous!

It's quite nice to walk around however we knew we were heading to Bkk the following day so I think that helped knowing that we didn't have long to put up with the endless honking of horns which was starting to drive me crazy! All sidewalks are blocked with mopeds so didn't feel very safe carrying my 1 and half year old. Definitely won't be going back to Vietnam at all.

After visiting most markets in Vietnam, this market seemed to lack charm and the manoeuvring between motor bikes parked at the front of most shopfronts took away from a leisurely stroll - rather requiring shoppers to walk into the traffic to manoeuvre the area. The products were mostly seen elsewhere else - hence no real pulling power to ever return. Highlight of this visit was to have a coffee at Coffee World at the beginning of the market and watch the world go by from the fourth floor of the building which also included a view over the lake. Save your pennies for Hoi An or Ho Chi Min markets.
We ve been there, and when we enter the room it was a disgusting humid smell, and the walls were broken and with water dots.

Not much to see really. Small shops selling bits and pieces. not many tourist / souvenir shops. Having done a tour of Vietnam, I would not bother with Hanoi as threes not much to see. The lake looks nice from a distance, but on close inspection it is riddled with green algae and lots of dead fish floating about. Saigon is much better and definitely worth a visit!

Ich war schon in vielen verschiedenen Städten dieser Erde aber so unwohl wie im old quater in hanoi hab ich mich noch nirgends gefühlt ... Obwohl die unzähligen Cafés und Street Restaurants durchaus eine Erfahrung wert sind.

Brouillant, très sale, on ne sais pas où nous pouvons manger tellement est dégoûtant tout par tout.  Par fois nous avons eu du mal à respirer. Il on du boulot pour rattraper leur retard.  L'art de vivre.... N'est pas la. Le seul bon côté dans cette ville, les gens son très gentils.  On reste juste deux jours, merci.
ツアーでほんの少し訪れました。ガイドさんの勧めでマンゴーを2個500円とランブータンを5〜6個で50円で買いました。とても甘くて美味しかったです。時間があれば他の色々な店に行きたかったです。移動は皆んなで横に並び道路を横断しましたが、本当に怖いです。

Bottom line = nothing great to see here.There are a few positives however, Bia Hoi is cheap and you can sit on many a street corner and enjoy a rather poor tasting, but cheap and refreshing beer. Always a nice place to meet new people.Walking around in the area is manic. Pavements (sidewalks) are blocked with all sorts, motorbikes, scooters, tables, chairs, cats, dogs, butchered pigs and bright red parrots. This leaves you to brave the streets where you will find yourself dodging cars and bikes ever other second. I know this is Vietnam and thats how they drive here, but it makes the simple task of walking from A to B, a pain.When not dodging scooters flying towards you, you will be accosted in the street by every man, woman and child, hooping and hollering, trying to get your money in one way or another.This is a place to stay between other destinations. Unless... you still enjoy Magaluf 18-30's style, EDM filled nightlife.The weather is similar to Oldham (in the North of England) but you will find people less friendly and less civilised, which is saying something!

Misschien lag het aan het weer (koud en nat) misschien aan ons humeur, maar dit 'hoogtepunt van Hanoi' kon ons niet bekoren. Er was een enorme drukte/chaos waardoor je vaker stilstaat dan loopt en de winkeltjes, streetfood tentjes zijn niet bijzonder te noemen. Daarbij zijn de prijzen ook nog eens enorm duur vergeleken met de rest van Vietnam! Wat ons betreft niet bijzonder genoeg om voor om te rijden.
การจราจรแออัดมาก รถมอเตอร์ไซค์พร้อมที่จะชนคุณตลอดเวลาคนไม่มีมารยาท เจอป้าหลอกขายขนม ทอนเงินผิดแล้วไม่ยอมคืนเงิน
何度か道路横断が大変です。落ち着きたい人には不向きかもしれません。雑貨に興味ある人には楽しいがもしれません。
Visited local bars and restaurants. Some nights there are live bands playing in the street and the police cordon off the area to make it safe from motorcycles.
Nach allen Kriegen gibt's nichts historisches in Vietnam. Daher sieht man dort verschwitzte Touristen, elende Backpacker, Diebe und schlechtes Essen. Jede Deitenstraße, jedes Dorf in Hanoi ist besser
Far better places in Hanoi. Walk to the far end of the lake, shops decent ( good book shops) and it's safer. Far less likely to be robbed or run over than in the very very overrated old town.
É um grande mercadão a céu aberto. A cidade não tem hábitos alimentares higiênicos, preparam a comida no chão em panelas.
C'est un incontournable à Hanoï mais reste trop bruyant, sale et surtout trop de motocyclistes !!! À voir en quelques heures ça suffit !!

The noise-level and air-quality in the old quarter is unbearable if you are not used to it. Take it from someone who has lived in Shanghai for four years! The streets are very narrow and completely packed with motorcycles spitting out smoke. The shops and parked motorcycles take up all sidewalks so you are walking in the street which means you will have people driving by on either side of you all the time and even touching you because they get so close. In one hour I saw two smaller accidents where motorcycles actually collided with each other. The offerings of the shops is very poor, old fashion, low quality, very little variation and is not catered to foreign tourists at all. This is where the locals buy their clothes and other things.

Deze buurt is onaangenaam om te wandelen. Auto's en brommers rijden je van je sokken. Geen respect voor de toerist, zelfs niet in de zogenaamde voetgangerszones. Daarenboven wordt je voortdurend aangeklampt door venters (vrouwen), riksja en xe om mannen die je achtervolgen. Maak de volledige buurt echt auto - en brommer vrij en het wordt heel wat aangenamer voor de toerist die nu opgejaagd wild is.

Local com comércio nas ruas, algumas ruas bem degradadas, restaurantes sujos e uma infinidade de motos que torna quase impossível atravessar as ruas.Comércio bem variado, principalmente com prudutos chineses de baixa qualidade nas ruas, e de melhor qualidade nas lojas.Não tive coragem de comer por lá.

We flew into Hanoi and were expecting a pieceful and quite country. The airport is, but then we came into the city and there are scooters/cars driving everywhere, too many people and it is one of the most dirty cities in SE-Asia. We would never go back and recommend to skip is and go immediately to Sapa or Halong Bay.

All the guidebooks say this area is the "must-see" of Hanoi, but really we found it just a crowded, chaotic, noisy cluster of souvenier shops. It was super stressful trying to avoid all the scooters and traffic. Would be nicer if they made it pedestrian-only.
