The Hanoi old Quarter may be iconic, but your lungs won't thank you. There were so many people without masks. Please realise that you are not doing yourselves any favours by breathing in the fumes. The shopping in Hanoi is better than Saigon. The food was not bad.  If you have the time, visit Aeon Mall.

Not a fan of chaos at all. This place is pure chaos. If you like crazy drivers, lots of bikes, dirty streets, dirtier street food and constant honking go here. I really don't know what people find attractive about this place.
dơ, hàng quán đông đúc, bán đồ ăn toàn bột ngọt, ai dị ứng sẽ sưng người đường nhỏ, xe chạy nhanh, không né là bị ủi,

Hanoi Old Quarter or just Hanoi in general is overcrowded. It is not worth staying for more than two days. The air is filthy and dusty. People wear masks in and around the city. Because the locals know that there are lots of tourists, they over charge tourists.
I don't know if they truly wash the bedsheets.

Can be very busy especially at the night market on the weekends, bikes parked on the footpath makes it difficult to walk around and you may have to walk on the road with oncoming traffic. Would not recommend if you have young children.

Little of the historic architecture or ambience left, unless you manage to find a spot to look up every now and then. The shopfronts are almost all vendors selling tourist tat - great if you want a north face jacket, postcards or t-shirts. The street food isn’t that great in my opinion, you end up perched on a tiny seat breathing in diesel fumes from the never ending stream of scooters. Worth seeing once but as soon as you’ve seen a couple of streets/alleys you’ve seen them all really.

I was not a fan of Old Quarter. It's very busy with motorbikes and cars driving completely crazy, I got hit by taxi doors and by motorbikes going by. The shop workers tended to be quite rude to us and the whole experience was unpleasant, the streets are over crowded and there's a lot of rubbish. The best thing to do is train street, but by the time we wanted to do it we were exhausted from the city and didn't go. Grab taxis also won't take you anywhere in the old quarter for less than 100,000 so getting grabs is very difficult.
Not good for shopping in Vietnam. Better to shop at Hcmc.

Chaos, noise, pushy vendors, trash everywhere, all sidewalks completely occupied by local restaurants, or shops, or parked motorbikes. Even in a pharmacy, when we wanted to buy something and asked for the price, they said a price about 6 times the real price (they said 100k). When we insisted and they scanned the item, the real price was 17k. And this was a pharmacy, not a local souvenir store... Those guys promoting their restaurants - I had to physically push a few out of the way. They just don't let you pass. Glad I saved only a couple of days for this area and used the rest of my vacation for the rest of the country. I would choose Hoi An any day over Hanoi.

Hanoi old quarter is old stated in the name. Its hotels are dirty and old not very well maintained. People are rude and I'm helpful and I'm of Vietnamese background. If you want the old quarter vibes that is clean and well preserved hoi an is where to be.

Very busy and the people are a mix of rude or "forced niceness". As a western tourist, be careful of scammers and people who take advantage.  The food is mostly good. I recommend sticking to street food vendors as the restaurants I visited were more expensive but less flavour.  There's a lot of culture and history in the old quarter, so definitely book a tour.
Do not stay at the Hanoi old quarter if you're visiting hanoi! Everything is more expensive and you're prone to scams. Better to stay a bit outside the old quarter, and rent a scooter to move around.

Crowded, dirty place, bustling with tourists and locals. The pedestrian alleys need urgent maintenance, as well as the buildings is all area. New buildings erected here and there are no architectural match with the rest of the neighborhood. No street cleaning, and the locals are throwing everything anywhere they please. There is no trash bin in the whole area. Stuff being sold in shops is low quality Chinese items. With a good management, this area could become a tourist icon, but currently it is left in ruin.

Annoying to be offered anything from a toothbrush to an "i can show you everything" ride every 5 minutes, seldomly accepting a no. When you turn it down, people turn sort of angry. Outside of this quarter, the real Hanoi is much more enjoyable I personally find. Vietnamese people are commonly very friendly and very authentic once you avoid the tourist traps.

Too busy, Vietnamese don't care if you are tourist or not, they don't care if they hurt anyone riding a motobike everywhere, no safe pavement for pedestrians, not a safe place to visit unless you are backpacker and want a drink. The good option is to get a tuk tuk or riksza.
This place is not for everyone. I find it quite dangerous to get around this place. It’s very chaotic and hectic. Cars and bikes everywhere. Not recommend to those with elderly or kids.
Very filthy borough and when you eat there, you re risking food poisoning in basically every restaurant within borough. I am never coming back there...
Đường xá chật trội và hay bị tắc đường. Đồ ăn ngon, đa dạng, hương vị tuyệt vời
Khá tệ, chật chội, đông đúc, chất lượng ko xứng với túi tiền
The traffic is not control well. Crowded at night.
Chỉ đường linh tính quá. Hôm đấy mình tìm mãi mới tới điểm đến
Rất lộn xộn và có tình trạng chặt chém
Chặt, chém ngày tết.
Địa điểm nên đến, xem phương tiện vận chuyển cổ đại
Old messy and dusty place. Motorcyclists are very rude.
Closes at 10pm. People always trying to rip you off!
Ko hợp lý. Dịch vụ kém.
Not a great place, clean but just not my taste
Xuống cấp
Very crowded, dirty, noisy and chaotic traffic
rooms are worn and not maintained well
Nhộn nhịp
Beware of spammers
Extremely crowed and loud.
по-сути это огромный рынок, даже жаль тех, кто поселился в местных отелях - постоянный шум, мусор, толпы людей.
住這邊飯店不要選靠街邊的，你會吵到無法睡覺，喇叭和車聲叫整晚的很誇張，老城區的人都不用睡覺嗎？？
とにかくバイクが多い 歩行者とか関係なく、クラクション鳴らしまくってガンガン攻めてきます ノーヘル4人乗りなんか当たり前、カオスです ノースフェイスのコピー商品が多々売っていますが、質は良く無いと思います
Vietnam'a geldigime pişman eden yer. Diğer bölgelerin güzel olduğunu bilmeseydim dönerdim. Hanoi bütünüyle kaos.
Trop bruyant pour moi comme une grosse partie de la ville . Les nombreuses motos sont reine !
短期間の滞在なら問題なし。今回は10日も居たので食べ物に飽きました。タイに比べると食べ物のバリエーション少なすぎ。
오토바이와 차사람이뒤섞여 혼란 그자체입니다 소매치기 조심해야합니다 2일간 뒤에서미수2번목격했습니다 소리지르자째려보고 갑니다 정신없습니다 가방조심 피해시경찰의 대응은 미흡합니다 외국인에게 호의적이지않습니다 당황하지마시고 대사관에꼭연락하고 도움을 받으세요차라리호엠끼엔 주변으로가세요 숙소도 구시가지보다 호엠끼안호수 주변을 추천드립니다
基本就是小商品批发市场，整个河内都不必来😂😂😂
食中毒に注意！焼き鳥は焼き加減がいいが、網の使い方が下手。中までひがとおったかな～ってくらいのを別の箇所でキープしといて、受注したら仕上げにジュージューしてるんだけど、それをする網が焼けていない肉用の網で共有してるから危ない！5日間トイレで過ごしました！🐔
개인적이지만... 너무 시끄럽고 혼란스럽고.  그게 다입니다.  좋을수도 있겠지만

Lieu animé où il est appréciable de passer quelques soirées, bonne ambiance. Points négatifs: • Des arnaques à chaque coins de rue (dans les boutiques toutes identiques, les vendeurs ambulants, les taxis, les restaurants et bars). En tant que touriste, vous devenez un porte-monnaie sur pattes, vous ne pouvez pas marcher tranquillement sans que l'on essaie de vous vendre quelque chose. Soyez prêt à fortement négocier les prix si vous voulez acheter des souvenirs par exemple, car on vous fera payer un prix parfois plus cher qu'en occident. • Du bruit, des klaxons et une circulation incessante, mais ça c'est partout à Hanoï.

Trop de commerces, surtout destinés aux touristes en plus, trop d'enseignes, plutôt déçu du coup  Les maisons coloniales d'époque dans leur jus sont sacrifiées au profit d'un tourisme de masse qui ne satisfait que l'économie vietnamienne mais pas les touristes eux-mêmes qui mériteraient beaucoup plus d'authenticité. Ne reste à apprécier que les étages supérieurs ou l'intérieur de certains cafés. Même problème qu'à Hoi An...
추천하지않습니다. 너무 혼잡하고 환경위생은 더 말할필요가없고.  그냥 St성당. 극장 백화점거리 돌고 커피숍 구경이나 하고. 코코넷커피 시원하게 한잔 하고.  호텔근처에서 맥주나
과일 2개 구매하고 기분 나빴어요 바가지씀
街道狹窄，狹窄的人行道上停滿機車，路上都是機車亂竄，還瘋狂按喇叭，如果台灣是行人地獄，這裡就是行人地獄的第18層。

여행가이드북에서 별 5개로 소개한 곳이라 너무 큰 기대를 했는가봅니다. 그냥 관광객들을 위한 상가들을 포함해서 우리나라 남대문 시장처럼 특정 테마를 가진 상가들이 특정 거리에 몰려 있는 그런 구역입니다.  실제 관광객들이 구경할 만한 것은 그리 많지 않다고 생각되며, 베트남 느낌을 주는 물품들은 관광객들을 위한 상품뿐이라 다른 관광지에서도 충분히 볼 수 있는 것들입니다.  건물들이 오래되었는데 이는 서울의 을지로골목같은 느낌을 주어 큰 새로움을 주지 않습니다.  위에 소개된 것들을 좋아하시는 분들이라면 모르겠지만.. 젊은 친구들에게는 그냥 오래된 거리들로 인식될 것 같습니다.

후기랑 사진 보고 흔히 알고 있는 기찻길 마을인 줄 알았는데 전혀 아닙니다. 기차가 지나가는 길도 7분 정도? 는 걸어야 나옵니다. 이 거리가 시작되는 길을 따라 걷다보니 동쑤언 시장 건물이 나오기도 하고 좌우에 여러가지 상가도 있어서 보기에 지루한 거리는 아니었지만 본 목적지였던 카페를 가던 길이어서 그랬던 것 같기도 하네요. 기찻길이 보고 싶으시면 기찻길 마을 검색이나 롱비엔 역을 검색하시는 게 낫겠네요.

Velmi pěkné místo, ale - motorky parkují na chodníku a turisté musí po silnici. Když vás někdo srazí, tak s nejvyšší pravděpodobností z místa ujede  - téměř nikde nejsou ceny a prodávající je určuje podle obličeje  - i v lékárně když potřebujete léky tak se vás snaží okrást a zaplatíte 100% až 200% přirážku oproti místním
雖然代表舊城區，可是真的很破舊，街道髒亂不堪，路很狹窄，經過很不舒服
Просто один большой рынок в перемешку с мотобайками. Грязный, неприятный и загазованный. Достопримечательностей практически нет и они не интересные.
여길 왜 갑니까...  올라온 풍경사진은 하나같이 열악한 환경 속에서도  구슬땀 흘리며 최선을 다하여 촬영하시고 수정하신 분들의 작품  실상은 참담
空気が悪いのと、バイクが多すぎて大変でした
ทางเดินเท้ามีของวาง เดินไม่สะดวก
Ziemlich nervig,weil man ständig angesprochen wird von den Verkäufern der Essen und Massageläden!!
非常擁擠 寸步難行
Sehr dreckig, Luftverschmutzung ausgereizt bis zum Maximum
No encontré nada que pudiera llamarse turístico, exótico u original
einmal gesehen reicht aus, nichts Besonderes
호객과 매연과 클락션만 있는곳
Sale, trop bruyant, inintéressant
有机会的话，还想再去！
엄청나게 번잡한 오토바이의 통행
Petit, vieux hotel

This part of Hanoi is famous for its old city charms. For an Indian its like Purani Dili. Crowded with lots of cheap shopping options where you need to bargain like crazy. Difficult to get around in a car. So you need to stop at one place and then walk around the area to actually enjoy it.

Having been born in Saigon 60 years ago, the culture of cleanliness is still not there.The city is noisy, and the traffic is each for himself. A lady fell on the roadway, with her child in her arms, no one stops to help her. People with their motorcycles go forward without yielding. Too bad.

Great food and great shopping.Dont be ripped off without a bargain.Haggle everything.. yes that's right everything unless its the food items at the foodaries.You will be surprised what you can get away with.

The Old Quarter in Hanoi is definitely a unique place with a lot of character. The narrow streets, old buildings, and buzzing atmosphere give you a real sense of local life and history. There’s always something happening, and the street food is a highlight – delicious and cheap!However, it can be overwhelming. The traffic is intense, with motorbikes coming from all directions, and walking around can feel chaotic and stressful at times. It’s also quite noisy and crowded, especially in the evenings.Overall, it’s worth a visit for the experience, but be prepared for the hustle and bustle. Not ideal if you’re looking for peace and quiet.

The old quarter is indeed to visit but especially not take a hotel there. The noise, the mopeds, the stools, every time you cross a street you think you are simply dying. That's terrible. Let's not forget the pollution that takes your throat. The 36 streets are there but not just the names of the corporations. They disappeared for restaurants, hotels, that's a shame. In this neighborhood the Vietnamese in this neighborhood are not very friendly
The street market is the place to shop. You will get everything from souvenir to clothes and at very reasonable rates. Though you need  bargaining skill..

The Old Quarter is just very busy and hectic for my liking. It was chaotic with cars and scooters and no one actually followed any lights. For local Hanoians, it’s their routine. For tourists, can be feeling frustrating at first. Needed a few days to get used to the vibe

The place is an old district in Hanoi which features the historical past if Hanoi...It is usually quiet in the morning up to around 6 pm, and the place turns into a vibrant place at night.  The streets will then be filled with people dining and drinking in shops mostly selling food and beer.  Hundreds of massage parlors, coffee shops, souvenir stores and specialty shops open in the afternoon until around midnight.  A night market also opens on weekends selling all kinds of stuff from food, clothes, toys, souvenir items etc

A visit to the old quarter is a must. However, I was here last about 20 years ago and it's changed for the worse. I remember having a cup of tea and listening to the blacksmiths working. Now it's all cars and motorbikes and horns. Guess it's called progress.
Khá khó tìm đường, nên chủ động tìm hiểu trước về đường đi trên google map hoặc hỏi dân bản địa. Giá thành đồ ăn đồ uống nếu không cẩn thận sẽ bị chặt chém.

I took a walk here and there without any particular purpose, but there were many attractive general stores and cafes, and foreign tourists stood out. In particular, many foreign tourists gathered in front of the Banh Mi store, which is Listing in the Guide, so that they did not miss the place. Incidentally, there was a call for cyclo, but it was not that easy to back down.

Went first to an opticians - within in an hour my friend had her eyes tested and came away with a pair of glasses. Very cheap compared to the UK and Australia.Walked around a little but very hot so hopped on a bus tour and sat on top deck. Unfortunately, the English audio was not working so we only had the map to look at. However, after we saw the main sights from the bus we did not feel that inspired to return.

Interesting area with numerous shops. Exceptionally good in the night with the night market. However Hanoi is full of interesting side streets and certainly a lot less touristy. Try and find them to get a better local experience!
Nice tourist spot. But it is losing its authenticity of Hanoi and Vietnam. It's too crowded. It's a normal shopping street. There's nothing special about it.
Crossing the street in Hanoi was an absolute nightmare! The motorcycles never seem to stop, and it honestly felt a bit dangerous.

Interesting shopping streets selling a variety of local products, food and clothes etc. each street used to specialise in certain areas eg one street sold lanterns and one silk etc back in the day but with times, this has changed but you can see the remnants of this.Not stroller or wheelchair friendly- please do not take young children as traffic is really bad. Uneven footpaths and broken pavings make it dangerous to walk and motorcycles regularly drop be in the footpath. This is an extremely busy area

Glad I came to see it.  But couldn’t waitTo leave.  Made the mistake of staying here three nights. Way too long for me.  It’s an absolute zoo.  Sidewalks are packed with scooter and vehicles so pedestrians are forced to walk on the street dodging cars and scooters.  Some of the quiet streets are cool to see but they are very far and few between. Morning are quieter. Once noon hits, it’s a golf show. 1 Full day in the old Quarter would have beenPlenty.

Crossing the road takes courage, and sometimes motorbikes run over your feet. On windless days, the smog is worse than in Beijing, and it makes my asthma worse. But it's fun. Unlike Ho Chi Minh City, touts are less prevalent. Fake? But you can get pretty good quality clothes at outrageous prices. "I shouldn't have brought a change of clothes like this," my companion muttered, but no, you're going to take those back, right? They won't fit in your wardrobe!

A chaotic, vibrant, dirty and polluted place. If you’re young, like to party and thrive in chaos this might be the place for you. The food is good, the coffee excellent and the people are friendly. I met some lovely young families around Hoan Kiem Lake which are memories I hold dear. Ultimately, though, after 1-2 days I really wanted to leave.

Old quarter is Hanoi's very center, a maze of streets dating back to medieval times built in free (not grid) style, later added with French influence.While many say it's the soul of the city, I'd say that soul is too chaotic and touristy. Therefore, I prefer Saigon city layout.Walking Old quarter streets is picturesque, but soon you get enough of it. Huge hustle and bustle of street sellers, thousands of mopeds, bars around Beer street reminding on Pattaya but in tiny streets hard to go through....really not my cup of tea.

Crowded Chaotic and nostalgic. Not the areas you wanna be taking a taxi through. Lots of 'factory outlets' and tourist priced restaurants. Eateries will cost almost double the prices of those outside the Quarter limits. Worth a couple of visits but recommend living just outside the quarter area.

It's very complicated, they took me around with buggies, there used to be knock-knocks, but there is no such thing as traffic rules, there are shops selling fake products left and right, food places selling street products (it smelled very bad), even though there are eye-catching products on some streets, it is very difficult to walk around there. It's difficult, no one follows the rules, sellers start bargaining with almost ten times the price, I didn't enjoy it
An amazing place to hang out, enjoying almost everything that Hanoi has to offer, from view, people, food and entertainment! Totally worth to visit.

Das Quartier ist mir zu laut, zu hektisch, Halligalli Party der Touristen die halbe Nacht, zu viele Touristen und dementsprechend ist das Benehmen der Einheimischen.  Es ist toll, dieses Quartier zu besichtigen, aber hier wohnen würde ich kein zweites Mal.

Really thought we would love this place.  Didn't hate it, but really didnt enjoy it either.  The traffic is crazy and the cafes are super cool and there is a lot going on, cool hotels, it is a tourist hotspot, so super crowded but filthy.  I think that was what made us not enjoy it, stuff dripping on you from overhead (not raining), blood or fish guts all over footpath, or grease from bike shop.  It was constant, much cleaner walking on the road.
Hanoi is not as large as Saigon, we manged to walk around most of the tourist areas in about 6 hours.  Very traditional but streets a bit dirty.  Would recommend trip to the old prison

This place has become too much of everything. Used to be a bit hectic a couple of years ago but now it's utterly mayhem to the point it's hard to enjoy the very cool things this neighborhood has to offer.Go on a weekend and find the streets where traffic is not allowed

El Old Quarter de Hanoi es un lugar de visita obligada. Su belleza arquitectónica no es muy destacable, pero sus calles estan siempre muy animadas, con música en vivo en muchas esquinas, y un sin fin de locales para comer y tomar algo. Pero me decepcionó un poco porque ha perdido su sabor local, lo cual es comprensible debido a la enorme cantidad de turistas que visitan esta zona.  La calle "beer street" está abarrotada de extranjeros, el mercadillo nocturno apenas tiene puestos con productos locales, son todo objetos y ropa importada de china, con copias de mala calidad de marcas de lujo. Si quieres comprar ropa local no la encontrarás en el Old Quarter. Vete a la tienda Huu la la, al lado de la catedral, encontrarás autenticas preciosidades bordadas a mano. Y otra zona con pequeñas tiendas locales que venden artesania vietnamita y ropa de diseñadores vietnamitas es la calle Hang Trong, a la altura del número 96.
The old Quarter is mainly shopping streets.Few interesting temples, nice street food, but nothing unique if you not knowthe exact places to visit.
La zona mas turística de Vietnam y yo diría que la mas sucia y descuidada de todo Hanoi. Un caos de gente, motos, ruido y contaminación.  No nos impresionó para bien, sino todo lo contrario.

real feel of old vietnam but motornikes everywhere honking and all parked on paths so can only walk on the road which causes more honking.  it all assails your senses.  find a temple for a bit of relief

This is Hanoi's historic center. Dotted with some souvenir shops and tourist restaurants, it is still largely a local neighborhood. Many of the buildings are quite rundown and the surrounding can be filthy, raucous and chaotic. Adjust your expectation before visiting.

The Old Quarter is a very chaotic area of Hanoi. We booked a hotel in the heart of the madness with our 7 month old baby and it was very challenging to get around the neighborhood with him. If I had known how active this area was I probably would have looked for a hotel elsewhere, but we did our best to try to find the quieter streets and to find restaurants that we could hang out at to take a break. The park and lake are gorgeous and we spent quite a bit of time there too. I think this area would be much more enjoyable for people without kids, but it's definitely not an area to be missed in Hanoi!

È il cuore pulsante della città Un caos, colori luci odori rumori motorini qst è Hanoi Street train e’ una cosa stranissima se vai al quartiere francese siediti in Street beer e scegli tra i tanti mini locali dove si mangia asiatico molto economico ( io al Bistro) Infine ti consiglio un massaggio ai piedi prima di andare a letto
Being an indian, we have such old quarters in almost every city and used to such rush. Luckily i just stayed only 1 day :)
We had a nice time wandering around, but be aware it was definitely some culture shock and took a little getting used to the chaos.

Văn hoá phố cổ, dù rằng nó là một nét văn hoá đặc sắc của Việt nam, nhưng liệu có tốt cho con người hiện đại của chúng ta: ngừoi dân sống như ổ chuột, hàng quán mở ra chiếm hết vỉa hè, giao thông lộn xộn, không mũ bảo hiểm,... Thấy nhiều điểm hay nhưng xét về khía cạnh cuộc sống hiện đại, nó dường như đang làm chúng ta tụt lùi hơn,

Vẫn mang một nét cổ kính trong văn hoá người dân . Ở đây kiến trúc đã thay đổi theo năm tháng nhiều , mọc lên nhiều hàng quán . Cá nhân tôi thấy trật tự vỉa hè cần xem lại , giao thông ở đây rất khó khăn cho du khách đi lại . Cần một sự chỉnh trang đô thị để phục vụ người dân và du khách

The streets are quaint and historically interesting, but many shops sell exactly the same thing and one is constantly harassed by pushy vendors trying to clean one's shoes, take one for a ride or whatever.
Je suis allé à Ta Hien stressé pour boire de la bière, le quartier était très animé. Plats variés servis avec de la bière.

El Old Quarter es un barrio histórico de la ciudad, centro de la misma con  gran movimiento, gran cantidad de  locales, mucha gente y un poco de caos. Recomendable de  visitar, para sentir un poco la escancia de la ciudad.

Purely due to its history, the old quarter is losing its "old" tag.  Many upscale places and too much attention to tourism has begun to chip away the character of its former self. Ta Hien Road is still fun to visit after dark, but most everywhere else has given into progress.
ฮานอยช่วงที่ไปอากาศเย็น เดินสบาย สายกิน ยิ้มได้เลย มีอาหารพื้นบ้าน ร้านกาแฟแนว สวยๆๆให้เลือกกินเยอะ เดินเพลินๆๆกับการชมวัฒนธรรม ศิลปะ ที่ยังคงความเก่าแก่อยู่
Phố cổ HN đất chật người đông, cứ chen chúc nhau. Nhiều con ngõ nhỏ chỉ đủ 1 người đi được. Nhiều lúc tự hỏi không hiểu sao họ sống ở đây bao năm được?

The streets of Old Hanoi are worth a look but you take your life in your hands with traffic ignoring the traffic lights and coming at you from every direction. Just when you think it’s safe to cross the road after looking every which way and step out, another scooter comes hurtling towards you. Lots of shops if you’re a shopaholic, many selling the same things. If you can get on the pavements they are very uneven but quite often you are forced to walk in the road because of the scooters parked there, along with the other debris. We were quite glad to get back to our hotel just a short stroll away.

As an old quarter there is nothing in particular to distinguish it from that of other cities. The old lake is nice (and the illuminations beautiful). As our French friends would say 'bof! but interesting from the point of view of seeing a different culture and the people going about their day to day trades.
Very noisy, polluted city. It is the capital so you have some interested historical museums to visit. Would limit it to 2 or 3 nights.
We spend the afternoon here but it was nothing spectacular. Lots of shops selling fake goods and lots of scooters. We have seen better Old Quarter areas in other cities.

I am not overly fond of markets, and this is a busy almost chaotic environment. Here in Vietnam stopping for red lights for the 45 m motor cycles/ mopeds is dependent on how the driver feels. You can be run down standing on the pavement so place little faith in the green man safe to cross lights, always look everyway possible and never relax.  The old town has an interesting street by street specialism. We asked our hotel to send us to the money change market but out taxi dropped us off in thr street that repairs motor scooter seats! All very confusing. The okd town market is open 7 days a week so dont wait for the evening! Overall not my cup of tea.....

The streets are too crowded and the traffic is dangerous. Cars and motorbikes are not careful with pedestrians.  There are plenty of places to eat and a uninteresting night market. But, the area around the lake is very pretty and easy to walk.
Super fun, muitas motos, quase fui atropelada 10 vezes em 5 minutos. Buzinas, estresse, loucura. Vale a pena conhecer e testar sua capacidade de ficar fria e zen.

I suppose I must have been expecting something more like a Latin American or European old quarter but Ha Noi’s is nothing like those. It seemed to us to be just a more concentrated collection of small shops on narrower streets. Worth a quick visit, I suppose, just to say one has been there/seen that, but I have no wish to revisit.

In the old quarter I walked around in amazement. There is many things to look at and see. Your choices of views are endless and your senses will be overcome with the joyfulness of this location.I have been visiting the Old Quarter since 2004 and each time I visit there is something that is new and interesting to feast my eyes on. You can look at Hoan Kiem lake, you can look at old buildings, you can sit in a cafe and look at the people. There is even more than that to look at.Go now and look at it yourself!
人潮洶湧、交通繁雜、景象多元、環境噪雜、、、 對觀光客來說，雖覺得不安全、忙亂、繁雜、、但也好像又有點亂中有序，逛了許久，也不見什麼不安全違法的事故、、但路邊小吃的衞生急須改善加強，在CoronaVirus 疫情發展的特別情況，如不改善，後果不堪設想！
Típica calle de ambiente asiático , q para mi gusto es un quiero y no puedo!! No obstante, unas birras sea donde sea siempre sientan bien!!!
È un tipico vecchio quartiere ma un po’ trascurato e non particolarmente ricco. Sembra abbastanza tranquillo.
La visita en bici es curiosa pero si ya estás alojado en el centro puedes visitar la zona paseando Si el alojamiento no está céntrico puede ser interesante
The Old Quarter is famous, and justly so. However, be prepared to encounter chaotic traffic, which is very noisy.

The locals seem to love it and eat all over the place on the street. The levels of hygiene are to say the least poor but it is a very popular place and very social. Not my idea of fun especially on a long holiday - do not want to get ill. Quite a few market stall type shops selling all the usual cotton scarves, purses etc... Worth a wander just don't eat!
2020年1月23日～30日まで旧正月（テト）のため、旧市街も閑散としておりました。事前に調べていかなかったため、とても静かな旧市街地を見学することになりました。皆さん、気を付けてくださいね。
While Halong Bay and Hoi An were spectacular, Hanoi was just okay. The Old Quarter is the most interesting area, so worth visiting for a day.
노상에서먹는 맥주 쵝오볼거리많고 맛집많고 호안끼엠호수에서 그리멀지안은곳에위치하고있음.낮과밤의색깔이 너무다른곳 밤에가길추천드림
정신없기도 하고...낮보다는 밤이 더 아름다운도시가 하노이인듯.낮의 지저분함은 사라지고 작은 조명들이 빛을내면 낭만적인곳이된다.
Beaucoup de monde, beaucoup de scooters (un peu dérangeant, bcp de claxons, beaucoup de bars, de petits restaurants, bars musicaux. Trés Européens et très jeune.

At the begining was a bit shocking but later we quick got used to it and just appreciate the way of their street living. In any case fruits and vegetables were fresh. Some images were not pleasent and crossing the streets for an old lady could be challenge. But for a few days could be tolerable and enjoyable. It all depends from where you are coming from and what do you expect.

Un caos ordenado como la gran ciudad que es. Muchisima gente, motos, muchisimo ruido al punto que te estresa el caminar unas pocas cuadras. No deja de ser interesante conocer diferentes culturas y formas de vida. La gente muy amable aunque siempre hay que estar atentos ante posibles fraudes y estafas.Aconsejo buscar mucha informacion antes de visitar Hanoi.
Phố cổ với kiến trúc cũ kỹ xen lẫn hiện đại. giá cả đắt đỏ và giao thông hỗn loạn. Tuy nhiên nơi này có nhiều điều hay ho để người nước ngoài khám phá về Việt Nam.
Take it easy crossing the busy streets as you enjoy the sights and sounds of the old quarter. Wonderful fast paced environments but not for the faint hearted

People will tell you that you have to see old quarter and to be honest it looks like everything else but with more people. If you are looking to relax and have a quiet day this is not the place. It is a busy area and people are hustling. Everyone is extremely friendly and they have a lot of street food their to try.

I think it's worth a visit to check out Hanoi's culture of street food, cafes, local merchandises, coffee, bars and etc. If you're looking to shop, north face jackets and bags might be worthwhile to take a look at. You may also want to visit some of the museums and churches around this area. Would recommend to visit this place on the weekends as well where there is a night market and some streets are closed so you will not have to worry about the crazy traffic while shopping.
Goed om eens door te lopen maar na 3u Hanoi was het goed geweest. Geen seconde rust. Je moet constant tussen de moto's en auto's manoeuvreren; de voetpaden dienen als parking.
Pictures differ from what we saw, it was pretty run town, and catered solely to tourists with overpriced food and merchandise. Overall ain't too pleasant.

We went walking around this part of the city and enjoyed the beautiful colonial architecture buildings all around. There is a large avenue in one of the roads with trees and water fountains. This place reminds us of France except for the heavier traffic and crazy motorcyclists - they are paper-thin close to each other and go practically anywhere they want barely colliding with each other. This was amusing to us. Nevertheless, we didn't quite like the habit of opening stalls, parking cars and motorbikes on the pavement - blocking pedestrian walkway causing us to walk on the road most of the time. There is all kinds of shops around - food stalls, restaurants, coffee shops, garment stores, etc.
ハノイのホアンキエム湖の北にある旧市街です。渋めの家が並んでいてまあ雰囲気はあるんですが、道が細いうえに無理やり車が入ってくるので歩くだけでストレスがたまります。
Shopping capital but very polluted and busy.  Need masks and lots of patience. Haggle and bargain.  It's worth it

It’s interesting from a cultural perspective, and it’s probably a great visit for backpackers, but can be exhausting. It’s very crowded and one spends their time avoiding being hit by scooters and cars as the sidewalks are full or shopwares and parked scooters. It’s fun to spend a few hours wandering around and looking at the shops, but after a while it gets old and one starts looking for quieter, wider streets just to get a break. I would not recommend staying there as it’s always noisy with traffic and ever-blowing horns.

Highly rated, "must see" location was very very touristy, detracting from the local vibes that I was interested in seeing here. Good to pop by just to see what it is, but not to spend too much time here.

Belle visite d'un quartier typique d'Halo. On y retrouve la vue des artisans et de tous les petits commerces. La visite peut se dire à pieds mais c'est assez grand, il est agréable de la faire en vélo pousse-pousse, c'est reposant et on se promène au milieu des motos avec une vitesse très agréable qui nous permets de bien découvrir ce quartier. Il est impératif de connaître cela lors de votre passage à Hanoï.
A typical quarter of a developing country. Very good tea shops and several good restaurants, among which Blue Butterfly (May Ma);is worth mentioning

It is a blend of frenetic activity, crazy traffic jams, hooting and motor bikes. We found some good places for pho and egg coffee, and managed around two hours walking through the old quarter until it became just too much on the senses. We did enjoy taking a cyclo tour as it got off us away from potentially being hit by a motor bike.
Nothing interesting, just some street shops and not a place we will spend long hours at. Weekend night market is definitely more interesting, more food stalls are open.

À cheval sur leur moto, masque anti-pollution, c’est un continuel va-et-vient. Les gens vont où à tout moment de la journée, c’est l’enfer, aucune sécurité, ils transportent tout, des matériaux de construction, des enfants, des produits alimentaires.C’est infernal à voir aller.Faut le voir pour le croire
Prices here are far more inflated as compared to the other parts of Hanoi. Clearly catered for tourists. Overall an average experience, visit for 1 hour just to explore will be enough.

Like drinking? trying different food? like to party with people with other people escaping the west? look no further. Loads of cheap hostels and distractions. Massage is popular, but alas the staff mostly have been on a 3 hour training course so dont expect miracles. Football matches play in many ex pat bars. Street food is popular too.

Pour un premier aperçu de la ville, c est intéressant de faire un tour en pousse pousse.On y voit à la fois des bâtiments issus de la période coloniale française et des bâtiments plus typiques.Circulation effrénée de motos, voitures, vélos ... dans tous les sens où le klaxon est roi et où le piéton traverse ou il peut 🙄. (Peu de trottoirs)Des Marchands ambulants viendront vous proposer des fruits ou des beignets.

Mixed feelings on this area as it's full of kitsch and tourist items. Some interesting galleries in between , and also wholesalers, but the traffic and lack of pedestrian space makes it tiring after a few days.  We stayed in a hotel in the area but in hindsight better to be in the French quarter and only visit this area as an attraction.
Watch out of traffic when you are at old quarter. Need to possess the skills to cross all roads as walker and biker must skillfully cross or use the road concurrently

Old quarter Hanoi is full of old world charm, shop houses, street food and peddlers. Do take note that traffic here can be craaazzy... with the motorbikes weaving in and out. Can be a little challenging to walk and shop at the same time
徒歩で観光しましたが後ろ前から走ってくるバイクが狭い通路まで入ってきて怖かったです。ホアンキエム湖北側から利用できる電気カートかシクロで廻る方が良いと思います。ディープなハノイを見ることが出来るので夕方の買い物時間に行くと活気があって面白いです。
中々観光地らしい観光地の少ないハノイですが、このエリアは観光地らしく特に週末に行われるウィークエンドナイトマーケットです。PM6：30くらいから22：00位までやっています。あらゆる偽物や本物の土産物を買うことが出来ます。大体90%以上は偽物ですが。

Quartier très  animé et bruyant le jour, impossible de marcher sur les trottoirs squattės par les scoots et les commerçants.Coups de klaxon incessants, circulation intense et très  confuse.Déchets présents partout dans les rues, des gens qui mangent assis sur les trottoirs pendant que d'autres lavent la vaisselle dans des bassines posées sur la route .... å voir par curiosité  !

A must visit place when you are at Hanoi. Visited this place twice for my last two trips in Vietnam.Must try egg coffee and local food. A lot of motorcycles. Eventually when you cross the road they will let you cross it safely!
It so crowded here also food are terrible     feel so badif have chance i ll never walk back through here
Excellent for wholeseller,but for tourist and retailers very disappointing. Shopkeepers do not show any interest in costumers just waist of time.”NO RETAIL” card shown even before entering the shop.
I was very disappointed with the old quarter. The Japanese 16th century bridge is beautiful, but all around it is thousands of vendors and a enormous crowd of tourists. We did have great tea!

The old quarter is a must-do once type of place. For the first few days it's exciting. Food is everywhere, as are shops selling both junk and treasure. ATMs are everywhere too, but be aware that most of them have very low cash limits, like 2,000,000 dong. HSBC allows about 7,000,000. Beer street can be a lot of fun, sometimes with a live band, and tons of food/drink opportunities. Drive a motorcycle at your own peril. Even walking here requires laser focus, head on a swivel. After a few days however, the thrill wears off and you may find yourself longing for some quiet. The only real respite is the lake, a place you can actually walk without the threat of being run down. Find a bench and chill, and be prepared to fend off touts (they are not that aggressive). Many hotel rooms are so noisy, rooms facing the street, especially streets with bus traffic, will rage with beeping horns well past midnight. Enjoy this place for a few days and then move on.
El espectáculo es curioso y no está mal aunque es un poco aburrido, me esperaba algo diferente. Todo y con eso lo recomiendo pues es parte de la cultura vietnamita y la duración es correcta.

Μένανε σε ενα ξενοδοχείο σχεδόν δίπλα.  Ετσι είχα την πολυτέλεια να περπατήσω όλη την παλιά πόλη.  Όμορφη, φασαριοζα, γεματη χρωματα, μυρωδιές, σκουπίδια, κόρνες μηχανάκια.... Κομφιουζιο!!!!  Πρεπει να πατε....  Αλλά οχι για πολυ!  Πολυ το καυσαέριο και κυρίως τα χιλιαδες μηχανάκια και ο τροπος οδήγησης ειναι πραγματικα αξιοθέατο!!!!
The Old quater are very crowded with a lot of motorcycles. This makes it very difficult to cross the street. Is not as nice as the old town in Hoi An

Walking around the Old Quarter in Hanoi is obviously a must-do on any travel itinerary, and is cheap/free and quick dependent on how long you want to spend down each street and in shops etc. We followed a simple walking tour we found online which took us down some of the specialised streets and to some infamous locations throughout the Old Quarter which was interesting.As the title suggests, it is very hectic walking along the streets; in Hanoi the walkways are just motorcycle parks so you have to walk in the road for basically the entirety of the journey which is fairly daunting. Although 'part of the experience', for that reason, and the fact that many of the streets are now just packed with hotel and restaurants rather than authentic Vietnamese shops/homes, we can't really give it more than three stars, none the less it is still worth visiting when in Hanoi.

Wij vonden de old quarter van Hanoi enorm chaotisch en druk. Wij zijn hier dan ook maar een halve dag gebleven. De train street is wel leuk om te bezoeken. We hoorden dat de meesten in het begin dezelfde indruk hebben van Hanoi maar je er na een paar dagen aan went en het dan wel fijn is. De plek rond de vijver is wel leuk en veel rustiger. In het weekend is deze afgesloten voor het verkeer.
ハノイの旧市街は複雑怪奇で地元の人でも迷ってしまいます。「本物は売っていない」と地元の人は言ってました。

If you read the reviews, the old quarter is a must go for anyone visiting Hanoi. I however, beg to differ. The old quarter is dusty and chaotic. I think it is more of a western backpacker fantasy than anything. You can find a cheap hostel here and eat some street food for cheap, and if you have enough filters on your phone, you could take a few photos that will make your western friends jealous.  I'm in my 30s now and I'm with the cheap hostels and food that might or might not make me sit on the toilet all night. The buildings are not very historic and the roads are borderline dangerous if you don't becareful. For old towns, I much prefer Penang's Georgetown than here. You can take a rickshaw around, it's nice, but I don't plan my entire trip around the Old Quarter if I could do it again.
A bustling touristy area great for energetic tourists but not if you just had a tiring day of meetings. The lakeside is good for a relaxing stroll. Not many wandering touts so it is fun.

Full of little shops for buying fruits veggies, meats, fish beans rice seeds etc.  You can get a real feel for life in this part of Vietnam with a walk through this area.  Warning, it is very congested with people and scooters.  Running joke...What do you call a Vietnamese sidewalk? A parking lot!  Therefore you need to walk in the street dodging the scooters in the street while winding around the ones on the sidewalk.

Non l'ho trovato particolarmente interessante a parte le caratteristiche case strette e lunghe.Diverse bancarelle offrono "street food" a prezzi economici. Noi abbiamo preso gli spiedini che erano molto buoni.Per fortuna alcune vie del vecchio quartiere vengono chiuse al traffico alle ore 19. È impossibile camminare sui marciapiedi che sono ingombri di merci e di moto e si è sfiorati dai motorini davanti, dietro, a destra e a sinistra e ad attraversare la strada si rischia la vita.
Same across Asia dodgy food and drinks with Harper’s everywhere Good to walk through but pick a bar or restaurant just outside the area near the lake

Traffic all over Vietnam is scary for pedestrians, but visitors to the Hanoi old quarter should be aware that the streets here are particularly narrow, with pavements entirely blocked by parked scooters and street vendors. Also be aware of scooters pulling out at speed from the tiniest of alleys and hallways between shops. You really can't afford to let your attention lapse for a moment here.Cars, scooters, cycles and bikes will pass within a hairs breadth and there's barely room to get out of the way. Locals are unsympathetic and are quite rude about shooing you away even when they've just seen you almost run over.Our hotel was in the middle of the old quarter and unfortunately we found this quite stressful. That said, certain streets cater for different shopping tastes. One street was mostly habedashery and sewing equipment, another was shoemakers, another jewellery. A bit of exploring can yield some bargain other than the generic souvenirs, which tend to be the same from shop to shop. As elsewhere in Vietnam, expect to haggle and if you can't be bothered you will pay over the odds, although things are generally still fairly inexpensive.The area also has several excellent hotels and restaurants, and is only a short walk from the lake. To make the most of an Old Quarter visit, go Friday - Sunday when streets near the lake are pedestrianised. You will still need to take care for cyclists, cycles and electric buggies but it is much easier to move around. Be particularly careful of children using the small electric toy cars as bumper cars, we suspect they make a game of driving at tourists.There are also a number of street performers, toys for hire for children and music stages. We found these spoiled the atmosphere somewhat, especially as one was directly facing the water puppet theatre and could clearly be heard over the live music inside during the show. We preferred other areas of Hanoi to the Old Quarter, a short visit is probably enough unless you enjoy hustle, bustle and near death experiences.
아기자기하고 고풍스런 건물들이 많고 하노이 시민들을 볼 수 있어 정감있는 도시지만 안전하게 걸을 수 있는 인도가 확보되지 않고 오토바이와 차량이 정신없이 다닙니다 도보 이동이 매우 불편합니다

Mostly shopping and eating. You have to be very careful when crossing the road and be vigilant of the motorbikes as they go all over the place. Most of them obey the law but a few of them just do what they want.
Un.fiume di motorini e bancarelle Un’area ricca di piccoli hotel, ristorantini e venditori ambulanti Massaggi ovunque Sembra di essere catapultati in un altro mondo

Are you 18-25? Is your ideal vacation to SE Asia getting hammered among a sea of peers from US, Europe and Australia with overpriced (for Asia) alcohol? Do you like banana shirts and garbag trinkets?If so, the Old Quarter is for you. An experience unlike any other (except touristy spots of Siem Reap, Bali, Bangkok, etc).

Stayed in the old quarter, really busy and cramped but plenty of nightlife and activity. You definitely have to visit when you go to Hanoi but more for the atmosphere than any particular architecture.

Old Quarter on tietenkin aivan pakko nähdä ja todella ainutlaatuinen. Ensimmäisen kerran kiersimme sen polkupyörätaksin kyydissä  kattilakadulta köydenpunojien kadulle ja silkkikadulta messinkikadulle ja edelleen vihannes- ja kukkatoreille ja kauppahallin alueelle. Kaupan oli kaikkea mahdollista. En olisi uskonut, että maailmassa on niin paljon tavaraa. Cykloajelulla ei kuitenkaan huomannut sitä kaaosta, joka tuli eteen, kun lähdimme liikkeelle jalan.  Kevytmoottoripyöriä, autoja, polkupyöriä vilahteli ohi kuka mistäkin suunnasta. Jalkakäytävät olivat täynnä myyjiä ja niille parkkeerattuja moottoripyöriä. Jalankukijan oli siirryttävä kadulle muiden kulkupelien joukkoon. Kaiken kaikkiaan Old Qurter kuvastaa pienoiskoossa koko Hanoin sykettä, joka ei sammu koskaan. Näytti siltä, että alueen jokainen asukas oli  perustanut pienen kaupan tai katukahvilan tai kuljetti hedelmiä, kukkia tai leivonnaisia polkupyörän kyydissä niitä kaupaten. Täällä kannattaa olla myös tarkkana katumyyjiltä ostoksia tehdessään ja pitää dongin kurssi mielessä; myyjät saattavat joskus lisätä nollia rahan suuruusluokkaan tottumattoman asikkaan laskuun.

Old quarter is central and you can obtain good quality hotels. There is cheap shopping around the area but I would suggest driving a bit out..get a Grab scooter taxi to one of the Vincom Plaza malls n then walk the streets there:)

חברת אסיאטיקה מהאנוי .ערכה לנו תוכנית הנפלאה .בהאנוי מספיק יומיים.שווה ללון במלון פורטל קרוב לאגם.בבוקר בשש להיות בחוץ.מתחת למלון שוק אוכל על המידרכה.ניסגר כבר בשבע מנקים מפנים וחנויות נפתחות.השוק והמסחר מענינים.אחכ לגשת לאגם לראות המתעמלים ואיתי ולהצטרף לאחת הקבוצות
Il vecchio quartiere di Hanoi è caotico e purtroppo sporco, ma anche questa realtà è il Vietnam, un pomeriggio basta per poi aver voglia di esserne fuori
보도가 너무 좁고 오토바이 등이 가로 막고 있어서 다니기 좀 힘듭니다. 다만 하노이의 삶의 다양한 모습을 볼수 있어서 좋네요

Старый квартал или Французский очень беспокойное место, шумно, нет покоя от мото, едущих хаотично.Много еды уличной, приставал с разными услугами.По сравнению с Хошимином менее чисто, менее аккуратно и больше беспорядка.
Haggle prices for many bargains but remember these people are working for a living. They are friendly and helpful. Careful of the unfinished footpaths and busy busy traffic

La ville d Hanoï ne dort jamais. C est constamment du bruit et particulièrement des Klaxons. Il y a une circulation trop importante qui pique rapidement les yeux et le george a cause de la pollution. Le vieux quartier est très vivant et x est la que se retrouve les locaux et les touristes. On y trouve plein de gargote ou l on mange très bien en s éloignant un peu des rues à touristes. Le street food est la règle. Le we le tour du lac est fermé à la circulation ce qui est plutôt agréable pour les piétons contrairement au reste de la ville.

This is a place busy with people, motorscooters, cars, street vendors, locals eating sitting on the street curbs, stray animals here and there, but also with families making a living and taking care of each other, fancy restaurants and shops and beautiful sights, all co-existing together.We felt safe day and night, but were careful what we ate and drank.  The experience was like none other (though a little like the Minnesota State Fair).  I can recommend The Light hotel (Upper end) and the Antique Legend hotel (Quite good).  We greatly enjoyed visiting Hanoi!

Hanois old quarter has it all and everything you ever wanted, and all and everything you never wanted. from shopping paradise to eateries of all kind, hectic traffic making walking hard and breathing even harder, a high concentration of tourists and scams, colorful propaganda poster shops... name it, it will be here. it can be exhausting, but also amazing to stroll these streets. take all the energy and courage to face the traffic and get entertained.
好き嫌いがはっきりするかもしれませんが、ベトナムの街らしくあちこちからバイクや車、人間が来て（交通ルールはないようなものです）、歩きづらいしうるさくて疲れます。
Ich mag das Essen und wenn asiatische Städte quirlig sind, Hanoi war gefühlt eher unentspannt und in der Altstadt die Wirte eher aggressiv.
There is not much to see and do apart from visiting the shopping areas. Old quarter is not any different. Just be careful, traffic could be a challenge at times.
ホーチミンよりも狭い道にあふれるバイクと車。そしてクラクション。これがいいという人もいるだろうけれど、気を張って歩かなければならず、距離以上に疲れる。店を眺める余裕もないくらい。

Weekend night market is located in this district. It is where you could buy cheap items (imitations), perfect for budget shoppers.   It opens 7pm and closes 11 pm.  Unlike in other night markets, this place is clean.

The Old Quarter is great. Loads of decent shops, places to eat and stay. It's where most tourists will base themselves for a stay in Hanoi and that makes sense. Be prepared though....The major gripe with the Old Quarter is the congestion and the dangerous traffic. It would be a lovely place to wander around if only the pavements were not completely blocked by parked motor cycles.Two simple solutions to the problems off the Old Quarter.1. Give the pavements back to pedestrians.2. Ban the snarl-up of tour buses driving around in circles picking up from hotels.

The Old Quarter is worth seeing, but not worth seeing over multiple days. You quickly realize every shop is selling the same tourist trinkets and shorts. However, it’s still pretty architecturally and there are a lot of nice cafes and restaurants as a few shops ARE different than the rest. You just have to be willing to walk around a lot and check out the shops down less lit roads. The night market here is the same items sold during the day, but the extra lights and the boats on the water give it a very pretty romantic feel.

Muy padre la visita por el centro de la ciudad, hay que hacerlo, da la impresión de que es inseguro pero noo, es muy cálido y de verdad puedes encontrar lo que quieras, las calles estan seccionadas por tipo de mercancia, esta súper padre andar caminando cuidandote de las motos, es cansado porqué se tiene que caminar bastante y mucho que comer.
Wow....what a crazy place. Very busy with traffic, street vendors and people, however some lovley rooftop bars should you wish to get away from the hustle and bustle

This place is mad! We arrived at 5.30 in the morning and the place is in full swing already with street vendors and mopeds everywhere.It is quite intimidating getting around with traffic coming at you from every direction mainly mopedsThe problem is you have to walk in the road as you cant walk on the path because they its crammed with parked up mopeds.It does have it's own sort of buzz about it but better suited to the back packer looking for great food and accommodation at ridiculously low prices.

Interesting but rather crazy to try to make across the road, too many motorbikes, so don't wait, just GO! Watch your bags & kids, this place is very busy! As usual, most shops sell touristic stuff, good to get your data card here but do bargain down.
Hard to follow directions to get anywhere ! Half the restaurant s don’t have a sign out the front or they are up 2 /3 floors and the taxi drivers are terrible try and rip you off very off putting !
Very interesting but after about 15 mins the smells and hustle and bustle becomes a bit too much. Pavements blocked everywhere. But hey thats the fun of Vietnam no rules :)
The hubbub that is Hanoi is definitely worth experiencing. It is busy and crazy with bikes and people, and if you are into tasting street food then you'll enjoy a couple of nights here.
לא משהו מיוחד , ממש רגיל , מכיוון שיש זמן ,נחמד לטייל ,אבל אם אין זמן ,אפשר לדלג, הבתים בסגנון הצרפתי
Lots of places to eat/coffee/drinks/shopping. Also lots of congestion. Nothing really stood out for me.

Lots of stalls selling the same thing. Our first hotel was in the old quarter and we found it very noisy from the traffic outside so we stayed in Hoan Kiem district the next time and found it much quieter and still within close walking distance to the old quarter. We went into many shops and found the people in the shops were not very friendly as we said hello to the staff and hardly got a response so we didn’t want to give them our money. Some of the clothes we wanted to buy were quite expensive so we decided to wait to we go back home to buy what we wanted.
雑貨屋や果物屋、洋服やなど歩道上にまで商品を並べて売っている。その間をベトナム特有の△帽子＝ノンラーをかぶり天秤棒を担いだ行商のおばちゃんが売り歩いている。電柱には切れた電線がビッシリとまきつけてある。仏植民地時代から変わらないだろう大木の下を歩く。ハノイに来た！という雰囲気だ。歩くなら、時間をかけて歩きたいものだ・・・！
Vous ne pouvez que trouver votre bonheur dans ce vieux quartier d’Hanoï. Les prix sont variés certains marchands ne veulent pas du tout marchander et d’autres se montrent très gentils  !

Streets are not kid friendly, spent more time watching out for bikes and cars and water puddles than to admire the city. Sanitary hygiene is also poor for street food. The city is probably good for letting kids experience a different type of city, other to do things are not so interesting for young children. 1 afternoon in the city is enough...Better to spend more time in the countryside.

I preferred the Old quarter to the French.  It was super busy at night when we were walking home from our cooking class.  The motor cycles go every where on the paths, in the shops, along the market and drive on the path if the road gets to busy.  the pedestrians were on the road as the path was covered in parked motor cycles - so beware in the evening if you have younger children that would be very challenging.
ごちゃごちゃしていて、楽しい街並みなのですが、とにかくバイクが狭い道をあちこちから走ってくるので危険です。スリも多いので、バッグは、リュックも身体の前で持つように言われました。

This place is very busy with many little street with lots of shops and restaurants. Night time is busier as everyone is out for dinner and hang out at the International cross road drinking and eating. Music is pretty laud at nigh.

There are probably a few unique hidden gems in here, but you've gotta wade through so much crap to find them.  As others have said, it's claustrophobic and stinky in many places. The shops all sell the same tourist kitsch that you've seen everywhere in SEA already. There are seemingly 2 cafes out of every 3 shop fronts...Can the tourist flow support that many?Traffic didn't bug me that much, since I live in Asia, but I'm sure it would upset others. Didn't really see much worth going back for, but didn't hate my experience there either, so 3 stars. Go if you want to check off a box.

Didn’t expect the Old Quarter to be clean and quiet, but this place, and Hanoi in general, was dirty and noisy even by South East Asia standards (coming from a fellow SEA citizen). Could be a really charming place if not for these unfortunate shortcomings.

murderous traffic  distracting from a charming area .Wide variety & standards of restaurants to cater for all . Well located for most tourist sites - most , easy access by foot .Very close to vibrant & interesting Hokiem Lake.All streets shaded by large & ancient street trees keeping temperature down .Beautiful architecture partly obscured by city dross .First impressions may be down heartening , but give it a couple of days & it will grow on you .
Il existe des petits boutique très jolie dans le quartier. Très animé et coloré! Mais trop de masse, j'ai pas de hygiène....
ひたすら商店街でした。スーパーコピー商品が堂々と売っていたり。おもちゃが売ってる通り靴が売ってる通りなどがありました。一日みたら十分だとおもいました。

The Old Quarter of Hanoi is interesting enough, but on a hot day in July there are definitely better places to spend one's time. Streets are busy and there's no shortage of activity: might be of interest if one hasn't seen this before. With the kids it tow it wasn't as enjoyable as it might have been. Probably better in winter, or at least when it's not quite so hot.

Its just another big market area in Hanoi and there is nothing great for which you should visit it. Hoi An market was much more beautiful and vibrant then this market.There are lot of options to eat and shop as this is a big market with many sectors.Best is to take a cyclo which will roam you around and you can stop at any shop any time and then take back ride.

From what I read before visiting, I knew Hanoi was “bustling & vibrant” as many travel writers have tried to put a positive spin on the thinly veiled chaos that is this city. We spent 3 days in total here and...honestly, it was so-so at best. The best things we had was one great Pho and coconut coffee. The rest was dodging mopeds, mediocre street food with minimal sanitation, super touristy & humid as f^€$. One night I was woken at 3:30 AM to a screaming fight that lasted about 45 mins with a group of 12 people shouting and throwing things on the street below. We are excited to support local, small business worldwide but honestly we were exhausted of always being on our guard on how much they are charging, haggling and getting correct change. Also the general lack of minimal hospitality (I work in the food and beverage industry) left me with the same feeling we received in every place we went: ambivalence. All of the best experiences we had in Vietnam were outside of Hanoi. Take it with a grain of salt and try it out. Only one way to know for sure...
基本的に交通ルールは、あってないようなものになってるので、信号が青でも渡る時は、慎重に。人よりバイクが優先されていると思って道を歩くのが良いです。1日あれば、大体の位置関係がつかめるので、気温が30台であれば、頑張って徒歩で回る方が良いです。(そんなに広くないです)各路地毎に銀製品・洋服・雑貨など分かれているので、見たい路地をあらかじめ決めて動く方が良いと思います。

Vietnam is all about garbage and noise. Locals love garbage and produce it everywhere. They also ride bikes and scooters to produce pollution and noise. This all you get in the old district. On the weekend there is no traffic but a lot of people, which makes the walk through very slow. The night life in Vietnam is still developing. Many bars are empty even on the weekend. Vietnamese don't like to dance and prefer cheap beer on the street.

The Old Quarter is definitely charming and a trip to Hanoi isn’t complete without going. That said, for a Westerner, be prepared to see poor hygienic standards for food, horrible traffic laws (basically nonexistent) and non-stop hecklers trying to sell things. You can’t escape it but just keep an open mind.
While shopping is bad (several shops selling the same thing with different marked up prices), the hustle and bustle in old quarter makes it worth the visit

We were expecting a really cool old quarter vibe, but it was a lot of repetitive “cheap” shopping. It’s HOT, so make sure to bring some cold water with you! Can knock it out, and check it off your list.

Tłum kłębi się zatłoczonymi ulicami. Samochody jadą powoli, ale nieprzerwanie. Nic nie zdoła zmusić kierowcy, żeby się zatrzymał. Skutery wymijają zwinnie samochody i przechodniów. Wszyscy nieustannie trąbią.Jeżeli jesteś pieszym - znajdujesz się na końcu układu pokarmowego. Przejdziesz na drugą stronę, jeżeli tylko nikt inny nie jedzie.W okolicy jest wiele warsztatów, ludzie pracują na ulicy. Pomiędzy nimi znajduje się wiele knajp, streetfoodów i lokali z tanim, młodym piwem.Atmosfera budzi nienawiść do miejscowej ludności, ale także daje intensywne przeżycie Azji - co dla wielu jest bardzo istotne. Trzeba tu być, będąc w Hanoi, ale trzeba też uzbroić się w anielską cierpliwość.
هو لازم تمرونها بس لا تتوقعوا تشوفون مناظر حلوة هناك. ريحة الاكل معبية الشوارع و البيارات عزكم الله. مو نظيفة ابدا و ارضها مكسرة يعني البسوا شوز مريح جدا و مغطى لأن الشبشب ما ينفع 😅👍🏼

저는 이국적이고 특이해서 즐겁기는 했는데살고 싶은 동네는 아님8월 말 날씨가 너무 더워서 불쾌지수가 조금 높았고더러운거 싫어하시는 분들은 안맞을것 같아요주말에는 차량 통제 해서 조금 낫지만 사람 엄청 많고 평일에 교통 상황 장난아니에요 경차랑 오토바이 많이 타고 다니는데 차선 개념이 없고 가끔 좌측통행 우측통행 무시하는 오토바이들도 많음길거리 음식이나 물건들 더럽고 바가지도 장난아님그냥 재미로 한번 와본다고 생각해야 할듯
Old part of town. Would not recommend spending all day there. Get out of it and go to the French quarter, the "new" quarter and other areas of Hanoi. Old Quarter is not Hanoi!
Molto suggestivo tanti negozi ma allo stesso tempo molta sporcizia il traffico i rumori dei clacson sono continui diventa difficile attraversare sulle strisce non si ferma nessuno

Este barrio de Hanoi es un conjunto de calles de mayor o menor tamaño longitud o anchura pero con la característica de estar atestado de peatones ,tiendas de barrio y sobre todo motocicletas 🏍, que conformar un sonido peculiar y característico único e inconfundible de la ciudad , solo que aquí resulta exagerado A parte de esto puedes encontrar una tienda en cada local comercial abierto , cientos de tiendas de ropa y telas , incluidos los famosos sastres de ropa de caballero, así como diminutas casa  de comida , y otras actividades de confusa utilidad ,a parte el olor y el color de sus moradores, una de las calles principal Hàng Bông , y al final de ella junto al lago la plaza plaza Dong Kinh Nghia Thuc interesante lugar por sus cafeterías con terrazas en distintos edificios Recomiendo comer en Coffee Club , buenas vistas ,buena comida ,economico En la calle Trân Phu te puedes hacer una fotografía con la vía del tren

Sempre interessanti i mercati in Asia ma se ne hai già visti altri non è più una sorpresa. Gran baccano di motorini e clacson. È bene fare attenzione ad i veicoli e motocicli  provenienti da ogni direzione

Bisogna prestare molta attenzione alle motorette che sfrecciano da tutte le parti, senza tener conto degli attraversamenti stradali dei pedoni. Molto fast food a disposizione degli abitanti e turisti . Fra negozi di massaggi di tutti i tipi, predomina il commercio di scarpe e borse di tutte le marche più conosciute e copiate malamente. Ogni strada è dedicata ad un tipo di vendita, ma le venditrici con i bilancieri e i venditori su biciclette non mancano

Was busier than we expected but if you look up you can see the original buildings. You get used to the mopeds. Just be brave when. Roasting the road and walk at a consistent pace - the traffic will go round you.

We are party of 3 signed up on free walking tour roaming around Old Quarter Hanoi.  The walk was nice, Hanoi was hot in June, but we had great time.  We stopped at old Chinese house, the guide gave us a walk through the house.  Then we roaming around the old quarter, the market and ended up at the oldest coffee shop.  Overall nice experience.   Our tour guide was nice and provide good information.

Leuk om te zien hoe alles werkt in zo'n drukke wijk. Voor jongeren waarschijnlijk een hotspot omdat er ook wat uitgaansgelegheden zijn waar backpackers elkaar kunnen ontmoeten. Voor mij als 56'jarige toerist bring it back old  sweet memories,  van het reizen toen ik een stuk jonger was.
皆さん口コミに書かれているように、バイクが怖い。歩道があっても信号らしいものがないので何時渡っていいのかわからず、現地の人についていくしかなかった。市内は思っていた以上に都会で、まさかここまで発展しているとは思わなかった。台湾より都会かも・・・。ノスタルジックな雰囲気でいい感じだが、空気が悪く現地人が暑い中マスクをしている理由が良く分かった。気管支の悪い人が行くとテキメン具合が悪くなるほど。
We found this very busy but exciting.  Take the electric bus tour to see all around as then you can focus on the areas you want to explore further.
フランス統治時代の雰囲気があって、悪くはないが、これといって楽しい物も買いたい物もない。暑さもあって長時間はいられない。はんこ屋は面白い。

The intimidating streets full of vehicles that don't respect pedestrians are the rule here. Proceed with caution when crossing the streets.  Side walks are already occupied by bikes and cars.  Not advisable for people in wheelchairs or some difficulty to walk or hearing.  Stalls full of items are worth to check but before buying check quality and prices around.
도로가 복잡해서   햇갈릴수있음 식당및 가계들은  비싸지않고  어므정도 흥정이 가능합나다  다만   더울때는 더무더우니 좀 선선할때 방믄을 추천합니다
Leuk om gezien te hebben, je vind veel kraampjes en kleine winkels en restaurants. Veel imitatie kleding ed en souvenirs shops
洒店附近便是老城區，晚飯後到處逛逛熱鬧非常。相信很多當地居民晚上出來乘涼，而且有不同的咖啡店，街檔和五光十色的霓虹燈。真的是遊客好去處！

Never experienced a nigh market as busy and as big as this. Great variety of clothing, food and electronics. Atmosphere amazing and found some pretty good bargains..A must for visitors coming to Hanoi to experience
The food street can be slightly overwhelming. But we still had fun. I find it to be chaotic on the streets with cars and bikes going every where. Might be back

At this time of the year, it was unbearably hot and humid. Scooters were everywhere and there is just no regard to traffic lights. The streets are dirty and food venders on the side of the road. Shops selling items which were overpriced. Not a place I'd visit again.

Had a great time here but were also glad when we left as it’s quite full on and in the July heat a little overbearing.  Take plenty of breaks if you’re there in the summer and watch out for crazy moped riders who won’t hesitate to run you over!
Very busy and crowded in this part of Hanoi. It’s great to enjoy the street food and drinks but don’t forget to discover other parts of Hanoi

ネットで調べたお店で、プラカゴを購入しました。日本でのイメージと違い、複雑な模様でかわいいです。でも、思ったより、高かったです。少し値引きしてくれましたが、特大サイズで¥2,000、中サイズ¥800、小¥300くらい。タンミーで刺繍入り巾着小が、¥350くらいです。欲しかったものが手に入ったので満足ですが、お値打ち感はなかったです。街は、車とバイクですごい喧騒です。涼めるような場所もなく、ゆっくりショッピングという感じではありません。行きたい店を目指して行動したほうがいいかな、と思います。
7月のハノイ。熱いのはわかっていたが、頭で考える暑さと、体で感じる暑さが違うことに、五十を過ぎてわかった。カフェは沢山あるので、冷房の効いたカフェ巡りをして、明日もまた旧市街を満喫予定です、

The Old Quarter is bustling, noisy,  vibrant - totally engages all of one's senses. A special treat in store is crossing the roads. Do not hesitate - keep moving forward. Cars and scooters do avoid hitting pedestrians.

初めてのハノイです。行くまでは友人とブラブラ散策だね等と悠長な気分でしたが、ハノイに到着するや否や友人と２人で歩くのは危険と判断しガイドさんと歩くことにしました。アジアが感じられ素敵な所だとは思いますが、とにかく危険な所です。バイクがあふれ交通ルールなんてものは無いにも等しく、道路を渡るのは命がけです。またひったくりも多く一平方メートルの中に７人いるので注意しなさいとガイドさんから案内がありました。
老城區(舊城區)是河內的心臟地帶，吃的買的都在這裡。有很多平民小食和特色商店，也有夜市。惟車多人多，街道很小，要小心交通和治安。
You gotta do it anyway as this is the most attraction to do in Hanoi but it’s crowded and if you are looking for tranquility hmm guess shorter your time here and move on to country side!
Best to walk around and enjoy the surroundings. Do not take tuktuk as they charge extremely high especially for tourists.
Very busy, you need to get used of it the first day. After that it is very nice. 2 days is enough to be here. After that you have seen everything
Most things are overpriced. Stick to local products and handicrafts. Good cultural experience. Lots of nice food places from Banh Mi, egg coffee to banh xeo.

old quarter is a must not miss place when you are around Hanoi. Just get an offline map or all the hotels around you stay will provide you a page of map and labelled the old quarter road. you can just walk around to shop, eat and drink. Drinks are very cheap. Beware of scam on roadside seller.
Not recommended because of pollution and millions of scooters.You can hardly walk or breathe plus too many travellers.
This is the "party": area of Hanoi. Nice, quaint but needs a makeover, better roads and lighting. The area to stay in when visiting Hanoi.

Interesting to see old buildings and livelihood.Countless stories of phone and bag snatchings. Prepare to be groped or pick pocketed in crowds.Don't buy food from a cart. I have seen a whole cart tipped over, food all over the road, put back on the cart and sold to little kids without as much as a dust off. So, only eat at places with permanent establishments.
ベトナムはどこにいてもバイクだらけで、ハノイ旧市街も同じです。人も多くバイクも多い。その代わり食べるところや飲むところ、スパなどには困りません
右から左からオートバイと車がやってくるので、充分注意が必要です。小さな子供や女性は特に気を付けてください。

Wir waren direkt im Backpackerviertel. Rund um die Uhr war Trubel auf den Straßen was keinerlei Langeweile aufkommen lies. Ich würde jedem dieses Viertel empfehlen, der nicht viel Wert auf Luxus legt. Der einzige negative Punkt war der unfreundliche Umgangston der Nordvietnamesen wenn sich jemand als Südvietnamese zu erkennen hab.
Take care about your belongings and do not let old aggressive ladies to sell you what you do not want
It’s was nice to walk around, but don’t expect too much of it. If you have been to many other Asian cities then this might not be very exciting

I hailed a taxi around 10pm from Hang Da street. He refused to go for some reason and directed me to go with the guy across the street. Assuming they know each other I was less skeptic and I went across the street to the other taxi. Both of them had proper taxi signs and stickers outside but both were not well know brands. I asked if it meter and he said meter, meter. He was very happy to take me and got me in, in a hurry. As soon as I got in I noticed the taxi meter had 50 without a decimal point and I asked him which he specifically said it meant 5000 dong. He didn't have a passenger seat belt as well. I even relaised he didn't have his taxi ID nera the meter but I didn't think it would be this bad at the time. So along we go and he was using Google translate to ask details about me and I thought he's being friendly. When we got to the destination it was showing 460. He didn't even put the hood light in the car for me to get the money out and it was dark. Then When I got my wallet and gave him 50K after struggling to find the 50 dong note he refused, I realized I got scammed and I was arguing that he said the dot is not working and etc and we got in to an argument and then he took out a screwdriver and threatened. When I tried to get down and walk away the doors were locked. he was very adamant and wanted the 500K I ended up giving him and he even gave 20k change back. On the way back I only paid 41K to the taxi.Vehicle registration number 30E 321.90When traveling solo please be cautiousThe taxi looked like a proper taxi but,1. The taxi driver was not in uniform2. Don't get on to unknown brands. Although I had read it every where yes I was stupid.3. There was no taxi driver ID4. No seat belt5. Meter was rigged. Make sure you see the decimal point.
하노이 보통 덥잖아요? 겨울 아니면 보통 한국의 한여름처럼 더운데 사람도많고 안그래도 좁은길에 테이블과 의자 호객들이 서로 얽혀서 쾌적한 환경은 아니에요. 물론 하노이 가시면 한번쯤 가게될곳이기는 하지만 딱 그정도 라고 생각해요
アジア感満載のベトナムはハノイの旧市街。バイクがひっきりなしに走ってて、注意して歩かないと危ないです。しかし、この喧騒こそがベトナムですね。

Had fun walking about but things r damned pricey even after haggling-which many vendors do to want to do. Lots of quality cafes n restaurants in every street.  Traffic is chaotic n crossing roads is an art lollll It's fun for a couple of days but not more. Nothing much to do once the markets r done.

Old town is exactly what it's name says.  It's a wonderful place where you can get the full atmosphere of by gone days.  There are lots of shops and vendors of every description. The streets are very narrow and  I really enjoyed just looking around and browsing through the shops and seeing the people.

En realidad no esperaba mucho de Hanoi y de su centro histórico (Old Quarter). Que les puedo decir ! Esta ciudad es de odio y amores . Todo es un caos en sus calles , mucha gente en las aceras , donde muchas veces no queda espacio para caminar , motos 🙈🙈🙈 por montones , como si las regalaran , comida callejera , muchos olores y sobre todo mucho de lo que no vemos en occidente .Si eres de esas personas que les gusta vivir y experimentar de todo un poco , creo que te va a gustar , sino te molestan el pito de los carros y motos , o sino te molesta tener tantas personas a tu alrededor , te va a gustar .Por lo demás , es un país asiático y sobre todo con una mezcla , de muchas culturas . Todos toman té en las calles , comen en las calles y se relacionan en la calle . Conclusión Caminar , comer , tomar y disfrutar de algo totalmente nuevo ... depende de tu nivel de adaptación , de tu capacidad de entender y en General de lo dispuesto (a) que estes de dejarte sorprender .
老城區顧名思義就是河內最早發展的區域，因此道路窄而曲折，建築也有點雜亂無章！ 不過也這樣才能保持老城區的感覺，也是吸引遊客的地方，值得花個時間細細品嚐，尤其是街邊小吃更是該要一一品嚐，各式河粉、法式吐司夾各式新鮮蔬菜與肉類，看似奇怪但意外的好吃、獨特的越南（蛋）咖啡值得一嚐⋯

I consider myself a tourist, but there maybe too many throngs of group tourist that may have spoilt the magic of Old Town Hanoi. The fresh markets in the morning still remains exciting, seeing the fresh produce on display and feeling the vibrancy of a thriving marketplace. However shopping, food and attraction are a little too tourist-centric and feels like prices are inflated with little consideration for the authenticity of the experience or the quality of the product. Around 5.30-8.30pm, to fight the throngs of other tourists, in groups, bicycle taxis, floating boats and walkways ruins the charm of this amazing place for me. In saying that, the lanterns at night are still magic!
Ze proberen er iets moois van te maken maar is ze nog niet helemaal gelukt. Blijft wel een mooie plek om te komen

Абсолютно не приспособлено для пеших прогулок. Лучше проехать на авто и посмотреть в окно, ну или на байке. Посмотреть есть что, но не для длинных прогулок.
2017.2018と訪問とにかく女の人が好きそうな雑貨屋さんやカフェがいっぱい。似たようなお店が多いので行きたいお店がある場合は必ず場所を確認してからの方が安全です。道が汚いし悪いので歩きやすい靴を履いて行ったほうがいいです。

Overall, I'm not a fan. It is incredibly busy and chaotic. Really a sensory overload but some people really love it! I think it just depends on what you are looking for... if I come back to Hanoi I will definitely stay outside of the quarter so I can just pop in as I want for a quick visit and then retreat to a more peaceful part of the city.
Enjoyed wandering around and exploring the Old Quarter. Little too crowded with motor bikes for our liking and have preferred other older towns/villages/markets.

Username: New Forest National Park Hampshire, United Kingdom
Comment: I had two free days in Hanoi, and spent most of the time wandering round the old quarter and Hoan Kiem Lake. Its easy to explore on foot, and the old quarter has a faded charm with run-down buildings, small cafés and street vendors. But the traffic and air pollution, particularly in the very hot and humid weather, were oppressive. It would be nice if this area could be pedestrianised, but with the Vietnamese passion for scooters and motorbikes I cant see that happening. And after all, its their city not ours. Some roads are closed in the evening for night markets and entertainments. Interested to discover that many of the street names refer to the old trades or merchandise which used to be found along them (a bit like mediaeval London - Pudding Lane, Milk Street, Birdcage Walk etc). Now its just too many shops all selling the same things, and sidewalks full of parked scooters and miniature tables and stools outside the cafés, so you have to walk in the road much of the time.

Amazed with the shops that sells great copy-cat products - good price and honestly good quality.:-)The taffic is most frightening - the motorcycles never follow traffic lights !Not a place to eat street food for most foreigners.The hotels are good and cheap.It is an exciting place - stay for a few days, fun !

I agree with the other posts that this is something you must see.  And that you can wander for hours.  Plus, in comparison to markets in Mexico, Africa and India I found the streets to be relatively clean and garbage free.  (You may want to invest in an air filter face mask like the locals.)    However, the sidewalks have been commandeered by motorcycles and illegal vendors making walking the area treacherous.  And at certain points simply crossing the street can be life-threatening.  For some, this is part of the charm of Hanoi.  For me, after a day or so, I really had enough of Hanoi and couldn't wait to get out of the city.

Als je er voor t eerst komt is t echt even wennen aan de chaos, stoepen die volstaan met brommers zodat je gedwongen op de straat moet lopen en talloze eettentjes. Eenmaal gewend is het bijzonder en kijk je je ogen uit!
The best way to expirience the hectic streets of old Hanoi, is by a cyclo tour. An hour is more than enough, sounds, smells and traffic all around you with high intense

We were shocked upon our arrival to see traffic of all means flying in all directions with little regard for lanes or safety.  We were unsure at first if we could even cross the street but eventually realized we had to just step out into the chaos and walk at a casual pace.  The traffic moved around us.  Many street vendors serve their food from very low tables and chairs like children would use in North America.  Scraps from the table are thrown onto the ground and cleaned up during the night.  Everything is unique here and interesting to see but maybe only once. Hoan Kiem Lake is beautiful and there are thousands of shops to visit in the Old Quarter.
Il faut le voir car ici bat aussi le cœur de la ville,Evidemment c'est très bruyant et très pollué (comme presque toute la ville)

The Old Quarter is worth a walk around during the day but we got bored of the same stuff after a while, coffee shops, souvenir stalls, garish art etc. I like Vietnamese coffee but how much can you drink? The streets are narrow, pavements crowded with parked mopeds and tables overflowing from tiny pho restaurants so you’re forced into the road dodging traffic, which gets wearisome. We enjoyed the walk around the nearby lake more. Better to go at night and escape to a rooftop bar.
Can't go to Hanoi without exploring here but traffic is crazy and pathways blocked by ad hoc eateries and motor scooters. Not easy to get around but lots of good coffee and eating places.

The Old Quarter is THE defining characteristic of Hanoi.  The merchant types are generally sorted by street; clothier types together, jewelers together; etc.  And the place is a continual buzz of activity.  *HOWEVER*, beware of the scooters, beware of the scooters, beware of the scooters!These two-wheeled nuisances are a serious safety threat to Western tourists.  The scooters are just plain dangerous:  they run red lights, which makes cross walks hazardous; drive on sidewalks; park en masse on sidewalks and thereby eliminate any pedestrian passage; and even drive in opposing traffic lanes through intersections - while running red lights (which is how I was clipped by one)!  And, of course, the 6 million scooters do nothing to clean up Hanoi’s near tragic air quality problem.  The city counsel intends to ban scooters in the city core by 2030 or so - definitely not soon enough.  Just hope they substitute German style public transport capacity in time.

The old quarter is the perfect place to do some shopping or simple ramble around Hanoi. However, the fish market can be quite off-putting, and prepare to me accosted by forceful vendors, especially along the more tourist-frequented locations. The food, however, is absolutely excellent and there is no end to the small shops ready to be explored. Be ready for the traffic though, it can be overwhelming!
The area is very busy, full of with people and motorbikes. The streets are a bit dirty, but anyways, it is interesting to see the area.

A bit of fun for sure as you enjoy a perilous ride about the busy market streets of the old quarter. In terms of architecture there is nothing that stands out much. It is just a hectic market area with traffic come at you from all directions. A good buzz for sure but would I wander around the market place itself? Probably not. Fascinating and vibrant. If you have never done something like it then recommended.

I can’t believe no one cares that pedestrians can’t get anywhere easily or safely.  Sidewalks are almost impossible to use because locals use all of them as a scooter parking lot or an extension of their shop. God help the poor soul who has a physical limitation or is in a wheelchair.
nothing much to see ..... real old houses and vendors on every corner.   shopping is limited as items mostly fake.   but got lots of small cafes

Oh the horns! So many scooters. So many horns! BEEP BEEP!And all the scooters parked on the sidewalks mean you have to walk in the streets, which means scooters honk at you. BEEP BEEP!Interesting place, but stressful and frustrating to walk around.

The new year celebrations around the lake were interesting to view, but again the traffic was nuts! Scooters from one side of the road to the other, and non-stop.
