If you're travelling to Hanoi as a tourist, the Old Quarter is the best  place to be. It's near to most of the places of interest in Hà Nội like the night market in Dung Xuan, shops,  budget hotels, cafes, street food, Water Puppet Show, Temple of Literature, St. Joseph Cathedral and the popular Hoan Kiem Lake.This is also the area where the motor bike or cyclo is king of the narrow roads. You have to be careful when crossing the street as the swarm of motor bikes might side swipe you.But, over all, it seems to be a throbbing place as fellow tourists  would bump into each other in cafes, restos, shops or on the streets. At nite, you will see locals in their favorite street food corner enjoying themselves.Each city has its own version of the old quarter. It reminds one  of District 1 in Ho Chi Minh City and the area near Sisavangvong, Fa Ngom and Kitsalat Roads in Luang Prabang.

Whenever we walked through the Old Quarter we returned exhausted  to our Hotel. The never stopping motorbikes do not care about Walking persons. And there is no proper sign board in English to guide tourists. Pollution is really bad and i wonder how people sit beside crowded road sides and eat with all the hustle, bustle, smoke and pollution. To add salt to the wound there is a lot of burning as tradition requires during the time of Chinese New Year. Added dose to the already polluted atmosphere.

This bustling area of endless narrow streets and alleys is utter chaos and craziness! Motorbikes on the move fill the roads and the pavements are packed with parked up motorbikes, so as a pedestrian you are literally forced to walk up the street - sometimes up the middle of the street due to parked cars/bikes with motorbikes whizzing past you at great speed, merely inches away!! I cannot say that we enjoyed this part of Hanoi and quickly just grew frustrated due to the crazy traffic... Our 3rd city stop though so maybe we were just a bit fed up of all the motorbikes by then...Also known as the ‘36 streets’, with each street originally specialising in different wares. Now-a-days literally thousands of shops, street vendors, restaurants, bars, massage parlors etc fill the streets with a constant flow of people. Get lost exploring the twisting narrow alleys, but keep a watchful eye out for the scooters as traffic is always very busy.St Joseph Cathedral is nearby and worthwhile visiting if only to take a few quick photos from outside. The impressive structure is worn out on the outside due to pollution and is very picturesque. We read that inside there are several lovely stained glass windows and entrance into the cathedral is free, but we just kept walking.We were advised to visit the Old Quarter several times as so much to see and do, with many photo opportunities all around. We were recommended to stop and have a drink or a bite of street food sitting on the small plastic chairs, look around the shops or relax with a drink and people watch.... but we quickly grew tired and frustrated with the crazy motorbike traffic.

道もかなり狭いのですが、バイクや車の交通量も多いです。日本では危険なタイミングでクラクションを鳴らすことが多いですが、ベトナムではそれに加えて自分の存在を他車に知らせる目的でも鳴らすので歩いていてかなりストレスです。道を横断するのも初めは苦労すると思いますが、バイクは基本的に避けてくれるので車を気にしていれば問題ないです。飲食店もすごく多いですが、空気も良くないので店内で食事ができるお店を選ばれるのが良いと思います。

Mycket överskattat plats. En turistfälla där alla gatusäljare jägar dig hela tiden. Trånga gator och det är inte så mycket att se. Vi var där under dagen i två timmar, det räckte för att kolla rund, äta lite och ta en taxi hem till Da Nang vid 14 tiden. Rekommenderar boka taxi via appen Indrive. Där vi erbjöd 200.000 och det var många bilar som tackade ja till vårt offert. Det är inte värt att åka ditt ifall du inte är sugen på att köpa dyra souvenirer.

Netter Altstadtbereich mit vielen kleinen Geschäften oft mit chinesischen Artikeln. Hoch gelobt wurde dieser Bereich von Hanoi leider fanden wir ihn nicht gerade spektakulär oder sonderlich schöner als andere Städte. Da ist Hoi An doch viel schöner und ruhiger. Wie überall in den Großstädten kann man als Fußgänger oft nur auf der Straße laufen da die Fußgängerwege durch Mopeds blockiert sind oder die kleinen Essensbereiche alles blockieren. Es gibt ein paar nette Gebäude allerdings oft in einem nicht so guten Zustand. Der Verkehr ist gefühlt besser als in Saigon aber trotzdem chaotisch, überfüllte Straßen und kaum ein sicheren Weg die Straße zu überqueren.

어디나 시장은 볼거리와 먹거리가 많지만 36거리는 우리로치면 남대문시장이나 동대문시장과 흡사하다 없는것 빼고 다있는 만물시장이다. 시장 사이로 세옴(오트바이 택시)과 이륜차가 좁은 시장길을 요리조리 빠져 다니는 것 또한 볼거리다. 시장 귀퉁이에서 작은 의자에 앉아 먹는 길거리 음식과 맥주 한잔도 운치가 있다. 필요한 기념품도 이곳에서 구입해 선물하면 좋다는 생각이 든다.
베트남 하노이 전경은 예전 우리 어릴때 풍경이 보여 정겹다. 시장에서 여러가지 먹거리와 기념품 생필품등을 싸게구입할수있어 좋은거같다.

솔직히 그냥 사진 한 장 찍고 오기 이상 이하도 아닌 길 같아여...... 돌길에 신발이 망가질 수 있으니 미리 주의하시길 바랍니다. 샌들 신고 갔다가 아작냈어요. 호텔에서 택시 부를 때 기찻길 사진 보여주면서 위치 찍어달라 했어요. 구글에 찍힌 위치랑 달라요 현지 사람한테 물어보고 위치 알려달라 하고 가세요. 택시 내릴 때 도로 기준 양 옆으로 기찻길이 있었는데 한쪽은 그냥 거주지역 느낌이라서 안가고 더 관광지같이 생긴 곳으루 갔어용 볼건 거의 없고 그냥 기찻길과 알록달록한 건물이 있고... 기찻길 따라서 카페와 음식점이 있습니다. 기찻길이 막 길고 그런것도 아니에여.. 그리고 거기 갔다가 다른 장소 가려고 도로쪽으로 내려왔는데 쓰레기 개많고 개더럽고 하여튼 사진 한장을 위해서라면 갈만 하지만 아니라면 절대 네버 가지마세여...

All the noise, colour and activity is something of a shock to the senses and with so much to see my advice is take a deep breath, walk slowly (you might not much choice on that one) and look around and when it comes to crossing the road: -- step out confidently.- maintain a steady pace.- walk a straight line.- do not stop or try to turn back.Once you start you will have been spotted and the traffic is honour bound to drive around you and not over you and you will make it.I wouldn't want to live here but for a couple of days, what an experience.

The Old Quarter is the best place to stay for first time travelers to Hanoi or for a mix of sightseeing, shopping, walking around  the Hoan Kiem Lake and relaxation amid noisy streets. The Old Quarter is alive far from the quiet atmosphere around the West Lake area. Don’t forget to haggle with  vendors who sell their wares at 100 to 150 percent up the price. When eating out along the small restaurants that line the streets be aware that the price on the menu is different from the final bill where they add 10 percent  more supposedly as service charge. Lastly, use grab when going around as many taxi drivers tend to scam tourists, well, even the one who was commissioned by our hotel to bring us to the airport. Also, be careful in crossing the street as motorbikes and cars hardly stop to give way to pedestrians.

There is so much to shop for! Prices are generally similar, so if you adjust well, you can buy at a decent price. However, there are places where things are not in good condition, check carefully before buying!
This is a nice area that is well worth visiting. Before walking we took a ride on a Tuk Tuk through and this was very pleasant. Recommended,
Nice for shopping (try local fruits, coffee, food, souvenir, and more), but very crowded. I would wear a mask because of all the waste gas from motorbikes and cars.

What a vibrant, joyful corner of the city! Bursting with cozy little bars and the infectious energy of people having a great time—it’s impossible not to smile as you wander through. Don’t miss it, even if just for a magical evening stroll through this utterly charming neighborhood! 🌃

Hanoi city is found as any major metropolis , deprived neighborhoods or is found in small alleys people can of means who live in unhygienic houses if you can call it that , but the atmosphere is warm and meals are taken in the street . You still have to see him to understand that life is not rosy for everyone. Go off the beaten track.

A delightful neighborhood—best enjoyed from a corner café soaking in the ambiance. That said, the streets are buzzing with motorbikes and the sidewalks are uneven, making leisurely walks a bit tricky.
Nice center and immediately a nice impression of street life in Vietnam. Lots of food tents and friendly people.  Etrn affordable.

Come and discover this picturesque neighborhood.You will be able to roam the alleys avoiding motorcycles , bicycles or taxis .dozens of restaurants , massage parlours and all kinds of souvenir shops in the evening the whole streets turn into a restaurant terraceFrom Friday to Sunday evening you can visit the night walk where you will have the choice to find all kinds of booth shoes souvenir clothes etc...

It’s been a pleasure visiting Hanoi’s old quarter ,places like Ho Chi Minh & Ninh binh was great experience.I highly recommend taking the half day Jeep tours in Hanoi it’s arguably the most underrated yet one off the best things to do .

Hanoi’s old quarter is full of history, culture and energy. Narrow alleys, shops, cafes and street food make it an ideal place to explore on foot. Certainly animated and noisy, but that is all its charm. The combination of ancient architecture and modern life creates a unique atmosphere, typically Hanoi.

Hanoi's old town is a vibrant area of history and culture, a city full of continual life. Every narrow alley has shops, cafes, and street food packing cars, which makes it ideal for walking. There's a lot of people and noise, but that's the charm of this place. The combination of old architecture and modern life creates a unique atmosphere where you can feel the true emotion of Hanoi.
Walk hand in hand through narrow alleyways and share Vietnamese coffee. Even in the hustle and bustle, there is quiet time between us. The old town is a fusion of romance and chaos!

Visiting Hanoi's Old Quarter during the Tet Festival is a vibrant experience. The streets are full with colorful decorations. Many of shops, making it an excellent destination for shopping enthusiasts. Additionally, there is a wide array of restaurants and cafes offering diverse culinary options. The pleasant weather during this period further enhances the experience, as the Old Quarter becomes a hub of activity, attracting visitors who come to immerse themselves in the cultural festivities.Overall, experiencing the Old Quarter during Tet offers a unique insight into Vietnamese culture and traditions, making it a memorable visit for anyone in Hanoi during this festive season.

Not to be missed if you are in Hanoi. The experience is worth the incredible chaos that characterizes it. Low tables and chairs / stool for food scattered everywhere, restaurants, shops, beauty spa, nails and on the backrooms some faux luxury well done... watch out for the life moped and cars do not stop even on the strips... crossing the road puts a derà anxiety, this is the old town that fascinates. There are also beautiful and very quiet hotels just look...better if a 5 star! From here you reach everything
We are really like here with good food, nice people, friendly ,many place we visited there,but too much traffic jam here.

Hanoi temperature and humidity very high, keep up fluid and electrolyte intake. Take care when walking to avoid the many uneven surfaces. Hold on to your belongings. Grab is a cheap alternative if you are tired from walking. Train street is interesting, as are the various streets, selling lots of same same. 2-3 days is more than enough.
Nhân viên khách sạn rất tinh tế và chuyên nghiệp. Món ăn rất ngon và hợp khẩu vị. Bữa sáng rất đa dạng đồ ăn Á-Âu.... Phòng rất sạch sẽ và rộng rãi....

I visited Hanoi Old Quarter for the first time. On the busy streets where motorcycles come and go, there are old-fashioned buildings and small cafes, and it feels as if time has stopped. The local pho and banh mi are also excellent, making it a fun area to just walk around.It is a tourist destination, but it is also a place where people can feel their lives. It is also a great place for adult women to walk around town.
Such a bustling part of town! Within walking distance to Train Street and Hoan Kiem Lake. Lots of great restaurants, cafes, markets, and bars. Plenty of fun things to do around here.

The Old Quarter experience was an incredibly good adventure in living amidst the mass of humanity and motorbikes. But to see the Hanoi residents living their daily life was such an enjoyable experience that I enjoyed being a part of during our visit for a week.

A fascinating labyrinth of narrow streets, old houses and lively clubs. The atmosphere is authentic and full of life. Sometimes it can be crowded and noisy, but it is still a must-see to discover the soul of Hanoi!

Things to do during a stay in Hanoi. The hardest part is not getting lost in the different streets in connection with different jobs but crossing the streets with the many scooters. Particular and confusing atmosphere

Old Quarter of Hanoi is full of life and culture! The narrow streets, local shops, and street food make it a unique experience. A great place to soak in the atmosphere and explore Vietnam’s rich heritage!

Various shops are lined up in a miscellaneous manner, and products are arranged so that they protrude from the storefront. Motorcycles are also cluttered on the sidewalk, so you have to walk along the roadway. I was scared until I got used to it, because of the constant traffic of cars and motorbikes.Motorcycle engines and horns are loud, but this hustle and bustle may symbolize the vibrancy of Vietnam.
funny for the amount of people, shops, street vendors, bikes, scooters, restaurants, etc. You get lost, in the literal sense of the word!

Full of locals and tourists, the streets exude energy as well as all manner of retail (including many places to get massages and similar treatments). Though known as the French Quarter, there's not much left of French architecture, but it has that rabbit warren of streets vibe and is great fun. Spend a few hours walking round, or get a pedicab tour.

Lots and lots of fake clothing stores but also lots and lots of great places to eat and drink so on balance a lovely place to base yourself on Hanoi - do understand it will be noisy though and bring ear plugs. We chose an Airbnb just north of the lake and this was a pretty much perfect position Rob

Street vendors sell everything from pho to bun cha, and the smells are irresistible. It’s an authentic taste of Hanoi. The noise can also be overwhelming at times, with motorbikes zooming by on the sidewalks.

West Lake was serene and beautiful. Road was closed to traffic on weekends. Weather was nice. Easy to walk around the Old Quarter. Lots of things to do and experience such as local food, sights and also cafe culture
Must try at least once. Weekend Night Market is a really interesting,Nice people, no haggling, crowded, bargains welcome, good food.

The unique culture is palpable, from the bustling street vendors to the charming cafes. As night fell, the vibrant nightlife came alive, with locals and tourists mingling over delicious street food. We enjoyed exploring the narrow streets, each turn revealing something new. While it can be chaotic, the energy is infectious and made our visit unforgettable. We left with a deeper appreciation for this lively area and its rich history!

Street life is incredible. Find a spot and order some spicy noodles or a coffee and watch the thousands of scooters speed by!Very touristy though. I estimate half the people in the old quarter were tourists when we were there. I actually prefer the other areas nearby which are less touristy.Shopping is great. Find a a street that seeks what you need, toy street, bag street, light street etc, they have so many streets selling all sorts of cheap quality goods.

The bustling streets are filled with vendors selling everything from fresh produce and street food to souvenirs and artisanal goods. Walking here, you’ll encounter the heart of Hanoi’s culture: scooters weaving through pedestrians, locals enjoying steaming bowls of pho on tiny plastic stools, and the hum of life that never seems to pause.The Night Market: A Shopper’s ParadiseWhen the sun sets, the Old Quarter transforms, and the night market comes alive. Stretching across Hang Dao Street and its surrounding lanes, this market is a must-visit for anyone in Hanoi.The night market offers an array of goods: clothing, accessories, handicrafts, and souvenirs, all at bargain prices. It’s a fantastic place to shop for gifts or pick up unique items that showcase Vietnamese artistry. Be prepared to haggle—it’s part of the fun and adds to the authentic experience!If you are a foodie, there is lots to experience.

This aspect of our visit to Hanoi was very authentic.  There were all manner of seafood, fruits and meats for sale in the narrow streets.  There was a lot of activity including the very present motor banks.  This was definitely a very unique experience.

We loved getting lost in the maze of streets, dodging scooters and slurping down delicious pho. The art scene is definitely vibrant - we stumbled upon some cool galleries tucked away down tiny alleys. The lakes offer a bit of serenity amidst the chaos, and the whole area has a charming, slightly chaotic atmosphere. It's not always relaxing, but it's definitely an experience!

The narrow streets are a chaotic mix of scooters, shops, and street food vendors.  We loved soaking up the charming atmosphere and trying all the delicious food, but be prepared to bargain hard and watch your step!

We spent two days wandering these narrow streets. The bustling cafes serve decent Vietnamese coffee, though some felt too touristy. Found some quaint shops selling everything imaginable - watch out for inflated prices! Food scene is vibrant; tried some delicious street food but stick to busy spots. Chaotic but worth experiencing.
A lively, colorful and hectic neighborhood. Many markets and corners of a city that, at times, seems to forget itself, in alleys that turn into the corridor of a large apartment building.
We stayed 2 nights in Hanoi and did a street food tour by scooter. Lot of things to do around the Old Quarter. Great place for food lovers. Highly recommend!
A very vibrant area Plenty to see and do No Irish bars though!A must see in Hanoi The bar street does get very loud though Enjoy
địa điểm này thích hợp cho những bạn nào thích sự ồn ào náo nhiệt. Nên ghé thư một lần nhé. Thật ấn tượng với hoàn kiếm về sự tấp nập

Phố Cổ Hà Nội là một điểm đến tuyệt vời với bầu không khí đậm chất lịch sử và văn hóa. Những con phố nhỏ, cổ kính kết hợp cùng nhịp sống hiện đại tạo nên sự giao thoa đầy thú vị. Ẩm thực đường phố ở đây thực sự là một trải nghiệm không thể bỏ qua với những món ăn truyền thống như phở, bún chả, và nem rán. Dạo quanh Phố Cổ vào buổi tối sẽ thấy nơi đây sôi động hơn với các khu phố đi bộ và chợ đêm. Tuy nhiên, khu vực có thể rất đông vào cuối tuần, và việc di chuyển có thể hơi khó khăn. Đây là nơi lý tưởng để khám phá nét đẹp và sự sôi động của Hà Nội!
Đúng kiểu Hà Nội không vộii được đâu, thích cách từ từ mà cũng không kém phần hối hả ở đây,mình mới ra lần đầu cungz hơi sợ nhưng mà tới rồi thì rất thích

The narrow streets are lined with galleries, street performers, and traditional shops selling everything from silk to souvenirs. However, be prepared for the crowds, especially on weekends when the area is bustling with locals and tourists alike. We enjoyed getting lost in the winding alleys, sampling street food, and taking in the sights and sounds of this fascinating neighborhood.
So much going on in the hustle and bustle of the old quarter. Lots of character in the buildings, decor etc all around. Hard to miss while you’re exploring the city

This place is nuts, but the shopping is great.  Mopeds everywhere, including the sidewalks.  We were there in August and the heat and humidity were oppressive.  We stayed in a hotel three nights here.  I think I would have given Old Town 5 stars if I had come in the spring or fall when the heat wasn't so intense. My husband loves to haggle and for him it was a kind of paradise.

Hanoi’s old town immediately captivated us with its charming atmosphere! The bustling markets, the abundance of street food and the teeming crowds of people - life is pulsating here!  It was a bit tight at times, but that's exactly what made it so appealing. An unforgettable experience for us as a couple!

This is a larger area in central Hanoi, maybe 8 blocks by 10 blocks.  Its called the old quarter but there is very little historic buildings.  Mostly shops, restaurants and hotels.  And LOTS of people and scooters!  Some great areas to do your shopping, get your nails done, and each lunch but OTHER than the train road (area) it was not that special compared to even other old areas in SE Asia.

Lots of businesses and shops and more mopeds than people.Train street is a must. It's utter chaos when the traffic is stopped and the crowds can be a lot but if you can get a space it's really fun to watch the train pass by.

What a place! So many shops, cafes, restaurants and motorbikes! They are all over and crossing the streets each time is an adventure. Somehow the motorbikes, pedestrians and cars have their own navigation on these narrow streets. Fun to watch. We found no harassing from shopkeepers; they were pleasant to meet. Wander around and take in the sights.

The old section of Hanoi is small, but offers a wide variety of shopping, food, and attractions. Wondering around you will see a mix of architecture (old to new). The area is worth visiting, especially for the night market which is interesting.
Lively neighborhood with sometimes intense impressions for the nose. Colorful mix of restaurants, cafes and shops of all kinds. Always well attended. Offer a lot of interesting impressions.

The best thing about the Old Quarter is the opportunity to visit the Mosuleaum and the neighboring museums. The Old Quarter is rich with history, food galore, and coffee galore. You will find a great abundance of travel agencies vying for your business for various tours. I went with Adventure Indochina Travel and they were excellent. I'm sure they're all good, they all offer the same tours for relatively the same prices. You will be met with street vendors and massage girls on every street calling out to you for your business. This gets to be annoying as you cannot walk down the street without being solicited. Whatever you do, you must try the famous Egg Coffee at The Note coffee shop. If you are American, I recommend that you use the HSBC in the center of the OLD QUARTER to withdraw funds from the ATM as it is otherwise difficult to find an ATM that works with our debit cards.
Here you can experience a very authentic vietnamese lifestyle with a lot of restaurants and cafés. Although a visit is a must, some may find it a bit littered and chaotic.

The Old Quarter is a whirlwind of sights, sounds, and smells! We loved getting lost in the maze of narrow streets, lined with beautiful temples and traditional architecture. It's a photographer's dream, and the perfect place to experience the heart of Hanoi.
Đi dạo vòng quanh phố cổ là một trải nghiệm khá là thú vị. Khi đi cùng với bạn bè, chúng tôi có thể thấy không khí nhộn nhịp ở nơi đây
It's an interesting place to walk around, with plenty of things to see buy, and barter for. Lots of restaurants to try the fantastic local food.

Username: THE LIGHTHOUSE AN BANG RESTAURANT & BAR
Comment: we have been here last time. it is big city with many traffice but we got a great time to walk along the lake . it is very cool and many activities .

Nice place to stay and stroll around. Everything is close but be aware of motor bikes. It feels like there are millions of them. Crossing a street or road is a challenge. Worth staying in Old Quarter many attractions in walking distance.

Hanoi Old Quarter is quite an interesting area. There are about 39 streets that make up this quarter. They are narrow, full of small shops and restaurants and full of life! This may not be everyone's favourite destination but many people choose to visit. We enjoyed our visit but were apprehensive with the street food on offer and the busy traffic on the roads. Cars and bikes everywhere and no footpaths to walk on.  A nice place full of history with lovely people everywhere.

After coming from Ho Chi Min City this was very  easy to get around. The weather was a little cooler but not much. No rain at all. The old Quarter had a lot of little shops. You could buy a lot of pearls at jewellery shops. Plenty of money exchange shops. We got a SitLo to get us around and stop at shops of interest like the famous train street. Plenty of restaurants. Three days is  probably enough.
It's too crowded and noisy but a must-see city. Lot of good and cheap restaurants. We did cyclo tour around the old Quarter. Great experience!

I had a picture of Vietnam and Hanoi in my head of hectic rickshaw and scooter traffic, vibrant stalls and vendors in conical hats.  And the Old Quarter is exactly that.  Even if you just go to see the design of the old French colonial buildings it is fascinating.  Very easy to get lost but always a friendly hotel desk or person nearby to ask for help.I was surprised at all the foot massage parlours, they are everywhere!  There are also lots of souvenir outlets but I found the quality to be low and things overpriced.  Wait and buy things elsewhere if you can as mainly tat in this bit of town.

If you want to shop, visit the popular temples, get massages, have street food or drink cheap beer, then Old Quarter is the place to visit. Maybe for this same reason, there are a lot of affordable hotel & hostel stays in the area.My family & I visited the Ngoc Son Temple, did street shopping & got foot massage. You can easily spend 3 hours & want to explore more. Wearing comfortable shoes is a must. Definitely worth a visit, if you do not mind super crowded streets.

Hanoi's old quarter is everything you could imagine. Cafes, restaurants, shops selling everything and anything, lots of people and thousands of motorbikes. It's just a  organized confusion. You can see everything here, from street food .  to even the most sophisticated restaurant. It's worth getting lost in this confusion of streets. Very very funny and interesting . But sometimes this disorganization becomes tiring and uncomfortable.

I stayed in a small Guest House here during my 3-day  visit of Hanoi. There are lots of shops, Guest Houses, restaurants, travel agencies,  bike rental places, etc.  My concern was the absence of sidewalks, they are being used to park the motorbikes for rent or to display the goods for sale. Pedestrians then walk in the narrow streets, with all the traffic coming and going. I’m just surprised not see pedestrians being hurt or accidents between cars and motor bikes.
Impressive first contact with the culture of Southeast Asia.Chaos, motorcycles, motorcycles, motorcycles...Life...on the street.Nice people.Recommended to take a good walk around the area
A great part of the city to get bargains and immerse yourself in the craziness that is Hanoi! Safe and friendly.Don't miss it!
I came here in 2023, everyone here is friendly. There are many places for people to visit and have fun. 1 place worth visiting

This is Vietnam... No more squeamish people or pussies. Basically nothing is understood here. Between locals who speak impossible English, cars, scooters, stalls, fumes and vapors coming from the rambling kitchens, you end up feeling stunned.It wouldn't be a unique experience if that wasn't the case.To wander around for a couple of days at most and an excellent starting point for various destinations. The lake area is nice.DRUMMER.
Must stay in the area. Lot of things to do around the hotel. We like local street food and tried many. Spend at least 2 nights here

Many cities in various Asian countries have crowded and noisy areas like this. Admittedly, with all the motorbikes, cars, lack of sidewalks and bikes parked on what’s left of any sidewalk, it can be difficult to walk. Not to mention the noise, food smells and people sitting in front of the restaurants and coffee shops.It makes for good photographs however. Walk carefully and don’t be careless with your cellphone and money as it makes for easy pickings for pickpockets.  It seems like this area has the most foreigners of any city in Vietnam. Overall it’s fun, especially in the evening when a zillion people are out. Howard S.

This part of the city is interesting and full of character - definitely worth a look.  However, it's an exhausting place to be a pedestrian.  Many of the pavements are completely blocked by parked scooters and motor bikes, and most of the rest are covered in chairs and tables from restaurants.  The roads, meanwhile, swarm with motorbikes.  It makes getting about quite tiring!  I was glad to have visited, but I would not have wanted to spend a week; to be honest, I was getting a bit fed up after four days.
A bustling neighborhood with many restaurants from luxury to affordable. This place has many historical relics about different periods of Vietnam

Old Street was full of energy, with motorbikes passing by non-stop. I ate some banh mi and walked around to the other side of the church, but there were quite a few tourists, so it was interesting to see all kinds of people. The buildings were a mix of Western and Chinese styles, which was nice.

The first time I came to Hanoi's Old Quarter, I was really impressed with this place.The streets are extremely crowded.I haven't adapted yet. u know. until i returned, i still coudnt cross the street by myself. But oh my god , street food is so good. Bun Cha, banh mi. pho. everyone sang and danced together . i will come back

Busy and bustling Old Quarter. Everything going on, in and around. From Restaurants for all tastes and budgets, to street food & local coffee shops.Not to mention the endless foot & body Massages places. I tried serene Spa which was double the price of the 100’s of standard massage places and for just under £20 it was so worth is to get a proper masseuse and the service is top class as soon as you walk in the door.
Yes, it's true that the old quarter of Hanoi is chaotic, dirty, full of people, cars, scooters and therefore smog, but this is also its charm.

The Hanoi Old Quarter is best explored on foot. The challenge is crossing the road (without getting hurt) when faced with a never ending stream of motorbikes coming at you from all directions. Not for the faint hearted. We stayed in a hotel in the Old Quarter so we enjoyed the convenience of living the heartbeat of Hanoi, taking in its light, sound, smell and pulse of this vibrant old city.I particularly enjoyed the walk from our hotel to Hoan Kiem Lake. Part of the fun was seeing and observing the locals’ daily activities: chatting, exercising and dancing with their fellow friends.

Very beautiful, magnificent architecture combining that of the French and that of the Vietnamese, wonderful food. I have the impression of Bun Cha, the egg cafe, but too crowded sometimes traffic jams and too much noise in the street

One place that I definitely spend my weekend for exploring is Hanoi's Old Quarter. Here I can freely shop for all kinds of products, from traditional food, clothes to personal items, etc. It feels like I can find everything here. It's more convenient when Hanoi's Old Quarter very close to the Ha Noi center and many hotels. Come and experience this place, I'm sure you'll have a great time
Nice place with recommendation from my hosrt. I will be back for the next time. And acient buildings were one of my favorite.
A distinctive neighborhood of Vietnam where you can admire French buildings. The shops, spas and restaurants are okay.

There are many ancient buildings here. Along the meticulous alleys, you can appreciate Hanoi's traditional cultural customs and experience the blend of different cultures. Here, you can taste a variety of delicious food and enjoy the relaxed atmosphere in lively cafes. In addition, the scenery of the lake can also make you relax and add more fun to your trip. Hanoi Old Quarter is really a place worth visiting, allowing you to experience the colorful cultural customs.

There are cafes everywhere, both local and modern, complex and very attractive. The old town is a microcosm of the life of local people. They live in the city and behave like they are in the countryside. They get up early and gather together to eat and chat because their houses are narrow. , so meals are all served outside. The old house carries long-lasting memories and gives tourists a retro and nostalgic feeling. This is my first trip to Hanoi.

It's very noisy and crowded all the day. Stayed in the center and we could find all just around us. It's hard and crazy when you want to cross the streets.  Good food and restaurants can be found any where! Make sure you stay some where in the Old Quarter!
A great place to stroll around. Just  look how vietnamees live en taste some streetfood. Where it is busy it is usual safe for us to eat. Nice place to sleep is tirant hotel.

Pretty walkable although you must be somewhat agile when crossing roads or stepping on the sidewalks. The bikers have a different perception of space with you! The open air eateries are all friendly and delicious and pretty cheap. Just make sure to come here in the right season. I went in late July and it was the height of summer so it was really really hot and humid with almost no place to hide from the hot wind.
A great place to explore every weekend, especially on holidays, it will be decorated quite eye-catching, with many activities on the street. The morning walk was great

Walking around was an amazing experience, regardless of what time and day you strolled around. Booking a hotel here is a must, especially for tourists. You'll definitely feel the essence of the country when you spend at least a day here. There's an endless amount of food in restaurants and stalls that you could even feel overwhelmed. Popular tourist sites are within walking distance as well.

Very noisy, crowded and full of motorcycles and tourist bus. Want the experience of street food and tourist shops, visit the old quarter. Haggle down to half price and be ready to walk away. Many stores with same things. It’s also very close to the lake of the turtle island. Be careful crossing and walking around. Download Grab app is you are traveling anywhere in Vietnam. Don’t just get into a taxi with a meter on the street waiting at the big tourist places.He  took us the long way and cost us 3X what Grab charged us. They also reached for our money and tried to hide what he grabbed behind him. Was not a good experience. Traveling long distance we used Govntravel. Half the price and reasonable. Very professional but they will offer tours that you can take or leave. There is also an HSBC bank close to the lake which gives a higher limit for withdrawals than local banks.

The messy electric wires and smelly atmosphere of 10 years ago are gone, but the number of motorbikes and people has increased, and the place is full of horns and energy. And one of our goals was to eat as much B-grade gourmet food as we could!
Hanoi is quite interesting, there are many dishes that I like, shopping is also cheap. I was able to sew a few clothes at quite reasonable prices. Maybe will come back here on the next vacation.

This is the bustling space in Hanoi full of  restaurants, shops and night market. There are massage parlours, shops selling variety of goods and two wheelers bustling in and out. One must visit during night time to see the full glamour of this area and enjoy the street food.

The Old Quarter is a fascinating place to just wander around.  There are shops everywhere, selling almost anything you could want.  There tend to be clusters of similar shops, for instance, one street we wandered down had shops for everything you need for your motorcycle.  Seats, accessories, repair, you name it.  Another street was ceramics, one was light bulbs and lanterns. The south end of the the Old Quarter is where it is most touristy, at the north end of the lake, so you will be accosted a little more in that area.
Oppressive heat and humidity. The evening is bedlam. Scooters whiz by everywhere. But it's worth it. Certainly one of the most curious attractions in Hanoi.
Walk, stroll and enjoy the sites and sounds of Hanoi, stop for coffee or sip a sugarcane juice. Take in the vibe

La primera toma de contacto con esta zona de Hanoi es agobiante, caos total pero más o menos organizado, muchísimas motos por todo, las aceras ocupadas con lo que tienes que andar casi siempre por la calzada, pero tiene ese arie tan peculiar del sudeste asiatico que te enamora. La gente hace la vida en la calle con lo cual a cada paso que das encuentras algo sorprendente.Comercios organizados por temáticas, bares, restaurantes con minisillas de plastico para locales y turistas, agencias de viaje por doquier, centros de masaje... Alojarse aquí es la mejor opción si quieres vivir al máximo la experiencia aunque prepárate para perder el miedo a cruzar la calle.
ハノイの夜はここで決まり！なんせ、人が多くて、呼び込みの人がいっぱいで、まぁー大変だー！価格も観光地価格
Nên đi giá hơi mắt xíu , nhưng  đáng để trải nghiệm , nên đi ăn phở , mua gì nên trả giá , hỏi giá trc khi dùng

The old quarter has many streets , some more interesting than others, selling everything from utility items for locals to souvenirs and craftworks for tourists. There were shops for jewellery, pearls ,clothes, footwear, ,handbags, luggage and accessories.  It was a pleasure to stroll through the roads and shop for things.We had been advised to bargain hard in Vietnam. What I observed - Not all shopkeepers quote escalated prices.Some of them quoted more than double , some even triple the price. But some also had no bargain prices and some bargained a mere 10 or 15 pct.So my advise is to window shop at few places ,check out and compare the price and quality.Then go-ahead and quote what you deem fit.One interesting thing I observed is that shops selling one kind of goods are generally placed together. So you have a row with all shops selling toys or kitchenware or footwear.The day we arrived , we went for a one hour Cyclo ride that gave us an opportunity to know the place ( 100 K VND per person for 1 hr )We also did the electric car ride (available from a station on Hoan Kiem Lake ) that took us around to some different streets. ( 245 K VND per electric car - 45 minutes)But most times we explored on foot.Grabs are very easily available and very reasonably priced ( beginning 33K VND for approx 5-7 kms )It has the hustle bustle and feel of old town ( obvious as per the name) , so do not expect a  fancy market.
東京の合羽橋みたいな感じで通りごとに専門店があり、整然とごちゃごちゃしていますね。調理器具を金属板からハサミで作っていてビックリ。さまよっていたら線路が出現。

Been here before few times before Covid period. I was here for business trip 29 May to 3 June 23. Weather is extremely hot in the day time. So on my first day of arrival. I managed only to walk a very short while around in the afternoon and then walk longer around the old quarters in the cooling night, on my first day of arrival.I managed to buy two hats, 3 sleeveless t-shirts, all each at 100k dong. The rest are coffee powders/ 3-1 & 2-1 instant coffee, and can drinks coffee from highlands coffee.Too hot to walk. so better to come at night. But some shops may close early around 9pm... In this trip, I ate chicken pho, beef pho, fried spring rolls, fried morning glory, fried eggs, sticky rice with pork, sweet corn milk drink, mango sticky rice, cold dessert etc.For me personally, I prefer Hanoi than HCM. More historic buildings and things to see, and more food choices for me.

Took a stroll through the souvenir shops,street food,,train line, on our own. It was amazing to see how modernisation took place from the ruins of past. Good to see how essence of past has been preserved.

We stayed in the Old quarters and spent most of our evenings walking around, from the streets to the lake and Night markets. It was overwhelming at first- the bustle, the traffic, the crowds. However after day two, we started to appreciate the Old quarter for what it was- an opportunity to see how life in Hanoi is. We saw walkways lined with people having their evening dinner at the hole in the wall stalls. We saw middle aged ladies dancing to country music at the lake. We saw friends, families, couples walking and enjoying the lake view. We saw tourists walking the quarter trying to make sense of the place. We saw the locals and tourist crowds enjoy food and drinks at night.One stand out was how safe the place was- we kinda expected pick pockets or scammers but didnt have any experiences or vibes. The other stand out was how wholesome the place was- Friday night and everyone is well behaved, sound music, families out and about.
It gives a feel for old Hanoi, worth a drive through and a roadside meal - certainly a nice vibe to experience
東南アジアの喧騒を代表する場所の一つですね。人もバイクも多い、多い。ほとんど信号も横断歩道もないので道路の横断には気をつけて下さいね。

This is a vibrant part of the city, with narrow bustling streets filled with atmospheric cafes, tourist bars, crazy traffic, authentic eateries and street food. It is heavily touristed and many businesses cater for this, but you can still easily find local food and coffee. The area has a high concentrration of affrodable accommodation.

Fantastisk opplevelse av denne delen av Hanoi! Det er travelt, men samtidig en ro oppi alt det hektiske. Et blikk inn i en annen verden og et helt annet liv gir perspektiv på egen del av verden og eget liv. Om du trenger stille stunder finner du også kafeer med ro og nydelig kaffe, prøv f eks kafé Nola!

This is the busy and lively part of town.  Everywhere is bustling with activities and lots of traffic.  The chaos gave the place character and charm.There are lots of small eatery and street food here.  There are also different streets specifically in selling one type of goods or service only.  For example, ceramics, toys, stationary, etc.
Rues avec chaque métier ruelles étroites circulation intenseL'occasion de découvrir les boutiques et de flâner pour acheter des souvenirs
We could spend hours just studying the rich diversity of activities on the streets, this part of the world is so different to live as we know it!
C'est le vieux quartier de Hanoi, je vous conseil d'y aller faire un tour, et vous promener et admirer les architectures des maisons, toutes sont différentes !
Old street is much more crowed. There are a lot of food here. Bring your camera , everything you look is something to photograph
Lugar dinâmico com muita gente circulando, muita "street food" e outras experiências diferentes para conhecer. Embora seja cheio de gente, motos, carros não me senti insegura ou sufocada.
Una bella passeggiata nel distretto n. 1, un'unica accortezza: attenzione ai motorini!Pe ril resto posto tranquillo e non ho avuto la sensazione di pericolo delle grandi città
muita pena que o grau de lixo na rua seja ainda muito grande.Passeio a pé com muta segurança.trânsito caótico
10月末に行ってから3ヶ月後にまたハノイを訪れて旧市街を歩きました。今回の訪問はテト、ベトナムの旧正月の時期だったので閉まってる店もありましたが10月末の時よりも現地の人で溢れてました。
the best part of hanoi small restaurants, the lake, shopping markets and much more. would suggest you book a hotel here

Sidewalks practically don't exist, since they are turned into motorbike parking lots, barber shops, restaurants, shops... Motorbikes everywhere (but they won't run you over, don't worry!) very nosy and terribly polluted (wear a mask!!!) in spite of all this you'll love it, it's very funny.

Very happening area of Hanoi,with narrow lanes andyou will also find lot of street food being sold but only for non vegeterians. it has lot of cafes,and you can enjoy coffee which comes in great varieties.Make sure to go to the night market and also try the beer at beer street.
Our local tour guide from Deluxe Vietnam Tours helped us to see the real Hanoi Old Quarter. Get off the beaten track - surely without him, we could not see these amazing daily life activities
Interesting to explore this area with street food tour and cyclo tour. Interesting to go into in the small alleys,but it is hard for us to find if we do not travel with a local tour guide
We explored the old town on our own but we did not get the real life of the local until our tour guide from a local tour company showed the inside - interesting
une belle promenade dans les quartiers de Hanoi, une activité belle, vivante et incessante , ne pas oublié de prendre un thé là ou passe le train
We had a Cyclos tour - which gave us a great perspective of the life here and of the old quarter. Would recommend this as there are a lot of motor bikes that could make walking difficult
Extremely busy and vibrant , lots of massage shops and food stalls. Some good hotels in area but if you don’t like crowded places not for you

It was an awesome experience except for the bike's horn and the trash being thrown on the street. Our guide from Tonquin Travel describes the area as the "Market Grid" in the ancient time where people from all around gathered on the 1st and 15th of every month in Lunar Calendar as something that never change over time and how they lived in the past. Kind of a "journey on the line between the Ages" that you can see small details in the way of people and architecture. It hinted at the atmosphere in the past and nowadays and you can feel it your way. Also, you can only find a good taste of food from the real locals here, not the 5 stars restaurant, that is the way people explore gastronomy here, in the Old quarter.
praticamente la Hanoi del centro è tutta in old quarter. molto piccoresca, con street food ad ogni angolo dove si mangia H24
Just go and walk around stopping by the many cafes and shops etc. it’s super friendly and there’s not a lot of tourists hustlers bothering you.

A bit tricky to walk, as the sidewalks are filled with motorbikes and restaurant seating. However, if you do not have any mobility issues, it is a fun place to explore. Lots of great food options. If you are sensitive to noise or pollution, ear plugs and a face mask will do you good.

The Old Quarter (Vietnamese: Phố cổ Hà Nội) is the name commonly given to the historical civic urban core of Hanoi, located outside the Imperial Citadel of Thăng Long. This quarter used to be the residential, manufacturing and commercial center, where each street was specialized in one specific type of manufacturing or commerce.Another common name referring to approximately the same area is the 36 streets (Vietnamese: Hà Nội 36 phố phường), after the 36 streets or guilds that used to make up the urban area of the city.
maravilloso.Una visita por los calles   de Vietnam es algo que siempre recordarás, pasea y piérdete y disfruta de este maravilloso pais.
We spent time walking along old street. Loved the crazy and noisy atmosphere. Tasted local food, very good and cheap. EnjoyED it alL!
Daerah dengan kepadatan wisatawan, jika hanya punya beberapa hari ke Hanoi, Old Quarter cocok untuk menjadi tempat tinggal, tetapi jika lebih dari itu, terlalu ramai menurutku.

Enjoyed a sunday afternoon of walking through the old quarter and seeing all the various types of products sold on the streets. Easy to get lost but then that is part of the fun, much easier to navigate during the day than the evening for sure

Coming from non-Asian countries, you would definitely find this place interesting.  But not for long time as it is not easy to walk from the motorbikes, it is not so safe in terms of traffic. Narrow lanes make you feel inerested first, but then feel not so fresh..

It is a real treat and experience to wander around the Old Quarter... on so many sensory levels... foremost amongst which is simply crossing the street. Best advice was to not stall or show hesitancy as this creates problems, just keep moving and the traffic will work its way around you.

old Quater is a busy market place where you can see shops selling  all types of products. its like mumbai crawford market but very well organised. you will find every street selling one single product . there is a shoe street,utensils,clothes, purses,etc... you will also find lot of street food being sold. delight for non vegeterians. it has lot of cafes. in the night on week ends it becomes only walking street. you can walk around through the area . there is a night market also. you can explore in the day time by the green cycle ride which takes you around the whole area for 45 mins.

Skip Ha Long Bay for these place if you can only choose one. You're not coming to Hanoi if you didn't visit the Old Quarter. Try to eat/taste as many as you can. Most of the Famous Hanoi local foods are here. Eat till you drop and you are almost eaten most of the Hanoi local food here.
I was spent a day to discover this place. Very nice! the people are friendly. They introduced me a lot of interesting in Hanoi and Sapa also

There're 36 streets and they have too many amazing things: foods, landmarks, historical destinations,... You can research on Google or use some app like Traveloka or Travelly to know some interesting dishes of local people besides just Pho and other places to go not just about Sword Lake or some other popular destinations.  I highly recommend you to try bun rieu!!! However, be careful or you can get lost :)

Old Quarter Rome or Rhodes it is not. Lots of the authentic sites to visit are here, and it is centrally located but for some,  day visits may be enough rather than located there, Midway north of the Hoah Kiem lake is perfect. Lots of the excursions pick up on the main highway to the right. Access to Westlake but yet on the doorstep on the Old Hanoi epicentre / food markets etc.
Zona muito movimentada, com muito transito. Muitas lojas, comida de rua e muitas atividades. A maior parte dos monumentos ficam nesta area e vale a pena ficar num hotel dentro da zona.

We’ll worth a visit to see how the real street life and businesses operate.  Everything from fruit & veg. Sales to shoe repairs. Street food, jewellery shops, banks and small hotels line the streets.  The walkways are covered by motorbikes so be prepared to brave walking on the road.  Very interesting and some of the best & tastiest food cafes.

The old quarter is the main attraction of Hanoi. Every square meter is turned into a little business or shop and the energy pumps non-stop. It pains me to see how much these entrepreneurial people have suffered during the last 2 years of Covid lock-downs ; a great number of shops are still shuttered up, possibly never to open again. Although it is the same everywhere in Vietnam, it baffles me that they still block all footpaths with parked scooters, forcing pedestrians into the crazy traffic on the road. They even park their scooter in the entrance of their own shop !! Not a place for those obsessed with "health & safety" either : because the shops are so small, all work is done on the footpath, exposing live wires, tools and sharps . . .It is all chaotic, but fun and interesting. Invariably, you will find a little shop with the perfect souvenir !

The Hanoi old quarter is a real hive of activity. Traffic is perhaps the craziest i've yet seen. Astonishing that in the 4 days i was there I didn't see a single accident, and also that the cars seem remarkably free of scratches and damage. I wouldn't want to drive in this area!
You should spend at least 2 days at the old quarter to experience. The street food is very good, diverse but the price is very different from shops so remember to ask the price before ordering.

(コロナ前の雨期に訪れました)沢山小道が入り組んでいるので、自分でマップ見ながら街歩きする前に、シクロに乗ってぐるぐる街を一通り周ってもらいました。旧市街のど真ん中のホテルだったのですが、ホテルの人にシクロを呼んでもらった方が、交渉してもらったり、シクロの番号も控えてくれていたので安心です。(シクロの運転手は英語が通じないし、愛想ないので注意が必要です。シクロがホテルに30分早く帰ったら、ホテルの人がシクロを"ズルした"と、問い詰め、私は、"疲れたから早くても別に良い"と言ったのだけど、ホテルの人は許さず、その分を返金するように言ったのか、シクロの運転手が、違う金額だった、と嘘をついてみたり、一旦部屋に戻ったのに、まだマネージャーがノックして、その話をしてきたので、私は"早めの帰宅でも満足だからもういいよ"と終わらせました。どっちも、気が強いなぁ、と思いました。しかし、あのくらい主張をするのが正しく、"まぁ、いいや"となりがちな観光客としては　見習わなければ、と反省もしました。)シクロでの散策は、風を感じながら、生活風景を眺め、フレンチコロニアルの建物を見たりして、最初に街の雰囲気を一通り知るには、地図見ながら歩くのより良いと思います。夜は歩行者天国でごちゃごちゃして来て、どの店も、道端に椅子とテーブルを並べはじめ、お祭りみたいになりました。そこで食べたくはないけど、眺めて歩くのはおもしろかったです。
Phố đi bộ cuối tuần đông vui, nhiều hoạt động thú vị, khách tham quan có thể thưởng thức nhiều món ăn ngon!
so much to do, delicious french food, late night parties with families, fun! beware of the swarms of scooter traffic
A must visit if you're looking for a happening night life experience. Great choice of souvenirs with affordable price.

Gia đình mình rất lâu mới quay trở lại Hà Nội. Hà Nội giờ thay đổi rất nhiều, đường xá đông đúc hơn, người như nêm nhưng vẫn có thể tìm những khoảng thời gian thư giãn tại đây với phố đi bộ mở rộng, rất nhiều hoạt động văn hóa diễn ra, ẩm thực cũng đa dạng và giá cũng tốt hơn hẳn so với bình thường.
This area of town has very small, old, twisty streets with loads of shops. It is very easy to get turned around. Many things to see and do here.

An amazing place to enjoy good time. Great variety of local food, street food, good restaurants, roadside cafes. Also the place has many hostels, hotels and airbnbs to stay. The market has a great variety of cheap but quality stuff. One can enjoy a good time with friends here.
Spent three days exploring this amazing place, people are very friendly the food is amazing its amust do if you go to Vietnam.

Đi vào tháng 11, thời tiết lạnh lạnh dễ chịu. Đi bộ qua gần hết các ngõ, ăn chè vỉa hè, bún ốc, không khí gần noel nên ngoài đường trang trí nhiều đồ. Mình ở Hostel tabalo, hình như trên phố Hàng Ngang. Rẻ, tầm 150k 1 đêm. Ở thoải mái, sạch sẽ. Ban ngày đi dạo và ăn thử nhiều món như bún đậu mắm tôm, bún chả, bún ốc, chè,... mình đi 1 mình. Những nơi như vậy đi 2 người sẽ rất vui
Phố cổ Hà Nội rất đẹp, nhất là đi dạo quanh những con phố cổ vào buổi tối, sắp đến trung thu những khu phố bán lồng đền là nơi lý tưởng để chụp ảnh.
Une explosion de sons, de couleurs et d'odeurs ! Très animé et très typique encore. Et on trouve de bonnes affaires !
העיר העתיקה בהנוי מאוד מיוחד . זה מקום הראשון שאני מביא חברים כדי לקבל את ויטנאם בנוק אוט . הבלגן הריחות הרעש ואוכל וחניות . הכל בגדול והכל צפוף מאוד . פשוט חייבה מאוד טובה . מומלץ להיות .

We stayed in the Old QuArter district,close to Beer Street not far from lots of eateries and bars. We were able to walk to many attractions when not on the tour. We walked around the lakes stopping at cafes. We found several roof top bars to enjoy a cool drink in thee endings. Loved the price of beer in the minimarts.
I was expecting the old town to be fantastic - but I would rate it as very good. Maybe a little dirty for my liking.

If you want to stay in an interesting, affordable, walkable area then this is the place. It's touristy and lots of businesses are oriented to tourists, small hotels with exceptional service, fantastic small restaurants, shops, easy walking (be brave), scenic. I loved the old quarter. It was exceptional

Una de las principales atracciones de la ciudad, abarrotadas calles estrechas, tráfico infernal, restaurantes, tiendas y mercados.El mayor peligro es ser atropellado por una moto.Poco agobio al turista.

Stayed in the old quarter - Adamas Hotel.  The area has so much history - good and bad. Ask guides for background if you haven't read up on it or if what you read was confusing without the context.  Look out for all the little alleys and explore inside.We were there just at the start of Corona, and things were shutting down from Hanoi down to the south.  We were so lucky - all the way down to HCMC - only having one trip cacelled  on our last day.  Many Hanoi people were not very friendly / approachable.  90% of tourists were caucasian and I believe locals felt we were bringing in the virus (?).  The small tourist numbers were definitely a boon for us!   Wander about, explore restauarants - found really nice ones,  check out street food vendors - some appear absolutely tip top whils others appear a bit dugies in terms of health.Shopping - lots of similar items all over.  Bargain!!! (about 60%?)Still have a lot of French arcitecture.  Narrow buildings but look up.  each floor often houses a different business - so you havw to climb.Cabs are cheap. Ask your hotel to call them -  Mai Linh and - they're there in no time - with a stated fare up front!  Streets are narrow, congested and doni't necessarily follow a grid pattern - taxis are much easier.  explore and enjoy!
Une vie grouillante, une multitude de commerces de toutes sortes. Au plus prés de la vie de tous les jours, le tout dans un environnement des plus typique.

this place is crazy /lively /and is a must we were there at the corona virus pandemic and it was bizzy enough and tourism was down about 50% so i would hate to see it at peak times, you must do it, and if your a candy lover do not miss candy lane , get lost go into the back streets you wont be disappointed

There are few main roads in Old Town that can have 2 way vehicle traffic, if it was not for the scooters. Most roads look like alleys. Walking the streets, and you do walk the streets as the sidewalks are full of parked scooters, offers a view into the life of the residents,but you have to be aware of the traffic. Old Town offers everything a resident needs from tin smith to bamboo ladders to food.
Hanoi is een leuke stad om je 2 dagen te vermaken. Wandel naar de bekende bezienswaardigheden en 's avonds door de gezellige straatjes van het oude centrum.
Ser ut som dom flesta "Old Quarter" i Asien. Massor med butiker men ofta relativt samma varor. Mestadels billigt...
Molto caratteristico, si vive e si respira l'aria di un tempo andato , peccato che si respiri anche lo smog e il rumore di mille motorini
We stayed here for two nights after travelling from the south and what can I say about Vietnam it’s a wonderful place . The people are so friendly the foods amazing and we just love the place .
It's the heart of the city and not to be missed.  Be careful walking but not fearful.  You must be on the street to feel the city and be a part of it.

Wir hatten überwiegend eine sehr schöne Zeit im Old Quarter. Viele gute Restaurants und interessante Shops. Die Verkäufer sind teilweise sehr aufdringlich. Beinahe wäre meine Freundin Opfer eines Taschendiebes geworden, aber da Sie es sofort gemerkt hat, konnte ich mir den Dieb mit Unterstützung eines anderen Touristen schnappen.
Only visited for an evening, stayed a little way away and we're glad we did! It's very noisy and exciting! Lots of bars, restaurants and shops. Worth a visit, but go prepared!

מהערים-הבירות הגדולות - אולי העניה ביותר שראיתי במזרח הרחוק. מרתקת  מבחינה חברתית. מוזיאון המלחמה - תזכורת מצמררת לזוועות שחוללו האמריקנים, בייחוד ההפצצות המרוכזות של מטוסי בי52 בימי ניכסון. ראוי לציון האופי התעמולתי החד צדדי של התערוכה (האמת תמיד נמצאת אי שם באמצע)
Go there for a walk if you're around, in evening or night if possible, but you will meet more tourists than locals.
Ample of local food stalls and pubs. very crowded place to be at night especially weekend. Basically meant for local crowd and cheap hangout area. Many foreigners aswell.
Wer es mag in typischen wuseligen Städten herumzustöbern ist hier richtig. Es gibt hier A L L E S zum Kaufen und zum Essen.
We spent three very interesting hours experiencing multiple family run restaurants and many booths with a multitude of wares (vegetables, fish, meat, seasonings, bamboo food products, pho, etc)!
Much nicer than Saigon. Pleasant and full of character. You have to try Pho when there. And walk round the lake
De oude stad van Hanoi is een drukke bedoening, maar je een prachtig beeld van hoe het er in Vietnam aan toe gaat.Ideaal om je in een fietsje te laten rondrijden
Old buildings, mixed in with the vibrant night life. Largely frequented by foreign tourists, but it was a good opportunity to get a taste of the local cuisine as well.
il caos di Hanoi non si può' spiegare, va vissuto...ma non si può' non perdersi fra le stradine del vecchio quartiere alla scoperta di questa parte della citta'
Interessante zona della citta di Hanoi in contrasto con la piú moderna zona del French Quarter, ma piena di vita locale,  di ristoranti e di occasioni per lo shopping.

Around every corner is something new, from egg coffees, pho, local food stalls or small eateries, boutique hotels, clothes, souvenirs, artwork, pot street, tailors, buttons, hardware, to the lake. Great place to spend a couple of days
Quartier très en demande. Et vraiment faite attention dans la rue.  On y trouve de tout. Jolie galerie d’art, bistro et restaurant.
The old Quarter is a blend if Vietnam and old French colonial buildings, shopping was excellent, pubs were good, street food wasexcellent

prendre du temps pou visiter, s'arrêter dans les boutiques pour parler avec  certains vendeurs ; chercher à savoir comment sont réalisés les instruments de musique par exemple... bref flâner lentement pour découvrir
Sentralt i byen, flere spisesteder rundt. Vanskelig å bevege seg til fots pga kaotisk trafikk og nærmest ikke -eksisterende fortau.

If you've never been to an Asian city before then you'll either love or hate the frenetic activity of the Old Quarter.The streets are alive with cars, bikes, scooters, people, dogs etc. Markets pop up and disappear at different times of the day and street food vendors and their stools block the pavements. You'll spend half your time walking in the road but just go with the flow, walk confidently and you'll be fine. It's real.
Vieux quartier des 36 corporations, des petites échoppes, bars, restaurants. Bruyant et coloré et ses innombrables scooters !

So much happening it this tightly packed area. Traffic is hectic and noisy, even so there are quieter corners and if you look carefully you can see the structure of the place behind the superficial brash commercialism.
日本にはない混然一体としたカオスな街並みが続きます。大雑把に商品カテゴリーごとの街区になっています。

Quartiere molto caratteristico ed affascinante con i nomi delle attività che vi si svolgevano anticamente. Vicino c'è il Lago della Spada Restituita con il Tempio di Ncoc Son.Sempre in zona c'è la Hanoi Train Street che è strettissima ed abitata dove passano vari treni.Le strade che incrociano vengono chiuse con le transenne.
Great experience on a weekday but weekends there is a higher risk of death due to traffic levels.  Food walking tours are a great way to try new foods.  Coconut coffee is my new favorite

It was so fun walking around and just soaking in the culture here.  People watching and watching the shop and restaurant owners go about their day.  There are so many shops to stop into, restaurants to grab a bite, and gorgeous photos to be taken everywhere!  Give yourself time to really explore this area at your leisure.
Life happens on the sidewalks including grinding, welding, cooking and parking. Interesting to walk through and see.
We enjoyed being close to the old quarter. We felt park of the local experience, walking alongside the street vendors and bring close to the Lake.
It was a fun place to explore, see period buildings, mixed with modern life.  I am sure it is much like many other Old sections of cities around the world.

Personally I love wandering around these old colourful streets. It's not for the faint hearted; there's a lot of strange sights, sounds and smells, there is plenty of traffic to negotiate and there are few rules to crossing the busy roads, plus pavements are full with families eating and scooters everywhere, so sometimes you are stepping into the road. But if you like a city adventure ... this is a really good one. And don't forget your camera.
If you are staying in Hanoi make sure you stay in the old quater! It‘s really noisy and untidy but this is what makes hanoi to the city it is. Everything important is in the old quater.
Its a really busy place and to loud for me but a lot to see and shop for those who like it. Wouldnt go there again but if you like busy streets go ahead.

As part of a Wendy Wu tour we did both cyclo tour and a walking street food tour through this area. It was jam packed with people selling just about anything you can imagine. Quite overwhelming and intriguing at the same time.

Great place to spend an evening, watching the world, mostly on 2 wheels, rush by.  It's so busy, buzzing.  Lots of bars, street food, and people!Look up, we found a bar with a 'Northern Soul' light advertising on a rooftop - went up in the lift, a little oasis of calm, sitting outside watching the world down below - a real highlight.

Leuk om in dit gebied een hotelletje te boeken! Het leven speelt zich hier op straat af en is geweldig om te zien! Restaurantjes in overvloed, van duur tot goedkoop. Van keurig tot nou- dat - weet - ik - zo - net - niet....Winkels en souvenirs, alles is hier te koop.Gewoon een paar dagen om te beginnen!

Savourez l’ambiance, mangez-y, baladez-vous mais les vendeurs de faux sont très peu agréables..La négociation n’est pas toujours au rendez-vous malgré des prix bien au-dessus de la valeur et des produits d’une qualité médiocre. Ceci dit, il serait dommage de venir jusqu’ici pour acheter des produits fabriqués en Chine et vendus sans le moindre sourire.
A good distance to walk for an hour or so because there's a nice wooden bridge at the end. Stopped and had a coffee at lpatissier.
Came here for a little mooch around and it’s really impressive and very authentic. There’s lots of small shops, coffee shops and street food vendors. It’s definitely worth a visit

The old quarter is a great spot to visit - it has a lot of charm with a decent balance between old and new that does not disappoint. I will say it is very tourist-centered. Our local guide noted that it has become tourist oriented, so make sure to visit other spots on the map that might now be as known to get the real Ha Noi experience.

We were staying at La Siesta hotel on Ma May so we were able to spend a few days doing a few tours around Hanoi but could also walk out of the hotel in the evening and just walk around the Old Quarter and the lake ( 10 minutes from the hotel) . The streets are very busy , wear sandals or sneakers because you need to be agile and prepared to walk mostly on the street as the sidewalks are basically parking lots for motorbikes ! Take a Street-map with you ( the street names are all very similar) The traffic is amazing , just waves of motor bikes , we never saw a single accident ( in 2 weeks !) but the advice to “ walk slowly but steadily” is important . The near -accident we did see was a tourist who panicked and tried to stop in the middle of the street ......Recommend doing one of the street-walking tours, best way to see the markets and get some food -tasting experience.
In the old quarter we walked around in amazement.the traditional mystique of Ha Noi or the tacky tourist trap

The Old Quarter in Hanoi is the most visited places in Hanoi because this is where you can find practically anything and everything (shops, eateries, attractions, souvenirs, and what have you).  As a first-time tourist, you can truly enjoy Vietnam culture in this old and quaint part of Hanoi.  My favorite experience was drinking my first egg coffee which is very popular in this town. I must say, I am not disappointed at all!

To visit the old quarter of Hanoi is very interesting, especially if you want to feel the spirit of the Vietnamese, their way of life, crafts, friendliness. Amazing architecture, organization of urban life
Tight spaces with many many many things. Always bustling. Always energetic Lots to explore on foot. Though the footpath is very poorly designed and provisioned
Będąc w Hanoi oczywiście warto zobaczyć mimo ze mało starych domów. Tłumy. Sklepy, miejsca z jedzeniem i wszystko co się da
特に旧市街は信号が少ないので面喰いました。どのようにお互い譲って通行するのか？？？って感じでハラハラものです。ですが物価は安いし、人はやさしいし、印象は悪くありません。ホテルマンは非常に教育されていておもてなし感を出しています。とにかく、食べる、買う等の楽しみより雰囲気を味わうって感じで新旧まぜこぜで雑多で慣れれば非常に楽しいのではないでしょうか。

Le vieux quartier d'Hanoi peut se visiter à pied ou en cycle pousse.On s'habitue rapidement à la cohabitation avec les motos et les piétons.La vie des habitants se passent beaucoup dans la rue: on est surpris de ce qu'on peut découvrir et chaque rue est différente.Prendre un plan sur soi!

Hanoi old quarter is full of narrow lanes and motorcycles, so you have to be careful when walking around and on the road. Lots of food along the street, if you are brave enough, get some local food sitting on a small stool by the roadside. Lots of cafes, with good coffee, tea and smoothies. My favourite Avocado and Passionfruit. Different lanes sell different type of stuff. Shops close early by 9pm or 10pm, best time to walk, about 6-7pm onwards. A must when in Hanoi.
Quite a hectic place but if you're not in a hurry and can react peacefully, it's a nice place to spend time and have a beer in a cute little beer house.
The food is varied and delicious, but the amount of motorbikes is too messy and polluted. I especially like "bia hoi" in Hanoi.

Charming area where stores spill out onto the streets but that also meant we were forced to walk in the streets much of the time. Takes a couple days to get used to. So much life at the street level that it’s easy to miss the old merchant houses above.
Fun to see the street vendors, coffee shops, and stores. The traffic takes some getting used to when crossing the street. But you get a good feel for the city in this area.
Its very good place to visit this is the good place in hanoi vietnam Especially with the friends and couples
We walked around this area for two nights lots to draw your attention , cheap shopping, make sure you have room to take it home
A interesting old town sights seen unbelievable but something you must do while visiting hanoi but watch out for bikes

this is a frantic stimulating city-endless retail opportunities. NorthFace is very inexpensive here-bargaining is a must. Look for small streetside food spots with long lines and no menu-point and enjoy. The traffic and street crossing can be intimidating

Centrum van Hanoi in de oude wijk is een erg toeristisch ingestelde wijk. Veel hostels en hotelletjes en eetgelegenheden Erg druk door scooter verkeer . Streetfood  tour viel ons erg tegen Fietstour doe de wijk en rode rivier delta wad erg leuk en aan te raden.
Walking in this area, trying to cross streets was a test of nerves. We picked where to eat by who had the longest b lines and we were not disappointed

Wow, it really took great courage to cross the busy noisy streets here, fighting our way through a sea of motorcyclists:)  No looking back once you decide to cross.  Like reviewers said, they'd zig-zag around you and you'd be totally untouched!It was a real experience, walking along the colourful streets lined with street food stalls, people perched on low stools.  We enjoyed some street food like pho gia, ban cuon, goi cuon and nem, King Roti too.  As advised by some TA members, we went where locals go.  Taste of food was good everywhere.Shopping at Old Quarters could be challenging.  There're lots of fake branded goods which outwardly look like the real thing (internally it's a slightly different story).  The prices vary from store to store.  They could start with 700,000 dong and finally agree with 300,000 dong even.  It was fun haggling.It being Tet Festival, prices of food was higher.  Good thing it was not difficult to find food at all.  Most restaurants were open.If coming to Hanoi, Old Quarters is surely a must-visit.
Var i området i februar i tiden omkring det kinesiske nytår hvor næsten alting var lukket. det er et skønt område at besøge, og udbudet af restauranter er fantastisk.

We stayed in the old quarter in Hanoi.  We arrived at night and were a bit nervous of the area.  After walking around we realized it was safe and felt comfortable in the area.There were a lot of shops and restaurants in the area.  Our hotel was great.  We had a good stay!

After the initial shock of navigating crazy traffic to cross roads and a good nights sleep, it doesn’t take long to get your bearings and begin to explore this amazing area. There are so many different things to see & experience around each corner. Walking is the best way to explore but make sure you have a drink at the railway cafes when the train passes you by.

The people are mostly friendly. Peddlars and street vendors don't seem to like their photos taken. The younger generation is very friendly and genuinely helpful. Street vendors are a norm and add character and charm to Hanoi. The food is, in a nutshell, good. The egg coffee is a personal preference. It is an egg custard densely foamed up and served on topof black coffee. I love custard, so I loved it. If you love soupy noodles you will love pho, which is noodle soup with chicken or beef. The spring rolls are actually easy to make and are mostly filled with vegetables. I found them to be overpriced for something so simple. We had something called corn rice with chicken. Loved it. We also saw something that looked like tiny live eels. Turned out to be worms. Eeek... Being animal lovers, we were glad to hear that the local government is discouraging the consumption of dog meat. Didn't notice a single place serving it. I am enthralled by the fact that the city has a history of over a thousand years. You just feel the history walking through its streets. A feeling that we don't get back home, being a fairly young country and all. The Chinese, French, American, Japanese and communist influences are evident everywhere if one only takes a closer look.

We stayed in Hanoi for 5 days and visited the Old Quarter a number of times.  We found some amazing little cafes and places to eat.  The Night Markets are highly recommended.Be wary of pickpockets operating in pairs on scooters....as soon as someone shoves a map at your chest, their hands are underneath trying to grab wallets / phones.  We saw it happen twice in 2 days.

This is a colorful, lively neighborhood that does not seem particularly old, just crowded and noisy.    Shops, restaurants, food stalls are packed together cheek by jowl and it is difficult to walk because there are motorbikes parked all over the sidewalks, forcing you out into the traffic clogged streets.  There are some old coffee houses where you can sample egg coffee and a few places where you can actually try roast dog or sautéed snake or sea worms.  But there are more 'orthodox' restaurants as well.  Definitely worth a look or even two or three.

정말 많은 오토바이와 소형차가 섞여서 다니지만 사고는 없어 보이는... 많은 먼지와 매연으로 좋지 않은 공기에 목이 답답하지만 그래도 걸어 볼 만한... 한국 사람의 안전과 환경 의식으로는 상당히 위험스럽지만, 현지인의 친절하고 행복한 미소에 매력을 느끼는 곳이었습니다 (복잡하죠?~^^) 따이엔 맥주거리가 재미있었구요. 몇몇 숨은 길거리음식점 (반꾸온)은 가 볼만 했습니다. 그리고 마사지 전문점을 찾는 재미도 있습니다.참... 동쑤언시장을 비롯한 주말에 문을 여는 야시장은 복잡하기만 하고, 별로 살 것은 없다는 생각입니다. 참고하세요.

We spent quite a few hours walking around this atmospheric area - street food, lots of shops, tonnes of people and a stack of the infamous Hanoi motorcycles  (they don’t always stop at traffic lights and sometimes take shortcuts down pavements).Keep your eyes peeled with regards to the rest of the traffic and soak in the street atmosphere - it’s certainly an experience

Un intricato reticolo di vie piene zeppe di hotel, negozi, ristoranti di tutti i tipi dove è facilissimo perdersi (meglio avere il lago come punto di riferimento). Tutto qui è interessante, il centro è costituito dal lago dove gli abitanti di Hanoi vanno a correre o a praticare zumba o Tai Qui all'alba, per cercare di sfuggire al traffico infernale.
I would recommend getting a guide, its hard to find things on your own. The lake on Sunday is a must!
Visitar Hanoi y no hacer un tour con un cyclo en la parte vieja de la ciudad es perder 50% de la visita. Fue una experiencia barbara, no se la pierdan
旧市街の一部道路は、週末の夜になると自動車の通行が禁止となり、歩行者天国になります。そして露店が出現するので、この時間帯に散策されればお祭り気分が味わえると思います。
Interesting. Similar to what you can find in India or China.I would not touch anything in the markets; live animals, meat in the sun with flies on it
하노이 구시가는  수십 개의 작은 골목으로 이루어져 있어 36거리, 여행자 거리라고도 불립니다.  호안끼엠 호수 북쪽에 있으며 저렴한 호텔과 여행사들이 많아 배낭여행자들을 많이 만날 수 있습니다.낮에는 복잡한 거리에서 현지인들의 삶을 느낄 수 있고, 밤에는 맥주 거리, 야시장 등 활기가 가득한 곳으로  하노이에서 진짜 베트남을 경험할 수 있는 곳입니다.

Ce quartier vaut évidemment le détour. Mais il faut s'accrocher car quel tumulte incessant ! Mais c'est l'authenticité vietnamienne qu'on prend en pleine face et ça c'est génial ! Des échoppes de partout, un trafic de fous, une multitude de détails à observer ci et là et des incontournables comme le marché couvert ou la street food. Je pense que 2 ou 3 heures sont nécessaires.
Well worth setting aside the time to explore the historic area. Full of history and, of course, egg coffee.
There is a wide variety of shops and a lot of old buildings with beautiful architecture. Night markets are mostly food markets so don’t go there to shop!

オランダの4つの美術館のうち、クレラーミュラー程心に染み入る美術館はない。渡航前に上野でゴッホ展、クレラーミュラー美術館展を見て、初めてクレラーミュラーを知った。ボランティアでガイドをしている友人が３月に行く、交通の便が悪くてと嘆いていた。確かに悪そう、でもそれを引いても余りあるすばらしいところ。まず広さに驚く。入り口に館までのレンタサイクルが並んでいる。私はツアーのバスで行ったけれど、この自転車もいいなと思った。庭は紅葉の季節がすばらしいそうだ。箱根の彫刻の森をもっとスケールを広げ庭にも彫刻が点在する。美術館は日本語対応のオーディオガイドで聞くことができる。全てではないけれど、かえってうるさくなくていい。ただ惜しむらくは日本の美術展のように作品名と作者のリストがあればよい。平日のせいか静かな管内でこれでもかと言うほどゴッホの作品、ピカソや他の人のも勿論あるけれど、堪能することができた。何より今は亡きクレラーミュラーの精神を引き継いで守られているのは伝わるのが嬉しい。今度は秋に尋ねたいと思う。

I enjoy the hustle and bustle for a few days. SO much great food and a couple nice craft breweries in the area. I recommend people see a place like this at least once in their lives. However, I know it's not for everyone.

アジアのパワーを存分に堪能できる。ハノイ滞在者の多くがこの近辺に宿を取ることになると思うが、とにかく楽しい。すべてのものが充実しており、飲食にも買い物にも困らない。ストリートごとに専門の問屋が並んでいて、ある通りはおもちゃ、ある通りはバッグ、ある通りは衣類と、同業者が軒を連ねているのが特徴。見て歩くだけでも楽しくてたまらない。あまり押し売りしてこないところも歓迎できる。目抜き通りでは週末になるとナイトマーケットが開かれ、数百ｍにわたって露店が並び、バックパッカーや現地の人が繰り出して大変な混雑になる。週末に滞在できるなら、出かけて損は絶対にない。土産物に最適なグッズから素性の怪しげなものまで摩訶不思議なものがいっぱい売っている。売り子の人たちと片言の英語で会話を楽しむのもいい。23時ごろまでは賑やかなので、近くで食事をした後に寄ってみるといいだろう。

A truly amazing neighbourhood.  We stayed here, amongst the hustle and bustle, and loved every minute of it.  Be prepared for:  horns, thousands of bikes, cars rushing past you, footpaths crowded with bikes, streetfood, shopwares, people, smells, sights and sounds.  There are some really cute little shops with wonderful things to take home for youself and family, great coffee can be found everywhere, food is on every corner, and also the most magnificent flowers in roadside florists.Walking the streets can be a little hair raising, as the footpaths are virtually impossible to walk on so you do have to share the road as a pedestrian, dodging bikes, people and cars.  But, you do soon get used to it, and find ways to more confidently cross the streets.  The night markets on the weekends are great to visit (and chaotic of course).  One of the best experiences we had was doing a one hour cyclo tour through the area - it was lovely because you didn't have to concentrate on where you were walking, and could just enjoy the beautiful architecutre and sights.I don't think you have experience Hanoi unless you have spent time exploring the Old Quarter - highly recommended.
I stayed in the Old Quarter in Hanoi. It is very interesting to see the streets dedicated to selling one specialty. I hope you have a good sense of direction because it is easy to get turned around.

Lots to see, plus numerous eating places to check out.  Our favorite is King Roti - the buns are fresh and to die for!  Bun Cha Ta is a good place for bun cha; Pho 20; Banh Mi 25 etc.  Cheap and delicious!

Be ready for an adventure.You have to be prepared for the most buoyant city in Vietnam, where everybody is busy, fast on their feet even though they have arrived at the crack of dawn (or earlier).  You will understand that this is a very Vietnamese way of living, the street is the extension of their home and every millimeters count.  It is colourful, quite busy looking, but if you ask they sure have it in the back of their shop ;-).   the smells of the food is hypnotizing and even if you are not hungry you will be compelled to have it, and yet no regret afterwards.  Enjoy but be aware that scooters are everywhere....
