We are a family of 4 (daughters 9 years and 17 years of age). We were in Hanoi for 1 day and 1 night only. And we spend the whole time in Old Quarter area. So, frankly we just know 2 things in Hanoi - the airport and Old Quarter area!Ok, there are thousands of reviews of Old Quarter. I want to try and make the review different. I will only give information from our experience which can be helpful to other readers. So, here goes (in no particular order):- Hanoi in December is cold, so please carry adequate warm clothes specially if you are out for walking tours or street food. It rains too.- Watch out for the huge number of motor bikes being ridden in the narrow roads of Old Quarter. You have to be careful while crossing the roads. The trick is not to stop in the middle no matter what happens and to maintain a steady pace while crossing. That way the motor bike riders can predict what you are doing and can make their way around you. - Old Quarter also has the famous Beer Street. This is a street with full of pubs serving different kinds of beer. Frankly we didn't experience it. We saw it because it was right next to Essence Hanoi Hotel and Spa (where we had lunch).- Old Quarter has many souvenir shops with good pricing. We found that the rates are the best here compared to souvenir shops elsewhere in Hanoi, Da Nang or Ho Chi Minh City. So, if you want to buy souvenir to take back with you, please buy here even if it is at the beginning of your trip. We made a mistake thinking we will get better stuff elsewhere.- It is advisable to carry VND (popularly called the Dong) currency here. Though many will tell you that USD is accepted in Vietnam, I would advise that you carry VND specially if you want to buy stuff from small shops. Also, many small cafe's accept only cash. - If you are taking a cruise of Halong Bay from Hanoi, let me tell you that most cruise companies provide pick up from Old Quarter area (in this regard check out my review of Unicharm Cruise). So, advisable to stay in a hotel in Old Quarter.- Old Quarter is at least 45 minutes away from the airport by Grab (e-hailing cab service). So, plan your movement from and to the airport accordingly. Keep a 1 hour margin if you are catching a flight so that you don't get a heart attack while going to the airport!- Grab cabs (e-hailing cab service) are easily available in this area and maximum waiting time will be 4-5 minutes for the car to come. In Vietnam, car number plates don't have alphabets. They only have numbers. So, checking for the cab which comes to pick you up becomes an activity which you have to focus on!- Vietnam has left hand drive, which means cars travel on the right side of the road. The first time I sat on a Grab cab, something funny happened. I went to the left front side of the car and opened the door and to my utter surprise saw the driver sitting. The driver also got a big surprise from me!!!- For Hanoi, I have done reviews of the following - 1. Peridot Grand Hotel under Essence Hanoi Hotel and Spa. 2. Essence Restaurant. 3. Unicharm Cruise. 4. Hanoi Old Quarter Walking Tour (in this review I have given details of places of interest visited in Old Quarter like Long Bien Bridge, Dong Xuan Market, Hang Ma Street, Phung Hung Street Art and Hoan Kiem Lake). 5. Ancient House. 6. Diamond Sky Bar and 7. Old Quarter (this review). You can get more information about Hanoi and Vietnam from here.- There is a alcohol retail shop next to Essence Hanoi Hotel and Spa. If you have your back towards the hotel, then walk to your right and on the left side down the road after few shops you will come across a very well decorated alcohol retail shop (am posting a photograph of the same).- Watch out for many local tea/coffee shops with interesting low level tables and chairs (see my picture on the same). You will see many Vietnamese local people sitting and having a coffee, and/or a smoke.- Watch out for interesting rickshaw rides in Old Quarter. In these rickshaws, the passenger sits in front and the rider is riding from behind. - We noticed lots of 7-11 kinds of convenience store chain named Circle K.- The famous Thong Long Water Puppet show is also located in Old Quarter area right next to the Hoan Kiem Lake.- Many areas in Old Quarter during the weekend gets shut for vehicles. You can only walk there. This is great for tourists who want to walk around Old Quarter area. - Must try the famous egg coffee of Vietnam here as there are so many beautiful cafe's all over the place.- You can expect people here to be honest, clever and down to earth. Almost all people we came across in the service industry are excellent in service standards. The best I have come across. They genuinely care for you. More so they will do things out of their job requirement. That's why you will see many reviews of Vietnam in TA will have names of people giving service. And most people here have a great sense of humor. And they are confident people which is great considering they have always been plundered and colonized by the western people.- People in the service industry understand English and can speak English even if not fluently. But, many people on the roads don't know how to speak in English. But, overall for tourists, language is not a problem in Vietnam if you communicate in English.- Food and drinks are cheap overall. So, in Vietnam you can really indulge. A top end dinner with cocktails for family of 4 will not cost more than USD 75. In VND terms (or Dong) this would be mean 1.7 million. So, in Vietnam you can easily have a VND 1 million meal. Therefore, you can easily be a millionaire here!!! That's great fun.- For our Vietnam trip we visited Hanoi, Da Nang and then Ho Chi Minh City. In terms of activities we really enjoyed the following - Halong Bay Cruise, Da Nang and from there Hoi An and Ba Na Hills. Also My Khe beach in Da Nang is a beautiful beach.You will also find my reviews for hotels, restaurants and activities in Da Nang and Ho Chi Minh City.

We had a private tour of Hanoi around the key places to visit, and had an absolutely wonderful time. With a visit to Ho Chi Minh's stilted house and also the One Pillar Pagoda. We also managed an inside visit of St Joseph's Cathedral.  The following morning we experienced a cyclo ride through empty streets (5.30am) and what a different view of Hanoi.  We saw the flag raising at the Mausoleum and then experienced local Pho noodle breakfast - not to be missed. And our final stop was the Hoan Kiem Lake and the Ngoc Son Temple. Stepping into Hanoi's Old Quarter is like entering a living museum of Vietnamese heritage. The atmosphere becomes electric, yet intimate, offering a deeper connection to the rhythm of local life.For those visiting over the weekend (Friday through Sunday), the night market is a must-experience. Stretching through the main thoroughfares, it’s a delightful blend of food stalls, local crafts, and live performances. More than just a shopping experience, it’s a lively, authentic way to engage with Hanoi's residents and soak in the spirit of Vietnamese culture.

If you're in Hanoi on a weekend night, the **Old Quarter** becomes an unforgettable experience—a vibrant, chaotic, and deeply atmospheric immersion into Vietnamese urban life.### 🌃 AtmosphereAs night falls, the Old Quarter transforms. What by day is already a busy maze of narrow streets turns into a carnival of colors, sounds, and smells. Streets are closed to vehicles from Friday to Sunday night, allowing crowds to spill into the roads. The air buzzes with live music, laughter, and the clang of street vendors preparing food. Bright lights from lanterns, beer stalls, and souvenir shops give the place a magical, almost surreal energy.### 🍜 Street Food & DrinksThis is a paradise for food lovers. You'll find everything from steaming bowls of *pho* and sizzling *banh xeo* to exotic snacks like grilled quail eggs and sweet sticky rice. Plastic stools crowd the sidewalks where locals and tourists sit elbow-to-elbow drinking *bia hoi* (fresh beer) for less than a dollar. For those feeling adventurous, the food tour possibilities are endless—and delicious.### 🎶 Entertainment & CultureThere’s live music in the street, both traditional and contemporary. Impromptu dance circles form around performers playing Vietnamese instruments or acoustic guitars. The weekend night market, stretching from Hang Dao Street to Dong Xuan Market, offers clothes, handicrafts, and quirky souvenirs, often at bargain prices.### 🧑‍🤝‍🧑 Crowd & VibeExpect crowds—lots of them. It’s loud, energetic, and sometimes overwhelming, but that’s part of the charm. You’ll see families, young locals, travelers, and backpackers all mixing in one pulsating cultural cocktail. While it’s generally safe, watch your belongings and avoid scams, especially around bar areas.### 💬 Final ThoughtsA weekend night in Hanoi’s Old Quarter isn’t just an activity—it’s an **experience**. Whether you’re sipping beer on a low stool, listening to a street band, or weaving through the market crowds, it offers a rare blend of sensory overload and authentic cultural engagement. Not for the faint of heart, but absolutely essential for anyone wanting to feel the true heartbeat of Hanoi.**Rating: 9/10**(Best enjoyed with open senses and a flexible schedule.)

But it’s also friendly, authentic, great value, colourful and buzzing. The old quarter is a compact area, but other interesting sites - West Lake, the French Quarter, Ho Chi Min’s mausoleum are all within walking distance, or a low cost Grab - so it makes a good base. There are lots of good places to eat, with decent meals being available for less than £10/head. Or go for a blowout on one of the rooftop bars, where you may spend £30-40/ head. There are plenty of bars with beer around £1 a pint. You can pay less in the very congested bia street, but this is also the one part of the area that feels a bit sleazy and where you might get hustled to buy things. At night stroll over to train street and join the crowd flirting with disaster.

Situated at the northern end of Hoan Kiem Lake, the Old Quarter is a must-see destination for tourists.  Chaos abounds with motor bikes and scooters parked over the footpaths and whizzing along the streets with gay abandon.  They do tend to avoid running into people so the trick to crossing a road is to look straight ahead and walk at a steady pace from one side to the other.  Don't try to dodge them - they'll dodge you.Apart from the traffic (and the large number of people) there is lots to see.  Small shops abound with many of the streets specialising in particular goods whether that be silk or paper/decorations or shoes or whatever.  There is no grid pattern in the streets so it’s easy to get disoriented and lost.  That’s part of the adventure, of course.A great place to visit.

Phố cổ Hà Nội là trái tim văn hóa và lịch sử của Thủ đô, nơi lưu giữ vẻ đẹp cổ kính và nhịp sống sôi động. Du khách có thể thưởng thức ẩm thực đường phố nổi tiếng như phở, bún chả, hay nhâm nhi cà phê trứng. Phố cổ Hà Nội không chỉ là điểm tham quan mà còn là nơi cảm nhận rõ nét nhất linh hồn Hà Nội.

Visiting Old Quarter is a must  it is lively noisy and full of character Yes the motorbikes are annoying and you cannot walk on footpaths as they seem to be the motorbike parking area but it shows the real Hanoi and you soon get used to it waving your hands in an effort to cross the road in a land where traffic lights and zebra crossings are merely artefacts added to street corners with no instructions provided on how they are to be usedNight market provides a wonderful array of goods and crafts which you can haggle for experience street food and meet some wonderful Vietnamese peopleYou can take a break and get refreshments and view the madness that is Old Quarter from a balconyIn my view it is a must

The 36 ancient streets that make up Hanoi’s Old Quarter are a maze of chaos and treasures. The streets are narrow and so are the houses, but that doesn’t stop the constant crush of motorbikes and, in an increasing number, cars mixing with bicycles, tourists and rickshaws known as cyclos. Despite the craziness, the Old Quarter should not be missed. Its lanes overflow with everything you need to fill your suitcase back home, from colourful kitschy trinkets to Vietnamese crafts to fine silks to hardwares and more. To fully explore the Old Quarter in Hanoi, prepare your feet for a day of walking street to street and taking in the locals’ daily life amidst these old-style narrow streets and houses. And if you have spare change, dining on these streets is a culinary requisite. more than just a tempting and adventurous bid – it’s actually required for a complete culinary excursion in Hanoi. It doesn’t take much effort to trace down an absolutely great bowl of humble Pho at ridiculously low prices.
Absolutely a must. Colourful, interesting, fun with lots of restaurants, small shops, little fruit markets and massage parlours. Some French Arquitecture.

Evaluation of the Tour Plan: "Hanoi 6 Hours: One Journey, A Thousand Experiences"OverviewThe proposed tour plan for a 6-hour exploration of Hanoi is well-structured, offering a blend of cultural, historical, and culinary experiences. The itinerary is designed to provide tourists with a comprehensive view of the city, showcasing its rich heritage and vibrant lifestyle.StrengthsDiverse Experiences: The tour effectively combines various aspects of Hanoi, including traditional transportation (cyclo), historical sites (Temple of Literature, O Quan Chuong), and local cuisine (egg coffee, Bun Cha). This diversity caters to different interests and enhances the overall experience.Cultural and Historical Significance: Each stop on the itinerary is carefully chosen for its cultural and historical importance. For instance, the Temple of Literature is not only a beautiful site but also a significant educational landmark in Vietnam's history.Culinary Highlights: The inclusion of local delicacies, such as egg coffee and Bun Cha, provides tourists with an authentic taste of Hanoi's culinary scene. This aspect is crucial for a well-rounded travel experience.Scenic Locations: The tour includes visits to picturesque locations like West Lake and Tran Quoc Pagoda, allowing tourists to enjoy the natural beauty of Hanoi while learning about its history.Estimated Costs: The breakdown of estimated costs for entrance fees, transportation, and meals is practical and helps potential tourists budget their trip effectively. Offering both budget and mid-range options makes the tour accessible to a wider audience.Practical Tips: The recommendations regarding the best time to visit, essentials to bring, and appropriate clothing are valuable for tourists, enhancing their overall experience and comfort.Areas for ImprovementTime Management: While the itinerary is packed with activities, the time allocated for each stop may be tight, especially considering travel time between locations. It might be beneficial to allow for more flexibility or to prioritize certain experiences.Transportation Details: While the plan mentions various transportation options, providing more specific recommendations or arrangements for tourists (e.g., pre-booking cyclo rides or taxis) could enhance convenience.Cultural Etiquette: Including a brief section on cultural etiquette, especially regarding dress codes and behavior at religious sites, would be beneficial for tourists unfamiliar with local customs.Interactive Elements: Incorporating interactive elements, such as a brief workshop on traditional crafts or a cooking demonstration, could enrich the experience and engage tourists more deeply.Sustainability Considerations: Addressing sustainability in tourism practices, such as encouraging the use of eco-friendly transportation or supporting local artisans, could enhance the tour's appeal to environmentally conscious travelers.Overall, the "Hanoi 6 Hours: One Journey, A Thousand Experiences" tour plan is a well-thought-out itinerary that effectively showcases the cultural, historical, and culinary richness of Hanoi. With minor adjustments to time management, transportation logistics, and the inclusion of cultural etiquette, this tour could provide an even more enriching experience for visitors. The combination of diverse activities and practical information makes it a strong offering for tourists looking to explore Hanoi in a limited timeframe.

Review: A Remarkable 8-Hour Journey Through HanoiRating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4.8/5)What I Loved:This tour exceeded my expectations and offered a seamless way to explore Hanoi’s rich history and culture in just one day. The breakfast at Bun Cha Huong Lien was a fantastic start – the flavors were authentic, and the connection to President Obama’s visit added a special touch.The itinerary was well-crafted, covering Hanoi’s most iconic landmarks, such as Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and the serene One Pillar Pagoda, both of which left a deep impression. The Temple of Literature stood out with its tranquil gardens and beautiful architecture, while the Hoa Lo Prison visit was an emotionally impactful highlight, offering a profound glimpse into Vietnam’s past.The cyclo ride through the Old Quarter added a touch of charm and authenticity. It was wonderful to slow down and absorb the vibrant streets and unique energy of the city. The guide was knowledgeable and friendly, making the experience all the more enriching.Minor Improvements:While the pacing was generally good, I felt a little rushed at the Temple of Literature, where more time to explore would have been ideal. The balance of historical landmarks, cultural immersion, and local flavors was spot on. A few minor tweaks could make it absolutely flawless, but as it stands, it’s a must-try experience!

Are you ready to immerse yourself in the heart of Vietnam’s culture, history, and flavors in just one day? This 8-hour tour of Hanoi is a must for travelers seeking a perfect blend of adventure, history, and culinary delights. Here's my experience:Morning BeginningsWe started the day with a steaming bowl of Phở at Phở Thìn—an unforgettable taste of Hanoi’s culinary soul. Then, visiting the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum was a humbling experience, allowing us to pay respects to one of Vietnam’s most revered leaders. The grandiose structure and serene gardens set a reflective tone for the morning.Mid-Morning ExplorationThe Temple of Literature was my next favorite stop—a tranquil oasis showcasing Vietnam’s devotion to education and Confucian values. I even tried my hand at traditional calligraphy! This serene escape in the middle of the bustling city was both peaceful and inspiring.Afternoon AdventuresDong Xuan Market was an explosion of energy, colors, and aromas. Bargaining for trinkets and sampling street foods like bánh cuốn and chè was so much fun! The highlight, however, was bún đậu mắm tôm—a dish packed with bold and distinctive flavors.The Imperial Citadel of Thăng Long was another highlight. Walking through this UNESCO World Heritage Site felt like stepping back in time. The history here is tangible—from ancient relics to the underground command center used during the war.The Grand FinaleWe ended the day with coffee at Hanoi Train Street. Watching the train pass by as I sipped on cà phê trứng (egg coffee) was surreal! It perfectly captured Hanoi's unique blend of tradition and modern charm.Why You Should GoThis itinerary is packed with Hanoi’s top highlights and hidden gems, offering a well-rounded experience of the city. The balance of cultural landmarks, delicious food, and unique experiences (like the cyclo ride through the Old Quarter) makes it perfect for first-time visitors.With costs ranging from $20 to $40, it’s affordable, flexible, and unforgettable. Don’t miss this opportunity to see Hanoi at its best—you won’t regret it!Have you been to Hanoi? Share your experiences below! 🌟

Hanoi, the thousand-year-old capital of Vietnam, is a blend of tradition and modernity. The city stands out with its bustling Old Quarter, the serene Hoan Kiem Lake, and historical landmarks like the Temple of Literature and the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum. Additionally, Hanoi attracts visitors with its diverse cuisine, featuring dishes like pho, bun cha, and the unique egg coffee. The city's atmosphere exudes rich culture, combining a leisurely pace of life with vibrant energy, creating a charm that's uniquely its own.This one-day Hanoi itinerary is ideal for those looking to explore the city's highlights in a short amount of time. 07:30 – 08:00: Breakfast at Banh Mi B+The day starts perfectly with a banh mi from Banh Mi B+ (71 Hang Non). This spot is famous for its crispy baguettes filled with flavorful and meticulously prepared ingredients in true Vietnamese style. It's a delicious and convenient meal to fuel your journey.08:30 – 09:30: Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum & MuseumThe Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum is a must-visit destination in Hanoi, where you can learn about the life and legacy of Vietnam’s great leader. The serene and spacious surroundings, coupled with the Ho Chi Minh Museum, provide deeper insights into the nation’s history.09:45 – 10:15: Temple of LiteratureIn just 30 minutes, you can marvel at the Temple of Literature, an iconic symbol of Vietnam’s tradition of learning. It exudes a sense of timelessness and preserves the cultural heritage of the nation.10:30 – 11:00: Hanoi CathedralThe St. Joseph's Cathedral, with its unique Gothic architecture, is a quick yet worthwhile stop. It's an ideal place for photography and enjoying a moment of peace in the bustling city.11:30 – 12:15: LunchThis is the perfect time to indulge in Hanoi’s culinary delights. Although the location is not specified, options like pho, bun cha, or fried spring rolls are always a satisfying choice.12:30 – 13:15: Hoan Kiem Lake and Ngoc Son TempleHoan Kiem Lake is another iconic symbol of Hanoi, offering a serene setting and picturesque views. The Ngoc Son Temple provides cultural and historical significance, making this stop both peaceful and educational.13:30 – 14:30: Hanoi Old QuarterThe Old Quarter is the perfect place to experience the daily life of Hanoians. Its narrow streets and bustling markets offer an authentic glimpse into local culture and traditions.14:30 – 15:30: Cafe Giang – End of TourThe journey concludes beautifully at Cafe Giang (39 Nguyen Huu Huan) with a taste of their legendary egg coffee. This unique drink is not only a culinary highlight but also a memorable experience to end the day. This trip offers a well-rounded experience, combining the exploration of Hanoi's history, culture, and distinctive cuisine. I can delve into Vietnam’s traditions and history through landmarks such as the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, the Temple of Literature, and Ngoc Son Temple; immerse themselves in the vibrant energy of the Old Quarter and Hoan Kiem Lake; and savor iconic dishes like banh mi, pho, and egg coffee. Moments of relaxation in green spaces and the blend of modernity with tradition create an unforgettable journey, offering a comprehensive and profound perspective of the thousand-year-old capital.

This six-hour tour focuses on Hanoi’s rich cultural and historical heritage, blending iconic landmarks, authentic local cuisine, and immersive experiences.Hanoi, the heart of Vietnam, gave me an unforgettable experience in just one short day. From starting the journey at Thong Nhat Park to ending it at St. Joseph's Cathedral, I felt a perfect blend of history, culture, and the vibrant modern life of this city.Timeline:8h15- 8h30 Gather at Thong Nhat Park and move to the first location9h00-10h00 Visit Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum10h00- 11h00 Move to Thang Long Imperial Citadel11h00- 12h30 Lunch and rest12h30- 13h00 Visit Hoan Kiem Lake13h00-14h30 Visit and explore the Old Quarter14h30- 15h00 Visit the Cathedral and end.The Day’s JourneyMorning:We gathered at Thong Nhat Park at 8:15 AM. The morning air was refreshing, filled with birdsong and the rhythmic sounds of people exercising. Soon after, we moved to the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, where I felt a deep sense of reverence and national pride. Remember to dress modestly when visiting this sacred site.Next, we explored the Thang Long Imperial Citadel, which felt like stepping into a giant history book. The ancient architecture and serene atmosphere made me forget I was in such a bustling city.Midday:After our explorations, we rested and enjoyed lunch. A steaming bowl of Hanoi pho and a glass of iced tea were just what I needed to recharge for the afternoon.Afternoon:Hoan Kiem Lake and its surroundings were our next stops. Seeing the red Huc Bridge standing out against the blue-green water was a breathtaking sight. Then came the Old Quarter, a lively maze filled with artisan shops, traditional eateries, and the energetic buzz of Hanoi’s streets.Finally, we visited St. Joseph's Cathedral, where the classic Gothic architecture amidst a modern city provided a perfect conclusion to the day.Tips for Traveling in HanoiTransportation:Wear comfortable walking shoes because you’ll be doing a lot of walking.Use ride-hailing apps or taxis to save time when traveling between destinations.Weather:If visiting in summer, bring a hat, sunglasses, and water to stay cool.In winter, pack a light jacket as mornings and evenings can get chilly.Food:Don’t miss iconic dishes like pho, bun cha, banh cuon, and egg coffee.Try eateries tucked away in small alleys—they often have unique and authentic flavors.Sightseeing:For sacred sites like the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and the Temple of Literature, dress modestly.Avoid visiting the Old Quarter during rush hours to steer clear of crowds.Shopping:When shopping in the Old Quarter, be ready to bargain for reasonable prices.Hanoi is truly a city worth exploring, and I can’t wait to return to discover even more of its wonderful charm!

A Perfect One-Day Tour in Hanoi: History, Culture, and Unique ExperiencesIf you only have one day in Hanoi, this itinerary is perfect for immersing yourself in the city's rich history, vibrant culture, and iconic landmarks. From historical sites to unique local experiences, here's how I spent a memorable day in Vietnam's capital:Morning: A Glimpse Into Vietnam’s Past- Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and One Pillar PagodaMy day began at the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, an iconic symbol of Hanoi and Vietnam. The solemn atmosphere and grandeur of the site were deeply moving, offering a glimpse into the life of one of Vietnam's greatest leaders. Just a short walk away is the One Pillar Pagoda, a small but beautiful structure steeped in Buddhist symbolism and history.- Temple of Literature (Văn Miếu - Quốc Tử Giám)Next, I visited the Temple of Literature, Vietnam’s first university. The peaceful courtyards and ancient architecture made this an inspiring stop, showcasing the importance of education and Confucian values in Vietnamese culture.Afternoon: Learning and Savoring Hanoi’s Delicacies- Hoa Lo Prison (Maison Centrale)Hoa Lo Prison Museum offered a sobering insight into Vietnam's colonial history and the resilience of its people during difficult times. The exhibits are well-curated, providing a balance of historical context and personal stories.- Lunch with Hanoi’s DelicaciesFor lunch, I enjoyed a bowl of bun cha, accompanied by nem cua be (crab spring rolls). The combination of savory grilled pork, fresh herbs, and sweet-sour dipping sauce was an explosion of flavors. I highly recommend trying this dish when in Hanoi!Traditional Arts and Unique Cafes- Thang Long Water Puppet TheatreThe evening began with a mesmerizing water puppet show at the Thang Long Water Puppet Theatre. The performance, accompanied by live traditional music, was entertaining and gave insight into Vietnam’s rural life and folklore.- Train Street CafeMy last stop was Hanoi’s Train Street, a narrow alley where trains pass just inches away from local houses and cafes. Sipping a traditional Vietnamese egg coffee here while watching the train pass by was an unforgettable experience, blending thrill and tranquility in a way only Hanoi can offer.Overall ExperienceThis one-day itinerary was a perfect introduction to Hanoi’s culture and history. Each stop was thoughtfully planned, and the experiences were diverse yet cohesive. Whether you’re a history buff, a foodie, or just looking for something unique, Hanoi has something for everyone.Would I recommend this tour? Absolutely! Hanoi left a lasting impression on me, and I hope to return one day to explore even more of this charming city.

We wandered through the streets, checking out all the quaint shops. Some were cool, some were... not so much. You can feel the rich history, though, with all the old buildings. We stopped at a few bustling cafes, which were pretty fun. It's a bit chaotic, but that's part of the charm, I guess.

Hanoi: 8-Hour Journey: Breathe in Hanoi Touch its SoulI recently had the opportunity to experience an incredible afternoon and evening in Hanoi, and I’d love to share my journey on TripAdvisor. The itinerary lasted 8 hours, starting in the afternoon and ending late at night. This schedule is perfect for those with limited time who still want to fully immerse themselves in the charm of Vietnam's capital.1:00 PM - Hoa Lo PrisonThe afternoon began with a visit to Hoa Lo Prison, a historical landmark that is a must-see. The space vividly recreates stories of patriotism and great sacrifices during the resistance periods. What impressed me was the discount policy: 50% off for students and senior citizens, free entry for children under 15 and war veterans, making the experience even more meaningful.3:00 PM - Hoan Kiem Lake Walking StreetAfter visiting Hoa Lo Prison, I moved to Hoan Kiem Lake Walking Street, which is about 3 km away and takes around 30 minutes to get to. This was the perfect time to explore Hanoi’s street food. Snacks like crispy bánh gối (fried dumplings), sweet bánh rán mật (glutinous rice doughnuts), warm bánh đúc (savory rice cake), bánh trôi tàu (sticky rice dumplings in ginger syrup), and the iconic Tràng Tiền ice cream left a lasting impression. Don’t miss trying egg coffee – a unique specialty of Hanoi!4:00 PM - St. Joseph’s CathedralThe next stop was St. Joseph’s Cathedral, only about 2 km from Hoan Kiem Lake. With its ancient Gothic architecture and serene atmosphere, this spot offers a sense of calm amid the bustling streets. It’s also a fantastic place for photography or simply taking a rest.6:00 PM - Dinner at Bếp Prime or Nearby RestaurantsDinner was a relaxing time after a busy afternoon. I chose Bếp Prime, located 5 km from the cathedral (15 minutes by car), to enjoy delicious dishes such as coconut shrimp salad, grilled goby fish with chili salt, and pigeon sticky rice. If you want to try other local specialties, nearby restaurants offer dishes like chả cá Lã Vọng (grilled fish with turmeric and dill), fried spring rolls, and crab hotpot.7:00 PM - Night Tour at Thang Long Imperial CitadelAs night fell, I joined a nighttime tour of the Thang Long Imperial Citadel, about 30 minutes from the dinner spot. With shimmering lights and vivid historical storytelling, this journey into the past was truly magical. The night tour includes great discounts: 50% off for children under 12 and free for those under 5, making it ideal for families.9:00 PM - Flag-Lowering Ceremony at Ho Chi Minh MausoleumThe itinerary concluded with the solemn flag-lowering ceremony at Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, 2.5 km from the Imperial Citadel. This special moment evoked a deep sense of national pride and respect for the values left by President Ho Chi Minh.ConclusionAn afternoon-to-evening journey in Hanoi is not only exciting but also deeply meaningful. Combining historical exploration, culinary experiences, and cultural discoveries, this is an itinerary I highly recommend for anyone looking to make the most of their time in the capital.

I just had the opportunity to experience a wonderful evening in Hanoi, and wanted to share my itinerary on TripAdvisor. The schedule lasts 8 hours, starting in the afternoon and ending in the late evening. This is a suitable itinerary for those who have little time but still want to fully experience the beauty of the capital.1:00 - Hoa Lo PrisonThe afternoon begins with a visit to Hoa Lo Prison - a historical landmark not to be missed. The space here vividly recreates stories about patriotism and noble sacrifice during the resistance period. What's impressive is the 50% discount policy for students and seniors, free for children under 15 years old and veterans, making this experience even more meaningful.3:00 - Hoan Kiem Lake Walking StreetAfter visiting Hoa Lo Prison, I moved to Hoan Kiem Lake walking street. This is the ideal time to explore street food. Snacks like crispy pillow cakes, sweet and fragrant honey donuts, hot Duc cake, Chinese cake, and Trang Tien ice cream really impressed me. Don't forget to try egg coffee - a unique specialty only available in Hanoi.4:00 - Hanoi CathedralThe next stop in the evening is Hanoi Cathedral. With ancient Gothic architecture and peaceful space, this place brings relaxation in the heart of bustling streets. This is also a great place to take photos or simply stop to rest.6:00 - Dinner at Prime KitchenDinner is a time to relax after an afternoon of walking. I chose Bep Prime restaurant to enjoy delicious dishes such as coconut shrimp salad, grilled goby fish with salt and chili, and pigeon sticky rice. If you want to try other specialty dishes, you can visit the surrounding restaurants to enjoy La Vong fish cakes, fried spring rolls or crab hotpot.7:00 - Thang Long Imperial Citadel night tourWhen night fell, I joined the night tour at Thang Long Imperial Citadel. With shimmering lights and vividly told historical stories, this is truly a magical journey back into the past. The night tour has attractive incentives: 50% discount for children under 12 years old and free for children under 5 years old, very suitable for families.9:00 - Flag lowering ceremony at Uncle Ho's MausoleumThe journey ends with a solemn flag-lowering ceremony at Uncle Ho's Mausoleum. This is a special moment, evoking national pride and respect for the values ​​left by Uncle Ho.This afternoon-to-evening itinerary in Hanoi was not only enjoyable but also deeply meaningful. Combining historical exploration, culinary delights, and cultural experiences, it’s a schedule I highly recommend to anyone looking to make the most of their time in the capital.

If you want to truly experience the soul of Hanoi, this day-long itinerary has everything you need! From serene lakes and ancient temples to bustling streets and mouthwatering local dishes, every stop offers something unique.Morning Highlights:Start your day at Hoan Kiem Lake and Ngoc Son Temple. The legend of the Sword Lake will transport you back in time, and the iconic red Huc Bridge is a perfect spot for photos. Want a touch of local life? A cyclo ride around the streets is both fun and relaxing.Next, we visited the Temple of Literature, Vietnam's first university. The stone steles and ancient architecture are awe-inspiring, making this a must-see for anyone interested in history and culture.Historical Wonders:The Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum is an emotional journey into Vietnam’s past, and the One Pillar Pagoda showcases stunning Buddhist architecture. Both sites are perfect for those looking to learn more about Vietnam's roots.Lunchtime Delight:Hanoi’s food is legendary, and Cha Ca Thang Long did not disappoint. Their grilled fish with vermicelli (Cha Ca La Vong) was the highlight of our culinary adventure—don’t miss it!Afternoon Adventures:We then explored the chilling history of the Hoa Lo Prison, a site that will give you deep insights into Vietnam's revolutionary struggle. The Thang Long Imperial Citadel, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, was a stunning dive into the grandeur of Vietnam's feudal era.A Sweet Ending:The day ended at St. Joseph's Cathedral, where we sipped coffee and enjoyed sticky rice while soaking in the peaceful Gothic beauty of the church. It was the perfect conclusion to a perfect day.Tips for Visitors:Dress modestly for the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum.Take plenty of photos at the Temple of Literature and St. Joseph's Cathedral.Don’t rush—every location has a story worth exploring.Costs:Entrance fees for all locations are reasonable, averaging around 320,000–370,000 VND If you’re up for a panoramic view of the city, hop on a double-decker bus for 450,000 VND—it’s worth it!If you’re visiting Hanoi, this itinerary is a fantastic way to explore the city’s charm in just one day. I can’t recommend it enough!

The Old Quarter in Hanoi, Vietnam, is a vibrant and historic area known for its bustling streets, rich culture, and unique architecture. It’s often referred to as the “36 Streets” because each street historically specialized in a particular trade or craft  Here are some highlights you might enjoy:  Hoan Kiem Lake: Located in the heart of the Old Quarter, this lake is a peaceful spot surrounded by legends and beautiful scenery.  Ngoc Son Temple: Situated on a small island in Hoan Kiem Lake, this temple is accessible via the iconic red Huc Bridge  Dong Xuan Market: The largest market in Hanoi, offering a wide variety of goods from fresh produce to souvenirs  Traditional Craft Streets: Each street in the Old Quarter is named after the goods that were traditionally sold there, such as Hang Bac (Silver Street) and Hang Gai (Silk Street)  The Old Quarter is a perfect place to explore on foot, allowing you to soak in the lively atmosphere and discover hidden gems around every corner.

📚交通  好多人話越南交通混亂。其實我既心得就係唔好當馬路係馬路，以無法為有法，去除人與車的觀念，總之大家都係路上移動的物件  地上的斑馬線連裝飾都不如，只是白色的長條灰塵  斑馬線前整一排電箱，非常神L奇。反映斑馬線在越南的作用為0  遇上了一個難得的交通燈。一開始大家都在等，可是等了好久都不變綠，某一時刻大家就一齊衝紅燈了。默契程度令我忍不住看了一眼交通燈是否有變綠，答案是沒有。但八九個人同時衝紅燈，路上的車也情緒很穩定  越南的人力車是乘客在前司機在後，在車禍發生時能夠很好的規避司機的風險😃而且人力車也有車牌  摩托車常常載著一家大細。三四個人等閒事，最多見過5個，2大人一青少年一兒童一BB，像越式法棍pro max  📚交通2  越南的喇叭聲沒有憤怒。香港的喇叭聲你可以聽得出是充滿感情的正在講粗口（長按、人聲墊底，某些行為後），一定會按到全場側目才會停止。越南喇叭聲更像是習慣或者在無聊的阻礙間隔中打個拍子。反正你擋他路他就按喇叭，不擋他路他也按喇叭。所以街上喇叭聲不絕，同時亦起不到任何作用  有輛警車開過，路上的車與人也是照擋不誤。穿著制服的警察一邊開一邊用車頂喇叭罵罵咧咧，但也沒什麼人讓路，所以他也就繼續一邊罵罵咧咧一邊烏龜爬，也不太動氣
非常熱鬧 有很多越南特色小吃、商店在裡面 人車很多 越南人開車、騎車、行人全部混在一起 沒在讓的 喇叭聲此起彼落 沒來過的人要過馬路都是問題 很刺激

2025年11月、初めてハノイを訪れ、旧市街に宿泊しました。これまでホーチミン、ムイネー、コンダオ、フーコックなどベトナムのいろいろな場所に行きましたが、旧市街の雰囲気はまさに別世界。雑貨巡りが本当に楽しく、歩いているだけでワクワクします。 夜になるとエリア全体がナイトマーケットに変わり、賑やかさと活気に包まれます。今回の旅行では旧市街しか訪れませんでしたが、それでも十分すぎるほど最高の時間を過ごせました。ハノイに行くなら、まずは旧市街を歩いてみることを強くおすすめします。

充滿著歐式風情的建築 到處是歐美背包客 許多自由行的旅客 這裡是河內的精華區 不適合懶惰的旅遊團 適合深度旅遊的獨立自由行 地面髒亂交通混亂 建築物很窄還好沒地震 附近有火車街 可以去看看 因為是國家文物 所以這裡的建築物受到保護 攤販的烤肉法棍三明治是台灣的三分之一價錢 很好喝的歐式冰飲料價錢也是 但是越南的物價並不便宜 跟台灣物價相同 難怪越南人要來台灣賺錢 貧富不均很嚴重 在越南人眼裡台灣充滿希望 銀樓換錢很方便 直接用台幣換 天氣很熱要有心裡準備

漫步在河內老城區，就像走進一個融合歷史與生活氣息的迷宮。狹窄的街道交錯縱橫，兩旁是色彩斑斕的法式老建築與越南傳統屋舍，牆面斑駁卻散發獨特韻味。街道上滿是小吃攤與咖啡館，濃郁的越南咖啡香、烤肉串的炭火味混雜在空氣裡，讓人忍不住停下腳步細細品嚐。騎樓下的小店販售著紀念品、絲綢與手工藝品，熙來攘往的摩托車穿梭其中，構成一幅極具活力的日常畫面。  白天的老城區熱鬧繁華，夜晚則更顯魅力。燈光映照在還劍湖畔，水面閃爍著粼粼波光，與街頭藝人的歌聲交織成一種溫暖的氛圍。坐在路邊的小椅子上喝一杯冰涼的啤酒，看著人群來來往往，既放鬆又充滿人情味。  老城區給我的感受，不只是旅遊景點的喧鬧，而是一種貼近越南人日常的真實體驗。它混亂卻有秩序，吵雜卻充滿溫度，讓人同時感受到歷史的厚度與生活的活力。走在這裡，每一步都像在與河內對話，也讓我更懂得欣賞這座城市獨特的靈魂。
繁華的老城區，日與夜都是遊客眾多， 沉浸熱鬧的氣氛中， 小不免有嘈雜的聲音，車內車往。有點昔日老香港的特色懷舊情懷。 心境也有點年青

Безусловно это необычное, колоритное место. Дух старого Ханоя. Утром, днем и вечером, кажется что попадаешь в разные места. Утром с 7.00 все пространство заполнено маленькими стульчиками. Сидят люди, пьют кофе. Запах жасмина и много много велосипедов с цветами. День жаркий, даже душный, в парках пусто. По узким улицам ездят торговцы. На прилавки выкладывается товар. Вечером все начинает гудеть, тысячи мопедов, тысячи людей разных национальностей начинают одновременно есть, пить, идти, сидеть. В первый раз можно испугаться всего этого хаоса, но потом становится все понятно и даже приспосабливаешься к этому и без проблем переходишь дорогу. Место которое обязательно к посещению в Ханое.

ベトナムに初めて来て楽しみにしていたのは、街並みを見て人々がどんな感じなのか、どんな文化なのか実際に見て、触れてみることでした。 実際にハノイの街を歩いてみてみると、高層ビルや高層マンションなどの近代的な高層建築物と一緒に昔ながらの古い平屋などが混在していて、昔の発展途上国とは違うんだなという風に感じました。 中心部は広い道路ですが、一つ道路を入るとすごく狭い路地があったりしてこのあたりもまだ成長している段階なんだなと思いました。 道路は車も多いのですが、なんといってもバイクがすごく多いのにはびっくりしました。 ベトナムの人々の交通手段として、重宝されているのがわかります。 実際に歩いてみて、ハノイの人たちの暮らしぶりも見られてとても良かったです。

Hanoï est ville très vivante, la vieille ville est tout le temps animée, surtout le soir. La circulation des motos et des voitures est dense, avec en permanence des bruits de klaxonne qui servent pas à grand chose puisqu’on ne sait plus d’où ils viennent. Ces bruits de klaxonne se mélangent à la musique des cafés qui font hurler leurs enceintes au maximum de leur capacité..et la nuit c’est pire. Mais c’est la vie de ce quartier qui mérite de s’y attarder.

老城區的中心就是著名的三十六古街，座落在河內的核心地帶，這次坐電瓶車遊覽古街，別有一番風味！也逛了附近的還劍湖和聖若瑟主教座堂，確實是感受當地日常生活及文化的絕佳地點。 三十六古街的每一條街都有不同主題，例如專門販賣飲料、藥材、編織品、玩具禮品、衣服等等，也是當地人平常生活購物的地方，白天時間的街道相當熱鬧，充滿在地的活力！

Le Vieux Quartier est le véritable cœur battant de Hanoï. Se perdre dans ses ruelles animées est une expérience unique, un mélange d'histoire, d'énergie et de saveurs incroyables. La street food est divine et l'ambiance autour du lac Hoan Kiem est magique. Un passage obligé pour ressentir l'âme du Vietnam.

雰囲気は良い。しかし交通状況がエキサイティングすぎるので歩行に注意。屋台や路面店の多くが料金を誤魔化して詐欺行為をする。注意点は500000ドン札を見せないこと。こちらがどのお札を出せば良いか迷っている時に500000ドン札を抜いて行く。もちろん全ての店では無い。真面目に商いをしている店もある。500000ドン札詐欺に気をつけること。

2015年の話で恐縮です。(備忘録)  ハノイはホーチミンより観光できる所が多いですね。その一つの旧市街です。  タンロン城の東にあった街の名残らしいです。古き良きベトナムの雰囲気を味わえます。お土産屋さんやお酒を飲めるお店が多く建ち並んでいるので外国人観光客だらけです。まあ実際行くと本当に雰囲気が良いので写真の腕が無い僕でも、それっぽい写真を撮ることができます。  まあちょっと見に行くと言う感じでしょうか。

Le cœur vibrant de Hanoï Le quartier vieux d'Hanoï est une expérience à part entière. C’est une fourmilière fascinante où chaque rue semble raconter une histoire différente. Malgré le bruit constant des klaxons et l’agitation permanente, on se laisse vite séduire par l'énergie unique qui se dégage de ces ruelles étroites.  On y trouve de tout : des échoppes artisanales, une cuisine de rue incroyable à chaque coin de trottoir et une architecture qui mélange habilement tradition et influences coloniales. C'est l'endroit idéal pour s'immerger dans la vie locale et ressentir l'âme de la capitale.  C’est un passage obligatoire pour comprendre Hanoï. Un désordre organisé qui finit par devenir très attachant.

🌟ベトナムの古都ハノイ。その中心部に位置する旧市街「36通り」は、一歩足を踏み入れるとまるで時間旅行をしているかのような感覚に包まれます🏮。細い路地や古い建物が立ち並び、歴史と文化が息づくこのエリアは、訪れる者を魅了してやみません。  🚶‍♂️まず最初に感じたのは、活気溢れる街並みと人々のエネルギーです。狭い通りにはバイクや自転車が行き交い、歩行者も多く、まさに生活の場といった雰囲気です。各通りには「Hàng（ハン）」という名前が付けられており、例えば「Hàng Bạc（銀製品通り）」や「Hàng Mã（紙装飾品通り）」など、その名の通り特定の商品を扱うお店が軒を連ねています🛍️。  ✨まずは「Hàng Bạc（ハンバック）通り」を歩いてみました。ここでは美しい銀製品やアクセサリーが数多く販売されており、細かな職人技に感心させられます💍。シルバーの指輪やネックレスが手頃な価格で手に入るので、自分へのご褒美やお土産にぴったりです。ただし、品質はお店によって異なるため、購入前によく確認することをおすすめします🔎。  🎋続いて「Hàng Mã（ハンマー）通り」へ。こちらはカラフルな紙製品や装飾品が所狭しと並べられており、祭事用の飾りやお供え物などが目を引きます。特に中秋節やテト（旧正月）の時期には、さらに華やかな雰囲気になるそうです🎎。写真映えするスポットが多いので、カメラ📸を持って散策すると楽しいですよ。  🍜歩き疲れたら、旧市街の至る所にあるローカルフードの屋台で一息つくのも良いでしょう。私は路地裏で見つけた小さなフォーのお店に立ち寄りました。熱々のスープに柔らかい麺、たっぷりのハーブが絶妙なバランスで、とても美味しかったです😋。価格もリーズナブルで、地元の人々に混じって食事をする体験は貴重でした。  🌳旧市街の中心に位置するホアンキエム湖は、街のオアシス的存在です。湖畔をゆっくりと散歩しながら、喧騒から離れてリラックスできます。湖に浮かぶ「玉山祠（ゴックソン祠）」は歴史的な建造物で、朱塗りの橋を渡って訪れることができます⛩️。内部には巨大な亀のはく製も展示されており、伝説や歴史に触れることができます。  🎭また、「タンロン水上人形劇場」での鑑賞もおすすめです。ベトナム北部の伝統芸能である水上人形劇は、独特のリズムと色彩で観る者を魅了します。言葉がわからなくても、音楽と動きで十分に楽しめます🎶。  🛍️旧市街最大の市場「ドンスアン市場」も外せません。ここでは衣料品や雑貨、食品などあらゆるものが手に入ります。ただし、市場内は非常に混雑しており、通路も狭いため、スリや置き引きには注意が必要です⚠️。バッグはしっかりと持ち、貴重品の管理を徹底しましょう。  🚦一方で、旧市街を歩く際に感じたのは、交通事情の混沌さです。道幅が狭い上にバイクや車が頻繁に行き交い、歩行者としては注意が必要です。信号があっても守られないことが多いため、道路を横断する際は地元の人々の動きを参考にすると良いでしょう🚶‍♀️。  🔊また、夜になるとバーやクラブから大音量の音楽が流れ、賑やかな雰囲気になります。特に「Tạ Hiện（ターヒエン）通り」はナイトライフの中心地で、多くの若者や旅行者で溢れかえります🍻。ビアホイ（生ビール）を片手に、新たな出会いや交流を楽しむことができますが、静かな環境を求める方には少し騒がしく感じるかもしれません。  🏨宿泊施設に関しては、旧市街には多くのゲストハウスやホテルがあります。リーズナブルな価格帯から高級ホテルまで選択肢は豊富ですが、事前に口コミや評価を確認しておくと安心です。私は小さなブティックホテルに滞在しましたが、スタッフが親切で居心地も良かったです🛌。  🚇ただし、交通手段に関しては少し戸惑う場面もありました。公共交通機関が複雑で、初めての訪問者には分かりにくいことがあります。タクシーや配車アプリを利用するとスムーズですが、料金交渉やぼったくりには注意が必要です💰。信頼できるアプリを使用するか、ホテルでタクシーを呼んでもらうと安心です。  🗺️旧市街は迷路のような構造になっており、道に迷うこともしばしば。地図アプリやガイドブックを活用して、目的地をしっかり確認しましょう。それでも迷ってしまった場合は、地元の人々に尋ねると親切に教えてくれることが多いです😊。  🌦️天候に関しては、季節によって大きく変わります。私が訪れた時期は暑さと湿度が高く、こまめな水分補給が欠かせませんでした🥤。また、突然のスコールもあるので、折りたたみ傘やレインコートを持参すると便利です。  💡旧市街を訪れる際のポイントをいくつかまとめます。  1. **歩きやすい靴を履くこと👟**：石畳や凸凹道が多いので、スニーカーなどの歩きやすい靴がおすすめです。  2. **小銭を用意すること💵**：屋台や小さなお店ではお釣りがない場合があるので、小額紙幣やコインを持っておくと便利です。  3. **衛生面に注意すること🚰**：屋台での食事は美味しいですが、敏感な方はボトル入りの飲み物を選ぶなどの対策を。  4. **価格交渉を楽しむこと🗣️**：市場や路面店では価格交渉が一般的です。無理のない範囲で交渉を楽しみましょう。  5. **文化や習慣を尊重すること🙏**：寺院や歴史的建造物を訪れる際は、服装やマナーに気を付けましょう。  🚮一方で、改善してほしい点も感じました。ゴミが散乱しているエリアがあり、美しい街並みが損なわれているのは残念です。環境保護の意識が高まれば、さらに魅力的な街になることでしょう。  📣また、一部の観光客向けのお店では強引な客引きがあり、少し戸惑うこともありました。はっきりと断る勇気も時には必要です。  🌍総じて、ハノイ旧市街「36通り」はベトナムの歴史と文化を深く感じることができる素晴らしい場所です。古き良き風景と現代の活気が融合したこのエリアは、何度訪れても新たな発見があります。  ✨もしハノイを訪れる機会があれば、ぜひ自分の足で旧市街を歩いてみてください。その独特な雰囲気と人々の温かさに触れることで、旅がより豊かなものになることでしょう。  🎒楽しい旅の思い出を作りつつ、マナーやルールを守って行動することで、お互いにとって素晴らしい時間を過ごせるはずです。安全に気を付けて、素敵な旅をお楽しみください🌈。

Zona brutal, varem aterrar a Hanoi i pensavem que seria una ciutat poc atractiva pero tot el contrari, el Old Quarter és increible, visita obligada, heu de anar a fer una volta, pendre algo i menjar en un dels "restaurants" locals. També trobareu les tipiques botigues amb roba North face, patagonia, etc i el carrer que passa el tren pel mig. En una part d' aquest carrer no hi varem poder caminar, hi havia un policia que no et deixava entrar, pero per un altre tros si tot i que no tenia tant encant.  També hi ha un mercat gegant amb escales mecàniques incloses on trobaràs tot el que necessitis.

Comment: 2024年10月　ベトナム1人旅の際、ローカルから有名店まで様々な飲食店がある。お土産を買うなら絶対にこのエリア。観光にも向いている。という情報をキャッチし、潜入。  ベトナムの首都ハノイの中心地とも言われるハノイ旧市街地とはどのような街なのか。  (結論) めっちゃ楽しい。  名前:ハノイ旧市街地 住所: P. Hàng Ngang, Hàng Đào, Hoàn Kiếm, Hà Nội 100000 ベトナム  ハノイ旧市街地は、ベトナム北部に位置し、ノイバイ空港から車でおよそ3-40分で到着出来ます。  フランス植民地時代の名残が残る建物が多く存在しているのが特徴で、平日休日問わず、常に地元民・観光客等で賑わっています。  キャッティがまず初めにオススメしたいのは、お金を日本円からベトナムドンに換金するなら、このハノイ旧市街地にあるhang bac(ハングバック)通り①Quang Huy    ②giang sonという換金所です。  街中には様々な換金所が溢れていますが、この2件は特にレートが良く、お店に訪れて「JAPAN」と伝えると、その日のレートを電卓で表示して教えてくれます。  ※「日本　ベトナム　レート」で調べると その日の1円の価値がベトナムドンで表示されます。 それを参考に、換金所が提示してきた額と比較し、2-3円でもお得であればレートとして良いです。  ※空港内は、すぐ換金出来るため便利ではありますが、かなり安く買い叩かれてしまいます。 ハノイ旧市街地に向かうタクシー代だけ換金し、残りはこちらのお店で換金した方が断然お得です。  レート確認し、問題なければ換金したい分だけのお金を店側に渡して下さい。スタッフは電卓で「その日のレート×渡した日本円」で合計金額を出してくれます。  それと同時に、ベトナムドンに換金されたお金を渡してくるので、電卓を見ながら枚数を確認しましょう。  さて、冒頭からお金の話をしてしまいましたが、ハノイ旧市街地のオススメは他にもあります。  例えば、街中のありとあらゆる所にあるのが、観光客を対象にしたギフトショップです。  キーホルダーといった小物から、衣類・食器・陶器物、大型のベトナム伝統人形やラグ・絨毯まで、お店によって似たり寄ったりな所はありますが、柄やデザイン、値段がお店によって異なるため、市内散策するだけで2-3日潰せてしまうほど、街中にお店が溢れています。  注意してほしいのが、お店に入るとスタッフが「これはどう？」「これ買ってよ」と営業トークをかけてくるという点です。  大阪のおばちゃんのように、観光客がお店入ってくる＝何か買ってもらおう、という感じのため、いらないなら「ノーセンキュー　いらない」「expinsive 値段が高い」と伝え、断りましょう。  買い物以外でオススメなのは飲食店です。 ハノイ市内では旧市街地を問わず、早朝は街のあちらこちらで、一般の家庭が自分たちの食事(だいたいフォー🍜)を家前の通りで作っています。  それと同時に、通りかかった人もフォーを食べられるよう、プラスチック製の椅子を通りに並べ、食べたいと申し出してきた人に１つ40000ドン(日本円で220円)で提供しています。  現地の人曰く、ベトナムでは定番の光景で、営業許可を取らなくても大丈夫とのこと。家庭によって味や具材も異なるらしく、一般家庭？飲食店？としてカウントして良いか不明な所がありますが、店側は小銭稼ぎでやっているらしいです。  ちなみにキャッティも、現地民に混じって食べましたが、家庭の味・本場のフォーのため、日本と異なりめちゃくちゃ美味しかったです。  パクチーは当たり前のように入ってますが、日本と異なり、ベトナムの料理はパクチーと食材が一体化しているため、ほとんど苦味を感じることなく食べられます。  ※キャッティは最初全く食べられなかったです。  ただし1点注意が必要で、絶対にスープは飲まないことです。きちんと手続きを踏んだ街中の飲食店は大丈夫ですが、一般家庭で提供されたフォーに使用されている水は、普通の水道水。いわば、飲水用の下処理がされていない水を使用しているということです。  現地の人は抗体があるため、飲んでも問題ないですが、日本人は腹を壊し、下痢の原因となります。注意しましょう⚠️  ※キャッティは、2時間後下痢となりました。マジで止まらなかったですね。  他にも街中には、ベトナムの代表料理であるバインミー・ブンボー・ブンチャーが食べられるお店がたくさんあるため、是非色んな飲食店をハシゴして見て下さい。  もちろん旧市街地は、観光地としてもオススメです。有名なのが「タンロン水上人形劇場」「ハノイ大教会」「ホアンキエム湖」金曜日の夜7時から日曜日の深夜まで続く「ナイトマーケット」そして、至る所にある寺院です。  ※口コミを投稿しています。よければそちらもご覧下さい。  旧市街地から外れた周辺にも色々と有名な観光地はありますが、市内だけでも十分魅力があるため、初めてベトナムに来国する場合、まずは市内観光をオススメします！  市内観光はもちろん徒歩が良いですが、移動手段の１つとして「シクロ」がオススメです。  シクロは、日本でいう人力車に近く、昔タクシーや車などの移動手段がなかった時に生まれた乗り物です。  現在は観光客を対象に、街中の至る所で見かけ、乗ることが出来ますが、ここも注意が必要です。  シクロの運転手は観光客を見つけると「乗らないか？」と営業トークをかけてきます。 乗る場合、ここに連れて行って欲しいと地図を見せれば、そこまでのんびり市内を見ながら移動出来るので、小休止にもちょうど良いです。  問題が料金で、人によって異なると思いますが、ぼったくりが多いと聞きます。1番安全なのが、ツアー会社を挟むこと。 2番目に安全なのが、ここまで連れて行ってもらう場合、料金は人数分も含めていくらになるのか、予め支払う料金を提示してもらう事です。  キャッティ的に、シクロは一回乗れば十分と考えているため、ベトナムの思い出として乗車するぐらいに留めましょう。  ※写真撮影も忘れなく。  最後に、旅行の必須パートナーをご紹介して終わりたいと思います。ベトナムはまだまだ発展途中の国であり、移動手段が豊富ではありません。  電車やバス、タクシー、シクロはありますが、場所が遠かったり、見つけにくかったり、ぼったくりと隣り合わせとなる可能性が高かったりします。  そこでオススメなのが「grab」という配車アプリです。使い方は簡単で、  アプリを開く→ bike(バイク)かcar(タクシー)を選択する→輸送欄の「どこへ？」に、自分が行きたい所の住所を入力、もしくはペースト→ choose  this pickupをクリック→ 近くにいるgrabタクシー一覧が表示され、そこまでかかる料金も出る→ 自分がいる現在地と、行きたい目的地が合っているか確認しつつ、そこに到着するのにかかる料金を比較。なるべく安いのを見つけてクリックする →最後に「を予約する　grab car」で、さらに対象のタクシーから絞る形で検索が始まります。  検索が終了し、車両が決定されると、タクシーもしくはバイクのナンバープレートと、運転手の顔写真が表示され、同時に自分がいる現在地、もしくはピックアップポイント(合流地点)に、リアルタイムで向かっている情報が表示されます。  後は待つか、合流地点に向かい、ナンバープレートと一致する車両を見つけて乗って、目的地に向かうだけです。  grabの特徴として 「支払い金額が事前に分かり、それ以上・以下の支払いは不要」 「ナンバープレートが登録されており、合流しやすい」 「顔写真登録　★で評価・口コミがあるため、安全かどうか判断出来る」 「誤操作・間違えて手配してもキャンセル可能　追加料金なし」 「タクシーもしくはバイクと、自分の旅行スタイルに合わせて選べる」 「リアルタイムで配車・自分の位置情報が分かる」 「ぼったくりがない」 「配車手配料金の上乗せがなく、送迎代のみでお手頃　チップ不要」 「登録者が多いため、市内のどこでも配車可能」　※市内から離れるとヒットしにくくなります 「1つ目の目的地で2-3分用を済ませた後、2つ目の目的地に向かうといった、連続送迎の選択も可能」 というのがあります。  欠点は、合流すると目的地に向かいますが、目的地手前で降ろしてもらっても、料金は最初に表示された金額を支払う事。目的地より少し先・全く違う所で降ろしてもらう場合、追加料金が必要となります。 ※金額は交渉次第です。Google翻訳機能にて交渉しましょう。  さて、色々と旧市街地の魅力をタラタラとお伝えしましたが、正直2割も満たない量しかお伝え出来てないと思っています。  後は実際に現地を訪れ、自分の目で見て体感し、良い思い出作りに繋げて頂ければと思います。  是非、旅行の際の参考に！ それでは、この辺で！ バイバイ👋　byキャット🐈

ハノイ旧市街は、ホアンキエム湖の北からドンスアン市場まで続く大規模な繁華街で、「ハノイ36通り」とも呼ばれています。通りごとに扱う品物が異なり、かご製品や銀細工、衣類などの問屋が並び、お土産探しにも最適です。地元の人々が利用するグルメ店も多く、フォーやブン・チャーなどのベトナム料理が楽しめます。週末にはナイトマーケットが開催され、屋台や雑貨店が並びます。また、ハノイ大教会やおしゃれなカフェも観光スポットとして人気です。ハノイ旧市街は、歴史と文化、グルメが詰まった散策にぴったりのエリアです。

ハノイを感じるならここですね。 映画やテレビでよく見る風景がそこにあります。フランス統治時代の洋風な建物と、いかにも東南アジアといった喧騒や活気が妙にマッチしています。 細い路地の道端に並べられた小さな椅子に腰かけて、地元の人や外国から来た観光客と一緒にローカルフードをつまみながらビールを飲むのは最高の贅沢ですね。

Wenn man nach Hanoi kommt, sollte man unbedingt sein Hotel I'm Alten Viertel wählen. Hier spielt sich das Leben ab. Es gibt viele Sehenswürdigkeiten, alles kann zu Fuß oder in kurzer Zeit mit öffentlichen Verkehrsmitteln erreicht werden und man kommt hier im Alten Zentrum schnell mm ist einheimischen ins Gespräch.

2025028星期五跟星期六有夜市，星期日不確定 路邊攤烤肉串很便宜，有一攤賣VND15,000/串,雞肉很嫩，另一攤豬肉VND10,000,但我只買雞肉，他的雞肉沒另一攤嫩！   有一攤檸檬茶中杯VND15,000/杯 好喝！ 沒拍到照片。 夜市很大！還劍湖附近也都可以逛，記得試試他們的水果，他們會加辣椒一起吃，水果也滿便宜的！ 在這邊買東西真的要殺價，有些開價滿離譜的。另外推薦這個椰子餅乾！ 只要有看的VND20,000直接買起來！ 比胡志明 芽莊 峴港 大叻 沙壩 還便宜😆

Quartire caratteristico immerso nella città, se potete prendere un albergo li è meglio, anche perche trovere di tutto, da locali a ristoranti alla via del black market, c'e tutto e di piu, a due passi dal laghetto artificiale, hanoi è una citta estremamente affascinante e non potete mancare in questa tappa, vedrete qualsiasi cosa e rimarrete a bocca aperta e stupiti della vita che fanno. Consiglio vivamente, non inoltratevi al difuori del perimetro della zona, perche si scende un pochino nel degrado, ma se siete dei fotografi sopratutto street invece consiglio vivamente di avventurarvi.
